# Kolm.ai 100 Percent Codebase Completion Blueprint

Date: 2026-05-25

Purpose: define what the current Kolm.ai codebase needs in order to reach a credible 100 percent completion state across functionality, UI/UX, docs, tests, operations, product truth, and claim honesty.

Scope: this document is based on a current worktree scan of `C:\Users\user\Desktop\kolmogorov-stack`. It is a planning and audit artifact only. It does not edit product code.

## Executive Standard

Kolm.ai is not done when pages render or tests pass. Kolm.ai is done when every product promise is:

1. implemented in the product path customers use;
2. represented in backend/API/CLI/TUI/account contracts;
3. visible through a clear, modern UI;
4. documented with a quick path and a deep reference;
5. tested by unit, integration, smoke, screenshot, and production probes;
6. backed by evidence, not unsupported claims;
7. consistent across pricing, docs, public pages, account, API metadata, OpenAPI, product graph, and sales copy;
8. accessible, responsive, fast, secure, and operationally observable;
9. honest about external gates such as live certifications, package releases, public benchmark data, and partner adoption.

The north-star product state:

> A user can route any model, capture approved behavior, distill repeated work into an owned artifact, verify the artifact, deploy it to chosen infrastructure or devices, operate it under reliability and governance controls, and understand every status, risk, cost, and next action from the account UI.

## External Standards To Bind Completion

UI/UX, accessibility, performance, security, and observability must be tied to external standards where practical.

Standards and references:

- WCAG 2.2: `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/`
- Core Web Vitals: `https://web.dev/articles/vitals`
- OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications / OWASP GenAI Security Project: `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/`
- OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions: `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/`
- NIST AI Risk Management Framework: `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework`
- SLSA provenance: `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.1/provenance`
- in-toto attestations: `https://in-toto.io/specs`
- CycloneDX ML-BOM: `https://www.cyclonedx.org/capabilities/mlbom`
- SPDX AI profile: `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/model/AI/AI/`
- C2PA specification: `https://spec.c2pa.org/specifications/specifications/2.1/specs/C2PA_Specification.html`

Completion thresholds derived from these:

- Accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA baseline, visible focus, keyboard navigation, labels, text contrast, no hover-only critical interactions, minimum target sizes, no layout requiring horizontal scroll.
- Performance: Core Web Vitals good thresholds at p75 across mobile and desktop: LCP <= 2.5s, INP <= 200ms, CLS <= 0.1. Lab evidence is useful but not enough; field measurement or production RUM is required for final proof.
- AI security: threat model and tests must cover prompt injection, insecure output handling, training-data poisoning, model denial-of-service, supply-chain vulnerabilities, sensitive information disclosure, insecure plugin/tool design, excessive agency, overreliance, and model theft risks.
- Observability: GenAI traces must include provider/model, route decision, artifact hash, token/cost metadata where safe, policy decisions, redaction/capture events, fallback cause, and error class without leaking secrets or private text.
- Provenance: build, artifact, model, dataset, eval, and runtime evidence must support signed, inspectable, revocable supply-chain metadata.

## Completion Definition By Product Surface

### Surface 1: Gateway, Capture, And Routing

Current promise:

- route model API traffic through Kolm;
- capture eligible behavior;
- preserve privacy and evidence;
- turn repeated usage into owned artifacts.

100 percent functionality requirements:

- OpenAI-compatible and provider-specific capture routes work for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, local gateway, and supported self-hosted runtimes.
- Provider/model catalog is generated or synchronized from a canonical registry, not repeated in page copy.
- Route decisions emit route receipts with provider, model, policy, confidence, fallback, cost, and evidence IDs.
- Capture data has rights, privacy, retention, poisoning, copyright, and tenant boundary checks before use in training or benchmarks.
- Repeated workflow detection creates actionable opportunities in account UI.
- Capture deletion, forget, opt-out, and revocation propagate into derived data and artifacts.
- Capture dashboards show volume, eligibility, privacy status, staleness, poisoning risk, and distill readiness.

100 percent UI/UX requirements:

- First setup must show a three-step path: connect provider, route first call, see captured call.
- Empty states must explain what is missing and how to fix it.
- Captured-call tables need clear status tokens: clean, quarantined, ineligible, redacted, eligible, stale, forgotten.
- Capture review must support keyboard actions, batch actions, undo, and visible audit history.
- No page may hide critical capture caveats behind hover-only UI.

Verification required:

- API tests for provider capture and route receipts.
- Account smoke for capture setup, capture log, capture review, analytics, and forget flows.
- CLI parity: `kolm capture`, `kolm tail`, privacy report, capture review.
- Production probe with real or mock provider credentials, with secret values redacted.

### Surface 2: Distillation, Training, Evaluation, And K-Score

Current promise:

- compile repeated work into smaller, cheaper, task-owned specialists;
- evaluate quality with K-Score and holdout evidence;
- improve via active learning and expert review.

100 percent functionality requirements:

- Distill-readiness engine explains whether a namespace has enough eligible traffic, labels, holdout, privacy clearance, and value to train.
- Teacher council, TAAS, curriculum distillation, DAQ, progressive distillation, contrastive distillation, reasoning-trace distillation, and active learning produce evidence packets, not just route entries.
- K-Score methodology is public, versioned, calibrated, and exportable.
- K-Score reports distinguish public benchmark, private holdout, synthetic fixture, and customer-private evidence.
- Quality calibration, redaction benchmark, benchmark evidence, and public leaderboard must not overclaim beyond the current evidence class.
- Training/distill runs produce reproducibility capsules with seeds, dataset hash, model/provider versions, artifact hash, runtime target, eval version, and cost.
- Every promotion has a rollback artifact and quality floor.

100 percent UI/UX requirements:

- Account surfaces show distill readiness as a decision dashboard, not a wall of metrics.
- Each run needs a concise status: ready, needs labels, needs holdout, needs privacy clearance, needs cloud config, failed, promoted.
- K-Score pages must show what the score means, what it does not mean, confidence interval, calibration date, benchmark scope, and examples.
- Expert review and active learning queues must prioritize expected product lift, not only volume.
- Distillation pages must avoid jargon-first copy. Lead with outcome: cost reduction, local ownership, reliable fallback, proof.

Verification required:

- `verify:redaction-benchmark`, `verify:kscore-calibration`, `verify:quality-calibration`, `verify:benchmark-evidence`.
- Distill-strategy simulation for at least one generation task and one extraction/redaction task.
- Account screenshot and interaction tests for distill runs, labeling, opportunities, active learning, datasets, bakeoffs, and K-Score.

### Surface 3: Compile, Artifact, Runtime, And Any-Device Deployment

Current promise:

- compile `.kolm` artifacts;
- sign and verify them;
- run them across cloud, browser, phone, VPC, BYOC, edge, and air-gap environments.

100 percent functionality requirements:

- Artifact passport includes manifest, model bundle, K-Score card, runtime profile, rights receipt, security profile, reliability profile, context profile, dependency graph, update policy, and revocation policy.
- Runtime passport lists target status: passed, degraded, untested, unsupported.
- Target claims require dated smoke evidence.
- BYOC, Kubernetes, air-gap, object storage, GPU, and deployment readiness expose clear missing configuration without leaking secret values.
- Runtime conformance matrix separates source-only adapters, package-published adapters, internal tests, third-party adoption, and production-supported targets.
- Artifact lifecycle supports release channels, promotion, rollback, revocation, lineage, and compatibility checks.
- Cost attribution measures baseline vs Kolm cost per successful task, including fallbacks, retries, reviews, storage, egress, and incident cost.

100 percent UI/UX requirements:

- Artifact detail page must answer: what does it do, what evidence supports it, where can it run, what changed, what can break, what is next.
- Runtime/deployment pages must not use generic "run anywhere" claims without target-specific readiness.
- Device fleet UI needs precise device status, memory, battery, thermal, offline mode, installed artifact, last smoke, and rollback.
- Air-gap and BYOC flows need operator-grade checklists and downloadable proof bundles.

Verification required:

- `verify:quant-oracle`, `verify:cloud-broker`, `verify:governance-packets`, `verify:package-release`.
- Local object-storage smoke and cloud readiness smoke.
- Runtime target smoke for at least local, browser, and one cloud/BYOC simulated target.
- Production smoke after deployment with configured credentials.

### Surface 4: Enterprise Governance, Security, Compliance, And Procurement

Current promise:

- make production AI auditable, governable, certifiable, and procurement-ready.

100 percent functionality requirements:

- Enterprise plan and billing state match public pricing and backend tiers.
- SSO/SCIM/RBAC/audit-log flows are either fully implemented or clearly marked as external/config-gated.
- Audit exports include actor, action, time, object, policy, evidence, hash, and tenant boundary.
- Compliance pages distinguish implemented controls from live certifications.
- Assurance case export pulls from product graph, evidence DAG, readiness ledger, controls, claims, and limitations.
- Security evidence covers OWASP LLM risks, supply-chain metadata, prompt/context poisoning, tool misuse, data disclosure, model theft, and incident replay.
- Procurement questionnaire answers are evidence-backed and never synthesize unsupported claims.

100 percent UI/UX requirements:

- Enterprise pages must route to contact sales / architecture review when sales review is required.
- Trust pages must be calm, structured, and date-scoped.
- Account governance pages need admin-grade density: filters, exports, status tokens, owners, next actions.
- Every compliance badge must have a status: implemented, in progress, external certification required, not supported.

Verification required:

- Claim-scope audit.
- Compliance certification packet verifier.
- Audit/export smoke.
- Account screenshots for billing, audit log, approvals, API keys, security, storage, settings, and enterprise readiness.

### Surface 5: Agents, Registry, Marketplace, And Ecosystem

Current promise:

- serve `.kolm` artifacts through agents, tools, SDKs, registry, marketplace, and developer workflows.

100 percent functionality requirements:

- Registry listings rank artifacts by comparable trust, not popularity alone.
- Marketplace listings show proof status, runtime targets, K-Score scope, license, price, publisher, update policy, and limitations.
- Agent artifacts include tool receipts, delegated authority, memory provenance, sandbox status, rollback or compensation path, and incident history.
- SDKs and loaders have conformance suites, package release status, install commands, and first-run smoke tests.
- CLI/TUI/account/API/docs all expose the same product graph and readiness counts.
- Ecosystem standard packet includes manifest schemas, media types, model/data/K-score cards, conformance suite, examples, limitations, and governance proposal.

100 percent UI/UX requirements:

- Registry and marketplace search must support filters by task, runtime, trust, evidence, license, price, and verified status.
- Listings must not bury limitations below marketing text.
- Developer onboarding should show a 5-minute path for API, CLI, SDK, and artifact verification.
- Agent pages need timeline views for task, tool, authority, evidence, and rollback status.

Verification required:

- Registry compare tests.
- SDK package readiness and smoke installers.
- CLI docs honesty tests.
- Account screenshots for marketplace, artifacts, agents, registry, API keys, and installs.

## Global UI/UX Completion Requirements

The current UI has many surfaces and many CSS systems. Completion requires consolidation around a product-grade design system.

### Navigation

Requirements:

- One canonical nav model for public site, docs, account, and marketing.
- No inconsistent underlines, hover-only reveals, or mismatched active states.
- Desktop mega menus must support keyboard and pointer.
- Mobile navigation must not hide core product surfaces.
- Account navigation must map to the 12 journeys and show current status or attention counts.
- Every nav item must have one primary destination and one reason to exist.

Current evidence to address:

- Product has hundreds of pages and many overlapping public categories.
- User previously reported underline/highlight/popout issues.
- The codebase has multiple CSS systems, which increases nav drift risk.

### Copy And Information Architecture

Requirements:

- Hero must explain all three surfaces in under five seconds:
  - route any model;
  - compile and distill repeated work;
  - run owned artifacts anywhere with proof.
- Copy should lead with customer outcomes, then technical mechanism.
- Every page needs one clear H1 and one primary action.
- Technical pages can go deep, but first screen must answer who it is for and what it does.
- Remove stale/contradictory pricing and plan language.
- Model/provider names must be generated from current catalog or clearly dated.

Current evidence to address:

- `public/index.html` currently has two H1s and an overloaded first H1.
- `public/product.html` currently has two H1s.
- Homepage metadata is long.
- Pricing metadata currently includes Business/$1,499 while prior product contract aimed at a Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise model.
- Public copy repeats broad category language across account pages.

### Visual System

Requirements:

- Define one enterprise SaaS visual language: restrained, precise, high-contrast, low-noise, operationally dense.
- Use a controlled token set for color, spacing, radius, shadow, typography, z-index, focus, and motion.
- Stop adding one-off CSS systems without mapping them into design tokens.
- Buttons, cards, tables, status pills, tabs, forms, nav, popovers, modals, and tooltips must share components.
- State colors must be semantic and accessible: success, warning, danger, info, neutral, disabled.
- Do not rely on color alone; status tokens need text or icons.

Current evidence to address:

- 20 public CSS files totaling 786 KB.
- `brand-refresh.css` and `surface-polish.css` each contain hundreds of unique color literals.
- `home-refresh.css` has many raw colors and no token definitions.

### Account UX

Requirements:

- Account overview becomes the command center for the full product matrix.
- It must show product graph readiness, open gates, next actions, storage/cloud readiness, billing, current plan, capture status, artifact status, and active risks.
- Every account page must have:
  - clear page title and useful meta description;
  - loading, empty, success, warning, and error states;
  - keyboard-operable controls;
  - export or CLI equivalent where relevant;
  - direct links to docs and API route references;
  - no fake data unless labeled as demo.

Current evidence to address:

- `public/account/overview.html` has a short description: `Operate Kolm`.
- `public/account/active-learning.html` has a truncated description.
- Some account pages have no `kfetch` calls and may be static shells; completion requires classifying each as live, local demo, static docs shell, or needing implementation.

### Docs UX

Requirements:

- Docs must have a clear left nav, search, copy buttons, version labels, SDK tabs, API route cross-links, and "last verified" status.
- Each guide needs:
  - prerequisites;
  - 5-minute happy path;
  - real commands;
  - expected output;
  - troubleshooting;
  - account/API/CLI/TUI equivalents.
- API docs must state auth, request, response, errors, rate limits, and proof/evidence IDs.
- CLI docs must not advertise commands that are not shipped.

Current evidence to address:

- Docs surface is large: 221 pages.
- CLI/API route generation exists, but stubs remain in route inventory.
- Completion requires docs generated from canonical contracts, then reviewed for product clarity.

### SEO And Conversion UX

Requirements:

- Canonical metadata per page.
- Clean titles under roughly 60 characters where possible.
- Descriptions generally under roughly 155-160 characters unless intentionally longer.
- One H1 per page.
- Schema.org where relevant: SoftwareApplication, Product, FAQPage, Article, BreadcrumbList, Organization.
- OpenGraph/Twitter images render without mojibake, wrong brand, or stale pricing.
- Conversion journeys:
  - developer quickstart;
  - CTO/enterprise trust;
  - cost/ROI calculator;
  - distillation demo;
  - runtime/device demo;
  - procurement/contact sales.

Current evidence to address:

- Titles/descriptions mostly exist but some are too long or stale.
- Current scan found missing/short descriptions and duplicate H1s.
- No video elements are currently present despite prior requirement for product demo video; completion must decide whether the video requirement is still strategic or replaced by interactive demos.

## Backend/API Completion Requirements

### Route Stub Burn-Down

127 route records are marked stub. This does not mean every stub is bad, but every stub must be classified.

Classifications:

- real implementation behind simple route wrapper;
- intentionally local/demo/simulated;
- external-provider gated;
- generated read-only inventory;
- incomplete and must be implemented;
- deprecated and must be removed from docs/routes.

High-priority stub prefixes:

- `/v1/ab-tests`
- `/v1/approvals`
- `/v1/cloud`
- `/v1/autopilot`
- `/v1/capture`
- `/v1/drift`
- `/v1/audio`
- `/v1/compliance`
- `/v1/kolmbench`
- `/v1/lake`
- `/v1/marketplace`
- `/v1/poisoning`
- `/v1/redteam`
- `/v1/registry`
- `/v1/residency`
- `/v1/security`
- `/v1/synthetic`
- `/v1/video`
- `/v1/xlang`

Completion rule:

- No public/account/docs page may treat a stub route as production unless route readiness says it is implemented or externally gated with explicit configuration.

### Router Size And Maintainability

Current evidence:

- `src/router.js` is about 953 KB.

Completion requirements:

- Route modules should be grouped by product surface and ownership.
- API envelope shape must be consistent.
- Auth, tenant boundary, audit logging, error handling, rate limits, and secret redaction should be middleware or shared helpers.
- Generated OpenAPI and API docs must remain in sync.
- Adding a route must require product-surface ownership, docs, tests, and smoke classification.

### Product Truth Spine

Completion requirements:

- `docs/product-journeys.json`, `docs/product-surfaces.json`, `docs/product-sota-readiness.json`, `public/product-graph.json`, OpenAPI, API routes, CLI docs, account pages, and claim-scope checks must agree.
- Every non-final readiness item must carry closeout proof.
- Account UI must consume product graph and readiness closeout directly.
- Product graph must identify API, account, docs, CLI, TUI, and smoke proof for each journey.

## Testing And Verification Completion Matrix

No single command proves the whole product. Completion needs layered gates.

Required local gates:

- `npm run lint`
- `npm run lint:refs`
- `npm run verify:depth`
- `npm run verify:claims-scope`
- `npm run verify:brand`
- `npm run local:surfaces`
- `npm run local:surfaces:deep`
- `npm run ui:audit:all:themes`
- `npm run release:verify`

Required focused gates:

- K-Score calibration.
- Redaction benchmark.
- Quality calibration.
- Benchmark evidence.
- Governance packets.
- Compliance packet.
- Package release readiness.
- Cloud broker.
- Distill strategy.
- Federated tests.
- Product frontier contracts and operator kernels.

Required visual gates:

- Dark/light screenshots.
- Desktop/mobile screenshots.
- Account authenticated screenshots.
- Public marketing screenshots.
- Docs screenshots.
- High-risk conversion pages: home, product, pricing, enterprise, ROI, quickstart, compile, distill, runtimes, account overview.
- Manual review for nav, hero, buttons, popovers, modals, tables, forms, and empty states.

Required production gates:

- `prod:surfaces:deep --require-auth` after deployment.
- Live cloud/storage/GPU/provider/SSO readiness with real production credentials.
- Core Web Vitals field telemetry or production RUM.
- Production screenshot audit.
- Search/social preview validation.
- Security headers and CSP validation.
- Smoke of signup/login/account/plan/contact-sales flows.

## 100 Percent Completion Backlog

P0 blockers:

- Resolve pricing/product contract drift: remove or intentionally support Business/Starter language everywhere.
- Fix homepage and product duplicate H1s.
- Rewrite homepage hero around the three product surfaces.
- Classify and burn down or scope the 127 stub route records.
- Classify every account page as live, demo, static shell, or incomplete.
- Consolidate public CSS into a token-governed design system.
- Ensure account overview covers full product matrix with live readiness and next actions.
- Make claims scope hard-fail when public copy advertises non-final readiness as shipped.
- Run full local and visual gates after frontend/backend edits settle.

P1:

- Provider/model name catalog sync.
- Docs IA cleanup and generated API/CLI consistency.
- Runtime target passport UI.
- Artifact lifecycle UI and API proof.
- Enterprise assurance case export UX.
- Registry comparable-trust ranking.
- Continuous background distillation candidate UX.
- ReliabilityIR and behavior status page.
- Security moat compiler and incident replay integration.

P2:

- Field RUM for Core Web Vitals.
- Interactive homepage `.kolm` demo or product-guided compiler flow.
- Product video or equivalent interactive proof demo.
- Marketplace publisher verification.
- SDK/package publication matrix.
- External runtime adoption packets.
- Public benchmark freshness scheduler.
- Mechanistic evidence packet for open-model artifacts.

P3:

- Full standard packet for `.kolm`.
- Partner certification workflows.
- Insurable AI/warranty model.
- Cross-enterprise private benchmark collaboration.
- Kernel marketplace and hardware co-design loop.
- Formal proof obligations beyond current artifact verification.

## Implementation Agent Work Packet Template

Every implementation ticket created from this blueprint should include:

- product surface;
- user outcome;
- current evidence/gap;
- exact files likely involved;
- API/CLI/account/docs surfaces affected;
- data model or contract;
- UI states;
- accessibility requirements;
- tests;
- screenshot requirements;
- claim-scope impact;
- rollback plan;
- verification commands.

Example:

```text
Surface: pricing and account billing
Outcome: public pricing, backend billing tiers, upgrade UI, account state, and sales CTAs agree.
Current evidence: public/pricing.html title and description mention Business $1,499; readiness prior expected Enterprise sales-reviewed.
Likely files: public/pricing.html, public/index.html, public/account/billing.html, src/router.js, billing modules, OpenAPI/API docs.
Tests: billing tiers, claim scope, refs, screenshot focused pricing/account.
Done when: no stale plan names remain except backward-compatible internal mapping explicitly marked legacy.
```

## Atomic Codebase Review And Build Redline V1

This section converts the blueprint from strategy into a finish line for the actual repository. The goal is not another scanner result. The goal is a review protocol and build redline that implementation agents can apply file by file until every Kolm surface is either production-complete, intentionally removed, or explicitly marked as an external proof gate.

### Repository Shape To Finish

Latest source inventory excluding heavy generated/dependency directories:

| area | files | bytes | redline meaning |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| public HTML | 729 | 14,786,312 | every page needs a clear owner, purpose, SEO contract, nav state, responsive screenshot, and stale-copy audit |
| account HTML | 48 | 1,054,844 | every post-auth page needs live/loading/empty/error states and product-graph/readiness alignment |
| docs HTML | 221 | 3,560,376 | every docs page needs a runnable path, adjacent API/CLI/SDK link, and no dead-end onboarding |
| public research HTML | 59 | included above | every research page must distinguish invention, benchmark evidence, and shipped behavior |
| src JS modules | 391 | multiple MB | every backend module needs route/API owner, tests, failure modes, and docs or explicit internal-only status |
| tests | 559 | 7,305,479 | tests must map to product surfaces and prove behavior, not only existence |
| scripts | 217 | 1,336,958 | scripts must be idempotent, documented, safe on dirty trees, and wired into release gates when critical |
| docs markdown | 101 | 5,731,729 | docs must be consolidated around the product graph and not contradict pricing, readiness, or support scope |
| package source files | 132 | 367,634 | package manifests must either build/publish or state local-source-only scope |
| SDK source files | 49 filtered source files | 439,312 | SDKs must compile, smoke, document install paths, and expose feature parity or explicit gaps |
| workers | 43 | 346,161 | worker deploy targets need runtime config, failure handling, and smoke tests |
| apps | 164 | 1,569,925 | non-JS services such as trainer/runtime apps need their own build and smoke lane |

Important backend clarification: the main web/API backend in this repository is Node/JavaScript (`server.js`, `src/router.js`, and the `src/*.js` modules). The broader backend stack is not only JavaScript: trainer and runtime app code also exists under `apps/`, with Python-heavy ML/distillation workers, plus SDK/package code across TypeScript, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, C, Rust, Python, MCP, VS Code, worker, and package manifests. The completion plan must treat Node as the API control plane and the non-JS apps/packages as first-class execution surfaces.

### Largest Files That Require Manual Completion Review

These files have enough surface area that "tests pass" is not sufficient. Each needs a deliberate code-read pass with an owner, route map, and split/refactor decision.

| file | reason it is high-risk | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `src/router.js` | very large route/control-plane file; central API behavior and many generated docs depend on it | route groups must be modularized or at least indexed by owner; every stub route must be classified; OpenAPI/API docs must regenerate cleanly |
| `public/index.html` | homepage is the conversion front door and currently has overloaded metadata/first-screen messaging risk | one H1, concise title/description, three-product-surface clarity, professional hero, no jargon-first copy |
| `public/pricing.html` | pricing copy is a high-conversion and high-trust surface with known plan-name drift risk | Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise contract must agree with backend billing, upgrade UI, docs, and sales flow |
| `public/docs/api.html` | large generated API reference; easy to publish stale or hidden routes | must match `public/docs/api-routes.json` and OpenAPI exactly after route changes |
| `public/account/overview.html` | post-auth product matrix command center | must show live readiness, product graph, closeout gates, billing, storage/cloud/provider state, and next actions without hidden caveats |
| `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs` | account page generator can multiply mistakes across post-auth UI | generator must enforce tokens, nav, loading/error/empty state conventions, and product-surface ownership |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` | screenshot proof system | must remain stable after frontend edits and produce route/theme/viewport evidence that humans can inspect |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` | release gate | must sequence generated artifacts serially and fail on stale docs, stale graph, stale OpenAPI, or claim-scope drift |

### Source Module Completion Redline

The source inventory maps to product surfaces. Each bucket below is not complete until every file in the bucket has: exported contract, tests, error handling, docs/API/account/CLI/TUI exposure where user-facing, claim-scope guardrails, and production smoke where applicable.

| bucket | files | current completion redline |
|---|---:|---|
| compile, artifact, runtime | 30 | `.kolm` compile/run/verify/import/export must have deterministic artifacts, versioned schemas, compatibility tests, runtime failure messages, and benchmark links |
| gateway, capture, privacy | 35 | capture wrappers, redaction, poisoning, analytics, multimodal capture, and smart filters must prove data-shape preservation and quarantine paths |
| distill, train, eval, K-Score | 56 | every distillation method needs teacher/student inputs, holdout metrics, K-Score report, failure diagnostics, rollback, and public benchmark scope |
| cloud, deploy, device, storage | 22 | every provider/device/storage path needs readiness, credentials-without-secrets display, local smoke, remote smoke, and max-size/region limits |
| governance, security, compliance | 24 | every compliance claim needs evidence ID, export path, audit log, scope label, and "implemented vs certified" wording |
| identity, access, billing | 15 | auth, teams, SSO, SCIM, RBAC, usage, billing, invoices, plans, and sales gating must agree across API, account, docs, and CLI |
| observability, reliability, ops | 24 | logs, OTEL, drift, failure modes, autopilot, events, changelog, and status must have dashboards plus machine-readable exports |
| marketplace, agents, ecosystem | 20 | registry, packages, agents, plugins, and marketplace flows must distinguish local artifacts, signed artifacts, third-party packages, and external adoption gates |
| product truth, research, frontier | 17 | product graph, readiness, models, opportunities, and frontier contracts must remain the single source of truth for public copy and account UI |

### Route Stub Redline

The generated API route inventory currently contains 582 records, 552 unique paths, and 127 stub records. A stub route is acceptable only if it is an intentional placeholder with visible scope, not if it silently pretends a feature is complete.

P0 stub groups to classify and close first:

| route group | stub count | redline |
|---|---:|---|
| `/v1/ab-tests` | 6 | either implement assignment/promote/rollback/stop with persistent experiment state, or hide from product copy until shipped |
| `/v1/approvals` | 5 | approval workflows need real states, notification failure modes, audit trail, and account UI parity |
| `/v1/cloud` | 5 | deploy/distill/broker stubs must connect to readiness and job status or be scoped as local-plan-only |
| `/v1/autopilot` | 4 | autopilot must prove human control, rollback, and no silent production mutation |
| `/v1/capture` | 4 | capture stubs must preserve SDK/API/account semantics and privacy quarantine paths |
| `/v1/drift` | 4 | drift routes must include baseline definition, alert thresholds, replay, and remediation |
| `/v1/audio` | 3 | audio capture/distill/tokenize needs multimodal eval evidence and failure-mode docs |
| `/v1/compliance` | 3 | compliance routes must not imply certification; they need evidence packets and export records |
| `/v1/kolmbench` | 3 | benchmark routes need reproducible datasets, raw reports, model versions, and freshness dates |
| `/v1/marketplace` | 3 | marketplace install/publish flows need signed artifact checks, ownership, rollback, and policy review |
| `/v1/poisoning` | 3 | poisoning detection needs quarantine decisions, explainability, and false-positive handling |
| `/v1/redteam` | 3 | red-team routes need fixtures, severity, reproducibility, and account export |
| `/v1/registry` | 3 | registry operations need trust model, signatures, namespace ownership, and artifact compatibility |
| `/v1/residency` | 3 | residency needs region enforcement, audit evidence, and user-visible data locality state |
| `/v1/security` | 3 | security routes need SBOM/SLSA/signing proof and dated evidence |
| `/v1/synthetic` | 3 | synthetic data generation must expose provenance, privacy risk, and eval impact |
| `/v1/video` | 3 | video multimodal paths need tokenization, eval, storage, and cost boundaries |
| `/v1/xlang` | 3 | cross-lingual distillation needs language-specific eval, fallback, and K-Score calibration |

Completion rule: every stub must become one of four states in the route ledger: `implemented`, `external_proof_gate`, `internal_only`, or `removed_from_public_surface`. No route may remain "stub" and simultaneously appear as a finished product capability in marketing, account, docs, or sales copy.

### Public Site And UI/UX Completion Redline

The UI surface is too broad to trust by visual feel alone. The following issues were found from source review and need completion passes after the frontend lane settles:

- `public/index.html` has overloaded title/description and two H1 candidates. Completion requires one category-defining H1 that explains all three product surfaces: model API ownership, compile/distill top models, and enterprise-to-device deployment.
- `public/product.html` has multiple H1s. Completion requires one primary page promise and a clear product matrix below it.
- `public/account/overview.html` has an insufficient meta description. Completion requires post-auth copy that explains the operational command center, not generic "Operate Kolm" text.
- `public/dpa.html` has insufficient metadata. Completion requires trust/legal metadata that is precise and not overclaiming certification.
- Public pages still contain hash-only links in five known pages. Completion requires either real anchors, buttons with JS behavior and ARIA state, or removal.
- There are zero video elements and zero iframe elements in the static scan. If the product strategy requires a demo video or interactive runner, this is not present as a real embedded media surface in the static HTML inventory.
- Public CSS is fragmented across large overlapping files. `public/brand-refresh.css`, `public/surface-polish.css`, `public/warm-paper.css`, `public/styles.css`, `public/frontier.css`, `public/ks.css`, and `public/home-refresh.css` together contain hundreds of color literals and duplicated style decisions. Completion requires token consolidation, component-state consistency, and removal of one-off page styling unless deliberately scoped.
- Account pages must not be ornamental dashboards. Every metric card, readiness token, table, CTA, and next action must be backed by live API data, a generated local fallback, or a visible unavailable state.

UI/UX done means every page has a defined type: conversion, docs, account operation, trust/legal, benchmark/evidence, marketplace, comparison, or research. A page without a type cannot be completed because there is no objective way to judge layout density, tone, CTAs, or success state.

### Build Completion Gates

The codebase can only enter a "finished build redline" state after all concurrent agents stop editing and the combined tree passes the following in sequence:

1. Generated artifacts are refreshed serially, not in parallel:
   - `npm run build:product-graph`
   - `npm run build:readiness-closeout`
   - API route ref/OpenAPI generation commands used by the repo
2. Static contract gates:
   - `node --check` for touched JS/CJS/MJS files
   - `npm run verify:sota`
   - `npm run verify:kernel`
   - `npm run verify:depth`
   - `npm run lint:refs`
3. Product-surface gates:
   - `npm run local:surfaces`
   - `npm run local:surfaces:deep` if available in the current script set
   - product graph route probes for `/v1/product/graph` and `/product-readiness-closeout.json`
4. Full behavior gates:
   - full Node test suite under the repo's canonical command
   - focused tests for every changed backend surface
   - SDK/package smoke for every changed SDK/package
5. UI evidence gates:
   - full dark/light desktop/mobile screenshot audit after frontend edits settle
   - focused manual screenshot review of changed conversion, pricing, docs, account, and trust pages
   - keyboard navigation, focus state, touch target, and reduced-motion checks on representative pages
6. Production gates after deploy:
   - production smoke of public pages, authenticated account pages, billing/plan endpoints, product graph, storage/cloud readiness, and API docs
   - confirmation that production env vars are present where local readiness cannot prove R2/S3/GPU/SSO/OTEL
   - no public copy claiming package releases, live certifications, public benchmark superiority, or third-party ecosystem adoption unless linked evidence exists

Until all gates above are green on the combined tree, "deployable" can be true for a lane, but "100 percent complete" is false.

### Atomic File Review Protocol

Every file in the repository should eventually receive a row in a per-file ledger with these fields:

```text
path:
owner surface:
file role:
runtime language:
public or internal:
current status: complete | needs implementation | needs refactor | generated | external proof gate | delete candidate
imports/dependencies:
exports/contracts:
routes/pages/scripts affected:
tests proving behavior:
docs/account/CLI/TUI exposure:
security/privacy impact:
performance impact:
accessibility/UX impact:
claim-scope impact:
known defects:
completion action:
done proof:
```

The first priority is not to rewrite every file. The first priority is to prevent hidden unknowns. Any file that cannot be classified with the ledger fields above is unfinished by definition.

### Code Completion Priority Matrix

P0 before the next "finished" claim:

- Classify all 127 API stubs and close or scope the highest-risk route groups.
- Confirm pricing plan names, amounts, Enterprise sales flow, and legacy aliases across API, account, CLI, docs, OpenAPI, and public pages.
- Reconcile homepage/product/pricing copy around the three real product surfaces instead of a single "one API" or vague "owned AI" story.
- Make account overview the operational product matrix: graph, readiness, storage/cloud, providers/models, billing, capture, compile/distill, devices, and closeout gates.
- Add source-level SEO/copy gates for duplicate H1s, thin descriptions, stale plan names, and unsupported claims.
- Consolidate design tokens and stop multiplying visual systems across public CSS.
- Run a current full release gate after all agents merge.

P1:

- Modularize or index `src/router.js` by route group so new waves do not hide in a monolith.
- Build public benchmark packets for K-Score, redaction, distillation quality, cost, latency, and device deployment.
- Add per-SDK package readiness proof and publish/dry-run artifacts.
- Add cross-language worker/app smoke for Python trainer/runtime code, not only Node tests.
- Create docs IA where every guide has a next step, API reference, CLI command, SDK example, and account UI path.

P2:

- Formalize third-party runtime adoption packets for `.kolm` loaders.
- Create marketplace trust and artifact policy review loops.
- Add compliance evidence exports for SOC 2, ISO, HIPAA, GDPR, EU AI Act, SBOM, and SLSA without overclaiming certification.
- Build an internal quality bar that treats design, docs, backend, account, SDKs, and deploy as one release system.

### Branding And Runtime-Language Redline

The Kolmogorov-to-Kolm cleanup is not cosmetic. It affects trust, search snippets, browser tabs, legal docs, SDK install commands, and every screenshot. A focused source/public scan found 679 included files with 1,936 `kolmogorov` occurrences. The distribution was:

| area | occurrences |
|---|---:|
| `public` | 1,926 |
| `tests` | 3 |
| `cli` | 2 |
| `docs` | 2 |
| `infra` | 1 |
| `scripts` | 1 |
| `tools` | 1 |

Top public files needing brand review include:

- `public/brand/github-org-decision.html`
- `public/sdks.html`
- `public/docs/multi-region.html`
- `public/index.html`
- `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html`
- `public/docs/quickstart/node.html`
- `public/manifesto.html`
- `public/about.html`
- `public/docs/quickstart/index.html`
- `public/faq.html`

Brand completion rule: every visible `Kolmogorov`, `kolmogorov-stack`, or legacy spelling must be replaced with `Kolm` or a deliberate historical/legal reference. Legal entity names, repository paths, package names, namespace IDs, and redirects must be reviewed before mechanical replacement. Public copy gets replaced first; code identifiers are replaced only after tests and compatibility aliases are planned.

#### Node 0 Brand Migration And Legacy Exception Ledger V2

Node `52` remains unused. The user's "remove all Kolmogorov to Kolm" request belongs to node `0`, because it is a truth and brand problem that crosses public copy, package names, GitHub URLs, deploy origins, SDK install commands, generated docs, tests, screenshots, logs, archives, and compatibility aliases. The goal is not a blind string replace. The goal is that every remaining legacy string is either gone from shipped product surfaces or recorded as a justified compatibility, legal, historical, deployment, archive, or evidence exception.

##### Current Brand Recheck V2

A current scan excluding `.git`, `node_modules`, `target`, large nested screenshot folders, `tmp`, `tmp-screenshots`, `data`, and `.tmp` still found `2,619` hit lines across `800` files for `kolmogorov`, `Kolmogorov`, or `KOLMOGOROV`.

| Top-level area | Files with hits | Hit lines | Completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `public` | 673 | 1,915 | Highest priority because it directly affects users, search, screenshots, docs, install commands, and buyer trust. |
| temp path artifact `C:...test-sweep-2.txt` | 1 | 332 | Should be classified as temp/evidence and either deleted, archived, or excluded from product scans. |
| `reports` | 37 | 133 | Evidence artifacts need retention policy; stale screenshots/reports should not drive public copy. |
| `docs` | 6 | 50 | Research or historical references need exception labels; product docs need cleanup. |
| `apps` | 35 | 35 | ML/runtime code needs identifier review before rename to avoid breaking imports or fixtures. |
| `packages` | 13 | 31 | Package metadata and install commands require compatibility migration, not blind replacement. |
| `.npm-cache` and package caches | 3 | 19 | Cache artifacts should not ship and should be excluded or purged from release evidence. |
| `backups`, `qa`, `_audit`, `audit-shots`, logs, TAP files | multiple | mixed | Evidence/archive paths need classification; shipped product scans should not treat them as live copy unless retained intentionally. |
| `vercel.json` | 1 | 5 | Deployment aliases and CSP/connect-src entries require coordinated backend-origin migration. |
| `sdk` | 7 | 12 | SDK package names, README examples, and repository URLs need source-compatible aliases. |
| `cli`, `scripts`, `tools`, `infra`, `tests` | small counts | small counts | Low count does not mean low risk; command names, fixtures, and routing aliases can break compatibility. |

Concrete high-risk examples from the current tree:

| Path | Current signal | Required decision |
|---|---|---|
| `public/docs/quickstart/node.html` | Advertises `npm install @kolmogorov/sdk`, `pnpm add @kolmogorov/sdk`, `yarn add @kolmogorov/sdk`, and `import { Kolm } from "@kolmogorov/sdk";`. | Replace with the actual current package name if published, or mark source/local package state honestly. If an old package exists, deprecate and redirect. |
| `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html` | Advertises `@kolmogorov/mcp` and `npx @kolmogorov/mcp`. | Align with the actual MCP package (`@kolm/recipe-mcp` currently appears in `sdk/mcp/package.json`) or provide compatibility alias proof. |
| `packages/*/package.json`, `sdk/*/package.json` | Repository metadata still points at `github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` or git URLs under that org. | Decide whether this is current canonical source, a temporary compatibility URL, or a stale metadata defect. |
| `vercel.json` | GitHub redirect points to `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`; backend rewrites and CSP include `kolmogorov-stack-production.up.railway.app`. | Backend-origin renames require DNS/deploy coordination, redirect checks, CSP update, and production smoke. |
| `public/brand/github-org-decision.html` | Published page says no rename is scheduled before Q3 2026 and discusses `sneaky-hippo`, `kolm-ai`, and `kolmogorov-ai`. | Either keep as a marked historical/internal decision page or remove from public navigation/search if it undermines enterprise brand trust. |
| `public/index.html` and `public/sdks.html` | Multiple links still point to `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`; homepage hidden copy includes install, release, support, and contribution claims tied to legacy URLs. | Public first-party surfaces should use one professional source-of-truth GitHub/release URL or a visible transitional policy. |

##### External Brand Migration Baseline V2

| Source | Current fact imported into node `0` | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| npm deprecation docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/deprecating-and-undeprecating-packages-or-package-versions` | npm strongly recommends deprecating packages or versions instead of unpublishing because unpublishing can break users without warning. | Old `@kolmogorov/*` packages, if published or documented, should be deprecated with install guidance rather than silently removed. |
| npm scope docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/using-npm/scope/` | npm scopes signal organization ownership and determine registry association. | Kolm package names should use a deliberate `@kolm/*` scope or documented unscoped package strategy, with compatibility aliases handled explicitly. |
| npm package.json docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/configuring-npm/package-json/` | Package names may be scoped and repository metadata is part of install/package discovery. | Every package manifest needs correct name, repository, bugs, homepage, bin, files, and release status. |
| GitHub repository rename docs, `https://docs.github.com/repositories/creating-and-managing-repositories/renaming-a-repository` | GitHub redirects most old repository information after rename, but project site URLs are exceptions and calls to actions hosted by renamed repositories are not redirected. | A repo/org rename needs action-path audit, GitHub Pages/custom domain check, CI workflow update, badges, package repository metadata, and clone/install migration docs. |
| Google site move docs, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/site-move-with-url-changes` | URL moves require redirect strategy and Google considers a move complete only after crawling old and new URLs. | Public URL and docs moves from legacy paths need 301 redirects, sitemap/canonical updates, Search Console monitoring, and stale-link audit. |

##### Brand Occurrence Classification V2

Every legacy occurrence must be classified as `kolm.brand_occurrence.v2`.

```json
{
  "path": "public/docs/quickstart/node.html",
  "line": 67,
  "literal": "@kolmogorov/sdk",
  "surface": "public_docs|package_metadata|deploy_config|code_identifier|test_fixture|archive|report|log|cache|historical",
  "visibility": "public|authenticated|internal|release_evidence|not_shipped",
  "classification": "replace|compatibility_alias|required_external_url|deployment_alias|historical_reference|legal_entity|archive_evidence|delete_or_exclude",
  "target": "@kolm/sdk",
  "owner_node": 0,
  "dependent_nodes": [5, 8, 17, 29, 32],
  "risk": "low|medium|high|breaking",
  "required_proof": [
    "public link check",
    "package install smoke",
    "redirect smoke",
    "docs screenshot"
  ],
  "status": "open"
}
```

Classification rules:

- `replace`: public copy, visible docs, metadata, screenshots, and examples should say `Kolm`, `kolm.ai`, or the current package/repo name.
- `compatibility_alias`: old package, import, CLI, or URL remains only because users may depend on it; docs must show it as legacy and point to the current path.
- `required_external_url`: the old external URL is still the real source, package, or deploy target; keep it only with a migration ticket and no misleading brand copy.
- `deployment_alias`: Railway/Vercel/backend origins may retain historical names only if production smoke, CSP, and DNS prove correctness.
- `historical_reference`: research, old release notes, archived plans, or legal history can keep the old term if labeled and excluded from public product claims.
- `archive_evidence`: old screenshots, TAP logs, audit reports, caches, and backups remain as evidence but must not count as live product copy.
- `delete_or_exclude`: temp/cache/log/backup files should be removed from release or excluded from brand gates.

##### Brand Migration Order V2

| Order | Layer | Required work |
|---:|---|---|
| 1 | Public visible copy | Replace legacy strings in homepage, docs, quickstarts, SDK pages, install pages, footer links, support links, SEO metadata, OG cards, and screenshots. |
| 2 | Package truth | Decide current npm/package names, package scopes, repository URLs, bugs URLs, homepage URLs, and source-only versus published state. |
| 3 | Compatibility aliases | If old `@kolmogorov/*` packages or repo URLs exist, deprecate or redirect with explicit migration message and smoke tests. |
| 4 | GitHub/repo identity | Decide canonical organization/repo path; update package metadata, docs, badges, action references, install commands, and public GitHub CTAs. |
| 5 | Deploy origins | Migrate or classify `kolmogorov-stack-production` backend origins; update CSP/connect-src, rewrites, health/ready routes, and production smoke. |
| 6 | Code identifiers | Rename internal code identifiers only after compatibility wrappers and tests exist. |
| 7 | Generated artifacts | Regenerate API docs, CLI docs, SDK docs, sitemap, OpenAPI, search metadata, screenshots, and media proof after brand changes. |
| 8 | Evidence/archive policy | Move old reports, logs, screenshots, backups, temp files, and caches into archive/evidence classes or delete them. |
| 9 | Production and search proof | Run deployed link checks, redirects, canonical/sitemap validation, screenshot audits, and Search Console migration monitoring where relevant. |

##### Brand Release Gates V2

| Gate | Required proof |
|---|---|
| Public brand gate | No visible public route, title, meta description, JSON-LD, footer, nav, CTA, install command, screenshot, OG card, or press asset contains unclassified `Kolmogorov` or `kolmogorov-stack`. |
| Package brand gate | Every `package.json` under `packages/` and `sdk/` has current name, repository, bugs, homepage, files, bin, package state, and alias/deprecation plan. |
| Repo URL gate | Every GitHub URL is current, historical, or compatibility-labeled; GitHub Actions and badges are audited because GitHub does not redirect action calls from renamed repos. |
| Deploy-origin gate | Vercel rewrites, CSP, backend origin, health, ready, and API proxy routes use current names or explicitly documented deployment aliases. |
| Docs/install gate | Node, Python, MCP, C, Rust, VS Code, package managers, Homebrew, winget, curl install, and source install docs point to actual working commands. |
| Screenshot gate | Final route screenshots are captured after brand cleanup so visual proof does not preserve stale terms. |
| Search/discovery gate | Canonicals, sitemap, redirects, OpenGraph, `llms.txt`, AI context, docs manifests, and structured data reflect the final brand state. |

| `W-BRAND-V2-014` | `0.1` | Add customer-facing rename note if needed. | If public repo/package names change, docs and changelog explain impact, aliases, and migration timeline. |
| `W-BRAND-V2-015` | `0.1` | Add final brand certificate to global completion packet. | Global certificate includes zero unclassified legacy brand occurrences in shipped surfaces. |

##### Node `0` V2 Relax Redline

Node `0` can relax only when:

- every legacy brand occurrence has a ledger classification;
- no public product surface contains unclassified `Kolmogorov`, `kolmogorov-stack`, `sneaky-hippo`, or old package names;
- every package, SDK, installer, and quickstart points to a working current package or clearly marked compatibility alias;
- GitHub organization/repository identity is decided and reflected in package metadata, docs, badges, actions, and support links;
- backend/deploy origins with historical names are either renamed or documented as deployment aliases with production smoke;
- generated docs, sitemap, structured data, OpenGraph, screenshots, and media proof are regenerated after cleanup;
- archive, temp, cache, report, and evidence files are excluded from live product brand gates unless intentionally public;
- production link and redirect smoke passes;
- the global completion certificate includes the brand certificate.

The hard rule is: legacy naming is allowed only when it is a compatibility or historical fact, not when it is accidental product copy.

#### Node 0 V3 Brand Completion, Package Rename, And Legacy Alias Release-Blocker Addendum

Node `52` remains unused. The current "Kolmogorov to Kolm" work is still node `0`, with cross-links to node `5` for package channels, node `6` for public pages, node `8` for docs, node `16` for commercial/legal-facing copy, node `23` for legal entity truth, node `29` for deploy origins, node `32` for search/discovery, node `43` for final build redline, and node `46` for traceability.

V3 turns the brand migration from a broad cleanup request into a release-blocking product requirement. It is not enough for the visible hero to say Kolm if install commands, GitHub links, generated docs, SDK package metadata, deploy origins, legal pages, OpenGraph cards, screenshots, status pages, and account surfaces still point to old names without an exception ledger.

##### Current Brand Evidence Delta V3

The latest current-tree scan excluded dependency/build/archive-heavy directories and this blueprint file, then searched for `kolmogorov` and `sneaky-hippo` case-insensitively. It found the following live risk profile:

| Root | Hit lines | Files with hits | Interpretation |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `public` | 2063 | 675 | Primary blocker. Public pages, docs, hidden copy, footer links, generated pages, social/discovery files, and screenshots can leak old identity. |
| `packages` | 62 | 32 | Package metadata and repository URLs need compatibility and publication decisions. |
| `apps` | 36 | 36 | Runtime/trainer source names may be internal, but release artifacts need classification before broad rename. |
| `sdk` | 23 | 13 | SDK package names, README snippets, import paths, and repository metadata need package-channel migration. |
| `scripts` | 18 | 13 | Build, deploy, generated-doc, and audit scripts may keep historical repo paths only if classified. |
| `docs` | 16 | 10 | Product docs, research docs, and governance docs need different treatment: public docs should be clean, research may be historical. |
| `cli` | 15 | 1 | CLI help/output must not expose old brand unless it is a compatibility alias. |
| `.github` | 4 | 4 | Workflow/action references must be audited because renamed GitHub action calls do not follow repository redirects. |
| `README.md` | 6 | 1 | Top-level public project introduction still needs brand/repo truth review. |
| `src` | 2 | 2 | Low count, but code identifiers can break compatibility if renamed blindly. |
| `vercel.json` | 5 | 1 | Deploy origin/CSP/API proxy names need production smoke and rollout sequencing. |

Representative high-priority files from the same scan:

| Path | Current hit count | Why it blocks completion |
|---|---:|---|
| `public/docs/quickstart/node.html` | 8 | Quickstart install commands and imports still refer to old package identity. |
| `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html` | 8 | MCP install commands still refer to old package identity. |
| `public/brand/github-org-decision.html` | 23 | Public-facing brand decision page discusses legacy org/repo identity and can undermine enterprise trust if indexed or linked. |
| `public/index.html` | 9 | Homepage is a first-viewport trust surface; hidden or visible legacy links still matter for search, accessibility, screenshots, and QA. |
| `public/sdks.html` | 9 | SDK install and source links are developer-critical. |
| `vercel.json` | 5 | Backend origin aliases and redirects cannot be renamed without production deploy proof. |
| `packages/sdk-ts/package.json` | 2 | Published package metadata must be current or explicitly compatibility-scoped. |
| `sdk/mcp/package.json` | 1 | MCP package identity must align with docs and install commands. |

##### External Migration Baseline V3

| Source | Current operational fact | Kolm V3 rule |
|---|---|---|
| npm deprecation docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/deprecating-and-undeprecating-packages-or-package-versions/` | npm deprecation messages are shown to users during install; npm strongly recommends deprecating old packages or versions instead of unpublishing because unpublishing can break users without warning. | If any old `@kolmogorov/*` package exists or has been publicly documented, provide a deprecation message that points to the current Kolm package. Do not silently delete a dependency path users may have copied. |
| npm scope and package metadata docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/using-npm/scope/` and `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/configuring-npm/package-json/` | Scopes express package ownership and `package.json` metadata drives package discovery, repository links, bin commands, bugs links, and package pages. | Decide the canonical npm scope, then update docs, package manifests, install commands, repository URLs, bins, provenance, and compatibility aliases as one packet. |
| GitHub repository rename docs, `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/creating-and-managing-repositories/renaming-a-repository` | GitHub redirects most web/git traffic after a repo rename, but local remotes should still be updated; GitHub Pages needs custom-domain planning; action calls hosted in renamed repos are not redirected; reusing the old repo name can break redirects. | A Kolm repo/org rename needs workflow/action audit, package metadata update, badges, clone docs, Pages/custom domain checks, action reference replacement, and old-name reservation policy. |
| Google site move docs, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/site-move-with-url-changes` | Google recommends server-side permanent redirects such as 301 or 308, avoiding redirect chains, updating canonicals/robots, testing redirects, keeping redirects generally at least one year, submitting the new sitemap, and updating internal/high-volume links. | Any public URL move or old brand path retirement needs URL mapping, direct permanent redirects, canonical/sitemap updates, Search Console monitoring, internal-link replacement, and production fetch evidence. |
| Python Packaging User Guide, `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/guides/distributing-packages-using-setuptools/` | Python distribution publishing requires sdists/wheels, package metadata, token-based upload, and validation such as `twine check`; published project names become install and discovery surfaces. | Python SDK/package rename work needs built artifacts, metadata checks, package-name availability, old-name guidance, and install smoke before docs claim a new package. |

##### Brand Migration Release Contract V3

Every legacy occurrence must become one of five states:

| State | Meaning | Allowed in shipped public product? |
|---|---|---|
| `removed` | Replaced with `Kolm`, `kolm.ai`, current package/repo name, or current deploy domain. | Yes. |
| `compatibility_alias` | Old package/import/repo/URL remains for user compatibility and points to the current path with tests. | Yes, only with visible migration copy where users may encounter it. |
| `legal_entity_truth` | Old name is legally required as company history, contract party, archived entity name, or counsel-approved text. | Yes, only on legal/history surfaces with context. |
| `deployment_alias` | Historical deploy origin remains because infrastructure has not been renamed or external DNS/provider path requires it. | No public marketing; allowed in config only with production smoke and migration ticket. |
| `archive_evidence` | Old name appears in logs, reports, screenshots, TAP files, caches, backups, or historical research. | No, unless the artifact is intentionally published as historical evidence with labels. |

Anything else is `unclassified_legacy_brand` and blocks final release.

##### Brand Occurrence Ledger V3 Schema

The V2 occurrence ledger remains valid; V3 adds release-critical fields so implementation agents cannot hide public drift inside a generic exception.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-brand-occurrence-ledger-v3",
  "path": "public/docs/quickstart/node.html",
  "line": 67,
  "literal": "@kolmogorov/sdk",
  "normalized_literal": "@kolmogorov/sdk",
  "owner_node": "0",
  "secondary_nodes": ["5", "8", "17", "32", "43"],
  "surface": "public_docs",
  "visibility": "public",
  "state": "unclassified_legacy_brand",
  "target": "@kolm/sdk",
  "compatibility_policy": {
    "needed": true,
    "old_path": "@kolmogorov/sdk",
    "new_path": "@kolm/sdk",
    "deprecation_message": "Use @kolm/sdk. The @kolmogorov/sdk package name is retained only as a migration alias.",
    "alias_smoke": null
  },
  "legal_policy": {
    "legal_entity_required": false,
    "counsel_review_required": false
  },
  "release_policy": {
    "blocks_public_release": true,
    "blocks_package_release": true,
    "blocks_production_deploy": false,
    "blocks_screenshot_certificate": true
  },
  "proof_required": [
    "public docs link check",
    "package install smoke",
    "generated docs refresh",
    "screenshot refresh"
  ],
  "status": "open"
}
```

##### Node `0` V3 Relax Redline

Node `0` is not relaxed by replacing visible homepage copy. It relaxes only when:

- `docs/brand-occurrence-ledger.v3.json` exists and classifies every current legacy occurrence;
- public/product/docs/account/search/media surfaces have zero unclassified legacy strings;
- package names and install commands are current or compatibility-labeled with smoke proof;
- old packages are deprecated or documented instead of silently removed;
- GitHub org/repo/action links are current or compatibility-labeled, with action-call exceptions handled explicitly;
- deploy-origin aliases have production smoke and migration tickets;
- legal entity references are counsel-scoped and not blindly renamed;
- generated docs, sitemap, OpenGraph, AI context, screenshots, and media proof are refreshed after cleanup;
- production brand smoke passes on the deployed commit;
- the final build-redline packet includes the brand certificate.

Until those conditions hold, any claim that the codebase is "100 percent Kolm" is false even if user-visible top navigation looks clean.

#### Node 1 Product Kernel Graph And Claim-Scope Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. Product-kernel work belongs to node `1`, because this branch defines what Kolm is allowed to claim, route, render, document, sell, and verify. This addendum converts scattered product-graph language into a single completion contract for the generated graph, readiness vocabulary, evidence envelope, API/account/CLI/TUI parity, and claim-scope gates.

##### Node `1.0` Current Product-Kernel Evidence

| Evidence path | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/product-kernel.js` | Defines `PRODUCT_KERNEL_VERSION` as `2026-05-22`, graph schema `kolm-product-graph-1`, readiness statuses, claim scopes, route classes, deployment modes, failure codes, proof kinds, stages, and helper validators. | This file is the vocabulary root; new statuses, proof kinds, route classes, and stages must not appear elsewhere first. |
| `docs/product-journeys.json` | Defines 12 user journeys: gateway-capture, privacy-lake, datasets-labeling, train-distill, models-backbones, multimodal-tokenization, compile-verify, runtime-inference, compute-cloud, devices-fleet, enterprise-governance, and agents-registry. | Product pages, account pages, docs, CLI, TUI, and API surfaces must attach to one of these journeys or update the journey schema intentionally. |
| `docs/product-surfaces.json` | Owns route groups, production smoke probes, evidence paths, and certified product-surface status. | No route group can remain hidden or unowned after router/API changes. |
| `docs/product-sota-readiness.json` | Holds readiness requirements and status legend for shipped, implemented, partial, benchmark-gated, package-gated, partner-gated, and certification-gated work. | Public copy cannot use a stronger claim than this ledger allows. |
| `docs/product-readiness-closeout.md` and `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` | Generated closeout artifacts make open proof gates visible. | Every non-final readiness item needs current scope, blocker, next wave, proof required, done-when, and verification command. |
| `public/product-graph.json` | Current generated counts show 12 journeys, 7 route surfaces, 163 route groups, 582 routes, 69 API routes in the product graph, 64 CLI commands, 32 TUI views, 33 account links, and 57 readiness requirements. | The graph is the public machine-readable contract; hand edits are prohibited and stale counts are release blockers. |
| `src/product-experience.js` | Connects journeys to account, CLI, TUI, API, dimensions, proof commands, and user-control requirements. | UX parity must be generated from this contract instead of reinvented page by page. |
| `src/product-frontier-contracts.js` | Exposes frontier/research implementation contracts to runtime/API/CLI surfaces. | Research-backed inventions belong in the product graph only when they have contract, experiment, metric, source, and implementation status. |
| `scripts/build-product-graph.cjs` and `scripts/product-graph-lib.cjs` | Generate the product graph from source docs and contracts. | Release verification must regenerate in a locked order and fail on stale output. |
| `scripts/audit-product-kernel.cjs`, `scripts/audit-product-journeys.mjs`, and `scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs` | Validate kernel, journey, and surface consistency. | These gates need to be mandatory before "done", deploy, screenshots, or public copy updates. |
| `tests/product-kernel-envelope.test.js` and product/frontier tests | Lock API/CLI/TUI/product graph contracts. | Test ownership should map each kernel field to the node or surface that depends on it. |

##### Node `1.1` External Baseline For A State-Of-The-Art Product Kernel

| Source | Current fact imported into node `1` | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| JSON Schema Draft 2020-12, `https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12` | The current meta-schema is Draft 2020-12, with core and validation vocabularies plus output schemas. | `public/product-graph.json`, readiness closeout, journey docs, surface docs, and frontier contracts need versioned JSON Schemas and validator output. |
| OpenAPI Specification 3.1.2, `https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.2.html` | The OpenAPI source of truth is the versioned HTML spec; 3.1.2 is a 2025 patch release. | Product graph API routes, generated OpenAPI, and API docs must agree on operation IDs, schemas, tags, examples, and deprecations. |
| W3C PROV-O, `https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-o/` | PROV-O is a W3C Recommendation for representing, exchanging, and integrating provenance information. | Product graph evidence should map to provenance concepts: entity, activity, agent, generation, derivation, attribution, and bundle. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | GenAI conventions define events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, and agent spans. | Product graph route classes and proof kinds should map to trace attributes for capture, teacher calls, distill jobs, artifacts, runtime inference, and agent actions. |
| AsyncAPI specification, `https://github.com/asyncapi/spec` | AsyncAPI defines machine-readable asynchronous APIs; the repository lists version 3.1.0 as latest. | Webhooks, SSE, jobs, notifications, drift alerts, capture events, and approval events need an event contract, not only REST route docs. |
| CloudEvents, `https://www.cncf.io/announcements/2024/01/25/cloud-native-computing-foundation-announces-the-graduation-of-cloudevents/` | CloudEvents graduated in CNCF and standardizes event metadata for interoperability across services and protocols. | Kolm event envelopes should use CloudEvents-compatible identifiers, source, type, subject, time, datacontenttype, and extension fields where possible. |
| WorkOS audit logs, `https://workos.com/docs/audit-logs` | Enterprise audit events use action, actor, targets, context, organization, export, and versioned schemas. | Product graph proof must connect customer-visible actions to audit events, exports, SIEM streams, and organization/tenant scope. |

##### Node `1.2` Product Kernel Contract V2

The kernel must emit and validate a single `kolm.product_kernel_contract.v2` object.

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.product_kernel_contract.v2",
  "product_kernel_version": "2026-05-22",
  "product_graph_schema": "kolm-product-graph-1",
  "generated_at": "iso-8601",
  "source_files": [
    "src/product-kernel.js",
    "src/product-experience.js",
    "docs/product-journeys.json",
    "docs/product-surfaces.json",
    "docs/product-sota-readiness.json",
    "docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json"
  ],
  "source_hashes": {
    "src/product-kernel.js": "sha256"
  },
  "generated_artifacts": [
    "public/product-graph.json",
    "public/product-readiness-closeout.json",
    "public/docs/api-routes.json",
    "public/openapi.json"
  ],
  "counts": {
    "journeys": 12,
    "route_surfaces": 7,
    "route_groups": 163,
    "routes": 582,
    "api_routes_in_graph": 69,
    "cli_commands": 64,
    "tui_views": 32,
    "account_links": 33,
    "readiness_requirements": 57
  },
  "vocabularies": {
    "readiness_statuses": "from src/product-kernel.js",
    "claim_scopes": "from src/product-kernel.js",
    "route_classes": "from src/product-kernel.js",
    "deployment_modes": "from src/product-kernel.js",
    "proof_kinds": "from src/product-kernel.js",
    "failure_codes": "from src/product-kernel.js"
  },
  "parity": {
    "account": "all exposed journeys have account path or explicit no-account reason",
    "api": "all route groups have surface owner and OpenAPI/docs state",
    "cli": "all user-facing journeys have CLI proof or explicit local-only reason",
    "tui": "all operator journeys have TUI view or explicit noninteractive reason",
    "docs": "all journeys have docs, quickstart, API, SDK, or migration path"
  },
  "claim_gate": {
    "every_claim_maps_to": [
      "journey_id",
      "surface_id",
      "requirement_id",
      "readiness_status",
      "proof_kind",
      "evidence_path"
    ],
    "open_gates_visible": true,
    "unsupported_claims_block_release": true
  },
  "drift_state": {
    "generated_in_locked_order": true,
    "manual_edits_to_generated_artifacts": "forbidden",
    "schema_validation": "required",
    "production_smoke": "required"
  }
}
```

##### Node `1.3` Kernel Ownership Rules

1. `public/product-graph.json` is generated output and must never be hand-edited.
2. New product journeys require a source change in `docs/product-journeys.json`, a branch-state update, account/API/CLI/TUI/docs parity, and smoke proof.
3. New readiness statuses, proof kinds, route classes, deployment modes, or failure codes must start in `src/product-kernel.js`.
4. Every public claim must map to a journey, surface, requirement, readiness status, claim scope, and evidence path.
5. Every route group must have a product-surface owner and an API-doc/OpenAPI state.
6. Every account product-matrix card must consume the graph or a generated derivative, not duplicate status copy.
7. Every CLI/TUI surface that prints product state must use the same readiness counts as the graph.
8. Generated artifacts must build in this order: kernel source, journey/surface/readiness ledgers, product graph, readiness closeout, API refs, OpenAPI, docs/account/public surfaces, screenshot reports, production smoke.
9. Any generator race that reads an artifact while another generator writes it is a release blocker.
10. External gates remain visible until public benchmark data, package release, partner adoption, or live certification proof is linked.

##### Node `1.4` Product Graph Drift Controls

The current graph is good enough to anchor the tree, but the completion bar is higher than "file exists." Drift control requires:

- JSON Schema 2020-12 schemas for product graph, journeys, surface catalog, readiness ledger, closeout ledger, and frontier contracts;
- source-hash manifest that records all inputs used to generate the graph;
- generated-artifact lock order that prevents API ref/OpenAPI/product graph races;
- route-count reconciliation between `src/router.js`, `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/openapi.json`, `docs/product-surfaces.json`, and `public/product-graph.json`;
- status-vocabulary lint that rejects unknown or typoed readiness values;
- claim-gate lint that blocks stronger language than the readiness status permits;
- production probe for `/v1/product/graph` and static `/product-graph.json` or the deployed static equivalent;
- graph diff report for every release, showing added/removed journeys, routes, claims, statuses, account links, CLI commands, TUI views, proof kinds, and open gates.
