| `W-KERNEL-V2-011` | `1.2` | Keep readiness closeout generated from the readiness ledger with schema validation. | Any open status without closeout contract fails. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-012` | `1.1` | Add AsyncAPI/CloudEvents event model for jobs, webhooks, approvals, capture, drift, security, billing, and artifact lifecycle. | Event schema validates and has sample payloads plus replay/idempotency proof. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-013` | `1.1` | Map GenAI telemetry fields from product graph proof kinds to OpenTelemetry spans, events, metrics, and exceptions. | Trace fixture shows capture, teacher, distill, compile, runtime, and agent spans with redacted attributes. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-014` | `1.1` | Map artifact/evidence lineage to PROV concepts and bundles. | Artifact proof export includes PROV-compatible JSON-LD or documented machine-readable equivalent. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-015` | `1.4` | Add graph diff and changelog report. | Release packet shows graph changes by journey, route, claim, account link, CLI command, TUI view, and readiness status. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-016` | `1.4` | Add local and production product graph smoke. | Local and deployed probes validate `/v1/product/graph`, static graph, closeout JSON, cache headers, and source version. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-017` | `1.2` | Add product graph versioning and migration policy. | Breaking graph changes require migration notes, old/new examples, and consumer compatibility tests. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-018` | `1.1` | Add external source freshness policy for product graph references. | Source references have owner, last checked date, freshness class, and stale-source failure mode. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-019` | `1.4` | Add generated docs lock for pages that present product graph, readiness, API, CLI, SDK, or account matrix data. | Docs cannot publish stale counts or old statuses after graph regeneration. |
| `W-KERNEL-V2-020` | `1.5` | Add node `1` certificate to the global completion packet. | Final certificate includes graph schema validation, drift report, parity report, claim-gate report, and production graph smoke. |

##### Node `1` V2 Relax Redline

Node `1` can relax only when:

- product graph, readiness closeout, journey docs, surface docs, frontier contracts, API refs, and OpenAPI are generated in a locked, race-free order;
- every product claim maps to journey, surface, requirement, readiness status, claim scope, proof kind, and evidence path;
- every route group has product-surface ownership and API/OpenAPI/docs state;
- account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, public copy, and screenshots agree on journeys and readiness counts;
- non-final external gates are visible in account, docs, CLI/TUI/API, and release evidence;
- graph JSON and closeout JSON validate against JSON Schema 2020-12;
- product graph proof maps to provenance, audit, telemetry, and event models where relevant;
- local and production smoke prove `/v1/product/graph` and static graph artifacts on the same deployed commit;
- the global completion certificate includes node `1` graph, parity, claim, schema, and production evidence.

Runtime-language completion rule: do not treat the backend as "all JS." The architecture should be documented and verified as:

- Node/JavaScript API control plane: `server.js`, `src/router.js`, `src/*.js`, CLI, account/public generators, product graph, readiness, billing, auth, storage, route docs.
- Python ML execution plane: `apps/trainer/*.py`, eval/judge scripts, distillation/quantization/runtime workers, benchmark runners, export utilities.
- Worker/deploy plane: `workers/`, provider deployment targets, edge/cloud storage integrations.
- SDK/package plane: `sdk/`, `packages/`, TypeScript, Python, C, Rust, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, MCP, VS Code, Homebrew, winget, and install scripts.
- Generated/public evidence plane: `public/`, `docs/`, OpenAPI, API routes, product graph, closeout ledgers, benchmark reports.

Each plane needs its own build command, smoke command, release artifact, and owner. A green Node test run is necessary but cannot certify Python trainer behavior, SDK package readiness, worker deployability, or public-page polish by itself.

#### Node 1 V3 Product-Feature Acceptance Matrix, UX State Coverage, And Journey Parity Ledger

Node `52` remains unused. This is still node `1` work because the unresolved gap is product-kernel acceptance: every feature must be represented as a journey with a claim scope, readiness status, account experience, API/CLI/TUI/docs parity, evidence path, and UI/UX state contract. Node `7` owns post-auth page execution, node `12` owns design-system quality, node `21` owns accessibility, node `43` owns file/build closure, and node `47` owns manual dogfood journeys. Node `1` owns the feature acceptance matrix that tells those nodes what "complete" means for each product feature.

The V3 rule is: a feature is not complete because it appears in the product graph, nor because a route, command, page, or test exists. A feature is complete only when the same journey can be understood, operated, proved, and scoped across public copy, docs, account UI, API, CLI, TUI, telemetry, screenshots, local smoke, and production smoke.

##### Node 1 V3 Current Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observed state | V3 consequence |
|---|---:|---|
| Product graph journey count | `public/product-graph.json` currently reports `12` journeys. | The acceptance matrix must have exactly one row per journey unless the graph intentionally changes. |
| Route scale | The graph reports `582` routes, `163` route groups, and `7` route surfaces. | Route ownership is broad enough that journey-level acceptance must be generated, not hand-maintained per route. |
| Account surface | The graph reports `33` account links, while a current account HTML scan found `48` account pages. | Account page existence is broader than graph links. V3 requires a live/demo/static/incomplete class and a journey owner for every account page. |
| API/CLI/TUI parity | The graph reports `69` API routes, `64` CLI commands, and `32` TUI views tied to journeys. | A feature cannot ship as account-only, API-only, or docs-only unless the graph states the missing channel and why. |
| Readiness state | Current readiness counts are `14` shipped, `35` implemented, `4` package-gated, `2` partner-gated, `1` certification-gated, and `1` public-benchmark-gated. | Product UI must distinguish shipped from implemented and external-proof-gated. "Implemented" still needs user proof, screenshots, docs, and production smoke before being marketed as done. |
| Closeout gates | `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` currently lists `8` open requirements: public leaderboard, compliance certification, one-line install, runtime adapters, foundation governance, SDK release matrix, mobile SDK release, and runtime WASM package. | Every feature that depends on these gates must display limitations in account/docs/public copy and cannot be used as proof for stronger claims. |
| Account data behavior | Account scan found `33` pages with `kfetch` and fetch, `9` pages with fetch but not `kfetch`, and `6` pages with no fetch call. | Account pages need a state class: live tenant data, generated shell, local demo, static docs shell, or incomplete. Static shells must not look like live product. |
| Account no-fetch pages | Current no-fetch account pages include `bakeoff.html`, `captures.html`, `continuous-monitoring.html`, `pipelines.html`, `sustainability.html`, and `security/2fa.html`. | These pages may be legitimate generated shells, but completion requires explicit shell status, real data path, or local/demo label. |
| Account fetch-without-kfetch pages | Current pages include `ab-tests.html`, `api-keys.html`, `audit-log.html`, `drift.html`, `sla.html`, `federated/consortium.html`, `namespaces/new.html`, and pipeline pages. | V3 requires auth/error/session handling parity; direct fetch is allowed only if the page explains auth and failure states as well as shared `kfetch`. |

##### Node 1 V3 Imported UX And Accessibility Baselines

| Source | Current fact imported into node `1` | Kolm feature rule |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2, `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | WCAG 2.2 is a W3C Recommendation; its success criteria are testable and technology-independent. It includes requirements for color not being the only signal, non-text contrast, hover/focus content behavior, focus visibility, target size, dragging alternatives, and accessible authentication. | Every feature acceptance row needs accessibility evidence for status tokens, controls, focus, keyboard/touch, auth flows, media, charts, and dynamic updates. |
| WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide, `https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/` | APG documents accessible semantics, design patterns, examples, keyboard support, landmarks, and accessible names/descriptions. | Account widgets, tabs, dialogs, popovers, command palettes, tables, tree views, menus, and status regions must use recognized patterns or document why native HTML is sufficient. |
| Material Design accessibility, `https://m2.material.io/design/usability/accessibility.html` | Material guidance reinforces accessible touch targets, labels, and usability for users with visual or motor limitations. | Feature UI must meet practical touch target, label, contrast, and feedback standards on account, docs, public demo, and mobile layouts. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | Priority rules emphasize accessibility, touch/interaction, performance, consistent style, responsive layout, typography/color, meaningful animation, forms/feedback, navigation, and charts. | A feature is not accepted until its user-visible states are coherent in layout, navigation, controls, empty/loading/error behavior, visual hierarchy, and copy. |

##### Product Feature Acceptance Contract V3

Every journey must serialize as `kolm.product_feature_acceptance.v3`.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.product_feature_acceptance.v3",
  "journey_id": "gateway-capture",
  "master_node": "1",
  "owner_nodes": {
    "product_truth": "1",
    "account_ui": "7",
    "api_backend": "2",
    "design_system": "12",
    "accessibility": "21",
    "build_redline": "43",
    "manual_use": "47"
  },
  "feature_state": "not_started|shell|implemented|usable|local_proven|production_proven|external_gated|retired",
  "readiness": {
    "strongest_public_claim": "shipped|implemented|external_gated|research|not_claimable",
    "requirements": [],
    "open_closeout_gates": []
  },
  "channels": {
    "public_pages": [],
    "account_pages": [],
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_commands": [],
    "tui_views": [],
    "docs_pages": [],
    "sdk_examples": []
  },
  "ui_states": [
    "empty",
    "loading",
    "ready",
    "success",
    "warning",
    "blocked",
    "error",
    "offline",
    "permission_denied",
    "external_gate",
    "stale_data"
  ],
  "proof": {
    "unit_tests": [],
    "integration_tests": [],
    "smoke": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_use": [],
    "production_smoke": [],
    "telemetry_events": [],
    "claim_scope_report": ""
  },
  "ux_acceptance": {
    "five_second_comprehension": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "keyboard": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "touch": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "screen_reader_names": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "color_not_only_signal": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "responsive": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "performance_budget": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "copy_scope": "pass|fail|not_applicable"
  },
  "decision": {
    "ship": false,
    "reason": "missing production proof",
    "next_action": "",
    "owner": ""
  }
}
```

##### V3 Journey Acceptance Matrix

| Journey | Required accepted user outcome | Feature cannot be accepted until |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway-capture` | User can route a provider call, capture it, inspect status, and understand cost/privacy/provider metadata. | Account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, demo, error states, provider credentials, and no-secret proof agree. |
| `privacy-lake` | User can see what is stored, redacted, exportable, deletable, and eligible for training. | Lake stats, tail, export, storage, privacy events, retention, deletion, redaction, and account explanations agree. |
| `datasets-labeling` | User can convert eligible captures into datasets, labels, splits, bakeoffs, and active learning queues. | Candidate logic, labels, holdouts, split proof, reviewer state, empty queues, and data-rights labels are visible. |
| `train-distill` | User can plan, run, inspect, block, or promote a distillation/training job. | Compute, teacher, dataset, eval, K-Score, cost, failure modes, and job status all have UI and command proof. |
| `models-backbones` | User can choose model/backbone/provider/device options with known fit and limits. | Model catalog, devices, cache, recommendation, license, context, hardware, and readiness limits are explained. |
| `multimodal-tokenization` | User can tokenize or redact media and understand what is extracted, stored, blocked, or unsupported. | Image/PDF/video/audio scope, doctor output, privacy labels, sample outputs, accessibility alternatives, and error states are present. |
| `compile-verify` | User can compile, sign, inspect, verify, diff, and download a `.kolm` artifact. | Artifact graph, dependency metadata, hash/signature, verification command, runtime target, and failure recovery are visible. |
| `runtime-inference` | User can run or serve an artifact and see latency, provider/runtime, target, and fallback status. | Runtime target list, serve/run docs, account devices/artifacts, streaming capability, and telemetry proof agree. |
| `compute-cloud` | User can see storage/cloud/GPU/readiness state and plan cloud or BYOC execution. | Cloud readiness, object storage readiness, provider missing groups, deploy plan, smoke command, and secret-safe labels are visible. |
| `devices-fleet` | User can detect, register, test, recommend, install, and monitor devices or fleets. | Device inventory, fit recommendation, install path, test output, offline/airgap states, and rollback are present. |
| `enterprise-governance` | Admin can manage keys, audit, billing, settings, SSO/SCIM readiness, approvals, and trust evidence. | RBAC, entitlements, plan state, audit export, support/contact-sales, closeout gates, and compliance scope are honest. |
| `agents-registry` | User can package, publish, install, run, and monitor agent/tool artifacts. | MCP/agent examples, registry policy, verified publisher status, install proof, telemetry, and ecosystem gate labels exist. |

##### Feature State Machine V3

| State | Meaning | Allowed public/account language |
|---|---|---|
| `not_started` | No meaningful implementation exists. | Do not market. May appear only in roadmap or internal ledger. |
| `shell` | Page, route, or command exists but lacks live data or proof. | "Preview", "demo shell", or "coming path"; never "available" without label. |
| `implemented` | Runtime code or UI exists and tests pass locally. | "Implemented locally" or "available in local build" only with scope. |
| `usable` | User can complete the journey locally with clear states. | "Usable locally" with requirements and missing production proof. |
| `local_proven` | Local smoke, docs, screenshots, and manual-use evidence exist. | "Local proof available"; production remains unclaimed. |
| `production_proven` | Deployed origin passes route/account/docs/screenshots/smoke on the same commit. | "Shipped" if no external gate applies. |
| `external_gated` | Product needs certification, package release, public benchmark, or partner adoption. | Claim must name gate and proof needed. |
| `retired` | Feature no longer belongs in product. | Redirect, archive, or compatibility note only. |

##### V3 Account Page Classification

Every account page must carry one of these classes in the feature matrix:

| Class | Meaning | Required visible behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `live_tenant_page` | Reads current authenticated tenant data through `kfetch` or equivalent shared authenticated client. | Loading, empty, success, warning, error, permission, and stale-data states. |
| `direct_fetch_page` | Uses direct `fetch` by design. | Same auth/session/error semantics as `kfetch`, with a documented reason direct fetch is safe. |
| `generated_shell` | Static or generated account shell awaiting data wiring. | Explicit shell/demo label, sample data marker, and next implementation path. |
| `static_account_doc` | Authenticated docs/reference page, not a live dashboard. | No fake live metrics; links to API/CLI/account action. |
| `incomplete_account_page` | Missing state, owner, API path, or proof. | Release blocker until reclassified or fixed. |

The current no-fetch account pages are not automatically failures, but they are not allowed to masquerade as live tenant dashboards. The final account matrix must make that distinction explicit.

##### V3 UX Acceptance Lattice

Every feature must prove these nine dimensions or explain non-applicability:

| Dimension | Required proof |
|---|---|
| `comprehension` | User can state what the feature does, who it is for, current status, and next action from the page or command output. |
| `state_complete` | Empty, loading, success, warning, blocked, error, permission, offline, and stale states exist where relevant. |
| `control_complete` | Primary action, secondary action, cancel/back, retry, export, and destructive confirmation exist where relevant. |
| `accessibility` | Keyboard path, focus visibility, names/descriptions, target size, non-color status, and reduced motion/media alternatives are verified. |
| `responsive` | Mobile and desktop layout have no overlap, horizontal scroll, hidden controls, or nav collision. |
| `performance` | Initial render, media, tables, charts, and async state have route budgets and no avoidable layout shift. |
| `trust_scope` | Readiness, open gates, credentials, retention, privacy, and certification/package/benchmark limits are visible. |
| `operator_efficiency` | Dense enterprise/account tables support filtering, sorting, search, export, pagination, and bulk action where needed. |
| `recovery` | Errors tell the user what failed, what data is safe, how to retry, and what command/API/docs path can recover. |

##### Node `1` V3 Relax Redline

Node `1` V3 cannot relax until:

- all 12 journeys have a `kolm.product_feature_acceptance.v3` record;
- every journey has a state no stronger than its current proof allows;
- every journey declares account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, public, SDK/example, screenshot, telemetry, and smoke channels or a scoped absence reason;
- every account page is classified and no generated shell looks like a live tenant dashboard;
- every direct-fetch account page has auth/session/error parity proof or is migrated to the shared authenticated client;
- shipped versus implemented versus external-gated readiness is visible in user-facing product surfaces;
- all eight open closeout gates are joined to affected features and copy restrictions;
- feature UI state coverage includes loading, empty, success, warning, blocked, error, offline, permission, and stale-data states where relevant;
- accessibility, responsive, performance, trust-scope, operator-efficiency, and recovery proof are attached per journey;
- production smoke proves the deployed feature matrix on the same commit used for release.

The V3 product-kernel standard is: every product feature has one truth row, one owner chain, one user outcome, one visible state model, one evidence packet, and one honest claim level.

Route stub completion rule: the route ledger must show the owner and intended state for every stub. Any route that affects money, customer data, model behavior, compliance evidence, or production automation is P0 until implemented, hidden, or marked external/internal-only.

### Official Standards To Bind The Redline

The completion gates should be tied to current external standards, not only internal preference:

| standard | source | implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C says WCAG 2.2 was published as a W3C Recommendation on 2023-10-05 and adds criteria including focus not obscured, dragging alternatives, target size, consistent help, redundant entry, and accessible authentication: https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/ | account and public UI completion must include keyboard-visible focus, non-hover alternatives, accessible auth flows, target-size checks, and no hidden focus behind sticky UI |
| Core Web Vitals | web.dev defines current stable page quality metrics as LCP, INP, and CLS with recommended good thresholds of LCP <= 2.5s, INP <= 200ms, CLS <= 0.1 at the 75th percentile: https://web.dev/articles/vitals | screenshot audits are not enough; conversion pages and account dashboards need performance budgets and field/RUM or lab evidence |
| OpenAPI | OpenAPI publishes current spec versions including 3.2.0 and 3.1.x, and notes schemas cannot catch every spec violation: https://spec.openapis.org/oas/ | API completion must validate generated OpenAPI plus route behavior and docs honesty; schema validation alone is not proof |
| SLSA | SLSA explains build tracks/levels and tamper-resistant evidence for software production: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0-rc2/about | package/SDK/release completion needs provenance, expected CI builders, artifact integrity, and supply-chain evidence |
| OWASP ASVS | OWASP ASVS 5.0.0 provides requirements for secure development and verification: https://owasp.org/www-project-application-security-verification-standard/ | API/auth/account/security completion should map sensitive flows to versioned ASVS requirement IDs |
| NIST AI RMF and GenAI Profile | NIST describes AI RMF as voluntary guidance for AI risk management and notes the 2024 Generative AI Profile: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework | K-Score, distillation, capture, redaction, governance, eval, and deployment claims should carry risk/evidence packets |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | OTel GenAI conventions are still marked Development and include events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, agent spans, and provider-specific conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | GenAI telemetry should version its emitted convention set and avoid pretending experimental semantic conventions are stable |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | OpenSSF Scorecard auto-generates a security score for open source projects: https://openssf.org/scorecard/ | public package/open-source readiness should include a Scorecard-style checklist for branch protection, pinned dependencies, token permissions, and release hygiene |

### Canonical Build Script Sequence From Current Package

Current `package.json` has enough product-specific gates that the final build redline should explicitly sequence them instead of relying on memory.

P0 generated-contract gates:

- `npm run build:product-graph`
- `npm run build:readiness-closeout`
- API route reference and OpenAPI generation scripts, run serially

P0 truth and claim gates:

- `npm run verify:kernel`
- `npm run verify:sota`
- `npm run verify:claims-scope`
- `npm run verify:brand`
- `npm run verify:journeys`
- `npm run verify:surfaces`

P0 product-depth gates:

- `npm run verify:depth`
- `npm run local:surfaces`
- `npm run local:surfaces:deep`
- `npm run release:verify`

P0 product-invention and frontier gates now present:

- `npm run verify:inventions`
- `npm run verify:frontier-contracts`
- `npm run verify:operator-kernels`
- `npm run verify:build-strategy`
- `npm run verify:readiness-workorders`

P0 model/eval/package gates now present:

- `npm run verify:redaction-benchmark`
- `npm run verify:kscore-calibration`
- `npm run verify:quality-calibration`
- `npm run verify:benchmark-evidence`
- `npm run verify:governance-packets`
- `npm run verify:compliance-packet`
- `npm run verify:package-release`
- `npm run verify:federated`

UI/product surface gates:

- `npm run qa:shots`
- `npm run qa:shots:quick`
- `npm run qa:shots:local`
- `npm run ui:audit`
- `npm run ui:audit:all`
- `npm run ui:audit:all:themes`

The final "relax" point cannot happen until these commands are green on the same settled tree, with production smoke run after deploy for live-env-only behavior.

### Account Product-Feature UX Ledger V1

The account surface is where Kolm becomes a product instead of a brochure. A current scan found 48 account HTML pages. Under a strict completion test, 46 need at least one pass for live data, metadata, state handling, hash links, or product-specific wording. This does not mean every page is broken; it means the post-auth system is not yet atomically proven complete.

Scan rules used:

- missing or thin description below 50 characters;
- H1 count not exactly 1;
- no live `fetch`/`kfetch` call on an account page that appears operational;
- fewer than two loading/empty/error/state terms;
- hash-only links that need real anchors, buttons, or ARIA-backed JS controls.

#### P0 Account Pages With No Live Data Fetch

These pages look like account/product surfaces but did not show a `fetch` or `kfetch` call in the current source scan. They require either live API wiring, generated static evidence with a visible scope label, or removal from the authenticated nav until real.

| page | current signal | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `public/account/ab-tests.html` | no live fetch, 6 buttons, 1 table, 2 hash links | wire to `/v1/ab-tests` implementation or mark as not shipped; actions need assignment, promote, rollback, stop, audit trail |
| `public/account/active-learning.html` | description length 27, no live fetch | wire to active-learning recommendations, reviewed labels, model impact, and next capture actions |
| `public/account/bakeoff.html` | no live fetch, no buttons, no table | consolidate with `bakeoffs.html` or make it a real read-only evidence page with route-backed results |
| `public/account/captures.html` | no live fetch, no buttons, no table | consolidate with `captured.html`/capture analytics or provide live capture table and filters |
| `public/account/confidence.html` | no live fetch | route to confidence-aware routing policy, thresholds, fallback history, and override actions |
| `public/account/continuous-monitoring.html` | no live fetch, H1 references SOC 2 Type II signals | either show implemented control signals only or link dated certification evidence; avoid implying certification |
| `public/account/federated/consortium.html` | no live fetch, generic H1 | show consortium members, approval state, aggregation rounds, privacy budget, and external-proof scope |
| `public/account/multimodal-bakeoff.html` | no live fetch | wire audio/video/VLM benchmark results, modality coverage, and unsupported modality states |
| `public/account/pipelines.html` | no live fetch, no buttons, no table | merge with `public/account/pipelines/index.html` or make it a real pipeline list with create/run/status actions |
| `public/account/routing.html` | no live fetch | show live routing decisions, confidence, cost, latency, fallback reason, and policy source |
| `public/account/security/2fa.html` | no live fetch | wire MFA status, enrollment, enforcement policy, recovery states, and admin/audit events |
| `public/account/sustainability.html` | no live fetch, no buttons | wire carbon/cost/efficiency data or move to static report with scope label |

#### P0 Account Copy And Metadata Debt

| page | current signal | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `public/account/overview.html` | description length 12 | describe the production AI console: product graph, capture, distill, artifacts, storage, providers, billing, governance, and readiness closeouts |
| `public/account/active-learning.html` | description length 27 | explain what active learning does and what action the operator can take |
| `public/account/failure-modes.html` | H1 is `Frontier AI on your own infrastructure.` | replace marketing H1 with the operational page purpose: failure-mode detection, examples, severity, and remediation |
| `public/account/federated/consortium.html` | H1 is `Frontier AI on your own infrastructure.` | replace marketing H1 with consortium/federated-learning state |
| `public/account/continuous-monitoring.html` | H1 says `SOC 2 Type II signals.` | scope to monitoring/control signals unless actual SOC 2 Type II evidence exists |
| multiple account pages | mixed title separators and entities | normalize account title style to `Page - Account - kolm.ai` or one chosen equivalent, then enforce in generator |

#### Account UX Contract For Every Product Feature

Every authenticated product-feature page must meet the same operational standard:

1. It states the user's current operational state in the first viewport.
2. It identifies the source of truth: API route, generated artifact, local-only file, or external proof gate.
3. It has loading, empty, partial, error, and permission-denied states.
4. Every command button has disabled, loading, success, failure, and audit-log behavior.
5. Destructive or production-changing actions have confirmation, undo/rollback where possible, and audit export.
6. Tables have sortable columns, stable empty states, keyboard focus, and mobile alternatives.
7. Filters and search preserve deep links.
8. Every status badge explains what "ready", "missing", "blocked", "external", or "partial" means.
9. Every page includes the relevant CLI command and API endpoint in a compact operator drawer or details section.
10. Every page has screenshot proof in dark/light desktop/mobile after changes.

This contract should be enforced by `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs` where pages are generated. Hand-authored pages need the same contract documented inline or in a page manifest.

| surface | account pages | completion bar |

| capture and privacy | `captured.html`, `captures.html`, `captures/analytics.html`, `captures/review.html`, `privacy-events.html`, `lake.html` | live capture stream, privacy labels, quarantine state, review queue, retention, opt-out, and redaction evidence |
| compile, artifacts, and bakeoffs | `artifacts.html`, `builds.html`, `bakeoff.html`, `bakeoffs.html`, `diagnose.html`, `failure-modes.html` | artifact lineage, compile status, evaluation reports, failure diagnostics, remediation, and rerun actions |
| distill and learning | `distill-runs.html`, `active-learning.html`, `labeling.html`, `synthetic.html`, `seasonal.html`, `staleness.html` | teacher/student evidence, label queue, synthetic gaps, drift/staleness, holdout metrics, and promotion gates |
| routing and reliability | `routing.html`, `confidence.html`, `drift.html`, `drift-alert.html`, `sla.html`, `agent-telemetry.html` | route decisions, confidence, fallback, drift thresholds, SLA metrics, agent failures, and alert routing |
| cloud, devices, and deployment | `devices.html`, `storage.html`, `federated/consortium.html`, `pipelines.html`, `pipelines/index.html` | provider readiness, storage readiness, device fleet, pipelines, federated rounds, and cloud credential caveats |
| security and governance | `audit-log.html`, `approvals.html`, `security/2fa.html`, `continuous-monitoring.html`, `settings.html` | RBAC, MFA, audit log, approval workflows, certification scope, and exportable evidence |
| commercial operations | `billing.html`, `chargeback.html`, `opportunities.html`, `api-keys.html`, `connectors.html`, `namespaces/new.html` | plan state, usage, savings, chargeback, namespace ownership, API keys, and connector status |

#### Additional Official UX/SEO/Security Bindings

| standard | source | account/product implication |
|---|---|---|
| Google Search Essentials | Google says Search Essentials cover technical requirements, spam policies, and key best practices; it explicitly calls out helpful people-first content, prominent keywords in titles/headings/link text, crawlable links, and image/video/structured-data best practices: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/essentials | public and docs pages cannot rely on abstract slogans; titles, H1s, descriptions, links, and media must match the actual product surface |
| Google SEO Starter Guide | Google says a good title is unique, clear, concise, and accurately describes the page; a good meta description is short, unique, and one or two sentences: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/seo-starter-guide | homepage/product/pricing/account metadata must be concise and page-specific; overloaded titles and 12-character descriptions fail the bar |
| WAI-ARIA APG | W3C APG provides accessible component patterns with ARIA roles, states, properties, keyboard support, landmarks, accessible names, and descriptions: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ | popouts, tabs, menus, nav underlines, account tables, command buttons, dialogs, and drawers need APG-backed keyboard and naming behavior |
| OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications | OWASP's GenAI security project identifies LLM risks including prompt injection, insecure output handling, excessive agency, overreliance, and model theft: https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/ | capture, routing, agents, artifact marketplace, tool-use, and autopilot pages must show policy controls, output validation, agency limits, and audit evidence |

After the account pass, the unauthenticated public website and docs were scanned separately. This excludes `public/account/**` and covers 681 HTML pages.

|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| docs | 221 | 221 | 14 | 0 | 45 | 0 | 502 | 493 | 0 | 3 | 122 |
| core marketing/product | 62 | 62 | 2 | 0 | 14 | 2 | 148 | 176 | 30 | 1 | 60 |
| research | 60 | 60 | 1 | 0 | 2 | 0 | 194 | 176 | 3 | 0 | 8 |
| cookbook | 35 | 35 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 35 | 105 | 0 | 0 | 0 |

| marketplace | 19 | 19 | 0 | 0 | 8 | 0 | 29 | 43 | 3 | 0 | 27 |
| compare | 14 | 14 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 15 | 42 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| vertical | 6 | 6 | 1 | 0 | 6 | 0 | 6 | 19 | 0 | 0 | 12 |
| other | 240 | 239 | 0 | 0 | 28 | 0 | 408 | 699 | 28 | 1 | 54 |

Total public non-account debt signals from this scan:

- 680 of 681 pages had at least one strict debt flag.
- 1,400 hash-only links.
- 1,815 `Kolmogorov` occurrences outside account pages.
- 70 `Business` references and 5 `Starter` references outside account pages.
- 308 `Open-source AI workbench` old-positioning references.
- 0 `<video>` elements and 0 `<iframe>` elements in public non-account HTML.
- 4 `<img>` elements, 1,703 inline SVGs, and 0 missing `alt` attributes on the `<img>` elements found.
- 503 JSON-LD blocks, 113 pages with `SoftwareApplication`, 254 with `Product`, and 170 with `BreadcrumbList`.
- 669 pages with Open Graph title/description, 680 with canonical links, and 100 with robots meta.

The high weak-page count is partly because the scan treats every `Kolmogorov` reference, hash-only link, or legacy positioning string as debt. That is intentional for completion planning: a page can render fine and still be unfinished if it ships old brand, old positioning, or inert navigation.

#### P0 Public Pages

| page | current signal | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| `public/index.html` | long title, long description, 2 H1s, 4 hash links, 9 Kolmogorov refs, 4 Business refs, old positioning | rebuild around one category-defining H1 and the three product surfaces: route/own model traffic, compile/distill top models, and run enterprise models on any device |
| `public/product.html` | multiple H1s already tracked in prior scan | one product-matrix H1, then sections for gateway, distillation/compile, and device deployment |
| `public/pricing.html` | 11 hash links, 12 Business refs, 1 Starter ref | pricing metadata and visible copy must match backend plans and Enterprise sales flow |
| `public/docs.html` | long description, Business ref, old generated/static positioning risk | docs landing page must route users by job: capture, compile, distill, deploy, govern, SDK/API |
| `public/api.html` | hash link and Kolmogorov refs | API docs front door must be generated from route inventory and explain envelope/auth/error conventions clearly |
| `public/enterprise.html` | long description, hash links, Kolmogorov refs | enterprise page must separate implemented controls from sales/contact and external certification proof |
| `public/security.html` | 281-character description, 20 hash links, Business ref | security page needs a trust-center IA, exact evidence links, no certification overclaiming |
| `public/compile.html` | 217-character description, hash link, Kolmogorov refs | compile page needs concise outcome copy, artifact demo path, schema/verify links |
| `public/distill.html` | 220-character description, hash link, Kolmogorov refs | distill page needs teacher/student workflow, eval proof, K-Score, benchmark limitations |
| `public/benchmarks.html` | benchmark claims with hash link and Kolmogorov refs | every benchmark claim needs dated dataset, model version, hardware, raw report, and rerun command |

#### Media And Demo Gap

The product strategy calls for a video explainer and interactive demo. The current static public scan found zero `<video>` and zero `<iframe>` elements outside account pages. If the demo/video exists through JavaScript only, it still needs a proof path in screenshots and source-level metadata. If it does not exist, it is a P0 conversion gap.

Completion requirement:

- homepage has one working above-the-fold interactive demo path;
- demo has loading, error, sample input, output artifact, copy/download, and privacy labels;
- video has poster, captions/transcript, duration, lazy loading, reserved aspect ratio, and `VideoObject` structured data if public;
- media is not dark/blank in light mode and is verified by screenshots;
- demo/video does not hide the three-product-surface story.

#### Public Navigation And Link Redline

Hash-only links are acceptable only for real in-page anchors that exist and are keyboard reachable. They are not acceptable as fake CTAs, fake tabs, fake dropdown items, or incomplete nav.

Completion requirement:

1. Every `href="#..."` points to an existing `id`.
2. Every disclosure, tab, menu, and popout follows WAI-ARIA APG keyboard behavior.
3. Active nav state uses one consistent visual system; no mixed underline/highlight styles.
4. Mobile nav and desktop nav expose the same product hierarchy.
5. Top nav names match the master spec tree, not whichever page was edited last.
6. Footer links are crawlable and not duplicated across stale generated templates.

#### Public SEO And Structured Data Redline

Public completion requires source-level SEO gates, not only screenshot gates.

Rules:

- Every page has one title under the chosen length budget, one H1, and one short page-specific description.
- Public pages use keywords that describe real product surfaces, not slogans: AI gateway, AI compiler, distillation, signed artifacts, on-device deployment, governance evidence.
- Article/research pages use `Article` or `TechArticle` where appropriate.
- Product/pricing pages use `SoftwareApplication`, `Product`, or `Offer` only when pricing and availability are accurate.
- Docs pages use `BreadcrumbList` and a clear docs IA.
- Video pages or homepage video use `VideoObject`.
- Organization/trust pages use `Organization` only with correct legal/entity details.
- Structured data must not describe unshipped package releases, certifications, benchmark superiority, or partner adoption.

- W-PUBLIC-001: master metadata generator for public pages, including title, description, H1, canonical, OG, and JSON-LD policy.
- W-PUBLIC-002: public Kolmogorov-to-Kolm brand sweep with legal/path exceptions.
- W-PUBLIC-003: legacy pricing term eradication outside backward-compatible internal aliases.
- W-PUBLIC-004: hash-link and nav-state audit across 681 public non-account pages.
- W-PUBLIC-005: homepage/product/pricing rewrite around the three product surfaces.
- W-PUBLIC-006: demo/video implementation proof packet with screenshots, captions, transcript, and structured data.
- W-PUBLIC-007: docs IA rebuild around job-to-be-done paths and adjacent CLI/API/SDK/account links.
- W-PUBLIC-008: trust/legal/security claim-scope pass with implemented/certified/external-proof labels.
- W-PUBLIC-009: benchmark evidence template for every public performance, cost, quality, or accuracy claim.
- W-PUBLIC-010: full public-page screenshot pack after frontend edits settle.

### Master Spec Tree V1

This document now maintains the master spec tree. Every future scan, ticket, implementation wave, UI change, route change, docs change, or release gate should attach to one node in this tree.

Current authoritative product graph:

- source: `public/product-graph.json`
- schema keys: `counts`, `definition`, `dimensions`, `journeys`, `kernel`, `kernel_version`, `readiness_counts`, `readiness_groups`, `references`, `route_surfaces`, `schema`, `source_docs`
- current counts: 12 journeys, 7 route surfaces, 163 route groups, 582 routes, 69 API routes in the product graph, 64 CLI commands, 32 TUI views, 33 account links, 57 readiness requirements
- readiness counts: 14 shipped, 35 implemented, 2 needs external partner, 1 needs live certification, 4 needs package release, 1 needs public benchmark data
- current closeout ledger: 8 open requirements, grouped as 2 P0 and 6 P1 in `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`

The earlier document sections may mention older open-readiness counts from prior concurrent work. The master spec tree treats the current worktree values above as authoritative until the next scan updates them.


#### Master Spec Tree Maintenance Rules

1. Every file belongs to exactly one primary tree node and can have secondary cross-links.
2. Every route belongs to a route group, product surface, account/docs/public exposure state, and proof command.
3. Every account page belongs to one node under `7`.
4. Every public page belongs to one node under `6` or `8`.
5. Every generated artifact names its source file and generation command.
6. Every claim points to a readiness requirement, benchmark report, certification evidence, package release, or closeout gate.
7. Every external proof gate has owner, blocker, done-when criteria, and public copy restrictions.
8. Every visual surface has screenshot evidence by route, theme, viewport, and post-edit timestamp.
9. Every backend surface has tests plus API/CLI/TUI/account/docs parity if user-facing.
10. The tree is updated before any "done", "deploy", or "finished build" claim.

#### Spec Node Record Format

Every future section should use this compact record when adding detailed nodes:

```text
node:
owner:
status: complete | incomplete | implemented | external-proof | package-release | certification | delete-candidate
source files:
routes:
public pages:
account pages:
docs:
CLI/TUI:
tests:
screenshots:
external sources:
current defects:
next implementation tickets:
done proof:
```

#### Master Spec Tree Maintenance Ledger

The master spec tree is maintained as the canonical index, not as a loose table of contents. When another agent, implementation wave, research pass, or release audit discovers work, it must either attach to an existing node or add the next numbered node with a detailed ledger and atomic tickets.

| Latest maintenance action V3.21 | 2026-05-25: kept `52` unused and extended node `27` with a V3 open ecosystem stewardship control plane for governance files, `.kolm` standardization, conformance, external adoption, community channels, marketplace trust, brand/trademark, OpenSSF/CNCF readiness, and honest ecosystem claims. |
| Latest maintenance action V3.22 | 2026-05-25: kept `52` unused and extended node `28` with a V4 repository architecture control plane for build graph evidence, CODEOWNERS, ADRs, workspace strategy, generated-file provenance, path classification, source/output budgets, package lanes, codegraph persistence, and clean-release structural proof. |
| Latest maintenance action V3.23 | 2026-05-25: kept `52` unused and extended node `29` with a V4 production evidence control plane for deploy IDs, target identity, route/header parity, env readiness, live public/auth smoke, screenshot proof, rollback drills, telemetry identity, status decisions, evidence retention, and post-deploy watch windows. |
| Latest maintenance action V3.24 | 2026-05-25: kept `52` unused and extended node `30` with a V3 product-experience implementation control plane for homepage clarity, three-surface IA, nav determinism, cascade cleanup, runtime guard retirement, product media, demo/video proof, pricing/enterprise coherence, account matrix UX, accessibility, Core Web Vitals, and production visual evidence. |
| Latest maintenance action V3.25 | 2026-05-25: kept `52` unused and extended node `31` with a V3 wave execution control plane for canonical registry generation, roadmap source reconciliation, duplicate/supersession rules, active lane locks, generated-artifact sequencing, wave packet provenance, evidence retention, DORA flow metrics, production-close separation, and post-ship review. |
| Patch discipline | Update the top tree, add or extend the detailed ledger, add work tickets, then run hygiene checks |
| Cross-agent rule | Treat other worktree edits as parallel user work; attach findings to the tree without overwriting their files |
| Claim rule | No node is finished until evidence links prove implementation, copy, docs, UI, tests, smoke, production state, and external gates where relevant |
| Drift rule | If a generated graph, route count, package state, screenshot report, benchmark, or external source changes, update the affected node instead of creating a disconnected note |

#### Master Spec Tree Branch State Register V3

This register is the live routing table for future work. It does not replace the detailed node ledgers below. It prevents duplicate branches by stating where new asks, code-review findings, product inventions, UI defects, backend contracts, docs issues, and release evidence should attach.

Current allocation guard:

- node `52` is reserved and remains unused;
- use node `46` for master-tree maintenance, requirements traceability, evidence graph, and implementation handoff;
- use node `43` for whole-codebase completion and build redline;
- use node `31` for roadmap waves, invention lineage, duplicate merges, and agent coordination;
- use node `29` for deploy, production smoke, environment parity, and post-deploy proof;
- allocate node `52` only if a new branch cannot be represented by any node `0` through `51` after checking this register.

| Node | Branch | Current state | Attach new work here when the ask is about |
|---:|---|---|---|
| `0` | Truth and brand | active control | Kolm naming, legacy Kolmogorov cleanup, package/repo URL migration, deploy-origin aliases, public claim language, plan names, product truth contradictions, and brand exception ledger. |
| `1` | Product kernel | active control | The 12 product journeys, generated product graph, readiness status taxonomy, claim scopes, evidence envelope, source schema/versioning, route-surface ownership, account/API/CLI/TUI parity, and product-vocabulary drift locks. |
| `2` | API and backend control plane | active backend lane | Router behavior, API envelopes, Problem Details migration, request IDs, idempotency, rate limits, auth/RBAC/audit, OpenAPI/API refs, route stubs, backend product truth, account/API/CLI/TUI parity. |
| `3` | ML execution plane | implementation branch | Python trainer/eval/runtime apps, mock versus real GPU mode, distillation workers, quantization workers, import/export utilities, model cards, benchmark runners, runtime hardware profiles, reproducibility, and ML build/smoke proof. |
| `4` | Worker and deploy plane | implementation branch | Isolated worker packages, edge/serverless workers, compile server, cloud readiness, object storage readiness, queue and retry semantics, worker identity, resource limits, regional modes, air-gapped deployment, deploy manifests, and worker smoke proof. |
| `5` | SDK and package plane | external-package gated | SDKs, package metadata, installers, npm/PyPI/crates/SwiftPM/Maven/Homebrew/apt/winget/direct-download/browser-extension channels, duplicate SDK roots, compatibility examples, package provenance, SBOMs, registry URLs, and release-channel proof. |

#### Master Spec Tree Control Snapshot V2

This snapshot is the maintenance record for the active master spec tree. It exists so future agents can attach work to the right branch without inventing a new parallel taxonomy, overwriting concurrent edits, or claiming "100 percent" from a stale count.

Current generated truth sources checked for this snapshot:

| source | current value | tree implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `public/product-graph.json` journeys | 12 | product journeys remain owned by nodes `1`, `7`, `11`, `47`, and surface-specific branches |
| `public/product-graph.json` route surfaces | 7 | route ownership remains controlled by nodes `2`, `37`, `38`, and generated surface catalog checks |
| `public/product-graph.json` route groups | 163 | route groups must attach to a product surface before deploy claims |
| `public/product-graph.json` routes | 582 | public/API/account route counts in older sections are historical unless refreshed from the graph |
| `public/product-graph.json` API routes | 69 | API parity work belongs to nodes `2`, `37`, and the relevant product branch |
| `public/product-graph.json` CLI commands | 64 | CLI parity work belongs to nodes `5`, `8`, `17`, `37`, `47`, or `50` depending on user journey |
| `public/product-graph.json` TUI views | 32 | TUI parity work belongs to nodes `7`, `17`, `37`, `47`, or `50` depending on user journey |
| `public/product-graph.json` account links | 33 | post-auth UX work belongs to node `7` plus the related product feature node |
| `public/product-graph.json` readiness requirements | 57 | readiness status cannot be marketed as done without graph and closeout evidence |
| `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` open requirements | 8 | open external/package/certification/benchmark gates remain visible and cannot be relaxed locally |

Active branch ownership map:

| workstream | primary tree node | secondary nodes | proof files and commands |
|---|---:|---|---|
| master spec tree maintenance | `46` | `0`, `31`, `43` | this section, `public/product-graph.json`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`, `npm run verify:depth`, document hygiene checks |
| whole-codebase completion and clean build redline | `43` | `14`, `28`, `40` | source completion ledger, build graph, release verify, file closure records |
| backend/API/product truth spine | `2` | `1`, `37`, `38`, `46` | `/v1/product/graph`, OpenAPI/API refs, local surface smokes, product kernel tests |
| frontend/site/account/design overhaul | `30` | `6`, `7`, `12`, `21`, `44`, `45`, `51` | screenshot reports, route QA, CSS/token registry, copy map, media manifest |
| commercial pricing/billing/entitlement truth | `16` | `25`, `34`, `38` | plan contract, billing routes, Stripe evidence, entitlement smoke, account billing screenshots |
| credentials/secrets/state lifecycle | `20` | `10`, `38`, `39` | key lifecycle evidence, secret scanning, state/readiness probes, audit logs |
| release/deploy/prod smoke | `29` | `9`, `13`, `22`, `39`, `40` | deployment packet, production smoke, rollback proof, DORA/incident evidence |
| code completion/IDE/MCP/build-finishing product | `50` | `17`, `28`, `37`, `43` | completions bridge, MCP tests, VS Code proof, codegraph, build-redline workflows |
| evaluation/K-Score/benchmarks | `42` | `11`, `18`, `24`, `48` | calibration reports, public benchmark evidence, judge/redaction evals, claim gates |
| demos/video/media/interactive runner | `51` | `6`, `12`, `21`, `44`, `45` | demo registry, video packet, runner smoke, media accessibility, screenshots |

Do not allocate node `52` for the following active asks because they already have owners:

- "maintain a master spec tree" -> node `46`;
- "100 percent codebase completion" -> node `43`;
- "backend bot" API/product truth -> nodes `2`, `37`, and `38`;
- "frontend website/state-of-the-art UX" -> nodes `30`, `12`, `44`, `45`, and `51`;
- "commercial/pricing/enterprise/contact sales" -> node `16`;
- "credentials, keys, secrets, API key UX" -> nodes `10` and `20`;
- "deploy, production smoke, should I deploy" -> node `29`;
- "research inventions/roadmap/waves" -> node `31`;
- "K-Score, evals, benchmark proof" -> node `42`;
- "coding assistant, code completion, finish my build" -> node `50`.

Spec tree write protocol:

1. Start every maintenance pass by reading this tree plus the generated product graph and closeout ledger.
2. Pick one primary owner node before writing; record cross-links only after the primary owner is clear.
3. Keep node `52` reserved until a future topic cannot be expressed under nodes `0` through `51`.
4. If a generated graph count changes, update this snapshot and the affected detailed ledger in the same patch.
5. If an implementation wave adds routes, account pages, public pages, CLI commands, TUI views, packages, or generated artifacts, attach each to a tree node before release.
6. If another agent edits code, treat it as user-owned concurrent work; update the spec around the combined tree and do not rewrite their files.
7. Never relax an open external gate locally. External partner, package release, live certification, and public benchmark items require their own proof artifacts.
8. Keep stale counts visible only as historical context; the latest generated graph is authoritative for route/surface/readiness counts.
9. Every detailed ledger must include current repo evidence, external baselines when relevant, completion spec, work tickets, relax redline, and proof commands.
10. Every final status report must include this document path, the exact section line, and hygiene results.

Master spec tree tickets to generate:

- W-SPEC-MAINT-001: add a machine-readable `docs/master-spec-tree.json` generated from this section and cross-checked against `public/product-graph.json`.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-002: add a verifier that fails when a new route, public page, account page, CLI command, TUI view, script, package, or generated artifact lacks a primary tree node.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-003: extend product graph generation to emit `master_node`, `secondary_nodes`, `owner`, `proof_commands`, and `last_verified_at` for every surface.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-004: create a file-ledger projection that maps every tracked file to exactly one primary node and zero or more secondary nodes.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-005: add a stale-count check that compares this blueprint's graph snapshot with the current generated graph and prints the exact lines to update.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-006: add an open-gate dashboard section that reads `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` and groups gates by blocker, priority, owner node, and next wave.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-007: require every implementation wave to include a `tree_node` field in its work packet, tests, and final evidence.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-008: require every frontend screenshot report to declare which public/account/docs nodes it proves.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-009: require every API route group to declare which product node owns the route, which UX surface exposes it, and which claim it supports.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-010: require every commercial, security, benchmark, package, and certification claim to link to an evidence object or a closeout gate.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-011: add a concurrent-agent merge checklist: write set, generated artifacts touched, tree nodes affected, verification run, and unresolved conflicts.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-012: add a release packet index that pulls proof from nodes `9`, `13`, `29`, `40`, and all modified feature nodes.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-013: add a tree-drift report to `verify:depth` after it is safe for backend agents to touch code.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-014: add a public docs page that summarizes the product graph and readiness scope without exposing internal speculation.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-015: add an account/admin view for operators that shows implementation state, closeout gates, and production smoke status by node.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-016: attach support/admin runbooks to the same tree nodes as their customer-facing features.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-017: require benchmark and K-Score reports to name the exact narrative, pricing, vertical, or model claim they unlock.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-018: require package/release evidence to name the SDK, installer, runtime, registry, or marketplace node it closes.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-019: require production smoke evidence to include route, tenant, auth mode, environment, screenshot/log artifact, and node mapping.
- W-SPEC-MAINT-020: add a final "tree closure" gate: no unowned files, no unowned routes, no stale generated graph, no hidden open gates, no claim without evidence, no product surface without user-visible proof.

#### Master Spec Tree Maintenance Snapshot V2

This snapshot is the current operating rule for maintaining the tree while backend, frontend, CLI, and research agents are active.

| Check | Current state | Maintenance rule |
|---|---|---|
| Canonical node range | Nodes `0` through `51` are the active canonical range. | Keep node `52` unused unless a future requirement cannot attach to an existing branch. |
| Unique node IDs | The current branch table resolves to `52` unique node IDs. | New work must attach to exactly one primary node and zero or more secondary nodes. |
| Duplicate-looking branch rows | A structural helper counted `58` branch-shaped rows because earlier bootstrap tables also mention nodes `0` through `5`. | Treat the top `Master Spec Tree V1` table as canonical; historical/bootstrap tables are context, not additional nodes. |
| Detailed ledgers | Every canonical node has a detailed ledger and at least V2-level maintenance coverage; nodes `39` through `51` already have V3 refinements. | Do not add duplicate V2 sections when a node already owns the topic. Extend the latest addendum or add a V3/V4 only when the current evidence materially changes. |
| Latest patch | Nodes `31`, `0`, `1`, `2`, `6`, `7`, `8`, `28`, `29`, `43`, `47`, `46`, `49`, `50`, and `51` have been extended without allocating node `52`: wave registry, brand migration, product-feature acceptance, API/backend route finality, public-website conversion/SEO/render proof, account-console workflow/state coverage, runnable docs/SDK truth, polyglot backend ownership, production-finality proof gates, atomic build-redline file review, release-candidate path resolution, hands-on journey acceptance, master-tree operating governance, regulated-vertical proof rooms, coding-product acceptance proof, and demo/media production-room proof. | Roadmap work stays in node `31`; brand/package rename in node `0`; product-feature acceptance and graph parity stay in node `1`; API/backend route behavior, envelopes, OpenAPI, generated API docs, auth/error/idempotency semantics, and route smoke stay in node `2`; public route purpose, conversion IA, SEO, metadata, and rendered page quality stay in node `6`; post-auth account workflow proof stays in node `7`; docs, quickstarts, tutorials, API refs, CLI docs, and SDK docs stay in node `8`; backend-language/build-lane proof in node `28`; deploy/prod smoke/rollback evidence in node `29`; codebase/file/build finality and artifact quarantine stay in node `43`; real task-use proof in node `47`; pure traceability and tree maintenance stay in node `46`; vertical solution proof stays in node `49`; code completion/build finishing stays in node `50`; demo/video/runner/media proof stays in node `51`. |
| Latest patch extension | Node `3` now also owns the V3 compile/distill/quantization/runtime acceptance ladder and the `kolm.ml_product_execution_contract.v3` shape. | Compiler IR, Python trainer objectives, Teacher Council, TAAS, quantization oracle, TSAC, ITKV, import/export, K-Score calibration inputs, model cards, SBOM/provenance, runtime serving, and production artifact execution stay in node `3`; metric governance remains cross-linked to nodes `11` and `42`, cost/capacity to node `19`, supply chain to node `22`, polyglot build to node `28`, deployment proof to node `29`, build redline to node `43`, and code-completion product proof to node `50`. |

| Discovery and ecosystem check | Nodes `32`, `33`, and `34` already have V2 operating-system addenda for discovery, artifact registry/marketplace, and async communications. | The next maintenance pass should skip redundant discovery/registry/event V2 text and move to the next genuine gap. |
| Active concurrent files | `public/nav.js`, `src/intent.js`, and `vercel.json` are currently modified outside this spec lane. | Treat these as parallel-agent/user work. Do not overwrite or normalize them from the spec lane. |
| Generated-artifact risk | API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, screenshots, and release reports can race if regenerated in parallel. | Use the node `31` generated-artifact sequence before any future release-gate claim. |
| Final tree closure | `W-SPEC-MAINT-020` remains open. | The tree is maintained, not complete, until a verifier proves no unowned files, routes, pages, commands, tests, generated assets, claims, screenshots, production smokes, or external gates remain. |

Maintainers must treat this document as a living specification, not a research dump. A useful addition either tightens a node's source evidence, clarifies owner boundaries, adds an implementation packet, closes a stale ambiguity, or links a product claim to proof. Broad prose that does not change a work packet, proof gate, owner boundary, or relax condition should be rejected.

Required maintenance steps for every future pass:

1. Inspect the current tree and pick the owning node before writing.
2. If the work is new, allocate the next node number and add six subnodes in the same style as the existing tree.
3. Add a detailed ledger section with current repo evidence, external baselines when relevant, completion spec, atomic work tickets, and relax redline.
4. Cross-link any related product surfaces, routes, pages, docs, account screens, CLI/TUI commands, tests, screenshots, generated artifacts, and production proof.
5. Preserve open external gates instead of relabeling them done.
6. Run document hygiene: ASCII-only, no trailing whitespace, and `git diff --check` for this file.
7. Report the exact line for the tree node and the exact line for the detailed ledger.

The master tree is now the organizing contract for the rest of this goal.

### Execution, SDK, Worker, And Package Release Ledger V1

This pass covers master spec tree nodes `3`, `4`, and `5`: ML execution plane, worker/deploy plane, and SDK/package plane. These are the surfaces most likely to be under-verified by a green Node web build.

The repo also has a local-only package release readiness script: `scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs`. Its own usage text says it can summarize, catalog, run installer smoke checks, run local checks, validate manifests, require local contracts, and require publish readiness, but it never publishes packages, contacts registries, or prints secrets. That script should become the master spec tree's package-readiness source of truth.

#### Official Release And Provenance Standards

| standard | source | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| npm trusted publishing | npm docs say trusted publishing uses OIDC with supported CI providers, recommends restricting token publishing, and automatically generates provenance attestations for public packages from public repositories using GitHub Actions or GitLab CI/CD: https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers/ | every npm package must either use trusted publishing/provenance or document why it is local-source-only |
| PyPI trusted publishing | PyPI docs describe OIDC trusted publishers that mint short-lived API tokens valid for 15 minutes and avoid long-lived release tokens: https://docs.pypi.org/trusted-publishers/ | every Python package should use Trusted Publishing or explicitly stay unpublished/local-only |
| GitHub artifact attestations | GitHub docs say attestations create cryptographically signed provenance claims including workflow, repo, commit SHA, triggering event, and can include SBOM; they are useful only when verified: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/security/artifact-attestations | every released binary/package/manifest needs attestation generation and verification commands |
| CycloneDX SBOM | OWASP CycloneDX guide describes CycloneDX as ECMA-424, a BOM standard for software supply-chain transparency, risk, provenance, licensing, and dependency relationships: https://cyclonedx.org/guides/OWASP_CycloneDX-Authoritative-Guide-to-SBOM-en.pdf | released packages, workers, SDKs, and native artifacts need SBOMs, not only source tests |

#### Execution Plane Completion Contract

Every `apps/**` ML execution surface must prove:

1. install command and Python version;
2. CPU/mock mode and GPU/real mode separated in docs and API state;
3. deterministic fixture command that runs without secrets;
4. training/eval inputs, holdout sets, metrics, and output artifacts;
5. failure modes for missing GPU, missing provider key, dataset too small, unsupported model, and out-of-memory;
6. generated model card and provenance packet;
7. security/privacy handling for captured data;
8. public copy scope: mock/local/simulated/real/cloud/production.

#### Worker Plane Completion Contract

Every `workers/**` package must prove:

1. `doctor` command;
2. local fixture smoke;
3. input/output schema;
4. resource limits and timeout behavior;
5. secret-free readiness output;
6. deploy target and environment variables;
7. log/trace fields, preferably aligned with the OpenTelemetry GenAI convention version being emitted;
8. production failure-mode page or docs section;
9. package/build provenance if distributed.

#### SDK And Package Plane Completion Contract

Every SDK/package must prove:

1. install command for the package manager;
2. build command;
3. test/smoke command;
4. artifact verification behavior;
5. API compatibility matrix against Node API/OpenAPI;
6. version and changelog;
7. package provenance or local-source-only status;
8. SBOM or dependency manifest;
9. examples for capture, compile, distill, verify, run, and account/auth where relevant;
10. docs page and account/CLI bridge if user-facing.

#### Execution/Distribution Tickets To Generate

- W-DIST-001: source manifest ledger generator for `apps`, `workers`, `sdk`, and `packages`.
- W-DIST-002: debt-token classifier for execution/distribution code; fail on unscoped production stubs.
- W-DIST-003: worker doctor/smoke matrix for all worker packages.
- W-DIST-004: Python trainer real-vs-mock readiness contract.
- W-DIST-005: SDK compatibility suite across Node, Python, Rust, C, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, MCP, and VS Code.
- W-DIST-006: npm trusted publishing/provenance plan for each npm package.
- W-DIST-007: PyPI trusted publishing plan for each Python package.
- W-DIST-008: GitHub artifact attestation and SBOM workflow for every release artifact.
- W-DIST-009: package docs status labels: published, dry-run artifact, local-source-only, external gate.
- W-DIST-010: production release manifest that ties package versions to product graph, OpenAPI, SDK docs, SBOM, and attestations.

#### Node 3 ML Execution Plane, Reproducibility, And Artifact Quality Addendum V2

This pass maintains master spec tree node `3`: ML execution plane. Node `52` remains unused because the Python trainer, eval, runtime, import/export, model-card, benchmark, and cloud-training surfaces already belong to node `3`; node `4` owns worker/deploy packaging, node `5` owns SDK/package release, node `11` owns AI quality metrics, node `42` owns eval governance, and node `43` owns whole-build redline closure.

The key correction for the master tree is that a green JavaScript route suite is necessary but not sufficient for the ML execution plane. Node `3` is only complete when every Python and ML runtime path has a declared proof class, the mock/real boundary is visible, generated artifacts carry model-card/provenance/eval evidence, cloud/GPU paths are either proven or explicitly gated, and release output excludes local cache artifacts.

##### Node 3.0 Current ML Execution Evidence

Local scan signal for `apps/**` and related ML entrypoints:

| evidence | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `apps/trainer/pyproject.toml` | `kolm-trainer` requires Python `>=3.10`; base dependencies include FastAPI, uvicorn, pydantic, httpx, and dotenv; optional `gpu` extras include torch, transformers, peft, accelerate, datasets, unsloth, and bitsandbytes | lock Python dependency modes separately for CPU/mock, deterministic fixture, GPU/real, and cloud runner proof; treat metadata mojibake as source hygiene debt |
| `apps/trainer/README.md` | documents `KOLM_TRAINER_MODE=mock` and `KOLM_TRAINER_MODE=real`; real mode installs `.[gpu]`; bridge env vars include `KOLM_TRAINER_BRIDGE` and `KOLM_TRAINER_BRIDGE_TOKEN` | account, API, CLI, and docs must expose mock-versus-real state; README examples with mojibake must be repaired before release docs are called polished |
| `apps/trainer/distill.py` | implements token-level response distillation, KD loss, alpha/temperature controls, importance-weighted sampling, progressive distill, and capability gating patterns | require deterministic fixture proof and at least one real GPU proof packet before marketing quality/cost claims from this path |
| `apps/trainer/contrastive_distill.py`, `apps/trainer/online_dpo.py`, `apps/trainer/moe.py` | advanced objective paths exist for contrastive, preference, and mixture-style training | each objective needs its own task fixture, expected metric movement, failure modes, and artifact manifest before it is product-complete |
| `apps/eval/*.py` | evaluator, judge, PRM, and pack files exist under the Python app layer | eval outputs must name model, dataset, scorer, judge version, calibration status, and known limits |
| `apps/runtime/serve.py` | serves generative `.kolm` artifacts through OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/completions`, and `/v1/models`; prefers vLLM and falls back to transformers; includes speculative/prefix cache notes | runtime conformance needs request/response fixture tests, streaming tests, model-list tests, and hardware capability labels |
| `apps/export/model_card.py` | generates model-card sections and uses explicit `not_yet_disclosed` rather than fabricating evidence | model card generation must become a required artifact gate for every trained, distilled, quantized, or imported model |
| `apps/import/*.py` and `apps/export/*.py` | import/export utilities cover model movement and documentation outputs | round-trip proof must cover GGUF, safetensors, ONNX, CoreML/MLX where supported, and `.kolm` artifact verification |
| `apps/modal/*` and `apps/replicate/*` | cloud-runner manifests and requirements exist for managed training execution | cloud dry-run and real-run proof must declare image, dependency lock, region, GPU class, secrets policy, cost, and timeout |
| `scripts/test-compute-e2e.mjs` | includes a Python dispatch phase but intentionally verifies imports without executing training | do not treat import-only smoke as real training proof |
| `scripts/test-complexity-ramp.mjs` | launches the FastAPI trainer in mock mode for complexity-ramp testing | useful for local API contract proof; insufficient for GPU, data quality, or benchmark claims |
| `apps/**/__pycache__` | scan found 42 Python cache files below `apps/` | release hygiene must purge or exclude cache artifacts and fail if caches are bundled into shipping packages |

Node `3` also needs to track file-count drift. The latest local scan found `apps` at 170 files and 1,596,253 bytes, including 132 Python files and 42 Python cache files. By subdirectory, the largest app areas are `apps/trainer` at 78 files, `apps/runtime` at 45 files, `apps/export` at 12 files, `apps/eval` at 7 files, and `apps/import` at 7 files. Those counts supersede the older broad source inventory table above and should be generated automatically by W-SPEC-MAINT-004.

##### Node 3.1 External ML Execution Baseline V2

| baseline | source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Reproducibility is scoped, not absolute | PyTorch says completely reproducible results are not guaranteed across releases, commits, platforms, or CPU/GPU execution, while deterministic algorithms can reduce nondeterminism at a performance cost: https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/notes/randomness.html | every train/eval/quantization run must record PyTorch version, device, seeds, deterministic flags, known nondeterminism, and performance tradeoff |
| Model cards are part of model release, not optional copy | Hugging Face model cards are Markdown plus metadata and should describe intended uses, limitations, training parameters, datasets, and evaluation results: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/model-cards | every artifact promotion must include a generated model card with data, eval, limits, license, base model, and relation metadata |
| Runtime portability is hardware-provider-specific | ONNX Runtime execution providers target different hardware libraries for cloud, edge, CPU, GPU, FPGA, and NPUs and can be prioritized per session: https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers/ | import/export/runtime claims must name execution provider, priority order, fallback behavior, and unsupported operators |
| Experiment tracking must bind params, code, metrics, and artifacts | MLflow Tracking records runs with metadata, parameters, metrics, artifacts, models, and experiments: https://mlflow.org/docs/latest/ml/tracking/ | every distill/quant/eval job needs a run object that binds inputs, code version, metrics, checkpoints, model card, and output artifact hash |
| LLM evals need reusable tasks, datasets, scorers, logs, and private-data handling | OpenAI Evals is a framework and registry for evaluating LLM systems and supports custom/private evals: https://github.com/openai/evals; Inspect is an open-source eval framework with tasks, datasets, solvers, scorers, logs, and over 200 prebuilt evals: https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/ | K-Score and artifact evals need versioned task specs, private-fixture handling, scorer provenance, and raw result export |
| Runtime acceleration is a first-class product area | NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM documentation exposes dedicated optimization, serving, MoE, speculative/MTP, and hardware-specific performance sections: https://nvidia.github.io/TensorRT-LLM/ | any "fastest runtime" or acceleration claim needs hardware class, engine build params, batch/latency profile, and baseline comparison |
| Distillation and post-training have named method families | Hugging Face TRL lists SFT, DPO, GRPO, reward modeling, online methods, offline methods, and knowledge-distillation trainers such as GKD and MiniLLM: https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/index | Kolm objectives must be named by method family, fixture, loss, teacher/student relation, and eval gate rather than generic "distill" |

##### Node 3.2 ML Execution Contract V2

Every ML job, artifact promotion, benchmark, and runtime build should be able to emit this contract or a lossless superset:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.ml_execution_contract.v2",
  "tree_node": 3,
  "entrypoint": "apps/trainer/distill.py",
  "runtime": {
    "language": "python",
    "python": ">=3.10",
    "mode": "syntax|import|mock|deterministic-fixture|real-gpu|cloud-dry-run|cloud-real|production",
    "service": "trainer|eval|runtime|import|export|quantize|model-card|benchmark"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "manifest": "apps/trainer/pyproject.toml",
    "lockfile": null,
    "extras": ["gpu"],
    "container_digest": null
  },
  "hardware_profile": {
    "provider": "cpu|cuda|rocm|mlx|directml|modal|replicate|runpod|lambda|together|vast|ssh",
    "device": null,
    "memory_gb": null,
    "execution_provider": null
  },
  "data": {
    "capture_snapshot_hash": null,
    "train_split_hash": null,
    "holdout_hash": null,
    "rights_policy": null,
    "redaction_policy": null
  },
  "model": {
    "teacher": null,
    "student": null,
    "base_model": null,
    "adapter": null,
    "license": null
  },
  "objective": {
    "family": "kd|sft|dpo|grpo|contrastive|reasoning-trace|reranker|quantization|runtime|eval",
    "loss": null,
    "temperature": null,
    "alpha": null,
    "routing_policy": null
  },
  "determinism": {
    "seed": null,
    "torch_deterministic": null,
    "cudnn_benchmark": null,
    "known_nondeterminism": []
  },
  "outputs": {
    "artifact": null,
    "adapter": null,
    "model_card": null,
    "eval_report": null,
    "provenance": null,
    "sbom": null
  },
  "proof": {
    "syntax": null,
    "import": null,
    "mock_smoke": null,
    "fixture_eval": null,
    "real_run": null,
    "benchmark": null,
    "production_smoke": null
  },
  "limits": {
    "not_proven": [],
    "external_gates": []
  }
}
```

The contract must be generated from the run, not hand-written after the fact. A product page, pricing page, account UI, API response, CLI output, or investor deck may cite only the strongest proof class present in this contract.

##### Node 3.3 Proof Classes

| proof class | what it proves | what it does not prove |
|---|---|---|
| `syntax` | Python files parse under the declared interpreter | imports, dependencies, model behavior, training quality |
| `import` | module imports and optional dependency guards behave | API service behavior, training, eval quality |
| `mock` | FastAPI or job lifecycle shape works without secrets/GPU | real training, GPU compatibility, quality improvement |
| `deterministic-fixture` | a small stable task runs end-to-end with expected metrics and hashes | large-model performance, cloud cost, frontier quality |
| `simulated-planner` | strategy selection or scheduling behaves under synthetic portfolios | actual model quality or production latency |
| `real-local-gpu` | at least one real train/eval/quant run completes on a named local GPU | cloud portability or all hardware classes |
| `cloud-dry-run` | cloud runner validates image, config, secrets presence, and request shape without training | GPU execution, artifact quality, cost |
| `cloud-real` | managed GPU job completes and returns artifact/eval/provenance | production tenant correctness or all regions |
| `public-benchmark` | reproducible external-style data backs a published claim | private tenant performance or future model versions |
| `production-artifact` | deployed tenant path can load/run/verify artifact with logs and rollback | external certifications or partner adoption |

Relax rule: a node `3` surface can remain in the product only if its visible copy names the proof class. "Mock", "simulated", "local fixture", "GPU proven", "cloud proven", and "production proven" are materially different claims.
