##### Node 3 V2 Relax Redline

Node `3` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. no `apps/**/__pycache__`, `.pyc`, raw dataset, local checkpoint, or local secret residue ships in release artifacts;
2. every Python entrypoint has an owner, dependency manifest, install command, smoke command, and declared proof class;
3. mock and real training modes are visible in API, CLI, account UI, docs, and product copy;
4. every trainer, eval, quantization, import/export, runtime, and model-card path emits or links to `kolm.ml_execution_contract.v2`;
5. deterministic fixture proof records seeds, data hashes, device, framework versions, and known nondeterminism;
6. real GPU and cloud-run proofs exist for marketed GPU/cloud claims, or the claim stays behind an explicit external gate;
7. every artifact promotion produces model card, eval report, provenance, SBOM, and signed manifest hashes;
8. runtime serving claims are verified through OpenAI-compatible request/response fixtures and hardware provider labels;
9. import/export claims prove round-trip behavior per target, not just file parsing;
10. benchmark and K-Score claims link raw data, commands, hardware, model versions, and freshness date;
11. cloud runners prove cleanup, timeout, cost accounting, and secret redaction;
12. production smoke verifies one tenant path from account/API trigger to Python job/run artifact to runtime load/verify.

#### Node 3 V3 Compiler-Distiller Execution, Artifact Quality, And Runtime Optimization Addendum

This pass maintains master spec tree node `3`. Node `52` remains unused. Compile, distill, quantization, model import/export, model cards, runtime serving, K-Score job inputs, artifact promotion, and device/cloud execution already have a home: node `3`. The V3 correction is that node `3` must no longer be described as a loose set of ML features. It is the product execution plane that converts captured model behavior into a proved, signed, portable `.kolm` artifact.

Node `3` is primary owner for:

- compiler IR, spec compiler, native compiler, runtime lowering, graph rewrites, and hardware/runtime target selection;
- Python trainer, cloud trainer, local trainer, distillation objectives, Teacher Council, TAAS, curriculum, reasoning-trace, contrastive, DPO/online, and importance-weighted training;
- quantization oracle, DAQ, NVFP4 and lower-precision experiments, sparse attention compiler, ITKV profile, runtime placement, and memory-aware serving inputs;
- import/export round trips for GGUF, safetensors, ONNX, CoreML, MLX, ExecuTorch, TensorRT-LLM, vLLM, and `.kolm` verification;
- artifact lineage, dependency graph, model-card emission, SBOM, provenance, signing, rollback, and promotion gates;
- production artifact execution proof from account/API/CLI trigger to job record to runtime load to verified output.

Cross-links are mandatory but do not transfer ownership. Node `11` owns quality metric definition, node `42` owns eval governance, node `19` owns cost/capacity accounting, node `22` owns supply-chain attestations, node `28` owns polyglot build/runtime language ownership, node `29` owns deploy/prod smoke, node `43` owns whole-build redline closure, node `50` owns code-completion/build-finishing product proof, and node `51` owns demo/runner proof. The node `3` contract must feed those nodes with evidence, not duplicate their ledgers.

The current local scan shows that the backend is not "just JS". The Node API plane is JavaScript-heavy, but the ML execution plane includes Python app services, runtime/export/import adapters, and JavaScript orchestration modules that bind them into the product.

|---|---|---|

| app directory spread | `apps/trainer` 78 files, `apps/runtime` 45, `apps/export` 12, `apps/showcase` 10, `apps/eval` 7, `apps/import` 7, `apps/replicate` 4, `apps/modal` 3, `apps/capture` 2, `apps/data` 2 | trainer, runtime, import/export, eval, and cloud runners each need independent proof packets |

The important reading is not that the files are large. It is that node `3` has enough real implementation surface to demand a generated ledger. A manual paragraph is no longer sufficient. Every node `3` file needs owner, proof class, entrypoint, dependency manifest, supported mode, and acceptance test.

##### Node 3.6 External Baseline V3

| baseline | primary source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Reproducibility is bounded by framework, platform, device, and version | PyTorch documents that complete reproducibility is not guaranteed across releases, commits, platforms, CPU/GPU, and that deterministic choices can reduce performance: https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/2.12/notes/randomness.html | every distill, eval, quantization, and runtime proof must record framework version, hardware, seeds, deterministic flags, known nondeterminism, and performance tradeoff |
| Modern compilers are IR, verifier, dialect, and lowering systems | MLIR frames itself as reusable and extensible compiler infrastructure for heterogeneous hardware, graph transforms, quantization, target-specific operations, and modular compiler libraries: https://mlir.llvm.org/ | Kolm compile must own an IR verifier, text dump/parse path, canonical fixtures, pass registry, target lowering map, and failure diagnostics |
| Edge/datacenter ML runtime is ahead-of-time plus runtime scheduling | IREE describes an MLIR-based end-to-end compiler/runtime that lowers models to a unified IR for datacenter through mobile/edge, with ahead-of-time compilation and compiled scheduling/execution logic: https://iree.dev/ | Kolm "run anywhere" requires per-target compilation artifacts, scheduling policy, target constraints, and device-specific acceptance proof |
| Runtime portability depends on execution providers and fallback | ONNX Runtime documents execution providers for different hardware libraries across cloud, edge, CPU, GPU, FPGA, and NPUs, with capability allocation to supported subgraphs: https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers/ | every imported/exported or runtime artifact must name provider priority, unsupported operators, fallback policy, and hardware acceleration status |
| High-performance serving is a whole runtime system | vLLM emphasizes PagedAttention, continuous batching, chunked prefill, prefix caching, CUDA/HIP graphs, quantization families, optimized kernels, speculative decoding, disaggregated prefill/decode, structured outputs, tool calling, and broad hardware/model support: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/ | Kolm runtime claims must include batching, cache, prefill/decode, quantization, kernel, structured-output, tool-call, and hardware support dimensions |
| TensorRT-LLM treats sparse attention, speculative decoding, KV cache, quantization, benchmarking, guided decoding, and Prometheus metrics as first-class docs/features | NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM docs expose these as named deployment, feature, CLI, and benchmarking areas: https://nvidia.github.io/TensorRT-LLM/ | TSAC, ITKV, DAQ, speculative student draft, and structured output features need metric-specific proof and should not be hidden as generic optimizations |
| Universal LLM deployment products combine compilation and platform matrices | MLC LLM describes a universal deployment engine with ML compilation and native deployment across Linux/Windows, macOS, browser WebGPU/WASM, iOS, and Android: https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm | enterprise "down to any device" must be backed by a device/runtime matrix and real round-trip artifact proof per target class |
| Model cards are release artifacts | Hugging Face model-card docs state that model cards accompany models and should include intended use, limitations, training parameters, datasets, and evaluation results: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/model-cards | every compiled/distilled/imported artifact promotion must emit a model card with intended use, limits, training/eval data, license, base-model relation, and scope |
| Distillation is a family of named methods, not one feature | Hugging Face TRL lists post-training and knowledge-distillation trainers such as GKDTrainer and MiniLLMTrainer: https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/index | Kolm objectives must be named and evaluated separately: KD, GKD-like, MiniLLM-like, DPO, online preference, contrastive, reasoning-trace, curriculum, and teacher-council paths |
| LLM evals need reusable registries, custom/private evals, and rights controls | OpenAI Evals describes a framework and registry for evaluating LLMs and LLM systems, with custom/private evals and rights caveats: https://github.com/openai/evals | K-Score, bakeoffs, public benchmarks, and private tenant evals need versioned task specs, scorer provenance, raw results, rights labels, and private-data controls |

##### Node 3.7 Product Execution Contract V3

Every compile, distill, quantization, runtime, import/export, and artifact-promotion job should emit this object or a lossless superset. If the product cannot emit it, the feature is not done.

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.ml_product_execution_contract.v3",
  "tree_node": 3,
  "surface": "capture-to-distill|teacher-council|taas|compile-ir|native-compile|quantization|runtime-placement|artifact-promotion|eval-k-score|device-deploy|import-export|code-completion",
  "entrypoint": "src/distill-pipeline.js",
  "python_entrypoint": "apps/trainer/distill.py",
  "mode": "syntax|import|mock|deterministic-fixture|real-local-gpu|cloud-dry-run|cloud-real|public-benchmark|production-artifact",
  "tenant_scope": {
    "tenant_id": null,
    "namespace": null,
    "auth_mode": "anonymous|api-key|session|service-account",
    "data_residency": null
  },
  "data_rights": {
    "capture_snapshot_hash": null,
    "train_split_hash": null,
    "holdout_hash": null,

  },
  "objective": {
    "family": "kd|gkd|minillm|sft|dpo|online-dpo|contrastive|reasoning-trace|curriculum|taas|teacher-council|rag-aware|agent-tool-use|multimodal|quantization|compile|runtime|eval",
    "loss": null,
    "temperature": null,
    "alpha": null,

    "routing_policy": null,
    "calibration_version": null
  },
  "compiler": {
    "ir_version": null,
    "passes": [],
    "target": "cpu|cuda|rocm|mlx|directml|webgpu|wasm|coreml|onnx|tensorrt|vllm|gguf|executorch|mobile",
    "provider_priority": [],

    "fallback_policy": null
  },
  "runtime": {
    "batching": null,
    "prefill_decode_policy": null,

  },
  "determinism": {
    "seed": null,
    "framework_versions": {},
    "device": null,
    "deterministic_flags": {},
    "known_nondeterminism": [],
    "performance_tradeoff": null
  },
  "metrics": {
    "kscore": null,
    "quality": null,
    "latency_p50_ms": null,
    "latency_p95_ms": null,
    "throughput_tps": null,
    "cost_per_1k": null,
    "memory_peak_mb": null,
    "energy_estimate": null,
    "redaction_f1": null,
    "numeric_accuracy": null
  },
  "outputs": {
    "artifact_hash": null,
    "model_card": null,
    "eval_report": null,
    "lineage": null,

    "sbom": null,
    "provenance": null,
    "signature": null,

    "import": null,

    "cloud_real": null,

    "account_label": null,
    "docs_label": null,
    "api_label": null,
    "cli_label": null
  }
}
```

The V3 contract is stronger than V2 in three ways. First, it binds the product claim to the proof class. Second, it includes runtime optimization decisions such as batching, cache, sparse attention, quantization, and structured output. Third, it makes model card, eval, provenance, SBOM, lineage, and rollback part of the output contract instead of optional side documents.

##### Node 3.8 Acceptance Ladder For Inventions And ML Waves

The W707 through W835 wave roadmap remains owned by node `31`; node `3` owns the proof ladder for the ML execution subset. A wave is additive only if it moves at least one node `3` surface up this ladder without making older claims less true.

| ladder stage | accepted evidence | product-copy ceiling |
|---|---|---|
| `invention-prose` | research doc or roadmap entry exists | "planned" only |
| `simulated-portfolio` | synthetic portfolio simulator shows expected metric movement and failure modes | "simulated" only |
| `module-implemented` | source module exists with owner, API, tests, and no hidden stub claim | "available in local build" only |
| `fixture-proven` | deterministic fixture passes with expected hashes and metrics | "fixture-proven" |
| `backend-routed` | API route, job state, errors, auth, and envelope are wired | "API-accessible" |
| `account-operated` | account UI can configure, launch, observe, and recover the job | "operator-ready" |
| `artifact-emitted` | signed `.kolm` artifact includes model card, eval, provenance, SBOM, and lineage | "artifact-emitting" |
| `runtime-loaded` | artifact loads in at least one declared runtime with conformance fixtures | "runtime-proven for target X" |
| `benchmarked` | public or customer-private benchmark has raw data, command, hardware, and freshness | "benchmark-backed" |
| `production-proven` | tenant production smoke proves trigger, job, artifact, runtime, logs, rollback, and support path | "production-proven" |

Any roadmap item that stops at prose or simulation is useful research, not shipped product. This rule applies to distillation research papers, K-Score calibration, DAQ, TSAC, ITKV, speculative decoding, active learning, poisoning detection, structured output validation, and enterprise device deployment.

##### Node 3.9 Feature Matrix For Completion

| feature family | current implementation anchors | V3 done state |
|---|---|---|
| compiler IR and lowering | `src/compile-pipeline.js`, `src/compile-ir.js`, `src/spec-compile.js`, `src/native-compile.js`, `src/compile.js` | IR parser/verifier/canonicalizer, deterministic golden fixtures, pass registry, native target matrix, failure diagnostics, artifact hash binding |
| distillation objectives | `src/distill-pipeline.js`, `apps/trainer/distill.py`, `apps/trainer/contrastive_distill.py`, `apps/trainer/online_dpo.py`, `apps/trainer/moe.py` | objective-specific fixture, real GPU proof when marketed, teacher/student metadata, holdout results, data-rights policy, artifact output |
| Teacher Council and TAAS | `src/teacher-council.js`, student architecture selectors, roadmap W716/W718 | teacher weighting, disagreement report, architecture-search trace, cost/latency/quality tradeoff, rollback if council underperforms |
| quantization and runtime optimization | `src/quantization-oracle.js`, `src/itkv-profile.js`, `src/tsac-compiler.js`, runtime policy modules | calibration dataset, quality-loss budget, memory/latency/throughput proof, device compatibility, exact fallback, no hidden "best" claims |
| runtime serving | `apps/runtime/serve.py`, runtime policy, model registry | OpenAI-compatible chat/completions/models/streaming fixtures, tool/schema limits, vLLM/transformers fallback proof, hardware/provider label |
| import/export | `apps/import/*`, `apps/export/*`, model weights manifest | round-trip proof per format, unsupported operator report, dependency hash, license/rights carry-through, runtime compatibility label |
| eval and K-Score | `src/kscore.js`, `src/kscore-calibration.js`, `src/eval-numeric.js`, `src/bakeoff.js`, `apps/eval/*` | calibrated task registry, raw reports, human-correlation claim scope, private/public fixture labels, freshness date, per-claim unlock mapping |
| artifact promotion | `src/artifact.js`, `src/artifact-lineage.js`, `src/model-card-emit.js`, `src/model-weights-manifest.js` | model card, eval, provenance, SBOM, signature, rollback, lineage diff, dependency graph, account/API/CLI visibility |
| code-completion/build finishing | node `50`, compile/eval/runtime contracts, K-Score inputs | generated patch/job artifacts must cite tests run, failure modes, codebase context, model provenance, and rollback path before being called build-ready |
| enterprise device deployment | runtime target matrix, ONNX/CoreML/MLX/ExecuTorch/TensorRT/vLLM/GGUF paths | one artifact can be inspected, compiled, loaded, and verified per target class with known limits and fallback |

##### Node 3.10 Additive Invention Rules

The research lane can invent aggressively, but node `3` needs a filter so inventions turn into product rather than drift. Each new ML invention must include:

1. target product surface: API traffic ownership, compile/distill top models, or enterprise device deployment;
2. tracked metric movement: quality, cost, latency, memory, reliability, reproducibility, auditability, conversion, retention, or support load;
3. baseline method and source;
4. required data and rights policy;
5. implementation anchors in `src`, `apps`, `tests`, `docs`, account, CLI, and API;
6. proof ladder target for the current wave;
7. artifact outputs, including model card and eval report;
8. failure modes and rollback;
9. copy ceiling: exactly what marketing/account/docs can say now;
10. next gate to move from simulation to production proof.

The following invention lanes are additive to the roadmap and should not replace W707 through W835:

| invention lane | why it matters | first implementation packet |
|---|---|---|
| compiler-guided student search | chooses the student architecture by target runtime, memory, latency, and eval loss rather than parameter count alone | extend TAAS to emit architecture candidates plus compile feasibility and runtime cost |
| evidence-weighted Teacher Council | reduces teacher hallucination and poisoned captures by weighting teachers by calibrated task evidence, rights, and disagreement | teacher-council report with disagreement, confidence, data-rights, and per-task weights |
| quantization-aware objective shaping | trains/distills with the future quantization target in the loop, reducing quality drop at low precision | DAQ fixture where quantization loss budget is part of objective selection |
| sparse-attention proof compiler | turns observed attention patterns into a safe sparse policy with exact fallback and quality delta | TSAC fixture with exactness flag, approximation bound, and fallback cost |
| importance-tiered cache scheduler | spends KV memory on evidence-bearing tokens and high-uncertainty spans instead of raw recency alone | ITKV fixture with memory, hit rate, latency, and quality metrics |
| K-Score claim unlocker | ties every public or sales claim to a calibrated eval report and blocks vague quality copy | K-Score report schema that names the exact UI/docs/pricing claim it unlocks |
| production artifact black box recorder | records every run's input policy, runtime path, model card, eval, provenance, and rollback pointer | artifact execution log that account/API/CLI can fetch without leaking secrets |
| device-fit compiler | chooses CoreML, MLX, ONNX, ExecuTorch, GGUF, TensorRT, vLLM, WebGPU, or WASM path from constraints | target selection report with unsupported ops, fallback, expected latency, and package size |

| W-ML-V3-019 | 3 | Add tenant production artifact path smoke from account/API launch to job record to artifact bundle to runtime load to verified output to rollback. |
| W-ML-V3-020 | 3 | Generate a node `3` V3 completion certificate that proves every ML execution surface is syntax/import/mock/fixture/GPU/cloud/benchmark/production proven or explicitly gated. |

##### Node 3 V3 Redline

Node `3` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every ML execution file in `apps`, `src`, and `tests` is mapped to `docs/ml-product-execution-ledger.json`;
2. every compile, distill, quantization, runtime, import/export, eval, and artifact-promotion path emits or links to `kolm.ml_product_execution_contract.v3`;
3. the product graph, API, account UI, CLI, TUI, docs, and support/admin surfaces show the same proof class and next action for every ML job;
4. `src/compile-pipeline.js` debt is classified and no blocker-class item is hidden behind green route tests;
5. deterministic fixtures record seeds, framework versions, device, data hashes, expected outputs, known nondeterminism, and performance tradeoffs;
6. every marketed distillation objective has a task fixture, eval report, teacher/student metadata, data-rights policy, and model card;
7. DAQ, TSAC, ITKV, speculative decoding, and runtime placement cannot be called shipped until each has quality, latency, memory, fallback, and runtime-target proof;
8. import/export support is claimed only per format and target that has round-trip proof and unsupported-operator reporting;
9. every artifact promotion emits model card, eval, lineage, dependency graph, SBOM, provenance, signature, rollback pointer, and visible copy ceiling;
10. K-Score and benchmark claims cite raw data, command lines, model versions, scorer versions, hardware, public/private scope, and freshness date;
11. cloud/GPU claims stay behind env-gated readiness until real credentials, runner logs, cleanup, timeout, cost, and artifact proof exist;
12. the production path proves at least one tenant can launch, observe, load, verify, and roll back a `.kolm` artifact without relying on mock-only evidence.

#### Node 4 Worker, Queue, Deploy, And Runtime-Isolation Addendum V2

This pass maintains master spec tree node `4`: worker and deploy plane. Node `4` owns isolated worker packages, edge/serverless execution, compile-server deployment, object-storage readiness adapters when used by workers, queue and retry semantics, worker identity, resource budgets, and production worker smoke. Node `3` remains the ML execution owner for Python training/eval/runtime logic; node `36` owns durable job orchestration; node `19` owns capacity and cost; node `22` owns supply-chain attestations; node `29` owns production deployment proof; node `39` owns observability.

The completion bar for node `4` is not "the worker package exists". It is that every worker has a typed job envelope, resource budget, doctor command, fixture smoke, retry/idempotency behavior, secret-free readiness output, deployment target, logs/traces/metrics, failure-mode documentation, and production or explicitly gated deployment proof.

##### Node 4.0 Current Worker And Deploy Evidence

Local worker scan signal, excluding dependency and build directories:

| evidence | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `workers/**/package.json` | 12 worker packages expose `doctor` scripts: audio-tokenize, constrained, distill, itkv, media-redact, multimodal-redact-audio, multimodal-redact-image, quantize, runtime-build, tsac, video-tokenize, and vision-tokenize | doctor coverage is good, but each doctor needs a uniform schema and a local fixture smoke separate from readiness |
| `workers/distill/distill.mjs` | supports `--doctor`, `--mode=collect`, `--mode=stub`, and `--mode=full`; intentionally keeps heavy ML dependencies out of the root install | modes must be surfaced as collect/stub/full proof classes and cannot be collapsed into a single "distill worker ready" claim |
| `workers/quantize/package.json` | declares per-method Python dependency groups for int4, int8, GPTQ, AWQ, AQLM, QuIP, EXL2/EXL3, HQQ, and QAT | doctor output must report readiness by method, hardware, calibration data, and external repo path, not just package presence |
| `workers/compile-server/*` | includes `server.mjs`, Dockerfile, docker-compose, Helm chart, values, deployment, service, and README; self-hosted compile server requires `KOLM_SHARED_SECRET` and documents no-egress verification | deployment artifacts need image SBOM/provenance attestations, NetworkPolicy proof, secret policy, health/readiness probes, and byte-identical artifact smoke |
| `workers/runtime-build/build.mjs` | largest resource-signal hit count among worker files for env/fetch/timeout/schema/log patterns | runtime build work needs explicit resource budgets, artifact output schema, cleanup, and failure states |
| `workers/constrained/constrained.mjs` | constrained decoding worker has schema/timeout/env/log signal and debt tokens | JSON/schema/grammar behavior must be fixture-proven, with invalid input and timeout cases |
| `workers/tsac/tsac.mjs` and `workers/itkv/itkv.mjs` | sparse-attention compiler and importance-tiered KV cache workers exist with doctor scripts and debt markers | compiler/cache workers require deterministic input/output fixtures, runtime profiles, memory budgets, and benchmark deltas |
| `workers/*tokenize*` | audio, video, vision, and tokenizer-train workers exist; media workers are heavy edge cases for file size and privacy | each media worker needs fixture corpus, max payload policy, streaming/chunking behavior, redaction/privacy handling, and output hash proof |
| `workers/*redact*` | media and multimodal redaction workers exist for image/audio flows | redaction workers need per-class precision/recall fixture proof and no raw media leakage in logs/artifacts |
| debt marker scan | debt tokens remain in distill, itkv, multimodal-redact-audio, tsac, constrained, video-tokenize, audio-tokenize, compile-server, tokenizer-train, and packages | every `stub`, `mock`, `placeholder`, `TODO`, or `FIXME` in worker code must be classified as test fixture, honest mode, or unfinished production path |

Latest worker file-count scan by top-level worker directory:

| worker area | files | bytes |
|---|---:|---:|
| `workers/compile-server` | 8 | 32,624 |
| `workers/distill` | 5 | 59,165 |
| `workers/quantize` | 5 | 52,835 |
| `workers/constrained` | 3 | 20,699 |
| `workers/itkv` | 3 | 20,197 |
| `workers/tsac` | 3 | 17,319 |
| `workers/audio-tokenize` | 2 | 20,710 |
| `workers/video-tokenize` | 2 | 23,955 |
| `workers/vision-tokenize` | 2 | 19,672 |
| `workers/runtime-build` | 2 | 14,379 |

Node `4` must also own deploy-manifest drift. The compile server currently has container, compose, and Helm artifacts. Other worker packages are local packages with doctor scripts but do not yet show a uniform deploy descriptor in the scan. That is acceptable only if their release status is explicitly "local worker package" rather than "managed production worker".

##### Node 4.1 External Worker And Deploy Baseline V2

| baseline | source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Edge workers have hard resource budgets | Cloudflare Workers plan limits include CPU time, memory, subrequests, simultaneous connections, worker size, startup time, request-body limits, and queue consumer duration: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/platform/limits/index.md | every edge/serverless worker must declare CPU, memory, body size, connection, subrequest, duration, and streaming/chunking limits |
| Queues require explicit batch, ack, retry, and delay behavior | Cloudflare Queues documents batch size/timeout, per-message ack/retry, whole-batch retry, default retry counts, dead-letter queues, and delay settings: https://developers.cloudflare.com/queues/configuration/batching-retries/ | every queued job needs idempotency key, ack policy, retry policy, DLQ policy, max attempts, backoff, and poison-message behavior |
| Batch jobs need completion, retries, deadlines, and cleanup | Kubernetes Jobs create pods until completions are reached, retry failed pods, support `backoffLimit`, `activeDeadlineSeconds`, and require cleanup of completed jobs: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/ | every self-hosted worker deploy needs job deadline, restart policy, backoff, cleanup, log retention, and terminal-state mapping |
| Worker identity matters operationally | Temporal defines worker entities/processes around task queues and recommends worker identity that links to process, machine, environment, region, or log stream: https://docs.temporal.io/workers | every worker run must emit worker identity, queue, version, environment, region, and log/trace correlation IDs |
| GenAI observability conventions are still evolving | OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions are in development and include events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, agent spans, OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure/Bedrock conventions, and stability opt-in rules: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | worker telemetry must declare the semantic-convention version emitted and avoid silently changing span/metric names |
| Container images need provenance and SBOM attestations | Docker BuildKit supports provenance and SBOM attestations, stores them as in-toto JSON attached to image indexes, and can enable SBOM/provenance flags: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/ | compile-server and worker images need SBOM/provenance generation and verification, not just Dockerfile presence |
| CI and deploy secrets need least privilege and masking | GitHub Actions secure-use guidance says automatic redaction is not guaranteed, secrets should be least-privilege, `GITHUB_TOKEN` permissions should be minimized, and sensitive data should not be plaintext in workflow files: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use | worker deploy workflows must minimize token permissions, avoid structured secrets in logs, and prove secret values are absent from readiness output |

##### Node 4.2 Worker Job Envelope Contract V2

Every worker command, queued job, deploy target, and account/API-triggered background action should emit this contract or a lossless superset:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.worker_job_contract.v2",
  "tree_node": 4,
  "worker": {
    "id": "distill|quantize|compile-server|constrained|itkv|tsac|runtime-build|audio-tokenize|video-tokenize|vision-tokenize|media-redact|multimodal-redact-image|multimodal-redact-audio|tokenizer-train",
    "package": "@kolm/distill-worker",
    "version": null,
    "entrypoint": "workers/distill/distill.mjs",
    "identity": null,
    "environment": "local|ci|staging|production|airgap|edge|cloud"
  },
  "job": {
    "id": null,
    "type": null,
    "mode": "doctor|fixture|stub|collect|full|dry-run|production",
    "idempotency_key": null,
    "tenant_id": null,
    "input_schema": null,
    "output_schema": null,
    "artifact_contract": null
  },
  "queue": {
    "backend": "none|cloudflare-queues|kubernetes-job|temporal|bullmq|sqs|pubsub|custom",
    "name": null,
    "batch_size": null,
    "batch_timeout_ms": null,
    "max_retries": null,
    "retry_backoff": null,
    "dead_letter_queue": null,
    "ack_policy": null
  },
  "resources": {
    "cpu_ms": null,
    "memory_mb": null,
    "duration_ms": null,
    "request_body_bytes": null,
    "subrequest_limit": null,
    "connection_limit": null,
    "artifact_bytes": null
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "node": null,
    "python": null,
    "container_digest": null,
    "optional_tools": [],
    "external_services": []
  },
  "security": {
    "secrets_in_output": false,
    "required_secret_groups": [],
    "egress_policy": null,
    "redaction_policy": null,
    "least_privilege_policy": null
  },
  "observability": {
    "request_id": null,
    "trace_id": null,
    "span_id": null,
    "otel_semconv_version": null,
    "log_stream": null,
    "metrics": []
  },
  "proof": {
    "doctor": null,
    "fixture_smoke": null,
    "deploy_dry_run": null,
    "container_attestation": null,
    "production_smoke": null,
    "rollback": null
  },
  "limits": {
    "not_proven": [],
    "external_gates": []
  }
}
```

This contract is the worker equivalent of the node `3` ML execution contract. Node `4` owns job handling, deployment, runtime limits, and worker observability. Node `3` may attach ML-specific model/data/eval fields, but it must not redefine the job, queue, retry, or deploy envelope.

##### Node 4.3 Worker Proof Classes

| proof class | what it proves | what it does not prove |
|---|---|---|
| `package-shape` | package, entrypoint, license, and doctor script exist | the worker executes real work |
| `doctor` | local prerequisites and optional tools are detected without secrets | fixture correctness or production readiness |
| `fixture-smoke` | a small input produces expected output, hashes, and failure states | scale, cloud execution, or tenant isolation |
| `schema-contract` | input/output/job envelopes validate and reject malformed data | runtime resource behavior |
| `queue-contract` | idempotency, ack, retry, DLQ, cancellation, and poison-message behavior are specified and tested | deploy or production availability |
| `deploy-dry-run` | container/compose/Helm/edge manifest renders and validates without secrets | real worker execution |
| `resource-profile` | CPU, memory, duration, payload, artifact, and connection budgets are measured against fixtures | all tenant workloads |
| `container-provenance` | image digest, SBOM, and provenance attestation can be generated and verified | runtime correctness |
| `production-worker-smoke` | deployed worker runs one tenant-scoped job, emits telemetry, returns artifact/status, and cleans up | all regions, all workloads, or external certification |

Relax rule: product copy, account UI, CLI, docs, and API readiness may cite only the strongest proof class available per worker. A doctor-only worker is not production-ready; a fixture-smoked worker is not cloud-proven; a deploy-dry-run worker is not production-smoked.

##### Node 4.4 Atomic Work Tickets V2

| ticket | owner node | outcome |
|---|---:|---|
| W-WORKER-V2-001 | 4 | Generate `docs/worker-deploy-ledger.json` mapping every `workers/**` package to owner, entrypoint, package name, doctor command, modes, dependencies, deploy targets, and proof class. |
| W-WORKER-V2-002 | 4 | Standardize all worker `--doctor --json` output around `kolm.worker_job_contract.v2` readiness fields and `secret_values_included: false`. |
| W-WORKER-V2-003 | 4 | Add fixture smoke commands for every worker package, separate from doctor, with expected output hashes and negative cases. |
| W-WORKER-V2-004 | 4 | Classify every worker debt token as test fixture, honest non-production mode, or unfinished production path; fail release on unclassified debt. |
| W-WORKER-V2-005 | 4 | Add input/output JSON schemas for every worker job type and a shared validator for malformed input, unsupported mode, oversized payload, and forbidden secrets. |
| W-WORKER-V2-006 | 4 | Add idempotency, ack, retry, backoff, DLQ, cancellation, and poison-message policy for every queued or async worker action. |
| W-WORKER-V2-007 | 4 | Add worker resource profiles: CPU, memory, duration, payload size, artifact size, subrequests, open connections, and streaming/chunking strategy. |
| W-WORKER-V2-008 | 4 | Add compile-server container proof: Docker build, compose render, Helm render, NetworkPolicy/no-egress proof, health/readiness probes, and byte-identical artifact smoke. |
| W-WORKER-V2-009 | 4 | Add SBOM and provenance attestations for compile-server and any publishable worker image; verify the attestation against image digest. |
| W-WORKER-V2-010 | 4 | Add worker identity fields for environment, region, process/container/task ID, package version, git SHA, trace ID, and log stream. |
| W-WORKER-V2-011 | 4 | Align worker logs/traces/metrics with a declared OpenTelemetry semantic-convention version and preserve backwards-compatible telemetry names. |
| W-WORKER-V2-012 | 4 | Add secrets policy for worker env vars, CI deploy tokens, provider keys, shared secrets, and readiness output; fail if secret values appear in logs or JSON. |
| W-WORKER-V2-013 | 4 | Add quantize worker per-method readiness and fixture smoke for int4, int8, GPTQ, AWQ, AQLM, QuIP, EXL2/EXL3, HQQ, and QAT, each with hardware and calibration labels. |
| W-WORKER-V2-014 | 4 | Add distill worker collect/stub/full proof ladder, including teacher bridge privacy, redaction maps, holdout logs, manifest hashes, and real full-mode gate. |
| W-WORKER-V2-015 | 4 | Add constrained, TSAC, ITKV, and runtime-build deterministic fixture suites with memory budgets and benchmark-delta evidence. |
| W-WORKER-V2-016 | 4 | Add media worker corpus and privacy proof for audio/video/vision tokenization plus image/audio/media redaction; include max payload and raw-media log checks. |
| W-WORKER-V2-017 | 4 | Add account/API/CLI worker status parity so users can see worker mode, proof class, missing dependency groups, queue backlog, last smoke, and production deployment state. |
| W-WORKER-V2-018 | 4 | Add deploy-target labels: local package, container, compose, Helm, Cloudflare Worker, queue consumer, Kubernetes Job, air-gapped server, or external cloud job. |
| W-WORKER-V2-019 | 4 | Add crash/restart drill: kill worker mid-job, verify retry/idempotency/cleanup, and confirm no duplicate artifact or unbounded retry loop. |
| W-WORKER-V2-020 | 4 | Add a node `4` completion certificate requiring package-shape, doctor, fixture, schema, queue, deploy, resource, provenance, and production-smoke evidence or explicit external gates for every worker. |

##### Node 4 V2 Relax Redline

Node `4` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. every `workers/**` package has a primary owner, entrypoint, package manifest, doctor command, fixture smoke command, and proof class;
2. every worker emits or links to `kolm.worker_job_contract.v2`;
3. every worker mode is labeled as doctor, fixture, stub, collect, full, dry-run, or production;
4. every queued or async action has idempotency, ack, retry, backoff, DLQ, cancellation, poison-message, and cleanup behavior;
5. every worker has CPU, memory, duration, payload, artifact, connection, and subrequest budgets where applicable;
6. every deployable worker has an explicit target label and deploy-dry-run evidence;
7. every containerized worker has SBOM/provenance attestation evidence and image digest verification;
8. compile-server deploy proofs include Docker, compose, Helm, secret policy, health/readiness probes, no-egress proof, rollback, and byte-identical artifact smoke;
9. worker telemetry declares log, metric, trace, request ID, worker identity, and OpenTelemetry semantic-convention version;
10. worker readiness and logs never print secret values;
11. all worker debt markers are classified or resolved;
12. production worker smoke proves at least one tenant-scoped job per deployed worker class, or the worker remains visibly local/dry-run only.

#### Node 4 V3 Worker Control Plane, Runtime Isolation, And Operator Drill Addendum

This pass maintains master spec tree node `4`. Node `52` remains unused. Node `4` is not the ML algorithm owner and not the production-deploy signoff owner. It is the worker control plane: how background work is packaged, launched, isolated, budgeted, retried, observed, secured, and proven by an operator.

Node `4` owns:

- worker package boundaries under `workers/**`;
- package-level `doctor` and fixture-smoke commands;
- queue, lease, ack, retry, backoff, DLQ, cancellation, timeout, poison-message, and idempotency semantics;
- worker runtime limits: CPU, memory, duration, payload, artifact size, open connections, subrequests, and chunking;
- worker identity, environment, version, process/container/task ID, region, log stream, and trace ID;
- compile-server deploy shape: Docker, compose, Helm, health/readiness, fail-closed shared secret, no-egress mode, artifact directory, rollback, and byte-identical artifact claim;
- runtime isolation and data handling for media, redaction, tokenization, distill, quantization, constrained decoding, TSAC, ITKV, and runtime-build workers;
- local, CI, staging, production, air-gapped, edge, queue-consumer, and Kubernetes job proof classes.

Node `3` attaches ML-specific model/data/eval artifacts to worker jobs. Node `4` owns the worker envelope itself. Node `36` owns durable workflow orchestration, node `19` capacity/cost, node `22` supply-chain attestations, node `29` production deployment proof, node `39` observability policy, and node `43` whole-build redline. Every one of those nodes consumes worker proof from node `4`; none should redefine worker semantics elsewhere.

##### Node 4.5 Current Worker Control Evidence V3

This pass used the worker packages directly, not only source scans. Local shell state matters: a worker can have a real doctor entrypoint and still truthfully report missing local Python/GPU/toolchain dependencies.

| evidence area | current signal | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| worker source inventory excluding dependency/build folders | `43` files, `346,161` bytes across `14` worker areas | worker state is small enough to require a complete generated ledger, not a sampled audit |
| package-bound workers | `12` package manifests expose `doctor`: audio-tokenize, constrained, distill, itkv, media-redact, multimodal-redact-audio, multimodal-redact-image, quantize, runtime-build, tsac, video-tokenize, vision-tokenize | package doctors are real operator handles; every one needs JSON schema, fixture smoke, and account/API/CLI parity |
| compile server | `workers/compile-server` has no package manifest but has `server.mjs`, Dockerfile, compose, Helm chart, service, deployment, values, and README | compile-server should be treated as a deployable self-hosted service, not a package-bound worker |
| worker directory sizes | distill `5` files / `59,165` bytes; quantize `5` / `52,835`; compile-server `8` / `32,624`; video-tokenize `2` / `23,955`; audio-tokenize `2` / `20,710`; constrained `3` / `20,699`; itkv `3` / `20,197`; vision-tokenize `2` / `19,672`; multimodal-redact-image `2` / `18,026`; tsac `3` / `17,319`; multimodal-redact-audio `2` / `16,616`; tokenizer-train `2` / `15,139`; media-redact `2` / `14,825`; runtime-build `2` / `14,379` | worker complexity is concentrated in distill/quantize/compile-server and performance/media workers; those need priority fixture and deploy drills |
| worker debt markers | distill `12`, constrained python `7`, itkv JS `5`, multimodal-redact-audio `3`, itkv python `3`, tsac JS `3`, tsac python `3`, constrained JS `3`, video-tokenize `2`, audio-tokenize `2`, plus isolated package/server/README hits | debt is concentrated in exactly the places being marketed as advanced worker features; each token must become an honest mode, a fixture-only label, or a production blocker |
| direct `distill` doctor | `npm --prefix workers/distill run doctor -- --json` exited `0` and returned JSON; local shell reports Node `24.14.0`, `python_ok: false`, `torch_ok: false`, `transformers_ok: false`, `ready_for_full_pipeline: false` | distill worker package is operable as a doctor but not full-pipeline-ready in this shell; copy must not imply full local training without Python/torch setup |
| direct `quantize` doctor | `npm --prefix workers/quantize run doctor -- --json` returned method-level JSON and exited nonzero because Python/toolchain dependencies are missing; every method from int4/int8 through GPTQ/AWQ/AQLM/QuIP/EXL/HQQ/QAT is `false` locally | nonzero readiness is useful, but release gates must treat JSON readiness as evidence and distinguish "doctor ran" from "quantization ready" |
| direct `constrained` doctor | `npm --prefix workers/constrained run doctor -- --json` exited `0`, returned JSON, and reported `ready: false` because Python and decoder packages are missing | constrained decoding has a real operator surface but needs decoder dependency and fixture proof before production claims |
| direct `runtime-build` doctor | `npm --prefix workers/runtime-build run doctor -- --json` exited `0`, returned per-target JSON for CUDA, ROCm, Vulkan, Metal, AVX2, and AVX512; all targets are false locally because `cmake`, `ninja`, CUDA/ROCm/Vulkan/macOS tools are missing | runtime-build has target-aware readiness but needs target labels, local-toolchain proof, CI/toolchain containers, and artifact output fixtures |
| compile-server fail-closed probe | `node workers/compile-server/server.mjs` refuses to start when `KOLM_SHARED_SECRET` is absent | fail-closed startup is real and should become part of production self-hosted proof |
| compile-server health probe | with `KOLM_SHARED_SECRET`, `KOLM_OFFLINE=1`, `HOST=127.0.0.1`, and a local port, `/v1/health` returned `{ ok: true, mode: self-hosted, version: 0.1.0-w264, offline: true, secret_configured: true }`; the artifact dir fell back to `.kolm-self-hosted-tmp` when the requested temp path was unavailable | health/readiness exists, but deploy proof must also verify artifact dir writability, configured path, network/no-egress behavior, and cleanup instead of accepting fallback silently |
| source/control-plane modules | filtered worker/queue/deploy/runtime files include `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/cloud-distill.js`, `src/cloud-sync.js`, `src/distill-approval-queue.js`, `src/cloud-compute-broker.js`, `src/active-learning-queue.js`, `src/runtime-placement.js`, `src/deployment-plans.js`, `src/load-queue.js`, `src/label-queue.js`, `src/jobs.js`, `src/output-retry.js`, and matching tests | node `4` must include both worker packages and central control-plane modules; otherwise queue/retry/runtime evidence is split |
| package scripts | root package exposes `local:surfaces`, `local:surfaces:deep`, `prod:surfaces`, `verify:platform`, `verify:cloud-broker`, `verify:depth`, `release:verify`, and `lint` over `workers` | release proof can consume worker contracts, but there is no visible dedicated `verify:workers` script in the script list, so node `4` needs its own verifier ticket |

Current local truth: worker operator entrypoints exist, but this shell is not configured for full Python/torch/quantization/runtime-build execution. That is not a failure if the product labels those modes correctly. It is a failure if public/account/CLI copy collapses doctor-ready, dependency-missing, fixture-smoked, deploy-dry-run, and production-smoked into the same word: "ready".

##### Node 4.6 External Baseline V3

| baseline | primary source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Edge execution is budgeted by CPU and other platform limits | Cloudflare Workers documents CPU time limits, termination behavior when limits are exceeded, and monitoring via logs/Logpush/DevTools: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/platform/limits/ | every edge/serverless worker needs declared CPU budget, wall time, body size, connection/subrequest limits, timeout error mapping, and monitoring fields |
| Queues are retry/ack/DLQ systems, not magic background threads | Cloudflare Queues documents default retry behavior, configurable `max_retries`, and dead-letter queue handling after retry exhaustion: https://developers.cloudflare.com/queues/configuration/batching-retries/ | every queued worker needs ack policy, retry count, backoff, idempotency key, poison-message handling, DLQ state, and operator replay command |
| Kubernetes jobs require deadlines and explicit failed states | Kubernetes documents `activeDeadlineSeconds`, `backoffLimit`, precedence of active deadlines over retries, and permanent failed job status requiring intervention: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/ | every container/Kubernetes worker needs deadline, retry/backoff, restart policy, cleanup, terminal state, and manual intervention runbook |
| Worker identity must be traceable to execution context | Temporal recommends worker identity that links to machine, process, execution environment, region, or log stream, because default identity can be weak in cloud environments: https://docs.temporal.io/workers | every worker run must emit identity fields strong enough for support to find the exact instance, logs, trace, deployment, and artifact |
| GenAI telemetry conventions are still development-stage and version-sensitive | OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions are marked Development and require careful opt-in/version handling for convention changes: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | worker spans/metrics/events must declare emitted semconv version and must not silently change names across releases |
| Container build attestations are part of deploy trust | Docker BuildKit generates provenance attestations by default, can emit SBOM attestations with flags, stores them as in-toto JSON attached to image indexes, and lets images be inspected for attestations: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/ | compile-server and any deployable worker image need image digest, SBOM, provenance, verification command, and release linkage |

##### Node 4.7 Worker Control Contract V3

Every worker package, compile-server run, queue consumer, scheduled/background job, and account/API-triggered async operation must emit this object or a lossless superset:


The V3 contract is stronger than V2 because it treats the worker as an operator-facing system. A worker is not "done" because it has a package, a script, or a passing unit test. It is done when an operator can discover it, run doctor, run a deterministic fixture, see missing dependencies, understand retry and isolation, deploy it, prove it with logs/traces/metrics, and roll it back.

##### Node 4.8 Worker State Ladder

| state | evidence | allowed copy |
|---|---|---|
| `source-present` | file exists | internal only |
| `package-shape` | package manifest or deploy manifest exists | "worker package exists" |
| `doctor-ready` | `doctor --json` returns stable schema, exit semantics, and secret-free missing groups | "operator-checkable" |
| `dependency-missing` | doctor runs and names missing dependencies | "requires setup" |
| `fixture-proven` | deterministic fixture produces expected output and negative cases | "fixture-proven" |
| `schema-contract` | malformed, oversized, unsupported, and forbidden-secret inputs are rejected | "schema-hardened" |
| `queue-contract` | idempotency, ack, retry, DLQ, cancellation, and replay behavior is tested | "queue-safe" |
| `resource-profiled` | CPU, memory, timeout, payload, artifact, connection, and chunking budgets measured | "resource-profiled" |
| `deploy-dry-run` | container/compose/Helm/edge/queue manifest validates without secrets | "deploy-plan-ready" |
| `attested-image` | image digest, SBOM, provenance, and verification command exist | "attested image" |
| `production-smoked` | tenant-scoped production/staging job runs, emits telemetry, returns output, cleans up, and can roll back | "production-proven" |

The product may show multiple states at once. For example, `quantize` can be `doctor-ready` and `dependency-missing` locally while a CI GPU lane is `fixture-proven` for int4 and a production lane is still external-gated. That nuance must be preserved in account UI, CLI, docs, and sales copy.

##### Node 4.9 Worker Families And Done State

| worker family | current anchors | V3 done state |
|---|---|---|
| distill worker | `workers/distill`, `src/cloud-distill.js`, `src/distill-approval-queue.js`, distill tests | collect/stub/full modes labeled; teacher bridge privacy; holdout/eval artifacts; idempotent job; fixture and real full-mode gate |
| quantize worker | `workers/quantize`, `src/quantization-oracle.js`, quantize tests | method-level readiness for int4, int8, GPTQ, AWQ, AQLM, QuIP, EXL, HQQ, QAT; calibration data; hardware label; quality-loss budget |
| compile server | `workers/compile-server/server.mjs`, Dockerfile, compose, Helm, README | package/deploy ledger, fail-closed startup, authenticated health, artifact dir proof, no-egress proof, byte-identical compile fixture, image SBOM/provenance, rollback |
| constrained decoding | `workers/constrained`, W809 tests | JSON/schema/grammar fixtures; invalid input; timeout; decoder dependency label; exact failure semantics |
| TSAC and ITKV | `workers/tsac`, `workers/itkv`, node `3` runtime optimization modules | deterministic fixture; memory and latency budget; approximation/fallback label; benchmark delta; runtime target label |
| runtime-build | `workers/runtime-build`, runtime placement and policy modules | target matrix for CUDA, ROCm, Vulkan, Metal, AVX2, AVX512; toolchain containers; artifact output; build cache; failure diagnostics |
| media tokenization | `workers/audio-tokenize`, `workers/video-tokenize`, `workers/vision-tokenize`, tokenizer-train | fixture corpus; max payload; chunking/streaming; privacy policy; output hash; unsupported codec behavior |
| media redaction | `workers/media-redact`, `workers/multimodal-redact-image`, `workers/multimodal-redact-audio` | per-class redaction fixture; no raw media leakage; precision/recall reports; OCR/audio dependency labels |
| queue/control modules | `src/load-queue.js`, `src/label-queue.js`, `src/active-learning-queue.js`, `src/jobs.js`, `src/output-retry.js` | queue contract with idempotency, lease, retry, DLQ, cancellation, replay, trace IDs, and support runbooks |
| cloud worker paths | `src/cloud-compute-broker.js`, `src/cloud-sync.js`, `src/deployment-plans.js`, cloud tests | env-gated readiness, missing secret groups, dry-run, real-run if configured, cleanup, timeout, cost, and trace correlation |

##### Node 4.10 Operator Drill Requirements

Every worker must have a drill that a backend or support engineer can run without reading source code. The drill packet is a first-class artifact, not a README suggestion.

1. Discover: list worker ID, package/deploy target, current proof state, owner node, and supported modes.
2. Doctor: run `doctor --json`; output stable schema, dependency groups, version, target, and `secret_values_included: false`.
3. Fixture: run a deterministic positive case and one negative case; record output hash, error code, and resource profile.
4. Queue: enqueue or simulate the job with an idempotency key; prove duplicate does not create duplicate artifact.
5. Retry: force one transient failure; prove retry/backoff and terminal failure state.
6. DLQ: force a poison message; prove DLQ or quarantine handling.
7. Cancel: cancel in-flight work; prove cleanup and observable terminal state.
8. Isolation: prove network/filesystem/secret policy for the worker mode.
9. Telemetry: record trace ID, log stream, worker identity, metrics, and semantic-convention version.
10. Deploy: render or validate deploy manifest; for containers, attach image digest, SBOM, and provenance.
11. Smoke: run staging or production tenant job when credentials exist.
12. Rollback: prove rollback command or manual recovery path.

If a worker cannot pass the drill locally because dependencies are intentionally absent, that is acceptable only if the drill returns a structured `dependency-missing` proof and the next action is clear.

##### Node 4.11 Atomic Work Tickets V3

| ticket | owner node | outcome |

##### Node 4 V3 Redline

Node `4` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every `workers/**` package, compile-server deploy artifact, and central worker/control-plane module is mapped in `docs/worker-control-ledger.json`;
2. every package-bound worker and compile-server has a stable operator drill with discover, doctor, fixture, queue/retry when applicable, isolation, telemetry, deploy, smoke, and rollback steps;
3. doctor output is JSON-schema-stable and distinguishes command crash, dependency-missing, fixture-ready, deploy-ready, and production-smoked states;
4. every worker has positive and negative fixture smokes with output hashes and explicit resource profiles;
5. queue behavior proves idempotency, ack, retry, backoff, DLQ/quarantine, cancellation, replay, poison-message handling, and cleanup;
6. compile-server proves fail-closed secret behavior, authenticated health, configured artifact directory, no-egress mode, byte-identical compile fixture, container/compose/Helm render, and rollback;
7. worker runtime isolation names network, filesystem, secret, tenant, payload, and redaction policies;
8. worker identity and telemetry can locate the exact process/container/task, environment, region, version, logs, trace, and metrics;
9. containerized workers have image digest, SBOM, provenance, and verification commands;
10. all worker debt markers are classified as fixture-only, honest local/mock mode, or production blocker;
11. public copy, account UI, API, CLI, TUI, and docs show the same worker proof state and next action;
12. at least one tenant-scoped production or staging smoke proves each deployed worker class, or that worker remains visibly local/dry-run/dependency-missing only.

#### Node 5 SDK, Installer, Package Channel, And Release Proof Addendum V2

This pass maintains master spec tree node `5`: SDK and package plane. Node `5` owns SDKs, package metadata, installers, package manager channels, release manifests, compatibility examples, install commands, registry URLs, signed artifacts, package SBOMs, provenance, and package-channel proof. Node `17` owns developer ecosystem integrations, node `22` owns broader supply-chain policy, node `27` owns external standardization, node `41` owns interoperability, and node `50` owns the code-completion/build-finishing product. Node `5` is the release-channel truth gate for anything that a customer can install.

The key correction for node `5` is that "source exists" is not the same as "SDK shipped." Local package manifests can prove structural readiness; registry URLs, signed artifacts, provenance, SBOMs, install smoke, and compatibility examples prove package release.

##### Node 5.0 Current SDK And Package Evidence

Local scan signal for `sdk/**`, `packages/**`, and package-release tooling:

| evidence | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/package-release-readiness.js` and `scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs` | define `kolm-package-release-readiness-1`, 16 release targets, local checks, installer smoke, manifest template, and release manifest validation | this is the canonical local package readiness source, but it cannot prove external registry publication without `reports/package-release-manifest.json` |
| `node scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs --summary --json` | reports `ok=true`, `targets=16`, `structural_ok=16`, `pending=16`, `blocked=0`, `publish_ready=false` | every package target is structurally present but package-channel pending; public copy must not imply all SDKs/installers are published |
| release targets | targets include `sdk-ts`, `sdk-rn`, `attestation-npm`, `langchain-npm`, `llamaindex-npm`, `sdk-python`, `langchain-python`, `llamaindex-python`, `runtime-rs`, `sdk-swift`, `sdk-kotlin`, `homebrew`, `apt`, `winget`, `install-scripts`, and `browser-extension` | completion requires target-by-target publish evidence, not a single generic SDK claim |
| pending blockers | most targets need signed release artifact or registry URL; Homebrew has release archive SHA placeholder; winget has installer SHA placeholder | package release remains externally gated until artifacts, hashes, registry URLs, SBOMs, and provenance exist |
| duplicate SDK roots | legacy/current roots coexist: `sdk/node`, `sdk/python`, `sdk/rust`, `sdk/mcp`, `sdk/vscode` plus newer `packages/sdk-ts`, `packages/sdk-python`, `packages/runtime-rs`, `packages/sdk-rn`, `packages/sdk-swift`, `packages/sdk-kotlin`, and adapters | node `5` must declare canonical roots, compatibility roots, or retirement plan per language; duplicate versions must not drift |
| version drift | root package is `0.2.6`; `packages/*` targets mostly use `0.2.6`; `sdk/node`, `sdk/python`, and `sdk/rust` show `0.2.0` | release manifest must detect and justify version divergence or align versions before a "single SDK release" claim |
| package naming | npm package names include `kolm`, `@kolm/kolm-sdk`, `@kolm/langchain`, `@kolm/llamaindex`, `kolm-rn`, `kolm-attestation`; Python packages include `kolm`, `kolm-langchain`, `kolm-llamaindex`; Rust crates include `kolm` and `kolm-runtime` | customer docs must avoid ambiguous install commands and show canonical package names per channel |
| install channels | Homebrew, apt, winget, direct install scripts, browser extension manifests, VS Code packages, SDK packages, and native runtime crates are present | each channel needs a channel-specific smoke, metadata audit, upgrade/uninstall behavior, and rollback path |
| debt/brand scan | installer and SDK files still contain package-channel, publish, placeholder, and legacy-brand markers in multiple files | node `5` must coordinate with node `0` brand cleanup and node `22` supply-chain gates before final release |

Current package/directory footprint by package root:

| package area | files | bytes |
|---|---:|---:|
| `packages/runtime-rs` | 18 | 103,171 |
| `sdk/python` | 17 | 164,754 |
| `packages/attestation` | 17 | 48,935 |
| `packages/sdk-rn` | 25 | 32,245 |
| `sdk/c` | 6 | 87,147 |
| `sdk/rust` | 6 | 75,905 |
| `sdk/node` | 7 | 45,941 |
| `sdk/vscode` | 10 | 52,444 |
| `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` | 9 | 40,259 |
| `packages/sdk-python` | 6 | 32,440 |
| `packages/sdk-ts` | 8 | 22,025 |
| `packages/sdk-swift` | 4 | 13,839 |
| `packages/sdk-kotlin` | 5 | 11,718 |
| `packages/langchain-kolm` | 8 | 13,813 |
| `packages/llamaindex-kolm` | 4 | 7,955 |
| `packages/homebrew`, `packages/apt`, `packages/winget` | 8 | 8,662 |

The largest risk is not lack of code. It is package identity drift: duplicate roots, duplicate package names, different versions, local-source-only packages beside release-target packages, and installer manifests that can look real while still missing release archive hashes.

##### Node 5.1 External Package And Installer Baseline V2

| baseline | source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| npm trusted publishing should replace long-lived tokens | npm trusted publishing uses OIDC from supported CI systems, requires newer Node/npm versions, can generate provenance automatically, and recommends restricting token access: https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers/ | npm targets need trusted publisher configuration, `id-token: write`, build/test/pack proof, provenance, and token fallback disabled or justified |
| PyPI trusted publishing avoids long-lived release tokens | PyPI trusted publishing exchanges CI OIDC tokens for short-lived project-scoped API tokens valid for 15 minutes: https://docs.pypi.org/trusted-publishers/ | Python packages need trusted publisher records, wheel/sdist build proof, upload artifact hashes, and no stored PyPI token dependency |
| crates.io packaging requires metadata and dry-run package checks | Cargo publishing verifies, compresses, extracts, compiles, and uploads `.crate`; docs recommend `cargo publish --dry-run`/`cargo package`, metadata, changelog, git tag, and size review: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/publishing.html | Rust packages need metadata completeness, `cargo package --list`, size audit, dry-run, changelog/tag, and secret leak review |
| GitHub artifact attestations must be generated and verified | GitHub artifact attestations create signed provenance claims tied to workflow, repo, commit, trigger, and can include SBOM; verification is required for security value: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/security/artifact-attestations | each binary/package/installer release must include attestation generation plus consumer-facing verification commands |
| winget manifests require installer URLs and SHA256 hashes | Microsoft winget manifests are YAML, require installer URL and installer SHA256, use multi-file manifests, and require silent install support for installers: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/package-manager/package/manifest | winget target cannot be release-ready with placeholder hashes or unverified silent install behavior |
| Homebrew formulae require formula audits and tests | Homebrew formulae are Ruby package definitions, can be created from URLs, installed with `brew install`, and should include tests and audits: https://docs.brew.sh/Formula-Cookbook | Homebrew target needs real release URL, SHA256, `brew audit --strict`, install-from-source smoke, and formula test |
| Swift Package manifests declare tools version, platforms, products, dependencies, and targets | Swift Package Manager requires `// swift-tools-version`, package metadata, products, targets, and platform declarations: https://docs.swift.org/package-manager/PackageDescription/PackageDescription.html | Swift target needs platform matrix, product API smoke, tag-based resolution, and sample app integration |
| Android library publication requires Gradle/Maven channel proof | Android library upload flows depend on Gradle publishing metadata and repository upload configuration: https://developer.android.com/build/publish-library/upload-library | Kotlin/Android target needs `publishToMavenLocal`, Maven Central or GitHub Packages plan, AAR/POM metadata, and sample Android app smoke |

##### Node 5.2 SDK Release Contract V2

Every SDK package, adapter, installer, extension, or direct-download artifact should emit this contract or a lossless superset:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.sdk_package_release_contract.v2",
  "tree_node": 5,
  "package": {
    "id": "sdk-ts",
    "name": "kolm",
    "channel": "npm|pypi|crates|swiftpm|maven|homebrew|apt|winget|direct-download|browser-extension|vscode|local-source",
    "root": "packages/sdk-ts",
    "version": "0.2.6",
    "canonical": true,
    "legacy_roots": [],
    "replacement": null
  },
  "metadata": {
    "license": null,
    "description": null,
    "repository": null,
    "homepage": null,
    "readme": null,
    "exports": [],
    "types": null,
    "platforms": [],
    "architectures": []
  },
  "compatibility": {
    "api_version": null,
    "artifact_format": null,
    "openapi_hash": null,
    "runtime_targets": [],
    "examples": [],
    "contract_tests": []
  },
  "build": {
    "commands": [],
    "toolchain": [],
    "lockfiles": [],
    "dry_run": null,
    "size_bytes": null
  },
  "release": {
    "registry_url": null,
    "artifact_url": null,
    "artifact_sha256": null,
    "sbom_sha256": null,
    "provenance_sha256": null,
    "signature_bundle_sha256": null,
    "published_at": null,
    "release_notes_url": null
  },
  "install": {
    "commands": [],
    "smoke_commands": [],
    "uninstall_commands": [],
    "upgrade_behavior": null
  },
  "security": {
    "trusted_publishing": null,
    "tokenless_release": null,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "least_privilege_permissions": null
  },
  "proof": {
    "metadata_audit": null,
    "build_check": null,
    "package_dry_run": null,
    "install_smoke": null,
    "registry_lookup": null,
    "attestation_verify": null,
    "example_run": null
  },
  "limits": {
    "release_status": "source-only|structural-ok|package-channel-pending|published|deprecated|retired",
    "not_proven": [],
    "external_gates": []
  }
}
```

The release contract must be generated from package metadata and release artifacts. It must not be edited manually after publication except to attach external proof URLs.

##### Node 5.3 Package Proof Classes

| proof class | what it proves | what it does not prove |
|---|---|---|
| `source-only` | package source exists in the repo | installability or public availability |
| `manifest-valid` | package metadata parses and required local files exist | build/test/pass or package content |
| `build-check` | package builds or compiles locally | publishability or install after release |
| `package-dry-run` | pack/build artifact can be created without publishing | registry availability or signed release |
| `install-smoke` | package can install into a clean local fixture and run a minimal example | public registry availability |
| `compatibility-smoke` | SDK talks to current API/artifact format and runs examples | every API route or runtime target |
| `attested-artifact` | artifact has SBOM/provenance/signature and verification command | registry publication |
| `registry-published` | package exists at a public/private registry URL for the release version | install command correctness across platforms |
| `channel-production-ready` | registry artifact, install smoke, docs, examples, SBOM, provenance, release notes, and rollback/deprecation behavior are proven | future compatibility or external marketplace approval |

Relax rule: a page may say "SDK source available" for `source-only`, "package preview" for `package-dry-run`, and "install from npm/PyPI/crates/etc." only after `registry-published` plus install smoke. "One-line install" requires all installer-channel targets to meet the channel-production-ready bar or to be visibly scoped by platform.
