```json
}
```

This state machine should be emitted into `docs/account-console-ledger.json` and joined with node `1` product graph, node `2` API route inventory, node `10` auth/security policy, node `16` plan/entitlement truth, node `29` production smoke, and node `45` screenshot evidence.

##### Node 7.8 Account Workflow Coverage Matrix V3

| workflow | required account pages | completion behavior |
|---|---|---|
| command overview | `overview.html` | show all three product pillars, twelve journeys, readiness counts, closeout gates, next actions, and API/CLI/docs equivalents |
| route and capture | `connectors.html`, `captured.html`, `captures.html`, `captures/analytics.html`, `captures/review.html` | show provider setup, live traffic, capture quality, redaction state, duplicates, poisoning warnings, review queue, promote-to-dataset, and rollback |
| privacy and audit | `privacy-events.html`, `audit-log.html`, `approvals.html`, `security/2fa.html` | show privacy events, audit export, approvals, 2FA/session state, policy violations, and certification scope caveats |
| datasets and labeling | `datasets.html`, `labeling.html`, `synthetic.html`, `repeated-workflows.html` | show data source, rights, lineage, labels, holdout, synthetic scope, drift, and whether examples are fixture/live |
| evals and bakeoffs | `bakeoff.html`, `bakeoffs.html`, `multimodal-bakeoff.html`, `failure-modes.html`, `confidence.html` | show K-score scope, benchmark status, model comparison, failure classes, confidence/routing thresholds, and calibration caveats |
| distill and compile | `distill-runs.html`, `builds.html`, `artifacts.html`, `pipelines.html` | show job lifecycle, worker state, artifact hash/signature, compile receipt, progress, failure reason, retry, and deploy target |
| runtime, storage, devices | `routing.html`, `storage.html`, `devices.html`, `sla.html` | show runtime target, storage readiness, device fleet health, latency/SLA, version, heartbeat, capacity, and rollback |
| reliability and monitoring | `drift.html`, `drift-alert.html`, `staleness.html`, `continuous-monitoring.html`, `seasonal.html` | show live alert state, severity, root cause, recommended capture, owner, escalation, and closed-loop learning |
| commercial and admin | `billing.html`, `chargeback.html`, `api-keys.html`, `settings.html`, `namespaces/new.html` | show plan, limits, spend, invoices, key scopes, namespace policy, enterprise sales-review state, and audit events |
| federated and governance | `federated/consortium.html`, `approvals.html`, `audit-log.html` | show consortium membership, partner/external gates, approvals, evidence exports, and no overclaiming external adoption |

The account console should not repeat marketing copy. It should present operational truth: state, action, evidence, caveat, and next command.

##### Node 7.9 Authenticated UX Acceptance Gates V3

| gate | pass condition |
|---|---|
| `all-pages-ledgered` | all 48 account pages represented in `docs/account-console-ledger.json` with workflow, owner nodes, API dependencies, state coverage, role coverage, and proof class |
| `kfetch-or-exception` | all dynamic account pages use `kfetch` or declare a reviewed direct-fetch exception with equivalent envelope/auth/error behavior |
| `no-static-shell-ambiguity` | every no-fetch page is marked route alias, static report, fixture/demo, source template, or incomplete workflow |
| `last-verified-visible` | every live or runnable account page shows freshness/proof state or links to the relevant evidence packet |
| `role-state-covered` | owner, admin, billing admin, security admin, developer, reviewer, auditor, and read-only roles have visible permission states |
| `plan-state-covered` | free, pro, team, enterprise, trial, suspended, delinquent, and sales-review states are visible where relevant |
| `auth-state-covered` | signed-out, expired, invalid key, insufficient scope, reauth required, rotated key, and logout states are covered |
| `mutation-safe` | every mutating action has validation, disabled/loading, idempotency, confirmation if destructive, audit event, and recovery |
| `table-operable` | all 41 table pages support accessible headers, empty state, loading state, filters/sort where useful, mobile overflow, and row action labels |
| `button-state-complete` | all 41 button pages have hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, success, destructive, and retry state definitions |
| `account-nav-consistent` | nav highlight, underline, breadcrumb, active section, mobile menu, and focus state are consistent across all pages |
| `secret-safe` | no account page prints, stores, screenshots, or logs API key values; provided key candidates are used only as redacted smoke inputs |
| `prod-auth-smoked` | production smoke verifies auth redirect, overview, high-risk pages, logout, and no secret leakage with seeded tenants |

These gates should feed node `9`, node `29`, node `45`, and node `47`. A page can pass visual screenshots and still fail V3 if it cannot prove auth state, permission state, or recovery state.

##### Node 7.10 Account UX Pattern Contract V3

| pattern | final product behavior |
|---|---|
| overview cards | show status, last update, blocker, primary action, and proof link; no paragraph-heavy marketing blocks |
| tables | use compact but readable rows, sticky or repeated column context where needed, explicit empty/error/loading states, and row actions with labels |
| filters | expose useful default filters, reset state, deep links, and no hidden hover-only menus |
| command blocks | show CLI/API equivalent with copy affordance and secret placeholders, never real key values |
| alerts | classify informational, warning, blocked, destructive, external-gate, and success states with text plus visual treatment |
| destructive actions | require confirmation, explain blast radius, disable during submission, create audit event, and show recovery path |
| readiness chips | show shipped, implemented, package-pending, benchmark-pending, certification-pending, partner-pending, or external; never imply finality silently |
| charts | include axis labels, units, empty state, color-plus-text encoding, and source timestamp |
| forms | labels always visible, validation near field, server errors near action, keyboard submit/cancel, and no placeholder-only labels |
| popovers and menus | click/tap accessible, keyboard navigable, dismissible, focus managed, and visually consistent with node `45` |

The account aesthetic should be quieter than the marketing site: work-focused, dense, status-first, restrained, and fast to scan. The account console should not use oversized hero copy, decorative cards, or marketing-style composition.

##### Node 7 V3 Relax Redline

Node `7` V3 is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every account page is represented in `docs/account-console-ledger.json`;
2. every dynamic account page uses `kfetch` or carries a reviewed direct-fetch exception with equivalent behavior;
3. every no-fetch account page is classified so static shells cannot masquerade as finished workflows;
4. every live/runnable account page displays last-verified or proof state;
5. every page maps to product graph journeys, account links, API routes, CLI/API/docs equivalents, and owner nodes where relevant;
6. role, plan, auth, permission, empty, loading, stale, error, offline, success, and rollback states are explicitly represented;
7. high-risk pages for API keys, billing, audit, approvals, storage, artifacts, builds, namespaces, and 2FA pass security and production-smoke gates;
8. table, button, form, popover, nav, breadcrumb, and command-copy patterns are consistent and accessible across all pages;
9. no account page leaks secret values, stores production secrets without an approved threat model, or screenshots provided key values;
10. authenticated production smoke proves overview, storage, billing, API keys, builds, artifacts, devices, audit log, 2FA/security, logout, and no-secret logging before finality.

#### Node 8 Documentation, Education, Examples, And Reference Freshness Addendum V2

This pass maintains master spec tree node `8`: documentation and education. Node `8` owns quickstarts, tutorials, how-to guides, cookbook recipes, CLI docs, API reference, SDK docs, spec docs, migration guides, install docs, localized docs, troubleshooting, example correctness, docs IA, docs metadata, and docs production proof. Node `1` owns product truth, node `2` owns API route behavior, node `5` owns package release truth, node `6` owns public page quality, node `7` owns account handoffs, node `12` owns docs UI components, node `17` owns ecosystem integrations, node `32` owns search/SEO, and node `46` owns traceability. Node `8` is the learning system that makes every product surface usable without a sales call or hidden maintainer knowledge.

The key correction for node `8` is that docs are not complete when they are numerous. Docs are complete when a new user can finish the intended task, every command is runnable or honestly scoped, every reference is generated from the current product contract, every package name matches release truth, and every non-final claim links to the closeout ledger.

##### Node 8.0 Current Docs Evidence V2

Current local scan of `docs/**` and `public/docs/**`:

| evidence | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Markdown docs under `docs/**` | 101 files, 7,547,583 bytes | source docs are substantial enough to need ownership, freshness, and archive policy |
| public docs HTML | 221 pages, 3,489,173 bytes | docs IA must be generated and verified, not manually curated page-by-page |
| public docs root pages | 81 pages, 1,900,887 bytes | reference, conceptual, install, and product docs are mixed at the root and need an IA map |
| CLI reference HTML pages | 94 pages, 986,287 bytes | CLI coverage is broad, but every page must be generated from a current verb source and smoke-tested against `kolm <verb> --help` or equivalent |
| CLI markdown source pages | 55 files, 95,455 bytes | generated CLI HTML does not yet cover every CLI HTML page with a markdown source; source-of-truth gaps need classification |
| quickstart HTML pages | 6 pages, 59,029 bytes | quickstarts exist, but they must be the fastest path for the three product surfaces, not only SDK install pages |
| SDK reference pages | 5 nested SDK pages plus `public/docs/sdk.html` | SDK docs must consume node `5` package-release truth so source-only packages are not documented as published |
| docs with code blocks | 220 of 221 public docs HTML pages | examples are everywhere; command/example checking must be release-critical |
| duplicate H1 in docs | 0 | heading invariant is good and should become a docs gate |
| missing H1 in docs | 0 | all docs pages have a main heading |
| docs titles over 65 characters | 37 | title length and scanability need cleanup for search and tabs |
| docs descriptions over 160 characters | 57 | many docs snippets are too long and should be rewritten around user task and proof |
| missing canonical links | 0 | canonical coverage is good |
| missing OpenGraph tags | 7 | social preview coverage is incomplete |
| missing JSON-LD schema | 88 | many docs pages lack machine-readable TechArticle/HowTo/FAQ/BreadcrumbList/etc. schema |
| docs mojibake markers | 48 public docs pages | generated title/metadata/content normalization remains incomplete |
| docs legacy brand/package tokens | `@kolmogorov` 23, `kolmogorov-stack` 491, `kolmogorov` 545 across docs/public docs | node `8` must coordinate with node `0` and node `5` before docs finality |
| docs plan tokens | `Business` 330, `Starter` 51, `$1,499` 8 across docs/public docs | docs must consume pricing truth from node `16`, not historical copy |
| docs debt markers | `TODO` 11, `FIXME` 4, `placeholder` 96, `coming soon` 2, `mock` 78, `stub` 291 | each marker must be classed as test fixture, honest reference state, or unfinished docs/product gap |
| API reference | `public/docs/api.html` says 582 wired routes, 455 reference-ready, 127 source-indexed, 163 groups, generated 2026-05-20 | API docs are broad but still distinguish source-indexed routes; source-indexed is not enough for complete API reference claims |
| OpenRouter capture docs | `public/docs/cli/capture.html` now documents OpenRouter, `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL`, and `Authorization: Bearer ks_*` | this previous blocker appears fixed in generated CLI HTML; keep it locked by docs honesty tests |
| quickstart package truth | Node quickstart still advertises `@kolmogorov/sdk`; MCP quickstart still advertises `@kolmogorov/mcp`; Python quickstart points to git subdirectory under `kolmogorov-stack` | quickstarts currently contradict the desired Kolm naming/package-release truth and cannot be final |

Representative current documentation defects:

| path | current signal | node 8 redline |
|---|---|---|
| `public/docs/quickstart/node.html` | says the SDK package is published as `@kolmogorov/sdk`, with npm/pnpm/yarn commands and imports from `@kolmogorov/sdk` | replace with actual node `5` package truth: published current package, source-only install, or compatibility alias with proof |
| `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html` | says the MCP server is published as `@kolmogorov/mcp` and uses `npx @kolmogorov/mcp` | align with actual MCP package/channel or mark as pending/source-only |
| `public/docs/quickstart/python.html` | installs from `github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` subdirectory | align with package-release truth and brand migration policy |
| `public/docs/api.html` | 127 routes are source-indexed and many source-indexed routes are high-risk surfaces such as ab-tests, approvals, cloud, compliance, capture, drift, and build strategy | every source-indexed route needs auth, request/response, errors, claim scope, owner, and proof or it remains preview-scoped |
| `public/docs/sdk.html` | JS SDK reference mentions Python, Rust, and Go while node `5` release state shows package-channel pending across many SDKs | docs must distinguish source SDKs, package previews, and published installable SDKs |
| `public/docs/cli/*.html` | 94 generated CLI pages exist, but only 55 markdown sources were found | either every generated page must trace to source docs, or generated-only pages must cite their CLI help/source and generated status |
| `docs/research/*` | large research docs exist, including a 3.9 MB distillation lab and the active 100-percent blueprint | research docs need archive/current status, not all research should be treated as product docs |

Node `8` must also own docs reading-order. A developer should not have to infer whether to start at `/quickstart`, `/docs/quickstart`, `/docs/quickstart/node`, `/api`, `/docs/api`, `/sdks`, `/docs/sdk`, `/compile`, or `/distill`. The docs must provide one route per user job and clear next-step links.

##### Node 8.1 External Documentation Baseline V2

| baseline | source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Documentation has four modes: tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation | Diataxis framework: https://diataxis.fr/ | classify every docs page into one primary mode and stop mixing tutorial prose, reference exhaustiveness, and marketing claims in the same page |
| Tutorials are learning-oriented, how-to guides solve real tasks, reference is complete and accurate, explanation builds understanding | Diataxis: https://diataxis.fr/ | quickstarts must guide first success; API/CLI/SDK references must be exhaustive and exact; architecture/spec pages must explain concepts without pretending to be runnable |
| API descriptions should be grounded in a machine-readable contract | OpenAPI Specification 3.2.0: https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.2.0.html | API docs, OpenAPI, SDK docs, examples, and route manifests must agree on paths, operations, schemas, security, examples, errors, tags, and deprecations |
| Developer docs need page-specific search metadata and title clarity | Google Search Central title/snippet docs: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link and https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/snippet | docs titles/descriptions must be concise, unique, and task-specific; generated mojibake or boilerplate metadata fails finality |
| Docs pages are still UI and must pass accessibility and interaction rules | WCAG 2.2: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/ and WAI ARIA APG: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ | docs navigation, code tabs, copy buttons, accordions, search, sidebars, tables, and anchors must work by keyboard and screen reader |
| Enterprise SaaS docs should prioritize task completion, scanability, and reliable state | `ui-ux-pro-max` local design guidance | docs should use dense but readable tables, exact commands, status labels, expected output, warnings, and next steps rather than vague prose |

##### Node 8.2 Docs Page Contract V2

Every docs page should satisfy this contract:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.docs_page_contract.v2",
  "tree_node": 8,
  "page": {
    "path": "/docs/quickstart/node",
    "source_path": "public/docs/quickstart/node.html",
    "source_of_truth": "manual-md|generated-cli|generated-api|generated-sdk|spec-source|research-source",
    "mode": "tutorial|how-to|reference|explanation|troubleshooting|migration|release-note|research",
    "audience": "developer|ml-engineer|platform-engineer|security|procurement|operator|auditor",
    "owner_nodes": [0, 5, 8, 17, 32, 46],
    "freshness_date": null
  },
  "task": {
    "user_question": null,
    "time_to_first_value_minutes": null,
    "prerequisites": [],
    "expected_outputs": [],
    "next_steps": []
  },
  "truth_sources": {
    "product_graph": null,
    "api_routes": null,
    "openapi": null,
    "cli_help": null,
    "sdk_package_release": null,
    "readiness_closeout": null,
    "pricing_truth": null,
    "claim_scope": null
  },
  "examples": {
    "commands": [],
    "code_blocks": [],
    "env_vars": [],
    "api_keys_redacted": true,
    "expected_output_present": true,
    "runnable_in_ci": null,
    "requires_live_secret": false
  },
  "reference": {
    "auth": null,
    "request_schema": null,
    "response_schema": null,
    "errors": [],
    "rate_limits": null,
    "idempotency": null,
    "pagination": null,
    "webhooks_or_events": null,
    "deprecation_state": null
  },
  "metadata": {
    "h1_count": 1,
    "title": null,
    "description": null,
    "canonical": true,
    "og": true,
    "json_ld_types": [],
    "breadcrumb": true,
    "sitemap_entry": true
  },
  "ux": {
    "left_nav": true,
    "breadcrumbs": true,
    "copy_buttons": true,
    "code_language_labels": true,
    "keyboard_accessible": true,
    "focus_visible": true,
    "mobile_readable": true,
    "anchor_links": true,
    "search_indexed": null
  },
  "verification": {
    "source_lint": null,
    "example_lint": null,
    "command_smoke": null,
    "api_contract_check": null,
    "link_check": null,
    "metadata_check": null,
    "schema_check": null,
    "screenshot_check": null,
    "production_smoke": null
  },
  "limits": {
    "not_proven": [],
    "legacy_terms": [],
    "external_gates": []
  }
}
```

Node `8` must use this contract to prevent docs drift. If package-release state, route inventory, product graph, billing tiers, or closeout status changes, affected docs must become stale until regenerated or manually recertified.

##### Node 8.3 Docs Proof Classes

| proof class | what it proves | what it does not prove |
|---|---|---|
| `page-present` | docs file exists and renders | task success or correctness |
| `metadata-valid` | title, description, canonical, OG, schema, sitemap, and breadcrumbs are valid | example correctness |
| `truth-linked` | page references product graph, package truth, route inventory, pricing, or closeout state where relevant | commands actually run |
| `example-runnable` | commands/code blocks execute against local fixtures or documented dry-run mode and expected output matches | production service availability |
| `reference-complete` | reference pages include auth, request, response, errors, rate limits, pagination/idempotency, examples, and deprecation state | tutorial usability |
| `journey-complete` | a user can complete a tutorial/how-to from prerequisites to expected output and next step | every platform or all edge cases |
| `accessible-docs` | docs nav, code blocks, tabs, copy buttons, headings, tables, and anchors work for keyboard/screen readers | content accuracy |
| `localized-current` | translated docs match canonical source version and carry freshness status | perfect translation quality |
| `production-docs-smoked` | deployed docs render, links work, search metadata is correct, copy buttons work, examples are current, and screenshots pass | future API/package changes |

Relax rule: a quickstart cannot be final unless it is `journey-complete` and `example-runnable`. A reference cannot be final unless it is `reference-complete` and generated or verified from the canonical contract. A docs page that mentions package availability, benchmark superiority, certification, or ecosystem adoption must be `truth-linked` to the relevant closeout state.

##### Node 8 V2 Relax Redline

Node `8` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. every docs Markdown file and every public docs HTML file is represented in `docs/docs-page-ledger.json`;
2. every docs page has one primary Diataxis mode, audience, owner nodes, source-of-truth, freshness date, and proof class;
3. quickstarts prove first value for route/capture, distill/compile, and run/deploy, with expected outputs and no secret leakage;
4. all package/install commands reflect node `5` release status and no docs page advertises unproven `@kolmogorov/*`, `kolmogorov-stack`, or legacy package names as current;
5. every CLI docs page maps to markdown source, CLI help/source metadata, or a declared generated status, and examples are smoke-tested or truthfully scoped;
6. API reference, API routes JSON, OpenAPI, route source comments, product surfaces, and SDK docs agree after generation;
7. the 127 source-indexed API routes either become reference-complete or remain visibly preview/source-indexed with owner and closeout path;
8. every API reference entry documents auth, request, response, error envelope, examples, rate limits, idempotency/pagination where relevant, deprecation, and proof state;
9. SDK docs distinguish source-only, package-channel pending, preview, published, deprecated, and compatibility aliases;
10. docs metadata has no mojibake, no duplicate H1s, no missing canonical, no missing required OG/schema, and concise unique titles/descriptions;
11. docs debt terms such as TODO, FIXME, placeholder, coming soon, mock, and stub are classified as fixture, preview, reference status, or unresolved defect;
12. docs nav, breadcrumbs, search, copy buttons, code tabs, code blocks, tables, anchors, and sidebars are accessible and responsive;
13. localized docs carry source-version freshness and cannot silently diverge from canonical English;
14. docs screenshots cover key docs classes in light/dark and desktop/mobile, including API/CLI pages with large tables/code blocks;
15. production docs smoke proves deployed docs freshness, redirects, links, metadata, schema, copy buttons, example commands, API ref route count, and no stale legacy/public claims.

#### Node 8 V3 Runnable Docs, SDK Truth, And First-Value Learning Paths

This pass maintains the master spec tree by extending existing node `8` instead of creating a duplicate docs node. Node `8` now has a V3 obligation: every documentation surface must be runnable, status-scoped, product-truth-linked, and attached to a first-value path. Node `1` continues to own product-feature truth, node `5` owns package and SDK release truth, node `12` owns reusable docs UI components, node `17` owns ecosystem integration truth, node `32` owns search and metadata, node `43` owns path and artifact hygiene, and node `46` owns the master spec tree mechanics. Node `8` owns the learning path that turns those facts into usable work.

The correction in V3 is that "lots of docs" is not the same as "usable docs." A state-of-the-art AI infrastructure company needs docs that behave like product UI: exact starting point, exact audience, exact task, exact command, expected output, visible prerequisites, package truth, live-secret warnings, copy affordances, last-verified evidence, and next action. Any page that cannot tell a user what to do next is not finished, even if it renders and has valid links.

##### Node 8.5 Current Runnable-Docs Evidence V3

Current local docs and SDK surface evidence:

| evidence | current signal | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| public docs HTML pages | 221 | large enough to require generated page classification and proof ownership |
| public docs markdown pages | 58 | markdown source coverage is not the same as generated HTML coverage |
| `public/docs/cli` files | 149 | CLI docs need source/help parity and command smoke ownership |
| public docs root files | 83 | root docs need a canonical IA so users do not have to guess where to start |
| `public/docs/integrations` files | 10 | integration docs must inherit partner/package/readiness scope from nodes `5`, `17`, and `18` |
| `public/docs/quickstart` files | 6 | quickstarts must prove first value for route/capture, compile/distill, and run/deploy |
| `public/docs/i18n` files | 6 | localized docs need freshness state and canonical-source pointers |
| `public/docs/sdk` files | 5 | SDK docs must show source-only, preview, published, deprecated, or compatibility state |
| `public/docs/cookbook` files | 4 | recipes need runnable inputs, commands, outputs, cost, quality, and rollback notes |
| `public/docs/connect` files | 4 | connect docs need provider, auth, security, and closeout scope labels |
| `public/docs/install` files | 3 | install docs need node `5` release truth and clean-host smoke evidence |
| `public/docs/multimodal` files | 3 | multimodal docs need modality support matrix and model/runtime constraints |
| `public/docs/runtime` files | 2 | runtime docs must link artifact compatibility, package state, and device proof |
| `public/docs/observability` files | 2 | telemetry docs must link route/API, account UI, and exported signal names |
| docs pages with code blocks | 220 of 221 | example checking must be a release gate, not optional review |
| pages with code but no copy affordance | 55 | docs UI is incomplete for developer productivity |
| pages missing last-verified signal | 195 | freshness is missing on most generated docs pages |
| multi-H1 docs pages | 0 | heading structure is currently healthy and should become a locked invariant |
| SDK scan dominated by build output | `sdk/rust/target` has 4,900 files and about 1.59 GB | docs and package truth must separate source, generated build artifacts, package output, and cache directories |

This evidence creates a hard rule for node `8`: every docs page needs a page record. The record must name the page mode, audience, owner node, source-of-truth, product journey, package state, command state, expected output state, last-verified state, and screenshot state. Rendering without this record is not enough.

##### Node 8.6 Imported Documentation And Developer-Experience Baseline V3

The V3 docs model uses three external baselines and one local product-design baseline:

| baseline | source | Kolm interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Documentation should be organized by user need: tutorial, how-to, reference, and explanation | Diataxis: https://diataxis.fr/ | every docs page gets one primary mode and cannot mix onboarding, exhaustive reference, marketing copy, and conceptual explanation without explicit sectioning |
| API docs should be derived from a language-agnostic machine-readable interface | OpenAPI Specification 3.2.0: https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.2.0.html | route docs, OpenAPI, SDK examples, API route JSON, account API calls, and CLI examples must agree on schemas, auth, examples, tags, and deprecation state |
| Page titles and snippets should be clear, page-specific, and useful in search | Google Search Central title-link guidance: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link | docs titles and descriptions cannot carry mojibake, stale brand names, redundant boilerplate, or vague claims |
| Docs are product UI | local `ui-ux-pro-max` guidance | docs need scanable hierarchy, copy buttons, status labels, code tabs, keyboard access, mobile table behavior, and no hidden hover-only meaning |

These baselines are not academic decoration. They define release behavior: tutorials prove first success, how-to guides prove a real task, references prove exactness, explanations prove understanding, API docs prove contract parity, and docs UI proves that the user can execute without fighting the interface.

##### Node 8.7 First-Value Path Contract V3

Add a generated ledger named `docs/docs-first-value-paths.json`. Each record must follow this shape:

```json
{
  "path_id": "developer-route-first-call",
  "audience": "developer",
  "starting_page": "/docs/quickstart",
  "target_outcome": "Capture or route one model call through Kolm and receive a verifiable artifact or response record.",
  "product_journey": "gateway-capture",
  "primary_node": 8,
  "owner_nodes": [1, 2, 5, 7, 8, 12, 17, 32, 43, 46],
  "mode_sequence": ["tutorial", "how-to", "reference", "explanation"],
  "prerequisites": ["Kolm API key or local mock mode", "supported provider key when using live routing"],
  "commands": ["kolm capture --help", "kolm route --help"],
  "expected_outputs": ["captured request id", "artifact or trace id", "no secret values printed"],
  "live_secret_requirements": ["KOLM_API_KEY", "provider key only for live provider examples"],
  "package_truth": "published, source-only, preview, compatibility, or blocked",
  "last_verified": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "screenshots": ["desktop-dark", "desktop-light", "mobile-dark", "mobile-light"],
  "proof": ["example smoke", "link lint", "metadata lint", "account/CLI/API parity"]
}
```

The ledger must be generated from actual docs files, route/API manifests, SDK package manifests, CLI help, and product graph data. It cannot be hand-maintained prose. When another bot changes frontend chrome, SDK packages, API routes, or CLI behavior, this ledger is the contract that tells node `8` what docs must be refreshed.

##### Node 8.8 Required First-Value Lanes V3

| lane | audience | first value | docs starting point | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `developer-route-first-call` | app developer | replace one model API call with Kolm routing/capture and see owned output evidence | `/docs/quickstart`, `/docs/quickstart/node`, `/docs/cli/capture` | command smoke, expected output, API key scope, provider scope, copy-button proof |
| `ml-compile-distill-first-artifact` | ML engineer | compile or distill a task-specific `.kolm` artifact from a teacher/model/capture set | `/docs/quickstart`, `/docs/cli/compile`, `/docs/cli/distill`, `/docs/api` | fixture run, artifact hash, K-score/eval scope, teacher/live-secret scope |
| `platform-run-deploy-first-service` | platform engineer | run a Kolm artifact locally or deploy it with storage/readiness proof | `/docs/install`, `/docs/runtime`, `/docs/cli/cloud`, `/docs/observability` | local smoke, storage readiness, route health, rollback command, no secret leakage |
| `security-admin-first-audit` | security/admin | create keys, view audit trail, export evidence, and verify trust boundaries | `/docs/security`, `/docs/audit-export`, `/docs/api`, `/account/overview` | RBAC scope, audit export sample, SIEM/export schema, secret redaction proof |
| `enterprise-buyer-first-evaluation` | buyer/procurement | understand plans, enterprise contact path, compliance scope, DPA/BAA state, and external gates | `/pricing`, `/enterprise`, `/trust`, `/docs` | pricing contract parity, closeout ledger links, sales-required state, no fake certifications |
| `sdk-integration-first-library` | SDK/integration developer | install or source-run the right SDK without relying on unpublished packages | `/docs/sdk`, `/docs/quickstart`, `/docs/install` | node `5` package truth, version badge, source-only labels, package smoke or blocked state |

Every public docs landing page should route to these lanes within one screen. Every lane should finish with one concrete next step: inspect artifact, deploy artifact, run eval, invite teammate, create key, contact sales, or open API reference. Avoid dead-end pages.

##### Node 8.9 Page Record Contract V3

Every docs page must have one generated record:

| field | required value |
|---|---|
| `path` | canonical public path and source path |
| `mode` | one of `tutorial`, `how-to`, `reference`, `explanation`, `troubleshooting`, `migration`, `release-note`, `research`, or `archive` |
| `audience` | primary user type; no generic `all` unless page is a router/index |
| `owner_nodes` | master spec tree nodes responsible for truth |
| `journey_ids` | product journey ids this page teaches or references |
| `source_of_truth` | route manifest, CLI help, SDK manifest, product graph, generated markdown, or hand-authored source |
| `package_state` | `not-applicable`, `published`, `source-only`, `preview`, `compatibility-alias`, `deprecated`, or `blocked` |
| `example_state` | `none`, `runnable-local`, `runnable-live`, `dry-run`, `fixture-only`, `conceptual`, or `broken` |
| `expected_output_state` | `present`, `not-needed`, or `missing` |
| `copy_affordance_state` | `present`, `not-needed`, or `missing` |
| `freshness_state` | `current`, `stale`, `generated`, `needs-review`, or `archive` |
| `claim_scope` | implemented, preview, package-pending, benchmark-pending, certification-pending, partner-pending, external, or deprecated |
| `metadata_state` | title, description, canonical, OG, schema, and no-mojibake checks |
| `ui_state` | desktop/mobile, light/dark, keyboard, code overflow, table overflow, nav, anchor, and copy-button proof |
| `production_state` | local-only, production-smoked, production-blocked, or not-deployed |

No docs page can be counted final without this record. Pages can still exist before finality, but they must be visibly scoped as preview/source-only/archive/etc.

##### Node 8.10 Code Example And Command Contract V3

Every code block and command shown in docs needs a generated example record:

| field | rule |
|---|---|
| language | required; no unlabeled code fences on docs pages |
| command_kind | shell, PowerShell, JavaScript, Python, Rust, C, curl, JSON, YAML, TOML, HTTP, or config |
| safe_to_copy | true only if command does not leak or destroy data and does not require hidden setup |
| secret_inputs | all required env vars named with no values |
| live_network | true/false |
| expected_output | exact output, output shape, or explicit "no output expected" |
| smoke_command | verifier command or explicit reason smoke cannot run |
| package_truth_source | package manifest, release ledger, source-only marker, or compatibility alias |
| stale_failure_mode | what breaks if route/package/provider/CLI changes |

The copy button contract is also functional, not decorative. A code block with no copy affordance is allowed only when it is intentionally non-copyable conceptual output. Otherwise node `12` and node `8` must treat the missing copy affordance as a docs UI defect.

##### Node 8.11 SDK Truth Contract V3

SDK docs must not be generic install marketing. Each SDK page must explicitly state:

1. current support state: source-only, published package, preview, compatibility alias, deprecated, or blocked;
2. canonical package name and old compatibility names, if any;
3. build/test command used to prove the SDK in the current repo;
4. install command only if the channel is actually published or a local/source install is clearly labeled;
5. API surface parity with product graph and route docs;
6. examples for route/capture, compile/distill, and run/deploy where supported;
7. unsupported surfaces stated before the user tries them;
8. last verified date and runtime/toolchain version;
9. migration guidance from `kolmogorov` names to `kolm` names where aliases still exist;
10. no dependency on local build output directories such as `sdk/rust/target` for public package claims.

This connects node `8` to node `5`: docs cannot say a package is published just because source code exists, a tarball exists, or a generated artifact exists locally.

##### Node 8.12 Docs UI And Search Contract V3

Node `8` and node `12` must jointly enforce:

| area | final requirement |
|---|---|
| top navigation | docs entry points are not buried under product marketing or duplicated across confusing labels |
| docs sidebar | current section, parent, sibling, and next-step location are visible without hover-only cues |
| code blocks | language label, copy button, wrap/scroll behavior, focus state, and no layout shift |
| tables | mobile overflow is readable and does not hide the first column or status labels |
| command tabs | OS/provider/language tabs keep selection state and do not imply unsupported packages |
| warnings | live-secret, destructive, external, preview, certification-pending, and package-pending states are visually distinct |
| search | titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, schema, and snippets are page-specific and free of mojibake |
| links | internal links point to current canonical paths; compatibility redirects are documented |
| last verified | every runnable docs page shows freshness and proof source |
| screenshots | docs pages with code, large tables, tabs, or sidebars are screenshot-smoked in dark/light desktop/mobile |

The docs aesthetic should support work, not decoration. Dense reference pages need compact hierarchy, tables, status chips, and sticky context. Tutorials need progressive steps, visible success criteria, and no side quest copy. Search landing pages need concise task labels, not paragraphs.

##### Node 8.13 Runnable Docs Verification V3

Add these verification gates before node `8` can be considered final:

| gate | command or artifact | pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| docs page ledger | `docs/docs-page-ledger.json` | every docs HTML and markdown page represented |
| first-value ledger | `docs/docs-first-value-paths.json` | six lanes present with start page, outcome, commands, proof, and next step |
| code example ledger | `docs/docs-example-ledger.json` | every code block/command classified with language, expected output, safety, and smoke state |
| SDK truth join | generated from node `5` package ledger | no install command advertises unpublished package channels |
| CLI truth join | CLI help/source to generated docs | every CLI docs page maps to current CLI help or generated source metadata |
| API truth join | OpenAPI, route manifest, product graph, API docs | no route count mismatch, no hidden source-indexed routes marketed as complete |
| docs metadata audit | HTML/head parser | no mojibake, no missing canonical, no duplicate H1, concise title/description, OG/schema where required |
| docs interaction audit | browser or Playwright | copy buttons, tabs, sidebars, anchors, tables, keyboard path, focus states, and responsive layout work |
| docs production smoke | deployed docs URLs | production docs match current generated artifacts and no stale legacy claims |

These gates should feed node `9` release evidence. Node `9` can then say whether docs are release-ready without manually rereading hundreds of pages.

##### Node 8 V3 Relax Redline

Node `8` V3 is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. the master spec tree top row, node `8` body, and generated docs ledgers agree on ownership and status;
2. every docs HTML and markdown page has a generated page record with mode, audience, owner nodes, journey ids, source-of-truth, claim scope, freshness, metadata, UI state, and production state;
3. the six first-value lanes can be completed by a new user without hidden maintainer knowledge;
4. every runnable example has language, copy affordance, safety classification, secret requirements, expected output, and smoke or explicit non-smoke rationale;
5. every SDK/install/quickstart page reflects actual package-channel truth from node `5` and never markets source-only or pending packages as published;
6. every CLI docs page maps to current CLI help/source metadata and advertised commands are smoked or honestly scoped;
7. API reference, OpenAPI, route manifests, SDK docs, account calls, and CLI examples agree on route shape and readiness state;
8. docs pages expose package-pending, benchmark-pending, certification-pending, partner-pending, and external gates where relevant instead of hiding them;
9. docs UI passes dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots for code, table, tab, sidebar, nav, and copy-button behavior;
10. production docs smoke proves deployed docs match the current generated artifacts before any final release claim.

#### Node 9 Verification, Release Evidence, And Finality Gates Addendum V2

This pass maintains master spec tree node `9`: verification and release evidence. Node `9` owns the proof that the whole current tree can be shipped together. It does not own individual feature behavior; nodes `0` through `8` and `10` through `51` own their surfaces. Node `9` owns the evidence runner, gate map, generated-artifact freshness, local and production smoke, screenshot evidence, test lanes, workflow proof, clean-git proof, release packet, and completion audit. Node `13` owns release operations and deployment mechanics, node `29` owns production environment/deploy proof, node `40` owns QA depth, node `43` owns build redline, and node `46` owns traceability. Node `9` is the final arbiter for "can we say this state is verified?"

The key correction for node `9` is that a green command is not a release. A release is a frozen source state plus a machine-readable evidence packet that names each requirement, each verifier, each generated artifact, each env assumption, each skipped gate, each screenshot set, each production probe, each open external gate, and each accepted risk.

##### Node 9.0 Current Verification Evidence V2

Current local scan of verification and release surfaces:

| evidence | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| package scripts | 57 scripts in `package.json` | script breadth is strong, but each script needs owner, read/write set, env needs, release-critical role, timeout, and artifact outputs |
| primary release driver | `scripts/release-verify.cjs` runs lint refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, full tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, and billing tiers | this is the local release driver, but it must emit durable release evidence packets, not only console output |
| full test files | 560 test files, 7,306,074 bytes | test scale is broad enough to require requirement-to-test mapping, shard stability, flake ledger, and changed-surface selection |
| verifier/audit/smoke scripts | many scripts exist for product kernel, product surfaces, SOTA readiness, claim scope, brand, package release, benchmarks, screenshots, local/prod surfaces, href/static refs, API ref, OpenAPI, sitemap, and docs | verification mesh exists, but gates need one policy catalog and evidence taxonomy |
| local surface smoke | `local:surfaces` and `local:surfaces:deep` are first-class package scripts | local smoke must map each probe to product surface, route group, auth state, plan state, cleanup, and production parity |
| production surface smoke | `prod:surfaces` and `prod:surfaces:deep --require-auth` exist | production proof is explicitly separate from local proof and cannot be assumed from local passes |
| screenshot audit scripts | `ui:audit`, `ui:audit:all`, `ui:audit:all:themes`, `qa:shots`, and many screenshot helpers exist | screenshot evidence must be tied to route, theme, viewport, commit, deployment ID, manual inspection, and requirement IDs |
| screenshot reports | `reports/ui-surface-audit` contains many large report trees; recent examples include V597 route chunks and focused-polish/manual-use reports | there is a lot of visual evidence, but finality requires the exact latest post-change/post-deploy report, not any prior passing report |
| GitHub workflows | 11 workflow files: compile, distill, CI pipeline, template, lint, SBOM, smoke, SDK C/Rust, KolmBench, test-suite, and push compile | workflows are release infrastructure and need owner, permissions, secrets policy, artifact outputs, and release-blocking status |
| current worktree state | current `git status --short` shows modified public CSS/JS/nav/studio/service-worker/intent/vercel paths plus untracked shot/failure/temp/doc files | final release cannot be called clean until every dirty/untracked file is committed, removed, generated with owner, or allowlisted in the release packet |
| generated-artifact race risk | product graph, closeout, API ref, OpenAPI, SDK docs, sitemap, screenshots, and release packets can be regenerated by multiple agents | node `9` needs a generation lock order and stale-artifact detector |

Important current tree observation: the active worktree is intentionally dirty due parallel frontend/backend/spec work. That is acceptable during the goal. It is not acceptable for finality. Node `9` must require a frozen commit, no unowned dirty files, no stale generated artifacts, and no untracked evidence/temp files outside a release packet.

##### Node 9.1 External Verification And Release Baseline V2

| baseline | source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA v1.2 is current and defines build/source tracks, provenance, verification summary attestations, and increasing security guarantees | SLSA v1.2: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/ | release packets must include source/build provenance, artifact identity, expected builders, verified properties, and consumer-verifiable summaries |
| Secure software development should reduce vulnerabilities, mitigate impact, and address root causes | NIST SSDF SP 800-218: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final | release verification must include secure development controls, vulnerability/secret checks, root-cause closeout, and acquisition-ready evidence |
| GitHub artifact attestations provide signed provenance and must be verified to provide security value | GitHub artifact attestations: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/security/artifact-attestations | Kolm release artifacts, packages, binaries, installer manifests, OpenAPI snapshots, and build packets need attestations and verification commands |
| Local green checks do not prove production | current repo local/prod smoke split | release proof must distinguish local, CI, preview, production, self-host, BYOC, airgap, package-registry, and external-certification evidence |
| UI screenshots prove rendering, not task success | current route screenshot reports and manual-use reports | release evidence must include manual task replays for critical journeys, not only route render audits |

##### Node 9.2 Release Evidence Packet Contract V2

Every release candidate should produce this packet or a lossless superset:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.release_evidence_packet.v2",
  "tree_node": 9,
  "release": {
    "id": null,
    "commit": null,
    "branch": null,
    "dirty_tree": false,
    "generated_at": null,
    "environment": "local|ci|preview|production|self-host|byoc|airgap",
    "operator": null
  },
  "source_state": {
    "git_status": null,
    "changed_files": [],
    "untracked_files": [],
    "allowlisted_dirty_files": [],
    "submodules": [],
    "lockfiles": [],
    "node_version": null,
    "os": null
  },
  "generated_artifacts": {
    "product_graph": null,
    "readiness_closeout": null,
    "api_routes": null,
    "openapi": null,
    "api_ref": null,
    "cli_docs": null,
    "sdk_docs": null,
    "sitemap": null,
    "screenshots": [],
    "release_notes": null
  },
  "gates": [
    {
      "id": "release-verify",
      "command": "npm run release:verify",
      "owner_node": 9,
      "status": "pass|fail|skipped|blocked|warning",
      "duration_ms": null,
      "stdout_path": null,
      "stderr_path": null,
      "json_path": null,
      "failure_reason": null,
      "skipped_reason": null,
      "env_profile": null,
      "proof_scope": "local|ci|preview|production",
      "requirements": []
    }
  ],
  "surface_proof": {
    "product_surfaces": null,
    "api_routes": null,
    "public_pages": null,
    "account_pages": null,
    "docs_pages": null,
    "cli_commands": null,
    "tui_views": null,
    "sdk_packages": null,
    "workers": null
  },
  "security_and_supply_chain": {
    "secret_scan": null,
    "claim_scope": null,
    "brand_contract": null,
    "dependency_audit": null,
    "sbom": null,
    "provenance": null,
    "artifact_attestations": [],
    "slsa_summary": null,
    "ssdf_mapping": null
  },
  "production": {
    "deployment_id": null,
    "base_url": null,
    "prod_surface_smoke": null,
    "prod_auth_smoke": null,
    "prod_screenshot_report": null,
    "prod_metadata_check": null,
    "prod_rum_window": null,
    "rollback_target": null,
    "rollback_smoke": null
  },
  "external_gates": {
    "package_releases": [],
    "certifications": [],
    "public_benchmarks": [],
    "partner_adoption": []
  },
  "decision": {
    "release_ready": false,
    "known_limits": [],
    "accepted_risks": [],
    "required_followups": [],
    "signoffs": []
  }
}
```

Node `9` should generate this packet after all source/generation/test/smoke lanes complete. It should not be manually written except for human signoff fields and accepted-risk explanations.

##### Node 9.3 Verification Proof Classes

| proof class | what it proves | what it does not prove |
|---|---|---|
| `syntax-clean` | files parse and static syntax checks pass | behavior or release readiness |
| `unit-green` | targeted unit tests pass | integration, UI, production, or external gates |
| `integration-green` | feature paths pass against local dependencies | production env parity |
| `generated-fresh` | generated artifacts match current source inputs | behavior, visuals, or package publication |
| `claim-clean` | public claims do not exceed current evidence gates | usability or product value |
| `visual-rendered` | screenshot audit renders route/theme/viewport without warnings/failures | task success or accessibility depth |
| `journey-replayed` | a human or automated journey reaches expected task result with evidence | all edge cases |
| `local-release-green` | `release:verify` and local/deep smokes pass on current tree | production or package registry state |
| `ci-release-green` | CI runs the required release gates on clean runner | production env variables and live services |
| `production-smoked` | deployed commit passes prod smokes, auth probes, metadata, screenshots, and no secret leakage | long-term reliability |
| `attested-release` | artifacts and packets have provenance/SBOM/signature verification | product correctness |

Relax rule: a release can be considered local-ready at `local-release-green`, deploy-ready only at `ci-release-green` plus clean source state, and complete only at `production-smoked` plus generated release evidence packet and explicit external-gate ledger.

##### Node 9.4 Atomic Work Tickets V2

| ticket | owner node | outcome |

| W-VERIFY-V2-001 | 9 | Generate `docs/verification-gate-ledger.json` mapping every package script, verifier, smoke script, screenshot script, workflow, and manual gate to owner node, read/write set, env needs, timeout, outputs, and release-critical status. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-002 | 9 | Add machine-readable output to `release:verify` with gate IDs, command lines, durations, stdout/stderr artifact paths, env profile, skipped reasons, failure reasons, and requirement IDs. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-003 | 9 | Add release evidence packet generation under `reports/release-evidence/<date>/packet.json` with the contract above. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-004 | 9 | Add generated-artifact freshness graph for product graph, readiness closeout, API routes, OpenAPI, API ref, CLI docs, SDK docs, sitemap, screenshots, and release notes. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-005 | 9 | Add generated-artifact lock order and concurrency guard so parallel agents cannot race API ref/OpenAPI/product graph/docs/screenshots. |

| W-VERIFY-V2-008 | 9 | Add changed-surface verifier selection so touched files map to required tests, docs checks, screenshots, local smokes, production smokes, and package checks. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-009 | 9 | Add local/deep surface smoke proof matrix mapping each probe to product surface, route group, auth state, plan state, data fixture, cleanup, and negative case. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-010 | 9 | Add production smoke evidence with deployment ID, base URL, auth key source, route count, status matrix, screenshots, account state, storage/cloud readiness, and secret-redaction check. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-011 | 9 | Add screenshot evidence manifest tying every screenshot report to route, theme, viewport, base URL, commit, deployment ID, changed-surface scope, manual review result, and requirement IDs. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-012 | 9 | Add manual journey replay packet for critical user journeys: homepage to quickstart, capture, distill/compile, artifact verify, runtime deploy, account billing, storage readiness, enterprise inquiry, docs/API/CLI. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-013 | 9 | Add clean-git finality gate: no modified/untracked files except release-packet outputs, and every generated output either committed or explicitly excluded. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-014 | 9 | Add workflow security/proof audit for all 11 `.github/workflows` files: triggers, permissions, secrets, pinned actions, artifacts, attestations, and release-blocking role. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-015 | 9 | Add SBOM/provenance/attestation verification for packages, SDKs, binaries, installer manifests, OpenAPI snapshots, and release packets. |

| W-VERIFY-V2-019 | 9 | Add post-deploy verification window: 0-hour smoke, 1-hour error/RUM/conversion scan, 24-hour monitoring summary, and rollback/no-rollback decision. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-020 | 9 | Add final completion audit rehearsal that samples every node, route/page/command/test/generated artifact, and claim against current evidence before any "100 percent complete" statement. |
| W-VERIFY-V2-021 | 9 | Add a node `9` completion certificate requiring clean source state, generated freshness, local/CI/prod gates, screenshot/journey proof, supply-chain evidence, external-gate ledger, release packet, and final decision record. |

##### Node 9 V2 Relax Redline

Node `9` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. every verification script, smoke script, screenshot script, package script, and workflow has an owner, read/write set, env needs, timeout, outputs, and release-critical status;
2. `release:verify` emits machine-readable evidence and every gate result maps to requirement IDs;
3. generated artifacts are fresh and regenerated in a documented serial order with no concurrent-agent race;
4. all tests, smokes, claim checks, brand checks, package checks, benchmark checks, docs checks, screenshots, and release verify gates run on the frozen source state or are explicitly blocked/skipped with owner-approved rationale;
5. local evidence is not used to claim production proof, package publication, certification, public benchmark superiority, or partner adoption;
6. production smoke runs against the deployed commit with auth, product graph, readiness closeout, storage/cloud readiness, public pages, docs, account pages, API/OpenAPI, screenshots, and secret-leak checks;
7. screenshots are tied to commit, route, theme, viewport, base URL, manual review, and requirement IDs;
8. critical user journeys are replayed end to end and produce task evidence, not only rendered screenshots;
9. source state is clean or every dirty/untracked file is committed, removed, or allowlisted in the release packet with release impact;
10. workflows prove permissions, pinned action policy, secrets policy, artifact outputs, and release-blocking status;
11. SBOM, provenance, and artifact attestations exist and are verified where release artifacts are produced;
12. the release packet records external gates, known limits, accepted risks, signoffs, rollback target, support notes, and monitoring window;
13. post-deploy verification runs and attaches to the exact deployed commit before any production-complete claim;
14. the final completion audit samples every master node and can falsify the "100 percent complete" claim if evidence is missing.

#### Node 9 V3 Verification Evidence Mesh, Release Finality, And Proof-Pack Contract

This pass maintains master spec tree node `9`, not a new node. Node `9` is the proof spine: it decides whether the codebase, generated artifacts, site, docs, SDKs, account surfaces, workers, external gates, and production deployment are all proven at the same frozen state. It does not own feature implementation. It owns whether any feature can be described as done.

The V3 correction is stricter than V2: a passing local smoke is only one evidence class. A 100 percent release requires a current, machine-readable, non-ambiguous proof pack that binds commit, generated artifacts, tests, route ownership, claims, screenshots, production smoke, external gates, and clean git state. If one of those proof classes is red, stale, missing, or scoped to local-only, node `9` must block "done."

##### Node 9.0 Current Evidence Delta V3

Current hands-on evidence from the live worktree:

| evidence lane | current result | release-finality read |
|---|---:|---|
| package scripts | 57 scripts in `package.json` | broad gate surface exists, but node `9` needs a generated owner/read-write/env/output policy for every script |
| test files | 557 total test files, 528 wave tests, 29 other tests | V2 count is stale; V3 release evidence must record the exact count on the frozen commit |
| release driver | `scripts/release-verify.cjs` has 10 semantic gates: refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, full tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, billing tiers | strong local release driver, but it still needs durable per-gate evidence artifacts and explicit production/external boundary labels |
| `verify:kernel` | pass, `journeys=12`, `route_surfaces=7`, `requirements=57`, `warnings=4` | core product graph is structurally alive, but open readiness warnings remain release-significant |
| `verify:sota` | pass with warning verdict; 8 open requirements: 2 external partner, 1 live certification, 4 package release, 1 public benchmark data | honest closeout exists; marketing and finality must continue to treat these as non-local gates |
| product-surface verifier | fail: 46 unowned route groups, `routes=582`, `mapped_routes=452` | release-blocking until route ownership catalog and generated route inventory are reconciled |
| local surface smoke | pass: 7 surfaces, 74 probes, 74 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked | local app smoke is healthy for declared probes, but it does not prove unowned route groups, prod, screenshots, claim scope, or external gates |
| claim-scope verifier | fail: `public/security.html:159` contains unscoped `SOC 2 Type I attested` while certification status is still open | release-blocking claim overreach until copy is scoped to available proof or certification evidence is attached |
| brand verifier | fail: legacy brand token found in `cli/kolm.js` and `scripts/nav-unify.cjs` | release-blocking for the "all Kolmogorov to Kolm" requirement |
| package-release readiness | `ok=true`, `publish_ready=false`, 16 targets, 16 pending, 0 blocked | package structure exists; registry/install-channel release is not done |
| local doctor with both provided API keys | `ok=true`, `blockers=0`, but cloud reachability warns `fetch failed`, server-side key validation unknown, optional Docker/Python/C compiler/provider/project-config warnings | local setup is not blocked, but no production/cloud validation can be claimed from this shell |
| latest focused UI report opened | `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tfocused-polish-pass/report.md`: fail, 24 failures, product media missing/weak on home/product/pricing across themes/viewports | latest opened focused evidence is not a release pass; frontend bot may later replace it, but node `9` must require a newer passing report |
| latest manual-use UI report opened | `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account/report.md`: fail, 36 failures, 44 warnings | manual-use proof is currently red and cannot be treated as product-complete |
| generated artifacts observed | `public/product-graph.json` 141,724 bytes; `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` 12,897 bytes; `public/docs/api-routes.json` 296,285 bytes; `public/openapi.json` 579,772 bytes | artifacts exist, but finality requires freshness hashes after route ownership, OpenAPI, product graph, docs, and screenshots settle |
| current spec file | untracked research document | acceptable for this doc lane, but final release cannot be clean until spec docs are intentionally committed or excluded |

Immediate node `9` redline: local smoke and kernel gates are not enough. The current tree is not release-final until product-surface route ownership, claim scope, brand cleanup, and final screenshot/manual-use reports are green on the same frozen source state.

##### Node 9.1 External Verification Baseline V3

| baseline | official source | Kolm V3 requirement |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA v1.2 provenance and verification summary attestations | https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/ and https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/verification_summary | every release artifact, package, installer, generated OpenAPI snapshot, product graph, and proof packet needs provenance or an explicit non-attested status |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218 | https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final | release proof must map to secure development practice evidence: prepare, protect, produce well-secured software, and respond to vulnerabilities |
| GitHub artifact attestations | https://docs.github.com/actions/security-guides/using-artifact-attestations-to-establish-provenance-for-builds | CI artifacts should be attested and verifiable by consumers, not only uploaded as opaque build outputs |
| GitHub Actions workflow permissions | https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-syntax | every workflow should declare minimal `permissions`, controlled triggers, concurrency, environment, secrets use, and artifact outputs |
| Playwright trace and visual evidence | https://playwright.dev/docs/trace-viewer-intro | screenshot evidence should include traces, DOM snapshots, network/console evidence, and failure artifacts for critical journeys, not only static PNGs |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | release smoke and prod verification should confirm AI/provider/model/agent spans are emitted with an explicit convention version where telemetry exists |
| Google Cloud DORA delivery metrics | https://cloud.google.com/developers/dora | release-finality should track deployment frequency, lead time, change fail rate, and restore time, but never substitute those metrics for feature proof |

##### Node 9.3 Verification Acceptance Ladder V3

| level | name | acceptance |
|---:|---|---|
| 0 | `script-exists` | a command or workflow exists, but no current run is attached |
| 1 | `local-ran` | command ran locally and result is recorded with exit code, date, env, and output path |
| 2 | `focused-pass` | targeted gate passed for the touched surface |
| 3 | `matrix-pass` | full related gate matrix passed, including generated artifact freshness |
| 4 | `claim-clean` | brand and claim scope gates passed after all copy/docs/site changes |
| 5 | `visual-clean` | latest screenshots and manual journeys passed after the current commit |
| 6 | `surface-owned` | every route group, page, command, account path, and generated artifact has a product-surface owner |
| 7 | `ci-clean` | gates passed on a clean CI runner with expected node/toolchain versions |
| 8 | `attested` | generated release artifacts have provenance, hashes, SBOM/signature state, and verification commands |
| 9 | `prod-smoked` | deployed commit passed public and authenticated production smoke, no secret leakage, and account readiness checks |
| 10 | `final-release` | clean source state, evidence packet, external-gate ledger, rollback proof, post-deploy window, and signoffs all exist |

The current live tree is below level 4 because claim and brand gates are red, below level 6 because route ownership is red, and below level 9 because production validation is not proven from this shell.

##### Node 9.4 Gate Catalog Requirements V3

Every release-critical gate must be listed in a generated ledger with these fields:

| field | required meaning |
|---|---|
| `gate_id` | stable ID such as `node9.release.verify`, `node9.claim.scope`, or `node9.ui.latest` |
| `owner_node` | master spec tree node that owns the proof |
| `command` | exact command including flags and required env |
| `proof_class` | one of syntax, unit, integration, generated, claim, visual, journey, local-smoke, prod-smoke, supply-chain, external |
| `reads` and `writes` | file and network side effects so parallel agents do not race |
| `timeout_ms` | hard timeout; no open-ended release gates |
| `release_required` | whether failure blocks local-ready, deploy-ready, or production-complete |
| `skip_policy` | who may skip, why, and what replacement proof is required |
| `artifact_outputs` | JSON, markdown, logs, screenshots, traces, SBOM, attestations, or registry URLs |
| `freshness_inputs` | source files that should invalidate the result when touched |

Minimum release-critical gates for node `9`:

| gate | current command or evidence source | release role |
|---|---|---|
| `node9.release.verify` | `npm run release:verify` | local release integration driver |
| `node9.kernel` | `npm run verify:kernel` | product graph structural truth |
| `node9.sota` | `npm run verify:sota` | open readiness and closeout honesty |
| `node9.surface.catalog` | `node scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs --json` | route ownership and product-surface completeness |
| `node9.claim.scope` | `npm run verify:claims-scope` | no unscoped certification/package/benchmark/partner claims |
| `node9.brand` | `npm run verify:brand` | no legacy Kolmogorov identity leakage |
| `node9.refs` | `npm run lint:refs` | refs, hrefs, product-surface catalog integrity |
| `node9.local.surfaces` | `npm run local:surfaces` | declared local safe smoke probes |
| `node9.local.surfaces.deep` | `npm run local:surfaces:deep` | deeper local product probes |
| `node9.prod.surfaces` | `npm run prod:surfaces` and `npm run prod:surfaces:deep` | deployed public/authenticated proof |
| `node9.ui.latest` | `npm run ui:audit:all:themes` or final split equivalent | latest route/theme/viewport screenshot proof |
| `node9.package.release` | `npm run verify:package-release` | installer/registry/package release proof |
| `node9.full.tests` | `npm test` or `npm run test:parallel` with release policy | full behavioral suite |
| `node9.generated.api` | API ref/OpenAPI/product graph/readiness closeout freshness checks | generated docs/API truth |
| `node9.workflow.security` | workflow permission/action/attestation audit | CI release integrity |
| `node9.sbom.provenance` | SBOM, hashes, SLSA/GitHub attestation verify commands | artifact trust |

| route ownership drift | 46 unowned route groups; 582 routes in API inventory; 452 mapped | node `2` owns route behavior, node `1` product truth, node `9` release proof | block deploy-ready until `docs/product-surfaces.json`, API routes, OpenAPI, and product graph agree |
| claim overreach | `public/security.html:159` says `SOC 2 Type I attested` while readiness still has live certification gate | node `10` security/compliance, node `6` public website, node `9` claim proof | block production-complete until copy is scoped or certification evidence is attached |
| brand leakage | `cli/kolm.js` and `scripts/nav-unify.cjs` contain legacy brand token | node `0` brand migration, node `5` package/CLI, node `9` proof | block final "all Kolmogorov to Kolm" claim |
| visual/manual proof red | latest opened focused/manual UI reports fail on media, tracking, loaded CSS/JS, and target-size issues | node `6` public website, node `7` account, node `51` media/demo, node `9` screenshot proof | block production-complete until a newer post-fix screenshot/manual report passes |
| package release pending | 16 package targets pending signed artifact or registry URL/hash proof | node `5` SDK/package, node `22` supply chain, node `9` proof | block external package availability claims |
| cloud key validation unknown | both provided local API keys make doctor return `ok=true`, but cloud reachability fetch fails and server validation is unknown | node `10` auth, node `29` deploy/env, node `9` production proof | block production-auth validation claims from local shell |
| optional toolchain gaps | local doctor warns Docker, Python, C compiler, Anthropic key, and project config missing | node `4` workers, node `5` install, node `29` env | block claims that this machine can run full local distill/bench/compile workflows without setup |

##### Node 9.6 Release Packet File Tree V3

The evidence packet should live under a deterministic release directory:

```text
reports/release-evidence/<utc-date>-<commit>/
  packet.json
  packet.md
  git-status.txt
  generated-artifacts.json
  gate-ledger.json
  gates/
    release-verify.json
    verify-depth.json
    verify-product-surfaces.json
    verify-claims-scope.json
    verify-brand.json
    local-surfaces.json
    prod-surfaces.json
  screenshots/
    ui-audit-report.json
    ui-audit-report.md
    manifest.json
    traces/
  supply-chain/
    sbom.spdx.json
    provenance.json
    attestations/
    workflow-permissions.json
  production/
    deployment.json
    smoke-public.json
    smoke-authenticated.json
    cloud-readiness.json
    rollback.json
    post-deploy-window.json
  signoff/
    decision.json
```

Anything outside this tree is not release evidence unless it is referenced by hash from `packet.json`.

##### Node 9 V3 Release Redline

Node `9` must block any "done", "deploy-ready", "production-complete", or "100 percent" claim until:

1. `verify-product-surfaces` is green with zero unowned route groups and no route count mismatch;
2. claim-scope verification is green with no unscoped certification, package, benchmark, or partner-adoption claims;
3. brand verification is green or legacy names are intentionally allowlisted only for historical/legal provenance;
4. the latest screenshot and manual-use reports after the current commit are green across required pages, themes, viewports, and critical tasks;
5. generated artifacts are fresh and hashed from the frozen source state;
6. local, deep local, production public, and production authenticated smokes are separately recorded;
7. package-release readiness distinguishes local structure from registry/install-channel publication;
8. both external readiness and production auth/cloud checks are either proven live or explicitly marked unavailable in the current environment;
9. release artifacts, package outputs, generated docs/API snapshots, and release packets have provenance/SBOM/signature or explicit non-attested status;
10. clean-git state is proven or every dirty/untracked file is intentionally included in the release packet;
11. every skipped gate has a human-approved reason and replacement proof;
12. "100 percent complete" cannot be emitted by docs, account UI, CLI, or release notes unless every master spec node has current proof.

### API And Backend Control Plane Ledger V1

This pass maintains master spec tree node `2`: API/backend control plane. This is the repo's largest risk concentration because the public API, account UI, CLI, TUI, product graph, OpenAPI, docs, generated SDKs, and release smoke all converge on the same route inventory.

#### Current API Inventory

| source | current value | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `public/docs/api-routes.json` generated date | 2026-05-20 | generated docs are not enough; every release needs a fresh generation proof on the shipping commit |
| route source files | 2 | `src/router.js` and `src/oauth.js` must stay the canonical API source set unless a route ownership manifest adds more files |
| route groups | 163 | every group must map to exactly one product surface and one master spec node |
| method-path records | 582 | every method-path must be implemented, internal-only, external-gated, or removed |
| stubs | 127 | stubs are not completion; each needs a closeout record with implementation proof or explicit non-shipping scope |
| methods | GET 284, POST 276, DELETE 13, PUT 7, PATCH 2 | mutation routes need idempotency, audit, auth, error, and rollback proof |
| OpenAPI version | 3.0.3 | acceptable current output, but 3.1 alignment should be evaluated because OAS 3.1 aligns more directly with modern JSON Schema |
| OpenAPI path count | 556 | reconcile path count versus 582 method-path records and prove this is expected method aggregation, not lost docs coverage |
| OpenAPI tag count | 163 | tag count matches route groups and should remain a verifier invariant |

#### Largest Route Groups

| route group | routes | stubs | current read |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `account` | 19 | 2 | central post-auth surface; SSO configure/status remain stubbed and must not be marketed as live SSO setup until wired |
| `capture` | 14 | 4 | core product surface; Gemini capture and capture RBAC policy/evaluation are P0 because capture is the top-funnel product action |
| `teams` | 12 | 0 | currently broad and implemented under route inventory; still needs uniform envelope/error/auth proof |
| `distill` | 11 | 1 | product-critical; `POST /v1/distill/strategy` must graduate from stub to real strategy contract or scope label |
| `marketplace` | 11 | 3 | public network-effect surface; listings/reviews must be real, moderated, and abuse-safe before marketing marketplace strength |
| `cloud` | 10 | 5 | high enterprise risk; broker, deploy-plan, and cloud distill job lifecycle stubs block hosted-cloud claims |
