#### API Semantics Contract

The current router has positive signals: `attachEnvelopeHeaders` appears 33 times, `secret_values_included` appears 31 times, `Retry-After` appears 6 times, and `Cache-Control` appears 15 times. It also shows incomplete standardization: `request_id` appears only 5 times, `application/problem+json` does not appear, `ETag` does not appear, `cursor` does not appear, and idempotency language appears only once.

The finished API contract is:

1. every response has a stable envelope or a documented media type exception;
2. every error response has RFC 9457-compatible Problem Details shape, with no stack traces or secret values;
3. every response includes or propagates a request id for support, account UI, CLI, and logs;
4. every mutating route has auth, RBAC, audit log, idempotency guidance, and replay behavior;
5. every list route has pagination semantics, either cursor-based or explicitly bounded;
6. every rate-limited route has 429 plus `Retry-After`; if RateLimit draft headers are emitted, they must be generated consistently and documented as draft/de facto;
7. every route with expensive compute has timeout, cancellation, and job-status behavior;
8. every provider/cloud readiness route guarantees `secret_values_included: false`;
9. every OpenAPI operation has request/response schemas, status codes, auth requirements, and examples;
10. every public SDK method is generated or checked against OpenAPI and smoke-tested against local server fixtures;
11. every route group maps to master spec tree node, product surface, account page, CLI/TUI equivalent, and release smoke where user-facing;
12. every stub route is either implemented, removed, or listed in a closeout ledger that blocks marketing copy from implying completion.

#### Official API And Control-Plane Standards

| standard | source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| HTTP Semantics | RFC 9110 defines HTTP core semantics across versions, including resources, methods, status codes, fields, content negotiation, authentication, and caching: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110 | route behavior must follow method semantics; GET remains safe/read-only, mutations use POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE consistently, and cache/auth/status behavior is explicit |
| Problem Details | RFC 9457 defines `application/problem+json` for machine-readable HTTP API errors and warns against exposing implementation internals: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457 | all API errors should converge on Problem Details plus Kolm-safe extensions such as `code`, `request_id`, and validation pointers |
| 429 Too Many Requests | RFC 6585 defines status 429 and says responses may include `Retry-After`: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6585 | rate limiting must use 429 and `Retry-After` consistently before adding any custom quota headers |
| RateLimit fields | the IETF HTTPAPI RateLimit draft defines `RateLimit` and `RateLimit-Policy` direction, while older `RateLimit-Limit`, `RateLimit-Remaining`, and `RateLimit-Reset` remain draft/de facto: https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers-08.html | do not call RateLimit headers an RFC standard; if emitted, document draft status and parse with structured-field-compatible behavior |
| Structured Fields | RFC 8941 defines structured values for HTTP fields and parsing rules: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8941 | any custom quota, readiness, or policy header should use structured field syntax or avoid custom headers |
| Web Linking | RFC 8288 defines typed links: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8288 | pagination and related-resource links should use `Link` headers or documented body links consistently |
| OpenAPI | the OpenAPI Specification defines a language-agnostic interface for HTTP APIs that humans and computers can understand without source-code access: https://spec.openapis.org/oas/ | generated `public/openapi.json` must be complete enough for SDK generation, docs, test coverage, and route diffing |
| JSON Schema 2020-12 | JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 provides current schema documents and validation vocabularies: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12 | API payload schemas, artifact metadata, product graph, and closeout ledgers should share JSON Schema where practical |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI | OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions are in development and define GenAI events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, and agent spans: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | telemetry must state the convention version emitted and avoid silently changing span/event names across releases |

#### Master Spec Tree Branch 2

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `2.0` | API source inventory | which files define public API behavior? | generated route ledger with source file, line, group, method, path, stub flag |
| `2.1` | HTTP semantics | do routes use correct methods, status, cache, auth, and content types? | semantic lint plus representative request/response fixtures |
| `2.2` | Error contract | can SDKs and account UI rely on stable errors? | Problem Details fixtures for validation, auth, quota, provider, and internal failures |
| `2.3` | Envelope and request id | can support trace every user-visible failure? | envelope tests and `request_id` propagation across API, CLI, account, logs |
| `2.4` | Auth/RBAC/audit | can enterprise buyers prove access control? | route auth matrix, RBAC denial tests, audit events, admin/account UI proof |
| `2.5` | Pagination and idempotency | can clients safely use large lists and mutation retries? | cursor/page fixtures and idempotency/replay tests for mutations |
| `2.6` | OpenAPI/schema/SDK | can generated clients trust the API? | OpenAPI completeness, JSON Schema validation, SDK smoke against local server |
| `2.7` | Stub closure | is every unimplemented route explicit and non-marketed? | closeout ledger for all 127 current stubs or implementation removal proof |
| `2.8` | Observability | can operators see latency, errors, model/provider, and cost? | OpenTelemetry GenAI versioned spans/events/metrics and dashboard smoke |
| `2.9` | Product-surface parity | does account/docs/CLI/TUI expose the same backend truth? | route group mapped to product graph, docs page, account page, CLI/TUI command, and smoke probe |

#### Node 2 API Backend Contract And Route Closure Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. API/backend work belongs to node `2`; security-specific auth policy still links to node `10`, product graph truth links to node `1`, OpenAPI schema compatibility links to node `37`, and global build closure links to node `43`. The node `2` job is narrower and harder: no API route, backend module, envelope, generated API reference, or account/CLI/docs integration may be treated as done until its runtime behavior is owned, documented, tested, and smokeable.

##### Node `2.0` Current Backend Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/router.js` | `953,495` bytes and centralizes a very large fraction of API behavior. | Keep route snapshot tests before any split; the state-of-art target is route modules by domain with a generated ownership index, not endless monolith growth. |
| `src/envelope.js` | Provides `okEnvelope`, `errorEnvelope`, `readinessEnvelope`, `jobEnvelope`, and `attachEnvelopeHeaders`; error shape dual-emits flat legacy fields and nested `error_detail`. | This is the bridge contract, but it still needs RFC 9457-compatible Problem Details content negotiation and request-id propagation. |
| `public/docs/api-routes.json` | Node `JSON.parse` succeeds; generated date is `2026-05-20`; sources are `src/router.js` and `src/oauth.js`; total routes `582`, groups `163`, stubs `127`, stub groups `56`, unique paths `552`, no-comment routes `127`. | The generated inventory is usable, but every stub/no-comment route needs owner, closeout, product-surface scope, and marketing block. |
| `public/openapi.json` | OpenAPI `3.0.3`, file size `579,772` bytes, paths `556`, operations `586`, tags `165`, missing summaries `0`, missing operation IDs `11`. | OpenAPI coverage is broad, but missing operation IDs block generated-client trust; 3.1 migration should be planned because API payloads increasingly depend on modern JSON Schema. |
| `tests/wave485-openapi-coverage.test.js` | Locks non-stub route coverage, operation shape, components, and operationId uniqueness. | Keep this gate mandatory and extend it to operationId completeness, auth/security metadata, schema examples, and 3.1 migration checks. |
| `tests/wave490-release-verify-openapi-gate.test.js` | Pins the OpenAPI sync gate inside release verification. | Generated API artifacts must not be regenerated in parallel or skipped silently. |
| `tests/wave-wc05-envelope.test.js` | Locks flat plus rich error envelope compatibility. | Keep legacy compatibility while adding Problem Details as an additive media type or nested compatible field. |
| `src/auth.js`, tenant tests, and hosted-auth tests | Auth, tenant resolution, hosted/local behavior, and key formats are present and tested in several waves. | Route-by-route auth/RBAC/audit matrix is still required; broad auth tests do not prove every object-level route. |
| `express-rate-limit` and load-queue handling | Router imports `express-rate-limit`; current scans show `Retry-After` emission on overload/rate-limit paths. | All expensive and sensitive routes need a standard 429 envelope, retry header, quota details, and account/CLI documentation. |

##### Node `2.1` Updated External API Baseline V2

| Source | Current fact imported into node `2` | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| RFC 9110 HTTP Semantics, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110` | HTTP semantics define method behavior, status codes, fields, content negotiation, authentication, and caching across HTTP versions. | Each route must declare safe/idempotent/cacheable behavior, auth mode, content type, and status code contract. |
| RFC 9457 Problem Details, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Problem Details defines `application/problem+json`, fields such as `type`, `status`, `title`, `detail`, `instance`, and extension members, and warns against leaking implementation internals. | Error responses should support Problem Details without breaking existing Kolm envelopes; stack traces, secrets, and raw provider internals must never be exposed. |
| Idempotency-Key HTTP header draft, `https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-httpapi-idempotency-key-header-01` | The IETF HTTPAPI draft standardizes the `Idempotency-Key` request header field pattern for idempotent POST/PATCH behavior. | Mutating routes should accept, persist, replay, reject-conflict, and document idempotency keys, while labeling the header as draft/current best practice until final RFC status is confirmed. |
| RFC 9562 UUIDs, `https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc9562` | RFC 9562 is the current UUID standard and obsoletes RFC 4122. | Request IDs, idempotency keys, job IDs, trace IDs, and support bundle IDs should have stable generation/validation rules and avoid collision-prone ad hoc strings. |
| RFC 7464 JSON Text Sequences, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7464.html` | JSON text sequences define a standard way to delimit streaming JSON texts. | Streaming endpoints should explicitly choose SSE, JSON text sequences, NDJSON, or OpenAI-compatible chunks and document the media type and parser contract. |
| RFC 9205 Building Protocols with HTTP, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9205` | Protocols built on HTTP should document complete request and response examples, headers, media types, and interaction semantics. | Kolm API docs should show complete examples with headers, auth, request ID, idempotency, rate limit, success, and problem/error responses. |
| OWASP API Security Top 10 2023, `https://owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x00-header/` | The 2023 list emphasizes broken object/function/property authorization, authentication, resource consumption, sensitive business flows, SSRF, misconfiguration, inventory, and unsafe API consumption. | Node `2` route matrix must prove object-level, property-level, and function-level authorization for tenant-owned resources before enterprise claims. |
| OpenAPI 3.1.2, `https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.2.html` | 3.1.2 is the current 3.1 patch release and OpenAPI 3.1 aligns its Schema Object more directly with JSON Schema. | Migrate or explicitly defer from 3.0.3 to 3.1.2; either way, generated docs must explain schema dialect and client-generation compatibility. |

##### Node `2.2` API Backend Contract V2

Every route must eventually validate against `kolm.api_route_contract.v2`.

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.api_route_contract.v2",
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/v1/example",
  "source": {
    "file": "src/router.js",
    "line": 1234,
    "route_group": "example",
    "owner_node": 2,
    "product_surface": "gateway-capture"
  },
  "status": "implemented|stub|external-gated|internal-only|deprecated|remove-candidate",
  "http_semantics": {
    "safe": false,
    "idempotent": "native|idempotency-key|required-not-implemented|not-applicable",
    "cache": "no-store|private|max-age|not-applicable",
    "content_types": ["application/json"]
  },
  "auth": {
    "mode": "none|api-key|session|admin|signed-webhook|local-daemon|mixed",
    "tenant_required": true,
    "rbac": ["role-or-scope"],
    "object_level_authorization": "required-and-tested"
  },
  "request_response": {
    "request_schema": "json-schema-or-openapi-ref",
    "response_schema": "json-schema-or-openapi-ref",
    "success_statuses": [200],
    "error_statuses": [400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500],
    "problem_details": true,
    "kolm_envelope": true,
    "request_id": true
  },
  "side_effects": {
    "writes_state": true,
    "audit_event": "required",
    "idempotency_store": "required",
    "rollback_or_compensation": "documented"
  },
  "limits": {
    "rate_limit": "required",
    "retry_after": "required-on-429",
    "timeout": "required-for-expensive-routes",
    "pagination": "required-for-lists"
  },
  "parity": {
    "openapi": "covered",
    "api_docs": "covered",
    "sdk": "covered-or-not-applicable",
    "account": "covered-or-not-applicable",
    "cli": "covered-or-not-applicable",
    "tui": "covered-or-not-applicable",
    "local_surface_smoke": "covered-or-not-applicable",
    "production_smoke": "covered-or-external-gated"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "tests": [],
    "fixtures": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "docs": [],
    "last_verified": "iso-8601"
  }
}
```

##### Node `2.3` API Done-State Rules

1. A route with `stub: true` is not done even when it returns an honest response.
2. A route without an operation ID cannot be considered generated-client ready.
3. A mutation without idempotency/replay policy is not enterprise-ready.
4. A tenant-owned route without object-level authorization tests is not secure enough for enterprise claims.
5. A list route without pagination or a documented hard bound is unfinished.
6. A route that returns secrets, provider keys, stack traces, raw prompts without policy, or ambiguous readiness is a release blocker.
7. A route that appears in OpenAPI but not in the product-surface route catalog is unowned.
8. A route that exists in source but not in OpenAPI/API docs is hidden.
9. A route that is implemented but has no local smoke or focused behavior test must have a documented reason and owner review.
10. API docs must show complete request/response examples for the critical journey routes, including headers, auth, request ID, rate-limit, success, and error cases.

##### Node `2.4` Stub And OpenAPI Closure Queue V2

The current route inventory has `127` stubs across `56` groups. Top stub groups are `ab-tests` with `6`, `approvals` with `5`, `cloud` with `5`, then `autopilot`, `capture`, and `drift` with `4` each. The completion order should be:

1. `capture`, because it is the first product promise and feeds distill/compile.
2. `cloud`, because hosted/BYOC claims require real provider/job/storage paths.
3. `approvals`, because enterprise governance cannot be a decorative workflow.
4. `drift`, `autopilot`, and `ab-tests`, because reliability/experimentation copy is unsafe if mutations are fake.
5. `marketplace`, `kolmbench`, `compliance`, and `security`, because they drive public trust and procurement claims.
6. multimodal stubs under `audio`, `video`, `vision`, `vlm`, and `xlang`, because "multimodal" cannot rest on partial doctor routes only.

OpenAPI closure requires:

- all `11` missing operation IDs fixed or explicitly internal;
- all non-stub routes in OpenAPI with request/response schemas and examples;
- all stubs either included as scoped 501/closeout operations or excluded with documented reason;
- route inventory, OpenAPI, product surfaces, product graph, SDK docs, and CLI/account docs regenerated in serial order;
- a diff report explaining why `582` route records, `552` unique paths, `556` OpenAPI paths, and `586` operations are all expected or identifying the exact drift.

| `W-API-V2-011` | `2.4` | Close P0 capture/cloud/approvals/drift/autopilot stubs. | Focused route tests, docs, account/CLI parity, and local surface smoke pass for each group. |
| `W-API-V2-012` | `2.4` | Fix OpenAPI count reconciliation. | Report explains route records, unique paths, OpenAPI paths, and operations with no unexplained drift. |
| `W-API-V2-013` | `2.4` | Add OpenAPI 3.1.2 migration plan or explicit deferral. | Schema dialect, client generation impact, JSON Schema alignment, and migration tests are documented. |
| `W-API-V2-014` | `2.0` | Split `src/router.js` by route family behind snapshot tests. | Route inventory, URLs, auth, envelopes, and OpenAPI output are byte/contract stable after extraction. |
| `W-API-V2-015` | `2.2` | Add route-level audit-event verification. | Mutations emit actor, tenant, target, action, result, request ID, trace ID, and timestamp with safe payload. |
| `W-API-V2-016` | `2.2` | Add schema validation and negative tests for critical requests. | Invalid body, path, query, auth, tenant, and provider inputs return typed errors with JSON pointers where possible. |
| `W-API-V2-017` | `2.2` | Add expensive-route timeout/cancellation/job-state contract. | Compile, distill, benchmark, cloud, media, and provider calls expose timeout, cancel, retry, and job status. |
| `W-API-V2-018` | `2.2` | Add API docs complete-example gate. | Critical routes show full HTTP examples with headers, auth, request ID, idempotency, success, 4xx, 429, and 5xx. |
| `W-API-V2-019` | `2.2` | Add SDK compatibility smoke against OpenAPI. | Generated or hand-written SDK methods match operation IDs, request schemas, auth, and local server fixtures. |
| `W-API-V2-020` | `2.5` | Add node `2` certificate to global completion packet. | Final certificate includes route contracts, stub ledger, OpenAPI diff, auth matrix, envelope/problem fixtures, idempotency proof, and local plus production API smoke. |

##### Node `2` V2 Relax Redline

Node `2` can relax only when:

- every route has a route contract with owner node, product surface, source path, auth, schema, docs, tests, and smoke state;
- `src/router.js` is either split by domain or has a generated route ownership index strong enough to prevent hidden additions;
- all current stubs are implemented, removed, or scoped in a stub closeout ledger that blocks public overclaims;
- OpenAPI has complete operation IDs, schemas, examples, auth/security metadata, and no unexplained count drift;
- every error response is safe, traceable, and compatible with both existing Kolm envelopes and RFC 9457 Problem Details where negotiated;
- every response has a request ID or a documented media-type exception;
- every mutation has auth/RBAC/audit/idempotency or explicit non-retryable semantics;
- every list route has pagination or a documented bounded maximum;
- every expensive route has timeout, cancellation, retry, and job-state behavior;
- local and production API smoke prove critical unauthenticated, authenticated, admin, mutation, streaming, rate-limit, and error paths on the same commit.

#### Node 2 V3 API Backend Route Finality, Consumer Contract, And Stub-Scope Ledger

Node `52` remains unused. This is still node `2` because the gap is not "more backend features" in the abstract. The gap is route finality: every API route, generated API document, OpenAPI operation, SDK method, account fetch, CLI/TUI equivalent, auth/error envelope, and production smoke must agree on one current backend contract. Node `1` owns product graph truth, node `7` owns post-auth UX, node `8` owns API docs and examples, node `10` owns security policy, node `28` owns polyglot backend lane ownership, node `29` owns production proof, node `37` owns API/schema compatibility, and node `43` owns build finality. Node `2` owns the runtime API behavior that all of those nodes consume.

The V3 correction is that a large route inventory is not the same as a finished backend. A route is finished only when the request, response, errors, auth, tenant fence, side effects, idempotency or replay semantics, rate limits, timeout/cancellation behavior, OpenAPI operation, docs examples, SDK/account/CLI parity, local smoke, and production smoke are all explicit.

##### Node 2.6 Current API Evidence V3

Current generated and source evidence:

| evidence | current signal | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `public/docs/api-routes.json` generated date | `2026-05-20` | generated artifacts need freshness proof on the shipping commit |
| API route records | 582 | every route must have an owner and done-state record |
| route groups | 163 | group-to-surface and group-to-node ownership must stay invariant |
| stub route records | 127 | stubs remain a product-completion blocker unless scoped in a closeout ledger |
| HTTP methods | GET 284, POST 276, DELETE 13, PUT 7, PATCH 2 | mutation semantics are a major enterprise risk surface |
| OpenAPI version | 3.0.3 | current file is usable but needs explicit 3.1/3.2 migration or deferral rationale |
| OpenAPI paths | 556 | must reconcile against 582 method-path records and generated API docs |
| OpenAPI tags | 163 | tag count matches route groups and should be locked as an invariant |
| OpenAPI schemas | 19 | schema depth is too shallow for a 582-route product if generated SDKs are expected to be trusted |
| OpenAPI security schemes | 2 | bearer/API key modes exist but route-level security requirements need per-operation proof |

| readiness requirements | 57 total, 8 groups | route copy and account/docs UI must expose open readiness states |
| readiness closeout open requirements | 8 | package, benchmark, certification, and partner gates must block API/docs overclaims |
| `src/router.js` size | 953,495 bytes / 20,688 lines | router monolith remains a maintainability and review risk |
| `src/router.js` envelope references | 389 | envelope discipline exists but needs route-wide conformance proof |
| `src/router.js` problem/status/detail references | 2,521 | error/status handling is widespread and must be standardized |
| `src/router.js` debt markers | 37 TODO/FIXME/stub/placeholder/mock matches | debt terms in the router must be classified as test fixture, honest stub, or unresolved backend defect |
| `src/envelope.js` | 5,151 bytes, 179 lines, 5 exports | central envelope helper exists and should become mandatory route infrastructure |
| `src/product-kernel.js` | 8,916 bytes, 240 lines, 16 exports | product graph vocabulary exists and should bind API route ownership |
| `src/oauth.js` | 7,775 bytes, 200 lines, 3 GET routes | auth routes are separate source and must remain in API route inventory |

Current top stub groups from `public/docs/api-routes.json`:

| group | stubs | examples | V3 priority |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `ab-tests` | 6 | `GET /v1/ab-tests`, `POST /v1/ab-tests/:id/promote` | experimentation claims and auto-rollback must stay scoped until real |
| `cloud` | 5 | `POST /v1/cloud/broker`, `POST /v1/cloud/deploy-plan` | hosted/BYOC/cloud claims depend on real provider/job behavior |
| `approvals` | 5 | `GET /v1/approvals`, `POST /v1/approvals/:id/approve` | enterprise governance cannot be decorative |
| `capture` | 4 | Gemini capture and capture RBAC policy/evaluate | first product promise depends on provider/RBAC capture parity |
| `drift` | 4 | alerts, auto-remediate, configure, status | reliability/autonomous monitoring copy must stay scoped |
| `autopilot` | 4 | disable, savings, status, tick | automation must prove safety, opt-out, and cost math |
| `marketplace` | 3 | listings and reviews | network-effect claims require trust/moderation behavior |
| `lake` | 3 | contribute, opt-out, trends | data network claims require consent/deletion/privacy behavior |
| `audio`, `video`, `xlang` | 3 each | bakeoff/captures/tokenize/per-language eval | multimodal claims require fixture and runtime proof |
| `compliance`, `security`, `residency`, `redteam`, `poisoning` | 3 each | governance/security/residency routes | trust claims must not outrun backend implementation |

The route inventory is broad enough to support the product ambition. It is not yet a completion proof. V3 requires a generated route contract ledger that makes every gap explicit and prevents public/docs/account/CLI copy from implying stubbed behavior is shipped.

##### Node 2.7 External API Baseline V3

| baseline | source | Kolm API requirement |
|---|---|---|
| HTTP semantics define methods, status codes, fields, content negotiation, authentication, and caching | RFC 9110: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110 | every route contract must declare method semantics, content type, status codes, auth, cache behavior, and safe/idempotent behavior |
| Problem Details defines a machine-readable error format for HTTP APIs and the `application/problem+json` media type | RFC 9457: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457 | API errors need safe Problem Details compatibility without breaking legacy Kolm envelopes |
| OpenAPI provides a language-agnostic HTTP API description that lets humans and computers understand capabilities without source inspection | OpenAPI 3.2.0: https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.2.0.html | OpenAPI must be complete enough for docs, SDK generation, route diffing, examples, security, and tests |
| OWASP API Security Top 10 2023 emphasizes broken object/function/property authorization, authentication, resource consumption, sensitive flows, SSRF, misconfiguration, inventory, and unsafe consumption | OWASP API Security Top 10 2023: https://owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x00-header/ | route matrix must prove object-level, property-level, and function-level authorization for tenant-owned and admin resources |

These baselines make node `2` a contract problem, not just a code problem. The API is the product boundary for external users, SDKs, account UI, CLI, TUI, docs, and production smoke.

##### Node 2.8 Route Finality Contract V3

Every API route must generate a record in `docs/api-route-finality-ledger.json`:

```json
{
  "schema_id": "kolm.api_route_finality.v3",
  "tree_node": 2,
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/v1/distill/run",
  "route_group": "distill",
  "source": {
    "file": "src/router.js",
    "line": 12345,
    "handler_id": null
  },
  "status": "implemented|stub|external-gated|internal-only|deprecated|remove-candidate",
  "owner_nodes": [1, 2, 7, 8, 10, 28, 29, 37, 43],
  "product_surface": "model-compiler-distiller",
  "readiness_scope": "shipped|implemented|package-pending|benchmark-pending|certification-pending|partner-pending|external-gated",
  "http": {
    "safe": false,
    "idempotent": "native|idempotency-key|required|not-applicable|explicitly-nonretryable",
    "cache": "no-store|private|max-age|not-applicable",
    "request_media_types": ["application/json"],
    "response_media_types": ["application/json", "application/problem+json"],
    "success_statuses": [200],
    "error_statuses": [400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500]
  },
  "security": {
    "auth": "none|api-key|session|admin|signed-webhook|local-daemon|mixed",
    "tenant_required": true,
    "rbac": [],
    "object_authorization_tested": false,
    "property_filtering_tested": false,
    "function_authorization_tested": false,
    "secret_values_included": false
  },
  "behavior": {
    "request_schema": null,
    "response_schema": null,
    "problem_details": false,
    "kolm_envelope": true,
    "request_id": false,
    "pagination": "none|required|bounded|cursor|offset",
    "rate_limit": "none|required|implemented",
    "retry_after_on_429": false,
    "timeout": "none|required|implemented",
    "cancellation": "none|required|implemented",
    "audit_event": "none|required|implemented",
    "rollback_or_compensation": "none|required|implemented"
  },
  "parity": {
    "openapi_operation_id": null,
    "openapi_schema_complete": false,
    "api_docs_complete": false,
    "sdk_method": null,
    "account_page": null,
    "cli_command": null,
    "tui_view": null
  },
  "proof": {
    "unit_tests": [],
    "integration_tests": [],
    "negative_tests": [],
    "local_smoke": null,
    "production_smoke": null,
    "last_verified": null
  }
}
```

This ledger must be generated from route source, `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/openapi.json`, product graph, product surfaces, account pages, CLI/TUI manifests, docs, and tests. Handwritten confidence is not acceptable.

##### Node 2.9 API Consumer Experience Contract V3

API finality has to include the people and programs using the API:

| consumer | required API behavior |
|---|---|
| public docs | complete examples for request, success, validation error, auth error, rate limit, and server/provider error |
| SDKs | stable operation IDs, schemas, auth metadata, pagination/idempotency helpers, and local fixture smoke |
| account UI | one envelope/error parser, request ID display, auth-expired handling, permission handling, retry, and no secret leakage |
| CLI | stable exit codes, JSON output, request ID on failure, `--json` parity, and no raw secrets |
| TUI | recoverable errors, stream parser state, loading/progress, cancellation, and request IDs |
| enterprise auditors | route auth matrix, audit events, data classes, retention/export behavior, and certification/closeout scope |
| support/operators | request ID, trace ID, tenant ID, route group, error code, safe payload, and runbook link |
| external integrators | OpenAPI, compatibility policy, deprecation notices, versioning, examples, and rate-limit semantics |

If any consumer needs a special-case parser for one route, that route must either document the media-type exception or be repaired.

##### Node 2.10 Stub Scope And Claim Blocker V3

The `127` current stub routes must be treated as first-class blockers, not embarrassment to hide. Each stub needs:

1. route group and source line;
2. owner node and product surface;
3. public/account/docs/CLI pages that mention the capability;
4. whether the stub should be implemented, removed, internalized, or externally gated;
5. expected response shape while stubbed;
6. claim blocker text that prevents marketing/docs overclaim;
7. implementation ticket or removal ticket;
8. local smoke behavior;
9. production behavior;
10. date reviewed and owner.

The highest-impact closeout order is:

| order | group | reason |
|---:|---|---|
| 1 | `capture` | gateway/capture is the first public promise and feeds the distill data loop |
| 2 | `cloud` | BYOC/cloud deployment and hosted distill claims depend on it |
| 3 | `approvals` | enterprise governance cannot ship as a static workflow |
| 4 | `drift` and `autopilot` | reliability automation is dangerous if fake or unbounded |
| 5 | `ab-tests` | experimentation/promote/rollback must be statistically and operationally real |
| 6 | `marketplace`, `registry`, `runtime` | ecosystem/network-effect claims require trust and publisher behavior |
| 7 | `compliance`, `security`, `residency` | regulated claims require hard scope and evidence |
| 8 | `audio`, `video`, `xlang`, multimodal-adjacent groups | multimodal claims require fixture-backed runtime/eval behavior |

##### Node 2.11 OpenAPI And Route Count Reconciliation V3

Current generated counts are not self-explanatory:

| artifact | current count |
|---|---:|
| route records | 582 |
| route groups | 163 |
| OpenAPI paths | 556 |
| OpenAPI tags | 163 |
| product graph routes | 582 |
| product graph API routes | 69 |

This is acceptable only if a generated diff explains the count relationships. Node `2` needs `reports/api-contract/<date>/route-openapi-reconciliation.json` with:

- routes grouped by method/path/group/source;
- unique path count;
- OpenAPI path count;
- OpenAPI operation count;
- missing operation IDs;
- routes intentionally excluded from OpenAPI;
- OpenAPI operations not present in source inventory;
- stub inclusion/exclusion policy;
- product-graph and product-surface ownership;
- docs/API reference freshness;
- SDK generation coverage.

Without that report, a green OpenAPI test is indirect evidence, not completion proof.

##### Node 2 V3 Relax Redline

Node `2` V3 is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. all 582 route records have generated finality records;
2. all 127 stubs are implemented, removed, internalized, externally gated, or listed in a closeout ledger with claim blockers;
3. route inventory, OpenAPI, product graph, product surfaces, API docs, account calls, CLI/TUI, and SDKs have no unexplained drift;
4. OpenAPI has complete operation IDs, useful schemas, security metadata, examples, error responses, and client-generation proof;
5. errors support safe Problem Details compatibility and legacy envelopes without leaking stack traces, provider internals, or secrets;
6. request IDs propagate through API, account, CLI, TUI, logs, audit, and support flows;
7. every sensitive route has auth, tenant fence, object/property/function authorization, rate limit, and negative tests;
8. every mutation has audit, idempotency or explicit non-retryable semantics, replay behavior, and rollback or compensation notes;
9. every list route has pagination or a documented bounded maximum;
10. every expensive route has timeout, cancellation, retry, job status, and provider-error mapping;
11. API consumer parity is proven for docs, SDKs, account UI, CLI, TUI, and support/operator workflows;
12. local and production API smoke prove the critical route classes on the same commit before any backend-complete claim.

### Security, Auth, Privacy, And Compliance Ledger V1

This branch spans master spec tree nodes `0`, `2`, `7`, `8`, and `9`: brand truth, API/backend control plane, account product surface, docs/education, and verification/release. Security is not a single page or route group. For Kolm, it is the promise that captured model traffic, compiled artifacts, distillation data, device deployment, enterprise tenancy, and compliance evidence can be trusted without hiding unfinished work.

#### Current Security Surface Inventory

| surface | current scan result | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| security/auth/privacy/compliance route groups matched | 96 | security-relevant behavior is distributed across the product, so a single `/security` page cannot be the source of truth |
| security/auth/privacy/compliance method-path matches | 218 | create an auth/security route matrix with owner, auth mode, tenant isolation, audit event, data class, and UI entrypoint |
| matched stubs | 23 | every security-related stub blocks enterprise trust claims unless explicitly scoped as a closeout gate |
| public HTML pages containing security/trust/privacy/compliance terms | 729 | every public page can affect trust; claim-scope lint must cover the whole site, not only trust pages |
| account pages containing security/trust/privacy/compliance terms | 48 | post-auth security UX must be operational, not decorative |
| account pages with no `kfetch` under this scan | 19 | security/account pages without live data need review for static-shell risk |
| source/scripts/apps/workers files scanned for security terms | 792 | security ownership must include backend, scripts, workers, apps, and CI |
| files with security-term matches | 628 | the security surface is broad enough to require an evidence graph, not manual confidence |
| possible key-shaped literals flagged | 10 files | classify each as fake/test/example or real; rotate and purge if real; never publish literal values in docs |
| workflow files | 11 | SBOM and npm audit exist; CodeQL/Semgrep/gitleaks/Scorecard-style gates were not obvious in workflow names or grep hits |

#### Security-Critical Route Groups

| route group | matched routes | matched stubs | current read |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `account` | 14 | 2 | audit log, compliance package, account export/delete, keys, receipt-secret prune, SSO configure/status; SSO stubs must be scoped |
| `teams` | 12 | 0 | team CRUD, invites, membership, roles; must prove tenant isolation and function-level authorization |
| `team` | 11 | 0 | older team routes and approvals; must prove no drift from `teams` contract |
| `admin` | 9 | 0 | tenant/admin/diagnostics routes need strict admin auth, audit, and no sensitive debug leakage |
| `compliance` | 7 | 3 | AI Act governance, human-in-loop, and risk-score are stubbed; public claims must stay scoped to packet/template/export where implemented |
| `privacy` | 7 | 0 | events, policy, redaction benchmark, reports, scans, tests; must connect to account UX and benchmark evidence |
| `audit` | 5 | 2 | export exists but formats/preview are stubbed; enterprise audit export copy must be scoped |
| `keys` | 5 | 0 | public key challenge/register/delete/get; must prove rotation, prefix deletion safety, and audit events |
| `redteam` | 4 | 3 | classifies but corpus/bakeoff/sanitize are stubbed; LLM-security claims need closeout |
| `residency` | 4 | 3 | tag-capture exists but configure/regions/capture-region are stubbed; data residency claims are not complete |
| `security` | 4 | 3 | SOC2 checklist exists but audit-retention, continuous-monitoring snapshot, and ISO controls are stubbed |
| `scim` | 3 | 2 | service provider config exists but Users list/create are stubbed; enterprise SCIM must not be marketed as full |
