#### Node 11 V2 Core AI Capability, Compiler Quality, And Build-Redline Addendum

This V2 addendum keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` remains unused because node `11` already owns the actual AI product capability quality. Node `11` is where Kolm stops being a well-presented AI website and becomes a reproducible product: capture traffic, turn it into owned evidence, distill or compile that evidence into artifacts, evaluate those artifacts, optimize them for a runtime or device, and prove the result.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not modify compiler code, distillation code, tests, public pages, generated route docs, model registries, workers, Python trainer code, or account surfaces while other implementation agents are active.

Current repo evidence checked for this pass:

| Evidence class | Current evidence | Node `11` completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| AI/product-capability API routes | A route scan matched 236 routes across 94 route groups, with 38 matched stubs. | The product surface is broad enough that capability quality must be generated as a control graph. Route existence is not proof of capability. |
| Top matched route groups | `capture` 14/4 stubs, `distill` 11/1, `devices` 10/0, `eval` 9/0, `audio` 8/3, `multimodal` 8/0, `runtime` 8/2, `cloud` 7/3, `models` 6/1, `video` 5/3, `compile` 4/0, `artifact` 3/2, `quantization` 3/1, `speculative` 3/2, `vision` 3/2. | The highest product risk is mixed maturity: core routes exist, but stubs remain exactly where state-of-the-art claims are tempting. |
| Core capability modules sampled | 19 modules, 430,454 bytes, 10,440 lines, 135 debt-token matches across compiler, IR, distill, model registry, quantization, K-Score, benchmark evidence, runtime policy, multimodal capture, workers, and Python trainer. | The codebase has the shape of a serious AI platform, but completion requires targeted debt burn-down and proof artifacts for each capability lane. |
| Capability tests | 103 matched tests, 1,513,888 bytes. Coverage includes compile, distill, K-Score, quantization oracle, runtime dispatch, model registry, multimodal, tenant holdout, capture provider honesty, frontier models, Gemma scope, speculative teacher, redaction benchmark, calibration, and wave 711-826 invention work. | Test breadth is strong; the missing proof is end-to-end capability certification by journey, model family, modality, runtime, and deployment target. |
| Product readiness | `docs/product-sota-readiness.json` marks K-Score calibration, distill strategy, incremental compile, compile diagnostics, async compile/webhooks, compile cache, export/quantize targets, runtime local artifact, runtime edge, runtime telemetry, and runtime LTS as shipped or implemented. | These can be used in copy only when route, CLI, account, docs, benchmark, and production smoke agree on the same scope. |
| Open capability gates | `benchmarking-infra` is still `needs_public_benchmark_data`; `ecosystem-runtime-adoption` is still `needs_external_partner`; WASM/mobile/package gates are package-release gated. | Public claims must not say "best", "leaderboard proven", "runs anywhere", or "native ecosystem support" without the external proofs named in the closeout ledger. |
| Invention portfolio | `docs/product-invention-portfolio.json` defines inventions such as Kolm-Q Oracle Quantizer, KolmIR AutoTarget Compiler, Distill Forge, Adaptive Runtime Router, Evidence Lattice, K-Score Public Standard, Device Fit Oracle, and Codegraph Product Index. | These are additive architecture targets, not shipped performance claims. Node `11` must convert each high-value invention into implementation contracts and acceptance gates. |
| Package scripts | `verify:depth` now chains invention, frontier, operator-kernel, benchmark, K-Score, quality calibration, redaction benchmark, quantization oracle, cloud broker, distill strategy, SOTA, claim-scope, and brand gates. | Verification is becoming serious. Finality still requires that each green gate maps to a user-visible capability and that any simulated gate is labeled. |

##### Node 11 V2 External Capability Baselines

These sources define the level of engineering seriousness expected before Kolm claims compiler, distillation, quantization, runtime, or deployment leadership.

| Capability area | Source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| Compiler IR | MLIR LangRef: `https://mlir.llvm.org/docs/LangRef/` | KolmIR must have typed operations, verifier, textual dump, parser/serializer, pass pipeline, target-lowering contracts, and filecheck-style fixtures. A JSON artifact manifest is not enough. |
| ML compiler/runtime | IREE: `https://iree.dev/` | "Run anywhere" must prove AOT packaging, runtime target constraints, device/accelerator coverage, binary/resource budgets, benchmark reports, and failure semantics. |
| Graph optimization | TVM Relax: `https://tvm.apache.org/docs/deep_dive/relax/index.html` | Compiler reports must expose graph-level transforms, dataflow boundaries, pattern rewrites, fusion/decomposition, external codegen, dynamic-shape decisions, and correctness checks. |
| Portable generative runtime | ONNX Runtime GenAI: `https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/` | Export/runtime proof must include tokenizer, preprocessing, logits processing, search/sampling, KV cache handling, structured output/tool-calling limits, and execution-provider compatibility. |
| Distillation | Hinton, Vinyals, Dean, `https://arxiv.org/abs/1503.02531` | Distill product UX must show teacher, student, temperature/objective, data split, holdout, loss, quality gates, and deployment benefit. |
| Quantization | GPTQ, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.17323`; AWQ, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.00978`; SmoothQuant, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.10438` | Quantization cannot be a dropdown. Kolm must prove calibration data, activation sensitivity, task loss, bit allocation, runtime kernel support, quality delta, latency delta, and rollback. |
| KV cache and serving | vLLM/PagedAttention, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.06180` | Runtime placement must track prompt length, batch, KV memory, fragmentation, scheduler policy, eviction/compression, long-context degradation, and latency-throughput curves. |
| Speculative inference | Speculative decoding, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.17192`; Medusa, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.10774` | Student-as-draft claims need acceptance rate, exactness/distribution scope, verifier behavior, rollback, target model cost, and quality invariance tests. |
| NVIDIA GPU inference | TensorRT-LLM docs: `https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/` | NVIDIA runtime support must state engine build path, supported models, precision modes, plugins, batching, KV cache, benchmarks, and deployment constraints. |

##### Node 11 V2 Capability Contract

Every AI capability must be expressible as a contract before it is marketed or surfaced as complete:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-ai-capability-contract-v2",
  "capability": "distill|compile|quantize|runtime|capture|eval|multimodal|device|cloud",
  "node": "11",
  "claim": "one_sentence_user_outcome",
  "scope": {
    "implemented_status": "shipped|implemented|partial|external_gated|benchmark_gated|package_gated",
    "model_families": [],
    "modalities": ["text"],
    "providers": [],
    "runtime_targets": [],
    "device_classes": [],
    "cloud_targets": [],
    "unsupported_cases": []
  },
  "inputs": {
    "fixtures": [],
    "datasets": [],
    "capture_sources": [],
    "holdouts": [],
    "calibration_rows": 0,
    "privacy_policy": "required_when_user_data"
  },
  "execution": {
    "pipeline": [],
    "ir_version": "required_when_compile",
    "worker": "required_when_async",
    "resource_budget": {
      "cpu": null,
      "gpu": null,
      "memory": null,
      "storage": null,
      "network": null
    },
    "real_vs_simulated": "must_be_explicit"
  },
  "outputs": {
    "artifact": "required_when_compile_or_distill",
    "signature": "required",
    "provenance": "required",
    "model_card": "required_when_model_output",
    "runtime_bundle": "required_when_export",
    "diagnostic_report": "required"
  },
  "quality": {
    "k_score": "required",
    "axis_scores": [],
    "baseline": "required",
    "confidence_interval": "required_for_benchmark_claims",
    "failure_cases": "required",
    "rollback_threshold": "required"
  },
  "economics": {
    "latency_delta": "required_when_perf_claimed",
    "cost_delta": "required_when_savings_claimed",
    "memory_delta": "required_when_device_claimed",
    "energy_delta": "required_when_efficiency_claimed"
  },
  "proof": {
    "unit_tests": [],
    "integration_tests": [],
    "benchmark_reports": [],
    "cli_commands": [],
    "api_routes": [],
    "account_pages": [],
    "docs": [],
    "production_smoke": []
  }
}
```

##### Node 11 V2 Build-Redline By Capability

| Capability | Required proof before complete | Current completion risk |
|---|---|---|
| Capture and route | Provider parity fixtures, streaming capture, redaction, capture RBAC, poisoning detection, review queue, capture-to-dataset promotion, and OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter/Gemini-compatible/self-hosted proof. | `capture` has 14 matched routes with 4 stubs; the story cannot rest on OpenAI-style traffic alone. |
| Distillation | Teacher council, TAAS, curriculum, contrastive/reasoning trace/progressive/importance-weighted paths, holdout independence, quality gates, failure diagnostics, cost quote, compute target, and artifact output. | Distill tests and strategy code exist, but public superiority depends on benchmark data and real-vs-simulated labels. |
| Compilation | Typed IR, parser/dump, verifier, pass pipeline, target lowering, dependency graph, artifact manifest, signature, provenance, reproducibility, diff, rollback, and explainable failure report. | `src/compile-pipeline.js` and `src/compile-ir.js` still carry the highest sampled debt counts. |
| K-Score and eval | Public methodology, calibration suite, task axes, confidence intervals, human preference correlation, judge calibration, holdout leakage detection, and benchmark evidence. | K-Score is implemented locally, but public claims must stay tied to calibration and benchmark evidence. |
| Quantization | Task/device/runtime-aware method selection, calibration rows, activation/weight sensitivity, bit allocation, kernel support, accuracy delta, latency/memory delta, and rollback to safer precision. | `src/quantization-oracle.js` exists with no sampled debt, but quantization marketing still needs benchmark deltas and runtime-kernel proof. |
| Runtime inference | Runtime policy, fallback chains, local/hosted/browser/edge/device support, streaming scope, KV cache accounting, resource budgets, telemetry, and deterministic receipt behavior. | Runtime surface exists, but external runtime adoption and package-release gates remain non-local. |
| Device/fleet | Detect, recommend, install, test, uninstall, resource warning, hardware class matrix, privacy/offline mode, and device-specific failure handling. | Device routes are comparatively mature, but proof must include fixture devices or honest simulation labels. |
| Multimodal | Image/audio/video/PDF/code sidecars, redaction, tokenization doctor, provenance, modality-specific eval, and artifact compile readiness. | Multimodal routes exist, while audio/video/vision groups still show stubs around bakeoff/captures/doctors. |
| Cloud/BYOC | Object storage, GPU readiness, compute broker, deploy plan, managed train, remote SSH, BYOC attestation, cancellation, metering, logs, and secret-free readiness. | Cloud matched routes still include stubs and local env may lack live credentials. |
| Speculative/runtime acceleration | Acceptance rate, exactness or distribution-scope statement, student draft quality, verifier behavior, fallback/rollback, latency/cost curves, and target compatibility. | Speculative route group has 3 matched routes with 2 stubs. Claims must stay scoped. |

##### Node 11 V2 Proof Classes

| Proof class | Meaning | Required for |
|---|---|---|
| `fixture-proven` | Deterministic fixture proves the code path. | Every capability. |
| `real-run-proven` | A non-simulated run produced an artifact/report on declared hardware or provider. | Distill, compile, quantize, cloud, device, runtime claims. |
| `simulation-labeled` | Simulated/local-only proof is explicitly labeled and cannot support external claims. | Any mock, dry-run, local-only, or synthetic proof. |
| `model-family-proven` | The capability is proven for named model families with source/version/license. | Model registry, distill, quantization, runtime, device fit. |
| `modality-proven` | Text/image/audio/video/PDF/code paths have independent fixtures and failures. | Multimodal claims. |
| `target-proven` | Runtime/device/cloud target has install, export, run, verify, telemetry, and rollback proof. | Run-anywhere claims. |
| `quality-proven` | K-Score/eval/baseline/holdout/failure cases are attached. | Distill, compile, eval, benchmark, registry ranking. |
| `economics-proven` | Latency, cost, memory, or energy deltas are measured against a named baseline. | ROI, cost savings, performance, device claims. |
| `privacy-proven` | Redaction, consent, data class, retention, and audit proof are attached. | Capture, privacy lake, healthcare, enterprise claims. |
| `production-smoked` | Deployed origin proves the path with env-provided credentials and secret-free output. | Public launch claims. |
| `externally-benchmarked` | Public raw reports, command lines, model versions, hardware, and date exist. | Best-in-class, leaderboard, superiority claims. |

##### Node 11 V2 Additive Invention Intake

The invention backlog is useful only when it becomes implementation-grade. New inventions must attach to node `11` or a sibling node and satisfy this intake:

1. name the exact capability gap it improves;
2. name current files, new files, tests, scripts, routes, CLI/TUI/account/docs surfaces, and generated artifacts;
3. specify the metric lift target and the baseline used;
4. include a synthetic smoke test and a real-run promotion path;
5. state privacy, security, compliance, and cost constraints;
6. define rollback and failure diagnostics;
7. include red-team or adverse-case fixtures;
8. state what marketing copy is allowed before public benchmarks exist.

The highest additive inventions for node `11` are:

| Invention | Product lift | Implementation target |
|---|---|---|
| KolmIR AutoTarget Compiler | Makes "compile once, run anywhere" concrete. | Typed IR, target lowering, pass manifest, export reports, target conformance suite, and account compile report. |
| Distill Forge | Makes "distill top models into owned artifacts" measurable. | Teacher council, data selection, holdout gates, K-Score lift per dollar, failure diagnostics, artifact lineage, and model card. |
| Kolm-Q Oracle Quantizer | Makes device deployment and cost reduction credible. | Mixed precision planner, calibration sensitivity, runtime kernel matrix, quality/latency/memory curves, and rollback. |
| Device Fit Oracle | Makes "enterprise models down to any device" actionable. | Model/runtime/device recommendation, resource prediction, install/test plan, offline/privacy mode, and failure warnings. |
| K-Score Public Standard | Makes quality claims defensible. | Public methodology, calibration data, raw reports, confidence intervals, human preference correlation, and update cadence. |
| Adaptive Runtime Router | Makes routing and optimization operational. | Provider/runtime cost/latency/quality policy, confidence-aware routing, fallback chains, and audit/telemetry proof. |

##### Node 11 V2 Relax Redline

Node `11` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. every AI capability claim maps to a capability contract and proof class;
2. every product-function stub in capture, distill, audio, video, vision, runtime, cloud, artifact, model-card, quantization, speculative, registry, and related groups is implemented or visibly scoped;
3. every simulated, dry-run, synthetic, local-only, benchmark-gated, package-gated, or external-partner path is machine-readable and blocked from public "shipped" claims;
4. compiler IR has schema, parser, serializer, verifier, textual dump, pass manifest, target-lowering report, and deterministic fixtures;
5. distillation has teacher/student/task/data/holdout/eval/cost/latency/privacy diagnostics and can produce a signed artifact or an honest refusal;
6. K-Score has public methodology, calibration, confidence intervals, failure cases, and benchmark provenance before it is used as a public yardstick;
7. quantization reports include calibration rows, method choice, activation/weight sensitivity, device/runtime compatibility, quality delta, latency delta, memory delta, and rollback precision;
8. runtime/device/cloud paths prove run, verify, telemetry, failure, and rollback on declared target classes or explicitly say simulated;
9. multimodal paths prove sidecar generation, redaction, tokenization doctor, modality eval, and compile readiness;
10. speculative/runtime acceleration claims prove acceptance rate, output-equivalence or distribution scope, rollback, and measured latency/cost benefits;
11. public claims about best-in-class quality, cost reduction, any-device deployment, leaderboard status, or native ecosystem support are backed by the external proof class required by the closeout ledger;
12. account UI, docs, API, CLI, TUI, OpenAPI, screenshots, and production smoke all tell the same capability story.

If any item fails, Kolm may still be a serious in-progress product, but node `11` cannot be called 100 percent complete.

#### Node 11 V3 Three-Product AI Capability, Benchmark, And Runtime Proof Contract

This pass stays inside node `11`. Node `11` is the capability proof branch for the three real product promises:

1. route and capture model traffic into owned evidence;
2. compile, distill, evaluate, quantize, and sign owned artifacts;
3. run those artifacts across hosted, cloud, local, browser, mobile, edge, and air-gapped targets.

The V3 correction is that Kolm cannot be judged by whether individual files or tests exist. The product is only complete when each promise has a user-visible capability packet: source data, route/API path, account path, CLI/TUI path, docs path, model family, runtime target, benchmark/eval proof, artifact output, and limitations.

##### Node 11.0 Current Capability Evidence Delta V3

| evidence lane | current result | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| AI capability source files sampled | `router.js`, `artifact.js`, `model-registry.js`, `spec-compile.js`, `distill-pipeline.js`, `compile-pipeline.js`, `artifact-runner.js`, `dataset-workbench.js`, `compile-ir.js`, `devices.js`, `quantization-oracle.js`, `distill-strategy.js`, `kscore-calibration.js`, `quality-calibration.js`, and many modality/runtime files are present | the product has serious capability surface area; finality requires capability packets, not isolated module presence |
| matched capability tests | over 100 focused capability tests exist across capture, compile, distill, K-Score, quantization, runtime, devices, multimodal, model registry, cloud, poisoning, and calibration | breadth is strong; V3 requires mapping each test to claim, journey, model family, and runtime target |
| focused compile/distill/runtime/device/quant/distill-strategy tests | 102/102 passed in the current run | local functional contracts are good for selected lanes |
| `verify:kscore-calibration` | 18/18 passed | K-Score calibration contract is active locally, but public yardstick claims still need public benchmark and human-preference correlation evidence |
| `verify:redaction-benchmark` | ok with synthetic fixture totals `tp=9`, `fp=0`, `fn=0`, precision/recall/F1 all `1`; tests 7/7 passed | strong harness proof for synthetic public fixture; not broad real-world PHI/PII superiority proof |
| `verify:quant-oracle` | ok for extraction on `rtx-4090-24gb`, primary recommendation `awq` score `0.9395`, fallback `exl2`, with GPTQ/HQQ/int4/int8 candidates and explicit external/runtime warnings | quantization planning is implemented; actual quantized artifact promotion still needs worker execution, calibration hash, holdout eval, runtime kernel proof, and benchmark deltas |
| `verify:distill-strategy` | ok in simulated Anthropic generation scenario; recommendation `kd_softmax`; command emitted for `Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct` | strategy oracle is implemented, but simulated teacher evidence is not a completed distillation run |
| source debt concentration | `src/compile-pipeline.js`: 45,233 bytes, 986 lines, 73 debt-token matches; `src/compile-ir.js`: 35,369 bytes, 783 lines, 37 debt-token matches; `src/distill-pipeline.js`: 55,081 bytes, 1,157 lines, 16 debt-token matches | compiler leadership cannot be claimed until debt is triaged into fixed, intentional, or externally gated states |
| mature zero-debt sampled modules | `runtime-policy.js`, `devices.js`, `quantization-oracle.js`, `distill-strategy.js`, `kscore-calibration.js`, and `quality-calibration.js` showed zero sampled debt tokens | good candidates for capability packet promotion after route/docs/account/production evidence binds to them |
| readiness ledger | `compile-train-distill` has implemented/shipped K-Score, distill strategy, incremental compile, diagnostics, async compile, cache, export/quantize; `runtime-compute` has shipped local artifact, implemented edge/cloud broker/telemetry/LTS; runtime WASM, mobile SDK, SDK depth, install remain package-gated | implementation breadth is real, but run-anywhere claims remain bounded by package and external adoption gates |
| external/open gates | `foundation-standardization` and `ecosystem-runtime-adoption` need external partners; `runtime-wasm`, `ios-android-sdk`, `sdk-depth`, `one-line-install` need package release; `benchmarking-infra` needs public benchmark data | public copy must avoid "standard", "native ecosystem support", "one-line install", and benchmark superiority until these gates close |

##### Node 11.1 Current Product Capability Map V3

| promise | current proof signals | current redline |
|---|---|---|
| route/capture model traffic | capture, capture analytics, capture store, capture importance, OpenRouter helper, provider honesty tests, tenant/media capture tests, runtime policy, privacy membrane | provider parity, capture RBAC, streaming/media capture, poisoning quarantine, privacy-lake opt-out/deletion, and production traffic smoke must be bound into one packet |
| compile/distill owned artifacts | distill strategy, distill pipeline, teacher council, progressive/contrastive/reasoning/curriculum tests, K-Score calibration, quality calibration, quantization oracle, compile pipeline, compile IR, artifact runner | simulated strategy/oracle output cannot count as artifact output; compiler debt and public benchmark gates block state-of-art claims |
| run anywhere | devices matrix, runtime policy, runtime placement, artifact runner, device-capabilities, runtime adoption packets, object storage readiness, cloud broker, package release readiness | local capability exists, but WASM/mobile/SDK/package and ecosystem adoption gates mean "anywhere" must be phrased as target matrix with status, not absolute completion |

##### Node 11.2 External Capability Baseline V3

| baseline | official source | Kolm V3 requirement |
|---|---|---|
| MLIR dialect conversion | https://mlir.llvm.org/docs/DialectConversion/ | KolmIR must declare legal targets, rewrite patterns, type conversion, partial/full conversion status, and blocked lowering reasons |
| TVM Relax | https://tvm.apache.org/docs/deep_dive/relax/index.html | Kolm compiler reports should separate behavior graph optimization from low-level tensor/runtime scheduling |
| IREE | https://iree.dev/ | run-anywhere proof should distinguish compile target, runtime driver, package format, device constraints, and benchmark output |
| ONNX Runtime GenAI | https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/ | runtime proof must include tokenizer, generation loop, logits/search/sampling behavior, KV cache, execution provider, and structured-output limits |
| vLLM/PagedAttention | https://www.vllm.ai/ and https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.06180 | serving proof must account for KV memory, scheduling, batching, cache sharing, throughput, and latency curves |
| NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM | https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/ | NVIDIA target proof must include engine build, precision, batching, paged attention/KV behavior, model support, and benchmark constraints |
| ExecuTorch | https://docs.pytorch.org/executorch/stable/index.html | mobile/edge proof must include export, backend delegate, packaging, resource limits, and device-run results |
| GGUF/ggml | https://github.com/ggml-org/ggml/blob/master/docs/gguf.md | local/llama.cpp-style artifact proof must include tensor metadata, tokenizer/chat-template compatibility, quantization, and model source fields |
| Ray Serve LLM | https://docs.ray.io/en/latest/serve/llm/index.html | cloud serving proof must separate OpenAI-compatible serving, autoscaling, monitoring, and distributed deployment |
| KServe | https://kserve.github.io/kserve/ | Kubernetes/BYOC proof must include inference service spec, autoscaling, model/runtime selection, and production readiness |

##### Node 11.4 Capability Acceptance Ladder V3

| level | name | acceptance |
|---:|---|---|
| 0 | `surface-present` | route/module/page/test exists but no packet binds it |
| 1 | `fixture-proven` | deterministic fixture or local test proves a narrow path |
| 2 | `planner-proven` | strategy/oracle returns correct plan with secret-safe output |
| 3 | `artifact-produced` | real command produces signed artifact/provenance/model card/report |
| 4 | `quality-proven` | K-Score/holdout/baseline/failure cases are attached |
| 5 | `runtime-proven` | artifact runs on declared runtime/device with metrics and failure behavior |
| 6 | `economic-proven` | cost/latency/memory/energy deltas are measured against named baseline |
| 7 | `journey-proven` | public/docs/account/CLI/TUI/API path takes a user from input to proof |
| 8 | `production-smoked` | deployed origin proves the path with env-provided credentials and no secret leakage |
| 9 | `externally-benchmarked` | raw public benchmark data, commands, model versions, hardware, and dates exist |
| 10 | `claim-unlocked` | public claim allowlist permits the exact phrasing because evidence class is sufficient |

Current state: K-Score/redaction have strong local fixture and API proof; distill strategy and quantization have planner proof; compile/distill/runtime/device selected tests are green; public "best", "anywhere", "industry standard", and external benchmark claims remain locked.

##### Node 11.5 Capability Product Requirements V3

| capability | required next proof |
|---|---|
| capture provider parity | one packet covering OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Gemini-compatible, self-hosted OpenAI-compatible, streaming, media, redaction, RBAC, poisoning, and production smoke |
| privacy lake to dataset | capture-to-lake-to-label-to-holdout flow with consent, delete/export, opt-out, and data class ledger |
| distill strategy | promote planner output to real-run artifact packet with teacher/source policy, data rights, K-Score, cost, latency, failure diagnostics, and rollback |
| compiler IR | reduce/triage compiler debt, add IR verifier/pass/legalization/lowering packet, and expose target-block reasons to docs/account/CLI |
| K-Score | attach public methodology, calibration version, raw reports, confidence intervals, and human-preference correlation before broad yardstick claims |
| quantization | run worker method on fixture model, record calibration hash, output hash, holdout eval, runtime kernel compatibility, and rollback precision |
| runtime policy | connect policy decisions to account UI, API, CLI, telemetry, cost/latency deltas, cache state, and privacy decision proof |
| device fit | produce per-class run or honest simulation packets for laptop CPU, NVIDIA GPU, browser/WebGPU, iOS, Android, edge, remote SSH, BYOC, and airgap |
| multimodal | each modality needs sidecar provenance, redaction, tokenization doctor, eval, compile-readiness, and unsupported-case list |
| cloud/BYOC | broker/deploy-plan/job lifecycle/storage/GPU readiness must be production-smoked with secret-free outputs before hosted claims |

| W-CORE-V3-016 | P0 | 11.6 | Add device fit evidence packets. | Each device class has detect, recommend, install/test, memory warning, runtime choice, privacy/offline state, and failure proof. |

| W-CORE-V3-020 | P0 | 11.9 | Add Node 11 completion certificate. | Every AI capability claim has packet, proof level, limitation, account/docs/CLI/API path, and release evidence. |

##### Node 11 V3 Capability Redline

Node `11` is not 100 percent complete until:

1. every AI capability has an evidence packet and exact public claim level;
2. planner-only or simulated outputs are never counted as artifact production;
3. compiler debt in `compile-pipeline` and `compile-ir` is triaged with tests or gates;
4. K-Score, quality calibration, redaction, and benchmark claims state fixture scope, calibration version, and public-data limits;
5. quantization recommendations are promoted to real worker artifacts with calibration and holdout proof before performance claims;
6. distillation produces signed artifacts or honest refusal packets with teacher/source/data/eval/cost details;
7. runtime/device/cloud claims include target matrix, package state, external adoption state, resource limits, telemetry, and failure behavior;
8. multimodal claims include per-modality sidecars, redaction, tokenization doctors, evals, and compile readiness;
9. public/account/docs/CLI/TUI/API surfaces expose the same status and limitations;
10. production smoke validates the shipped commit before any broad "state of art", "best", or "run anywhere" language.

### Frontend Design System, Conversion, Docs, And Account UX Ledger V1

This branch maintains the visible product. A technically correct backend is not enough if the first screen, nav, docs, account pages, media, pricing, and trust states feel inconsistent. For Kolm, the frontend must make the three product promises obvious in under five seconds, then let a developer or enterprise buyer prove them without reading a wall of copy.

#### Current Frontend Inventory

| surface | current scan result | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| public HTML pages | 729 | every page needs page-purpose, metadata, nav state, design tokens, and screenshot proof |
| account HTML pages | 48 | post-auth account UX must cover the whole product matrix with live data and next actions |
| docs HTML pages | 221 | docs are a product surface, not an afterthought |
| public CSS files | 20 | there are too many styling surfaces for a cohesive brand without a token/component governance plan |
| public JS files | 16 | navigation, account shell, widgets, and service worker behavior need shared regression coverage |
| raw color/function color usages in public CSS/JS/HTML scan | 16,021 | color usage must be consolidated into semantic tokens, with contrast verification |
| CSS variable references | 34,268 | token usage exists, but raw color count shows incomplete normalization |
| `!important` usages | 4,560 | excessive specificity overrides signal design-system drift |
| hover selector usages | 1,539 | every hover affordance needs keyboard/touch equivalent |
| focus selector usages | 361 | focus states exist but must be audited for every interactive component |
| reduced-motion references | 62 | motion preference support exists but must be complete for all animation-heavy surfaces |
| old `Kolmogorov` brand matches in public CSS/JS/HTML scan | 1,924 | brand cleanup is not done until zero live public matches remain outside intentional historical docs |

| issue | current count | completion rule |

| missing meta descriptions | 0 | maintain zero |
| descriptions under 80 chars | 57 | rewrite or explicitly exempt utility pages |
| descriptions over 170 chars | 143 | shorten to useful search/social snippets |
| pages with H1 count not equal to 1 | 2 | every public page must have one clear H1, with exceptions documented |
| `<img>` without `alt` | 0 | maintain zero when real product media is added |
| video or iframe media elements | 0 | homepage/product/pricing/docs/account demos still lack product media proof |
| buttons missing explicit `type` | 765 | every non-submit button must declare `type=\"button\"`; submit/reset must be intentional |
| pages with buttons missing explicit `type` | 571 | button audit must become a gate, especially for forms and account tools |
| pages with inline styles | 437 | move reusable styling into tokens/components and leave only generated/prose exceptions |
| pages with inline SVG | 667 | use one icon strategy and accessible naming; SVG sprawl makes state consistency hard |
| mojibake/corruption pages | 0 | maintain zero; prior separator corruption proves this must stay gated |
| account pages without `kfetch` | 15 | static account shells must either fetch live state or be demoted to docs/examples |
| pages with old brand matches | 670 | zero live `Kolmogorov` public matches is a hard clean-git requirement |

#### Official Frontend Quality Standards

| standard | source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C says WCAG 2.2 covers recommendations for making web content more accessible across devices and disabilities: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/ | all public, docs, account, and interactive widget routes need WCAG 2.2 AA-oriented checks, including focus, target size, labels, contrast, status messages, and accessible authentication |
| WCAG 2.2 new criteria | W3C notes WCAG 2.2 adds criteria including focus not obscured, dragging movements, target size, consistent help, redundant entry, and accessible authentication: https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/ | account forms, auth/security pages, calculators, demos, and nav menus must not regress on target size, focus, or repeated data entry |
| Core Web Vitals | Google Search Central says good user experience targets include LCP within 2.5 seconds and uses LCP, INP, and CLS for page experience: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals | every template family needs lab and field-style budgets for LCP, INP, CLS, JS weight, CSS weight, fonts, and media dimensions |
| Lighthouse | Chrome docs state Lighthouse audits performance, accessibility, progressive web apps, SEO, and more, and can run from DevTools, command line, or as a Node module: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse | release proof must include Lighthouse or equivalent checks for representative public, docs, account, and demo pages |
| Google title links | Google says every page should have a specified title and title text should be descriptive and concise, avoiding vague or unnecessarily long text: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link | page title generator must reject overly long, vague, duplicate, stale-brand, or product-inaccurate titles |
| Google snippets | Google Search Central guidance for snippets and meta descriptions applies to search result descriptions: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/snippet | meta descriptions should be unique, useful, and scoped to current product truth |
| Google structured data gallery | Google says structured data helps Search understand content and can enable rich results: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/guides/search-gallery | docs/articles/case studies/product pages need valid, appropriate JSON-LD and no stale claims |
| W3C image alt decision tree | W3C WAI gives a decision tree for choosing alt text depending on whether an image is functional, informative, complex, redundant, or decorative: https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/images/decision-tree/ | new product screenshots, diagrams, videos, and media assets need alt/caption/transcript policy from the moment they are added |
| WAI-ARIA disclosure pattern | WAI-ARIA APG defines disclosure as a button that toggles content visibility using Enter/Space and `aria-expanded`: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ | nav popouts and docs/account disclosure controls should use disclosure semantics unless they genuinely need complex menubar behavior |
| WAI-ARIA menubar caution | WAI-ARIA APG cautions that disclosure is better suited for most web site navigation than the complex menubar pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menubar/examples/menubar-navigation/ | Kolm nav should not imitate desktop-app menus unless keyboard behavior is fully implemented and tested |
| MDN button behavior | MDN documents that `button` defaults to submit when no type is specified in form-associated contexts, and recommends `type=\"button\"` for script-only buttons: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/button | the 765 missing button types become a concrete bug class, not polish |
| MDN reduced motion | MDN says `prefers-reduced-motion` detects a user preference to minimize non-essential motion: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/%40media/prefers-reduced-motion | all animations, product media, nav transitions, and demo widgets need reduced-motion behavior |

#### Design System Completion Contract

Every visible Kolm surface must prove:

1. one source of truth for tokens: color, typography, spacing, radius, elevation, motion, focus, z-index, and layout;
2. one navigation system with predictable active state, no random underlines, no hover-only access, and keyboard/touch parity;
3. one icon system with accessible names and consistent stroke/fill behavior;
4. one button system with explicit type, target size, focus, disabled/loading states, and hierarchy;
5. one form system with labels, help text, inline errors, async state, success state, and no placeholder-only fields;
6. one account shell with live state, product matrix navigation, readiness scope, and next actions;
7. one public page template family for marketing/product/pricing/enterprise/docs/articles/use cases/case studies;
8. one media policy for hero demo, product screenshots, explainer video, code comparison, calculators, and diagrams;
9. one SEO metadata generator with title, description, canonical, OG/Twitter, JSON-LD, and sitemap proof;
10. one screenshot and interaction audit covering dark/light, desktop/mobile, auth/anon, and critical flows;
11. zero stale brand matches, mojibake, negative letter spacing, missing product media, or unscoped claim copy.

#### Product Media And Demo Completion Contract

The site cannot be state-of-art for AI companies without showing the product. Required media surfaces:

1. homepage hero media: a real interactive or recorded product moment showing capture to `.kolm` artifact, not abstract decoration;
2. side-by-side code comparison: before raw SDK call, after Kolm capture/route/compile swap;
3. interactive `.kolm` demo: paste a provider call, see captured metadata and artifact-style output;
4. ROI calculator: current spend, traffic shape, model mix, target runtime, confidence range, shareable result;
5. product architecture visual: capture, privacy lake, distill/compile, artifact registry, runtime/device fleet;
6. account preview: product matrix, readiness, storage, keys, audit, devices, builds, captures;
7. 90-second explainer video with transcript, captions, poster, dimensions, and reduced-motion fallback;
8. docs quickstart media: install, capture, compile, verify, deploy screenshots or terminal recordings;
9. trust media: evidence packet preview, audit log, compliance scope, object-storage readiness;
10. all media must be visible in light and dark mode and pass screenshot checks.

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |

| `12.0` | design tokens | is there one visual system instead of layered CSS patches? | token inventory, raw-color diff, contrast matrix, no uncontrolled overrides |

#### Node 12 V2 Frontend Design-System Governance And State-Of-Art UX Addendum

This V2 addendum keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` remains unused because node `12` already owns design-system and UX quality. Another agent may own website implementation, but the master tree must still say exactly what "state of the art" means for the visible product so implementation work does not become a sequence of subjective fixes.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not modify HTML, CSS, JavaScript, images, videos, screenshots, nav code, account pages, generated docs, or service worker files.

Current repo evidence checked for this pass:

| Evidence class | Current evidence | Node `12` completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Public frontend files scanned | 765 public HTML/CSS/JS files. | The frontend needs system governance, not per-page taste corrections. |
| Public CSS | 19 files, 779,198 bytes. | Too many styling surfaces remain unless there is a token/component ownership map and deprecation plan. |
| Public JS | 16 files, 324,664 bytes. | Nav, account shell, demos, widgets, service worker, and local auth helpers need shared behavior contracts. |
| HTML surfaces | 147 top-level public HTML pages, 48 account pages, 221 docs pages. | Public, authenticated, and docs UX must share one design system while honoring different user intents. |
| Dirty frontend files | Current public status reports 11 modified/untracked frontend paths under `public`. | Final screenshot proof must wait until the active frontend bot settles those edits; historical screenshot success is not final evidence. |
| Raw colors/functions | 16,416 raw hex/rgb/hsl/function color tokens across public HTML/CSS/JS. | Semantic token coverage is incomplete. Color cannot be considered polished until raw values are minimized or allowlisted. |
| Specificity overrides | 4,560 `!important` usages. | This is design-system drift and cascade fragility. It must be burned down or locked behind generated/legacy exceptions. |
| Interaction selectors | 1,539 hover selectors, 357 focus/focus-visible selectors, 62 reduced-motion refs. | Hover coverage exceeds focus coverage. Every hover-only affordance needs keyboard and touch parity. |
| Buttons | 765 `<button>` elements without an explicit `type`. | This is a concrete bug class because buttons inside forms default to submit. It must be fixed or intentionally exempted. |
| Product media | 0 `<video>` and 0 `<iframe>` elements in the public scan. | The site still needs actual product media or an equivalent live product demo; abstract hero art is not enough. |
| Brand drift | 1,924 old-brand `Kolmogorov`/`kolmogorov` matches in public HTML/CSS/JS scan. | Public-facing brand migration is not done until every match is classified as intentional archive/legal/history or removed. |

##### Node 12 V2 UI/UX Baseline

`ui-ux-pro-max` adds these non-negotiable product-interface constraints for Kolm:

1. accessibility is priority one: contrast, alt text, labels, keyboard navigation, visible focus, status messages, and accessible authentication;
2. touch and interaction are priority two: 44px to 48px target guidance, spacing between targets, no hover-only critical controls, loading feedback, and safe disabled states;
3. performance and layout stability are priority three: reserved media dimensions, no layout shift, route-level CSS/JS discipline, lazy non-critical media, and responsive constraints;
4. style selection must match the product type: Kolm is an enterprise AI infrastructure product, so the UI should be quiet, precise, high-trust, and operational rather than decorative or marketing-heavy;
5. typography and color must be semantic: base readable sizes, line-height discipline, contrast, no raw per-component colors, no one-note hue dominance, and no negative letter spacing;
6. forms and feedback must be explicit: visible labels, inline errors, helper text, progress, success/failure states, and no placeholder-only fields;
7. navigation must be predictable: clear active state, deep links, keyboard/touch parity, and no desktop menubar complexity unless fully implemented.

##### Node 12 V2 Current External Baselines

| Baseline | Source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C says WCAG 2.2 became a W3C Recommendation on 2023-10-05 and adds criteria including focus not obscured, target size, and accessible authentication: `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/` | Public, docs, demo, pricing, enterprise, and account workflows need keyboard, focus, touch target, auth, labels, errors, motion, and status-message proof. |
| Core Web Vitals | Google Search Central uses LCP, INP, and CLS for page experience: `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals` | Every template family needs LCP/INP/CLS budgets and media/layout dimensions. |
| WAI-ARIA disclosure navigation | W3C APG disclosure navigation uses buttons, Enter/Space, and `aria-expanded` without pretending site nav is a desktop app menu: `https://www.w3.org/TR/2021/NOTE-wai-aria-practices-1.2-20211129/examples/disclosure/disclosure-navigation.html` | Kolm nav popouts should be disclosure-based unless a real menubar is fully tested. |
| MDN button behavior | MDN documents that a button without `type="button"` inside or associated with a form behaves as submit: `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button` | Every script-only button needs explicit `type="button"`; submit/reset buttons need intentional tests. |

##### Node 12 V2 Design-System Contract

Every visible surface must resolve to one component contract before final release:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-design-system-contract-v2",
  "surface": "public|docs|account|demo|pricing|enterprise|article|tool",
  "node": "12",
  "purpose": "one_user_intent",
  "tokens": {
    "color": "semantic_only_or_allowlisted",
    "typography": "scale_and_line_height_defined",
    "spacing": "scale_defined",
    "radius": "scale_defined",
    "elevation": "scale_defined",
    "motion": "duration_and_reduced_motion_defined",
    "focus": "visible_and_consistent"
  },
  "components": {
    "nav": "disclosure_or_proven_menubar",
    "button": "explicit_type_target_size_state",
    "form": "label_error_help_loading_success",
    "card": "single_card_layer_no_nested_card",
    "table": "dense_scannable_accessible",
    "modal": "focus_trap_escape_close_return_focus",
    "media": "real_product_media_or_exempted"
  },
  "states": ["loading", "empty", "error", "success", "degraded", "unauthorized", "offline"],
  "proof": {
    "screenshots": ["dark_desktop", "dark_mobile", "light_desktop", "light_mobile"],
    "keyboard": "required",
    "touch": "required",
    "reduced_motion": "required",
    "seo_metadata": "required_when_public",
    "accessibility": "required"
  }
}
```

##### Node 12 V2 Surface-Specific UX Rules

| Surface | Completion rule |
|---|---|
| Homepage | First viewport must state the three product outcomes: route model traffic, compile/distill owned artifacts, and run enterprise AI on chosen infrastructure/devices. The hero must use real product media or an interactive proof widget, not abstract jargon. |
| Product page | Must explain the system as lanes with evidence: capture, privacy, data/eval, distill, compile, artifact registry, runtime/device/cloud, governance. |
| Pricing | Must show Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise/contact-sales truth, ROI calculator, entitlement table, annual/monthly clarity, billing FAQ, and no stale plan names. |
| Enterprise | Must show procurement path, trust posture, deployment options, SSO/SCIM/MFA scope, compliance evidence, data residency, support/SLA, and contact-sales CTA. |
| Docs | Must be task-first: install, capture, distill, compile, verify, run locally, deploy cloud, run on device, govern. API/CLI/SDK references must be generated or verified fresh. |
| Account overview | Must become the product matrix console: current state, missing prerequisites, next action, CLI/API equivalent, proof freshness, cost/quality/privacy tradeoffs. |
| Account tools | Every post-auth page must fetch live state or clearly say it is setup/documentation. Static account shells are not product completion. |
| Demo widgets | Must never log secrets, must have loading/error/success states, must work in light and dark, and must degrade gracefully when backend/API key is unavailable. |
| Nav | Must have one active-state model, no random underline/highlight styles, no hover-only popouts, keyboard/touch support, and consistent mobile behavior. |
| Product media | Screenshots, videos, diagrams, and demos must show actual Kolm workflow states and have dimensions, alt/captions/transcripts, reduced-motion fallback, and screenshot proof. |

| `W-FE-V2-002` | `12.0` | Reduce CSS surface sprawl. | The 19 public CSS files are classified as base, component, page, legacy, generated, or delete-candidate with owner and load path. |
| `W-FE-V2-003` | `12.0` | Burn down `!important`. | The 4,560 `!important` usages are removed or allowlisted by generated/legacy/component exception with explanation. |

| `W-FE-V2-023` | `12.9` | Add copy-density redline. | Paragraph walls, jargon, vague category labels, duplicate CTAs, and unsupported claims are rejected by content QA. |

##### Node 12 V2 Relax Redline

Node `12` is not done until all of the following are true:

1. every public/docs/account page resolves to one design-system contract;
2. raw color/function tokens, `!important`, duplicated component styles, and page-specific overrides are minimized or allowlisted;
3. navigation active state, underline behavior, popouts, mobile behavior, keyboard behavior, and touch behavior are consistent across the site;
4. every script-only button has explicit `type="button"` and every submit/reset behavior is intentional;
5. homepage and product pages show actual Kolm product proof, not abstract visual filler;
6. product copy explains all three product outcomes without over-serving only "one AI API";
7. account pages are operational consoles with live state, next actions, proof freshness, and clear empty/error/degraded states;
8. docs, API, CLI, SDK, public pages, and account UI use the same plan, product, route, and claim vocabulary;
9. old-brand matches are zero on live public surfaces except intentional archive/legal/history records;
10. WCAG 2.2, Core Web Vitals, disclosure-nav semantics, button behavior, reduced motion, and screenshot evidence are release-blocking gates;
11. all-route screenshots pass on the final frontend commit after current dirty files settle;
12. production smoke rechecks deployed pages and authenticated account flows before any final frontend claim.

If any item fails, the website may be improved, but node `12` is not state-of-the-art complete.

#### Node 12 V3 Visible Product Quality, Conversion, Account UX, And Screenshot Finality Contract

This V3 extension keeps the master spec tree disciplined: node `52` remains unused because node `12` already owns the visible product. This section does not edit frontend implementation while another frontend agent is active. It defines the exact product, design, copy, accessibility, media, account, docs, SEO, and verification bar that implementation agents must satisfy before Kolm can be called a finished state-of-the-art website for AI companies.

The key correction is first-principles positioning. The visible product cannot over-index on "one AI API" because Kolm has three product surfaces: route and own model traffic, compile and distill top models into signed `.kolm` artifacts, and run enterprise models down to any cloud, private cluster, browser, or device. Every public, docs, demo, pricing, and post-auth account surface must make those three outcomes understandable within one scan without becoming jargon-heavy.

##### Node 12 V3 Current Evidence Delta

| area | current evidence | completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| public HTML pages | 729 | large static surface; final quality requires route classes, shared templates, and generated checks instead of page-by-page subjective polish |
| account HTML pages | 48 | post-auth product is broad enough to need operational workflows, live states, permission states, degraded states, and proof freshness on every account page |
| docs HTML pages | 221 | docs are a product surface, not a content dump; every guide/API/SDK page needs a runnable next action and freshness proof |
| public HTML bytes | 14,789,813 | visible product is large enough that design tokens, nav, metadata, media, and component rules must be enforced mechanically |
| CSS files | 20 files, 786,393 bytes | CSS is substantial enough to require a token ownership map and override budget |
| JS files | 16 files, 324,664 bytes | browser behavior must be mapped to routes, account flows, demo widgets, nav, and error handling |
| raw color/function refs | 16,077 | color still needs strict tokenization or allowlisted exceptions; one-off colors are a consistency risk |
| CSS variable refs | 34,269 | token system exists, but raw color volume means implementation is not yet fully canonical |
| `!important` refs | 4,560 | override debt must be reduced, isolated to known compatibility layers, or blocked before final design-system certification |
| hover selectors | 1,539 | hover behavior is extensive and must have tap/keyboard equivalents |
| focus selectors | 357 | focus coverage is materially lower than hover coverage; keyboard and visible focus need route-level proof |
| reduced-motion refs | 62 | motion accommodation exists, but all animations and video/demo elements must prove reduced-motion behavior |
| old brand refs in public scan | 4 | old-brand public exposure is far lower than the earlier 1,924-match baseline, but final live surfaces still need a zero-or-classified result |
| buttons without explicit type | 765 | MDN warns default submit behavior can destroy state; every script-only button needs `type="button"` |
| pages with inline style | 438 | inline styles block cohesive visual governance unless generated, audited, and tokenized |
| pages with inline SVG | 667 | inline SVG is not automatically wrong, but icon/product-media usage must be classified, accessible, and consistent |
| images without alt | 0 | static alt coverage is currently good, but product-media quality and purpose still fail in audits |
| video or iframe elements | 0 | current public surface does not yet have real embedded product video/demo media proof |
| pages with H1 count not equal to 1 | 2 | H1 semantics still need correction before SEO/accessibility finality |
| missing meta descriptions | 0 | coverage exists, but length and quality are inconsistent |
| descriptions under 80 chars | 57 | many pages undersell the page outcome in snippets |
| descriptions over 170 chars | 143 | many pages are too verbose for clean search/social snippets |
| latest focused polish report | FAIL, 24 failures, 0 warnings | `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tfocused-polish-pass/report.md` shows missing or invisible product media on `/`, `/product`, and `/pricing` across dark/light and desktop/mobile |
| latest manual use report | FAIL, 36 failures, 44 warnings | `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account/report.md` shows missing media, negative H1 letter spacing, shared CSS/nav loading warnings, and small action targets |
| active frontend dirty files | `public/brand-refresh.css`, `public/home-refresh.css`, `public/kolm-chat.js`, `public/nav.js`, `public/studio.html`, `public/styles.css`, `public/supplement.css`, `public/surface-polish.css`, `public/sw.js`, `public/w604.css`, `public/warm-paper.css` | final UI proof is not valid until these parallel-agent edits settle and screenshots are rerun on the exact release commit |

The current state is improved but not final. A green backend truth spine does not make the website state of the art if the visible product still fails media proof, copy clarity, nav consistency, account workflow completeness, or production screenshot evidence.

##### Node 12 V3 Official Baselines

| baseline | primary source | Kolm release rule |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C WCAG 2.2 Recommendation, 2024-12-12: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/ | public, docs, account, pricing, demos, modals, nav, and forms must target WCAG 2.2 AA, with explicit treatment for focus, reflow, non-text contrast, target size, redundant entry, and accessible authentication |
| WCAG 2.2 changes | WAI "What's New in WCAG 2.2": https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/ | login, account setup, checkout, enterprise inquiry, and admin flows cannot require memory/copy tests without supported alternatives; repeated data entry must be minimized |
| ARIA disclosure nav | WAI ARIA APG disclosure pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ | nav popouts, docs menus, mobile menus, accordions, pricing details, and account panels need `button`, `aria-expanded`, optional `aria-controls`, Enter/Space behavior, escape/click-out behavior, and visible focus |
| Core Web Vitals | Google Search Central Core Web Vitals: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals | target LCP under 2.5s, INP under 200ms, CLS under 0.1 on homepage, product, pricing, docs, API, demo, and account entry routes |
| Lighthouse | Chrome Lighthouse docs: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse | every critical route must archive Lighthouse categories or equivalent local metrics for performance, accessibility, best practices, and SEO before final release |
| title links | Google Search Central title-link guidance: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link | every page gets descriptive, concise, unique titles with concise Kolm branding and no boilerplate spam |
| snippets | Google Search Central snippet guidance: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/snippet | every page gets a specific human-readable description; critical pages cannot be too short, too long, duplicated, or vague |
| image alt decisions | WAI alt decision tree: https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/images/decision-tree/ | product images, diagrams, screenshots, logos, and icons must be classified as informative, functional, decorative, text alternative, or complex image with matching alt/label treatment |
| button semantics | MDN button element: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button | every non-submit button has `type="button"`; destructive, copy, demo, nav, and async buttons preserve state and expose disabled/loading/error states |
| reduced motion | MDN prefers-reduced-motion: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@media/prefers-reduced-motion | animation, hero media, video, demos, carousels, popouts, and account transitions need reduced-motion alternatives and must avoid essential information conveyed only by motion |

##### Node 12 V3 Product Story Contract

Every first-screen and major route must answer five questions without forcing users through paragraphs:

1. What is Kolm? A production AI artifact system that captures behavior, compiles/distills it into signed `.kolm` artifacts, and runs it across providers, clouds, and devices.
2. Who is it for? AI teams shipping production agents, regulated AI workflows, cost-sensitive model traffic, enterprise private deployments, and teams that need audit-ready reproducibility.
3. Why now? Model spend, vendor lock-in, compliance evidence, device deployment, and model churn are now operational risks, not research topics.
4. Which product path am I in? Route owned model traffic, compile/distill top models, or deploy enterprise models anywhere.
5. What can I do next? Paste an SDK call, compile an artifact, run an evaluation, calculate savings, inspect docs, or contact sales.

The hero cannot use abstract copy like "Turn model traffic into owned AI" unless it is paired with the full three-surface explanation and concrete proof. The preferred hero structure is:

- eyebrow: `Kolm is the AI artifact layer for production teams`
- headline: one short line that covers all three surfaces, such as `Capture, compile, and run AI your team can own`
- support: one sentence, maximum 24 words, naming route, distill, and deploy outcomes
- proof row: three measurable outcomes, each with evidence link or scoped status
- primary action: live demo or compile path
- secondary action: enterprise/runtime path

No hero, pricing, docs, or account page may bury the product category under vague brand language, protocol jargon, or paragraph walls.

##### Node 12 V3 Master Spec Tree Branch

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `12.0` | product narrative | does every route map to route/compile/deploy outcomes without over-serving one surface? | route-purpose map, H1/subcopy audit, screenshots, and user-path matrix |
| `12.1` | visual identity | does Kolm look like an enterprise AI infrastructure product, not a patchwork static site? | token ledger, typography scale, color/elevation/radius map, icon system, and before/after screenshots |
| `12.2` | navigation | do desktop, mobile, docs, account, and popout nav states behave consistently? | active-state map, underline rules, disclosure semantics, keyboard/tap proof, and screenshot set |
| `12.3` | components | are buttons, cards, panels, forms, tables, tabs, banners, modals, popouts, toasts, and empty states canonical? | component registry, state matrix, `type` audit, focus proof, and no unowned overrides |
| `12.4` | homepage conversion | does the homepage prove the product in under 5 seconds? | above-fold screenshot, demo media, code comparison, ROI module, social proof or scoped quote block, and analytics events |
| `12.5` | product pages | do route, compile/distill, and deploy-anywhere pages each have outcome-led proof? | page-specific demos, diagrams/screenshots, use-case flows, claims tied to backend evidence, and CTA intent map |
| `12.6` | docs and API | can a developer start, run, debug, and verify without guessing? | quickstart, tutorials, API routes, SDK pages, CLI/TUI parity, runnable snippets, freshness stamps, and broken-link proof |
| `12.7` | account console | does post-auth UX cover the whole product matrix operationally? | live product graph, readiness, storage, keys, billing, team, audit, compile/distill jobs, devices, evals, alerts, and next actions |
| `12.8` | media and demo | do images/video/demo elements show real product value in light/dark and mobile/desktop? | generated or captured assets, thumbnails, transcripts, dimensions, reduced motion, nonblank canvas/media checks, and product-media audit pass |
| `12.9` | accessibility and interaction | can the product be used by keyboard, screen reader, touch, and reduced-motion users? | WCAG 2.2 AA matrix, target-size proof, focus order, labels, ARIA disclosure proof, contrast, and no hover-only workflows |
| `12.10` | SEO and social | does search/social metadata match actual route value? | unique titles, clean descriptions, canonical/OG/Twitter/schema, sitemap, robots, structured snippets, and preview captures |
| `12.11` | performance | do critical pages meet Core Web Vitals and asset budgets? | LCP/INP/CLS, CSS/JS budgets, font loading, media dimensions, caching, service worker behavior, and route-level performance report |
| `12.12` | production visual proof | can final deployed pages be visually trusted? | dark/light desktop/mobile all-route screenshots, focused manual-use routes, production re-screenshot, and archived report paths |

##### Node 12 V3 Visible Product Quality Contract

```json
{
  "contract": "kolm.visible_product_quality_contract.v3",
  "scope": [
    "public marketing pages",
    "product and use-case pages",
    "pricing and enterprise pages",
    "interactive demos and media",
    "docs, API reference, SDK pages, tutorials",
    "post-auth account console",
    "navigation, popouts, mobile menus, forms, buttons, empty states",
    "SEO, social metadata, structured data, sitemap, service worker",
    "dark mode, light mode, desktop, mobile, reduced motion"
  ],
  "product_surfaces": {
    "route_owned_model_traffic": "capture provider calls, route across models, reduce spend, preserve audit evidence",
    "compile_and_distill": "turn captured behavior and teacher outputs into signed .kolm artifacts with evals and provenance",
    "deploy_anywhere": "run enterprise models and artifacts across cloud, private infrastructure, browser, local device, and auditor paths"
  },
  "acceptance": {
    "clarity": "every critical page answers what, who, why now, product path, and next action within one screen",
    "cohesion": "all visible pages use the same tokens, typography, spacing, nav states, component states, and copy vocabulary",
    "proof": "every claim links to shipped, implemented, benchmark, package, external, or certification status",
    "accessibility": "WCAG 2.2 AA is the minimum, with explicit target-size, focus, reflow, redundant-entry, authentication, and reduced-motion proof",
    "performance": "critical routes target LCP under 2.5s, INP under 200ms, CLS under 0.1, with documented waivers only for unavoidable authenticated workflows",
    "media": "homepage, product, pricing, demo, docs, and account routes show real product media or live UI proof visible in dark/light and mobile/desktop",
    "account": "post-auth UX shows the full product matrix, live status, next best action, proof freshness, empty states, and errors",
    "docs": "docs are runnable, role-based, route-current, SDK-current, and clear about local versus external readiness",
    "finality": "no frontend finality claim without current all-route local screenshots, focused manual-use screenshots, and production screenshots on the deployed commit"
  },
  "release_blockers": [
    "red UI screenshot report",
    "missing or invisible product media",
    "unscoped or unsupported marketing claim",
    "stale API/docs route count",
    "old brand token on live public surfaces",
    "hover-only nav or popout interaction",
    "keyboard-inaccessible account workflow",
    "button without intentional type",
    "critical route without unique title and description",
    "Core Web Vitals regression without waiver",
    "dirty frontend files with no final screenshot rerun"
  ]
}
```

##### Node 12 V3 Acceptance Ladder

| level | name | done when |
|---:|---|---|
| 0 | route exists | route resolves without 404/500 and has one owner in the surface map |
| 1 | route renders | dark/light desktop/mobile render without blank media, overlap, console-breaking behavior, or hidden primary actions |
| 2 | route explains | H1, subcopy, CTA, metadata, and first-screen content match the route purpose and one of the three product surfaces |
| 3 | route is accessible | WCAG 2.2 AA checks, focus order, keyboard behavior, target size, labels, ARIA, and reduced-motion behavior pass |
| 4 | route is responsive | mobile/desktop layouts have no horizontal scroll, unstable dimensions, text overflow, or tap-only/hover-only traps |
| 5 | route is live | account/docs/demo routes fetch real state or clearly labeled fixture/demo state with loading, empty, error, degraded, and permission variants |
| 6 | route proves | route includes real product media, runnable demo, code comparison, artifact proof, ROI proof, or evidence link appropriate to its purpose |
| 7 | route converts | route has one primary action, one secondary action, analytics events, funnel ownership, and no CTA contradiction |
| 8 | route performs | LCP, INP, CLS, CSS, JS, font, media, and service-worker behavior meet budgets or have a documented waiver |
| 9 | route screenshots | local and production screenshots pass in dark/light desktop/mobile on the final commit |
| 10 | route certified | claims, docs, backend status, account state, screenshots, and production smoke all agree |

##### Node 12 V3 Design System Redline

| dimension | redline |
|---|---|
| typography | no negative letter spacing on H1 or compact UI text; type scale must distinguish hero, section, panel, table, and badge contexts without viewport-width font scaling |
| color | raw colors are removed from route/component code or allowlisted; semantic tokens cover brand, surface, line, text, success, warning, danger, info, focus, disabled, and chart states |
| spacing | spacing scale is shared across public/docs/account, with fixed-format controls using stable dimensions and no layout jumps from dynamic labels |
| elevation | panels, modals, nav popouts, cards, and account surfaces use one elevation/radius system; no card-inside-card shells for page sections |
| nav | active underline, hover, focus, open, selected, disabled, mobile, and mega-menu states are visually consistent and semantically correct |
| buttons | each button has type, label, icon rule, loading state, disabled state, pressed/focus state, error behavior, minimum target, and no accidental submit behavior |
| forms | labels are visible, errors are field-local, helper text is specific, destructive actions require confirmation, and enterprise forms map to sales workflow not fake checkout |
| media | product screenshots/video/demo/canvas are visible in dark/light and mobile/desktop, have dimensions, alt/transcript, reduced-motion behavior, and are not stock-like abstraction |
| demos | homepage demo accepts input, returns a scoped artifact or fixture with honest state, shows loading/error/copy/download state, and never implies unsupported backend execution |
| copy | every paragraph earns its space; jargon is replaced with outcome language unless the reader is already in docs/API context |
| account | every account page has live state, proof freshness, owner action, empty/error/degraded states, and a path back to the product matrix |

##### Node 12 V3 Account Console Completion Contract

Post-auth cannot be a decorative dashboard. It must cover the whole product matrix:

| account area | required visible behavior |
|---|---|
| overview | product graph, readiness counts, open closeout items, recent model traffic, compile/distill jobs, deployed artifacts, savings, alerts, and next best actions |
| API keys | create, rotate, revoke, scope, last-used, copied-once secret behavior, audit log link, environment guidance, and permission-gated empty states |
| providers | configured/missing provider groups, route policy, fallback behavior, spend controls, live health, and secret-values-never-shown label |
| storage | selected object store, local/R2/S3/Supabase readiness, missing variables, size limits, smoke command, and artifact retention state |
| compile/distill | job queue, teacher/student policy, dataset/capture source, K-Score, eval status, artifact hash, model card, failure explanation, and retry path |
| artifacts | artifact registry, signature, provenance, version diff, deploy targets, runtime compatibility, rollback, SBOM, and audit export |
| devices/runtimes | target devices, runtime fit, quantization profile, latency/cost/quality tradeoff, deployment status, and unsupported target explanation |
| evals/benchmarks | K-Score calibration status, redaction benchmarks, public benchmark scope, raw report links, and no unsupported leaderboard claims |
| team/security | users, roles, SSO/SCIM/MFA/passkeys, audit events, access reviews, tenant boundary, compliance packet, and trust status |
| billing | Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise plan vocabulary, sales-required enterprise path, usage, chargeback, savings, invoices, credits, and no legacy plan drift |

Every account page should fail closed: if a live endpoint is unavailable, the page must show exactly what failed, what is local-only, what needs credentials, and what action the operator should take.

##### Node 12 V3 SEO, Docs, And Content Contract

| content class | completion rule |
|---|---|
| homepage | one product category, one three-surface explanation, concrete demo/proof, clean title, clean snippet, and no abstract-only tagline |
| product pages | one page each for route/capture, compile/distill, and deploy-anywhere, with code, artifact, runtime, and account evidence |
| pricing | Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise vocabulary only, enterprise sales path, ROI calculator, usage assumptions, and no stale legacy tiers |
| enterprise | trust, deployment, governance, procurement, security, and sales workflow with scoped certifications and no fake self-serve enterprise checkout |
| docs landing | task-first IA: start, capture, compile, distill, route, deploy, verify, account, security, API, SDKs, CLI, TUI, troubleshooting |
| API reference | route count and OpenAPI must match generated inventory; endpoints show auth, scopes, request/response, errors, and linked guides |
| SDK docs | each SDK page states install status, package channel, version, smoke command, minimum example, and external package limitations |
| tutorials | each tutorial has prerequisite, time to complete, commands, expected output, failure cases, and cleanup |
| snippets | every critical route has unique description; short/long meta outliers are corrected or programmatically justified |
| schemas | OG/Twitter/schema/canonical/sitemap data match page purpose and do not include mojibake, stale brand, or unsupported claims |
