| `W-FE-V3-014` | `12.7` | Complete post-auth page states. | All 48 account pages have live/empty/loading/error/degraded/permission states, primary action, and product-matrix context. |
| `W-FE-V3-015` | `12.8` | Replace abstract/trash media with product media. | Homepage, product, pricing, docs, and account routes show real screenshots/video/demo/canvas with dimensions, alt/transcript, and light/dark visibility. |
| `W-FE-V3-016` | `12.8` | Add 90-second explainer or equivalent proof. | Video or interactive demo is above fold or one scroll away, loads in light/dark, has poster/transcript/captions, and does not block LCP. |
| `W-FE-V3-017` | `12.9` | Run WCAG 2.2 AA remediation. | Contrast, focus, target size, reflow, labels, disclosure, redundant entry, accessible authentication, and reduced motion pass critical routes. |
| `W-FE-V3-018` | `12.10` | Repair metadata and social previews. | The 57 short and 143 long descriptions are corrected or generated with route-specific purpose; titles are unique and concise. |
| `W-FE-V3-019` | `12.10` | Finish brand cleanup. | The 4 remaining old-brand public refs are removed or classified as archive/legal/history with no live marketing exposure. |
| `W-FE-V3-020` | `12.11` | Add performance budgets. | Critical routes meet LCP/INP/CLS, CSS/JS/font/media budgets locally and after deploy, with no hidden heavy media regressions. |
| `W-FE-V3-021` | `12.12` | Rerun all-route local screenshots after frontend dirty files settle. | Dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots pass on final local commit with report paths in the release packet. |
| `W-FE-V3-022` | `12.12` | Rerun production screenshots after deploy. | Deployed `kolm.ai` public/docs/account flows are re-screenshotted with production URL, commit, auth class, and no local-only assumptions. |
| `W-FE-V3-023` | `12.12` | Add focused manual-use journeys. | A human/manual browser pass exercises homepage demo, product path choice, pricing/enterprise, docs quickstart, account overview, API key, storage, and compile/distill job flows. |
| `W-FE-V3-024` | `12.12` | Issue Node 12 completion certificate. | Visual design, copy, account, docs, media, accessibility, SEO, performance, screenshots, and claim scope all pass on one final release commit. |

##### Node 12 V3 Redline

Node `12` cannot be relaxed or marked complete until all of the following are true:

1. the homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs, API, demo, and account entry routes explain the three Kolm product surfaces without jargon-heavy paragraph walls;
2. the visible product uses one canonical design system across public, docs, and account surfaces;
3. nav active states, underline behavior, popouts, mobile menu, disclosure semantics, keyboard behavior, and tap behavior are consistent;
4. every critical UI control has explicit type, label, focus, loading, disabled, error, and target-size behavior;
5. product media is real, visible, useful, accessible, and tested in dark/light desktop/mobile;
6. account pages are operational product surfaces, not static dashboards;
7. docs and API pages are runnable and route-current;
8. SEO metadata is unique, concise, human-readable, and page-specific;
9. WCAG 2.2 AA, reduced motion, Core Web Vitals, and button/disclosure semantics are release-blocking gates;
10. current red UI reports are superseded by green final reports after parallel frontend edits settle;
11. production `kolm.ai` is re-screenshotted after deploy with authenticated account checks;
12. the release packet contains Node 12 evidence paths, not chat-only claims.

Until this redline passes, Kolm can be materially better, but it is not the best website for AI companies and not 100 percent visible-product complete.

### Verification, Release, Deployment, And Production Operations Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `13`: verification, release, deployment, and production operations. A local green suite is necessary but not enough. A finished Kolm build must prove that the exact same commit can be generated, tested, packaged, deployed, smoked in production, rolled back, observed, and explained through durable evidence.

This pass is intentionally scoped to the master spec document. It does not edit workflows, deployment config, public pages, or product code while other frontend and backend agents are active.

#### Current Verification And Operations Inventory

| area | current evidence from worktree scan | completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| scripts | 251 files, 1,493,875 bytes | strong automation surface, but large enough to require ownership, sharding, and generated-artifact sequencing |
| scripts with debt tokens | 17 files | triage signal; not proof of broken code, but every TODO/FIXME/HACK in release-critical scripts needs an owner or removal |
| scripts with key-shaped literals | 6 files | must be classified as fixture, route false positive, redacted example, or real secret risk |
| tests | 560 files, 7,306,074 bytes | broad coverage, but needs a test pyramid map and release-blocker classification |
| largest wave tests | `wave834-regulatory`, `wave773-video-distill`, `wave772-audio-distill`, `wave771-vlm-distill`, `wave417-definition-of-done` | these are likely integration-spec tests and should be stable, sharded, and explicitly owned |
| workflows | 11 GitHub workflow files | CI exists, but workflow hardening and release evidence need to be proven |
| workflow `uses:` refs | 37 total, 36 not SHA-pinned by the static scan | tag/local refs are not automatically wrong, but immutable SHA pinning or a documented action policy is needed for release workflows |
| workflows with explicit `permissions` | 4 of 11 | every workflow should declare least-privilege permissions, with write scopes only where justified |
| workflows with `id-token` | 0 of 11 | package publishing, cloud deploy, and provenance flows cannot claim modern OIDC-based trust until this exists |
| workflows with `contents: write` | 2 | must be narrowed, documented, and protected by branch/environment rules |
| workflows using secrets | 5 | secrets should be environment-scoped, rotated, masked, and replaced with OIDC where possible |
| CodeQL/Gitleaks/Semgrep/Scorecard/attestation matches | 0 in workflow scan | add or document equivalent SAST, secret scanning, OpenSSF Scorecard, and artifact attestation gates |
| configs scanned | `vercel.json`, `Dockerfile`, `package.json`, `eslint.config.js` | core deploy/build config exists, but production proof needs a release manifest |
| Dockerfile | base image digest-pinned, uses `npm ci --omit=dev || npm install --omit=dev`, no non-root user or healthcheck visible | digest pinning is good; fallback install, root runtime, and missing healthcheck are release-hardening gaps |
| Vercel config | 2,390 lines, many redirects/rewrites, no obvious smoke/rollback/screenshot markers | routing is substantial; deploy completion needs an explicit production smoke and rollback contract |
| release gate | `scripts/release-verify.cjs` runs lint refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, billing tiers | strong local release driver; still needs CI binding, production evidence, artifact retention, and deploy rollback proof |

The two `vercel.json` key-shaped hits observed in the scan are route-like strings under `/insurance/templates/...`, not proof of secrets. They still belong in a secret-literal classifier so future findings do not depend on human memory.

#### Release Gate Map

| gate | current role | redline before final release |
|---|---|---|
| `npm run release:verify` | local release umbrella for refs, OpenAPI, SDKs, tests, local surfaces, CLI doctor, whoami, artifact verification, billing | must run clean on the final commit after all frontend/backend generated assets settle |
| `npm run verify:depth` | product truth spine, codegraph, platform readiness, claims, inventions, benchmarks, package and compliance checks | must distinguish local proof from external package/certification/benchmark blockers |
| `npm run lint:refs` | static refs, hrefs, product-surface route ownership | must run after every generated docs/OpenAPI/product-graph change |
| `npm run local:surfaces` and `npm run local:surfaces:deep` | local HTTP product smoke | must cover every user-facing public/account/API route that claims shipped status |
| `npm run prod:surfaces` and `npm run prod:surfaces:deep` | production HTTP product smoke | must run against the deployed production URL and archive URL, commit, env class, and result |
| `npm run ui:audit:all:themes` | all-route visual screenshot audit across themes/viewports | must run after frontend dirty files settle and become a release blocker for public/account/docs UI |
| `npm run verify:claims-scope` | prevents marketing from overstating open readiness gates | must cover public pages, docs, generated metadata, product graph, account UI, CLI/TUI text |
| `npm run verify:brand` | brand and stale-name hygiene | must enforce Kolm naming and reject unintended Kolmogorov public copy |
| `npm run verify:package-release` | local package release readiness | must emit installability status per SDK/package and never imply registry publication without proof |
| `npm run verify:sota` | readiness matrix and closeout ledger | must keep external partner, package, benchmark, and certification gates visible |

#### Official Release And Operations Standards

| standard | source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA | The SLSA v1.2 spec describes incremental supply-chain security levels and emphasizes provenance, verified properties, and traceability to source: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/ | every release artifact, package, image, generated SDK, and `.kolm` example bundle should have provenance metadata and verification instructions |
| GitHub artifact attestations | GitHub documents artifact attestations for build provenance and requires workflow permissions such as `id-token: write`, `contents: read`, and `attestations: write`: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/secure-your-work/use-artifact-attestations/use-artifact-attestations | release workflows should generate and verify attestations for binaries, packages, containers, SBOMs, and generated release bundles |
| GitHub Actions secure use | GitHub recommends least-privilege `GITHUB_TOKEN`, careful secrets handling, SHA pinning for immutable third-party actions, CodeQL/code scanning, OIDC for cloud resources, dependency review, and OpenSSF Scorecard: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use | every workflow must declare permissions, pin or justify actions, avoid long-lived deploy secrets where OIDC is possible, and publish security scan evidence |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | OpenSSF Scorecard auto-generates a security score to help assess open-source trust, risk, and security posture: https://openssf.org/scorecard/ | release readiness should include a Scorecard run or equivalent policy report, especially for pinned dependencies, token permissions, branch protection, and dangerous workflow patterns |
| Docker build best practices | Docker recommends trusted small base images, pinning base versions, avoiding unnecessary packages, building/testing images in CI, and careful handling of environment variables: https://docs.docker.com/build/building/best-practices/ | Kolm's Dockerfile should keep digest pinning, remove fallback nondeterminism, run as non-root, include healthcheck, minimize context, and test the image in CI |
| Vercel rollback | Vercel documents production rollback, promote, logs comparison, and bisect workflows: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/rollback-production-deployment | every deploy needs a rollback URL, smoke result, log check, and documented last-known-good path before being called production-complete |
| DORA metrics | DORA describes delivery performance metrics for throughput and instability, including deployment and recovery outcomes: https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | Kolm should track deployment frequency, lead time, failed deployment recovery time, change failure rate, and rework rate for release quality |
| OpenTelemetry | OpenTelemetry is a vendor-neutral framework for traces, metrics, and logs: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/ | production routes, model calls, distillation jobs, capture flows, artifact builds, cloud storage, and account admin actions need trace, metric, and log coverage with stable names |

#### Release Architecture Target

The target release architecture is a single evidence pipeline:

1. normalize generated assets in a serial order: API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, SDK manifest, docs, service worker, sitemap, and search artifacts;
2. run fast deterministic checks first: syntax, formatting, stale brand, claim scope, refs, route ownership, schema validation, fixture health;
3. run product correctness gates: unit, integration, route smoke, CLI/TUI parity, SDK smoke, benchmark fixtures, package readiness, cloud readiness;
4. run UI proof after frontend changes settle: dark/light, desktop/mobile, critical account flows, screenshots, visual diff thresholds, media visibility, button/nav/accessibility checks;
5. build packages and containers reproducibly with locked dependencies and no fallback install path;
6. generate SBOMs and provenance attestations for release artifacts;
7. deploy a candidate to a preview URL, then run deep prod-equivalent smoke with only environment-provided keys;
8. promote to production only after preview smoke, release manifest, and rollback target are attached;
9. run production smoke against the canonical domain after promotion;
10. archive evidence: commit, build ID, route count, test count, screenshot count, package versions, artifact hashes, attestation IDs, env class, smoke URL, and rollback URL.

#### Master Spec Tree Branch 13

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `13.0` | CI inventory | do all workflows have owners, permissions, triggers, secrets, and release relevance? | workflow ledger with permissions, action refs, secrets, environments, protected branches, and release-blocker status |
| `13.1` | generated artifacts | are generated files built in a deterministic serial order? | one generation graph, stale-check commands, and no race-prone parallel generation |
| `13.2` | test suite | is the 560-test suite mapped to product risk and sharded predictably? | unit/integration/e2e/perf/security/visual taxonomy, flaky-test ledger, time budgets, and shard reports |
| `13.3` | release verify | can one command prove local release readiness on a clean checkout? | `release:verify` transcript, machine-readable JSON, no skipped required gates, no hidden env assumptions |
| `13.4` | deploy config | is Vercel/container deployment reproducible and reviewable? | deployment manifest, env matrix, redirects/rewrites owner map, Docker image check, preview URL proof |
| `13.5` | production smoke | does the deployed domain prove the same product surface as local? | prod smoke transcript, route count, auth class, key source, HTTP status matrix, screenshot sample |
| `13.6` | observability | can operators debug a broken release or model route quickly? | OpenTelemetry traces/metrics/logs, request IDs, model/provider dimensions, dashboards, alert thresholds |
| `13.7` | rollback and incident | can a bad deploy be detected, rolled back, and explained? | rollback runbook, Vercel rollback/promote command proof, incident template, DORA recovery metrics |
| `13.8` | supply chain | can users verify what was built, by whom, from what source? | SLSA-aligned provenance, SBOMs, artifact attestations, Scorecard, SHA-pinned actions or policy exceptions |
| `13.9` | evidence archive | can a future auditor reconstruct the release without rerunning everything? | immutable release evidence bundle with checksums, screenshots, logs, smoke reports, package tarballs, and attestations |

#### Release And Operations Tickets To Generate

- W-REL-001: create a release manifest schema containing commit, version, generated artifact hashes, route counts, test counts, screenshot counts, package versions, env class, smoke URLs, deployment URL, rollback URL, and attestation IDs.
- W-REL-002: build a gate dependency graph so generated assets run serially before OpenAPI, product graph, SDK manifest, docs, service worker, sitemap, and screenshot gates.
- W-REL-003: inventory all 11 workflows with owner, trigger, permissions, secrets, environments, protected branches, and release-blocker status.
- W-REL-004: enforce workflow least privilege: every workflow declares `permissions`, every write permission is justified, and PR workflows cannot inherit deploy/package secrets.
- W-REL-005: implement action pinning policy: full SHA pin for release workflows or a documented exception list with Dependabot/action review coverage.
- W-REL-006: add OIDC and provenance for publish/deploy jobs: `id-token: write`, `attestations: write`, short-lived deploy auth where supported, and no long-lived cloud deploy token unless justified.
- W-REL-007: add CodeQL or equivalent SAST, Gitleaks or equivalent secret scanning, Semgrep or equivalent workflow/code policy checks, OpenSSF Scorecard, dependency review, and npm audit evidence to the release bundle.
- W-REL-008: harden Docker release path: remove `npm install` fallback from production build, add non-root user, add healthcheck, restrict copy context, test image in CI, and attach image SBOM/provenance.
- W-REL-009: create Vercel deploy contract: preview deploy, preview smoke, production promote, production smoke, log check, last-known-good URL, rollback command, and deploy evidence archive.
- W-REL-010: make production surface smoke key-safe: read only environment keys, print no secret values, support both provided `ks_` candidates through env selection, and classify auth failures separately from route failures.
- W-REL-011: make all-route UI screenshot audit a release blocker after frontend dirty files settle, including dark/light, desktop/mobile, public/docs/account, media visibility, nav states, and reduced-motion checks.
- W-REL-012: classify the 17 script debt-token files and 6 key-shaped-literal script files into release blocker, tracked debt, fixture-only, or false positive.
- W-REL-013: introduce test-suite sharding policy for 560 tests with deterministic order, no hidden network, timeout budgets, retry/flake ledger, and machine-readable duration history.
- W-REL-014: define production SLO and observability contract for API latency, model/provider routing, capture ingest, artifact generation, distillation jobs, storage readiness, account admin actions, and auth failures.
- W-REL-015: build the immutable release evidence archive: one directory per release with manifest, logs, screenshots, smoke JSON, OpenAPI/product graph/readiness files, package tarballs, SBOMs, attestations, and checksums.

#### Release Redline

Kolm cannot be called 100 percent release-complete until this branch passes these exact conditions:

1. `release:verify`, `verify:depth`, `lint:refs`, `local:surfaces:deep`, `verify:claims-scope`, `verify:brand`, and `verify:package-release` pass on the same final commit;
2. generated artifacts are fresh and generated serially with no race between API ref and OpenAPI builders;
3. all workflows have least-privilege permissions and release workflows have action pinning or documented exceptions;
4. provenance, SBOM, and artifact attestation evidence exists for every release package, container, and generated distribution bundle;
5. production deploy has preview smoke, production smoke, rollback target, and archived logs;
6. frontend all-route screenshots pass on the same deployed commit after current parallel frontend edits settle;
7. production smoke uses real production configuration without printing secrets;
8. the evidence archive lets an engineer or auditor reconstruct what shipped without relying on chat history.

#### Node 13 V2 Release, Deployment, Rollback, And Evidence Archive Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `13`. It is a release-operations specification only. It does not edit workflows, deployment config, generated docs, product code, frontend assets, or package manifests while parallel agents are active. It converts the current codebase evidence into a stricter release contract so a later implementation pass can harden CI, deployment, provenance, production smoke, rollback, and evidence retention without guessing.

Node `9` remains the final release-verification arbiter. Node `13` owns the machinery that makes that proof repeatable in CI and production: workflow permissions, generated-artifact order, package/container reproducibility, deployment promotion, production smoke, rollback, observability, release metrics, and immutable evidence archives. Node `29` owns production environment parity, node `40` owns QA depth, node `43` owns whole-build redline, and node `46` owns traceability.

##### Node 13 V2 Current Evidence

| area | current evidence | release interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| `package.json` package name | `kolm-stack` | package naming is now aligned to Kolm, but registry/channel names still need package-release proof before public install claims |
| `package.json` version | `0.2.6` | release manifest must bind this version to commit, generated files, package artifacts, image digests, screenshots, and smoke reports |
| package scripts | 57 | script surface is broad enough to require a generated script catalog with owner, class, inputs, outputs, timeout, and release relevance |
| release/build/verify-like package scripts | 54 | build discipline is strong, but finality depends on ordering, CI binding, and evidence archive, not script count |
| `scripts/` files | 343 files / 25,958,930 bytes | automation has become a product surface; each release-critical script needs ownership, deterministic output, and machine-readable failure semantics |
| `tests/` files | 567 files / 7,319,376 bytes | breadth is strong; completion requires test taxonomy, shard history, flake policy, env isolation, and failure triage ownership |
| `reports/` files | 95,988 files / 11,302,406,649 bytes | evidence volume is high enough to require retention, pruning, naming, checksum, and release-bundle policy |
| UI audit reports | 279 `report.md` files | screenshot proof exists, but final release must point to the exact final-commit dark/light desktop/mobile reports and manual route-review reports |
| latest UI report examples | focused polish, manual-use home/product/pricing/account, frontend light mobile chunks | useful evidence, but not final proof unless commit, route set, theme, viewport, and dirty-tree status are bound in the release manifest |
| GitHub workflows | 11 files | CI exists, but release ownership is incomplete without a workflow ledger |
| workflows with explicit `permissions` | 3 of 11 | every workflow must declare least-privilege permissions; implicit token permissions are unacceptable for a state-of-the-art release path |
| workflows using secrets | 5 of 11 | secrets must be environment-scoped, rotated, masked, and replaced with OIDC where the provider supports it |
| workflows with `contents: write` | 2 of 11 | write permissions must be narrowed to publish/deploy jobs with protected environments and explicit review gates |
| workflows with `id-token: write` | 0 of 11 | release jobs cannot claim modern OIDC cloud auth or provenance generation until OIDC is added where applicable |
| workflows with attestation permissions/references | 0 of 11 | package, container, SBOM, and release-bundle provenance is not complete until artifact attestations exist |
| workflow `uses:` refs | 20 total / 0 SHA-pinned by static scan | third-party action immutability needs SHA pinning or a documented exception policy with review automation |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` | release umbrella covers refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, npm tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, `kolm doctor`, `kolm whoami`, artifact verify, billing tiers, sharding, JSON, and skip flags | strong local release driver; needs CI-first JSON packet, generated manifest, final-commit clean-tree binding, and production follow-up gates |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` and `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | separate local and production smoke runners exist | local/prod parity needs route-count binding, auth-class classification, key-safe logging, and final production URL evidence |
| `Dockerfile` | base image digest-pinned but uses `npm ci --omit=dev || npm install --omit=dev`, runs as default user, and has no visible healthcheck | digest pinning is good; fallback install, root/default runtime, and missing healthcheck are release-hardening gaps |
| `vercel.json` | 2,390 lines with security headers, large redirect/rewrite surface, CSP, and production/backend origin references | deploy config is substantial and must be owned by route group; legacy backend/domain exceptions need explicit production migration or exception status |
| current doc file state | blueprint remains untracked | no final release claim can be made until the release branch has clean-tree proof including this blueprint or an intentional archive policy |
| current product code state | `vercel.json` and multiple public assets are dirty from parallel work | release must wait for the final frontend/backend snapshot, then rerun all release, surface, screenshot, and production gates on one commit |

##### Node 13 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | current official source | Kolm release rule |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA v1.2 | https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/ | release artifacts must have source traceability, builder identity, provenance metadata, and verification instructions before they count as release-grade |
| GitHub artifact attestations | https://docs.github.com/actions/security-for-github-actions/using-artifact-attestations/using-artifact-attestations-to-establish-provenance-for-builds | release workflows must use `id-token: write`, `contents: read`, and attestation permissions for packages, containers, SBOMs, SDK bundles, and signed evidence archives |
| GitHub Actions secure use | https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use | workflows must declare least privilege, pin or justify third-party actions, constrain secrets, prefer OIDC, and block dangerous PR/deploy permission paths |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | https://openssf.org/scorecard/ | release evidence must include Scorecard or equivalent policy results for branch protection, token permissions, dependency review, dangerous workflows, and pinned dependencies |
| Docker build best practices | https://docs.docker.com/build/building/best-practices/ | containers must use deterministic installs, trusted minimal bases, non-root runtime where possible, healthchecks, CI image tests, and scanned SBOM/provenance |
| Vercel production rollback | https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/rollback-production-deployment | production deploy cannot be called complete without preview smoke, production smoke, log check, rollback target, and last-known-good proof |
| DORA delivery metrics | https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | release operations must track deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, failed deployment recovery time, and rework/fix-forward rate |
| OpenTelemetry | https://opentelemetry.io/docs/ | production release proof must include trace, metric, and log coverage for API, model, provider, artifact, distill, capture, account, storage, and auth flows |

##### `kolm-release-evidence-contract-v2`

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-release-evidence-contract-v2",
  "release_identity": {
    "commit": "required_git_sha",
    "branch": "required_branch",
    "version": "package_json_version",
    "build_id": "ci_or_local_release_id",
    "released_by": "human_or_automation_identity",
    "started_at": "iso8601",
    "completed_at": "iso8601"
  },
  "source_state": {
    "git_status": "clean_required_for_final",
    "allowed_dirty_paths": [],
    "untracked_paths": [],
    "submodule_state": "required_if_present",
    "parallel_agent_note": "required_when_dirty"
  },

      "sitemap",

    "lint_refs": "required",
    "local_surfaces_deep": "required",
    "claims_scope": "required",
    "brand": "required",
    "package_release": "required",
    "test_count": "required",
    "skips": "explain_required"
  },
  "workflows": {
    "workflow_ledger": "required",
    "permissions_explicit": "all_workflows_required",
    "write_permissions_justified": true,
    "third_party_action_policy": "sha_pin_or_exception",
    "oidc_for_deploy_or_publish": "required_where_supported",
    "attestations": "required_for_release_artifacts"
  },
  "supply_chain": {
    "sbom": "required",
    "provenance": "required",
    "artifact_attestations": "required",
    "scorecard_or_equivalent": "required",
    "secret_scan": "required",
    "dependency_review": "required"
  },
  "deployments": {
    "preview_url": "required",
    "preview_smoke": "required",
    "production_url": "required",

  },

    "dark_desktop": "required_for_frontend_release",
    "dark_mobile": "required_for_frontend_release",
    "light_desktop": "required_for_frontend_release",
    "light_mobile": "required_for_frontend_release",
    "manual_changed_routes": "required",
    "authenticated_account": "required_when_account_changed"
  },
  "observability": {
    "release_trace": "required",
    "api_slo_dashboard": "required",
    "model_provider_dashboard": "required",
    "distill_job_dashboard": "required",
    "storage_readiness_dashboard": "required",
    "auth_error_dashboard": "required",
    "alerts": "required"
  },
  "rollback": {
    "rollback_command": "required",
    "rollback_owner": "required",
    "rollback_smoke": "required",
    "incident_template": "required",
    "dora_metrics_updated": true
  },
  "external_gates": {
    "package_releases": "may_be_blocked_but_visible",
    "live_certifications": "may_be_blocked_but_visible",
    "public_benchmarks": "may_be_blocked_but_visible",
    "partner_adoption": "may_be_blocked_but_visible"
  },
  "evidence_archive": {
    "directory": "required",
    "manifest": "required",
    "checksums": "required",
    "logs": "required",
    "screenshots": "required",
    "smoke_json": "required",
    "generated_files": "required",
    "packages": "required_when_built",
    "containers": "required_when_built",
    "attestations": "required_when_built"
  }
}
```

##### Node 13 V2 Release Proof Classes

| proof class | release evidence required | failure mode it prevents |
|---|---|---|
| source proof | clean git status, commit SHA, branch, submodule state, allowed dirty-path list, untracked-path list | deploying a state that cannot be reconstructed from source |
| generated proof | ordered generation DAG, hashes, stale-check output, generator versions, no parallel race | docs/OpenAPI/product graph/service worker drift |
| test proof | test taxonomy, shard list, count, duration, skips, flake ledger, env isolation | treating a green partial run as full release proof |
| product-surface proof | local and prod route/group counts, auth classes, smoke result JSON, route-owner map | hidden broken surface after deploy |
| screenshot proof | dark/light desktop/mobile reports, manual changed-route screenshots, account screenshots where applicable | visually broken live product despite passing API tests |
| package proof | package manifests, tarballs, registry dry-run or publish evidence, install smoke, SBOM, provenance | claiming installability before package channels are real |
| container proof | Docker build output, image digest, healthcheck result, non-root policy, scan result, SBOM/provenance | shipping a non-reproducible or weak container path |
| workflow proof | permissions ledger, action refs, secrets/env mapping, trigger map, branch/environment protection | CI permissions and supply-chain drift |
| deployment proof | preview URL, production URL, Vercel deployment ID, log archive, environment class, release owner | losing the link between tested build and deployed build |
| rollback proof | rollback target, command, owner, last-known-good, rollback smoke, incident note | discovering rollback only after production failure |
| observability proof | traces, metrics, logs, dashboards, alerts, request IDs, provider/model dimensions | inability to debug bad releases or provider/model regressions |
| DORA proof | deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, failed deployment recovery time, fix-forward/revert label | optimizing release speed without reliability feedback |

##### Node 13 V2 Gate Sequencing Redline

The release pipeline must become a deterministic DAG, not a pile of commands:

1. freeze source state and write a draft release manifest;
2. install dependencies from lockfiles with no fallback to floating install paths;
3. run syntax/static safety checks that do not depend on generated files;
4. generate API route inventory, API reference, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, SDK manifest, sitemap, service worker, and AI/search context in a fixed serial order;
5. run stale-file checks immediately after generation;
6. run unit/integration tests with deterministic sharding and machine-readable results;
7. run claim-scope, brand, security, package, benchmark, and surface ownership gates;
8. run local HTTP deep surface smoke with env-secrets redaction;
9. build packages/containers and generate SBOM/provenance/attestation artifacts;
10. run preview deploy and preview smoke;
11. run visual screenshot audits after frontend dirty files settle;
12. promote to production only after preview evidence is archived;
13. run production smoke and production screenshot/account checks;
14. write final release manifest and checksums;
15. record rollback target, DORA metrics, and release owner signoff.

No final release should depend on chat transcript summaries. Every result must be reconstructable from files, commands, URLs, hashes, and archived outputs.

##### Node 13 V2 Workflow Hardening Redline

| workflow requirement | completion rule |
|---|---|
| explicit permissions | every workflow starts with `permissions` and the default is read-only or none |
| write scope | `contents: write`, `packages: write`, `deployments: write`, and similar scopes are allowed only on release jobs with protected environments |
| OIDC | deploy/publish jobs use OIDC where the target provider supports it instead of long-lived deploy secrets |
| artifact attestations | release artifacts, package bundles, containers, SBOMs, and evidence bundles receive attestations |
| action pinning | third-party actions in release workflows are pinned by SHA or listed in an exception file with owner, update cadence, and risk acceptance |
| secret containment | PR workflows cannot access deploy/package secrets; secrets are environment-scoped and never printed by smoke scripts |
| workflow lint | CI rejects implicit permissions, unpinned release actions, unsafe `pull_request_target` patterns, shell injection risks, and unmasked secrets |
| security scans | CodeQL or equivalent SAST, secret scanning, dependency review, npm audit policy, workflow policy scan, and OpenSSF Scorecard or equivalent run before release |
| generated assets | workflows run generation in one process order and do not run API ref/OpenAPI/product graph builders in parallel if they share files |
| archive upload | release workflows upload manifest, logs, generated artifacts, screenshots, SBOMs, provenance, and smoke outputs with checksums |

##### Node 13 V2 Deployment And Production Smoke Redline

| deployment area | required final behavior |
|---|---|
| preview deploy | every release candidate gets a preview URL tied to commit, branch, build ID, env class, and manifest |
| preview smoke | preview smoke runs public, docs, account-safe, API, storage readiness, product graph, billing, and route ownership checks without printing secrets |
| production promote | production promotion happens only after preview smoke, release verify, visual proof, and manifest checks pass |
| production smoke | production smoke runs against the canonical domain, records route count and auth class, separates unauthenticated, authenticated, provider-config, and external-gate failures |
| API keys | smoke accepts environment-provided `ks_` candidates, reports which class succeeded without printing values, and treats auth failure as a separate diagnostic |
| account checks | authenticated account smoke verifies overview, product matrix, storage readiness, billing state, key pages, and readiness closeout when account surfaces changed |
| screenshot checks | final frontend release includes dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots plus manual changed-route screenshots and media/video/demo visibility checks |
| logs | deploy logs, runtime logs, smoke logs, screenshots, route JSON, and trace IDs are archived and linked from the release manifest |
| rollback | every production deploy records last-known-good URL, rollback command, rollback owner, rollback smoke plan, and incident template |
| old-brand origins | any Kolmogorov-era backend/repo/domain URL in deploy config is either migrated, removed, or recorded as an explicit compatibility exception with owner and deadline |

| `W-REL-V2-024` | `13.9` | Add release completion certificate. | Release is complete only when manifest, local gates, prod smoke, screenshots, package/container proof, provenance, rollback, observability, external-gate scope, and clean-tree proof agree. |

##### Node 13 V2 Relax Redline

Node `13` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. all 57 package scripts and 343 script files are classified by owner, release role, inputs, outputs, timeout, env needs, and generated-artifact impact;
2. all 11 workflows declare least-privilege permissions, constrain write scopes, protect secrets, and either SHA-pin external actions or list reviewed exceptions;
3. deploy/publish workflows use OIDC where supported and generate SLSA-aligned provenance plus artifact attestations for release artifacts;
4. the Docker path is deterministic, non-root or explicitly justified, healthchecked, scanned, SBOMed, and smoke-tested;
5. `vercel.json` route, redirect, rewrite, header, function, CSP, and backend-origin entries are owned and old-brand compatibility entries are documented or removed;
6. generated API, docs, SDK, product graph, service worker, sitemap, and AI/search artifacts are generated in a serial race-free order and verified fresh;
7. local `release:verify`, `verify:depth`, `lint:refs`, `local:surfaces:deep`, claims, brand, package, and screenshot gates pass on the same clean final commit;
8. preview deploy has preview smoke, logs, screenshots where relevant, and manifest evidence before promotion;
9. production deploy has canonical-domain smoke, authenticated account checks when account changed, key-safe logging, trace IDs, and archived logs;
10. every release has a last-known-good rollback target, rollback owner, rollback smoke plan, and incident template;
11. DORA and release-quality metrics are updated from actual deploy history, not hand-written summaries;
12. report retention is controlled so the 95,988 current report files become intentional evidence, not unmanaged build debris;
13. external package, certification, benchmark, and partner gates remain visible and are never marketed as shipped unless evidence exists;
14. the final release evidence bundle lets an engineer or auditor reconstruct exactly what shipped without reading chat history.

If any item fails, Kolm may have a good local build, but node `13` is not production-release complete.

### Source Ownership, Architecture, And Code Quality Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `14`: source ownership and code quality. The point is not to run another scanner and pretend that means "reviewed." The point is to make every file class accountable: owned, generated, archived, fixture-only, release-critical, public-facing, or delete-candidate. A state-of-the-art product cannot have its most important code trapped in giant files, stale generated HTML, unclear scratch artifacts, or unclassified legacy brand strings.

This pass used tracked-file inventory from the current worktree. It did not edit source code, because active frontend/backend agents are changing product files.

#### Current Tracked File Inventory

| measurement | current value | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| tracked files | 3,651 | the repository now needs a file ledger; ad hoc review will not scale |
| largest file-count directory | `public`, 1,088 tracked files | public/generated pages dominate codebase hygiene and must be owned by generator, template, and live-route class |
| largest byte directory | `qa`, 89,027,228 bytes across 92 files | QA artifacts need retention policy, not permanent undifferentiated repository weight |
| tests | 567 tracked files | tests are broad enough to require taxonomy, shard ownership, and failure semantics |
| source modules | `src`, 392 files, 6,540,534 bytes | shared backend logic is substantial and needs module-boundary enforcement |
| scripts | 267 files, 1,593,968 bytes | build/release scripts need ownership because they directly mutate generated assets |
| docs | 157 files, 6,858,891 bytes | docs are product surface, not collateral; docs drift must be gated like code drift |
| archive | 455 files, 10,436,894 bytes | archive files must not leak stale product claims into current public truth |
| CLI | 2 files, 2,042,926 bytes | CLI is too concentrated; one file contains most behavior |
| untracked generated/scratch items | `.w850-shots`, `scripts/w852-*`, malformed Windows-path failure files, temp observation JSON | final clean git requires explicit keep/ignore/delete decisions from the owning agent |

The two giant files are the clearest architecture smell in the current codebase. They may be functioning, but they make review, parallel development, security audit, and state-of-the-art iteration harder than necessary.

#### Current Marker Inventory

Debt markers are a triage signal, not proof that each occurrence is wrong. Some are fixtures, intentional mock strings, or test labels. They still need classification because "100 percent complete" cannot depend on unknown TODO/mock/stub text.

Brand and encoding marker scan:

Current examples show the legacy brand problem is not just historical research docs. Public pages still link to `https://github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`; that may be the actual repository URL today, but the product rule needs to decide whether that is an allowed legal/repo exception or a rename/mirror blocker. Until classified, "remove all Kolmogorov to Kolm" remains unproven.

#### Official Code Quality Standards

| standard | source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| ISO/IEC 25010 | ISO says the 2023 product quality model applies to software/ICT products and defines quality characteristics and subcharacteristics for specifying, measuring, and evaluating product quality: https://www.iso.org/standard/78176.html | every master spec node should map to quality attributes such as functional suitability, reliability, performance efficiency, interaction capability, security, compatibility, maintainability, flexibility, and portability |
| NIST SSDF | NIST SP 800-218 recommends secure development practices to reduce released vulnerabilities, mitigate exploitation impact, and address root causes: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final | release-critical code must have secure-development evidence, not only feature tests |
| OWASP ASVS | OWASP ASVS provides a basis for testing web application technical security controls and secure-development requirements: https://owasp.org/www-project-application-security-verification-standard/ | API, account, auth, billing, admin, and public web surfaces should map security requirements to ASVS identifiers where practical |
| ESLint | ESLint statically analyzes JavaScript to find problems and can run in CI: https://eslint.org/ | all JavaScript/CJS/MJS public, server, CLI, and script code should be covered by a documented lint profile with exceptions reviewed |
| TypeScript | TypeScript describes itself as a static typechecker for JavaScript programs that runs before code executes: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/intro.html | the JS-heavy backend should gain type contracts through TypeScript, JSDoc checking, generated `.d.ts`, or equivalent schema validation where rewrite is not practical |
| Node test runner | Node's test runner can execute matching test files with `node --test`, supports concurrency and timeout control, and runs files in child processes when isolation is enabled: https://nodejs.org/api/test.html | the 560+ tests need deterministic discovery, concurrency policy, timeout budgets, and explicit skipped/todo semantics |

#### Source Ownership Completion Contract

Every tracked and generated file must eventually answer:

1. Is it source, generated output, fixture, benchmark data, screenshot/media, archive, scratch, config, package, or deploy artifact?
2. Which master spec node owns it?
3. Which command creates, verifies, or refreshes it?
4. Is it allowed in release builds and published packages?
5. Does it contain public copy, product claims, secrets, customer-like data, or benchmark evidence?
6. Is old `Kolmogorov` wording intentional, historical, or a blocker?
7. Is non-ASCII text intentional and correctly encoded?
8. Does it have tests, smoke coverage, or screenshot coverage if user-facing?
9. Can it be split, deleted, regenerated, archived, or excluded from release?
10. What is the proof required to mark it complete?

#### Master Spec Tree Branch 14

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `14.0` | file ledger | does every tracked file have a primary owner and class? | generated `file-ledger.json`, root counts, owner node, release inclusion status |
| `14.1` | directory architecture | do roots map cleanly to product, build, docs, test, archive, and artifact responsibilities? | root ownership table and no ambiguous public/generated/template drift |
| `14.2` | single-file complexity | are giant files split into reviewable modules? | refactor plan and passing tests for `cli/kolm.js`, `src/router.js`, major builders, and ML pipelines |
| `14.3` | generated artifacts | can generated outputs be reproduced and stale-checked? | generator source map, serial build graph, stale checks, and generated-file header policy |
| `14.4` | archive and fixture policy | are archives/fixtures isolated from live claims and release packages? | archive allowlist, fixture naming, no public SEO indexing of stale archives unless intentional |
| `14.5` | brand and encoding hygiene | are legacy names and mojibake classified or eliminated? | brand exception ledger, encoding scan, public zero-defect rule, archive/historical exceptions |
| `14.6` | static analysis and typing | does code have automated maintainability feedback? | ESLint coverage, type-check/JSDoc/schema coverage, complexity thresholds, no silent excludes |
| `14.7` | secrets and data hygiene | are key-shaped strings, temp files, logs, and customer-like data classified? | secret scanner, fixture allowlist, redaction policy, no sensitive scratch artifacts |
| `14.8` | test and fixture quality | are mocks/stubs/todos intentional and scoped? | debt-marker classifier, skipped/todo test ledger, fixture inventory, mock boundary policy |
| `14.9` | clean working tree | can release be made from an intentional diff only? | no malformed path artifacts, no untracked scratch unless ignored, no unrelated dirty files |

- W-CODE-003: create a generated-file source map for `public`, docs HTML, OpenAPI/API refs, product graph, SDK manifests, account pages, service worker, sitemaps, screenshots, and QA reports.

- W-CODE-005: split `src/router.js` by route group and middleware: auth, billing, capture/privacy, distill/eval, artifacts/runtime, cloud/devices, governance/security, marketplace/agents, docs/spec.

- W-CODE-012: add `.gitignore` or cleanup guidance only after owner approval so parallel-agent evidence files are not accidentally destroyed.
- W-CODE-013: add ESLint rule coverage for server, public JS, CLI, scripts, workers, and tests, with documented exceptions and no silent directory skips.
- W-CODE-014: introduce type contracts incrementally: JSDoc `checkJs`, generated types from OpenAPI/product graph, or TypeScript modules for high-churn boundaries.
- W-CODE-015: create maximum file-size and complexity budgets, with temporary exemptions for `cli/kolm.js` and `src/router.js` that shrink every wave.
- W-CODE-016: create dependency-boundary checks so public pages do not import backend-only code, tests do not rely on production secrets, and scripts do not mutate unrelated outputs.
- W-CODE-017: classify archive files as historical evidence and prevent archive content from polluting current brand, SEO, claim, or release readiness scans.
- W-CODE-018: create fixture taxonomy for benchmark, redaction, model, API, auth, billing, screenshot, and route fixtures, including synthetic-data labeling.
- W-CODE-019: add a clean-tree preflight that detects malformed path artifacts, untracked scratch files, generated drift, and unrelated dirty files before deploy.
- W-CODE-020: produce a codebase redline dashboard with counts for file ownership, giant files, generated drift, debt markers, brand exceptions, encoding findings, skipped tests, and release blockers.

#### Codebase Redline

Kolm cannot be called codebase-complete until this branch proves:

1. every tracked file is in the file ledger with owner, class, release policy, and proof command;
2. `cli/kolm.js` and `src/router.js` have explicit decomposition plans and shrinking complexity budgets;
3. generated public/docs/account files can be regenerated from known templates without hidden manual edits;
4. public legacy-brand strings are zero or listed in a reviewed exception ledger;
5. mojibake and malformed path artifacts are gone from release outputs;
6. debt markers are classified and no release-blocking TODO/stub/mock text remains hidden;
7. static analysis and type/schema contracts cover the JS-heavy backend, CLI, public JS, scripts, and workers;
8. scratch artifacts and screenshots are either intentionally committed evidence, ignored local output, or removed before release;
9. the final release diff can be reviewed by humans without a giant undifferentiated pile of generated churn.

#### Node 14 V2 Source Ownership, Architecture, And Code Quality Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `14`. It is a codebase-completion specification only. It does not rewrite source files while frontend, backend, research, and release agents are active. It turns the current architecture evidence into an atomic handoff: every file must have an owner, class, generator, proof command, release policy, and cleanup/refactor status before the codebase can be called state-of-the-art complete.

Node `14` is where "review every Kolm file" becomes tractable. Manual review still matters, but it must be backed by a ledger that prevents files from disappearing into public/generated/archive/scratch ambiguity. A 3,651-file repository cannot be completed by memory or chat transcript. It needs path-level ownership, generated-file provenance, module boundaries, lint/type/schema coverage, artifact retention rules, and clean-diff constraints.

##### Node 14 V2 Current Evidence

| area | current evidence | completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| tracked files | 3,651 | every file needs owner, class, release policy, generator/proof command, and master spec node |
| tracked public files | 1,088 / 35,987,034 bytes | public pages/assets dominate product surface and must be mapped to templates, generators, live routes, screenshots, SEO, and claim scope |
| tracked tests | 567 / 7,319,376 bytes | tests are broad, but codebase completion needs ownership, coverage intent, shard class, fixture class, and failure semantics |
| tracked `src` files | 392 / 6,540,534 bytes | backend is JavaScript-heavy and needs module boundaries, type/schema contracts, and ownership down to route group |
| tracked scripts | 267 / 1,593,968 bytes | tracked automation must be distinguished from untracked in-progress scripts before release |
| current `scripts/` directory | 343 files / 25,958,930 bytes | active local work has added many script artifacts; the final source ledger must separate tracked source from untracked/generated/scratch scripts |
| tracked docs | 157 / 6,858,891 bytes | docs are a product surface and research archive; public claims require freshness and scope classification |
| tracked `qa` artifacts | 92 files / 89,027,228 bytes | large screenshot evidence should move under release archive policy or LFS/artifact storage if retained |
| tracked `audit-shots` artifacts | 54 files / 30,400,361 bytes | screenshots are useful evidence but should not remain unmanaged source-tree weight |
| `CODEOWNERS` | absent in root and `.github/` | no platform-level owner enforcement exists yet |
| `docs/file-ledger.json` | absent | the source ownership contract is not machine-enforced yet |
| `docs/generated-files.json` | absent | generated output provenance is still implicit |
| `docs/repo-workspaces.json` | absent | workspace/app/package ownership is not machine-readable |
| TypeScript/JS project config | `tsconfig.json` absent, `jsconfig.json` absent | the JS-heavy codebase does not yet have repo-wide static type/project boundaries |
| ESLint config | present, 3,424 bytes | correctness lint exists, but public JS, data, trainer, VS Code package, SDK roots, generated/dist/build paths are excluded and need explicit policy |
| largest tracked source file | `cli/kolm.js`, 2,021,134 bytes / 42,842 lines / 103 debt-token matches | CLI remains a review bottleneck and must be split by command family without behavior drift |
| largest backend route file | `src/router.js`, 953,495 bytes / 19,912 lines / 38 debt-token matches | router remains a security/API/review bottleneck and must be decomposed by route group |
| major artifact compiler files | `src/binder.js` 2,601 lines, `src/artifact.js` 2,194 lines, `src/distill-pipeline.js` 1,128 lines | high-value AI paths need smaller seams, fixtures, schemas, and benchmark/provenance tests |
| generated docs builders | `scripts/build-api-ref.cjs`, `scripts/build-openapi.cjs`, `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs` | generators are release-critical and should expose inputs/outputs/freshness hashes |
| active frontend files | `public/nav.js`, `public/styles.css`, `public/brand-refresh.css`, `public/home-refresh.css` | frontend dirty work must be integrated only after ownership and screenshot evidence settle |
| malformed tracked path evidence | quoted path resembling `C...UsersuserDesktopkolmogorov-stackpublicjszip.min.js` appears in `git ls-files` | path corruption must be repaired or explicitly classified before final clean-git proof |
| largest tracked content artifact | `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png`, 7,031,506 bytes | public hero media must have source, compression, dimensions, licensing/provenance, and performance budget |
| largest tracked research doc | `docs/research/kolm-billion-dollar-distillation-lab-2026-05-24.md`, 3,910,157 bytes / 113,727 lines | research is valuable but should be archived/indexed so product docs and code review remain navigable |
| largest tracked QA images | multiple `qa/v720*` and `qa/v718*` PNGs around 1.8 MB to 2.9 MB | visual evidence should move to release-bundle or artifact policy instead of permanent unmanaged source bloat |

##### Node 14 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | current official source | Kolm codebase rule |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners | source ownership should be enforced through `.github/CODEOWNERS` or an equivalent generated verifier, with root/codeowner self-ownership protected |
| GitHub rulesets | https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-rulesets/about-rulesets | branch/ruleset policy should require codeowner review, release gates, signed/protected release paths, and status checks from trusted apps |
| ESLint | https://eslint.org/ | every JS/CJS/MJS surface must either be linted or listed in an explicit generated/vendor/legacy exception ledger |
| TypeScript JavaScript checking | https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/intro.html | JS can stay JS, but high-risk boundaries need JSDoc/checkJs/generated type/schema contracts so API, CLI, product graph, and route contracts are statically checked |
| Git LFS | https://git-lfs.com/ | large binary evidence/media should be tracked through LFS/artifact storage or kept out of source control with a release-bundle pointer |
| NIST SSDF | https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final | source ownership, secure coding, review, dependency, and vulnerability response practices must be documented and verifiable |
| ISO/IEC 25010 | https://www.iso.org/standard/78176.html | maintainability, modularity, analyzability, modifiability, testability, portability, security, and reliability should map to concrete codebase gates |

##### `kolm-source-ownership-contract-v2`

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-source-ownership-contract-v2",
  "path": "repo_relative_path",
  "path_state": {
    "valid_path": true,
    "normalized_separators": true,
    "case_policy": "canonical",
    "malformed_path_exception": "not_allowed_for_release"
  },
  "classification": {
    "file_class": "source|generated|fixture|benchmark|screenshot|media|archive|config|package|deploy|scratch|vendor|research",
    "master_spec_node": "0-51",
    "product_surface": "optional_surface_id",
    "release_inclusion": "ship|test_only|docs_only|archive_only|exclude|artifact_bundle",
    "public_claim_surface": false,
    "customer_data_risk": "none|synthetic|possible|real_forbidden"
  },
  "ownership": {
    "primary_owner": "required",
    "backup_owner": "required_for_p0",
    "review_level": "self|codeowner|security|release|legal|ml",
    "parallel_agent_lane": "frontend|backend|research|release|none"
  },
  "provenance": {
    "manual_or_generated": "manual|generated|vendored|external",
    "generator_command": "required_if_generated",
    "source_template": "required_if_generated",
    "input_paths": [],
    "output_paths": [],
    "freshness_check": "required_if_generated"
  },
  "quality": {
    "linted": "required_or_exception",
    "type_or_schema_checked": "required_for_api_cli_runtime_boundaries",
    "unit_tests": [],
    "integration_tests": [],
    "smoke_tests": [],
    "screenshot_tests": [],
    "debt_markers": "classified_required",
    "max_lines_budget": "required_for_source",
    "max_bytes_budget": "required_for_media_or_generated"
  },
  "security": {
    "secret_scan_status": "required",
    "pii_phi_risk": "required",
    "auth_boundary": "required_if_route_or_account",
    "tenant_boundary": "required_if_tenant_data",
    "dependency_license": "required_if_vendored_or_package"
  },
  "cleanup": {
    "delete_candidate": false,
    "split_candidate": false,
    "archive_candidate": false,
    "lfs_or_artifact_candidate": false,
    "deadline": "required_if_exception"
  }
}
```

##### Node 14 V2 Architecture Budgets

| budget | target | exception policy |
|---|---:|---|
| ordinary source file | 500 lines or less | exception requires owner, split plan, and shrinking budget |
| route module | 800 lines or less | route groups can exceed only with generated code or documented middleware density |
| CLI command module | 600 lines or less | shared renderer/client helpers live outside command modules |
| build/audit script | 400 lines or less | longer scripts require input/output contract and tests |
| generated public HTML | generated with source template and route owner | manual edits to generated HTML are forbidden unless generator is updated |
| CSS page file | page-owned and tokenized | global overrides require design-system owner approval |
| public JS | linted or generated/vendor exception | navigation/account/demo JS cannot be excluded silently |
| media asset | performance budget and source/provenance | assets above 500 KB require compression/LFS/artifact decision |
| research document | indexed and scoped | docs above 250 KB require summary, table of contents, and archive/search policy |
| screenshot/QA artifact | release-bundle pointer preferred | committed screenshots require manifest, date, route, viewport, and retention status |

##### Node 14 V2 Monolith Decomposition Plan

| file | current size | target decomposition |
|---|---:|---|
| `cli/kolm.js` | 42,842 lines | split into command modules for auth/account, gateway/capture, compile/artifact, distill/eval, cloud/storage, billing/plans, surfaces/readiness, TUI, renderers, shared API client, and output schemas |
| `src/router.js` | 19,912 lines | split by route group with a route registry preserving paths, auth, envelope, OpenAPI tags, product-surface owner, and smoke probe metadata |
| `src/binder.js` | 2,601 lines | separate artifact parsing, validation, dependency/provenance, signing, error normalization, and fixture helpers |
| `src/artifact.js` | 2,194 lines | separate format schema, IO, hash/signature, metadata, runtime adapters, and verification helpers |
| `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs` | 1,258 lines | separate account template data, nav/sidebar, page renderer, route registry, and screenshot hooks |
| `public/brand-refresh.css` | 4,442 lines | split into token layer, base components, page-specific rules, motion, utilities, and legacy removal queue |
| `public/styles.css` | 2,718 lines | consolidate overlap with brand refresh, nav, account, and page CSS under node `12` token ownership |
| `docs/research/kolm-billion-dollar-distillation-lab-2026-05-24.md` | 113,727 lines | keep as research archive but create a concise indexed implementation brief and search metadata |

The goal is not file splitting for its own sake. The goal is reviewability: each module should have one reason to change, one product owner, one test/smoke strategy, and one failure vocabulary.

##### Node 14 V2 File Classes

| file class | release rule |
|---|---|
| `source` | hand-authored production code; must have owner, tests or smoke, lint/type/schema policy, and max-size budget |
| `generated` | never manually edited; must have generator command, inputs, output hash, and stale check |
| `fixture` | synthetic/test data only; must state data class and be excluded from live product claims |
| `benchmark` | evidence data; must include method, date, model/provider/runtime versions, raw report hash, and claim scope |
| `screenshot` | visual evidence; must include route, theme, viewport, date, commit, and retention policy |
| `media` | shipped asset; must include source/provenance, dimensions, alt/caption need, performance budget, and license status |
| `archive` | historical; excluded from live brand/claim/SEO unless explicitly surfaced |
| `config` | build/deploy/dev config; must have owner and release impact classification |
| `package` | package metadata/source; must have install smoke and package-release state |
| `deploy` | deployment/runtime config; must have environment and rollback policy |
| `scratch` | local temporary output; cannot be part of final release unless promoted to evidence |
| `vendor` | third-party code; must have source URL/version/license/hash and update policy |
| `research` | strategic/spec content; must have summary, source list, implementation handoff, and archive/index policy |

| `W-CODE-V2-001` | `14.0` | Build `docs/file-ledger.json`. | All 3,651 tracked files have path state, class, owner, master node, release policy, generator/proof command, and cleanup status. |
| `W-CODE-V2-002` | `14.0` | Add `.github/CODEOWNERS` or equivalent verifier. | P0 roots and release-critical files require owner review and the ownership file owns itself. |
| `W-CODE-V2-003` | `14.0` | Add repo ruleset/branch-policy spec. | Required status checks, codeowner review, signed/reviewed release paths, protected envs, and trusted check sources are documented. |
| `W-CODE-V2-004` | `14.1` | Create `docs/repo-workspaces.json`. | Every root/app/package/worker/SDK has purpose, owner, build command, test command, smoke command, and release inclusion. |

| `W-CODE-V2-006` | `14.1` | Repair malformed tracked path evidence. | Quoted Windows-path-like `publicjszip.min.js` entry is removed, restored to a valid repo path, or documented as a release-blocking artifact until fixed. |
| `W-CODE-V2-007` | `14.2` | Split `cli/kolm.js` by command family. | CLI command modules preserve output, help text, exit codes, JSON schemas, and tests while the root file shrinks every wave. |
| `W-CODE-V2-008` | `14.2` | Split `src/router.js` by route group. | Route registry preserves path, method, auth, envelope, product surface, OpenAPI tag, and smoke ownership with no route drift. |
| `W-CODE-V2-009` | `14.2` | Add monolith complexity budgets. | Any source file above budget has owner, split plan, tests, deadline, and trend-to-zero exception status. |
