| `W-CODE-V2-016` | `14.6` | Expand ESLint coverage policy. | Public JS, scripts, workers, CLI, tests, and server code are linted or explicitly excluded as generated/vendor/legacy with owner and deadline. |
| `W-CODE-V2-017` | `14.6` | Add JS type/schema contracts. | API envelopes, product graph, OpenAPI, route registry, CLI JSON, account state, and artifact metadata have JSDoc/checkJs/TypeScript/schema validation. |
| `W-CODE-V2-018` | `14.6` | Add dependency-boundary rules. | Public code cannot import backend-only modules, tests cannot require production secrets, scripts cannot mutate unrelated generated outputs. |
| `W-CODE-V2-019` | `14.7` | Add secret/data hygiene ledger. | Key-shaped strings, local temp JSON, logs, fixture data, benchmark data, and customer-like examples are classified and scanned. |
| `W-CODE-V2-020` | `14.8` | Add debt-marker classifier. | TODO/FIXME/HACK/stub/mock/placeholder/not-implemented markers become fixture, test label, intentional mock, tracked debt, or release blocker. |
| `W-CODE-V2-021` | `14.8` | Add fixture taxonomy. | Test, benchmark, model, auth, billing, screenshot, privacy, and route fixtures declare synthetic/real status and claim eligibility. |
| `W-CODE-V2-022` | `14.9` | Add scratch/untracked policy. | `.w850-shots`, temp observations, malformed path outputs, TAP/log files, and local screenshots are kept, ignored, archived, or deleted by owner decision. |
| `W-CODE-V2-023` | `14.9` | Add clean-diff review budget. | Final release diff separates source, generated, docs, screenshots, package artifacts, and cleanup so human review is possible. |
| `W-CODE-V2-024` | `14.9` | Add codebase completion certificate. | Codebase is complete only when ownership, generated provenance, lint/type/schema, monolith budgets, brand/encoding, artifact policy, tests, and clean-tree proof agree. |

##### Node 14 V2 Relax Redline

Node `14` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every tracked file and release-relevant untracked file is represented in `docs/file-ledger.json`;
2. every file has owner, class, master spec node, release policy, generator/proof command, and cleanup status;
3. CODEOWNERS or an equivalent verifier enforces review ownership for P0 roots and release-critical files;
4. malformed tracked path artifacts are removed or repaired before final clean-git proof;
5. generated outputs are reproducible from known generators and cannot be manually patched into drift;
6. `cli/kolm.js`, `src/router.js`, and other monoliths have shrinking budgets and module-level tests;
7. public JS, server JS, CLI, scripts, workers, tests, and package code are linted or explicitly classified as generated/vendor/legacy exceptions;
8. high-risk JS boundaries have type or schema contracts even if the codebase remains primarily JavaScript;
9. old-brand strings, mojibake, key-shaped literals, temp files, screenshots, and customer-like data are classified with release-blocker status where needed;
10. archive, research, QA, screenshot, and media files have retention, indexing, artifact, or LFS policy;
11. debt markers are triaged so release-blocking TODO/stub/mock/placeholder text cannot hide in production paths;
12. the final diff is reviewable by humans, separating source changes from generated outputs and evidence artifacts.

If any item fails, the codebase may contain strong features, but it is not atomically complete or state-of-the-art maintainable.

#### Node 14 V3 Atomic File Ownership, Workspace Quarantine, And Codebase Finish Contract

This V3 extension keeps node `14` as the owner for "review every Kolm file" and leaves node `52` unused. The important correction is that codebase completion has two layers that must not be confused:

1. tracked release source, which currently has 3,652 Git-indexed paths and must be owned, classed, generated, tested, and reviewable;
2. local workspace artifacts, which currently include 122,453 non-ignored-by-this-scan files and must be quarantined, ignored, archived, or promoted to explicit evidence before any clean build claim.

This section is doc-only. It does not delete reports, screenshots, tmp bundles, cache outputs, data files, Rust targets, frontend files, or backend files while other agents are active. It makes the release redline explicit so a later cleanup/build-finishing agent can act without guessing.

##### Node 14 V3 Current Evidence Delta

| area | current evidence | completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| tracked paths from Git | 3,652 | the file-ledger requirement is still real and must cover every tracked path |
| tracked paths present on disk | 3,650 observed by filesystem lookup | at least one malformed tracked path is not a normal file and blocks clean source ownership |
| tracked bytes present | 200,774,308 | source tree is large enough that media, QA, research, and generated files need retention policy |
| largest tracked roots | `public` 1,088, `tests` 567, `archive` 455, `src` 392, `scripts` 267, `docs` 157 | ownership cannot be centralized in one person or one giant catch-all rule |
| tracked extension mix | `.html` 1,187, `.js` 1,011, `.md` 229, `.svg` 188, `.json` 179, `.png` 165 | static/public/generated and JavaScript-heavy backend code are the two largest finish risks |
| malformed tracked path | `"C\\357\\200\\272UsersuserDesktopkolmogorov-stackpublicjszip.min.js"` plus valid `public/jszip.min.js` | path corruption must be repaired or quarantined before final clean-git proof |
| CODEOWNERS | absent in root and `.github/` | no platform owner review enforcement exists |
| `docs/file-ledger.json` | absent | file ownership is not machine-enforced |
| current dirty status entries | 47 total: 13 modified, 34 untracked | final release cannot be claimed from this workspace until these are owned, committed, ignored, archived, or removed by owners |
| active modified roots | 11 public files, `src/intent.js`, `vercel.json` | parallel frontend/backend/deploy edits are active; node `14` must not overwrite them |
| untracked screenshot evidence | `.w850-shots` has 29 untracked entries in status | likely useful visual evidence, but not final source until retained or ignored by owner decision |
| malformed untracked roots | status includes two entries under root `"C` | cleanup must handle malformed path artifacts without deleting unrelated user work |
| workspace scan total | 122,453 files, 17,032,411,030 bytes | local workspace is far larger than the tracked source tree and requires artifact quarantine |
| largest local roots by size | `reports` 10,778.81 MB, `tmp` 2,619.16 MB, `sdk` 1,519.38 MB, `data` 718.01 MB, `tmp-screenshots` 607.10 MB | ignored/generated outputs dominate disk usage and must not leak into release diffs |
| ignored release evidence | `.gitignore` already ignores `reports/`, `tmp/`, `.shots/`, `.npm-cache*`, `data/*.json`, `data/*.sqlite`, Rust targets, logs, and UI debug outputs | ignore policy exists, but owner decisions are still needed for which evidence becomes release archive |
| largest local files | npm cache object 441.50 MB, observations JSON/bak/tmp around 173 MB each, launch bundles around 141 MB, SQLite 37.63 MB, Rust target libraries 18-27 MB | local cache/data/build outputs are not product source and need quarantine, backup, or documented retention |
| largest tracked source files | `cli/kolm.js` 2,021,134 bytes, `src/router.js` 953,495 bytes, `docs/research/kolm-billion-dollar-distillation-lab-2026-05-24.md` 3,910,157 bytes | reviewability redline remains: CLI/router/research must be split, indexed, or summarized |
| debt-like markers in broad workspace scan | 1,003 files with debt-like text, including generated docs and tmp copies | debt scans must be scoped by file class so generated/temp copies do not hide real production blockers |
| key-shaped strings in broad workspace scan | 195 files with key-shaped/provider-shaped matches | many are fixtures, SDK names, or docs, but every match needs class-based handling before secret-hygiene finality |

The current workspace should be treated as an active build lab, not a release-clean repository. The finish line is not "delete everything big." The finish line is that every path has an owner and class, every generated or ignored artifact has a retention rule, and final release evidence is archived intentionally.

##### Node 14 V3 Current Primary-Source Baselines

| baseline | current source | Kolm codebase rule |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | GitHub documents CODEOWNERS as the file that defines responsible people/teams, with locations in `.github/`, root, or `docs/`, automatic review requests, branch protection integration, a 3 MB size limit, and a recommendation to protect the CODEOWNERS file itself: https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners | add `.github/CODEOWNERS` or an equivalent verifier; protect ownership files; use wildcard groups so the ownership map stays under platform limits |
| GitHub rulesets | GitHub rulesets control branch/tag interactions, can require signed commits and block force pushes, and push rules can restrict paths, path lengths, and file sizes: https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/managing-rulesets/about-rulesets | define source-entry rules that block malformed paths, forbidden artifact roots, excessive file sizes, unreviewed generated outputs, and release-branch bypasses |
| Git LFS | Git LFS replaces large files such as datasets and graphics with text pointers while storing content remotely: https://git-lfs.com/ | large media, screenshots, benchmark datasets, and release evidence must be LFS/artifact-backed or intentionally excluded from source control |
| ESLint configuration | ESLint supports configuration files, rules, plugins, globals, parsers, and ignore configuration: https://eslint.org/docs/latest/use/configure/ | all JS/CJS/MJS roots must be linted or explicitly classed as generated/vendor/scratch with owner and deadline |
| TypeScript JavaScript checking | TypeScript documents type checking JavaScript files and JSDoc-supported JS projects: https://www.typescriptlang.org/docs/handbook/type-checking-javascript-files.html | Kolm can remain JavaScript-heavy, but route envelopes, CLI JSON, product graphs, account state, artifact metadata, and generated contracts need type/schema checking |
| Node test runner | Node's `node:test` module is stable and supports process-level isolation and `--test-concurrency`: https://nodejs.org/api/test.html | source extraction and file ownership changes need deterministic test discovery, isolation policy, concurrency budgets, and shard ownership |

##### Node 14 V3 Source Versus Workspace Model

| class | examples currently visible | final handling |
|---|---|---|
| tracked release source | `src`, `cli`, `scripts`, `public`, `docs`, `tests`, `packages`, `workers`, `.github` | must be in `docs/file-ledger.json` with owner, node, class, proof command, release policy, and cleanup state |
| tracked generated output | OpenAPI, API refs, product graphs, generated docs/account pages, service worker outputs | must be in `docs/generated-files.json` with generator, inputs, outputs, freshness check, and no manual-patch drift |
| tracked evidence/media | `qa`, `audit-shots`, `screenshots`, large PNGs, research docs | must have retention, LFS/artifact, source/provenance, date, commit, and release-bundle policy |
| ignored local evidence | `reports/`, `.shots/`, `.w850-shots`, `.ui-debug/`, `tmp-screenshots/` | must be promoted to release evidence or remain local ignored output with owner decision |
| ignored local data | `data/*.json`, `data/*.sqlite`, observation backups/tmp files | must not enter release source; backup/retention/PII policy belongs to node `18` and node `20` |
| ignored build products | `sdk/rust/target`, package `dist`, tmp launch bundles, npm cache, binary outputs | must be reproducible from source or attached as release artifacts with hashes, never confused with source |
| malformed paths | `C...Users...publicjszip.min.js` tracked/untracked variants | must be repaired or removed under owner-controlled cleanup before final release |
| parallel-agent edits | public CSS/JS/HTML, `src/intent.js`, `vercel.json` | must be integrated by owner lane, then file-ledger and screenshot/release proof rerun |

##### Node 14 V3 Atomic Review Contract

```json
{
  "contract": "kolm.atomic_codebase_review_contract.v3",
  "path": "repo_relative_path",
  "source_state": {
    "git_state": "tracked|untracked|ignored|deleted|missing|malformed",
    "workspace_state": "source|generated|evidence|cache|data|build_output|scratch|vendor|archive",
    "release_state": "include|exclude|test_only|docs_only|archive_only|artifact_bundle|must_delete_or_ignore",
    "path_validity": "valid|malformed|windows_path_artifact|case_conflict|missing_tracked_file"
  },
  "ownership": {
    "master_spec_node": "0-51",
    "primary_owner": "required",
    "reviewers": ["code", "security", "frontend", "backend", "docs", "release", "legal", "ml"],
    "parallel_agent_lane": "frontend|backend|research|release|none"
  },
  "provenance": {
    "manual_or_generated": "manual|generated|external|vendored|local_output",
    "generator_command": "required_if_generated",
    "source_inputs": [],
    "freshness_check": "required_if_generated",
    "artifact_hash": "required_if_release_artifact"
  },
  "quality_gates": {
    "lint": "pass|not_applicable|exception",
    "type_or_schema": "pass|not_applicable|exception",
    "tests": [],
    "smoke": [],
    "screenshot": [],
    "security_scan": "pass|not_applicable|exception",
    "claim_scope": "pass|not_applicable|exception"
  },
  "size_and_complexity": {
    "bytes": 0,
    "lines": 0,
    "large_file_policy": "ok|compress|lfs|artifact_store|split|archive",
    "monolith_policy": "ok|split_required|generated_exception|deadline_exception"
  },
  "cleanup_decision": {
    "decision": "keep|split|regenerate|archive|ignore|delete|promote_to_release_evidence",
    "owner_required_before_delete": true,
    "deadline": "required_for_exception"
  }
}
```

##### Node 14 V3 Completion Lanes

| lane | work | done when |
|---|---|---|
| tracked source ledger | build the file ledger for all 3,652 tracked paths | every path has owner, class, node, release policy, proof command, path validity, and cleanup state |
| workspace quarantine | classify the 122,453-file workspace into source, ignored evidence, cache, data, build output, scratch, and malformed artifact | release prep can prove exactly what is source and exactly what is local output |
| malformed path repair | repair or remove the corrupted `C...Users...publicjszip.min.js` tracked path and root `"C` untracked artifacts | no malformed path appears in `git ls-files`, `git status`, source archive, or release bundle |
| ownership enforcement | create CODEOWNERS or equivalent verifier | P0 roots and ownership files require owner review and cannot be bypassed silently |
| ruleset policy | define protected branch/push ruleset requirements | release branches block force push, malformed paths, forbidden artifact roots, oversized files, and missing status checks |
| generated provenance | build generated-file source map | every generated public/docs/API/account/SDK/product graph output has generator, inputs, outputs, and stale check |
| artifact retention | define storage for reports, screenshots, QA images, tmp launch bundles, cache, data, Rust targets, and release evidence | large local outputs are ignored, archived, LFS/artifact-backed, or intentionally committed with manifest |
| monolith decomposition | split or budget `cli/kolm.js`, `src/router.js`, large builders, and giant research docs | each monolith has owner, extraction order, contract tests, and shrinking line/byte budget |
| JS quality contracts | extend lint/type/schema boundaries | public JS, CLI, server, scripts, workers, and tests are linted or exceptioned; route/CLI/account/artifact schemas are checked |
| marker and secret classification | scope TODO/mock/stub and key-shaped matches by file class | production paths have no hidden blockers; fixtures/docs/examples are allowlisted with reason |
| final clean diff | separate source, generated, evidence, and cleanup changes | release reviewers can inspect meaningful source changes without being buried under generated or local-output churn |

| malformed `C...Users...` paths | tracked and untracked corruption signals | repair before clean release; no release archive may contain malformed path entries |

##### Node 14 V3 Redline

Node `14` cannot be marked complete until all of the following are true:

1. every tracked path is in `docs/file-ledger.json` with owner, file class, master node, release policy, generator/proof command, path validity, and cleanup decision;
2. the malformed `C...Users...publicjszip.min.js` tracked path and root `"C` untracked artifacts are repaired or owner-dispositioned;
3. CODEOWNERS or an equivalent verifier exists and protects P0 roots plus its own ownership files;
4. branch/push ruleset policy blocks malformed paths, forbidden artifact roots, oversized unapproved files, force pushes, and missing status checks on release paths;
5. generated outputs have source maps and cannot drift through manual patching;
6. ignored artifacts are classified so `reports`, `tmp`, cache, data, screenshots, Rust targets, and launch bundles cannot pollute release source;
7. `cli/kolm.js` and `src/router.js` have decomposition plans, shrinking budgets, and contract tests before further feature waves increase their size;
8. JS lint/type/schema boundaries cover production server, CLI, public JS, scripts, workers, account state, route envelopes, product graph, and artifact metadata;
9. debt-like markers and key-shaped strings are classified by file class so real blockers are not hidden by generated/tmp/archive copies;
10. large media, QA screenshots, research docs, benchmark data, and release evidence have compression, LFS/artifact, archive, or manifest-backed retention policy;
11. final release diff is human-reviewable and separates source, generated outputs, screenshots, reports, package artifacts, config, and cleanup decisions;
12. codebase finality is proven by current files and commands, not chat memory or the absence of obvious errors.

Until this redline passes, Kolm can have excellent features, but the codebase is not atomically reviewed or build-finished.

### Measurement, Analytics, And Experimentation Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `15`: the measurement system. Kolm cannot be optimized to the best version of itself unless every product surface has a defined outcome metric, guardrail metric, diagnostic metric, event contract, and decision rule. The current codebase has many strong primitives, but they are still scattered across K-Score, usage analytics, A/B tests, capture analytics, SLA rollups, benchmark evidence, ROI pages, account telemetry, and research portfolios.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change telemetry, analytics, account pages, or public marketing pages while the other agents are editing.

#### Current Measurement Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| A/B traffic router | `src/ab-router.js`, `src/ab-routes.js`, `tests/wave777-ab-router.test.js`, `tests/wave822-ab-testing.test.js` | deterministic split, tenant fencing, outcomes, promotion/rollback concepts, API and CLI coverage | needs one experiment registry, metric dictionary, SRM checks, guardrail policy, and product-surface decision rules |
| A/B metrics and promotion | `src/ab-metrics.js`, `src/ab-promote.js` | aggregates K-Score, latency, fallback rate, thumbs feedback, bootstrap significance, promote/rollback decisions | needs traceable OEC/guardrail/local-feature/data-quality metric classes and archived decision evidence |
| capture analytics | `src/capture-analytics.js`, `public/account/captures/analytics.html`, `tests/wave811-capture-analytics.test.js` | namespace clustering, per-cluster K-Score, IDR staleness, CSV export, gap signals | dashboard copy still exposes wave labels and needs product-language UX, privacy labels, and outcome-to-action mapping |
| usage analytics | `src/usage-analytics.js`, `src/usage.js`, `tests/wave265-usage-analytics.test.js` | captures, invocations, latency percentiles, errors, daily buckets, drift axes | needs product metric naming, account UX parity, retention/expansion mapping, and pricing/account linkage |
| agent telemetry | `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `public/account/agent-telemetry.html`, `tests/wave383-agent-telemetry.test.js`, `tests/wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope.test.js` | agent/session grouping and acceptance heuristic with tenant scope | needs confidence disclosure in UI, OpenTelemetry GenAI mapping, and explicit privacy/event retention controls |
| SLA rollup | `src/sla-rollup.js`, `public/account/sla.html`, `tests/wave788-sla.test.js` | p50/p95/p99 and uptime windows by surface with honest empty-window behavior | needs SLO thresholds, burn-rate alerts, incident links, and production dashboard proof |
| K-Score calibration | `src/kscore-calibration.js`, `public/k-score-calibration.html`, `tests/wave587-kscore-calibration-contract.test.js`, `tests/wave810-kscore-calibration.test.js` | calibration packs, minimum sample thresholds, category scope, public route honesty | needs quarterly public calibration release process, human-preference correlation evidence, and stable methodology docs |
| benchmark evidence | `src/benchmark-evidence.js`, `docs/benchmark-evidence.md`, `tests/wave589-benchmark-evidence-contract.test.js` | provider/runtime lane template, raw prompt/output restrictions, claim gate | needs complete real provider matrix under `reports/benchmarks/` before broad competitive claims |
| redaction benchmark | `src/redaction-benchmark.js`, `public/benchmarks/redaction-public-benchmark.json`, tests `wave581`, `wave586` | synthetic PII/PHI precision/recall/F1 benchmark exists | needs live benchmark cadence, adversarial sets, per-class drift, and false-positive/false-negative review loops |
| ROI calculator | `public/roi.html`, `public/og/roi.svg` | public cost model exists | needs formula source, pricing freshness, shareable result, confidence interval, model-mix assumptions, and account billing reconciliation |
| product invention metrics | `docs/product-invention-portfolio.json` | 9 metric classes with weights and synthetic lift: quality, latency, cost, portability, proof, security, enterprise, DX, conversion | needs bridge from synthetic lift to real experiment/benchmark/product telemetry before prioritization claims |
| product graph metrics | `public/product-graph.json` | product graph references latency, usage, cost, quality, benchmark, telemetry, readiness, and account links | needs one canonical metric dictionary so pages, APIs, CLI, TUI, and docs do not invent names independently |

Current tracked measurement-related files include `src/ab-metrics.js`, `src/ab-promote.js`, `src/ab-router.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/benchmark-evidence.js`, `src/capture-analytics.js`, `src/kscore-calibration.js`, `src/sla-rollup.js`, `src/trace-translator.js`, `src/usage-analytics.js`, `public/account/ab-tests.html`, `public/account/captures/analytics.html`, `public/account/sla.html`, `public/roi.html`, `public/kscore-leaderboard.json`, `docs/benchmark-evidence.md`, and more than twenty measurement-focused tests.

#### Measurement Quality Risks Observed

1. The implementation exists in many places, but there is no single metric dictionary mapping each metric to formula, source table, owner, privacy class, UI label, alert threshold, and decision use.
2. Account analytics pages still expose internal wave identifiers in user-facing metadata and copy, which is useful for development but not enterprise-grade product language.
3. Some generated pages still have mojibake in metadata separators, which affects measurement page polish and SEO trust.
4. A/B code has promotion and rollback primitives, but trustworthy experimentation also needs sample-ratio mismatch checks, data-quality metrics, guardrails, segmentation policy, and peeking/multiple-testing rules.
5. ROI and conversion metrics are underweighted in the invention portfolio (`conversion` weight is 0.02), even though the website and onboarding experience are currently a major business constraint.
6. Product metrics mix local synthetic lift, benchmark fixture performance, account usage telemetry, public claims, and business conversion. These must be separated so the company does not confuse internal prioritization with public proof.
7. Privacy promises require opt-in/out and minimization labels for telemetry, especially where model calls, prompts, captures, or agent sessions could contain customer data.

#### Official Measurement And Experimentation Standards

| standard | source | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | OpenTelemetry defines GenAI signals for events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, and agent spans, and notes that convention versions and emitted names should remain stable unless explicitly opted in: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | every AI gateway, capture, distill, compile, runtime, and agent metric should map to a stable OTel/GenAI name or documented Kolm extension with version |
| W3C Trace Context | W3C recommends Trace Context for distributed trace propagation on the web: https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/ | every API request, provider call, artifact build, cloud job, webhook, and account action should carry trace context or record why it cannot |
| Microsoft ExP trustworthy experimentation | Microsoft recommends holistic metrics, data-quality checks, OECs, local feature metrics, guardrails, early/frequent monitoring, SRM alerts, auto-shutdown, and stable segmentation: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/articles/patterns-of-trustworthy-experimentation-during-experiment-stage/ | Kolm A/B tests must include data-quality metrics, OECs, guardrails, SRM checks, segment balance, and automatic rollback before promotion |
| W3C Privacy Principles | W3C states data minimization applies to all personal data, even if it is not known to be identifying, sensitive, or harmful: https://www.w3.org/TR/privacy-principles/ | telemetry must be purpose-limited, minimized, scoped, retained, and visible to users, with raw prompt/output collection disabled unless explicitly justified |
| Google HEART | Google's HEART framework maps product goals to user-centered metrics for happiness, engagement, adoption, retention, and task success: https://research.google.com/pubs/pub36299.html | public website, docs, account, demo, SDK, CLI, and onboarding surfaces need goals-signals-metrics records, not only traffic and conversion counts |
| DORA metrics | DORA delivery metrics track throughput and instability for software delivery performance: https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | release and operational metrics should connect product quality to deployment frequency, lead time, recovery time, change failure rate, and rework |

#### Canonical Metric Taxonomy

Kolm should use one metric taxonomy across public website, account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, benchmarks, and internal prioritization:

| class | product question | example metrics | guardrails |
|---|---|---|---|
| quality | did the artifact or route produce correct, useful, auditable output? | K-Score, pass rate, judge calibration, redaction F1, tool success, acceptance confidence | holdout independence, calibration status, false positive/negative review |
| latency | did the user or runtime wait too long? | p50/p95/p99, queue time, first-token time, compile time, distill time, account page load | error rate, timeout rate, cold-start rate |
| cost | did Kolm reduce avoidable spend? | avoided provider calls, monthly savings, teacher cost, storage cost, compute cost, cost per successful artifact | quality floor, fallback rate, hidden cloud costs |
| portability | can output run where promised? | runtime target pass, device fit, memory fit, package/install success, offline success | feature loss, unsupported operator count |
| proof | can a buyer/auditor verify the claim? | signed receipt coverage, evidence packet completeness, benchmark lane completeness, provenance availability | claim-scope gate, missing raw report hash |
| security/privacy | did we protect data and isolate tenants? | redaction coverage, tenant-fence violations, secret detections, auth failures, policy denials | privacy budget, raw prompt retention, cross-tenant join failures |
| enterprise | can a team govern it? | SSO/SCIM readiness, audit export success, policy approval latency, DPA/BAA status, admin task success | unsupported compliance claim, missing audit row |
| DX | can developers complete work quickly? | quickstart completion, docs search success, CLI command success, SDK smoke success, time to first artifact | docs broken refs, install failure, confusing errors |
| conversion | does the site and product turn intent into usage? | visitor to demo, demo to signup, signup to first capture, first capture to artifact, artifact to paid, enterprise inquiry | misleading claim, poor-fit signup, support burden |
| reliability | does it stay healthy in production? | uptime, SLO compliance, error budget burn, queue depth, storage readiness, provider fallback | rollback trigger, incident severity, data loss |

#### Product Surface Metric Map

| surface | north-star outcome | primary metrics | guardrails |
|---|---|---|---|
| homepage/public website | qualified users understand the three product promises and start a real workflow | 5-second comprehension, demo start, signup, enterprise inquiry, ROI share, docs clickthrough | misleading claim rate, bounce, accessibility failures, LCP/INP/CLS |
| docs and quickstarts | developers reach a working artifact fast | time to first capture, time to first `.kolm`, code sample pass rate, search success, SDK install success | broken refs, stale API count, unsupported package claims |
| AI gateway/capture | model traffic becomes owned evidence without breaking apps | captured-call success, provider coverage, durable capture rate, redaction coverage, latency overhead | data loss, prompt retention policy violation, tenant leak |
| distill/train | repeated traffic becomes smaller, cheaper, high-quality artifacts | distill success, K-Score lift, teacher cost, student latency, holdout pass, dataset coverage | holdout leakage, quality regression, poison signal |
| compile/verify/artifacts | artifacts are portable, signed, reproducible, and auditable | compile success, signature valid, provenance complete, runtime target pass, verify success | unsigned artifacts, unsupported runtime, stale evidence |
| runtime/devices | artifacts run anywhere promised | device fit, p95 latency, memory peak, fallback rate, offline success, energy estimate | quality below floor, battery/thermal regression, unsupported ops |
| account product surface | operators see state and act without support | task success, time to action, live fetch coverage, error recovery, next-action completion | hidden readiness caveat, stale data, inaccessible control |
| enterprise/governance | buyers can prove control, compliance, and ROI | evidence packet completeness, audit export success, policy approvals, usage attribution, SSO/SCIM readiness | certification overclaim, missing audit row, unclear sales path |
| marketplace/agents | verified artifacts and tools become discoverable and trusted | search success, install success, verified publisher coverage, agent-call success, utility per artifact | stale K-Score, malicious artifact, dependency risk |

#### Experiment Decision Contract

Every experiment must include:

1. hypothesis and product surface;
2. experiment owner and master spec node;
3. unit of randomization and sticky assignment key;
4. OEC metric;
5. data-quality metrics, including sample-ratio mismatch;
6. guardrail metrics for latency, errors, fallback, quality, privacy, cost, and accessibility where relevant;
7. local diagnostic metrics for the feature being changed;
8. minimum sample size and planned duration;
9. peeking and multiple-testing policy;
10. stable segments, with no treatment-dependent dynamic segment unless weighted and documented;
11. rollback and auto-shutdown thresholds;
12. evidence archive: configuration, assignment counts, metric deltas, p-values or credible intervals, screenshots if UI, and decision rationale.

For Kolm-specific AI experiments, add:

1. K-Score axis deltas, not only composite;
2. teacher/student/model/provider versions;
3. prompt/output retention and hashing policy;
4. holdout independence proof;
5. fallback-to-frontier rate;
6. cost-per-successful-task;
7. security/privacy class;
8. reproducible artifact or report hash.

#### Master Spec Tree Branch 15

| node | branch | owner question | required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `15.0` | metric dictionary | does every metric have formula, source, owner, privacy class, and UI label? | generated metric dictionary, schema validation, product graph links |
| `15.1` | product funnels | can every public/account/docs/SDK journey be measured from intent to value? | funnel map, event names, conversion/dropoff definitions, screenshot/task proof |
| `15.2` | AI quality metrics | are K-Score, calibration, benchmarks, judge quality, redaction, and runtime metrics separated and honest? | calibration pack, benchmark matrix, per-axis reports, claim-scope links |
| `15.3` | experimentation | do A/B tests produce trustworthy decisions? | OEC, guardrails, SRM, segment balance, sample size, p-value/interval, decision archive |
| `15.4` | telemetry contracts | are events/traces/logs stable, minimized, and interoperable? | OTel/GenAI mapping, W3C trace context, event schema versions, retention rules |
| `15.5` | privacy and consent | does measurement respect data minimization and user scope? | telemetry opt-in/out, raw prompt/output policy, retention, tenant fence tests |
| `15.6` | account analytics UX | do operators see metrics that lead to actions? | account screenshots, loading/empty/error states, next actions, no internal wave jargon |
| `15.7` | business metrics | do product metrics connect to revenue, expansion, support, and enterprise sales? | ROI formula, plan conversion, enterprise inquiry, support burden, expansion reports |
| `15.8` | release metrics | do engineering quality metrics feed the same system? | DORA metrics, release evidence, incident and rollback metrics |
| `15.9` | metric evidence archive | can future reviewers reproduce decisions? | immutable metric/experiment/benchmark evidence bundles with hashes |

- W-MET-007: extend A/B decision evidence to include OEC, guardrails, local feature metrics, data-quality metrics, segment balance, and decision archive.

- W-MET-009: map Kolm telemetry to OpenTelemetry GenAI names or explicit `kolm.*` extensions with versioned stability policy.

- W-MET-011: add telemetry minimization policy: no raw prompts, outputs, secrets, or customer documents in analytics unless explicitly opted in and encrypted with retention scope.
- W-MET-012: redesign account analytics copy to remove internal wave IDs and explain what action each metric supports.

- W-MET-019: build a metric glossary for public docs so customers understand K-Score, fallback rate, avoided calls, confidence, device fit, and evidence completeness.
- W-MET-020: create a metric redline dashboard that shows unknown metrics, missing formulas, stale telemetry names, unowned events, unscoped raw data, and experiments lacking guardrails.

#### Measurement Redline

Kolm cannot be called fully optimized until this branch proves:

1. every product feature has a measurable outcome, guardrail, and diagnostic metric;
2. every metric has one formula and one owner;
3. public proof metrics are separated from synthetic internal prioritization scores;
4. A/B tests have SRM detection, guardrails, planned duration, and archived decision evidence;
5. telemetry uses stable names and trace propagation across the AI stack;
6. account dashboards show actionable metrics without internal implementation jargon;
7. conversion and ROI metrics are wired from public website through account activation and billing;
8. privacy minimization prevents raw prompt/output leakage into analytics by default;
9. every claimed improvement can be traced to benchmark, experiment, telemetry, or release evidence.

#### Node 15 V2 Measurement, Experimentation, And Decision Evidence Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `15`. It does not edit telemetry code, account pages, public pages, or measurement scripts while parallel agents are active. It turns the current measurement system into a state-of-the-art decision contract: every metric must have one formula, owner, source, privacy class, UI label, product surface, and decision rule.

The current codebase already has meaningful measurement primitives. The gap is not absence of analytics code. The gap is governance: A/B, usage, capture, agent telemetry, SLA, K-Score, benchmark evidence, ROI, release metrics, and conversion data must all speak the same metric language. Otherwise Kolm cannot know whether a change improves quality, latency, cost, portability, proof, security, enterprise readiness, developer experience, conversion, or reliability.

##### Node 15 V2 Current Evidence

| area | current evidence | completion interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| measurement-matched `src` files | 243 of 392 scanned JS/JSON-like files | measurement language is pervasive; a metric dictionary is required to prevent naming drift |
| measurement-matched public files | 1,021 of 1,132 scanned public files | public/account/docs pages contain measurement language and must be claim-scoped, user-readable, and telemetry-safe |
| measurement-matched tests | 282 of 567 scanned tests | test coverage is broad, but completion requires a measurement taxonomy, not only individual wave tests |
| measurement-matched scripts | 136 of 343 scanned scripts | build/release/report scripts produce measurement artifacts and need output contracts |
| measurement-matched docs | 138 of 158 scanned docs | research/docs already discuss metrics heavily and need consolidation into one product metric model |
| `docs/metric-dictionary.json` | absent | no machine-readable canonical metric dictionary exists yet |
| `docs/event-taxonomy.json` | absent | event names, privacy classes, retention, and source modules are not centrally governed |
| `docs/experiment-registry.json` | absent | A/B tests and product experiments cannot be audited from one registry |
| `docs/analytics-schema.json` | absent | account/public/API analytics are not schema-governed |
| `docs/measurement-plan.md` | absent | there is no single measurement doctrine tying product metrics to release and business decisions |
| `src/ab-router.js` | 946 lines | deterministic split and event-store integration exist, but UI/user copy still exposes internal wave framing |
| `src/ab-metrics.js` and `src/ab-promote.js` | 620 combined lines | statistical gates exist; SRM, guardrails, peeking, segmentation, and decision archive need canonicalization |
| `src/capture-analytics.js` | 692 lines | capture insight exists; account UX must translate clusters into next actions and data/privacy scope |
| `src/usage-analytics.js` | 143 lines | usage aggregation exists but needs canonical event taxonomy and commercial/account linkage |
| `src/agent-telemetry.js` | 572 lines | developer-agent telemetry exists; privacy, retention, and OpenTelemetry GenAI mapping need explicit policy |
| `src/sla-rollup.js` | 338 lines | reliability rollups exist; SLO thresholds, burn alerts, and incident links need completion |
| `src/kscore-calibration.js` | 401 lines | calibration pack loader exists; public methodology, quarterly cadence, and human-preference correlation evidence remain gating |
| `src/benchmark-evidence.js` | 404 lines | benchmark evidence model exists; broad competitive claims need full public raw report matrix |
| `src/redaction-benchmark.js` | 157 lines | public synthetic redaction benchmark path exists; adversarial and live cadence need expansion |
| `src/trace-translator.js` | 253 lines | trace translation exists; stable trace/event taxonomy must bind API, jobs, provider calls, and account actions |

| `public/account/captures/analytics.html` | 305 lines | capture analytics UI exists and should become action-oriented rather than report-only |

| measurement test examples | `wave265`, `wave383`, `wave424`, `wave455`, `wave457`, `wave506`, `wave581`, `wave587`, `wave588`, `wave589`, `wave760`, `wave777`, `wave788`, `wave810`, `wave811`, `wave822` | strong coverage exists; final state needs tests linked to metric IDs and decision contracts |

##### Node 15 V2 Metric Dictionary Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-metric-dictionary-v2",
  "metric_id": "stable_snake_case",
  "class": "quality|latency|cost|portability|proof|security_privacy|enterprise|dx|conversion|reliability|release",
  "surface": ["public", "docs", "account", "api", "cli", "tui", "sdk", "runtime", "enterprise"],
  "owner_node": "0-51",
  "owner": "required",
  "formula": "required",
  "unit": "required",
  "source": {
    "table_or_file": "required",
    "api_route": "optional",
    "event_name": "optional",
    "trace_span": "optional",
    "job_id": "optional"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "class": "none|aggregate|tenant_scoped|prompt_sensitive|customer_sensitive",
    "raw_prompt_allowed": false,
    "retention_days": "required",
    "redaction": "required_if_sensitive"
  },
  "presentation": {
    "public_label": "required",
    "account_label": "required_if_account",
    "tooltip": "required",
    "empty_state": "required",
    "failure_state": "required"
  },
  "decision_use": {
    "oec": false,
    "guardrail": false,
    "diagnostic": true,
    "release_blocker": false,
    "public_claim_allowed": "requires_evidence"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "tests": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "benchmark_reports": [],
    "calibration_reports": [],
    "freshness": "required"
  }
}
```

##### Node 15 V2 Experiment Registry Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-experiment-registry-v2",
  "experiment_id": "stable_id",
  "surface": "product_surface",
  "owner_node": "0-51",
  "hypothesis": "required",
  "randomization_unit": "tenant|user|namespace|request|artifact|session",
  "sticky_assignment_key": "required",
  "arms": [
    {
      "id": "control",
      "artifact_or_feature": "required",
      "traffic_percent": 50
    }
  ],
  "primary_metric": "metric_id",
  "guardrail_metrics": ["metric_id"],
  "data_quality_checks": ["sample_ratio_mismatch", "event_loss", "bot_filter", "clock_skew"],
  "minimum_sample": "required",
  "planned_duration": "required",
  "peeking_policy": "required",
  "multiple_testing_policy": "required_if_many_metrics",
  "auto_shutdown": {
    "latency": "threshold",
    "error_rate": "threshold",
    "quality_drop": "threshold",
    "cost_spike": "threshold",
    "privacy_violation": "immediate"
  },
  "decision": {
    "status": "not_started|running|promote|rollback|inconclusive|stopped",
    "date": "required_when_final",
    "evidence_archive": "required_when_final"
  }
}
```

##### Node 15 V2 Metric Surface Rules

| surface | required measurement behavior |
|---|---|
| Homepage | measure five-second comprehension for all three product promises, hero/demo engagement, signup, enterprise inquiry, ROI use, docs continuation, and misleading-claim reports |
| Docs | measure quickstart completion, command success, SDK install success, search success, broken example rate, stale route/API count, and time to first `.kolm` |
| Account overview | show live metric status with freshness, missing data, next action, CLI/API equivalent, and readiness closeout where proof is incomplete |
| Capture/gateway | measure captured calls, provider coverage, latency overhead, redaction coverage, durable capture rate, failed capture class, and privacy scope |
| Distill/train | measure teacher cost, student quality, holdout independence, K-Score axis deltas, fallback rate, distill time, GPU/runtime class, and poisoning/drift flags |
| Compile/artifact | measure compile success, verify success, signature/provenance completeness, dependency fit, runtime target pass, and artifact size |
| Runtime/device | measure p50/p95 latency, memory, energy/thermal where available, unsupported ops, fallback, offline success, and quality floor |
| Enterprise/governance | measure SSO/SCIM readiness, audit export success, policy approval latency, data residency, evidence packet completeness, and support/SLA outcomes |
| Pricing/ROI | measure calculator completion, assumptions changed, savings confidence, share/export, plan fit, contact-sales conversion, and formula freshness |
| Release operations | measure deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, failed deployment recovery time, rollback rate, and release evidence completeness |

##### Node 15 V2 Public/Account Copy Redline

Measurement UI must be understandable by a buyer, operator, or developer. Internal wave labels can remain in code/tests, but public/account UX should not lead with them.

| current pattern | completion rule |
|---|---|
| `W777 A/B testing dashboard` in account metadata | replace with product language such as "Experimentation dashboard" while keeping wave IDs in internal evidence |
| `W778 Welch t-test gate` in public-facing copy | expose "statistical guardrail" and link technical details in docs |
| ROI metadata mojibake separator | metadata must be ASCII-clean or intentionally Unicode-reviewed under node `12`/`21` |
| benchmark/K-Score claims without date/source | every public metric claim needs methodology, date, raw report hash, and scope |
| account analytics without next actions | every insight should have an action, owner, CLI/API equivalent, and degraded/empty state |
| synthetic invention lift presented like product proof | synthetic planning scores must be separated from real telemetry, benchmark, and customer evidence |

##### Node 15 V2 Relax Redline

Node `15` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every metric used in code, docs, product graph, account UI, public pages, benchmarks, ROI, release reports, and invention planning exists in `docs/metric-dictionary.json`;
2. every event has a taxonomy entry with schema, source, owner, privacy class, retention, redaction, and trace correlation;
3. every experiment has a registry entry with hypothesis, unit, assignment, primary metric, guardrails, data-quality checks, sample plan, and decision archive;
4. public/account analytics copy uses product language and moves wave IDs into technical evidence;
5. K-Score, benchmark, redaction, quality, and ROI claims include method, date, scope, freshness, and raw evidence pointers before being public claims;
6. telemetry respects data minimization and prompt/output privacy by default;
7. account analytics views lead to concrete actions with owner, CLI/API equivalent, freshness, and success metric;
8. DORA and release-quality metrics connect node `13` release evidence to product reliability decisions;
9. synthetic invention portfolio scores are used for internal prioritization only until validated by real telemetry, experiments, benchmarks, or customer evidence;
10. every metric decision can be reconstructed from an evidence archive rather than chat history.

If any item fails, Kolm can still measure many things, but it cannot claim state-of-the-art optimization discipline.

#### Node 15 V3 Instrumentation Completeness, Decision Evidence, And Metric Truth Contract

This V3 extension keeps node `15` as the measurement owner and leaves node `52` unused. V2 defined the dictionary and experiment governance shape. V3 adds the stricter operating contract: every product feature must produce reliable, privacy-scoped, customer-meaningful evidence that can drive a decision.

The goal is not "more analytics." The goal is a decision machine. Kolm should know when a feature improves cost, quality, latency, portability, proof, security, enterprise readiness, developer experience, conversion, or reliability, and should be able to show the exact evidence without leaking prompts, outputs, secrets, tenant data, or unsupported claims.

| `public` files with metric/analytics/event language | 827 | nearly all public files contain metric-like language, so copy and claim scope must be governed |
| `public` files with event/trace/emit language | 780 | public/account pages likely present or fetch telemetry concepts but lack one event taxonomy |

| OpenTelemetry tests | `tests/wave733-otel.test.js` says no hard `@opentelemetry/*` dependency and optional tracer behavior | honest lightweight instrumentation exists, but final observability needs stable convention versioning and exporter readiness |
| A/B tests | `tests/wave777-ab-router.test.js`, `tests/wave822-ab-testing.test.js` | router and promotion concepts exist; V3 requires SRM, guardrails, peeking policy, event loss, and decision archive as release blockers |

##### Node 15 V3 Primary-Source Baselines

| baseline | current source | Kolm measurement rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | The current OpenTelemetry GenAI page is status `Development` and defines GenAI events, exceptions, metrics, model spans, and agent spans while warning instrumentations not to change emitted convention versions by default: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/ | Kolm must version its GenAI convention set, expose opt-in for experimental/latest naming, and map every AI operation to stable OTel or `kolm.*` extensions |
| W3C Trace Context | W3C Trace Context defines standard HTTP headers and value formats for propagating distributed trace context across services: https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/ | every API request, capture, provider call, compile job, distill job, artifact verification, webhook, account action, and support bundle must carry or link trace context |
| OpenFeature flag evaluation | OpenFeature specifies typed flag evaluation, detailed evaluation metadata, provider readiness, status, and non-throwing behavior for hot paths: https://openfeature.dev/specification/sections/flag-evaluation/ | experiments, feature gates, rollout flags, and kill switches should return typed defaults, status, variant, reason, and error metadata without crashing product paths |
| CloudEvents | CloudEvents provides a common specification for describing event data and SDKs across languages: https://cloudevents.io/ | Kolm's event taxonomy should use CloudEvents-compatible `id`, `source`, `type`, `subject`, `time`, `datacontenttype`, and extension fields where events cross systems |
| Web Vitals | web.dev states Core Web Vitals are field-measurable metrics for loading, interactivity, and visual stability, with LCP <= 2.5s, INP <= 200ms, CLS <= 0.1 at p75 across mobile and desktop, and recommends real-user monitoring because lab data is not a substitute for field data: https://web.dev/articles/vitals | node `12` screenshots are not enough; node `15` needs RUM events and p75 reporting for public/docs/account critical routes |
| W3C Privacy Principles | W3C Privacy Principles states data minimization applies to all personal data and actors should restrict transferred data to what is necessary for user goals or wishes: https://www.w3.org/TR/privacy-principles/ | telemetry must default to aggregate/minimized fields, no raw prompts/outputs/secrets, explicit retention, and visible tenant/admin controls |
| Microsoft trustworthy experimentation | Microsoft ExP recommends guardrail metrics and trustworthy experimentation practices during experiment stage: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/articles/patterns-of-trustworthy-experimentation-during-experiment-stage/ | Kolm experiments must include data-quality checks, SRM, guardrails, OEC, local feature metrics, monitoring, and automatic rollback thresholds |
| DORA metrics | DORA's 2026 guidance identifies throughput metrics and instability metrics including change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate: https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | release metrics must be part of the same metric spine and must avoid Goodhart-style single-metric goals |

##### Node 15 V3 Measurement Operating Model

| layer | purpose | required artifact |
|---|---|---|
| metric dictionary | stable metric IDs, formulas, units, owners, privacy class, labels, thresholds, and decision use | `docs/metric-dictionary.json` |
| event taxonomy | stable event names, schema, CloudEvents fields, producer, consumer, privacy class, retention, and trace correlation | `docs/event-taxonomy.json` |
| experiment registry | all experiments, feature flags, randomization, variants, guardrails, SRM, sample plan, and decisions | `docs/experiment-registry.json` |
| analytics schema | API/account/public payload schemas for events, metrics, rollups, and dashboards | `docs/analytics-schema.json` |
| RUM contract | field Core Web Vitals and route task metrics for homepage, product, pricing, docs, API, account, and demo | `public/rum.js` plus backend ingest or documented equivalent |
| evidence archive | immutable decisions, raw aggregate outputs, hashes, reports, screenshots, experiment configs, and signed summaries | `reports/metrics/` or release evidence bundle |
| UI glossary | public/account labels that explain K-Score, fallback, savings, route, distill, device fit, evidence completeness, and confidence | docs and account glossary generated from dictionary |

##### Node 15 V3 Event Envelope Contract

```json
{
  "contract": "kolm.measurement_event_envelope.v3",
  "id": "uuid_or_ulid",
  "source": "kolm.surface.module",
  "type": "kolm.metric_class.event_name.v1",
  "subject": "tenant_or_artifact_or_route_or_job_hash",
  "time": "iso_8601",
  "datacontenttype": "application/json",
  "traceparent": "w3c_traceparent_optional_but_required_when_available",
  "tenant_scope": "tenant_id_or_hash",
  "privacy_class": "aggregate|tenant_scoped|prompt_sensitive|customer_sensitive|public",
  "metric_ids": ["stable_metric_id"],
  "surface": "public|docs|account|api|cli|tui|sdk|gateway|distill|compile|runtime|enterprise|release",
  "payload_schema": "schema_id",
  "payload": {},
  "redaction": {
    "raw_prompt_included": false,
    "raw_output_included": false,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "customer_document_included": false
  },
  "retention_days": 30,
  "evidence_hash": "sha256_optional"
}
```

##### Node 15 V3 Feature Scorecard Contract

Every shipped product feature needs a scorecard row before it can be called optimized:

| field | required value |
|---|---|
| feature ID | stable product feature or wave ID linked to master spec node |
| product surface | route/capture, compile/distill, deploy-anywhere, governance, docs, account, public, marketplace, or release |
| activation metric | first successful customer action that proves feature value |
| quality metric | correctness, K-Score axis, task success, or benchmark result |
| latency metric | p50/p95/p99 or user-perceived timing |
| cost metric | provider cost, compute cost, avoided calls, unit economics, or storage cost |
| reliability metric | error rate, timeout, fallback, retry, SLO, queue, or incident link |
| privacy metric | redaction, retention, raw data inclusion, tenant fence, or consent status |
| conversion metric | next action, upgrade, inquiry, usage expansion, or retained workflow |
| guardrail | metric that must not regress |
| owner | team or node responsible for action |
| evidence | test, trace, benchmark, screenshot, experiment, account state, or production report |

##### Node 15 V3 Redline

Node `15` cannot be marked complete until all of the following are true:

1. the metric dictionary, event taxonomy, analytics schema, experiment registry, and measurement plan exist and are verified;
2. every product feature has an activation metric, guardrail metric, diagnostic metric, owner, and decision rule;
3. all critical public/docs/account routes have RUM or explicitly scoped field-measurement evidence, not just screenshot/lab proof;
4. A/B and rollout systems include SRM, event loss, segment balance, peeking policy, guardrails, auto-shutdown, and decision archives;
5. feature flags and experiments expose typed default values, variant/reason/error metadata, provider status, and safe fallback behavior;
6. OpenTelemetry/GenAI mapping is versioned and trace context joins API, jobs, providers, artifacts, account actions, webhooks, and support bundles;
7. CloudEvents-compatible event envelopes are used for cross-system event delivery;
8. telemetry defaults to minimized aggregate data, no raw prompts/outputs/secrets/customer documents, and explicit retention/opt-out policy;
9. account analytics are actionable and customer-readable, with wave IDs reserved for internal evidence;
10. ROI, K-Score, benchmark, savings, reliability, and conversion claims link to dated evidence bundles;
11. release metrics include DORA throughput and instability metrics without turning one metric into an unbalanced goal;
12. every metric decision can be reconstructed from current files and archived evidence, not chat history.

Until this redline passes, Kolm may have many metrics, but it is not yet a 100x optimized product-learning system.

### Commercial, Pricing, Billing, And Customer Operations Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `16`: the commercial system. Kolm cannot be 100 percent complete if a buyer, account owner, finance reviewer, enterprise security reviewer, or support contact sees a different product than the one enforced by backend billing and entitlement code. Pricing is product truth, not a marketing afterthought.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change pricing pages, billing code, Stripe integration, onboarding, support, account UI, or enterprise pages while other agents are editing.

#### Current Commercial Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| public pricing | `public/pricing.html`, `public/pricing/*.html`, `tests/wave273-pricing-tiers.test.js` | visible Free, Pro, Team, Business, Enterprise pricing grid, ROI, startup/nonprofit/regional/purchase-order variants, structured data, and legacy hidden anchors | needs one canonical plan taxonomy; current public page and tests still preserve Business/Starter aliases in ways that can conflict with the intended Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise contract |
| enterprise sales | `public/enterprise.html`, `public/enterprise/inquiry.html`, `public/enterprise/console.html`, `public/enterprise/self-hosted.html` | enterprise copy, structured inquiry form, self-hosted/BYOC positioning, BAA/DPA/security links | needs one enterprise offer object with custom pricing, no fake `price: 0` offer semantics, and a CRM/proposal/evidence workflow tied to backend state |
| account billing | `public/account/billing.html`, `public/account/sla.html`, `public/account/overview.html` | billing, usage, SLA, and readiness surfaces exist post-auth | needs invoice history, entitlement state, plan source, usage caps, payment portal, cancellation, and support escalation to be one coherent operator flow |
| billing meters | `src/usage.js`, `src/usage-analytics.js`, `src/billing-breakdown.js`, `src/cost-estimator.js` | ten billing units, local-only no-metering promise, per-period counters, namespace cost breakdown, provider cost extraction | tier comments and tier IDs still use `indie`, `starter`, `team`, `business`, and `enterprise`; commercial completion requires canonical IDs plus explicit legacy aliases |
| Stripe checkout and webhooks | `src/stripe.js`, `src/billing-upgrade.js`, `scripts/stripe-provision.mjs`, `tests/stripe.test.js`, `tests/wave363-billing-upgrade.test.js` | signature verification, amount-to-plan mapping, checkout URL fallback chain, payment link provisioner, and tests exist | needs customer portal, invoice history, idempotent webhook event ledger, subscription lifecycle handling, entitlement summary handling, and production Stripe test/live evidence |
| plan and entitlement APIs | `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`, `/v1/billing/usage`, `/v1/billing/meters`, account links, product graph | buyer and account paths can ask backend what plans and meters exist | needs generated parity from a single commercial contract file rather than repeated HTML, comments, tests, Stripe maps, OpenAPI, docs, and account copy |
| onboarding and upgrades | `public/onboard/index.html`, `public/onboard/developer.html`, `public/onboard/cto.html`, `public/onboard/researcher.html`, `public/onboard/compliance.html`, `public/upgrade.html` | segmented onboarding and upgrade pages exist | needs activation definitions, plan-aware next actions, account-aware upgrade paths, downgrade/cancel handling, and support handoff |
| support and success | `public/for/support-automation.html`, `public/verticals/support.html`, trust/legal/security pages | support use cases and enterprise procurement content exist | needs named customer-success lifecycle, support SLA entitlements, incident/update communication, and post-sale implementation checklists |

#### Commercial Redlines Observed In The Current Tree

1. Pricing taxonomy drift is still the largest commercial risk. The public pricing page presents `Free`, `Pro`, `Team`, `Business`, and `Enterprise`; earlier product-contract work intended `Free`, `Pro`, `Team`, and `Enterprise`; `src/usage.js` speaks in `indie`, `team`, and `enterprise`; Stripe helpers preserve `starter`, `teams`, `business`, and legacy amount mappings.
2. `Business` can exist only as either a deliberate public tier or a backward-compatible alias. It cannot be both a visible `$1,499/mo` checkout tier and an alias that maps to Enterprise custom without a single contract explaining why.
3. Enterprise custom pricing must not be represented as a zero-dollar purchase offer. If JSON-LD uses `Offer` or `AggregateOffer`, the visible price, currency, availability, and sales-required semantics must match the page and account behavior.
4. Stripe provisioning still creates historical tiers including `starter`, `business`, and fixed-price `enterprise`. If those are legacy links, the provisioner needs a migration mode, not a default that can recreate retired products.
5. `src/stripe.js` maps legacy amounts into current plan IDs, which is good for backward compatibility, but the mapping needs to be generated from the commercial contract and audited against Stripe products/prices.
6. `src/billing-upgrade.js` correctly returns a working URL even when Stripe is not configured, but final commercial completion cannot rely on manual JSONL fallback as the normal customer lifecycle.
7. `src/usage.js` contains a strong no-metering privacy promise for local-only tenants. That promise must be visible in pricing, account billing, docs, signup, and upgrade flows, and tests must prove it cannot regress.
8. Billing meters are stronger than the public copy. Current units cover captures, storage, optimization, builds, distillation, hosted inference, seats, signing, registry artifacts, and enterprise sync; public pricing must state which units are included, unlimited, capped, overage-billed, or local-only.
9. Account billing cannot be considered done until a tenant can see plan, entitlements, usage, cap state, invoice history, payment method/portal link, receipts, export, cancellation policy, and support escalation in one path.
10. Several commercial pages still carry historical wave anchors, hidden compatibility copy, or mojibake separators. Those can be useful for tests during migration but cannot remain visible or semantically active in the final buyer experience.
11. Pricing claims such as percent savings, cheaper multipliers, first-invoice refund, support response times, SOC 2, BAA, and compliance hand-off require evidence IDs, dates, and scope labels.
12. Startup, student, nonprofit, regional, purchase-order, savings-based, and enterprise add-on pages need the same plan IDs, eligibility language, and approval workflow as the main pricing page.

#### External Standards Bound To Node 16

| standard source | relevant standard | Kolm completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Stripe usage-based billing docs: `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/usage-based/how-it-works` | usage billing has ingestion, product catalog setup, billing, and monitoring phases | `src/usage.js` counters, Stripe meters/prices, account billing, and threshold alerts must reconcile by customer, period, unit, and plan |
| Stripe customer portal docs: `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/customer-portal` | customers should manage billing info, payment methods, subscriptions, cancellation, and invoices in one portal | account billing needs a portal session route or clearly scoped hosted portal handoff, plus invoices and cancellation/reason capture |
| Stripe subscription webhook docs: `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/webhooks` | subscription changes, invoice changes, payment failures, cancellation, and entitlement updates are asynchronous | webhook handling must be idempotent, signature-verified, event-ledgered, replay-safe, and tied to entitlement provisioning/deprovisioning |
| Stripe entitlements docs: `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/entitlements` | active entitlements describe feature access for a customer | every plan feature must be an entitlement or local entitlement rule, with account/API/CLI visibility and tests |
| WorkOS Directory Sync docs: `https://workos.com/docs/directory-sync` | enterprise customers expect SCIM/ULM provisioning and deprovisioning through their directory source of truth | Enterprise and Team identity features must support lifecycle events, group mapping, deprovisioning, and admin-visible sync state |
| WorkOS Admin Portal docs: `https://workos.com/docs/admin-portal` | enterprise IT contacts need self-service setup for SSO and Directory Sync | enterprise onboarding should include an IT admin setup path for SSO/SCIM, with status, test connection, and support fallback |
| Google Product structured data docs: `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/product-snippet` | product structured data can surface price and availability, and must be validated before release | pricing JSON-LD must not invent prices or expose misleading zero-dollar enterprise offers; every generated offer must match visible copy |

#### Commercial Contract Source Of Truth

Final commercial state needs a generated source of truth, not scattered constants. The contract can be JSON, JS, or another structured format, but it must generate or verify every buyer-facing and billing-facing surface.

Minimum fields:

```json
{
  "plan_id": "team",
  "public_name": "Team",
  "legacy_aliases": ["teams"],
  "status": "active",
  "price": { "amount_usd_month": 499, "amount_usd_year": 4990, "custom": false },
  "sales_required": false,
  "stripe": {
    "product_env": "KOLM_STRIPE_PRODUCT_TEAM",
    "price_env": "KOLM_STRIPE_PRICE_TEAM",
    "legacy_price_envs": ["KOLM_STRIPE_PRICE_TEAMS"]
  },
  "entitlements": ["private_registry", "approvals", "audit_log", "team_seats_5"],
  "billing_units": {
    "captured_events": { "included": 1000000, "overage_policy": "blocked_or_contract" },
    "hosted_inference": { "included_tokens": 10000000, "overage_policy": "metered" }
  },
  "support": { "channel": "priority", "response_window": "business_hours_4h" },
  "procurement": { "purchase_order": false, "dpa": false, "baa": false },
  "cta": { "label": "Start Team", "href": "/signup?plan=team" },
  "structured_data": { "offer_type": "Offer", "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock" }
}
```

Rules:

- Active public plans appear in pricing, account upgrade, docs, API plans, OpenAPI, CLI billing tiers, tests, structured data, and Stripe provisioning.
- Legacy aliases exist only in backend compatibility, migration notes, and tests that prove old customers map safely.
- Retired plans cannot show as active CTAs.
- Enterprise custom plans use `sales_required: true`, no fake zero-dollar checkout, and no Stripe price unless a signed/self-serve enterprise SKU is deliberately active.
- Entitlements, quotas, and usage meters are generated from the same contract that powers public pricing.
- Every commercial claim has a scope: included, add-on, contract-scoped, beta, local-only, external certification, benchmarked, or not offered.

#### Node 16 Completion Specification

| node | name | done state | proof required |
|---|---|---|---|
| 16.1 | pricing source of truth | one generated plan contract controls all plan names, aliases, prices, CTAs, Stripe IDs, quotas, entitlements, docs, account copy, and structured data | diff proves public pricing, account billing, `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`, Stripe provisioner, OpenAPI, CLI, and tests read or validate the same contract |
| 16.2 | billing meters and quotas | every billable unit has owner, source event, period, retention, cap/overage policy, local-only behavior, account display, and export | usage meter tests, account screenshot, CLI/API output, tenant-fence tests, local-only no-metering tests |
| 16.3 | Stripe lifecycle | checkout, customer portal, webhooks, invoices, subscription changes, payment failures, entitlements, and cancellation are implemented and observable | Stripe test-mode smoke, webhook replay/idempotency tests, event ledger, account portal route, invoice list, entitlement update tests |
| 16.4 | enterprise sales and procurement | inquiry, architecture review, quote/proposal, BAA/DPA/security packet, purchase order, SSO/SCIM setup, BYOC scope, CSM/SLA, and handoff are one workflow | enterprise inquiry API/account state, generated proposal packet, CRM/export record, security/legal evidence links, SSO/SCIM setup status |
| 16.5 | onboarding and customer success | every segment has activation milestones and plan-aware next actions; upgrade/downgrade/cancel/support paths are clear | onboarding task tests, account empty/loading/error states, support SLA labels, cancellation reason capture, post-upgrade checklist |
| 16.6 | commercial SEO and claim honesty | titles, descriptions, schema, CTAs, refunds, support, savings, compliance, and pricing claims match the commercial contract | claim-scope audit, structured-data validation, screenshot audit, production search/social metadata capture, pricing evidence archive |

#### Commercial Tickets To Generate

- W-COM-001: create `docs/commercial-contract.json` or `src/commercial-contract.js` as the only plan/price/entitlement source of truth.
- W-COM-002: generate `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`, pricing-page plan cards, CLI billing tiers, account plan labels, and OpenAPI examples from the commercial contract.

- W-COM-005: replace Stripe provisioner defaults with active-plan-only mode plus explicit legacy migration mode.
- W-COM-006: add Stripe customer portal session support with account billing CTA, invoice list, subscription status, and cancellation state.

- W-COM-012: create enterprise inquiry state model: submitted, qualified, architecture review, security review, proposal, legal, procurement, closed-won, closed-lost.
- W-COM-013: build purchase-order quote workflow for procurement buyers, including quote ID, terms, expiry, PO number, and finance contact.
- W-COM-014: generate an enterprise evidence packet with security, BAA/DPA, subprocessors, data residency, SSO/SCIM, SLA, audit export, and BYOC scope.

- W-COM-016: define support entitlement matrix: community, email, priority, named CSM, business-hours SLA, incident escalation, and security support.
