- W-COM-018: fix product structured data generation so Enterprise custom/contact-sales pages do not publish misleading zero-dollar offers.
- W-COM-019: create a commercial release evidence archive with pricing screenshots, API outputs, Stripe test-mode event receipts, account billing screenshots, and structured-data validation output.
- W-COM-020: add production commercial smoke: pricing page, signup plan parameter, upgrade URL, billing usage, portal session, webhook health, enterprise inquiry, and invoice export.

#### Commercial Redline To Relax

Node 16 is not complete until all of these are true:

- one plan taxonomy is canonical across public copy, backend constants, Stripe, tests, docs, account, OpenAPI, CLI, and product graph;
- every legacy tier is either retired, migrated, hidden, or explicitly labeled as an alias;
- Stripe test-mode smoke proves checkout, webhook signature verification, entitlement update, invoice availability, portal session, and cancellation handling;
- account billing gives owners a complete finance view: plan, entitlements, usage, caps, invoices, payment portal, exports, support tier, and next action;
- Enterprise is a real sales/procurement workflow, not a fake self-serve plan or a zero-dollar offer;
- commercial structured data matches visible plan truth and passes validation;
- local-only no-metering remains enforced and visible;
- savings, refund, support, compliance, and security claims all have evidence IDs or closeout labels.

#### Node 16 Commercial Billing Entitlement Addendum V2

This addendum keeps commercial completion attached to master spec tree node `16`. It updates the V1 ledger with the current code evidence and current external billing baselines. It is still a documentation/spec edit only; no billing, router, Stripe, tests, pricing page, enterprise page, account UI, or generated artifact code is changed in this lane.

Current repo evidence checked in this pass:

| evidence | current state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/router.js` `PLAN_CATALOG` | active backend IDs are `free`, `pro`, `teams`, and `enterprise`; `teams` is labeled `Team`; `enterprise` is custom/contact-sales unless a link is configured | the commercial contract must decide whether the canonical public ID is `team` or backend `teams`; both cannot be primary forever |
| `src/router.js` `PLAN_ALIASES` | `developer -> free`, `starter -> pro`, `team -> teams`, `business -> enterprise` | aliases are correctly preserved server-side, but final user-facing surfaces must not treat aliases as active public tiers |
| `/v1/plans` and `/v1/billing/tiers` | both serialize `PLAN_CATALOG` and expose Stripe link readiness | these are good public truth endpoints but still need generated linkage to a single plan contract, entitlements, and usage meters |
| signup and change-plan routes | paid/self-serve requests provision Free first and set `pending_plan`; enterprise without a checkout link returns `sales_required` and `/enterprise/inquiry` | payment-gated activation is structurally correct, but subscription lifecycle, portal, invoice, cancellation, and entitlement state are not yet the complete customer finance path |
| `src/stripe.js` | verifies webhook signatures and maps legacy/current amounts: `900 -> pro`, `4900 -> pro`, `14900 -> teams`, `49900 -> teams`, `149900 -> enterprise`, `299900 -> enterprise` | backward compatibility exists; final state needs contract-generated amount/price maps plus live Stripe product/price reconciliation |
| `src/usage.js` | billing units cover captures, stored events, optimization runs, builds, distillation, hosted inference tokens, seats, signing, registry artifacts, and enterprise sync bytes | billing units are stronger than the pricing story; each unit needs customer-visible inclusion, cap, overage, local-only, and invoice treatment |
| `src/usage.js` tier map | internal tiers remain `free`, `indie`, `team`, `enterprise`; comments still describe old `indie ($9/mo)` and `team ($49/mo)` pricing | stale commercial vocabulary exists in source comments and tier buckets; final completion requires canonical tier naming or a documented compatibility layer |
| `tests/wave273-pricing-tiers.test.js` | comments say four-plan model, but tests still require `data-tier="starter"` and `data-tier="business"` cards and order five markers | test contract currently preserves alias visibility; final completion must separate hidden compatibility markers from visible buyer cards |
| enterprise inquiry path | public inquiry exists; `enterpriseLeads` is in-memory with best-effort email | enterprise sales cannot be complete until inquiry state is durable, owned, auditable, and integrated with quote/procurement/security review |
| account billing surfaces | billing pages and `/v1/billing/usage` exist; tests lock billing/settings/API triangle | account owners still need one finance control room: plan, entitlements, usage, caps, invoices, portal, cancellation, exports, support tier, and proof state |

Current external baselines bound to this addendum:

| source | current baseline | Kolm implementation requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Stripe Customer Portal docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/customer-management` | customer portal lets customers manage billing information, payment methods, subscription status, invoices, and cancellation; sessions are temporary | account billing must create portal sessions, show invoice/payment/subscription status locally, and explain session expiry/fallback |
| Stripe Entitlements docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/entitlements?dashboard-or-api=api` | features are attached to Stripe products; active entitlements are read through the API and updated through the active-entitlement summary webhook | every paid feature must have a feature key, source plan, active entitlement state, local fallback rule, and account/API/CLI explanation |
| Stripe subscription webhook docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/webhooks` | subscriptions and invoices change asynchronously; failures such as `invoice.finalization_failed` can leave access active while collection fails | webhook handling must drive a state machine for paid, failed, past-due, canceled, unpaid, trialing, pending, and sales-required states |
| Stripe webhook docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks?lang=node` | live webhooks require HTTPS, signature verification, duplicate-event handling, async processing, secret rotation, and tolerance for unordered events | Kolm needs an event ledger keyed by Stripe event ID plus object/type, async processing, replay tools, secret rotation support, and out-of-order reconciliation |
| Stripe Checkout Session API, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/checkout/sessions/create` | Checkout can use an existing customer, create customers in subscription mode, carry `client_reference_id`, and collect customer details | upgrade/signup must prefer stable `stripe_customer_id`, pass tenant reference, avoid duplicate customers, and reconcile returned customer/subscription IDs |
| Stripe tax docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/taxes/collect-taxes` | recurring billing tax can use Stripe Tax or tax rates; location can affect invoice finalization and tax status | account checkout and invoices must surface tax mode, address requirements, tax IDs, failed tax finalization, and regional availability without legal ambiguity |
| Google product structured data docs, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/product-snippet` | product snippets should provide unambiguous currency/pricing; active `offers.price` wins over complex price specs | pricing schema must not publish fake zero-dollar Enterprise, stale Business/Starter offers, or ambiguous prices; structured data must match visible plans |
| WorkOS Admin Portal docs, `https://workos.com/docs/admin-portal` | enterprise IT contacts expect hosted setup for SSO, Directory Sync, domain verification, and related resources | Enterprise onboarding must include an IT admin setup path with clear owner, status, test connection, support fallback, and sales handoff |
| WorkOS Directory Sync docs, `https://workos.com/docs/directory-sync` | Directory Sync provides APIs and admin tools for enterprise user lifecycle management, including SCIM-style provisioning | Team/Enterprise entitlement copy must distinguish SSO login, SCIM/ULM lifecycle, group mapping, deprovisioning, and audit evidence |

Commercial truth model V2:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-commercial-contract-2",
  "canonical_public_plans": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
  "backend_compatibility_aliases": {
    "developer": "free",
    "starter": "pro",
    "teams": "team",
    "business": "enterprise"
  },
  "implementation_decision_required": {
    "team_id": "Either rename backend canonical `teams` to `team` with alias support, or declare `teams` as internal ID and generate public `team` slugs everywhere.",
    "business_visibility": "Business can remain a hidden migrated alias, not a buyer-facing active tier.",
    "starter_visibility": "Starter can remain a hidden migrated alias, not a buyer-facing active tier."
  },
  "plan_contract_fields": [
    "plan_id",
    "public_slug",
    "public_name",
    "legacy_aliases",
    "status",
    "price",
    "billing_interval",
    "self_serve",
    "sales_required",
    "stripe_product_id",
    "stripe_price_ids",
    "stripe_payment_link_env",
    "entitlement_keys",
    "meter_limits",
    "local_only_policy",
    "support_policy",
    "procurement_policy",
    "tax_policy",
    "cancellation_policy",
    "structured_data_policy",
    "claim_scope"
  ]
}
```

State machine required for commercial completion:

```text
anonymous -> free_trial -> free_active
free_active -> checkout_pending -> paid_active
paid_active -> past_due -> grace_period -> suspended_or_free
paid_active -> portal_cancel_requested -> cancel_at_period_end -> free_active_or_deleted
paid_active -> upgrade_pending -> paid_active
paid_active -> downgrade_pending -> paid_active_until_period_end -> lower_plan_active
any_paid -> entitlement_summary_updated -> entitlement_reconciled
any_paid -> invoice_finalization_failed -> finance_action_required
any_plan -> enterprise_sales_required -> enterprise_inquiry -> architecture_review -> security_review -> proposal -> procurement -> contract_active
```

Account finance UX contract:

1. The first account billing screen shows current plan, pending plan, billing status, renewal/cancel date, payment method status, invoice status, tax/address status, support tier, and entitlement summary.
2. Entitlements are grouped by outcome, not internal feature names: route model traffic, compile artifacts, distill students, run on devices, govern enterprise controls, publish registry artifacts, and receive support.
3. Every gated feature shows why it is gated, which plan or contract unlocks it, whether an alias/migration is involved, and whether the current tenant is local-only.
4. Usage meters show included amount, current period usage, soft/hard caps, overage policy, last updated time, invoice treatment, export link, and privacy class.
5. Local-only tenants see an explicit no-metering proof: no central usage submission, local usage scope, and how hosted features change billing behavior.
6. Stripe portal actions are clearly labeled as temporary external sessions; failures return a retry path and support path.
7. Enterprise sales-required status becomes a guided checklist: architecture review, security/legal packet, BAA/DPA, SSO/SCIM, BYOC/self-hosting, purchase order, quote, and owner.
8. Cancellation and downgrade flows are easy to find, preserve access-period expectations, capture cancellation reason, and show data export/offboarding actions.
9. Every billing error is near the affected control, written in buyer language, and includes a next action.
10. Finance exports include invoices, usage CSV, chargeback/namespace allocation, procurement contacts, tax IDs, and support evidence.

- W-COM-V2-007: add a durable Stripe customer/subscription state table with tenant ID, customer ID, subscription ID, price ID, status, current period, cancel-at-period-end, trial state, and last reconciled event.

- W-COM-V2-017: update pricing structured data so active self-serve plans have accurate `Offer` data and Enterprise custom/contact-sales pages avoid misleading zero-dollar offer semantics.
- W-COM-V2-018: add commercial claim-scope gates for savings, first-invoice refund, support response time, compliance readiness, BAA/DPA availability, local-only no-metering, and enterprise procurement claims.
- W-COM-V2-019: add production commercial smoke covering pricing, plan APIs, signup plan params, checkout URL creation, webhook replay, entitlement sync, portal session, invoice export, enterprise inquiry, tax state, and cancellation.
- W-COM-V2-020: create a finance evidence packet per release with pricing screenshots, API outputs, account screenshots, Stripe test-mode receipts, structured-data validation, reconciliation report, and open-gate list.

Node `16` V2 relax redline:

Commercial completion can relax only when all of the following are true:

1. exactly one commercial contract generates or verifies every plan, alias, price, entitlement, meter, Stripe env, CTA, support tier, tax rule, cancellation path, and structured-data object;
2. public buyer surfaces show only the intended active plan model, while legacy aliases are hidden compatibility paths with migration proof;
3. the account billing UX is a complete finance control room, not a set of disconnected usage cards and upgrade links;
4. Stripe customer, subscription, invoice, entitlement, portal, tax, and webhook states have durable local state and replay/reconciliation proof;
5. usage meters reconcile from local counters to customer exports and invoices without violating the local-only no-metering promise;
6. Enterprise sales-required state is durable, auditable, owned, and tied to procurement/security/legal/support handoff;
7. cancellation, downgrade, refund/credit, failed payment, failed invoice finalization, and tax-location failures all have customer-visible next actions;
8. pricing JSON-LD, metadata, CTAs, public copy, account copy, API responses, CLI output, docs, tests, screenshots, and production smokes agree.

#### Node 16 V2.1 Commercial Drift Supplement

This supplement keeps the existing node `16` V2 branch current with the live worktree. It does not replace the commercial contract above. It adds the latest drift evidence and closes the ticket sequence to the same 24-ticket pattern used by the adjacent nodes.

Latest local evidence:

| check | result | implication |
|---|---:|---|
| commercial-matched `src` files | 237 of 392 scanned | billing/commercial vocabulary remains spread across backend code and needs contract verification |
| commercial-matched public files | 1,035 of 1,132 scanned | commercial copy is effectively site-wide and must be generated or audited centrally |
| commercial-matched tests | 324 of 567 scanned | test breadth is high, but tests must stop locking stale public tier visibility |
| commercial-matched scripts | 144 of 343 scanned | docs/API/site generation likely emits commercial language and must be contract-driven |
| commercial contract files | `docs/commercial-contract.json`, `src/commercial-contract.js`, `docs/pricing-contract.json`, `docs/entitlement-matrix.json`, `docs/support-policy.json`, `docs/enterprise-sales-contract.json` are absent | contract implementation is still future work |
| pricing page | `public/pricing.html` still advertises `Business` and `$1,499/mo` in title, metadata, structured data, nav anchors, cards, and CTAs | either Business is intentionally reactivated or this is release-blocking commercial drift |
| usage source | `src/usage.js` still uses `indie`, legacy `starter`, `business -> team`, and comments with old prices | backend compatibility is acceptable only if hidden behind canonical public IDs |
| Stripe helper | `src/stripe.js` maps legacy and current amounts to canonical plan IDs | good compatibility, but map must be generated from the contract and reconciled against live Stripe price IDs |

Additional node `16` V2 tickets:

| `W-COM-V2-021` | `16.3` | Decide Business reactivation versus removal. | Product leadership chooses visible Business or hidden alias, and pricing metadata, structured data, tests, API, account, CLI, and Stripe mapping all match. |

| `W-COM-V2-024` | `16.9` | Add commercial completion certificate. | Node `16` is complete only when plan contract, pricing, billing, entitlements, Stripe, ROI, support, enterprise, account, docs, screenshots, and production smoke agree. |

Node `16` remains blocked from "100 percent complete" until the Business/alias decision is resolved and encoded in a canonical contract.

#### Node 16 V3 Commercial Contract, Billing Lifecycle, And Enterprise Finance Control Room

This addendum keeps work attached to master spec tree node `16`. It does not edit pricing, Stripe, billing, account, router, tests, generated docs, or frontend code while other agents are active. It turns the commercial drift into a buildable contract: one plan graph, one entitlement matrix, one meter dictionary, one billing lifecycle, one enterprise procurement workflow, and one commercial proof gate.

The goal is not "make the pricing page look better." The goal is that a buyer, account admin, finance owner, implementation engineer, backend route, Stripe webhook, invoice export, support policy, plan test, structured-data snippet, and enterprise sales handoff all describe the same product.

##### Node 16 V3 Current Evidence Delta

Fresh local review found the following commercial truth gaps:

| evidence path | current signal | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `public/pricing.html` | 103,817 bytes, 1,221 lines; `Business` appears 28 times, `Starter` 7, `Developer` 2, `$1,499` 6, `$499` 16, `$49` 34 | public pricing is still a five-tier artifact even though backend plan APIs are a four-plan model |
| `public/pricing.html` metadata | title and descriptions advertise `Free, Pro, Team, Business, Enterprise`; JSON-LD offers include `Business` at `1499` and `Enterprise` at `0`; AggregateOffer says `highPrice` `499` and `offerCount` `4` | search/social/schema output is internally contradictory and can mislead crawlers and buyers |
| `public/pricing.html` tier grid | `data-tier="starter"` renders Pro and `data-tier="business"` renders Business; a hidden span says Business maps to Enterprise custom while the visible card prices Business at `$1,499/mo` | compatibility aliases are visible as products; the page cannot be considered canonical |
| `public/pricing.html` quotas | public copy says Free `50,000` gateway calls, Pro `500,000`, Team `5M`; hidden W273 span still says Free `100 compiles`, `1k captures`, `1 GB`; ROI uses Enterprise `5000` as a fixed monthly cost | quota, compile credit, and ROI assumptions are not generated from a shared commercial contract |
| `src/router.js` | `PLAN_CATALOG` has `free`, `pro`, `teams`, `enterprise`; `PLAN_ALIASES` maps `developer -> free`, `starter -> pro`, `team -> teams`, `business -> enterprise`; `/v1/plans` and `/v1/billing/tiers` serialize this catalog | backend canonical ID is `teams`, while buyer-facing copy uses `Team`; alias compatibility is real but not yet isolated from public taxonomy |
| `src/router.js` signup/change-plan | paid signup provisions Free plus `pending_plan`; enterprise with no billing URL returns `sales_required: true` and `/enterprise/inquiry`; Stripe webhook activates plan on `checkout.session.completed` | good lifecycle core exists, but it needs a formal state machine, reconciliation job, and account UI for every state |
| `src/stripe.js` | amount map includes `900 -> pro`, `4900 -> pro`, `14900 -> teams`, `49900 -> teams`, `149900 -> enterprise`, `299900 -> enterprise` | legacy webhooks survive, but amount mapping must be generated from product/price IDs and reconciled against Stripe, not hard-coded cents alone |
| `src/billing-upgrade.js` | checkout URL fallback chain is Payment Link, Checkout Session, self-hosted billing URL, manual fallback; legacy price envs include Starter/Teams/Business | useful fallback behavior exists, but commercial source-of-truth is still split across env names and comments |
| `src/usage.js` | `BILLING_UNITS` has 10 units; `TIER_LIMITS` uses `free`, `indie`, `team`, `enterprise`; `tierForPlan` maps Pro/Starter to `indie` and Business to `team` | metering tier buckets do not match plan taxonomy; implementation must separate public plans from internal limit profiles explicitly |
| `public/account/billing.html` | UI lists 12 meter rows: `captured_events`, `redacted_events`, `raw_cloud_synced_bytes`, `redacted_cloud_synced_bytes`, `hosted_build_minutes`, `training_gpu_minutes`, `synthetic_examples_generated`, `eval_examples_run`, `artifacts_built`, `artifact_installs`, `team_seats`, `device_sync_targets` | account billing meter vocabulary is not the same as `src/usage.js`; only `captured_events` and `team_seats` clearly overlap |
| `public/account/sla.html` | SLA page still names `Business` commitments and `Tier: Business`; page says backend placeholders remain until `/v1/sla/dashboard` is wired | SLA commitments need to be generated from support/SLA contract and cannot keep a retired tier name silently |
| `tests/wave273-pricing-tiers.test.js` | tests explicitly expect five data-tier markers and order `free`, `starter`, `team`, `business`, `enterprise`; tests say four-plan model in comments but lock five visible tiers | tests are preserving commercial drift; they must be changed after leadership chooses visible Business or hidden alias |
| contract files | `docs/commercial-contract.json`, `src/commercial-contract.js`, `docs/pricing-contract.json`, `docs/entitlement-matrix.json`, `docs/support-policy.json`, and `docs/enterprise-sales-contract.json` are absent | node `16` cannot be final until these are created or one consolidated replacement owns their fields |
| scoped worktree status | scoped status shows only this research document as untracked; commercial code files above were not dirty in this spec lane | safe to treat observed commercial drift as current baseline, not a half-applied local patch by this agent |

##### Node 16 V3 External Baselines

Primary sources reviewed for this node:

| source | relevant baseline | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Stripe usage-based billing docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/usage-based/how-it-works` | Stripe frames usage billing as ingestion, product catalog, billing, and monitoring; meters aggregate usage events over a billing period | Kolm needs explicit event ingestion, product/price mapping, invoice reconciliation, and threshold alerting for every billable meter |
| Stripe usage meter event model, same source | meter events include event name, customer identifier, value, optional timestamp, idempotency identifier, and dimensions; summaries are asynchronous | every Kolm billing increment must carry customer/tenant ID, unit, amount, idempotency key, dimensions, period, source route, and reconciliation status |
| Stripe customer portal docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/customer-management` | portal lets customers manage payment methods, invoices, subscriptions, cancellation, localization, tax IDs, and branding; some subscription changes are limited for usage-based or complex subscriptions | account billing must expose portal deep links where valid and fall back to Kolm-native flows for usage-based plan changes, cancellation, and enterprise contracts |
| Stripe subscription webhook docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/webhooks` | subscription activity is asynchronous; webhooks must be verified and handle subscription status changes, payment failures, and cancellation | router webhook logic needs idempotent event storage, replay, reconciliation, customer-visible status, failed-payment actions, and proof that access changes follow events |
| Stripe Entitlements docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/billing/entitlements` | features attach to products; active entitlements should drive provisioning; entitlement summary webhooks grant/revoke access | Kolm feature gating should be generated from entitlements, not scattered plan checks; account UI must show active and pending entitlements |
| Stripe Tax docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/tax` | Stripe Tax covers sales tax, VAT, GST, registrations, calculations, collections, filings, product tax codes, and threshold monitoring | pricing/checkout must track tax behavior, customer location, tax IDs, invoice tax status, and fallback when tax location prevents invoice finalization |
| WorkOS Directory Sync docs, `https://workos.com/docs/directory-sync` | enterprise user lifecycle management requires syncing users/groups from directory providers, usually through SCIM and webhook/event updates | enterprise plan must include directory sync state, group mapping, deprovisioning, and seat/entitlement reconciliation in billing and account UI |
| WorkOS Admin Portal docs, `https://workos.com/docs/admin-portal` | IT contacts can self-serve domain verification, SSO, Directory Sync, Log Streams, and setup links with redirect URIs | enterprise sales handoff should create an IT setup path, not just a "contact sales" CTA |
| Google Product structured data docs, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/product-snippet` | product snippets require consistent product/offer information for search appearance | pricing JSON-LD must not advertise mismatched offer counts, hidden aliases, or zero-dollar custom enterprise pricing as a sellable offer |
| Schema.org Offer and AggregateOffer, `https://schema.org/Offer` and `https://schema.org/AggregateOffer` | offers describe sellable terms; AggregateOffer describes ranges across offers | Kolm should publish explicit Offers for self-serve plans and a separate contact/sales action for Enterprise, with accurate `lowPrice`, `highPrice`, and `offerCount` |

##### Node 16 V3 Commercial Source Of Truth

Implement one source of truth named `kolm.commercial_contract.v3`. It may ship as `docs/commercial-contract.json` plus `src/commercial-contract.js`, or as one JS module that can emit JSON. It must generate or verify these surfaces:

- public pricing cards, pricing metadata, pricing JSON-LD, FAQ, ROI assumptions, support/SLA tables, and CTA destinations;
- `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`, `/v1/billing/meters`, `/v1/billing/usage`, `/v1/account`, `/v1/account/change-plan`, `/v1/account/cancel`, `/v1/stripe/webhook`, `/v1/chargeback`, and enterprise inquiry routes;
- account billing, account chargeback, account SLA, account overview, enterprise setup, upgrade, invoice/export, and admin console states;
- Stripe product IDs, price IDs, payment links, checkout-session price env vars, webhook events, entitlement lookup keys, tax behavior, and customer portal configurations;
- CLI billing/surfaces/account commands, docs, OpenAPI, API reference, tests, product graph, claim-scope audit, screenshot route lists, and production smoke expectations.

Canonical public plan taxonomy:

| public ID | label | monthly price | buyer state | Stripe mode | support posture | notes |
|---|---|---:|---|---|---|---|
| `free` | Free | `0` | self-serve, immediate | no checkout | community / best effort | no hidden trial language; all free quotas generated from contract |
| `pro` | Pro | `49` | self-serve, checkout required | Payment Link or Checkout Session | email or documented response target | one builder / small product plan |
| `team` | Team | `499` | self-serve, checkout required | Payment Link or Checkout Session | team support target | buyer-facing ID is singular `team`; backend may keep `teams` only as a legacy alias |
| `enterprise` | Enterprise | `custom` | sales-required | contract or manually approved checkout only | named enterprise support / SLA contract | no zero-dollar Offer; sales state persists in tenant and CRM ledger |

Legacy alias rule:

| alias | canonical plan | allowed public visibility | migration behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| `developer` | `free` | hidden compatibility alias only | accept in API/CLI with warning and emit canonical `free` |
| `starter` | `pro` | hidden compatibility alias only | accept in existing links/tests only until migrated |
| `indie` | `pro` limit profile or deprecated bucket | internal-only if still needed | never appear in public buyer copy |
| `teams` | `team` | backend compatibility alias only | API may accept both but serializes public `team` |
| `business` | unresolved until product leadership decision | either hidden alias to `enterprise` or explicitly reactivated as a fifth plan | cannot be both visible fixed-price tier and hidden Enterprise alias |

Business decision gate:

| option | requirements | release implication |
|---|---|---|
| Retire Business | remove visible Business card, metadata, JSON-LD offer, nav anchor, SLA rows, tests, and old fixed price; keep `business -> enterprise` only as compatibility alias | aligns with four-plan model and previous backend contract |
| Reactivate Business | add first-class `business` to `PLAN_CATALOG`, Stripe price IDs, entitlement matrix, support/SLA contract, usage limits, account UI, OpenAPI, tests, JSON-LD, docs, CLI, and plan serializer | makes five-plan model real; cannot be merely a marketing card |

##### `kolm.commercial_contract.v3`

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.commercial_contract.v3",
  "canonical_public_plans": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
  "internal_aliases": {
    "developer": "free",
    "starter": "pro",
    "indie": "pro",
    "teams": "team",
    "business": "enterprise"
  },
  "business_decision": {
    "state": "must_decide_before_release",
    "allowed_values": ["retired_alias", "first_class_plan"],
    "current_conflict": "public/pricing.html and W273 tests expose Business as visible fixed-price tier while router aliases business to enterprise"
  },
  "plans": {
    "free": {
      "price_usd_month": 0,
      "checkout": "none",

      "public_offer": true
    },
    "team": {

      "checkout": "stripe_self_serve",
      "activation": "webhook_after_checkout",

      "public_offer": true
    },

      "price_usd_month": null,
      "checkout": "sales_required",
      "activation": "contract_or_operator_approval",
      "support_policy": "contractual_sla",
      "tax_behavior": "contract_specific",

      "sales_url": "/enterprise/inquiry"
    }
  },
  "entitlements": {
    "route_model_traffic": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
    "capture_and_privacy_lake": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
    "compile_distill_quantize": ["pro", "team", "enterprise"],
    "private_registry": ["team", "enterprise"],
    "approvals_and_audit": ["team", "enterprise"],
    "sso_scim": ["team", "enterprise"],
    "baa": ["enterprise"],
    "byoc_airgap": ["enterprise"],
    "device_fleet": ["enterprise"]
  },
  "billing_lifecycle_states": [
    "anonymous_pricing_view",
    "signup_requested",
    "free_active",
    "pending_checkout",
    "checkout_completed",
    "active_paid",
    "payment_failed",
    "past_due",
    "portal_session_opened",
    "plan_change_pending",
    "sales_required",
    "contract_review",
    "enterprise_approved",
    "cancellation_requested",
    "cancelled",
    "exported",
    "deleted"
  ],
  "usage_event_envelope": {
    "tenant_id": "required",
    "plan_id": "required_canonical_public_id",
    "meter_id": "required_contract_meter",
    "amount": "positive_number",
    "period": "YYYY-MM",
    "source": "route_or_worker",
    "idempotency_key": "required",
    "dimensions": ["namespace", "artifact_id", "provider", "runtime", "region", "department", "project"],
    "privacy_scope": "local_only_events_must_not_leave_device"
  },
  "stripe_contract": {
    "products_and_prices": "generated_or_verified",
    "payment_links": "generated_or_verified",
    "checkout_sessions": "supported",
    "webhook_signature_verification": "required",
    "idempotent_event_store": "required",
    "entitlement_summary_webhook": "required_for_access_provisioning",
    "tax_status": "required_for_paid_checkout",
    "customer_portal": "required_where_valid"
  },
  "structured_data": {
    "self_serve_offers_only": true,
    "enterprise_representation": "ContactPoint_or_sales_action_not_zero_price_offer",
    "aggregate_offer_must_match_offer_array": true
  }
}
```

##### Billing Lifecycle State Machine

| state | entry event | customer-visible surface | backend proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `anonymous_pricing_view` | visitor opens pricing/ROI/enterprise | pricing page, ROI calculator, structured data | pricing contract hash embedded in page and JSON-LD |
| `signup_requested` | `/signup` or `/v1/signup` with plan | signup confirmation | canonical plan ID and alias warning |
| `free_active` | free signup or paid tenant pre-checkout | account overview and billing | tenant row plan `free`, no paid entitlements |
| `pending_checkout` | paid self-serve signup/change-plan | account billing banner and checkout URL | `pending_plan`, checkout source, idempotency key |
| `checkout_completed` | Stripe checkout webhook | billing activated email/account event | verified signature, stored event ID, subscription/customer IDs |
| `active_paid` | entitlement grant complete | account plan, entitlements, limits | active entitlement snapshot and limit profile |
| `payment_failed` | invoice or subscription failure | account billing warning and payment action | Stripe event ID, failed invoice, retry state |
| `past_due` | subscription past due/unpaid | feature downgrade policy visible | entitlement grace policy and audit event |
| `portal_session_opened` | account billing opens Stripe portal | payment/invoice/subscription management | portal session ID, allowed actions, expiry |
| `plan_change_pending` | upgrade/downgrade requested | current plan plus pending plan | mutation ledger and next action |
| `sales_required` | enterprise request without checkout | enterprise inquiry and sales handoff | tenant `pending_plan`, `billing_status`, inquiry record |
| `contract_review` | enterprise intake accepted | security/procurement checklist | organization, legal, tax, DPA/BAA, SSO/SCIM setup state |
| `enterprise_approved` | contract and provisioning done | enterprise account setup room | entitlement grants, SLA/support contract, deploy proof |
| `cancellation_requested` | account cancel or portal cancellation | retention/cancel confirmation | cancellation reason, immediate/end-period choice |
| `cancelled` | subscription deleted or local cancel | free plan or offboard state | Stripe cancel proof or manual fallback audit |
| `exported` | invoice/data/account export requested | downloadable finance/data package | export ID, checksum, retention/deletion policy |
| `deleted` | account delete | deletion receipt | account tombstone, billing cancellation attempt, audit chain |

##### Account Finance Control Room

`/account/billing` should become a finance control room, not a meter table:

- plan card: current plan, canonical plan ID, legacy alias if any, billing status, pending plan, renewal date, cancellation status, tax status, support/SLA tier, and contract hash;
- entitlement matrix: route/capture/compile/distill/registry/device/governance/enterprise controls with active, pending, blocked, and requires-sales states;
- meter ledger: generated from `BILLING_UNITS` or the replacement contract, with usage, soft/hard cap, projected end-of-period, source routes, and customer-visible explanation;
- invoice history: invoice status, tax, amount, credits/refunds, download link, webhook event ID, and reconciliation status;
- payment actions: portal, update payment method, pay invoice, retry, cancel, downgrade, change plan, contact sales, export;
- chargeback: namespace, project, department, provider, runtime, artifact, and region dimensions with CSV/JSON exports and formulas;
- enterprise procurement: DPA, BAA, security packet, tax forms, PO, vendor onboarding, SSO/SCIM/Admin Portal setup, support contacts, and architecture review status;
- local-only guarantee: explicit "not metered and not uploaded" evidence for local/device paths.

| `W-COM-V3-002` | `16.1` | Decide Business. | A single decision file says `business` is retired alias or first-class plan; every public/backend/test surface follows that decision. |

| `W-COM-V3-019` | `16.7` | Make chargeback formulas auditable. | CSV/JSON exports include formulas, dimensions, source event IDs, period, and rounding policy. |

| `W-COM-V3-024` | `16.9` | Emit commercial completion certificate. | release proof includes contract hash, diff summary, tests, screenshot report, Stripe readiness, tax readiness, entitlement readiness, and production smoke links. |

##### Node 16 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `16` remains red until all of these are true:

1. Business is either truly retired or truly reactivated; no surface can present both meanings.
2. `team`/`teams`, `starter`, `developer`, `indie`, and `business` aliases are hidden compatibility inputs, not public product names, unless made first-class in the contract.
3. public pricing, router plans, usage limits, account billing, Stripe amounts, tests, support/SLA, ROI, JSON-LD, docs, CLI, OpenAPI, and product graph share one contract hash.
4. account billing meter rows exactly match the backend meter dictionary or show an explicit derived-meter mapping.
5. Stripe checkout, webhook, portal, entitlements, invoice, tax, cancellation, failed-payment, and reconciliation states are durable and customer-visible.
6. Enterprise sales-required flow is not just a CTA; it has a state machine, procurement checklist, security/legal handoff, support/SLA contract, SSO/SCIM setup path, and provisioning proof.
7. local-only work remains provably unmetered and unuploaded.
8. commercial production smoke proves the deployed site and APIs match the local contract before any deploy is called final.

### Developer Ecosystem, Integrations, And Adoption Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `17`: the developer ecosystem. Kolm cannot become a default infrastructure layer for AI companies unless developers can install it, verify it, wire it into their existing tools, migrate from adjacent stacks, and trust the compatibility story across package managers, CI systems, IDEs, model runtimes, and agent frameworks.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not modify SDKs, package manifests, docs pages, loaders, GitHub Actions, MCP installers, or integration code while other agents are editing.

#### Current Ecosystem Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| browser and TypeScript SDK | `public/sdk.js`, `public/sdk-current.json`, `public/sdk-versions.json`, `packages/sdk-ts/package.json`, `scripts/build-sdk-version.js`, `tests/wave505-sdk-manifest-release-gate.test.js` | content-addressed browser SDK assets, SRI manifest, TypeScript package manifest, build/dist verifier, release gate coverage | needs one compatibility matrix against OpenAPI/product graph, provenance for published packages, changelog, and explicit support policy |
| Python SDK and runtime options | `packages/sdk-python/pyproject.toml`, `packages/sdk-python/kolm`, `sdk/python`, optional `gguf`, `onnx`, `transformers`, `remote` extras | Python packaging metadata and optional runtime dependencies exist | needs PyPI status, trusted publishing, compatibility tests, install smoke, and docs that match whether Python is official package, HTTP-only path, or runtime SDK |
| mobile SDKs | `packages/sdk-swift`, `packages/sdk-kotlin`, `packages/sdk-rn` | SwiftPM, Android/Kotlin, and React Native package shapes exist | needs package-manager publication or local-source labels, native fixture apps, platform support policy, and artifact verification smoke |
| Rust/native runtime | `packages/runtime-rs`, `sdk/rust`, `sdk/c`, tests `wave219`, `wave457`, `runtime-rs/tests` | native verification and runtime code exists | needs crate/package release proof, ABI policy, tamper fixture coverage across SDKs, SBOM, and supported target triples |
| framework adapters | `packages/langchain-kolm`, `packages/llamaindex-kolm`, `packages/python-langchain-kolm`, `packages/python-llamaindex-kolm`, `public/docs/integrations/langchain-*.html`, `public/docs/integrations/llamaindex-*.html` | LangChain and LlamaIndex adapter packages and docs exist in JS and Python | needs package publication status, version compatibility per upstream framework, examples that run, error handling, auth/capture parity, and framework-owned tests |
| IDE and agent integrations | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag`, `sdk/vscode`, `public/docs/vscode.html`, `public/install/vscode.html`, `sdk/mcp`, `services/mcp`, `scripts/install-mcp.cjs`, `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html` | VS Code extension paths, MCP server/installers, and docs exist | duplicate VS Code surfaces need ownership decision; MCP install must match current harness config schemas; extension packaging and Marketplace status must be explicit |
| runtime loaders | `tools/llama-cpp-kolm-loader`, `tools/ollama-kolm`, `tools/hf-hub-kolm`, `tools/vllm-kolm`, `src/runtime-adoption-packets.js`, `docs/runtime-adoption-packets.md`, `tests/wave818-ecosystem-loaders.test.js` | loader scaffolds and adoption packet verification exist | needs external adoption proof, upstream PR/package links, loader conformance fixtures, support levels, and runtime compatibility matrix |
| cloud and automation docs | `public/docs/integrations/index.html`, cloud guides, Zapier/Make guides, `public/integrations/*.html`, archived integrations | broad integration docs exist for clouds, automation, editors, provider SDKs, and deployment platforms | needs current IA cleanup, link validity, scope labels, screenshots, real deploy smoke, and archived-vs-live ownership |
| CI/CD actions | `.github/actions/kolm-*`, `.github/workflows/kolm*.yml`, `tools/gha/kolm-distill-action`, `tools/github-actions-kolm`, `public/docs/github-actions.html` | action templates and docs exist for compile, publish, test, verify, distill, smoke, SBOM | needs one published action identity, action metadata validation, secret handling, workflow permissions, attestations, and self-test examples |

#### Ecosystem Drift Observed In The Current Tree

1. SDK docs say "one official JS SDK" while the repository also contains Python, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, Rust, C, MCP, and VS Code surfaces. Final docs must distinguish official, preview, local-source-only, and experimental packages.
2. Package manifests and public docs still point to multiple repository identities, including `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack`, `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`, and `kolm-ai/kolm-sdk-python`. Node `0` owns brand truth, but node `17` owns install-link truth.
3. `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` and `sdk/vscode` both look like VS Code integration surfaces. Completion requires one canonical extension or explicit split, with Marketplace status and packaging proof.
4. Runtime loader work is strong locally, but loader scaffolds are not the same as ecosystem adoption. Third-party support claims require external PRs, packages, compatibility tests, or clear "adapter packet only" labels.
5. GitHub Actions docs mention `kolm/distill-action@v1`, while the repo also has local actions and tool directories. Completion requires one published action name, install path, permission model, and template source.
6. Cloud integration pages still carry broad "Frontier AI on your own infrastructure" framing and must be reconciled with the final three-surface product narrative.
7. Browser SDK versioning is one of the stronger pieces: `scripts/build-sdk-version.js` creates content-addressed assets and SRI, and release tests guard manifest visibility. That pattern should be copied into every package channel.
8. MCP installation is pragmatic and broad, but completion needs current harness schemas, dry-run output examples, conflict behavior screenshots/logs, and security notes about what the MCP server can access.
9. Integration docs are split between `public/docs/integrations/*`, `public/integrations/*`, archived snapshots, and package READMEs. Completion requires one live integration index and clear archival policy.
10. Examples exist, but final developer experience requires executable examples with expected output, fixture credentials or mock servers, teardown instructions, and CI smoke.

#### External Standards Bound To Node 17

| standard source | relevant standard | Kolm completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| npm package entry points and `exports`: `https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html` | Node package resolution, `exports`, dual CJS/ESM behavior, and package boundaries affect install compatibility | every JS package needs explicit export maps, supported runtime versions, types, import examples, and smoke under supported Node versions |
| npm package manifest docs: `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v10/configuring-npm/package-json` | package metadata, files, repository, bin, scripts, engines, and publish behavior define package quality | npm package manifests must have canonical repo URLs, license, files, types, engines, keywords, publish config, and provenance policy |
| PyPA packaging guide: `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/guides/writing-pyproject-toml/` | `pyproject.toml` project metadata, dependencies, optional dependencies, URLs, and classifiers define Python install behavior | Python packages need accurate metadata, extras, Python version support, wheels/sdist proof, and import smoke |
| Semantic Versioning 2.0.0: `https://semver.org/` | public APIs must communicate breaking changes through version numbers | all SDKs, loaders, actions, and public artifact formats need version policy, changelog, deprecation windows, and compatibility tests |
| GitHub Actions metadata syntax: `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/sharing-automations/creating-actions/metadata-syntax-for-github-actions` | actions define inputs, outputs, runs, branding, and execution model in `action.yml` | every Kolm action needs validated metadata, least-privilege workflow docs, outputs, examples, and self-test workflow |
| GitHub workflow security hardening: `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions` | actions must handle secrets, permissions, untrusted input, and third-party dependencies safely | Kolm CI templates must use minimum permissions, avoid secret exposure in forks, pin dependencies where required, and document trust boundaries |
| VS Code extension manifest docs: `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/extension-manifest` | VS Code extensions require manifest fields, activation events, contribution points, engines, categories, and Marketplace metadata | VS Code packages need one canonical manifest, command naming, activation discipline, configuration docs, build/test/package proof, and Marketplace or local-only status |
| Model Context Protocol docs: `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs` | MCP servers expose tools/resources/prompts over defined transports and security boundaries | Kolm MCP must document tools, scopes, local filesystem/network access, auth, install config, and per-client support matrix |

#### Developer Adoption Contract

Every developer-facing surface needs a common record:

```json
{
  "integration_id": "langchain-js",
  "status": "published | local-source-only | adapter-packet | external-pr | deprecated",
  "owner_node": "17.2",
  "package_manager": "npm",
  "install": "npm install @kolm/langchain",
  "supported_versions": { "kolm": "0.2.x", "upstream": "langchain>=0.x" },
  "auth_model": "api-key | local-only | byo-provider-key | none",
  "core_examples": ["capture", "run", "verify", "distill"],
  "smoke_command": "npm test",
  "docs_url": "https://kolm.ai/docs/integrations/langchain-js",
  "support_level": "ga | beta | preview | reference-only",
  "evidence": ["test output", "package registry URL", "screenshot", "release attestation"]
}
```

Rules:

- An integration is not "supported" until it has install instructions, smoke proof, docs, version compatibility, error behavior, and owner.
- Adapter packets can be marketed as "available for integration" but not "native support" until external adoption exists.
- Every public integration page must link to current install docs, source, support level, and last verified date.
- All package READMEs must share the same top-level story: route traffic, compile/distill owned artifacts, deploy/run anywhere with target-specific proof.
- Every framework adapter must show how to preserve Kolm's unique value: capture receipts, artifact hashes, K-Score evidence, and runtime verification.
- Every CI template must be safe by default for forks and pull requests.
- Every install path must avoid leaking `KOLM_API_KEY`, provider keys, prompts, capture content, or artifact secrets in logs.

#### Node 17 Completion Specification

| node | name | done state | proof required |
|---|---|---|---|
| 17.1 | SDK installability and package metadata | every SDK/package has canonical metadata, install docs, build/test command, version policy, provenance status, and support level | package manifest audit, install smoke, import smoke, changelog, SBOM/provenance or local-source-only label |
| 17.2 | framework and agent adapters | LangChain, LlamaIndex, MCP, VS Code, Cursor/Continue/Claude Desktop style integrations have owned packages/docs and real examples | adapter tests, example outputs, MCP installer dry-run, VS Code package proof, public docs screenshots |
| 17.3 | runtime loaders | llama.cpp, Ollama, HF Hub, vLLM, LM Studio, ONNX, GGUF loaders have local conformance tests and external adoption state | loader fixture tests, manifest validation, external PR/package links or adapter-packet labels |
| 17.4 | CI/CD integrations | GitHub Actions and workflow templates are safe, published or explicitly local, and pinned to one canonical action identity | action metadata validation, workflow security review, fork/secret behavior test, artifact publish/verify smoke |
| 17.5 | cloud, automation, and migration docs | cloud guides, Zapier/Make, provider SDK migrations, Ollama/LM Studio migrations, and examples are current and job-to-be-done oriented | link audit, command smoke, screenshots, expected output, archive policy |
| 17.6 | adoption evidence and support matrix | every integration has status, owner, support level, last verified date, version compatibility, and evidence links | generated `public/integrations-support-matrix.json`, account/docs parity, product graph references |

#### Developer Ecosystem Tickets To Generate

- W-ECO-001: create a generated integration support matrix covering SDKs, package managers, framework adapters, IDEs, MCP clients, runtime loaders, cloud guides, CI actions, and automation integrations.
- W-ECO-002: normalize repository URLs across package manifests, docs, public nav, GitHub links, and structured metadata.
- W-ECO-003: classify every package as published, release-ready dry run, local-source-only, adapter-packet, preview, or deprecated.
- W-ECO-004: create package metadata audit for npm, PyPI, SwiftPM, Maven/Gradle, CocoaPods, Cargo, VS Code Marketplace, Homebrew, winget, and MCP.
- W-ECO-005: add import/install smoke commands for every SDK and adapter in a single package compatibility suite.
- W-ECO-006: reconcile SDK docs with package reality: official JS browser SDK, Node/TS package, Python package, mobile SDKs, Rust/native runtime, MCP, and VS Code.
- W-ECO-007: add OpenAPI/product-graph compatibility tests for every SDK method and example.
- W-ECO-008: choose canonical VS Code extension ownership between `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` and `sdk/vscode`, or document a split with separate support levels.
- W-ECO-009: publish or explicitly scope LangChain and LlamaIndex packages in JS and Python, with runnable examples and upstream version pins.
- W-ECO-010: harden MCP installer docs and tests for Cursor, Continue, Claude Desktop, VS Code, Windsurf, and manual JSON install paths.
- W-ECO-011: convert runtime loader scaffolds into conformance fixtures: `.kolm` layout, manifest validation, weights policy, attestation check, error output.
- W-ECO-012: attach external adoption evidence for llama.cpp, Ollama, HF Hub, vLLM, and LM Studio, or keep them labeled adapter packets.
- W-ECO-013: define one GitHub Action identity and one workflow-template source for compile, verify, distill, publish, and smoke.
- W-ECO-014: add GitHub Actions security gate: minimum permissions, fork-secret skip, dependency pinning policy, artifact attestations, and log redaction.
- W-ECO-015: make integration docs generated from the support matrix so `public/docs/integrations/*`, `public/integrations/*`, package READMEs, and product graph cannot drift.
- W-ECO-016: add migration guides with executable examples for OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Ollama, LM Studio, and Docker Compose.
- W-ECO-017: require every example to include expected output, failure output, cleanup, and fixture/mock mode.
- W-ECO-018: add developer support lifecycle: support level, deprecation policy, changelog, issue template, security contact, and compatibility window.
- W-ECO-019: create ecosystem release evidence archive with package registry URLs, checksums, provenance, screenshots, docs URLs, and smoke outputs.
- W-ECO-020: add production adoption smoke that checks public docs, package install paths, action metadata, MCP installer dry-run, VS Code package, and runtime loader fixtures on the shipping commit.

#### Ecosystem Redline To Relax

Node 17 is not complete until all of these are true:

- every package, SDK, adapter, loader, action, and integration page has one support status and owner;
- public docs never imply a package is published or natively supported unless registry/external evidence proves it;
- all install commands work or are labeled local-source-only with exact local instructions;
- every integration has an executable example and expected output;
- every SDK method/example is compatible with the current OpenAPI/product graph;
- every CI template is safe for forks and secrets by default;
- every runtime loader has fixture proof and target-specific limitations;
- every ecosystem support claim has a dated last-verified signal and linkable evidence.

#### Node 17 V2 Developer Ecosystem, Integrations, And Adoption Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `17`. It is a specification and handoff edit only. It does not modify SDKs, package manifests, integration docs, local dependency folders, GitHub actions, MCP services, runtime loaders, examples, or public pages while other agents are active.

The current ecosystem surface is larger than the V1 ledger captured. Kolm now has enough SDK, adapter, runtime-loader, CI, and IDE material to look broad, but breadth is dangerous unless every integration has one status, one owner, one install path, one compatibility range, one smoke command, one security model, and one last-verified date. "Source exists" is not the same as installable, published, native, supported, or externally adopted.

##### Node 17 V2 Current Evidence

| area | current evidence | ecosystem interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| `packages/` roots | 16 directories / 139 files / 380,618 bytes | package shapes exist across SDKs, adapters, browser extension, installers, attestation, and runtime |
| `sdk/` roots | 6 directories / 4,956 current files / 1,593,186,765 bytes | current local SDK tree contains large dependency/artifact material; final source must separate real SDK source from `node_modules`, generated, cache, or release artifacts |
| `tools/` roots | 11 directories / 29 files / 151,974 bytes | runtime loaders, GitHub action tooling, Helm/Grafana/edge helpers exist but need support/adoption status |
| `.github/actions` | 4 local actions / 4 files / 9,280 bytes | compile, publish, test, and verify actions exist locally but need metadata validation, Marketplace/published identity, permissions, and examples |
| integration docs | 10 `public/docs/integrations` pages and 18 `public/integrations` pages | docs are broad but split across two URL families and need one generated support matrix |
| MCP service | `services/mcp` has 1 file / 12,616 bytes; `sdk/mcp` package exists | MCP exists but needs current host/client config, tool/resource/prompt list, permissions, and security notes |
| compute/runtime sources | `src/compute` 26 files, `src/runners` 4 files | runtime/cloud integration code exists but needs ecosystem compatibility mapping and target proof |
| ecosystem-matched packages | 89 of 139 scanned package files | package/adoption vocabulary is widespread and should be contract-governed |
| ecosystem-matched public files | 801 of 1,132 scanned public files | public pages discuss integrations frequently; claims must match actual package/adoption status |
| ecosystem-matched tests | 234 of 567 scanned tests | local tests exist but must be linked to integration IDs and support levels |
| support matrix files | `public/integrations-support-matrix.json`, `docs/integration-support-matrix.json`, `docs/ecosystem-support-matrix.json`, `docs/package-support-matrix.json`, `docs/runtime-loader-matrix.json`, `docs/migration-guides.json`, `docs/sdk-compatibility.json`, `docs/developer-ecosystem-contract.json` are absent | ecosystem state is not machine-readable yet |
| npm-like manifests | `packages/sdk-ts`, `packages/langchain-kolm`, `packages/llamaindex-kolm`, `packages/sdk-rn`, `packages/vscode-kolm-rag`, `sdk/node`, `sdk/mcp`, `sdk/vscode`, local actions | manifest breadth is high; every manifest needs repo URL, publish status, files list, engines, types, license, provenance, and support status |
| Python manifests | `packages/sdk-python`, `packages/python-langchain-kolm`, `packages/python-llamaindex-kolm`, `sdk/python` | Python packaging exists but package-channel proof and trusted publishing state must be explicit |
| Rust/native manifests | `packages/runtime-rs`, `sdk/rust`, `sdk/c` | native runtime exists; crate/ABI/target-triple/release proof must be explicit |
| mobile SDK manifests | `packages/sdk-swift`, `packages/sdk-kotlin`, `packages/sdk-rn` | mobile package shapes exist; SwiftPM/Maven/npm/CocoaPods publication or local-source-only status must be declared |
| duplicate VS Code surfaces | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` and `sdk/vscode` both have package manifests | one canonical VS Code extension or split support model is required |
| runtime loader roots | `tools/llama-cpp-kolm-loader`, `tools/ollama-kolm`, `tools/hf-hub-kolm`, `tools/vllm-kolm`, `tools/lm-studio-kolm` | loaders should be labeled adapter packet, local support, published plugin, or externally adopted per target |
| local dependency/brand drift | manifests include repo-brand hits including `kolmogorov-stack`, `kolm-stack`, `sneaky-hippo`, `kolm-ai`; `sdk/mcp/node_modules/@kolmogorov/recipe` appears locally | install-link truth and local dependency cleanup are part of ecosystem completion |

##### Node 17 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | current official source | Kolm ecosystem rule |
|---|---|---|
| npm trusted publishing and provenance | https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers | npm packages should use OIDC trusted publishing where possible, avoid long-lived npm tokens, and publish provenance for public packages |
| npm package provenance viewing | https://docs.npmjs.com/viewing-package-provenance | public package docs should link or state provenance status so developers can verify build source and workflow |
| PyPI Trusted Publishers | https://docs.pypi.org/trusted-publishers/ | Python packages should prefer OIDC Trusted Publishing over long-lived tokens and document package-to-workflow trust |
| GitHub Actions metadata syntax | https://docs.github.com/actions/automating-your-workflow-with-github-actions/metadata-syntax-for-github-actions | every Kolm action needs valid `action.yml`, inputs, outputs, `runs`, examples, README, versioning, and secret-safety docs |
| VS Code extension manifest | https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/extension-manifest | VS Code integration must have one canonical manifest, engines, activation events, contributions, commands, categories, Marketplace/Open VSX/local status, and package proof |
| MCP specification | https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/index | MCP tools/resources/prompts must be listed with user consent, host boundaries, auth, filesystem/network access, and prompt-injection/tool-poisoning mitigations |
| LlamaIndex docs | https://docs.llamaindex.ai/ | LlamaIndex integrations must declare Python/TypeScript package compatibility, examples, dependency behavior, and whether they are official, community, or local-source-only |
| LangChain integrations reference | https://reference.langchain.com/python/integrations/overview | LangChain adapters must map to upstream package/version expectations and not imply native upstream support without external evidence |
| Hugging Face GGUF with llama.cpp | https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/gguf-llamacpp | runtime loader claims for GGUF/llama.cpp/HF Hub must include model file format, repo/file loading behavior, cache behavior, and compatibility limitations |
| Docker Model Runner inference engines | https://docs.docker.com/ai/model-runner/inference-engines/ | local-runtime docs should treat llama.cpp/GGUF and related engines as specific target contracts with quantization, memory, and engine behavior documented |

##### `kolm-developer-ecosystem-contract-v2`

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-developer-ecosystem-contract-v2",
  "integration_id": "stable_id",
  "category": "sdk|package|framework_adapter|runtime_loader|ide|mcp|ci_action|cloud|automation|migration_guide|example",
  "owner_node": "17.x",
  "support_status": "ga|beta|preview|adapter_packet|local_source_only|external_pr|deprecated|archive",
  "public_claim_allowed": false,
  "package": {
    "manager": "npm|pypi|cargo|swiftpm|maven|cocoapods|vscode|homebrew|winget|none",
    "name": "required_if_package",
    "registry_url": "required_if_published",
    "source_path": "required",
    "version": "required",
    "published": false,
    "provenance": "required_if_published",
    "sbom": "required_if_published",
    "license": "required",
    "install_command": "required_or_local_source_label"
  },
  "compatibility": {
    "kolm_versions": "required",
    "upstream_versions": "required_if_adapter",
    "runtime_versions": "required",
    "os_arch": [],
    "node_python_rust_java_versions": "required_when_relevant"
  },
  "security": {
    "auth_model": "api_key|local_only|byo_provider_key|none|oauth",
    "secret_logging": "forbidden",
    "network_access": "declared",
    "filesystem_access": "declared",
    "prompt_or_capture_access": "declared",
    "mcp_tool_scope": "required_if_mcp",
    "fork_pr_secret_policy": "required_if_ci"
  },
  "developer_experience": {
    "quickstart": "required",
    "runnable_example": "required",
    "expected_output": "required",
    "failure_output": "required",
    "cleanup": "required",
    "last_verified": "required",
    "support_contact": "required"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "tests": [],
    "smoke_commands": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "registry_receipts": [],
    "external_prs": [],
    "docs_urls": [],
    "known_limitations": []
  }
}
```

##### Node 17 V2 Support Status Vocabulary

| status | public language allowed |
|---|---|
| `ga` | supported integration, installable from registry/channel, tested in CI, docs current, support path active |
| `beta` | supported preview with known limitations, registry or package artifact exists, smoke tests pass |
| `preview` | usable but not default; docs must include limitations, support caveats, and last verified date |
| `adapter_packet` | local adapter or spec packet exists; do not call native support or upstream adoption |
| `local_source_only` | source exists in repo; install docs must be local checkout commands, not registry install claims |
| `external_pr` | upstream PR/package is pending or linked; public claim must say pending/experimental |
| `deprecated` | kept for migration only; docs must route to replacement and define removal window |
| `archive` | historical material; excluded from live integration claims and SEO unless intentionally exposed |

##### Node 17 V2 Surface Rules

| surface | completion rule |
|---|---|
| package manifests | canonical repository URL, license, files/export map, supported runtimes, version, publish status, package manager, provenance/SBOM state, and support status |
| SDK docs | install path, import example, auth model, first call, error handling, version compatibility, local/mock mode, expected output, and package proof |
| framework adapters | capture/receipt/artifact/K-Score preservation, upstream version compatibility, runnable examples, and non-native support labels unless upstream adoption exists |
| MCP | exact tools/resources/prompts, filesystem/network/prompt access, host/client config, auth, consent boundaries, prompt-injection and tool-poisoning warnings, dry-run installer output |
| VS Code/IDE | one canonical extension or explicit split, activation policy, commands, settings, package/VSIX proof, marketplace/local status, screenshots, and uninstall/cleanup |
| runtime loaders | `.kolm` manifest conformance, model/weight policy, signature verification, target runtime versions, unsupported ops, error examples, external adoption links or adapter-packet labels |
| CI actions | action metadata validation, inputs/outputs, fork/secret policy, permissions, pinned dependencies, examples, artifact attestations, and self-test workflows |
| migration guides | source stack, before/after code, required env vars, mock mode, expected output, rollback, cleanup, and preserved Kolm proof features |
| public integration index | generated from support matrix, sorted by job-to-be-done, shows status, last verified, package proof, docs, limitations, and owner |
