| `W-ECO-V2-014` | `17.3` | Add runtime loader conformance fixtures. | llama.cpp, Ollama, HF Hub, vLLM, LM Studio, ONNX, GGUF, and Docker runtime paths validate `.kolm` manifest/signature/runtime limitations. |
| `W-ECO-V2-015` | `17.3` | Separate adapter packets from adoption. | Loader scaffolds without external PR/package/registry evidence are labeled `adapter_packet`, not native support. |
| `W-ECO-V2-016` | `17.3` | Add runtime error contract. | Unsupported ops, missing weights, invalid signature, incompatible target, memory limits, and fallback paths produce consistent examples and docs. |
| `W-ECO-V2-017` | `17.4` | Validate local GitHub actions. | Four local actions plus tool action metadata validate inputs/outputs/runs, README examples, permissions, fork behavior, and self-test workflows. |
| `W-ECO-V2-018` | `17.4` | Define one action identity. | Compile, verify, publish, test, distill, smoke, and benchmark actions use one namespace/versioning strategy or clear local-only labels. |
| `W-ECO-V2-019` | `17.4` | Add CI secret-safety policy. | All workflow templates are safe for forks, redact keys, use minimum permissions, and document OIDC/provenance expectations. |
| `W-ECO-V2-020` | `17.5` | Generate migration guide registry. | OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK, OpenRouter, LiteLLM, Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Ollama, LM Studio, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions, and MCP guides have runnable examples. |
| `W-ECO-V2-021` | `17.5` | Add example evidence standard. | Every example includes mock/fixture mode, env vars, expected output, failure output, cleanup, and last verified date. |
| `W-ECO-V2-022` | `17.5` | Consolidate integration docs IA. | `public/docs/integrations/*`, `public/integrations/*`, package READMEs, and archive pages are generated or validated from one matrix. |
| `W-ECO-V2-023` | `17.6` | Add ecosystem production smoke. | Shipping commit checks docs links, install commands, package metadata, MCP dry-run, action metadata, VS Code package, and runtime loader fixtures. |
| `W-ECO-V2-024` | `17.6` | Add ecosystem completion certificate. | Node `17` is complete only when support matrix, packages, SDKs, adapters, MCP, IDEs, CI, loaders, migrations, examples, security, docs, and production smoke agree. |

##### Node 17 V2 Relax Redline

Node `17` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every ecosystem surface has exactly one support status, owner, install path, compatibility range, security model, smoke command, and last-verified date;
2. public integration docs are generated or validated from a machine-readable support matrix;
3. package manifests use canonical Kolm repository URLs and do not leak legacy `kolmogorov-stack` identity except approved archive/compatibility records;
4. SDK/package channels distinguish published, provenance-backed, local-source-only, adapter-packet, external-PR, preview, and deprecated status;
5. `sdk/` local dependency/artifact bloat is either cleaned, ignored, archived, or proven intentional before release;
6. every install command works in a clean environment or is labeled local-source-only with exact checkout/build instructions;
7. every adapter preserves Kolm-specific value: capture receipts, artifact hashes, K-Score evidence, runtime verification, and claim scope;
8. MCP and IDE integrations document tool permissions, filesystem/network access, prompt/capture access, auth, and prompt-injection/tool-poisoning mitigations;
9. runtime loaders prove target compatibility with conformance fixtures and do not imply external/native adoption without external evidence;
10. CI actions and templates are safe for forks and secrets, validate metadata, and publish/verify artifacts with provenance where applicable;
11. examples include expected output, failure output, cleanup, mock mode, and current screenshots/logs where relevant;
12. production adoption smoke proves docs, package metadata, install paths, actions, MCP, VS Code, runtime loaders, and migration guides on the final shipping commit.

If any item fails, Kolm may have many integration files, but it is not developer-ecosystem complete.

#### Node 17 V3 Adoption Proof, Package Source Cleanliness, And Integration Support Contract

This addendum keeps work attached to master spec tree node `17`. It does not edit packages, SDKs, actions, MCP, docs, or generated artifacts. It upgrades the ecosystem branch from "we have many integration surfaces" to "every integration has an honest support status, install proof, source hygiene, compatibility range, security boundary, and adoption evidence."

Node `17` must avoid two opposite failures:

1. underselling real breadth: the tree has LangChain, LlamaIndex, MCP, VS Code, GitHub Actions, runtime loaders, Python/JS/native/mobile SDKs, cloud guides, and migration docs;
2. overclaiming adoption: source scaffolds, local package manifests, or adapter packets are not the same as published packages, upstream integrations, Marketplace listings, or native support.

##### Node 17 V3 Fresh Evidence Delta

| evidence path | current signal | implication |
|---|---|---|
| ecosystem keyword review | 604 matched files across `sdk`, `packages`, `public/docs`, `docs`, `examples`, `.github/actions`, and `.github/workflows` | ecosystem vocabulary is broad enough to require machine-readable ownership instead of hand-maintained docs |
| top matched roots | `public/docs` 307 files, `docs/research` 58, `.github/workflows` 11, `packages/runtime-rs` 16, `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` 9, `packages/attestation` 9, `sdk/vscode` 8, `sdk/python` 10 | developer adoption is spread across docs, packages, SDKs, workflows, and research; one support matrix must bind them |
| package/source tree file types | `sdk`, `packages`, `examples`, actions, and workflows include 5,264 files; notable build-output shaped extensions include 727 `.timestamp`, 667 `.d`, 570 `.rmeta`, 323 `.rlib`, 136 `.o`, 60 `.exe`, 30 `.bin`, 14 `.so`, 6 `.dll`, and 1,654 files with no extension | node `14` owns quarantine, but node `17` cannot call packages clean until package roots separate source from build output and local dependency artifacts |
| JS framework packages | `packages/langchain-kolm/package.json` and `packages/llamaindex-kolm/package.json` are present, `0.2.6`, ESM, Node `>=18`, optional peer dependencies, `files` arrays, homepage `/integrations`, repo `https://github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` | package shape is usable, but registry status, provenance, repo brand, upstream version testing, and examples need proof |
| Python framework packages | `packages/python-langchain-kolm/pyproject.toml` and `packages/python-llamaindex-kolm/pyproject.toml` are present, Python `>=3.10`, optional deps for LangChain/LlamaIndex and HTTP, repo `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` | packaging metadata exists, but wheel/sdist, PyPI/trusted-publishing status, import smoke, and docs parity are still required |
| MCP package | `sdk/mcp/package.json` points `main` and `bin` to `server.mjs`; `server.mjs` exists; `server.js` is absent; `sdk/mcp/node_modules` is present | docs/tests must use `server.mjs`, and package roots need local dependency cleanup before release |
| VS Code packages | `sdk/vscode/package.json` version `0.3.0` and `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json` version `0.1.0` both define extension manifests, commands, activation, and repo paths | there are two plausible VS Code products; final state needs one canonical extension or a declared split with separate support levels |
| GitHub Actions | `.github/actions/kolm-compile/action.yml` and `.github/actions/kolm-publish/action.yml` are composite actions; both install via `npm i -g github:sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack`; compile uses `KOLM_KEY`; publish includes a mojibake marker in the default visibility description | actions are useful but not release-clean: install source, secret naming, brand/repo URL, action metadata, shell dependencies, and encoded text need proof |
| integration docs entrypoint | `public/docs/integrations.html` is missing; `public/docs/sdk.html` and `public/docs/migrate.html` exist; integration pages are split elsewhere | final docs need one canonical integrations index generated from the support matrix |
| repo identity | sampled package manifests still use `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` | node `0` owns rename strategy, but node `17` owns package install-link and repository URL truth |

##### Node 17 V3 External Baselines

Primary-source baselines reviewed:

| source | baseline | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| LangChain integrations docs, `https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/providers/overview` | provider integrations are package-backed, component-specific, and expose standard interfaces across chat models, embeddings, tools, retrievers, loaders, and vector stores | Kolm LangChain support must identify component type, upstream version range, package name, install command, examples, and whether it is upstream/native or first-party local package |
| LlamaIndex integrations docs, `https://developers.llamaindex.ai/python/framework/community/integrations/` | ecosystem integrations span loaders, agent tools, LLMs, observability/tracing/evaluation, structured output, storage, apps, and distributed compute | Kolm LlamaIndex support must state which LlamaIndex extension point it implements and must include runnable RAG/agent/eval examples |
| Model Context Protocol spec, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/index` | MCP uses JSON-RPC with hosts, clients, and servers; server features are resources, prompts, and tools; security principles require user consent, data privacy, tool safety, and LLM sampling controls | Kolm MCP must list each tool/resource/prompt, what data it can access, auth required, network/filesystem permissions, prompt/capture visibility, and consent boundaries |
| VS Code extension manifest docs, `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/extension-manifest` | a VS Code extension needs a root `package.json`; marketplace presentation depends on README, icon/banner, repository, license, categories, commands, and cleanup hooks where needed | Kolm VS Code surfaces need canonical manifest, command namespace, activation discipline, build/package proof, local/Marketplace status, README/icon, and uninstall/cleanup behavior |
| GitHub Actions metadata syntax, `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/metadata-syntax` | actions require `runs`; JavaScript/composite/Docker actions define runtime, inputs, outputs, main/pre/post behavior, and shell steps | Kolm actions need metadata validation, one published identity or local-only label, minimum permissions, outputs, shell dependency declarations, and examples |
| npm package manifest docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json/` | package quality depends on fields such as name, version, files, bin, scripts, repository, license, engines, and dependency declarations | npm packages need repository cleanup, files allowlist, engines, exports, type declarations, provenance state, install smoke, and publish/dry-run evidence |
| Python `pyproject.toml` spec, `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/` | Python package metadata, dependencies, optional dependencies, URLs, build system, and classifiers are standardized through `pyproject.toml` | Python packages need wheel/sdist smoke, classifiers, URLs, dependency extras, import tests, and explicit PyPI or local-source-only status |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | GenAI systems should report operation, provider/system, request/response, token usage, and model information through standard attributes and events | adapters and SDKs should propagate trace/span context and not invent incompatible integration telemetry |

##### Node 17 V3 Ecosystem Support Matrix Contract

Create or generate `docs/developer-ecosystem-contract.json` and `public/integrations-support-matrix.json` with this minimum record:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.developer_ecosystem_contract.v3",
  "integration_id": "langchain-js",
  "category": "framework_adapter",
  "source_paths": ["packages/langchain-kolm", "public/docs/integrations/langchain-js.html"],
  "canonical_package": {
    "manager": "npm",
    "name": "@kolm/langchain",
    "version": "0.2.6",
    "registry_url": null,
    "status": "local_source_only",
    "repository_url": "must_be_canonical_kolm_repo",
    "provenance": "missing_until_published"
  },
  "support": {
    "level": "preview",
    "owner": "developer-ecosystem",
    "last_verified": "required_iso_date",
    "upstream_version_range": "required",
    "kolm_version_range": "required",
    "known_limitations": []
  },
  "security": {
    "auth_model": "KOLM_API_KEY_or_local",
    "network_access": "declared",
    "filesystem_access": "declared",
    "captures_prompts": "declared",
    "secret_redaction": "required"
  },
  "proof": {
    "install_smoke": "required",
    "example_command": "required",
    "expected_output": "required",
    "negative_case": "required",
    "docs_url": "required",
    "screenshots": [],
    "external_adoption": []
  },
  "claim_policy": {
    "can_say_supported": false,
    "can_say_preview": true,
    "can_say_native_upstream": false
  }
}
```

##### Node 17 V3 Support Status Vocabulary

| status | public language allowed | required proof |
|---|---|---|
| `published_ga` | "supported package" | registry URL, version, provenance/SBOM, install smoke, examples, compatibility tests |
| `published_preview` | "preview package" | registry URL, version, install smoke, limitations, support contact |
| `local_source_only` | "available from source" | local install command, tests, docs, no registry claim |
| `adapter_packet` | "adapter packet available" | conformance fixtures and implementation guide, no native-support claim |
| `external_pr_open` | "external integration proposed" | upstream PR URL, status, fixture output |
| `external_merged` | "native support" | merged upstream link or official package listing |
| `deprecated` | "deprecated" | replacement path and removal window |
| `archive` | "archived example" | no current support claim |

##### Node 17 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `17` remains red until:

1. every developer-facing integration has a support status that maps to public language;
2. package roots are source-clean or generated artifacts are owned and ignored;
3. LangChain, LlamaIndex, MCP, VS Code, runtime loaders, and GitHub Actions each have runnable examples with expected outputs;
4. repo/brand/install URLs are canonical or deliberately marked legacy;
5. MCP tools and IDE extensions disclose data access, network access, filesystem access, and auth requirements;
6. GitHub Actions are safe for secrets and forks;
7. no public page claims native or published support without registry, upstream, or external adoption proof;
8. one generated support matrix drives docs, product graph, account, and release checks.

### Data, Datasets, Labels, Privacy Lake, And Rights Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `18`: the data substrate. Kolm's product promise depends on capturing behavior, turning approved rows into datasets, preserving holdouts, proving redaction and privacy boundaries, generating synthetic gaps honestly, and binding all of it into artifacts without leaking raw customer data. If the data lifecycle is not precise, every downstream claim about distillation, K-Score, auditability, privacy, compliance, and any-device deployment becomes fragile.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not modify capture storage, privacy code, dataset code, benchmark fixtures, account pages, or public docs while other agents are editing.

#### Current Data Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| capture and lake analytics | `src/lake.js`, `src/capture-analytics.js`, `public/account/captured.html`, `public/account/captures.html`, `public/account/captures/analytics.html`, docs for capture and lake | tenant-fenced event aggregation, repeated prompt clustering, sensitivity counts, provider/model/cost/latency summaries, and account dashboards exist | needs row lifecycle state, consent basis, retention, discard binding, deletion propagation, and train/eval eligibility as first-class row fields |
| privacy membrane | `src/privacy-membrane.js`, `src/phi-redactor.js`, `public/account/privacy-events.html`, `public/docs/privacy.html`, `public/docs/copyright-scan.html` | deterministic JS redaction/policy engine, PHI/PII redaction, policy classes, vault concept, privacy-event account page | needs redaction-before-persist policy, raw sidecar boundary, vault retention/deletion, utility-vs-privacy scoring, and per-class benchmark drift |
| datasets and labels | `public/account/datasets.html`, `public/account/labeling.html`, docs for datasets/labels, dataset routes in API inventory | accepted labels can become datasets with train/holdout split and disjointness UI concepts | needs dataset cards, labeler provenance, acceptance policy, conflict resolution, quality scores, split reproducibility, holdout contamination proof, and account operator remediation |
| holdouts and benchmarks | `holdouts/catalog.json`, `holdouts/external/*.jsonl`, `holdouts/adversarial/*.jsonl`, `public/benchmarks/*.json`, `docs/benchmark-evidence.md`, `docs/benchmark-results-v0.1.0.md` | holdout catalog, synthetic public benchmark fixtures, adversarial cross-family fixtures, and benchmark evidence contract exist | needs a generated benchmark corpus registry, raw-data policy, public/private split labels, freshness date, license validation, and claim mapping |
| synthetic data | `public/account/synthetic.html`, `public/docs/synthetic.html`, `src/synthetic-augment.js`, `docs/product-frontier-lab.json` | account UX and research concepts for coverage, Gini, rare buckets, synthetic gap fill, and `kolm_synthetic:true` tagging | needs row-level synthetic provenance, teacher/source model info, contamination guard, utility delta, and strict "synthetic-only is not production-ready" enforcement |
| distill strategy gates | `docs/distill-strategy.md`, strategy scripts/tests referenced in package scripts | real pairs, holdout pairs, privacy, teacher availability, and synthetic-only rejection are explicit planning gates | needs account/API/CLI parity so every train/distill path exposes the same data blockers and next actions |
| trace import policy | `docs/research/trace-import-privacy-policy-2026-05-13.md` | mature policy exists for hash-only/redacted/raw/blocked imports, manifests, purge scope, and artifact boundary | current policy itself states gaps: no namespace retention, no redaction-before-persist, no observation purge route, no raw-payload sidecar separation, no deletion certificate, and capture triage not binding |
| artifact data boundary | `docs/kolm-format-v1.md`, example `.kolm` artifacts, registry-pack assets | artifacts can include eval rows, train/eval split provenance, receipts, hashes, and offline verification rules | needs hard rules for when rows are embedded vs hashed, how DSR/deletion interacts with already distributed artifacts, and how private provenance stays outside portable artifacts |

#### Current Data Lifecycle Redlines

1. A captured row is not just a prompt/response. It needs lifecycle fields: source, consent basis, privacy state, rights state, retention policy, purge deadline, train eligibility, eval eligibility, label state, review state, poisoning/copyright risk, and deletion lineage.
2. Trace import policy is stricter than current capture. The policy explicitly says local capture can store prompt/response text and lacks namespace retention, redaction-before-persist, purge routes, raw-payload sidecars, deletion certificates, and binding triage. That gap must become a P0 implementation queue.
3. Discarded observations must be binding. If a reviewer discards or quarantines a row, label export, active learning, synthetic augmentation, distillation, eval generation, and benchmark export must not use it unless an authorized override is recorded.
4. Holdout independence must be mathematically proven, not a UI pill alone. Train/eval splits need deterministic seed, source row hashes, duplicate/semantic-near-duplicate exclusion, and contamination checks against synthetic/generated rows.
5. Synthetic data must never quietly graduate to real-data evidence. Every synthetic row needs `kolm_synthetic:true`, generation source, model/provider, prompt template, seed, policy, and claim-scope restrictions.
6. Redaction quality and utility are separate. A row can be privacy-safe and useless for training; a row can be high-utility and too risky to persist. Completion requires both scores and a policy decision.
7. Privacy vaults are sensitive storage. Vault retention, access audit, encryption/key boundary, purge, export exclusion, and incident behavior must be explicit before enterprise privacy claims.
8. Public benchmark fixtures and private customer holdouts need different rules. Public fixtures need license/source/provenance and reproducibility. Private holdouts need tenant isolation, export limits, and deletion handling.
9. Artifacts must not become unbounded data leak containers. The artifact boundary in `docs/kolm-format-v1.md` should allow approved eval rows or hashes but keep source IDs, raw payload refs, deletion tokens, user IDs, session IDs, and importer manifests out of portable artifacts.
10. Data residency and storage plane choices must attach to rows, not only tenants. A tenant-level setting is not enough when imports, BYOC, local-only, object storage, and cloud training paths can differ.
11. Copyright and ToS rights must be row-level and dataset-level. Rows sourced from provider outputs, user prompts, web corpora, synthetic teachers, public datasets, or customer documents carry different distillation rights.
12. Account pages for datasets, labeling, lake, privacy events, and synthetic data currently exist, but final UX needs one operator story: what data exists, what can train, what cannot train, why, what risk remains, and the next action.

#### External Standards Bound To Node 18

| standard source | relevant standard | Kolm completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| NIST AI RMF: `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | NIST frames AI risk management across governance, mapping, measuring, and managing risks throughout the AI lifecycle | dataset, label, benchmark, synthetic, and artifact evidence must expose risk mapping, measured evidence, and management decisions rather than hidden internal state |
| NIST Privacy Framework: `https://www.nist.gov/privacy-framework` | the framework is a voluntary tool for identifying and managing privacy risk while building products and services | row lifecycle, privacy state, retention, deletion, redaction, vault, and data-sharing controls need explicit privacy risk records |
| W3C PROV: `https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-overview/` | PROV defines interoperable provenance concepts for entities, activities, agents, derivations, and responsibility | capture rows, labels, dataset transformations, synthetic generations, benchmark runs, and artifact builds should map to entity/activity/agent lineage |
| OpenLineage: `https://openlineage.io/docs/` | OpenLineage defines dataset, job, and run entities for interoperable lineage metadata | Kolm should emit lineage for capture import, filtering, dedupe, split, labeling, redaction, synthetic augmentation, distill, eval, and artifact publication |
| W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary DCAT: `https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/` | DCAT describes datasets, distributions, catalogs, provenance, access, and metadata for data discovery | public benchmark corpora, holdout catalogs, data cards, and registry-pack datasets need machine-readable catalog metadata |
| HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification guidance: `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/special-topics/de-identification/index.html` | Safe Harbor requires removal of specified identifiers and no actual knowledge that remaining data identifies an individual | PHI redaction copy must distinguish deterministic identifier replacement from full de-identification/expert determination |

#### Canonical Row And Dataset Contract

Completion requires one row schema shared across capture, trace import, labels, datasets, evals, synthetic generation, and artifact provenance. A minimal row should be shaped like this:

```json
{
  "row_id": "row_...",
  "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
  "namespace": "support-prod",
  "source": {
    "kind": "capture | trace-import | manual-label | synthetic | public-fixture",
    "system": "openai-compatible | langsmith | langfuse | account-ui | benchmark",
    "source_ref": "hash-or-external-id",
    "source_checksum": "sha256:..."
  },
  "privacy": {
    "state": "hash-only | redacted | raw | blocked",
    "consent_basis": "customer-import | explicit-user-consent | synthetic | public-domain | unknown",
    "retention_days": 30,
    "purge_after": "2026-06-24T00:00:00.000Z",
    "data_residency": "us | eu | customer-hosted | local-only | unknown",
    "redaction_report_id": "red_...",
    "vault_ref": null,
    "pii_classes": ["email", "phone"]
  },
  "rights": {
    "license": "customer-owned | CC0-1.0 | internal-use-only | unknown",
    "tos_training_allowed": true,
    "copyright_risk": "low | review | block",
    "reuse_scope": "train | eval | benchmark | audit-only | blocked"
  },
  "quality": {
    "label_state": "unlabeled | accepted | corrected | rejected | conflict",
    "row_quality": 0.87,
    "novelty": 0.42,
    "poisoning_risk": 0.02,
    "utility_score": 0.74
  },
  "split": {
    "eligible_train": true,
    "eligible_eval": false,
    "dataset_id": "ds_...",
    "split_name": "train",
    "split_seed": "seed_...",
    "holdout_contamination": "clear"
  },
  "lineage": {
    "derived_from": ["row_parent_..."],
    "activity_ids": ["act_redact_...", "act_label_..."],
    "artifact_refs": []
  }
}
```

Rules:

- Raw sensitive fields must be sidecars or vault references unless an explicit raw policy allows persistence.
- Every transformation creates a lineage activity with inputs, outputs, agent, parameters, code version, and timestamp.
- Every dataset has a dataset card: purpose, source mix, row counts, privacy mix, rights mix, label quality, split seed, holdout policy, known gaps, and allowed uses.
- Every benchmark corpus has a catalog entry with license, source, checksum, accessed date, data class, raw-data policy, and claim scope.
- Every train or distill plan consumes only eligible rows and emits excluded-row reasons.
- Every artifact records only approved row data or row hashes and must not embed raw source metadata or deletion tokens.

#### Node 18 Completion Specification

| node | name | done state | proof required |
|---|---|---|---|
| 18.1 | capture row lifecycle | every row has consent, privacy, rights, retention, eligibility, deletion, and lineage state | schema tests, migration proof, account row detail, purge smoke, trace import parity |
| 18.2 | datasets and labels | labels and datasets have quality, reviewer, split, disjointness, duplicate/semantic-dedupe, and contamination evidence | dataset card generator, split fixture, holdout overlap test, label conflict test, account UX screenshots |
| 18.3 | privacy membrane and vaults | redaction, policy, vault, raw sidecars, per-class benchmarks, and utility tradeoff are visible and enforceable | redaction benchmark, vault access audit, retention/purge tests, privacy utility report |
| 18.4 | benchmark and synthetic corpora | public/private corpora have catalog metadata, checksums, licenses, freshness, source, raw-data policy, and claim scope | corpus registry, checksum verifier, benchmark evidence validator, synthetic-only claim blocker |
| 18.5 | provenance and rights | lineage covers capture, import, redaction, labeling, split, synthetic generation, distill, eval, and artifact build | W3C PROV/OpenLineage mapping, lineage export, rights scanner, artifact boundary test |
| 18.6 | account/operator UX | operators can see what data can train, what is blocked, why, and what next action fixes it | account pages for lake/datasets/labeling/privacy/synthetic with loading/empty/error states and screenshot proof |

#### Data Completion Tickets To Generate

- W-DATA-001: create a canonical row schema and migrate capture, trace import, labels, datasets, evals, synthetic rows, and benchmark rows to it.
- W-DATA-002: add row lifecycle states: captured, redacted, reviewed, discarded, quarantined, accepted, labeled, split, trained, evaluated, forgotten, purged.
- W-DATA-003: implement namespace retention policies with scheduled purge and deletion certificate output.

- W-DATA-007: add dataset card generator with source mix, privacy mix, rights mix, label quality, split seed, contamination report, and intended use.
- W-DATA-008: add deterministic split engine with exact hash-based train/holdout disjointness and semantic-near-duplicate exclusion.
- W-DATA-009: add label quality ledger: reviewer, correction, conflict, adjudication, confidence, and downstream use.
- W-DATA-010: add privacy utility scoring that reports redaction recall, row utility delta, excluded fields, and policy blockers.
- W-DATA-011: make vault access auditable and add vault retention/purge tests.
- W-DATA-012: create benchmark corpus registry from `holdouts/catalog.json`, `public/benchmarks`, reports, and docs, with checksums and license validation.

- W-DATA-014: add synthetic-only claim blocker to distill, K-Score, benchmark, product graph, public copy, and account readiness.
- W-DATA-015: implement row-level rights/copyright scanner with `train`, `eval`, `benchmark`, `audit-only`, and `blocked` scopes.
- W-DATA-016: emit W3C PROV/OpenLineage-compatible records for import, redaction, dataset split, label adjudication, synthetic generation, distill, eval, and artifact publish.
- W-DATA-017: add artifact boundary tests proving raw source IDs, user IDs, session IDs, raw payload refs, delete tokens, and importer manifests are not embedded.
- W-DATA-018: add account data command center tying lake, privacy events, labeling, datasets, synthetic, and distill readiness into one "can train / cannot train / why" surface.
- W-DATA-019: create production data smoke: capture row, redact, label, split, verify holdout, run distill dry-run, purge row, confirm excluded from future artifacts.
- W-DATA-020: add data evidence archive with row schema version, dataset cards, lineage export, benchmark corpus checksums, purge certificates, screenshots, and claim-scope report.

#### Data Redline To Relax

Node 18 is not complete until all of these are true:

- every row has a lifecycle, privacy, rights, retention, eligibility, and lineage record;
- raw sensitive data never persists or travels into artifacts unless an explicit policy and audit trail allows it;
- discard/quarantine/forget/purge decisions propagate into every derived dataset, label export, synthetic plan, distill run, eval, and artifact;
- train/holdout separation is deterministic, reproducible, and contamination-checked;
- synthetic data is always labeled and never treated as production proof without real-data or benchmark evidence;
- benchmark corpora are cataloged with checksums, license, source, freshness, and claim scope;
- account UX makes data readiness operational, not hidden in logs or scattered pages;
- deletion certificates, lineage exports, and dataset cards exist for production smoke on the shipping commit.

#### Node 18 V2 Data Lifecycle, Rights, And Lineage Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `18`. It does not edit capture storage, privacy code, dataset code, benchmark fixtures, account pages, or public docs while other agents are active. It turns data completion into row-level contracts: every capture, label, dataset row, holdout, synthetic row, benchmark fixture, and artifact input must have lifecycle, privacy, rights, retention, lineage, and eligibility state.

The current data surface is broad. The key risk is not that Kolm lacks data features. The key risk is that capture, privacy, labels, holdouts, synthetic augmentation, benchmarks, distillation, and artifacts can each develop their own row meanings. State-of-the-art data infrastructure needs one row vocabulary and one lineage chain from capture to artifact, including deletion and rights propagation.

##### Node 18 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | current official source | Kolm data rule |
|---|---|---|
| NIST Privacy Framework | https://www.nist.gov/privacy-framework | data operations must manage privacy risk across collection, processing, retention, sharing, and disposal |
| W3C PROV | https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-dm/ | capture, redaction, labeling, split, synthetic generation, distill, eval, and artifact publish should map to entity/activity/agent lineage |
| W3C DCAT 3 | https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/ | public benchmark corpora, holdout catalogs, dataset cards, and data services should have catalog metadata |
| OpenLineage | https://github.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage | data transformations should emit run/job/dataset metadata and facets for interoperable lineage |
| HIPAA de-identification guidance | https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/special-topics/de-identification/index.html | PHI copy must distinguish deterministic redaction from de-identification, Safe Harbor, and expert determination |

##### `kolm-data-lifecycle-contract-v2`

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-data-lifecycle-contract-v2",
  "row": {
    "row_id": "required",
    "tenant_id": "required",
    "namespace": "required",
    "source": "capture|trace_import|label|synthetic|benchmark|holdout|manual|external_dataset",
    "created_at": "iso8601",
    "content_hash": "required",
    "raw_payload_ref": "forbidden_or_vault_ref",
    "current_state": "captured|redacted|reviewed|accepted|discarded|quarantined|labeled|split|trained|evaluated|forgotten|purged",
    "states": []
  },
  "privacy": {
    "classification": "none|pii|phi|secret|customer_sensitive|unknown",
    "redaction_state": "not_needed|pending|redacted|blocked|failed",
    "vault_ref": "optional",
    "retention_policy": "required",
    "delete_after": "optional",
    "deletion_certificate": "required_if_purged"
  },
  "rights": {
    "source_terms": "required",
    "copyright_risk": "low|review|block",
    "tos_risk": "low|review|block",
    "reuse_scope": "train|eval|benchmark|audit_only|blocked",
    "license": "required_if_external_or_public"
  },
  "quality": {
    "label_state": "unlabeled|accepted|corrected|rejected|conflict",
    "reviewer": "optional",
    "row_quality": "optional",
    "utility_score": "optional",
    "poisoning_risk": "optional",
    "novelty": "optional"
  },
  "split": {
    "eligible_train": false,
    "eligible_eval": false,
    "eligible_benchmark": false,
    "dataset_id": "optional",
    "split_name": "none|train|holdout|eval|benchmark",
    "split_seed": "required_if_split",
    "contamination_check": "required_if_split"
  },
  "lineage": {
    "prov_entities": [],
    "prov_activities": [],
    "prov_agents": [],
    "openlineage_run_id": "optional",
    "derived_from": [],
    "derived_outputs": []
  },
  "artifact_boundary": {
    "portable_payload_allowed": false,
    "hash_only_allowed": true,
    "embedded_eval_allowed": "policy_required",
    "delete_token_embedded": false,
    "raw_source_metadata_embedded": false
  }
}
```

##### Node 18 V2 Row State Propagation Rules

| state/action | propagation requirement |
|---|---|
| discard | row cannot enter labels, datasets, synthetic augmentation, distill, eval, benchmark export, or active learning without recorded override |
| quarantine | row is isolated from training/eval/export and appears in account remediation with risk reason |
| redact | downstream rows reference redaction activity, redaction version, class counts, and utility delta |
| accept label | row can enter eligible dataset only if privacy, rights, and holdout contamination gates pass |
| split | train/holdout/eval assignment is deterministic by seed/hash and cannot be changed without new dataset version |
| synthetic generate | synthetic row records teacher/model/provider, prompt template, seed, source row hashes, objective, and `kolm_synthetic:true` |
| purge | row and all derived indexes/caches/exports are marked purged or blocked, and deletion certificate is emitted |
| artifact publish | artifact embeds only approved payloads or hashes and records dataset/artifact lineage without raw source metadata |

##### Node 18 V2 Account Data Command Center

The post-auth data UX should converge from scattered pages into an operator command center:

1. live row counts by state, privacy class, rights scope, and training eligibility;
2. blocked rows grouped by reason: privacy, rights, consent, retention, poisoning, copyright, label conflict, holdout contamination, missing review;
3. next action for each blocker with owner, CLI/API equivalent, and estimated effect on data readiness;
4. dataset cards with source mix, privacy mix, rights mix, label quality, split seed, holdout contamination, synthetic ratio, and intended use;
5. privacy/redaction dashboard with per-class precision/recall/F1, drift, utility loss, and false-positive/false-negative review;
6. deletion/purge dashboard with pending deletions, completed certificates, derived-output propagation, and artifact boundary explanation;
7. synthetic data panel with generation source, gap objective, risk, allowed uses, and public-proof restriction;
8. benchmark corpus panel with license/source/checksum/freshness/claim-scope status;
9. one "can train" readiness summary for each namespace and distillation target;
10. production smoke evidence for capture, redact, label, split, dry-run distill, purge, and excluded-row proof.

##### Node 18 V2 Relax Redline

Node `18` is not complete until all of the following are true:

1. every capture, label, dataset, holdout, synthetic, benchmark, and artifact-input row uses one lifecycle schema;
2. privacy, rights, retention, consent, deletion, lineage, and train/eval/benchmark eligibility are first-class row fields;
3. discard, quarantine, forget, and purge decisions propagate to every derived dataset, label export, synthetic plan, distill run, eval, benchmark export, cache, and artifact;
4. redaction-before-persist and raw sidecar/vault boundaries are configurable, audited, and visible in account UX;
5. train/holdout/eval separation is deterministic, reproducible, and contamination-checked;
6. synthetic rows are always labeled, source-bound, and blocked from public proof unless validated by real/benchmark evidence;
7. benchmark corpora and holdouts have catalog metadata, checksums, licenses, source, freshness, and claim scope;
8. W3C PROV and OpenLineage-compatible lineage can reconstruct capture-to-artifact transformations;
9. account operators can see exactly what data can train, what cannot, why, and what next action fixes it;
10. production data smoke proves capture, redact, label, split, dry-run distill, purge, and excluded-row behavior on the shipping commit.

If any item fails, Kolm can still have useful data features, but the data substrate is not complete enough to support state-of-the-art distillation, privacy, audit, and any-device claims.

#### Node 18 V3 Data Command Center, Row Eligibility, Purge Proof, And Corpus Registry

This addendum maintains node `18` in the master spec tree. It does not create node `52`, does not edit runtime code, and does not overwrite parallel-agent work. The purpose is to convert the current data substrate into an implementation-ready operating contract for finishing the build: every data object that can influence distillation, evaluation, K-Score, routing, redaction, artifacts, or customer proof must have one eligibility decision, one lineage chain, one rights state, one retention state, and one operator-visible next action.

The correct product shape is not "we have a privacy lake" or "we have datasets." The state-of-the-art shape is: Kolm can tell an operator, a compiler, a distiller, an auditor, and a device-runtime packager exactly which rows may train, which rows may evaluate, which rows may benchmark, which rows are audit-only, which rows must be purged, which artifacts must be rebuilt, and which claim each corpus is allowed to support.

##### Node 18 V3 Fresh Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state on 2026-05-25 | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| core capture and data files reviewed | `src/capture-store.js` 15,252 bytes / 338 lines; `src/capture-forget.js` 10,728 / 319; `src/capture-copyright-filter.js` 7,931 / 192; `src/capture-anomaly.js` 14,099 / 342; `src/lake.js` 9,396 / 274; `src/capture-analytics.js` 28,187 / 733. | Capture, lake, forget, copyright, anomaly, and analytics behavior exists, but final proof needs one row contract instead of per-module assumptions. |
| privacy and redaction files reviewed | `src/privacy-membrane.js` 39,106 bytes / 1,115 lines; `src/phi-redactor.js` 30,320 / 740; `src/redaction-benchmark.js` 6,258 / 174. | Redaction and policy code is real. The final product must expose redaction state, vault state, benchmark scope, utility loss, and de-identification claim scope as data decisions. |
| dataset and synthetic files reviewed | `src/dataset-workbench.js` 35,401 bytes / 786 lines; `src/training-planner.js` 10,698 / 244; `src/synthetic-augment.js` 20,428 / 506; `src/tenant-holdout.js` 11,982 / 281. | Deterministic split, dedupe, synthetic, and holdout ideas exist. They must become a generated dataset card and train/eval eligibility gate before distillation claims are final. |
| benchmark and lineage files reviewed | `src/benchmark-evidence.js` 17,696 bytes / 431 lines; `src/artifact-lineage.js` 24,366 / 557; `src/distill-provenance.js` 8,033 / 176; `src/data-residency.js` 19,687 / 493; `src/event-store.js` 17,927 / 433. | Evidence, lineage, provenance, residency, and event storage exist. They need one graph that can reconstruct capture-to-artifact state and deletion impacts. |
| account data pages reviewed | `public/account/datasets.html` 19,283 bytes / 280 lines; `public/account/labeling.html` 21,510 / 309; `public/account/privacy-events.html` 19,960 / 290; `public/account/synthetic.html` 30,338 / 559; `public/account/captured.html` 21,708 / 326; `public/account/captures/analytics.html` 23,487 / 330. | Account pages exist, but node `18` still needs one command-center view that explains can-train/cannot-train state, blockers, and next actions across all data pages. |

Every retained artifact must be classified as fixture, benchmark, cache, archive, generated output, external source, or release-excluded data. |
| broad data keyword review | 1,444 matched files across code, docs, public assets, scripts, tests, examples, and packages. | Data semantics are codebase-wide. This cannot be fixed by one helper; it needs a generated contract and verifier tied to node `18`. |
| contract files missing | `docs/data-lifecycle-contract.json`, `docs/capture-row-schema.json`, `docs/dataset-lineage-schema.json`, `docs/rights-policy.json`, `docs/holdout-registry.json`, `docs/synthetic-data-policy.json`, `public/data-catalog.json`, and `public/benchmark-corpus-registry.json` are absent. | The codebase has implementation pieces but lacks the durable public/internal contracts that would let other agents finish without inventing incompatible schemas. |
| forget behavior reviewed | `src/capture-forget.js` records forget markers, explicitly does not mutate or delete the original capture row, and states that re-distill is required to actually purge already trained artifacts. | This is honest and useful, but not final. The product needs hard purge, derived-output invalidation, artifact rebuild queues, and deletion certificates. |
| capture-store behavior reviewed | `src/capture-store.js` treats capture-store rows as post-redaction, stamps sensitivity/redaction/provenance data, filters discarded rows unless requested, and best-effort marks copyright/quarantine flags. | Final eligibility must not depend on "post-redaction" convention. The row must carry machine-enforced privacy, rights, retention, and use-scope state. |
| dataset-workbench behavior reviewed | `src/dataset-workbench.js` performs deterministic hash-based train/holdout split, content dedupe before split, tenant-scoped dataset creation, `holdout_only` handling, and accepted-label filtering. | The split foundation is strong. It still needs exported dataset cards, semantic duplicate checks, lineage, holdout contamination proof, and account/API/CLI parity. |

##### Node 18 V3 External Baselines

| primary source | imported rule for Kolm |
|---|---|
| NIST AI RMF, `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | Data operations must support AI lifecycle risk governance, mapping, measurement, and management. Kolm should expose dataset risk, synthetic risk, benchmark risk, and artifact-data risk as operational state. |
| NIST Privacy Framework, `https://www.nist.gov/privacy-framework` | Privacy risk must be identified and managed while building products and services. Kolm rows need privacy state, retention, sharing, deletion, and risk decisions, not only redaction output. |
| W3C PROV-DM, `https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-dm/` | Provenance must model entities, activities, agents, usage, generation, invalidation, and responsibility. Kolm capture rows, labels, redactions, splits, synthetic generations, distill runs, evals, and artifacts must map to those concepts. |
| W3C DCAT 3, `https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-dcat-3/` | Data catalogs distinguish datasets, distributions, access URLs, download URLs, media type, and catalog metadata. Kolm benchmark and holdout corpora need a machine-readable catalog, not only docs prose. |
| OpenLineage spec, `https://openlineage.io/docs/spec/` | Data lineage should use run, job, dataset, input, output, and facet entities. Kolm should emit OpenLineage-compatible facets for capture import, redaction, split, synthetic augmentation, distill, eval, and artifact publication. |
| HHS HIPAA de-identification guidance, `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/special-topics/de-identification/index.html` | HIPAA de-identification has Expert Determination and Safe Harbor methods, and de-identified data can still carry residual re-identification risk. Kolm must not equate deterministic redaction with full de-identification unless a method and evidence are attached. |
| EUR-Lex GDPR Regulation 2016/679, Article 17, `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/2016-05-04` | Erasure rights and exceptions must be represented as processing state. A forget marker is not enough unless downstream disclosures, derived datasets, caches, and artifacts are accounted for. |
| Datasheets for Datasets, `https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010` | Dataset transparency requires documented motivation, composition, collection, processing, use, distribution, and maintenance. Kolm dataset cards should be mandatory build outputs. |
| Data Cards, `https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.01075` | Data cards structure facts needed by stakeholders across the dataset lifecycle. Kolm account and docs surfaces should present intended use, sensitive attributes, quality, limitations, and maintenance cadence. |

##### `kolm.data_operating_contract.v3`

Every data-bearing object must be reducible to this contract before node `18` can be called complete.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.data_operating_contract.v3",
  "row": {
    "row_id": "row_...",
    "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
    "namespace": "support-prod",
    "source_kind": "capture | trace_import | manual_label | synthetic | public_fixture | holdout | benchmark",
    "source_ref": "hash-or-external-id",
    "content_hash": "sha256:...",
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "state": "hash_only | redacted | raw_sidecar | vault_ref | blocked",
    "sensitive_classes": ["email", "phone", "phi"],
    "redaction_policy_id": "policy_...",
    "redaction_report_id": "red_...",
    "deidentification_claim": "none | redacted_only | hipaa_safe_harbor | expert_determination",
    "utility_delta": 0.0
  },
  "rights": {
    "basis": "customer_owned | consent | contract | public_license | synthetic | unknown",
    "license": "customer-internal",
    "tos_training_allowed": false,
    "copyright_risk": "clear | review | block",
    "reuse_scope": "train | eval | benchmark | audit_only | blocked"
  },
  "retention": {
    "policy_id": "ret_...",
    "region": "us | eu | customer_hosted | local_only | unknown",
    "purge_after": "2026-06-24T00:00:00.000Z",
    "forget_state": "none | requested | marker_written | hard_purged | derived_outputs_invalidated | certificate_issued"
  },
  "eligibility": {
    "train": "allow | block | require_review",
    "eval": "allow | block | require_review",
    "benchmark": "allow | block | require_review",
    "artifact_embed": "hash_only | approved_excerpt | blocked",
    "reason_codes": ["rights_unknown", "raw_phi", "holdout_only"]
  },
  "quality": {
    "label_state": "unlabeled | accepted | corrected | rejected | conflict",
    "labeler_ref": "hash:...",
    "row_quality": 0.0,
    "poisoning_risk": 0.0,
    "novelty": 0.0,
    "synthetic_marker": false
  },
  "lineage": {
    "prov_entities": ["entity:row_..."],
    "prov_activities": ["activity:redact_...", "activity:split_..."],
    "prov_agents": ["agent:kolm-redactor"],
    "openlineage_run_id": "run_...",
    "derived_from": ["row_parent_..."],
    "artifact_refs": []
  },
  "operator": {
    "readiness": "can_train | cannot_train | can_eval | can_benchmark | audit_only",
    "next_action": "review_rights | redact | purge | relabel | rebuild_artifacts | no_action",
    "visible_in_account": true,
    "last_verified_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

##### Row Eligibility Decision Table

| decision | may train | may eval | may benchmark | may enter artifact | required proof |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|
| `can_train` | yes | yes, unless reserved | no, unless public/approved | hash or approved excerpt only | rights allow training, privacy state safe, not holdout-only, no active quarantine, lineage complete |
| `cannot_train` | no | conditional | no | hash only or blocked | explicit reason code: rights blocked, raw sensitive data, copyright risk, poisoning risk, delete request, retention expired, or review required |
| `can_eval` | no by default | yes | no, unless public/approved | hash or approved eval row | holdout/eval split proof, no train contamination, rights allow eval, privacy state safe |
| `can_benchmark` | no | yes | yes | public fixture only | source/license/checksum/freshness/raw-data policy and claim scope are present in corpus registry |
| `audit_only` | no | no | no | hash only | retention/legal/audit basis exists, operator sees why it is retained and when it can be purged |
| `purge_required` | no | no | no | blocked | forget or retention event exists, derived outputs identified, rebuild queue or exception certificate exists |

##### Purge, Forget, And Redistill Contract

The current forget-marker behavior is useful as a first step, but the product cannot claim deletion completion until the following chain exists and is visible:

1. write `forget_requested` with requester, basis, scope, namespace, and target row hashes;
2. stop future train/eval/benchmark/export use immediately through the eligibility gate;
3. hard purge raw payloads, vault refs, raw sidecars, caches, search indexes, and temporary workspaces where policy permits;
4. mark derived datasets, labels, synthetic rows, eval fixtures, model students, and artifacts as impacted;
5. enqueue redistill/recompile/re-eval or attach a lawful exception record;
6. update artifact lineage with `derived_outputs_invalidated` where needed;
7. issue deletion certificate with row hashes, derived-output status, residual exceptions, and timestamp;
8. expose the same state in account, API, CLI, TUI, and release proof.

The honest UX string stays: "Re-distill required to actually purge trained behavior." The final implementation must make that sentence actionable with a rebuild queue, affected artifact list, and completion certificate.

##### Data Command Center Specification

The final post-auth data command center should replace scattered uncertainty with one operating surface:

| panel | operator question | required implementation |
|---|---|---|
| readiness summary | What data can train right now? | counts by `can_train`, `cannot_train`, `can_eval`, `can_benchmark`, `audit_only`, `purge_required`, with freshness timestamp |
| blocker stack | Why can data not train? | reason-code histogram across privacy, rights, retention, poisoning, copyright, label conflict, holdout-only, missing lineage, and package/external proof |
| dataset cards | What dataset is this and what is it for? | generated cards with source mix, sensitive mix, rights mix, label quality, split seed, intended use, limitations, maintenance, and checksums |
| corpus registry | Which holdouts and benchmarks support which claims? | `public/benchmark-corpus-registry.json` with license, source, checksum, date, data class, public/private state, and allowed claim IDs |
| lineage graph | How did this row reach this artifact? | capture/import -> redact -> review -> label -> split -> synthetic/eval/distill -> artifact with PROV and OpenLineage export |
| deletion workbench | What happens if this customer asks to delete? | forget queue, hard purge state, affected artifacts, redistill/recompile queue, exception records, and certificate download |
| synthetic guard | Where are synthetic rows helping or hurting? | teacher/source model, prompt template, seed, source row refs, utility delta, contamination checks, and "not production proof alone" labels |
| release evidence | Can we ship claims from this data? | data evidence archive hash, screenshots, local/prod smoke result, stale/missing state, and claim-scope report |

##### Node 18 V3 Corpus Registry Contract

Every benchmark, holdout, eval corpus, and public fixture must have:

- stable corpus ID and version;
- source URL or source owner;
- license and allowed use;
- public/private/customer/internal state;
- content checksums and row count;
- sensitive data class and de-identification method;
- freshness date and recertification date;
- split role: train, eval, holdout, benchmark, red-team, synthetic seed, or audit;
- contamination policy and duplicate policy;
- claim IDs it may support;
- forbidden claim IDs it cannot support;
- owner and deletion/retention policy.

No homepage, docs, sales, benchmark, K-Score, or distillation claim may cite a corpus that lacks this registry entry.

| Ticket | Node | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DATA-V3-001` | `18.1` | Create `docs/data-lifecycle-contract.json`. | All capture/import/label/dataset/eval/synthetic/benchmark/artifact row states validate against one schema. |
| `W-DATA-V3-002` | `18.1` | Create `docs/capture-row-schema.json`. | Capture-store rows no longer rely on "post-redaction" convention; privacy, rights, retention, and eligibility are explicit. |
| `W-DATA-V3-003` | `18.1` | Add row eligibility engine. | Every row returns can-train/cannot-train/can-eval/can-benchmark/audit-only/purge-required plus reason codes. |
| `W-DATA-V3-004` | `18.1` | Wire eligibility into capture-store, labels, dataset creation, synthetic augmentation, distill planning, eval, benchmark export, and artifact build. | Discard/quarantine/forget/purge decisions cannot be bypassed by downstream code paths. |
| `W-DATA-V3-005` | `18.2` | Generate dataset cards. | Each dataset has source mix, rights mix, privacy mix, label quality, split seed, contamination report, intended use, limitations, and checksum. |
