| `W-DATA-V3-007` | `18.2` | Add label quality ledger. | Reviewer hash, adjudication, conflict, correction history, confidence, and downstream use state are visible and testable. |
| `W-DATA-V3-008` | `18.3` | Add privacy utility ledger. | Redaction report includes per-class decision, utility delta, blocked fields, and de-identification claim scope. |
| `W-DATA-V3-009` | `18.3` | Add raw sidecar and vault boundary contract. | Raw storage, vault refs, access audits, retention, purge, and artifact exclusion rules are enforced and tested. |
| `W-DATA-V3-010` | `18.3` | Convert redaction benchmark output into public evidence packet. | Benchmark reports include fixture version, row class coverage, precision/recall/F1, residual risk, and copy claim limits. |
| `W-DATA-V3-011` | `18.4` | Create `public/benchmark-corpus-registry.json`. | Every public/private benchmark and holdout has source, license, checksum, freshness, raw-data policy, data class, and claim scope. |
| `W-DATA-V3-012` | `18.4` | Create `docs/holdout-registry.json`. | Tenant holdouts, public holdouts, adversarial holdouts, and eval-only rows have split role, retention, and contamination policy. |
| `W-DATA-V3-013` | `18.4` | Create `docs/synthetic-data-policy.json`. | Synthetic rows expose teacher, source model, template, seed, objective, source row refs, utility delta, and proof limitations. |
| `W-DATA-V3-014` | `18.4` | Add synthetic-only proof blocker. | Distill, benchmark, K-Score, homepage copy, docs, and account readiness cannot imply production proof from synthetic-only data. |
| `W-DATA-V3-015` | `18.5` | Create `docs/rights-policy.json`. | Training/eval/benchmark/audit-only/blocked scopes map to source, license, provider ToS, customer contract, and copyright risk. |
| `W-DATA-V3-016` | `18.5` | Add copyright and ToS rights propagation. | Rights state follows rows into labels, datasets, synthetic generations, distill jobs, evals, benchmarks, and artifacts. |
| `W-DATA-V3-017` | `18.5` | Emit PROV-compatible lineage. | Capture/import/redact/label/split/synthetic/distill/eval/artifact activity can be reconstructed from exported entities, activities, and agents. |
| `W-DATA-V3-018` | `18.5` | Emit OpenLineage-compatible events and facets. | Dataset/job/run/input/output metadata exists for each data transformation and can be validated by a schema fixture. |
| `W-DATA-V3-019` | `18.5` | Add artifact boundary verifier. | Artifacts never embed raw source IDs, user IDs, session IDs, delete tokens, raw refs, importer manifests, or vault refs without explicit policy. |
| `W-DATA-V3-020` | `18.5` | Implement purge/redistill certificate chain. | Forget request blocks use, hard purge runs where allowed, affected artifacts are listed, rebuild is queued, and certificate is issued. |
| `W-DATA-V3-021` | `18.6` | Build account data command center. | Operators see readiness, blockers, dataset cards, corpus registry, lineage graph, deletion workbench, synthetic guard, and release evidence in one flow. |
| `W-DATA-V3-022` | `18.6` | Add API/CLI/TUI parity for data readiness. | Account, `/v1`, CLI, and TUI return the same eligibility counts, blockers, next actions, and proof freshness. |
| `W-DATA-V3-023` | `18.6` | Classify local `data/` artifacts. | The 718.01 MB local data tree is classified as fixture, benchmark, cache, archive, generated output, external source, or release-excluded data with retention policy. |
| `W-DATA-V3-024` | `18.6` | Add data completion certificate. | Release proof includes row schema hash, dataset-card bundle, corpus-registry hash, lineage export, purge certificates, screenshots, local/prod smoke, and claim-scope report. |

##### Node 18 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `18` remains red until:

1. every data-bearing object resolves to `kolm.data_operating_contract.v3`;
2. capture-store no longer relies on undocumented post-redaction assumptions;
3. forget markers are backed by use blocking, hard purge, derived-output invalidation, artifact rebuild, and certificates;
4. dataset cards are generated and visible for every train/eval/holdout corpus;
5. corpus registries define license, source, checksum, freshness, sensitivity, and claim scope;
6. redaction claims distinguish redacted, Safe Harbor, Expert Determination, and residual risk;
7. synthetic rows are useful but never counted as production proof without explicit real/benchmark corroboration;
8. W3C PROV and OpenLineage exports can reconstruct row-to-artifact lineage;
9. row-level rights and ToS decisions propagate through distill, eval, benchmark, artifact, and public claims;
10. account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, product graph, tests, screenshots, and release proof all agree on data readiness;
11. local and production smoke prove capture, redact, label, split, holdout verification, distill dry-run, forget/purge, and excluded-row behavior;
12. the 718.01 MB local data tree is fully classified and release-safe.

Until these relax conditions are true, Kolm can market strong data capabilities only with scoped language. It cannot honestly claim fully closed-loop, enterprise-grade, deletion-safe, benchmark-backed, state-of-the-art distillation data infrastructure.

### Performance, Scalability, Reliability, And Cost Efficiency Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `19`: the operating envelope. Kolm cannot be the production AI compiler if compile, capture, distill, routing, audit, account, registry, storage, and device workflows are only correct in unit tests. They must stay fast, measurable, recoverable, and economically bounded under real workloads. Performance is not a decoration here; it is part of the product claim because Kolm sells lower latency, lower cost, safer routing, and auditable production execution.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not modify queue code, telemetry code, account SLA pages, pricing code, alert files, runtime code, API routes, or frontend styling while other agents are editing.

#### Current Performance And Reliability Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| SLA rollups | `src/sla-rollup.js`, `tests/wave788-sla.test.js`, `public/account/sla.html` | Kolm has per-tenant latency and uptime samples with p50/p95/p99 rollups and no fake zeroes for empty windows. | The frozen surface list covers only a subset of product behavior; the account page still contains placeholder/live-wiring language and needs production sample proof. |
| health and status | `public/status.html`, `public/sla.html`, health/ready tests, API route probes | The product has public status and readiness concepts. | Status must be derived from live SLIs, incidents, dependency health, queue depth, object storage readiness, cloud credentials, and customer-visible degradation states. |
| jobs and long-running work | `src/jobs.js`, CLI job commands, compile/distill/eval/replay/capture-export job kinds | Local job manifests, logs, pruning, and status transitions exist. | Final production needs durable multi-worker queues, idempotency keys, retry state, cancellation guarantees, orphan recovery, and restart/concurrency proof. |
| load shedding and degradation | `src/load-queue.js`, `tests/wave729-load-queue.test.js` | Priority lanes, timeout, 429/Retry-After behavior, queue-full rejection, and optional teacher overflow are represented. | Priority lane names still include legacy `starter` and `business`; they must align with Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise contracts and customer entitlements. |
| caching | `src/cache.js` | L1 memory cache plus L2 disk cache exist for local/runtime speedups. | Disk L2 needs TTL, max-size, tenant scoping proof, eviction metrics, corruption handling, and explicit "not source of truth" wording across docs and code comments. |
| preload and memory planning | `src/preload-scheduler.js`, `src/runtime-preload.js`, `public/docs/runtime/memory-tiers.html` | Predictive preload and memory-tier ideas are encoded. | Production preloading must prove miss-rate reduction, cold-start reduction, memory pressure safety, tenant isolation, and rollback when predictions hurt latency or cost. |
| retries and structured output repair | `src/output-retry.js` | Parse-failure retries, temperature decay, and teacher splice attempt envelopes exist. | Retry policies need budgets, max-cost controls, circuit breakers, idempotency, customer-visible attempt metadata, and no silent quality masking. |
| cloud and capacity routing | `src/cloud-compute-broker.js`, `scripts/cloud-compute-broker.mjs`, `docs/cloud-compute-broker.md` | Compute lanes and readiness/ranking metadata exist for local, remote, GPU, serverless, and cloud paths. | It must be connected to real capacity telemetry, queue pressure, cost caps, GPU availability, region/data residency, and per-tenant policy. |
| observability and traces | `src/otel.js`, `src/otel-attrs.js`, `public/docs/observability.html`, `public/docs/observability/opentelemetry.html` | Kolm has an OTLP/HTTP exporter and canonical `kolm.*` attributes with tenant hashing. | Custom telemetry must prove OpenTelemetry compatibility, GenAI semantic convention mapping, queue/drop behavior, secret redaction, collector integration, and on-by-default production posture where appropriate. |
| metrics and alerts | `public/docs/observability/prometheus.html`, `tools/alerts/datadog-kolm.yaml`, `tools/alerts/grafana-kolm.yaml`, `tools/alerts/honeycomb-kolm.yaml` | Prometheus/exporter and vendor alert artifacts exist. | Every alert must match a real emitted metric name, have a runbook, threshold rationale, owner, test fixture, and noise budget; alert docs must not drift from code. |
| cost and usage | `src/cost-estimator.js`, `src/usage.js`, `src/usage-analytics.js`, `src/billing-breakdown.js`, cost docs | Usage and cost surfaces exist. | Unit economics must reconcile provider bills, cached hits, fallback calls, retries, distill jobs, cloud/GPU minutes, storage, egress, human review, discounts, taxes, and plan entitlements. |
| drift and continuous monitoring | `src/continuous-monitoring.js`, `src/drift-alert.js`, account monitoring pages | There are monitoring and drift alert concepts. | Monitoring must be tied to SLO burn, K-Score drift, data freshness, teacher version drift, model degradation, rollback automation, and account next actions. |

#### Primary Standards Imported Into Node 19

| source | URL | spec implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google SRE Service Level Objectives | `https://sre.google/sre-book/service-level-objectives/` | Every production claim needs an SLI, SLO, evaluation window, and error-budget policy. "100% uptime" is not the target; measurable user journeys are. |
| Google SRE Workbook SLO implementation | `https://sre.google/workbook/implementing-slos/` | A service without formal objectives and budget policy is not operationally complete, even if monitoring exists. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | AI calls, model operations, token counts, provider/model identity, and GenAI errors need standard attributes where possible, with Kolm-specific extensions only when necessary. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/semantic-conventions/` | Custom `kolm.*` attributes must not replace standard HTTP, service, exception, database, queue, and GenAI attributes. |
| Prometheus alerting practices | `https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/alerting/` | Alerts should page on symptoms that require action, not every component-level signal. Kolm alert rules need `for` windows, severity, runbooks, and inhibition/aggregation plans. |
| Prometheus alerting rules | `https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/2.53/configuration/alerting_rules/` | Alert files must be syntactically valid and executable by Prometheus, not just documentation examples. |
| DORA software delivery metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Build completion needs deployment frequency, lead time, failed-deployment recovery time, and change fail rate evidence, not just test counts. |
| HTTP 429 / Retry-After semantics | `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/429` | Queue-full and rate-limited responses must carry actionable backoff semantics, envelope fields, and SDK behavior. |
| Kubernetes autoscaling | `https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/` | Cloud and BYOC deployments need autoscaling contracts based on observed resource/utilization metrics, not static worker counts. |

#### Product-Surface SLO Model

Kolm needs SLOs by product journey, not a single site uptime number. The master SLO set should include at least:

| product journey | primary user-visible success | required SLIs | initial SLO draft |
|---|---|---|---|
| API gateway and capture | User can route a provider call and capture the event with correct tenant/security boundaries. | request success, p95/p99 latency, capture write freshness, provider fallback rate, structured error rate. | p95 gateway overhead budget by route, 99.9% monthly availability for paid gateway surfaces, capture freshness under 60 seconds for normal load. |
| .kolm compile | User can compile captured behavior into a signed artifact or see a clear queued/error state. | job accepted latency, queue wait, compile completion time, compile success rate, artifact verification rate. | job creation p95 under 500 ms; small recipe-tier compile p95 under 60 seconds when capacity is available; no hidden failed artifacts. |
| distill and train | User can run distillation safely within cost/capacity constraints. | queue wait, teacher call error rate, student quality gates, cost cap adherence, cancellation success, resume success. | every run has cost estimate, budget stop, resumable state, and visible failure reason; no tenant can starve another paid tenant outside entitlement policy. |
| runtime inference | Artifact can run locally, cloud, edge, or BYOC with known performance envelopes. | cold start, warm p95/p99, memory peak, fallback count, draft accept rate, quantization error, device compatibility. | every runtime target has measured baseline, supported model/artifact class, and a failure/degrade mode. |
| account and admin | Operators can see truth, risks, costs, incidents, and next actions. | dashboard freshness, API error rate, missing-data states, authz failures, account page JS errors. | account pages cannot show fake healthy states; each page must distinguish live, stale, missing, external, and unavailable data. |
| registry and docs | Users can find artifacts, SDKs, docs, API specs, and closeout ledgers. | page availability, broken-link rate, API spec freshness, search/index health, CLI/doc parity. | docs and API references regenerate from source; stale generated artifacts fail verification. |
| storage and artifact lake | Artifacts, receipts, privacy sidecars, and logs persist without leaks or corruption. | object-store readiness, write/read/delete smoke, checksum mismatch, egress, retention, purge success. | each provider has readiness, smoke proof, max-object notes, secret redaction, and recovery path. |

#### Canonical SLO Contract Shape

Each production surface should emit or store a contract shaped like this:

```json
{
  "surface_id": "gateway_capture",
  "owner": "backend-runtime",
  "user_journey": "route_provider_call_and_capture_event",
  "customer_tiers": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
  "sli_events": [
    "http_request_success",
    "http_request_latency_ms",
    "capture_write_committed",
    "provider_fallback_used",
    "structured_error_returned"
  ],
  "slo": {
    "availability_monthly_pct": 99.9,
    "latency_p95_ms": 120,
    "capture_freshness_p95_seconds": 60
  },
  "error_budget": {
    "window_days": 30,
    "burn_alerts": ["2h_fast_burn", "24h_slow_burn"],
    "release_policy": "freeze risky releases when budget is exhausted"
  },
  "measurement": {
    "source": "otel_and_sla_rollup",
    "tenant_isolation": "hashed_tenant_id",
    "missing_data_state": "not_measured"
  },
  "alerts": [
    {
      "name": "GatewayCaptureHighErrorBudgetBurn",
      "severity": "page",
      "runbook": "docs/runbooks/gateway-capture.md"
    }
  ],
  "rollback": {
    "safe_mode": "disable_nonessential_fallbacks",
    "manual_command": "kolm ops rollback gateway-capture"
  }
}
```

This contract is not optional metadata. It is the join key between code, account UI, CLI, product graph, public status, customer SLA language, alerts, incident review, and pricing entitlements.

#### Redlines Found In Current Tree

1. `public/account/sla.html` still contains placeholder/live-wiring language. It cannot be considered final until it is backed by live `/v1/sla/series` data, stale/missing-data states, and production tenant samples.
2. `src/sla-rollup.js` samples only frozen surfaces such as `router_http`, `cli_compile`, `cli_distill`, `capture_log`, `intent_ask`, and `bakeoff`. Every route group and product journey must either be measured or explicitly marked `not_measured`.
3. `src/jobs.js` is a local file-backed registry. That is useful proof for local operators, but it is not yet proof of production-grade durable queueing across multiple workers, restarts, concurrency, and region failover.
4. `src/load-queue.js` has priority lanes named `enterprise`, `business`, `starter`, and `free`. Node 16 established Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise as the customer-facing plan taxonomy. Performance policy must stop using retired plan IDs or provide a migration shim with tests.
5. `src/cache.js` has local L1/L2 cache mechanics, but the production spec needs TTL, max-size, tenant scoping, corruption recovery, eviction metrics, and no "cache as source of truth" ambiguity.
6. `src/otel.js` is custom and disabled unless configured. The final build must prove OTLP collector compatibility, standard semantic attributes, GenAI conventions, queue/drop behavior, secret redaction, and backpressure behavior.
7. Alert YAMLs and observability docs must be generated or validated against emitted metric names. Dead alert names are worse than no alert because they create false operational confidence.
8. Cost savings cannot be marketed as a generic percentage without a calculator path that reconciles provider price, cache rate, fallback rate, retry rate, distill cost, GPU/cloud minutes, object storage, egress, review labor, and plan discount.
9. Load and chaos proof is missing if only unit tests exist. Node 19 requires scripted load profiles, dependency failure simulations, object-store outage tests, provider 429/5xx simulations, queue saturation tests, and rollback drills.
10. The account surface must make reliability truth legible: live, stale, missing, external, degraded, disabled, not configured, and not measured are separate states.

#### Completion Specification For Node 19

| area | done means | proof required |
|---|---|---|
| SLO registry | Every product surface has owner, SLI events, SLO objective, window, error budget, alert, and runbook. | `docs/slo-registry.json`, account SLO page, CLI SLO command, product graph link, tests. |
| full-surface measurement | Every route group, CLI command class, TUI/account page, job kind, storage provider, and runtime target is measured or explicitly not measured. | Generated coverage report comparing product graph to SLI event inventory. |
| hot-path latency | API, capture, compile enqueue, artifact verify, account dashboards, and docs/spec endpoints have latency budgets. | Load-test report with p50/p95/p99 by route and plan tier. |
| queue durability | Long-running work survives process restart, worker crash, duplicate submit, cancellation, retry, and idempotent resume. | Multi-worker integration test and runbook with failure injection. |
| autoscaling and capacity | Cloud/BYOC lanes scale from real resource and queue metrics with caps and tenant fairness. | Capacity model, autoscaling config, saturation test, and cost cap proof. |
| graceful degradation | Overload produces predictable 429/Retry-After, degraded modes, fallback controls, and account alerts. | Synthetic saturation tests and SDK behavior tests. |
| telemetry | Traces, metrics, logs, and events use standard attributes plus controlled `kolm.*` extensions. | Collector smoke, Prometheus scrape, trace fixture, no-secret assertions. |
| alerts | Alerts are actionable, linked to emitted metrics, owners, runbooks, and customer impact. | Alert lint, replay fixture, runbook links, paging/noise review. |
| cost accounting | Every API call, capture event, distill job, GPU minute, storage byte, egress byte, fallback call, and review action can be attributed. | Reconciliation report against usage/billing fixtures and provider invoices. |
| incident response | Incidents have detection, mitigation, communication, postmortem, and regression tests. | Incident template, game-day report, DORA recovery metrics. |
| production evidence | Local and production gates both run; production smoke cannot be replaced by local proof. | Prod smoke report after deploy with environment-specific caveats. |

#### W-PERF Ticket Ledger

| ticket | priority | node | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| W-PERF-001 | P0 | 19.1 | Create `docs/slo-registry.json` from product graph, route groups, jobs, account pages, storage providers, and runtime targets. | Every surface has owner, SLI, SLO, error-budget policy, runbook, and customer-tier scope. |
| W-PERF-002 | P0 | 19.1 | Add SLO coverage verifier. | Build fails when a shipped public surface has no SLO contract or explicit `not_measured` state. |
| W-PERF-003 | P0 | 19.1 | Replace placeholder account SLA copy with live/stale/missing/error states. | `/account/sla` can never imply health without fresh samples. |
| W-PERF-004 | P0 | 19.2 | Align load-queue priority lanes with Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise. | Legacy `starter` and `business` are either removed or covered by migration tests and not shown to users. |
| W-PERF-005 | P0 | 19.2 | Define HTTP 429/Retry-After envelope and SDK behavior. | API, CLI, SDK, docs, and tests agree on retry/backoff semantics. |
| W-PERF-006 | P0 | 19.2 | Add cache policy contract. | L1/L2 cache has TTL, max size, tenant scope, eviction metrics, corruption recovery, and source-of-truth warning. |
| W-PERF-007 | P0 | 19.3 | Promote jobs from local registry proof to production durable queue contract. | Multi-worker restart, duplicate submit, cancel, resume, retry, and orphan recovery tests pass. |
| W-PERF-008 | P0 | 19.3 | Add capacity model for compile/distill/runtime/cloud lanes. | Queue depth, worker count, GPU availability, cost cap, and region policy determine admission. |
| W-PERF-009 | P1 | 19.3 | Connect preload scheduler to measured outcomes. | Preload has miss-rate/cold-start improvement reports and rollback when it hurts memory or cost. |
| W-PERF-010 | P0 | 19.4 | Map telemetry to OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions. | Standard attributes are emitted for provider/model/token/error spans with safe Kolm extensions. |
| W-PERF-011 | P0 | 19.4 | Add collector/exporter smoke test. | OTLP collector receives traces/metrics/logs; queue drops are bounded and reported; secrets are redacted. |
| W-PERF-012 | P0 | 19.4 | Validate alert files against emitted metrics and runbooks. | Grafana/Datadog/Honeycomb/Prometheus rules cannot reference missing metrics or missing runbooks. |
| W-PERF-013 | P1 | 19.4 | Create incident response and game-day runbooks. | Provider outage, object-store outage, queue saturation, bad deploy, and auth outage have drills and postmortem templates. |
| W-PERF-014 | P0 | 19.5 | Build unit-economics reconciliation ledger. | Provider bill, usage meter, cache hit, retry, fallback, GPU, storage, egress, human review, and plan discount reconcile. |
| W-PERF-015 | P1 | 19.5 | Add customer-visible cost anomaly detection. | Account UI flags abnormal spend, fallback spikes, cache misses, and distill overruns with next actions. |
| W-PERF-016 | P0 | 19.6 | Add product load-test suite by journey. | Gateway/capture, compile enqueue, account dashboards, registry/docs, storage readiness, and artifact verify have p95/p99 reports. |
| W-PERF-017 | P0 | 19.6 | Add dependency failure simulations. | Provider 429/5xx, object-store failure, DB/file lock, OTLP collector down, worker crash, and network timeout are tested. |
| W-PERF-018 | P1 | 19.6 | Add production smoke report template. | Deploy is not final until production URL, auth, account, API, telemetry, object storage, and critical paths are smoke-tested. |
| W-PERF-019 | P1 | 19.6 | Add DORA metrics ledger. | Deployment frequency, change lead time, failed-deployment recovery time, and change fail rate are measured from CI/CD evidence. |
| W-PERF-020 | P1 | cross-node | Add performance claim-scope gate. | Marketing cannot claim speed, uptime, savings, or scale unless backed by SLO/load/cost evidence with date and environment. |

#### Redline To Relax For Node 19

Node `19` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from live or fixture-backed evidence, not memory:

- What is the p95 and p99 latency for every shipped product journey, by tenant tier and environment?
- Which user journeys are currently not measured, and why?
- How much error budget remains for each customer-visible surface?
- What queue or dependency is the current bottleneck?
- What will happen if the main provider, object store, telemetry collector, worker, or GPU lane fails?
- Which retries, fallbacks, cache hits, and preloads saved cost, and which increased risk?
- Can a customer see the same truth in account UI, CLI, API, docs, and status pages?
- Can the team prove the last deploy improved or preserved DORA, SLO, cost, and quality metrics?

The relax condition for node `19` is not "the app starts" or "release verification passes locally." It is: every performance, reliability, scale, and cost claim is tied to measured SLOs, generated coverage, live/stale state, cost reconciliation, alert/runbook proof, load/chaos evidence, and production smoke after deploy.

#### Node 19 V2 Performance, Scalability, Reliability, And Cost Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `19` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change runtime code, queues, account pages, alert YAML, API routes, billing, or generated assets while other agents are editing. The purpose is to turn the current "it works locally" state into a complete operating-envelope contract for every shipped Kolm surface.

The working standard is simple: Kolm is not complete if the product can compile, distill, route, capture, verify, or display account health but cannot state the latency, throughput, error budget, cost, degradation mode, live/stale evidence, alert, runbook, and production smoke proof for that surface.

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| broad repository scan | `src`: 372 performance-matched files out of 392 scanned; `public`: 1,082 out of 1,132; `tests`: 513 out of 567; `scripts`: 243 out of 343; `docs`: 150 out of 158; `tools`: 25 out of 29. | Performance, reliability, scalability, and cost are cross-cutting concerns. They cannot be owned by one helper file or one account page. |

##### Node 19 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google SRE SLOs | `https://sre.google/sre-book/service-level-objectives/` | Use SLIs, SLOs, and SLA language separately. A customer-visible surface is not operationally complete until its indicator, objective, window, and response policy are explicit. |
| Google SRE Workbook SLO implementation | `https://sre.google/workbook/implementing-slos/` | SLO rollout requires ownership, measurement, alerting, error-budget policy, and iterative review, not a one-time dashboard. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | AI operations should expose provider/model/request/token/error semantics with standard attributes before custom `kolm.*` extensions. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/semantic-conventions/` | HTTP, exception, service, database, messaging, queue, and deployment attributes should use standard names where available. |
| Prometheus alerting practices | `https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/alerting/` | Page on actionable symptoms with owner, duration, severity, and runbook. Do not page on every internal component signal. |
| Kubernetes workload autoscaling | `https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/` | Autoscaling must be driven by measured resource/utilization or custom workload metrics, with caps and safe behavior under missing metrics. |
| DORA delivery metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Completion needs change lead time, deployment frequency, failed-deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate from release evidence. |
| HTTP 429 semantics | `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/429` | Rate limiting and queue saturation must return actionable backoff behavior, including `Retry-After` where appropriate. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Website/account performance cannot ignore user-perceived loading, interaction, and layout stability. Node `19` must coordinate with node `12` for UX performance proof. |

##### Node 19 V2 Operating Envelope Contract

Every product surface must either implement this contract or be explicitly marked `not_measured` with owner, reason, and completion ticket.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-operating-envelope-contract-v2",
  "surface_id": "gateway.capture",
  "owner": "backend-runtime",
  "journey": "route_model_call_capture_receipt",
  "customer_tiers": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
  "claim_scope": ["latency", "availability", "cost", "auditability"],
  "slo": {
    "availability": {
      "window_days": 30,
      "target_pct": 99.9,
      "excluded_states": ["blocked_external", "not_configured"]
    },
    "latency": {
      "p50_ms": 50,
      "p95_ms": 120,
      "p99_ms": 300,
      "environment": "production"
    },
    "freshness": {
      "capture_commit_p95_seconds": 60
    }
  },
  "sli_events": [
    "http_request_started",
    "http_request_completed",
    "provider_call_completed",
    "capture_event_committed",
    "structured_error_returned"
  ],
  "error_budget": {
    "policy": "freeze_risky_release_when_exhausted",
    "fast_burn_alert": "2h",
    "slow_burn_alert": "24h"
  },
  "capacity": {
    "admission_metric": "queue_depth_and_provider_budget",
    "autoscale_metric": "p95_queue_wait_ms",
    "max_inflight_per_tenant": 100,
    "degrade_mode": "return_429_with_retry_after"
  },
  "queue": {
    "durability": "multi_worker_required",
    "idempotency_key": "required",
    "cancellation": "required",
    "orphan_recovery": "required"
  },
  "cache": {
    "allowed": true,
    "tenant_scoped": true,
    "ttl_seconds": 3600,
    "max_bytes_per_tenant": 1073741824,
    "source_of_truth": false
  },
  "retry": {
    "max_attempts": 2,
    "budget_usd": 0.25,
    "requires_attempt_metadata": true,
    "circuit_breaker": "required"
  },
  "telemetry": {
    "otel_required": true,
    "genai_semconv_required": true,
    "secret_redaction_required": true,
    "drop_policy": "bounded_queue_with_drop_counter"
  },
  "alerts": [
    {
      "name": "GatewayCaptureHighErrorBudgetBurn",
      "severity": "page",
      "runbook": "docs/runbooks/gateway-capture.md",
      "customer_impact": "capture_and_gateway_degraded"
    }
  ],
  "cost": {
    "unit": "request",
    "must_attribute": ["provider", "tokens", "cache", "retry", "fallback", "storage", "egress", "discount"]
  },
  "load_tests": ["gateway-capture-normal", "gateway-capture-saturation"],
  "chaos_tests": ["provider-429", "object-store-timeout", "otel-collector-down"],
  "production_smoke": {
    "required_after_deploy": true,
    "latest_report": null
  },
  "evidence": {
    "local": [],
    "production": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "source_paths": []
  }
}
```

##### Node 19 V2 Reliability State Vocabulary

The account UI, CLI, API, public status, docs, and release evidence must use the same state names. Inventing one-off labels is forbidden because it makes operational truth impossible to compare.

| state | meaning | UI/API rule |
|---|---|---|
| `live` | Fresh measured production data exists and the surface is inside its SLO. | May show healthy, but must include freshness and environment. |
| `stale` | Last sample is older than the freshness policy. | Must not imply health; show last sample time and next probe. |
| `missing` | Measurement is expected but no sample exists. | Treat as incomplete proof, not healthy. |
| `external` | State depends on external provider, package release, partner, certification, or benchmark data. | Show dependency and closeout owner. |
| `degraded` | The surface works with reduced latency, throughput, quality, feature set, or reliability. | Show impact, fallback, and incident/runbook link. |
| `disabled` | Feature is intentionally turned off by policy, plan, environment, or operator. | Show who/what disabled it and how to enable if allowed. |
| `not_configured` | Required environment, provider, storage, model, or credential is absent. | Show missing configuration groups without secret values. |
| `not_measured` | The surface has no SLI instrumentation yet. | Must carry a ticket and cannot support speed, uptime, or scale claims. |
| `blocked_external` | Completion depends on a third-party condition outside local code. | Must map to the readiness closeout ledger. |
| `incident` | Active customer-impacting failure or SLO breach. | Show severity, start time, affected surfaces, mitigation, and update cadence. |
| `recovering` | Mitigation landed and system is returning to normal. | Show validation window and when the incident can close. |

##### Node 19 V2 Load, Chaos, And Capacity Matrix

| scenario | path under test | required simulation | pass condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| gateway normal load | provider routing and capture | steady request mix by plan tier and provider | p95/p99 within SLO; no tenant leakage; capture freshness holds. |
| gateway saturation | queue admission and rate limiting | exceed configured capacity with mixed Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise tenants | 429/Retry-After is correct; paid tiers follow entitlement policy; no process crash. |
| compile enqueue storm | compile API and jobs | burst compile requests with duplicate idempotency keys | exactly-once job creation; bounded queue wait; clear accepted/queued/rejected states. |
| distill long run | teacher calls, worker, cost caps | long-running distill with retryable and non-retryable provider errors | resumable state, budget stop, failure reason, and no hidden spend. |
| artifact verify | verify/read object path | concurrent artifact reads and checksum verification | no checksum mismatch; cache cannot mask corrupt source data. |
| account SLA | account dashboard and API | live, stale, missing, external, and incident fixtures | every state renders legibly and consistently across light/dark/mobile/desktop. |
| object store outage | storage readiness and artifact writes | R2/S3/Supabase/local read/write/delete failure | readiness degrades, writes fail closed, delete/purge does not silently pass. |
| provider 429/5xx | provider adapter and fallback | provider returns 429, 500, timeout, malformed stream | backoff, fallback, circuit breaker, and attempt metadata behave as documented. |
| OTLP collector down | telemetry exporter | collector unavailable or slow | app stays responsive; bounded drops are counted; secrets remain redacted. |
| worker crash | job runner | crash during compile/distill/eval | orphan recovery, resume/cancel, and duplicate prevention are proven. |
| cache corruption | L2 disk cache | corrupt cached object and stale tenant entry | corruption is detected, evicted, and never becomes source of truth. |
| cloud broker no capacity | compute broker | no GPU/worker/region capacity available | user sees waitlist/retry/degrade options and no false "ready" state. |
| GPU shortage | distill/train lane | GPU unavailable after admission | job pauses or reroutes within policy; cost cap and data residency are preserved. |
| bad deploy | production release path | new deploy breaks one critical journey | rollback metric, recovery time, postmortem, and regression test are recorded. |

##### Node 19 V2 Cost Attribution Contract

Kolm's savings claims are not complete until every unit of cost can be traced. The cost ledger must attribute at least:

| cost source | required fields | product consequence |
|---|---|---|
| provider calls | provider, model, input tokens, output tokens, request class, region, tenant, namespace, artifact, retry/fallback attempt. | ROI and account spend charts can explain why a cost occurred. |
| cache hits and misses | cache tier, key class, tenant scope, hit/miss, stale reject, corruption reject, saved provider call estimate. | Savings claims can separate real avoided calls from guessed savings. |
| retries and repairs | failure class, attempt count, retry policy, extra tokens, extra latency, extra cost. | Quality repair cannot silently inflate spend. |
| fallback routing | original provider/model, fallback provider/model, reason, quality/cost/latency delta. | Hybrid routing claims can be audited by customer and by support. |
| distill teacher calls | teacher, task, examples, tokens, synthetic ratio, holdout class, budget cap, stop reason. | Distillation ROI includes training cost, not just inference savings. |
| GPU/cloud minutes | target, accelerator, region, queue wait, active time, idle time, preemptions, cost rate. | Cloud/BYOC choice can be optimized and defended. |
| storage and egress | provider, bucket, object class, bytes, retention class, read/write/delete count, egress region. | Artifact lake and audit receipt costs are visible. |
| human review | review queue, reviewer class, minutes, approval result, escalation. | Enterprise workflows do not hide manual operating cost. |
| discounts and credits | plan tier, contract discount, credit bundle, marketplace fee, tax/fee adjustment. | Billing can reconcile account UI with invoices. |
| chargeback | tenant, team, namespace, project, artifact, API key, cost center. | Enterprise customers can allocate spend without support tickets. |

Cost evidence must flow into pricing, ROI calculator, account analytics, billing breakdown, support exports, and public claim-scope checks. If the cost ledger cannot explain a savings number, the number must be removed or scoped as illustrative.

##### Node 19 V2 UI/UX Operating Rules

These rules attach node `19` to node `12` without taking over frontend ownership:

1. no account or public status surface may use a green/healthy visual state without freshness, environment, and source;
2. every performance chart must show whether it is live production, local fixture, benchmark, or sample data;
3. latency, cost, and uptime summaries must have compact first-screen hierarchy: current state, customer impact, next action, then details;
4. skeleton states must reserve stable dimensions so loading telemetry does not shift layouts;
5. degraded and incident states must use text plus icon/shape, not color alone;
6. mobile account views must keep state, owner, and next action visible without hover-only affordances;
7. demo/video/media surfaces must not imply live performance unless backed by current measured data;
8. public marketing claims such as "faster", "cheaper", "run anywhere", "enterprise-grade", or "production-ready" need date/environment/evidence links.

##### Node 19 V2 Relax Redline

Node `19` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every product graph surface has an operating-envelope contract or explicit `not_measured` state with owner and ticket;
2. customer-visible performance, uptime, reliability, savings, scale, and "run anywhere" claims are backed by dated local and production evidence;
3. account, CLI, API, TUI, docs, public status, and release reports use one reliability state vocabulary;
4. overload behavior returns correct 429/backoff/idempotency semantics and does not leak retired plan names;
5. long-running jobs survive restart, duplicate submit, cancellation, retry, orphan recovery, and worker crash in a durable multi-worker model;
6. cache, preload, retry, fallback, and repair logic have cost, latency, quality, and safety budgets;
7. telemetry maps to OpenTelemetry standard and GenAI attributes, redacts secrets, handles exporter failure, and has collector smoke proof;
8. every alert references an emitted metric, owner, severity, runbook, threshold rationale, and customer-impact condition;
9. cost attribution reconciles provider calls, retries, fallbacks, cache, distill teacher calls, GPU minutes, storage, egress, human review, discounts, credits, taxes, and chargeback;
10. scripted load, chaos, capacity, incident, rollback, and production smoke reports exist for the shipping commit.

If any of these fail, Kolm may still have useful operational features, but it is not a 100% complete production operating envelope.

#### Node 19 V3 Operating-Proof Mesh, Load Finality, And Unit-Economics Contract

This addendum maintains node `19` in the master spec tree. It does not create node `52`, does not edit code, and does not overwrite parallel-agent work. It turns node `19` from a reliability wish list into a build-redline packet: every shipped surface must prove what users experience, how overload behaves, how cost is bounded, what telemetry says, which alert fires, which runbook resolves it, and whether production smoke agrees with local proof.

The state-of-the-art operating standard for Kolm is stricter than "the app starts" or "release verify passed once." Kolm is complete only when routing, capture, compile, distill, runtime inference, account operations, registry/docs, object storage, and enterprise admin have measurable SLOs, capacity/admission rules, graceful degradation, cost reconciliation, live/stale/missing state, alert/runbook evidence, load/chaos reports, and post-deploy production proof.

| evidence class | observed state on 2026-05-25 | completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| broad operating keyword review | `src`: 339 matched of 392 files; `public`: 1,043 of 1,132; `tests`: 441 of 567; `scripts`: 166 of 343; `docs`: 151 of 158; `tools`: 25 of 29. | Reliability, performance, cost, queues, telemetry, and load behavior are codebase-wide. The final gate must be generated from the product graph, not hand-curated from a few files. |
| SLO and status code reviewed | `src/sla-rollup.js` 12,147 bytes / 338 lines; `public/account/sla.html` 24,450 / 553; `public/status.html` 19,672 / 284; `public/sla.html` 7,521 / 125. | SLA rollup and pages exist. The account page still contains placeholder rows, live-wiring caveats, retired Business plan copy, and a dependency on `/v1/sla/series` finality. |

Completion still requires durable multi-worker semantics, idempotency, cancellation, orphan recovery, and plan-lane alignment with Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise. |
| load queue inspected | `PRIORITY_LANES` still includes `enterprise`, `business`, `starter`, and `free`; queue comments state 429/Retry-After and queue-full behavior. | Node `16` standardized current tiers. Node `19` must stop leaking retired `business` and `starter` lane names or prove they are internal aliases with tests and no public leakage. |

Final proof needs TTL, max-size, tenant scope, eviction metrics, corruption handling, cold-start delta, memory pressure safety, and rollback if preload hurts. |

Final proof needs OpenTelemetry/GenAI mapping, collector smoke, exporter drop metrics, cardinality controls, and no-secret assertions. |

They need live capacity, queue pressure, storage readiness, GPU/provider availability, region/data-residency, egress, and cost caps in one admission controller. |
| monitoring/drift files reviewed | `src/continuous-monitoring.js` 13,108 bytes / 350 lines; `src/drift-alert.js` 16,262 / 369. | Drift and monitoring concepts exist. They must be tied to SLO burn, K-Score drift, teacher version drift, rollback policy, and customer next actions. |
| alert files reviewed | Datadog 3,240 bytes / 84 lines; Grafana 4,508 / 126; Honeycomb 2,896 / 103. | Vendor alert artifacts exist. They reference runbook URLs, metric names, datasource placeholders, and namespace placeholders; no matching docs runbook files were found under `docs`. |
| contract/report files absent | `docs/slo-registry.json`, `docs/sli-event-inventory.json`, `docs/load-test-profiles.json`, `docs/capacity-model.json`, `docs/cost-attribution-contract.json`, `docs/incident-playbooks.json`, `reports/load`, `reports/chaos`, `reports/slo`, and `reports/capacity` are absent. | Implementation pieces exist, but the completion artifacts needed to prove production operation do not yet exist. |

Node `19` still needs one release evidence packet that runs local/prod smoke, load, chaos, SLO, cost, telemetry, and rollback proof in a documented order. |

##### Node 19 V3 External Baselines

| primary source | imported rule for Kolm |
|---|---|
| Google SRE Service Level Objectives, `https://sre.google/sre-book/service-level-objectives/` | Choose SLIs from user-visible behavior, define SLO targets, distinguish SLOs from SLAs, and use distributions rather than averages for latency. |
| Google SRE Workbook SLO implementation, `https://sre.google/workbook/implementing-slos/` | Starter SLOs need concrete SLI formulas, measurement windows, latency percentiles, availability calculations, and error-budget policy. |
| Google SRE Handling Overload, `https://sre.google/sre-book/handling-overload/` | Overload must be graceful, capacity should be modeled from actual resource consumption, and per-customer limits plus adaptive throttling are required to avoid one tenant harming others. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | GenAI spans, events, and metrics should use standard provider/model/operation/token/error semantics before Kolm-specific attributes. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions and Metrics SDK, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/semantic-conventions/` and `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/metrics/sdk/` | Telemetry must handle resources, services, metrics, histograms, exporters, cardinality, flush, shutdown, and compatibility as a system, not just custom attributes. |
| Prometheus alerting practices, `https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/alerting/` | Alerts should be few, actionable, symptom-oriented, linked to consoles/runbooks, and should include metamonitoring so monitoring failure is visible. |
| Kubernetes autoscaling, `https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/` | Autoscaling must be driven by observed resource utilization, custom metrics, event queues, schedules, or cluster/node capacity, with safe missing-metric behavior. |
| DORA software delivery metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Release finality must measure change lead time, deployment frequency, failed-deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate in context. |
| HTTP 429 semantics, `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Status/429` | Queue saturation and rate limiting must return 429 and may include `Retry-After`; SDKs and CLI must use that backoff contract. |

##### `kolm.operating_proof.v3`

Each shipped journey must reduce to this proof object before node `19` can relax:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.operating_proof.v3",
  "surface_id": "gateway.capture",
  "owner": "backend-runtime",
  "journey": "route_provider_call_capture_receipt",
  "environment": "local | staging | production",
  "tiers": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
  "sli": {
    "availability_formula": "successful_requests / eligible_requests",
    "latency_histogram": "kolm_gateway_latency_ms_bucket",
    "freshness_seconds": "capture_commit_delay_seconds",
    "correctness": "structured_receipt_valid"
  },
  "slo": {
    "window": "28d rolling",
    "availability_target": 0.999,
    "latency_p95_ms": 120,

  },
  "admission": {

      "idempotency_key_required": true
    }
  },
  "capacity": {

    "autoscale_policy": "hpa_or_keda_or_host_specific",
    "missing_metric_behavior": "fail_closed_or_degrade"
  },
  "cost": {
    "meters": ["provider_tokens", "retries", "fallbacks", "cache", "teacher_calls", "gpu_minutes", "storage_bytes", "egress_bytes"],
    "unknown_cost_behavior": "block_savings_claim",
    "budget_stop": true
  },

  },

    {
      "name": "GatewayCaptureErrorBudgetBurn",
      "metric": "kolm_gateway_error_budget_burn",

    }
  ],
  "evidence": {

    "chaos_report": "reports/chaos/gateway-capture-provider-429.json",
    "prod_smoke": "reports/slo/prod-gateway-capture.json",
    "last_verified_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00.000Z"
  },
  "state": "live | stale | missing | degraded | disabled | external | not_configured | not_measured"
}
```

##### Node 19 V3 Build Redlines Found In Current Tree

1. `public/account/sla.html` still shows a "Placeholder rows until live wiring lands" state and retired `Business` SLA copy. It cannot be production-final until it is sourced from fresh SLA data and aligned to Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise.
2. `src/load-queue.js` still encodes `business` and `starter` priority lanes. These can remain only as hidden legacy aliases with tests; they cannot be customer-facing operating policy.
3. `src/cache.js` has no visible TTL, max disk size, tenant scoping, corruption quarantine, eviction metric, or purge integration. It correctly warns not to treat cache as source of truth, but final proof needs enforcement.
4. `src/output-retry.js` tracks attempts and teacher splice, but it does not itself enforce cost ceilings, circuit breakers, idempotency, or "retry masked low quality" warnings for account and receipts.
5. `src/cost-estimator.js` returns zero for unknown model price. That is safer than inventing cost, but final product must treat unknown price as `not_costed`, not as free.
6. `src/otel.js` has exporter queues and tenant hashing, but final proof needs standard GenAI mapping, cardinality caps, drop counters, collector smoke, force-flush/shutdown behavior, and alerting when telemetry itself fails.
7. Alert YAMLs reference runbooks under `https://kolm.ai/docs/runbook/...` or `/docs/runbooks/...`, but no matching docs runbook files were found in the local `docs` tree. Runbook URLs must resolve before alerts can count as proof.
8. Grafana, Datadog, and Honeycomb alert metric names are not yet proven against emitted metrics. Alerts that reference non-emitted metrics create false confidence.
9. `docs/slo-registry.json`, `docs/sli-event-inventory.json`, `docs/load-test-profiles.json`, `docs/capacity-model.json`, `docs/cost-attribution-contract.json`, and `docs/incident-playbooks.json` are absent.
10. `reports/load`, `reports/chaos`, `reports/slo`, and `reports/capacity` are absent, so local tests cannot stand in for production operating evidence.
11. Smoke scripts exist but are not unified into one operating proof packet that proves local plus production URL, authenticated account, API, object storage, telemetry, queue, cost, and rollback behavior on the same commit.
12. Several inspected comments and alert titles still show text-corruption markers such as `??` in source or tool output. Operational docs and comments are part of correctness because operators read them during incidents.

##### SLO And Admission Matrix V3

| journey | admission rule | overload rule | success SLI | failure/degrade state |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| API gateway and capture | plan/tenant quota, provider key readiness, storage readiness, request cost estimate | 429 with `Retry-After`, structured envelope, optional safe fallback, no silent drop | successful response plus capture receipt freshness | `degraded_provider`, `queue_full`, `capture_stale`, `not_configured` |
| compile and artifact verify | idempotency key, artifact size budget, storage readiness, worker capacity | queue with visible position; reject when capacity or storage is unsafe | compile completes and artifact verifies | `queued`, `capacity_limited`, `storage_unready`, `compile_failed` |
| distill and train | data eligibility, teacher availability, budget cap, GPU/cloud capacity, object storage | pause or reject before runaway spend; no unbounded teacher retries | job completes with quality, cost, and artifact receipt | `budget_stopped`, `teacher_unavailable`, `gpu_unavailable`, `quality_failed` |
| runtime inference | device/runtime compatibility, memory budget, quantization profile, fallback policy | use cheaper/degraded path or return explicit unsupported target | p95/p99 latency, memory peak, correctness gate | `device_unsupported`, `memory_limited`, `fallback_used`, `not_measured` |
| account operations | fresh auth/session, API reachability, graph/readiness freshness | show stale/missing/external state, never fake green | page renders current metrics and next actions | `stale`, `missing`, `external`, `backend_unreachable` |
| registry/docs/API | generated artifacts current, refs valid, route catalog owned | fail stale generated assets before deploy | specs and docs match shipped routes | `stale_generated`, `route_unowned`, `doc_mismatch` |
| storage/artifact lake | selected provider configured, object-size policy, write/read/delete smoke | block cloud claims and large artifacts when path cannot support them | object round trip verified and checksums match | `local_only`, `cloud_missing`, `max_object_exceeded`, `delete_failed` |

##### Unit-Economics Reconciliation V3

Savings and ROI claims are blocked unless the reconciliation ledger includes:

- provider input/output tokens by provider, model, region, and price table version;
- unknown model prices as `not_costed`, never zero-cost savings;
- retries, teacher splice, fallback, repair, moderation, embedding, rerank, and tool-call costs;
- compile/distill teacher calls, student training compute, GPU minutes, cloud job queue time, and cancellation costs;
- cache hit/miss savings with TTL, invalidation, and corruption exclusions;
- object storage bytes, request fees, egress, backup, lifecycle, and delete cost;
- human review, labeling, redaction, legal/compliance review, and support labor where used in ROI;
- plan discounts, credits, tax, overage, enterprise contract exceptions, and chargeback dimensions;
- date, currency, provider price source, and environment for each calculation;
- a claim ID that maps the calculation to homepage, pricing, ROI calculator, sales deck, docs, and account copy.

##### Operating Evidence Packet V3

The release evidence packet for node `19` must contain:

1. `docs/slo-registry.json` generated from product graph, route groups, CLI/TUI/account surfaces, jobs, storage providers, and runtime targets;
2. `docs/sli-event-inventory.json` mapping every SLO to emitted metrics/traces/logs/events;
3. `docs/capacity-model.json` with queue, worker, GPU, provider, object-store, region, tenant, and cost-cap rules;
4. `docs/load-test-profiles.json` with load profiles by product journey and plan tier;
5. `docs/cost-attribution-contract.json` with meter names, price sources, unknown-cost behavior, and claim mapping;
6. `docs/incident-playbooks.json` plus human-readable runbooks for provider outage, object-store outage, auth outage, telemetry outage, queue saturation, bad deploy, cache corruption, and GPU shortage;
7. `reports/load/*` with p50/p95/p99, throughput, error rate, capture freshness, queue wait, and account page timings;
8. `reports/chaos/*` with dependency failures, expected degradation, actual behavior, rollback, and residual risk;
9. `reports/slo/*` with local and production SLO snapshots, freshness, stale/missing states, and environment;
10. `reports/capacity/*` with max safe load, bottleneck, autoscaling signal, cap, and cost ceiling;
11. production smoke after deploy, not substituted by local smoke;
12. DORA ledger for the release: change lead time, deployment frequency, failed-deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate.

##### Node 19 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `19` remains red until:

1. every shipped product journey has `kolm.operating_proof.v3` evidence or explicit `not_measured` state with owner and ticket;
2. account, public status, CLI, API, TUI, docs, and release reports use the same live/stale/missing/degraded/disabled/external/not-configured/not-measured vocabulary;
3. `/account/sla` is free of placeholders, retired plan language, and fake-fresh metrics;
4. load queues, priority lanes, 429 envelopes, idempotency, client backoff, and tenant throttling match current commercial entitlements;
5. jobs are durable across restart, duplicate submit, cancellation, retry, orphan recovery, worker crash, and region/provider failure;
6. cache, retry, fallback, preload, and repair paths have latency, cost, quality, memory, safety, and rollback budgets;
7. telemetry maps to OpenTelemetry/GenAI semantics, controls cardinality, redacts secrets, counts drops, and survives exporter failure;
8. every alert maps to emitted metrics, owner, severity, runbook, threshold rationale, customer impact, and metamonitoring;
9. unit-economics reconcile provider costs, retries, fallbacks, cache, distill, GPU, storage, egress, labor, discounts, tax, credits, and unknown-cost blockers;
10. journey-level load reports and dependency-failure chaos reports exist for the shipping commit;
11. production smoke runs after deploy with real URL, auth, account, API, storage, telemetry, cost, queue, and critical journeys;
12. performance, uptime, savings, scale, and "run anywhere" claims are impossible to publish without dated environment-scoped evidence.

Until these are true, Kolm may have strong operational components, but it is not a 100% complete operating envelope for an enterprise AI compiler, distiller, and run-anywhere runtime.

### Persistent State, Configuration, Secrets, And Disaster Recovery Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `20`: the state plane. Kolm has multiple stores because the product spans local CLI workflows, hosted API/account workflows, capture logs, artifacts, secrets, jobs, pricing, tenancy, and generated docs. That is acceptable only if every state class has an explicit source of truth, schema/version contract, migration path, backup/restore plan, tenant boundary, secret boundary, and production readiness probe. A codebase can pass thousands of tests and still be incomplete if production state is ambiguous.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not read private `.env` values, change secrets, alter schema code, change storage drivers, or rewrite generated config while other agents are editing.

#### Current State And Configuration Inventory

| state class | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| row store | `src/store.js`, `KOLM_STORE_DRIVER`, `KOLM_DB_PATH`, `KOLM_DATA_DIR` | Kolm has a durable JSON local store and a SQLite production-capable path with WAL, transactions, backups for JSON tables, and JSON seed import into SQLite. | Production must prove the selected driver at boot, fail closed when an unsafe fallback would be used, and document which tables are source-of-truth versus cache/seed. |
| event store | `src/event-store.js`, `tests/wave411-migration-backfill.test.js`, `tests/wave411-tenant-isolation.test.js` | Capture/event logs have SQLite and JSONL paths, canonical validation, tenant filters, legacy backfill, media-column migration, and export/purge APIs. | There is no single migration ledger covering every event-schema version, rollback, PITR, compaction, replay, and production restore drill. |
| project-local state | `src/kolm-state.js`, `tests/wave232-kolm-state.test.js` | CLI project checkpoints, imports, and merges can live in `.kolm-state` with artifact hashes and git-friendly files. | The state directory needs schema versioning, corruption repair, privacy classification, backup guidance, and cleanup rules for sensitive imports. |
| object and artifact storage | `src/object-storage.js`, `src/r2.js`, `tests/wave558-object-storage-cloud-contract.test.js` | Local artifacts, Cloudflare R2 REST, R2 S3-compatible, AWS S3, generic S3, and Supabase S3 are discoverable with readiness and smoke paths. | Artifact storage needs lifecycle, versioning/immutability, cross-provider restore, checksum reconciliation, object inventory, delete propagation, and provider-specific caveats. |
| secrets | `src/secrets-vault.js`, `.env.example`, secret-ref helpers | Local AES-256-GCM vault records and external references for AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, 1Password, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, Doppler, and Infisical exist. | Local vault is not a production secret manager. Production needs external manager resolution, rotation, least privilege, audit logging, no persistent secret caching, and break-glass procedures. |
| environment config | `.env.example`, `vercel.json`, `railway.toml`, `Dockerfile`, readiness scripts | Deploy configuration and env names are documented in code and example files. | `.env.example` still carries some stale commercial copy and mojibake-like comment artifacts; config needs a generated schema, required/optional classification, owner, default, validation, and per-host parity. |
| tenant and auth state | `src/auth.js`, `src/sessions.js`, `src/oauth.js`, tenant-scope tests | API keys, sessions, OAuth, anonymous tenants, and tenant-fenced data paths are represented. | Tenant identifiers, session revocation, key rotation, SSO/SCIM linkage, deleted-account residue, and cross-store tenant invariants need one state model. |
| retention and purge | `src/event-store.js`, `tests/wave444-lake-storage-retention.test.js`, privacy/data docs | Some purge and retention behavior exists for event/lake surfaces. | Purge must prove propagation across event store, row store, artifact metadata, object storage, exports, cache, account pages, backups, and derived datasets. |
| generated docs and specs | `public/openapi.json`, `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/product-graph.json`, generated verify scripts | Generated public artifacts can be regenerated and checked. | Generated files need ownership, provenance, build order, stale detection, and "do not edit by hand" policy across all generated state. |
| test and fixture state | `tests/*`, `test/fixtures/*`, `.tmp/*` | Tests use temp dirs and fixtures for many stores. | Fixture state must be classified as synthetic, safe-to-ship, secret-free, versioned, and not accidentally used as production seed data. |

#### Primary Standards Imported Into Node 20

| source | URL | spec implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| The Twelve-Factor App config principle | `https://www.12factor.net/config` | Deploy-specific config belongs in environment variables or external config systems; code must be open-source-safe without credentials. |
| SQLite WAL documentation | `https://sqlite.org/wal.html` | WAL mode changes backup/restore requirements; copying only the main database file is not sufficient when WAL data may be separate. |
| PostgreSQL continuous archiving and PITR | `https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/continuous-archiving.html` | Production-grade database state needs base backups plus WAL/archive strategy when point-in-time recovery is expected. |
| PostgreSQL reliability and WAL | `https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/wal.html` | Durable systems must reason about write-ahead logs, crash recovery, checkpointing, and recovery targets. |
| AWS Secrets Manager best practices | `https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/best-practices.html` | Secrets should be centrally stored, encrypted, rotated, least-privileged, replicated where needed, monitored, and retrieved without leaking values. |
| OWASP Secrets Management Cheat Sheet | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Secrets_Management_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Secret storage, provisioning, auditing, rotation, and lifecycle must be explicit; hardcoded secrets and unmanaged config files are release blockers. |
| Cloudflare R2 durability docs | `https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/durability/` | Object storage claims need provider-specific durability, consistency, lifecycle, and deletion semantics, not generic "S3" wording. |
| Supabase S3 compatibility docs | `https://supabase.com/docs/guides/storage/s3/compatibility` | S3-compatible providers differ; unsupported features such as versioning or object lock must be reflected in readiness and backup plans. |
| NIST SP 800-53 contingency controls | `https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-53/rev-5/final` | Backup, restore, contingency planning, and recovery testing are security controls, not optional ops polish. |

#### State Source-Of-Truth Model

Kolm needs a canonical source-of-truth registry for every persistent class:

```json
{
  "state_id": "capture_events",
  "owner": "backend-data-plane",
  "primary_store": "event-store-sqlite",
  "local_fallback": "jsonl",
  "production_driver": "sqlite-or-managed-postgres",
  "schema_version": "event-schema/w411+media",
  "tenant_key": "tenant_id",
  "retention_policy": "tenant-plan-and-data-policy",
  "backup_policy": {
    "method": "sqlite-online-backup-or-wal-aware-snapshot",
    "rpo_minutes": 15,
    "rto_minutes": 60,
    "restore_test_cadence": "monthly"
  },
  "migration_policy": {
    "forward_only": true,
    "backfill_function": "backfillLegacy",
    "rollback": "restore-from-backup-or-replay-export"
  },
  "readiness_probe": "GET /v1/storage/object-readiness plus event-store smoke",
  "secret_values_included": false
}
```

This registry must be used by `/ready`, product graph, account admin, CLI, docs, release verification, backup tooling, and production smoke. If a state class has no registry row, it is not production-ready.

#### Redlines Found In Current Tree

1. `.env`, `.env.local`, and `.env.cloudflare` exist in the worktree. This branch did not read their values, but the build must have a secret-leak gate proving these files are ignored, scrubbed from commits, and never printed by diagnostics.
2. `.env.example` still contains legacy pricing/payment-link names and mojibake-like comment artifacts. Config examples are product surfaces; they must match the current Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise taxonomy and ship clean text.
3. `src/store.js` can fall back to JSON in production-like environments when `node:sqlite` is unavailable. That may preserve local operation, but production readiness must fail closed unless an explicit temporary single-node JSON allowance is set and surfaced.
4. `src/event-store.js` and `src/store.js` are separate state engines. That separation is reasonable, but there is no unified state registry saying which engine owns which data class, how they are backed up, and how tenant deletion crosses both.
5. SQLite WAL use means backup instructions must be WAL-aware. A naive file copy is not enough proof of a recoverable production backup.
6. JSON and JSONL fallbacks are valuable for local/air-gapped workflows, but final product copy must not imply they are horizontally scalable production stores.
7. `src/secrets-vault.js` creates local encrypted secrets with a local key file. That is useful for development and air-gapped modes, but it is not equivalent to AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, GCP Secret Manager, Azure Key Vault, or another audited production secret manager.
8. Object storage readiness reports configured providers, but the completion spec needs backup, lifecycle, object inventory, retention, versioning/object-lock capability, and restore proof per provider.
9. Backfills exist in targeted tests, especially W411. The master state spec needs a complete migration ledger across all schemas, tables, generated artifacts, and compatibility shims.
10. Purge/delete flows must cross caches, JSON row store, event store, object storage, exported files, generated artifacts, datasets, backups, and account UI. A narrow purge test does not prove full erasure.
11. Generated public state (`openapi.json`, `api-routes.json`, `product-graph.json`, readiness ledgers) needs build-order locking. Earlier concurrent generation already showed races can corrupt assumptions if commands run in parallel.
12. State comments in some files still show text corruption markers. Even comments matter when they document state boundaries, backup behavior, or security assumptions.

#### Completion Specification For Node 20

| area | done means | proof required |
|---|---|---|
| state inventory | Every persistent file, table, object prefix, cache, generated artifact, fixture, env file, and secret source has an owner and classification. | `docs/state-registry.json`, generated coverage report, repo scan, and code owner mapping. |
| backend selection | Production boot refuses unsafe storage fallbacks unless explicitly allowed and disclosed. | `/ready` proof, env schema validation, production smoke, and tests for bad/missing drivers. |
| schemas and migrations | Every table/object/event schema has version, migration, backfill, rollback/restore policy, and fixture proof. | `docs/state-migrations.json`, migration verifier, legacy fixture suite. |
| tenant isolation | Every state class carries or derives tenant scope and rejects cross-tenant reads/writes by default. | Cross-store tenant isolation tests and route/CLI/account probes. |
| secrets | Secrets are externalized, rotated, scoped, audited, and never logged or embedded in artifacts. | Secret scanner, external manager smoke, rotation drill, no-secret diagnostics tests. |
| config schema | Every env var has type, owner, default, requiredness, host scope, secret flag, readiness impact, and docs link. | Generated `.env.example`, config verifier, deploy-host parity report. |
| backup and restore | Each durable state class has RPO/RTO, backup method, retention, encryption, restore drill, and checksum reconciliation. | Restore report from clean environment, backup inventory, PITR or export replay proof. |
| object lifecycle | Artifact/object stores have lifecycle, delete/restore behavior, version/immutability capability, checksum, and provider caveats. | Provider matrix and smoke tests for put/read/head/delete/list/restore where supported. |
| purge and retention | Delete/forget/purge propagates through all state classes and derived artifacts. | End-to-end purge certificate and negative lookup tests across stores. |
| generated state | Generated specs cannot drift, race, or be hand-edited without detection. | Build-order script, stale detection, checksum/provenance header, CI lock. |
| disaster recovery | A new operator can restore a production-like environment from backups plus config and verify it. | DR runbook, timed game-day, production smoke report, and incident postmortem template. |
