| W-STATE-020 | P1 | cross-node | Add state claim-scope gate. | Public/product copy cannot claim enterprise durability, deletion, residency, backup, or secret safety without state-registry proof. |

#### Redline To Relax For Node 20

Node `20` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from code and current artifacts:

- What is the source of truth for every piece of persistent state?
- Which state classes are local-only, production-ready, generated, cached, fixture-only, secret-bearing, or external?
- Which schema version is live, how was it migrated, and how is it restored?
- What happens if the JSON store, SQLite file, WAL file, event log, object bucket, cache, local vault, env var, or generated spec is missing or corrupt?
- How does a tenant deletion propagate through every store, artifact, cache, export, backup, and account page?
- Can production be restored without the developer workstation?
- Can secrets rotate without leaking, breaking receipts unexpectedly, or requiring code changes?
- Do `.env.example`, deploy config, `/ready`, CLI doctor, docs, and account UI agree on what is required?

The relax condition for node `20` is not "there is a store module" or "the tests use temp dirs." It is: every state path is classified, versioned, tenant-scoped, migratable, recoverable, secret-safe, backed up, restore-tested, and visible through a readiness surface that never exposes secret values.

#### Node 20 V2 Persistent State, Configuration, Secrets, And DR Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `20` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not read private environment values, change storage drivers, rewrite `.env` files, touch secrets, alter generated specs, or modify deploy config while other agents are editing.

The completion bar for this node is higher than "the server starts." Kolm is not complete until every durable row, event, object, cache, generated artifact, local project file, environment variable, secret reference, migration, backup, and restore path has an owner, state class, schema/version, tenant boundary, recovery policy, readiness probe, and no-secret evidence path.

##### Node 20 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| primary state modules | `src/store.js`: 24,709 bytes, 689 lines; `src/event-store.js`: 17,927 bytes, 433 lines; `src/kolm-state.js`: 12,580 bytes, 313 lines. | Kolm has real state engines for local rows, event logs, and project-local CLI state. The missing piece is one registry that names which engine owns each class of truth. |
| object storage modules | `src/object-storage.js`: 21,421 bytes, 551 lines; `src/r2.js`: 5,495 bytes, 134 lines. | Local, R2, S3-compatible, AWS S3, generic S3, and Supabase-style object paths are implemented enough to probe. The missing layer is lifecycle, inventory, restore, versioning/immutability capability, delete propagation, and provider caveat proof. |
| secret module | `src/secrets-vault.js`: 8,781 bytes, 271 lines. | A local vault exists and should remain useful for developer/air-gapped modes. It must not be marketed as a production secret manager without external manager resolution, audit, rotation, and break-glass proof. |
| deploy and env surfaces | `.env.example`: 6,703 bytes, 144 lines, 7 marker hits; `vercel.json`: 57,184 bytes, 2,390 lines, 2 marker hits; `server.js`: 24,877 bytes, 478 lines, 7 marker hits. | Config is broad and deploy-specific. Completion requires an env/config registry that links requiredness, defaults, host scope, secret flags, readiness impact, and owner. |
| tenant state | `src/tenant-holdout.js`: 11,982 bytes, 281 lines; many tenant-scope tests exist. | Tenant isolation is a serious product concern already. Completion requires cross-store invariants for row store, event store, jobs, objects, sessions, datasets, account views, exports, backups, and derived artifacts. |
| missing registries | `docs/state-contract.json`, `docs/config-registry.json`, `docs/secret-inventory.json`, `docs/env-var-registry.json`, `docs/migration-ledger.json`, `docs/backup-restore-runbook.md`, `docs/disaster-recovery-runbook.md`, `docs/tenant-state-boundary.json` are absent. | State-plane truth is currently distributed through code and tests. A 100% build needs generated, reviewed, source-controlled contracts. |
| missing restore reports | `reports/restore`, `reports/dr`, and `reports/migration` are absent. | Backup and restore are not proven just because storage code exists. Restore drills must produce artifacts. |
| relevant tests | 35 state-relevant tests were identified, including `store.test.js`, W309 config show, W409 event store, W411 migration/backfill, W411 tenant isolation, W444 retention, W492 ready gate, W502 storage route honesty, W558 object storage, and tenant-scope waves. | There is a useful test base. It needs a registry-driven end-to-end state readiness and restore gate. |
| scripts | No matching state/config/backup/restore/migration scripts were found by the targeted script-name scan. | Release operations need explicit command paths for state inventory, migration, backup, restore, secret check, and DR evidence. |

##### Node 20 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Twelve-Factor config | `https://www.12factor.net/config` | Deploy-varying config belongs outside code and should be granular, environment-addressed, and open-source-safe without credentials. |
| SQLite WAL | `https://www.sqlite.org/wal.html` | WAL mode creates extra files and checkpointing rules; backup and restore must be WAL-aware and host/filesystem aware. |
| PostgreSQL PITR | `https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/continuous-archiving.html` | If managed Postgres is used, point-in-time recovery requires base backups plus WAL/archive policy and restore targets. |
| OWASP Secrets Management | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Secrets_Management_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Secret lifecycle must cover creation, storage, access, rotation, auditing, revocation, and incident response. |
| AWS Secrets Manager best practices | `https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/best-practices.html` | Production secrets should use least privilege, encryption, monitoring, caching controls, rotation, and replication where appropriate. |
| Cloudflare R2 data location | `https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/data-location/` | Object storage readiness must state region/data-location behavior and jurisdiction caveats, not only "configured." |
| Supabase S3 compatibility | `https://supabase.com/docs/guides/storage/s3/compatibility` | S3-compatible storage is not identical to AWS S3. Unsupported API features must be reflected in the provider matrix. |
| NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final` | Contingency planning, backups, recovery, access control, and audit are security controls and need evidence. |

##### Node 20 V2 State Registry Contract

Every state class must have a row shaped like this:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-state-registry-v2",
  "state_id": "capture_events",
  "owner": "backend-data-plane",
  "classification": "durable_customer_state",
  "primary_store": "event-store",
  "drivers": ["sqlite", "jsonl-local"],
  "production_allowed_drivers": ["sqlite", "managed-postgres"],
  "local_allowed_drivers": ["sqlite", "jsonl-local"],
  "schema_version": "capture-event-v411",
  "tenant_scope": {
    "field": "tenant_id",
    "required": true,
    "anonymous_tenant_policy": "scoped_ephemeral"
  },
  "retention": {
    "policy_id": "tenant-plan-data-retention",
    "purge_propagates_to": ["event_store", "row_store", "object_storage", "cache", "exports", "derived_datasets"]
  },
  "migration": {
    "ledger": "docs/migration-ledger.json",
    "forward_only": true,
    "legacy_fixture_required": true,
    "rollback_policy": "restore_or_replay"
  },
  "backup": {
    "rpo_minutes": 15,
    "rto_minutes": 60,
    "method": "wal_aware_snapshot_or_managed_pitr",
    "encryption": "required",
    "restore_test_cadence": "monthly"
  },
  "secrets": {
    "contains_secret_values": false,
    "secret_refs_allowed": true,
    "secret_values_included_in_readiness": false
  },
  "readiness": {
    "probe": "state.capture_events",
    "states": ["ready", "degraded", "not_configured", "unsafe_fallback", "migration_required", "restore_unproven"],
    "account_visible": true,
    "cli_visible": true
  },
  "evidence": {
    "source_paths": ["src/event-store.js"],
    "tests": ["tests/wave409a-canonical-event-store.test.js", "tests/wave411-migration-backfill.test.js"],
    "latest_restore_report": null
  }
}
```

No durable feature is complete until its state registry row is validated and linked to account, CLI, API, docs, and release evidence.

##### Node 20 V2 Config And Secret Inventory Contract

Every environment variable or external secret reference must be represented without revealing values:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-config-secret-inventory-v2",
  "key": "KOLM_STORE_DRIVER",
  "kind": "config",
  "owner": "backend-runtime",
  "type": "enum",
  "allowed_values": ["json", "sqlite", "managed-postgres"],
  "default": "json",
  "production_required": true,
  "local_required": false,
  "secret": false,
  "hosts": ["local", "vercel", "railway", "docker", "byoc"],
  "readiness_impact": "unsafe_fallback_if_json_in_production",
  "docs": [".env.example", "docs/deployment.md"],
  "validated_by": ["ready_gate", "cli_doctor", "release_verify"],
  "value_included": false
}
```

Secret entries follow the same shape but must set `secret: true`, must not define plaintext defaults, and must state the external manager or local-vault mode. Readiness output must include missing groups and configured providers, never raw values.

##### Node 20 V2 State Readiness Vocabulary

| state | meaning | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| `ready` | Store/config/secret state is configured, probed, current, and restore evidence is not stale. | May support production claims for that state class. |
| `degraded` | State works with reduced durability, performance, provider capability, or recovery guarantee. | Must show customer/operator impact and next action. |
| `not_configured` | Required store, object provider, secret manager, or env var is missing. | Cannot be marketed as configured or production-ready. |
| `unsafe_fallback` | Local fallback is active where production requires durable/external state. | Production boot should fail closed unless temporary allowance is explicit and visible. |
| `migration_required` | Code expects a newer schema than the stored state. | Writes must stop or enter maintenance mode until migration is complete. |
| `restore_unproven` | Backup exists or is assumed, but no recent restore drill proves it. | Do not claim recoverability, DR, or enterprise durability. |
| `secret_rotation_due` | Secret exists but rotation freshness is outside policy. | Account/admin should show the impacted integration without revealing the value. |
| `purge_pending` | Delete/forget operation has not propagated to all state classes. | Must block "deleted" certification until the purge certificate completes. |
| `external_blocked` | Recovery, secret, storage, or region proof depends on a third-party configuration. | Must map to closeout/readiness and not be hidden behind green UI. |

##### Node 20 V2 Disaster Recovery Drill Matrix

| drill | failure simulated | required restore proof |
|---|---|---|
| row-store restore | main row store missing, corrupt, or at old schema | restore from backup, run migrations, verify checksums, prove tenant isolation. |
| event-store replay | event log partially missing or stale | replay from export/WAL/archive, verify capture counts and retention state. |
| object-store restore | artifact bucket unavailable or object corrupted | restore/copy object, verify checksum, receipt, metadata, and delete behavior. |
| local project recovery | `.kolm-state` corrupted or absent | rebuild from artifact receipts, imports, and manifests without leaking sensitive source data. |
| secret manager outage | external manager unavailable | fail closed for production secrets, use break-glass policy where approved, no value logging. |
| secret rotation | provider/API/Stripe/object-storage key rotation | rotate without downtime, confirm old key rejection, update audit log, avoid artifact invalidation unless expected. |
| config drift | Vercel/Railway/Docker/local env mismatch | readiness reports exact missing groups and deploy host; no fake healthy state. |
| migration failure | migration stops halfway | rollback/restore policy works and writes cannot proceed on mixed unknown schema. |
| tenant deletion | tenant requests deletion/forget | delete rows, events, jobs, sessions, objects, caches, exports, derived datasets; retain only scoped legal/audit records. |
| generated state race | API ref/OpenAPI/product graph generated in wrong order or concurrently | build-order lock prevents stale/corrupt artifacts and reports the correct regeneration sequence. |

| W-STATE-V2-014 | P0 | 20.4 | Create `docs/tenant-state-boundary.json`. | Every state class defines tenant key, anonymous policy, export behavior, deletion behavior, legal hold behavior, and cross-tenant denial tests. |
| W-STATE-V2-015 | P0 | 20.4 | Add account deletion residue scanner. | Deleted tenant cannot access rows, events, artifacts, jobs, sessions, caches, exports, derived datasets, or account panels outside scoped legal/audit holds. |
| W-STATE-V2-016 | P0 | 20.5 | Create backup/restore runbook and command path. | A clean environment restore proves row store, event store, object artifacts, generated state, config registry, and readiness output. |
| W-STATE-V2-017 | P1 | 20.5 | Add object lifecycle/provider matrix. | R2, AWS S3, generic S3, Supabase S3, and local artifacts list versioning, object lock, lifecycle, data location, delete, restore, max-object, and unsupported capabilities. |
| W-STATE-V2-018 | P1 | 20.5 | Add artifact checksum reconciliation. | Object storage, receipt metadata, artifact dependency graph, account registry, and local cache agree on checksums or fail closed. |
| W-STATE-V2-019 | P0 | 20.6 | Add state readiness API/CLI/TUI/account surface. | Operators see driver, schema, migration status, backup freshness, object provider, secret mode, tenant boundary, missing proof, and no secret values. |
| W-STATE-V2-020 | P1 | 20.6 | Add generated-state build-order lock. | API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness ledgers, SDK version, and docs cannot be generated concurrently into stale/corrupt state. |
| W-STATE-V2-021 | P1 | 20.6 | Add generated artifact provenance headers. | Generated files state source, command, input checksum, timestamp policy, and do-not-edit guidance where safe. |
| W-STATE-V2-022 | P1 | 20.7 | Add DR drill report template. | Restore, migration failure, secret outage, object-store outage, config drift, tenant deletion, and generated-state race drills produce evidence. |
| W-STATE-V2-023 | P0 | cross-node | Add state and secret claim-scope gate. | Public/account copy cannot claim durability, deletion, residency, backup, PITR, secret safety, or enterprise readiness without registry and restore evidence. |
| W-STATE-V2-024 | P0 | 20.8 | Add Node 20 completion certificate. | Node `20` is complete only when state registry, config registry, secret inventory, migration ledger, backup/restore proof, DR drills, tenant boundaries, object lifecycle, generated-state ordering, readiness surfaces, and claim gates agree. |

##### Node 20 V2 Relax Redline

Node `20` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every durable, generated, cached, local, fixture, exported, secret-bearing, and external state class is registered;
2. production boot cannot silently fall back to unsafe local state;
3. config examples are generated from a registry and contain no stale plan names, mojibake, placeholders, or secret values;
4. every secret has owner, manager, rotation policy, audit path, break-glass path, and no plaintext default;
5. row store, event store, object storage, project state, caches, exports, jobs, sessions, datasets, and generated artifacts have source-of-truth boundaries;
6. migrations and backfills are ledgered, fixture-tested, and restore-aware;
7. backups are WAL/PITR/object-lifecycle aware and have recent restore reports;
8. tenant deletion, retention, legal hold, purge, and account residue are proven across all state classes;
9. generated-state commands have build-order locking and cannot race into corrupt docs/specs;
10. account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, `/ready`, release verification, and production smoke show the same secret-safe state readiness truth.

If any item fails, Kolm can still run, but it does not yet have a 100% complete state plane for enterprise production.

#### Node 20 V3 State-Plane Proof Mesh, Secret-Safe Config, And Disaster Recovery Contract

This V3 extension maintains master spec tree node `20`; it does not create a new branch and it does not move state ownership into a frontend, research, or marketing node. This is a documentation/spec edit only. It intentionally did not read private `.env` values, did not rewrite deploy config, did not normalize generated API artifacts, and did not touch product code while other agents are editing.

The completion rule for node `20` is now stricter: Kolm is not state-complete until every durable row, append log, object, cache, local project file, generated artifact, environment variable, secret reference, migration, backup, restore, delete residue, and DR run has a machine-readable owner and a no-secret proof path. The system may still use local SQLite, JSON, JSONL, local artifacts, R2, S3-compatible buckets, Supabase S3, or future managed Postgres, but production copy and readiness must say exactly which mode is configured, which mode is local-only, which mode is degraded, and which proof is missing.

##### Node 20 V3 Fresh Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| row store | `src/store.js`: 24,709 bytes, 632 observed lines; supports `json` and `sqlite`, uses `KOLM_DATA_DIR`, `KOLM_STORE_DRIVER`, `KOLM_DB_PATH`, defaults production-like mode toward SQLite, and uses SQLite WAL when available. | Real persistence exists. Completion still needs a state registry, production fail-closed policy, migration ledger, WAL-aware backup proof, and explicit managed-DB decision record. |
| event store | `src/event-store.js`: 17,927 bytes, 411 observed lines; stores events in SQLite under `~/.kolm/events/events.sqlite`, supports JSONL fallback through `KOLM_EVENT_STORE_DRIVER=jsonl`, and uses tenant filters on reads. | Event capture is durable enough for local and hosted proof, but replay, retention, tenant purge, schema migration, and restore reports need first-class evidence. |
| project state | `src/kolm-state.js`: 12,580 bytes, 281 observed lines. | CLI/project-local state must be classified separately from customer production truth so local developer files never become hidden production dependencies. |
| object storage | `src/object-storage.js`: 21,421 bytes, 500 observed lines; `src/r2.js`: 5,495 bytes, 121 observed lines; providers include local artifacts, Cloudflare R2 REST, Cloudflare R2 S3-compatible, AWS S3, generic S3, and Supabase S3; readiness reports `secret_values_included:false`. | Provider probing is real. Completion still needs object lifecycle, versioning, immutability, checksum, delete, restore, residency, and max-object-size caveats in one provider matrix. |
| secret handling | `src/secrets-vault.js`: 8,781 bytes, 245 observed lines; `.env.example` exposes secret placeholders such as receipt, Stripe, OAuth, bridge, and object-store/provider credentials. | Local vault and placeholders are not enough for enterprise claims. Completion requires a generated secret inventory, no-secret diagnostics, external manager smoke, rotation drills, and break-glass runbooks. |
| auth/session state | `src/auth.js`: 28,692 bytes, 579 observed lines; `src/sessions.js`: 7,509 bytes, 164 observed lines; `src/oauth.js`: 7,775 bytes, 179 observed lines. | Identity state must be tied into tenant purge, session revocation, OAuth secret rotation, audit trails, and backup/restore tests. |
| config and deploy shape | `.env.example`: 6,703 bytes, 127 observed lines; `vercel.json`: 57,184 bytes, 2,389 observed lines; `railway.toml`: 184 bytes, 8 observed lines; `Dockerfile`: 282 bytes, 9 observed lines. | Config is broad and deploy-specific. Completion requires one env/config registry covering local, Vercel, Railway, Docker, BYOC, cloud providers, pricing plan names, and readiness impact. |
| missing registries | `docs/state-registry.json`, `docs/state-migrations.json`, `docs/config-schema.json`, `docs/secret-inventory.json`, `docs/backup-restore-plan.json`, `docs/object-lifecycle-matrix.json`, `docs/dr-runbook.md`, `reports/restore`, `reports/dr`, and `reports/state` were not present in the targeted pass. | State truth is still distributed through code and tests. A 100% build needs generated and reviewed contracts, not tribal knowledge. |
| generated artifacts | `public/openapi.json`: 579,772 bytes, 18,528 observed lines; `public/docs/api-routes.json`: 296,285 bytes, 8,408 observed lines; `public/product-graph.json`: 141,724 bytes, 4,790 observed lines; `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`: 12,897 bytes, one-line generated JSON. | Generated state is now part of the state plane. Build order, stale detection, corruption detection, and provenance must be owned by node `20` and cross-linked to node `31`. |
| env/secret token surface | A token-oriented scan over `src`, `scripts`, `docs`, `public`, `.github`, and `tools` observed 735 unique uppercase/env-like tokens, including true secret/config keys and many false positives. | Completion requires a scanner that classifies true env vars, public constants, metric names, generated labels, false positives, secret keys, and host-specific values without printing real values. |

##### Node 20 V3 External Baselines

| baseline | source | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Twelve-Factor config | `https://www.12factor.net/config` | Deploy-varying config must live outside code as granular environment controls, and the repo should be open-source-safe without credentials. |
| SQLite WAL | `https://sqlite.org/wal.html` | WAL mode changes backup, checkpoint, host/filesystem, concurrency, and durability rules; restore tests must include WAL and checkpoint behavior. |
| SQLite Backup API | `https://sqlite.org/backup.html` | SQLite backups should use a safe backup path rather than ad hoc file copying while a database is live. |
| PostgreSQL PITR | `https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/continuous-archiving.html` | Managed Postgres readiness must define base backups, WAL/archive retention, point-in-time targets, and restore rehearsal evidence. |
| OWASP Secrets Management | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Secrets_Management_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Secret lifecycle must include creation, storage, access control, rotation, monitoring, revocation, and incident response. |
| AWS Secrets Manager best practices | `https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/best-practices.html` | Production secrets should use encryption, least privilege, monitoring, caching discipline, rotation, and replication where applicable. |
| Docker build secrets | `https://docs.docker.com/build/building/secrets/` | Build-time secrets must not be baked into image layers or committed build inputs. |
| Cloudflare R2 durability | `https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/durability/` | R2 readiness must state durability assumptions and provider caveats, not only "bucket configured." |
| Cloudflare R2 data location | `https://developers.cloudflare.com/r2/reference/data-location/` | Data residency claims need bucket/data-location proof and customer-visible region behavior. |
| Supabase S3 compatibility | `https://supabase.com/docs/guides/storage/s3/compatibility` | S3-compatible storage must be tested feature by feature because compatibility does not imply every AWS S3 capability. |
| NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final` | Backup, contingency planning, access control, audit, and recovery controls need current evidence, not static copy. |

##### kolm.state_plane_contract.v3

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.state_plane_contract.v3",
  "state_plane": {
    "registry_required": true,
    "no_unowned_state": true,
    "source_of_truth_required": true,
    "tenant_scope_required_for_customer_state": true,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "production_unsafe_fallback_allowed": false
  },
  "state_class": {
    "id": "capture_events",
    "node": 20,
    "owner": "backend-state-plane",
    "kind": "durable_customer_event_log",
    "primary_store": "event-store",
    "allowed_drivers": ["sqlite", "jsonl-local"],
    "production_allowed_drivers": ["sqlite", "managed-postgres"],
    "local_allowed_drivers": ["sqlite", "jsonl-local"],
    "source_paths": ["src/event-store.js"],
    "schema_version": "required",
    "migration_ledger": "docs/state-migrations.json",
    "tenant_scope": {
      "field": "tenant_id",
      "required": true,
      "anonymous_scope": "ephemeral_or_explicit"
    },
    "retention": {
      "policy_id": "tenant-plan-retention",
      "purge_certificate_required": true,
      "propagates_to": ["row_store", "event_store", "object_storage", "cache", "exports", "derived_data", "backups"]
    },
    "backup": {
      "method": "wal_aware_snapshot_or_managed_pitr",
      "encrypted": true,
      "restore_report_required": true,
      "rpo_minutes": "declared_per_plan",
      "rto_minutes": "declared_per_plan"
    },
    "readiness": {
      "api": "/v1/state/readiness",
      "cli": "kolm state readiness --json",
      "tui": "state",
      "account": "/account/storage",
      "states": ["ready", "degraded", "not_configured", "unsafe_fallback", "migration_required", "restore_unproven", "purge_pending", "secret_rotation_due"]
    },
    "evidence": {
      "registry": "docs/state-registry.json",
      "restore_reports": "reports/restore",
      "dr_reports": "reports/dr",
      "secret_inventory": "docs/secret-inventory.json",
      "object_lifecycle": "docs/object-lifecycle-matrix.json"
    }
  }
}
```

##### State Class Closure Matrix V3

| state class | must own | completion proof |
|---|---|---|
| row store | Accounts, tenants, billing rows, plans, captures, jobs, labels, receipts, registry rows, governance rows. | Registry rows, schema versions, migration tests, tenant-boundary tests, production driver fail-closed gate, and restore report. |
| event store | Capture events, audit events, readiness events, privacy events, eval events, route events, state transition events. | Replay test, retention clock, append integrity, tenant filtering, purge propagation, corruption quarantine, and checkpoint/backup evidence. |
| project-local state | `.kolm-state`, local CLI cache, artifact import state, local receipts, local config. | Local-only classification, no production dependency, corruption recovery path, and redaction/no-secret checks. |
| object storage | `.kolm` artifacts, manifests, compiled bundles, benchmark outputs, evidence exports, logs, attachments, generated bundles. | Provider matrix, checksum reconciliation, versioning/immutability capability, max object size, delete behavior, restore drill, and residency caveats. |
| cache and ephemeral state | In-memory cache, preloads, compute decisions, local temporary files, browser storage, CDN/cacheable public files. | Cache classification, invalidation policy, no primary truth, no secret values, TTL, purge behavior, and rebuild path. |
| generated public artifacts | OpenAPI, API route docs, product graph, readiness closeout, codegraph, generated SDK metadata, screenshots/reports. | Build-order lock, checksum/provenance header, stale detection, non-concurrent generation rule, and source inputs listed. |
| env and config | `KOLM_*`, provider keys, Stripe, OAuth, storage, telemetry, deployment host, pricing plan links, feature flags. | Generated config schema, `.env.example` sync, Vercel/Railway/Docker/BYOC host scope, readiness impact, requiredness, and no stale plan taxonomy. |
| secrets | Provider API keys, receipt signing secrets, webhook secrets, OAuth secrets, storage credentials, bridge tokens, DB credentials. | Secret inventory, no plaintext defaults, no-value scanner, external manager smoke, rotation reports, access owners, and incident runbook. |
| auth and sessions | Sessions, OAuth grants, device/session tokens, invite tokens, SSO/SCIM references, admin audit. | Revocation tests, tenant isolation, deletion propagation, backup/restore behavior, expiry policy, and no secret/session logging. |
| backups and DR | Row backups, event archives, object replicas, config export, secret references, restore reports, DR drills. | Clean-environment restore, RPO/RTO proof, dependency graph, rollback plan, drill transcript, and production-smoke after restore. |
| fixtures and test data | Synthetic PII/PHI, benchmark fixtures, generated sample artifacts, demos, screenshots. | Fixture classification, synthetic/license basis, no customer data, deterministic rebuild, and deletion from production paths. |

##### Secret-Safe Config And Inventory Rules V3

- Every environment variable, config token, secret reference, provider credential, webhook secret, signing key, and deployment flag must appear in `docs/config-schema.json` or `docs/secret-inventory.json` with owner, host scope, requiredness, type, default policy, readiness impact, and source paths.
- Secret entries must never include plaintext defaults, example live-looking values, generated previews, or diagnostic excerpts. Readiness may report configured or missing groups, but `secret_values_included` must stay false.
- The scanner must classify uppercase token hits into `env_var`, `secret_ref`, `public_constant`, `metric_label`, `generated_label`, `fixture_label`, `documentation_example`, or `false_positive`. A raw count alone is not useful enough for release.
- `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.cloudflare`, and any host-specific env file may be detected as present without reading values. Release should fail if a private env file is tracked, copied into public output, packed into an image, or referenced by docs as a required artifact.
- `.env.example`, deployment config, CLI doctor, `/ready`, `/v1/state/readiness`, account setup, docs, and tests must all agree on required names and current plan taxonomy. Legacy `Starter`, `Business`, or ambiguous `ENT` naming must be either removed or explicitly marked legacy compatibility.
- Build-time secrets must use build secret mechanisms and must not be baked into Docker layers, package tarballs, generated pages, screenshots, or source maps.
- External secret-manager support must be probed without reading values into logs. The proof artifact should name provider, reference path, access mode, rotation status, and missing permissions only.
- Rotation drills must cover provider API keys, receipt secrets, Stripe webhooks, OAuth clients, storage credentials, bridge tokens, and future DB credentials. Each drill must prove old key rejection, new key acceptance, audit logging, and expected artifact-signature behavior.

##### Backup, Restore, And DR Flow V3

1. Generate `docs/state-registry.json`, `docs/state-migrations.json`, `docs/config-schema.json`, `docs/secret-inventory.json`, `docs/object-lifecycle-matrix.json`, and `docs/backup-restore-plan.json`.
2. Create a clean restore environment that has no dependency on a developer workstation, private `.env` files, local `.kolm-state`, or generated files from a previous build.
3. Snapshot or export the row store using the driver-safe method: SQLite backup API/WAL-aware copy, managed Postgres base backup plus WAL/PITR, or an explicitly local-only JSON export.
4. Snapshot or replay the event store, including WAL/checkpoint state or JSONL append-log integrity, and verify event counts by tenant and event class.
5. Reconcile object storage by provider and prefix: list, head, checksum, manifest, receipt, delete marker, version/lifecycle caveat, and max object size.
6. Restore config references by name only, then resolve secrets from the approved manager or local-vault mode without printing values.
7. Run migrations in a recorded order. Writes remain blocked if schema state is mixed, unknown, or partially migrated.
8. Rebuild generated artifacts in the canonical order owned by node `31`, then write provenance and checksum evidence owned by node `20`.
9. Run tenant isolation, purge residue, auth/session revocation, object checksum, route smoke, product graph, API docs, account readiness, CLI readiness, and TUI readiness checks.
10. Emit `reports/restore/<timestamp>.json` and `reports/dr/<timestamp>.json` with RPO, RTO, source inputs, commands, result hashes, missing external proof, and `secret_values_included:false`.

##### Node 20 V3 Work Ticket Ledger

| ticket | priority | node | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| W-STATE-V3-001 | P0 | 20.1 | Generate `docs/state-registry.json`. | Every row store, event log, object prefix, cache, generated file, fixture, local project file, backup, export, and derived artifact has owner, class, source of truth, schema, tenant scope, retention, backup, and readiness state. |
| W-STATE-V3-002 | P0 | 20.1 | Add source-of-truth verifier. | Build fails when a state path is unowned, has multiple primaries without replica policy, or lacks tenant/deletion semantics where customer state is involved. |
| W-STATE-V3-003 | P0 | 20.1 | Add production driver fail-closed policy. | Production-like boot cannot silently use JSON/local-only fallback unless explicitly allowed and visibly degraded. |
| W-STATE-V3-004 | P0 | 20.2 | Generate `docs/state-migrations.json`. | Every schema has version, forward migration, fixture, backfill strategy, write-block condition, restore/rollback note, and owner. |
| W-STATE-V3-005 | P0 | 20.2 | Add SQLite WAL backup/restore tool. | Row store and event store restore from a clean environment with WAL/checkpoint behavior verified and reported. |
| W-STATE-V3-006 | P1 | 20.2 | Write managed database decision record. | The team chooses and documents SQLite-only, managed SQLite, Postgres, or hybrid per environment with PITR, cost, scale, migration, and rollback implications. |
| W-STATE-V3-007 | P0 | 20.3 | Generate env/config registry. | `.env.example`, Vercel, Railway, Docker, BYOC docs, `/ready`, CLI doctor, and account setup agree on every required and optional variable. |
| W-STATE-V3-008 | P0 | 20.3 | Regenerate `.env.example` and remove stale taxonomy. | Deprecated Starter/Business/ENT names are removed or marked legacy; Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise, and compatibility aliases are consistent. |
| W-STATE-V3-009 | P0 | 20.3 | Generate secret inventory and token classifier. | Uppercase/env-like hits are classified; true secret refs are owned; false positives are explainable; raw values are never printed. |
| W-STATE-V3-010 | P0 | 20.3 | Add no-secret diagnostics gate. | Logs, readiness, API docs, screenshots, generated artifacts, reports, and packages fail verification if they include secret values or private env files. |
| W-STATE-V3-011 | P0 | 20.3 | Add external secret-manager smoke. | Configured AWS/GCP/Azure/Vault/1Password/Doppler/Infisical or equivalent references resolve metadata-only proof without storing values in code. |
| W-STATE-V3-012 | P1 | 20.3 | Add rotation and break-glass runbooks. | Rotation drills cover provider keys, receipt secrets, Stripe, OAuth, storage, bridge, DB credentials, and emergency access with audit evidence. |
| W-STATE-V3-013 | P0 | 20.4 | Add tenant-state-boundary contract. | Rows, events, jobs, sessions, OAuth grants, artifacts, caches, exports, backups, and derived datasets enforce tenant scope. |
| W-STATE-V3-014 | P0 | 20.4 | Add account deletion residue scanner. | Purge certificates prove no accessible residue across stores, objects, caches, generated exports, derived data, and sessions except scoped legal/audit holds. |
| W-STATE-V3-015 | P0 | 20.5 | Generate object lifecycle/provider matrix. | Local, R2 REST, R2 S3, AWS S3, generic S3, and Supabase S3 list versioning, lifecycle, object lock, max size, residency, delete, and restore caveats. |
| W-STATE-V3-016 | P1 | 20.5 | Add artifact checksum reconciliation. | Object bytes, manifests, receipts, dependency graph, account registry rows, and generated docs agree on hashes and sizes. |
| W-STATE-V3-017 | P1 | 20.5 | Add cache classification and cleanup. | Every cache has TTL, invalidation owner, no-primary-truth label, purge behavior, and rebuild proof. |
| W-STATE-V3-018 | P1 | 20.6 | Add generated-state build-order lock. | API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, codegraph, SDK metadata, and screenshot reports cannot be generated concurrently into corrupt/stale output. |
| W-STATE-V3-019 | P1 | 20.6 | Add generated artifact provenance. | Generated files include source inputs, build command, timestamp, checksum, and stale-check command where practical. |
| W-STATE-V3-020 | P0 | 20.6 | Emit restore reports. | `reports/restore` contains machine-readable clean-environment restore evidence with RPO/RTO, commands, checksums, state counts, and no-secret proof. |
| W-STATE-V3-021 | P0 | 20.6 | Emit DR drill reports. | `reports/dr` proves row/event/object/config/secret/generation failure scenarios and production-smoke recovery. |
| W-STATE-V3-022 | P0 | 20.6 | Add state readiness API/CLI/TUI/account surfaces. | Operators can see driver, schema, migration, backup freshness, object provider, secret mode, purge state, and missing proof without secret values. |
| W-STATE-V3-023 | P0 | cross-node | Add state claim-scope gate. | Public, docs, account, CLI, and sales copy cannot claim enterprise durability, deletion, residency, backup, or secret safety without registry and restore proof. |
| W-STATE-V3-024 | P0 | 43 | Add Node 20 completion certificate. | Build redline cannot relax until state registry, config schema, secret inventory, object matrix, restore report, DR drill, readiness surface, and claim-scope gates are green. |

##### Node 20 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `20` remains open until these are true from code and current artifacts:

- No persistent state path is unowned or hidden behind a generic helper.
- No customer state lacks tenant scope, retention, purge, export, and backup semantics.
- No production deploy can silently fall back to local-only JSON, JSONL, temp files, or developer workstation state.
- No generated artifact can be stale, concurrently corrupted, or source-ambiguous without detection.
- No secret value appears in readiness, logs, reports, screenshots, docs, packages, source maps, generated pages, or test fixtures.
- No `.env` file is required as an operational artifact or read by documentation tooling for values.
- No object-storage provider is marketed beyond its proven capabilities for lifecycle, immutability, residency, max size, delete, and restore.
- No migration can leave mixed schema state while writes continue.
- No restore claim exists without a clean-environment restore report.
- No DR claim exists without row, event, object, config, secret, generated artifact, auth/session, and account readiness proof.
- No deletion claim exists without cross-store residue scanning and a purge certificate.
- No enterprise durability or compliance copy can pass claim-scope gates until state registry, backup, restore, secret, and DR evidence are linked.

The relax condition for Node `20` V3 is: every state class is registry-owned, source-of-truth-clean, schema-versioned, tenant-scoped, migratable, recoverable, purge-aware, object-reconciled, secret-safe, restore-tested, DR-tested, claim-scoped, and visible through API/CLI/TUI/account readiness with `secret_values_included:false`.

### Accessibility, Internationalization, Content Integrity, And Inclusive UX Ledger V1

This branch maintains master spec tree node `21`: the inclusive interaction and content-quality layer. Kolm is an enterprise AI infrastructure product, which means the UI will be used by security reviewers, ML engineers, compliance officers, finance operators, procurement, founders, and support teams under pressure. "Looks good in screenshots" is not enough. Every surface must be operable by keyboard, understandable with assistive technology, readable under zoom and mobile constraints, locale-safe, plain enough for buyers, precise enough for engineers, and free of placeholder or corrupted content.

This pass is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not modify public HTML, generated nav, account pages, scripts, styling, or frontend assets while other agents are editing.

#### Current Accessibility And Content Surface Inventory

| surface | current evidence | what it proves | remaining spec gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| page foundations | Many `public/**/*.html` files include `html lang="en"`, viewport meta, skip links, landmarks, `aria-current`, and labeled account navigation. | The codebase has accessibility intent and some reusable primitives. | There is no generated accessibility inventory proving every route has one `h1`, valid landmarks, correct skip target, correct `lang`, and no duplicate confusing chrome. |
| navigation and mega menus | Generated docs/integration pages show repeated `role="menu"` mega-menu blocks; account pages often include hidden W221 nav anchors. | Navigation is centralized enough to audit and regenerate. | ARIA menu roles require a real keyboard contract; ordinary site navigation should not use application-menu semantics unless arrow-key behavior, focus management, escape, and screen-reader announcements are proven. |
| forms and filters | Account pages use many `aria-label` fields and some associated labels; storage radio cards wrap inputs in labels. | Search/filter/admin controls are mostly reachable by assistive tech. | Several fields are placeholder-led, many generated textareas lack visible labels, groups of radios/checkboxes need fieldset/legend semantics, and errors must be near-field plus announced. |
| destructive and sensitive actions | Account pages use browser `alert()` and `confirm()` for key rotation, purge, revoke, auto-allow, 2FA, and storage wipe flows. | The product exposes important actions and warnings. | Browser dialogs are inconsistent, hard to style, hard to localize, and weak for audit trails; destructive flows need accessible modal patterns, typed confirmation where needed, undo/receipt where possible, and screen-reader status messages. |
| dynamic data and loading states | Some account surfaces use `aria-busy`, `aria-live`, loading tokens, and empty states. | Async state is partially represented. | Every dynamic panel needs a standard live-region contract for loading, stale data, empty data, error, permission denied, external proof missing, and success. |
| charts and visual data | Some SVG charts carry `role="img"` and `aria-label`; tables exist on many pages. | Data visualizations can be named. | Charts need text summaries, keyboard-accessible details, color-not-only encodings, table fallbacks, and downloadable data for every metric surface. |
| media, demo, and video | Marketing pages contain demo/media concepts; user feedback flagged demo/video quality and light-mode loading issues. | Media is central to conversion. | Every video/demo must have captions, transcript, poster/fallback, reduced-motion path, keyboard controls, loading/error state, and light/dark contrast proof. |
| placeholder links and commands | Scans found `href="#"` in `public/community.html`, `public/builder.html`, `public/marketplace.html`, `public/roi.html`, and account download actions. | Some placeholders are still visible routes or JS-controlled links. | No public CTA may be a dead `#`; if a link is a button, it must be a button; if a download is generated, disabled/loading/error states must be explicit. |
| localization and formatting | Some account JS uses `toLocaleString()`; most pages are hard-coded English. | There is minimal browser-locale awareness. | Time, currency, units, date windows, number compaction, pluralization, text direction, legal region, and translation readiness need one i18n contract. |
| visual QA automation | Playwright screenshot scripts, mobile audits, render audits, and UI surface audits exist. | The team can capture render stability across pages. | There is no first-class axe/pa11y/Lighthouse/accessibility tree gate visible in package dependencies; screenshots do not prove keyboard, screen-reader, form, focus, or localization correctness. |
| text integrity | Prior scans found mojibake-like comments and generated metadata issues; scripts include fixers for mojibake. | Text corruption has been recognized as a real product risk. | Text integrity needs a route-wide gate for mojibake, stale brand names, fake placeholders, broken Unicode entities, orphaned generated fragments, and inconsistent product taxonomy. |

#### Primary Standards Imported Into Node 21

| source | URL | spec implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | Use WCAG 2.2 AA as the minimum product bar, with selected AAA practices for focus appearance, cognitive clarity, and complex enterprise workflows. |
| W3C WAI ARIA overview | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/aria/` | ARIA is for making dynamic controls accessible, not for papering over bad semantics; every ARIA role needs correct name, role, value, and keyboard behavior. |
| WAI ARIA Authoring Practices Guide | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/` | Dialogs, menus, disclosure widgets, tabs, comboboxes, accordions, and live regions need APG-compatible patterns and tests. |
| W3C WAI forms tutorial | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/forms/` | Labels, instructions, validation, grouping, and status messages must help all users, not only screen-reader users. |
| W3C labels or instructions guidance | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/labels-or-instructions.html` | Placeholder-only instructions are not enough; users need visible, persistent labels or instructions for inputs. |
| W3C internationalization quick tips | `https://www.w3.org/International/quicktips/index` | Language, direction, encoding, text authoring, dates, numbers, and translated expansion need to be designed before localization begins. |
| Google Search Central Core Web Vitals | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals` | Performance and UX quality must include real-user experience metrics, not only local screenshots. |
| W3C Cognitive Accessibility guidance | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/cognitive/` | Complex AI governance workflows need plain language, predictable help, memory aids, low cognitive load, and recoverable actions. |

#### Inclusive UX Contract Shape

Every public, account, docs, and generated route should have a machine-readable contract:

```json
{
  "route": "/account/storage",
  "owner": "account-product",
  "primary_user": "security_operator",
  "wcag_target": "2.2 AA",
  "language": "en",
  "i18n_ready": false,
  "landmarks": ["header", "nav", "main"],
  "interactive_patterns": ["radio_group", "destructive_confirmation", "async_status"],
  "keyboard_contract": {
    "tab_order_matches_visual_order": true,
    "focus_not_obscured": true,
    "escape_closes_modal": true
  },
  "status_messages": ["loading", "saved", "failed", "stale", "permission_denied"],
  "media_contract": {
    "captions": "not_applicable",
    "transcript": "not_applicable",
    "reduced_motion": true
  },
  "content_quality": {
    "dead_links": 0,
    "placeholder_ctas": 0,
    "mojibake_markers": 0,
    "taxonomy_drift": 0
  },
  "evidence": ["axe", "keyboard_walk", "screen_reader_smoke", "mobile_zoom_200", "screenshot"]
}
```

This route-level contract must be generated from the route inventory and compared against DOM scans, screenshot audits, manual keyboard walkthroughs, and assistive-tech smoke checks.

#### Redlines Found In Current Tree

1. Screenshot audits are necessary but insufficient. A page can render cleanly while failing keyboard access, focus visibility, form labeling, status-message announcement, or destructive-action recoverability.
2. Repeated `role="menu"` mega-menu markup in docs and integration pages must be audited against the ARIA APG. If these are normal site-navigation flyouts, use semantic nav/disclosure patterns; if they are true menus, implement arrow-key, escape, focus, and announcement behavior.
3. Some pages include both `ks-skip` and `skip-link` routes to the same content. Duplicate skip links may be acceptable only if focus order and visibility are intentional and tested.
4. `href="#"` appears in public CTAs and JS-controlled actions. Dead hash links are not a state-of-the-art product surface; use buttons for actions and real URLs for navigation.
5. `alert()` and `confirm()` are used in sensitive account flows. Replace them with accessible, product-consistent dialogs that announce context, preserve focus, support escape/cancel, and record audit receipts where appropriate.
6. Many account controls rely on placeholder text or `aria-label` without a visible persistent label. The visible UI should tell every user what the field does, what format is expected, and what happens next.
7. Radio and checkbox card groups need fieldset/legend or equivalent grouping, especially for storage/privacy/security choices where the relationship between options matters.
8. Charts and gauges should never encode state by color alone. Every visualization needs text summary, table fallback, and data export path.
9. Light/dark mode, reduced motion, 200 percent zoom, 400 percent zoom/reflow, high contrast, and mobile landscape need explicit verification. A desktop screenshot pass does not prove them.
10. Locale formatting is inconsistent by design because the site is mostly hard-coded English and uses ad hoc `toLocaleString()`. Time zones, currency, token counts, units, and dates must be deterministic in audit/export surfaces and user-local where appropriate.
11. Generated docs and account pages show enough repeated chrome that a single broken generator can create hundreds of accessibility defects. The generator must own the a11y contract, not each page by hand.
12. Text corruption, stale brand fragments, and placeholder community CTAs are product-quality defects, not cosmetic details.

#### Completion Specification For Node 21

| area | done means | proof required |
|---|---|---|
| route accessibility inventory | Every route has language, title, h1, landmarks, skip link, focus target, interactive patterns, and owner. | Generated `docs/accessibility-route-registry.json` and verifier. |
| keyboard support | Every control and flow works without a mouse and has visible focus. | Playwright keyboard walks plus manual spot checks for account, docs, nav, modals, demos, calculators, and checkout/sales flows. |
| screen-reader support | Names, roles, values, dynamic updates, errors, dialogs, charts, and route changes are announced correctly. | Accessibility tree snapshots, axe/pa11y gate, NVDA/VoiceOver smoke notes for critical flows. |
| forms and errors | Inputs have visible labels/instructions, grouped options, clear required states, near-field errors, summary errors, and status messages. | Form audit across account, enterprise inquiry, healthcare assessment, labs, signup, builder, ROI, marketplace upload. |
| destructive flows | Purge, revoke, rotate, uninstall, delete, reject, auto-allow, and 2FA actions use accessible dialogs and receipts. | Modal pattern tests, focus trapping, escape behavior, typed confirmation where needed, audit log proof. |
| media and demos | Demo video, interactive runner, charts, SVGs, images, and animations have accessible alternatives. | Captions/transcripts/posters, alt inventory, chart summaries, reduced-motion tests, light/dark media checks. |
| responsive and zoom | All routes work at mobile, tablet, desktop, 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow, landscape, and high contrast. | Screenshot plus DOM overflow/focus checks, no horizontal scroll except intentional code blocks/tables. |
| localization readiness | Strings, dates, numbers, currency, units, pluralization, direction, and legal-region text are centralized or explicitly non-localized. | i18n string registry and locale smoke for `en-US`, `en-GB`, `de-DE`, `fr-FR`, `ja-JP`, and RTL pseudo-locale. |
| content integrity | No placeholder CTAs, stale taxonomy, mojibake, broken entities, fake logos, fake proof, or unsupported claims. | Text-integrity scanner, claim-scope gate, manual content QA checklist. |
| generator safety | Shared nav/docs/account generators encode a11y rules once and fail on drift. | Generator tests and rendered route diff checks. |

#### W-A11Y Ticket Ledger

| ticket | priority | node | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| W-A11Y-001 | P0 | 21.1 | Build route accessibility registry. | Every public/account/docs/generated route has owner, language, h1, landmarks, skip target, interactive patterns, and proof status. |
| W-A11Y-002 | P0 | 21.1 | Add axe or equivalent automated accessibility gate. | CI fails on critical WCAG violations, missing names, invalid ARIA, color contrast, and duplicate IDs. |
| W-A11Y-003 | P0 | 21.1 | Add keyboard-walk verifier for critical routes. | Nav, mega menus, docs search/API filters, account tables, modals, forms, ROI, builder, signup, and enterprise inquiry work by keyboard. |
| W-A11Y-004 | P0 | 21.1 | Fix nav semantics contract. | Mega menus either use native disclosure navigation or APG-compatible menu behavior with tests. |
| W-A11Y-005 | P0 | 21.2 | Replace `alert()` and `confirm()` account flows with accessible dialogs. | Destructive flows have focus trap, labels, escape/cancel, typed confirmation where needed, and status announcement. |
| W-A11Y-006 | P0 | 21.2 | Add form label/error audit. | No placeholder-only critical inputs; all required fields, groups, errors, and instructions are visible and programmatically linked. |
| W-A11Y-007 | P1 | 21.2 | Standardize async status messages. | Every loading/save/error/stale/permission state uses an accessible live-region pattern without noisy announcements. |
| W-A11Y-008 | P0 | 21.3 | Add reduced-motion and animation policy. | All animated demos, hero media, charts, and transitions respect `prefers-reduced-motion` and have static alternatives. |
| W-A11Y-009 | P0 | 21.3 | Add zoom/reflow/mobile-landscape gate. | Critical routes pass 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow where applicable, no unintended horizontal scroll, and focus remains visible. |
| W-A11Y-010 | P1 | 21.3 | Add chart and data-visualization accessibility contract. | Every chart/gauge has text summary, table fallback, keyboard-accessible details, and color-not-only encodings. |
| W-A11Y-011 | P0 | 21.3 | Add media accessibility contract. | Product video/demo assets have captions, transcripts, posters, failure states, keyboard controls, and light/dark proof. |
| W-A11Y-012 | P0 | 21.4 | Create i18n string and formatting registry. | Dates, times, currency, token counts, percentages, units, and pluralization are centralized or explicitly scoped. |
| W-A11Y-013 | P1 | 21.4 | Add pseudo-locale and RTL smoke. | Layout survives text expansion and right-to-left direction without breaking nav, cards, tables, or account controls. |
| W-A11Y-014 | P1 | 21.4 | Add deterministic audit/export formatting policy. | Audit logs, receipts, exports, invoices, and legal docs use explicit locale/time-zone rules. |
| W-A11Y-015 | P0 | 21.5 | Remove placeholder and dead-hash CTAs. | Public CTAs and account actions are real links or real buttons with disabled/loading/error states. |
| W-A11Y-016 | P0 | 21.5 | Add content integrity scanner. | Mojibake, stale brand names, stale plans, unsupported claims, fake proof, orphaned generated fragments, and broken entities fail verification. |
| W-A11Y-017 | P1 | 21.5 | Add plain-language microcopy pass for all account risk surfaces. | Storage, privacy, security, billing, approvals, drift, and compliance pages explain risk/action in user language without hiding technical truth. |
| W-A11Y-018 | P1 | 21.6 | Add assistive-tech manual smoke template. | Release evidence includes NVDA/Firefox, VoiceOver/Safari, keyboard-only, reduced-motion, high-contrast, and mobile screen-reader notes for critical flows. |
| W-A11Y-019 | P1 | 21.6 | Add generator a11y lock-in tests. | Nav/account/docs generators produce valid landmarks, names, focus states, and no duplicated interactive fragments. |
| W-A11Y-020 | P1 | cross-node | Add inclusive UX claim gate. | Product cannot be called enterprise-ready until accessibility, localization readiness, content integrity, and manual assistive-tech evidence are present. |

#### Redline To Relax For Node 21

Node `21` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Can a keyboard-only user complete signup, API-key creation, capture review, storage setup, artifact build, billing, enterprise inquiry, and support/contact flows?
- Can a screen-reader user understand navigation, product claims, forms, tables, charts, destructive warnings, and async status changes?
- Are route language, page titles, h1s, landmarks, skip links, focus order, and focus visibility correct on every route?
- Are all videos, demos, images, SVGs, charts, calculators, and code widgets accessible in light and dark mode?
- Does the UI survive 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow, mobile, tablet, desktop, high contrast, and reduced motion?
- Are dates, numbers, currencies, costs, model sizes, token counts, storage units, and legal-region copy deterministic and localization-ready?
- Are all placeholder CTAs, fake social/community links, stale plan names, stale brand names, mojibake markers, and unsupported claims gone?
- Is accessibility evidence generated from the same route graph used by screenshots, SEO, product surfaces, and release verification?

The relax condition for node `21` is not "the page looks polished." It is: every user-facing route and generated surface has accessibility, localization, content-integrity, keyboard, screen-reader, responsive, media, and manual QA evidence strong enough for enterprise procurement and real users.

#### Node 21 V2 Accessibility, Internationalization, And Content Integrity Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `21` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not modify public HTML, generated navigation, account pages, scripts, CSS, copy, or media while other agents are editing the website. It converts inclusive UX from a broad aspiration into a route-level completion contract.

Kolm cannot be a state-of-the-art AI infrastructure product if it only works for sighted mouse users on a default desktop viewport. Completion requires every public, account, docs, generated, demo, media, and operator surface to be usable by keyboard, understandable by screen readers, robust under zoom/reflow, safe under reduced motion, clear in error states, locale-aware where needed, and free of placeholder or corrupted content.

##### Node 21 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| public surface scan | 1,132 public files scanned; 1,004 files matched accessibility/content markers; 18,056 marker hits. | Inclusive UX is a generator and route-graph problem, not a few hand-edited pages. |
| test surface scan | 567 test files scanned; 224 files matched accessibility/content markers; 1,234 marker hits. | There is existing UX/test intent, but it needs one route-level evidence model. |
| script surface scan | 343 script files scanned; 164 files matched accessibility/content markers; 854 marker hits. | Scripts already touch generated pages and QA; accessibility/content gates should be generated and automated. |
| docs surface scan | 158 docs files scanned; 79 files matched accessibility/content markers; 994 marker hits. | Docs must be part of inclusive UX, especially API/CLI/SDK examples, tutorials, and generated references. |
| focused tests present | `wave202-cli-ux`, `wave204-post-auth-ui`, `wave205-website-copy`, `wave207-a11y-perf`, `wave209-i18n-refresh`, `wave225-seo-infrastructure`, `wave256-copy-scrub`, `wave260-copy-refresh`, `wave409j-copy-sweep`, `wave431-source-mojibake-clean`, `wave470-auth-recovery-ux`, `wave493-trust-copy-honesty`, `wave501-quickstart-capture-bench-route-honesty`. | The project already recognizes UX/copy/a11y/integrity as release concerns. The missing proof is complete route coverage and manual assistive-tech evidence. |
| known redline classes | `role=`, `aria-*`, `tabindex`, skip links, placeholder, `href="#"`, `alert(`, `confirm(`, locale/i18n, mojibake markers, stale copy, media/demos. | These are not all defects, but each class needs ownership and proof so the final product is not held together by incidental markup. |
| UI/UX skill baseline | Applied `ui-ux-pro-max` priorities: accessibility, touch/interaction, performance, layout/responsive, typography/color, forms/feedback, navigation patterns, reduced motion, progressive loading. | State-of-art UI means interaction quality and accessibility are part of product function, not optional polish. |

##### Node 21 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | Use WCAG 2.2 AA as the minimum gate and test perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust behavior, including focus not obscured, target size, status messages, labels, accessible authentication, and reflow. |
| WAI-ARIA overview | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/aria/` | ARIA must be used only with correct semantics and behavior. Native HTML is preferred when it gives the right name, role, and value. |
| ARIA Authoring Practices Guide | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/` | Menus, dialogs, disclosures, tabs, comboboxes, accordions, and live regions need APG-compatible keyboard and focus behavior. |
| WAI forms tutorial | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/forms/` | Labels, instructions, grouping, errors, and validation must be visible and programmatically associated. |
| WCAG labels or instructions | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/labels-or-instructions.html` | Placeholder-only fields are not enough for critical forms. |
| W3C i18n quick tips | `https://www.w3.org/International/quicktips/` | Language, encoding, direction, text expansion, dates, numbers, and translated content need early structural support. |
| W3C cognitive accessibility | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/cognitive/` | Complex AI/security/billing workflows need predictable help, plain language, error prevention, recovery paths, and low memory burden. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals` | UX quality must include loading, interaction, and layout stability evidence, not only screenshot appearance. |

##### Node 21 V2 Route Inclusive UX Contract

Every route must map to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-inclusive-route-contract-v2",
  "route": "/account/storage",
  "owner": "account-product",
  "surface_type": "post_auth_operator",
  "primary_users": ["security_operator", "ml_engineer", "admin"],
  "wcag_target": "2.2_AA",
  "language": {
    "html_lang": "en",
    "i18n_ready": false,
    "rtl_safe": "not_proven"
  },
  "structure": {
    "title": "required",
    "h1": "exactly_one",
    "landmarks": ["header", "nav", "main"],
    "skip_link": "required",
    "heading_order": "valid"
  },
  "interaction": {
    "keyboard_only": "required",
    "focus_visible": "required",
    "focus_not_obscured": "required",
    "touch_target_min_px": 44,
    "hover_required": false
  },
  "screen_reader": {
    "names_roles_values": "required",
    "status_messages": ["loading", "saved", "error", "stale", "permission_denied"],
    "route_change_announcement": "required_for_spa_like_updates"
  },
  "forms": {
    "visible_labels": "required",
    "group_labels": "required_for_radio_checkbox_sets",
    "near_field_errors": "required",
    "error_summary": "required_for_multi_field_forms"
  },
  "media": {
    "captions": "required_for_video",
    "transcript": "required_for_product_demo",
    "poster_or_fallback": "required",
    "reduced_motion": "required"
  },
  "responsive": {
    "mobile": "required",
    "desktop": "required",
    "zoom_200": "required",
    "reflow_400": "required_where_applicable",
    "high_contrast": "required"
  },
  "content_integrity": {
    "placeholder_ctas": 0,
    "dead_hash_links": 0,
    "mojibake_markers": 0,
    "stale_plan_names": 0,
    "unsupported_claims": 0
  },
  "evidence": {
    "automated": ["axe_or_equivalent", "keyboard_walk", "route_dom_scan", "screenshot"],
    "manual": ["screen_reader_smoke", "zoom_reflow_review", "content_review"]
  }
}
```

##### Node 21 V2 Critical Flow Evidence Matrix

| flow | must work without mouse | must work with screen reader | content-integrity requirement |
|---|---|---|---|
| homepage and product nav | tab through nav, mega/disclosure, CTAs, demo controls | announce category, current page, expanded/collapsed state, video/demo status | hero says what Kolm does across all three product surfaces without jargon or stale claims. |
| signup/login/recovery | complete forms, error recovery, show/hide secret controls | field labels, errors, password/help text, recovery status | no fake auth promise; no placeholder support link. |
| API key creation/rotation | create, copy, rotate, revoke, confirm destructive actions | announce one-time key display, danger states, audit receipts | no secret value in logs or docs; no ambiguous "copied" state. |
| capture review | filter, select, inspect, quarantine, approve | announce row status, privacy class, selected state, loading/error updates | no hidden mock data or unsupported provider promise. |
| storage/cloud setup | choose provider, see missing groups, run readiness | announce provider cards, missing config, smoke result | no secret values; no green state without configured proof. |
| distill/compile job | enqueue, cancel, inspect progress, view failure | announce queue state, cost cap, quality gate, error reason | no simulated result marketed as real compute. |
| billing/pricing/account plan | compare, change plan, contact sales, see enterprise gate | announce plan names, quotas, error/preventive warnings | Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise names only; no retired Business/Starter leakage. |
| enterprise inquiry | complete procurement/security fields | announce required fields, validation, submission result | contact-sales language, no fake self-serve enterprise checkout. |
| docs/API/SDK | search/filter, copy code, navigate headings | announce code tabs, route filters, version, examples | generated docs match current routes and packages. |
| ROI calculator | enter spend, compare scenario, share/export | announce formulas, assumptions, result changes | savings scoped by evidence/date/env and never a universal guarantee. |
| video/demo/interactive runner | play/pause, transcript, paste/run, error states | announce media controls, runner output, artifact readiness | demo assets have poster/fallback and no light-mode failure. |

##### Node 21 V2 Content Integrity Taxonomy

| class | examples | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| placeholder CTA | `href="#"`, "Coming soon" without scope, empty download action | Must be removed, disabled with reason, or linked to a real route. |
| stale taxonomy | Starter/Business where current plan is Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise; "Kolmogorov" where product is Kolm; old product category copy. | Fails content integrity unless it is in a named legacy/compatibility context. |
| unsupported claim | "certified", "SOC 2", "best", "guaranteed savings", "run anywhere" without proof. | Must link to evidence, closeout status, date, environment, and caveats. |
| fake proof | invented logos, fake case studies, fake benchmark/certification indicators. | Forbidden unless clearly labeled as design partner, example, synthetic, or illustrative. |
| mojibake/corruption | broken separators, replacement characters, corrupted metadata/title/schema. | Fails release; metadata is user-facing. |
| inaccessible media | video without captions/transcript, image without alt, chart with color-only meaning. | Must have alternative, transcript/summary, and keyboard path. |
| hidden interaction | hover-only nav, icon-only button without name, focus trap, action as link. | Must be fixed or contract-scoped with tests. |
| cognitive overload | long dense paragraphs, jargon-first copy, unclear next action. | Must be rewritten into outcome-first headings, concise body, and explicit next action. |
