##### Node 21 V2 Relax Redline

Node `21` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every route is represented in an accessibility/content registry generated from the same route graph used by screenshots and SEO;
2. keyboard-only users can complete all critical public, account, docs, billing, storage, API-key, distill/compile, demo, and support flows;
3. screen-reader users receive correct names, roles, values, errors, loading states, stale states, route changes, dialog context, chart summaries, and media alternatives;
4. visible labels, group labels, near-field errors, and error summaries exist for all critical forms;
5. destructive and sensitive actions use accessible product dialogs with focus management, status announcements, and receipts;
6. route layouts survive mobile, desktop, 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow where applicable, high contrast, reduced motion, and mobile landscape;
7. videos, demos, images, SVGs, calculators, and charts have captions/transcripts/alt/summaries/fallbacks and light/dark proof;
8. dates, numbers, currency, token counts, model sizes, storage units, percentages, plurals, time zones, and legal-region text follow one registry;
9. placeholder CTAs, dead hash links, stale plan names, stale brand names, mojibake markers, fake proof, unsupported claims, and orphaned generated fragments are gone or explicitly scoped;
10. automated gates and manual assistive-tech smoke evidence are included in the release packet.

If any item fails, Kolm may look better than before, but it is not yet inclusive, accessible, procurement-ready, or state-of-the-art.

#### Node 21 V3 Inclusive-Product Proof Mesh, Content Integrity, And Assistive-Tech Evidence Contract

This V3 extension maintains master spec tree node `21` and does not create a new node. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not patch HTML, CSS, nav JavaScript, media, generated docs, screenshots, or account pages while the frontend lane is active.

The standard is no longer "most pages render and have basic ARIA." Kolm is complete only when every public, docs, account, demo, generated, media, and operator route can prove usable interaction for keyboard users, screen-reader users, zoom/reflow users, reduced-motion users, mobile users, and buyers under cognitive load. The proof must be route-owned, generated from the same route graph used by screenshots and SEO, and tied to content integrity so polished visuals cannot hide broken copy, stale taxonomy, placeholder links, inaccessible dialogs, or corrupted metadata.

##### Node 21 V3 Fresh Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| route scale | Current public HTML inventory observed 729 `.html` files, including 48 account HTML pages and 221 docs HTML pages. | Inclusive UX must be generated and registry-driven. Hand review of a few hero pages cannot prove completion. |
| structural coverage | The same pass observed 729 pages with `lang`, 729 with `h1`, 725 with `main`, 725 with skip-link markers, and 729 with ARIA markers. | The foundation is real, but four pages still need main/skip-link ownership and every route needs exact landmark, heading, focus, and manual-use evidence. |
| interaction debt | The public HTML pass observed 5 `href="#"` pages, 52 placeholder-bearing pages, 25 `alert`/`confirm` pages, 2 `role="button"` pages, and 132 `tabindex` pages. | These are not automatically all defects, but every one needs classification. Sensitive flows should not depend on browser modal dialogs or div/button substitutions. |
| media evidence | Static HTML inventory observed zero `<video>` and zero `<iframe>` elements, while the product asks for demo/video and interactive runner proof. | Node `21` must require accessible media alternatives and input/output/error contracts, while node `51` owns production media assets. |
| content corruption | A current public text sweep found 477 public HTML/JS/CSS files with mojibake or corruption markers and 1,731 total marker hits; top offenders included `public/dashboard.html`, `public/index.html`, `public/threat-model.html`, and generated research/cookbook pages. | Content integrity remains a live release blocker. Metadata, titles, social cards, mobile labels, generated pages, and CSS pseudo-content are user-facing. |
| nav/accessibility runtime repair | `public/nav.js` adds touch-target guards, active nav state, keyboard handlers, `aria-expanded`, `aria-current`, mobile nav toggles, focus behavior, generated product spine, and form-control label repair. | Runtime repair is useful, but it should not be the only accessibility source of truth. Generators and static HTML must carry durable contracts, with runtime as enhancement. |
| current scripts | Matching scripts include `audit-href.cjs`, `fix-mojibake.mjs`, `qa-screenshots.mjs`, `screenshot-audit.mjs`, `seo-audit.cjs`, `seo-sweep.cjs`, and `sweep-public-copy.mjs`. | There are useful tools, but node `21` still needs a dedicated accessibility route registry, ACT-style assertions, keyboard-walk proof, and assistive-tech smoke artifacts. |
| current tests | Matching tests include W202 CLI UX, W204 post-auth UI, W205 website copy, W207 a11y/perf, W209 i18n, W225 SEO, W256/W260 copy, W409j copy sweep, W431 mojibake, W470 auth recovery UX, and route-honesty waves. | Existing tests are strong fragments. Completion needs one inclusive UX proof packet that ties route, screenshot, content, assistive-tech, and manual-use evidence together. |
| UI/UX skill baseline | `ui-ux-pro-max` critical priorities applied: accessibility, touch/interaction, performance, style consistency, responsive layout, typography/color, animation, forms/feedback, navigation, and accessible charts. | Node `21` must make these rules executable across the route graph, not leave them as design taste. |

##### Node 21 V3 External Baselines

| baseline | source | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | WCAG 2.2 AA is the minimum user-facing gate; focus appearance, focus not obscured, target size, labels, status messages, accessible authentication, and reflow need route evidence. |
| Accessibility Conformance Testing | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/act/` | Automated assertions should be traceable, repeatable, and scoped; each failed or inapplicable rule needs an explanation rather than a silent pass. |
| WAI-ARIA | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/aria/` | ARIA must supply correct name, role, state, and value only when native HTML cannot do it; custom widgets require behavior tests. |
| ARIA Authoring Practices Guide | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/` | Dialogs, disclosures, menus, tabs, comboboxes, accordions, and live regions need APG-compatible keyboard and focus behavior. |
| WAI forms tutorial | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/tutorials/forms/` | Labels, instructions, grouping, validation, and error messages must be visible and programmatically associated. |
| W3C internationalization quick tips | `https://www.w3.org/International/quicktips/` | Language, encoding, direction, text expansion, dates, numbers, and translated text need structural support from the start. |
| W3C cognitive accessibility | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/cognitive/` | Complex AI/security/billing workflows need predictable navigation, plain language, error prevention, recovery, and low memory burden. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals` | UX quality includes layout stability, loading, and interaction responsiveness in addition to accessibility and visual polish. |

##### kolm.inclusive_route_contract.v3

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.inclusive_route_contract.v3",
  "route": "/account/api-keys",
  "node": 21,
  "owner": "account-product-ux",
  "surface": "post_auth_operator",
  "wcag_target": "2.2_AA",
  "route_structure": {
    "html_lang": "required",
    "title": "required",
    "h1": "exactly_one",
    "landmarks": ["header", "nav", "main", "footer_or_reason"],
    "skip_target": "required",
    "heading_order": "valid",
    "duplicate_id_count": 0
  },
  "keyboard": {
    "tab_order_matches_visual_order": true,
    "focus_visible": true,
    "focus_not_obscured": true,
    "escape_behavior": "defined_for_dialogs_and_menus",
    "hover_only_required": false,
    "touch_target_min_px": 44
  },
  "screen_reader": {
    "names_roles_values": "required",
    "status_messages": ["loading", "saved", "failed", "stale", "missing_config", "permission_denied"],
    "dialogs": "focus_trapped_labelled_and_restored",
    "tables_and_charts": "summary_plus_structured_data"
  },
  "forms": {
    "visible_labels": true,
    "programmatic_labels": true,
    "group_labels": "required_for_option_sets",
    "required_state": "visible_and_programmatic",
    "near_field_errors": true,
    "error_summary": "required_for_multi_field_flows"
  },
  "media_and_motion": {
    "video_captions": "required_if_video_exists",
    "transcripts": "required_for_product_demo",
    "poster_or_static_fallback": "required",
    "prefers_reduced_motion": "required",
    "light_dark_media_proof": "required"
  },
  "locale_and_formatting": {
    "string_registry": "docs/i18n-string-registry.json",
    "pseudo_locale": "required",
    "rtl_smoke": "required_for_layout_shells",
    "audit_export_time_zone": "explicit",
    "currency_and_units": "registry_owned"
  },
  "content_integrity": {
    "mojibake_markers": 0,
    "placeholder_ctas": 0,
    "dead_hash_links": 0,
    "stale_taxonomy": 0,
    "unsupported_claims": 0,
    "fake_proof": 0
  },
  "evidence": {
    "automated": ["route_registry", "act_rules", "keyboard_walk", "screenshot_audit", "content_integrity"],
    "manual": ["nvda_firefox", "voiceover_safari", "mobile_screen_reader", "zoom_reflow", "high_contrast", "reduced_motion"],
    "secret_values_included": false
  }
}
```

##### Node 21 V3 Proof Mesh

| proof layer | routes covered | evidence required |
|---|---|---|
| static route registry | Every public, account, docs, generated, demo, media, auth, and operator route. | `docs/accessibility-route-registry.json` with owner, route type, WCAG target, structure, known widgets, proof status, and exceptions. |
| ACT-style automated gate | Every generated page and representative dynamic state. | Rule IDs, pass/fail/inapplicable state, route path, DOM target, screenshot link, and owner. |
| keyboard-walk gate | Top nav, account nav, docs rail, API filters, tables, modals, dialogs, forms, calculators, demo runner, media controls, and destructive flows. | Ordered key sequence, expected focus target, focus visibility, escape behavior, and failure screenshots. |
| screen-reader smoke | Homepage, docs, account overview, API keys, storage, billing, enterprise inquiry, builder/demo, docs search, and one high-risk destructive flow. | NVDA/Firefox and VoiceOver/Safari notes with route, task, observed announcement, issue list, and owner. |
| zoom/reflow gate | Mobile, desktop, 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow where applicable, mobile landscape, and high contrast. | No blocked content, no unusable nav, no hidden primary action, no horizontal scroll except intentional code/table regions. |
| media alternative gate | Hero assets, demos, explainer video, interactive runner, SVG illustrations, charts, badges, screenshots. | Alt inventory, transcript/captions, static fallback, keyboard controls, error state, poster state, and light/dark proof. |
| content-integrity gate | Titles, metadata, nav labels, CTAs, docs, schema, generated pages, account copy, pseudo-content, screenshots. | Zero mojibake/corruption markers, zero unsupported claims, zero stale plan taxonomy, zero fake proof, and explicit legacy exceptions. |
| cognitive-load review | Homepage, pricing, enterprise, security/trust, docs quickstart, account onboarding, storage, API keys, billing, builder. | Outcome-first heading, short body copy, user action, proof link, owner, and risk wording for high-stakes choices. |

##### Node 21 V3 Current Red Flags To Track

| finding | why it matters | owner node |
|---|---|---|
| 477 public text assets still matched corruption/mojibake markers in a current source sweep. | Browser tabs, metadata, social cards, mobile pseudo-labels, docs, and generated pages can look amateur even when screenshots are green. | Node `21`, cross-link node `31` and node `43`. |
| Static HTML inventory found zero product `<video>` or `<iframe>` surfaces. | The product can still lack an accessible explainer/demo medium even if the marketing page claims one. | Node `21`, cross-link node `51`. |
| Browser `alert()` and `confirm()` remain in sensitive account and public flows. | Browser dialogs are not product dialogs; they break polished UX, are hard to audit, and often fail focus/status requirements. | Node `21`, cross-link nodes `10`, `20`, and `25`. |
| `public/nav.js` repairs labels and touch targets at runtime. | Useful enhancement, but route generators should emit correct markup before JavaScript repair. Runtime repair must be tested as fallback, not treated as the primary contract. | Node `21`, cross-link node `45`. |
| `href="#"`, placeholder fields, `role="button"`, and `tabindex` still exist in current HTML. | Some are legitimate, but all need classification because each can become a keyboard, screen-reader, or dead-action defect. | Node `21`, cross-link node `44`. |
| Existing screenshot audits do not by themselves prove screen-reader comprehension, keyboard completion, labels, dialog behavior, content meaning, or locale correctness. | Visual proof and accessibility proof are related but not interchangeable. | Node `21`, cross-link nodes `9`, `40`, and `47`. |

##### Node 21 V3 Work Ticket Ledger

| ticket | priority | node | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| W-A11Y-V3-001 | P0 | 21.1 | Generate `docs/accessibility-route-registry.json`. | Every public/account/docs/generated/demo route has owner, route type, widgets, WCAG target, evidence links, and known exceptions. |
| W-A11Y-V3-002 | P0 | 21.1 | Add ACT-style accessibility result ledger. | Automated checks record rule, route, target, state, owner, screenshot, and exception; critical failures block release. |
| W-A11Y-V3-003 | P0 | 21.1 | Add full keyboard-walk proof for critical paths. | Signup, nav, docs search, API keys, storage, billing, enterprise inquiry, builder/demo, ROI, and destructive actions complete without mouse. |
| W-A11Y-V3-004 | P0 | 21.1 | Replace runtime-only nav correctness with generator contracts. | Static nav/docs/account shells emit valid landmarks, labels, focus order, disclosure state, and mobile behavior before runtime repair. |
| W-A11Y-V3-005 | P0 | 21.2 | Replace `alert()` and `confirm()` in product flows. | API key, purge, revoke, rotate, uninstall, reject, billing, tunnel, deploy, and builder flows use accessible dialogs, live status, and receipts. |
| W-A11Y-V3-006 | P0 | 21.2 | Classify all `href="#"`, `role="button"`, and `tabindex` cases. | Every case is removed, converted to semantic HTML, or justified with keyboard/screen-reader tests. |
| W-A11Y-V3-007 | P0 | 21.2 | Add form label and validation proof. | Placeholder-bearing fields are not placeholder-only; groups have legends; errors are visible, near-field, summarized, and announced. |
| W-A11Y-V3-008 | P0 | 21.2 | Standardize status/live-region vocabulary. | Loading, saving, copied, failed, stale, missing config, external blocked, permission denied, and completed states announce consistently. |
| W-A11Y-V3-009 | P0 | 21.3 | Add screen-reader manual smoke packet. | NVDA/Firefox, VoiceOver/Safari, and one mobile screen reader complete named tasks with issue notes and route evidence. |
| W-A11Y-V3-010 | P0 | 21.3 | Add media alternative registry. | Every image, SVG, chart, badge, video, demo, canvas, and interactive runner has alt/summary/transcript/caption/fallback policy. |
| W-A11Y-V3-011 | P0 | 21.3 | Add explainer/demo accessibility contract. | Product video and `.kolm` runner provide captions, transcript, poster, keyboard controls, paste/run errors, reduced motion, and light/dark proof. |
| W-A11Y-V3-012 | P0 | 21.3 | Add zoom/reflow/high-contrast gate. | Critical routes pass mobile, desktop, 200 percent zoom, 400 percent reflow, high contrast, and mobile landscape without blocked actions. |
| W-A11Y-V3-013 | P0 | 21.3 | Add reduced-motion and animation budget gate. | Hero, nav, demos, loaders, charts, product spine, and account state changes respect reduced motion and have static alternatives. |
| W-A11Y-V3-014 | P1 | 21.3 | Add chart/table accessibility layer. | Every ROI, SLA, benchmark, K-score, cost, drift, and readiness visualization has text summary, table fallback, and color-not-only encoding. |
| W-A11Y-V3-015 | P0 | 21.4 | Create `docs/i18n-string-registry.json`. | User-facing strings, metadata, nav, account labels, errors, dates, currency, token counts, sizes, and legal-region text are owned or scoped. |
| W-A11Y-V3-016 | P1 | 21.4 | Add pseudo-locale and RTL smoke. | Text expansion and right-to-left layout do not break nav, account cards, docs, tables, CTAs, code blocks, or media captions. |
| W-A11Y-V3-017 | P1 | 21.4 | Add deterministic audit/export formatting. | Receipts, audit logs, invoices, compliance exports, and security evidence use explicit locale, time zone, and parseable units. |
| W-A11Y-V3-018 | P0 | 21.5 | Add content corruption hard gate. | Known mojibake/corruption markers in public HTML/JS/CSS/metadata/schema fail verification until zero or explicitly scoped legacy exceptions. |
| W-A11Y-V3-019 | P0 | 21.5 | Add placeholder and dead-action hard gate. | `href="#"`, fake community/social CTAs, empty downloads, disabled-by-omission buttons, and dead share actions fail unless scoped. |
| W-A11Y-V3-020 | P0 | 21.5 | Add stale taxonomy and claim-scope gate. | Retired plan names, old brand fragments, unsupported certifications, fake logos, and unproven savings/performance claims fail release. |
| W-A11Y-V3-021 | P1 | 21.5 | Add cognitive-load copy review for high-risk pages. | Homepage, pricing, enterprise, trust, quickstart, storage, API keys, billing, and builder use short outcome-first copy with one next action. |
| W-A11Y-V3-022 | P1 | 21.6 | Add touch target and focus visibility proof. | Header, mobile nav, tabs, cards, icon buttons, table actions, account controls, and demos meet target/focus rules in light and dark mode. |
| W-A11Y-V3-023 | P0 | 21.6 | Generate inclusive UX release packet. | Release evidence links route registry, ACT checks, keyboard walks, screenshots, screen-reader notes, content scan, i18n smoke, and manual exceptions. |
| W-A11Y-V3-024 | P0 | 43 | Add Node 21 completion certificate. | Build redline cannot relax until accessibility, i18n, content integrity, media alternatives, screen-reader smoke, keyboard proof, and route registry are green. |

##### Node 21 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `21` remains open until current evidence proves all of the following:

- Every route has a generated inclusive-route contract and owner.
- Every critical user journey can be completed with keyboard only.
- Every high-risk route has screen-reader smoke evidence, not just ARIA markers.
- Every custom widget has native semantics or APG-compatible behavior proof.
- Every destructive or sensitive flow uses an accessible product dialog and receipt.
- Every form has visible labels, grouped controls, status text, and announced errors.
- Every video, image, SVG, chart, calculator, and demo has an accessible alternative.
- Every route survives mobile, desktop, zoom, reflow, high contrast, reduced motion, and light/dark mode.
- Every title, metadata string, generated page, pseudo-content string, and social card is free of mojibake/corruption markers.
- Every placeholder CTA, dead hash link, fake proof point, stale plan name, stale brand fragment, and unsupported claim is removed or explicitly scoped.
- Every date, number, currency, percentage, token count, model size, and storage unit follows an i18n/formatting registry or declared exception.
- The release packet links route registry, automated checks, screenshots, keyboard walks, assistive-tech notes, content integrity, i18n smoke, and manual exceptions.

The relax condition for Node `21` V3 is: Kolm can be used and understood by real enterprise users under keyboard, screen-reader, zoom, mobile, reduced-motion, high-contrast, non-default-locale, and high-cognitive-load conditions, with route-level proof and zero hidden content-integrity defects.

### Supply Chain, Dependency Integrity, Build Provenance, And Release Artifact Ledger V1

Node `22` is the supply-chain and release-evidence branch of the master spec tree. It exists because Kolm cannot claim enterprise-grade trust if the code builds locally but release artifacts, package channels, SBOMs, provenance, action permissions, dependency policy, and customer evidence are not tied into one verifiable chain.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not change workflows, packages, source files, generated assets, or tests.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Area | Current evidence found | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Node dependency graph | `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `docs/cleanup/wc01-dep-audit-2026-05-24.md`, `tests/wavewc01-dep-audit.test.js` | The repo has exact top-level pins and deterministic lockfile evidence, but the two documented moderate advisories need a recurring review date, owner, and expiry condition. |
| SBOM generation | `.github/workflows/sbom.yml`, `src/sbom-emit.js`, `apps/export/sbom.py`, `tests/wave763-sbom.test.js`, `public/sbom.html`, `public/security/sbom.html`, `public/research/sbom.html` | SBOM coverage exists for Node/package-lock and a Python fallback, but release completion requires attested SBOMs for every shipped artifact and ecosystem, not just local shape tests. |
| Signing and attestations | `src/sigstore.js`, `tests/wave150-sigstore.test.js`, `tests/wave162-sigstore-rekor.test.js`, `packages/attestation` | Dry-run Sigstore and optional Rekor support are useful, but public transparency-log evidence is not done until release artifacts are signed, logged, linked, and verified from the outside. |
| Package release readiness | `scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs`, `src/package-release-readiness.js`, `docs/package-release-readiness.md`, package manifests under `packages/` and `sdk/` | The local readiness tool is strong, but publish readiness must prove registry provenance, clean-environment install, package-manager visibility, rollback, and customer evidence. |
| GitHub Actions and CI | `.github/actions/kolm-compile/action.yml`, `.github/actions/kolm-publish/action.yml`, `.github/actions/kolm-test/action.yml`, `.github/actions/kolm-verify/action.yml`, `.github/workflows/sbom.yml` | CI actions and workflows exist, but completion requires permission minimization, SHA or policy pinning, OIDC provenance, artifact attestations, and workflow change controls. |
| Release gates | `scripts/release-verify.cjs`, release-verify wave tests, `verify:depth`, `verify:package-release`, `release:verify` | Local release gates are extensive, but state-of-art release proof must bind gates to the exact commit, artifact digests, SBOM hashes, production deployment, and rollback plan. |
| License and trust pages | `public/license.html`, `public/security.html`, `public/slsa.html`, `public/.well-known/security.txt`, `tests/wave279-site-license.test.js` | Public trust surfaces exist, but every customer-facing claim must distinguish local controls from published certification, attestation, and registry proof. |
| Native and generated outputs | `sdk/rust/Cargo.toml`, `sdk/rust/Cargo.lock`, `packages/runtime-rs/Cargo.toml`, `packages/runtime-rs/target/package`, `sdk/rust/target/release` | Generated/native build outputs appeared in the scan. Completion requires a source-versus-build-artifact policy, explicit ignore/retention rules, and release artifacts created only from clean builders. |
| Multi-ecosystem packages | `packages/sdk-ts`, `packages/sdk-rn`, `packages/sdk-swift`, `packages/vscode-kolm-rag`, `packages/langchain-kolm`, `packages/llamaindex-kolm`, `sdk/node`, `sdk/mcp`, `sdk/rust`, `sdk/vscode` | The release story spans npm, VS Code, Rust/Cargo, SwiftPM, React Native, installers, and docs. Each ecosystem needs a specific publish, provenance, install, license, and rollback contract. |

#### Official Standards And External References

These are the external standards this branch should bind to:

| Reference | URL | What Kolm should adopt |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA 1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` | Use SLSA as the release provenance ladder. Every release artifact needs source identity, builder identity, dependencies, artifact digest, and verification instructions. |
| SPDX | `https://spdx.dev/` and `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/` | Emit SPDX for license and component transparency, including AI/data/model references where applicable. |
| CycloneDX | `https://cyclonedx.org/specification/overview/` | Emit CycloneDX for SBOM and broader BOM use cases, including SaaS, cryptography, vulnerability, and ML-BOM extensions where useful. |
| Sigstore | `https://docs.sigstore.dev/` | Prefer keyless or short-lived identity-backed signing, public transparency logs, and verifiable bundles for release files, packages, containers, and SBOMs. |
| GitHub artifact attestations | `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/secure-your-work/use-artifact-attestations/use-artifact-attestations` | Use `id-token: write`, `attestations: write`, artifact subject paths/digests, and verification commands for each built artifact and SBOM. |
| npm provenance | `https://docs.npmjs.com/generating-provenance-statements/` | Publish npm packages with provenance from supported hosted CI, repository metadata, and tokenless trusted publishing where possible. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/` | Track repository health signals such as branch protection, pinned dependencies, token permissions, dangerous workflow patterns, fuzzing, and vulnerability reporting. |
| CISA secure software attestation | `https://www.cisa.gov/secure-software-attestation-form` | Map enterprise procurement evidence to secure development practices instead of vague security claims. |
| NIST SSDF | `https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/ssdf` | Use the secure software development framework as the procurement and internal-control vocabulary for release discipline. |

#### Supply-Chain Redlines

1. `npx -y -p @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm@latest` in `.github/workflows/sbom.yml` is not reproducible enough for a state-of-art release path. The final state needs an exact version or an explicit update policy with digest/provenance validation.
2. GitHub Actions references such as `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `actions/upload-artifact@v4`, and `actions/github-script@v7` are semver-pinned but not SHA-pinned. Final policy must choose SHA pinning, organization allowlisting, or a documented risk acceptance gate.
3. The SBOM workflow uses `continue-on-error: true` for CycloneDX generation. That is acceptable only if the fallback SBOM is first-class, verified, attested, and release-blocking when both primary and fallback evidence are absent.
4. Local Sigstore dry-run bundles are not public release proof. Public proof requires Rekor or equivalent transparency-log entries, signed bundles, release artifact digests, and public verification commands.
5. `docs/cleanup/wc01-dep-audit-2026-05-24.md` records two moderate advisories through `express`/`qs`. The mitigation is rational, but the final state needs an expiry date, owner, and automated re-evaluation gate.
6. Optional soft dependencies such as `better-sqlite3`, `@vercel/kv`, `@vercel/postgres`, `onnxruntime-node`, `pdf-parse`, and `puppeteer` must be represented in docs and readiness surfaces as optional capability gates, not missing packages or silent runtime surprises.
7. Generated/native build directories surfaced during the scan, including Rust `target` paths. The final repository policy must classify generated outputs, release artifacts, cache directories, and source files so the tree stays clean and reproducible.
8. Package-release readiness is local-only by design. It cannot be used as a public package release claim until registry artifacts, checksums, provenance, install smoke, and rollback evidence exist.
9. SBOM coverage must span Node, Python, Rust/Cargo, SwiftPM, Kotlin/Gradle, React Native, VS Code extensions, browser assets, installers, Docker/OCI images, and generated `.kolm` artifacts where applicable.
10. License compliance must include transitive code dependencies, SDK templates, docs/media assets, model weights, datasets, benchmark fixtures, generated artifacts, and third-party provider snippets.
11. Every public trust page must avoid implying SOC 2, ISO, FedRAMP, package publication, public benchmark proof, or partner adoption until evidence exists in the closeout ledger.
12. Release artifacts must be tied to an immutable evidence bundle that survives retention limits and can be handed to an enterprise buyer without asking an engineer to reconstruct history from CI logs.

#### Canonical Release Evidence Packet

Every production release, package release, SDK release, extension release, container release, installer release, and model/artifact release should emit the same evidence shape:

```json
{
  "release_id": "kolm-YYYY.MM.DD-N",
  "source": {
    "repo": "owner/repo",
    "commit": "sha",
    "branch": "main",
    "tag": "vX.Y.Z",
    "review_policy": "two_person_or_documented_exception"
  },
  "builder": {
    "system": "github_actions",
    "workflow": ".github/workflows/release.yml",
    "run_id": "numeric_or_url",
    "runner": "github_hosted_or_hardened_self_hosted",
    "permissions": ["contents:read", "id-token:write", "attestations:write"]
  },
  "inputs": {
    "package_lock_sha256": "hex",
    "cargo_lock_sha256": "hex_or_null",
    "source_manifest_sha256": "hex",
    "generated_artifact_sources": ["path:command"]
  },
  "outputs": [
    {
      "name": "artifact-name",
      "type": "npm_package|binary|container|sbom|installer|kolm_artifact|docs_bundle",
      "path_or_registry_ref": "string",
      "sha256": "hex",
      "size_bytes": 0,
      "sbom_refs": ["cyclonedx", "spdx"],
      "signature_ref": "sigstore_bundle_or_detached_signature",
      "attestation_ref": "github_attestation_or_slsa_predicate",
      "provenance_ref": "slsa_or_in_toto_statement"
    }
  ],
  "checks": {
    "tests": "passed",
    "release_verify": "passed",
    "local_surfaces": "passed",
    "prod_smoke": "passed",
    "lint_refs": "passed",
    "vulnerability_policy": "passed_or_accepted_with_expiry",
    "license_policy": "passed",
    "secret_scan": "passed",
    "scorecard": "recorded"
  },
  "rollback": {
    "previous_release_id": "string",
    "restore_command": "string",
    "data_migration_reversibility": "reversible|forward_only_with_plan",
    "customer_notice_required": false
  },
  "customer_evidence": {
    "public_urls": ["security", "sbom", "slsa", "release_notes"],
    "enterprise_packet_path": "path",
    "retention_days": 365
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `22.1` Dependency inventory, vulnerability posture, lockfiles, licenses, and policy | Every dependency ecosystem has an owner, lockfile strategy, vulnerability SLA, allowed-license policy, optional dependency explanation, update cadence, and documented exception expiry. |
| `22.2` SBOM, SLSA, Sigstore, provenance, attestations, and artifact signing | Every release output has CycloneDX and SPDX SBOMs, artifact digest, signed provenance, Sigstore or equivalent bundle, attestation verification command, and a customer-visible evidence link. |
| `22.3` Package managers, generated assets, installers, containers, and release channels | Every package channel has publish proof, registry provenance, clean install smoke, uninstall/rollback proof, supported platform matrix, and no claims beyond local-source-only until shipped. |
| `22.4` CI/CD workflows, GitHub Actions, permissions, branch protection, and environment gates | Every workflow has minimal permissions, pinned or policy-approved actions, OIDC where needed, environment approvals, branch protections, required checks, and no hidden write tokens in read-only jobs. |
| `22.5` Reproducible builds, checksums, build isolation, artifact retention, and rollback evidence | Builds run from clean environments, produce stable manifests, record checksums, isolate caches, retain evidence beyond CI artifact expiry, and prove rollback against the previous production release. |
| `22.6` Supply-chain incident response, dependency updates, disclosure, and customer evidence | Security.txt, advisory handling, dependency update windows, revocation process, package deprecation, customer notification, and enterprise trust packet generation are tested and owned. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-SUPPLY-001` | `22.1` | Create a single dependency ecosystem inventory for npm, Cargo, SwiftPM, Gradle/Kotlin, React Native, VS Code, Python scripts, browser assets, installers, containers, and generated `.kolm` artifacts. | The inventory links every package manifest, lockfile, owner, update command, audit command, and exception policy. |
| `W-SUPPLY-002` | `22.1` | Convert the dated dependency audit into a recurring gate with owner, review cadence, and expiry dates for the two moderate advisories. | `verify:depth` or a dedicated supply-chain gate fails when an accepted advisory expires or lacks owner/rationale. |
| `W-SUPPLY-003` | `22.1` | Add a license policy matrix for direct and transitive dependencies, docs/media, SDK templates, datasets, fixtures, model weights, and generated artifacts. | Public license/trust pages and enterprise packets report allowed, review-required, and blocked licenses from the same source. |
| `W-SUPPLY-004` | `22.1` | Formalize optional dependency policy for heavy/native/cloud packages. | Every soft dependency has install docs, runtime readiness behavior, test coverage, and account/CLI wording that does not look broken when absent. |
| `W-SUPPLY-005` | `22.2` | Replace or policy-wrap `@cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm@latest` in SBOM generation. | The SBOM workflow uses an exact version or approved resolver policy and records the emitter version in evidence. |
| `W-SUPPLY-006` | `22.2` | Attest every SBOM, not just upload it. | GitHub artifact attestations or equivalent signed statements exist for CycloneDX and SPDX outputs with verification commands. |
| `W-SUPPLY-007` | `22.2` | Promote Sigstore/Rekor from dry-run/local test to release evidence. | At least one release artifact has public transparency-log proof, detached bundle, digest, verification instructions, and customer-facing link. |
| `W-SUPPLY-008` | `22.2` | Generate an in-toto/SLSA provenance predicate for release artifacts. | Release evidence packet includes source, builder, materials, products, run id, and verification summary for each artifact. |
| `W-SUPPLY-009` | `22.3` | Build npm package provenance for `packages/sdk-ts`, `sdk/node`, `sdk/mcp`, `packages/attestation`, and integration packages. | Each npm package publishes or dry-runs with provenance, repository metadata, clean install smoke, and rollback/deprecation steps. |
| `W-SUPPLY-010` | `22.3` | Define non-npm channel contracts for Rust, Swift, React Native, VS Code, Homebrew, winget, deb/rpm, Docker/OCI, and browser CDN assets. | Each channel has exact build, publish, verify, uninstall, rollback, and support-matrix evidence. |
| `W-SUPPLY-011` | `22.3` | Classify generated and native build output directories. | Build artifacts, caches, lockfiles, generated docs, screenshots, and source files have explicit keep/ignore/rebuild rules. |
| `W-SUPPLY-012` | `22.4` | Audit GitHub Actions permissions and third-party action pinning. | Every workflow declares minimal permissions; action references are SHA-pinned or covered by an approved allowlist/update policy. |
| `W-SUPPLY-013` | `22.4` | Add OIDC and environment gate policy for release, deploy, publish, SBOM, and attestation workflows. | Release workflows use `id-token: write` only where needed, environments require approval, and secrets are unavailable to untrusted paths. |
| `W-SUPPLY-014` | `22.4` | Add workflow injection and shell-safety review to the release gate. | Scripts and actions that interpolate user/context data are reviewed or linted for command injection and token leakage. |
| `W-SUPPLY-015` | `22.5` | Create reproducible build manifests for core app, CLI, SDKs, packages, installers, docs, and public assets. | A clean checkout can rebuild artifacts and compare source manifest, hashes, generated file list, and expected drift. |
| `W-SUPPLY-016` | `22.5` | Make release evidence retention independent of GitHub artifact expiry. | Evidence packets are stored in durable storage with retention policy, index, hashes, and customer export path. |
| `W-SUPPLY-017` | `22.5` | Prove rollback. | Every production release records previous release, rollback command, migration reversibility, data-impact notes, and a successful rollback drill or documented exception. |
| `W-SUPPLY-018` | `22.6` | Build a supply-chain incident runbook. | The runbook covers compromised dependency, leaked token, bad release artifact, malicious package, revoked signature, SBOM correction, customer notification, and public advisory flow. |
| `W-SUPPLY-019` | `22.6` | Tie public trust pages to the evidence packet. | `/security`, `/slsa`, `/sbom`, `/license`, docs, CLI, and account trust surfaces read or link the same evidence IDs and never drift. |
| `W-SUPPLY-020` | `22.6` | Add a customer export bundle for enterprise procurement. | A single command or account action exports SBOMs, provenance, signatures, license report, vulnerability status, release notes, compliance packet refs, and verification instructions. |

#### Node 22 Relax Redline

Node `22` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current release evidence:

- Which exact source commit, workflow, runner, dependency graph, and generated files produced each shipped artifact?
- Can a customer verify every npm package, binary, installer, container, SBOM, and `.kolm` artifact without trusting a screenshot or Slack message?
- Are all package channels either actually published with provenance or visibly marked as local-source-only?
- Are dependency vulnerabilities handled by automated gate, documented exception, owner, expiry, and customer-visible posture?
- Are SBOMs complete across Node, Python, Rust, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, VS Code, installers, browser assets, containers, and model/artifact outputs?
- Are GitHub Actions, release workflows, and publish jobs protected from broad permissions, unpinned unreviewed dependencies, token leakage, and script injection?
- Can the team revoke, deprecate, replace, roll back, and notify customers after a compromised package or release?
- Does the enterprise trust packet prove the same state as product graph, release verify, SBOM pages, SLSA pages, package pages, and account UI?

The relax condition for node `22` is not "we have an SBOM page." It is: every artifact a customer can install, import, run, audit, or buy has durable, signed, reproducible, ecosystem-aware supply-chain evidence tied to the exact release and recoverable during an incident.

#### Node 22 V2 Supply Chain, Provenance, And Release Artifact Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `22` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change workflows, packages, source files, generated assets, release scripts, SBOM emitters, or tests while other agents are editing.

The completion standard is stronger than "release verify passed." Kolm is not complete until every installable, importable, runnable, auditable, downloadable, generated, and marketplace-distributed artifact has a source commit, builder identity, dependency graph, SBOM, license posture, checksum, provenance, signature or attestation, rollback path, retention path, and customer verification command.

##### Node 22 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| workflows | `.github/workflows/kolm.yml`, `kolmbench-submission.yml`, `kolm-ci-pipeline.yml`, `kolm-compile-on-push.yml`, `kolm-distill.yml`, `kolm-template.yml`, `lint.yml`, `sbom.yml`, `sdk-c-rust.yml`, `smoke.yml`, `test-suite.yml`. | CI exists across core, benchmarks, distill, SDKs, lint, smoke, and SBOM. Completion requires minimal permissions, action policy, OIDC/attestation policy, artifact retention, and workflow-change controls. |
| local actions | `.github/actions/kolm-compile/action.yml`, `kolm-publish/action.yml`, `kolm-test/action.yml`, `kolm-verify/action.yml`. | Local actions can standardize user workflows, but they need shell-safety review, input validation, token/secret boundaries, versioning, and public usage examples. |
| package graph | `package.json`: 9,856 bytes; `package-lock.json`: 120,073 bytes. | Node dependency graph is locked. Completion requires vulnerability SLA, license policy, package provenance, and transitive dependency evidence. |
| SBOM/signing code | `src/sbom-emit.js`: 23,005 bytes, 631 lines, 117 supply-chain marker hits; `src/sigstore.js`: 27,754 bytes, 614 lines, 64 marker hits. | There is serious implementation surface for SBOM and signing. Completion needs public release evidence, not only local emitters/tests. |
| release scripts | `scripts/release-verify.cjs`: 32,595 bytes, 699 lines; `scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs`: 11,790 bytes, 323 lines. | Local release and package-readiness checks exist. Completion requires exact artifact subjects, registry proof, attestation verification, and production/customer evidence. |
| deploy/package config | `Dockerfile`, `vercel.json`, workflow files, package manifests. | Build/deploy artifacts exist across multiple ecosystems. Completion needs source-vs-generated policy, clean-builder proof, container provenance, and deploy rollback evidence. |
| dependency audit | `docs/cleanup/wc01-dep-audit-2026-05-24.md` records vulnerability posture and exceptions. | Exceptions need owner, expiry, re-evaluation cadence, and customer-facing posture. |
| absent registries | `docs/supply-chain-contract.json`, `docs/release-evidence-packet.json`, `docs/artifact-provenance.json`, `docs/dependency-policy.json`, `docs/license-policy.json`, `docs/sbom-policy.json`, `docs/package-channel-registry.json`, `docs/workflow-security-registry.json` are absent. | Supply-chain truth is currently scattered through code, workflows, scripts, and trust pages. |
| absent durable reports | `reports/supply-chain`, `reports/provenance`, `reports/sbom` are absent. | Release evidence retention is not proven outside transient CI/output directories. |

##### Node 22 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA 1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` | Release provenance must state source, builder, build type, parameters, materials, subjects, and verification path; SLSA level claims require evidence, not aspiration. |
| GitHub artifact attestations | `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/secure-your-work/use-artifact-attestations/use-artifact-attestations` | Built artifacts and SBOMs should be attested by trusted workflow identity and verifiable by subject digest. |
| npm provenance | `https://docs.npmjs.com/generating-provenance-statements/` | npm package publication should use trusted publishing/provenance where supported and should not depend on long-lived publish tokens when avoidable. |
| Sigstore | `https://docs.sigstore.dev/` | Signing should prefer identity-bound, transparency-log-backed bundles with verification commands that customers can run. |
| SPDX | `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/` | License and component evidence should be machine-readable, standardized, and suitable for customer procurement. |
| CycloneDX | `https://cyclonedx.org/specification/overview/` | SBOM/BOM output should cover components, services, vulnerabilities, cryptography, and other artifact classes where useful. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/` | Repository health needs continuous evidence for dangerous workflow patterns, token permissions, branch protection, pinned dependencies, vulnerabilities, and security policy. |
| CISA Secure Software Development Attestation | `https://www.cisa.gov/secure-software-attestation-form` | Enterprise and government procurement may require secure development attestations; Kolm evidence should map to those control questions. |
| NIST SSDF | `https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/ssdf` | Secure software development practices should map to a stable procurement vocabulary for planning, protection, production, and response. |

##### Node 22 V2 Artifact Provenance Contract

Every artifact must map to this contract. If an artifact cannot meet it, its public surface must say `local_source_only`, `not_published`, `not_attested`, or the exact scoped state.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-artifact-provenance-v2",
  "artifact_id": "npm:kolm@0.2.6",
  "artifact_type": "npm_package",
  "channel": "npm",
  "source": {
    "repo": "kolm-ai/kolm",
    "commit": "git_sha",
    "tag": "v0.2.6",
    "dirty_tree_allowed": false
  },
  "builder": {
    "system": "github_actions",
    "workflow": ".github/workflows/release.yml",
    "run_id": "url_or_id",
    "runner": "github_hosted_or_hardened_self_hosted",
    "permissions": ["contents:read", "id-token:write", "attestations:write"]
  },
  "materials": {
    "lockfiles": ["package-lock.json"],
    "source_manifest_sha256": "hex",
    "generated_inputs": ["public/openapi.json", "public/product-graph.json"]
  },
  "subject": {
    "path_or_registry_ref": "npm:kolm@0.2.6",
    "sha256": "hex",
    "size_bytes": 0
  },
  "sbom": {
    "cyclonedx": "path_or_url",
    "spdx": "path_or_url",
    "attested": true
  },
  "signature": {
    "method": "sigstore_keyless_or_equivalent",
    "bundle": "path_or_url",
    "transparency_log": "rekor_or_equivalent",
    "verify_command": "cosign verify-blob ..."
  },
  "provenance": {
    "slsa_predicate": "path_or_url",
    "github_attestation": "path_or_url",
    "verify_command": "gh attestation verify ..."
  },
  "license": {
    "policy_state": "allowed",
    "notices": "path_or_url"
  },
  "install_smoke": {
    "clean_environment": true,
    "command": "npm install kolm@0.2.6",
    "result": "passed"
  },
  "rollback": {
    "deprecate_command": "npm deprecate ...",
    "previous_good_artifact": "npm:kolm@0.2.5",
    "customer_notice_required": false
  },
  "retention": {
    "evidence_store": "reports/provenance",
    "minimum_days": 365
  }
}
```

##### Node 22 V2 Package Channel Registry

| channel | artifact examples | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| npm | `packages/sdk-ts`, `sdk/node`, `sdk/mcp`, integration adapters. | Trusted publishing/provenance, exact package manifest, clean install smoke, SBOM, license, deprecation/rollback command, provenance verification. |
| VS Code marketplace | `sdk/vscode`, `packages/vscode-kolm-rag`. | Extension manifest, package hash, marketplace listing, install smoke, permission review, uninstall proof, no secret collection by default. |
| Cargo/Rust | `sdk/rust`, `packages/runtime-rs`. | `Cargo.lock` policy, crate publish or local-source state, build provenance, license, binary/artifact hashes, target directory exclusion policy. |
| SwiftPM | `packages/sdk-swift`. | Package manifest validation, tagged release, platform matrix, install smoke, license/provenance state. |
| Kotlin/Gradle | `packages/sdk-kotlin` if present in release set. | Maven/Gradle coordinates or local-source state, dependency lock policy, signed artifacts, install smoke. |
| React Native | `packages/sdk-rn`. | npm provenance plus mobile build smoke, native dependency policy, platform matrix. |
| Docker/OCI | `Dockerfile`, deployment images. | Reproducible build inputs, image digest, SBOM, provenance attestation, vulnerability scan, rollback tag. |
| browser/CDN assets | `public/sdk.js`, workers, generated browser assets. | Subresource/version integrity where applicable, source map policy, minifier/build provenance, CDN purge/rollback plan. |
| installers | scripts, Homebrew, winget, apt/rpm, direct downloads. | Signed installers, checksums, package-manager manifests, clean install/uninstall smoke, revocation plan. |
| `.kolm` artifacts | examples, marketplace artifacts, generated artifacts. | Artifact digest, dependency graph, signature, receipt, model/license/data rights, runtime compatibility, verification command. |

##### Node 22 V2 Workflow Security Contract

Every workflow and local action must declare:

| field | requirement |
|---|---|
| purpose | release, test, lint, SBOM, publish, deploy, benchmark, compile, distill, smoke, or docs. |
| trigger trust | trusted branch, pull request, fork, manual dispatch, tag, schedule, or external submission. |
| permissions | minimal explicit `permissions:` block; default write permissions forbidden. |
| secrets | which secrets are available, on which triggers, and why. |
| actions | third-party actions are SHA-pinned or covered by an allowlist/update policy. |
| shell safety | user-controlled inputs are not interpolated into shell without validation/escaping. |
| artifacts | paths, retention, digest, attestation, and customer export rules. |
| environments | production/publish/deploy workflows use protected environments and approvals. |
| OIDC | `id-token: write` only for provenance/cloud/publish steps that need it. |
| evidence | workflow run URL, logs, attestations, SBOMs, and artifact digests are retained outside short-lived CI if release-critical. |

##### Node 22 V2 Supply-Chain Incident Matrix

| incident | detection | required response |
|---|---|---|
| compromised dependency | audit, advisory, Scorecard, runtime report, customer report | freeze releases, assess exposure, patch or pin, regenerate SBOM, publish advisory/customer notice if impacted. |
| malicious package publish | registry alert, checksum mismatch, customer report | deprecate/yank where possible, rotate tokens, publish replacement, revoke trust page evidence, notify customers. |
| leaked publish token | secret scanner, registry audit log, unusual publish | revoke token, rotate credentials, audit publish history, re-attest known-good artifacts. |
| bad release artifact | smoke failure, checksum mismatch, install failure | halt rollout, roll back/deprecate, regenerate evidence, add regression test. |
| invalid SBOM | customer finding, verifier failure, missing component | issue corrected SBOM, mark old one superseded, update release packet. |
| revoked signature/key | Sigstore/Rekor/CA/key event | publish new signature or explanation, update verification commands, notify affected customers. |
| compromised workflow | GitHub audit, branch protection event, workflow change | disable workflow, rotate secrets, audit artifacts, require review, rebuild known-good release. |
| dependency license conflict | license scanner, customer procurement, counsel review | block release or document exception, replace dependency, update notices and customer packet. |

##### Node 22 V2 Relax Redline

Node `22` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every dependency ecosystem has an owner, lockfile strategy, vulnerability SLA, license policy, update cadence, and exception expiry;
2. every customer-installable or auditable artifact has SBOM, checksum, provenance, signature or attestation, install smoke, rollback path, and retention evidence;
3. package channels are either actually published with provenance or visibly scoped as local-source-only/not-yet-published;
4. workflows have explicit minimal permissions, controlled secrets, trusted trigger policy, OIDC only where needed, and action pinning or allowlist policy;
5. release evidence survives CI artifact expiry and can be exported to enterprise buyers;
6. Sigstore/Rekor/GitHub attestation/npm provenance claims are backed by public verification commands;
7. generated/native/cache/release/evidence files are classified and cannot pollute clean source reviews;
8. license evidence covers code, packages, docs, media, datasets, models, benchmark fixtures, templates, provider snippets, and generated artifacts;
9. supply-chain incidents have runbooks for dependency compromise, token leak, malicious publish, invalid SBOM, revoked signature, workflow compromise, and bad release;
10. public trust pages, account surfaces, CLI, docs, package pages, release notes, and customer packets all point to the same evidence IDs.

If any item fails, Kolm can still ship software, but it cannot honestly claim a state-of-the-art enterprise supply chain.

#### Node 22 V3 Supply-Chain Control Room, Artifact Attestation, And Package-Channel Evidence Contract

This V3 extension maintains master spec tree node `22` and does not create a new node. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not edit workflows, package manifests, source modules, release scripts, generated SBOMs, package channels, Dockerfiles, or tests while other agents are active.

The completion standard is now artifact-by-artifact, not "we have an SBOM workflow." Kolm is not supply-chain-complete until every source manifest, lockfile, workflow, local action, package channel, container, SDK, installer, generated public artifact, `.kolm` artifact, SBOM, signature, provenance subject, release evidence packet, and customer trust page resolves to one control room with owner, policy, digest, builder identity, verification command, retention, rollback, and incident response.

##### Node 22 V3 Fresh Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| workflow estate | Current `.github/workflows` inventory has 11 workflows: `kolm.yml`, `kolmbench-submission.yml`, `kolm-ci-pipeline.yml`, `kolm-compile-on-push.yml`, `kolm-distill.yml`, `kolm-template.yml`, `lint.yml`, `sbom.yml`, `sdk-c-rust.yml`, `smoke.yml`, and `test-suite.yml`. | CI is broad enough to matter. Completion requires workflow ownership, required-check mapping, minimal permissions, OIDC/attestation design, retention, and branch/environment policy. |
| workflow hardening gaps | Current workflow scan found zero SHA-pinned `uses:` refs; several workflows lack explicit top-level `permissions`; no workflow currently exposes `id-token: write`; no workflow currently emits attestations; `sbom.yml` and `kolm-distill.yml` have `continue-on-error:true`; `sbom.yml` and `kolm-distill.yml` still include `@latest` usage. | These are not all immediate blockers, but they must be explicitly owned. Release-hardening policy must choose SHA pinning, approved moving tags, trusted-publisher controls, and exception expiry. |
| local actions | `.github/actions/kolm-compile/action.yml`, `kolm-publish/action.yml`, `kolm-test/action.yml`, and `kolm-verify/action.yml` exist. | Local actions are part of the public developer surface. They need input validation, shell-safety review, artifact output contracts, secret boundaries, and versioned release evidence. |
| package/build manifests | Current non-temp manifest inventory found 51 files: 25 npm, 9 Python, 3 Cargo, 1 Swift, 2 Gradle/Kotlin, 1 CocoaPods, 5 container, and 5 GitHub Action manifests. | Kolm is a multi-ecosystem stack. Node/npm-only SBOM proof is insufficient for a complete enterprise evidence packet. |
| duplicate/temp manifests | A broader scan found 92 manifests when `tmp/kolm-launch-*` copies are included. | Generated/temporary launch bundles need source-vs-artifact classification so scanners do not confuse stale copies with canonical release input. |
| SBOM/signing modules | `src/sbom-emit.js`: 23,005 bytes, 631 lines, 210 supply-chain marker hits; `src/sigstore.js`: 27,754 bytes, 614 lines, 110 marker hits. | Core implementation exists. Completion requires subject-level release proof, public verification commands, and durable evidence retention. |
| release scripts | `scripts/release-verify.cjs`: 32,595 bytes, 699 lines; `scripts/package-release-readiness.mjs`: 11,790 bytes, 323 lines. | Local verification and package readiness are real. They must be tied to signed release subjects, registry evidence, package-channel smoke, and customer exports. |
| package script surface | `package.json` is `kolm-stack` version `0.2.6`, has 57 scripts, and includes `verify:package-release`, `verify:depth`, `release:verify`, and many product gates. | Completion requires supply-chain gates to enter the same release/depth path with exact coverage, not remain a separate manual checklist. |
| missing control-room artifacts | `docs/supply-chain-contract.json`, `docs/release-evidence-packet.json`, `docs/artifact-provenance.json`, `docs/dependency-policy.json`, `docs/license-policy.json`, `docs/sbom-policy.json`, `docs/package-channel-registry.json`, `docs/workflow-security-registry.json`, `docs/artifact-retention-policy.json`, `reports/supply-chain`, `reports/provenance`, `reports/sbom`, and `reports/attestations` were absent in the targeted pass. | Supply-chain truth remains scattered. A 100% build needs generated contracts and durable reports, not CI logs alone. |

##### Node 22 V3 External Baselines

| baseline | source | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA specification v1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` | Any SLSA claim must map to source, builder, build type, materials, subjects, provenance, and verification level. |
| GitHub artifact attestations | `https://docs.github.com/actions/security-for-github-actions/using-artifact-attestations/using-artifact-attestations-to-establish-provenance-for-builds` | GitHub-built artifacts should have subject-digest attestations tied to workflow identity and verifiable by customers. |
| npm provenance | `https://docs.npmjs.com/generating-provenance-statements` | Published npm packages should expose provenance linking package, source commit, and build instructions. |
| npm trusted publishing | `https://docs.npmjs.com/trusted-publishers` | OIDC trusted publishing should replace long-lived npm tokens where package channels support it and can emit automatic provenance. |
| CycloneDX specification | `https://cyclonedx.org/specification/overview` | BOM evidence should cover more than npm dependencies where useful: components, services, vulnerabilities, cryptography, and deployed artifact classes. |
| SPDX specifications | `https://spdx.dev/use/specifications/` | License and SBOM evidence should use a recognized machine-readable standard and be suitable for procurement review. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` | Repository health should track branch protection, pinned dependencies, token permissions, vulnerabilities, maintained status, and dangerous workflows. |
| NIST SSDF | `https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/ssdf` | Secure development evidence should map to a stable procurement vocabulary for planning, protecting, producing, and responding. |
| CISA secure software attestation | `https://www.cisa.gov/resources-tools/resources/secure-software-development-attestation-form` | Enterprise/government buyers may ask for explicit secure-development attestation; Kolm should generate evidence rather than improvise answers. |

##### kolm.supply_chain_control_room.v3

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.supply_chain_control_room.v3",
  "release_id": "kolm-2026-05-25-local",
  "node": 22,
  "source": {
    "repo": "kolm-ai/kolm-stack",
    "commit": "required",
    "tree_status": "clean_or_declared_dirty",
    "source_manifest": "docs/source-manifest.json",
    "generated_inputs": "docs/generated-artifact-order.json"
  },
  "workflow": {
    "workflow_path": ".github/workflows/release.yml",
    "run_id": "required_for_ci",
    "runner_class": "github_hosted_or_hardened_self_hosted",
    "permissions": "minimal_declared",
    "oidc": "required_for_publish_or_attest",
    "action_policy": "sha_pinned_or_approved_moving_tag",
    "untrusted_input_policy": "required"
  },
  "artifact_subjects": [
    {
      "name": "kolm-stack",
      "type": "npm_package|container|binary|sdk|installer|docs_bundle|kolm_artifact|sbom",
      "channel": "npm|oci|github_release|public_site|registry|local_only",
      "path_or_ref": "required",
      "sha256": "required",
      "size_bytes": "required",
      "sbom_refs": ["cyclonedx", "spdx"],
      "license_refs": ["license_policy"],
      "provenance_ref": "slsa_or_in_toto",
      "attestation_ref": "github_or_sigstore",
      "signature_ref": "sigstore_or_detached",
      "verification_command": "required",
      "published_state": "published|dry_run|local_source_only|not_published"
    }
  ],
  "policy": {
    "vulnerability_sla": "docs/dependency-policy.json",
    "license_policy": "docs/license-policy.json",
    "package_channels": "docs/package-channel-registry.json",
    "workflow_security": "docs/workflow-security-registry.json",
    "artifact_retention": "docs/artifact-retention-policy.json",
    "incident_runbook": "docs/supply-chain-incident-runbook.md"
  },
  "customer_evidence": {
    "export": "reports/supply-chain/latest.json",
    "public_pages": ["/security", "/sbom", "/slsa", "/license"],
    "secret_values_included": false
  }
}
```

##### Node 22 V3 Artifact Class Matrix

| artifact class | required evidence | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| npm packages | Package manifest, lock/materials hash, trusted publishing or scoped token policy, provenance, install smoke, rollback/deprecate instructions, license report. | Cannot claim public package availability without registry URL, package version, provenance state, and clean install evidence. |
| Python packages | `pyproject.toml` or requirements, build backend, wheel/sdist digest, license/dependency policy, install/import smoke. | Cannot be marketed as installable unless wheel/sdist or explicit local-source instructions exist. |
| Rust/Cargo artifacts | `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock` where applicable, build target, crate or binary digest, license policy, reproducibility notes. | Runtime/native claims need target/platform matrix and provenance. |
| Swift/Kotlin/React Native SDKs | Package manager manifest, toolchain version, build smoke, publish/dry-run output, mobile platform support, rollback/deprecation policy. | SDK docs must distinguish source-available from published package channels. |
| VS Code/MCP/GitHub Actions | Extension/action manifest, marketplace or local-install state, permissions, input validation, versioning, signing/provenance where supported. | Developer tooling claims need install proof and safe input/secret boundaries. |
| Containers and workers | Dockerfile/compose inputs, base image digest, SBOM, image digest, vulnerability scan, provenance, runtime config, rollback. | Cannot claim production container readiness without base-image and published-image digest evidence. |
| Generated public artifacts | OpenAPI, API routes, product graph, docs, screenshots, readiness ledgers, codegraph. | Must link to source commands, input checksums, generation order, stale detection, and retention path. |
| `.kolm` artifacts | Artifact hash, receipt chain, manifest, model/data/license provenance, SBOM/AI-BOM where applicable, verifier command, registry/publisher state. | Marketplace or download pages must show whether the artifact is verified, illustrative, synthetic, or customer-specific. |
| SBOM/provenance bundles | CycloneDX/SPDX outputs, SLSA/in-toto predicates, GitHub/Sigstore attestations, signatures, transparency-log refs. | Evidence must be attached to exact subject digests and retained beyond transient CI artifact expiry. |

##### Node 22 V3 Workflow Security Matrix

| workflow control | current signal | completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| explicit permissions | Some workflows declare permissions; several do not. | Every workflow declares minimal top-level permissions and escalates only per job with reason. |
| OIDC/id-token | Current targeted scan found no `id-token: write`. | Publish, provenance, cloud deploy, and attestation workflows use OIDC where possible and avoid long-lived publish secrets. |
| action pinning | Current scan found zero 40-character SHA-pinned `uses:` refs. | Policy must require SHA pinning or a named allowlist for moving tags with update cadence and risk acceptance. |
| `@latest` usage | `sbom.yml` and `kolm-distill.yml` currently include `@latest`. | `@latest` cannot be used in release-critical paths unless sandboxed and explicitly non-blocking with pinned fallback. |
| `continue-on-error` | SBOM and distill workflows currently use `continue-on-error:true`. | Any non-blocking evidence step must emit a blocking absence check before release claims are made. |
| secrets in workflows | Several workflows use `KOLM_KEY`, `KOLM_API_KEY`, and `GITHUB_TOKEN`. | Each secret use must map to owner, scope, environment protection, fork safety, log redaction, and rotation policy. |
| local actions | Local actions wrap compile, publish, test, and verify. | Inputs must be validated, shell commands reviewed, outputs typed, and API-key handling secret-safe. |
| artifact retention | `sbom.yml` uploads SBOM artifacts with 90-day retention. | Customer evidence must be retained durably outside transient workflow artifacts and indexed by release ID. |

| W-SUPPLY-V3-015 | P1 | 22.4 | Add multi-ecosystem SBOM coverage. | CycloneDX/SPDX evidence spans Node, Python, Rust, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, VS Code, containers, workers, generated docs, and `.kolm` artifacts where applicable. |
| W-SUPPLY-V3-016 | P0 | 22.4 | Add SBOM emitter version lock. | SBOM generation records exact emitter versions and forbids unbounded `@latest` in release evidence unless explicitly sandboxed. |
