| W-SUPPLY-V3-024 | P0 | 43 | Add Node 22 completion certificate. | Build redline cannot relax until supply-chain contract, dependency/license policies, workflow registry, artifact provenance, SBOM/provenance/attestation reports, retention, and incident runbook are green. |

##### Node 22 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `22` remains open until current evidence proves all of the following:

- Every package/build manifest and generated artifact source is classified as canonical, generated, fixture, temp, archive, or release output.
- Every workflow and local action has owner, explicit minimal permissions, action-pin policy, secret boundary, OIDC/trusted-publish stance, and untrusted-input review.
- Every release-critical `@latest`, moving action tag, and `continue-on-error` evidence step is removed, sandboxed, or covered by a release-blocking absence check.
- Every package channel has published/dry-run/local-only state, digest, install smoke, support matrix, rollback/deprecation path, and customer-facing wording.
- Every artifact subject has digest, size, SBOM reference, license reference, provenance reference, signature/attestation reference, verifier command, and retention path.
- SBOM/provenance coverage spans npm, Python, Rust, Swift, Kotlin/Gradle, React Native, VS Code, workers, containers, browser assets, generated docs, and `.kolm` artifacts where applicable.
- Vulnerability exceptions have owner, rationale, expiry, recheck command, customer impact, and fail after expiry.
- Public trust pages and enterprise packets cannot imply package publication, SLSA level, attestation, SBOM completeness, or provenance beyond the current evidence packet.
- Supply-chain incident response can revoke, deprecate, replace, roll back, notify, and regenerate evidence after a compromised dependency, workflow, token, package, container, or artifact.

The relax condition for Node `22` V3 is: every installable, importable, runnable, downloadable, generated, auditable, and purchasable Kolm artifact has durable supply-chain evidence tied to exact source, builder, materials, subject digest, package channel, verification command, retention, rollback, and customer trust surfaces.

### Legal, IP, Data Rights, Model-Provider Terms, And Contract Evidence Ledger V1

Node `23` is the legal-operating-system branch of the master spec tree. It exists because Kolm is selling an AI infrastructure product that touches customer data, provider model traffic, distilled artifacts, healthcare workflows, enterprise procurement, open-source licensing, proprietary teachers, open-weight models, generated outputs, and benchmark claims. Those surfaces cannot be left as independent pages, tests, and assumptions. They need one versioned contract map that product, docs, account UI, CLI, sales, and release evidence all obey.

This is a research/specification edit only. It is not legal advice and does not edit contracts, pages, source code, workflows, or tests.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Area | Current evidence found | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Public legal pages | `public/terms.html`, `public/tos.html`, `public/privacy.html`, `public/dpa.html`, `public/baa.html`, `public/legal.html`, `public/legal-v2.html`, `public/license.html`, `public/subprocessors.html` if present, `public/hipaa-mapping.html` | Legal content exists, but final completion needs one canonical legal source of truth, effective-date ledger, review owner, and drift checks across duplicate pages. |
| Contract and procurement pages | `public/enterprise.html`, `public/security.html`, `public/slsa.html`, `public/sbom.html`, `public/baa.html`, `public/dpa.html`, `public/legal/liability.html`, `public/legal/architecture.html`, `public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html` | Enterprise buyers need a cohesive packet that distinguishes implemented controls, contractual commitments, optional addenda, and external certifications not yet granted. |
| Privacy and data rights implementation | `src/privacy-membrane.js`, `tests/wave370-privacy.test.js`, `tests/wave409b-privacy-failclosed.test.js`, `public/account/privacy-events.html`, `public/docs/privacy.html` | Privacy controls exist in code and account docs, but legal completion requires linking them to data categories, lawful bases, deletion/export paths, retention, subprocessors, and DSAR workflows. |
| Copyright and capture rights | `src/capture-copyright-filter.js`, `src/copyright-detector.js`, `tests/wave708-copyright-and-geo.test.js`, `tests/wave750-copyright.test.js`, `public/docs/copyright-scan.html` | Heuristic copyright risk scanners exist, but final state needs a rights pipeline that covers capture eligibility, quarantine, review, training exclusion, appeal, deletion, and audit evidence. |
| Teacher/provider source policy | `tests/wave708-teacher-source-policy.test.js`, `src/distill-pipeline.js`, `public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html`, `public/legal/distillation-tos-clarity.html` | The code can classify proprietary versus open-weight teachers, but product/legal completion requires mapping every provider and model to allowed capture, distill, store, resale, and derivative-artifact rules. |
| Geo and sanctions posture | `tests/wave708-copyright-and-geo.test.js`, `src/auth.js`, signup geo-fence behavior | A denylist exists for signup, but final state needs export-control ownership, source of country policy, update cadence, appeal path, and account/admin evidence. |
| Healthcare/HIPAA surfaces | `public/baa.html`, `public/hipaa-mapping.html`, `public/articles/hipaa-ai-from-prompts.html`, `public/articles/hipaa-on-device.html`, `public/cookbook/hipaa-summarizer.html` | HIPAA and BAA copy exists, but final state must distinguish covered-entity customer obligations from Kolm business-associate obligations and live certification status. |
| Claims and advertising controls | `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/fix-forbidden-claims.cjs`, `docs/product-sota-readiness.json`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` | Claim-scope tooling exists, but final legal state requires every benchmark, savings, compliance, accuracy, safety, and "AI" claim to map to substantiation evidence and current terms. |
| Brand/legal entity drift | Public pages still show both `kolm.ai` and references such as `Kolmogorov Stack, Inc.` or repository URLs containing `kolmogorov-stack` | Completion must separate product brand from legal entity truth. If the legal entity remains `Kolmogorov Stack, Inc.`, contracts may need it; product copy should not drift. If the legal entity changes, legal pages must change through counsel-reviewed migration. |
| Text integrity | Several legal/security files and comments surfaced mojibake-like separators such as `U+7E5A`, `??`, or replacement artifacts in scanned output | Legal docs are high-trust surfaces; final state needs text-integrity gates for metadata, headings, copied clauses, dates, symbols, and jurisdiction names. |

#### Official References And Legal-Posture Inputs

| Reference | URL | What Kolm should bind into the product spec |
|---|---|---|
| EU AI Act and GPAI obligations | `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32024R1689` and `https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/factpages/general-purpose-ai-obligations-under-ai-act` | Classify whether Kolm is a provider, deployer, distributor, importer, or tool provider per surface. Track GPAI obligations for technical documentation, copyright policy, training-content summary, systemic-risk notification, incident reporting, and cybersecurity where applicable. |
| GDPR Article 28 | `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32016R0679` | DPA must define controller/processor roles, subject matter, duration, processing purpose, categories of data, subprocessors, security measures, assistance, return/deletion, audit, and instructions. |
| CCPA/CPRA | `https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa` and `https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa/regs` | Privacy surfaces must support notice at collection, access, deletion, correction, opt-out/sale/share posture, sensitive-data limits where applicable, non-discrimination, and request verification. |
| HIPAA business associate guidance | `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/guidance/business-associates/index.html` and `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/covered-entities/sample-business-associate-agreement-provisions/index.html` | BAA must define permitted PHI uses, safeguards, breach reporting, subcontractor flow-down, access/amendment/accounting assistance, HHS access, termination return/destruction, and material-breach termination. |
| U.S. Copyright Office AI reports | `https://www.copyright.gov/ai/` | Product policy must distinguish AI-generated outputs, human authorship, digital replicas, AI training, copyrightability, and capture/training-rights risk. |
| FTC AI enforcement and claim posture | `https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence` and `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes` | Marketing copy, benchmark claims, legal/medical/professional-service claims, savings claims, AI-detection claims, and testimonials must be substantiated and scoped. |
| Open-source and package licensing | `https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0` and `https://spdx.org/licenses/` | Compiler, SDK, package, generated artifact, and dependency licenses need clear source, notices, trademark boundaries, patent terms, and redistribution limits. |

#### Legal Redlines

1. Do not blindly replace every legal occurrence of `Kolmogorov Stack, Inc.` with `Kolm` unless the legal entity has actually changed. Product brand can be `Kolm`; contract party identity must remain legally true.
2. Do not claim `.kolm artifacts are owned by the customer` without defining boundaries for customer inputs, captured provider outputs, open-weight licenses, proprietary teacher outputs, eval packs, generated weights, marketplace recipes, and third-party components.
3. Do not imply hosted Kolm is always a processor. Some surfaces may act as controller, processor, subprocessor, independent provider, or purely local software depending on deployment and data flow.
4. Do not market HIPAA, SOC 2, ISO, FedRAMP, GDPR, or EU AI Act compliance as fully complete unless the exact certification, contract, audit packet, or implementation evidence exists and is current.
5. Do not let legal pages duplicate each other with different dates, terms, plan names, liability caps, breach windows, subprocessors, or support commitments.
6. Do not let DPA/BAA pages say data is processed only through the gateway if account, support, billing, analytics, registry, crash logs, telemetry, object storage, or email workflows also process data.
7. Do not let teacher-source policy stop at a binary proprietary/open-weight label. Each provider/model needs rules for capture, storage, distillation, derived artifacts, eval publication, benchmark reuse, resale, and customer export.
8. Do not treat copyright detection as legal determination. The current detectors are heuristics and must feed review/quarantine workflows, not definitive infringement labels.
9. Do not claim AI-generated content is copyrightable, uncopyrightable, safe to use, or free to sell without jurisdiction-specific context and human-authorship metadata.
10. Do not use customer captures, feedback, prompts, completions, files, or outputs for Kolm model improvement unless the terms, UI consent, audit log, deletion path, and opt-out language all say so.
11. Do not publish fake or unverified social proof, case studies, logos, savings numbers, benchmark superiority, "best" claims, or medical/legal accuracy claims.
12. Do not let signup, account, pricing, checkout, enterprise inquiry, or sales docs create conflicting promises about refunds, SLA, support, privacy, training use, or liability.
13. Do not ship legal pages with mojibake, broken metadata, stale effective dates, schema descriptions like only `kolm.ai`, or old navigation patterns; legal pages are procurement UI.
14. Do not leave export-control/sanctions denylist ownership ambiguous. The source, review cadence, escalation path, and blocked-country customer message must be explicit.
15. Do not rely on one static public page as legal truth. Legal state must be machine-readable enough for account UI, CLI, docs, API metadata, and sales packets to share.

#### Canonical Legal Evidence Packet

Every release that changes product behavior, privacy posture, billing, package distribution, marketplace behavior, capture/training flow, provider routing, compliance posture, or enterprise commitments should update or verify this evidence shape:

```json
{
  "legal_packet_version": "YYYY.MM.DD-N",
  "effective_dates": {
    "terms": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "privacy": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "dpa": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "baa": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "subprocessors": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "contract_parties": {
    "product_brand": "Kolm",
    "website": "kolm.ai",
    "legal_entity": "counsel_verified_name",
    "contracting_address": "counsel_verified_address"
  },
  "data_categories": [
    {
      "category": "captures",
      "location": "customer_local_or_customer_object_store",
      "role": "processor|controller|local_software|not_processed_by_kolm",
      "retention": "policy_ref",
      "deletion_path": "api_or_cli_ref",
      "training_use": "never|customer_opt_in|contracted"
    }
  ],
  "rights_and_ip": {
    "customer_inputs": "policy_ref",
    "provider_outputs": "provider_terms_matrix_ref",
    "derived_artifacts": "artifact_ownership_policy_ref",
    "open_weight_licenses": "license_matrix_ref",
    "marketplace_resale": "marketplace_terms_ref"
  },
  "regulatory_posture": {
    "gdpr": "dpa_ref",
    "ccpa_cpra": "privacy_rights_ref",
    "hipaa": "baa_ref_or_not_available",
    "eu_ai_act": "role_classification_ref",
    "export_controls": "geo_policy_ref",
    "copyright": "capture_rights_policy_ref"
  },
  "claims": {
    "claim_scope_gate": "passed",
    "benchmark_claims": "evidence_refs",
    "savings_claims": "methodology_refs",
    "compliance_claims": "certification_or_closeout_refs",
    "testimonials": "permission_refs"
  },
  "customer_packet": {
    "public_pages": ["terms", "privacy", "dpa", "baa", "subprocessors", "security", "license"],
    "enterprise_export": "path_or_route",
    "review_owner": "role",
    "last_counsel_review": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `23.1` Legal entity, brand, terms, privacy, DPA, BAA, order forms, and contracting authority | One canonical legal-source file drives public legal pages, account legal status, sales packets, effective dates, entity names, contact emails, governing law, liability caps, order-form hierarchy, and change notices. |
| `23.2` Customer data, captures, artifacts, derived models, evals, feedback, and ownership boundaries | Every data and artifact class has owner, role, lawful basis or contract basis, storage location, retention, deletion/export path, training-use status, marketplace status, and UI disclosure. |
| `23.3` Provider terms, teacher-source policy, open-weight licenses, copyright quarantine, and resale rights | Every provider/model/source class has an allowed-use matrix covering route, capture, store, train, distill, compile, benchmark, publish, resell, and delete. |
| `23.4` Privacy rights, subprocessors, SCCs, HIPAA, CCPA/CPRA, GDPR, AI Act, and regional obligations | Legal pages and product controls implement DSAR/export/delete/correct/opt-out workflows, subprocessor notice, SCCs, BAA terms, AI Act role classification, and regional data handling. |
| `23.5` Marketing claims, benchmarks, warranties, liability, indemnity, support, SLA, and procurement evidence | Every claim, warranty, support promise, SLA, refund statement, indemnity statement, benchmark, and savings number links to substantiation evidence and the controlling contract text. |
| `23.6` Legal change management, review cadence, notice workflows, incident response, and customer exports | Legal changes have owners, counsel review state, diffable history, customer notice workflow, rollback plan, incident/legal-hold runbook, and exportable enterprise evidence packet. |

#### Node 23 Relax Redline

Node `23` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- What is the exact legal entity, product brand, contracting party, governing law, notice address, and effective-date hierarchy for every customer flow?
- Which data classes does Kolm process, where do they live, who owns them, who controls them, how long are they retained, how are they deleted, and can customers export proof?
- Which provider/model outputs can be captured, distilled, stored, benchmarked, published, resold, or used to improve Kolm?
- Which `.kolm` artifact contents belong to the customer, which inherit third-party restrictions, and which cannot enter the marketplace?
- Which laws and contract frameworks apply to each deployment mode: local-only, hosted gateway, BYOC, enterprise single-tenant, healthcare, EU customer, marketplace, and public benchmark?
- Can legal, sales, docs, account UI, API metadata, CLI, and product graph all report the same DPA/BAA/privacy/claim/compliance state?
- Are every benchmark, savings number, case study, logo, customer quote, and compliance claim backed by current permission and substantiation evidence?
- Can Kolm notify customers, freeze data, preserve legal hold, revoke artifacts, purge captures, update subprocessors, and handle regulator/customer inquiries from a documented workflow?

The relax condition for node `23` is not "legal pages exist." It is: every product promise involving data, AI outputs, model providers, artifacts, ownership, compliance, privacy rights, warranties, and enterprise procurement is contractually scoped, technically enforced where possible, customer-visible, evidence-backed, and reviewable by counsel.

#### Node 23 V2 Legal, IP, Provider Terms, And Claim Substantiation Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `23` and does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only, not legal advice. It does not edit contracts, public pages, source code, provider integrations, account UI, generated docs, or tests while other agents are working.

Kolm is not legally complete because legal pages exist. It is complete only when every claim about customer data, provider traffic, captured outputs, distilled artifacts, open-weight models, marketplace recipes, privacy rights, healthcare use, enterprise procurement, savings, benchmarks, support, and compliance is tied to a versioned legal source, technical enforcement path, customer-visible disclosure, evidence packet, and review owner.

##### Node 23 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| public legal pages | `public/terms.html`, `public/tos.html`, `public/privacy.html`, `public/dpa.html`, `public/baa.html`, `public/legal.html`, `public/legal-v2.html`, `public/license.html`, `public/subprocessors.html`, `public/hipaa-mapping.html`. | Legal surfaces exist but are distributed. Completion requires one canonical legal source and drift checks across duplicate/overlapping pages. |
| procurement/trust pages | `public/security.html`, `public/slsa.html`, `public/sbom.html`, `public/legal/architecture.html`, `public/legal/liability.html`, `public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html`, `public/legal/distillation-tos-clarity.html`. | Enterprise evidence exists in public pages, but it must distinguish implemented controls from certifications, external releases, and closeouts. |
| copyright/right modules | `src/capture-copyright-filter.js`: 7,931 bytes; `src/copyright-detector.js`: 13,664 bytes. | Rights heuristics exist; legal completion needs quarantine, review, exclusion, appeal, deletion, and audit workflows. |
| provider/model modules | `src/provider-registry.js`: 5,460 bytes; `src/model-registry.js`: 65,422 bytes; `src/distill-pipeline.js`: 55,081 bytes. | Provider/model capability data exists; completion needs allowed-use terms per provider/model for route, capture, distill, train, benchmark, publish, resell, and delete. |
| claim tooling | `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `docs/product-sota-readiness.json`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`. | Claim-scope gates exist. Legal completion needs claim-substantiation evidence with owner, date, source, expiry, and customer-visible caveats. |
| absent registries | `docs/legal-evidence-packet.json`, `docs/provider-terms-matrix.json`, `docs/artifact-rights-policy.json`, `docs/data-processing-roles.json`, `docs/copyright-capture-policy.json`, `docs/claim-substantiation-ledger.json`, `docs/compliance-claim-registry.json`, `docs/contract-surface-registry.json` are absent. | Legal truth is not yet machine-readable enough for account, CLI, docs, sales, release, and product graph parity. |

##### Node 23 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| EU AI Act GPAI obligations | `https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/factpages/general-purpose-ai-obligations-under-ai-act` | For any surface that could make Kolm a GPAI model provider or support provider, track technical documentation, copyright policy, training-content summary, systemic-risk obligations, incident reporting, and cybersecurity scope. |
| GDPR Article 28 | `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32016R0679` | DPA and account/legal packet must define controller/processor roles, instructions, subprocessors, security, assistance, return/deletion, audit, and international transfer posture. |
| CCPA/CPRA | `https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa` | Consumer/business privacy requests need notice, access, deletion, correction, opt-out/share posture, sensitive-data posture, verification, and non-discrimination language where applicable. |
| HHS HIPAA business associates | `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/guidance/business-associates/index.html` | BAA posture must cover permitted PHI use, safeguards, breach reporting, subcontractor flowdown, access/amendment/accounting assistance, termination, and return/destruction. |
| U.S. Copyright Office AI resources | `https://www.copyright.gov/ai/` | AI output, training, authorship, and digital-replica issues need scoped policy; copyright detectors must not be treated as legal determinations. |
| FTC AI guidance and enforcement | `https://www.ftc.gov/industry/technology/artificial-intelligence` | AI claims, savings claims, accuracy claims, testimonials, detection claims, and professional-use promises must be truthful, substantiated, non-deceptive, and scoped. |
| SPDX/license identifiers | `https://spdx.org/licenses/` | Open-source, model, dataset, SDK, and generated-artifact licensing should use standardized identifiers and notices. |

##### Node 23 V2 Legal Evidence Contract

Every legal claim, data class, provider rule, and contract surface must map to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-legal-evidence-v2",
  "policy_id": "provider_terms_openai_capture_distill",
  "owner": "legal-product",
  "review_state": "needs_counsel_review",
  "effective_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "last_reviewed": null,
  "surfaces": ["public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html", "src/distill-pipeline.js", "account/distill"],
  "applies_to": {
    "deployment_modes": ["hosted", "byoc", "local"],
    "customer_tiers": ["free", "pro", "team", "enterprise"],
    "providers": ["openai"],
    "data_classes": ["prompt", "completion", "capture_metadata", "derived_artifact"]
  },
  "allowed_uses": {
    "route": "allowed",
    "capture": "conditional",
    "store": "conditional",
    "distill": "needs_contract_or_customer_provider_terms",
    "benchmark": "needs_review",
    "publish": "blocked",
    "resell": "blocked"
  },
  "technical_controls": {
    "gate": "teacher_source_policy",
    "receipt_fields": ["provider", "model", "terms_policy_id", "allowed_use_state"],
    "account_visible": true,
    "cli_visible": true
  },
  "customer_disclosure": {
    "public_page": "public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html",
    "account_page": "account/distill",
    "sales_packet": "enterprise_legal_packet"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "source_urls": [],
    "source_paths": [],
    "tests": [],
    "counsel_notes": "private_reference_only"
  },
  "expiry": {
    "review_due": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "fails_claim_after": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  }
}
```

##### Node 23 V2 Provider And Artifact Rights Matrix

| class | required policy fields | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| proprietary API provider output | provider, model, terms URL/date, customer contract dependency, capture permission, storage permission, distill permission, benchmark permission, resale/export limits. | Distill/capture/benchmark flows must stamp the policy ID and block or warn when terms are unknown. |
| open-weight model | license ID, model card, allowed commercial use, derivative weights, attribution, redistribution, evaluation/publication, safety restrictions. | Artifact builds and marketplace publishes must fail or scope unsupported licenses. |
| customer BYOC provider | customer owns contract, Kolm role, credential boundary, terms responsibility, audit responsibility, indemnity/support boundary. | UI/terms must say what Kolm can and cannot verify. |
| customer captures | input ownership, output ownership, provider rights, privacy class, consent/training-use state, retention, delete/export path. | Customer cannot be told they own "everything" without restrictions and source policy. |
| distilled artifact | source data, teacher outputs, student architecture, weights, recipe, evals, dependencies, licenses, redistribution policy. | Artifact manifest must expose rights state, not just technical hash. |
| marketplace recipe | author permission, dependency licenses, dataset/model rights, safety policy, resale terms, takedown path, support owner. | Marketplace publish requires rights receipt and takedown process. |
| benchmark result | dataset license, model/provider terms, date, environment, scoring method, allowed publication, caveats. | Public benchmark/savings claims need substantiation record and expiry. |

##### Node 23 V2 Claim Substantiation Contract

Every marketing, docs, account, sales, benchmark, legal, or trust claim must have:

| field | requirement |
|---|---|
| claim text | Exact visible claim or normalized claim family. |
| claim class | savings, speed, accuracy, privacy, security, compliance, certification, benchmark, support, SLA, ownership, provider portability, any-device, case study, testimonial, logo, AI safety. |
| evidence | Source data, benchmark, screenshot, audit report, contract, certification, permission, customer approval, or closeout ledger reference. |
| scope | Environment, date, dataset, model/provider, customer tier, deployment mode, region, and caveats. |
| owner | Product/legal/marketing/security/ML owner responsible for freshness. |
| expiry | Review due date or automatic de-scope date. |
| enforcement | Claim-scope audit, copy generator, account UI gate, docs gate, release gate, or sales-packet gate. |
| customer-visible caveat | Plain-language scope visible near the claim when needed. |

Unsupported "best", "guaranteed", "certified", "HIPAA compliant", "SOC 2", "run anywhere", "cuts costs", "production-ready", "ownership", "portable", "safe", and "auditable" claims must be blocked unless the ledger proves them at the exact scope used.

##### Node 23 V2 Relax Redline

Node `23` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. legal entity, product brand, contracting party, effective dates, notice addresses, hierarchy, and review owners are canonicalized;
2. public legal pages, account UI, docs, API metadata, CLI, sales packet, and product graph read the same legal state;
3. every data class has processing role, storage, retention, deletion/export, training-use, subprocessor, and DSAR behavior;
4. every provider/model/source has allowed route/capture/store/distill/train/benchmark/publish/resell/export/delete terms;
5. artifact ownership and redistribution are scoped by customer inputs, provider outputs, open-weight licenses, recipes, evals, dependencies, datasets, and marketplace policy;
6. copyright flags lead to review/quarantine/exclusion/purge workflows and are never treated as legal verdicts;
7. GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, HIPAA/BAA, EU AI Act, geo/sanctions/export, and residency obligations are mapped to product controls and closeouts;
8. warranties, liability, indemnity, SLA, refunds, support commitments, and enterprise order-form language do not conflict across surfaces;
9. every savings, speed, accuracy, compliance, certification, benchmark, support, ownership, portability, any-device, testimonial, logo, and case-study claim has evidence, scope, owner, expiry, and visible caveat where needed;
10. legal changes, customer notice, legal hold, regulator/customer inquiries, and enterprise exports have repeatable workflows.

If any item fails, Kolm may have legal pages, but it is not yet legally coherent enough for state-of-the-art enterprise AI infrastructure.

#### Node 23 V3 Legal Evidence Control Plane, Provider Terms, And Data-Rights Contract

This V3 addendum maintains master spec tree node `23`. It does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only, not legal advice. It does not edit contracts, public pages, source code, provider integrations, account UI, generated docs, or tests while other agents are working. Its purpose is to make legal completeness testable: every provider route, model license, customer-output rule, capture/training-use rule, benchmark claim, healthcare addendum, subprocessor, geo/export-control policy, marketplace artifact, and public claim must resolve to a dated evidence packet before the build redline in node `43` can be relaxed.

##### Node 23 V3 Fresh Evidence Register

| evidence class | current observed evidence | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| public legal pages | `public/terms.html` 31,835 bytes, 502 lines, 79 legal hits; `public/privacy.html` 26,197 bytes, 412 lines, 53 hits; `public/acceptable-use.html` 12,402 bytes, 192 lines, 20 hits; `public/dpa.html` 13,697 bytes, 206 lines, 29 hits; `public/baa.html` 28,687 bytes, 397 lines, 114 hits; `public/subprocessors.html` 23,991 bytes, 445 lines, 61 hits; `public/security.html` 60,729 bytes, 723 lines, 110 hits; `public/trust.html` 8,564 bytes, 128 lines, 0 hits; `public/license.html` 22,318 bytes, 336 lines, 91 hits; `public/eu.html` 19,663 bytes, 271 lines, 53 hits; `public/hipaa-mapping.html` 46,890 bytes, 782 lines, 68 hits; `public/copyright.html` is missing. | Existing legal pages are broad, but they are not enough by themselves. V3 requires one generated contract matrix, effective-date ledger, supersession map, and missing-page gate so legal copy cannot drift by page. |
| public legal subfolder | `public/legal/architecture.html` 15,982 bytes, `public/legal/distillation-tos-clarity.html` 12,964 bytes, `public/legal/liability.html` 12,453 bytes, `public/legal/teacher-source-disclosure.html` 11,625 bytes. | The legal subfolder must be tied to node `23` as supporting analysis, not free-floating proof. Each page needs source, review owner, linked contract field, and date. |
| docs legal sources | `docs/LEGAL.md` missing; `docs/SECURITY.md` missing; `docs/kolm-format-v1.md` exists at 4,450 bytes, 93 lines, 9 legal/license hits; `docs/product-sota-readiness.json` exists at 39,673 bytes, 968 lines, 47 hits; `docs/product-readiness-closeout.md` exists at 11,725 bytes, 306 lines, 12 hits. | The repo needs canonical legal-source docs or generated legal ledgers so public pages, readiness, and claims are not maintained only as HTML islands. |
| legal-relevant backend modules | `src/privacy-membrane.js` 39,106 bytes; `src/capture-copyright-filter.js` 7,931 bytes; `src/copyright-detector.js` 13,664 bytes; `src/provider-registry.js` 5,460 bytes; `src/reg-eu-aiact-docs.js` 19,988 bytes; `src/site-license.js` 6,354 bytes; plus `src/ai-act-export.js`, `src/ai-act-risk.js`, `src/artifact.js` license normalization and license references. | Runtime behavior already touches legal boundaries. V3 requires the code paths to consume generated provider/model/data-rights policy rather than relying on prose claims. |
| legal-relevant tests | Existing coverage includes `wave279-site-license`, `wave370-privacy`, `wave409b-privacy-failclosed`, `wave493-trust-copy-honesty`, `wave503-capture-provider-route-honesty`, `wave557-provider-gemma-model-scope`, `wave592-compliance-certification-packet`, `wave708-copyright-and-geo`, `wave708-teacher-source-policy`, `wave750-copyright`, `wave766-eu-ai-act`, and `wc04-privacy-membrane`. | Tests exist, but V3 requires them to verify a single contract source: provider terms, model licenses, data rights, copyright, geo controls, healthcare roles, and claim substantiation. |
| currently missing registries | `docs/legal-contract-matrix.json`, `docs/provider-terms-registry.json`, `docs/model-license-registry.json`, `docs/data-rights-registry.json`, `docs/training-use-policy.json`, `docs/copyright-capture-policy.json`, `docs/output-ownership-policy.json`, `docs/subprocessor-registry.json`, `docs/geo-export-control-policy.json`, `docs/legal-claim-substantiation.json`, `docs/legal-evidence-packet.json`, `reports/legal`, `reports/provider-terms`, `reports/data-rights`. | These are the canonical V3 build artifacts. Until they exist, node `23` must remain redline-controlled and cannot be considered complete. |
| discovered legal/provider assets | Search also found `public/compliance/eu-ai-act.html`, `public/docs/copyright-scan.html`, `public/docs/privacy.html`, `src/airgap-teacher.js`, teacher-source modules, `src/artifact-lineage.js`, `src/artifact.js` license fields, and registry-pack legal artifacts. | Existing evidence is scattered. The master spec tree needs the generated matrix to collect and deduplicate it before release. |

##### Node 23 V3 External Baselines

These baselines are not embedded as legal advice. They define the minimum facts that node `23` must track as dated, counsel-reviewed references.

| baseline | official source | imported control requirement |
|---|---|---|
| EU AI Act | `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32024R1689` and `https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai` | Every EU-facing AI feature needs role classification, risk class, technical documentation owner, transparency obligations, deployer/provider split, and evidence export mapping. |
| GDPR processor contract baseline | `https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj` | Hosted processing needs controller/processor roles, Article 28 terms, subprocessors, cross-border transfer basis, deletion/export rules, and data-subject workflow evidence. |
| HIPAA BAA baseline | `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/covered-entities/sample-business-associate-agreement-provisions/index.html` and `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/guidance/business-associates/index.html` | Healthcare mode needs BAA status, permitted uses/disclosures, safeguards, breach workflow, subcontractor flowdown, return/destroy rules, and explicit PHI service boundaries. |
| FTC AI claim substantiation | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/blog/2023/02/keep-your-ai-claims-check` and `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes` | Public claims about savings, accuracy, safety, autonomy, compliance, benchmarks, and "AI" capability need evidence ID, scope, comparison set, date, owner, expiry, and review status. |
| US Copyright Office AI baseline | `https://www.copyright.gov/ai/` | Capture, training, output, artifact packaging, marketplace, and synthetic data flows need rights source, license status, provenance, human-review threshold, quarantine state, and takedown process. |
| OpenAI provider terms | `https://openai.com/policies/business-terms/` | Provider use must track output ownership, input rights, service restrictions, model-development restrictions, customer-content use, PHI addendum boundaries, and third-party service terms. |
| Anthropic provider terms | `https://www.anthropic.com/legal/commercial-terms` | Provider use must track customer content, output rights, acceptable use, training/data-use commitments, resale or competitive-use restrictions, and model-family scope. |
| Google Gemini API terms | `https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/terms` | Provider use must track API-specific use restrictions, safety policy, data-use rules, model scope, terms date, and user/developer obligations. |
| Meta Llama/open-weight model licenses | `https://www.llama.com/llama3/license/` and current model-family license pages | Open-weight import, distillation, redistribution, marketplace, and device deployment need per-model license, acceptable-use policy, attribution, derivative limits, and commercial restrictions. |
| Apache License 2.0 | `https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0` | Open-source components and permissive model/tooling licenses need SPDX ID, notices, attribution, patent terms, redistribution terms, and bundled license evidence. |

##### Node 23 V3 Machine Contract

Every generated legal artifact should be reducible to this shape:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.legal_evidence_control_plane.v3",
  "node": 23,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "legal_documents": [
    {
      "id": "terms",
      "path": "public/terms.html",
      "effective_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "supersedes": null,
      "owner": "legal",
      "counsel_verified": false,
      "source_of_truth": "docs/legal-contract-matrix.json"
    }
  ],
  "data_roles": [
    {
      "mode": "hosted_gateway",
      "customer_role": "controller",
      "kolm_role": "processor",
      "subprocessors_required": true,
      "dpa_required": true,
      "baa_required_for_phi": true,
      "export_delete_sla": "defined_in_policy"
    }
  ],
  "provider_terms": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "models": ["provider_model_ids"],
      "terms_url": "https://openai.com/policies/business-terms/",
      "terms_checked_at": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "data_usage": "registry_value",
      "training_allowed": "allow_or_deny_or_review",
      "output_rights": "registry_value",
      "distillation_allowed": "allow_or_deny_or_review",
      "resale_allowed": "allow_or_deny_or_review",
      "benchmark_publication_allowed": "allow_or_deny_or_review",
      "review_required": true
    }
  ],
  "model_licenses": [
    {
      "model": "model_id",
      "license": "SPDX_or_custom",
      "license_url": "https://example.invalid/license",
      "allowed_uses": ["inference"],
      "forbidden_uses": ["unknown"],
      "attribution_required": false,
      "derivative_allowed": "review",
      "redistribution_allowed": "review",
      "commercial_use_allowed": "review",
      "safety_policy": "provider_or_model_policy_id"
    }
  ],
  "capture_training_use": [
    {
      "data_class": "prompt_completion_pair",
      "basis": "customer_instruction_or_contract",
      "consent_required": true,
      "opt_out_supported": true,
      "quarantine_required_for_unknown_rights": true,
      "deletion_supported": true,
      "retention_policy": "policy_id",
      "audit_event": "capture_rights_checked"
    }
  ],
  "claims": [
    {
      "claim_id": "claim_savings_percent_example",
      "surface": "public_homepage",
      "substantiation_id": "evidence_id",
      "owner": "product_marketing",
      "evidence_paths": ["reports/legal/claims/claim_savings_percent_example.json"],
      "expires_at": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "status": "blocked_until_evidence"
    }
  ]
}
```

##### Node 23 V3 Legal Surface Ownership Matrix

| surface | V3 owner inside node 23 | required generated evidence |
|---|---|---|
| terms | Contract source of truth, effective date, supersession chain, plan terms, enterprise exceptions, refund/payment interactions. | `docs/legal-contract-matrix.json`, page hash, counsel-review state. |
| privacy | Data categories, product modes, retention, deletion, support access, telemetry, subprocessors, cross-border transfers. | `docs/data-rights-registry.json`, DPA map, DSAR workflow evidence. |
| DPA | GDPR role mapping, processor instructions, subprocessor flowdown, transfer mechanism, deletion/export obligations. | DPA clause map and subprocessor registry link. |
| BAA | Covered-entity/business-associate boundary, PHI service scope, permitted uses, breach workflow, subcontractors, return/destroy rules. | `reports/legal/baa-evidence-packet.json` and healthcare mode gate. |
| subprocessors | Vendor, service purpose, data category, region, transfer mechanism, terms URL, last-reviewed date, customer notice status. | `docs/subprocessor-registry.json` and public page generator. |
| acceptable use | Prohibited uses, provider-policy references, safety enforcement, account suspension triggers, marketplace removal triggers. | Acceptable-use matrix tied to provider/model route policy. |
| license | Site license, SDK/source license, .kolm format license, artifact terms, marketplace artifact license, notices. | `docs/model-license-registry.json` plus OSS notice bundle. |
| legal subpages | Architecture, distillation terms clarity, liability, teacher source disclosure, copyright scan, EU/AI Act support pages. | Source-to-page map with review owner and node pointer. |
| provider terms | Terms URL, checked date, model scope, output rights, training rights, distillation rights, benchmarking rights, resale rights. | `docs/provider-terms-registry.json` and route admission gate. |
| model licenses | SPDX/custom license, acceptable use, attribution, derivatives, redistribution, commercial use, weights packaging, device deploy. | `docs/model-license-registry.json` and artifact manifest license fields. |
| claims | All public savings, quality, benchmark, safety, compliance, model support, and "state of art" claims. | `docs/legal-claim-substantiation.json` with expiry and owner. |
| geo/export controls | Region availability, sanctions/export review, data residency, model/license geography restrictions. | `docs/geo-export-control-policy.json` and deploy/gateway guard. |
| copyright | Capture rights, source provenance, training authorization, quarantine, takedown, marketplace rights, output ownership. | `docs/copyright-capture-policy.json` and copyright scan reports. |

##### Node 23 V3 Provider And Model Terms Matrix

| provider/model class | required fields | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI | terms URL, checked date, model list, output ownership, input rights, competitive/model-development restriction, PHI addendum boundary, distill/eval/benchmark policy, resale policy. | Any OpenAI-backed capture, distill, eval, benchmark, or marketplace path must be `allow`, `deny`, or `legal_review`; `unknown` blocks release. |
| Anthropic | terms URL, checked date, model list, output rights, customer content rules, acceptable use, training/data-use commitments, distill/eval/benchmark policy, resale policy. | Any Anthropic route must expose the same rights fields as OpenAI so account, CLI, and API docs can compare providers honestly. |
| Google Gemini | Gemini API terms URL, checked date, model list, safety policy, data-use rules, user/developer obligations, output/use restrictions, benchmark/distill policy. | Any Gemini route must keep provider-specific terms explicit instead of inheriting another provider's policy. |
| Meta/Llama/open weights | license URL, model-family version, acceptable-use policy, commercial terms, derivative/redistribution rights, attribution, hosted/local/device scope. | Open-weight import, distillation, registry publish, device deploy, and marketplace export must fail closed until license fields are known. |
| local models/Ollama/llama.cpp | model source, license, weights hash, runtime license, user-provided path, redistribution status, artifact export scope. | Local-only mode may run unknown local weights, but cannot market, publish, benchmark, or redistribute them without license classification. |
| BYOK customer provider | customer's provider account, customer responsibility, data role, terms delegation, provider chosen, model chosen, rights assertion. | BYOK flows must record customer instruction and avoid Kolm claiming provider rights beyond the customer's contract. |
| AWS Bedrock, Vertex, Azure, and other hosted providers | cloud/provider terms URL, model provider passthrough terms, region, data-use rules, output rights, HIPAA/BAA status, marketplace/resale limits. | Cloud-provider aggregation must preserve both cloud terms and underlying model-provider terms. |

##### Node 23 V3 Data Rights And Training Use Matrix

| data class | default rights question | required control |
|---|---|---|
| prompts | Did the customer have rights to provide the input and did it contain sensitive or third-party content? | Input-rights representation, sensitivity class, quarantine on unknown rights, deletion/export link. |
| completions | Who owns the output and can it be stored, evaluated, distilled, reused, or redistributed? | Output ownership policy, provider-specific limitations, similarity disclaimer, training-use gate. |
| files/uploads | Does the file contain copyrighted, PHI, PII, trade secret, or third-party data? | Upload provenance, data classification, copyright filter, privacy membrane, retention policy. |
| evals | Can eval examples and labels be stored, shared, benchmarked, or used to tune models? | Eval data-rights tag, benchmark publication permission, anonymization state. |
| feedback | Is user feedback usable for product improvement, model tuning, K-score calibration, or only support? | Feedback rights basis, opt-out state, use class, retention. |
| logs | Are request, provider, latency, cost, trace, and error logs personal data or confidential data? | Log minimization, secret redaction, retention, subprocessor and region tag. |
| derived artifacts | Does the `.kolm` artifact embed data, weights, prompts, evals, traces, or distilled behavior subject to limits? | Artifact rights manifest, source lineage, license block, export/marketplace gate. |
| model weights | Are weights proprietary, open, customer-owned, fine-tuned, distilled, or generated from provider outputs? | Model license registry, derivative rights, redistribution rights, device deploy scope. |
| marketplace artifacts | Can an artifact be sold, shared, downloaded, composed, or transferred to another tenant? | Marketplace legal metadata, takedown workflow, license acceptance, provenance proof. |
| support data | Can support tickets, screenshots, traces, or debug bundles include confidential, PII, PHI, or provider data? | Support-access policy, redaction, retention, access log, deletion. |
| billing data | Which billing/customer metadata is needed for payments, tax, fraud, sales, and audits? | Privacy category, processor list, retention, export/delete exception. |
| telemetry | Can product telemetry be collected in local, air-gapped, healthcare, enterprise, or BYOC modes? | Telemetry mode flag, opt-out, anonymization, region, secret redaction. |

##### Node 23 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `23` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- `docs/legal-contract-matrix.json` is the canonical source for public legal surfaces and effective dates.
- `docs/provider-terms-registry.json` and `docs/model-license-registry.json` cover every provider, model, local runtime, open-weight import, marketplace artifact, and device-deployed model.
- `docs/data-rights-registry.json`, `docs/training-use-policy.json`, and `docs/output-ownership-policy.json` cover prompts, completions, files, evals, feedback, logs, artifacts, weights, support data, billing data, and telemetry.
- Provider/model/capture/distill/eval/benchmark/marketplace/deploy paths fail closed on unknown rights, unknown terms, unknown model license, or unknown geo/export state.
- DPA, BAA, subprocessors, HIPAA, EU AI Act, privacy, copyright, license, and acceptable-use pages resolve to the same evidence packet as account, CLI, API docs, and release proof.
- Every public claim has substantiation ID, evidence, date, owner, scope, expiry, and review state; unsupported claims block release.
- Legal evidence exports include no secret values and include all source paths, URLs, hashes, review owners, and stale-evidence warnings.
- Node `43` build redline includes Node `23` checks as a hard gate before any "100 percent complete" release declaration.

### AI Safety, Abuse Prevention, Red-Team, And Model-Security Operations Ledger V1

Node `24` is the AI safety and model-security operating branch of the master spec tree. It exists because Kolm is not just a static SaaS site. It captures model traffic, distills behavior, compiles artifacts, routes providers, runs agents/tools, publishes recipes, and may deploy models to devices. That creates safety and security classes that ordinary web-app security does not cover: prompt injection, system-prompt leakage, tool poisoning, data poisoning, model extraction, membership inference, memorization, harmful output, excessive agency, unsafe automation, and red-team drift.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not change guardrail code, tests, docs, account pages, API routes, or generated artifacts.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Area | Current evidence found | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Guardrail compilation | `src/guardrails.js`, `tests/wave736-guardrails.test.js`, `public/docs/guardrails.html` | Guardrail rules exist and can block/warn/rewrite, but final completion requires policy versioning, tenant UX, eval replay, false-positive review, bypass testing, and artifact-level provenance. |
| Prompt extraction defense | `src/extraction-guard.js`, `tests/wave765-prompt-extraction.test.js`, `public/security/prompt-extraction.html` | Extraction patterns and policies exist, but final state needs indirect prompt-injection coverage, system-prompt leakage monitoring, customer policy controls, and live bypass regression suites. |
| Poisoning detection | `src/poisoning-orchestrator.js`, `tests/wave761-model-poisoning.test.js`, `tests/wave808-capture-poisoning.test.js`, `public/security/model-poisoning.html` | Poisoning risk signals combine anomaly, copyright, and HMAC checks. Completion needs data-source provenance, poisoning incident workflow, reviewer UX, dataset purge, and teacher-key rotation playbooks. |
| Membership inference | `src/membership-inference-test.js`, `tests/wave764-membership-inference.test.js`, `public/security/membership-inference.html` | Membership-inference harness exists, but completion requires artifact runtime integration, threshold calibration, tenant-specific holdout policy, and account-level leak-risk reporting. |
| Adversarial prompts and red-team | `src/adversarial-prompts.js`, `public/security/red-team.html`, `public/security/threat-model.html`, `public/research/guarded-prompts.html` | Red-team primitives exist, but final state needs a coverage matrix across OWASP LLM risks, MITRE ATLAS techniques, model families, modalities, tool permissions, and release gates. |
| Runtime policy | `src/runtime-policy.js`, `tests/wc04-runtime-policy.test.js`, route/runtime surfaces | Runtime policy exists, but final state must bind policy to tenant roles, tool scopes, data scopes, artifact scopes, safe-fallback behavior, and emergency disable/rollback. |
| Eval and frozen-eval surfaces | `public/frozen-eval.html`, `public/docs/evals.html`, `src/eval-mmlu.js`, `src/eval-mtbench.js`, `src/eval-humaneval.js`, `src/eval-numeric.js`, `tests/wave186-frozen-eval.test.js` | Eval infrastructure exists, but safety completion needs adversarial evals and safety regressions, not only capability metrics. |
| Security public pages | `public/security.html`, `public/security/posture.html`, `public/security/red-team.html`, `public/security/threat-model.html`, `public/security/model-poisoning.html`, `public/security/prompt-extraction.html` | Public trust pages exist, but safety claims need current test evidence, scoped caveats, and no implication that prompt injection or model abuse is fully solved. |

#### Official Standards And Reference Models

| Reference | URL | What Kolm should adopt |
|---|---|---|
| OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 | `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/` | Map every AI product surface to LLM01 prompt injection, LLM02 sensitive information disclosure, LLM03 supply chain, LLM04 data/model poisoning, LLM05 improper output handling, LLM06 excessive agency, LLM07 system prompt leakage, LLM08 vector/embedding weakness, LLM09 misinformation, and LLM10 unbounded consumption. |
| OWASP MCP Top 10 | `https://owasp.org/www-project-mcp-top-10/` | Treat tools, agent connectors, MCP servers, and registry integrations as security-critical surfaces with tool poisoning, authorization, credentials, and context-isolation risk. |
| NIST AI RMF and GenAI Profile | `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` and `https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf` | Use govern, map, measure, and manage as the safety-management loop, with generative-AI risk categories included in product and release evidence. |
| MITRE ATLAS | `https://atlas.mitre.org/` | Map red-team and incident response to adversary tactics such as AI model access, prompt injection, data poisoning, model extraction, credential access, collection, exfiltration, and impact. |
| ISO/IEC 42001 | `https://www.iso.org/standard/42001` | Treat AI safety as a management system with policy, objectives, risk treatment, evidence, internal audit, management review, and continual improvement. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | Emit safe traces for model, provider, route, policy, guardrail decision, tool action, refusal, fallback, cost, and error class without leaking sensitive content. |

#### Safety Redlines

1. Do not claim prompt injection is solved. The product can reduce, detect, isolate, and respond to it; it cannot honestly guarantee elimination across all models, tools, data sources, and future attacks.
2. Do not treat regex prompt-extraction detection as a complete safety layer. Regex guards are a first line of defense and must be paired with untrusted-context separation, tool permissioning, output validation, evals, telemetry, and incident response.
3. Do not allow guardrails to become training signal only. If a customer expects a hard constraint, the artifact, runtime, verifier, account UI, and CLI must report hard enforcement and bypass results.
4. Do not let a safety test pass without specifying model, provider, artifact hash, policy version, dataset version, attack suite version, and threshold.
5. Do not let safety and red-team results become marketing claims without scope. "Passed red-team" must name the suite, date, model family, modalities, tool permissions, and known gaps.
6. Do not treat membership-inference Jaccard overlap as legal truth. It is a risk signal that needs calibrated thresholds, reviewer context, and privacy response actions.
7. Do not ignore indirect prompt injection from retrieved documents, websites, emails, PDFs, tool outputs, registry recipes, or marketplace artifacts.
8. Do not let agent/tool execution inherit broad ambient authority. Tools need explicit scopes, tenant roles, dry-run modes, approvals, rate limits, and post-action receipts.
9. Do not let poisoning alerts stop at dashboards. A high-risk capture must trigger quarantine, dataset exclusion, artifact rebuild eligibility, teacher-key review, purge propagation, and customer evidence.
10. Do not use customer safety incidents as training data without consent, minimization, redaction, retention policy, and legal basis.
11. Do not hide safety state from operators. Account, API, CLI, and docs must show policy status, last test, open risks, recommended actions, and whether the result is local, simulated, or production-probed.
12. Do not ship public safety/trust pages with stale certifications, stale red-team dates, unsupported "secure by default" claims, or unlinked evidence.

#### Canonical Safety Evidence Packet

Every artifact build, model deployment, runtime route, agent workflow, marketplace publish, or enterprise release should be able to produce this evidence shape:

```json
{
  "safety_packet_version": "YYYY.MM.DD-N",
  "subject": {
    "type": "artifact|route|agent|tool|provider|marketplace_recipe|deployment",
    "id": "stable-id",
    "artifact_hash": "sha256-or-cid",
    "tenant_scope": "tenant-or-public",
    "policy_version": "version"
  },
  "risk_map": {
    "owasp_llm": ["LLM01", "LLM02", "LLM04", "LLM06", "LLM07"],
    "owasp_mcp": ["MCP03", "MCP05"],
    "mitre_atlas": ["technique-ids-or-names"],
    "nist_ai_rmf": ["govern", "map", "measure", "manage"]
  },
  "controls": {
    "prompt_injection": "control_ref",
    "system_prompt_leakage": "control_ref",
    "tool_permissions": "control_ref",
    "output_validation": "control_ref",
    "poisoning_detection": "control_ref",
    "membership_inference": "control_ref",
    "rate_limits": "control_ref",
    "human_review": "control_ref"
  },
  "evals": {
    "attack_suite": "version",
    "dataset_hash": "sha256",
    "model_or_runtime": "provider:model-or-artifact",
    "results_hash": "sha256",
    "thresholds": "policy_ref",
    "known_gaps": ["gap"]
  },
  "runtime_receipts": {
    "guardrail_decisions": "log_or_trace_ref",
    "tool_actions": "receipt_ref",
    "refusals": "aggregate_ref",
    "fallbacks": "aggregate_ref",
    "blocked_abuse": "aggregate_ref"
  },
  "response_plan": {
    "severity": "none|low|medium|high|critical",
    "owner": "role",
    "customer_notice": "none|required|sent",
    "remediation": ["action"],
    "retest_required": true
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `24.1` Prompt injection, prompt extraction, system prompt leakage, and untrusted-context isolation | Every route, agent, retrieval source, marketplace recipe, browser/document input, and tool output has context-isolation rules, prompt-leakage controls, indirect-injection tests, and operator-visible bypass evidence. |
| `24.2` Guardrails, output validation, unsafe action prevention, excessive agency, and tool permissions | Guardrails and validators are policy-versioned, artifact-bound where needed, replayable in verification, attached to tool scopes, and shown in account/API/CLI with enforcement mode and bypass risk. |
| `24.3` Poisoning, contamination, membership inference, model extraction, privacy leakage, and memorization | Capture, dataset, train, distill, artifact, benchmark, and marketplace flows have poisoning controls, contamination checks, leak tests, purge propagation, and privacy-risk evidence. |
| `24.4` Red-team harnesses, adversarial evals, safety regression suites, risk scoring, and incident evidence | Release gates run adversarial suites across model families, modalities, tools, routes, and artifacts with versioned attacks, thresholds, failures, known gaps, and incident follow-up. |
| `24.5` Abuse monitoring, rate limits, misuse policy, tenant controls, escalation, and human review | Hosted and BYOC surfaces have misuse categories, rate limits, abuse telemetry, review queues, escalation owners, appeal paths, tenant controls, and privacy-preserving evidence. |
| `24.6` Account, API, CLI, docs, and trust UX for safety state, caveats, and operator action | Safety state is visible through account pages, API envelopes, CLI/TUI commands, docs, and trust pages with clear local/simulated/production labels and no unsupported absolute claims. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-SAFETY-001` | `24.1` | Build an OWASP LLM risk map across gateway, capture, distill, compile, runtime, registry, marketplace, agents, docs widgets, and account flows. | Every product route group has OWASP risk tags, controls, tests, owner, and status. |
| `W-SAFETY-002` | `24.1` | Add indirect prompt-injection coverage for retrieval, PDFs, websites, emails, tool outputs, recipe metadata, and marketplace artifacts. | Attack fixtures prove untrusted content cannot override system/developer/customer policy or gain tool authority. |
| `W-SAFETY-003` | `24.1` | Promote prompt-extraction defense to a policy matrix. | Each tenant can choose log, block, safe response, or review; API/CLI/account show matched family, confidence, and action without leaking protected prompt text. |
| `W-SAFETY-004` | `24.1` | Create system-prompt leakage regression tests for every model adapter and route mode. | Tests cover streaming, tool calls, JSON mode, retries, fallback, cache, batch, local runtime, and BYOC paths. |
| `W-SAFETY-005` | `24.2` | Version guardrail policies and bind them into artifact receipts. | Artifact manifest, verifier, runtime, account UI, and CLI all report guardrail version, rules hash, enforcement mode, and test result. |
| `W-SAFETY-006` | `24.2` | Add structured output validation as a safety control. | JSON schemas, function-call contracts, parsers, validators, and repair loops are tested against malformed, adversarial, and over-permissive outputs. |
| `W-SAFETY-007` | `24.2` | Implement excessive-agency and tool-permission budgets. | Agents/tools have least-privilege scopes, dry-run/confirm modes, per-action receipts, rate limits, and human approval for destructive or external actions. |
| `W-SAFETY-008` | `24.2` | Add unsafe action prevention for deployment, billing, registry, artifact publish, training, and cloud operations. | Sensitive actions require role, policy, confirmation, audit log, and rollback or compensating action. |
| `W-SAFETY-009` | `24.3` | Turn poisoning risk into a full quarantine workflow. | High-risk captures are excluded from datasets, linked to review decisions, purge propagation, teacher-key checks, and artifact rebuild eligibility. |
| `W-SAFETY-010` | `24.3` | Add contamination checks for train/eval/benchmark splits. | K-score, redaction, quality, and public benchmark claims cannot use contaminated holdouts or duplicated capture rows without disclosure. |
| `W-SAFETY-011` | `24.3` | Wire membership-inference harness to actual artifact runtimes. | The harness can run against built `.kolm` artifacts and reports calibrated leak risk by namespace, dataset, model, and task. |
| `W-SAFETY-012` | `24.3` | Add model extraction and inversion risk tests. | Runtime routes and artifacts are probed for excessive logit/detail exposure, repeated-output memorization, unique-token leakage, and policy bypass. |
| `W-SAFETY-013` | `24.4` | Build a versioned adversarial evaluation suite. | Red-team suites cover jailbreaks, prompt injection, harmful requests, policy conflicts, tool abuse, data exfiltration, and provider-specific bypasses. |
| `W-SAFETY-014` | `24.4` | Attach safety regression gates to release verification. | Release verify fails or blocks deployment when critical safety suites regress outside accepted, owner-approved thresholds. |
| `W-SAFETY-015` | `24.4` | Map incidents and red-team findings to MITRE ATLAS. | Each finding has tactic/technique tags, severity, affected surface, reproduction, mitigation, owner, retest date, and customer-impact status. |
| `W-SAFETY-016` | `24.5` | Create hosted abuse taxonomy and enforcement workflow. | Abuse categories, detection signals, rate limits, review queue, account holds, appeal process, privacy minimization, and retention rules are documented and tested. |
| `W-SAFETY-017` | `24.5` | Add tenant-controlled safety policy profiles. | Customers can choose default, regulated, local-only, healthcare, marketplace, or custom policy profiles with visible effects and constraints. |
| `W-SAFETY-018` | `24.5` | Add safety telemetry without sensitive content leakage. | GenAI traces include provider/model, policy, guardrail decision, tool decision, refusal/fallback, and error class while redacting prompts and completions by default. |
| `W-SAFETY-019` | `24.6` | Build account safety center UX. | Account UI shows policy status, open risks, last adversarial run, poisoning quarantine, leak risk, guardrail status, abuse limits, and next actions. |
| `W-SAFETY-020` | `24.6` | Align public trust/docs copy with scoped safety evidence. | Security, red-team, guardrail, prompt-extraction, poisoning, membership-inference, docs, and API pages show current evidence and avoid absolute "solved" claims. |

#### Node 24 Relax Redline

Node `24` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Which AI-specific risks apply to each route, product surface, artifact type, agent/tool flow, provider, runtime, and marketplace publish path?
- Which controls prevent prompt injection, prompt extraction, data exfiltration, unsafe tool execution, poisoning, memorization, model extraction, and harmful output?
- Which controls are hard-enforced, which are heuristic, which are simulated, and which are only documentation or customer-responsibility controls?
- What adversarial suite, model/runtime version, dataset hash, policy version, and threshold proved the current safety state?
- What happens when a capture is poisoned, a prompt-injection succeeds, an artifact leaks training data, a tool is abused, or a customer reports a bypass?
- Can account UI, API envelopes, CLI/TUI commands, docs, public trust pages, and release evidence all show the same safety state without leaking private content?
- Can enterprise buyers receive a safety packet that maps controls to OWASP LLM, OWASP MCP, NIST AI RMF, MITRE ATLAS, and ISO/IEC 42001 style management evidence?

The relax condition for node `24` is not "we have guardrails." It is: every AI-specific risk class has a mapped control, attack fixture, release gate, runtime signal, incident response path, operator UX, and honestly scoped public claim.

#### Node 24 V2 AI Safety, Abuse, Red-Team, And Model-Security Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `24` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change guardrail code, evals, account pages, public trust pages, API routes, generated artifacts, or tests while other agents are editing.

The completion bar is not "Kolm has guardrails." Kolm is complete only when every AI-specific risk class has a risk owner, mapped framework category, control, attack fixture, test/eval result, runtime signal, customer/operator surface, incident response path, and scoped public claim. Heuristic controls must be labeled as heuristic; simulated safety must be labeled as simulated; production-probed controls must carry production evidence.

##### Node 24 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OWASP GenAI / LLM Top 10 | `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/` | Map every AI surface to prompt injection, sensitive disclosure, supply-chain risk, poisoning, improper output handling, excessive agency, system prompt leakage, vector/embedding weakness, misinformation/overreliance, unbounded consumption, and model theft as applicable. |
| OWASP MCP / agentic tool risk | `https://genai.owasp.org/` | Treat tool connectors, agent workflows, registry recipes, and MCP integrations as permissioned security surfaces, not just developer conveniences. |
| MITRE ATLAS | `https://atlas.mitre.org/` | Red-team findings and safety incidents should map to adversary tactics and techniques for AI systems. |
| NIST AI RMF | `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | Use govern, map, measure, and manage as the AI risk loop; evidence must support each stage. |
| NIST Generative AI Profile | `https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf` | GenAI-specific risks need risk measurement, mitigation, monitoring, incident handling, and documentation. |
| ISO/IEC 42001 | `https://www.iso.org/standard/42001` | AI safety needs a management-system posture: policy, risk treatment, review cadence, internal audit, and continual improvement. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | Safety telemetry should use safe model/provider/tool/policy/error attributes without logging sensitive prompt or completion content by default. |

##### Node 24 V2 AI Safety Risk Register Contract

Every AI product surface must have a risk row shaped like this:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-ai-safety-risk-register-v2",
  "risk_id": "gateway.indirect_prompt_injection",
  "surface": "gateway_capture",
  "owner": "safety-runtime",
  "framework_map": {
    "owasp_llm": ["LLM01", "LLM02", "LLM06", "LLM07"],
    "mitre_atlas": ["prompt_injection", "exfiltration"],
    "nist_ai_rmf": ["map", "measure", "manage"]
  },
  "threat_model": {
    "attacker": "untrusted_content_or_user",
    "asset": "system_policy_customer_data_tool_authority",
    "failure_mode": "untrusted content changes model/tool behavior"
  },
  "controls": [
    {
      "control_id": "context_isolation_v1",
      "mode": "hard_enforced",
      "source_paths": ["src/runtime-policy.js"],
      "account_visible": true
    }
  ],
  "evals": {
    "suite_id": "indirect-injection-v1",
    "dataset_hash": "sha256",
    "threshold": "no_critical_bypass",
    "latest_result": "not_run"
  },
  "runtime_signals": ["guardrail_decision", "tool_denied", "unsafe_context_detected"],
  "incident_response": "docs/model-security-incident-runbook.md",
  "claim_scope": "reduces_and_detects_risk_not_guaranteed_elimination",
  "status": "not_complete"
}
```

##### Node 24 V2 Safety Eval Registry

| suite class | must cover | release rule |
|---|---|---|
| direct prompt injection | jailbreaks, instruction hierarchy conflicts, policy conflicts, role-play bypass, refusal-bypass prompts. | Critical bypasses block release unless explicitly accepted with owner, customer impact, and expiry. |
| indirect prompt injection | retrieved docs, PDFs, websites, emails, tool outputs, marketplace recipes, registry metadata, uploaded files. | Any tool-authority escalation or data exfiltration path blocks affected tool/runtime release. |
| prompt extraction/system leakage | direct asks, encoded asks, streaming leakage, retries, fallback, cache, batch, JSON/tool modes. | Protected prompts/policies must not appear in outputs, logs, telemetry, or support bundles. |
| output validation | malformed JSON, schema drift, prompt-injected JSON, unsafe function args, overbroad tool calls. | Unsafe downstream actions are blocked or require explicit approval. |
| poisoning and contamination | poisoned captures, label poisoning, holdout contamination, duplicate leakage, malicious examples. | Quarantine, exclusion, purge, and artifact rebuild eligibility must be proven. |
| privacy leakage | membership inference, memorization, rare-token leakage, PII/PHI leakage, tenant cross-talk. | Leak-risk thresholds and response actions must be calibrated and account-visible. |
| excessive agency | destructive tools, billing/deploy/publish actions, external writes, cloud spend, email/support actions. | Least-privilege, dry-run, confirmation, approval, rate limit, and receipt are required. |
| model extraction/theft | repeated queries, logit/detail exposure, artifact extraction, API abuse, marketplace scraping. | Rate limits, telemetry, anomaly detection, and incident response must be present. |
| harmful output/abuse | cyber abuse, self-harm, regulated advice, fraud, harassment, malware, privacy abuse. | Abuse policy, monitor, review queue, customer controls, and appeal path are required for hosted surfaces. |

##### Node 24 V2 Tool Permission Contract

Every agent/tool/runtime action must declare:

| field | requirement |
|---|---|
| tool id | Stable id, owner, version, docs, source path. |
| authority | read-only, write-local, write-external, deploy, billing, publish, delete, support, admin, legal, cloud-spend. |
| tenant scope | tenant, namespace, artifact, project, user, API key, and role constraints. |
| input trust | user input, model output, retrieved content, uploaded file, external tool output, marketplace recipe. |
| approval | none, dry-run, user confirm, admin approve, two-person approve, support/legal approve. |
| rate/cost budget | max calls, max spend, timeout, retry policy, circuit breaker. |
| safety gates | schema validation, policy check, prompt-injection check, PII/PHI check, copyright/terms check. |
| receipt | action id, actor, policy, input hash, output hash, result, rollback/compensation path. |
| incident hooks | anomaly, abuse, exfiltration, unsafe action, policy bypass, customer complaint. |
