##### Node 24 V2 Relax Redline

Node `24` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every AI product surface has a registered risk row with owner, framework mapping, controls, evals, runtime signals, incident response, and claim scope;
2. direct and indirect prompt injection, prompt extraction, system prompt leakage, output validation, poisoning, contamination, membership inference, memorization, model extraction, excessive agency, and harmful output have versioned suites;
3. guardrail policies are versioned, tenant-scoped, artifact-bound where needed, replayable, false-positive-reviewed, bypass-tested, and account/API/CLI visible;
4. tool actions have least-privilege authority, tenant scope, input trust classification, approval policy, cost/rate budgets, safety gates, receipts, and incident hooks;
5. poisoning and contamination states propagate to quarantine, dataset exclusion, purge, artifact rebuild eligibility, and teacher-key review;
6. safety telemetry follows safe GenAI conventions and does not leak sensitive prompts/completions by default;
7. hosted abuse operations have taxonomy, monitoring, review queue, appeal, rate limits, account action, privacy minimization, and retention policy;
8. red-team and safety regression evidence is durable, dated, scoped by model/artifact/policy/suite, and release-blocking for critical regressions;
9. account UI, API, CLI/TUI, docs, public trust pages, release evidence, and enterprise packets show the same safety state and known gaps;
10. public safety claims are scoped to evidence and never imply prompt injection, poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, or abuse are solved absolutely.

If any item fails, Kolm may have useful safety features, but it is not yet a state-of-the-art AI safety and model-security operating system.

#### Node 24 V3 AI Safety Control Plane, Agentic Tool Authority, And Red-Team Evidence Contract

This V3 addendum maintains master spec tree node `24`. It does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only. It does not edit guardrail code, tests, account pages, public trust pages, API routes, generated artifacts, or safety reports while other agents are working. The purpose is to make AI-safety finality concrete: Kolm is not complete because it has individual guardrail, poisoning, extraction, and membership-inference modules. It is complete only when every AI-specific risk, tool action, red-team run, safety eval, runtime signal, abuse event, incident, account surface, CLI/API proof, and public trust claim reads from one current safety packet.

##### Node 24 V3 Current Evidence Delta

This pass used PowerShell-native recursive search because `rg` is not available in the current shell. The evidence below is current-worktree signal, not a claim that the implementation is complete.

| evidence class | observed state | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| primary safety modules | `src/guardrails.js` 15,588 bytes; `src/runtime-policy.js` 24,854 bytes; `src/extraction-guard.js` 8,954 bytes; `src/poisoning-orchestrator.js` 21,116 bytes; `src/membership-inference-test.js` 12,579 bytes; `src/adversarial-prompts.js`; `src/adversarial-bakeoff.js`; `src/runtime-sanitizer.js`; `src/tool-runtime.js`; `src/tool-training-format.js`; `src/tool-use-capture.js`; `src/capture-anomaly.js`; `src/teacher-response-hmac.js`; `src/sandbox.js`. | The repo has real safety primitives. V3 requires a unifying control plane so those primitives are invoked consistently across gateway, compile, distill, runtime, agents, registry, marketplace, and account flows. |
| safety and security pages | `public/security.html`, `public/threat-model.html`, `public/security/threat-model.html`, `public/security/red-team.html`, `public/security/prompt-extraction.html`, `public/security/model-poisoning.html`, `public/security/membership-inference.html`, `public/docs/guardrails.html`, `public/docs/sandbox.html`, `public/docs/capture-anomaly.html`, `public/docs/copyright-scan.html`, `public/compliance/nist-ai-rmf.html`, `public/compliance/eu-ai-act.html`. | Public surfaces exist, but V3 requires every page claim to show evidence date, suite version, model/artifact scope, production/simulated label, and known limitations. |
| safety tests | `tests/wave736-guardrails.test.js`, `tests/wave761-model-poisoning.test.js`, `tests/wave764-membership-inference.test.js`, `tests/wave765-prompt-extraction.test.js`, `tests/wave808-capture-poisoning.test.js`, `tests/wave809-structured-output.test.js`, `tests/wc04-runtime-policy.test.js`, plus redaction/leak/sandbox/shell-injection tests. | Tests exist, but V3 requires a named safety release gate that proves coverage by risk class, model/runtime mode, tool authority, attack suite version, threshold, and release consequence. |
| research and frontier artifacts | `docs/product-research-atlas.json`, `docs/research/product-research-atlas-2026-05-23.md`, `docs/product-frontier-lab.json`, `docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json`, `docs/product-frontier-operator-kernels.json`, and related simulator scripts/tests. | Research artifacts should feed node `24` only when they produce implementation contracts, not vague inspiration. Safety inventions need attack fixtures, metrics, and deployment constraints. |
| operational gap | Package scripts contain many verification gates, but no dedicated `verify:safety` or `verify:model-security` gate that collects guardrails, injection, poisoning, membership inference, structured output, abuse, red-team, telemetry, and claim-scope tests into one command. | A 100 percent build needs one named safety gate and a generated report, even if lower-level tests remain separate. |
| absent canonical safety artifacts | `docs/ai-safety-risk-register.json`, `docs/owasp-llm-risk-map.json`, `docs/mitre-atlas-map.json`, `docs/safety-eval-registry.json`, `docs/guardrail-policy-registry.json`, `docs/tool-permission-registry.json`, `docs/abuse-operations-runbook.md`, `docs/model-security-incident-runbook.md`, `reports/safety`, `reports/red-team`, and `reports/abuse` are absent. | These remain the V3 hard artifacts. Without them, safety is implemented in pieces rather than as an auditable operating system. |

##### Node 24 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| baseline | current source | V3 rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OWASP GenAI / LLM Top 10 2025 | `https://genai.owasp.org/resource/owasp-top-10-for-llm-applications-2025/` and `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/` | Use the current OWASP GenAI framing, not only the archived v1.1 categories. Product risk maps must distinguish prompt injection, sensitive disclosure, supply-chain, poisoning, improper output handling, excessive agency, system prompt leakage, vector/embedding risk, misinformation/overreliance, unbounded consumption, and model theft as applicable. |
| OWASP Agentic AI threats | `https://genai.owasp.org/resource/agentic-ai-threats-and-mitigations/` | Agents are not just chat flows. Tool selection, delegated actions, planning, memory, connectors, external writes, and autonomous retries need explicit threat models and mitigations. |
| MITRE ATLAS | `https://atlas.mitre.org/` | Red-team findings, model-security incidents, poisoning, exfiltration, model extraction, prompt injection, credential access, and impact events should map to adversary tactics and techniques. |
| NIST Generative AI Profile | `https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ai/NIST.AI.600-1.pdf` | Safety needs lifecycle risk management: govern, map, measure, and manage. Each Kolm control should show which lifecycle stage it satisfies and what evidence proves it. |
| CISA/NSA/NCSC secure AI deployment guidance | `https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2024/04/15/joint-guidance-deploying-ai-systems-securely` and `https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/collection/guidelines-secure-ai-system-development` | Secure AI is a deployment and operations discipline: secure deployment environment, protect AI assets and logs, monitor continuously, prepare incident response, and dispose/retire safely. |
| Google SAIF | `https://saif.google/` | Treat AI security as layered controls across risks, development, deployment, operations, and agents. Adopt a control-map style that is readable by security buyers. |
| UK AISI Inspect | `https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/` | Evaluation infrastructure should support datasets, solvers, scorers, logs, agents, tool approvals, sandboxing, and repeatable eval evidence. Kolm's safety evals need the same reproducibility qualities even if implemented internally. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | Safety telemetry must represent provider, model, tool, policy, guardrail, refusal, fallback, error, and cost state without storing sensitive prompt/completion text by default. |

##### Node 24 V3 Unified Safety Packet Contract

Every route, artifact, provider path, runtime target, agent/tool action, registry recipe, marketplace publish, eval suite, red-team run, hosted-abuse event, and public safety claim must be able to emit or link to this packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.ai_safety_control_plane.v3",
  "node": 24,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "subject": {
    "kind": "route|artifact|provider|runtime|agent|tool|recipe|marketplace_artifact|eval|claim",
    "id": "stable-id",
    "owner": "safety-runtime",
    "tenant_scope": "public|tenant|namespace",
    "artifact_hash": "sha256-or-null",
    "policy_version": "policy-version",
    "model_or_runtime": "provider:model-or-artifact"
  },
  "risk_register": {
    "owasp_genai": ["LLM01"],
    "mitre_atlas": ["technique-or-tactic"],
    "nist_ai_rmf": ["govern", "map", "measure", "manage"],
    "agentic_risks": ["tool_authority_escalation", "untrusted_context_override"]
  },
  "controls": [
    {
      "control_id": "context_isolation_v3",
      "control_type": "hard_enforced|heuristic|human_review|monitor_only|customer_responsibility",
      "source_paths": ["src/runtime-policy.js"],
      "policy_hash": "sha256",
      "account_visible": true,
      "cli_visible": true,
      "api_visible": true
    }
  ],
  "tool_authority": {
    "tool_id": "tool-or-null",
    "authority": "read_only|write_local|write_external|deploy|billing|publish|delete|admin",
    "input_trust": "trusted_user|untrusted_content|model_output|tool_output|marketplace_recipe",
    "approval": "none|dry_run|user_confirm|admin_approve|two_person_approve",
    "budget": {
      "max_calls": 0,
      "max_spend_usd": 0,
      "timeout_ms": 0
    },
    "receipt_required": true
  },
  "eval_evidence": {
    "suite_id": "suite-version",
    "dataset_hash": "sha256",
    "attack_families": ["indirect_prompt_injection"],
    "threshold": "no_critical_bypass",
    "latest_result": "pass|fail|not_run|accepted_risk",
    "report_path": "reports/safety/suite-version.json",
    "known_gaps": []
  },
  "runtime_signals": {
    "guardrail_decision": "allow|warn|rewrite|block|review",
    "tool_decision": "allow|deny|approval_required",
    "poisoning_state": "clean|suspect|quarantined",
    "leak_risk": "unknown|low|medium|high",
    "abuse_state": "none|rate_limited|review|suspended"
  },
  "incident_response": {
    "runbook": "docs/model-security-incident-runbook.md",
    "severity": "none|low|medium|high|critical",
    "owner": "role",
    "customer_notice": "none|required|sent",
    "retest_required": true
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "public_claim_allowed": false,
    "claim_language": "reduces_and_detects_risk_not_guaranteed_elimination",
    "evidence_expires_at": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  }
}
```

##### Node 24 V3 Control Plane Matrices

| product surface | minimum safety controls | release redline |
|---|---|---|
| gateway and routing | Direct/indirect injection tests, provider safety policy, rate limits, sensitive disclosure checks, moderation/abuse state, safe telemetry. | No route can claim safe routing without safety packet, provider/model policy, and current eval evidence. |
| capture and datasets | Poisoning detection, copyright/legal handoff to node `23`, PII/PHI redaction, contamination checks, retention/deletion propagation, quarantine workflow. | Captured data cannot train, distill, benchmark, or publish until safety and rights states are known. |
| distillation and K-score | Holdout contamination checks, teacher poisoning checks, memorization/leak tests, harmful-output regressions, safety-axis calibration, public claim scope. | A student cannot be promoted on quality score alone; safety, privacy, and abuse regressions must be part of promotion. |
| compile and `.kolm` artifact | Guardrail manifest, policy hash, runtime limits, sandbox mode, provenance, tool permissions, verifier safety receipt, marketplace publish gate. | Artifact verification must expose safety policy and bypass status, not only byte stability and signatures. |
| runtime and any-device deployment | Runtime policy, local/cloud/device constraints, unsafe action prevention, telemetry mode, offline policy, emergency disable, fallback behavior. | Device/local claims must disclose what safety is local-enforced, cloud-enforced, simulated, or customer-responsibility. |
| agents and tools | Least privilege, input trust labels, dry-run/confirm/approval modes, cost/rate budgets, schema validation, action receipts, rollback/compensation. | Model output must never inherit broad ambient authority; external writes and destructive actions require explicit approval state. |
| registry and marketplace | Recipe scanning, tool scope declaration, artifact license/legal link, abuse review, safety eval report, takedown path, versioned risk badge. | Marketplace artifacts cannot be published with unknown tool authority or unknown safety eval status. |
| account and enterprise evidence | Safety center, open risks, last eval, poisoning quarantine, leak risk, abuse controls, red-team report, runbook links, next actions. | Account/API/CLI/docs must show the same safety state with local/simulated/production labels. |
| public trust and docs | Scoped language, evidence date, suite version, known gaps, no absolute "solved" claims, no stale red-team/certification implication. | Public copy cannot claim injection-proof, abuse-proof, privacy-preserving, or red-team-passed without current evidence. |

| ticket | priority | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---:|---|---|
| W-SAFETY-V3-001 | P0 | Generate `docs/ai-safety-control-plane.json`. | All risk maps, control registries, eval registries, tool permissions, runbooks, reports, API/account/CLI status, and public claims can resolve to one packet schema. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-002 | P0 | Add `verify:safety` or `verify:model-security` command. | One command runs guardrails, extraction, poisoning, membership inference, structured output, runtime policy, sandbox/shell-injection, red-team fixtures, and claim-scope checks with a report. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-003 | P0 | Generate `docs/ai-safety-risk-register.json`. | Every product graph surface and route group has risk class, owner, OWASP/MITRE/NIST map, controls, evals, status, and claim scope. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-004 | P0 | Generate `docs/guardrail-policy-registry.json`. | Guardrail policies have id, version, hash, tenant scope, enforcement mode, artifact binding, false-positive path, bypass suite, and owner. |

| W-SAFETY-V3-009 | P0 | Bind poisoning state to dataset and artifact promotion. | Suspect captures cannot enter distill/train/eval/benchmark/publish paths until reviewed, excluded, purged, or accepted with owner and expiry. |

| W-SAFETY-V3-011 | P1 | Add model extraction and inversion abuse suite. | Repeated query, detail/logit exposure, memorized output, marketplace scraping, and artifact extraction attempts are tested and rate-limited. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-012 | P0 | Generate `docs/safety-eval-registry.json`. | Every safety suite has id, version, dataset hash, model/runtime target, thresholds, latest result, report path, known gaps, and release consequence. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-013 | P0 | Produce durable `reports/safety` and `reports/red-team` outputs. | Red-team and safety runs persist suite, date, target, policy, findings, severity, mitigation, retest, accepted risk, and customer-impact status. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-014 | P0 | Add hosted abuse taxonomy and operations runbook. | `docs/abuse-operations-runbook.md` covers abuse categories, signals, rate limits, account holds, review, appeal, retention, and customer controls. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-015 | P0 | Add model-security incident runbook. | `docs/model-security-incident-runbook.md` covers injection success, prompt leakage, poisoning, memorization, model extraction, unsafe tool action, and harmful output. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-016 | P0 | Add safe GenAI telemetry contract. | Traces/logs expose provider/model/policy/tool/refusal/fallback/error/cost state without storing prompts or completions by default. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-017 | P1 | Build account safety center contract. | Account can show policy status, last safety eval, red-team report, quarantine, leak risk, abuse limits, tool approvals, open risks, and next actions. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-018 | P1 | Add CLI/API/TUI safety state parity. | Operators can query safety packet, risk register, guardrail registry, red-team status, and open risks without secrets. |

| W-SAFETY-V3-020 | P1 | Link safety profiles to tenant and deployment mode. | Default, regulated, healthcare, local-only, BYOC, marketplace, and custom profiles change enforcement, telemetry, approval, and evidence output visibly. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-021 | P1 | Add marketplace safety badge and takedown workflow. | Marketplace recipes/artifacts show safety eval status, tool authority, policy hash, known gaps, takedown path, and review owner. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-022 | P0 | Add safety packet to enterprise procurement export. | Enterprise packet includes OWASP, MITRE, NIST, SAIF-style controls, eval evidence, runbooks, open gaps, and claim scope. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-023 | P0 | Integrate node `24` into build redline node `43`. | Build cannot be called finished unless safety control plane, `verify:safety`, red-team reports, runbooks, account/API/CLI parity, and claim gate are green. |
| W-SAFETY-V3-024 | P0 | Create Node 24 completion certificate. | Certificate proves every AI-specific risk class has owner, controls, evals, runtime signals, operator UX, incident response, evidence reports, and scoped public claims. |

##### Node 24 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `24` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- A generated `kolm.ai_safety_control_plane.v3` packet exists for every AI-facing route, artifact, provider, runtime, agent, tool, registry recipe, marketplace publish path, eval suite, red-team run, hosted-abuse event, and public safety claim.
- `verify:safety` or `verify:model-security` exists and covers guardrails, prompt extraction, direct and indirect prompt injection, structured output, runtime policy, poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, sandbox/tool safety, abuse workflow, telemetry safety, and claim-scope checks.
- Guardrail and runtime policies are versioned, tenant-scoped, artifact-bound where needed, bypass-tested, false-positive-reviewed, and visible through account, API, CLI/TUI, docs, and enterprise evidence.
- Agent and tool authority is least-privilege by default, with explicit input-trust labels, approvals, budgets, receipts, and rollback or compensation paths.
- Poisoning, contamination, privacy leakage, memorization, and model extraction results propagate into dataset eligibility, artifact promotion, marketplace publish, customer evidence, and incident response.
- Safety telemetry follows safe GenAI conventions and does not log prompt/completion bodies by default.
- Hosted abuse operations have taxonomy, monitoring, review queue, appeal path, retention policy, customer controls, and privacy minimization.
- Red-team and safety eval evidence is durable, dated, scoped by model/artifact/policy/suite, and release-blocking for critical regressions.
- Account UI, API, CLI/TUI, docs, public trust pages, release evidence, and enterprise packets show the same safety state, open risks, and known gaps.
- Public claims are scoped to evidence and never imply that prompt injection, poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, unsafe agency, harmful output, or abuse are solved absolutely.

### Customer Lifecycle, Onboarding, Support, Feedback, Status, And Success Operations Ledger V1

Node `25` is the customer-operations branch of the master spec tree. It exists because an enterprise AI infrastructure product is not complete when the core API works. It is complete only when users can sign up safely, reach first value quickly, migrate from current tools, receive operational notices, collect support evidence, escalate incidents, export procurement packets, provide feedback, understand roadmap status, renew or expand, and recover from failure without founder intervention.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not change onboarding pages, support flows, email code, notification code, status routes, account UI, tests, or generated docs.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Area | Current evidence found | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Onboarding pages | `public/onboard/index.html`, `public/onboard/developer.html`, `public/onboard/cto.html`, `public/onboard/compliance.html`, `public/onboard/researcher.html` | Persona onboarding exists, but final state needs instrumentation, account-state continuity, first-value progress, and route-specific next actions. |
| Enterprise inquiry | `public/enterprise/inquiry.html`, enterprise/pricing pages, account/business-plan surfaces | Enterprise contact flows exist, but final state needs lead routing, SLA, required fields, procurement packet generation, and sales-engineering handoff. |
| Email and setup | `src/email.js`, setup-token helpers in the same file, welcome/billing email functions | Email exists, but a scan shows possible drift between legacy raw-key welcome email content and the setup-token safety design. Completion must prove no onboarding path leaks raw keys into email or provider logs. |
| Notifications | `src/notifications.js`, `tests/wave515-notifications-route-docs.test.js`, threshold alerts, push subscriptions | Notification primitives exist, but final state needs preference center, unsubscribe/consent posture, channel routing, retries, delivery status, and sensitive-content minimization. |
| Status page | `public/status.html`, `public/status.rss`, `tests/wave304-kolm-status.test.js`, `tests/wave491-status-subscribe-surface.test.js`, `public/docs/cli/status.html` | Status and anonymous subscription routes exist, but final state needs incident lifecycle, maintenance windows, component mapping, customer segment impact, update cadence, and postmortem evidence. |
| Support bundle | `tests/wave307-kolm-support-bundle.test.js`, CLI support-bundle command | Support bundle exists and redacts API keys, but final state needs customer preview, consent, attachment size policy, secure upload, retention, ticket linkage, and support-only access controls. |
| Ticket/support docs | `public/docs/tickets.html`, `public/docs/cookbook/support-bot.html`, `public/for/support-automation.html`, support cookbook/vertical pages | Support content exists, but product support operations need a real triage model, severity taxonomy, customer-visible expectations, and feedback loop into roadmap. |
| Feedback and examples | `docs/sim-100-feedback-2026-05-11.md`, cookbook pages such as `nps-classifier`, `churn-predict`, `support-reply`, `incident-summarizer` | Feedback examples exist, but Kolm's own product feedback loop needs production intake, classification, routing, prioritization, and close-the-loop communication. |
| Marketplace/support artifacts | `public/marketplace/kolm-support-7b.html`, `public/docs/showcase/member-support-triage.spec.json` | Support automation demos exist, but final state needs dogfooding: Kolm support should use Kolm artifacts with safety, privacy, and escalation controls. |

#### External References And Operating Models

| Reference | URL | What Kolm should adopt |
|---|---|---|
| ISO 10002 customer complaints handling | `https://www.iso.org/standard/71580.html` | Use a complaint-handling process with receipt, tracking, acknowledgement, assessment, response, closure, trend analysis, and continual improvement. |
| Google SRE incident response | `https://sre.google/sre-book/emergency-response/` and `https://sre.google/resources/practices-and-processes/incident-management-guide/` | Use incident command, severity, clear roles, impact tracking, communication cadence, drills, and post-incident learning. |
| Atlassian incident management and postmortems | `https://www.atlassian.com/incident-management` and `https://support.atlassian.com/statuspage/docs/create-a-postmortem/` | Status updates and postmortems should explain what happened, customer impact, mitigation, and prevention plan. |
| DORA software delivery metrics | `https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/devops-sre/announcing-the-2024-dora-report` | Track deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, and failed-deployment recovery time as operational maturity evidence, not vanity metrics. |
| FTC CAN-SPAM guide | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/can-spam-act-compliance-guide-business` | Separate transactional from commercial email, keep routing information truthful, avoid deceptive subjects, include opt-out where required, and respect unsubscribe timelines. |
| RFC 9457 problem details for HTTP APIs | `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Customer-facing API errors should use structured, actionable, supportable problem details with stable codes and links. |

#### Customer-Operations Redlines

1. Do not let onboarding email send raw API keys if the setup-token flow is the intended safety contract. Every signup path must be proven to use a short-lived reveal token or an equivalent one-time secure display.
2. Do not let email, push, status subscription, billing, or support notifications operate without preference, unsubscribe, consent, and sensitive-content rules.
3. Do not allow status pages to be static marketing pages. A status page must reflect real component health, incident state, maintenance windows, subscriber updates, RSS, and postmortems.
4. Do not let support bundles include raw secrets, private captures, prompt/completion bodies, tokens, credentials, environment variables, stack traces with secrets, or unreviewed logs.
5. Do not let support bundles become founder-only artifacts. Support must be able to attach them to tickets, search them safely, expire them, and show customers what was included.
6. Do not let enterprise inquiry forms disappear into email. They need lead classification, owner, SLA, enrichment policy, follow-up history, and procurement packet handoff.
7. Do not treat onboarding pages as content-only. They must connect to account state, current plan, API key state, provider setup, first route, first capture, first artifact, and first deployment.
8. Do not let feedback, NPS, churn, support tickets, and failed onboarding live outside the product roadmap loop. Each must map to product-surface owner, severity, revenue impact, and customer notification.
9. Do not use customer support data to improve Kolm models or support artifacts without consent, redaction, retention, and opt-out policy.
10. Do not mix transactional email with marketing claims in a way that changes its legal/compliance posture or hides opt-out requirements.
11. Do not make incident communications overconfident. Early updates should separate confirmed impact, suspected impact, mitigation, next update time, and what customers should do.
12. Do not hide failed notifications. Email/push/webhook delivery state should be observable enough for support to answer "did the customer receive it?"
13. Do not let pilots/trials have unclear success criteria. Each pilot needs outcome metrics, start/end date, owner, security/procurement blockers, first-value plan, and expansion path.
14. Do not let account owners, support owners, incident commanders, and sales owners be implicit. Ownership must be explicit and visible to operators.
15. Do not let public demos of support automation imply customer support will be handled by AI without escalation, human review, privacy, and quality controls.

#### Canonical Customer Operations Evidence Packet

Every customer, tenant, trial, enterprise inquiry, support case, incident, and renewal should be able to generate or link to this evidence shape:

```json
{
  "customer_ops_packet_version": "YYYY.MM.DD-N",
  "tenant": {
    "tenant_id": "stable-id",
    "plan": "free|pro|team|enterprise",
    "lifecycle_stage": "lead|trial|active|at_risk|renewal|churned",
    "owner": "role-or-user",
    "region": "region",
    "support_tier": "community|standard|priority|enterprise"
  },
  "onboarding": {
    "persona": "developer|cto|compliance|researcher|operator",
    "first_value_goal": "route_first_call|capture_first_trace|build_first_artifact|deploy_first_device",
    "progress": ["step"],
    "blocked_by": ["missing_provider_key", "storage_not_configured"],
    "next_action": "route-or-command"
  },
  "communications": {
    "email_preferences": "ref",
    "push_preferences": "ref",
    "status_subscription": "ref",
    "last_sent": "timestamp",
    "last_delivery_status": "delivered|bounced|skipped|unknown"
  },
  "support": {
    "open_cases": 0,
    "severity": "sev0|sev1|sev2|sev3|question",
    "sla": "policy_ref",
    "support_bundle_ref": "path-or-id",
    "redaction_report": "ref",
    "escalation_owner": "role"
  },
  "incidents": {
    "affected_components": ["gateway", "registry", "billing"],
    "status_page_ref": "url",
    "customer_impact": "summary",
    "next_update_at": "timestamp",
    "postmortem_ref": "url-or-null"
  },
  "success": {
    "activation_metrics": ["metric"],
    "usage_health": "green|yellow|red",
    "cost_savings_evidence": "ref",
    "expansion_signals": ["signal"],
    "churn_risks": ["risk"],
    "roadmap_requests": ["request-id"]
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `25.1` Signup, activation, setup-token, onboarding paths, first value, and migration handoff | Every persona has a first-value path that starts at signup, uses safe key reveal, records progress, handles missing prerequisites, and produces a next action in account, docs, CLI, and email. |
| `25.2` Support channels, support bundles, ticketing, escalation, response SLAs, and customer evidence | Support cases have severity, owner, SLA, secure support bundle, redaction proof, customer consent, escalation path, retention, and closure reason. |
| `25.3` Status page, incident communications, maintenance windows, postmortems, and customer notifications | Component health, incidents, maintenance, RSS, subscribers, customer segment impact, update cadence, and postmortems are wired to real operational state. |
| `25.4` Feedback, product telemetry, roadmap intake, churn signals, expansion signals, and customer success loops | Feedback and usage signals enter a governed roadmap loop with owner, priority, product surface, customer impact, revenue impact, close-the-loop message, and privacy posture. |
| `25.5` Enterprise inquiry, sales handoff, procurement packets, trials, pilots, renewals, and account ownership | Enterprise inquiries become tracked opportunities with owner, SLA, qualification, procurement packet, pilot plan, success metrics, security blockers, renewal date, and expansion path. |
| `25.6` Lifecycle UX across account, docs, CLI, email, webhooks, RSS, API envelopes, and admin workflows | Customer lifecycle state is consistent across account UI, CLI/TUI, docs, API envelopes, emails, webhooks, status page, RSS, admin tools, and sales/support exports. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-CUSTOMER-001` | `25.1` | Audit signup and welcome email paths for raw API-key exposure. | Every signup path uses setup-token or one-time secure reveal, tests prove raw keys are not sent through email/provider logs, and docs match behavior. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-002` | `25.1` | Build persona onboarding state machine. | Developer, CTO, compliance, researcher, and operator onboarding pages map to account state, prerequisites, completed steps, blockers, and next action. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-003` | `25.1` | Define first-value milestones for all product surfaces. | Routing, capture, distill, compile, deploy, govern, and marketplace journeys each have success event, time-to-value metric, docs link, and recovery path. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-004` | `25.1` | Create migration handoff flows. | OpenAI wrapper, OpenRouter, LangChain, LlamaIndex, Ollama, LM Studio, OpenPipe, and local runtime migrations have checklist, code path, validation, and support trigger. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-005` | `25.2` | Formalize support severity and SLA taxonomy. | Support pages, enterprise contracts, account UI, CLI support output, and ticket routing agree on severity, response time, escalation, and coverage hours. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-006` | `25.2` | Upgrade support bundle into secure customer evidence flow. | Bundle preview, redaction report, consent, secure upload, ticket link, retention, access log, and deletion are implemented or explicitly scoped. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-007` | `25.2` | Add structured support ticket intake. | Tickets capture product surface, route, artifact hash, tenant, severity, reproduction, logs, support bundle, customer impact, and requested outcome. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-008` | `25.2` | Build escalation and handoff workflow. | Support, engineering, security, legal, sales, and incident owner transitions are logged with timestamps, owner, next action, and customer-facing update. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-009` | `25.3` | Turn status page into component-backed operations surface. | Gateway, registry, billing, docs, account, storage, cloud train, provider routing, and marketplace components have health state and history. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-010` | `25.3` | Add incident communication workflow. | Incidents have severity, commander, customer-impact summary, next update time, subscriber delivery state, status-page update, and closure state. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-011` | `25.3` | Add maintenance windows and postmortems. | Planned maintenance and resolved incidents can publish scheduled notices, updates, postmortems, remediation actions, and customer-facing follow-up. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-012` | `25.3` | Add notification deliverability tracking. | Email, push, webhook, RSS, and status subscriptions report sent/skipped/bounced/failed states without exposing secrets or sensitive content. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-013` | `25.4` | Build feedback intake and classification. | Feedback, bug reports, feature requests, NPS, support themes, and churn signals are classified by product surface, severity, persona, and revenue impact. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-014` | `25.4` | Close the customer feedback loop. | Each accepted/requested/declined item can notify affected customers, link to roadmap status, and preserve privacy/consent constraints. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-015` | `25.4` | Dogfood Kolm support artifacts safely. | Support automation uses Kolm artifacts with guardrails, privacy redaction, quality checks, human review, escalation, and audit receipts. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-016` | `25.5` | Convert enterprise inquiry into tracked opportunity. | Inquiry submissions create owner, qualification, company profile, requested use case, security blockers, procurement needs, trial path, and SLA. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-017` | `25.5` | Build pilot/trial success plans. | Trials have start/end date, success criteria, activation milestones, usage target, security/procurement tasks, owner, and go/no-go decision. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-018` | `25.5` | Build renewal and expansion health ledger. | Enterprise accounts track contract dates, usage, support history, savings evidence, champion, blockers, expansion signals, and renewal risk. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-019` | `25.6` | Create customer lifecycle API/envelope contract. | Account UI, CLI/TUI, admin tools, docs, emails, webhooks, and sales/support exports share the same lifecycle state and next-action vocabulary. |
| `W-CUSTOMER-020` | `25.6` | Add customer-operations release gate. | Release verification proves signup, setup-token, status subscribe, notification preferences, support bundle, enterprise inquiry, and lifecycle docs still work. |

#### Node 25 Relax Redline

Node `25` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Can every new user reach first value for each major product surface without founder handholding?
- Can a user safely reveal, rotate, and recover API keys without secrets traveling through email, logs, support bundles, or screenshots?
- Can customers subscribe to the right operational notices and verify what was sent, skipped, bounced, or failed?
- Can support receive a useful bundle that is redacted, consented, attached to a case, retained correctly, and deleted on request?
- Can the team manage a real incident with severity, commander, component status, customer impact, update cadence, subscribers, closure, and postmortem?
- Can feedback, NPS, churn risk, support themes, and roadmap requests flow into product planning and back to affected customers?
- Can enterprise inquiries turn into qualified pilots with owner, SLA, procurement packet, security evidence, success criteria, and renewal/expansion path?
- Can account UI, docs, CLI, emails, RSS, webhooks, status page, support exports, and sales packets all report the same customer lifecycle state?

The relax condition for node `25` is not "there is a support page." It is: every customer lifecycle moment from signup to renewal has a tracked owner, safe communication path, product-state evidence, support path, feedback loop, operational status, and next action.

#### Node 25 V2 Customer Lifecycle, Support, Status, And Success Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `25` and does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only. It does not change onboarding pages, email code, notification code, status routes, support flows, account UI, generated docs, tests, or styling while other agents are editing.

The completion bar is not "there is a support page" or "there are onboarding pages." Kolm is complete only when every user can reach first value, recover from blocked setup, receive the right communications, get help without leaking secrets, understand incident impact, export procurement evidence, feed product decisions, and move from trial to renewal through a state machine that account, docs, CLI, API, email, webhooks, status, support, and sales all share.

##### Node 25 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| onboarding pages | `public/onboard/index.html`, `developer.html`, `cto.html`, `compliance.html`, `researcher.html` total five persona pages. | Persona onboarding exists, but completion requires account-state continuity, progress tracking, first-value milestones, blockers, and next actions. |
| enterprise inquiry | `public/enterprise/inquiry.html`: 21,611 bytes, 384 lines. | Contact-sales path exists. Completion requires lead routing, SLA, owner, qualification, procurement packet, security evidence, and sales-engineering handoff. |
| status surfaces | `public/status.html`: 19,672 bytes; `public/status.rss`: 843 bytes; status tests exist. | Status exists. Completion requires component-backed health, incidents, maintenance, subscribers, delivery state, postmortems, and customer segment impact. |
| support docs/demos | `public/docs/tickets.html`, `public/docs/cookbook/support-bot.html`, `public/for/support-automation.html`, `public/marketplace/kolm-support-7b.html`. | Support content and demos exist; final state needs operational support policy, secure bundle flow, ticket states, escalation, and safe dogfooding controls. |
| email/notifications | `src/email.js`: 8,168 bytes, 209 lines; `src/notifications.js`: 9,970 bytes, 256 lines. | Communication primitives exist. Completion requires preference center, opt-out/consent posture, deliverability, sensitive-content minimization, and raw-key safety proof. |
| tests | support/status/notification/enterprise tests include W304 status, W307 support bundle, W491 status subscribe, W515 notifications, W583 enterprise identity. | There is useful lock-in coverage, but no single customer lifecycle state contract or release evidence packet. |
| absent contracts | `docs/customer-lifecycle-contract.json`, `docs/onboarding-state-machine.json`, `docs/support-severity-policy.json`, `docs/support-bundle-policy.json`, `docs/incident-communication-policy.json`, `docs/customer-notification-policy.json`, `docs/enterprise-inquiry-sla.json`, `docs/feedback-roadmap-loop.json` are absent. | Customer operations truth is not yet machine-readable enough to unify product surfaces and support/sales operations. |
| absent reports | `reports/customer-lifecycle`, `reports/support`, and `reports/incidents` are absent. | Lifecycle and incident evidence is not yet durable release proof. |

##### Node 25 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google SRE emergency response | `https://sre.google/sre-book/emergency-response/` | Incident response requires preparation, tested rollback/recovery, communication, role clarity, and periodic drills. |
| Google SRE incident management | `https://sre.google/sre-book/managing-incidents/` | Incidents need command, roles, severity, customer impact, updates, and post-incident learning. |
| Atlassian Statuspage postmortems | `https://support.atlassian.com/statuspage/docs/create-a-postmortem/` | Customer-facing postmortems should explain what happened, impact, resolution, and prevention work. |
| FTC CAN-SPAM guide | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/can-spam-act-compliance-guide-business` | Commercial email needs truthful routing/subject lines, identification where required, physical address, opt-out, and timely unsubscribe handling. |
| RFC 9457 Problem Details | `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Customer-facing API errors should be structured, actionable, stable, and supportable. |
| ISO 10002 customer complaints handling | `https://www.iso.org/standard/71580.html` | Complaints should have receipt, tracking, assessment, response, closure, trend analysis, and improvement loop. |
| UI/UX operational rules | local `ui-ux-pro-max` skill | Lifecycle UX must prioritize accessibility, keyboard/touch, visible feedback, progressive loading, clear errors, predictable navigation, and low cognitive load. |

##### Node 25 V2 Customer Lifecycle Contract

Every tenant, lead, trial, support case, incident, and renewal must map to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-customer-lifecycle-v2",
  "subject_id": "tenant_or_lead_id",
  "subject_type": "lead|trial|tenant|enterprise_opportunity|support_case|incident",
  "owner": "customer-ops",
  "stage": "lead|signup|activation|active|blocked|at_risk|renewal|churned",
  "persona": "developer|cto|compliance|researcher|operator|buyer",
  "plan": "free|pro|team|enterprise",
  "first_value_goal": "route_call|capture_trace|compile_artifact|distill_student|deploy_device|export_procurement_packet",
  "progress": {
    "completed": [],
    "blocked_by": [],
    "next_action": {
      "surface": "account|docs|cli|email|support|sales",
      "label": "Configure storage",
      "url_or_command": "/account/storage"
    }
  },
  "communications": {
    "preferences_ref": "customer-notification-policy",
    "last_sent": null,
    "last_delivery_state": "unknown",
    "unsubscribe_available": true,
    "sensitive_content_minimized": true
  },
  "support": {
    "severity": "none|question|sev3|sev2|sev1|sev0",
    "sla_ref": "support-severity-policy",
    "support_bundle_ref": null,
    "redaction_report_ref": null,
    "escalation_owner": null
  },
  "status_and_incidents": {
    "affected_components": [],
    "status_page_ref": null,
    "incident_ref": null,
    "next_update_at": null,
    "postmortem_ref": null
  },
  "success": {
    "activation_metrics": [],
    "usage_health": "not_measured",
    "roi_evidence_ref": null,
    "feedback_refs": [],
    "roadmap_refs": [],
    "renewal_or_expansion_refs": []
  },
  "evidence": {
    "account_visible": true,
    "cli_visible": true,
    "admin_visible": true,
    "release_report": null
  }
}
```

##### Node 25 V2 First-Value Matrix

| journey | first-value event | required recovery path |
|---|---|---|
| route model traffic | first successful provider-routed call with receipt and cost trace | missing API key, provider 401, quota, rate limit, unsupported model, unsafe fallback, docs link, support trigger. |
| capture behavior | first capture appears in account with privacy/rights status | missing wrapper, provider mismatch, redaction blocked, rights quarantine, no events, stale events. |
| compile artifact | first signed `.kolm` artifact verifies locally | bad recipe, missing data, policy block, signing failure, unsupported runtime, docs/CLI next action. |
| distill student | first distill job reaches evaluated student candidate | no teacher, data insufficient, budget cap, safety block, quality fail, compute unavailable, resume/cancel state. |
| deploy/run anywhere | first artifact runs on target runtime/device/cloud | target unsupported, storage missing, package missing, device fit fail, runtime error, rollback. |
| governance/audit | first evidence packet export proves artifact/data/provider state | missing legal, security, data, supply-chain, readiness, or production evidence. |
| enterprise procurement | buyer receives complete procurement packet and sales owner | missing DPA/BAA/security/SBOM/provenance/pricing, unassigned lead, stale promise, no SLA. |

##### Node 25 V2 Support And Incident Policy Shape

| area | required fields |
|---|---|
| support severity | severity, examples, customer impact, response target, update cadence, escalation owner, covered hours, exclusions, customer next action. |
| support bundle | preview, consent, redaction, excluded fields, max size, secure upload, ticket link, retention, deletion, access log, customer export. |
| ticket intake | product surface, route, artifact hash, tenant, severity, reproduction, logs, bundle, business impact, requested outcome, owner, SLA. |
| incident | severity, incident commander, affected components, affected customers/segments, start time, detection source, mitigation, next update time, status page, postmortem. |
| notification | channel, legal class, opt-out state, preference, template version, delivery state, retry state, sensitive-content rule, unsubscribe path. |
| feedback | type, product surface, customer/persona, revenue impact, severity, roadmap owner, decision, close-the-loop message, privacy/consent. |

##### Node 25 V2 Relax Redline

Node `25` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every persona and product journey has a first-value event, blocker model, recovery path, next action, and owner;
2. no onboarding, setup-token, welcome, support, or recovery flow exposes raw API keys or secrets through unsafe channels;
3. support severity, support bundles, ticket intake, escalation, retention, consent, redaction, and customer evidence are one policy;
4. status, incidents, maintenance, subscribers, RSS, notifications, delivery state, postmortems, and customer segment impact are component-backed;
5. notifications distinguish transactional, product, support, security, billing, incident, and marketing messages with opt-out/consent rules;
6. feedback, NPS, churn, support themes, bugs, feature requests, and roadmap items enter a closed-loop product process;
7. enterprise inquiry, procurement packet, sales-engineering handoff, pilot/trial plan, success criteria, renewal, and expansion have owners and SLAs;
8. account, docs, CLI, API, email, webhooks, status, support exports, admin, and sales packets report the same customer lifecycle state;
9. lifecycle UX is accessible, plain-language, low-friction, and explicit about impact and next action;
10. release evidence includes customer-lifecycle, support, and incident reports.

If any item fails, Kolm can still onboard some users, but it is not yet a 100% complete customer operating system.

#### Node 25 V3 Customer Lifecycle Control Plane, First-Value UX, And Customer-Ops Evidence Contract

This V3 addendum maintains master spec tree node `25`. It does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only. It does not edit onboarding pages, email code, notification code, status routes, support flows, account UI, generated docs, tests, or styling while other agents are editing. The purpose is to turn scattered lifecycle surfaces into one product-quality operating system: leads, signups, setup-token reveal, first provider, first route, first capture, first artifact, first deployment, support cases, incidents, enterprise pilots, feedback, renewals, and lifecycle UX must all share one current evidence packet.

##### Node 25 V3 Current Evidence Delta

This pass used the `ui-ux-pro-max` checklist as the UX baseline: accessibility, visible loading/error states, touch-safe controls, clear labels, keyboard paths, no hidden hover-only state, predictable navigation, and plain-language next actions. It also scanned current files and source references with PowerShell because `rg` is not installed in this shell.

| evidence class | observed state | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| persona onboarding | `public/onboard/index.html` 11,476 bytes; `public/onboard/developer.html` 15,756 bytes; `public/onboard/cto.html` 16,118 bytes; `public/onboard/compliance.html` 15,369 bytes; `public/onboard/researcher.html` 15,084 bytes. | Persona pages exist, but V3 requires stateful first-value flows, account continuity, prerequisite checks, blockers, progress persistence, and direct next actions. |
| post-auth account surfaces | `public/account/overview.html` 36,970 bytes; `storage.html` 33,320; `approvals.html` 26,484; `billing.html` 26,329; `sla.html` 24,450; `audit-log.html` 22,743; `captured.html` 21,708; `settings.html` 20,601; `privacy-events.html` 19,960; `devices.html` 19,522. | Account pages are substantial. V3 requires them to share lifecycle state, show first-value progress, expose support/status readiness, and use consistent empty/loading/error/permission states. |
| setup and key reveal | `src/email.js` contains setup-token helpers, one-time raw key cache, TTL, and explanatory comments, but also still has `sendWelcome({ email, apiKey, plan, billingUrl })` that includes `Your API key:` and the raw key in email content; `src/router.js` calls `sendWelcome({ email, apiKey: t.api_key, ... })` during signup. | This is a P0 Node 25 redline. Completion requires code proof that no production onboarding, welcome, setup, recovery, or support path emails raw keys or places them in provider logs. |
| notification and status primitives | `src/notifications.js` 9,970 bytes has preference/state/push tables and push endpoint SSRF mitigation; `public/status.html` 19,672 bytes; `public/status.rss` 843 bytes; `/v1/status/subscribe` exists with rate limiting and dedupe. | Primitives exist. V3 requires one notification policy that separates transactional, status, security, billing, support, product, and marketing messages with consent, unsubscribe, delivery state, and sensitive-content minimization. |
| enterprise inquiry and sales handoff | `public/enterprise/inquiry.html` 21,611 bytes; `src/router.js` has enterprise inquiry intake comments and rate-limit posture. | Contact-sales exists. V3 requires inquiry-to-opportunity routing, SLA, owner, qualification, procurement packet, pilot plan, security blockers, follow-up state, and close-loop tracking. |
| support and success content | `public/docs/tickets.html` 28,572 bytes; `public/docs/cookbook/support-bot.html` 14,009; `public/for/support-automation.html` 16,743; `public/marketplace/kolm-support-7b.html` 12,115; cookbook pages include churn, email reply, incident summarizer, support reply, NPS classifier, on-call classifier, Slack summarizer. | Support demos exist, but V3 requires Kolm's own support operation to have secure bundles, severity, owner, SLA, escalation, consent, retention, redaction, and safe dogfooding. |
| lifecycle tests | Tests include status, support bundle, account, account-console honesty, notifications route docs, enterprise identity, SLA, CLI/TUI/account coherence, and account route docs. | Useful test coverage exists, but V3 needs a single customer-lifecycle release gate that proves signup, setup-token reveal, status subscribe, notifications, support bundle, enterprise inquiry, lifecycle APIs, and account UX parity together. |
| absent customer-ops artifacts | `docs/customer-lifecycle-contract.json`, `docs/onboarding-state-machine.json`, `docs/first-value-milestones.json`, `docs/support-severity-policy.json`, `docs/support-bundle-policy.json`, `docs/incident-communication-policy.json`, `docs/customer-notification-policy.json`, `docs/enterprise-inquiry-sla.json`, `docs/pilot-success-plan-contract.json`, `docs/feedback-roadmap-loop.json`, `docs/customer-ops-ux-standard.json`, `reports/customer-lifecycle`, `reports/support`, and `reports/incidents` are absent. | These are the V3 hard artifacts. Without them, customer operations are pages and tests, not a provable lifecycle system. |

##### Node 25 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| baseline | current source | V3 rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google SRE emergency response | `https://sre.google/sre-book/emergency-response/` | Customer operations need preparation, periodic exercises, rollback practice, clear communication, documented follow-up, and response paths that work under degraded conditions. |
| Google SRE incident management | `https://sre.google/sre-book/managing-incidents/` | Incidents need roles, severity, commander, communication cadence, customer impact, state document, handoff, and learning loop. |
| Atlassian Statuspage postmortems | `https://support.atlassian.com/statuspage/docs/create-a-postmortem/` | Resolved incidents need customer-facing postmortems with timeline, impact, root cause, mitigation, prevention, and follow-up. |
| FTC CAN-SPAM guide | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/can-spam-act-compliance-guide-business` | Marketing and lifecycle email must distinguish transactional/support/security from commercial messages and preserve truthful headers, non-deceptive subjects, physical address where required, opt-out, and unsubscribe handling. |
| RFC 9457 problem details | `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Account, setup, support, notification, enterprise, and status APIs should return stable, actionable, supportable error details without exposing internals or secrets. |
| ISO 10002 complaint handling | `https://www.iso.org/standard/71580.html` | Complaints and support issues need receipt, tracking, acknowledgement, assessment, response, closure, trend analysis, and continual improvement. |
| DORA software delivery metrics | `https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/devops-sre/announcing-the-2024-dora-report` | Customer-success and incident evidence should include deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, and recovery time where release quality affects customers. |
| WCAG 2.2 and local UI/UX skill | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/` and local `ui-ux-pro-max` | Lifecycle UX must meet accessibility basics: visible focus, keyboard paths, labels, contrast, touch targets, loading feedback, plain-language errors, and non-color-only state. |

##### Node 25 V3 Customer Lifecycle Packet Contract

Every lead, user, tenant, trial, enterprise opportunity, support case, notification, incident, status update, feedback item, and renewal must be able to emit or link to this packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.customer_lifecycle_control_plane.v3",
  "node": 25,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "subject": {
    "kind": "lead|signup|tenant|trial|enterprise_opportunity|support_case|incident|feedback|renewal",
    "id": "stable-id",
    "owner": "role-or-user",
    "plan": "free|pro|team|enterprise",
    "persona": "developer|cto|compliance|researcher|operator|buyer",
    "lifecycle_stage": "anonymous|signup|setup|activated|active|blocked|at_risk|renewal|churned"
  },
  "safe_key_reveal": {
    "raw_key_emailed": false,
    "setup_token_used": true,
    "setup_token_ttl_seconds": 1800,
    "one_time_reveal": true,
    "rotation_path": "/account/settings",
    "recovery_path": "/password-reset"
  },
  "first_value": {
    "goal": "route_first_call|capture_first_trace|compile_first_artifact|distill_first_student|deploy_first_device|export_procurement_packet",
    "completed_steps": [],
    "blocked_by": [],
    "next_action": {
      "surface": "account|docs|cli|email|support|sales",
      "label": "Configure provider",
      "href_or_command": "/account/settings"
    },
    "time_to_value_minutes": null
  },
  "communications": {
    "message_class": "transactional|security|billing|support|status|product|marketing",
    "preferences_ref": "docs/customer-notification-policy.json",
    "unsubscribe_required": false,
    "delivery_state": "queued|sent|delivered|bounced|failed|skipped|unknown",
    "sensitive_content_minimized": true
  },
  "support": {
    "severity": "none|question|sev3|sev2|sev1|sev0",
    "sla_ref": "docs/support-severity-policy.json",
    "ticket_ref": null,
    "support_bundle_ref": null,
    "redaction_report_ref": null,
    "customer_consent": false,
    "retention_policy": "support-bundle-policy"
  },
  "status_and_incident": {
    "component": "gateway|account|billing|registry|storage|cloud_train|provider_routing|marketplace",
    "incident_ref": null,
    "customer_impact": "none|suspected|confirmed",
    "next_update_at": null,
    "postmortem_ref": null
  },
  "success": {
    "health": "unknown|green|yellow|red",
    "usage_metrics": [],
    "cost_savings_evidence": null,
    "feedback_refs": [],
    "pilot_success_plan_ref": null,
    "renewal_risk": "unknown|low|medium|high"
  },
  "ux_contract": {
    "accessible_labels": true,
    "keyboard_path": true,
    "loading_state": true,
    "empty_state": true,
    "error_state": true,
    "mobile_safe": true,
    "plain_language_next_action": true
  }
}
```

##### Node 25 V3 Lifecycle State Matrix

| lifecycle state | required UX and product state | release redline |
|---|---|---|
| anonymous visitor | Clear product promise, demo path, pricing path, docs path, enterprise path, no misleading claims, no dead CTA. | Visitor must know what Kolm does and how to try or buy without reading a wall of copy. |
| signup | Plan selected, email validated, geo/legal checks, billing state, secure session, one-time key reveal state. | Signup cannot send raw keys through email or logs. |
| setup token reveal | Token valid/expired/consumed states, reveal once, copy confirmation, rotation path, CLI instructions, recovery path. | Every state must have a safe next action and must not leak secrets in HTML, logs, support bundles, or provider email payloads. |
| first provider | Provider selected, BYOK/cloud/local mode, readiness, missing credentials, legal/provider terms status, test call. | User must see exactly what is missing and whether the blocker is local, provider, billing, or product scope. |
| first route | Paste SDK call, route via Kolm, verify provider, latency/cost trace, fallback state, error recovery. | API and account must expose the same route status and problem details. |
| first capture | Captured call, privacy/redaction status, rights/safety state, retention, dataset eligibility, next action. | Capture cannot silently enter training or benchmark state without rights/safety eligibility. |
| first artifact | Compile/distill result, K-score scope, safety/legal packet, download/run path, verification command, deploy option. | Artifact promotion must include quality, safety, legal, and runtime readiness. |
| first deployment | Runtime target, device/cloud readiness, storage, rollback, monitoring, cost, operator proof. | Deployment must show what is configured, simulated, external, or missing. |
| support case | Severity, owner, SLA, consent, support bundle preview, redaction report, escalation path, closure reason. | No support flow can require founder memory or raw secret access. |
| incident/status | Component, impact, severity, commander, subscriber delivery state, next update, mitigation, postmortem. | Status must be operational state, not a static page. |
| enterprise pilot | Use case, success criteria, security/procurement blockers, owner, SLA, data/legal packet, go/no-go date. | Contact-sales must become a tracked opportunity with measurable next steps. |
| renewal/success | Usage health, savings evidence, support history, blockers, expansion signals, renewal date, customer feedback. | Success cannot rely on ad hoc calls; it must be visible and evidence-backed. |

##### Node 25 V3 Lifecycle UX Acceptance Matrix

| UX dimension | minimum bar for every lifecycle surface | examples in scope |
|---|---|---|
| accessibility | Visible focus, semantic labels, keyboard path, contrast, non-color-only status, screen-reader friendly errors. | Signup, setup, account overview, status, support ticket, enterprise inquiry, billing. |
| touch and mobile | 44px minimum targets, spacing between actions, no hover-only controls, no horizontal overflow, safe mobile forms. | Account cards, setup copy button, support upload, status subscribe, enterprise inquiry. |
| loading and latency | Skeleton/progress for async calls, disabled duplicate-submit buttons, timeout state, retry action. | Provider readiness, storage readiness, support bundle generation, inquiry submit, notification save. |
| empty states | Explain what is missing, why it matters, and the next action. | No captures, no artifacts, no incidents, no support tickets, no notifications, no enterprise packet. |
| error states | RFC 9457-style code, concise human explanation, recovery action, support reference, no internal secret/debug leak. | Signup 409, geo 451, setup token expired, invalid key, failed notification, support upload failure. |
| navigation | Deep links, predictable back path, active nav state, no broken CTAs, consistent account/docs/CLI cross-links. | Onboarding to account, docs to CLI, status to subscribe, support to bundle, enterprise to packet. |
| copy | Outcome-led, low-jargon, explicit scope, no overpromise, one primary action per screen. | Onboarding, account next actions, emails, status updates, support messages, enterprise inquiry. |
| evidence | Every lifecycle claim has current proof, owner, freshness, and link to packet. | SLA, first-value, support response, status uptime, procurement packet, customer success claims. |

| ticket | priority | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---:|---|---|
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-001 | P0 | Generate `docs/customer-lifecycle-control-plane.json`. | Every lifecycle subject maps to schema, owner, state, next action, evidence, UX contract, and current proof. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-002 | P0 | Remove or prove-disabled raw API key email path. | Tests and source review prove no production welcome, setup, support, recovery, or notification path emails raw API keys or stores them in provider logs. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-003 | P0 | Add setup-token end-to-end lifecycle gate. | Signup, token mint, email link, reveal, consumed, expired, rotate, and recovery states are covered by tests and account UX. |
