| W-CUSTOMER-V3-009 | P0 | Create `docs/support-severity-policy.json`. | Severity, SLA, coverage hours, escalation owner, customer-facing language, and enterprise exceptions are consistent across terms, account, support, and sales. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-010 | P0 | Create `docs/support-bundle-policy.json`. | Bundle preview, consent, redaction, size limits, secure upload, ticket link, access log, retention, deletion, and customer-visible contents are defined. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-011 | P0 | Create structured support ticket intake. | Tickets include product surface, route, artifact hash, tenant, severity, reproduction, logs, support bundle, impact, requested outcome, and owner. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-012 | P0 | Add support escalation and handoff workflow. | Support, engineering, security, legal, sales, and incident transitions have timestamp, owner, reason, next action, and customer update. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-013 | P0 | Create `docs/incident-communication-policy.json`. | Incidents have severity, commander, affected components, customer impact, subscriber groups, next update time, closure, and postmortem requirement. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-014 | P0 | Make status component-backed. | Gateway, account, billing, registry, storage, cloud train, provider routing, docs, and marketplace components have state, source, freshness, and history. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-015 | P1 | Add maintenance and postmortem workflow. | Planned maintenance and resolved incidents publish timeline, impact, root cause, mitigation, prevention, owner, and follow-up tasks. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-016 | P0 | Create `docs/customer-notification-policy.json`. | Transactional, security, billing, support, status, product, and marketing messages have preference, consent, unsubscribe, retention, and delivery-state rules. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-017 | P1 | Add notification delivery ledger. | Email, push, webhook, RSS, and status subscriptions report queued/sent/delivered/bounced/failed/skipped without sensitive content leakage. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-018 | P0 | Add customer lifecycle API/CLI/TUI parity. | Operators and users can query lifecycle state, first-value progress, blockers, support status, notification state, and incident impact consistently. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-019 | P0 | Create `docs/feedback-roadmap-loop.json`. | Feedback, bugs, NPS, churn, support themes, feature requests, and failed onboarding map to product owner, severity, revenue impact, and close-loop message. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-020 | P1 | Dogfood Kolm support artifacts safely. | Internal support artifacts use privacy redaction, guardrails, quality checks, human review, escalation, audit receipt, and opt-out policy. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-021 | P0 | Create `docs/enterprise-inquiry-sla.json`. | Inquiry submissions have qualification, routing, owner, SLA, procurement packet, security blockers, trial path, and follow-up state. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-022 | P1 | Create `docs/pilot-success-plan-contract.json`. | Pilots have start/end date, success metrics, first-value plan, usage targets, security/procurement tasks, go/no-go criteria, and renewal path. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-023 | P1 | Create `docs/customer-ops-ux-standard.json`. | Lifecycle surfaces pass accessibility, touch, loading, empty, error, mobile, copy, nav, and evidence-state requirements. |
| W-CUSTOMER-V3-024 | P0 | Add Node 25 completion certificate tied to node `43`. | Build redline cannot relax until lifecycle control plane, safe key reveal, first-value, support, status, notifications, feedback, enterprise pilots, reports, and UX parity pass. |

##### Node 25 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `25` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- No production onboarding, setup, welcome, support, recovery, notification, or email-provider path exposes raw API keys or secrets.
- `docs/customer-lifecycle-control-plane.json`, `docs/onboarding-state-machine.json`, and `docs/first-value-milestones.json` are generated or checked sources for account, docs, CLI, emails, and onboarding pages.
- Every major product surface has a first-value event, blocker model, recovery path, next action, owner, and time-to-value evidence.
- Support severity, ticket intake, support bundles, redaction, consent, retention, escalation, closure, and customer evidence are one policy.
- Status page, RSS, incidents, maintenance windows, notifications, delivery state, postmortems, and customer impact are component-backed and evidence-linked.
- Notifications distinguish transactional, security, billing, support, status, product, and marketing messages with consent, unsubscribe, sensitive-content, and delivery-state rules.
- Feedback, NPS, churn, support themes, bugs, feature requests, failed onboarding, and roadmap items flow into product planning and customer close-loop messaging.
- Enterprise inquiry, procurement packet, sales handoff, pilot/trial success plan, security blockers, renewal, and expansion have owners, SLAs, and current status.
- Lifecycle UX meets accessibility, keyboard, touch, loading, empty, error, mobile, navigation, plain-language, and evidence-state requirements.
- Release evidence includes `reports/customer-lifecycle`, `reports/support`, and `reports/incidents`, and node `43` treats Node `25` as a hard build-redline gate.

### Internal Admin, Operator Console, Privileged Workflows, And Backoffice Governance Ledger V1

Node `26` is the internal-operations and administrative-control branch of the master spec tree. It exists because Kolm cannot be run as a serious enterprise platform if privileged actions depend on ad hoc scripts, founder memory, direct database edits, or pages that exist without proven permissions. The internal control plane must let operators support customers, approve regulated workflows, investigate incidents, manage releases, review risky captures, export audit evidence, and recover production safely without weakening tenant isolation or leaking customer data.

This is a research/specification edit only. It does not change admin pages, audit code, approval queues, RBAC logic, runbooks, tests, scripts, or generated docs.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Area | Current evidence found | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Admin and operator pages | `public/admin.html`, `public/audit-log.html`, `public/enterprise/console.html`, `public/account/approvals.html`, `public/account/audit-log.html`, `public/account/captures/review.html` | Admin surfaces exist, but final completion needs proof they are not shells, have shipped routes, and enforce privileged access consistently. |
| Approval queues | `src/distill-approval-queue.js`, `src/federated-approvals.js`, `tests/wave782-approval-queue.test.js`, `tests/wave461-federated-approvals.test.js`, `public/docs/approvals.html` | Approval primitives exist for distillation and federated sharing, but the full operator system needs approval ownership, SLA, reminders, audit, escalation, and UI state. |
| Tenant/team authorization | `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, tenant scope tests such as `tests/wave411-tenant-isolation.test.js`, `tests/wave420-pipeline-tenant-force.test.js`, `tests/wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope.test.js` | RBAC primitives exist, but admin completion requires a complete permission matrix for every privileged action and cross-tenant read path. |
| Audit chain | `src/audit.js`, `src/audit-export.js`, `src/audit-retention.js`, `tests/wave770-audit-export.test.js`, `tests/wave448-audit-log-triangle.test.js` | Append-only audit chain exists, but final state needs review cadence, retention policy, export authorization, tamper alerts, and customer/operator UX. |
| Route honesty and admin docs | `tests/wave500-account-console-route-honesty.test.js`, `tests/wave502-admin-registry-storage-route-honesty.test.js`, `tests/wave516-admin-route-docs.test.js` | Route honesty tests exist, but final state requires every admin page/action to be route-owned, smoke-tested, and visible in product surface maps. |
| Review/moderation queues | `public/account/captures/review.html`, capture poisoning/copyright tests, marketplace/registry governance docs | Review flows exist in pieces, but final state needs one queue contract for capture risk, artifact publish, marketplace publish, abuse, data deletion, and support escalations. |
| Internal runbooks and audits | `public/cookbook/runbook-step.html`, many `docs/research/*audit*`, `scripts/audit-*`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/sitewide-audit.mjs` | Runbook/audit content exists, but final state needs operational ownership, schedule, evidence storage, and a live admin cockpit that reports what is stale. |
| Feature and release controls | Feature/routing flags appear across env, scripts, runtime policy, and tests; SRE-style canary/rollback is not a single visible contract | Final state needs a feature-flag registry, owner, expiry, blast radius, rollback plan, test matrix, and admin UI/CLI controls. |

#### External References And Operating Models

| Reference | URL | What Kolm should adopt |
|---|---|---|
| NIST RBAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Role-Based-Access-Control` | Model privileged roles, permissions, operations, objects, assignments, hierarchies, and constraints explicitly. |
| NIST ABAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/162/upd2/final` | Use attributes such as tenant, role, plan, environment, region, data sensitivity, action risk, and time/context for just-in-time authorization decisions. |
| OWASP Authorization Testing Automation | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Authorization_Testing_Automation_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Generate tests from an authorization matrix so every admin route and action is tested for allow/deny cases. |
| OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Logging_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Log security-relevant events consistently while excluding secrets, tokens, sensitive personal data, and high-risk payloads. |
| CIS Control 8 audit log management | `https://www.cisecurity.org/controls/audit-log-management` | Treat audit log collection, storage, time sync, review, retention, protection, and alerting as a control family. |
| NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final` | Map admin controls to access control, audit and accountability, configuration management, incident response, and system maintenance control families. |
| Google SRE release engineering and canarying | `https://sre.google/sre-book/release-engineering/` and `https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/` | Manage releases, flags, canaries, rollback, and configuration changes through repeatable, auditable operational processes. |

#### Internal Operations Redlines

1. Do not ship admin pages that are visually present but route-dead, permissionless, or backed by placeholder data.
2. Do not let privileged support, admin, audit, billing, registry, storage, artifact, or tenant actions bypass the same RBAC/ABAC engine used by account APIs.
3. Do not permit cross-tenant reads for support unless there is a ticket, reason, time-bound approval, audit row, customer-visible policy, and data-minimization view.
4. Do not implement "impersonation" without visible banner, reason, approval, TTL, audit chain row, restricted actions, and automatic revocation.
5. Do not let break-glass access be a silent environment variable or direct database edit. It needs multi-party authorization, reason, expiration, post-review, and customer impact record.
6. Do not let audit export leak secrets, raw prompts, raw completions, PHI/PII, provider keys, cookies, setup tokens, or internal-only incident notes.
7. Do not let approval queues deadlock regulated workflows. Every pending approval needs owner, SLA, expiry, reminders, delegation, escalation, and terminal state.
8. Do not let feature flags or kill switches live only as undocumented env vars. Each flag needs owner, purpose, default, environments, test coverage, expiry, and cleanup rule.
9. Do not allow operators to mutate billing, plan, registry, storage, artifact, or tenant state without preview, confirmation, audit, and rollback/compensation path.
10. Do not mix compliance audit logs, debug logs, support notes, incident notes, and customer-visible audit exports without classification and retention policy.
11. Do not let moderation/review queues produce final decisions without reviewer identity, reason code, evidence link, appeal path, and downstream purge/rebuild propagation where applicable.
12. Do not let internal dashboards report "green" from stale audits, local-only checks, or missing production probes.
13. Do not let public/account/admin nav expose privileged paths to users who cannot use them without clear disabled states and explanations.
14. Do not let operator tools become a second product surface with weaker accessibility, localization, keyboard support, or responsive behavior.
15. Do not rely on a single founder account, direct shell access, or manual database edits as a backoffice process.

#### Canonical Admin Evidence Packet

Every privileged action and internal workflow should be able to produce or link to this evidence shape:

```json
{
  "admin_packet_version": "YYYY.MM.DD-N",
  "action": {
    "id": "stable-action-id",
    "surface": "admin|account|cli|api|script|support",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high|critical",
    "environment": "local|staging|production",
    "tenant_scope": "single_tenant|multi_tenant|system"
  },
  "authorization": {
    "actor_id": "user-or-service",
    "actor_role": "role",
    "required_role": "role",
    "required_scopes": ["scope"],
    "attributes": ["tenant", "region", "support_case", "approval_id"],
    "approval_ref": "approval-or-null",
    "break_glass": false
  },
  "change": {
    "preview": "summary",
    "confirmed_at": "timestamp",
    "idempotency_key": "key",
    "rollback_or_compensation": "plan-ref",
    "affected_resources": ["resource-id"]
  },
  "audit": {
    "audit_event_id": "aud-id",
    "chain_hash": "hash",
    "retention_policy": "policy-ref",
    "customer_visible": true,
    "secret_values_included": false
  },
  "operations": {
    "ticket_ref": "support-or-incident-ref",
    "runbook_ref": "runbook-ref",
    "owner": "role",
    "review_due_at": "timestamp",
    "status": "pending|done|failed|rolled_back"
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `26.1` Admin surfaces, operator console, support tools, feature flags, kill switches, and environment controls | Every admin/operator page and command has a real route/action, permission gate, owner, environment scope, smoke test, empty/error state, and public/internal visibility classification. |
| `26.2` Privileged access, RBAC/ABAC, impersonation, break-glass, separation of duties, and approvals | Every privileged action has an authorization matrix, allow/deny tests, approval requirement where needed, separation-of-duty rule, break-glass workflow, and audit trail. |
| `26.3` Audit logs, audit export, retention, tamper evidence, review cadence, and compliance evidence | Audit events are chained, tenant-fenced, redacted, exportable, reviewed, retained by policy, tamper-alerted, and mapped to enterprise evidence requirements. |
| `26.4` Review queues, moderation, capture/artifact approvals, marketplace approvals, and human-in-loop controls | Review queues have intake, priority, assignment, SLA, decision reasons, evidence links, appeal/override rules, downstream propagation, and metrics. |
| `26.5` Operational runbooks, internal playbooks, release toggles, rollback controls, and change management | Every operational workflow has runbook, owner, precheck, rollback, feature flag/kill switch state, incident linkage, and post-change review. |
| `26.6` Internal UX, admin APIs, CLI/TUI operator flows, metrics, alerts, and admin evidence packets | Operators get dense, accessible, consistent UX across admin pages, account pages, CLI/TUI, APIs, alerts, and evidence exports without leaking customer data. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-ADMIN-001` | `26.1` | Inventory every admin/operator surface and action. | Admin pages, account privileged pages, CLI verbs, scripts, routes, and support tools map to one owner, route, permission, and proof command. |
| `W-ADMIN-002` | `26.1` | Build a feature-flag and kill-switch registry. | Every flag has owner, description, default, environments, rollout strategy, expiry, cleanup date, tests, and admin/CLI visibility. |
| `W-ADMIN-003` | `26.1` | Verify admin pages are not shells. | `public/admin.html`, `public/audit-log.html`, `public/enterprise/console.html`, approvals, audit-log, and review pages load real state or explain unavailable state truthfully. |
| `W-ADMIN-004` | `26.2` | Generate a privileged-action authorization matrix. | Every admin/support/billing/registry/storage/artifact/tenant operation lists required role, scope, tenant relation, approval, and deny cases. |
| `W-ADMIN-005` | `26.2` | Add authorization testing automation. | Tests are generated from the matrix and prove viewers, members, admins, owners, support, service accounts, and cross-tenant actors get correct allow/deny results. |
| `W-ADMIN-006` | `26.2` | Design support impersonation safely or prohibit it. | If enabled, impersonation has banner, reason, ticket, approval, TTL, restricted action set, audit chain row, and customer-visible policy; otherwise every UI says unavailable. |
| `W-ADMIN-007` | `26.2` | Implement break-glass governance. | Break-glass requires multi-party approval, time limit, reason, incident ref, scoped permissions, automatic revocation, and post-event review. |
| `W-ADMIN-008` | `26.2` | Enforce separation of duties for regulated workflows. | Distill approvals, marketplace publish, artifact deletion, billing changes, and audit export cannot be requested and approved by the same actor where policy forbids it. |
| `W-ADMIN-009` | `26.3` | Expand audit op coverage to every privileged action. | All plan, key, tenant, support, admin, artifact, registry, storage, approval, feature flag, and release-toggle operations append redacted audit rows. |
| `W-ADMIN-010` | `26.3` | Add audit review cadence and tamper alerts. | Audit chain verification, stale-review detection, tamper breaks, and export anomalies produce operator alerts and evidence records. |
| `W-ADMIN-011` | `26.3` | Classify audit/log/export data. | Compliance audit logs, debug logs, incident notes, support notes, and customer exports each have retention, redaction, access, and deletion policies. |
| `W-ADMIN-012` | `26.3` | Make audit export enterprise-ready. | Exports include scope, filters, chain verification, redaction report, actor identities, timestamps, and customer-safe evidence packet. |
| `W-ADMIN-013` | `26.4` | Unify review queues. | Capture risk, copyright, poisoning, artifact publish, marketplace publish, abuse, deletion, and support escalations share queue schema, states, owner, and metrics. |
| `W-ADMIN-014` | `26.4` | Add reviewer decision contracts. | Every review decision includes reviewer, role, reason code, evidence, affected resources, appeal/override path, and downstream propagation state. |
| `W-ADMIN-015` | `26.4` | Add approval queue operational controls. | Approvals have SLA, reminders, delegation, expiry, escalation, bulk review safety, and terminal-state analytics. |
| `W-ADMIN-016` | `26.5` | Build an operational runbook registry. | Every runbook has owner, last tested date, prerequisites, risk, rollback, evidence, and link to related routes/scripts. |
| `W-ADMIN-017` | `26.5` | Bind release toggles to rollout/canary/rollback. | Feature flags and kill switches connect to release evidence, canary stage, health metrics, rollback command, and stale-flag cleanup. |
| `W-ADMIN-018` | `26.5` | Add admin change management. | High-risk internal changes require preview, confirmation, idempotency, ticket/incident ref, audit row, and post-change verification. |
| `W-ADMIN-019` | `26.6` | Build an internal operator UX standard. | Admin pages and CLI/TUI operator flows use dense operational layouts, keyboard support, clear risk labels, no hidden caveats, and consistent empty/error/loading states. |
| `W-ADMIN-020` | `26.6` | Add admin evidence packet export. | Operators can export a customer-safe packet containing privileged actions, approvals, audit verification, review decisions, feature flags, incidents, and runbook evidence. |

#### Node 26 Relax Redline

Node `26` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Which internal/admin/operator actions exist, who can perform them, what route or command executes them, and what evidence proves the permission boundary?
- Can support help a tenant without raw cross-tenant access, secret exposure, direct database edits, or untracked founder-only actions?
- Can every privileged mutation be previewed, confirmed, audited, rolled back or compensated, and tied to a ticket, incident, approval, or runbook?
- Can audit logs prove integrity, tenant scope, actor identity, redaction, retention, export scope, and review status?
- Can review queues move risky captures, artifacts, marketplace submissions, approvals, abuse cases, and deletion requests to terminal states with reason and downstream effects?
- Can feature flags and kill switches be listed, tested, rolled out, reverted, expired, and cleaned up without hidden environment-variable drift?
- Can operators use admin pages, CLI/TUI, APIs, alerts, and exports with the same product-quality bar as customer-facing UI?

The relax condition for node `26` is not "there is an admin page." It is: every privileged workflow has a real control, permission matrix, audit record, review/approval lifecycle, rollback path, runbook, operator UX, and customer-safe evidence trail.

#### Node 26 V2 Internal Admin, Operator Console, And Privileged Workflow Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `26` and does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only. It does not change admin pages, audit code, approval queues, RBAC logic, feature flags, runbooks, tests, routes, generated docs, or styling while other agents are editing.

The completion bar is not "an admin page exists." Kolm is complete only when every privileged action has an owner, route/command, authorization rule, deny tests, approval or separation-of-duty policy, preview, confirmation, audit row, rollback/compensation path, runbook, operator UX state, and customer-safe evidence trail.

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |

Completion requires route ownership, real-state proof, permission gates, smoke tests, and non-placeholder operator states. |
| audit modules | `src/audit.js`, `src/audit-export.js`, `src/audit-retention.js`, `src/auditor-attestation.js`. | Audit chain and export concepts exist. Completion requires coverage of every privileged action, redaction classification, tamper alerts, review cadence, export authorization, and customer-safe packets. |
| approval modules | `src/distill-approval-queue.js`: 22,153 bytes, 660 lines; `src/federated-approvals.js`: 12,953 bytes. | Approval queues exist. Completion requires owner, SLA, reminder, escalation, delegation, terminal states, separation-of-duty, and downstream propagation. |
| authorization modules | `src/team-capture-rbac.js` and many tenant-scope tests. | RBAC primitives exist. Completion needs an operator permission matrix and generated allow/deny tests for every privileged action and cross-tenant read path. |

| absent registries | `docs/admin-action-registry.json`, `docs/operator-permission-matrix.json`, `docs/impersonation-policy.json`, `docs/break-glass-policy.json`, `docs/approval-workflow-registry.json`, `docs/audit-export-policy.json`, `docs/feature-flag-registry.json`, `docs/operator-runbook-registry.json` are absent. | Backoffice governance is not yet machine-readable enough for release/account/CLI/admin parity. |
| absent reports | `reports/admin`, `reports/audit`, and `reports/approvals` are absent. | Privileged-action and admin evidence is not yet durable release proof. |

##### Node 26 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| NIST RBAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Role-Based-Access-Control` | Model users, roles, permissions, operations, objects, role assignment, constraints, and administrative functions explicitly. |
| NIST ABAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/162/upd2/final` | Use attributes such as tenant, role, environment, region, data sensitivity, action risk, support case, approval, and time for contextual authorization. |
| OWASP authorization testing automation | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Authorization_Testing_Automation_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Generate allow/deny tests from the permission matrix rather than manually sampling admin routes. |
| OWASP logging guidance | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Logging_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Security logs must be consistent, useful, time-synced, protected, and free of secrets or sensitive payload leakage. |
| CIS Control 8 audit log management | `https://www.cisecurity.org/controls/audit-log-management` | Audit logs need collection, retention, review, protection, alerting, time sync, and centralized management. |
| Google SRE canarying releases | `https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/` | Operational toggles, rollout, canary, rollback, and blast-radius controls need measured health and repeatable processes. |

##### Node 26 V2 Admin Action Registry Contract

Every privileged action must map to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-admin-action-registry-v2",
  "action_id": "tenant.api_key.revoke",
  "surface": "account|admin|cli|api|script|support",
  "owner": "security-ops",
  "risk_level": "critical",
  "environment_scope": ["production", "staging", "local"],
  "tenant_scope": "single_tenant",
  "route_or_command": "POST /v1/account/keys/:id/revoke",
  "authorization": {
    "model": "rbac_abac",
    "required_roles": ["owner", "admin"],
    "required_attributes": ["same_tenant", "mfa_recent"],
    "deny_cases": ["viewer", "other_tenant", "expired_session", "support_without_ticket"]
  },
  "approval": {
    "required": false,
    "separation_of_duties": false,
    "break_glass_allowed": false
  },
  "operator_ux": {
    "preview_required": true,
    "confirmation": "explicit",
    "loading_empty_error_states": true,
    "accessibility_contract": "node_21"
  },
  "audit": {
    "event_type": "api_key_revoked",
    "chain_required": true,
    "redaction_policy": "no_secret_values",
    "customer_visible": true
  },
  "rollback": {
    "type": "compensating_action",
    "description": "issue_new_key"
  },
  "runbook": "docs/runbooks/api-key-revoke.md",
  "tests": ["allow_owner", "deny_viewer", "deny_cross_tenant"],
  "evidence": {
    "last_smoke": null,
    "latest_report": null
  }
}
```

##### Node 26 V2 Privileged Workflow Classes

| workflow | required proof |
|---|---|
| support access | support case, customer policy, data minimization, time-bound access, same-tenant scope, audit row, no secrets, review. |
| impersonation | if allowed: visible banner, reason, support case, approval, TTL, restricted actions, automatic revocation, audit, customer-visible policy; if not allowed: explicit unavailable state. |
| break-glass | multi-party approval, incident ref, reason, scoped permissions, TTL, secret-safe audit, automatic revocation, post-review, customer-impact note. |
| audit export | authorization, scope, filters, redaction report, chain verification, retention, customer-safe packet, export access log. |
| approval queue | intake, priority, owner, SLA, reminder, delegation, escalation, terminal states, reason code, downstream propagation, metrics. |
| feature flag | owner, purpose, default, environments, rollout stage, blast radius, expiry, tests, kill switch, rollback, cleanup. |
| admin mutation | preview, confirmation, idempotency, affected resources, audit row, rollback/compensation, post-change verification. |
| review/moderation | reviewer, reason, evidence, appeal path, override policy, purge/rebuild downstream effects, customer visibility. |

##### Node 26 V2 Relax Redline

Node `26` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. every privileged admin/account/API/CLI/script/support action is registered with owner, route/command, risk, permission, audit, rollback, and runbook;
2. admin pages and operator tools are real, permission-gated, smoke-tested surfaces, not placeholder shells;
3. RBAC/ABAC matrix generates allow/deny tests for every privileged action and cross-tenant path;
4. impersonation and break-glass are either prohibited or implemented with banner, reason, approval, TTL, restricted scope, audit, revocation, and review;
5. separation of duties exists for regulated approvals, marketplace publish, artifact deletion, billing changes, audit export, and legal hold;
6. audit logs and exports are redacted, tamper-evident, tenant-scoped, reviewed, retained, alerting, and customer-safe;
7. review/approval queues have owner, SLA, reminders, delegation, escalation, terminal states, reason codes, appeal/override, and downstream propagation;
8. feature flags, kill switches, release toggles, canaries, rollback controls, and stale flag cleanup have registry proof;
9. operator UX meets the same accessibility, clarity, loading/error, and evidence standards as customer-facing surfaces;
10. release evidence includes admin, audit, approval, runbook, and privileged-action reports.

If any item fails, Kolm may have admin tools, but it is not yet a safe enterprise backoffice control plane.

#### Node 26 V3 Privileged Operations Control Plane, Operator UX, And Backoffice Evidence Contract

This V3 addendum maintains master spec tree node `26`. It does not create a new branch. It is a research/specification edit only. It does not edit admin pages, audit modules, approval queues, RBAC code, routes, generated artifacts, tests, scripts, runbooks, feature flags, or styling while other agents are working. The purpose is to make privileged-operations finality concrete: Kolm is not complete because it has admin pages, approval modules, or audit exports. It is complete only when every privileged action and every operator screen is permissioned, reviewed, logged, reversible where possible, runbook-backed, test-generated, UX-complete, and tied to one current evidence packet.

##### Node 26 V3 Current Evidence Delta

This pass used the `ui-ux-pro-max` checklist as the internal UX baseline: dense operational layouts, clear labels, keyboard paths, visible loading/error/empty states, no hidden hover-only actions, strong contrast, and explicit risk/permission state. It also scanned the current worktree with PowerShell because `rg` is not available in this shell.

V3 requires an inventory proving each page has owner, auth mode, data source, route smoke, real states, deny states, keyboard path, and customer-safe evidence. |
| admin docs and public operator docs | `public/docs/approvals.html`, `public/docs/audit.html`, `public/docs/audit-export.html`, `public/docs/cli/auditor.html`, and admin route docs tests exist. | Documentation exists, but V3 requires docs, account, API, CLI/TUI, and admin console to describe the same privileged action states and limitations. |
| audit modules | `src/audit.js`, `src/audit-export.js`, `src/audit-retention.js`, `src/auditor-attestation.js`, `tests/wave166-auditor-attestation.test.js`, `tests/wave448-audit-log-triangle.test.js`, `tests/wave770-audit-export.test.js`. | Audit primitives are substantial. V3 requires complete coverage of privileged actions, redaction classification, chain verification, retention, tamper alerts, export authorization, and release-time reports. |

V3 requires one approval/review queue contract with states, owner, SLA, reminder, delegation, escalation, reason codes, appeal/override, downstream purge/rebuild, and metrics. |

V3 should prevent research artifacts from drifting away from admin execution. |

##### Node 26 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| baseline | current source | V3 rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| NIST RBAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Role-Based-Access-Control` | Model subjects, roles, permissions, operations, objects, role assignments, constraints, and administration explicitly. No privileged action can rely only on route naming or UI hiding. |
| NIST ABAC | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/162/upd2/final` | Add attributes beyond role: tenant relation, data sensitivity, environment, region, support case, incident, approval state, time, device/session posture, and action risk. |
| OWASP authorization testing automation | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Authorization_Testing_Automation_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Generate authorization tests from a matrix. Every privileged route/action needs allow and deny cases for positive, horizontal, vertical, unauthenticated, stale-session, and cross-tenant scenarios. |
| OWASP logging guidance | `https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Logging_Cheat_Sheet.html` | Logs must be useful, consistent, protected, time-synced, monitored, and free of secrets, tokens, raw prompts, PHI/PII payloads, raw keys, and excessive debug data. |
| CIS Control 8 audit log management | `https://www.cisecurity.org/controls/audit-log-management` | Audit logs need collection, centralization, retention, review, alerting, time synchronization, protection, and investigation workflow, not only write-time append. |
| NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final` | Map privileged operations to access control, audit/accountability, configuration management, incident response, risk assessment, system maintenance, and supply-chain families. |
| Google SRE release engineering and canarying | `https://sre.google/sre-book/release-engineering/` and `https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/` | Feature flags, canaries, rollbacks, release freeze, staged rollout, monitoring, and change verification must be repeatable and auditable, not manual founder memory. |
| WCAG 2.2 and local UI/UX skill | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/` and local `ui-ux-pro-max` | Internal tools still need accessibility, visible focus, keyboard paths, labels, contrast, touch-safe controls, clear errors, loading states, and non-color-only risk indicators. |

##### Node 26 V3 Privileged Operations Packet Contract

Every admin page, support tool, API route, CLI/TUI command, internal script, feature flag, kill switch, approval queue item, review decision, release toggle, audit export, break-glass event, impersonation attempt, and runbook execution must be able to emit or link to this packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.privileged_operations_control_plane.v3",
  "node": 26,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "subject": {
    "kind": "admin_page|account_page|api_route|cli_command|tui_view|script|flag|approval|review_decision|audit_export|runbook",
    "id": "stable-id",
    "owner": "role-or-team",
    "product_surface": "identity-access-billing|capture-data-privacy|compile-artifacts|distill-train-evaluate|runtime-compute|governance-compliance-security|deployment-edge-federated|public-docs-sdk",
    "risk": "low|medium|high|critical",
    "tenant_scope": "none|single_tenant|cross_tenant|system"
  },
  "authorization": {
    "rbac_roles_allowed": ["owner", "admin"],
    "abac_requirements": ["same_tenant", "support_case_open", "mfa_recent", "environment_production"],
    "deny_cases_generated": true,
    "support_access_allowed": false,
    "service_account_allowed": false,
    "cross_tenant_allowed": false
  },
  "approval": {
    "required": false,
    "policy": "none|self_confirm|admin_approve|two_person|security_approve|legal_approve",
    "separation_of_duties": true,
    "approver_role": null,
    "sla": null,
    "appeal_or_override": "not_allowed|documented_override|customer_visible_appeal"
  },
  "impersonation": {
    "allowed": false,
    "policy_ref": "docs/impersonation-policy.json",
    "visible_banner_required": true,
    "ttl_seconds": 0,
    "restricted_actions": []
  },
  "break_glass": {
    "allowed": false,
    "policy_ref": "docs/break-glass-policy.json",
    "incident_ref_required": true,
    "multi_party_approval": true,
    "auto_revoke": true,
    "post_review_required": true
  },
  "change_safety": {
    "dry_run_available": true,
    "preview_diff": true,
    "confirmation_required": true,
    "idempotency_key_required": true,
    "rollback_or_compensation": "rollback_command_or_compensation_path",
    "blast_radius": "tenant|region|provider|global"
  },
  "audit": {
    "audit_event": "privileged.action.executed",
    "redaction_policy": "docs/audit-log-policy.json",
    "chain_verified": true,
    "retention_policy": "docs/audit-retention-policy.json",
    "customer_visible": false,
    "export_policy": "docs/audit-export-policy.json"
  },
  "review_queue": {
    "queue_ref": null,
    "state": "not_applicable|queued|reviewing|approved|denied|expired|escalated|overridden",
    "reason_code_required": true,
    "downstream_propagation": ["none"]
  },
  "runbook": {
    "path": "docs/operator-runbook-registry.json#runbook-id",
    "last_tested_at": null,
    "stale_after_days": 90,
    "evidence_report": "reports/operator/runbook-id.json"
  },
  "ux_contract": {
    "real_state": true,
    "permission_denied_state": true,
    "empty_state": true,
    "loading_state": true,
    "error_state": true,
    "keyboard_path": true,
    "risk_label_visible": true,
    "no_hover_only_action": true,
    "mobile_or_small_view_safe": true
  },
  "evidence": {
    "source_paths": [],
    "test_paths": [],
    "smoke_paths": [],
    "report_paths": [],
    "claim_scope": "internal_only|customer_visible|public_claim_allowed"
  }
}
```

##### Node 26 V3 Privileged Action Matrix

| privileged class | minimum controls | release redline |
|---|---|---|
| admin console access | MFA/session freshness, admin role, environment label, route ownership, deny state, audit, no secret render. | `/admin` cannot be a static or founder-only surface; it must prove access control and real-state behavior. |
| support access | Support case, tenant consent or contract policy, least-privilege view, data minimization, TTL, no raw secrets, audit, post-review. | Support cannot browse customer data by ambient admin power or founder memory. |
| impersonation | Explicit policy; if allowed, visible banner, reason, ticket, approval, TTL, restricted actions, audit, customer-visible record where applicable. | Silent impersonation is forbidden. If unsupported, UI/API must show explicit unavailable state. |
| break-glass | Incident ref, two-person approval, scoped permission, TTL, automatic revocation, chain audit, postmortem, customer impact decision. | Emergency access cannot become a permanent role or an unreviewed backdoor. |
| tenant data export | Scope filters, legal/data basis, redaction, chain verification, access log, retention, customer-safe packet, deletion impact. | Export cannot include raw secrets, unrelated tenant data, or unbounded prompt/completion payloads. |
| audit export | Export authorization, filter preview, redaction report, tamper verification, retention, reviewer, customer visibility class. | Audit export is itself a privileged audited action and cannot bypass the audit chain. |
| billing and plan changes | Plan authority, pricing contract, payment state, sales approval where required, customer notice, rollback, audit. | Operators cannot silently change billing, quota, enterprise state, or sales-required plan status. |
| API key and credential actions | No raw reveal after creation, rotation path, revoke/restore policy, affected integrations preview, audit, notification. | Admin tools must not expose raw keys or provider credentials. |
| artifact deletion or quarantine | Owner, affected deployments, legal/safety/data reason, preview, approval, downstream purge/rebuild, restore/appeal if applicable. | Destructive artifact actions require review and propagation proof. |
| marketplace/registry publish | Publisher identity, license/security/safety scan, review, takedown policy, rating/payout constraints, audit. | Marketplace publish cannot be an unreviewed upload path. |
| distill/federated approval | Requester/approver separation, data/legal/safety state, quality threshold, reason code, downstream promotion gate. | No regulated or cross-party data/model movement without approval state and audit. |
| feature flag and kill switch | Owner, purpose, default, envs, rollout, blast radius, expiry, monitoring, rollback, cleanup ticket. | No stale or anonymous production flag can gate a product promise. |
| canary and rollback | Canary stage, metric guard, abort condition, rollback command, owner, evidence, customer impact. | Release toggles cannot ship without rollback and observability. |
| internal scripts | Script owner, dry-run, environment guard, idempotency, destructive action review, log redaction, report path. | Production scripts cannot be undocumented one-off operator powers. |

##### Node 26 V3 Operator UX Matrix

| UX dimension | internal product bar | examples in scope |
|---|---|---|
| information density | Dense but scannable tables, filters, sort, bulk-safe selection, timestamps, owner, state, and risk visible without paragraphs. | Admin home, audit log, approvals, capture review, enterprise console. |
| permission clarity | Show current role, tenant scope, disabled reason, approval needed, and escalation path. | Denied support access, cross-tenant export, billing change, artifact quarantine. |
| risk communication | High-risk actions carry visible blast radius, irreversible/rollback label, affected objects, and confirmation copy that names the action. | Delete artifact, revoke key, run break-glass, publish marketplace item, rollback release. |
| empty/loading/error states | Every async panel has loading, empty, error, timeout, retry, and support/runbook action. | Audit export, readiness checks, approval queues, feature flag registry, runbook reports. |
| accessibility | Visible focus, keyboard path, semantic buttons, labels, contrast, non-color-only state, safe target sizing. | Account admin pages, internal console, CLI/TUI parity outputs. |
| evidence links | Each page links or displays source packet, test/report, route/API/CLI equivalent, freshness, and owner. | Admin action registry, audit policy, approval registry, runbook registry, feature flag registry. |
| copy and terminology | Plain operator language: action, impact, owner, state, next action. No undefined internal acronyms. | Error banners, confirmation modals, empty states, audit export descriptions. |
| safe bulk actions | Bulk actions require selection review, filtered scope summary, undo/rollback where possible, rate limits, and audit batching. | Capture review, marketplace moderation, approvals, audit exports. |

##### Node 26 V3 Work Ticket Ledger

| ticket | priority | deliverable | acceptance |
|---|---:|---|---|
| W-ADMIN-V3-001 | P0 | Generate `docs/admin-surface-inventory.json`. | Every admin/account privileged page, route, CLI/TUI command, script, and support tool has owner, auth mode, data source, smoke, UX states, and evidence paths. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-002 | P0 | Generate `docs/privileged-action-matrix.json`. | Every privileged action declares subject, risk, RBAC roles, ABAC attributes, approval policy, audit event, rollback, runbook, tests, and claim scope. |

| W-ADMIN-V3-005 | P0 | Create `docs/feature-flag-registry.json`. | Every flag has owner, purpose, default, environments, rollout, blast radius, expiry, cleanup ticket, tests, admin/CLI visibility, and kill-switch behavior. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-006 | P0 | Add kill switch, canary, and rollback registry. | High-risk rollout controls have metric guard, abort condition, rollback command, owner, customer impact state, and release report. |

| W-ADMIN-V3-008 | P0 | Create `docs/break-glass-policy.json`. | Emergency access requires incident ref, two-person approval, scoped permissions, TTL, auto-revoke, audit chain, post-review, and customer-impact decision. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-009 | P0 | Govern support access. | Support access requires case, tenant relation, data minimization, no raw secrets, sensitive field masking, TTL, audit, post-review, and customer policy mapping. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-010 | P0 | Add cross-tenant privileged data minimization. | Any cross-tenant/system view proves aggregation, masking, purpose, authorization, and absence of raw customer content unless explicitly approved. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-011 | P0 | Create `docs/audit-log-policy.json`. | Audit events define event ids, actor, subject, redaction, retention, tamper check, time sync, review cadence, alert thresholds, and export class. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-012 | P0 | Expand audit coverage to all privileged actions. | Plan/key/tenant/support/admin/artifact/registry/storage/approval/flag/release/script operations append redacted audit rows with chain verification. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-013 | P1 | Add audit tamper and stale-review reports. | Chain break, missing event, anomalous export, stale review, and retention breach produce alerts and `reports/audit` evidence. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-014 | P0 | Create `docs/audit-export-policy.json`. | Audit exports define authorization, filters, redaction report, chain proof, retention, customer visibility, access log, and export revocation. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-015 | P0 | Create `docs/review-queue-contract.json`. | Distill, federated, capture risk, copyright, poisoning, marketplace, deletion, support, and abuse queues share schema, states, owners, SLAs, metrics, and reason codes. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-016 | P0 | Add reviewer decision and propagation contract. | Decisions include reviewer, role, reason, evidence, affected resources, appeal/override, downstream purge/rebuild, customer visibility, and audit. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-017 | P1 | Add approval queue operations. | Reminders, delegation, expiry, escalation, bulk-review safety, terminal-state analytics, and stale queue alerts are visible in account/admin/CLI. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-018 | P0 | Create `docs/operator-runbook-registry.json`. | Every runbook has owner, prerequisites, risk, dry-run, scripts/routes, rollback, last tested date, stale threshold, and evidence report. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-019 | P0 | Add privileged change-management policy. | High-risk internal changes require preview, confirmation, idempotency, ticket/incident ref, audit, post-change verification, and rollback/compensation. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-020 | P1 | Create `docs/admin-ux-standard.json`. | Admin/account/CLI/TUI operator flows define layout, labels, permission states, risk labels, loading/error/empty states, focus, keyboard, and screenshot/manual verification requirements. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-021 | P1 | Add CLI/TUI/API parity for privileged operations. | Operators can query privileged action registry, approval queues, audit verification, feature flags, runbooks, and open admin redlines without secrets. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-022 | P0 | Inventory privileged scripts and env powers. | Production-capable scripts have owner, env guard, dry-run, idempotency, destructive action review, log redaction, and report path. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-023 | P1 | Generate admin evidence reports. | `reports/admin`, `reports/audit`, `reports/operator`, and `reports/approvals` capture release-time privileged action, audit, approval, flag, runbook, UX, and route smoke evidence. |
| W-ADMIN-V3-024 | P0 | Add Node 26 completion certificate tied to node `43`. | Build redline cannot relax until surface inventory, privileged action matrix, generated auth tests, policies, registries, reports, UX standard, and claim gates agree. |

##### Node 26 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `26` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- `docs/admin-surface-inventory.json` and `docs/privileged-action-matrix.json` exist, are checked by verification, and cover every admin/account/API/CLI/TUI/script/support privileged action.
- Authorization tests are generated from the matrix and prove allow/deny cases for owner, admin, member, viewer, support, service account, cross-tenant, unauthenticated, expired session, and stale MFA contexts.
- Admin pages and operator tools are real, permission-gated, smoke-tested surfaces with loading, empty, denied, error, timeout, and recovery states.
- Impersonation and break-glass are either prohibited with explicit unavailable states or implemented with reason, approval, TTL, restricted scope, audit, auto-revoke, and review.
- Support access is governed by case, purpose, tenant relation, minimization, no raw secrets, TTL, audit, post-review, and customer policy.
- Audit logs, retention, exports, chain verification, redaction, tamper alerts, review cadence, customer-safe packets, and export access logs are one policy family.
- Review and approval queues have owners, SLAs, reminders, delegation, expiry, escalation, terminal states, reason codes, appeals/overrides, downstream propagation, and metrics.
- Feature flags, kill switches, canaries, rollback controls, release toggles, blast radius, stale cleanup, and operator visibility are one registry.
- Internal scripts and runbooks are owner-tagged, dry-run capable where relevant, environment-guarded, idempotent, redacted, tested, and stale-reviewed.
- Operator UX meets accessibility, keyboard, loading/error/empty, permission, risk label, dense layout, evidence-link, and no-hover-only-action requirements.
- Release evidence includes `reports/admin`, `reports/audit`, `reports/operator`, and `reports/approvals`, and node `43` treats Node `26` as a hard build-redline gate.

If any item fails, Kolm may have useful admin pages and audit primitives, but it is not yet a state-of-the-art privileged operations system for enterprise AI.

### Open-Source Governance, Standardization, Community, And Ecosystem Stewardship Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not edit product code. It defines what the repository must prove before Kolm can credibly claim `.kolm` is an open standard, an ecosystem primitive, or a community-governed format rather than a company-controlled product feature.

#### Why Node 27 Exists

Kolm has a strong internal product thesis: capture useful model behavior, distill it into owned artifacts, verify those artifacts, and run them across providers, clouds, and devices. If that artifact format is intended to become the durable layer of the AI stack, then open-source governance is not a side project. It is part of the product. A developer, auditor, runtime maintainer, marketplace creator, enterprise buyer, and foundation reviewer all need the same answers:

- Who controls the `.kolm` format?
- How does a breaking change get proposed, debated, accepted, rejected, versioned, and tested?
- Can third parties implement the format without private Kolm code?
- Can outside maintainers gain authority through public contribution?
- Can users trust registry artifacts, marketplace ratings, payouts, takedowns, and provenance?
- Can the project show adoption, maintainers, releases, security policy, license clarity, and public discussion channels?
- Can Kolm avoid marketing a company-owned spec as an industry standard before neutral governance and external adoption actually exist?

This node is the bridge between "Kolm ships a spec page" and "the `.kolm` ecosystem is trustworthy enough for the market to build on."

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Evidence path | What exists now | Completion redline |
|---|---|---|
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Contribution paths exist for registry recipes, issues, code, code of conduct reference, and Apache-2.0 contributor licensing. | Needs root-level `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `GOVERNANCE.md`, `MAINTAINERS.md`, and explicit decision/maintainer promotion process. A link inside `CONTRIBUTING.md` is not enough. |
| `LICENSE` | Apache-2.0 license exists at the repository root. | Need project-wide file-level license metadata policy for generated assets, SDKs, examples, research docs, third-party snippets, and marketplace samples. |
| `docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md` | Strong local `.kolm` change process: RFC issue prefix, minimum 14-day review, substantive comment response target, two maintainer signoff, test vector requirement, reference SDK parity, and semver classification. | Must be wired to public issue templates, maintainer roster, external implementer list, conformance CI, release notes, and real accepted/rejected RFC history. |
| `docs/format-governance-packet.md` | Explicitly states local packet is for neutral venue submission and must not claim outside acceptance. | Needs actual external submission artifact, neutral venue status, trademark policy hash, accepted scope, and public change-control URL before "industry standard" copy is allowed. |
| `docs/runtime-adoption-packets.md` | Defines target adoption packets for Hugging Face Hub, Ollama, llama.cpp, ONNX/GGUF tooling, and hardware partners, with external completion rule. | Needs public merged PRs, published plugins, signed partner artifacts, conformance report hashes, supported artifact subsets, and adoption evidence. |
| `docs/spec/test-vectors/MANIFEST.md` | A test-vector manifest path exists. | Need a conformance suite with fixtures that run in JavaScript, C, Python, Rust, and at least one independent external implementation. |
| `.github/dependabot.yml` | Dependabot exists for npm, GitHub Actions, and pip under `/apps`. | Dependabot is not open-source governance. Needs OpenSSF Scorecard, branch protection evidence, pinned workflow action policy, read-only default token permissions, disclosure policy, and maintainer security runbook. |
| `.github/workflows/*.yml` | CI, smoke, test-suite, SBOM, SDK C/Rust, benchmark submission, compile, distill, and template workflows exist. | Need contributor-facing status docs, minimum required checks, permissions audit, release signing, package publishing evidence, and standard "how to reproduce CI locally" docs. |
| `public/community.html` | Public community surface exists. | Current copy is launch/creator oriented, includes placeholder `href="#"` Discord links, and should not imply an active Discord/leaderboard/creator economy unless those channels, rules, moderation, and payout systems are real. |
| `public/spec.html` | Public spec page exists and describes RS-1 / `.kolm` as MIT licensed. | Needs clean brand/repo links, exact source-of-truth mapping to `docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md`, conformance badge, current version, deprecation policy, maintainer list, and no mojibake in title/social/schema metadata. |
| `public/registry.html`, `public/marketplace.html`, `public/docs/marketplace/publish.html` | Registry and marketplace surfaces exist. | Need review SLA, takedown process, malware/license checks, payout policy, creator dispute workflow, rating anti-manipulation rules, and artifact provenance gate. |
| `public/brand/github-org-decision.html` | Brand/GitHub org decision surface exists. | Must resolve public `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` vs Kolm naming before standardization, package publication, or foundation submission. Brand drift erodes trust. |

Root governance file probe on 2026-05-25:

| File | Root present | `.github` present | Required state |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` | no | no | Add root copy or generated public mirror with enforcement contacts. |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | yes | no | Keep, but expand with issue templates, PR checklist, local build/test matrix, maintainer review SLAs, and spec RFC process. |
| `GOVERNANCE.md` | no | no | Add project governance, maintainer powers, promotion/removal, voting, conflict resolution, and decision records. |
| `SECURITY.md` | no | no | Add vulnerability disclosure, private contact, triage SLA, embargo process, advisory publication, supported versions. |
| `MAINTAINERS.md` | no | no | Add maintainers, areas, term/review cadence, emeritus process, required reviewers, and bus-factor evidence. |
| `ADOPTERS.md` | no | no | Add only when real public adopters consent; never fake logos or "design partner" claims. |
| `LICENSE` | yes | no | Keep Apache-2.0 root license and add file-level license compliance policy. |
| `NOTICE` | no | no | Add if Apache-2.0 notices, third-party notices, trademarks, or generated/public assets require it. |

#### External Governance Standards Bound To This Node

| External reference | URL | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OpenSSF Best Practices WG and Badge | `https://best.openssf.org/`, `https://www.bestpractices.dev/en/criteria/0` | Public repo, license, docs, discussion mechanisms, issue response, vulnerability reporting, working build, tests, CI, HTTPS, and secure development criteria must become launch gates. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/`, `https://github.com/ossf/scorecard/blob/main/docs/checks.md` | Add Scorecard/SARIF evidence for branch protection, code review, token permissions, pinned dependencies, signed releases, SAST, fuzzing, packaging, and security policy. |
| CNCF lifecycle and metrics | `https://contribute.cncf.io/projects/lifecycle/`, `https://www.cncf.io/project-metrics/` | Treat maturity as adoption plus governance plus committers plus health, not self-assigned status. Foundation readiness needs due-diligence evidence, adopters, public comment, and neutral review. |
| The Apache Way | `https://www.apache.org/theapacheway/` | Authority must be earned in public by contribution, decision-making should be transparent, and community roles must not depend only on employment or title. |
| Contributor Covenant 2.1 | `https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/` | Code of conduct must define acceptable behavior, unacceptable behavior, scope, enforcement responsibilities, and contact path in a root file. |
| REUSE Specification 3.3 | `https://reuse.software/spec-3.3/` | Every covered file needs machine-readable licensing information through headers, adjacent `.license` files, or `REUSE.toml`; license files should map to SPDX identifiers. |
| Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 | `https://semver.org/` | `.kolm` format, SDKs, CLI, APIs, and conformance suites need declared public APIs and major/minor/patch compatibility semantics. |

#### Product And Copy Redlines

These are hard product-truth rules for public copy, docs, account UI, CLI/TUI output, API envelopes, and sales collateral:

1. Do not claim `.kolm` is an industry standard until at least one neutral venue, foundation process, or independent ecosystem body accepts the governance path and the evidence is public.
2. Do not claim "open ecosystem" if contributors cannot find root-level governance, security, maintainer, issue, PR, release, and decision-process documents.
3. Do not claim "any runtime" unless conformance fixtures prove compatibility across first-party SDKs and at least one independent runtime or implementation.
4. Do not claim "community" if the public community page points to placeholder links, inaccessible channels, unmoderated spaces, or fake activity.
5. Do not claim registry or marketplace trust until review, takedown, malware/license checks, rating integrity, payout, appeals, and dispute workflows are implemented and audited.
6. Do not claim package or install maturity until package channels are published or visibly marked local-source-only.
7. Do not claim external adoption from local packets. `docs/format-governance-packet.md` and `docs/runtime-adoption-packets.md` are preparation evidence, not acceptance evidence.
8. Do not present `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` as the primary public repo once the product is branded Kolm unless there is a deliberate brand/legal reason documented in `public/brand/github-org-decision.html`.
9. Do not ship public pages with mojibake, stale repo names, hash-only Discord links, or inconsistent `.kolm`/RS-1/source-of-truth references.
10. Do not let a company-only maintainer process govern a supposedly neutral spec without a clear transition path to independent maintainers and public decision records.

#### Canonical Ecosystem Evidence Packet

Every release that claims open-source, spec, standardization, or ecosystem maturity should be able to generate a secret-free packet with this shape:

```json
{
  "governance_version": "2026-05-25",
  "project_identity": {
    "brand": "Kolm",
    "public_repo": "https://github.com/...",
    "legal_owner": "entity-ref",
    "trademark_policy": "url-or-path",
    "source_of_truth": "url-or-path",
    "kolmogorov_to_kolm_status": "resolved|documented-exception|blocked"
  },
  "maintainers": {
    "roster_path": "MAINTAINERS.md",
    "areas": ["format", "runtime", "registry", "docs", "security"],
    "promotion_policy": "GOVERNANCE.md#maintainer-promotion",
    "removal_policy": "GOVERNANCE.md#maintainer-removal",
    "last_reviewed_at": "timestamp",
    "employment_independence_policy": "url-or-path"
  },
  "contribution_process": {
    "contributing_path": "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    "code_of_conduct_path": "CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md",
    "security_policy_path": "SECURITY.md",
    "issue_templates": ["bug", "feature", "spec-rfc", "security-safe"],
    "pr_checklist": "path",
    "required_checks": ["test", "lint", "conformance", "scorecard"],
    "review_sla": "duration",
    "decision_records": "docs/decisions/"
  },
  "spec_process": {
    "format_spec": "docs/spec/kolm-format-v1.0.md",
    "change_process": "docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md",
    "current_version": "1.0.0",
    "semver_policy": "major-minor-patch",
    "rfc_index": "docs/spec/rfcs/index.md",
    "accepted_rfcs": [],
    "rejected_rfcs": [],
    "external_implementer_review": "required"
  },
  "conformance": {
    "test_vectors": "tests/fixtures/format-v1/",
    "manifest": "tests/fixtures/format-v1/MANIFEST.sha256.txt",
    "reference_implementations": ["javascript", "c", "python", "rust"],
    "independent_implementations": [],
    "ci_command": "npm run verify:conformance",
    "latest_report": "reports/conformance/latest.json"
  },
  "registry_governance": {
    "submission_policy": "public/registry/submit.html",
    "review_policy": "docs/registry-review-policy.md",
    "takedown_policy": "docs/registry-takedown-policy.md",
    "license_scan_policy": "docs/registry-license-policy.md",
    "malware_scan_policy": "docs/registry-security-policy.md",
    "rating_integrity_policy": "docs/marketplace-rating-integrity.md",
    "payout_policy": "docs/marketplace-payout-policy.md",
    "appeals_policy": "docs/marketplace-appeals.md"
  },
  "community": {
    "discussion_channels": [],
    "public_archive": "url",
    "roadmap": "public/roadmap.html",
    "changelog": "public/changelog.html",
    "release_notes": "url-or-path",
    "support_boundary": "url-or-path",
    "moderation_policy": "url-or-path"
  },
  "ecosystem_metrics": {
    "contributors_total": 0,
    "contributors_external": 0,
    "maintainer_orgs": 0,
    "adopters_public": 0,
    "runtime_integrations_published": 0,
    "conformance_runs_external": 0,
    "package_channels_published": 0,
    "scorecard_score": null,
    "best_practices_badge": "none|in_progress|passing"
  },
  "foundation_readiness": {
    "neutral_venue": "none|candidate|submitted|accepted",
    "submission_url": null,
    "public_comment_url": null,
    "due_diligence_url": null,
    "adopter_interviews": [],
    "status": "local_packet_only|external_review|accepted"
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `27.1` Contributor governance, maintainer model, code of conduct, review process, and decision records | Root governance files exist, are linked from public docs, and define contributor flow, code of conduct enforcement, maintainer authority, promotion/removal, issue/PR templates, review SLAs, security reporting, decision records, and release responsibility. |
| `27.2` `.kolm` format change process, compatibility, test vectors, reference implementations, and conformance | The format spec has a declared public API, semver policy, RFC index, accepted/rejected decisions, conformance fixtures, SHA manifests, cross-SDK tests, and independent implementation evidence before any standardization claim. |
| `27.3` Community channels, roadmap, changelog, issues, discussions, releases, and contribution docs | Every public community CTA points to a real channel with moderation, searchable archive, support boundary, onboarding path, roadmap/changelog, release notes, and no placeholder Discord or fake activity. |
| `27.4` Registry and marketplace governance, artifact review, ratings, payouts, takedowns, and trust | Registry/marketplace submissions have review rules, license/security scans, provenance requirements, takedown/appeal path, rating integrity controls, payout evidence, abuse handling, and public policy pages. |
| `27.5` Partner integrations, runtime adoption, GitHub Actions, package ecosystem, and compatibility matrix | Runtime adoption packets graduate from local templates to public integrations with merged PRs, published packages, compatibility matrix, conformance reports, package install evidence, and support lifecycle. |
| `27.6` Foundation readiness, neutral governance, ecosystem metrics, public evidence, and adoption loops | Foundation/neutral governance claims require submitted or accepted external process, public due-diligence evidence, adopter list with consent, maintainer diversity metrics, OpenSSF/CNCF-style maturity evidence, and recurring ecosystem health reviews. |

#### Node 27 Relax Redline

Node `27` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Can a serious external contributor understand how to file an issue, propose an RFC, submit a PR, become a maintainer, report a vulnerability, and appeal a decision?
- Can a runtime author implement `.kolm` from public docs and pass a conformance suite without private Kolm code?
- Can an enterprise buyer see license, governance, security, release, vulnerability, maintainer, and adoption evidence in standard files and public pages?
- Can a marketplace creator understand review, payout, ranking, takedown, license, provenance, and dispute rules before publishing?
- Can public copy distinguish local governance readiness from neutral external acceptance?
- Can Kolm prove public discussion, decision history, maintainer independence path, and external adoption instead of asserting them?
- Can every GitHub Action, package, spec, SDK, registry submission, community CTA, and public repo link survive scrutiny from OpenSSF, CNCF-style due diligence, and an enterprise procurement team?

The relax condition for node `27` is not "the spec page exists." It is: the project has foundation-grade governance, root community/security files, conformance evidence, trustworthy registry policy, public adoption metrics, real community channels, brand-clean repository identity, and honest copy that never overstates external standardization.

#### Node 27 V2 Open Governance, Standardization, And Ecosystem Stewardship Addendum

This addendum maintains master spec tree node `27` and does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change root governance files, public pages, registry/marketplace code, package metadata, workflows, tests, or generated docs while other agents are editing.

The completion bar is not "there is a spec page." Kolm is complete only when external contributors, runtime implementers, enterprise buyers, marketplace creators, auditors, and potential foundation reviewers can verify governance, security reporting, maintainer authority, `.kolm` compatibility, conformance, adoption, registry trust, trademark boundaries, and community channels from public artifacts. Local preparation packets are useful, but they are not external acceptance.

##### Node 27 V2 Current Evidence Register

| evidence class | observed state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| root files | `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `LICENSE` exist; `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `GOVERNANCE.md`, `MAINTAINERS.md`, `ADOPTERS.md`, and `NOTICE` are absent in the probe. | Open-source maturity is not complete. Contribution docs cannot replace conduct, security, governance, maintainer, adopter, and notice decisions. |
| spec process | `docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md` exists and is detailed; `docs/spec/test-vectors/MANIFEST.md` exists. | Local change process exists. Completion requires public RFC index, issue templates, conformance CI, cross-language vectors, and independent implementation evidence. |
| governance packets | `docs/format-governance-packet.md` and `docs/runtime-adoption-packets.md` exist. | These are preparation evidence. They must not be marketed as neutral acceptance or external adoption until public submissions/merges exist. |
| public community/spec/marketplace | `public/community.html`, `public/spec.html`, `public/registry.html`, `public/marketplace.html`, `public/docs/marketplace/publish.html`, `public/brand/github-org-decision.html`. | Public surfaces exist but need real channel links, source-of-truth links, marketplace policy, adoption evidence, and brand/repo resolution. |
| automation/workflows | `.github/dependabot.yml` and workflows exist. | Open-source governance needs Scorecard, security policy, branch/review posture, action permissions, release signing, and contributor-facing CI reproduction docs. |
| absent registries | `docs/open-governance-contract.json`, `docs/kolm-format-rfc-index.json`, `docs/conformance-suite-registry.json`, `docs/runtime-adoption-registry.json`, `docs/community-channel-policy.json`, `docs/maintainer-roster.json`, `docs/trademark-policy.md`, `docs/marketplace-governance-policy.json` are absent. | Ecosystem governance is not yet machine-readable or exportable for release evidence. |
| absent reports | `reports/governance`, `reports/conformance`, and `reports/adoption` are absent. | Standardization/adoption evidence is not durable release proof. |

##### Node 27 V2 External Baselines

| baseline | URL | exact rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OpenSSF Best Practices | `https://best.openssf.org/` and `https://www.bestpractices.dev/en/criteria/0` | Project website/repo must explain software purpose, contribution process, license, docs, HTTPS, discussion, maintenance, source repository, release notes, issue reporting, vulnerability reporting, build, and tests. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/` | Track branch protection, dangerous workflow patterns, token permissions, pinned dependencies, vulnerabilities, code review, fuzzing, SAST, packaging, and security policy. |
| REUSE 3.3 | `https://reuse.software/spec-3.3/` | Use machine-readable SPDX licensing metadata for source, generated assets, examples, docs, and packaged artifacts. |
| Contributor Covenant 2.1 | `https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/` | Public community needs acceptable/unacceptable behavior, scope, enforcement responsibilities, and contact path. |
| Apache Way | `https://www.apache.org/theapacheway/` | Open governance should reward public contribution, transparent decisions, community over code, and merit-based authority. |
| Semantic Versioning | `https://semver.org/` | `.kolm` format, SDKs, CLI/API, and conformance suites need explicit public API and compatibility semantics. |
| CNCF lifecycle and metrics | `https://contribute.cncf.io/projects/lifecycle/` | Maturity should be demonstrated through adoption, contributors, maintainers, releases, governance, security, and community health, not self-labeling. |

##### Node 27 V2 Open Governance Contract

Every standardization or ecosystem claim must map to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-open-governance-v2",
  "project_identity": {
    "brand": "Kolm",
    "repo": "canonical_public_repo",
    "legal_owner": "legal_entity_ref",
    "trademark_policy": "docs/trademark-policy.md",
    "kolmogorov_legacy_status": "documented_exception_or_resolved"
  },
  "governance_files": {
    "contributing": "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    "code_of_conduct": "CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md",
    "security": "SECURITY.md",
    "governance": "GOVERNANCE.md",
    "maintainers": "MAINTAINERS.md",
    "license": "LICENSE",
    "notice": "NOTICE_or_not_required"
  },
  "maintainers": {
    "roster": "docs/maintainer-roster.json",
    "areas": ["format", "runtime", "registry", "docs", "security"],
    "promotion_policy": "GOVERNANCE.md",
    "removal_policy": "GOVERNANCE.md",
    "review_cadence": "quarterly"
  },
  "spec": {
    "current_version": "1.0.0",
    "change_process": "docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md",
    "rfc_index": "docs/kolm-format-rfc-index.json",
    "semver_policy": "major_minor_patch",
    "deprecation_policy": "documented",
    "compatibility_policy": "documented"
  },
  "conformance": {
    "registry": "docs/conformance-suite-registry.json",
    "test_vectors": "docs/spec/test-vectors",
    "reference_implementations": ["javascript", "c", "python", "rust"],
    "independent_implementations": [],
    "latest_report": null
  },
  "ecosystem": {
    "runtime_adoption_registry": "docs/runtime-adoption-registry.json",
    "community_channel_policy": "docs/community-channel-policy.json",
    "marketplace_governance": "docs/marketplace-governance-policy.json",
    "scorecard_report": null,
    "best_practices_badge": "none"
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "industry_standard": "blocked_until_neutral_acceptance",
    "open_standard": "blocked_until_governance_and_conformance",
    "community": "blocked_until_real_channels",
    "runtime_adoption": "blocked_until_public_integrations"
  }
}
```

##### Node 27 V2 Standardization Claim States

| state | allowed copy | blocked copy |
|---|---|---|
| `local_spec` | "Kolm publishes a local `.kolm` format spec." | "industry standard", "open standard", "neutral standard". |
| `governance_ready` | "Kolm has a proposed public change process and conformance plan." | "foundation governed", "community governed" without accepted process. |
| `external_review` | "Kolm has submitted the spec/governance process for external review." | "accepted standard" unless accepted. |
| `neutral_accepted` | "The format is governed through [venue]." | claims beyond the accepted scope. |
| `first_party_conformance` | "First-party implementations pass conformance." | "any runtime" or "independent ecosystem" without external implementation. |
| `independent_conformance` | "At least one independent implementation passes conformance." | broad "all runtimes" claims. |
| `published_integration` | "Integration is published for [runtime]." | "native support everywhere." |
| `real_community` | "Public discussion/channel exists at [URL]." | fake Discord/community/leaderboard/creator claims. |

##### Node 27 V2 Marketplace Governance Contract

| area | required policy |
|---|---|
| submission | author identity, artifact hash, license, provenance, safety policy, data rights, support owner, update path. |
| review | reviewer, SLA, checklist, malware/license/safety scan, conformance, reason codes, appeals. |
| trust | signature, SBOM/provenance, dependency graph, runtime compatibility, review status, rating integrity. |
| takedown | security, copyright, terms, abuse, customer complaint, maintainer dispute, emergency removal path. |
| payouts | eligibility, tax/compliance, fraud checks, refund/chargeback, creator dispute, hold periods. |
| ratings | anti-manipulation, verified use, abuse reports, moderation, transparency. |
| support | support owner, support boundaries, compatibility window, deprecation, abandonment, fork/transfer policy. |
