##### Node 27 V2 Relax Redline

Node `27` cannot be relaxed until all of the following are true:

1. root governance, conduct, security, maintainer, license, notice, adopter, and contributor files are present or explicitly scoped;
2. `.kolm` change process is connected to RFC index, issue templates, public decisions, semver policy, deprecation policy, and conformance suite;
3. conformance vectors run across first-party implementations and at least one independent implementation before open-standard/runtime-adoption claims expand;
4. community CTAs point to real moderated searchable channels or are removed;
5. registry and marketplace governance covers review, provenance, malware/license/safety scans, takedown, appeals, ratings, payouts, support, and deprecation;
6. runtime adoption claims are backed by public merged PRs, published plugins/packages, signed partner artifacts, or explicit local-source-only status;
7. foundation/neutral governance claims are blocked until actual external submission or acceptance evidence exists;
8. brand/repo/trademark identity is clean enough for public standards work and package publication;
9. ecosystem health metrics, OpenSSF Scorecard, Best Practices status, conformance reports, adoption reports, and governance reports are durable release evidence;
10. public copy never markets local prep packets as external acceptance.

If any item fails, Kolm can publish a useful spec, but it cannot honestly claim foundation-grade open governance or standardization.

#### Node 27 V3 Open Ecosystem Stewardship, Standardization Claim States, And Adoption Evidence Contract

This V3 addendum maintains master spec tree node `27`. It does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not change root governance files, public pages, registry/marketplace code, package metadata, tests, workflows, generated docs, or screenshots while other agents are working. The purpose is to close the gap between "Kolm has a local spec and preparation packets" and "Kolm has a credible open ecosystem." Open standard, community, runtime adoption, marketplace trust, package maturity, and foundation readiness are product claims. They must be versioned, evidence-backed, externally scoped, and blocked when only local preparation exists.

##### Node 27 V3 Current Evidence Delta

This pass scanned the current worktree and ran the local governance packet verifier. The evidence below is current signal, not completion proof.

| evidence class | observed state | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| root governance files | `CONTRIBUTING.md` 2,469 bytes, `LICENSE` 11,276 bytes, and `README.md` 13,419 bytes exist; `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `GOVERNANCE.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `MAINTAINERS.md`, `ADOPTERS.md`, and `NOTICE` are absent. | Root-level open governance is not complete. A contributor should not have to infer conduct, vulnerability disclosure, maintainer authority, adopters, or notice obligations from scattered public pages. |
| local format and runtime packet code | `docs/spec/CHANGE_PROCESS.md`, `docs/format-governance-packet.md`, `docs/runtime-adoption-packets.md`, `docs/spec/test-vectors/MANIFEST.md`, `src/format-governance-packet.js`, `src/runtime-adoption-packets.js`, `scripts/format-governance-packet.mjs`, `scripts/runtime-adoption-packets.mjs`, and `tests/wave590-governance-runtime-packets.test.js` exist. | This is real local contract work. V3 must preserve the distinction: local contract is ready, but external acceptance and external runtime adoption are not verified. |
| verifier output | `npm.cmd run verify:governance-packets` returned local `ok=true` for format governance and runtime adoption, with `external_acceptance_verified=false`, `external_adoption_verified=false`, missing `reports/format-governance-submission.json`, missing neutral venue acceptance, missing public change-control record, and five external runtime target blockers. | Node `27` should treat this as honest partial readiness. Product copy can say local packets exist, but cannot say neutral standard, foundation-governed, externally adopted, or runtime ecosystem complete. |
| public community page | `public/community.html` describes "the forge, the leaderboard, the discord", includes `href="#"` Discord links, creator rankings, challenge rewards, and creator payout claims such as 70% or 80%. | Community/creator/payout claims need live channels, moderation, rules, payout policy, eligibility, anti-abuse controls, consent, and evidence. Placeholder links or speculative payout percentages are redlines. |
| public spec page | `public/spec.html` references MIT licensing for RS-1 while the repository `LICENSE` and page footer use Apache-2.0, links to `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`, and also contains a `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` source link. | License and repo identity are not clean enough for standardization. The spec needs one source-of-truth license, source repo, format version, conformance badge, public API boundary, and compatibility policy. |
| registry and marketplace public surfaces | `public/registry.html`, `public/marketplace.html`, and `public/docs/marketplace/publish.html` exist; registry copy includes takedown text, marketplace copy includes artifact rows, license filters, publish path, and third-party submission language. | Public surfaces imply marketplace trust. V3 requires review SLA, takedown, malware/license/safety scans, rating integrity, payouts, disputes, creator identity, artifact provenance, and public policy evidence. |
| brand and repo identity | `public/brand/github-org-decision.html` says the product brand is `kolm.ai`, but the repo remains `github.com/sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` until a future decision; it warns `kolm-ai/*` and `kolmogorov-ai/*` URLs are not yet real. | Brand/repo drift is a standardization blocker. The product can document the exception, but cannot finish node `27` until package, spec, docs, GitHub, and public trust links have a deliberate canonical identity. |
| GitHub automation | `.github/dependabot.yml` and workflows for CI, compile, distill, templates, KolmBench, lint, SBOM, SDK C/Rust, smoke, and test suite exist. Issue templates, PR templates, security policy, Scorecard, and open governance evidence were not found in this pass. | Automation exists, but open-source governance needs contributor-facing process, required checks, branch/review policy, security disclosure, Scorecard evidence, and reproducible CI docs. |
| absent external evidence | `reports/format-governance-submission.json`, `docs/format-governance-external-submission.json`, `reports/runtime-adoption-manifest.json`, `docs/runtime-adoption-manifest.json`, `reports/governance`, `reports/conformance`, and `reports/adoption` are absent. | The release cannot claim external standardization, neutral governance, or runtime adoption until these evidence artifacts or their successors exist and pass validation. |

##### Node 27 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| baseline | current source | V3 rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OpenSSF Best Practices Badge | `https://www.bestpractices.dev/criteria` and `https://openssf.org/projects/best-practices-badge/` | Public project maturity needs visible purpose, contribution process, license, source repo, issue reporting, vulnerability reporting, release notes, build, tests, and secure development practices. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/` and `https://github.com/ossf/scorecard` | Security posture must be measured, not asserted: branch protection, code review, dangerous workflows, token permissions, pinned dependencies, security policy, vulnerabilities, SAST, fuzzing, packaging, and signed releases. |
| CNCF lifecycle and metrics | `https://contribute.cncf.io/projects/lifecycle/` and `https://www.cncf.io/project-metrics/` | Foundation readiness depends on governance, release process, project metrics, adoption, maintainers, communication channels, security posture, and maturity evidence reviewed by an external body. |
| Apache Way | `https://www.apache.org/theapacheway/` | Public technical decisions, open communication, community over code, merit-based authority, and asynchronous transparent collaboration are governance requirements, not marketing language. |
| REUSE and SPDX | `https://reuse.software/spec-3.3/` and `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/` | Source, docs, examples, generated assets, test vectors, packages, and public pages need machine-readable licensing and SPDX identifiers, especially where MIT/Apache messaging diverges. |
| Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 | `https://semver.org/` | `.kolm` format, SDKs, CLI, APIs, and conformance suites must declare their public API and map major/minor/patch changes to compatibility, deprecation, and verifier behavior. |
| Contributor Covenant | `https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/` | Conduct policy needs scope, accepted behavior, unacceptable behavior, enforcement responsibility, contact path, and consequences. It cannot be replaced by vague community copy. |
| GitHub community health and security policy | `https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions` and `https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/getting-started/adding-a-security-policy-to-your-repository` | Public GitHub projects should expose contributing, support, security, issue templates, PR templates, code of conduct, license, and maintainer expectations from conventional locations. |

##### Node 27 V3 Open Ecosystem Packet Contract

Every open-source, format, standardization, community, registry, marketplace, package, GitHub, runtime-adoption, foundation, and ecosystem claim must be able to emit or link to this packet:

##### Node 27 V3 Claim State Matrix

| state | minimum evidence | allowed copy | blocked copy |
|---|---|---|---|
| `local_spec` | Public spec page and repo source exist. | "Kolm publishes a local `.kolm` format spec." | "open standard", "industry standard", "foundation-governed". |
| `local_contract_ready` | Local governance packet, runtime adoption packet, verifier command, and tests pass with external gates marked false. | "Kolm has local governance and adoption packets ready for external review." | "accepted by a neutral venue", "used by external runtimes". |
| `external_submitted` | Submission manifest links public venue, date, scope, and packet hashes. | "Kolm submitted the `.kolm` process for external review." | "approved", "accepted", "standardized". |
| `external_accepted` | Neutral venue acceptance, public change-control record, maintainer policy, trademark policy, and conformance suite hash exist. | "The `.kolm` process is accepted by [venue] for [scope]." | Claims beyond accepted scope or without current compatibility evidence. |
| `runtime_packet_ready` | Target packets exist for external runtime integrations. | "Kolm prepares integration packets for Hugging Face Hub, Ollama, llama.cpp, ONNX/GGUF, and hardware partners." | "integrated with" or "native support" without merged PR/package/plugin. |
| `external_runtime_adopted` | External PR, package, plugin, or partner artifact exists with conformance report. | "This runtime supports the documented `.kolm` subset." | "runs anywhere" without target, version, subset, and test report. |
| `community_prelaunch` | Public community page exists but channel, moderation, creator, and payout proof are missing or partial. | "Community channels are being prepared." | Fake Discord activity, fake rankings, unsupported payout percentages, fake partners. |
| `marketplace_governed` | Submission, review, takedown, payout, dispute, rating, license, safety, and malware policies exist and are enforced. | "Marketplace artifacts follow the published review and trust policy." | "trusted marketplace" without enforcement reports and appeal/takedown evidence. |

##### Node 27 V3 Ecosystem Evidence Matrices

| ecosystem surface | required artifact | completion redline |
|---|---|---|
| root repository | `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `GOVERNANCE.md`, `MAINTAINERS.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `LICENSE`, `NOTICE` or explicit not-required record, issue templates, PR template, support policy. | Root repo cannot be foundation-ready while conduct/security/governance/maintainer files are absent. |
| `.kolm` format | Versioned spec, declared public API, compatibility policy, RFC index, accepted/rejected decisions, deprecation policy, conformance vectors, verifier behavior. | A spec page alone is not an open standard. |
| conformance | Registry of suites, manifests, fixture hashes, first-party implementations, independent implementation report, CI command, public badge. | Runtime adoption claims cannot exceed conformance evidence. |
| external governance | Submission packet, venue, status, public review record, accepted scope, neutral change-control URL, trademark policy, maintainer policy. | Local packets are preparation evidence, not external acceptance. |
| package and install ecosystem | Published package/channel evidence, version badges, install commands, package manager metadata, signed releases, support window. | Package maturity cannot be claimed from local manifests only. |
| community channels | Real links, archived decisions, moderation rules, code of conduct enforcement, response expectations, roadmap/changelog, contributor onboarding. | Placeholder `href="#"` Discord/community links are release-blocking for community claims. |
| marketplace creators | Creator identity, license and rights, safety/legal scans, payout policy, tax/payment eligibility, dispute process, anti-manipulation, takedown. | Payout percentages or creator rankings are blocked without policy and evidence. |
| brand and trademark | Canonical repo/org decision, trademark policy, package namespace, redirect policy, historical exceptions, docs rewrite plan. | `kolm.ai`, `kolmogorov-stack`, `kolm-stack`, and `sneaky-hippo` drift cannot remain ambiguous in a standardization packet. |
| security and sustainability | OpenSSF badge, Scorecard report, branch protection, workflow permissions, pinned dependencies, security policy, vulnerability process, maintainer bus factor. | Security posture cannot rely on Dependabot and CI presence alone. |
| adoption metrics | Public adopters with consent, external contributors, runtime integrations, conformance runs, downloads, issue response, release cadence, maintainer orgs. | No fake logos, no inferred adopters, no local-only adoption claims. |

##### Node 27 V3 Redline To Relax

Node `27` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- Root governance files and GitHub health files exist or are explicitly scoped: conduct, security, governance, maintainers, contributing, license, notice, support, issue templates, and PR template.
- `.kolm` format, SDK, CLI, API, verifier, and conformance suites have a declared public API, versioning policy, RFC index, deprecation policy, and compatibility rules.
- Local governance and runtime packets stay green while external acceptance/adoption blockers remain visible until real external evidence exists.
- Neutral/foundation/standardization claims require public venue, status, accepted scope, change-control URL, maintainer policy, trademark policy, and conformance hashes.
- Runtime adoption claims require external PR/package/plugin/partner evidence and conformance report per target and supported artifact subset.
- Public community links are real; Discord, creator, challenge, leaderboard, payout, ranking, and "community" claims have policy and evidence or are scoped as prelaunch.
- Marketplace and registry trust claims have submission, review, takedown, malware/license/safety scans, payouts, disputes, appeals, rating integrity, creator identity, and enforcement reports.
- License and source-of-truth references are consistent across root license, spec page, docs, package metadata, marketplace, registry, footer copy, and generated metadata.
- Brand/repo identity is deliberately resolved or explicitly documented with migration and redirect policy for `kolm.ai`, `kolmogorov-stack`, `kolm-stack`, and `sneaky-hippo`.
- OpenSSF Best Practices, Scorecard, branch protection, token permissions, signed releases, pinned dependencies, security policy, and contributor CI evidence are generated or explicitly blocked.
- Release evidence includes `reports/governance`, `reports/conformance`, `reports/adoption`, and marketplace/community governance reports, and node `43` treats Node `27` as a hard build-redline gate.

If any item fails, Kolm can have useful local packets and public pages, but it is not yet a foundation-grade open ecosystem or standardization-ready project.

### Repository Architecture, Build Graph, Code Ownership, And Monorepo Hygiene Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It answers the user's recurring backend question directly: the main API/web control plane is Node/JavaScript, but the repository is a multi-runtime product stack. A 100 percent codebase cannot be reviewed as "the JS app" or "the website." It needs a source-of-truth architecture map, build graph, ownership model, generated-file policy, and per-subtree build lanes.

#### Why Node 28 Exists

The repository has the shape of a product platform, not a single service:

- Node/JavaScript API control plane: `server.js`, `src/router.js`, `src/*.js`, `cli/kolm.js`, `scripts/*.cjs`, and generated API/product graph scripts.
- Python/ML execution surfaces: `apps/trainer`, `apps/modal`, `apps/replicate`, Python SDKs, benchmark tooling, and worker requirements.
- Rust/C/runtime surfaces: `sdk/rust`, `sdk/c`, `packages/runtime-rs`, conformance/runtime packages.
- TypeScript/extension/mobile surfaces: `packages/sdk-ts`, `packages/sdk-rn`, `packages/vscode-kolm-rag`, `packages/browser-extension`, Kotlin and Swift SDK packages.
- Public/generated website surface: 729 public HTML pages, 20 public CSS files, many generated API/docs/account/spec pages, and media assets.
- CI/release/product-truth surface: `.github/workflows`, `scripts/build-codegraph.mjs`, `scripts/release-verify.cjs`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, product graph, closeout ledgers, and route inventories.

The completion risk is that every lane can be locally "green" while the repo remains structurally hard to finish: giant central files, missing ownership, unclear generated-file boundaries, ad hoc temp artifacts, package manifests outside a root workspace model, and test/build commands that do not prove all subtrees. Node 28 makes the codebase itself a product surface.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Evidence | Current state observed | Completion redline |
|---|---|---|
| Root package | `package.json` is private, `type: module`, Node `>=20`, `bin.kolm`, and many verification scripts. | Root must declare the authoritative monorepo/build strategy: workspaces or explicit non-workspace rationale, per-package build lanes, generated outputs, and release inclusion rules. |
| Workspaces | No root `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `turbo.json`, `nx.json`, `lerna.json`, `rush.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `jsconfig.json`, or root npm `workspaces` field was observed in the scanned root package excerpt. | Every package/app/worker/SDK must be discoverable by a machine-readable build graph, either via npm workspaces or a Kolm-owned manifest with the same rigor. |
| Ownership | No `CODEOWNERS`, `OWNERS`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, or architecture decision root file was observed in the ownership probe. | Add ownership and architecture files or an equivalent generated ownership ledger. Every path must map to owner, surface node, test gate, generated/manual status, and review requirement. |
| Code graph | `node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --json` reports `ok: true`, 2,212 files, 7,806 imports, 9,221 symbols, 788 routes, 57 scripts, and 57 readiness requirements. | Code graph is a good base, but completion requires path ownership, dependency direction checks, circular import policy, generated-file provenance, and route-to-module decomposition targets. |
| Largest control-plane file | `src/router.js` is 953,495 bytes. | Route modules must be decomposed by product surface or at least indexed by owner, route group, auth boundary, envelope shape, smoke probe, and generated docs source. |
| Other large source modules | Large modules include `src/binder.js`, `src/artifact.js`, `src/intent.js`, `src/model-registry.js`, `src/spec-compile.js`, `src/distill-pipeline.js`, `src/compile-pipeline.js`, `src/marketplace.js`, and `src/daemon-connector.js`. | Each large file needs an explicit split/keep decision, public/internal contract, dependency direction, tests, and reviewer owner. |
| Largest scripts | Large scripts include `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs`, `scripts/build-docs-w374.cjs`, `scripts/write-missing-cli-docs.cjs`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/release-verify.cjs`, and `scripts/build-api-ref.cjs`. | Generators and verifiers need idempotency, serial execution rules, stale-output checks, source/destination manifest, and dirty-tree safety. |
| Public surface | `public` contains 729 `.html`, 188 `.svg`, 59 `.md`, 48 `.png`, 43 `.json`, 20 `.css`, 16 `.js`, plus video/media artifacts. | Generated vs hand-edited public files must be tagged. Human edits to generated files should fail verification unless source is updated. |
| Packages/apps/workers | Manifests exist across Python, Rust, C, Node, Swift, Kotlin/RN, Docker, and worker packages. `workers/media-redact/node_modules` appears in the manifest scan, indicating vendored dependency output inside a worker subtree. | Every subtree must have a build/smoke command, lockfile policy, dependency policy, publish/local-only state, and ignored/generated/dependency output policy. |
| Temporary artifacts | A directory scan found 270 test temp directories matching `_tmp*` or `tmp*` under test areas. | Tests must use isolated temp roots and cleanup contracts. Persistent temp output must be either intentionally archived evidence or removed from source control and ignored. |
| Lockfiles | Root `package-lock.json` exists; observed Rust and Python lock/requirements files are scattered by subtree. | Lockfile ownership and refresh cadence must be explicit per package manager, with release gates proving root and subpackage dependency integrity. |

#### External Architecture And Build References Bound To This Node

| External reference | URL | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | Use CODEOWNERS or an equivalent generated ownership ledger; code owner review should be required for sensitive paths, and the CODEOWNERS file itself needs protected ownership. |
| npm workspaces | `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/workspaces/` | If npm remains the root package manager, package build/test commands should be targetable by workspace or by a documented equivalent manifest. |
| Node.js test runner | `https://nodejs.org/api/test.html` | JS tests should stay aligned with stable `node:test`, command-line test discovery, reporters, isolation, concurrency, and deterministic reproduction patterns. |
| Python `pyproject.toml` | `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/` | Python apps/packages need build-system metadata, project metadata, dependency declarations, entry points where relevant, and package-local smoke gates. |
| Cargo workspaces | `https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html` | Rust crates should use workspace membership or documented non-workspace rationale, shared package metadata, dependency/lint inheritance where appropriate, and package selection gates. |
| C4 architecture model | `https://c4model.com/` | Architecture docs should cover system context, containers, components, code, deployment, and dynamic behavior at a level developers and auditors can navigate. |

#### Architecture Redlines

1. Do not describe the backend as a single language. The correct architecture is: Node API/control plane plus Python/ML workers/apps plus Rust/C/runtime/SDK surfaces plus generated public/documentation surfaces.
2. Do not allow any file to be ownerless. Every source, script, generated asset, fixture, SDK, worker, package, and public page needs a primary tree node and owner.
3. Do not keep `src/router.js` as the only understandable route map. Either split it or generate a durable route-to-module-to-owner index that lets implementers finish one group without breaking another.
4. Do not let generated outputs be edited as source. Every generated public/API/docs/account/product graph artifact needs source path, command, destination, freshness check, and diff policy.
5. Do not let temporary test output live as unclassified repo state. Test temp directories must be isolated, ignored, cleaned, or intentionally archived as reports with metadata.
6. Do not let package directories exist without a build/test/smoke/publish status. "Source exists" is not package readiness.
7. Do not let frontend, backend, research, and CLI bots collide on shared generated files. Generated artifact ownership and sequencing must be explicit.
8. Do not let verification scripts run in parallel if one reads a file another writes. Serial generation dependencies must be encoded in release verification.
9. Do not let public codegraph success imply architecture completion. A graph with files/imports/routes is necessary; owners, boundaries, dependencies, and refactor gates are the missing completion layer.
10. Do not let archive, backup, temp, media, benchmark, report, or generated directories inflate the codebase without retention rules.

#### Canonical Repository Evidence Packet

Every release candidate should be able to emit a secret-free repository packet:

```json
{
  "repo_architecture_version": "2026-05-25",
  "source_root": "C:/Users/user/Desktop/kolmogorov-stack",
  "architecture_model": {
    "system_context": "docs/architecture/system-context.md",
    "containers": ["node-api-control-plane", "python-training-workers", "runtime-sdk-packages", "public-site", "cli-tui", "background-workers"],
    "components": "docs/architecture/components.json",
    "deployment": "docs/architecture/deployment.md",
    "decision_records": "docs/decisions/"
  },
  "ownership": {
    "codeowners_path": ".github/CODEOWNERS",
    "generated_owner_ledger": "docs/repo-ownership.json",
    "unowned_paths": [],
    "sensitive_paths": ["src/router.js", "src/auth.js", "src/billing.js", "scripts/release-verify.cjs", ".github/workflows/"],
    "review_required": true
  },
  "build_graph": {
    "graph_command": "node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --json",
    "workspace_manifest": "package.json#workspaces|docs/repo-workspaces.json",
    "files": 2212,
    "routes": 788,
    "symbols": 9221,
    "imports": 7806,
    "dependency_direction_policy": "docs/architecture/dependency-direction.md",
    "cycles": []
  },
  "subtrees": [
    {
      "path": "src/",
      "kind": "node-api-control-plane",
      "owner": "backend",
      "build": "node --check selected-files",
      "test": "npm run test",
      "smoke": "npm run local:surfaces",
      "generated": false,
      "release_gate": "npm run release:verify"
    },
    {
      "path": "public/",
      "kind": "public-generated-and-hand-authored-site",
      "owner": "frontend-docs",
      "build": "npm run build:product-graph && node scripts/build-api-ref.cjs && node scripts/build-openapi.cjs",
      "test": "npm run lint:refs && npm run ui:audit:all:themes",
      "generated": "mixed",
      "release_gate": "screenshot-and-link-audit"
    },
    {
      "path": "apps/trainer/",
      "kind": "python-ml-worker",
      "owner": "ml-platform",
      "build": "python packaging check",
      "test": "worker smoke",
      "generated": false,
      "release_gate": "training readiness smoke"
    }
  ],
  "generated_files": [
    {
      "path": "public/docs/api-routes.json",
      "source": "src/router.js",
      "command": "node scripts/build-api-ref.cjs",
      "stale_check": "release:verify"
    }
  ],
  "large_file_redlines": [
    {
      "path": "src/router.js",
      "bytes": 953495,
      "status": "split_or_index_required",
      "owner": "backend"
    }
  ],
  "temporary_artifacts": {
    "tmp_dirs_found": 270,
    "policy": "ignore-clean-archive",
    "allowed_report_roots": ["reports/", "screenshots/"],
    "cleanup_gate": "npm run verify:repo-hygiene"
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `28.1` System architecture map, runtime boundaries, language roles, and ownership model | The repo has a current architecture map showing Node control plane, Python/ML workers, Rust/C/runtime packages, SDKs, public generated assets, services, workers, CLI/TUI, and deployment boundaries, with primary owners for every subtree. |
| `28.2` Build graph, workspace manifests, package boundaries, generated assets, and dependency direction | A machine-readable build graph maps every package/app/worker/script/public generated artifact to its build command, test command, owner, dependency direction, generated/manual state, and release gate. |
| `28.3` Route/module decomposition, large-file redlines, shared contracts, and refactor sequence | Large modules and scripts have keep/split decisions; `src/router.js` route groups map to modules, owners, tests, docs, auth boundaries, envelopes, and OpenAPI/API generation. |
| `28.4` Test topology, temporary artifacts, fixtures, deterministic local runs, and coverage evidence | Tests are grouped by product surface and package, temp artifacts are cleaned or archived intentionally, fixtures are owned, flaky/order-dependent tests are tracked, and local commands reproduce CI. |
| `28.5` Package/app/worker/SDK build lanes, smoke commands, release readiness, and environment parity | Every `apps/`, `packages/`, `sdk/`, `workers/`, `services/`, and `api/` subtree has build/test/smoke/release status, lockfile policy, env requirements, and package publication or local-only scope. |
| `28.6` Codebase hygiene, cleanup policy, generated-file provenance, CODEOWNERS, and architecture decisions | CODEOWNERS or equivalent exists, architecture decisions are recorded, generated files have source/command metadata, temp/backup/archive retention is enforced, and repo hygiene fails on unowned or stale structural drift. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-ARCH-001` | `28.1` | Create the canonical architecture map. | `docs/architecture/` contains system context, containers, components, deployment, and dynamic flow diagrams or equivalent markdown for every major runtime. |
| `W-ARCH-002` | `28.1` | Define language/runtime roles. | Node, Python, Rust, C, TypeScript, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, worker, browser, and generated public surfaces each have a declared role, owner, and release expectation. |
| `W-ARCH-003` | `28.1` | Add source ownership. | Every top-level directory and critical file maps to owner, backup owner, product-tree node, and required review level. |
| `W-ARCH-004` | `28.1` | Add CODEOWNERS or generated ownership equivalent. | Ownership is enforced by GitHub CODEOWNERS, branch protection, or a verifier that fails unowned path changes. |
| `W-ARCH-005` | `28.2` | Build a repo workspace manifest. | Every package/app/worker/SDK/service appears in `package.json#workspaces` or `docs/repo-workspaces.json` with build/test/smoke commands and dependency policy. |
| `W-ARCH-006` | `28.2` | Extend code graph with ownership and generated-file data. | `build-codegraph` or a companion script emits owners, generated/manual status, source command, dependency direction, cycles, and release gate per path. |
| `W-ARCH-007` | `28.2` | Add dependency direction rules. | Public pages cannot import server internals, scripts cannot depend on generated stale outputs without declaring order, SDKs cannot depend on app-private code, and cycles are reported. |
| `W-ARCH-008` | `28.2` | Make generated artifact provenance mandatory. | API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout ledgers, account pages, marketplace pages, SDK version files, screenshots, videos, and registry packs name source and command. |
| `W-ARCH-009` | `28.3` | Route-map `src/router.js`. | Every route group maps to owner, module, auth mode, envelope shape, product surface, smoke probe, API docs, OpenAPI entry, and test. |
| `W-ARCH-010` | `28.3` | Split or index giant source modules. | Files above a set threshold, starting with `src/router.js`, `src/binder.js`, and `src/artifact.js`, get split plans or explicit keep rationale with subcomponent index. |
| `W-ARCH-011` | `28.3` | Normalize shared contract modules. | Envelope, readiness, product graph, billing, provider catalog, artifact schema, and route receipt contracts live in small owned modules with tests and docs. |
| `W-ARCH-012` | `28.3` | Add refactor sequencing guardrails. | Generated docs/OpenAPI/product graph regenerate serially after route changes, and release verification prevents API-ref/OpenAPI races. |
| `W-ARCH-013` | `28.4` | Build the test topology map. | Every test file maps to product node, package/subtree, behavior proved, fixtures used, and release gate coverage. |
| `W-ARCH-014` | `28.4` | Clean temp artifact policy. | Test temp directories are ignored/cleaned by default; intentionally archived evidence lives under `reports/` with metadata and retention. |
| `W-ARCH-015` | `28.4` | Add deterministic test reproduction. | Failed tests print exact command, env class, fixture seed, randomized-order seed where used, temp path, and cleanup state. |
| `W-ARCH-016` | `28.5` | Complete package/app/worker build lanes. | Every manifest under `apps/`, `packages/`, `sdk/`, `workers/`, `services/`, and `api/` has build, test, smoke, publish/local-only status, and env prerequisites. |
| `W-ARCH-017` | `28.5` | Normalize lockfile policy. | Root npm, Python subprojects, Rust crates, worker packages, mobile SDKs, and extension packages have lockfile rules and update cadence. |
| `W-ARCH-018` | `28.5` | Add environment parity matrix. | Local, CI, preview, production, air-gapped, and customer self-hosted environments list required services, secrets, optional providers, and smoke commands. |
| `W-ARCH-019` | `28.6` | Add repo hygiene verifier. | Verification fails on unowned files, stale generated outputs, forbidden temp/backup files, missing source command, package without build lane, and unmanaged dependency output. |
| `W-ARCH-020` | `28.6` | Add architecture decision records. | Major decisions about Node control plane, Python worker boundary, Rust runtime boundary, package manager/workspace strategy, generated assets, and route decomposition are recorded in ADRs. |

#### Node 28 Relax Redline

Node `28` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current evidence:

- Which runtime owns each part of the product, and why is that runtime the right boundary?
- Who owns every file and route, and which reviewer must approve changes to sensitive paths?
- Which files are generated, from what sources, by which commands, and how does CI prove they are current?
- Which top-level directories are source, generated output, archived evidence, dependency output, temp output, or deployment state?
- Can every app, package, worker, SDK, and service be built, tested, smoked, and released or intentionally marked local-source-only?
- Can a developer reproduce CI locally without guessing command order or colliding with generated files?
- Can the repo explain why the control plane is Node while ML workers, runtimes, SDKs, and packages use other languages?
- Can the product team split or safely modify large files without breaking route docs, OpenAPI, account UI, product graph, or release verification?
- Can temp artifacts, backups, screenshots, reports, and generated files be retained or cleaned by policy instead of accumulating accidentally?

The relax condition for node `28` is not "the tests pass." It is: the repository has an inspectable architecture, enforceable ownership, machine-readable build graph, generated-file provenance, package build lanes, clean temp policy, and route/module boundaries that let implementation agents finish the product without stepping on each other.

#### Node 28 V2 Master Spec Tree, Build Graph, And Codebase Completion Addendum

This addendum maintains the master spec tree itself. The tree is now treated as a product artifact: every file, package, route, generated output, build product, report, screenshot, benchmark, worker, SDK, and doc must be attached to exactly one owning node before any implementation agent can claim the codebase is complete. This is the answer to the recurring "why is it all JS?" question: Kolm is not all JS. The current API and orchestration control plane is Node/JavaScript, but the product is a multi-runtime stack with Python ML workers, Rust/C runtimes and SDKs, Swift/Kotlin/RN/mobile packages, generated public docs and website assets, Vercel/static deployment files, Express API routes, local/production smoke scripts, and report archives. Node `28` is the branch that keeps those lanes from being reviewed as one pile of files.

##### Node 28 V2 Current Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current value observed in this pass | Redline added to the master spec tree |
|---|---:|---|
| Root package identity | `package.json` name `kolm-stack`, private, `type: module`, Node `>=20.0.0`, 57 scripts, 9 dependencies, 4 devDependencies, no root `workspaces` field. | Either add a real workspace model or generate `docs/repo-workspaces.json` with equivalent package discovery, ordering, build/test/smoke, and release semantics. |
| Root workspace files | Missing `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `turbo.json`, `nx.json`, `lerna.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `jsconfig.json`, root `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `docs/architecture`, `docs/adr`, `docs/build-graph.json`, `docs/generated-assets.json`, `docs/test-topology.json`, and `docs/repository-boundaries.json`. | No "100 percent" claim until these are either implemented or replaced by a Kolm-owned manifest and verifier. |
| Source roots | `api` 2 files, `apps` 170, `packages` 139, `sdk` 4956, `workers` 1030, `services` 3, `src` 392, `scripts` 343, `public` 1132, `docs` 158, `tests` 567, `test` 8. | Every root needs a runtime classification, owner, source/output/dependency/report classification, and build lane. |
| Package manifests | 39 `package.json` files excluding `.git`, root dependency caches, and `node_modules`. | Root package scripts alone cannot prove the package estate. Each manifest needs a lane record and an owner. |
| Python manifests | 10 `pyproject.toml` files and 8 requirements files were observed. | Python packages and ML apps need build-system metadata, dependency lock policy, and per-package smoke or explicit local-only scope. |
| Rust manifests | 4 `Cargo.toml` files were observed, including active SDK/runtime crates and temp launch copies. | Rust crates need workspace membership or explicit non-workspace rationale, lockfile policy, target exclusion, and cargo check/test gates. |
| Other package lanes | Swift `Package.swift`, RN Android `build.gradle`, RN `.podspec`, Dockerfiles, worker compose files, and extension package manifests exist. | Mobile, worker, Docker, and extension lanes need explicit command parity with CI and release. |
| Ignored dependency/build outputs | `.gitignore` ignores `node_modules/`, `.env*`, `dist/` except selected SDK dists, `sdk/rust/target/`, `packages/runtime-rs/target/`, reports, shots, temp dirs, generated QA images, traineddata, and release outputs. | Ignore policy exists, but completion requires a verifier that fails if dependency/build outputs appear as tracked or unmanaged source. |
| Heavy local output | Observed local output includes `reports` 95,988 files / 11.3 GB, `tmp` 13,168 files / 2.75 GB, `sdk/rust/target` 4,900 files / 1.59 GB, `data` 3,576 files / 753 MB, `tmp-screenshots` 3,197 files / 637 MB, `workers/media-redact/node_modules` 987 files / 66 MB. | These are allowed only as local evidence/dependency/cache output. The spec tree must separate source, generated release output, archived evidence, local scratch, dependency cache, and forbidden output. |
| Largest source-shaped files | Current largest scan is dominated by Rust target artifacts, proving why output classification matters. Prior source redline still includes `src/router.js` as the largest control-plane source module. | Large-file reporting must exclude ignored build output when measuring source debt, and must separately report unmanaged output debt. |
| Git hygiene | Blueprint remains untracked; unrelated `src/intent.js` is modified by another agent/user. | This lane must not touch `src/intent.js`; master doc updates must stay isolated and diff-clean. |

##### Node 28 V2 External Standards Bound To This Addendum

| Standard or reference | URL | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | CODEOWNERS can live in `.github/`, root, or `docs/`; owners can be auto-requested and required by protected branches; file size, syntax, case sensitivity, and invalid-line behavior must be verified before relying on it. |
| npm workspaces | `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/workspaces/` | Workspaces are explicit root package links to local packages, support targeted and all-workspace scripts, and run in root-declared order. Kolm either needs npm workspaces or a generated manifest with the same order and targeting guarantees. |
| Node.js test runner | `https://nodejs.org/download/release/v22.17.0/docs/api/test.html` | `node --test` discovers specific file patterns, supports explicit globs, process isolation, concurrency, name patterns, coverage, and warnings for async work after a test ends. Kolm test topology must encode these semantics. |
| PyPA `pyproject.toml` spec | `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/` | Python subprojects need clear build-system/project metadata, import-name intent where relevant, dynamic metadata rules, and tool namespace ownership. |
| Cargo workspaces | `https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html` | Rust workspace members share lockfile/output and can inherit package metadata, dependencies, lints, and profiles. Kolm Rust packages need workspace or explicit non-workspace policy. |
| C4 model | `https://c4model.com/` | Architecture docs should use system context, container, component, code, deployment, dynamic, and landscape views where useful, while staying notation/tool independent. |
| SLSA provenance | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2-rc1/build-provenance` | Build evidence needs builder identity, build type, external/internal parameters, resolved dependencies, run metadata, byproducts, and verification semantics. Generated Kolm artifacts and release outputs should carry this shape. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` | Open-source/security posture must be measurable by automated checks, not just described in docs. Repo hygiene should eventually emit a Scorecard-style security and reliability posture packet. |

##### Node 28 V2 Master Spec Tree Contract

The master tree is not a checklist of ideas. It is the routing table for codebase completion. Every future implementation wave must update this contract or it is not part of the finished product.

```json
{
  "master_spec_tree_version": "2026-05-25.node28.v2",
  "source_of_truth": "docs/research/kolm-ai-100-percent-codebase-completion-blueprint-2026-05-25.md",
  "repo_root": "C:/Users/user/Desktop/kolmogorov-stack",
  "tree_rules": {
    "no_duplicate_nodes": true,
    "attach_new_work_to_existing_node_when_possible": true,
    "node_52_reserved_for_future_unowned_branch": true,
    "every_file_has_one_primary_node": true,
    "every_generated_output_has_source_and_command": true,
    "every_package_has_build_lane": true,
    "every_route_has_surface_owner": true,
    "every_claim_has_proof_or_closeout": true,
    "every_temp_or_report_output_has_retention_policy": true
  },
  "path_record_shape": {
    "path": "relative/path",
    "kind": "source|generated|dependency-output|local-scratch|archived-evidence|fixture|secret-template|release-artifact",
    "runtime": "node|browser|python|rust|c|swift|kotlin|react-native|docker|static|docs|mixed",
    "primary_node": 28,
    "product_surface": "public-docs-sdk|runtime-compute|deployment-edge-federated|...",
    "owner": "backend|frontend|ml-platform|sdk-runtime|docs|release|security",
    "generated_from": [],
    "generate_command": null,
    "build_command": null,
    "test_command": null,
    "smoke_command": null,
    "release_gate": null,
    "allowed_to_edit_directly": true,
    "sensitive": false,
    "retention": "source-controlled|ignored-local|report-archive|release-archive",
    "last_verified_by": []
  },
  "completion_invariant": "A release is structurally incomplete if any tracked or generated path lacks this record, any output directory is misclassified as source, or any build lane has no proof command."
}
```

##### Node 28 V2 Runtime Ownership Model

| Runtime lane | Primary paths | Role in Kolm | Completion requirement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Node API and orchestration control plane | `server.js`, `src/`, `api/`, `cli/kolm.js`, `scripts/*.cjs`, `scripts/*.mjs` | Auth, billing, routing, product graph, capture, compile/distill orchestration, registry, account APIs, release gates, code generation. | Must be modular enough that route groups, product surfaces, auth boundaries, API docs, OpenAPI, smoke probes, and account UI cannot drift. |
| Browser/static product surface | `public/`, `public/assets`, `public/account`, `public/docs`, `public/enterprise`, `public/video`, `vercel.json` | Website, docs, account UI, generated product/API docs, pricing, demos, SEO, screenshots. | Every generated page and asset must know its source command; every hand-authored page must have owner and screenshot/link coverage. |
| Python ML and benchmark lane | `apps/`, `bench/`, Python SDK/package dirs, selected `workers/*/requirements.txt` | Training, Modal/Replicate adapters, benchmarks, redaction and eval tooling, Python SDKs. | Each package needs `pyproject.toml` or explicit requirements-only rationale, build/test/smoke, lock policy, and env manifest. |
| Rust/C/runtime lane | `sdk/rust`, `sdk/c`, `packages/runtime-rs`, runtime import/export paths | Fast local runtimes, binary SDKs, conformance, artifact verification and execution. | Cargo/C build output must be ignored and never treated as source; crates need workspace policy, `cargo check/test`, and release artifact provenance. |
| SDK and integration lane | `sdk/node`, `sdk/python`, `sdk/mcp`, `sdk/vscode`, `packages/sdk-*`, `packages/langchain-*`, `packages/llamaindex-*`, browser extension | Developer adoption, package releases, editor/agent framework integrations. | Each SDK needs install path, package status, smoke example, API compatibility contract, and version alignment with product graph. |
| Worker and deployment lane | `workers/`, `Dockerfile`, Helm, compose, `.github/workflows`, `railway.toml`, `.vercelignore` | Compile/distill/runtime workers, self-host/BYOC, CI, deployment. | Each worker needs build, local smoke, env contract, image/release policy, health checks, and rollback evidence. |
| Evidence/report lane | `reports/`, `screenshots/`, `.shots`, `.ui-debug`, audit dirs, benchmark reports | Proof artifacts for UI, smoke, benchmark, release, incidents, audits. | Must be ignored or archived intentionally with metadata; report sprawl must not inflate source counts or deploy bundles. |

##### Node 28 V2 Relax Redline

Node `28` cannot be relaxed because "tests pass" or because a backend/frontend/research bot reports its lane green. It relaxes only when the current codebase can answer, mechanically and from checked-in or generated evidence, all of the following:

- What is every file, who owns it, what runtime owns it, and which master-tree node owns its completion?
- Is the path source, generated output, dependency output, local scratch, archived evidence, release artifact, fixture, or secret template?
- Which command builds it, tests it, smokes it, regenerates it, deploys it, or proves it should not ship?
- Which generated files will be overwritten by bots or generators, and what is the safe serial order?
- Which packages are publishable, local-only, externally blocked, or missing toolchain proof?
- Which route groups, account pages, docs pages, SDKs, workers, and runtime modules are coupled, and what test catches drift?
- Which heavy outputs are allowed locally, which must never be tracked, and which reports are release evidence?
- Can a clean release candidate produce provenance, local/deep smoke, UI screenshots, route/API docs, package lanes, and prod smoke without ad hoc founder memory?

Until then, the right status is not "done"; it is "codebase structurally converging, with Node 28 as the build redline owner."

#### Node 28 V3 Polyglot Backend, Runtime-Language Ownership, And Build-Lane Redline

Node `52` remains unused. The recurring backend-language question belongs here: Kolm is not "all JS." The current API and orchestration control plane is Node/JavaScript, but the product backend is a polyglot platform with Python ML workers, Rust/C runtime and SDK lanes, TypeScript/browser/extension packages, Swift/Kotlin/mobile packages, Docker/worker deploy lanes, generated public/account/docs surfaces, and evidence/report artifacts.

Node `2` owns API route behavior. Node `3` owns ML execution. Node `4` owns worker/deploy runtime isolation. Node `5` owns SDK/package release. Node `28` owns the architecture map that proves those lanes are part of one build rather than a pile of unrelated files.

##### Current Polyglot Evidence V3

The latest scan excluded major dependency, build, report, temp, data, archive, backup, and screenshot roots, then grouped the remaining worktree by extension and top-level root.

| Signal | Current observed value | Completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `.js` files | 1015 files / 16,524,940 bytes | Node/browser/CLI/scripts are the largest active language lane and remain the API/control-plane center. |
| `.html` files | 734 files / 14,850,095 bytes | Public/account/docs/generated pages are product surfaces, not incidental frontend output. |
| `.py` files | 151 files / 1,254,766 bytes | Python ML/app/benchmark lanes are real backend execution surfaces and need build/test/smoke contracts. |
| `.rs` files | 17 files / 128,512 bytes | Rust runtime/SDK lanes exist and need Cargo workspace or explicit non-workspace policy. |
| `.ts` files | 13 files / 59,795 bytes | TypeScript package/extension lanes need package build ownership separate from raw JS scripts. |
| `.swift` files | 4 files / 13,954 bytes | Swift SDK/package support exists and needs SwiftPM proof or local-source-only scope. |
| `.kt` files | 3 files / 11,432 bytes | Kotlin/mobile support exists and needs Android/Gradle build proof. |
| `.json`, `.md`, `.css`, `.svg`, `.png`, `.kolm` | Hundreds of files | Generated product truth, docs, UI, media, and artifacts need source/provenance records. |

| Root | Files | Bytes | Completion implication |

| `public` | 1132 | 64,617,317 | Public and generated product surface; needs generated/hand-authored split, metadata, screenshots, and brand/content proof. |
| `tests` | 567 | 7,319,376 | Behavior proof estate; needs topology map to product nodes and package lanes. |
| `src` | 391 | 6,528,450 | Node API/control plane; route ownership, module boundaries, and generated docs are the redline. |

| `apps` | 168 | 1,565,938 | Python/ML app lane; cannot be proven by Node tests alone. |

| `sdk` | 49 | 439,312 | SDK implementations and package metadata; needs install and smoke proof. |

Manifest probe excluding dependency/build/report/temp/archive roots:

| Manifest pattern | Count | Completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `package.json` | 23 | npm/package lanes exist outside the root and need workspace or equivalent manifest coverage. |
| `pyproject.toml` | 5 | Python projects need package metadata, build ownership, and smoke gates. |
| `requirements*.txt` | 4 | Requirements-only Python lanes need explicit local-only or packaging rationale. |
| `Cargo.toml` | 2 | Rust crates need workspace or non-workspace policy, lockfile/output rules, and cargo checks. |
| `Package.swift` | 1 | Swift package support needs SwiftPM proof or local-source-only scope. |
| `*.podspec` | 1 | CocoaPods/React Native packaging needs package-channel status. |
| `build.gradle` | 1 | Android/Kotlin packaging needs Gradle build/check proof. |
| `Dockerfile` | 4 | Container lanes need production image proof and multi-stage source/output boundaries. |
| `docker-compose*.yml` | 1 | Local/self-host orchestration exists and needs env/readiness parity. |

##### External Build-Lane Baseline V3

| Standard source | Current rule to absorb | Kolm architecture requirement |
|---|---|---|
| npm workspaces, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/workspaces/` | npm workspaces explicitly define nested packages in root `package.json`, auto-link them during install, support `--workspace` and `--workspaces`, and run workspace scripts in root-declared order. | Either add real npm workspaces or generate an equivalent `docs/repo-workspaces.json` with package order, package names, build/test/smoke commands, and missing-script policy. |
| PyPA `pyproject.toml`, `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/` | The `[project]` table specifies core metadata; `name` is required, `version` must be static or dynamic, and tools should normalize names; `tool.*` namespace ownership must avoid collisions. | Every Python lane must state project name, version policy, dependencies, build backend, tool namespace, and whether it is publishable, local-only, or worker-only. |
| Cargo workspaces, `https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html` | Cargo workspaces let common commands run across members, share one lockfile, share one output directory, and share package metadata. | Rust lanes need workspace membership or explicit non-workspace rationale, shared lock/output policy, and `cargo check/test` proof. |
| Docker multi-stage builds, `https://docs.docker.com/build/building/multi-stage/` | Docker multi-stage builds use multiple `FROM` stages and copy only needed artifacts into the final image, leaving build tools and intermediate artifacts behind. | Worker/container builds should prove final images contain only runtime artifacts, not source caches, secrets, test data, or build toolchains unless explicitly needed. |

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-polyglot-runtime-ownership-v3",
  "generated_at": "2026-05-25",
  "control_plane": {
    "runtime": "nodejs",
    "paths": ["server.js", "src/", "api/", "cli/kolm.js", "scripts/"],
    "owns": ["api_routes", "product_graph", "auth_billing_policy", "capture_gateway", "artifact_registry_api", "generated_docs", "release_gates"],
    "must_not_claim": ["python_training_quality", "rust_runtime_package_readiness", "mobile_sdk_release"]
  },
  "execution_lanes": [
    {
      "id": "python-ml-workers",
      "runtime": "python",
      "paths": ["apps/", "bench/", "selected sdk/python paths"],
      "owns": ["training", "distillation_jobs", "evals", "benchmarks", "worker_adapters"],
      "required_proof": ["pyproject_or_requirements_policy", "unit_or_smoke", "env_contract", "artifact_io_contract"]
    },
    {
      "id": "rust-c-runtime",
      "runtime": "rust|c",
      "paths": ["sdk/rust", "sdk/c", "packages/runtime-rs"],
      "owns": ["native_runtime", "verification_bindings", "binary_sdk", "conformance"],
      "required_proof": ["cargo_or_c_build", "fixture_verify", "package_status", "artifact_provenance"]
    },
    {
      "id": "sdk-integration-packages",
      "runtime": "node|typescript|python|swift|kotlin|react-native|vscode|mcp",
      "paths": ["packages/", "sdk/"],
      "owns": ["developer_adoption", "install_examples", "package_release", "editor_and_agent_integrations"],
      "required_proof": ["package_matrix", "install_smoke", "api_compatibility", "brand_package_metadata"]
    },
    {
      "id": "public-generated-product",
      "runtime": "browser|static|generated",
      "paths": ["public/"],
      "owns": ["website", "docs", "account_ui", "api_reference", "search_ai_discovery", "screenshots"],
      "required_proof": ["generated_source_manifest", "link_check", "screenshot_audit", "metadata_claim_scope"]
    }
  ],
  "release_invariant": "A green Node release gate is insufficient unless each non-Node lane is built, smoked, explicitly local-only, or external-gated with proof."
}
```

##### Required Build-Lane Matrix V3

Every row in the future `docs/repo-workspaces.json` must carry these fields:

| Field | Required reason |
|---|---|
| `path` | Unique subtree root. |
| `runtime` | Node, browser, Python, Rust, C, Swift, Kotlin, RN, Docker, static/generated, docs, or mixed. |
| `owner_node` | Primary master spec node, with secondary nodes for cross-cutting surfaces. |
| `owner_lane` | Backend, frontend, ML, runtime, SDK, docs, release, security, or research. |
| `source_class` | Source, generated, fixture, dependency output, local scratch, archive evidence, release artifact, or external package. |
| `build_command` | Exact command or explicit `none_required` rationale. |
| `test_command` | Exact command or explicit test gap. |
| `smoke_command` | Minimal proof that a user-visible or runtime behavior works. |
| `package_status` | `published`, `dry_run`, `local_source_only`, `external_gated`, `deprecated_alias`, or `not_a_package`. |
| `env_contract` | Required variables, optional providers, secrets policy, and offline behavior. |
| `generated_outputs` | Files generated by this lane and the serial order needed to avoid races. |
| `release_gate` | Command/report that must pass before this lane can be considered release-ready. |

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance proof |

| `W-ARCH-V3-001` | `28.1` | Generate `docs/polyglot-runtime-ownership.json`. | Node, Python, Rust/C, TypeScript, Swift, Kotlin/RN, Docker, public static, generated docs, and evidence lanes have owner, role, and proof contract. |
| `W-ARCH-V3-002` | `28.2` | Generate `docs/repo-workspaces.json` from manifest discovery. | All `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, requirements, `Cargo.toml`, `Package.swift`, podspec, Gradle, Docker, and compose lanes are listed or explicitly excluded. |
| `W-ARCH-V3-003` | `28.2` | Add root package workspace decision. | Root either uses npm workspaces or has an equivalent verifier that provides package targeting/order semantics. |

##### Node `28` V3 Relax Redline

Node `28` is not relaxed until the repository can answer, from generated files and commands:

- which runtime owns each source root and package lane;
- whether each non-JS backend/execution lane is buildable, smoked, local-only, or external-gated;
- whether the root package manager strategy is npm workspaces or a verified Kolm workspace manifest;
- whether Python, Rust/C, Swift/Kotlin/RN, Docker, SDK, public-generated, and evidence/report lanes have release states;
- whether source counts are separated from dependency/build/report/temp artifact counts;
- whether a green Node test run is prevented from being marketed as whole-codebase completion;
- whether package/install docs and public claims reflect the real package status for every language lane.

Until that proof exists, the correct statement is: Kolm has a Node/JavaScript API control plane inside a polyglot product platform. It is not "all JS," and no final codebase-completion claim may treat it that way.

#### Node 28 V4 Repository Architecture Control Plane, Generated Provenance, And Clean-Build Evidence Contract

This V4 addendum maintains master spec tree node `28`. It does not create a new branch. It is a documentation/spec edit only. It does not edit package manifests, generators, public assets, source modules, tests, workflows, or generated artifacts while other agents are working. The purpose is to make repository completion mechanically provable: every file and generated output must have an owner, runtime lane, source/output class, build/test/smoke command, generator source, retention policy, and release consequence.

##### Node 28 V4 Current Evidence Delta

This pass used the current worktree as authority and ran the checked codegraph verifier. The evidence below is current signal, not completion proof.

| evidence class | observed state | V4 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| architecture ownership files | `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/decisions`, `docs/adr`, `docs/codeowners.md`, `docs/generated-files.md`, and `docs/build-graph.json` are absent. | Architecture ownership is not yet a checked source of truth. The repo needs CODEOWNERS or equivalent verifier, ADRs, C4-style architecture docs, generated-file provenance, and a build graph manifest. |
| package-manager root | Root `package.json` exists with `name: "kolm-stack"`, `type: "module"`, 57 scripts, 9 dependencies, 4 dev dependencies, `package-lock.json`, and no `workspaces` field. | The root is an npm-managed Node control plane, but nested package manifests are not first-class workspaces. Either add npm workspaces or generate an equivalent repo workspace manifest with order and script targeting. |
| manifest discovery | Current non-output scan found 23 `package.json`, 5 `pyproject.toml`, 4 `requirements.txt`, 2 `Cargo.toml`, 1 `Package.swift`, 1 `build.gradle`, 4 `Dockerfile`, and 1 `docker-compose.yml`. | Kolm is a multi-lane repo. A green root Node test cannot prove Python, Rust, Swift, Kotlin, Docker, worker, SDK, or extension completion. |
| codegraph verifier | `node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --check --json` returned `ok=true` with 2,212 files, 7,806 imports, 9,221 symbols, 788 routes, 57 scripts, 57 readiness requirements, and 0 missing readiness evidence. | Codegraph is real and useful, but no durable `reports/codegraph.json` was found. V4 requires the codegraph to become a release artifact with path ownership and source/output classification. |
| generated public artifacts | `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/openapi.json`, `public/product-graph.json`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`, `docs/product-readiness-closeout.md`, and `public/docs/cli` exist. | Generated artifacts exist, but completion requires each generated path to declare generator command, inputs, serial order, direct-edit policy, stale check, and downstream consumers. |
| source-shaped large files | Largest current source-shaped files include `cli/kolm.js` 2,021,134 bytes, `src/router.js` 953,495 bytes, `public/openapi.json` 579,772, `public/docs/api.html` 551,204, `public/index.html` 214,568, `public/frontend-version.json` 204,659, `public/brand-refresh.css` 169,200, `src/binder.js` 137,810, `src/artifact.js` 121,813, and `scripts/build-account-pages.cjs` 98,858. | Large files are not automatically bad, but they require owner, split/keep rationale, generator/source classification, and blast-radius tests so agents can change one surface without reading the whole platform. |
| source/output taxonomy | Even after excluding major dependency/build/report/temp/archive roots, the scan still saw 4,590 files / 717,416,888 bytes, with `.tmp`, `_audit`, `qa`, `screenshots`, and `.w850-shots` among top roots. | The output taxonomy is still incomplete. Local scratch, audit output, QA images, screenshots, source assets, release evidence, and tracked public artifacts need separate budgets and cleanup/retention rules. |
| concurrent worktree state | `public/nav.js`, `src/intent.js`, and `vercel.json` are modified by parallel work; the master blueprint remains untracked. | Node `28` must own clean-release rules: no final claim while parallel edits, untracked docs, generated outputs, or dirty deploy config remain unresolved or explicitly bundled. |

##### Node 28 V4 External Baseline Refresh

| baseline | current source | V4 rule imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | CODEOWNERS can live at `.github/`, root, or `docs/`; code owner reviews can be required by branch protection. Kolm needs valid ownership syntax and a verifier before relying on it. |
| npm workspaces | `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/using-npm/workspaces/` | Nested packages should be explicitly defined in root `package.json` or an equivalent manifest, with targeted/all-workspace script semantics and root-declared execution order. |
| Node.js test runner | `https://nodejs.org/api/test.html` | `node --test` has explicit discovery, concurrency, coverage, and reporter semantics. Kolm test topology must encode which tests are root-wide, package-local, generated, skipped, or release-blocking. |
| PyPA `pyproject.toml` | `https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/pyproject-toml/` | Python lanes need build-system/project metadata or explicit requirements-only rationale, import-name policy, dynamic version policy, and tool namespace ownership. |
| Cargo workspaces | `https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/workspaces.html` | Rust workspaces share `Cargo.lock`, output directory, package metadata, dependencies, lints, and profiles. Kolm Rust crates need workspace or explicit non-workspace policy. |
| SLSA build provenance | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2-rc1/build-provenance` | Build provenance must capture builder identity, build type, external parameters, resolved dependencies, run details, materials, byproducts, and verification semantics. |
| C4 model | `https://c4model.com/` | Architecture docs should provide context, container, component, code, deployment, dynamic, and landscape views when useful, while staying tool-independent. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` | Repo security and maintainability posture should be measurable. Architecture evidence should eventually feed Scorecard-style branch, workflow, dependency, review, and security-policy checks. |

##### Node 28 V4 Repository Architecture Packet Contract

Every tracked path, generated output, package lane, test file, script, workflow, report, local scratch root, route group, public page, and release artifact must be able to emit or link to this packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.repo_architecture_control_plane.v4",
  "node": 28,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "path": {
    "relative_path": "src/router.js",
    "kind": "source|generated|fixture|dependency_output|local_scratch|archived_evidence|release_artifact|secret_template",
    "runtime": "node|browser|python|rust|c|swift|kotlin|react_native|docker|static|docs|mixed",
    "primary_node": 28,
    "secondary_nodes": [],
    "owner_lane": "backend|frontend|ml|runtime|sdk|docs|release|security|research",
    "codeowners_rule": ".github/CODEOWNERS:line"
  },
  "build_lane": {
    "manifest": "package.json|pyproject.toml|Cargo.toml|Dockerfile|none",
    "workspace": "npm|custom_manifest|cargo|swiftpm|gradle|none",
    "build_command": null,
    "test_command": null,
    "smoke_command": null,
    "release_gate": null,
    "package_status": "published|dry_run|local_source_only|external_gated|not_a_package"
  },
  "generated_provenance": {
    "direct_edit_allowed": true,
    "generated_from": [],
    "generate_command": null,

    "stale_check": null,
    "downstream_consumers": []
  },
  "graph": {
    "imports": [],
    "exports": [],
    "route_groups": [],
    "scripts": [],
    "tests": [],
    "product_surfaces": []
  },
  "output_policy": {
    "retention": "source_controlled|ignored_local|report_archive|release_archive|delete_after_run",
    "budget_bytes": null,
    "cleanup_command": null,
    "must_not_ship": false
  },
  "provenance": {
    "builder_id": "local|github-actions|vercel|railway|docker-buildx",
    "build_type": "source|generated-docs|release|package|container|screenshot|benchmark",
    "materials": [],
    "byproducts": [],

  },
  "completion": {
    "owned": true,
    "classified": true,

    "tested_or_scoped": true,
    "generated_fresh_or_source": true,
    "release_blocking_gaps": []
  }
}
```

##### Node 28 V4 Source And Output Classification Matrix

| class | examples | required rule |
|---|---|---|
| source | `src/**/*.js`, `cli/kolm.js`, hand-authored `public/*.html`, SDK source, worker source. | Direct edits allowed only through owner lane; build/test/smoke and node ownership required. |
| generated | `public/openapi.json`, `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/product-graph.json`, generated docs/account pages. | Direct edits blocked unless emergency; generator, inputs, serial order, stale check, and consumers required. |
| fixture | examples, test fixtures, `.kolm` samples, benchmark fixtures. | Owner, data/license/safety scope, deterministic generation or source rationale required. |
| dependency output | `node_modules`, Rust `target`, Python caches, build directories. | Must be ignored and never counted as source; any tracked exception needs explicit release-artifact reason. |
| local scratch | `.tmp`, temp screenshots, debug folders, local launch snapshots. | Must have retention, cleanup, and no-deploy rule. |
| archived evidence | selected `reports/**`, audit reports, benchmark reports, screenshot reports. | Must have metadata, owner, freshness, and release relevance; not all local reports should be committed. |
| release artifact | package tarballs, Docker images, SDK bundles, generated release packets. | Needs provenance, digest, signing/attestation where applicable, and retention policy. |
| secret template | `.env.example`, provider config templates, CI secret names. | Secret values forbidden; allowed keys, owner, and readiness mapping required. |

##### Node 28 V4 Build Graph And Generator Matrix

| edge class | required evidence | release redline |
|---|---|---|
| route to docs | Router route groups, API route inventory, OpenAPI tags, API docs pages, smoke probes. | API docs cannot be regenerated in parallel with route inventory writes; serial order must be enforced. |
| product graph | Product kernel, product surfaces, journeys, readiness closeout, public graph, account UI consumers. | Product graph cannot be stale relative to route/product surface ownership. |
| CLI docs | CLI help source, generated CLI markdown/html, docs nav, link checker. | CLI docs cannot be hand-edited after generation without source update. |
| account pages | Account page generator, shared account data contracts, public account pages, screenshots. | Generated account pages need direct-edit policy and screenshot proof. |
| screenshots/reports | Screenshot audit scripts, route list, viewport/theme matrix, report path. | Screenshot evidence must be tied to route build and not confused with source assets. |
| package lanes | Manifests, package build commands, smoke examples, release metadata, package-status dashboard. | A package cannot be marketed as installable without lane evidence. |
| provenance | Release verify, codegraph, product graph, generated docs, package builds, deploy packet. | Final release must write one architecture/provenance packet that names all generated outputs and materials. |
