##### Node 28 V4 Redline To Relax

Node `28` remains redline-controlled until all of the following are true:

- Every tracked, generated, fixture, evidence, and release path has owner, runtime lane, node, class, edit policy, and release consequence.
- CODEOWNERS or an equivalent generated ownership verifier exists and covers high-risk paths.
- Architecture docs and ADRs explain Node API, public site, Python ML, Rust/C runtime, SDK packages, workers, state, generated assets, and deployment boundaries.
- Root workspace strategy is explicit: npm workspaces or a generated equivalent with package order and targeted scripts.
- Every package manifest and non-Node lane has build/test/smoke or explicit local-only/external-gated status.
- Generated files declare generator command, inputs, serial order, stale check, downstream consumers, and direct-edit policy.
- Codegraph, route inventory, product graph, readiness closeout, OpenAPI, CLI docs, and account/docs generation cannot race.
- Source counts are separated from dependency output, local scratch, reports, screenshots, audit output, generated public assets, and release artifacts.
- Large source-shaped files have owner, split/keep rationale, targeted tests, and blast-radius notes.
- Final release produces architecture/provenance evidence and refuses unresolved dirty worktree state, untracked release files, stale generated assets, or parallel-agent edits.

If any item fails, Kolm can have many green product gates, but it is not yet a structurally complete codebase.

### Production Deployment, Environment Parity, Launch Control, And Post-Deploy Proof Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It defines what must be true before a deploy can be called finished. A local green test suite, a Vercel static publish, or a backend "you can deploy" answer is not enough. The production product is finished only when the exact deployed origin, CDN, route map, environment variables, API backend, account UI, status page, smoke probes, screenshots, and rollback path prove the same product contract.

#### Why Node 29 Exists

Kolm has multiple deployment modes:

- Public website/static surface: `vercel.json` with `outputDirectory: "public"`, redirects, rewrites, global headers, and Vercel project metadata.
- Express API/control plane: `server.js`, `src/router.js`, `/health`, `/ready`, `/v1/health`, `/v1/ready/deep`, authentication, billing, capture, compile, registry, provider, and account APIs.
- Product-surface probes: `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs`, `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs`, and `docs/product-surfaces.json` production smoke rows.
- Release gate: `scripts/release-verify.cjs` and package scripts for local/product/deep verification.
- Worker and self-host deployments: Docker, Helm, worker packages, `apps/trainer/Dockerfile`, `workers/compile-server/helm`, AWS Marketplace infra, and air-gapped docs.
- Multi-region plan: `infra/dns-schema.json`, `public/docs/multi-region.html`, Route53/Cloudflare-style regional routing, and region health checks.
- Status/customer communication: `public/status.html`, `public/status.rss`, incident UI, and status subscription/webhook surfaces.

The completion risk is split-brain production: Vercel can serve updated public pages while API traffic, readiness, CSP, redirects, account state, and storage are controlled by different origins or environment variables. The final product must prove that local, preview, and production all run the same contract or clearly explain the differences.

#### Current Workspace Evidence

| Evidence | Current state observed | Completion redline |
|---|---|---|
| `vercel.json` | Parsed successfully with `framework: null`, `outputDirectory: "public"`, 49 redirects, 523 rewrites, 1 global headers block, and 2 function entries. | Redirects, rewrites, headers, API proxy behavior, and Express route mirrors must have one source of truth and drift checks. |
| `server.js` | Express disables `x-powered-by`, uses `helmet`, compression, cookie parser, raw Stripe webhook body, `trust proxy` in production-like runtime, explicit directory-collision handlers, and runtime CSP. | Express headers and Vercel static headers must be reconciled, especially CSP and WASM/script requirements, so local, Railway-direct, and Vercel production render the same feature set. |
| `.vercel/project.json` | Vercel project metadata exists for project `kolm`. | Production evidence packet must include deploy URL, alias/domain, commit SHA, build logs, environment class, and smoke result, without exposing org/project secrets. |
| `src/env.js` | Production-like runtime is detected from `NODE_ENV=production`, `RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT`, `VERCEL`, or `AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME`; `/ready` evaluates receipt secret, store driver, data dir, artifact dir, and non-blocking provider/admin checks. | Accepted production profiles must be explicit: temporary JSON, SQLite, durable database/object storage, self-host, or air-gapped. Temp artifact/data fallbacks must be visible and not marketed as durable. |
| Environment variable surface | Source scan found a large `process.env` surface covering API keys, cloud/storage, OAuth, billing, capture, compile, distill, teacher, OTEL, SSO/SAML, Sigstore/Rekor, workers, smoke, release, and runtime flags. | Required, optional, secret, public, environment-specific, deprecated, and test-only variables need a machine-readable manifest and production parity check. |
| `.env` files | Root `.env`, `.env.example`, `.env.cloudflare`, and `.env.local` exist by filename/size. Contents were not expanded in this doc lane. | No secret-bearing env file may be committed or deployed accidentally. `.env.example` must be complete but secret-free; production values must be verified by readiness without printing secrets. |
| `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | Production smoke defaults to `https://kolm.ai`, supports `KOLM_BASE`, `KOLM_API_KEY`, `--deep`, `--require-auth`, per-surface filtering, auth blockers, `/v1/ready` preflight, and checks health/ready/OpenAPI/API routes/sdk/plans/whoami. | Final deploy requires authenticated production deep smoke against the deployed base and the exact deployed commit, not just local smoke or unauthenticated public probes. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` | Boots local server with isolated temp data/artifact dirs, provisions disposable enterprise tenant, and reuses the production smoke runner. | Local certification is necessary but not sufficient; production must prove real domain, real cookies, real CSP, real storage/provider readiness, and deployed account UI. |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` | Release driver runs refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, test suite, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, and billing tiers. | Release verification must include generated artifact sequencing, production smoke after deploy, and a deploy evidence file. Local `release:verify` alone cannot answer whether `https://kolm.ai` works. |
| `docs/research/deployment-readiness-truth-2026-05-12.md` | Existing readiness truth notes `/ready` semantics, production-like triggers, SQLite vs temporary JSON profiles, and artifact temp fallback ambiguity. | This must be promoted into the live deploy contract and resolved so storage durability claims match readiness behavior. |
| `docs/research/ci-test-deploy-health-audit-2026-05-12.md` | Existing audit found historical drift among Vercel, Railway, Docker, GitHub Actions, `/health` vs `/ready`, and build/start commands. | The old audit must become a current deployment gate, not remain an archival note. |
| `infra/dns-schema.json` | Multi-region DNS schema describes US/EU/APAC, GeoDNS, health checks, fallback region, and Route53/Cloudflare style consumers. | Multi-region copy must stay starter/future-scoped until real DNS records, per-region origins, health checks, and residency smoke are live. |
| `workers/compile-server/helm/values.yaml` | Helm values define image, service, shared secret, offline mode, artifact PVC, resources, and pod security context. | Self-host/BYOC deployment must have install, upgrade, secret, storage, health, rollback, and smoke proof before being sold as turnkey. |
| `.github/workflows/smoke.yml` and `test-suite.yml` | GitHub workflows run Node 20, install, start server, smoke script, and test suite on selected paths. | CI must match the release Node/runtime version, include generated artifact checks, product-surface local deep smoke where feasible, and publish evidence artifacts. |
| `public/status.html` and `public/status.rss` | Public status surface and RSS exist with component list and incident UI. | Status data must be generated from real probes/incidents or visibly marked synthetic/static; no "all systems operational" claim without live monitoring input. |

#### External Deployment References Bound To This Node

| External reference | URL | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel project configuration | `https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration` | `vercel.json` controls output directory, redirects, regions, failover, and functions; Kolm needs a generated or audited deployment config source of truth. |
| Vercel environment variables | `https://vercel.com/docs/environment-variables` | Production, Preview, Custom, and Development variables have different scope; env changes apply only to new deployments; Kolm needs env parity checks per environment. |
| Vercel deployment management | `https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/managing-deployments` | Deployments can be filtered, redeployed, manually promoted, and managed by dashboard/CLI/API; Kolm needs deploy ID, promotion, rollback, and smoke evidence. |
| Railway deployments | `https://docs.railway.com/deployments/reference` | Railway build/deploy status, healthchecks, active state, rollback, overlap, and draining behavior must be reflected if Railway remains an origin. |
| Railway variables | `https://docs.railway.com/variables` | Service/shared variables and `.env` import behavior require a secret-safe env manifest and deployment parity gate. |
| Google SRE release engineering | `https://sre.google/sre-book/release-engineering/` | Release should be self-service, hermetic, repeatable, and governed by automated tooling rather than founder memory or manual production guessing. |

#### Deployment Redlines

1. Do not call a deploy successful until `https://kolm.ai` and any API origin behind it pass the product-surface production smoke for the deployed commit.
2. Do not rely on `/health` for readiness. `/health` is liveness; `/ready` and product-surface probes are deployment certification.
3. Do not let Vercel static headers and Express/Helmet headers drift. CSP, frame, WASM, Stripe, fonts, connect sources, and Vercel Insights must be coherent across static and dynamic paths.
4. Do not let Vercel rewrites, Express directory-collision handlers, and generated public pages define routes separately without a drift test.
5. Do not market artifact durability if production readiness permits temp artifact/data fallbacks.
6. Do not treat a root `.env` as deploy evidence. The evidence is secret-free readiness output proving required variables are configured in the target environment.
7. Do not treat local smoke as production proof. Local smoke uses disposable tenant, temp dirs, localhost cookies, and local CSP; production has real CDN, cookies, domains, auth, rate limits, storage, billing, and provider credentials.
8. Do not leave status page health static if public copy says "live status."
9. Do not deploy while multiple bots are writing generated public/API/graph outputs unless generation order and final diff are frozen.
10. Do not ship a preview-to-production promotion without rollback target, customer impact notes, and post-deploy smoke timestamps.

#### Canonical Deployment Evidence Packet

Every production or preview deploy that can be called "done" should emit a secret-free packet:

```json
{
  "deployment_evidence_version": "2026-05-25",
  "commit": {
    "sha": "git-sha",
    "branch": "main",
    "dirty": false,
    "generated_artifacts_current": true
  },
  "targets": [
    {
      "name": "kolm.ai",
      "provider": "vercel",
      "environment": "production",
      "deployment_id": "vercel-or-provider-id",
      "url": "https://kolm.ai",
      "api_origin": "https://api-or-railway-origin",
      "promoted_at": "timestamp",
      "rollback_target": "deployment-id-or-image-digest",
      "status": "active"
    }
  ],
  "config": {
    "vercel_json_sha256": "hash",
    "redirects": 49,
    "rewrites": 523,
    "headers": 1,
    "functions": 2,
    "express_route_mirror_ok": true,
    "csp_static_dynamic_match": true,
    "cache_policy_ok": true
  },
  "environment": {
    "production_like": true,
    "required_present": ["RECIPE_RECEIPT_SECRET", "KOLM_STORE_DRIVER"],
    "optional_present": ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "ADMIN_KEY"],
    "missing_required": [],
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "storage_profile": "durable-object-store|sqlite-single-node|temporary-json",
    "provider_profile": "configured|partial|missing"
  },
  "readiness": {
    "health": {"path": "/health", "status": 200},
    "ready": {"path": "/ready", "status": 200, "body_status": "ready"},
    "v1_ready_deep": {"path": "/v1/ready/deep", "status": 200},
    "object_storage": {"path": "/v1/storage/object-readiness", "configured": true, "secret_values_included": false}
  },
  "smoke": {
    "command": "KOLM_BASE=https://kolm.ai KOLM_API_KEY=*** npm run prod:surfaces:deep -- --json",
    "surfaces": 7,
    "probes": 83,
    "passed": 83,
    "failed": 0,
    "blocked": 0,
    "deep": true,
    "report_path": "reports/prod-surface-smoke/latest.json"
  },
  "screenshots": {
    "command": "npm run ui:audit:all:themes -- --base=https://kolm.ai",
    "routes": 510,
    "themes": ["dark", "light"],
    "viewports": ["desktop", "mobile"],
    "failures": 0,
    "warnings": 0,
    "report_path": "reports/ui-surface-audit/prod/latest/report.md"
  },
  "status_and_comms": {
    "status_page_updated": true,
    "status_rss_updated": true,
    "incident_open": false,
    "maintenance_window": null,
    "customer_notice_required": false
  },
  "rollback": {
    "tested": true,
    "method": "vercel-rollback|railway-rollback|image-digest|dns-weight",
    "owner": "release-engineering",
    "max_time_to_restore_minutes": 15
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `29.1` Deployment topology, domains, CDN, redirects, rewrites, headers, cache, and origin routing | Domain, CDN, static host, API origin, redirects, rewrites, headers, CSP, cache rules, cookies, and route mirrors have one source of truth and drift checks across Vercel, Express, Railway/direct, and self-host. |
| `29.2` Environment variables, secrets, readiness profiles, storage, provider credentials, and runtime modes | Required/optional/deprecated/test-only variables are machine-readable, secret values never print, production profiles are explicit, storage/provider/cloud readiness is visible, and temp fallbacks cannot masquerade as durable production. |
| `29.3` Release pipeline, preview/production promotion, generated artifacts, migrations, and deploy sequencing | Release starts from clean git, regenerates artifacts serially, runs local gates, deploys preview, smokes preview, promotes production, smokes production, records deploy evidence, and handles migrations/backfills safely. |
| `29.4` Production smoke, authenticated probes, screenshot audits, status checks, and live evidence packets | Production runs authenticated deep product-surface smoke, homepage/account/docs visual audits, API/OpenAPI/SDK hash checks, status page checks, and records a timestamped evidence packet for the deployed commit. |
| `29.5` Rollback, incident response, maintenance windows, status page, customer comms, and audit trail | Every deploy has rollback target, rollback command, owner, maintenance/incident decision, customer notice policy, status/RSS update path, and audit record. |
| `29.6` Self-host, BYOC, multi-region, worker, Docker, Helm, marketplace, and air-gapped deployment paths | Each alternate deploy mode has install, configure, secret, storage, health, upgrade, rollback, smoke, and support-scope proof before public copy calls it production-ready. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DEPLOY-001` | `29.1` | Build a deployment topology manifest. | `docs/deployment-topology.json` maps Vercel, API origin, Railway/direct, DNS, CDN, static files, functions, workers, self-host, and multi-region targets. |
| `W-DEPLOY-002` | `29.1` | Generate redirects/rewrites from source. | Vercel redirects, Vercel rewrites, Express route mirrors, sitemap, canonical URLs, and route inventory share a source or drift verifier. |
| `W-DEPLOY-003` | `29.1` | Reconcile static and dynamic security headers. | Vercel global headers and Express Helmet produce compatible CSP, HSTS, frame, connect, WASM, Stripe, fonts, and asset policies. |
| `W-DEPLOY-004` | `29.1` | Add cache and CDN proof. | HTML, JSON, SDK assets, images, videos, API responses, status feeds, and account pages have correct cache headers and purge strategy. |
| `W-DEPLOY-005` | `29.2` | Build an environment variable manifest. | Every env var has owner, scope, environment, required/optional status, secret flag, default, allowed values, readiness check, and docs link. |
| `W-DEPLOY-006` | `29.2` | Define accepted production profiles. | SQLite, durable object storage, JSON temporary, BYOC, air-gapped, and multi-region profiles state what is durable, temporary, unsupported, or external-gated. |
| `W-DEPLOY-007` | `29.2` | Add secret-safe environment parity checks. | Local, CI, preview, production, Railway, Vercel, self-host, and worker envs can be compared without printing secret values. |
| `W-DEPLOY-008` | `29.2` | Resolve `/ready` durability semantics. | Artifact/data temp fallback behavior is either blocked in production or visibly scoped in readiness, account UI, docs, and sales copy. |
| `W-DEPLOY-009` | `29.3` | Write the deploy sequencing contract. | Release sequence freezes other bots, regenerates artifacts serially, runs local checks, deploys preview, smokes preview, promotes prod, smokes prod, and records evidence. |
| `W-DEPLOY-010` | `29.3` | Add generated-artifact deploy lock. | API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout ledgers, account pages, SDK version files, screenshots, and registry packs cannot be stale at deploy. |
| `W-DEPLOY-011` | `29.3` | Add migration/backfill deploy gates. | Store schema changes, auth/billing changes, artifact storage changes, and capture data changes include migration, rollback, idempotency, and dry-run evidence. |
| `W-DEPLOY-012` | `29.4` | Require authenticated production deep smoke. | `prod:surfaces:deep` passes against `https://kolm.ai` with a valid production API key and records surfaces/probes/pass/fail/block counts. |
| `W-DEPLOY-013` | `29.4` | Add production screenshot audit. | All public/account/docs critical routes render on the production domain across dark/light and desktop/mobile after deploy. |
| `W-DEPLOY-014` | `29.4` | Add live account post-auth proof. | Production account overview, storage, billing, capture, compile, artifacts, and readiness pages are tested with a safe tenant and no secret leakage. |
| `W-DEPLOY-015` | `29.4` | Validate public status surfaces from real data. | `public/status.html`, `/status.rss`, status API, subscription, and webhook reflect real monitoring or visibly declare static/demo state. |
| `W-DEPLOY-016` | `29.5` | Build rollback runbook and evidence. | Vercel/Railway/image/DNS rollback target, command, owner, expected RTO, smoke-after-rollback, and customer impact template exist. |
| `W-DEPLOY-017` | `29.5` | Add maintenance and incident comms workflow. | Deploys decide whether status/RSS/customer email/in-app notice is required, and incident updates have owner, severity, timestamps, and postmortem link. |
| `W-DEPLOY-018` | `29.6` | Certify self-host Docker/Helm. | Docker and Helm install, configure, health, ready, storage, secret, upgrade, rollback, and smoke commands pass or are clearly marked starter-only. |
| `W-DEPLOY-019` | `29.6` | Certify multi-region and BYOC. | DNS schema, region origins, residency routing, health checks, failover, region pinning, and customer BYOC instructions have real or explicitly simulated proof. |
| `W-DEPLOY-020` | `29.6` | Add production deployment evidence archive. | Every deploy writes a report under `reports/deployments/` with commit, config hashes, env readiness, smoke, screenshots, rollback, status, and owner signoff. |

#### Node 29 Relax Redline

Node `29` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current production evidence:

- What exact commit is live at `https://kolm.ai`, what origin serves APIs, and what deploy ID or image digest can be rolled back to?
- Do Vercel static routes, Express dynamic routes, generated docs/OpenAPI/product graph, and account pages all agree?
- Are required production variables present in the target environment without leaking secret values?
- Is storage durable or temporary, and does every UI/copy claim reflect that truth?
- Did authenticated `prod:surfaces:deep` pass on the production domain after the deploy?
- Did dark/light desktop/mobile screenshot audits run against production after the deploy?
- Can account post-auth pages show real readiness, billing, storage, provider, capture, compile, and artifact state for a safe tenant?
- Can the status page and RSS reflect real health and incidents, or do they clearly state static/demo scope?
- Can rollback happen within the stated time and be proven by smoke after rollback?
- Are self-host, BYOC, multi-region, Docker, Helm, worker, marketplace, and air-gapped paths either certified or plainly scoped as starter/local/external-gated?

The relax condition for node `29` is not "the deploy command finished." It is: the deployed domain, API origin, environment, generated artifacts, account UI, product-surface probes, screenshots, status page, rollback path, and customer communication record all prove the same current product state.

#### Node 29 Release-Control-Room Addendum V2

This addendum keeps the master tree disciplined: release control belongs to node `29`, so node `52` must not be allocated for the same concern. The new requirement is a single release control room that joins local verification, generated artifacts, deploy target state, environment readiness, production smoke, screenshots, rollback, incident communications, and DORA metrics into one evidence packet per deploy.

The current repo has many strong pieces, but they are not yet one operator-grade control room:

| Evidence | Current state observed | Completion gap |
|---|---|---|
| `scripts/deploy-verified.ps1` | Verifies branch `main`, runs release verification, stages/commits/pushes, then checks the live homepage for expected copy. | The live smoke is homepage-copy-specific, not a full release manifest, environment parity proof, authenticated surface smoke, rollback target, status decision, or artifact-hash archive. |
| `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | Reads `docs/product-surfaces.json`, supports `--deep`, `--require-auth`, `KOLM_BASE`, and secret-safe API-key loading. | It should emit a durable report under `reports/deployments/<timestamp>/prod-surfaces.json` and be required by deploy evidence, not merely runnable. |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` and `npm run release:verify` | A broad local release gate exists and has been used by backend agents. | The deploy packet must bind the exact command output, commit SHA, generated artifacts, route counts, test counts, warnings, skipped tests, and exceptions to the deployed URL. |
| `railway.toml` | Uses Nixpacks, starts `node server.js`, and configures `/health` as Railway healthcheck with retry policy. | Railway readiness should use `/ready` or a documented split where `/health` means process-alive and `/ready` means dependency-ready; deploy proof must record both. |
| `Dockerfile` | Uses pinned `node:22-alpine` digest, installs production deps, runs `server.js`. | Production container proof needs image digest, SBOM/provenance, non-root/user decision, health/ready probes, storage mount policy, and rollback by digest. |
| `.env.example` | Documents many required and optional variables, including production store and receipt-secret readiness semantics. | It still carries stale billing tier references and visible mojibake markers in comments; release readiness must include env-doc freshness, canonical pricing contract, and no encoding corruption. |
| `server.js` | Mirrors many Vercel rewrites and separates `/health`, `/ready`, static routes, and Vercel/Railway/self-host parity concerns. | Route mirror drift must be machine-checked against `vercel.json` and the production deployed route list in the same release packet. |
| `vercel.json` | Large production routing and header surface. | Any route/header/cache change needs a generated diff summary, preview proof, production proof, and rollback note. |
| `npm run prod:surfaces:deep` | Script exists and can require authentication. | Deploy cannot be called done until it has run against the deployed domain with the intended production key class and recorded pass/fail/block counts. |
| `npm run ui:audit:all:themes` | Screenshot audits exist and have generated extensive report trees. | The production release packet must link the exact report created after the deploy, not rely on a prior local or pre-deploy audit. |

Current external release baseline:

| Source | What it requires for Kolm |
|---|---|
| DORA metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Track deployment frequency, change lead time, change fail rate, failed deployment recovery time, and rework together. Kolm must not optimize for "deploy happened" without also tracking recovery, rollback, rework, and user-visible activation. |
| Google SRE release engineering, `https://sre.google/sre-book/release-engineering/` | Release engineering should cover release strategy, safe rollout, and rollback. Kolm needs explicit owner, release train, automated checks, artifact reproducibility, and rollback readiness. |
| Google SRE canarying releases, `https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/` | Canarying reduces blast radius and lets teams learn at lower cost before full rollout. Kolm needs preview/canary smoke and guardrails before production promotion. |
| Vercel rollback production deployment, `https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/rollback-production-deployment` | Production rollback, promote, log comparison, and bisect workflows exist. Kolm deploy evidence must capture last-known-good URL, rollback command, log comparison result, and smoke-after-rollback instructions. |
| Vercel CLI rollback, `https://vercel.com/docs/cli/rollback` | Rollback is a first-class CLI operation. Kolm should include exact rollback command or dashboard action in every deploy packet. |
| Railway healthchecks, `https://docs.railway.com/reference/healthchecks` | Railway healthchecks help guarantee zero-downtime deployments by waiting for an HTTP 200 before replacing the previous deployment. Kolm must define which endpoint is process health versus dependency readiness and record Railway deployment status. |
| Railway deployment reference, `https://docs.railway.com/deployments/reference` | Deployment healthcheck behavior and rollback availability depend on retention. Kolm must record active deployment ID, retention/rollback availability, and runtime logs link or export. |
| GitHub deployment environments, `https://docs.github.com/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/deployments-and-environments` | Environments and protection rules can gate deployments. Kolm should require protected production deploys, reviewer or automated policy gates, scoped secrets, and deploy records. |
| OpenTelemetry service semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/resource/service/` | `service.name`, `service.version`, and service instance identity are standard resource attributes. Kolm should stamp deploy SHA/version/environment into traces, logs, metrics, and support bundles. |
| NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/61/r3/final` | Incident response should be integrated into cyber risk management. Kolm releases need severity, responder, timeline, containment, eradication/recovery, and post-incident evidence for failed deploys and security events. |
| SLSA provenance, `https://slsa.dev/provenance` | Provenance is verifiable information about where, when, and how artifacts were produced. Kolm release evidence should include build provenance and material hashes for app bundles, SDK assets, Docker images, generated docs, and `.kolm` examples. |

Release control room packet schema:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-release-control-room-v2",
  "release_id": "2026-05-25T00-00-00Z-main-commit",
  "owner": "release-engineering",
  "commit": {
    "sha": "full-git-sha",
    "branch": "main",
    "dirty": false,
    "untracked_allowed": []
  },
  "scope": {
    "changed_paths": [],
    "generated_artifacts": ["public/openapi.json", "public/docs/api-routes.json", "public/product-graph.json"],
    "claims_changed": true,
    "ui_changed": true,
    "backend_changed": true,
    "migration_required": false
  },
  "gates": {
    "release_verify": {"command": "npm run release:verify", "status": "passed", "test_count": 0, "warnings": []},
    "depth": {"command": "npm run verify:depth", "status": "passed", "external_gates": []},
    "refs": {"command": "npm run lint:refs", "status": "passed", "refs_ok": 0, "broken": 0},
    "claim_scope": {"command": "npm run verify:claims-scope", "status": "passed"},
    "brand": {"command": "npm run verify:brand", "status": "passed"}
  },
  "environments": {
    "local": {"ready": true, "secret_values_included": false},
    "preview": {"url": "https://preview.example", "ready": true, "smoke_passed": true},
    "production": {"url": "https://kolm.ai", "api_origin": "https://kolm.ai", "ready": true, "smoke_passed": true}
  },
  "deploy_targets": [
    {"provider": "vercel", "deployment_id": "dep_id", "url": "https://kolm.ai", "rollback_target": "previous_dep_id"},
    {"provider": "railway", "deployment_id": "deploy_id", "healthcheck": "/health", "readiness": "/ready"}
  ],
  "runtime_identity": {
    "service_name": "kolm-web",
    "service_version": "git-sha-or-release-id",
    "deployment_environment": "production",
    "otel_resource_ok": true
  },
  "smoke": {
    "prod_surfaces_deep": {"passed": 83, "failed": 0, "blocked": 0, "report_path": "reports/deployments/.../prod-surfaces.json"},
    "screenshots": {"routes": 510, "themes": ["dark", "light"], "viewports": ["desktop", "mobile"], "failures": 0, "warnings": 0},
    "account_auth": {"safe_tenant": "release-smoke", "passed": true, "secret_values_included": false}
  },
  "rollback": {
    "available": true,
    "method": "vercel rollback or railway rollback",
    "command": "vercel rollback <deployment>",
    "last_known_good": "deployment-url-or-image-digest",
    "smoke_after_rollback": "npm run prod:surfaces:deep",
    "target_recovery_minutes": 15
  },
  "incident_decision": {
    "status_page_required": false,
    "customer_notice_required": false,
    "severity_if_failed": "SEV2",
    "postmortem_required_if_rollback": true
  },
  "provenance": {
    "slsa_predicate": "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1",
    "subjects": [],
    "materials": [],
    "attestations": []
  }
}
```

Release-control-room work tickets:

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-001` | `29.3` | Create the release control room packet generator. | A command emits `reports/deployments/<release_id>/release-control-room.json` with commit, changed paths, generated artifacts, gate results, env profiles, deploy IDs, rollback, screenshots, smoke, and provenance. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-002` | `29.3` | Make `deploy-verified.ps1` evidence-first. | The deploy script writes evidence before and after push, does not depend on homepage copy strings, and fails unless packet fields are complete or explicitly waived. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-003` | `29.4` | Persist production smoke output. | `prod-surface-smoke.cjs` writes JSON and markdown summaries under the release packet path with no secret values and exact route/probe counts. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-004` | `29.4` | Require production account smoke. | A safe production tenant proves account overview, storage readiness, billing tier, capture status, compile state, artifact inventory, API key trust label, and no secret leakage. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-005` | `29.4` | Bind screenshot audits to deploy IDs. | Production screenshot reports record base URL, deployment ID, commit, route count, theme, viewport, warnings, failures, and reviewed representative screenshots. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-006` | `29.2` | Build secret-safe environment diff. | Local, CI, preview, Vercel, Railway, Docker, self-host, worker, and airgap profiles compare presence, class, and readiness without printing values. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-007` | `29.2` | Fix env-doc freshness as a release gate. | `.env.example` matches canonical plan names, pricing, required production variables, storage semantics, and contains zero mojibake or deprecated public claims. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-008` | `29.1` | Add Vercel/Express route mirror proof. | Release packet records `vercel.json` route/header/cache hash, Express mirror hash, generated route inventory, and any intentional mismatch exceptions. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-009` | `29.5` | Add rollback proof, not rollback hope. | Every production deploy records rollback target, exact command, owner, target recovery time, expected data impact, smoke-after-rollback command, and whether a drill has passed. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-010` | `29.5` | Integrate incident and status decisions. | Failed smoke, failed readiness, rollback, degraded provider/storage, or security signal produces severity, status-page decision, customer-notice decision, owner, timeline, and postmortem requirement. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-011` | `29.4` | Stamp runtime identity into telemetry. | Logs/traces/metrics include service name, service version, deployment environment, commit, release ID, and deployment provider using OpenTelemetry-compatible resource attributes. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-012` | `29.3` | Add DORA and release-activation metrics. | Each release updates deployment frequency, change lead time, change fail rate, failed deployment recovery time, rework, activation lag, and post-deploy defects. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-013` | `29.3` | Add preview/canary decision gate. | Preview deploy, canary/rolling eligibility, guardrail thresholds, promotion decision, and abort criteria are recorded before production traffic is moved. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-014` | `29.6` | Add container and self-host release proof. | Docker image digest, SBOM/provenance, health/ready smoke, storage mount, non-root/security decision, upgrade path, and rollback by digest are recorded. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-015` | `29.6` | Add BYOC and multi-region deploy proof. | Provider, region, object store, credentials class, routing, failover, residency, customer owner, support boundary, rollback, and smoke are attached per target. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-016` | `29.3` | Freeze concurrent-agent deploy windows. | A release cannot start while frontend/backend/research agents are still editing target files unless a release owner records owned paths, excluded paths, and merge order. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-017` | `29.4` | Add production claim-scope parity. | Deployed public pages, account pages, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout ledger, pricing, docs, and API envelopes agree on shipped versus external-gated state. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-018` | `29.5` | Archive logs and evidence with retention. | Build logs, runtime logs, smoke logs, screenshot reports, release manifests, status decisions, and rollback notes are retained beyond CI artifact expiry. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-019` | `29.3` | Add generated-artifact serial lock. | OpenAPI, API ref, product graph, readiness closeout, sitemap, SDK version, and screenshot reports are regenerated serially or verified unchanged before deploy. |
| `W-DEPLOY-RC-020` | `29.4` | Add production "done" hard stop. | A release is not done until production smoke, account smoke, screenshots, rollback target, status decision, telemetry identity, claim-scope parity, and evidence archive are all present. |

Node `29` V2 relax redline:

- Can a release owner open one packet and know exactly what commit, generated artifacts, routes, docs, UI screenshots, API schemas, SDK assets, and product claims went live?
- Can the packet prove the target environment is production-safe without exposing secret values?
- Can it prove `/health`, `/ready`, `/v1/ready/deep`, object storage readiness, billing, auth, account, and product-surface smoke on the deployed origin?
- Can it prove every customer-visible claim on the deployed site is still scoped to the actual shipped/external-gated state?
- Can an operator roll back by following the packet without reading chat history?
- Can a failed deploy produce a status/customer-communication decision, timeline, and postmortem evidence automatically?
- Can DORA metrics and product activation metrics be computed from the packet without manual archaeology?

If any answer is no, node `29` remains open even if local tests are green.

#### Node 29 V3 Production Finality, Deploy Freeze, And Live-Proof Addendum

Node `52` remains unused. The user has deployed while frontend, backend, CLI, and research lanes have also been moving. That is not a new branch; it is exactly why node `29` exists. The master tree must now distinguish four different states that are often collapsed in chat:

1. local code is structurally green;
2. a provider accepted a deploy;
3. the public origin serves the intended build;
4. production is proven complete by live public, authenticated, visual, operational, and rollback evidence.

Only the fourth state can relax node `29`. A deployed-but-unverified origin must be labelled `prod_deployed_unverified`, even if the local backend gate, frontend screenshot audit, or provider build succeeded.

##### Node 29 V3 Current Evidence Delta

Current repo evidence inspected for this addendum:

| Evidence path | Current evidence | Completion implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `vercel.json` | 57,184 bytes; 2,389 lines; modified in the current worktree outside this spec lane. | Production routing, rewrites, headers, and cache behavior are not final evidence until this file is reconciled with the deployed commit and route mirror proof. |
| `.vercel/project.json` | Vercel project link exists locally. | The repo knows a project target, but that is not a deploy ID, production alias, route proof, or rollback target. |
| `railway.toml` | 184 bytes; 8 lines. | Railway is part of the deploy topology and needs its own healthcheck, deployment ID, logs, retention, and rollback evidence. |
| `server.js` | 24,877 bytes; 442 lines. | The local Express entrypoint must be proven to match the production route behavior behind Vercel/Railway/proxy routing. |
| `src/env.js` | 12,538 bytes; 268 lines. | Environment readiness exists in code, but production finality needs a secret-free deployed env profile, not local assumptions. |
| `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | 13,860 bytes; 354 lines. | Production smoke tooling exists. It must emit immutable release artifacts tied to the exact deploy and key class used. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` | 7,508 bytes; 208 lines. | Local smoke remains useful but cannot substitute for deployed-origin proof. |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` | 32,595 bytes; 659 lines. | Release verification is substantial, but V3 requires binding its output to a release packet and deploy target. |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` | 33,546 bytes; 726 lines. | Screenshot automation exists. Production finality requires screenshots from the deployed origin after the final build, not only local route screenshots. |
| `docs/product-surfaces.json` | 64,084 bytes; 2,109 lines. | Surface smoke ownership exists and must be checked against the deployed route inventory after generated artifacts settle. |
| `public/status.html` | 19,672 bytes; 284 lines. | A status surface exists. Production finality requires component-backed status state, not static reassurance. |
| `public/status.rss` | 843 bytes; 18 lines. | RSS exists and must reflect deploy incidents, maintenance, degraded components, and postmortems. |
| `infra/dns-schema.json` | 7,828 bytes; 186 lines. | DNS and routing schema exists, but current production proof must attach DNS, CDN, TLS, and redirect evidence. |
| `.github/workflows/smoke.yml` | 742 bytes; 29 lines. | CI smoke exists but does not prove the currently deployed origin unless its report includes the production base URL and deploy ID. |
| `.github/workflows/test-suite.yml` | 786 bytes; 36 lines. | Test-suite workflow exists; it must feed the release packet or remain pre-release evidence only. |
| `public/nav.js` | Modified by a parallel frontend lane. | The production UI cannot be final until nav/chrome changes settle and screenshot evidence is regenerated from the same commit. |
| `src/intent.js` | Modified by a parallel lane. | Any runtime behavior touched by intent routing must be in the deploy diff and smoke packet before production finality. |

The V3 state rule is strict:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-production-finality-v3",
  "current_default_state": "prod_deployed_unverified",
  "why": "A provider deploy or local green gate does not prove the public origin, authenticated account flows, generated artifacts, claim scope, screenshots, rollback, status, and telemetry identity all match the intended release.",
  "parallel_worktree_inputs": [
    "public/nav.js",
    "src/intent.js",
    "vercel.json"
  ],
  "node_owner": "29",
  "cross_links": ["12", "13", "25", "30", "31", "39", "43", "45", "46"]
}
```

##### Official Deployment Baselines Refreshed For V3

| Source | V3 requirement for Kolm |
|---|---|
| Vercel rolling back a production deployment, `https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/rollback-production-deployment` | Every production release packet must include the rollback target, exact rollback command, status check, log comparison, and smoke-after-rollback command. A rollback path that exists only in chat is not sufficient. |
| Vercel CLI rollback, `https://vercel.com/docs/cli/rollback` | The packet must store the specific `vercel rollback` or `vercel promote` command shape and the plan limitation that determines how far back rollback can go. |
| Vercel Skew Protection, `https://vercel.com/docs/skew-protection` | If old browser clients can call new server routes or new clients can call old server routes, the release packet must state whether version locking/skew protection is enabled or why the deploy is safe without it. |
| Vercel Instant Rollback, `https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/instant-rollback` | Rollback can restore a previous deployment but may restore stale config, env, or cron state; Kolm must record config/env deltas and smoke the restored state. |
| Railway healthchecks, `https://docs.railway.com/reference/healthchecks` | Railway healthchecks gate deployment replacement, but they are not continuous monitoring after go-live. Kolm needs separate live readiness and monitor evidence after deploy. |
| GitHub deployment environments, `https://docs.github.com/actions/how-tos/deploy/configure-and-manage-deployments/control-deployments` | Production deployment should use environment protection, branch controls, concurrency, approvals, and deployment history, not ad hoc local pushes during active agent edits. |
| Google SRE canarying releases, `https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/` | Promotion must be a measured rollout decision with guardrail metrics and abort criteria; "it deployed" is not a rollout success metric. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/semantic-conventions/` | Logs, metrics, and traces must carry common deployment and service identity so production smoke, incidents, and rollback can be correlated to one release. |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final` | Release integrity evidence must prove the deployed software is the intended artifact and has not drifted from the reviewed source, build, and generated outputs. |

##### Production Finality State Machine

Node `29` V3 uses these states:

| State | Meaning | Allowed public claim |
|---|---|---|
| `not_started` | No release candidate exists. | No deploy claim. |
| `local_candidate` | Local gates passed on a candidate tree. | "Local release candidate passed local verification." |
| `preview_deployed` | Preview target exists. | "Preview is deployed for review." |
| `preview_smoked` | Preview public, auth, and screenshot probes passed. | "Preview passed release smoke." |
| `prod_deployed_unverified` | Production provider accepted a deploy, but full live evidence is not attached. | "Production deploy occurred; verification pending." |
| `prod_smoked_public` | Public routes, docs, SEO, status, and API metadata passed on production. | "Public production smoke passed." |
| `prod_smoked_auth` | Safe authenticated account and API-key flows passed on production. | "Authenticated production smoke passed." |
| `rollback_ready` | Rollback target, command, owner, and smoke-after-rollback path are proven. | "Rollback path is proven for this release." |
| `production_closed` | Public, auth, screenshots, readiness, telemetry, status, rollback, claim-scope, and archive evidence are complete. | "Release is production verified." |
| `rollback_required` | A hard gate failed after production deploy. | "Release failed; rollback or incident handling active." |
| `blocked_external` | Final proof depends on external cert, package, partner, provider, or credential state. | "Blocked by named external gate." |

No implementation agent may write `production_closed` unless the release packet contains the exact deployed base URL, deploy ID, commit, generated-artifact hashes, smoke reports, screenshots, env readiness classes, rollback target, and status decision.

##### V3 Release Packet Shape

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-production-finality-v3",
  "release_id": "2026-05-25T00-00-00Z-prod",
  "decision_state": "prod_deployed_unverified",
  "commit": {
    "sha": null,
    "branch": "main",
    "dirty_tree": true,
    "parallel_modified_paths": ["public/nav.js", "src/intent.js", "vercel.json"]
  },
  "deployment_targets": [
    {
      "provider": "vercel",
      "project_id_present": true,
      "production_url": "https://kolm.ai",
      "deployment_id": null,
      "alias_verified": false,
      "skew_protection_state": "unknown",
      "rollback_target": null
    },
    {
      "provider": "railway",
      "healthcheck_path": null,
      "deployment_id": null,
      "rollback_target": null,
      "runtime_logs_attached": false
    }
  ],
  "generated_artifacts": {
    "api_routes": "required",
    "openapi": "required",
    "product_graph": "required",
    "readiness_closeout": "required",
    "sitemap": "required",
    "sdk_version": "required",
    "cli_docs": "required"
  },
  "live_probes": {
    "public_smoke": "required",
    "authenticated_account_smoke": "required",
    "storage_object_readiness": "required",
    "product_graph": "required",
    "claim_scope": "required",
    "seo_discovery": "required"
  },
  "visual_evidence": {
    "dark_desktop": "required",
    "dark_mobile": "required",
    "light_desktop": "required",
    "light_mobile": "required",
    "manual_representative_review": "required"
  },
  "operations": {
    "telemetry_identity": "required",
    "status_page_decision": "required",
    "incident_decision": "required",
    "customer_notice_decision": "required",
    "rollback_drill_or_reason": "required"
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "public_copy_matches_closeout": "required",
    "pricing_matches_billing_api": "required",
    "brand_legacy_aliases_accounted": "required",
    "non_final_gates_visible": "required"
  }
}
```

##### Production Smoke Matrix V3

| Probe class | Must include | Hard fail if |
|---|---|---|
| Public origin | `/`, `/product`, `/pricing`, `/enterprise`, `/docs`, `/openapi.json`, `/product-graph.json`, `/product-readiness-closeout.json`, `/status`, `/status.rss`, `/sitemap.xml`, `/llms.txt`, `/.well-known/ai-context.json`. | Any route returns wrong status, stale brand/pricing, broken metadata, broken nav, invalid JSON, or mismatched readiness. |
| API readiness | `/health`, `/ready`, `/v1/ready/deep`, `/v1/product/graph`, `/v1/storage/object-readiness`, `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`. | Health and readiness are conflated, secrets appear, storage/provider readiness is missing, or pricing differs from public copy. |
| Authenticated account | `/v1/whoami`, account overview, storage, billing, API keys, capture status, artifacts, product matrix readiness, and logout/expired-session handling. | Safe production key cannot load account state, secret values are exposed, or account matrix hides non-final gates. |
| Frontend visual | Dark/light desktop/mobile for homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs, status, account overview, storage, demo/video, ROI, and nav menu states. | Video/demo fails in either theme, nav underline/highlight drifts, buttons overflow, text overlaps, media is placeholder-grade, or account surfaces are incoherent. |
| Claim scope | Public pages, docs, account, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, pricing, enterprise CTA, and sales copy. | Any external/package/benchmark/certification-gated item is marketed as fully shipped. |
| SEO/discovery | Title, description, canonical, OpenGraph, schema, sitemap, robots, `llms.txt`, `ai-context`, docs IA, internal links. | Browser tab/social/search snippets show mojibake, stale brand, stale price, duplicate canonical, or missing core product positioning. |
| Operations | Logs, traces, metrics, status page, RSS, deployment ID, deploy owner, incident decision, customer notice decision, rollback command. | A failed probe cannot be tied to a release, owner, status decision, and rollback target. |

##### Deploy Freeze And Concurrent-Agent Rule

Production deploys during active multi-agent editing are allowed only as `prod_deployed_unverified`. To promote to `production_closed`, the release owner must record:

1. which agent owns each dirty path;
2. which paths are included in the deploy commit;
3. which paths are intentionally excluded;
4. whether generated artifacts were rebuilt after all included edits;
5. whether `vercel.json`, route inventory, OpenAPI, product graph, sitemap, docs, screenshots, and smoke probes all describe the same release;
6. which API key class was used for authenticated smoke;
7. whether rollback target still works after any environment or config drift;
8. whether the frontend screenshot evidence was captured from the deployed origin and final commit.

If any of those are unknown, node `29` must remain open and the release cannot be described as finished.

##### V3 Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-001` | `29.3` | Generate the production-finality packet. | A command writes `reports/deployments/<release_id>/production-finality.json` using the V3 schema and refuses `production_closed` with missing deploy IDs or smoke evidence. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-002` | `29.3` | Add deploy freeze lock. | The release script detects active dirty paths, records owners/exclusions, and blocks production-close unless a release owner signs the merge order. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-003` | `29.1` | Detect deployed target identity. | The packet records Vercel deployment URL/ID, production alias, project ID class, Railway service/deployment ID, DNS target, TLS state, and CDN/header route hashes. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-004` | `29.3` | Bind generated artifacts to the release. | API routes, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, sitemap, SDK version, CLI docs, and screenshot manifests are hashed and verified unchanged before production-close. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-005` | `29.4` | Persist public production smoke. | `prod-surface-smoke.cjs` or successor stores JSON/markdown results for public routes with base URL, route counts, statuses, failures, warnings, and no secrets. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-006` | `29.4` | Add authenticated production account smoke. | A safe production tenant proves `whoami`, overview, storage, billing, API keys, artifacts, capture, product matrix, expired-session, and logout flows. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-007` | `29.4` | Attach production screenshot evidence. | Dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots are captured from the production origin after deployment, linked to deploy ID, and manually spot-reviewed for changed critical surfaces. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-008` | `29.2` | Add secret-safe production env readiness. | Local, CI, Vercel, Railway, and BYOC profiles compare env presence/classes/readiness without exposing values or key-shaped strings. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-009` | `29.2` | Gate storage, cloud, GPU, SSO, and OTEL readiness. | Production readiness reports selected provider, configured groups, missing groups, object size notes, `secret_values_included:false`, and explicit external blockers. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-010` | `29.1` | Prove route/header/cache parity. | `vercel.json`, Express routes, generated API refs, public redirects, headers, CSP, cache rules, and route mirrors have hashes and mismatch exceptions. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-011` | `29.5` | Add rollback drill evidence. | Each production release records rollback target, exact command, status check, expected data/config impact, last drill time, smoke-after-rollback command, and owner. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-012` | `29.5` | Make status and incident decisions explicit. | Failed smoke or degraded readiness creates severity, status/RSS decision, customer-notice decision, owner, timeline, and postmortem requirement. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-013` | `29.4` | Add production claim-scope audit. | Deployed public pages, docs, account, OpenAPI, product graph, pricing, and closeout ledger agree on shipped versus external-gated state. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-014` | `29.4` | Add SEO/discovery production smoke. | Production titles, descriptions, canonicals, OpenGraph, schema, sitemap, robots, `llms.txt`, and `ai-context` are checked for stale brand, stale pricing, mojibake, and broken links. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-015` | `29.4` | Stamp production telemetry identity. | Logs/traces/metrics include service name, service version, deploy environment, commit, release ID, provider, and route/surface IDs. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-016` | `29.3` | Add skew/static-asset safety. | The release records whether Vercel Skew Protection/version locking is enabled or proves old/new client/server compatibility for changed JS, CSS, and API schemas. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-017` | `29.6` | Add self-host/BYOC production-finality parity. | Docker, Railway, Vercel, BYOC, worker, and air-gapped deploys all use the same finality schema with target-specific probes and rollback commands. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-018` | `29.5` | Add post-deploy watch windows. | The packet records 15-minute, 1-hour, 24-hour, and 7-day checks for error rate, latency, auth failures, billing events, route 404/5xx, account activation, support tickets, and rollback decision. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-019` | `29.6` | Add production evidence archive retention. | Smoke, screenshot, logs, telemetry links, deploy IDs, generated-artifact hashes, rollback, and status decisions are retained outside ephemeral CI logs. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V3-020` | `29.6` | Issue a node `29` completion certificate. | The certificate says `production_closed` only when public smoke, auth smoke, visual proof, env readiness, rollback, status, telemetry, claim scope, and archive retention all pass for the exact deploy. |

##### Node 29 V3 Relax Redline

Node `29` is not relaxed by deployment alone. It relaxes only when the current production release packet can answer:

- What exact commit, generated artifacts, deploy IDs, domains, DNS targets, headers, rewrites, cache rules, and runtime env classes are serving `https://kolm.ai`?
- Did public production smoke pass on the deployed origin, and are the JSON route counts, API metadata, docs, status feed, and discovery files valid?
- Did authenticated production smoke pass with a safe key, including account overview, product matrix, storage readiness, billing, API keys, artifacts, capture, and expired-session handling?
- Did production screenshots pass in dark and light, desktop and mobile, after the final frontend/navigation/media/account changes?
- Are all non-final gates from the closeout ledger still visible and not marketed as shipped?
- Can an operator execute rollback without reading chat history, and has smoke-after-rollback been defined or drilled?
- Are status, incident, maintenance, customer-notice, telemetry, and retention decisions attached to the release?
- Does the release archive survive CI log expiry and point to all evidence by path or URL?

If any answer is missing, the correct state is not "done"; it is a named open state in the V3 state machine.

#### Node 29 V4 Production Evidence Control Plane, Target Identity, And Post-Deploy Watch Contract

Node `52` remains unused. V3 defined the state machine; V4 defines the evidence plane that makes the state machine executable. A deploy is not a chat statement, a dashboard screenshot, or a green local command. A deploy is a versioned packet that binds the source tree, generated artifacts, deploy target, platform config, runtime env class, public routes, authenticated routes, visual proof, telemetry identity, rollback target, status decision, and watch window into one secret-safe record.

This node is intentionally operational rather than architectural. Node `28` owns repo/build graph shape. Node `29` owns whether a specific release is actually serving users and can be proven, rolled back, monitored, and explained.

##### Node 29 V4 Current Evidence Delta

Current repo evidence inspected for this V4 pass:

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V4 interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel project config | `vercel.json` exists, is large, and is currently modified outside this spec lane. It declares file-based deployment behavior for the public output. | The production packet must hash `vercel.json`, classify every redirect/rewrite/header/cache rule, and prove parity with the Express mirror before deploy finality. |
| Vercel linked project identity | `.vercel/project.json` exists with project and org identifiers. | The packet must record the presence and classification of these identifiers without leaking values into public docs or logs. |
| Vercel bundle policy | `.vercelignore` exists and excludes Docker, Railway, tests, bench, workers, apps, packages, audits, archives, data, build outputs, and many docs; it also contains a visible mojibake comment. | The packet must prove the Vercel bundle contains exactly the intended static/server assets and must flag malformed comments or unclear exclusion policy as release-blocking polish debt. |
| Railway runtime config | `railway.toml` starts `node server.js`, uses Nixpacks, and points healthchecks at `/health`. | The packet must distinguish `/health` process liveness from `/ready` dependency readiness, and must state which one Railway uses for traffic switching. |
| Express route mirror | `server.js` contains Vercel/Railway proxy comments, security headers, Vercel rewrite mirrors, `/health`, `/ready`, and extensionless static fallback behavior. | The packet must include a route/header parity report between `vercel.json`, Express, generated API docs, and production probes. |
| Production smoke tooling | `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` exists and supports base URL selection, deep mode, auth-required mode, JSON output, API key loading, and secret-safe headers. `prod:surfaces:deep` maps to this script. | This is the correct proof tool, but its output must be retained under a release-specific evidence directory with base URL, deploy identity, key class, route counts, failure class, and report hash. |
| Deployment evidence directory | `reports/` currently contains `kolm-live-smoke-data` and `ui-surface-audit`; no `reports/deployments` directory was found in this pass. | Production proof must not rely on console scrollback. V4 requires `reports/deployments/<release-id>/` or an equivalent immutable evidence path. |
| Env profile docs | `.env.example` exists and documents health/readiness classes, but still contains old Stripe payment link names for Starter/Teams/Business and no `.env.local.example` was found. | Env drift is a production-control issue, not just docs polish. Pricing-plan env names must match current product contracts or be explicitly marked legacy-compatible. |
| Container/self-host target | `Dockerfile` exists; no root `docker-compose.yml` was found. `.vercelignore` intentionally excludes Docker/Railway from the Vercel bundle. | Docker/self-host is a separate target and needs its own build/run/smoke packet, not accidental coverage from Vercel or Railway. |
| Status communications | `public/status.html` and `public/status.rss` exist. | Every production deploy packet must include a status-page decision: silent, scheduled maintenance, incident, or customer notice, with RSS/status proof when applicable. |
| Production API topology | No root `api/index.js` was found while the repo uses `server.js` and static public output behavior. | V4 requires an API topology proof so operators know whether production is Vercel static/proxy, Railway Express, serverless API, or mixed. |
| Concurrent tree | `public/nav.js`, `src/intent.js`, and `vercel.json` are modified outside this spec lane; this blueprint file is untracked. | The correct production state remains `prod_candidate_dirty_tree` until those edits are owned, committed or intentionally excluded, then deployed and probed. |

##### Node 29 V4 External Baseline Refresh

| Baseline | Current source | Operational implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Vercel file-based config | Vercel documents `vercel.json` and `vercel.ts` as repo-based, version-controlled project configuration, with only one config file allowed per project: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration | `vercel.json` changes must be treated as release-critical source changes and hashed in the production evidence packet. |
| Vercel rollback | Vercel Instant Rollback can point production domains back to an eligible prior deployment, but rollback may restore stale config/env behavior and disables automatic production-domain assignment until undone: https://vercel.com/docs/instant-rollback | Kolm rollback proof must record eligible target, domain list, env assumptions, auto-assignment state, and smoke-after-rollback. |
| Vercel skew protection | Vercel Skew Protection uses deployment IDs/version locking for supported frameworks and documents custom deployment ID requirements for prebuilt workflows: https://vercel.com/docs/skew-protection | Long-lived account/docs sessions and static assets need deploy identity proof; custom/non-framework flows need explicit skew or reload strategy. |
| Railway healthchecks | Railway queries the configured endpoint until it returns HTTP `200`, then switches the new deployment active; it does not monitor the endpoint after the deployment is live: https://docs.railway.com/deployments/healthchecks | `/health` can gate initial traffic, but post-deploy monitoring still needs separate probes and watch windows. |
| GitHub deployment environments | GitHub environments can require approvals, branch restrictions, protection rules, and restricted secrets for deployment jobs: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/deploy/configure-and-manage-deployments/control-deployments | Production deploys should use protected environment records and not just ad hoc local CLI deploys. |
| OpenTelemetry service identity | OpenTelemetry semantic conventions recommend service resource attributes including `service.version`: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/resource/service/ | Every production deploy must stamp traces/logs/metrics with commit and deploy version identity. |
| Canary release control | Google SRE recommends exposing small portions of production traffic to new releases and evaluating whether the canary is good or bad before wider rollout: https://sre.google/workbook/canarying-releases/ | Kolm needs an explicit canary/gradual exposure model or a documented reason each target is all-at-once with fast rollback. |
| Software delivery metrics | DORA tracks throughput and instability through change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate: https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | Node `29` evidence should feed release metrics instead of being one-off release theater. |

##### Node 29 V4 Production Evidence Packet Contract

Every production promotion creates or updates one packet with this shape:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.production_evidence_control_plane.v4",
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "state": "prod_candidate_dirty_tree | prod_candidate_clean | prod_deployed_unverified | prod_live_verified | prod_rollback_verified | production_closed",
  "release": {
    "release_id": "string",
    "commit": "string",
    "branch": "string",
    "dirty_tree": false,
    "generated_artifacts_hash": "string",
    "created_at": "iso-8601"
  },
  "target_identity": {
    "primary_domain": "https://kolm.ai",
    "canonical_origin": "string",
    "vercel_project_link_present": true,
    "vercel_deployment_id": "secret-safe string or hash",
    "railway_service_id": "secret-safe string or hash",
    "docker_image_digest": "string or null",
    "api_topology": "vercel-static | railway-express | docker-self-host | mixed"
  },
  "bundle": {
    "vercel_json_hash": "string",
    "vercelignore_hash": "string",
    "included_manifest_path": "string",
    "excluded_manifest_path": "string",
    "malformed_text_markers": 0
  },
  "route_and_header_parity": {
    "source_paths": ["vercel.json", "server.js", "public/docs/api-routes.json", "public/openapi.json"],
    "routes_declared": 0,
    "routes_probed_public": 0,
    "routes_probed_authenticated": 0,
    "headers_verified": ["content-security-policy", "cache-control", "x-content-type-options"],
    "redirects_verified": 0,
    "mismatches": []
  },
  "env_profiles": {
    "production_profile_hash": "string",
    "required_groups_present": ["auth", "billing", "storage", "providers", "telemetry"],
    "legacy_vars_allowed": [],
    "missing_groups": []
  },
  "smoke": {
    "public_report": "reports/deployments/<release-id>/prod-surfaces-public.json",
    "auth_report": "reports/deployments/<release-id>/prod-surfaces-auth.json",
    "safe_key_class": "prod-smoke-least-privilege",
    "route_count": 0,
    "surface_count": 0,
    "failures": []
  },
  "visual": {
    "screenshot_report": "reports/deployments/<release-id>/ui-surface-audit/report.md",
    "themes": ["dark", "light"],
    "viewports": ["desktop", "mobile"],
    "routes": 0,
    "failures": []
  },
  "readiness": {
    "health": "pass",
    "ready": "pass",
    "cloud_readiness": "pass | scoped | fail",
    "object_storage_readiness": "pass | scoped | fail",
    "closeout_open_requirements": 0
  },
  "telemetry": {
    "service_name": "kolm",
    "service_version": "commit-or-release",
    "deployment_environment": "production",
    "trace_query": "string",
    "log_query": "string",
    "metric_query": "string"
  },
  "rollback": {
    "eligible_target": "string",
    "rollback_drilled_at": "iso-8601 or null",
    "smoke_after_rollback_report": "string or null",
    "auto_assignment_state": "normal | disabled_after_rollback | unknown"
  },
  "status_decision": {
    "decision": "silent | maintenance | incident | customer_notice",
    "status_page_path": "string",
    "rss_path": "string",
    "customer_notice_path": "string or null"
  },
  "watch_windows": [
    {"duration_minutes": 15, "owner": "string", "checks": ["5xx", "latency", "auth", "billing", "capture", "account"]},
    {"duration_minutes": 60, "owner": "string", "checks": ["errors", "traffic", "provider", "storage", "telemetry"]}
  ],
  "evidence_retention": {
    "archive_path": "reports/deployments/<release-id>",
    "retention_days": 180,
    "sha256_manifest": "reports/deployments/<release-id>/SHA256SUMS"
  }
}
```

##### Node 29 V4 Deployment Target Matrix

| Target | Owned config/evidence | Required proof | Completion state |
|---|---|---|---|
| Vercel production domain | `vercel.json`, `.vercel/project.json`, `.vercelignore`, public static output, deploy ID, domain alias | File hash, bundle manifest, route/header parity, public smoke, auth smoke, screenshots, rollback target, skew/reload strategy | `prod_live_verified` only after current deploy packet exists |
| Railway Express runtime | `railway.toml`, `server.js`, `/health`, `/ready`, process logs, service env | Healthcheck switch proof, ready proof, route parity, dependency readiness, restart behavior, post-live monitor | `prod_live_verified` only after Railway target identity and probes pass |
| Docker/self-host | `Dockerfile`, env profile, volume/data policy, exposed ports, health command, image digest | Build digest, run command, health/ready, local/self-host smoke, artifact storage policy, shutdown behavior | Separate proof from Vercel/Railway |
| BYOC or multi-region | Deployment manifest, region map, storage/provider/env profiles, DNS/traffic policy | Region smoke, data residency proof, rollback route per region, customer boundary proof | Cannot inherit single-region proof |
| Preview/staging | Preview URL, branch, env profile, deployment protection, auth bypass policy | Public and auth smoke against preview plus clean promotion path | Never counts as production without production alias proof |
| Local verification | `release:verify`, `local:surfaces`, `local:surfaces:deep`, screenshots, generated docs | Necessary precondition only | Local green is not production closed |

##### Node 29 V4 Live Proof Matrix

| Proof class | Minimum command or artifact | Must record | Failure meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| Public production smoke | `npm run prod:surfaces` with explicit base | base URL, deploy ID, route count, public route pass/fail, report hash | `prod_deployed_unverified` |
| Authenticated production smoke | `npm run prod:surfaces:deep` with least-privilege key | key class, tenant, scopes, account route pass/fail, expired-session checks, report hash | no account/product surface completion |
| Health/readiness | Direct probes for `/health` and `/ready` plus cloud/storage readiness endpoints | response bodies, status codes, dependency groups, missing env groups | production liveness may exist but product readiness is scoped |
| Visual proof | Post-deploy screenshot audit on production URL | routes, themes, viewports, media/demo/video load state, report path | frontend cannot be called complete |
| SEO/discovery proof | robots, sitemap, llms/ai context, OpenGraph/Twitter cards, schema, status RSS | metadata hashes and rendered snippets | acquisition/discovery node remains open |
| Route/header parity | Generated parity report from `vercel.json`, `server.js`, API docs, production headers | redirects, rewrites, cache, CSP, security headers, API routes, unknown drift | platform config drift |
| Env profile proof | Secret-safe env readiness report | required groups, legacy aliases, missing groups, provider status, secret redaction | unsafe deploy if required groups absent |
| Rollback proof | Vercel/Railway/Docker rollback target and smoke-after-rollback plan | target ID, domain list, env caveats, operator command, expected smoke | cannot claim operational readiness |
| Telemetry proof | Logs/traces/metrics query with service/version identity | `service.name`, `service.version`, deploy env, dashboard links, alert state | cannot watch or debug production |
| Status decision | Status page/RSS/customer notice record | decision, owner, timestamp, customer impact, rollback/incident link | poor customer communication control |
| Watch window | 15, 60, and 24 hour post-deploy checkpoints | owner, check results, incident threshold, final disposition | deploy remains under observation |

##### Node 29 V4 State Machine Refinement

| State | Entry condition | Exit condition |
|---|---|---|
| `prod_candidate_dirty_tree` | Any deploy-relevant file is modified/untracked or generated outputs are stale. | Worktree is intentionally frozen for the release packet, with exclusions documented. |
| `prod_candidate_clean` | Release candidate has a clean or intentionally-scoped tree and local gates are green. | Platform deploy target is created and target identity is recorded. |
| `prod_deployed_unverified` | Domain or runtime now points at a new deployment, but production proof is incomplete. | Public smoke, auth smoke, health/ready, route/header parity, env profile, and screenshots pass for the exact deploy. |
| `prod_live_verified` | Current production deploy has full public/account/product proof. | Rollback target, telemetry identity, status decision, and watch windows are attached. |
| `prod_rollback_verified` | Rollback procedure and smoke-after-rollback are defined or drilled. | Evidence packet is archived with retention and release metrics. |
| `production_closed` | Full packet exists, retained, secret-safe, linked, and no unresolved release blockers remain. | Next release starts a new packet; closure does not transfer automatically. |
