| `W-DEPLOY-V4-021` | P0 | `29.1` | Enforce dirty-tree deploy freeze. | Deploy-relevant untracked/modified files block `production_closed` unless explicitly scoped as non-release or parallel-agent pending. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V4-022` | P0 | `29.1` | Add API topology proof. | The packet states whether production is Vercel static/proxy, Railway Express, Docker/self-host, serverless API, or mixed, and names the owning entrypoints. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V4-023` | P0 | `29.5` | Add evidence retention manifest. | Release packets include `SHA256SUMS`, retention days, source paths, public/private classification, and archive location outside ephemeral logs. |
| `W-DEPLOY-V4-024` | P0 | `29.6` | Issue the node `29` V4 production certificate. | The certificate can only say `production_closed` when the V4 packet is complete, current, secret-safe, retained, and matches production. |

##### Node 29 V4 Redline

Kolm cannot be called production complete until:

1. the current deployment has a V4 production evidence packet;
2. `vercel.json`, `.vercel/project.json`, `.vercelignore`, `railway.toml`, `Dockerfile`, `server.js`, `src/env.js`, generated artifacts, status files, and smoke scripts are all classified in that packet;
3. no deploy-relevant dirty file is unowned, including `vercel.json`, `public/nav.js`, `src/intent.js`, and this master blueprint;
4. Vercel/static, Railway/Express, Docker/self-host, preview, and local proofs are not confused with each other;
5. public and authenticated production smoke pass against the exact domain and deploy identity that users hit;
6. `/health`, `/ready`, cloud readiness, object storage readiness, billing, auth, account, docs, and product graph routes are probed in production;
7. production screenshots are run after final frontend/account/nav/media changes and tied to the deploy ID;
8. env profile drift, legacy pricing vars, malformed comments, and stale generated outputs are either fixed or explicitly scoped with owner and date;
9. telemetry can filter logs, traces, metrics, and frontend events by the exact release;
10. rollback target, rollback caveats, and smoke-after-rollback are documented or drilled;
11. status/RSS/customer communication decisions are attached; and
12. the release evidence survives CI log expiry and can be audited without reading chat history.

If any of these fail, node `29` remains open even if the site is reachable.

### Design System, Brand Execution, Visual QA, And Interaction Polish Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not patch CSS, nav, account pages, screenshots, videos, or media assets directly. It defines the master execution spec that those implementation lanes must satisfy before Kolm can be called visually state-of-the-art instead of merely rendered without crashes.

Node `12` already owns the broad frontend design system and UX branch. Node `30` is the stricter execution layer: it turns the user's exact failure modes into enforceable product rules. It covers nav underline/highlight drift, button sizing, color discipline, brand cohesion, typography, spacing, copy density, media quality, demo/video credibility, account post-auth ergonomics, and proof that every public/account/docs surface has the same system rather than a pile of page-specific fixes.

#### Why Node 30 Exists

The site cannot win on product substance if the interface makes buyers work to decode the category. Kolm is already structurally complex: API routing, .kolm artifacts, distillation, compilation, deployment to devices, readiness, account controls, docs, pricing, enterprise, trust, and research. That complexity must be made visually calm and operationally obvious.

The current repository shows real design investment, but also signs of design-system drift:

- `public/design-tokens.css` declares a token system and a cool slate monochrome v3 direction.
- `public/ks.css` claims to be the single design-system source of truth, imports a font from `fonts.bunny.net`, and remaps many legacy aliases.
- `public/design-system.html` still loads `design-tokens.css`, `wf01-components.css`, and `warm-paper.css`, and its metadata contains a mojibake title separator.
- `public/nav.js` injects runtime CSS guards for letter spacing, target size, mobile CTA shrinkage, and accessibility repair; those guards are useful as emergency protection but are not a substitute for correct source CSS.
- `public/nav.js` active-section logic has overlapping route ownership between product, solutions, models, docs, and enterprise paths, which creates a plausible source of underline/highlight inconsistency.
- The latest focused UI audit reports found failures on product-media presence for `/`, `/product`, and `/pricing`, plus warnings for missing shared CSS/nav assets and target-size issues on interactive controls.
- Large raster brand assets exist, including multi-megabyte hero backgrounds and generated brand PNGs, so the media system needs explicit budgets, dimensions, formats, preload/lazy policy, and real-product relevance.

Node `30` is the answer to "make this the best website for AI companies" at the system level: no component ships because it happens to look acceptable on one viewport. It ships only when the token source, component anatomy, state model, copy density, accessibility, performance, media, route ownership, screenshots, and production proof all agree.

#### External Design And Accessibility Baseline

| Source | Relevant standard for Kolm | Required interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| W3C WCAG 2.2, `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | WCAG 2.2 adds criteria including focus not obscured, target size minimum, consistent help, redundant entry, and accessible authentication. | Kolm's public, docs, pricing, account, enterprise, and demo flows target WCAG 2.2 AA as the minimum product bar, with AAA-level focus appearance where feasible. |
| WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide, `https://wai-aria-practices.netlify.app/aria-practices/` | Navigation, menus, menu buttons, dialogs, comboboxes, tabs, breadcrumbs, and meters need correct roles, labels, expanded state, focus movement, and keyboard behavior. | Kolm nav, mobile sheets, account menus, command palettes, pricing toggles, demo runners, dialogs, and readiness meters must follow APG patterns instead of ad hoc ARIA. |
| Google Core Web Vitals, `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Current core metrics are LCP, INP, and CLS; field data and real-user monitoring are recommended because lab-only proof is insufficient. | Kolm must attach lab and production evidence for hero media, nav JavaScript, account interactivity, layout stability, and route-level visual performance. |
| Apple Human Interface Guidelines accessibility, `https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/accessibility` | Interfaces should adapt to user accessibility preferences, avoid color-only meaning, support clear indicators, and break complex workflows into focused steps. | Kolm cannot rely on subtle color, hover, dense paragraphs, or hidden states; workflow-heavy account pages need visible progress, scope, undo/exit, and plain-language state. |
| Material accessibility guidance, `https://m2.material.io/design/usability/accessibility.html` | Material frames accessibility as a product-wide usability foundation and points to motion, contrast, touch, and inclusive component behavior. | Kolm's component system needs stable tap targets, reduced-motion support, semantic status color plus text/icon, and reusable component behavior, not per-page patching. |
| Nielsen Norman Group usability heuristics, `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/` | Visibility of status, real-world language, user control, consistency, error prevention, recognition over recall, efficiency, minimalism, recoverable errors, and contextual help remain the evaluation frame. | Kolm copy must speak buyer/operator language, expose system status, keep surfaces consistent, prevent destructive account mistakes, and remove nonessential paragraphs from first-screen flows. |

#### Current Repo Evidence Snapshot

| Evidence | Finding | Design-system implication |
|---|---|---|
| `public/design-tokens.css` | Token file exists and defines color, spacing, radius, shadow, motion, typography, z-index, and legacy aliases. | Keep tokens, but establish one canonical import path and prohibit raw component colors that bypass the system. |
| `public/ks.css` | Claims "ONE design system. ONE source of truth." | Either make `ks.css` truly canonical across generated pages or downgrade the claim and define the actual cascade graph. |
| `public/brand-refresh.css`, `public/surface-polish.css`, `public/warm-paper.css`, `public/wf01-components.css` | Multiple large CSS systems coexist. | Create a cascade ledger: canonical, transitional, deprecated, generated, page-local, and emergency runtime guard. |
| `public/nav.js` | Injects shared nav/chat/theme behavior, active-route mapping, and runtime UI guards. | Move layout/visual fixes into source CSS; keep JS for behavior, state, analytics, and accessibility orchestration only. |
| `public/design-system.html` | Uses mixed CSS sources and a mojibake title separator. | The design-system page must be the living contract, not a separate old showcase. |
| `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tfocused-polish-pass/report.md` | 4 audited routes failed with 24 product-media failures. | Media/demo/video are P0 acceptance criteria, not optional decoration. |
| `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account/report.md` | 36 failures and 44 warnings, including product-media failures, negative `h1` tracking, missing shared assets, and target-size warnings. | A clean all-route screenshot pass is necessary but not sufficient; manual-use route audits must also pass. |
| `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png` and generated brand PNGs | Hero/media assets can be large. | Establish image budgets, AVIF/WebP variants, intrinsic dimensions, preload policy, and real product-state relevance. |
| Account overview/readiness work from backend lane | Account can expose readiness, product graph, closeout, storage, and surface state. | UI must present this as an operator dashboard with dense, scannable, honest status rather than marketing cards. |

#### Non-Negotiable Design Redlines

- No runtime style guard may be the permanent source for typography, target size, spacing, theme, or nav visuals. Runtime guards can catch regressions, but source CSS must own the fix.
- No negative letter spacing ships anywhere if the design standard says zero tracking. This includes headings, nav, buttons, cards, pricing toggles, docs titles, and generated pages.
- No active nav state may depend on overlapping route regexes without deterministic ownership. Every route has one primary nav owner and optional secondary breadcrumb owner.
- No hover-only interaction may hide required information or controls. Menus, tooltips, popouts, dropdowns, command panels, and account actions must work by keyboard, pointer, and touch.
- No control below the target-size standard ships unless it is inline text and exempt by WCAG. Icon buttons, toggles, segmented controls, pills, copy buttons, pricing switches, and account actions are minimum 44 CSS pixels, preferably 48 for touch-heavy contexts.
- No hero, demo, video, or product-media slot ships as decorative filler. It must show real Kolm product state: an API capture, .kolm artifact, compile/distill result, device deployment, readiness proof, or account workflow.
- No video/demo element ships without dark/light loading proof, poster/fallback, dimensions, keyboard access, caption/transcript path if spoken, and failure state.
- No page gets a one-off palette. Color, contrast, theme, status semantics, focus rings, chart colors, and syntax colors come from tokens.
- No first viewport should carry paragraphs that require a technical reader to infer the product. The first screen says what Kolm is, who it is for, and which of the three core surfaces it activates.
- No account page ships without loading, empty, error, partial, blocked, and next-action states.
- No component can be called done if it only passes desktop dark mode. Done means dark/light, desktop/mobile, keyboard, touch, reduced motion, screen reader names, and production rendering.

#### Three-Surface Brand Architecture

The homepage and global product language must not collapse Kolm into one API gateway line. Kolm has three top-level product surfaces that must each be visible above the fold or immediately below it:

| Surface | Buyer-language outcome | Product proof required |
|---|---|---|
| Capture and own model traffic | Convert existing OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini/OpenRouter calls into governed .kolm artifacts with routing, cost control, provenance, and replay. | Live demo runner, before/after code, artifact JSON or signed receipt, savings calculator, provider readiness, account capture state. |
| Compile and distill top models | Turn frontier behavior into smaller, cheaper, task-specific students with evals, K-Score, trace/curriculum/teacher council, quantization, and regression gates. | Distillation workflow, benchmark scope, K-Score methodology, teacher/student comparison, failure-mode dashboard, open closeout gates for public benchmark data. |
| Run enterprise models anywhere | Deploy signed artifacts to cloud, BYOC, edge, mobile, browser, worker, local, or air-gapped runtime with audit and rollback proof. | Runtime matrix, device/cloud readiness, storage readiness, deployment proof, account fleet view, BYOC/self-host docs, certification scope. |

The hero may use one concise umbrella line, but the page architecture must make these three surfaces impossible to miss. The user should not have to decode "owned AI" or "model traffic" as shorthand for the full product. Each surface needs one outcome, one proof artifact, one visual, one CTA, and one honest readiness state.

#### Canonical UI Evidence Packet

Every route group should be able to emit or link a design evidence packet shaped like this:

```json
{
  "route": "/pricing",
  "surface_owner": "marketing-pricing",
  "design_system_version": "ks-v3",
  "canonical_css": ["public/ks.css", "public/design-tokens.css"],
  "deprecated_css_loaded": [],
  "runtime_guards_triggered": [],
  "nav": {
    "primary_owner": "pricing",
    "active_state": "pricing",
    "breadcrumbs": ["Home", "Pricing"],
    "keyboard_menu_passed": true,
    "touch_menu_passed": true
  },
  "tokens": {
    "raw_hex_in_component_css": 0,
    "negative_letter_spacing": 0,
    "contrast_failures": 0,
    "focus_visible": true,
    "target_size_failures": 0
  },
  "content": {
    "first_viewport_answers": ["what", "who", "why_now"],
    "primary_cta_count": 1,
    "paragraphs_above_fold": 0,
    "jargon_terms_unexplained": 0,
    "pricing_contract_current": true
  },
  "media": {
    "required_slots": ["hero-product-proof", "demo", "video-or-fallback"],
    "slots_rendered": 3,
    "real_product_state": true,
    "dark_light_loaded": true,
    "intrinsic_dimensions": true,
    "largest_asset_bytes": 180000,
    "lazy_policy": "hero-preload-rest-lazy"
  },
  "accessibility": {
    "wcag_target": "2.2 AA",
    "keyboard_path_passed": true,
    "screen_reader_names_present": true,
    "forms_labeled": true,
    "reduced_motion_supported": true,
    "accessible_authentication": "not_applicable|passed"
  },
  "responsive": {
    "viewports": ["desktop", "mobile"],
    "horizontal_scroll": false,
    "text_overlap": false,
    "tap_targets": "passed"
  },
  "performance": {
    "lcp_ms_lab": 1800,
    "inp_ms_lab": 120,
    "cls_lab": 0.02,
    "field_data_attached": false,
    "third_party_blocking_ms": 0
  },
  "audits": {
    "screenshot_report": "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/report.md",
    "manual_use_report": "reports/ui-surface-audit/manual-latest/report.md",
    "axe_report": "reports/accessibility/latest.json",
    "visual_diff_report": "reports/visual-diff/latest.json"
  },
  "production": {
    "domain": "https://kolm.ai",
    "commit": "git-sha",
    "checked_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "passed": true
  }
}
```

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `30.1` Design-token source of truth, CSS cascade, typography, color, spacing, radius, elevation, and motion | One canonical design-system cascade exists; every CSS file is classified as canonical, generated, transitional, deprecated, or page-local; raw colors and negative tracking are eliminated or justified; typography, spacing, radius, shadow, motion, z-index, syntax colors, chart colors, and status colors come from tokens; dark/light themes meet contrast requirements. |
| `30.2` Navigation, active states, headers, footers, mega menus, mobile sheets, links, and focus behavior | Every route has a deterministic nav owner; active underline/highlight states are tested; desktop and mobile nav use APG-compatible state and keyboard behavior; popouts do not clip, flicker, or depend on hover; skip links, breadcrumbs, focus order, and current-page state are correct. |
| `30.3` Buttons, forms, tables, cards, modals, toasts, status tokens, controls, and interaction states | Components have anatomy specs, token mappings, target size, keyboard/touch states, loading/disabled/error/success states, reduced-motion behavior, labels, ARIA names, density variants, and screenshot fixtures; no page ships custom button/card/table styling without an approved variant. |
| `30.4` Media, demo widgets, video, screenshots, product visualization, hero assets, and performance budgets | Every media slot renders real Kolm product state, has dark/light proof, dimensions, alt text or accessible name, fallback state, asset budget, loading policy, and product-relevance owner; live demos work without account friction where promised and fail gracefully when backend/env is unavailable. |
| `30.5` Page templates for marketing, docs, account, pricing, enterprise, marketplace, status, and research | Each page family has a template contract for first viewport, primary CTA, secondary CTA, proof object, content density, section rhythm, nav/breadcrumb owner, metadata, footer, account state, and route-specific do-not-say copy. |
| `30.6` UI evidence: screenshot audits, accessibility, responsive, theme, visual diff, and production proof | All local and production critical routes pass screenshot audits across dark/light desktop/mobile; manual-use audits pass for homepage, product, pricing, docs, account, enterprise, demo, and status; accessibility and performance reports are attached; production evidence uses the deployed domain and current commit. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DESIGN-001` | `30.1` | Build the CSS cascade ledger. | `docs/design-css-cascade.json` lists every CSS file, import order, owner, status, route usage, token dependency, deprecation plan, and allowed selectors. |
| `W-DESIGN-002` | `30.1` | Declare one canonical token source. | `public/ks.css`, `public/design-tokens.css`, generated pages, account pages, docs pages, and design-system page agree on canonical tokens and aliases. |
| `W-DESIGN-003` | `30.1` | Add raw-style audits. | CI fails on unapproved raw hex, negative letter spacing, one-off font families, un-tokened shadows, un-tokened radii, and inconsistent focus ring colors. |
| `W-DESIGN-004` | `30.1` | Define typography rules by context. | Hero, section, card, table, nav, button, docs, code, account, chart, toast, modal, and footer type scales are documented and screenshot-tested. |
| `W-DESIGN-005` | `30.2` | Build deterministic route-to-nav ownership. | Every route maps to exactly one primary nav owner and zero conflicting active-state regexes. |
| `W-DESIGN-006` | `30.2` | Rebuild nav and popout proof. | Desktop nav, mobile nav, dropdowns, command surfaces, theme toggle, account menus, breadcrumbs, and footer links pass keyboard, touch, focus, and screenshot checks. |
| `W-DESIGN-007` | `30.2` | Remove permanent visual fixes from JS guards. | `public/nav.js` keeps behavior and regression detection, while source CSS owns spacing, tracking, target size, theme, and component visual state. |
| `W-DESIGN-008` | `30.3` | Create component anatomy specs. | Buttons, links, pills, toggles, segmented controls, cards, tables, forms, modals, toasts, tabs, accordions, meters, and status chips have documented variants and states. |
| `W-DESIGN-009` | `30.3` | Enforce target-size and focus gates. | All clickable controls in public, docs, account, pricing, enterprise, and demo routes meet target-size and visible-focus requirements or have explicit inline-text exemptions. |
| `W-DESIGN-010` | `30.3` | Normalize form and error behavior. | Inputs, selects, textareas, checkboxes, radios, file pickers, API key fields, demo paste areas, billing forms, and enterprise inquiry forms have labels, hints, errors, loading, and recovery. |
| `W-DESIGN-011` | `30.4` | Replace decorative media with product proof. | Homepage, product, pricing, docs, enterprise, account, compile, distill, runtime, and trust pages show real product state instead of generic abstract assets. |
| `W-DESIGN-012` | `30.4` | Add media performance budgets. | Hero images, generated PNGs, videos, posters, demos, screenshots, and docs illustrations have size budgets, WebP/AVIF variants where appropriate, dimensions, preload/lazy rules, and cache policy. |
| `W-DESIGN-013` | `30.4` | Certify demo and video elements. | Live runner, ROI calculator, side-by-side code comparison, product videos, screenshots, and fallbacks render in dark/light, desktop/mobile, logged-out/logged-in states. |
| `W-DESIGN-014` | `30.5` | Rewrite page-template contracts. | Marketing, docs, account, pricing, enterprise, marketplace, status, blog/research, benchmarks, and trust pages each have one purpose, first-screen contract, CTA model, and proof object. |
| `W-DESIGN-015` | `30.5` | Align copy density to buyer tasks. | First screens avoid long paragraphs, unexplained jargon, and vague slogans; each surface uses outcome, proof, action, scope, and next step. |
| `W-DESIGN-016` | `30.5` | Build post-auth account UX matrix. | Every account route exposes real data, empty/loading/error/partial states, readiness closeouts, billing/storage/provider state, and next action without marketing filler. |
| `W-DESIGN-017` | `30.6` | Require all-route screenshot evidence. | Dark/light desktop/mobile all-route audits pass with no failures/warnings on local and production after the final commit. |
| `W-DESIGN-018` | `30.6` | Add manual-use route audits. | Homepage, product, pricing, demo, docs, account overview, account storage, enterprise, status, and trust are manually walked with keyboard, pointer, and mobile/touch simulation. |
| `W-DESIGN-019` | `30.6` | Attach accessibility and performance reports. | Axe or equivalent accessibility reports, Core Web Vitals lab reports, route-level bundle/media budgets, and reduced-motion checks are archived under `reports/`. |
| `W-DESIGN-020` | `30.6` | Create production design evidence archive. | Every deploy writes `reports/ui-release/<timestamp>/` with commit, route list, screenshots, visual diffs, accessibility, performance, media, nav, account, and production domain proof. |

#### Node 30 Relax Redline

Node `30` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current code and production evidence:

- Which file is the design-system source of truth, and which CSS files are canonical, transitional, deprecated, generated, or page-local?
- Can every route explain its primary nav owner, active state, breadcrumbs, footer placement, and account context?
- Are nav underlines, popouts, focus rings, mobile sheets, and dropdowns deterministic across dark/light and desktop/mobile?
- Are all buttons, links, controls, toggles, pills, cards, forms, tables, modals, toasts, and meters tokenized and component-owned?
- Does every first viewport say the outcome, audience, and urgency without paragraphs of jargon?
- Are Kolm's three surfaces visible and balanced: capture/own model traffic, compile/distill top models, and run enterprise models anywhere?
- Do hero media, demo widgets, videos, code comparisons, ROI calculators, and screenshots render real product state rather than decorative abstractions?
- Do all media and demo/video elements work in light mode, dark mode, desktop, mobile, production, and fallback/error state?
- Do account post-auth pages show operational truth: data, readiness, billing, storage, provider state, closeout gates, next actions, and no hidden blockers?
- Do local and production screenshot, manual-use, accessibility, responsive, theme, visual-diff, and performance reports all pass on the same commit?

The relax condition for node `30` is not "the site looks better." It is: the design-system cascade, token contract, nav ownership, component states, page templates, product media, account UX, accessibility, performance, screenshots, manual-use audits, and production evidence all prove one cohesive Kolm interface.

#### Node 30 V2 AI-Company Website, Visual Build Redline, And Product UX Addendum

This addendum turns the user's website critique into a binding implementation spec. It does not edit frontend files in this lane. It tells the frontend lane exactly what must be true before Kolm can be called the best website for an AI company: the site must explain the three-product-surface company in one glance, show real product proof instead of decorative media, use one design system, remove jargon and paragraph sprawl, make nav and buttons feel intentional, make demos and videos load in both themes, make post-auth account pages operational, and prove every claim with screenshots, accessibility, performance, and production evidence.

| Evidence | Current value observed in this pass | Design redline |
|---|---:|---|

Every CSS file must be classified as canonical, transitional, deprecated, generated, or page-local, and route usage must be known. |
| Raw color drift | Raw hex counts: `ks.css` 53, `design-tokens.css` 118, `brand-refresh.css` 587, `surface-polish.css` 233, `warm-paper.css` 163, `wf01-components.css` 0. | Tokens may contain raw colors; component CSS should not. Raw hex outside token files needs an allowlist or migration. |

Hidden test anchors and legacy copy must not control the visible product story or confuse SEO/assistive tech. |

Run anywhere.`, and `OpenAI-compatible gateway`. | Hero must name the category and three outcomes without vague slogans. Hidden contract text must not preserve stale or weaker positioning as the canonical truth. |
| Three-surface section | Homepage has a `Three surfaces. One .kolm file at the end.` section with Route/Capture, Distill/Compile, Run/Govern. | This is structurally right, but it must be elevated into the page information architecture, nav, product pages, screenshots, demo, pricing, docs, and account onboarding. |

All failures and target warnings must be cleared in the implementation lane and retested after production deploy. |

The master spec needs a nav ownership map and an audit that proves no active underline/popout/button drift. |

##### Node 30 V2 External Standards Bound To This Addendum

| Standard or reference | URL | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 new criteria | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/new-in-22/` | Kolm must satisfy focus not obscured, target size minimum, dragging alternatives, consistent help, redundant entry, and accessible authentication on public, demo, pricing, and account surfaces. |
| WAI-ARIA menu button pattern | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menu-button/` | Menus and nav popouts must set expanded/haspopup/controls state, move focus predictably on Enter/Space, and expose real menu behavior instead of hover-only visual dropdowns. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | LCP target is 2.5s or less, INP 200ms or less, CLS 0.1 or less at the 75th percentile across mobile/desktop, with lab plus field/RUM evidence where possible. |
| NN/g usability heuristics | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/` | Interfaces must expose system status, match user language, stay consistent, prevent errors, minimize irrelevant information, and show concise task-focused help. |
| ui-ux-pro-max skill | `C:/Users/user/.agents/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md` | Apply accessibility first, 44 px target minimum, 8 px touch spacing, no hover-only required controls, reserved media dimensions, reduced motion, visible labels, predictable navigation, semantic tokens, and performance budgets. |

##### Node 30 V2 Website Product Story Contract

Kolm cannot lead with one narrow product surface. The site has to teach the category in this order:

```json
{
  "website_story_contract_version": "2026-05-25.node30.v2",
  "category": "open-source AI compiler",
  "umbrella_outcome": "compile AI workloads into owned, signed, portable artifacts",
  "surfaces": [
    {
      "id": "route-capture",
      "plain_language": "Route existing model calls and capture the behavior worth keeping.",
      "proof": ["OpenAI-compatible code swap", "live capture/demo runner", ".kolm artifact preview", "provider readiness", "ROI calculator"],
      "avoid": ["one AI API as the whole company", "model traffic as unexplained jargon"]
    },
    {
      "id": "distill-compile",
      "plain_language": "Distill frontier behavior into smaller specialist models with evals and K-Score gates.",
      "proof": ["teacher/student comparison", "strategy picker", "K-Score report", "failure-mode dashboard", "benchmark scope"],
      "avoid": ["fine-tuning alternative as the only frame", "unproven benchmark claims"]
    },
    {
      "id": "run-govern",
      "plain_language": "Run signed artifacts across cloud, BYOC, edge, mobile, local, browser, and air-gapped targets.",
      "proof": ["runtime matrix", "device/fleet account view", "storage readiness", "deployment receipt", "audit/export proof"],
      "avoid": ["run anywhere without runtime proof", "enterprise claims without readiness scope"]
    }
  ],
  "hero_requirements": {
    "what": "Kolm is an open-source AI compiler.",
    "who": "For AI teams that need to reduce model cost, distill specialist models, and deploy governed models outside one vendor.",
    "why_now": "Frontier API usage is expensive, hard to audit, and hard to move; Kolm turns production behavior into portable artifacts.",
    "max_visible_paragraphs": 1,
    "required_visual": "real product proof, not decorative abstraction",
    "required_ctas": ["Compile a .kolm", "See how it works"],
    "required_below_fold_hint": true
  }
}
```

The hero can be short, but it cannot be cryptic. `Turn model traffic into owned AI` is not enough because it only fits one buyer interpretation and hides distillation and run-anywhere. The preferred structure is category plus concrete outcomes:

- H1 pattern: `The open-source AI compiler for models you own.`
- Support pattern: `Route existing API calls, distill specialist models, and run signed .kolm artifacts on cloud, edge, mobile, or your own infrastructure.`
- Proof rail pattern: `OpenAI-compatible swap`, `K-Score gated distill`, `signed .kolm artifact`, `runs after compile with no per-token bill`.

The implementation lane may choose better words, but it must preserve the three-surface truth and eliminate vague terms that require prior Kolm knowledge.

##### Node 30 V2 Visual System Contract

```json
{
  "design_system_contract_version": "2026-05-25.node30.v2",
  "canonical_sources": {
    "tokens": "public/design-tokens.css|public/ks.css",
    "components": "docs/design-components.json",
    "route_templates": "docs/design-route-templates.json",
    "nav_map": "docs/design-nav-map.json",
    "media_manifest": "docs/design-media-proof.json"
  },
  "hard_failures": [
    "negative_visible_letter_spacing",
    "raw_component_color_without_token",
    "hover_only_required_control",
    "button_or_control_target_under_44px_without_inline_text_exemption",
    "hero_or_required_product_media_missing",
    "video_or_demo_not_loading_in_dark_or_light",
    "nav_active_state_conflict",
    "menu_popout_without_keyboard_focus_management",
    "account_route_missing_loading_empty_error_partial_state",
    "desktop_only_or_dark_only_proof",
    "production_screenshot_missing_after_deploy"
  ],
  "route_classes": {
    "marketing": ["home", "product", "pricing", "enterprise", "verticals", "compare"],
    "developer": ["docs", "quickstart", "api", "sdk", "integrations", "cookbook"],
    "operator": ["account", "admin", "status", "storage", "billing", "captures", "artifacts"],
    "proof": ["benchmarks", "security", "trust", "spec", "registry", "case-studies"]
  },
  "evidence_required": ["screenshot", "manual_use", "keyboard", "touch", "a11y", "core_web_vitals", "claim_scope", "prod_domain"]
}
```

##### Node 30 V2 Page Family Requirements

| Page family | Required first-screen job | Required proof | Forbidden pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | Teach the three-surface category in under 5 seconds. | Product visual, live or simulated demo, proof rail, concise CTA pair, next-section hint. | Abstract slogans, multiple paragraphs, generic gradients, or hero media that does not show Kolm state. |
| Product | Show how capture, distill/compile, and run/govern fit as one workflow. | Workflow diagram, before/after code, artifact anatomy, readiness scope. | Feature piles with no outcome or artifact proof. |
| Pricing | Make Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise custom obvious and honest. | Plan table, ROI calculator, contact sales for Enterprise, claim-scope notes. | Hidden enterprise pricing, self-serve enterprise conflict, or dense caveat paragraphs above action. |
| Enterprise | Prove governance, deployment, security, identity, procurement, and support. | SSO/SCIM/readiness, storage, audit export, BAA/DPA status, sales SLA, proof packet. | Certification or run-anywhere claims that exceed the closeout ledger. |
| Docs | Help a developer get first value fast. | Quickstart, SDK/API, CLI, recipes, copyable commands, version and auth state. | Long conceptual pages before a working path. |
| Account overview | Give an operator the product matrix state. | Readiness counts, storage provider, captures, distill runs, artifacts, billing, API key trust, next actions. | Marketing cards, hidden blockers, or no empty/error/partial state. |
| Demo runner | Produce an aha moment without signup when promised. | Paste OpenAI call, emit .kolm preview/artifact, show cost/proof, fail safely when backend is unavailable. | Fake terminal, dead video, or light-mode invisible demo. |
| Status/trust | Show current truth and evidence. | Live probes or static-scope label, incidents, RSS, security/compliance scope, audit exports. | "All systems operational" without data source. |

##### Node 30 V2 Relax Redline

Node `30` stays open until the live site proves all of the following:

- A 5-second visitor understands that Kolm routes existing model calls, distills/compiles specialist models, and runs signed artifacts anywhere the enterprise needs them.
- Homepage/product/pricing/enterprise/docs/account pages look like one system, not layered repairs from many waves.
- Nav underline/highlight/popout behavior is deterministic and keyboard/touch safe.
- Buttons, toggles, presets, copy controls, demo controls, account actions, and pricing controls meet target-size, focus, disabled, loading, and error standards.
- Product media is real, visible, relevant, and performant in dark/light desktop/mobile.
- Video/demo/ROI/code comparison elements are not placeholders and do not disappear in light mode.
- Post-auth account pages cover the product matrix with loading, empty, partial, blocked, error, ready, and next-action states.
- Accessibility and Core Web Vitals are part of the evidence packet, not manually asserted.
- Production screenshots and manual-use audits are run after deploy on the same commit customers see.

If any item is missing, the site may be improving, but it is not done.

#### Node 30 V3 Product-Experience Implementation Plane, Cascade Burn-Down, And Account Matrix UX Addendum

Node `52` stays unused. V2 defined the website story and visual system contract. V3 turns the current codebase evidence into an implementation control plane: the site cannot be "best in the world for AI companies" while product truth, visual hierarchy, navigation, CSS authority, product media, account UX, accessibility, and production proof are distributed across hidden anchors, runtime guards, broad CSS patches, and manual memory.

This addendum is still doc-only in this lane. It does not edit `public/nav.js`, CSS, HTML, account pages, or assets while the frontend bot is active. It defines the finish line those edits must satisfy and the evidence packet that proves they did.

##### Node 30 V3 Current Evidence Delta

Current tree evidence inspected for this addendum:

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| Public CSS footprint | 19 top-level public CSS files, 779,198 bytes. Largest layers: `brand-refresh.css` 169,200 bytes, `surface-polish.css` 126,141, `warm-paper.css` 83,097, `styles.css` 76,894, `frontier.css` 55,666, `ks.css` 49,262, `home-refresh.css` 48,970. | The design system is still a cascade stack, not a single source of truth. V3 needs route-level CSS ownership and a retirement path for broad patch layers. |

| Runtime visual guard | `public/nav.js` is 49,237 bytes / 874 lines and injects a `kolm-surface-guard` style block with `!important` rules for heading tracking, nav sizing, mega-menu links, theme toggle, mobile CTA labels, and shared controls. | Runtime guards may be temporary safety nets, but they cannot be the design system. V3 requires each JS-injected visual rule to be moved into governed CSS or retained with an owner and expiry. |
| Nav behavior complexity | `public/nav.js` owns active-route regexes, mega-menu open/close timing, focus handling, theme toggle, auth pill, CTA normalization, local key cleanup, copy buttons, control repair, and mobile nav. | Navigation needs a formal state machine and route ownership map. Regex drift or hidden JS mutations are not acceptable as the long-term nav contract. |
| Style debt counters | Current PowerShell scan across public HTML/CSS/JS found 4,560 `!important` matches, 1,138 negative `letter-spacing` matches, 11,822 raw hex matches, 1 raw `rgb(` match, and no `hsl(` matches. | V3 must set numeric burn-down budgets and exception ledgers. "Looks better" is not enough when the cascade remains unbounded. |

Visible H1 says `Compile your AI workload into a model you own.` Hidden/test anchors still include `owned AI`, `Route any model`, `Distill specialists`, `Run on any device`, and `Route models. Distill specialists. Run anywhere.` | The visible direction is better than "turn model traffic," but hidden anchors and SEO/test anchors must not preserve stale or ambiguous category language as product truth. |

It also found `$149.70` in regional pricing, which is a regional Team price rather than the old stale `$149` Teams plan. | V3 must distinguish real stale pricing copy from numeric false positives and keep pricing/enterprise CTAs consistent with backend plan contracts. |
| Product media and demos | Existing spec evidence already records product-media failures and demo/video problems from focused audits; the current source tree still includes broad product-media and demo responsibilities spread across homepage, studio, chat, CSS, and JS. | Media cannot be decorative or hidden. Each product surface needs a real visual proof asset with dark/light, reduced-motion, loading, error, transcript/caption, and production screenshot evidence. |
| Account overview | `public/account/overview.html` is 36,970 bytes / 468 lines and is part of the current route family. | Post-auth UX must become the operational cockpit for route/capture, distill/compile, run/govern, storage, billing, readiness, closeout, and next actions. |
| Search tool limitation | `rg` is unavailable in this shell, so current scans used PowerShell `Select-String`. | Spec evidence must not depend on one local tool. Future audit scripts should run with Node or PowerShell fallbacks. |

##### Node 30 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| Baseline | Current source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C WCAG 2.2 is a W3C Recommendation dated 2024-12-12 and includes focus, target size, hover/focus content, consistent navigation, consistent identification, redundant entry, and accessible authentication criteria: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/ | Public, docs, demo, pricing, auth, and account routes must meet WCAG 2.2 AA-oriented evidence unless an explicit exception is scoped. |
| Core Web Vitals | web.dev states good thresholds of LCP <= 2.5s, INP <= 200ms, CLS <= 0.1 at the 75th percentile across mobile and desktop: https://web.dev/articles/vitals | Node `30` needs performance budgets by page family and production/RUM evidence when available. |
| INP responsiveness | web.dev defines good INP as 200 ms or less and poor over 500 ms at the 75th percentile: https://web.dev/articles/inp | Runtime nav guards, demos, account widgets, ROI calculators, and capture runners must prove responsive input handling. |
| WAI-ARIA menu button | W3C APG says menu buttons need open/expanded state and keyboard behavior for Enter, Space, and arrow keys: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/menu-button/ | Mega menus/popouts cannot be hover-only and must expose deterministic focus, escape, outside-click, and `aria-expanded` behavior. |
| Reduced motion | MDN documents `prefers-reduced-motion` as the browser signal for users who want non-essential motion minimized: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@media/prefers-reduced-motion | Product videos, demo transitions, hero motion, nav popouts, charts, and account loading states need reduced-motion behavior. |
| Image alt and media semantics | MDN documents `alt` as the text replacement for images and recommends meaningful alternate descriptions for non-decorative images: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/img | Product screenshots, diagrams, logos, proof media, and demo assets need useful alt/caption/transcript contracts. |

##### Node 30 V3 Product-Experience Packet Contract

Every visual/product-experience release must generate one packet:


##### Node 30 V3 Page-Family Acceptance Matrix

| Page family | Must be true | Acceptance proof |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage | First screen says what Kolm is, who it is for, why now, and all three product outcomes without paragraph sprawl. | Five-second copy review, hidden-anchor audit, screenshot, mobile screenshot, metadata/OG check. |
| Product pages | Capture, distill/compile, and run/govern are presented as one workflow that ends in a signed `.kolm` artifact. | Product workflow screenshot, before/after code, artifact proof, route-to-docs links. |
| Pricing pages | Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise custom are aligned across visible copy, CTAs, purchase order pages, regional pricing, backend plan contracts, and docs. | Pricing contract scan that distinguishes stale plan names from valid regional values. |
| Enterprise pages | Contact sales, governance, deployment, SSO/SCIM, security, procurement, and readiness scope are concrete. | Enterprise CTA scan, account/admin links, claim-scope gate, sales form smoke. |
| Docs pages | A developer can get first value without reading marketing prose first. | Quickstart path test, API/SDK nav proof, copyable commands, auth/key state proof. |
| Account pages | The signed-in user sees product matrix status and next actions. | Account screenshot and smoke for overview, storage, billing, captures, artifacts, distill runs, API keys, devices, readiness, closeout. |
| Demo/video/ROI | Interactive assets load in dark/light and are meaningful without signup when promised. | Media manifest, video transcript/caption, reduced-motion snapshot, error fallback, ROI calculation fixture. |
| Trust/status/proof | Trust pages and status pages expose scoped evidence rather than generic assurances. | Status/RSS proof, security/compliance scope check, readiness ledger link, production probe. |

##### Node 30 V3 Cascade Burn-Down Policy

| Debt class | Current signal | V3 target | Exception rule |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `!important` | 4,560 matches | 0 in source-owned component/page CSS, except allowlisted emergency guards with expiry | Each exception needs selector, owner, reason, expiry, screenshot, and deletion ticket. |
| Negative `letter-spacing` | 1,138 matches | 0 visible text occurrences, except logo/brand asset text if proven non-content | A runtime guard that overwrites negative tracking is not enough; source must stop emitting it. |
| Raw hex colors | 11,822 matches | Token files and generated assets only, with component CSS using semantic vars | Each non-token raw color needs contrast proof and route owner. |
| Runtime visual guards | `public/nav.js` injects broad CSS guard rules | 0 permanent visual rules in JS | JS may orchestrate state; styles live in governed CSS. Temporary guards require expiry. |
| Hidden/test copy anchors | Homepage hidden H1 and test anchors include older broad language | 0 stale hidden copy that conflicts with visible product truth | Tests should pin semantic contract fixtures, not invisible marketing copy. |
| Broad page-specific CSS | Multiple large patch CSS files coexist | Route-class ownership and deletion/migration sequence | Transitional layers must have route inventory and target deletion wave. |

##### Node 30 V3 Redline

Node `30` cannot relax until:

1. every current dirty frontend file is owned and either shipped, reverted by its owner, or intentionally excluded from the release packet;
2. the site has a generated cascade ledger and exception ledger;
3. `public/nav.js` no longer acts as the permanent source of visual truth;
4. visible copy, hidden anchors, SEO metadata, tests, screenshots, and page contracts agree on the three-surface Kolm story;
5. stale pricing terms are fixed or explicitly classified as valid legacy/regional/accounting language;
6. every page family has first-screen, proof, CTA, media, accessibility, and performance acceptance criteria;
7. account pages prove product-matrix operations rather than only marketing status;
8. product media, demos, videos, and ROI widgets work in dark/light, desktop/mobile, no-auth/auth, loading/error/success, and reduced-motion states;
9. nav active states and popouts are deterministic across pointer, keyboard, touch, and mobile;
10. WCAG 2.2-oriented accessibility evidence and Core Web Vitals budgets are attached; and
11. final screenshots and manual-use proof run against the same deployed production build certified by node `29`.

If any item is missing, Kolm may look better, but the product experience is not complete.

### Wave Roadmap, Invention Backlog, Agent Coordination, And Execution Governance Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not edit implementation files, generated product graphs, API routes, CSS, CLI, tests, or deployment config. It defines the governance system that keeps the roadmap additive while multiple agents are building frontend, backend, research, CLI, and production-readiness work in parallel.

Node `31` exists because Kolm now has more product ambition than a human can safely hold in one chat window. The repo contains a W682-W831 frontend launch plan, a W707-W806 system upgrade plan, W807-W835 tier queues from the product roadmap, W593-W606 invention/frontier implementation contracts, and many generated JSON contracts. Without a master wave governance layer, "never stop" turns into duplicated waves, stale plans, broken generated artifacts, concurrent file collisions, and optimistic "done" claims that do not survive production proof.

#### Why Node 31 Exists

Kolm should keep moving fast, but the motion must be ordered. The current workstreams are high value:

- `KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md` is a 150-wave frontend/site launch plan with positioning, page consolidation, color discipline, and ship rules.
- `KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md` is a system-wide W707-W806 upgrade plan with 114 external-review recommendations, "already implemented" evidence, partial items, and not-implemented waves.
- The user roadmap adds W807-W835, including confidence-aware routing, capture poisoning detection, structured output validation, K-Score calibration, active learning, A/B testing, ecosystem loaders, marketplace, memory scheduling, compliance toolkit, and savings-based pricing.
- `docs/product-invention-portfolio.json` tracks 12 invention items and 5 implementation rules.
- `docs/product-invention-implementation-spec.json` tracks 14 inventions, 33 research sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-invention-buildbook.json` tracks 14 inventions, 13 categories, 28 research sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-frontier-map.json` tracks 16 programs, 15 competitors, 12 capability axes, 31 sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-math-frontier.json` tracks 14 inventions, 17 math primitives, 10 categories, 34 sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-frontier-lab.json` tracks 15 experiments, 14 categories, 36 sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json` tracks 15 implementation contracts, 12 implementation research sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `docs/product-frontier-operator-kernels.json` tracks 12 operator kernels, 12 categories, 23 sources, and 9 tracked metrics.
- `package.json` now has a dense verification mesh: `verify:inventions`, `verify:frontier-map`, `verify:math-frontier`, `verify:frontier-lab`, `verify:frontier-contracts`, `verify:operator-kernels`, `verify:readiness-workorders`, `verify:depth`, `release:verify`, local/prod surface smoke, screenshot audits, and package/compliance/benchmark gates.

The problem is no longer lack of ideas. The problem is turning the ideas into a sequenced, conflict-free, evidence-backed execution system where each wave either lands, merges into a canonical successor, gets killed, or stays visibly external-gated.

#### External Governance Baseline

| Source | Relevant standard for Kolm | Required interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| RFC 2119, `https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc2119/` | Defines `MUST`, `MUST NOT`, `SHOULD`, `MAY`, and related requirement terms for specifications. | Kolm implementation contracts should use normative words only where necessary and attach evidence for every `MUST`. |
| RFC 8174, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc8174` | Clarifies that RFC 2119 requirement words carry special meaning only when uppercase. | Roadmap docs must distinguish normal prose from binding requirements; uppercase `MUST` words need owner, verifier, and proof path. |
| NASA Technology Readiness Levels, `https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/somd/space-communications-navigation-program/technology-readiness-levels/` | TRL is a nine-level maturity system; TRL 1 is lowest and TRL 9 highest. | Kolm inventions should not jump from research idea to "shipped"; they need maturity levels from paper/idea to local prototype, integrated product, production proof, and external adoption. |
| DORA metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | DORA tracks delivery throughput and instability through change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate, and warns against single-metric gaming. | Wave execution should track speed and stability together: small batch size, lead time, rollback/rework, post-ship defects, and product metric lift. |
| JSON Schema specification, `https://json-schema.org/specification` | Current JSON Schema version is 2020-12, split into Core and Validation, with meta-schemas for validation. | Every machine-readable wave, invention, readiness, frontier, and evidence packet should have a schema version and validator, not just freeform JSON. |

#### Current Roadmap Collision Map

| Roadmap family | Evidence | Collision risk | Required governance |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frontend/site launch waves | `KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md` covers W682-W831 with page consolidation, CSS color discipline, screenshot obligations, and launch posture. | Overlaps backend/user roadmap W807-W831 and contains older positioning locks that may conflict with the three-surface product narrative. | Convert to canonical page/template tickets under nodes `6`, `12`, `21`, `30`, and `31`; mark superseded positioning as historical, not binding. |
| System upgrade waves | `KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md` covers W707-W806, 114 recommendations, already-implemented items, partials, and not-implemented waves. | Some items were superseded by W807-W835 or by backend invention contracts; mojibake markers reduce plan trust. | Normalize wave statuses, merge duplicates into canonical successors, repair encoding before using as execution authority, and attach proof commands per wave. |
| User Tier 0/Tier 1/Tier 2/Tier 3/Tier 4 roadmap | User provided W807-W835 priorities and duplicate notes. | High-priority waves can be blocked by unfinished W720-W722 or duplicate earlier W709/W710/W727/W731/W732/W738/W747/W777/W778/W779/W791 items. | Maintain a `canonical_wave_id`, `supersedes`, `blocked_by`, `depends_on`, and `merge_into` field for every wave. |
| Invention portfolio | `docs/product-invention-portfolio.json`, `docs/product-invention-implementation-spec.json`, and `docs/product-invention-buildbook.json`. | Synthetic lift scores can be mistaken for public proof or implementation status. | Require maturity level, metric class, simulated result, implementation owner, real benchmark plan, and public-claim scope. |
| Frontier research contracts | `docs/product-frontier-map.json`, `docs/product-math-frontier.json`, `docs/product-frontier-lab.json`, `docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json`, `docs/product-frontier-operator-kernels.json`. | Research, experiment, contract, and kernel layers can drift if one is updated without the others. | Add cross-ledger referential integrity: every experiment maps to one contract, one owner file set, one smoke path, one product journey, and one metric lift target. |
| Parallel agents | Current user workflow has frontend, backend, research, CLI, and this spec lane active. | Concurrent edits can break generated artifacts, public files, CLI docs, or shared tests if agents overlap write sets. | Require write-set declarations, generated-artifact lock, serial regeneration, and final combined-tree verification before deploy. |

#### Canonical Wave State Machine

Every wave must have exactly one state:

| State | Meaning | Allowed next states |
|---|---|---|
| `candidate` | Valuable idea, not accepted into execution yet. | `accepted`, `rejected`, `merged` |
| `accepted` | Accepted into backlog with owner, node, scope, metrics, and proof. | `ready`, `blocked`, `merged`, `rejected` |
| `ready` | Dependencies, write set, acceptance tests, smoke, rollback, and evidence path are known. | `in_progress`, `blocked`, `merged` |
| `in_progress` | One owner or coordinated agent set is actively editing. | `review`, `blocked`, `split`, `reverted` |
| `review` | Code/docs/tests landed locally and await verification on combined tree. | `shipped_local`, `rework`, `reverted` |
| `shipped_local` | Local gates pass on current tree. | `deployed`, `rework`, `blocked_external` |
| `deployed` | Production deploy and production smoke/screenshots pass. | `observed`, `hotfix`, `rollback` |
| `observed` | Post-ship telemetry confirms no regression and expected metric movement. | `closed`, `rework` |
| `closed` | Wave is complete with evidence archived. | none |
| `blocked` | Internal blocker exists with owner and unblock action. | `ready`, `rejected`, `merged` |
| `blocked_external` | External proof, package release, certification, partner, or production access is required. | `deployed`, `closed_external`, `rejected` |
| `merged` | Wave is intentionally merged into a canonical successor. | none |
| `rejected` | Wave is not worth building or conflicts with product truth. | none |
| `split` | Wave is too large and was broken into child waves. | none |
| `reverted` | Wave was backed out with reason and rollback evidence. | `ready`, `rejected` |

No wave may be called done from `review`. "Tests green" is not enough unless the wave's own proof contract says local-only is the final state.

#### Wave Contract Schema

Each wave should have a machine-readable contract shaped like this:

```json
{
  "wave_id": "W807",
  "canonical_id": "W807-confidence-aware-adaptive-routing",
  "title": "Confidence-aware adaptive routing",
  "state": "ready",
  "master_nodes": ["2", "11", "15", "19", "31"],
  "product_surfaces": ["capture-own-model-traffic", "compile-distill-top-models", "run-enterprise-models-anywhere"],
  "supersedes": ["W709"],
  "merged_from": [],
  "duplicates": [],
  "depends_on": ["W720", "W722"],
  "blocked_by": [],
  "owner": {
    "lane": "backend",
    "agent": "single-owner-or-coordinated-pair",
    "reviewer": "release-owner"
  },
  "write_set": {
    "owned_paths": ["src/runtime-confidence-router.js", "tests/wave807-confidence-routing.test.js"],
    "read_only_paths": ["docs/product-surfaces.json", "public/product-graph.json"],
    "generated_paths": ["public/docs/api-routes.json", "public/openapi.json"],
    "conflict_paths": []
  },
  "requirements": [
    {
      "id": "W807-R1",
      "level": "MUST",
      "text": "Route low-confidence student generation to a configured teacher without leaking tenant data.",
      "evidence": ["unit-test", "integration-test", "account-screenshot", "trace-sample"]
    }
  ],
  "maturity": {
    "current": "TRL-4-local-component",
    "target": "TRL-7-production-pilot",
    "promotion_evidence": ["local smoke", "production smoke", "telemetry report"]
  },
  "metrics": {
    "primary": ["cost_per_successful_task", "teacher_fallback_rate"],
    "guardrails": ["latency_p95", "quality_regression", "privacy_violation_count", "error_rate"]
  },
  "acceptance": {
    "tests": ["node --test --test-concurrency=1 tests/wave807-confidence-routing.test.js"],
    "smoke": ["npm run local:surfaces:deep"],
    "generated_artifacts": ["node scripts/build-api-ref.cjs", "node scripts/build-openapi.cjs"],
    "release_gates": ["npm run verify:depth", "npm run release:verify"],
    "production_proof": ["npm run prod:surfaces:deep", "ui production screenshot audit"]
  },
  "rollback": {
    "required": true,
    "plan": "disable by feature flag and restore prior routing path",
    "data_migration_reversible": true
  },
  "public_claim_scope": {
    "may_market": false,
    "reason": "not production-observed yet",
    "copy_guard": "describe as beta or internal until production telemetry exists"
  }
}
```

#### Maturity Model For Inventions

Kolm needs a product-specific readiness ladder. NASA TRL is the inspiration, but Kolm should adapt it to AI infrastructure:

| Level | Name | Meaning | Public claim allowed |
|---|---|---|---|
| `KRL-1` | Research observed | Paper, competitor capability, or customer problem is documented. | No product claim. |
| `KRL-2` | Product hypothesis | Linked to Kolm surface, user, metric, and moat thesis. | Roadmap/research only. |
| `KRL-3` | Simulated algorithm | Synthetic simulator or local fixture shows plausible lift. | Internal prioritization only. |
| `KRL-4` | Local prototype | Isolated module or script exists with tests and fixtures. | Labs/preview only with scope. |
| `KRL-5` | Integrated local product | API/CLI/account/docs path exists and local smoke passes. | Beta if clearly labeled. |
| `KRL-6` | Production pilot | Deployed behind flag for safe tenant or internal account with telemetry. | Limited pilot claim. |
| `KRL-7` | Production observed | Live usage confirms quality, cost, latency, privacy, and reliability guardrails. | Product claim with dated evidence. |
| `KRL-8` | External benchmarked | Public benchmark, package, certification, or partner proof exists where relevant. | Strong claim with source/date. |
| `KRL-9` | Ecosystem standard | External users, integrations, or standards bodies adopt it independently. | Category leadership claim. |

Every invention, frontier experiment, operator kernel, and W807-W835 roadmap item must carry `current_krl`, `target_krl`, and `promotion_evidence`.

#### Agent Coordination Rules

- Every active implementation lane declares write ownership before editing shared surfaces.
- No two agents edit the same generated file simultaneously. API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, screenshots, and site audit reports regenerate serially.
- Broad public-file mechanical rewrites require a restore plan and post-rewrite syntax proof. The previous mojibake/HTML corruption incident becomes a standing caution in the wave contract.
- Research-only lanes may edit research docs and machine-readable research contracts, but do not edit source code unless explicitly reassigned.
- Frontend lanes own visual/page implementation and screenshots. Backend lanes own APIs, stores, product graph, contracts, and local/prod smoke. CLI lanes own CLI/TUI/docs. This spec lane owns the master completion document.
- A wave that touches pricing, auth, storage, product graph, generated docs, or public claim copy triggers combined-tree verification before deploy.
- A wave cannot close while another active agent has unmerged changes to the same ownership boundary.
- Every wave final note must list changed paths, verification commands, failed/skipped commands, external blockers, and whether public claims may change.

#### Prioritization Model

The roadmap must optimize the whole product, not the loudest local idea. Score every candidate wave with:

| Factor | Weight | Definition |
|---|---:|---|
| Product-surface coverage | 0.15 | Improves one or more of capture/own traffic, compile/distill, run anywhere. |
| Metric lift | 0.15 | Improves quality, latency, cost, portability, proof, security, enterprise, DX, or conversion. |
| User pain severity | 0.12 | Removes a blocker for signup, first value, deployment, trust, cost, reliability, or account operation. |
| Moat depth | 0.12 | Creates proprietary data, algorithms, artifacts, ecosystem effects, or trust evidence. |
| Verification strength | 0.10 | Can be proven by tests, smoke, screenshots, telemetry, benchmark, or production evidence. |
| Dependency unlock | 0.10 | Unblocks many later waves or removes a high-risk dependency. |
| Delivery risk | 0.08 | Smaller, reversible, well-scoped waves score higher. |
| Claim risk reduction | 0.08 | Reduces overclaim, legal exposure, readiness ambiguity, or customer trust gap. |
| Revenue path | 0.06 | Improves pricing, enterprise sales, activation, expansion, marketplace, or support cost. |
| Cross-agent conflict cost | 0.04 | Lower conflict with active lanes scores higher. |

Priority is not a vibes label. A Tier 0 item with weak proof, high collision risk, or missing dependency can be split or delayed; a lower-tier item that unlocks production proof can jump if the score and evidence justify it.

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `31.1` Roadmap inventory, wave lineage, supersession, duplicate merges, priority queues, and kill switches | Every wave from W593-W606, W682-W831, W707-W806, W807-W835, and future waves has canonical ID, status, owner, master node, supersedes/duplicates/merge fields, priority score, proof state, and public-claim scope. |
| `31.2` Invention, frontier, and research contracts with maturity levels, proof states, and implementation handoffs | Every invention/research/frontier item has KRL maturity, source evidence, implementation handoff, owner files, proposed files, metrics, acceptance tests, smoke paths, failure modes, and external proof gates. |
| `31.3` Agent ownership, write-set isolation, generated-artifact sequencing, branch hygiene, and merge discipline | Active agents cannot silently collide; generated artifacts regenerate serially; shared files have ownership boundaries; final verification runs on the combined tree before deploy. |
| `31.4` Atomic implementation packets, acceptance tests, smoke tests, rollback, and evidence archives | Every wave ships as an atomic packet with requirements, tests, smoke, generated artifacts, rollback, release notes, changed paths, and evidence archive. |
| `31.5` Progress telemetry, DORA/flow metrics, roadmap scoring, customer impact, and product-metric feedback loops | Roadmap execution tracks change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, rework rate, wave cycle time, post-ship defects, product metric lift, and customer impact. |
| `31.6` Public roadmap, changelog, release notes, deprecation policy, customer comms, and post-ship accountability | Public-facing roadmap and changelog distinguish shipped, beta, research, external-gated, deprecated, and removed features; customers receive accurate comms and claims do not outrun proof. |

#### Node 31 Relax Redline

Node `31` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current files and evidence:

- What is the canonical status of every known W593-W606, W682-W831, W707-W806, W807-W835, and future wave?
- Which waves are duplicates, which wave is canonical, and which documents are historical rather than binding?
- Which active agent owns each path, and can two agents collide on `src/router.js`, `cli/kolm.js`, `package.json`, generated docs, public pages, CSS, screenshots, or product graph?
- Does every invention have a KRL maturity state and a promotion path from research to local prototype, integrated product, production proof, external benchmark, and ecosystem adoption?
- Do synthetic lift scores stay internal until real benchmark or production telemetry exists?
- Can every wave show requirements, owner, write set, dependencies, acceptance tests, smoke, rollback, evidence archive, and public-claim scope?
- Do generated artifacts regenerate serially after implementation and before release verification?
- Does roadmap priority reflect product-surface coverage, metric lift, user pain, moat, proof strength, dependency unlock, risk, claim reduction, revenue path, and conflict cost?
- Does public roadmap/changelog copy distinguish shipped, beta, research, external-gated, deprecated, removed, and killed items?
- Is post-ship telemetry reviewed before a wave is finally closed?

The relax condition for node `31` is not "there is a roadmap." It is: every roadmap and invention item has a canonical state, owner, maturity level, proof path, conflict boundary, metric target, rollback path, and customer-facing truth state, and the combined tree proves each shipped wave rather than trusting agent memory.

#### Node 31 V2 Canonical Wave Registry, Agent Locks, And Production-Close Governance Addendum

This addendum turns node `31` into the operating system for the master spec tree. The rule is simple: every roadmap item, invention, frontend wave, backend wave, CLI/TUI wave, research contract, generated artifact, and external proof gate must have one canonical record. Chat transcripts, agent final messages, and local green tests are useful evidence, but they are not the source of truth.

The master spec tree must stay stable while many agents work at once. That means new work attaches to an existing node whenever that node already owns the problem. A new top-level node is allowed only when no existing branch can own the requirement without ambiguity. Node `52` remains unused until there is a truly new domain that cannot be represented by nodes `1` through `51`.

##### Current Wave And Roadmap Evidence Delta

| Evidence path | Current signal | Master-tree interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| `KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md` | Large wave plan covering W707-W835, with W720-W722 ship notes, W829/W830 ship notes, duplicate notes, Tier 0 through Tier 4 queues, and visible non-ASCII status glyphs in PowerShell output. | This is a historical plus active planning source, not the canonical registry. It must be normalized before it can close waves. |
| `KOLM_W851_CLI_TUI_100X_PLAN.md` | Separate CLI/TUI improvement plan exists. | W851 must register under node `31`, map into nodes `21`, `22`, and `39`, and declare generated docs/API collision boundaries before implementation. |
| `KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md` | Separate website/product/design lane exists. | Frontend waves must map into nodes `25`, `30`, `32`, `41`, `42`, and `43` and must not silently modify backend/API truth files. |
| `docs/product-invention-portfolio.json` | Invention portfolio exists and reports portfolio lift. | Synthetic lift is a research signal until mapped to local prototypes, product tests, and production telemetry. |
| `docs/product-invention-implementation-spec.json` | Implementation handoffs exist for inventions. | Each item needs canonical wave linkage, owner lane, target files, acceptance tests, and public claim scope. |
| `docs/product-invention-buildbook.json` | Build instructions exist. | Buildbook steps must become atomic implementation packets before any agent edits product code. |
| `docs/product-frontier-map.json` | Frontier map exists. | Frontier entries need KRL maturity and a promotion path into shipped product surfaces. |
| `docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json` | Frontier implementation contracts exist. | Contracts must validate against schema and map to route/API/account/CLI proof where relevant. |
| `docs/product-frontier-lab.json` | Lab evidence exists. | Lab results stay internal unless benchmarked or production-proven. |
| `docs/product-math-frontier.json` | Mathematical frontier contracts exist. | Math-backed inventions need proof tiers: derivation, simulation, local implementation, benchmark, production, external adoption. |
| `docs/product-frontier-operator-kernels.json` | Operator kernel proposals exist. | Kernel work needs hardware/runtime targets, numerical error bounds, rollback, and benchmark packets. |
| `package.json` | Root package has many verification scripts. | The registry must name which scripts prove which waves; "ran tests" is too vague. |
| `public/nav.js`, `src/intent.js`, `vercel.json` | Currently modified by parallel work. | This spec lane treats them as owned by other lanes unless the user explicitly reassigns them. |

##### External Execution Baseline

| Source | Standard imported into node `31` | Kolm rule |
|---|---|---|
| DORA software delivery metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | DORA defines delivery throughput and instability metrics including change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate. | Wave governance must track speed and stability together. A wave is not mature because it shipped quickly if it raises rework, rollback, or post-ship defect rate. |
| GitHub Actions concurrency, `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/control-workflow-concurrency` | GitHub supports workflow/job concurrency groups to ensure only one workflow or job using the same group runs at a time. | Generated artifact stages need explicit concurrency groups so API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, CLI docs, screenshots, and release reports cannot race. |
| JSON Schema 2020-12, `https://json-schema.org/specification` | JSON Schema lists 2020-12 as the current version and splits the specification into core and validation documents. | Wave registries, invention contracts, closeout ledgers, proof packets, and generated manifests should validate against versioned schemas instead of ad hoc JSON. |
| OpenTelemetry resource semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/resource/` | OpenTelemetry resource attributes describe consistent resource identity; `service.name` is specially handled and `service.version` is the exact version used to identify an artifact. | Wave/prod telemetry must tag service, version, deployment environment, route group, wave ID, and product surface so shipped impact can be traced back to the roadmap. |

##### Canonical Wave Registry Contract

The required registry is a generated and reviewed file, not a prose table. The target file is `docs/wave-registry.json`; a public-safe projection may live at `public/wave-registry-summary.json` once claim-scope rules are enforced.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-wave-registry-v2",
  "schema_version": "2026-05-25",
  "generated_from": [
    "KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md",
    "KOLM_W851_CLI_TUI_100X_PLAN.md",
    "KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md",
    "docs/product-invention-portfolio.json",
    "docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json",
    "docs/product-sota-readiness.json",
    "docs/product-readiness-closeout.md"
  ],
  "waves": [
    {
      "canonical_wave_id": "W807",
      "title": "Confidence-aware adaptive routing",
      "state": "planned",
      "state_scope": "local|production|external",
      "owner_lane": "backend",
      "master_nodes": ["3", "12", "21", "31", "35", "46"],
      "supersedes": ["W709"],
      "duplicates": [],
      "merge_into": null,
      "dependencies": [],
      "blocked_by": [],
      "write_set": {
        "owned_paths": [],
        "read_only_paths": [],
        "generated_paths": [],
        "conflict_paths": []
      },
      "generated_artifact_lock": "api-openapi-product-graph-serial",
      "maturity": {
        "current_krl": "KRL-2",
        "target_krl": "KRL-6",
        "promotion_evidence": []
      },
      "metrics": {
        "primary": ["quality", "cost", "latency", "reliability"],
        "guardrails": ["privacy", "claim_scope", "rollback_rate"],
        "expected_lift": {},
        "actual_local": {},
        "actual_production": {}
      },
      "acceptance": {
        "tests": [],
        "smoke": [],
        "screenshots": [],
        "manual_use": [],
        "external_proof": []
      },
      "release_close": {
        "rollback": [],
        "changed_paths": [],
        "generated_hashes": [],
        "deploy_proof": [],
        "post_ship_review": []
      },
      "public_claim_scope": "do_not_market|research|local_beta|production_beta|shipped|external_proven"
    }
  ]
}
```

##### Authoritative State Rules

- `planned` means a wave exists but has no implementation packet.
- `designed` means requirements, owner lane, write set, dependencies, metrics, and acceptance gates are defined.
- `in_progress` means an agent owns the write set and the active tree shows edits.
- `local_green` means focused local verification passed on the agent slice.
- `combined_green` means verification passed after all active lanes stopped or were intentionally excluded.
- `production_deployed` means deploy evidence exists for the production target.
- `production_smoked` means production routes, account flows, CLI/API where relevant, screenshots where relevant, and live configuration checks passed.
- `external_proven` means public benchmark, package release, certification, partner adoption, or standardization proof exists where required.
- `closed` means the above state matches the wave contract. A wave that needs external proof cannot close at `local_green`.
- `killed` means a wave was intentionally retired with rationale and no dangling public promise.
- `merged` means the wave is not implemented independently and has a canonical `merge_into` target.

If two documents disagree, the registry wins only after the disagreement is explicitly recorded. Until then the state is `reconcile_required`.

##### Active Lane Ownership Map

| Lane | Owns | Must not edit without handoff |
|---|---|---|
| Frontend/site | Public pages, CSS, visual system, navigation behavior, media modules, screenshots, conversion copy, post-auth page UI. | Product kernel, router/API behavior, generated API docs, package scripts, backend smoke contracts. |
| Backend/product truth | API routes, stores, product graph, readiness, claim gates, smoke probes, provider/model/runtime catalogs, account data contracts. | Broad CSS/page redesign, screenshot baselines, generated public pages not owned by backend. |
| CLI/TUI | CLI commands, TUI flows, CLI docs, command smoke, terminal UX, operator scripts. | Frontend nav/design, backend route semantics unless paired with backend ownership. |
| Research/invention | Research docs, invention contracts, frontier maps, math specs, simulated evidence. | Product code, generated API docs, public claims, package metadata unless reassigned. |
| Spec/master tree | This blueprint, node ownership, redlines, work packet definitions, status reconciliation. | Product code, generated artifacts, screenshots, or deploy files. |

Every lane can read everything. Write authority is what needs control.

##### Generated Artifact Sequencing

The master spec tree requires a serial stage for generated outputs. No agent should run these in parallel with another generator:

1. Build or patch route/source files.
2. Run syntax checks for touched source.
3. Regenerate API route inventory.
4. Regenerate OpenAPI.
5. Regenerate product graph and readiness closeout if route/readiness state changed.
6. Regenerate CLI docs if CLI/TUI behavior changed.
7. Regenerate docs manifests, SDK version files, and static references if public docs changed.
8. Run product-surface verification.
9. Run screenshots only after page/CSS/nav churn is stable.
10. Run release verification after all active lane writes are either merged or intentionally excluded.

The race condition previously observed between API ref and OpenAPI generation is a standing example: even correct generators can create false failures or corrupt reads when they run simultaneously.

##### Wave Packet Template

Each wave gets one packet before implementation and one closeout packet after implementation:

```json
{
  "wave_id": "W000",
  "packet_type": "implementation|closeout",
  "master_nodes": [],
  "problem": "",
  "user_value": "",
  "non_goals": [],
  "owner_lane": "",
  "write_set": {
    "owned_paths": [],
    "read_only_paths": [],
    "generated_paths": [],
    "conflict_paths": []
  },
  "acceptance_tests": [],
  "manual_use_cases": [],
  "smoke_tests": [],
  "screenshots_required": false,
  "telemetry_required": [],
  "rollback_plan": [],
  "public_claim_change": "none",
  "external_gates": [],
  "closeout_evidence": {
    "changed_paths": [],
    "commands": [],
    "failed_or_skipped": [],
    "reports": [],
    "deploy": [],
    "post_ship_review_due": ""
  }
}
```

##### Roadmap Conflict Redlines

- W720, W721, and W722 need one registry reconciliation pass because multiple notes say "done" or "awaiting batch ship"; the registry must distinguish local implementation, batch ship, production deploy, and production smoke.
- W807 through W810 are Tier 0 only after dependency, write-set, and acceptance-test state is explicit.
- W851 cannot edit CLI docs, route docs, or generated product graph concurrently with backend agents unless the generated-artifact lock is held.
- W682-W831 frontend/design waves must map to the page families in node `30`; they cannot create a separate visual truth tree.
- W775 continuous background distill is a high-magnitude product wave and must not bypass security, cost, privacy, rollback, or customer opt-in gates.
- Invention waves may be additive, but every invention must map to one of the three product surfaces: own model traffic, compile/distill top models, or run enterprise models on any device.
- Any wave that improves public copy must cite the product truth source it depends on and the external proof state it is allowed to claim.
