##### Node 31 V2 Relax Redline

Node `31` is not relaxed until Kolm has a canonical, schema-validated, agent-safe wave registry; each roadmap and invention item has one state, one owner, one write-set contract, one claim scope, one proof path, and one closeout packet; generated artifacts are sequenced; public claims read from registry state; and production-close status is separate from local green tests.

#### Node 31 V3 Wave Execution Control Plane, Registry Builder, And Generated-Artifact Lock Addendum

Node `52` remains unused. V2 defined the need for a canonical wave registry. V3 turns that need into a concrete execution control plane because the current repo now has hundreds of W-numbered tests, multiple root roadmap files, many invention/frontier ledgers, large verification scripts, concurrent agents, and no committed `docs/wave-registry.json` source of truth.

This addendum is still doc-only in this lane. It does not create the registry or modify implementation code while other agents are active. It defines exactly what the implementation lane must build so roadmap work can accelerate without corrupting generated artifacts or making false "done" claims.

##### Node 31 V3 Current Evidence Delta

Current repo evidence inspected for this addendum:

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| W-numbered tests | 528 test files match `waveNNN`, spanning at least W144 through W843. Recent tests cover W807-W835 and W843. | Wave execution is already encoded in tests, but tests are not a canonical roadmap registry. They need to be joined to source plans, owners, maturity state, proof scope, and production state. |
| Root roadmap files | `KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md` is 139,819 bytes, `KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md` is 58,529 bytes, `KOLM_W851_CLI_TUI_100X_PLAN.md` is 8,281 bytes, and `KOLM_VALUATION_PLAN_2026_05_23.md` is 32,718 bytes. | Multiple high-authority plans exist, so execution cannot rely on whichever document was read last. Node `31` needs source precedence and historical/non-authoritative markers. |
| Registry files | `docs/wave-registry.json`, `docs/wave-registry.schema.json`, `docs/wave-reconcile-report.json`, and `docs/agent-write-locks.json` were not found. | The V2 work is not implemented yet. V3 must make absence of these files a hard open item, not a vague future improvement. |
| Package verification surface | `package.json` already has many verification lanes for depth, inventions, frontier map, math frontier, build strategy, readiness workorders, claim scope, package release, benchmarks, cloud broker, distill strategy, and more. | Verification is rich but not wave-indexed. The registry must map each command to the waves and master nodes it proves. |
| Invention/frontier scripts | Scripts exist for invention portfolio/spec/buildbook, frontier map/lab/contracts/operator kernels, math frontier, readiness workorders, and research atlas. | Research-to-product handoff has implementation hooks, but wave state must distinguish simulated lift, local contract, integrated product, public benchmark, and production proof. |
| Missing wave-specific scripts | No `build-wave-registry`, `verify-wave-registry`, `build-wave-packet`, or `build-active-lanes` script was found in package scripts. | Node `31` needs first-class scripts, not manual PowerShell searches. |
| Generated-artifact risk | Existing package scripts regenerate API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, codegraph, screenshots, and release reports; prior work already observed generation races. | Generated files need a single lock protocol and serial order before any release gate or deploy recommendation. |
| Concurrent lanes | The current workflow has frontend, backend, research, CLI, and spec lanes active, with dirty frontend/deploy files outside this spec lane. | Active lane ownership must be machine-readable so agents can coordinate without relying on chat history. |
| Root malformed path artifacts | Root contains nonstandard path-like files such as `C...site-failures.txt` with mojibake-like characters. | Build redline owns final cleanup, but node `31` must prevent roadmap/screenshot artifacts from becoming unowned release inputs. |

##### Node 31 V3 External Baseline Refresh

| Baseline | Current source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| JSON Schema 2020-12 | JSON Schema describes a media type for defining JSON document structure and extracting/validating information: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/json-schema-core | `docs/wave-registry.json`, active lane locks, and wave packets need schemas so missing owner/state/proof fields fail mechanically. |
| GitHub Actions concurrency | GitHub Actions supports workflow/job concurrency groups so only one run in the same group executes at a time: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/write-workflows/choose-when-workflows-run/control-workflow-concurrency | Generated artifacts and deploy gates need named concurrency groups for OpenAPI/API docs/product graph/screenshots/release reports. |
| GitHub workflow artifacts | GitHub defines workflow artifacts as files produced during runs and cites logs, test results, screenshots, binaries, performance output, and coverage as common artifacts: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/workflows-and-actions/workflow-artifacts | Wave packets need durable artifacts for command logs, screenshots, smoke outputs, performance reports, and generated hashes. |
| SLSA provenance | SLSA provenance records how artifacts were produced, including build definition, run details, builder identity, parameters, dependencies, and subjects: https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.1/provenance | Wave closeout packets should record input plans, changed paths, command invocations, generated outputs, and artifact hashes in a provenance-like shape. |
| DORA metrics | DORA tracks change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate: https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/ | Wave governance must measure flow without turning one metric into the goal; production-impacting waves need post-ship recovery and rework fields. |

##### Node 31 V3 Wave Registry Contract

The canonical registry must be a generated-plus-reviewed document with this minimum shape:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.wave_execution_control_plane.v3",
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "generated_at": "iso-8601",
  "sources": [
    "KOLM_W707_SYSTEM_UPGRADE_PLAN.md",
    "KOLM_ULTRA_PLAN_2026_05_24.md",
    "KOLM_W851_CLI_TUI_100X_PLAN.md",
    "docs/product-invention-portfolio.json",
    "docs/product-invention-implementation-spec.json",
    "docs/product-frontier-implementation-contracts.json",
    "tests/wave*.test.js",
    "package.json"
  ],
  "waves": [
    {
      "canonical_wave_id": "W807",
      "title": "Confidence-aware adaptive routing",
      "source_ids": ["W709", "W807"],
      "state": "planned | in_progress | local_green | batch_ready | prod_deployed_unverified | production_verified | external_gated | superseded | killed | historical",
      "state_scope": "research | local | integration | production | external",
      "priority": "T0 | T1 | T2 | T3 | T4 | P0 | P1 | P2",
      "master_nodes": ["2", "11", "15", "19", "31"],
      "product_surfaces": ["route_capture", "distill_compile", "run_govern"],
      "owner_lane": "backend | frontend | research | cli | docs | production | spec",
      "write_set": ["src/example.js"],
      "generated_outputs": ["public/openapi.json"],
      "depends_on": [],
      "blocks": [],
      "supersedes": [],
      "merge_into": null,
      "claim_scope": "none | internal | docs_scoped | beta | production | external_gated",
      "krl": {"current": 0, "target": 5, "promotion_evidence": []},
      "proof": {
        "unit_tests": [],
        "integration_tests": [],
        "smoke_commands": [],
        "screenshot_reports": [],
        "production_reports": [],
        "external_evidence": []
      },
      "metrics": {
        "target_metric_classes": ["cost", "latency", "quality", "reliability"],
        "baseline": null,
        "local_result": null,
        "production_result": null
      },
      "packet_paths": {
        "implementation": "reports/waves/W807/implementation.json",
        "closeout": "reports/waves/W807/closeout.json",
        "provenance": "reports/waves/W807/provenance.json"
      }
    }
  ],
  "reconcile": {
    "duplicates": [],
    "orphan_tests": [],
    "orphan_plan_items": [],
    "missing_packets": [],
    "generated_artifact_conflicts": [],
    "active_lane_conflicts": []
  }
}
```

##### Node 31 V3 State Model

| State | Meaning | Public claim ceiling |
|---|---|---|
| `historical` | Source text is retained for context but is not execution authority. | No claim. |
| `planned` | Canonical record exists, dependencies and acceptance criteria are known. | Roadmap only. |
| `in_progress` | An active lane owns the write set and implementation is underway. | Internal only. |
| `local_green` | Local tests/smoke pass for the wave on the current tree. | Docs may say "local contract exists" if claim-scope allows. |
| `batch_ready` | Wave is ready for combined-tree ship but not yet deployed/probed. | No production claim. |
| `prod_deployed_unverified` | Production target changed but post-deploy proof is incomplete. | No "shipped" claim. |
| `production_verified` | Production smoke, screenshots if applicable, telemetry, and release evidence passed. | Shipped claim allowed within scope. |
| `external_gated` | Local implementation exists but certification, package release, benchmark, or partner evidence is external. | Scoped claim only. |
| `superseded` | Work moved into a canonical successor. | Mention only through successor. |
| `killed` | Explicitly removed with rationale. | No claim except changelog/deprecation. |

##### Node 31 V3 Generated-Artifact Lock Matrix

| Artifact group | Lock name | Required serial order |
|---|---|---|
| API inventory and OpenAPI | `generated-api-docs` | Router/API source changes, then API ref, then OpenAPI, then route coverage tests. |
| Product graph/readiness | `generated-product-truth` | Product surfaces, journeys, readiness closeout, product graph, then product/depth verifiers. |
| CLI/TUI docs | `generated-cli-docs` | CLI behavior, help output, generated docs, CLI route honesty tests. |
| Screenshots/UI reports | `visual-proof` | Frontend source settled, local server, screenshots, manual-use audit, report retention. |
| Release verify/prod smoke | `release-proof` | Combined tree, generated artifacts, release verify, prod deploy, prod smoke, production screenshots. |
| Wave registry | `wave-registry` | Source plan parse, test inventory, duplicate resolver, registry build, schema validate, reconcile report. |

| `W-WAVE-V3-025` | P0 | `31.6` | Wire wave registry into `verify:depth`. | Depth verification cannot pass while high-priority waves lack owner, proof path, claim scope, or production-close state. |
| `W-WAVE-V3-026` | P0 | `31.6` | Issue the node `31` V3 certificate. | It can say `node31_closed` only when the registry, schema, reconcile report, active locks, generated-artifact locks, wave packets, evidence retention, and production-close separation are green. |

##### Node 31 V3 Redline

Node `31` cannot relax until:

1. `docs/wave-registry.json`, `docs/wave-registry.schema.json`, `docs/wave-reconcile-report.json`, `docs/active-lanes.json`, and `docs/agent-write-locks.json` exist and validate;
2. every W-numbered test maps to a canonical wave or is explicitly historical/orphaned;
3. every root roadmap and invention/frontier ledger has source precedence and execution authority status;
4. duplicate and superseded waves resolve to one canonical successor;
5. active frontend/backend/research/CLI/production/spec lanes have write-set locks and conflict rules;
6. generated API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, screenshots, release reports, and registry builds run in a serial order;
7. every wave packet records source docs, changed paths, tests, smoke, generated artifacts, rollback, claim scope, metrics, and evidence retention;
8. local-green, batch-ready, production-verified, external-gated, superseded, killed, and historical states are distinct;
9. public copy, public roadmap, docs, release notes, and sales claims read from registry state rather than chat summaries;
10. DORA flow metrics and post-ship reviews are captured without letting metrics become the goal; and
11. no deploy recommendation is made while active lane locks or generated-artifact conflicts are unresolved.

If any item is missing, roadmap execution may continue, but the wave governance system is not complete.

### Search, AI Discovery, Content Graph, And Documentation Findability Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not rewrite page metadata, robots rules, sitemap generation, `llms.txt`, structured data, docs pages, search UI, or crawler policy directly. It defines the discovery system required for Kolm to be understandable by buyers, developers, search engines, AI answer engines, and citation surfaces without hiding readiness limits or overclaiming shipped functionality.

Node `32` exists because "state-of-the-art website" is not only visual polish. It also means the right pages are indexable, the right snippets are concise, the right facts are machine-readable, docs are findable by task, public claims are backed by product truth, and AI assistants can cite Kolm without inventing what the product does.

#### Current Repo Discovery Evidence

A current source scan shows that Kolm has important discovery primitives, but they are not yet governed as one system:

- `public/robots.txt` exists, allows the public site, disallows `/v1/`, `/api/`, `/admin`, `/dashboard`, `/onboarding`, `/signin`, `/signup`, `/account`, `/playground`, and references `https://kolm.ai/sitemap.xml`.
- `public/sitemap.xml` currently lists 453 URLs, while the public tree contains 729 HTML files. This may be correct if many pages are intentionally noindex/private/orphaned, but it is not proven without an indexability manifest.
- `public/llms.txt` exists and is 15,915 bytes. It still frames Kolm primarily as "private AI stack for captured traffic" and "one OpenAI-compatible gateway," which is too narrow relative to the current three-surface product architecture.
- `public/.well-known/ai-context.json` exists and uses a stronger three-surface tagline: "Route models. Distill specialists. Run on any device." It also exposes endpoints, `.kolm` concepts, K-Score, receipt chain, and pricing tiers.
- `public/openapi.json`, `public/product-graph.json`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`, `public/docs-manifest.json`, `public/status.rss`, `public/articles/rss.xml`, and benchmark JSON files all provide machine-readable product truth.
- A current public HTML scan found 729 HTML files, 507 with JSON-LD, 726 with canonical links, 714 with OpenGraph metadata, and 146 with missing or weak meta descriptions under the 50-to-170-character heuristic used for this scan.
- Existing build/audit scripts reference sitemap, orphan, metadata, static refs, API docs, docs manifest, changelog, structured data, and product claim scope, but there is no single discovery manifest tying page purpose, indexability, metadata, structured data, AI-readable summaries, sitemap inclusion, and public-claim scope together.

Discovery completion therefore requires a product-content graph, not just "add SEO tags." Each route must know whether it should be indexed, what buyer/developer task it serves, which master product surface it supports, what structured data it may publish, which machine-readable files should point to it, and what evidence proves the snippet is current.

#### External Discovery Baseline

| Source | Relevant standard for Kolm | Required interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Google Search Essentials, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/essentials` | Google describes technical requirements, spam policies, and key best practices for web content, including people-first content, prominent user terms in titles/headings/link text, crawlable links, and specific image/video/structured-data practices. | Kolm pages must be useful to humans first, use buyer/developer language, keep links crawlable, and make media/structured data reflect visible product truth. |
| Google SEO Starter Guide, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/fundamentals/seo-starter-guide` | Google says titles should be unique, clear, concise, and accurate; meta descriptions should be short, unique, and one or two sentences; topic grouping can affect crawling on large sites. | Kolm needs generated and reviewed titles/descriptions/H1s, route clusters, and page-group freshness policy across hundreds of URLs. |
| Google Structured Data Guidelines, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/sd-policies` | Google recommends JSON-LD and warns that structured data must represent visible page content, be up to date, and not mislead. | Kolm JSON-LD must not invent prices, certifications, reviews, benchmark wins, package releases, or partner adoption; every structured fact must match visible content and readiness evidence. |
| Google sitemap docs, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/sitemaps/build-sitemap` | Sitemaps should contain URLs for the site, can be submitted in Search Console, and can be referenced from robots.txt. | Kolm must explain why each public URL is in or out of the sitemap and submit/verify production sitemaps after deploy. |
| Google robots.txt interpretation, `https://developers.google.com/crawling/docs/robots-txt/robots-txt-spec` | Robots rules apply by host/protocol/port, must be top-level, are cached, support `sitemap`, and do not hide content from search results by themselves. | Kolm robots policy must be explicit for public pages, account/private pages, API paths, generated JSON, screenshots, archives, staging, and AI crawlers; no team should use robots as a privacy boundary. |
| Google common crawlers, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/google-common-crawlers` | Google documents crawler tokens including `Googlebot`, media bots, `GoogleOther`, `Google-CloudVertexBot`, and `Google-Extended`; `Google-Extended` controls Gemini/Vertex training/grounding use and does not affect Search inclusion. | Kolm should deliberately decide Google Search indexing separately from Gemini/Vertex training and grounding permissions. |
| OpenAI crawler docs, `https://platform.openai.com/docs/bots` | OpenAI documents `OAI-SearchBot` and `GPTBot` robots tags, and says settings are independent so search appearance can be allowed while training use is disallowed. | Kolm needs an explicit AI crawler policy that separates citation/search discovery from training reuse and legal/content-rights posture. |
| Schema.org `SoftwareApplication`, `https://schema.org/SoftwareApplication` | Schema.org supports JSON-LD examples for software/application/product entities. | Kolm should publish an Organization/Product/SoftwareApplication graph for the product, but only with properties that match visible content and current capability proof. |
| llms.txt draft reference, `https://llmtxt.info/` | `llms.txt` is an emerging, voluntary Markdown convention, not W3C/IETF-standardized enforcement. | Kolm should treat `llms.txt` as a curated AI-readable map, useful but non-authoritative; product graph, OpenAPI, docs manifest, and visible pages remain the actual truth sources. |

#### Discovery Product Architecture

Kolm's discovery system should make five audiences converge on the same truth:

| Audience | What they need | Canonical surfaces |
|---|---|---|
| Enterprise buyer | Clear category, trust, pricing, compliance scope, ROI, deployment, and contact path. | Homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, trust, security, case studies, readiness closeout, status. |
| Developer evaluator | Quickstart, SDKs, API reference, examples, CLI, local smoke, integration guides, OpenAPI. | Docs, quickstart, `/openapi.json`, docs manifest, SDK pages, cookbook, examples, CLI docs. |
| Search crawler | Crawlable pages, sitemap, canonical URLs, concise metadata, visible structured data, healthy redirects. | `robots.txt`, `sitemap.xml`, HTML pages, JSON-LD, canonical links, internal links. |
| AI answer engine | Concise product definition, task-oriented docs, stable machine-readable links, citation-friendly pages, crawler policy. | `llms.txt`, `.well-known/ai-context.json`, product graph, OpenAPI, docs manifest, README-style pages, public evidence ledgers. |
| Implementation agent | Exact product graph, API routes, readiness states, specs, generated docs, source paths, smoke commands. | Product graph, OpenAPI, API routes JSON, readiness closeout, wave registry, master spec, docs manifests. |

The same fact cannot have five different versions. If pricing, package status, certification scope, benchmark status, API count, route count, or product positioning changes, the discovery graph must regenerate or fail.

#### Canonical Discovery Manifest

Each route should be represented in a generated manifest shaped like this:

```json
{
  "url": "https://kolm.ai/distill",
  "path": "/distill",
  "route_type": "marketing|docs|account|trust|benchmark|research|marketplace|status|integration|api-json",
  "indexability": {
    "robots_allowed": true,
    "meta_robots": "index,follow",
    "sitemap_included": true,
    "canonical": "https://kolm.ai/distill",
    "redirect_target": null,
    "private_or_account": false
  },
  "content_contract": {
    "primary_surface": "compile-distill-top-models",
    "audience": ["developer", "ml-lead", "enterprise-buyer"],
    "search_intent": ["distill AI models", "compile model behavior", "reduce AI API cost"],
    "first_screen_answer": ["what", "who", "why_now"],
    "readiness_scope": "shipped|beta|research|external-gated",
    "claim_evidence_ids": ["product-graph", "benchmark-scope", "readiness-closeout"]
  },
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Distill AI workflows into signed .kolm artifacts - Kolm",
    "description": "Capture repeated model behavior, build evals, distill specialists, and ship signed artifacts with evidence.",
    "h1": "Distill repeated AI workflows into owned artifacts",
    "og_image": "/og/distill.png",
    "json_ld_types": ["SoftwareApplication", "BreadcrumbList"]
  },
  "ai_readable": {
    "included_in_llms_txt": true,
    "included_in_ai_context": true,
    "markdown_variant": "/docs/distill.md",
    "openapi_refs": ["/v1/specialists/auto-distill"],
    "product_graph_refs": ["compile-verify"]
  },
  "freshness": {
    "last_source_update": "2026-05-25",
    "last_verified": "2026-05-25",
    "owner": "docs-product",
    "review_cadence_days": 30
  },
  "evidence": {
    "metadata_lint": "passed",
    "structured_data_validation": "passed",
    "screenshot_report": "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/report.md",
    "search_console_status": "submitted|indexed|not-indexed|unknown",
    "ai_citation_probe": "reports/discovery/latest-ai-citation.json"
  }
}
```

#### Discovery Redlines

- No page appears in the sitemap without a page owner, indexability reason, canonical URL, title, description, H1, visible body purpose, and claim-scope state.
- No page is excluded from the sitemap without a reason: private, duplicate, archive, redirect, staging, account-only, API JSON, or intentionally unlisted support content.
- No structured data may describe content hidden from users or unproven product claims.
- No pricing, plan, certification, benchmark, partner, package, or compliance claim may appear in JSON-LD unless it matches visible page copy and product readiness evidence.
- No `llms.txt` or `.well-known/ai-context.json` sentence may contradict the product graph, OpenAPI, pricing contract, readiness closeout, or final three-surface positioning.
- No AI crawler policy may be implicit. Kolm must decide separately for Google Search, Google-Extended, OAI-SearchBot, GPTBot, Bing, and other major crawlers whether the goal is search citation, AI answer visibility, training reuse, or exclusion.
- No account, admin, API key, signup/signin, playground, dashboard, or private tenant page may be made discoverable merely to increase page count.
- No archive, generated screenshot, old plan, old brand, stale research, or historical wave plan should be indexed unless it is intentionally framed as historical evidence.
- No docs page is done unless a developer can find it through nav, search, sitemap or docs manifest, `llms.txt` where appropriate, and internal links from the relevant product journey.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `32.1` Indexability, robots policy, sitemap coverage, canonical URLs, redirects, and crawl budget | Every public route has an indexability decision, sitemap inclusion/exclusion reason, canonical URL, redirect status, robots policy, account/private classification, and crawler-log or production-fetch proof. |
| `32.2` Metadata, titles, descriptions, H1s, OpenGraph, social cards, snippets, and structured data | Titles, descriptions, H1s, OG/Twitter cards, social images, JSON-LD, breadcrumbs, and snippets are generated from page contracts, concise, unique, current, and claim-scoped. |
| `32.3` Content graph, topic clusters, internal links, breadcrumbs, docs IA, examples, and site search | Pages are grouped by product surface and task intent; docs search returns useful results; breadcrumbs/internal links connect homepage, product, docs, pricing, trust, examples, and account journeys. |
| `32.4` AI-readable files, llms.txt, ai-context, OpenAPI, product graph, crawler controls, and citation policy | `llms.txt`, `.well-known/ai-context.json`, product graph, OpenAPI, docs manifest, README-style docs, and crawler policy agree on product truth and expose stable, citation-friendly content without overclaiming. |
| `32.5` Content quality, freshness, claim scope, authorship, benchmarks, changelog, cases, and research truth | Content has owner, date, freshness cadence, source of truth, visible claim evidence, benchmark/certification/package scope, and stale-content retirement or historical framing. |
| `32.6` Discovery evidence: Search Console, rich-results validation, crawler logs, AI citation tests, and production snippets | Production evidence includes sitemap submission, URL inspection samples, rich-results tests, robots fetch, crawler logs, social preview screenshots, AI citation probes, and search/snippet captures for priority pages. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-DISC-001` | `32.1` | Build the route discovery manifest. | Every HTML/JSON/public route has path, type, owner, indexability, sitemap decision, canonical, redirect, robots policy, and page purpose. |
| `W-DISC-002` | `32.1` | Reconcile sitemap coverage. | The 453 sitemap URLs and 729 HTML files are reconciled with inclusion/exclusion reasons and production fetch proof. |
| `W-DISC-003` | `32.1` | Formalize robots and private-surface policy. | Account, admin, API, signin/signup, dashboard, playground, archives, generated assets, and public JSON have explicit crawl/index decisions. |
| `W-DISC-004` | `32.1` | Add redirect/canonical drift checks. | Canonicals, redirects, sitemap URLs, nav links, footer links, and public route aliases cannot disagree. |
| `W-DISC-005` | `32.2` | Build metadata generator and lint gate. | Every indexable page has title, description, H1, canonical, OG, social image, and snippet contract; weak/missing descriptions fail. |
| `W-DISC-006` | `32.2` | Normalize structured data by page type. | Product, SoftwareApplication, Organization, BreadcrumbList, Article, TechArticle, FAQPage, VideoObject, Dataset, and Product/Offer schemas are allowed only where visible and true. |
| `W-DISC-007` | `32.2` | Add social preview proof. | Homepage, product, pricing, docs, quickstart, enterprise, trust, benchmarks, articles, and case studies have screenshot proof for social cards without mojibake or stale pricing. |
| `W-DISC-008` | `32.2` | Block misleading JSON-LD claims. | Structured data cannot publish unshipped packages, fake reviews, stale plan names, benchmark superiority, certification status, or partner adoption without evidence IDs. |
| `W-DISC-009` | `32.3` | Build content topic clusters. | Capture/gateway, distill/compile, run-anywhere/runtime, trust/compliance, pricing/ROI, docs/SDK, marketplace, and research clusters have pillar pages, supporting pages, and internal links. |
| `W-DISC-010` | `32.3` | Add docs findability proof. | Docs nav, search, docs manifest, internal links, task pages, API refs, SDK tabs, examples, and breadcrumbs let a developer reach first capture/artifact quickly. |
| `W-DISC-011` | `32.3` | Add internal link graph audit. | Orphaned public pages, thin archive pages, dead-end docs, duplicate pages, and isolated research pages are removed, redirected, noindexed, or linked intentionally. |
| `W-DISC-012` | `32.4` | Rewrite `llms.txt` around the three-surface truth. | `llms.txt` concisely maps capture/own traffic, compile/distill, and run-anywhere surfaces, with priority docs, API, pricing, trust, readiness, and no outdated one-surface framing. |
| `W-DISC-013` | `32.4` | Align `.well-known/ai-context.json`. | AI context, product graph, OpenAPI, pricing tiers, closeout ledger, docs manifest, and public copy agree on names, claims, endpoints, pricing, and readiness. |
| `W-DISC-014` | `32.4` | Add AI crawler policy. | Robots policy explicitly covers Googlebot, Google-Extended, OAI-SearchBot, GPTBot, Bingbot, media crawlers, and training/citation distinctions. |
| `W-DISC-015` | `32.4` | Publish agent-readable docs variants where useful. | Priority docs and API references have clean Markdown/plain-text or JSON equivalents linked from `llms.txt`, docs manifest, and product graph. |
| `W-DISC-016` | `32.5` | Add content freshness ledger. | Each content page has owner, date, source, review cadence, stale threshold, readiness state, and evidence link. |
| `W-DISC-017` | `32.5` | Separate research from shipped product. | Research, labs, frontier, invention, benchmark, roadmap, and historical pages clearly distinguish idea, beta, shipped, external-gated, and deprecated states. |
| `W-DISC-018` | `32.6` | Add production discovery smoke. | Production check fetches robots, sitemap, llms, ai-context, OpenAPI, product graph, priority pages, metadata, JSON-LD, and social cards from `https://kolm.ai`. |
| `W-DISC-019` | `32.6` | Add Search Console and rich-results packet. | Priority URLs have sitemap submission, URL inspection or equivalent status, rich-results validation where applicable, and screenshot/archive evidence. |
| `W-DISC-020` | `32.6` | Add AI citation and snippet probes. | Safe probes ask major AI/search surfaces or local retrieval tests what Kolm is, compare answers against product truth, and archive corrections needed. |

#### Node 32 Relax Redline

Node `32` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current production evidence:

- Which of the 729 public HTML pages are meant to be indexed, and why are only the chosen URLs in the sitemap?
- Do robots, sitemap, canonical links, redirects, nav links, footer links, docs manifest, and product graph agree?
- Do all indexable pages have concise unique titles, descriptions, H1s, canonical URLs, OG/social cards, and valid visible structured data?
- Do homepage, product, pricing, docs, quickstart, enterprise, trust, benchmarks, case studies, and research snippets say the same three-surface product truth?
- Does structured data avoid fake prices, fake reviews, unearned certifications, unshipped package claims, stale plan names, and hidden page content?
- Is `llms.txt` current, concise, three-surface aware, and honest about shipped versus beta/research/external-gated capabilities?
- Does `.well-known/ai-context.json` agree with pricing, OpenAPI, product graph, route counts, readiness closeout, and public copy?
- Are AI crawler policies deliberate for search citation, training use, grounding, and exclusion?
- Can a developer find quickstart, OpenAPI, SDKs, CLI, examples, and first artifact path through docs nav, search, internal links, sitemap, and AI-readable files?
- Are production robots, sitemap, metadata, structured data, social previews, Search Console samples, and AI citation probes archived for the deployed commit?

The relax condition for node `32` is not "SEO tags exist." It is: Kolm's human pages, search metadata, structured data, sitemap, robots policy, AI-readable files, docs graph, product graph, public claims, and production snippets all describe the same current product.

#### Node 32 Discovery Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. SEO, AI search discoverability, crawler policy, metadata, structured data, `llms.txt`, `.well-known/ai-context.json`, sitemap governance, social previews, docs findability, and AI citation probes are node `32` scope. Node `6` owns public page implementation, node `21` owns accessible content integrity, node `44` owns narrative truth, node `46` owns traceability, and node `51` owns demo/video media proof. Node `32` is the discovery operating system that forces those surfaces to publish one current, citable product truth.

##### Node `32.0` Current Repo Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Public crawl files | `public/robots.txt`, `public/sitemap.xml`, `public/llms.txt`, and `public/.well-known/ai-context.json` exist. | The primitives exist; the gap is governance, freshness, and consistency across human pages, machine-readable files, product graph, and claim scope. |
| Sitemap coverage | Current local scan found `729` public HTML files and `453` URLs in `public/sitemap.xml`. | This may be intentional, but every omission needs a generated inclusion/exclusion reason: private, duplicate, archive, cut path, noindex, redirect, account-only, API JSON, or intentionally unlisted. |
| Metadata coverage | Scan found `507` HTML files with JSON-LD, `726` with canonical links, `714` with OpenGraph title metadata, and `147` weak or missing meta descriptions by the local 50-to-170-character heuristic. | SEO quality is not closed until every indexable page has a route contract, concise unique metadata, visible H1 alignment, structured data scope, and proof. |
| AI-readable drift | `public/llms.txt` still opens with "private AI stack for captured traffic" and "One OpenAI-compatible gateway" framing, while `public/.well-known/ai-context.json` uses the stronger three-surface line: "Route models. Distill specialists. Run on any device." | `llms.txt` is stale relative to current positioning and should be regenerated from the product graph and narrative map. |
| Robots policy | `robots.txt` allows `/`, disallows `/v1/`, `/api/`, `/admin`, `/dashboard`, `/onboarding`, `/signin`, `/signup`, `/account`, `/playground`, and `/launch-checklist.md`, and points to the sitemap. | Robots is useful crawl guidance but is not privacy or authorization. Node `32` must coordinate with nodes `10`, `20`, and `38` so private surfaces are protected by auth and not merely crawler rules. |
| Sitemap builder | `scripts/build-sitemap.cjs` excludes account/admin/auth surfaces, cut paths, and legacy/thin pages, but the exclusion list includes routes such as `/trust`, `/distill`, `/roadmap`, and `/why-now` that may be strategically important if rebuilt. | Exclusions need a manifest-backed decision, not a hidden hardcoded list. If a strategic page is cut, there must be canonical replacement or noindex reason. |
| SEO scripts | `scripts/seo-audit.cjs`, `scripts/seo-sweep.cjs`, `scripts/build-sitemap.cjs`, `scripts/find-missing-sitemap.cjs`, `scripts/audit-orphans.mjs`, `scripts/surface-orphans.mjs`, `scripts/fix-seo-twitter-cards.mjs`, `scripts/audit-href.cjs`, and `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs` exist. | Tooling is fragmented. The final state needs one discovery manifest that all scripts read or verify. |
| Product truth counts | Current `public/product-graph.json` reports `12` journeys, `7` route surfaces, `163` route groups, `582` routes, `69` API routes, `64` CLI commands, `32` TUI views, `33` account links, and `57` readiness requirements; closeout reports `8` open requirements. | Discovery pages, snippets, structured data, `llms.txt`, AI context, docs manifest, and sitemap must not publish counts or claims that contradict these current generated sources. |

##### Node `32.1` Current Standards Delta

| Standard source | Relevant current rule | Kolm V2 requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Google Search Essentials | Public content must be technically accessible, avoid spam, and be useful to people. | Do not optimize for page count. Prioritize crawlable, useful, buyer/developer-task pages with visible product proof and stable links. |
| Google SEO Starter Guide | Titles and snippets should be clear, unique, concise, and descriptive; site structure should help crawling and users. | Generate and review title, description, H1, canonical, breadcrumbs, and internal links from the page contract. |
| Google structured data policies | Structured data must match visible page content and not mislead. | JSON-LD cannot publish fake package availability, fake reviews, stale pricing, unearned certifications, hidden video/demo claims, or benchmark superiority without evidence IDs. |
| Google sitemap guidance and sitemaps.org | Sitemaps are URL discovery hints and should list canonical URLs intended for crawling. | The sitemap builder must explain inclusion/exclusion and emit only canonical public pages with valid lastmod source. |
| Google robots interpretation | Robots rules are per host/protocol/port and are crawl directives; they are not a privacy boundary. | Account/API/admin/private surfaces must be auth-protected and excluded for product reasons, not treated as safe because of `robots.txt`. |
| Google common crawlers and AI features | Google exposes distinct crawler tokens, including search, media, and AI-related crawlers with different purposes. | Kolm must decide separately for search indexing, media indexing, AI grounding/citation, and training use. |
| OpenAI crawler docs | OpenAI documents crawler tokens such as `OAI-SearchBot` and `GPTBot`, with independent controls for search/citation and training. | Kolm's crawler policy must allow or deny citation/search and training reuse deliberately, with legal and content-rights alignment. |
| Schema.org `SoftwareApplication` | Software/application structured data can describe application category, operating system, offers, publisher, and URLs. | Kolm should publish a restrained Organization/Product/SoftwareApplication graph only where visible page copy and product graph evidence support it. |

##### Node `32.2` Discovery Manifest V2 Contract

Every public route, generated discovery file, and machine-readable product artifact should be represented by one generated discovery manifest.

```json
{
  "path": "/quickstart",
  "url": "https://kolm.ai/quickstart",
  "master_node": "32",
  "secondary_nodes": ["6", "8", "17", "44"],
  "route_type": "public-docs",
  "audiences": ["developer", "ml-lead", "enterprise-buyer"],
  "intent": ["first artifact", "install cli", "capture model call"],
  "indexability": {
    "decision": "index",
    "reason": "primary developer onboarding page",
    "robots_allowed": true,
    "meta_robots": "index,follow",
    "sitemap_included": true,
    "canonical": "https://kolm.ai/quickstart",
    "redirect_target": null,
    "private_surface": false
  },
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Quickstart - build your first signed .kolm artifact",
    "description": "Connect a model call, capture evidence, compile a signed .kolm artifact, and verify it locally.",
    "h1": "Build your first signed .kolm artifact",
    "og_image": "https://kolm.ai/og/quickstart.png",
    "json_ld_types": ["SoftwareApplication", "BreadcrumbList"]
  },
  "machine_readable": {
    "llms_txt": true,
    "ai_context": true,
    "docs_manifest": true,
    "openapi_refs": ["/v1/wrap/verified", "/v1/artifacts"],
    "product_graph_refs": ["capture-route", "compile-verify"]
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "allowed_claims": ["capture", "artifact", "verify"],
    "blocked_claims": ["certified compliance", "public benchmark superiority"],
    "evidence_ids": ["product-graph", "readiness-closeout"]
  },
  "freshness": {
    "owner": "docs-product",
    "review_cadence_days": 30,
    "last_verified_at": "2026-05-25"
  },
  "proof": {
    "metadata_lint": "passed",
    "structured_data_validation": "passed",
    "sitemap_check": "passed",
    "screenshot_report": "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/report.md",
    "production_fetch": "reports/discovery/latest/quickstart-fetch.json",
    "ai_citation_probe": "reports/discovery/latest/quickstart-ai-citation.json"
  }
}
```

The manifest is authoritative for discovery. `robots.txt`, sitemap generation, metadata linting, JSON-LD checks, `llms.txt`, AI context, docs manifest, social previews, and production discovery smoke should either generate from it or verify against it.

##### Node `32.3` AI Discovery Packet V2

Kolm needs an AI discovery packet because buyers and developers increasingly ask assistants what a product is before reading the site. The packet should make AI systems cite Kolm correctly without giving them unsupported claims.

Required packet files:

| File | Purpose | V2 completion bar |
|---|---|---|
| `public/llms.txt` | Human-curated, AI-readable map of the best public docs. | Starts with three-surface product truth, links only priority pages, includes shipped/beta/external-gated scope, and avoids old one-gateway-only framing. |
| `public/.well-known/ai-context.json` | Structured context for product identity, endpoints, concepts, pricing, and policy. | Generated or checked against product graph, OpenAPI, pricing contract, readiness closeout, and narrative map. |
| `public/product-graph.json` | Generated product truth. | Discovery files read this for journeys, routes, account links, CLI/TUI counts, readiness, and route ownership. |
| `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` | Open external/package/benchmark/certification gates. | AI-readable files expose closeout scope where relevant and never present gated items as shipped. |
| `public/openapi.json` | API truth for developer agents and SDK generation. | Linked from AI context and docs, versioned, and not contradicted by marketing pages. |
| `public/docs-manifest.json` | Docs navigation and task map. | Priority docs are included in `llms.txt` and internal links; stale docs are noindexed, archived, or removed. |

AI citation probes should ask:

1. What is Kolm?
2. Who is Kolm for?
3. What are the three product surfaces?
4. What is a `.kolm` artifact?
5. Is Kolm a model provider, gateway, compiler, runtime, marketplace, or compliance product?
6. What is shipped versus gated?
7. How much does it cost?
8. What should a developer do first?

The answer is acceptable only if it matches product graph, pricing, readiness closeout, public pages, and current hero/narrative copy.

##### Node `32.4` Crawler And Indexability Policy V2

Crawler policy should be explicit by purpose, not accidental by user-agent string.

| Purpose | Policy question | Required owner nodes |
|---|---|---|
| Search indexing | Should public buyer, docs, trust, pricing, articles, and case pages be indexed? | `32`, `6`, `8`, `21`, `44` |
| Media indexing | Should product video, screenshots, OG images, and press assets be discoverable? | `32`, `51`, `45` |
| AI citation and grounding | Should AI answer engines use the public site as a source? | `32`, `23`, `44`, `46` |
| AI training reuse | Should public pages be reusable for model training? | `23`, `32`, `10` |
| Private routes | Are account, admin, API, billing, keys, and tenant data protected regardless of robots? | `10`, `20`, `38`, `32` |
| Archives and research | Should historical wave plans and invention docs be indexed or framed as historical evidence? | `31`, `32`, `48` |

Policy rules:

- `robots.txt` is crawl guidance, not a security control.
- crawler tokens must be reviewed for Google Search, Google media crawlers, Google-Extended, OAI-SearchBot, GPTBot, Bingbot, social preview bots, and major enterprise AI retrieval crawlers where policy is known;
- AI citation should prefer public stable docs and product graph pages over internal speculative research;
- no private route may rely on crawler exclusion for confidentiality;
- staging, preview, generated screenshots, and account pages must have defense-in-depth: auth, noindex where rendered, no sitemap inclusion, and no internal links from public pages unless intentionally public;
- legal/content-rights decisions for training reuse belong to node `23` and must be reflected in node `32` crawler files.

##### Node `32.5` Discovery Verification Gates V2

Node `32` should not relax on local string scans alone. It needs local, generated, and production evidence.

Required gates:

| Gate | What it proves |
|---|---|
| `verify:discovery-manifest` | Every route/file has owner node, indexability decision, metadata contract, claim scope, AI-readable inclusion, and freshness. |
| `verify:sitemap` | Sitemap output equals manifest decisions; excluded pages have reasons; canonical/redirect conflicts fail. |
| `verify:metadata` | Titles, descriptions, H1s, canonicals, OG/Twitter cards, and social images are unique, concise, and current for indexable pages. |
| `verify:structured-data` | JSON-LD parses, allowed types match page type, visible facts match rendered content, and product claims have evidence IDs. |
| `verify:ai-context` | `llms.txt`, `.well-known/ai-context.json`, product graph, OpenAPI, docs manifest, pricing, and readiness closeout agree. |
| `verify:crawler-policy` | Robots rules, meta robots, sitemap inclusion, private-route exclusion, AI crawler policies, and legal training/citation stance are explicit. |
| `verify:discovery-prod` | Deployed robots, sitemap, priority pages, metadata, JSON-LD, social previews, and AI-readable files fetch from `https://kolm.ai` and match the local release packet. |
| `verify:ai-citation` | AI/search/local retrieval probes return answers that match the three-surface product truth and do not invent gated claims. |

| `W-DISC-V2-002` | `32.2` | Replace hardcoded sitemap exclusions with manifest decisions. | `/trust`, `/distill`, `/roadmap`, `/why-now`, and other strategic exclusions have explicit replacement, noindex, archive, or rebuild decisions. |
