##### Node `32` V2 Relax Redline

Node `32` can relax only when all of the following are proven:

- every public HTML route and generated public artifact has a discovery manifest row;
- sitemap inclusion and exclusion decisions are generated from the manifest, not hidden in script-only hardcoded lists;
- weak or missing metadata count is zero for indexable pages;
- canonical, redirect, nav, footer, docs manifest, sitemap, and product graph references agree;
- `llms.txt` and `.well-known/ai-context.json` describe the same three-surface Kolm product as the current homepage and product graph;
- JSON-LD is valid, visible, truthful, current, and claim-scoped;
- crawler policy separates search indexing, media indexing, AI citation, AI training reuse, private routes, and historical research;
- account/private/API routes are protected by auth and route policy, not merely by robots exclusion;
- production discovery smoke has fetched `https://kolm.ai/robots.txt`, sitemap, `llms.txt`, AI context, product graph, OpenAPI, and priority pages;
- AI citation probes do not describe Kolm as only an OpenAI-compatible gateway or invent ungated certifications, benchmark superiority, packages, partnerships, pricing, or device support.

### Artifact Registry, Marketplace, Verified Publishers, And Trust Lifecycle Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not patch `src/marketplace.js`, `src/verified.js`, artifact bytes, registry pack manifests, marketplace pages, API routes, CLI commands, entitlement code, or account screens directly. It defines the product and engineering contract required for `.kolm` artifacts to become a credible ecosystem instead of a static download shelf.

Node `33` exists because the artifact is the product object that binds all three Kolm surfaces together:

- captured model traffic becomes owned artifacts;
- compile and distillation work outputs artifacts;
- enterprise model deployment to cloud, edge, and devices consumes artifacts.

If the artifact registry is weak, the whole product becomes weak. Buyers need to know what an artifact is, who published it, what it can do, what evidence backs it, where it runs, what it depends on, what changed, whether it can be revoked, and whether installing it is safe. Developers need deterministic pull, verify, inspect, diff, install, compose, and rollback flows. Enterprises need rights, provenance, audit, SBOM, policy, entitlement, incident, and takedown controls.

#### Current Repo Artifact And Marketplace Evidence

A current source scan shows that Kolm already has the beginnings of an artifact ecosystem:

| Area | Current evidence | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Public marketplace | `public/marketplace` contains 18 HTML pages for listings, provider marketplace pages, upload, and artifact details. | Marketplace is visible, but completion requires listing contracts, trust state, install flows, entitlement, reviews, moderation, and production evidence. |
| Registry pack | `public/registry-pack` contains 6 `.kolm` artifacts, `manifest.json`, and one seed fixture. | Registry-pack proves artifact bytes exist locally; completion requires content-addressed identities, signed manifest policy, compatibility, revocation, and import/export. |
| Marketplace source | `src/marketplace.js` states the catalog is the public marketplace source of truth and reads hashes/K-scores from `public/registry-pack/manifest.json`. | Good foundation, but a static catalog must graduate into registry-backed artifact lifecycle and trust workflows. |
| Verified status | `src/marketplace.js` deliberately keeps sync-layer `verified` false and separates provisional readiness from live verified badge overlays. | This is the correct honesty pattern. Completion must preserve it across UI, API, CLI, search metadata, and marketplace badges. |
| Signature hooks | `src/marketplace.js` imports signature block builders/verifiers and carries Ed25519 receipt fields. | Completion requires external-verifiable signature bundles, transparency log or equivalent, key rotation, offline verification, and revocation. |
| Artifact core | `src/artifact.js` and `src/binder.js` are large core modules for artifact creation/binding. | Completion requires schema migration tests, compatibility rules, media types, artifact passport generation, and manifest linting. |
| Dependency/diff | `src/artifact-dependency-graph.js` and `src/artifact-diff.js` exist. | Completion requires first-class dependency graphs, version diff UX, breaking-change detection, and rollout policy. |
| Airgap | `src/airgap-sneakernet.js` references signed bundles, receipts, and artifacts. | Completion must make artifact verification work online, offline, and air-gapped with the same identity and receipt semantics. |
| Docs | `public/docs/marketplace.html`, `public/docs/marketplace-import.html`, `public/docs/model-card.html`, and artifact CLI docs exist. | Docs need an integrated artifact journey: create, verify, publish, install, run, update, revoke, audit. |
| API inventory | The blueprint scan identifies `/v1/marketplace` as 11 routes with 3 stubs and `/v1/registry` as 3 stubs. | Stub routes must either become working lifecycle APIs or be scoped as explicit closeout gates. |

#### External Baseline For A State-Of-The-Art Artifact Ecosystem

Kolm should not invent every trust primitive from scratch. It should map `.kolm` artifacts onto patterns users already understand from package registries, container registries, model hubs, and software supply-chain security.

| External baseline | What it contributes to node `33` | Source |
|---|---|---|
| OCI Distribution Spec and OCI artifact/image specs | Content-addressed distribution, manifests, descriptors, media types, referrers, tags, and registry API expectations. | `https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec` and `https://oci-playground.github.io/specs-latest/specs/image/v1.1.0-rc1/oci-image-spec.html` |
| Sigstore Cosign and Rekor | Artifact signing, verification, identity-bound signatures, attestations, and transparency log patterns. | `https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/verifying/verify/` and `https://docs.sigstore.dev/logging/overview/` |
| SLSA v1.2 | Provenance, build/source tracks, incremental supply-chain security levels, and attestation framing. | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` |
| SPDX 3.0.1 | International SBOM/BOM standard, profiles, software and AI transparency metadata. | `https://spdx.dev/use/specifications/` and `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/scope/` |
| CycloneDX | Machine-readable BOM object model, components, services, dependencies, vulnerabilities, attestations, lifecycle stages, and ML-BOM support. | `https://cyclonedx.org/specification/overview` |
| Model cards | Intended use, limitations, datasets, evaluation, training details, ethics, and discoverability metadata. | `https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/model-cards` |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | Publisher/project health signals that can inform verified publisher policy without pretending to be a complete trust oracle. | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` |

The completion rule is not "Kolm uses every standard literally." The rule is: every `.kolm` artifact must be inspectable through familiar trust concepts. A buyer should be able to answer the same questions they ask of an OCI image, npm package, Hugging Face model, signed binary, SBOM, and SLSA provenance statement.

#### Artifact Passport Contract

Every artifact should render one canonical passport used by API, account UI, marketplace UI, docs, CLI/TUI, install commands, audit exports, and search metadata. The passport is a product object, not just a manifest dump.

```json
{
  "artifact_id": "kolm:namespace/name",
  "artifact_digest": "sha256:...",
  "artifact_version": "1.2.3",
  "artifact_media_type": "application/vnd.kolm.artifact.v1+zip",
  "manifest_schema": "kolm-artifact-manifest-v1",
  "publisher": {
    "publisher_id": "pub_...",
    "display_name": "Example Publisher",
    "verified_state": "unverified|verified_identity|verified_org|verified_partner|suspended",
    "verification_evidence": ["identity", "domain", "billing", "legal", "security_review"]
  },
  "signature": {
    "algorithm": "Ed25519|sigstore-bundle|customer-kms",
    "signature_digest": "sha256:...",
    "signed_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "key_id": "key_...",
    "transparency_log": "rekor|private-log|none",
    "offline_verifiable": true
  },
  "receipt_chain": {
    "capture_receipts": [],
    "training_receipts": [],
    "eval_receipts": [],
    "publish_receipts": [],
    "install_receipts": []
  },
  "bill_of_materials": {
    "spdx": "/artifact/spdx.json",
    "cyclonedx": "/artifact/cyclonedx.json",
    "ai_bom": "/artifact/ai-bom.json"
  },
  "provenance": {
    "slsa_level": "unknown|build-l1|build-l2|build-l3",
    "builder": "local|ci|kolm-cloud|customer-byoc",
    "source_refs": [],
    "dataset_hashes": [],
    "model_provider_versions": [],
    "reproducibility": "deterministic|seeded|best_effort|external"
  },
  "model_card": {
    "intended_use": [],
    "limitations": [],
    "datasets": [],
    "evaluation": [],
    "risk_notes": []
  },
  "quality_card": {
    "k_score": null,
    "k_score_version": "k-score-v...",
    "benchmark_scope": "private_holdout|public_fixture|synthetic|live_frontier|not_claimed",
    "confidence_interval": null,
    "regression_policy": "block|warn|allow"
  },
  "rights_and_policy": {
    "license": "MIT|Apache-2.0|commercial|custom|unknown",
    "training_rights": "declared|verified|restricted|unknown",
    "privacy_class": "public|internal|sensitive|phi|pii|tenant_private",
    "safety_policy": "allowed|review_required|quarantined|blocked",
    "export_controls": []
  },
  "runtime_targets": [
    {
      "target": "node|wasm|gguf|onnx|ios|android|browser|edge-worker|gpu-server",
      "status": "supported|experimental|blocked|deprecated",
      "min_version": "..."
    }
  ],
  "dependencies": {
    "artifacts": [],
    "models": [],
    "tools": [],
    "services": [],
    "datasets": []
  },
  "compatibility": {
    "kolm_min": "0.2.6",
    "kolm_max": null,
    "breaking_changes": [],
    "migration_guide": null
  },
  "lifecycle": {
    "channel": "dev|beta|stable|enterprise-lts|private",
    "state": "draft|signed|staged|published|deprecated|revoked|quarantined|superseded",
    "published_at": null,
    "deprecated_at": null,
    "revoked_at": null,
    "successor": null
  },
  "install": {
    "cli": "kolm artifact install kolm:namespace/name@1.2.3",
    "api": "POST /v1/registry/install",
    "account_url": "/account/artifacts/..."
  },
  "marketplace": {
    "listing_state": "unlisted|private|review|listed|suspended|removed",
    "price_model": "free|paid|usage|enterprise|private",
    "entitlement_required": true,
    "refund_policy": "not_applicable|self_serve|support|contract"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "manifest_url": "/registry/.../manifest.json",
    "signature_url": "/registry/.../signature.json",
    "provenance_url": "/registry/.../provenance.json",
    "sbom_url": "/registry/.../sbom.json",
    "model_card_url": "/marketplace/.../model-card",
    "audit_export_url": "/account/artifacts/.../audit"
  }
}
```

#### Trust State Machine

Artifact state must be finite, visible, and enforced. "Verified" cannot be a decoration.

| State | Meaning | Allowed actions | Blocked actions |
|---|---|---|---|
| `draft` | Artifact metadata exists but bytes or manifest are incomplete. | edit, local inspect, local delete | publish, install from marketplace, verified badge |
| `local` | Artifact can run locally but has no publishable signature/evidence. | local run, local eval, sign | public listing, verified publisher claims |
| `signed` | Bytes, manifest, and signature bind together. | verify, stage, export, airgap bundle | verified badge unless policy and publisher gates pass |
| `verified-local` | Local production-readiness gates pass, but public registry trust is not complete. | show "local proof" badge, upload for review | show marketplace "Verified" badge |
| `registry-staged` | Artifact uploaded to registry and awaiting automated checks. | scan, policy review, owner edit | public install, search indexing |
| `policy-review` | Human or automated trust review is active. | approve, reject, quarantine, request changes | paid listing, featured placement |
| `published` | Artifact is listed and installable under current policy. | install, update, rate, report, audit | mutate bytes under same digest |
| `quarantined` | Artifact has a suspected issue. | investigate, notify, block new installs, export evidence | new installs, featured placement |
| `revoked` | Artifact is unsafe, unlawful, invalid, or withdrawn. | audit, explain, migrate, block install | run by default, reinstall, advertise |
| `deprecated` | Artifact is valid but should be replaced. | install with warning, migrate, pin | default recommendation |
| `superseded` | New artifact replaces it. | diff, rollback if allowed, migrate | new default installs |

#### Marketplace Listing Redlines

- No listing may show "Verified" unless artifact bytes, manifest, signature, publisher identity, policy review, and current verification state all pass.
- No listing may show a benchmark, K-Score, quality rank, or "best" claim unless the passport links benchmark scope, date, dataset, evaluator, and raw report.
- No listing may expose a download/install button unless license, rights, privacy class, runtime targets, and safety policy are known or explicitly marked as restricted.
- No listing may accept payment unless entitlement, refund, invoice, tax, support, takedown, abuse-report, and revocation behavior exist.
- No listing may contain ratings, review counts, star scores, customer logos, install counts, or "popular" badges unless they come from real telemetry with anti-abuse controls.
- No publisher may be called "partner", "verified", "certified", "HIPAA", "SOC 2", "enterprise-ready", or "trusted" without a visible evidence basis.
- No artifact may silently mutate under an existing digest. Tag changes must resolve to immutable digests and preserve prior install reproducibility.
- No revoked or quarantined artifact may be recommended, indexed as installable, or used as a default dependency.
- No imported artifact from Hugging Face, OCI, GGUF, ONNX, safetensors, npm, GitHub, or customer upload may skip malware, license, rights, provenance, and unsafe-code checks merely because the source is popular.
- No account UI may hide dependency, runtime, license, rights, or revocation risk behind a hover-only popout.

#### Registry API Completion Contract

The registry API should support these lifecycle capabilities before the ecosystem is complete:

| Capability | Required behavior |
|---|---|
| Push | Upload bytes by digest, attach manifest, reject mutable digest collisions, support resumable upload for large artifacts. |
| Pull | Resolve namespace/name/tag/channel to digest, enforce entitlement, return manifest, bytes, signature, provenance, SBOM, and cache policy. |
| Verify | Validate digest, signature, publisher state, provenance, policy, revocation, dependency graph, and runtime compatibility. |
| Inspect | Return artifact passport, model card, K-Score card, SBOM, dependency graph, install commands, and lifecycle state. |
| Diff | Compare two artifact versions across manifest, weights, recipe, runtime targets, dependencies, evals, policy, and expected behavior. |
| Promote | Move artifact between dev, beta, stable, enterprise-LTS, and private channels with gates and rollback. |
| Deprecate | Mark old versions, set successor, migration guide, warning severity, and sunset date. |
| Revoke | Block unsafe or invalid artifacts, publish reason code, notify installs, and preserve audit evidence. |
| Entitle | Enforce free, paid, team, enterprise, private, and BYOC licenses without leaking tenant information. |
| Search | Query by task, runtime, license, publisher, verified state, quality scope, price, privacy class, and dependency safety. |
| Import | Ingest external model/package artifacts with source provenance and conversion evidence. |
| Export | Emit `.kolm`, OCI-style manifest, SBOM, provenance, model card, and audit bundle for external systems. |

#### Artifact UX Completion Contract

Every surface that touches artifacts must answer the same user questions with the same vocabulary:

| Surface | Questions it must answer |
|---|---|
| Marketplace listing | What does it do, who published it, what proof exists, what it costs, where it runs, what it depends on, and what can go wrong? |
| Artifact detail page | What changed, what evidence backs it, how to install, how to verify, what versions exist, what is deprecated, and what is revoked? |
| Account artifact page | Which artifacts does this tenant own, use, pay for, deploy, compose, pin, update, rollback, quarantine, or revoke? |
| CLI | Can I `inspect`, `verify`, `pull`, `install`, `diff`, `run`, `promote`, `deprecate`, `revoke`, and `export` without opening the web UI? |
| TUI | Can operators see artifact state, install risk, dependency graph, and closeout proof in a dense keyboard-first view? |
| Docs | Can a new developer create first artifact, publish privately, verify locally, install in app, and revoke safely? |
| Agents | Can agent/tool artifacts show delegated authority, tool receipts, sandbox policy, memory provenance, and rollback/compensation behavior? |
| Devices/runtimes | Can runtime compatibility, required memory, quantization, target profile, and fallback behavior be inspected before deployment? |
| Enterprise/trust | Can procurement export artifact passport, SBOM, provenance, policy, incidents, audit logs, and data-processing evidence? |

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `33.1` Artifact identity, manifests, media types, digests, signatures, receipts, SBOMs, and provenance | Every `.kolm` artifact has immutable digest identity, canonical media type, manifest schema, signed receipt chain, offline verification, SBOM/AI-BOM, SLSA-style provenance, model card, K-Score card, rights policy, and artifact passport. |
| `33.2` Registry API, namespaces, versioning, channels, compatibility, dependency graph, diff, and revocation | Registry APIs can push, pull, inspect, verify, diff, promote, deprecate, revoke, import, export, and resolve namespaces/tags/channels to immutable digests with dependency and compatibility proof. |
| `33.3` Marketplace listings, search, install, entitlements, pricing, ratings, reviews, payouts, and takedowns | Marketplace is not just pages. It supports honest listing state, search filters, install flows, entitlement/pricing, publisher payouts where applicable, abuse reports, takedowns, revocation notices, and anti-fake metrics. |
| `33.4` Publisher verification, moderation, malware/abuse scanning, licenses, rights, safety, and policy review | Publisher identity, domain/org verification, security health, upload scanning, license/rights checks, unsafe code detection, policy review, quarantine, appeals, and trust badge rules are enforced and visible. |
| `33.5` Artifact UX across public, account, CLI/TUI, docs, agents, devices, runtimes, and deployment flows | Artifact state and actions use one vocabulary across marketplace, account, docs, CLI, TUI, API, agents, devices, runtimes, deploy flows, audit exports, and search metadata. |
| `33.6` Ecosystem evidence: conformance tests, import/export, runtime compatibility, provenance verification, and adoption metrics | Completion evidence includes artifact conformance suite, manifest fixtures, signature verification tests, registry smoke, import/export tests, runtime compatibility matrix, adoption telemetry, and production install/revoke drills. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-REG-001` | `33.1` | Define the `.kolm` artifact media type and passport schema. | API, CLI, account, marketplace, docs, audit export, and search metadata consume one passport object. |
| `W-REG-002` | `33.1` | Add manifest/schema conformance fixtures. | Valid and invalid `.kolm` artifacts cover digest, manifest, model card, K-Score card, SBOM, provenance, policy, runtime targets, and dependency fields. |
| `W-REG-003` | `33.1` | Add signature bundle policy. | Artifacts support local Ed25519 verification, customer key verification, and Sigstore-style bundle mapping without claiming public transparency unless configured. |
| `W-REG-004` | `33.1` | Generate SBOM/AI-BOM for every artifact. | Each registry artifact links SPDX or CycloneDX output plus model/dataset/dependency/service components. |
| `W-REG-005` | `33.1` | Generate SLSA-style provenance for compile/distill/publish. | Provenance records builder, source refs, dataset hashes, teacher/model versions, eval version, and reproducibility class. |
| `W-REG-006` | `33.2` | Implement immutable registry identity. | Tags/channels resolve to digests; digest collisions are rejected; mutable byte replacement under the same digest is impossible. |
| `W-REG-007` | `33.2` | Complete registry push/pull/inspect/verify APIs. | API tests prove push, pull, inspect, verify, entitlement checks, secret redaction, and artifact passport parity. |
| `W-REG-008` | `33.2` | Complete artifact diff and compatibility gates. | Diff detects manifest, behavior, runtime, dependency, eval, policy, and breaking-change differences before promotion. |
| `W-REG-009` | `33.2` | Add deprecation, revocation, quarantine, and successor policy. | Revoked/quarantined artifacts cannot be newly installed; deprecated artifacts warn and link migration guidance. |
| `W-REG-010` | `33.3` | Replace static marketplace cards with listing contracts. | Every listing has digest, publisher state, proof state, price state, license, runtime targets, install state, and trust state. |
| `W-REG-011` | `33.3` | Add marketplace install and entitlement proof. | Free/paid/private/enterprise installs enforce entitlement, log receipt, and show refund/support/takedown policy. |
| `W-REG-012` | `33.3` | Add honest marketplace ranking and metrics. | Search, featured, popular, ratings, reviews, downloads, and badges are backed by telemetry or intentionally hidden. |
| `W-REG-013` | `33.4` | Add verified publisher workflow. | Publisher verification distinguishes identity, domain, organization, partner, security-reviewed, suspended, and revoked states. |
| `W-REG-014` | `33.4` | Add upload scanning and moderation queue. | Uploaded artifacts run malware, unsafe-code, license, privacy, rights, prompt-injection, and model-risk checks before public listing. |
| `W-REG-015` | `33.4` | Add abuse report, takedown, appeal, and incident flow. | Marketplace users can report artifacts; operators can quarantine, notify, revoke, appeal, and export incident evidence. |
| `W-REG-016` | `33.5` | Normalize artifact actions across UI/API/CLI/TUI. | `inspect`, `verify`, `install`, `run`, `diff`, `promote`, `deprecate`, `revoke`, and `export` mean the same thing everywhere. |
| `W-REG-017` | `33.5` | Add artifact account dashboard. | Tenant view shows owned, installed, deployed, paid, pinned, stale, deprecated, quarantined, revoked, and update-available artifacts. |
| `W-REG-018` | `33.5` | Add docs quick paths for artifact lifecycle. | Docs cover create, sign, verify, publish privately, publish publicly, install, update, rollback, revoke, audit, and airgap export. |
| `W-REG-019` | `33.6` | Add registry conformance and production smoke. | Local and production probes verify registry pack, public listing, API passport, signature, install, revoke, and rollback paths. |
| `W-REG-020` | `33.6` | Add ecosystem import/export compatibility. | OCI-style export, Hugging Face/model-card import, GGUF/ONNX/safetensors import, and airgap bundle verification are covered by tests and docs. |

#### Node 33 Relax Redline

Node `33` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from product behavior and current evidence:

- What immutable digest identifies this artifact, and can every install prove it received those exact bytes?
- Who published it, what verification state do they have, and what evidence supports that state?
- What is in the artifact: manifest, model card, K-Score card, SBOM/AI-BOM, provenance, runtime targets, dependencies, rights, and policy?
- What signature verifies it, what key or identity signed it, can it be verified offline, and is there transparency-log or private-log evidence if claimed?
- What version, channel, compatibility range, dependency graph, and breaking-change policy govern updates?
- What happens if the artifact is deprecated, superseded, quarantined, or revoked?
- Can a user inspect, verify, install, run, diff, promote, rollback, deprecate, revoke, and export it through API, account, CLI/TUI, and docs?
- Does the marketplace listing show only real badges, real proof, real ratings, real pricing, real install state, and real publisher status?
- Can enterprise users export the artifact passport for procurement, security review, audit, compliance, and incident response?
- Do production smoke tests prove registry, marketplace, install, verification, entitlement, revocation, and rollback on the deployed commit?

The relax condition for node `33` is not "marketplace pages exist." It is: `.kolm` artifacts are distributed, installed, verified, revoked, explained, monetized, and audited as trustworthy product objects across the full Kolm ecosystem.

#### Node 33 Artifact Ecosystem Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. Artifact registry, marketplace, verified publishers, artifact install UX, trust states, revocation, model-card evidence, SBOM/AI-BOM, signatures, provenance, artifact dependency graphs, and `.kolm` lifecycle governance belong to node `33`. Node `22` owns supply-chain release artifacts, node `27` owns open standardization, node `41` owns portability/import/export, node `42` owns evaluation evidence, node `16` owns commercial entitlement and payouts, node `10` owns security/privacy policy, node `23` owns rights/legal claims, and node `51` owns marketplace/demo media proof.

##### Node `33.0` Current Repo Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Marketplace pages | `public/marketplace` currently contains `18` nested HTML pages, plus top-level `public/marketplace.html`; generated OG cards exist for marketplace listings. | Public marketplace surface exists, but completion requires every listing to be generated from an artifact passport rather than page-specific drift. |
| Registry pack | `public/registry-pack` has `8` files: `6` `.kolm` artifacts, one seed file, and `manifest.json`; the manifest has `8` artifact entries. | Static pack is a credible fixture seed, not a full registry. The V2 bar is immutable digest identity, lifecycle state, signatures, SBOM/AI-BOM, provenance, revocation, and install receipts for every entry. |
| Manifest quality | `public/registry-pack/manifest.json` carries useful fields such as owner, handle, vertical, summary, task, base model, size, sha256, K-Score, license, tags, and updated date; some task strings show mojibake-style `??` separators in terminal output. | Registry manifest needs schema validation, text encoding checks, claim scope, and visible listing parity before it can be treated as production-quality catalog data. |
| Marketplace implementation | `src/marketplace.js` reads hashes and K-Scores from the registry pack, imports signature helpers, distinguishes provisional readiness from live verified overlays, and deliberately strips sync-layer `Verified` badges. | This honesty pattern is valuable. V2 must make it universal across UI, API, CLI, docs, JSON-LD, search, and account surfaces so no stale verified badge leaks back in. |
| Registry and trust modules | Current tree includes `src/registry.js`, `src/marketplace-routes.js`, `src/marketplace-store.js`, `src/marketplace-ratings.js`, `src/marketplace-payouts.js`, `src/publisher-verification.js`, `src/verified.js`, `src/sigstore.js`, and `src/sbom-emit.js`. | The codebase has many primitives; V2 needs one lifecycle contract, one state machine, one policy vocabulary, and one proof command per lifecycle transition. |
| Artifact core modules | `src/artifact.js`, `src/artifact-runner.js`, `src/artifact-lineage.js`, `src/artifact-diff.js`, `src/artifact-dependency-graph.js`, and provenance modules exist. | Registry must treat dependency/diff/lineage/provenance as first-class listing and install risk, not hidden developer details. |
| CLI surface | CLI help exposes `inspect`, `verify`, `diff`, `publish`, `pull`, `hub list|show`, `ship`, `sigstore-attest`, `sbom`, and artifact commands. | CLI vocabulary is rich but must be normalized to the artifact passport: every web/account action needs a CLI equivalent and every CLI action needs API/docs/account state parity. |
| Test coverage | Tests include marketplace, registry, sigstore, Rekor, artifact verification, runtime consistency, SBOM, route docs, and marketplace gate waves. | Good coverage exists, but V2 needs an end-to-end conformance gate that proves artifact lifecycle transitions, install, quarantine, revoke, rollback, and production smoke. |
| Badge risk | Seed marketplace entries include terms such as `HIPAA`, `BAA`, and `Verified` while implementation comments describe stripping or overlaying some verified state. | Trust badges must become evidence-scoped claims with current policy source, proof link, and visible state. A seed label is not enough. |

##### Node `33.1` Current Standards Delta

| Standard or ecosystem baseline | Relevant current concept | Kolm V2 interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| OCI Distribution Spec and OCI Image/Artifact concepts | Content-addressed distribution, manifests, descriptors, tags, registry API, conformance tests, and generic artifact distribution. | `.kolm` should not copy OCI blindly, but must provide digest identity, media type, immutable bytes, tag/channel resolution, referrers/evidence, and conformance semantics users already understand. |
| Sigstore Cosign and Rekor | Signature verification, identity-bound signatures, attestations, and transparency log patterns. | Kolm should support local Ed25519/offline verification plus optional Sigstore/Rekor or private transparency logs without claiming public log inclusion unless proof exists. |
| SLSA v1.2 | Source/build provenance, build levels, tracks, and attestation framing. | Compile/distill/publish should emit SLSA-style provenance fields: builder, source refs, dataset hashes, model/provider versions, environment, and reproducibility class. |
| SPDX and CycloneDX | SBOM/BOM representation for software, hardware, services, dependencies, vulnerabilities, attestations, declarations, and ML-related inventory. | Every marketplace-grade artifact needs SBOM/AI-BOM output that procurement and security can inspect, with incomplete BOMs explicitly marked. |
| Hugging Face model cards | Intended use, limitations, training/eval data, license, tasks, metrics, and discoverable metadata. | A `.kolm` passport must include model-card-like sections even when the artifact is rules, recipe, router, distilled model, agent, or workflow. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | Project/publisher health signals for open-source security posture. | Verified publisher status can use security-health signals as inputs but must not present them as a complete trust guarantee. |
| OWASP Machine Learning Security Top 10 | ML-specific risks around supply chain, poisoning, model attacks, output integrity, and abuse. | Artifact intake and marketplace review must include ML-specific safety checks, not just file malware scanning. |
| Package registry conventions | README, version, license, changelog, install command, ownership, deprecation, vulnerability, and maintainership norms. | Marketplace listings need clear docs, semantic version/channel policy, deprecation/revocation notices, install command, ownership, support, and changelog. |

##### Node `33.2` Artifact Passport V2 Delta

The V1 passport is the right product object. V2 adds these required fields before marketplace or registry can be considered production-grade:

```json
{
  "artifact_id": "kolm:namespace/name",
  "digest": "sha256:...",
  "media_type": "application/vnd.kolm.artifact.v1+zip",
  "source_of_truth": "registry|local|customer-private|airgap",
  "publisher_trust": {
    "publisher_id": "pub_...",
    "identity_state": "anonymous|email|domain|org|contracted|suspended",
    "security_state": "unknown|basic|reviewed|monitored|incident",
    "claim_badges": [
      {
        "label": "Verified",
        "state": "hidden|provisional|visible|revoked",
        "evidence_id": "publisher-verification:..."
      }
    ]
  },
  "artifact_trust": {
    "signature_state": "unsigned|local-ed25519|customer-kms|sigstore-bundle|private-log",
    "provenance_state": "missing|local|slsa-style|externally-attested",
    "sbom_state": "missing|partial|complete|complete-with-vex",
    "policy_state": "unknown|allowed|review-required|quarantined|revoked"
  },
  "marketplace_state": {
    "listing_state": "unlisted|private|review|listed|featured|suspended|removed",
    "install_state": "blocked|warning|required-entitlement|allowed",
    "commercial_state": "free|paid|enterprise-contract|private",
    "support_state": "none|community|publisher|kolm|enterprise"
  },
  "runtime_state": {
    "targets": [],
    "memory_budget": null,
    "latency_budget": null,
    "device_fit": [],
    "fallbacks": []
  },
  "risk_state": {
    "privacy_class": "public|internal|pii|phi|tenant-private|unknown",
    "license_state": "declared|verified|restricted|unknown",
    "export_state": "allowed|restricted|unknown",
    "ml_security_checks": []
  },
  "evidence_links": {
    "manifest": "/registry/.../manifest.json",
    "signature": "/registry/.../signature.json",
    "provenance": "/registry/.../provenance.json",
    "sbom": "/registry/.../sbom.json",
    "model_card": "/marketplace/.../model-card",
    "install_receipt": "/account/artifacts/.../receipts",
    "revocation_status": "/registry/.../revocation.json"
  }
}
```

Passport completion rule: if a field is unknown, the UI/API/CLI must show `unknown`, `partial`, `provisional`, or `blocked`; it must not infer trust from a pretty listing, a seed catalog entry, or a green test that covers only local fixtures.

##### Node `33.3` Registry Lifecycle Contract V2

The registry needs one state transition table used by API, account, marketplace, CLI, TUI, docs, and tests.

| Transition | Required input proof | Required output proof | Failure behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| `local -> signed` | artifact bytes, canonical manifest, signing key, K-Score/quality scope if claimed | digest, signature block, receipt, verifier output | refuse if manifest hash or byte digest is unstable |
| `signed -> staged` | upload auth, namespace entitlement, digest, manifest, signature, publisher ID | registry upload receipt, immutable digest row, staged passport | quarantine if upload contains malware, unsafe code, unknown license, or mismatched digest |
| `staged -> review` | completed automated scan packet | policy queue item, scan results, reviewer checklist | block listing until review state is explicit |
| `review -> listed` | publisher state, artifact state, rights, license, safety, runtime, support, price state | public listing row, install policy, search metadata, docs links | list as private or unlisted if any required proof is missing |
| `listed -> installed` | entitlement, policy, runtime compatibility, revocation check, user confirmation when risky | install receipt, pinned digest, local verification, audit log | block or warn depending on policy severity |
| `installed -> updated` | successor digest, diff, compatibility, migration policy, tenant approval if required | update receipt, rollback pointer, account event | require explicit approval for breaking changes |
| `listed -> deprecated` | reason, successor, sunset date, migration guide | visible warning, CLI warning, API state, search demotion | prevent silent deprecation |
| `listed -> quarantined` | abuse report, scan finding, legal/security incident, publisher issue | blocked new installs, incident row, notifications, operator review | fail closed for new installs and featured placement |
| `quarantined -> revoked` | confirmed safety/legal/identity/signature issue | revocation record, install block, tenant notification, audit export | preserve evidence and rollback/migration guidance |
| `revoked -> restored` | appeal and signed operator decision | restored state, incident closure, immutable audit trail | require elevated approval and public explanation where public listing was affected |

##### Node `33.4` Marketplace And Account UX Contract V2

The marketplace is an enterprise utility, not a gallery. Following the UI/UX checklist, the experience should be dense, legible, keyboardable, and operationally honest.

Every listing card must show:

- task/outcome in plain language;
- publisher and trust state;
- digest or version pin;
- price/entitlement state;
- install state;
- runtime/device fit;
- K-Score scope and date when shown;
- license and privacy class;
- policy state: allowed, warning, review required, quarantined, revoked;
- primary action and disabled-state reason.

Every artifact detail page must show:

- artifact passport summary;
- install command and API equivalent;
- signature verification result;
- SBOM/AI-BOM/provenance/model-card links;
- dependency graph;
- version/channel history;
- diff from prior version;
- deprecation/revocation state;
- runtime compatibility matrix;
- support/contact/takedown path.

Every account artifact page must show:

- owned artifacts;
- installed artifacts;
- deployed artifacts;
- pinned versions;
- update availability;
- deprecated/quarantined/revoked warnings;
- billing/entitlement state;
- tenant policy blocks;
- receipts and audit exports;
- rollback and revoke actions.

No trust, install, price, rights, or runtime risk may be hidden behind hover-only popovers, decorative badges, or ambiguous colors.

##### Node `33.5` Registry And Marketplace Verification Gates V2

| Gate | What it must prove |
|---|---|
| `verify:artifact-passport` | Every registry-pack artifact emits a complete or explicitly partial passport with digest, publisher, signature, SBOM/provenance/model-card, policy, runtime, and lifecycle fields. |
| `verify:registry-conformance` | Push/pull/inspect/verify/diff/promote/deprecate/revoke/entitle/search/import/export behavior works against local fixtures and rejects invalid state transitions. |
| `verify:marketplace-trust` | Public listings cannot show verified/compliance/benchmark/ratings/install badges unless the passport and evidence source allow them. |
| `verify:publisher` | Publisher identity states, domain/org checks, suspended/revoked states, and badge visibility are enforced consistently. |
| `verify:artifact-security` | Uploaded/imported artifacts are scanned for malware, unsafe code, prompt injection, license/rights gaps, private data, and known ML security risks. |
| `verify:install-receipts` | Installs write pinned digest receipts, entitlement proof, verifier output, runtime target, and rollback pointer without leaking tenant secrets. |
| `verify:revocation-drill` | Quarantine and revocation block new installs, warn existing tenants, update marketplace/search/API/CLI/account state, and preserve audit evidence. |
| `verify:registry-prod` | Deployed marketplace, registry pack, API routes, public listing, account listing, CLI pull/install/verify, and revoke smoke pass on the current release. |

##### Node `33.6` Additive Work Tickets V2

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-REG-V2-001` | `33.2` | Generate artifact passports for all `public/registry-pack` entries. | All `8` manifest entries produce passport JSON with explicit complete/partial/unknown fields and no mojibake text. |
| `W-REG-V2-002` | `33.2` | Add registry-pack encoding and schema lint. | Manifest rejects invalid encoding, missing digest, missing license, unsupported badge, unknown runtime, and unsupported K-Score scope. |
| `W-REG-V2-003` | `33.2` | Bind marketplace pages to passports. | Every public marketplace listing renders from passport data and cannot drift from manifest/signature/provenance fields. |
| `W-REG-V2-004` | `33.3` | Implement lifecycle transition checks. | Tests cover local, signed, staged, review, listed, installed, updated, deprecated, quarantined, revoked, and restored transitions. |
| `W-REG-V2-005` | `33.3` | Add install receipt contract. | CLI/API/account installs write pinned digest, entitlement, verifier output, runtime target, user, tenant, timestamp, and rollback pointer. |
| `W-REG-V2-006` | `33.3` | Add revocation and quarantine drill. | New installs fail closed, existing installs warn, marketplace/search/API/CLI/account reflect state, and audit packet is preserved. |
| `W-REG-V2-007` | `33.4` | Add trust-badge evidence gate. | `Verified`, `HIPAA`, `BAA`, partner, certified, popular, benchmark, and featured badges require evidence IDs or are hidden. |
| `W-REG-V2-008` | `33.4` | Add publisher verification matrix. | Publisher states distinguish anonymous, email, domain, org, contracted, security-reviewed, suspended, and revoked. |
| `W-REG-V2-009` | `33.4` | Add artifact intake security scan. | Import/upload path checks malware, unsafe code, license/rights, private data, prompt injection, dependency risk, and ML security risks. |
| `W-REG-V2-010` | `33.4` | Add marketplace operational filters. | Users can filter by task, runtime, device fit, license, privacy class, price, publisher trust, K-Score scope, policy state, and dependency risk. |
| `W-REG-V2-011` | `33.4` | Add account artifact risk dashboard. | Account UI shows installed/pinned/deployed artifacts, update risk, entitlement, revocation, dependency risk, and audit export. |
| `W-REG-V2-012` | `33.5` | Normalize artifact verbs everywhere. | API, CLI, TUI, account, docs, and marketplace use the same semantics for inspect, verify, install, run, diff, promote, deprecate, revoke, and export. |
| `W-REG-V2-013` | `33.5` | Add dependency graph UI and API parity. | Artifact detail and account pages expose dependency graph, affected artifacts, breaking changes, and rollout risk. |

| `W-REG-V2-018` | `33.5` | Add production marketplace smoke. | Deployed site proves listing, detail, passport, install disabled/enabled states, account handoff, CLI pull/verify, and revoke warning. |

| `W-REG-V2-020` | `33.6` | Add final registry ecosystem release packet. | Packet includes registry manifest, passports, signatures, SBOM/provenance/model cards, marketplace screenshots, trust-badge proof, install receipts, revocation drill, and production smoke. |

##### Node `33` V2 Relax Redline

Node `33` can relax only when these are true:

- every marketplace listing renders from an artifact passport;
- every registry-pack artifact has schema-clean text, immutable digest, license, K-Score scope, runtime target, publisher state, and lifecycle state;
- trust badges are evidence-backed or hidden;
- install buttons are disabled or warned when entitlement, rights, signature, runtime, policy, or revocation state is incomplete;
- CLI/API/account/TUI/docs use the same artifact verbs and state transitions;
- artifact dependency, diff, SBOM/AI-BOM, provenance, model card, signature, and receipt evidence are visible where users decide whether to install or deploy;
- quarantine and revocation drills are proven locally and in production smoke;
- registry, marketplace, search metadata, AI-readable files, and docs never present static fixture artifacts as fully production-trusted unless proof exists;
- enterprise audit export can reconstruct who installed what digest, under which entitlement, with which verification result, and what happened if it was revoked.

### Event Delivery, Notifications, Webhooks, Emails, Status, And Customer-Visible Async State Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not patch `src/notifications.js`, `src/email.js`, webhook routes, Stripe handlers, status pages, docs, account pages, delivery queues, tests, or generated API assets directly. It defines the event-delivery contract that implementation lanes must satisfy before Kolm can be called operationally complete.

Node `34` exists because Kolm is an asynchronous product. Capture thresholds, compile jobs, distill jobs, artifact signing, marketplace review, drift alerts, billing state, approval queues, deployment health, security incidents, retention purges, and support handoffs cannot rely on users refreshing dashboards. If users cannot trust Kolm to tell them what happened, what failed, what requires action, and whether delivery succeeded, then the product feels unfinished even when the backend action completed.

This node extends, but does not replace:

- node `15` for measurement and telemetry;
- node `16` for billing/customer lifecycle events;
- node `19` for reliability alerts;
- node `25` for customer lifecycle communications;
- node `26` for admin/operator queues;
- node `29` for deploy/status/incident communications.

#### Current Repo Event-Delivery Evidence

The current tree already has meaningful primitives:

| Area | Current evidence | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Notification engine | `src/notifications.js` supports threshold preferences, WebPush subscriptions, VAPID config, allowlisted push endpoints, threshold state, email fallback, and test alerts. | Good capture-threshold foundation; completion requires generic event taxonomy, durable delivery state, diagnostics, replay, and multi-surface parity. |
| Transactional email | `src/email.js` wraps Resend and includes welcome, billing activated, billing failed, setup-token, and one-time key reveal behavior. | Good security direction, but completion requires global email catalog, template evidence, deliverability state, unsubscribe/preference rules, and no raw secret leakage. |
| Notification API routes | `public/docs/api-routes.json` lists `/v1/notifications/config`, preferences, push-subscriptions, state, and test routes; `tests/wave515-notifications-route-docs.test.js` locks generated docs/OpenAPI coverage. | Route docs are not enough. Delivery attempts, retries, failure state, replay, event history, and support visibility need first-class product contracts. |
| Status subscription | `/v1/status/subscribe`, `public/status.html`, and `public/status.rss` exist. | Status is a channel, not a static page. It must be tied to component health, subscriber preferences, incident updates, delivery state, RSS, email, and webhooks. |
| Webhook docs | `public/docs/webhooks.html` describes event types, HMAC signing, retry policy, and dead-letter queue expectations. | Docs describe more than the scan proves. Completion requires implemented event catalog, endpoint management UI, signed delivery tests, retries, DLQ, replay, and production attempts. |
| Drift and alerting | `src/drift-alert.js`, `src/drift-alert-w813.js`, `src/drift-alert-store.js`, `public/account/drift-alert.html`, docs, and tests exist. | Drift events should use the same delivery substrate as capture, status, incidents, and approvals instead of one-off alert paths. |
| Billing webhook | `src/router.js` imports billing email helpers and exposes `/v1/stripe/webhook`; the blueprint already tracks Stripe webhook/idempotency needs under node `16`. | Incoming vendor webhook reliability and outgoing customer webhook reliability need separate ledgers but shared raw-body, signature, idempotency, audit, and replay rules. |
| Product surface ownership | `docs/product-surfaces.json` names notifications under Governance, Compliance, Admin, Audit, Privacy, Trace, And Notifications and includes notification smoke probes. | The surface is owned, but the master spec needs event delivery as cross-product infrastructure. |

#### External Baseline For Event Delivery

| External baseline | What it contributes to node `34` | Source |
|---|---|---|
| CloudEvents | Vendor-neutral event metadata such as `id`, `source`, `type`, `specversion`, `subject`, `time`, and content type. | `https://cloudevents.io/` and `https://github.com/cloudevents/spec` |
| HTTP Message Signatures RFC 9421 | Standardized signing and verification over HTTP message components, with replay and coverage considerations. | `https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc9421.html` |
| Standard Webhooks | Practical webhook headers for message id, timestamp, and signature, plus replay protection and interoperability patterns. | `https://github.com/standard-webhooks/standard-webhooks/blob/main/spec/standard-webhooks.md` |
| W3C Push API and RFC 8030 Web Push | Browser push subscription and push-service delivery expectations. | `https://www.w3.org/TR/push-api/` and `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8030.html` |
| Stripe webhook docs | Raw-body signature verification, endpoint secrets, retry behavior, and webhook hardening for billing events. | `https://docs.stripe.com/webhooks` |
| GitHub webhook validation docs | HMAC-SHA256 validation, constant-time comparison, UTF-8/body preservation, and secret management. | `https://docs.github.com/en/webhooks/using-webhooks/validating-webhook-deliveries` |
| Atlassian webhook retry docs | Retry count visibility and randomized backoff patterns for delivery attempts. | `https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/software/webhooks/` |

The completion rule is: Kolm events must be durable, signed where they cross trust boundaries, replayable where safe, diagnosable by support, quiet by default where consent matters, and visible enough that customers understand both product progress and delivery failure.

#### Canonical Event Envelope

Every emitted product event should have one canonical envelope before channel-specific adaptation. It can map to CloudEvents, internal audit rows, webhook payloads, account inbox items, email templates, RSS entries, WebPush payloads, and CLI/TUI notifications.

```json
{
  "specversion": "1.0",
  "id": "evt_...",
  "type": "kolm.capture.threshold_crossed",
  "source": "kolm://capture",
  "subject": "tenant/tenant_.../namespace/support",
  "time": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "datacontenttype": "application/json",
  "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
  "actor": {
    "type": "system|user|admin|provider|job|webhook",
    "id": "actor_or_system_id"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "class": "public|tenant_private|sensitive|pii|phi|secret|security",
    "redaction": "none|required|applied|blocked",
    "customer_visible": true
  },
  "severity": "info|success|warning|action_required|critical",
  "surface": "capture|compile|distill|artifact|registry|billing|status|security|approval|drift|support",
  "object": {
    "type": "namespace|job|artifact|invoice|subscription|approval|incident|deployment|capture_row",
    "id": "object_id",
    "digest": "sha256_or_null"
  },
  "data": {
    "summary": "Namespace crossed 1000 eligible captures.",
    "metrics": {},
    "links": []
  },
  "routing": {
    "default_channels": ["account_inbox", "webhook", "email"],
    "required_channels": [],
    "suppressed_channels": [],
    "preference_key": "capture_thresholds",
    "rate_limit_key": "tenant_...:capture_thresholds"
  },
  "delivery": {
    "dedupe_key": "tenant_...:capture.threshold:support:1000",
    "idempotency_key": "evt_...",
    "retry_policy": "standard-low|standard-high|incident|billing-critical",
    "ttl_seconds": 1209600
  },
  "audit": {
    "audit_event_id": "audit_...",
    "trace_id": "trace_...",
    "request_id": "req_..."
  }
}
```

#### Event Taxonomy Completion Contract

Kolm should not emit ad hoc strings forever. The event catalog must cover at least:

| Product area | Required event families |
|---|---|
| Capture and routing | capture created, quarantined, redacted, eligible, threshold crossed, provider fallback, route policy blocked, capture forgotten. |
| Distill/train/eval | readiness changed, job queued, job started, phase completed, eval failed, quality gate passed/failed, promotion ready, rollback recommended. |
| Compile/artifact | compile queued, artifact signed, artifact verified, artifact failed verification, artifact published, artifact installed, artifact revoked, artifact deprecated. |
| Marketplace/registry | listing submitted, review requested, approved, rejected, quarantined, takedown requested, entitlement granted, install failed. |
| Billing/entitlement | checkout started, subscription active, payment failed, invoice available, usage threshold crossed, plan changed, entitlement downgraded. |
| Security/compliance | API key rotated, suspicious token use, audit export ready, DSR export ready, retention purge completed, incident opened, customer notice required. |
| Reliability/status | component degraded, incident update, maintenance scheduled, SLO burn alert, cloud/storage/provider readiness changed, rollback started/completed. |
| Approvals/admin | approval requested, reminder, delegated, expired, approved, rejected, overridden, break-glass opened/closed. |
| Support/customer success | support bundle created, enterprise inquiry received, onboarding step completed, trial risk detected, renewal risk detected. |

Each event family must define owner, schema version, producer module, consumer surfaces, retention, privacy class, redaction policy, trigger condition, dedupe key, replay behavior, and evidence tests.

#### Delivery Semantics

| Channel | Required semantics |
|---|---|
| Account inbox/activity | Durable tenant-visible record with status, severity, object link, read/unread state, owner, and next action. |
| Webhooks | HTTPS endpoint validation, signed payload, message id, timestamp, replay protection, idempotency key, attempt log, retry schedule, DLQ, manual replay, endpoint disable policy, and payload preview. |
| Email | Template registry, transactional vs marketing classification, preference/opt-out, bounce/suppression handling, provider response logging, secret redaction, and test send. |
| WebPush | User consent, browser support detection, endpoint allowlist/SSRF prevention, VAPID readiness, dead subscription removal, payload minimization, and test alert. |
| RSS/status | Component-backed status feed, incident update cadence, maintenance windows, postmortem links, and no fake "all good" state. |
| CLI/TUI | Pollable event feed, watch mode for jobs/artifacts/incidents, terse terminal rendering, JSON output, and same event IDs as account/API. |
| Admin/support | Tenant delivery trace, failed delivery diagnostics, replay controls, suppression state, incident notification state, and customer-safe export. |

#### Webhook Delivery State Machine

| State | Meaning | Required behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `pending` | Event accepted for delivery. | Persist before sending; return quickly from producer; assign event id and idempotency key. |
| `signing` | Payload and headers are being prepared. | Include timestamp, event id, signature version, digest, and covered fields. |
| `sending` | Attempt is in flight. | Enforce timeout, no private IP/localhost targets, TLS-only, and per-endpoint concurrency limit. |
| `delivered` | Endpoint returned accepted 2xx. | Store status, latency, attempt number, response digest, and delivered time. |
| `retry_scheduled` | Retriable failure occurred. | Backoff with jitter, max attempts, retry reason, next attempt time, and customer-visible state. |
| `dead_lettered` | Retries exhausted or endpoint disabled. | Stop automatic sends, show DLQ, allow safe replay after endpoint fix. |
| `suppressed` | Preferences, rate limit, policy, or unsubscribe blocked send. | Record suppression reason without treating as delivery failure. |
| `replayed` | Operator or customer replayed an event. | Preserve original event id plus replay attempt id and idempotency warning. |
| `disabled` | Endpoint disabled for safety. | Require explicit re-enable and test delivery. |

#### Redlines

- Do not emit an event unless it has owner, type, schema version, privacy class, retention, and consumer contract.
- Do not send secrets, raw API keys, raw prompts, raw completions, PHI/PII, provider tokens, setup tokens, or private incident notes through email, push, or webhooks unless explicitly allowed and redacted by policy.
- Do not parse or mutate incoming vendor webhook bodies before signature verification.
- Do not process incoming vendor webhook events without idempotency keyed by provider event id.
- Do not deliver outbound webhooks without signature, timestamp, event id, endpoint ownership, replay window, and attempt log.
- Do not treat webhooks as exactly-once. Product behavior must be idempotent under duplicate, delayed, parallel, and replayed deliveries.
- Do not retry forever. Every delivery path needs max attempts, dead-letter state, customer-visible diagnostics, and manual replay.
- Do not let user-configured endpoints target localhost, private IP ranges, metadata services, or non-HTTPS URLs.
- Do not show "sent" when a provider accepted a request but the customer cannot prove content, endpoint, attempt, and response.
- Do not mix incident, security, billing, product, and marketing preferences into one undifferentiated email toggle.
- Do not call a status page live unless it is connected to real component health or visibly scoped as static/demo.
- Do not hide failed notifications from support; support must answer "was this sent, skipped, bounced, suppressed, retried, or dead-lettered?"

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `34.1` Event taxonomy, CloudEvents-style envelope, source ownership, routing rules, severity, and privacy class | Every product event has canonical type, schema version, owner, source, subject, severity, privacy class, redaction policy, routing rules, dedupe key, retention, and consumer list. |
| `34.2` Webhook subscriptions, signed delivery, idempotency, retries, dead-letter queues, replay, and endpoint safety | Webhook system validates endpoints, signs messages, logs attempts, retries with jitter, dedupes/replays safely, blocks unsafe endpoints, exposes DLQ, and includes signature test vectors. |
| `34.3` Product notifications across account, email, WebPush, RSS, Slack-style integrations, CLI/TUI, and admin inbox | Account inbox, email, WebPush, RSS/status, CLI/TUI watch, admin/support diagnostics, and future chat integrations use the same event IDs and delivery status vocabulary. |
| `34.4` Billing, status, incident, approval, drift, artifact, compile, distill, capture, and security event workflows | Priority event families are implemented end-to-end with trigger, payload, delivery, audit, UI state, docs, test, and production smoke. |
| `34.5` Deliverability, preference center, unsubscribe, rate limits, audit logs, support visibility, and failure diagnostics | Users can configure preferences by event family/channel; operators can see attempts, bounces, suppressions, retries, rate limits, and replay outcomes without leaking sensitive data. |
| `34.6` Evidence: local and production delivery smoke, signature vectors, retry drills, incident drills, and customer proof | Evidence includes unit tests, route docs, signature fixtures, retry/DLQ drills, endpoint safety tests, production test sends, incident drill packet, and screenshot proof for account/admin/docs surfaces. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-EVENT-001` | `34.1` | Create canonical event catalog. | Every current and planned event type has owner, schema, producer, consumers, privacy class, retention, trigger, and tests. |
| `W-EVENT-002` | `34.1` | Normalize event envelope. | Internal events, account inbox, webhooks, email, push, RSS, audit, CLI/TUI, and admin diagnostics map from one envelope. |

| `W-EVENT-007` | `34.2` | Add retry, jitter, DLQ, and replay. | Retriable failures back off, terminal failures enter DLQ, and manual replay preserves original event lineage. |
| `W-EVENT-008` | `34.2` | Add incoming vendor webhook ledger. | Stripe and future provider webhooks preserve raw-body verification, event-id idempotency, replay, reconciliation, and diagnostics. |

| `W-EVENT-012` | `34.3` | Harden WebPush lifecycle. | Browser support, consent, VAPID, allowlisted endpoints, dead subscription removal, payload minimization, and test send are covered. |
| `W-EVENT-013` | `34.4` | Wire compile/distill/artifact events. | Jobs emit queued/started/progress/completed/failed/promoted/revoked events with account, webhook, CLI, and docs parity. |
| `W-EVENT-014` | `34.4` | Wire incident/status events. | Component health, maintenance, incident updates, postmortems, RSS, status subscribers, and customer-impact segmentation share one workflow. |
| `W-EVENT-015` | `34.4` | Wire approval/moderation events. | Approval requests, reminders, delegations, expiries, decisions, appeals, and escalations are delivered and audited. |
| `W-EVENT-016` | `34.4` | Wire billing and entitlement events. | Stripe webhook processing, billing emails, account billing status, entitlement changes, and invoice notices reconcile and dedupe. |
| `W-EVENT-017` | `34.5` | Build preference center. | Users can choose channel/event family preferences, unsubscribe, suppress noisy classes, and test each channel. |
| `W-EVENT-018` | `34.5` | Add support delivery diagnostics. | Support/admin can answer delivery state without seeing secrets or private payloads and can export customer-safe evidence. |
| `W-EVENT-019` | `34.6` | Add event delivery smoke suite. | Local and production smoke test webhook signing, retry, DLQ, replay, email skip/send, WebPush config, status subscribe, and account notification rendering. |
| `W-EVENT-020` | `34.6` | Add incident communication drill. | A simulated incident produces component state, status page update, RSS item, email/webhook notification, admin evidence, and postmortem packet. |

#### Node 34 Relax Redline

Node `34` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- What event types exist, who owns each one, and what schema/version/privacy class governs the payload?
- Which events go to account inbox, webhooks, email, WebPush, RSS/status, CLI/TUI, admin, support, or nowhere?
- Can a customer add a webhook endpoint, verify ownership, rotate the secret, test it, inspect delivery attempts, and replay failures?
- Are outbound webhook signatures verified with published test vectors and a clear replay window?
- Are incoming Stripe/provider webhooks verified from raw body, deduped by event id, idempotent, and reconcilable?
- What happens when a delivery endpoint returns 500, times out, rejects TLS, points to a private IP, or fails for days?
- Can support prove whether an incident, billing, approval, artifact, compile, distill, drift, or capture notification was sent, skipped, suppressed, retried, bounced, dead-lettered, or replayed?
- Do user preferences distinguish security, billing, incidents, product progress, marketing, thresholds, and noisy alerts?
- Does production smoke prove actual delivery paths on the deployed commit without leaking secrets or customer payloads?
- Does the status page reflect real health or clearly disclose static/demo state?

The relax condition for node `34` is not "there is a notifications module." It is: every important asynchronous Kolm event has a trustworthy lifecycle from producer to customer-visible state, with signatures, idempotency, retries, diagnostics, preferences, and production proof.

#### Node 34 Async Communications Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. Events, webhooks, notifications, email, RSS/status, account inbox, CLI/TUI watch modes, retry state, dead-letter queues, incident communication, and async customer-visible state all belong to node `34`. Node `36` owns job orchestration internals, node `39` owns observability, node `29` owns production deployment/status proof, node `16` owns billing event business rules, node `25` owns customer lifecycle messaging, and node `26` owns admin/operator consoles. Node `34` is the customer communication layer that makes async work understandable.

##### Node `34.0` Current Repo Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Canonical event store | `src/event-schema.js` and `src/event-store.js` provide strict capture-event schemas, tenant fencing, SQLite/JSONL fallback, stream/export/count/list APIs, and tests for capture-to-event-store bridging. | Strong telemetry foundation, but this is mostly capture/lake event storage. Product-delivery events still need CloudEvents-style taxonomy, channel routing, delivery attempts, and customer-facing state. |
| Notifications | `src/notifications.js` supports threshold alerts, preferences, WebPush endpoint allowlisting, VAPID readiness, email fallback, threshold state, and test alerts. | Threshold alerting should become one producer inside a generic event delivery substrate, not a standalone notification path. |
| Email | `src/email.js` wraps Resend, includes welcome/billing/setup-token emails, and uses one-time setup token logic. Terminal reads show legacy separator artifacts such as `??kolm` in email copy. | Email needs a template registry, preview screenshots, deliverability evidence, bounce/suppression state, encoding checks, and event-family preferences. |
| Webhook docs | `public/docs/webhooks.html` documents event types, HMAC signing, retry policy, and a 14-day DLQ. The rendered title and metadata still contain mojibake-style separators, and docs mention `/account/connectors`. | Docs describe a stronger system than current code evidence proves. V2 requires implementation parity, route/API ownership, screenshots, and docs encoding cleanup. |
| Drift webhooks | `src/router.js` drift-alert webhooks accept `http(s)` URLs, perform fire-and-forget fetch, do not sign, do not retry, and comments explicitly say "no retries". | This is a real divergence from the generic webhook contract. Drift alerts must move to signed HTTPS-only delivery with endpoint safety, attempts, retries, DLQ, and replay. |
| Status subscribe | `/v1/status/subscribe`, `public/status.html`, `public/status.rss`, and tests exist; RSS currently has a baseline "All systems operational" item. | Status subscription is wired, but status must be component-backed and incident-backed before it can imply live operational truth. |
| Stripe webhook | `/v1/stripe/webhook` verifies signature from raw body, records Stripe event IDs, and handles idempotent plan mutation. | Incoming vendor webhook posture is stronger than outbound customer webhook posture; node `34` should make both ledgers explicit and separate. |
| SSE/streaming | Router has several `text/event-stream` paths for routing and synthesis streams. | Streaming product updates must share event names, cancellation/error vocabulary, and account/CLI parity with durable event state where user decisions depend on them. |
| Queues and approvals | Active-learning queue, distill approval queue, label queue, load queue, jobs, and approval tests exist. | Queue state should emit customer-visible events and admin diagnostics instead of requiring users to infer progress from separate pages. |

##### Node `34.1` Current Standards Delta

| Baseline | Relevant current concept | Kolm V2 interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| CloudEvents | Common event metadata such as `id`, `source`, `type`, `specversion`, `subject`, and `time`. | Internal product events should normalize to a CloudEvents-compatible envelope before channel adaptation. |
| AsyncAPI 3.0 | Machine-readable contracts for event-driven APIs, channels, messages, operations, bindings, and schemas. | Webhook/event docs should be generated or validated from an AsyncAPI-style contract, not maintained as prose only. |
| HTTP Message Signatures RFC 9421 | Standard framework for signing selected HTTP message components. | Kolm can keep HMAC compatibility but should define covered fields, replay window, timestamp, digest, and migration path to standard HTTP signatures where needed. |
| Standard Webhooks | Practical headers for message id, timestamp, and signature across webhook senders. | Outbound webhooks should expose a simple verification pattern and test vectors that customers can implement quickly. |
| W3C Push API and RFC 8030 Web Push | Browser subscription, push service, permission, and delivery model. | WebPush payloads must be minimal, consented, endpoint-safe, and treated as best-effort with stale-subscription cleanup. |
| Stripe webhooks | Raw-body verification, endpoint secrets, quick 2xx acknowledgement, async processing, and event-type handling. | Incoming vendor webhooks must preserve raw body, verify before parse/mutation, dedupe by provider event ID, and reconcile later if needed. |
| GitHub webhook validation | HMAC validation, payload body preservation, and constant-time comparison. | Customer-facing docs and SDK snippets should include raw body and timing-safe comparison guidance. |
| OpenTelemetry event semantic conventions | Events should carry consistent names and attributes so they can be correlated with traces/logs/metrics. | Customer-visible events, audit rows, traces, and support bundles should share `trace_id`, `request_id`, `event_id`, and object identifiers. |
| RFC 9457 problem details | Standard shape for HTTP API errors. | Webhook/API delivery diagnostics should use structured problem details for invalid endpoint, signature failure, replay window, unsafe URL, DLQ state, and replay errors. |

##### Node `34.2` Unified Event Contract V2

The V1 envelope is directionally correct. V2 requires a generated event catalog plus channel adapters.

```json
{
  "event_contract_version": "kolm-events-v2",
  "type": "kolm.artifact.revoked",
  "cloudevents": {
    "specversion": "1.0",
    "id": "evt_...",
    "source": "kolm://registry",
    "subject": "tenant/tenant_.../artifact/sha256:...",
    "time": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "datacontenttype": "application/json"
  },
  "ownership": {
    "master_node": "34",
    "feature_node": "33",
    "producer": "src/registry.js",
    "schema_owner": "backend-product",
    "support_owner": "customer-ops"
  },
  "routing": {
    "account_inbox": "required",
    "webhook": "tenant_preference",
    "email": "security_or_action_required_only",
    "webpush": "tenant_preference",
    "status_rss": "not_applicable",
    "cli_tui": "available"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "class": "tenant_private",
    "raw_payload_allowed": false,
    "redaction_policy": "applied",
    "email_payload_policy": "summary_only",
    "webhook_payload_policy": "structured_minimal"
  },
  "delivery": {
    "dedupe_key": "tenant:artifact:sha256:revoked:v1",
    "idempotency_key": "evt_...",
    "retry_policy": "security-critical",
    "ttl_seconds": 1209600,
    "requires_attempt_log": true,
    "requires_replay": true
  },
  "diagnostics": {
    "trace_id": "trace_...",
    "request_id": "req_...",
    "audit_id": "audit_...",
    "problem_type_base": "https://kolm.ai/problems/events/"
  }
}
```

The catalog must reject events without owner, schema, privacy class, routing state, dedupe key, retention, channel adapters, and tests.

##### Node `34.3` Outbox, Inbox, And Replay Contract V2

Kolm needs a durable outbox/inbox split:

| Plane | Required contract |
|---|---|
| Producer outbox | Business action writes event and domain change atomically or with recoverable outbox reconciliation; producer does not block on webhook/email latency. |
| Tenant inbox | Account-visible record persists event ID, title, severity, object link, read state, action required, delivery state summary, and retention. |
| Webhook outbox | One row per endpoint delivery, with event ID, endpoint ID, attempt number, signed payload digest, response status, response digest, latency, retry reason, next attempt, and DLQ state. |
| Email outbox | One row per recipient/template send, with template version, provider ID, bounce/suppression state, unsubscribe reason, and redacted variables. |
| WebPush outbox | One row per subscription attempt, with push-service endpoint hash, status, stale-subscription cleanup, and payload class. |
| Replay ledger | Replay creates a new attempt ID linked to original event ID, preserves original event body, warns about idempotency, and records actor. |
| Support view | Support can see delivery state, not secrets: sent/skipped/suppressed/bounced/retried/dead-lettered/replayed with timestamps and safe summaries. |

No customer-impacting async path should rely only on in-memory fire-and-forget fetch. If the path is intentionally best-effort, the UI and docs must say best-effort and show no delivery guarantee.

##### Node `34.4` Webhook Security Contract V2

Outbound customer webhooks must meet these requirements before broad launch:

- HTTPS-only except local test mode with explicit test flag;
- endpoint ownership verification or signed challenge response;
- SSRF defenses: reject localhost, loopback, private IPs, link-local, metadata services, and DNS rebinding patterns;
- HMAC or HTTP message signature with event ID, timestamp, body digest, and signature version;
- replay window with timestamp tolerance and customer test vectors;
- endpoint secret rotation with overlapping validation window;
- per-endpoint event-family subscription;
- per-endpoint rate limit, timeout, concurrency cap, and max body size;
- retry with jitter and finite attempts;
- DLQ with customer-visible diagnostics and manual replay;
- payload preview with redaction before enabling;
- audit log for endpoint create/update/delete/secret-rotate/test/replay/disable;
- automatic disable or quarantine for repeated unsafe failures.

Inbound vendor webhooks must meet a separate contract:

- raw body preserved before JSON parsing;
- provider signature verified before mutation;
- event ID idempotency before side effects;
- tenant/account context verified;
- fast acknowledgement where provider expects it;
- reconciliation path if related resource fetch is needed;
- replay/drill support;
- structured diagnostics for signature failure, stale timestamp, unknown event type, duplicate, and storage failure.

##### Node `34.5` Customer UX Contract V2

The notification system is part of product UX. It should be operational, not noisy.

Account notification center requirements:

- dense list with severity, event family, object, timestamp, read state, and next action;
- filters for capture, compile, distill, artifact, registry, billing, security, incident, approval, drift, support;
- clear labels for delivered, skipped, suppressed, retrying, failed, DLQ, replayed;
- no hover-only critical state;
- accessible keyboard navigation and visible focus;
- empty state that says what events will appear and how to test;
- per-event detail showing safe payload summary, channels attempted, and related audit/log/trace IDs;
- preference center grouped by security, billing, incidents, product progress, thresholds, approvals, marketing, and noisy alerts;
- test-send buttons per channel with immediate feedback;
- admin/support diagnostic mode with sensitive values redacted.

Status/incident UX requirements:

- component-backed status, not static reassurance;
- incident timeline with detected, investigating, identified, mitigating, monitoring, resolved, postmortem;
- customer-impact labels by product surface and region;
- RSS/email/webhook subscriber state;
- maintenance windows and expected impact;
- postmortem links and follow-up tasks.

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance proof |
