##### Node `34` V2 Relax Redline

Node `34` can relax only when all of the following are true:

- every customer-relevant async event has a catalog row, owner, schema, privacy class, routing rule, retention, and test;
- capture, compile, distill, artifact, registry, billing, security, status, approval, drift, and support events use one event vocabulary;
- outbound webhooks are signed, HTTPS-only, SSRF-safe, retried, logged, dead-lettered, and replayable;
- inbound Stripe/provider webhooks preserve raw body, verify before mutation, dedupe by provider event ID, and expose diagnostics;
- account inbox, email, WebPush, RSS/status, CLI/TUI, admin, and support show the same event IDs and delivery state vocabulary;
- user preferences distinguish security, billing, incidents, progress, thresholds, approvals, drift, support, and marketing;
- email templates and webhook docs are encoding-clean, versioned, previewed, and redaction-tested;
- status page claims are backed by component health or visibly scoped as static;
- local and production smokes prove event send, skip, retry, DLQ, replay, subscribe, and incident drill paths.

### Provider, Model, Runtime, Hardware, Pricing, And Capability Catalog Ledger V1

This branch is doc-only in this lane. It does not patch provider routing, model registries, cost estimators, runtime dispatch, device detection, account pages, docs pages, tests, generated OpenAPI, or pricing code directly. It defines the catalog contract those implementation lanes must satisfy before Kolm can honestly claim model-agnostic routing, compile/distill choice, cost optimization, and run-anywhere deployment.

Node `35` exists because provider, model, runtime, hardware, and pricing facts are high-churn product truth. A stale catalog can break every Kolm surface:

- gateway/capture routes pick the wrong provider, model, auth mode, endpoint, or token accounting;
- distillation selects a teacher or student that is unavailable, wrongly licensed, too expensive, or unsupported by the target runtime;
- compile/runtime placement claims a device can run an artifact that cannot fit in memory or lacks the backend;
- pricing/ROI pages quote costs that no longer match upstream providers;
- account setup shows provider readiness but not which models, modalities, context windows, regions, quotas, and billing limits are actually usable.

The completion rule is simple: model/provider/runtime facts must come from one dated, testable catalog layer, not from copy-pasted page text.

#### Current Repo Catalog Evidence

| Area | Current evidence | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Provider registry | `src/provider-registry.js` defines OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, and Gemini upstreams, auth schemes, env vars, paths, and price-per-1K token tables. | Useful but risky because pricing and model IDs are time-sensitive. Completion requires source URLs, checked dates, freshness lint, provider discovery, and no silent zero-cost fallback in customer-facing estimates. |
| Model registry | `src/model-registry.js` separates verified `FRONTIER_MODELS` from candidate watchlist rows and records source URLs, license URLs, verification dates, hardware tiers, quantization, context, modality, and backend fields. | Strong honesty pattern. Completion requires automated source checks, revision pinning where possible, stale-date gates, account/API parity, and clear unverified-model UX. |
| Base model defaults | `src/models.js` contains curated Qwen, Llama, Phi, Gemma, MedGemma, and other model rows with license, VRAM, context, tokenizer, tool-use, and use-case notes. | Needs reconciliation with `src/model-registry.js`, runtime targets, public `/models`, API `/v1/models`, docs, and artifact passports. |
| Runtime placement | `src/runtime-placement.js` implements W826 memory-aware placement using GPU VRAM, RAM, NVMe estimates, and placement decisions. | This should become part of the catalog contract, with runtime/device evidence and account-visible rationale. |
| Runtime dispatch | `src/artifact-runner.js`, `src/runtime.js`, `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/runtime-perf-estimate.js`, and runners for GGUF/ONNX exist. | Completion requires a unified runtime matrix with supported target, package state, smoke command, latency/cost/quality/energy evidence, and failure modes. |
| Device catalog | `src/device-capabilities.js`, `src/devices.js`, `src/device-install.js`, `public/account/devices.html`, `public/docs/devices.html`, and CLI docs exist. | Device profiles need freshness, real smoke, thermal/battery/memory caveats, install/uninstall/rollback evidence, and artifact compatibility proof. |
| Cost and benchmark evidence | `src/cost-estimator.js`, `src/billing-breakdown.js`, `src/benchmark-evidence.js`, benchmark reports, and provider-matrix requirements exist. | Price estimates must cite catalog snapshot date and provider source; benchmark comparisons must pin model, provider, runtime, hardware, latency, cost, and quality separately. |
| Public/docs pages | `public/models.html`, `public/runtimes.html`, `public/docs/runtime.html`, `public/docs/hardware.html`, provider connector docs, integration pages, and compare pages exist. | Pages must be generated or checked against the catalog. Static model/provider copy should fail when stale. |
| Tests | Tests cover model registry split, runtime dispatch, models/devices routes, provider Gemma scope, capture provider honesty, runtime placement, cost estimator, and route docs. | Existing tests are valuable but must be mapped into one catalog release gate. |

#### External Baseline For Catalog Truth

Provider and runtime catalogs are unstable. Kolm should treat official provider endpoints and docs as current-source inputs, not as prose that can be frozen forever.

| External baseline | What it contributes to node `35` | Source |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI model docs | Model availability, capabilities, pricing, and model comparison need to be read from official OpenAI docs/API surfaces, not remembered. | `https://platform.openai.com/docs/models` and `https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/compare` |
| Anthropic model/context/pricing docs | Claude model context, pricing, long-context behavior, and validation rules are provider-specific and change over time. | `https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/context-windows` and `https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/pricing` |
| Gemini API model docs | Gemini model capabilities, multimodal inputs, context windows, and API versions are provider-specific and change over time. | `https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models` |
| OpenRouter Models API | Programmatic catalog of model IDs, pricing, context length, modality, architecture, and deprecation/endpoint data. | `https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/api-reference/models/get-models` |
| Hugging Face model cards | Model identity, intended use, limitations, datasets, evaluation, tags, and metadata for local/open models. | `https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/model-cards` |
| ONNX Runtime execution providers | Runtime/hardware support must name execution providers and capability limits rather than generically saying "ONNX works." | `https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers` |
| ONNX Runtime GenAI | Generative model execution config and runtime options for ONNX-based deployment. | `https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/` |
| Apple Core ML | Apple-device deployment requires Core ML conversion/runtime constraints and supported model formats. | `https://developer.apple.com/documentation/CoreML` |
| Google LiteRT | Android/edge deployment requires LiteRT model/runtime/device constraints. | `https://ai.google.dev/edge/litert` |
| NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM | NVIDIA GPU deployment requires explicit runtime, batching, quantization, engine, Triton, and hardware notes. | `https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/` |

#### Canonical Catalog Object

Every provider/model/runtime/device row should be normalized into one shape before it feeds routing, pricing, compile selection, docs, account UI, and claims.

```json
{
  "catalog_version": "kolm-catalog-v1",
  "id": "openai:gpt-4o",
  "kind": "provider_model|local_model|runtime|device|hardware_tier|provider_endpoint",
  "display_name": "GPT-4o",
  "owner": "catalog",
  "status": "available|beta|deprecated|removed|unverified|external_gated",
  "source": {
    "source_url": "https://...",
    "source_type": "official_api|official_docs|model_card|benchmark|manual_verified|local_probe",
    "checked_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "freshness_ttl_days": 7,
    "revision": "sha-or-version-or-null"
  },
  "provider": {
    "id": "openai",
    "auth_mode": "bearer|x-api-key|query-key|oauth|local",
    "env_key": "OPENAI_API_KEY",
    "base_url": "https://api.openai.com",
    "regions": [],
    "data_policy": "provider_default|zero_retention|byoc|local_only|unknown"
  },
  "capabilities": {
    "modalities_in": ["text", "image"],
    "modalities_out": ["text"],
    "context_tokens": 128000,
    "max_output_tokens": null,
    "tool_use": "native|limited|none|unknown",
    "structured_output": "native|json_mode|prompted|none|unknown",
    "streaming": true,
    "batching": true,
    "vision": true,
    "audio": false,
    "video": false,
    "embeddings": false,
    "fine_tuning": false,
    "distillation_teacher_ok": true,
    "student_compile_ok": false
  },
  "pricing": {
    "currency": "USD",
    "unit": "per_1m_tokens|per_1k_tokens|per_second|per_image|local_estimate",
    "input": null,
    "cached_input": null,
    "output": null,
    "effective_at": "2026-05-25",
    "source_url": "https://...",
    "quote_requires_live_check": true
  },
  "limits": {
    "rate_limit": null,
    "quota": null,
    "max_request_bytes": null,
    "max_batch_size": null,
    "deprecation_date": null
  },
  "runtime": {
    "targets": ["js", "wasm", "native", "gguf", "onnx", "vllm"],
    "required_packages": [],
    "smoke_command": null,
    "streaming_supported": true,
    "known_failure_modes": []
  },
  "hardware_fit": {
    "min_vram_gb": null,
    "min_ram_gb": null,
    "recommended_quant": null,
    "thermal_notes": null,
    "battery_notes": null,
    "attestation": null
  },
  "evidence": {
    "provider_smoke": null,
    "runtime_smoke": null,
    "device_smoke": null,
    "benchmark_snapshot": null,
    "account_ui_path": null,
    "docs_path": null
  }
}
```

#### Catalog Source-Of-Truth Rules

- Provider model availability should prefer live official APIs when they exist, such as OpenRouter's `/api/v1/models`, and fall back to dated official docs only with a visible freshness state.
- Provider pricing must carry source URL, effective date, currency, unit, and whether a live quote is required.
- Local/open model rows must carry exact model-card URLs, license URLs, revision pins where possible, and verified/candidate state.
- Runtime support must be a matrix, not a slogan. `gguf`, `onnx`, `wasm`, `native`, `cloud`, `vllm`, `llama.cpp`, `TensorRT-LLM`, `Core ML`, `LiteRT`, `MLX`, and `WebGPU` each need package state, smoke command, supported modality, failure modes, and benchmark evidence.
- Hardware fit must distinguish can-download, can-load, can-run-one-token, can-stream, can-hold-context, can-run-under-thermal-load, and can-pass-quality gate.
- Account UI, CLI, TUI, API, docs, homepage copy, pricing/ROI, and benchmark reports must use the same catalog IDs and freshness labels.
- Stale data should fail closed for route/price recommendations and fail visible for docs/marketing pages.

#### Catalog Freshness Policy

| Data class | Freshness requirement | Failure behavior |
|---|---|---|
| Provider availability | Recheck at least daily for providers with list APIs; at least weekly for provider docs. | Mark unknown/stale; do not recommend as default without manual override. |
| Provider pricing | Recheck before public pricing/ROI/benchmark claims and at least weekly. | Show "requires live check"; block exact savings claims. |
| Model context/capabilities | Recheck at least weekly or when provider/model changes. | Show stale badge; block docs from saying "supports" without date. |
| Open model license/source | Recheck before compile default promotion and at least monthly. | Move to candidate/unverified if stale, missing, or license ambiguous. |
| Runtime package support | Recheck on every release and package update. | Mark package-gated or local-source-only. |
| Device/hardware fit | Recheck on hardware profile changes and benchmark runs. | Block run-anywhere claim for that profile. |
| Benchmark snapshots | Pin date, commit, model revision, provider pricing, hardware, runtime version, and raw report. | Do not roll into public comparison if any field is missing. |

#### Redlines

- Do not hardcode a provider/model name in public copy unless it is generated from the catalog or visibly dated.
- Do not quote provider pricing, savings, ROI, or "40 percent cheaper" style claims without a pricing snapshot and source date.
- Do not return cost `0` in a customer-facing estimate for unknown paid provider models; use `unknown` and explain missing price.
- Do not recommend a model whose license, context window, runtime target, or provider availability is unverified.
- Do not claim "model-agnostic" if provider auth, endpoint, request shape, streaming, tool use, structured output, and error mapping are not tested for that provider.
- Do not claim "run anywhere" if the artifact has not passed runtime smoke for the named runtime/device class.
- Do not conflate model card metadata with runtime compatibility. A Hugging Face model existing does not mean it runs in GGUF, ONNX, Core ML, LiteRT, WebGPU, or TensorRT-LLM.
- Do not conflate context window with reliable long-context behavior. Long-context claims need evals or explicit provider scope.
- Do not hide deprecation or retirement dates; stale/retired provider models must show replacement guidance.
- Do not use candidate/research models as compile defaults unless the user explicitly opts into unverified/candidate state.
- Do not let account, docs, OpenAPI examples, SDK examples, CLI help, and public pages diverge on model IDs.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |

| `35.1` Provider registry, credentials, endpoints, auth modes, model discovery, freshness, and deprecation | Provider catalog covers OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, local OpenAI-compatible, and self-hosted targets with endpoint shapes, auth modes, credential readiness, model discovery, deprecation, region/data policy, and smoke evidence. |
| `35.2` Model catalog, modalities, context windows, tool use, structured output, licenses, safety, and source evidence | Model rows carry exact IDs, source URLs, checked dates, modalities, context, output limits, tool/JSON support, safety limits, license, data policy, verified/candidate state, and account/docs/API parity. |
| `35.3` Pricing, token accounting, quotas, rate limits, caching, latency, quality, cost estimation, and benchmark snapshots | Cost estimator, ROI calculator, billing breakdown, benchmarks, traces, and route receipts use dated pricing snapshots, token accounting, cached-input rules, rate limits, latency/quality evidence, and unknown-price behavior. |
| `35.4` Runtime catalog: JS, WASM, native, GGUF, ONNX, vLLM, llama.cpp, TensorRT-LLM, Core ML, LiteRT, MLX, and WebGPU | Every runtime target has supported artifact type, package state, install command, smoke command, capability limits, known failure modes, performance evidence, and claim-scope label. |
| `35.5` Hardware and device catalog: GPU, CPU, NPU, mobile, browser, edge, BYOC, cloud, memory, thermal, and attestation fit | Device/hardware profiles include memory, backend, modality, runtime, battery/thermal caveats, attestation/confidential compute, install/test flow, artifact fit, and production or fixture smoke evidence. |
| `35.6` Evidence: catalog sync, stale-data lint, provider smoke, runtime smoke, device fit, pricing diff, and UI parity | Release verification includes catalog freshness lint, provider smoke, runtime smoke, device fit tests, pricing/source diff, stale public-copy scan, route/API/docs/CLI/account parity, and production readiness proof. |

#### Node 35 Relax Redline

Node `35` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current catalog evidence:

- Which provider/model IDs are currently available for each connected tenant, and when were they checked?
- Which provider endpoints, auth modes, request shapes, response shapes, streaming modes, tool/JSON modes, and error mappings are actually supported?
- What exact source proves model context, modality, price, license, and availability?
- What happens when a provider changes pricing, retires a model, changes context behavior, adds/removes tool support, or rate-limits a tenant?
- Which models can be teachers, students, compile targets, runtime targets, benchmark lanes, or marketing examples?
- Which runtime/device combinations can actually load, run, stream, and pass quality/latency/cost/thermal gates?
- Do account model selectors, routing pages, ROI calculators, API examples, SDK examples, CLI docs, OpenAPI, public pages, and artifacts all use the same catalog IDs?
- Can production smoke prove provider readiness without exposing secret values?
- Can benchmarks cite a dated pricing snapshot, model revision, runtime version, hardware profile, and raw report?
- Does stale or unknown catalog data fail visible instead of producing confident recommendations?

The relax condition for node `35` is not "there is a model list." It is: every provider, model, runtime, device, price, and capability fact that Kolm uses to route, distill, compile, price, benchmark, or market the product is current, sourced, testable, visible, and safely degraded when stale.

#### Node 35 Catalog Truth Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The instruction to maintain a master spec tree strengthens node `35` rather than creating another branch. Provider, model, runtime, hardware, pricing, and capability truth is a shared product control plane for nodes `1`, `2`, `6`, `7`, `8`, `11`, `12`, `16`, `17`, `19`, `20`, `29`, `42`, `44`, `45`, `47`, and `50`.

This addendum is still spec-only. It does not edit provider routing, model registries, runtime dispatch, account pages, docs, generated assets, tests, or package files in this lane. It defines the exact state a build-finishing agent must implement before Kolm can safely claim model-agnostic routing, compile and distill choice, ROI optimization, and any-device deployment.

It also has 27 `verified_at` rows, 29 `source_url` rows, 2 `official_source_url` rows, and comments that candidates must never become compile defaults. | Strong honesty primitive. Completion requires a generated catalog manifest that preserves this distinction in API, account UI, docs, CLI/TUI, artifact passports, and benchmark reports. |

Public claims should fail if they cite retired model IDs, stale prices, or unsupported runtimes. |

##### Current External Catalog Baselines

The catalog cannot be finished from memory because provider and runtime facts are high-churn. Build agents should treat these primary sources as live inputs or dated evidence, not as permanent prose.

| Source | Current standard to absorb | Required Kolm behavior |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI models docs, `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models` | OpenAI publishes current model families, model guides, cost features, rate-limit/deprecation links, and API model concepts in official docs. | OpenAI rows must carry source URL, checked date, available endpoints, request shape, streaming/tool/structured-output scope, pricing source, and deprecation status. |
| Anthropic pricing docs, `https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing` | Anthropic pricing depends on model, token class, tools, web search, computer use, prompt caching, and session/runtime modifiers. | Claude cost rows cannot be a single input/output pair. They must model cache reads/writes, tool overhead, web-search charges, beta modifiers, and unknown charges as visible estimates. |
| Anthropic context docs, `https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/context-windows` | Context limits and long-context behavior differ by model and can include document/image count constraints. | Context-window labels must distinguish nominal limit, request-size limit, document/image count, long-context behavior, and provider-specific caveats. |
| Gemini model docs, `https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models` | Gemini docs list text, multimodal, live/audio, TTS, media-generation, embedding, and specialized model classes. | Gemini rows must not be flattened into "chat models." Modality, live API support, speech, image/video/media capabilities, and API family must be explicit. |
| OpenRouter Models API, `https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/api-reference/models/get-models` | OpenRouter exposes `/api/v1/models` with model IDs, architecture, modalities, context length, pricing, supported parameters, per-request limits, and provider details. | OpenRouter should be a live adapter with cache freshness, tenant provider preferences, privacy/guardrail filters, model deprecation, and no stale hardcoded model IDs in public copy. |
| Hugging Face model cards, `https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/model-cards` | Model cards combine Markdown descriptions and metadata for discoverability, reproducibility, limitations, and sharing. | Open/local model rows must include exact model-card URL, license URL, revision or commit when available, intended-use and limitation fields, and promotion rules from candidate to verified. |
| ONNX Runtime execution providers, `https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers/` | ONNX Runtime uses ordered execution providers and can fall back from one provider to another if a node is unsupported. | ONNX support must name exact EPs, priority order, provider options, fallback behavior, and unsupported-op errors. "ONNX works" is not enough. |
| ONNX Runtime GenAI, `https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/genai/` | ONNX GenAI is preview and covers tokenization, preprocessing, inference, logits processing, sampling/search, and KV cache management for ONNX models. | Kolm must label ONNX GenAI support as preview/package-gated until versioned package, smoke command, KV-cache behavior, and generated-output quality proof exist. |
| Apple Core ML, `https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coreml` | Apple-device deployment depends on Core ML model conversion, runtime constraints, and platform-specific capabilities. | Core ML catalog rows need platform version, model type, conversion path, package state, device class, memory fit, and on-device smoke evidence. |
| Google LiteRT, `https://ai.google.dev/edge/litert` | LiteRT is Google's on-device ML framework with sample apps, optimized models, hardware-selection APIs, NPU paths, and edge deployment docs. | LiteRT rows must track package maturity, device/NPU family, sample app proof, quantization format, async execution support, and Android/browser/edge limits. |
| NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM docs, `https://docs.nvidia.com/tensorrt-llm/` | TensorRT-LLM has current release notes, support matrix, Python and C++ runtimes, quantization docs, and supported GPU/model/software matrices. | TensorRT-LLM rows need release version, support-matrix link, GPU/software requirements, engine build path, quantization support, batching support, Triton path, and benchmark proof. |

##### Catalog Snapshot V2

Every release should generate a single catalog snapshot. The snapshot is not just data for docs. It is a release-critical proof artifact that controls routing, account selectors, ROI, benchmark reporting, runtime placement, device fit, artifacts, and claims.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-catalog-snapshot-v2",
  "generated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "git_commit": "unknown-until-release",
  "source_inputs": [
    "src/provider-registry.js",
    "src/model-registry.js",
    "src/models.js",
    "src/runtime-policy.js",
    "src/runtime-placement.js",
    "src/device-capabilities.js",
    "src/compute/registry.json"
  ],
  "external_sources_checked": [
    "openai",
    "anthropic",
    "gemini",
    "openrouter",
    "huggingface",
    "onnxruntime",
    "coreml",
    "litert",
    "tensorrt_llm"
  ],
  "counts": {
    "providers": 0,
    "provider_models": 0,
    "verified_models": 0,
    "candidate_models": 0,
    "runtimes": 0,
    "devices": 0,
    "hardware_profiles": 0,
    "pricing_rows": 0,
    "benchmarks_bound": 0,
    "artifact_passports_bound": 0
  },
  "freshness": {
    "stale_provider_rows": 0,
    "stale_pricing_rows": 0,
    "stale_model_rows": 0,
    "unknown_price_rows": 0,
    "candidate_default_violations": 0,
    "unsupported_claim_violations": 0
  },
  "consumers": {
    "api": [],
    "account": [],
    "public_pages": [],
    "docs": [],
    "cli": [],
    "tui": [],
    "benchmarks": [],
    "artifacts": []
  },
  "proof": {
    "catalog_lint": null,
    "provider_smoke": null,
    "runtime_smoke": null,
    "device_smoke": null,
    "pricing_diff": null,
    "claim_scope": null,
    "screenshot_audit": null,
    "production_smoke": null
  }
}
```

##### Catalog Row V2

Every row consumed by routing, compile, distill, pricing, runtime, devices, account, docs, or public copy must preserve the same evidence fields.

| Field family | Required fields | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| Identity | `id`, `kind`, `display_name`, `provider_id`, `canonical_slug`, `status`, `owner`, `deprecated_at`, `replacement_id` | Prevents orphan IDs, retired defaults, and inconsistent public examples. |
| Source | `source_url`, `source_type`, `checked_at`, `freshness_ttl_days`, `revision`, `license_url`, `verification_evidence` | Makes every model, price, runtime, device, and claim auditable. |
| Capability | `modalities_in`, `modalities_out`, `context_tokens`, `max_output_tokens`, `tool_use`, `structured_output`, `streaming`, `batching`, `fine_tuning`, `teacher_ok`, `student_ok`, `runtime_target_ok` | Stops the site from selling only one API gateway story when the product also compiles, distills, and deploys to devices. |
| Pricing | `currency`, `unit`, `input`, `output`, `cached_input`, `cache_write`, `tool_costs`, `request_costs`, `effective_at`, `source_url`, `quote_requires_live_check`, `unknown_reason` | Prevents fake ROI, stale savings, and hidden tool/cache cost drift. |
| Routing | `auth_mode`, `base_url`, `request_shape`, `response_shape`, `stream_shape`, `error_map`, `retry_policy`, `fallback_policy`, `tenant_allowed`, `region_policy`, `data_policy` | Makes model-agnostic routing an implementation proof, not a slogan. |
| Runtime fit | `runtime_ids`, `package_state`, `install_command`, `smoke_command`, `unsupported_ops`, `fallback_behavior`, `known_failure_modes`, `runtime_version` | Proves which artifacts can actually run in JS, WASM, native, GGUF, ONNX, vLLM, llama.cpp, TensorRT-LLM, Core ML, LiteRT, MLX, or WebGPU. |
| Hardware fit | `min_vram_gb`, `min_ram_gb`, `storage_bytes`, `context_budget`, `quantization`, `npu_support`, `thermal_notes`, `battery_notes`, `attestation`, `can_load`, `can_stream`, `can_hold_context`, `can_pass_quality_gate` | Converts "run anywhere" into a device-specific proof surface. |
| UI state | `selector_badge`, `readiness_label`, `blocking_reason`, `next_action`, `docs_path`, `account_path`, `secret_values_included` | Makes catalog truth visible in account and docs without leaking secrets or hiding blockers. |
| Claim scope | `marketing_allowed`, `benchmark_required`, `package_required`, `external_adoption_required`, `certification_required`, `copy_restrictions` | Lets claim-scope gates stop overclaiming before deploy. |

##### Consumer Parity Rules

The same catalog IDs and status labels must appear across all user-visible surfaces:

- API: `/v1/models`, provider readiness routes, product graph, OpenAPI examples, route receipts, cost and benchmark endpoints.
- Account: provider setup, model selectors, routing policies, distill teacher/student selection, compile targets, runtime placement, device pages, ROI, benchmark detail, and artifact passports.
- Public site: homepage claims, product pages, pricing/ROI pages, compare pages, runtime pages, model pages, vertical pages, docs, articles, and demo runner examples.
- CLI/TUI: provider doctor, model list, compile defaults, distill options, runtime smoke, device fit, ROI estimates, and catalog readiness.
- Artifacts: `.kolm` manifests, passports, signatures, runtime compatibility, benchmark evidence, package state, and source model provenance.
- Benchmarks: raw reports, K-Score reports, latency/cost/quality charts, provider bakeoffs, runtime bakeoffs, device reports, and public leaderboard rows.

No consumer may invent a model ID, provider name, price, runtime label, or device-fit badge outside the catalog snapshot.

##### Account And Selector UX Requirements

Provider, model, runtime, and device selectors are dense operational controls. They should feel like an enterprise command surface, not a marketing gallery.

- Every selector must support search, filter, sort, keyboard navigation, mobile wrapping, and no horizontal overflow.
- Critical state cannot live only in hover text. Availability, stale source, unknown price, candidate model, package-gated runtime, missing credential, quota block, unsupported modality, and deprecation must be visible in text or badge form.
- Selection rows must show enough information to decide quickly: provider, model/runtime/device ID, capability class, cost state, source date, readiness, and next action.
- Loading, empty, error, stale, and permission-denied states must have specific recovery actions.
- Cost and ROI surfaces must show `quote_requires_live_check` when prices are stale, unknown, cached-token-specific, tool-specific, region-specific, or provider-account-specific.
- Secret readiness must preserve the existing product rule: show whether a credential exists and whether smoke passed, never show secret values.
- Runtime and device fit must show the reason for failure: memory, context, unsupported operator, missing package, unsupported modality, thermal/battery risk, or unverified benchmark.
- The mobile account view must keep tap targets at least 44px high, preserve focus states, and avoid hidden popovers for required explanation text.
- Public pages may summarize, but they must link to the dated catalog evidence or product graph when making provider/model/runtime/device claims.

##### Node `35` V2 Relax Redline

Node `35` can relax only when all of the following are true:

- every provider, model, runtime, device, hardware, pricing, and capability row is generated from or checked against `kolm-catalog-snapshot-v2`;
- stale provider availability, stale price, stale model metadata, unknown paid price, missing license, unsupported runtime, and candidate default use fail release or fail visible with explicit override;
- public pricing, ROI, benchmark, savings, model-agnostic, run-anywhere, compile, distill, and device claims cite a catalog snapshot and source dates;
- account selectors show availability, candidate state, package state, unknown price, provider readiness, source date, license state, and next action without hover-only critical information;
- OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, local OpenAI-compatible, and self-hosted providers have auth, endpoint, request, response, streaming, tool/JSON, error, quota, privacy, and data-policy contracts;
- local/open models have exact model-card URLs, license URLs, verified/candidate state, revision policy, runtime compatibility, and artifact-role labels;
- runtime rows for JS, WASM, native, GGUF, ONNX, vLLM, llama.cpp, TensorRT-LLM, Core ML, LiteRT, MLX, and WebGPU have package state, smoke command, negative tests, and known failure modes;
- hardware and device rows distinguish can-download, can-load, can-run-one-token, can-stream, can-hold-context, can-run-under-thermal-load, and can-pass-quality-gate;
- cost estimates never treat unknown paid prices as zero and never hide cache, tool, request, region, or provider-account modifiers;
- benchmarks, artifact passports, route receipts, docs examples, API examples, SDK examples, CLI help, and public pages use the same catalog IDs;
- production smoke proves provider/model/runtime/device readiness without printing secret values;
- the final release packet includes snapshot, source diff, stale-data lint, consumer parity, runtime smoke, device smoke, screenshot proof, and production proof.

### Job Orchestration, Workflow Execution, Workers, Queues, And Long-Running Operations Ledger V1

This node turns every slow or asynchronous Kolm action into one durable execution product surface. Compile, distill, train, benchmark, artifact publish, registry moderation, cloud deploy, device install, media tokenization/redaction, audit export, purge, approval, active learning, billing reconciliation, and production smoke cannot each invent their own local "job" language. They need one workflow substrate with the same tenant identity, state machine, idempotency, progress, event, log, artifact, cancellation, retry, resume, and support model.

This is a research/spec-only pass. It does not edit backend implementation. It records what the implementation must converge on.

#### Why Node 36 Exists

Kolm already has many job-shaped paths, but they are not yet a single product contract. A user will judge reliability by whether long-running work can be submitted once, watched from account/API/CLI/TUI, retried safely, cancelled honestly, resumed after failure, audited later, and supported by an operator without asking engineering to inspect private files.

The standard for Kolm is stricter than "return `job_id`." A job is only product-grade if:

- duplicate submission cannot create duplicate paid work;
- progress reflects real phases, not optimistic UI placeholders;
- retries are bounded by policy, budget, and idempotency;
- cancellation has explicit semantics per phase;
- worker crash, deploy restart, host loss, and queue outage are expected paths;
- every output has artifact provenance and cleanup rules;
- logs, payloads, and errors are privacy-classed and redact secrets, prompts, PHI, and credentials;
- account, API, CLI, TUI, webhooks, email, status, and support see the same state.

#### Current Repo Evidence

| Area | Current evidence | 100 percent completion interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Generic jobs | `src/jobs.js`, `GET /v1/jobs/:id`, `public/docs/cli/jobs.md`, `public/docs/cli/jobs.html` | Good base for status lookup, but it must become or delegate to the canonical job record with tenant, owner, state, phase, idempotency, retry, cancel, resume, lineage, and support visibility. |
| Compile jobs | `src/compile.js`, `src/compile-pipeline.js`, `src/compile-ir.js`, `src/compile-targets.js`, `GET /v1/compile`, `POST /v1/compile`, `GET /v1/compile/:id`, `GET /v1/compile/:id/.kolm` | Compile needs durable build phases, compiler target metadata, cache hits, artifact digest, logs, diagnostics, expiry, and cancellation semantics. |
| Pipeline jobs | `src/pipeline-orchestrator.js`, `src/pipeline-runner.js`, `src/pipeline-routes.js`, `src/pipeline-make.js`, `src/pipeline-ship.js`, `src/pipeline-train.js`, `src/pipeline-yaml.js`, `/v1/pipeline/full`, `/v1/pipeline/jobs/:id`, `/v1/pipeline/jobs/:id/stream` | Pipeline SSE is a strong UX path, but every phase must persist and replay after worker/app restart with the same state source as account and CLI. |
| Distill and train jobs | `src/cloud-distill.js`, `src/airgap-distill.js`, `src/distill-pipeline.js`, `src/distill-approval-queue.js`, `src/progressive-distill.js`, `src/improvement-orchestrator.js`, `src/teacher-council.js`, `/v1/cloud/distill`, `/v1/cloud/distill/:job_id`, `/v1/cloud/distill/meter/:job_id`, `/v1/distill/runs` | Distillation jobs need dataset snapshot, teacher/student versions, approval state, cost meter, GPU/provider scheduling, checkpoints, eval reports, rollback, and public claim-scope binding. |
| Active learning and labels | `src/active-learning-queue.js`, `src/label-queue.js`, active learning tests, label queue routes | Label and active-learning queues must use the same priority, privacy, reviewer, assignment, retry, stale-work, and audit rules as other workflows. |
| Load queue and scheduling | `src/load-queue.js`, `src/preload-scheduler.js`, `tests/wave729-load-queue.test.js`, `tests/wave725-preload-scheduler.test.js` | Admission control must be attached to job creation, not bolted onto runtime only. Queue pressure, overload, and degraded mode must be user-visible. |
| Specialized workers | `workers/compile-server`, `workers/distill`, `workers/quantize`, `workers/runtime-build`, `workers/tokenizer-train`, `workers/tsac`, `workers/itkv`, `workers/video-tokenize`, `workers/vision-tokenize`, `workers/audio-tokenize`, `workers/media-redact`, `workers/multimodal-redact-audio`, `workers/multimodal-redact-image` | Every worker needs a manifest: queue names, job types, required env, resource class, lease/heartbeat interval, max concurrency, retry policy, outputs, smoke command, and supported cancellation points. |
| Account surfaces | `public/account/distill-runs.html`, `public/account/pipelines.html`, `public/account/pipelines/index.html`, `public/account/repeated-workflows.html` | Account UX must show one job center, not isolated job islands: active work, blocked work, failed work, approvals, artifacts, logs, costs, cancel/resume, retry, and next action. |
| Tests | `tests/wave429-pipeline-job-ownership.test.js`, `tests/wave459-distill-reliability.test.js`, `tests/wave710-active-learning-queue.test.js`, `tests/wave729-load-queue.test.js`, compile/distill/pipeline suites | Existing tests prove pieces. Node 36 requires cross-family crash/restart, duplicate-submit, cancellation, retry, resume, orphan recovery, and account/API/CLI/TUI parity tests. |

#### External Baseline

| Reference | Baseline to absorb | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| Temporal durable execution docs, `https://docs.temporal.io/temporal` | Workflow executions keep state/progress through failures via event history; workers execute application code; workflows can resume after crashes and react to events. | Kolm long-running jobs need a persisted event history or equivalent append-only phase log, not only transient process memory. |
| Kubernetes Jobs docs, `https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/` | Jobs define completions, parallelism, indexed work, backoff limits, active deadlines, suspend/resume behavior, and terminal states. | Kolm cloud/GPU/device jobs need explicit parallelism, backoff, deadline, suspension, replacement, and terminal-state semantics. |
| Argo Workflows fields docs, `https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/release-3.7/fields/` | Workflows model active deadlines, retries, templates, artifacts, and workflow-level execution policy. | Kolm pipelines should be executable workflow specs with phase templates, artifacts, cache keys, retries, and deadlines. |
| BullMQ retry docs, `https://docs.bullmq.io/guide/retrying-failing-jobs` | Failed jobs can retry with fixed/exponential backoff, jitter, attempts, and priority-respecting requeue. | Kolm queue policies must include attempts, backoff, jitter, priority, max retry cost, and dead-letter visibility. |
| Celery task docs, `https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/tasks.html` | Production task systems emphasize idempotent task functions and retry backoff/jitter/max retries. | Every Kolm task handler must be idempotent or explicitly single-shot with compensation/cleanup. |
| Stripe idempotent requests docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/idempotent_requests` | Idempotency keys make retries safe by returning the first result for the same key and rejecting parameter mismatches. | `POST` job creation must accept idempotency keys, bind them to tenant and normalized inputs, and replay the original job/result. |
| OpenTelemetry messaging semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/messaging/messaging-spans/` | Messaging traces distinguish create/send/receive/process operations and carry destination, system, operation, message, and consumer-group attributes. | Kolm queues need trace spans for enqueue, lease, process, retry, dead-letter, webhook, and artifact publication. |

#### Canonical Job Object

Every long-running operation should expose a canonical object. Family-specific details can sit under `inputs`, `outputs`, `metrics`, or `extensions`, but the top-level shape must remain stable:

```json
{
  "job_id": "job_...",
  "workflow_id": "wf_...",
  "tenant_id": "tn_...",
  "namespace": "default",
  "family": "compile | distill | train | benchmark | artifact | registry | cloud | device | media | audit | purge | approval | active_learning | billing | smoke",
  "type": "compile.kolm.v1",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "idempotency_key_hash": "sha256:...",
  "owner": {
    "user_id": "usr_...",
    "team_id": "team_...",
    "role": "admin | developer | auditor | service"
  },
  "status": "accepted",
  "phase": "validate_inputs",
  "state_version": 1,
  "priority": "interactive | normal | batch | background | compliance",
  "queue": {
    "name": "compile",
    "partition": "tenant_or_region",
    "position": 0,
    "admitted_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "worker": {
    "worker_id": null,
    "lease_id": null,
    "leased_at": null,
    "heartbeat_at": null,
    "host": null,
    "version": null
  },
  "attempts": {
    "current": 0,
    "max": 3,
    "backoff": "exponential_jitter",
    "last_error_code": null
  },
  "timeouts": {
    "queue_deadline_ms": 600000,
    "run_deadline_ms": 3600000,
    "lease_ttl_ms": 60000,
    "heartbeat_ttl_ms": 120000
  },
  "budgets": {
    "max_cost_usd": 0,
    "max_tokens": 0,
    "max_gpu_seconds": 0,
    "max_storage_bytes": 0
  },
  "resources": {
    "provider": null,
    "region": null,
    "gpu": null,
    "cpu": null,
    "memory_bytes": null,
    "storage_provider": null
  },
  "inputs": {
    "artifact_id": null,
    "dataset_id": null,
    "model_id": null,
    "config_hash": "sha256:..."
  },
  "outputs": {
    "artifact_id": null,
    "manifest_url": null,
    "report_url": null,
    "logs_url": null
  },
  "progress": {
    "percent": 0,
    "message": "Queued",
    "current_step": 0,
    "total_steps": 0
  },
  "events": {
    "stream_url": "/v1/jobs/job_.../stream",
    "webhook_event_types": ["job.succeeded", "job.failed"]
  },
  "audit": {
    "privacy_class": "internal | restricted | regulated",
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "prompt_values_included": false,
    "retention_days": 90,
    "trace_id": "..."
  },
  "control": {
    "can_cancel": true,
    "can_retry": true,
    "can_resume": false,
    "cancel_requested_at": null,
    "resume_from_checkpoint": null
  },
  "lineage": {
    "parent_job_id": null,
    "child_job_ids": [],
    "artifact_ids": [],
    "receipt_ids": []
  },
  "created_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "terminal_at": null
}
```

#### Canonical State Machine

| State | Meaning | Required behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `accepted` | Request validated and idempotency record created. | Safe to return to user; duplicate same key returns this job. |
| `queued` | Work is persisted and waiting for admission. | Queue name, priority, rough position, and waiting reason are visible. |
| `admitted` | Capacity, quotas, budgets, and provider readiness allow execution. | Budget is reserved or explicitly soft-reserved. |
| `leased` | A worker has claimed the job. | Lease id, worker id, heartbeat deadline, and attempt number are recorded. |
| `running` | Worker is executing a phase. | Progress events, logs, metrics, and trace spans are appended. |
| `checkpointed` | Phase state is persisted for resume. | Checkpoint digest and resume boundary are visible. |
| `waiting` | Workflow is blocked on approval, quota, user input, external provider, or timer. | Waiting reason and next action are visible in account/API/CLI/TUI. |
| `retry_scheduled` | Attempt failed and policy permits retry. | Next attempt time, reason, backoff, jitter, and cost impact are visible. |
| `cancelling` | Cancel requested; worker is reaching a safe stop point. | No false instant-cancel claim; phase-specific semantics are shown. |
| `cancelled` | Job stopped by user/system before success. | Partial outputs are either deleted, retained with labels, or quarantined. |
| `succeeded` | Terminal success. | Outputs, artifact digests, receipts, metrics, and next actions are present. |
| `failed` | Terminal failure. | Error code, safe message, retryability, support bundle, and cleanup state are present. |
| `timed_out` | Deadline exceeded. | Timeout type and cleanup state are visible. |
| `orphaned` | Lease expired or worker disappeared. | Recovery scanner owns the next transition. |
| `recovered` | Orphaned job was resumed or safely failed. | Recovery reason and new attempt lineage are recorded. |
| `dead_lettered` | Automatic processing is exhausted or unsafe. | Operator review path and customer-safe status are visible. |
| `archived` | Retention window closed. | Metadata remains sufficient for audit; regulated data follows retention policy. |

#### Job Families And Required Product Semantics

| Family | Examples | Must carry |
|---|---|---|
| `compile` | `.kolm` compile, IR lowering, runtime target build, signed artifact creation | compiler version, target runtime, input digest, build cache, diagnostics, artifact digest, signature, SBOM, expiry, and downloadable output. |
| `distill` / `train` | local distill, cloud distill, teacher council, progressive distill, DAQ, TAAS, active learning | dataset snapshot, capture window, teacher/student model versions, consent/license class, checkpoint, eval/K-Score, cost meter, GPU/provider, rollback, and approval gates. |
| `benchmark` / `eval` | K-Score calibration, redaction benchmark, provider bakeoff, runtime/device perf | eval version, fixture set, model/runtime/hardware/pricing snapshot, raw report, pass/fail thresholds, public/private label. |
| `artifact` / `registry` | publish, import, compose, diff, revoke, verify publisher, marketplace review | namespace, version, digest, signature, license, provenance, malware/policy scan, entitlement, moderation state, takedown state. |
| `cloud` / `deploy` | BYOC deploy, hosted worker, R2/S3 storage smoke, edge deploy, multi-region route | provider, region, credentials readiness, storage readiness, capacity, secret redaction, deployment id, rollback plan, smoke result. |
| `device` / `runtime` | device install, runtime smoke, phone/laptop/edge artifact fit | device profile, runtime, memory/thermal/battery, model fit, install log, benchmark result, offline state. |
| `media` / `tokenize` / `redact` | image/audio/video tokenization, media redaction, multimodal fixture processing | media hashes, privacy class, redaction report, chunk plan, derived artifacts, failure quarantine. |
| `audit` / `export` / `purge` | audit bundle, SIEM export, GDPR/tenant purge, evidence package | requester authority, scope, legal basis, retention, export destination, irreversible actions, receipt. |
| `approval` / `moderation` | distill approval, marketplace moderation, security exception, enterprise change | approver set, SLA, policy, decision log, escalation, final disposition. |
| `active_learning` / `self_improvement` | capture triage, label request, rare-case generation, feedback loop | source event, uncertainty score, reviewer, labeling state, privacy boundary, training eligibility. |
| `billing` / `reconciliation` | usage aggregation, invoice adjustment, credits, chargeback | period, tenant, source receipts, idempotent ledger entries, Stripe/finance ids, audit trail. |
| `smoke` / `release` | production route smoke, provider smoke, screenshot audit, post-deploy proof | target environment, commit, route list, credential class, logs, artifacts, report URL, promotion decision. |

#### Redlines

- No long-running work may depend only on an HTTP request staying open.
- No job may be created without tenant, owner/service actor, idempotency key or explicit non-idempotent reason, status, phase, and visibility class.
- No `queued` response may be returned if no worker or documented manual operator path can execute that job.
- No in-process-only queue may be marketed as production durable.
- No retry policy may exist without maximum attempts, backoff, jitter, retryable error classes, cost caps, and idempotency.
- No cancellation button may appear unless the current phase defines whether cancel is immediate, cooperative, deferred, compensating, or impossible.
- No progress UI may show fake precision; percentages must come from phase weights, artifact counts, bytes, tokens, records, or explicit rough estimate labels.
- No job log may leak API keys, provider secrets, prompts, PHI, customer data, raw media, or unredacted stack traces across tenants.
- No cloud/GPU job may start unless provider, storage, quota, cost, and region readiness are checked and recorded.
- No orphaned job may stay invisible; recovery scans must mark, resume, fail, or dead-letter it.
- No artifact output may be downloadable without digest, media type, expiration, access policy, and provenance.
- No job status may drift between API, account, CLI, TUI, webhook, email, docs, and support/admin views.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `36.1` Job identity, workflow schema, phases, state machine, tenant ownership, idempotency, and lineage | A canonical job schema covers every family, every creating route accepts or generates safe idempotency, duplicate submits replay the original result, state transitions are validated, parent/child lineage is preserved, and account/API/CLI/TUI/docs use the same status vocabulary. |
| `36.2` Durable queues, worker leases, heartbeats, checkpoints, retries, timeouts, cancellation, resume, and orphan recovery | Jobs persist outside process memory; workers use leases and heartbeats; retries use bounded jittered backoff; timeouts are explicit; cancellation is phase-aware; checkpoints are resumable; orphan recovery is tested by killing workers mid-job. |
| `36.3` Compile, distill, train, benchmark, artifact, registry, cloud, device, media, audit, purge, and approval job families | Every job family has a manifest with inputs, outputs, phases, resources, privacy class, cancellation points, retry policy, cost budget, artifacts, logs, metrics, and product owner. |
| `36.4` Capacity, admission control, priorities, quotas, cost caps, GPU/cloud scheduling, backpressure, and fairness | Job creation and dispatch enforce tenant plan limits, spend limits, concurrency, provider readiness, storage readiness, GPU availability, regional policy, priority, backpressure, and visible wait reasons. |
| `36.5` Job UX across account, API, CLI/TUI, SSE, notifications, webhooks, logs, artifacts, and support/admin operations | Account has a unified job center; API has list/detail/events/control endpoints; CLI/TUI can list/watch/cancel/retry/resume; webhooks/email/status emit canonical events; support/admin can inspect without secrets. |
| `36.6` Evidence: multi-worker tests, crash/restart drills, duplicate-submit tests, cancellation/retry/resume probes, and production job smoke | Release gates run duplicate-submit, worker crash, app restart, lease expiry, retry, timeout, cancellation, resume, dead-letter, webhook, account/API/CLI/TUI parity, and production smoke tests across representative job families. |

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |

| `W-JOB-001` | `36.1` | Define canonical job schema and JSON envelope. | All existing job routes can map to one schema without losing family-specific data. |
| `W-JOB-002` | `36.1` | Add state transition validator. | Invalid transitions fail tests and return safe errors instead of mutating state. |
| `W-JOB-003` | `36.1` | Add idempotency table/ledger. | Replayed `POST` requests return the original job/result and parameter mismatch is rejected. |
| `W-JOB-004` | `36.1` | Add job lineage and artifact binding. | Parent jobs, child jobs, artifacts, receipts, evals, and reports are queryable from one record. |

| `W-JOB-006` | `36.2` | Implement worker leases and heartbeats. | Dead workers are detected, leases expire, and duplicate workers cannot both commit final output. |
| `W-JOB-007` | `36.2` | Add checkpoint/resume boundaries. | Distill, compile, benchmark, pipeline, and media jobs can resume or explain why resume is impossible. |
| `W-JOB-008` | `36.2` | Add retry/dead-letter policy. | Attempts, backoff, jitter, retryable classes, max cost, and dead-letter review are visible and tested. |
| `W-JOB-009` | `36.2` | Add cancellation semantics by phase. | Account/API/CLI/TUI show whether cancellation is immediate, cooperative, deferred, compensating, or unsupported. |
| `W-JOB-010` | `36.3` | Write job family manifests. | Compile, distill, train, benchmark, artifact, registry, cloud, device, media, audit, purge, approval, active-learning, billing, and smoke families have machine-readable manifests. |
| `W-JOB-011` | `36.3` | Normalize compile/pipeline job status. | `/v1/compile`, `/v1/pipeline/*`, and CLI/docs use the canonical schema and event stream. |
| `W-JOB-012` | `36.3` | Normalize distill/train job status. | Local/cloud/airgap/progressive distill jobs expose dataset, teacher/student, checkpoint, eval, cost, and approval metadata. |
| `W-JOB-013` | `36.3` | Normalize benchmark/eval jobs. | K-Score, redaction, provider, runtime, and device benchmarks publish raw reports, thresholds, and claim-scope state. |
| `W-JOB-014` | `36.3` | Normalize audit/export/purge jobs. | Irreversible or compliance-sensitive jobs require authority, scope, legal basis, receipt, and retention evidence. |
| `W-JOB-015` | `36.4` | Add admission control. | Queue admission enforces plan, quota, budget, provider/storage readiness, capacity, region, and priority. |
| `W-JOB-016` | `36.4` | Add queue fairness and backpressure UI. | Tenants see wait reasons and retry-after/backpressure without noisy internal details. |
| `W-JOB-017` | `36.5` | Build unified account job center. | Users can see, filter, watch, cancel, retry, resume, download outputs, and open support bundles across all job families. |
| `W-JOB-018` | `36.5` | Add CLI/TUI parity. | `kolm jobs` or equivalent can list/watch/cancel/retry/resume and print safe support bundles. |
| `W-JOB-019` | `36.6` | Add chaos and restart tests. | Tests kill workers, restart app, expire leases, duplicate submits, cancel active work, and prove recovery. |
| `W-JOB-020` | `36.6` | Add production job smoke pack. | Preview/production deploy runs representative compile, distill fixture, benchmark, artifact publish, storage smoke, webhook, and cleanup jobs with evidence report. |

#### Node 36 Relax Redline

Node `36` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from live product evidence:

- What is every active, blocked, failed, retrying, cancelling, and succeeded job for a tenant?
- Which worker owns each running job, when was its last heartbeat, and what happens if that worker disappears?
- Can the same job be safely submitted twice without duplicate cost, duplicate artifacts, or duplicate emails/webhooks?
- What exact phase is running, what has been checkpointed, and what is the next safe cancellation point?
- Which jobs are waiting on capacity, quota, provider readiness, user approval, external credentials, or cost budget?
- Which outputs were produced, what are their digests, where are they stored, who can download them, and when do they expire?
- Which retries are scheduled, why are they retryable, and how much more cost or time can they consume?
- Which jobs are local-only and which are production-durable?
- Can support investigate a failed job without seeing customer secrets or raw regulated data?
- Does production smoke prove at least one representative job from compile, distill/train, benchmark, artifact/storage, cloud/deploy, webhook, and cleanup?

The relax condition for node `36` is not "there is a job endpoint." It is: every long-running operation in Kolm behaves like a durable, observable, idempotent, cancel-aware, recoverable workflow with the same truth across API, account, CLI/TUI, events, logs, artifacts, docs, support, and production evidence.

#### Node 36 Durable Execution Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. This work belongs to node `36` because the product already has job-shaped code across compile, distill, pipelines, media, labels, active learning, workers, package release readiness, and account progress pages. The problem is not absence of jobs; it is that job semantics are still split across family-specific modules, file stores, transient queues, and page-specific UX.

This addendum is spec-only. It does not edit `src/jobs.js`, pipeline code, distill code, worker code, account pages, tests, generated docs, or package files in this lane. It records the durable execution contract that implementation agents must converge on.

##### Current Worktree Delta

| Evidence checked on 2026-05-25 | Current signal | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/jobs.js` | Generic local job registry has five statuses: `queued`, `running`, `completed`, `failed`, and `cancelled`. It stores per-job JSON files, mirrors a legacy JSONL transcript, and supports log tail/prune. | Good local operator base, but not enough for production durable execution. It lacks tenant, idempotency, phase, attempt, lease, heartbeat, checkpoint, retry, timeout, budget, worker, lineage, and support bundle fields. |
| `src/cloud-distill.js` | Cloud distill has an append-only tenant-scoped JSONL ledger, backend status honesty, cost meter rows, `queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled`, and tenant-fenced cancel/list/status paths. | Strong honesty pattern. It should be upgraded into the canonical job schema so all other job families inherit backend readiness, cost meter, tenant fence, cancellation, and no-pool-visible states. |
| `src/pipeline-orchestrator.js`, `src/pipeline-runner.js`, `src/pipeline-routes.js` | Pipeline rows are tenant-scoped, pipelines can run with measured phases, and routes expose list/create/get/delete/run/report flows. Pipeline runner comments describe idempotent inputs at the functional level. | Pipeline execution needs durable event history and restart/replay semantics, not only synchronous route execution or transient SSE. |
| `src/active-learning-queue.js`, `src/label-queue.js`, `src/distill-approval-queue.js` | Queues exist for learning, labels, and approval workflows with tenant fields and statuses. | These must become job families or job-linked queues so reviewer assignment, stale work, retries, escalations, and audit trails are uniform. |
| `src/load-queue.js`, `src/preload-scheduler.js` | Load queue and preloading logic exists, with lease-related concepts in `load-queue.js`. | Runtime admission control should be connected to job admission, capacity, quotas, and visible wait reasons. |
| `workers/compile-server`, `workers/distill`, `workers/quantize`, `workers/runtime-build`, `workers/tokenizer-train`, `workers/tsac`, `workers/itkv`, media workers | Specialized worker directories exist, and the compile server has Docker, compose, Helm, offline, auth, health, and compile endpoints. | Every worker needs a machine-readable worker manifest with job types, queue names, env, resource class, lease, heartbeat, concurrency, retry, cancellation points, outputs, and smoke command. |
| `public/account/pipelines.html`, `public/account/distill-runs.html`, approvals and related account pages | Account pages exist, but some pipeline rows are placeholder or illustrative until signed-in hydration. | Finished job UX needs one operational job center with real data, not isolated placeholder panels. |
| Tests | Existing tests cover pipeline ownership, distill reliability, load queue, active learning queue, approvals, cloud distill, media workers, release verify, and many compile/distill paths. | V2 needs cross-family tests: duplicate submit, worker crash, lease expiry, retry, cancel, resume, dead-letter, account/API/CLI/TUI parity, support bundle, and production smoke. |

##### Current External Durable-Execution Baselines

| Source | Current standard to absorb | Required Kolm behavior |
|---|---|---|
| Temporal durable execution, `https://docs.temporal.io/temporal` | Temporal describes durable workflow executions that keep state and progress through failures using event history, and workflows can be resumable, recoverable, and reactive. | Kolm jobs need an append-only event history or equivalent canonical transition log, not only last-known status fields. |
| Kubernetes Jobs, `https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/` | Kubernetes Jobs define completions, parallelism, backoff limits, active deadlines, terminal failure, indexed work, pod failure policy, and cleanup. | Cloud/GPU/device jobs need explicit completions, parallelism, backoff, active deadline, terminal state, cleanup, and manual intervention semantics. |
| Argo Workflows field reference, `https://argo-workflows.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fields/` | Argo models workflow-level priority, retry strategies, templates, pod spec patches, artifacts, and execution policy. | Kolm pipelines and multi-step compile/distill/media jobs should be executable workflow specs with phase templates, resource needs, artifact outputs, retries, and deadlines. |
| BullMQ retry docs, `https://docs.bullmq.io/guide/retrying-failing-jobs` | BullMQ retry policy includes attempts, fixed/exponential backoff, and optional jitter. | Kolm retry policy must record attempts, retryable error classes, fixed/exponential backoff, jitter, next attempt time, max retry cost, and DLQ path. |
| Celery task docs, `https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/tasks.html` | Celery exposes task retry options such as autoretry classes, max retries, retry backoff, max backoff, and jitter. | Kolm job handlers must declare retryable exceptions, max attempts, backoff max, jitter, idempotency requirement, and compensation/cleanup rules. |
| Stripe idempotency docs, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/idempotent_requests` | Idempotency keys are random high-entropy strings, should avoid sensitive data, are accepted for POST, and replay prevents duplicate work. | Every Kolm job-creating POST must accept idempotency, hash it, bind it to tenant and normalized input, reject mismatches, and replay original job/result. |
| OpenTelemetry messaging spans, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/messaging/messaging-spans/` | Messaging spans require system and operation attributes and distinguish send, receive, process, publish, consume, poll, message ID, destination, and batch count. | Kolm enqueue, lease, process, retry, dead-letter, webhook, and artifact-publication paths must emit consistent trace attributes. |

##### Durable Job Ledger V2

Every long-running operation should be backed by a canonical ledger. Family-specific data stays in `inputs`, `outputs`, `metrics`, and `extensions`, but the top-level contract must be stable.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-job-ledger-v2",
  "job_id": "job_...",
  "workflow_id": "wf_...",
  "family": "compile",
  "type": "compile.kolm.v1",
  "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
  "namespace": "default",
  "environment": "local|preview|production|self_hosted|airgap",
  "idempotency": {
    "key_hash": "sha256:...",
    "normalized_input_hash": "sha256:...",
    "first_request_id": "req_...",
    "replay_policy": "return_existing|reject_mismatch|not_idempotent",
    "expires_at": "2026-05-26T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "state": {
    "status": "queued",
    "phase": "validate_inputs",
    "phase_index": 0,
    "terminal": false,
    "transition_seq": 1,
    "transition_reason": "submitted"
  },
  "queue": {
    "name": "compile",
    "partition": "tenant_or_region",
    "priority": "interactive|normal|batch|background|compliance",
    "position": null,
    "admission_state": "accepted|waiting_capacity|waiting_quota|waiting_approval|waiting_provider|waiting_budget|rejected",
    "wait_reason": null
  },
  "worker": {
    "worker_id": null,
    "worker_version": null,
    "lease_id": null,
    "leased_at": null,
    "heartbeat_at": null,
    "heartbeat_deadline_at": null,
    "host": null,
    "resource_class": null
  },
  "attempts": {
    "current": 0,
    "max": 3,
    "retryable_error_codes": [],
    "backoff": "exponential_jitter",
    "jitter": 0.5,
    "next_attempt_at": null,
    "dead_letter_at": null
  },
  "timeouts": {
    "queue_deadline_at": null,
    "run_deadline_at": null,
    "lease_ttl_ms": 60000,
    "heartbeat_ttl_ms": 120000,
    "active_deadline_ms": null
  },
  "budgets": {
    "max_cost_usd": null,
    "reserved_cost_usd": 0,
    "max_tokens": null,
    "max_gpu_seconds": null,
    "max_storage_bytes": null
  },
  "progress": {
    "source": "phase_weight|bytes|records|tokens|rough_estimate",
    "percent": null,
    "message": "Queued",
    "current_step": 0,
    "total_steps": 0,
    "updated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "control": {
    "can_cancel": true,
    "cancel_semantics": "immediate|cooperative|deferred|compensating|unsupported",
    "can_retry": true,
    "can_resume": false,
    "checkpoint_id": null,
    "safe_resume_from_phase": null
  },
  "privacy": {
    "privacy_class": "internal|restricted|regulated",
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "prompt_values_included": false,
    "raw_customer_data_included": false,
    "retention_days": 90
  },
  "observability": {
    "request_id": "req_...",
    "trace_id": null,
    "messaging_system": null,
    "queue_span_id": null,
    "support_bundle_id": null,
    "event_stream_url": null
  },
  "inputs": {},
  "outputs": {},
  "metrics": {},
  "lineage": {
    "parent_job_id": null,
    "child_job_ids": [],
    "artifact_ids": [],
    "receipt_ids": [],
    "report_ids": []
  },
  "created_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "terminal_at": null
}
```

##### Worker Manifest V2

Every worker directory should ship a manifest, even if it is local-only. A worker without a manifest is not production-owned.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-worker-manifest-v2",
  "worker_id": "compile-server",
  "owner_node": "36",
  "job_families": ["compile"],
  "queues": ["compile"],
  "entrypoint": "workers/compile-server/server.mjs",
  "runtime": "node",
  "resource_class": "cpu|gpu|npu|io|network|browser|edge",
  "required_env": ["KOLM_SHARED_SECRET"],
  "optional_env": ["KOLM_OFFLINE", "KOLM_ARTIFACT_DIR"],
  "secrets_redacted": true,
  "max_concurrency": 1,
  "lease_ttl_ms": 60000,
  "heartbeat_interval_ms": 30000,
  "retry_policy": {
    "max_attempts": 3,
    "backoff": "exponential_jitter",
    "retryable_errors": [],
    "non_retryable_errors": []
  },
  "cancellation_points": ["queued", "before_artifact_write"],
  "checkpoint_points": [],
  "outputs": ["artifact", "manifest", "logs"],
  "smoke_commands": [],
  "production_ready": false,
  "local_only_reason": null
}
```

##### Job Center UX Requirements

The account job center is an operational screen. It should be dense, filterable, accessible, and honest.

- Top summary: active, waiting, blocked, failed, retrying, cancelling, succeeded, and dead-lettered counts by family.
- Filters: family, status, phase, namespace, owner, environment, priority, waiting reason, worker, date range, artifact, and support bundle.
- Every row must show `job_id`, family, status, phase, priority, owner, created time, last update, progress source, wait reason, cost/budget state, and next action.
- Critical controls must be keyboardable and at least 44px high: watch, cancel, retry, resume, open logs, download output, open support bundle, and view lineage.
- Cancel/retry/resume buttons must disable during submission, show loading state, and explain phase semantics before action.
- Progress bars cannot show fake precision. If progress is estimated, label it as estimated and show the underlying phase or unit.
- Waiting states must say what the user can do: add credentials, approve, raise quota, wait for capacity, lower budget, change region, fix validation, or contact support.
- Error states must show safe error code, retryability, last attempt, next retry time, support bundle, and no raw secret/prompt/regulated data.
- Logs must default to redacted summaries and require explicit authorized drill-in for sensitive data.
- Mobile must avoid horizontal scroll, preserve focus rings, and keep explanation text visible without hover dependency.
- CLI/TUI watch output must mirror the same states and next actions as account; JSON mode must be schema-stable.

##### Family Manifests Required By V2

| Family | Required manifest fields beyond the canonical ledger |
|---|---|
| Compile | compiler version, source digest, target runtime, cache key, artifact digest, signature, SBOM, diagnostics, package state, download expiry, and no-egress state. |
| Distill/train | dataset snapshot, capture window, teacher/student versions, approval state, GPU/provider lane, checkpoint path, eval/K-Score report, training meter, rollback artifact, and data-rights class. |
| Pipeline | YAML hash, classifier artifact, specialist artifacts, route frequencies, phase graph, partial-failure behavior, run report, and event stream replay. |
| Benchmark/eval | fixture set, public/private evidence class, model/runtime/hardware/pricing snapshot, thresholds, raw report, confidence interval, and claim-scope label. |
| Artifact/registry | namespace, version, digest, signature, provenance, malware/policy scan, entitlement, publisher, review state, takedown state, and compatibility matrix. |
| Cloud/deploy/storage | provider, region, credential readiness, object-store readiness, capacity, secret-redaction state, deployment id, smoke result, rollback plan, and data-residency label. |
| Device/runtime | device profile, runtime, install state, memory/thermal/battery caveat, artifact fit, runtime smoke, offline state, uninstall/rollback, and attestation. |
| Media/tokenize/redact | media hashes, chunk plan, privacy class, redaction report, derived artifact IDs, quarantine state, preview-safe thumbnail state, and retention policy. |
| Approval/moderation | approver set, policy, SLA, escalation, decision log, conflict-of-interest rule, final disposition, and audit export. |
| Active learning/labeling | source event, uncertainty score, privacy boundary, reviewer assignment, stale timeout, training eligibility, holdout protection, and label quality check. |
| Billing/reconciliation | billing period, source receipts, idempotent ledger entries, provider invoice link, Stripe/finance IDs, adjustment reason, and audit trail. |
| Release/smoke | target environment, commit, generated artifact versions, route list, credential class, screenshot report, logs, production evidence URL, rollback decision, and promotion result. |
