| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-JOB-V2-001` | `36.1` | Generate `docs/job-ledger-v2.schema.json`. | Canonical job schema validates generic jobs, cloud distill jobs, pipeline runs, benchmarks, approvals, and release smokes without losing family data. |
| `W-JOB-V2-002` | `36.1` | Add job state transition table. | Legal transitions are generated into tests and invalid transitions fail with safe errors. |
| `W-JOB-V2-003` | `36.1` | Add idempotency ledger for job creation. | Every job-creating POST hashes tenant, key, and normalized input; replay returns original; mismatch rejects. |
| `W-JOB-V2-004` | `36.1` | Add job lineage graph. | Parent/child jobs, artifacts, reports, receipts, events, and support bundles are queryable from one graph. |
| `W-JOB-V2-005` | `36.2` | Add durable event history. | Job transitions are append-only and replay can reconstruct current state after app restart. |
| `W-JOB-V2-006` | `36.2` | Add worker leases and heartbeats. | Running jobs show worker, lease, heartbeat deadline, and recovery behavior. |
| `W-JOB-V2-007` | `36.2` | Add orphan recovery scanner. | Expired leases become recovered, retried, failed, or dead-lettered with evidence. |
| `W-JOB-V2-008` | `36.2` | Add checkpoint/resume manifest. | Each job family declares checkpoint points and whether resume is possible or intentionally unsupported. |
| `W-JOB-V2-009` | `36.2` | Add retry and DLQ policy. | Attempts, retryable errors, backoff, jitter, next attempt, max retry cost, and DLQ review path are visible and tested. |
| `W-JOB-V2-010` | `36.2` | Add phase-aware cancellation. | Cancel semantics are immediate, cooperative, deferred, compensating, or unsupported per phase, with account/API/CLI parity. |
| `W-JOB-V2-011` | `36.3` | Add worker manifests for every worker directory. | Compile, distill, quantize, runtime-build, tokenizer, TSAC, ITKV, media, and constrained workers have machine-readable manifests and smoke commands. |
| `W-JOB-V2-012` | `36.3` | Normalize generic, cloud-distill, pipeline, approval, label, active-learning, load, and release jobs. | All job-shaped modules can export canonical ledger rows or documented local-only exceptions. |
| `W-JOB-V2-013` | `36.3` | Add family-specific manifest registry. | Compile, distill, pipeline, benchmark, artifact, cloud, device, media, approval, active-learning, billing, and release families have required fields and owners. |
| `W-JOB-V2-014` | `36.4` | Add admission control to job creation. | Plan, entitlement, quota, budget, provider readiness, storage readiness, region, capacity, and priority are checked before work starts. |
| `W-JOB-V2-015` | `36.4` | Add queue fairness and backpressure contract. | Tenants see wait reasons, retry-after, and capacity state without leaking other tenants or internal topology. |
| `W-JOB-V2-016` | `36.4` | Add cost reservation and budget guardrails. | Expensive jobs reserve or soft-reserve budget, show cost risk, and stop before unapproved overrun. |
| `W-JOB-V2-017` | `36.5` | Build unified account job center. | Account users can filter, watch, cancel, retry, resume, inspect lineage, download outputs, and open support bundles across all families. |
| `W-JOB-V2-018` | `36.5` | Add CLI/TUI job parity. | `kolm jobs` or successor commands list/watch/cancel/retry/resume with the same states, next actions, and JSON schema as account/API. |
| `W-JOB-V2-019` | `36.5` | Add event/webhook/email parity. | Job created, phase changed, waiting, retry scheduled, cancelled, failed, succeeded, dead-lettered, and recovered events share schema and delivery proof. |
| `W-JOB-V2-020` | `36.5` | Add support-safe job bundles. | Failed jobs export safe problem details, redacted logs, trace IDs, event history, inputs hash, outputs, and next actions without secrets. |
| `W-JOB-V2-021` | `36.6` | Add deterministic job chaos suite. | Tests kill workers, restart app, expire leases, duplicate submits, cancel active work, retry failures, resume checkpoints, and recover or dead-letter jobs. |
| `W-JOB-V2-022` | `36.6` | Add account/API/CLI/TUI parity tests. | The same seeded job set renders consistently in API, account, CLI JSON, CLI text, and TUI views. |
| `W-JOB-V2-023` | `36.6` | Add production job smoke pack. | Preview/production run representative compile, distill fixture, pipeline, benchmark, artifact/storage, webhook, and cleanup jobs with evidence. |
| `W-JOB-V2-024` | `36.6` | Add durable execution release packet. | Release packet includes schema, manifests, state table, chaos logs, smoke logs, screenshot proof, event/webhook proof, and rollback decision. |

##### Node `36` V2 Relax Redline

Node `36` can relax only when all of the following are true:

- every long-running operation emits or maps to `kolm-job-ledger-v2`;
- every job creation path is idempotent or explicitly documented as non-idempotent with compensation;
- every worker has a manifest, smoke command, resource class, lease, heartbeat, retry policy, and cancellation/checkpoint declaration;
- every job transition is append-only, replayable, tenant-scoped, support-safe, and observable;
- worker crash, app restart, lease expiry, duplicate submit, retry, cancel, resume, timeout, and dead-letter behavior are tested;
- queue admission records quota, entitlement, budget, provider readiness, storage readiness, region, capacity, priority, and wait reason;
- account, API, CLI/TUI, SSE, webhooks, email, support, docs, and admin use the same status, phase, progress, and next-action vocabulary;
- progress UI avoids fake precision and labels estimates, units, phases, and blocked states clearly;
- failed jobs produce safe support bundles without secrets, raw prompts, PHI, customer media, or cross-tenant data;
- local-only queues and in-process simulations are visibly scoped and never marketed as production durable;
- production smoke proves representative jobs across compile, distill/train, pipeline, benchmark, artifact/storage, webhook/event, and cleanup paths.

### API Schemas, Validation, Errors, Versioning, And Generated-Client Compatibility Ledger V1

This node turns the API contract into a product primitive. Kolm cannot be state of the art if every route, account panel, CLI command, SDK method, webhook, SSE stream, and OpenAI-compatible response has a slightly different shape. The finished product needs one schema system that makes every public and post-auth surface predictable, typed, versioned, validated, documented, and safe to automate.

This is a research/spec-only pass. It records the contract layer the backend/frontend/SDK agents must converge on.

#### Why Node 37 Exists

The current codebase has good raw ingredients: `src/envelope.js`, `src/kolm-error.js`, `src/event-schema.js`, `src/output-schema.js`, `src/model-card-schema.js`, `src/streaming-contract.js`, `src/product-frontier-contracts.js`, OpenAPI generation, API route docs, SDK smoke tests, and hundreds of route-honesty tests. But a finished enterprise API is not just "routes exist" or "OpenAPI generated." It must provide a compatibility promise:

- response envelopes do not drift between route groups;
- errors are machine-actionable and support-safe;
- request and response schemas are source-controlled and tested;
- SDKs are generated or checked against the same contract;
- account UI and CLI/TUI consume the same shapes as external customers;
- webhook, SSE, streaming, and OpenAI-compatible responses are schema-bound;
- breaking changes are versioned, documented, diffed, and gated before release.

#### Current Repo Evidence

| Area | Current evidence | 100 percent completion interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Envelope helpers | `src/envelope.js`, `okEnvelope`, `attachEnvelopeHeaders`, `product-kernel-envelope.test.js`, `wave-wc05-envelope.test.js` | Good start, but every JSON API response needs a documented envelope or an explicit media-type exception. |
| Error helpers | `src/kolm-error.js`, many `{ ok:false, error }` JSON branches in `src/router.js` | Error codes exist, but the contract must converge on Problem Details compatibility with `code`, `request_id`, validation pointers, support-safe detail, and secret redaction. |
| Schema modules | `src/event-schema.js`, `src/output-schema.js`, `src/model-card-schema.js`, `src/streaming-contract.js`, `src/product-frontier-contracts.js` | Schema logic exists by domain; node 37 requires a central registry, version policy, fixtures, and schema-diff gate. |
| OpenAPI | `public/openapi.json` currently reports OpenAPI `3.0.3`, 556 paths, 586 operations, 163 tags, no `jsonSchemaDialect`, and no `webhooks` object | OpenAPI coverage is substantial; 100 percent requires request/response schemas, examples, security, errors, pagination, webhooks, SSE docs, and a migration plan toward 3.1/JSON Schema alignment if tooling supports it. |
| API route docs | `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `scripts/build-api-ref.cjs`, `scripts/build-openapi.cjs`, route-honesty tests | Route inventory is mature; it must become the source for route-to-schema-to-SDK-to-docs parity, not only descriptive docs. |
| SDK/package checks | `tests/wave470-sdk-node-smoke.test.js`, `tests/wave482-sdk-catalog-honesty.test.js`, `scripts/verify-sdk-dist.mjs`, SDK package directories | SDKs need generated or contract-checked clients with stable typed responses and backwards-compatibility tests. |
| OpenAI-compatible surface | `src/completions-api.js`, `tests/wave144-completions-api.test.js`, `tests/wave409k-openai-compat-surface.test.js` | OpenAI-compatible endpoints need explicit compatibility scope, streaming chunk schema, error mapping, and provider-specific caveat labels. |
| Webhooks/events | `src/event-schema.js`, event delivery node 34, notification/webhook work | Every webhook event needs a versioned schema, example payload, signature fixture, replay/idempotency semantics, and OpenAPI `webhooks` or companion event catalog. |

#### External Baseline

| Reference | Baseline to absorb | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAPI 3.1.1, `https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.1.html` | OAS defines required root fields, paths, components, security, tags, and a `webhooks` top-level map; 3.1 adds `jsonSchemaDialect` for Schema Objects. | Kolm should keep OpenAPI complete and evaluate 3.1 migration so JSON Schema, webhook documentation, and generated clients stop diverging. |
| JSON Schema 2020-12, `https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/json-schema-core` | JSON Schema defines schemas over JSON documents, schema identifiers, keywords, assertions, annotations, and vocabularies. | Kolm request/response bodies, artifact metadata, product graph, readiness ledgers, event payloads, and job objects should share versioned JSON Schemas where practical. |
| RFC 9457 Problem Details, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Problem Details provides `application/problem+json` and fields like `type`, `title`, `status`, `detail`, and `instance` for machine-readable API errors. | Kolm errors should be compatible with Problem Details while preserving Kolm-specific `code`, `request_id`, `retryable`, `doc_url`, `support_bundle_id`, and validation pointer extensions. |
| Stripe API errors/idempotency, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/errors` and `https://docs.stripe.com/api/idempotent_requests` | Stripe documents conventional HTTP status classes, stable error types, request logs, and idempotency-key reuse behavior. | Kolm mutation routes and job creation need stable error classes, request IDs, idempotency replay, mismatch detection, and support visibility. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/` | OTel defines versioned semantic attributes, spans, logs, metrics, and domain conventions including messaging and GenAI. | API contracts must bind request IDs, trace IDs, model/provider spans, queue spans, and event delivery spans into the same observability contract. |

#### Canonical Envelope Contract

Every JSON response should be one of these documented shapes:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "data": {},
  "meta": {
    "schema": "kolm.example.v1",
    "api_version": "2026-05-25",
    "trace_id": "..."
  },
  "links": {},
  "warnings": [],
  "next_actions": [],
  "evidence": {
    "source_paths": [],
    "generated_from": null,
    "secret_values_included": false
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "type": "https://kolm.ai/problems/validation_failed",
  "title": "Request validation failed",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "One or more fields are invalid.",
  "instance": "/v1/example/request/req_...",
  "code": "validation_failed",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "trace_id": "...",
  "retryable": false,
  "errors": [
    {
      "pointer": "/model",
      "code": "unknown_model",
      "message": "Model is not in the tenant catalog."
    }
  ],
  "doc_url": "https://kolm.ai/docs/api/errors#validation_failed",
  "secret_values_included": false
}
```

Documented exceptions are allowed only for:

- file downloads such as `.kolm` artifacts, signed bundles, CSV exports, and media outputs;
- Server-Sent Events and streaming chunks;
- OpenAI-compatible endpoints when strict compatibility requires another shape;
- health/status probes where plain text or minimal JSON is intentional;
- redirects and static assets.

Every exception still needs a contract: content type, status codes, auth, cache, errors, examples, and tests.

#### Schema Registry Requirements

| Schema class | Required fields | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| Request body schemas | `$id`, `$schema`, `title`, version, required fields, unknown-field policy, examples, invalid examples | route validation tests, fuzz/negative tests, OpenAPI reference, SDK request type |
| Response body schemas | envelope/exception type, `data` schema, `meta` schema, warnings, next actions, evidence, links | route fixture tests, SDK response type, account/CLI consumer proof |
| Error schemas | Problem Details fields, Kolm `code`, `request_id`, `retryable`, `doc_url`, validation pointers, redaction flags | negative route tests for validation/auth/quota/provider/internal errors |
| Event/webhook schemas | event type, version, source, subject, tenant, timestamp, data schema, signature, replay/idempotency | signature vectors, replay tests, webhook delivery docs, OpenAPI webhook catalog or event registry |
| SSE/streaming schemas | stream name, event type, sequence, chunk shape, done/error chunks, retry behavior | stream replay tests, client parser tests, browser/account proof |
| OpenAI-compatible schemas | compatibility level, request subset, response subset, stream chunk subset, unsupported parameters, error mapping | compatibility fixtures and public caveat docs |
| Artifact/job/readiness schemas | version, producer, source paths, readiness status, evidence, closeout gates, generated command | schema validation in generation scripts and stale-artifact diff gate |

#### Compatibility Rules

- Additive optional fields are allowed when they do not change existing meaning.
- New required fields require a new schema version and migration note.
- Enum expansion is treated as potentially breaking for generated clients unless clients are explicitly open-enum safe.
- Removing fields, renaming fields, changing types, changing status codes, or changing error codes is breaking.
- Changing pagination, idempotency, retry, cache, auth, or rate-limit semantics is breaking even if JSON shape is unchanged.
- Experimental/beta fields must be marked in schema and docs with graduation/removal policy.
- Internal-only routes must not leak into public SDK docs unless intentionally exposed.
- Deprecations need first-seen date, replacement, removal date or condition, usage telemetry, and account/docs/CLI warning path.

#### Contract Parity Matrix

| Consumer | Required contract |
|---|---|
| Public API users | OpenAPI operation, examples, request/response/error schema, auth, rate limit, idempotency, pagination, and changelog entry. |
| Account UI | Typed fetch helper, loading/empty/error states, same envelope/error mapping as API docs, and no hidden shape assumptions. |
| CLI/TUI | Same schema semantics as API; JSON mode must be machine-stable and non-JSON mode must preserve actionable error codes. |
| SDKs | Generated or schema-checked types, typed errors, pagination helpers, idempotency helpers, and smoke tests against local fixtures. |
| Webhooks | Versioned event schemas, signatures, retries, replay, dedupe, and test vectors. |
| SSE/streams | Event schema, sequence behavior, resume/replay policy, done/error semantics, and browser/CLI parser tests. |
| Docs | Every visible example is contract-tested or generated from fixtures; stale examples fail lint. |
| Support/admin | Request IDs, trace IDs, safe problem instances, support bundles, and no secret/raw-data exposure. |

#### Redlines

- No route may return an undocumented JSON shape.
- No mutation route may omit idempotency guidance or an explicit "not idempotent" reason.
- No error may return stack traces, raw provider secrets, prompts, PHI, or tenant data outside the caller's authority.
- No SDK method may use a shape not present in OpenAPI or a checked companion schema.
- No account page may rely on undocumented response fields.
- No generated doc example may be hand-edited without a fixture/source marker.
- No `ok:false` shape may coexist indefinitely with incompatible Problem Details fields without a migration plan.
- No breaking field/status/error change may ship without version, migration, changelog, and compatibility test updates.
- No webhook or stream event may be emitted without event type, version, schema, and parser fixture.
- No OpenAI-compatible endpoint may silently accept unsupported parameters or silently return incompatible chunks.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `37.1` Canonical envelope, media types, request IDs, correlation IDs, pagination, idempotency, and trace binding | Every route emits a canonical envelope or documented exception; every response carries request/support traceability; list routes define pagination; mutation routes define idempotency; route logs, traces, CLI, account UI, and support use the same IDs. |
| `37.2` Request/response schemas, JSON Schema/OpenAPI alignment, examples, fixtures, validation, and generated clients | Every public and post-auth operation has request, response, and error schemas; examples are fixture-backed; OpenAPI and JSON Schema generation are checked; SDKs compile against the same contract. |
| `37.3` Error taxonomy, Problem Details compatibility, validation pointers, provider failures, quota failures, and safe support details | Error responses are Problem Details compatible; validation errors use JSON Pointer; provider/quota/auth/idempotency/rate-limit/job errors have stable codes; support details are actionable and secret-safe. |
| `37.4` API versioning, compatibility policy, deprecations, beta/experimental gates, changelog, and migration tooling | Compatibility rules are written and enforced; breaking changes require new version/migration docs; deprecated fields/routes show replacements; beta features have scope labels and graduation/removal rules. |
| `37.5` Route-to-SDK/docs/account/CLI/TUI parity, webhook schemas, SSE schemas, streaming chunks, and OpenAI-compatible surfaces | Contract parity is proved across API docs, generated SDKs, account fetchers, CLI/TUI JSON output, webhooks, SSE streams, and OpenAI-compatible endpoints. |
| `37.6` Evidence: schema diff, contract tests, fuzz/negative tests, SDK smoke, compatibility matrix, and production contract smoke | Release gates run schema diff, OpenAPI diff, route contract tests, negative/fuzz tests, generated-client compile, SDK smoke, webhook/SSE parser tests, and production contract smoke. |

#### Node 37 Relax Redline

Node `37` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current contract evidence:

- What is the schema for every public and post-auth request, response, error, event, stream chunk, artifact, job, and readiness object?
- Which routes are canonical JSON envelopes, and which are intentional media-type exceptions?
- Can a user give support a request ID and have support find the safe trace, problem instance, logs, and related job/artifact without seeing secrets?
- Which errors are retryable, which are user-fixable, which are provider failures, and which require support?
- Can SDKs, account UI, CLI/TUI, docs examples, and OpenAPI all parse the same response shapes?
- What changed in the API contract since the last release, and is each change additive, deprecated, experimental, or breaking?
- Do webhook and SSE consumers have versioned schemas and parser fixtures?
- Does OpenAI-compatible behavior have an explicit tested subset instead of vague compatibility claims?
- Does production smoke prove the contract on the deployed domain, not only local fixtures?

The relax condition for node `37` is not "OpenAPI generated." It is: every machine-facing Kolm surface has a versioned, validated, documented, tested, support-safe contract that can evolve without surprising customers, SDKs, account UI, CLI/TUI, docs, webhooks, streams, or production operators.

#### Node 37 API Contract Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. API contracts, error contracts, stream contracts, webhook contracts, generated clients, account fetch behavior, and OpenAI-compatible compatibility scope belong to node `37`. This pass strengthens the existing node instead of adding a parallel taxonomy.

This is a spec-only edit. It does not modify route code, OpenAPI generation, SDKs, account JavaScript, CLI/TUI commands, tests, generated docs, or package artifacts in this lane.

##### Current Worktree Delta

| Evidence checked on 2026-05-25 | Current signal | Completion implication |

The next finish line is per-operation request, response, error, pagination, idempotency, stream, webhook, and SDK contracts. |

V2 needs a completeness grade per operation, not just presence. |

Completion requires a documented migration to Problem Details-compatible errors while keeping legacy aliases during a versioned transition. |
| `src/kolm-error.js` | Pure typed error class exists, but the file comment still references retiring hundreds of untyped `throw new Error(...)` sites. Current source scan found 828 `throw new Error` call sites in `src`. | Typed errors are seeded but not complete. Every public/post-auth route needs stable code, status, retryability, docs URL, support-safe detail, and redaction flags. |

The correct path is a compatibility migration, not a big-bang removal. New routes should emit canonical envelopes; old routes need an audit and deprecation plan. |
| `src/event-schema.js` | Event schema has strict fields, required fields, closed enums, multimodal fields, privacy provenance, vendor normalization, and validation helpers. | Strong foundation for event/webhook schemas. V2 needs contract completeness tests, schema diff, breaking-change diff, SDK generated type compile, negative/fuzz validation, webhook/SSE parsers, and production contract smoke. |

##### Current External Contract Baselines

| Source | Current standard to absorb | Required Kolm behavior |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAPI 3.1.1, `https://spec.openapis.org/oas/v3.1.1.html` | OAS is a standard interface description for humans and computers. A valid description must contain at least one of paths, components, or webhooks, and `jsonSchemaDialect` can set the default Schema Object dialect. | Kolm should keep 3.0.3 only if tooling requires it. Otherwise, create a migration decision for 3.1.x so JSON Schema dialect and webhooks can be first-class. |
| JSON Schema 2020-12, `https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/json-schema-core` | `$schema` identifies a dialect and schemas use vocabularies for identifiers, assertions, annotations, applicators, and unevaluated behavior. | Kolm schemas need `$id`, `$schema`, version, vocabulary assumptions, examples, invalid examples, and validator parity across routes, generated docs, SDKs, and scripts. |
| RFC 9457 Problem Details, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457` | Problem Details uses `application/problem+json` and fields such as `type`, `title`, `detail`, and `instance`, with extensions for validation pointers or domain-specific details. | Kolm errors should be parseable as Problem Details while preserving `ok:false`, `error`, `code`, `request_id`, `trace_id`, `retryable`, `doc_url`, and `secret_values_included` during migration. |
| Stripe API errors, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/errors` | Stripe error objects include programmatic codes, doc URL, message, param, request log URL, error type, and HTTP status classes including idempotency and rate-limit cases. | Kolm errors need stable code, field pointer/param, docs link, request/support log reference, retryability, status class, and user-safe message. |
| Stripe idempotency, `https://docs.stripe.com/api/idempotent_requests` | POST requests accept idempotency keys; keys should be high-entropy and avoid sensitive data. Reuse with mismatched parameters becomes an idempotency error. | Kolm mutation and job-creation routes need idempotency schema, key constraints, mismatch errors, replay behavior, and docs/examples. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/` | OTel semantic conventions define stable attributes across signals and domains. | API contract objects should bind `request_id`, `trace_id`, provider/model spans, queue spans, event delivery spans, and support bundle IDs. |
| AsyncAPI 3.1.0, `https://www.asyncapi.com/docs/reference/specification/v3.1.0` | AsyncAPI models channels, operations, messages, replies, bindings, security, traits, and reusable components for event-driven APIs. | Kolm webhooks/events should either be represented in OpenAPI `webhooks` or a companion AsyncAPI/event catalog with message schemas, signatures, retries, replay, and bindings. |
| WHATWG Server-Sent Events, `https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html` | SSE uses `text/event-stream`, event/data framing, reconnection behavior, status/content-type constraints, and line-by-line parsing. | Kolm SSE streams must document content type, event names, data schema, retry/resume behavior, done/error chunks, parser fixtures, and account/CLI handling. |
| OpenAI API reference, `https://developers.openai.com/api/reference/resources/chat` | OpenAI streaming includes `stream_options`, usage chunks before `data: [DONE]`, and stream-interruption caveats. | Kolm OpenAI-compatible routes need exact supported stream options, usage behavior, unsupported parameter errors, provider caveats, and compatibility tests. |

##### Contract Completeness Grade V2

Every operation, event, stream, SDK method, CLI JSON output, and account fetch should receive a grade in the generated contract matrix.

| Grade | Meaning | Release behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `A` | Full request, response, error, auth, idempotency/pagination, examples, SDK/account/CLI consumers, negative tests, and production smoke. | Eligible for public stable docs and SDK generation. |
| `B` | Request/response/error schemas and examples exist, but one consumer or production smoke is missing. | Can ship as implemented with visible evidence gap. |
| `C` | Operation is listed in OpenAPI/API routes but uses generic envelope or source-indexed description. | Must not be marketed as fully documented; implementation ticket required. |
| `D` | Route exists but lacks schema, examples, or consumer contract. | Internal/beta only; public docs must show caveat or hide it. |
| `F` | Route, docs, SDK, account UI, CLI/TUI, or OpenAPI disagree on shape/status/auth. | Release blocker for public/post-auth surfaces. |

Minimum release target: no public or account operation below `B`; no stable SDK method below `A`; no route group with unknown contract owner.

##### Contract Registry V2

Every contract should be represented by a registry row so generated artifacts and implementation agents can stop guessing.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-contract-registry-v2",
  "contract_id": "api.v1.product.graph.get",
  "kind": "route|webhook|sse|cli_json|tui_json|sdk_method|artifact_schema|job_schema|readiness_schema",
  "owner_node": "37",
  "surface": "public-docs-sdk",
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/v1/product/graph",
  "auth": "none|api_key|bearer|session|admin|service|webhook_signature",
  "stability": "stable|beta|experimental|internal|deprecated",
  "schema_version": "2026-05-25",
  "request_schema": "schemas/api/v1/product.graph.request.schema.json",
  "response_schema": "schemas/api/v1/product.graph.response.schema.json",
  "error_schema": "schemas/errors/problem-details-kolm-v1.schema.json",
  "examples": {
    "valid": [],
    "invalid": []
  },
  "semantics": {
    "pagination": null,
    "idempotency": null,
    "rate_limit": null,
    "cache": null,
    "streaming": null,
    "webhook_replay": null
  },
  "consumers": {
    "openapi": true,
    "sdk": true,
    "account": true,
    "cli": true,
    "tui": false,
    "docs": true,
    "support": true
  },
  "evidence": {
    "contract_tests": [],
    "negative_tests": [],
    "sdk_smoke": null,
    "account_screenshot": null,
    "production_smoke": null
  },
  "grade": "A",
  "gaps": []
}
```

##### Error Migration V2

Kolm should not abruptly break clients that already read `{ ok:false, error, detail }`. The finished path is a two-layer contract:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": "validation_failed",
  "detail": "One or more fields are invalid.",
  "type": "https://kolm.ai/problems/validation_failed",
  "title": "Request validation failed",
  "status": 422,
  "instance": "/v1/example/request/req_...",
  "code": "validation_failed",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "trace_id": "trace_...",
  "retryable": false,
  "doc_url": "https://kolm.ai/docs/api/errors#validation_failed",
  "errors": [
    {
      "pointer": "/model",
      "param": "model",
      "code": "unknown_model",
      "message": "Model is not in the tenant catalog."
    }
  ],
  "support": {
    "support_bundle_id": "sup_...",
    "request_log_url": null
  },
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "prompt_values_included": false,
  "raw_customer_data_included": false
}
```

Migration rules:

- keep legacy `ok`, `error`, and `detail` until a major API version removes them;
- add Problem Details fields for every new public/post-auth error;
- forbid stack traces and raw upstream messages in customer errors;
- require JSON Pointer or `param` for validation errors when a field is responsible;
- require `retryable`, `doc_url`, and safe next action for user-fixable errors;
- map provider, storage, worker, quota, billing, authorization, idempotency, and rate-limit failures to stable classes;
- route support bundles through redaction and tenant authorization before exposure.

##### Stream, Webhook, And Event Contracts V2

| Contract type | Required fields | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| SSE stream | stream ID, URL, auth, `text/event-stream`, event names, sequence, retry, resume policy, data schema, done chunk, error chunk, heartbeat, parser fixture | account parser test, CLI parser test, browser smoke, production stream smoke |
| OpenAI-compatible stream | supported endpoint, parameter subset, unsupported parameter errors, chunk shape, usage chunk behavior, `[DONE]` behavior, provider caveats | OpenAI-compatible fixture tests, stream interruption tests, docs caveat |
| Anthropic-compatible stream | supported endpoint, event names, content block deltas, stop events, tool events if supported, error mapping | Anthropic-compatible fixture tests and docs caveat |
| Webhook event | event type, version, subject, tenant, timestamp, data schema, signature header, retry policy, replay ID, idempotency key, dead-letter state | signature vectors, replay tests, delivery logs, docs examples |
| Internal event | source, event type, privacy class, schema version, trace ID, redaction state, retention, consumer list | event schema validation, OTel span linkage, privacy tests |
| AsyncAPI/OpenAPI catalog | channel/path, operation, message schema, security, bindings, examples, consumer docs | generated docs, schema diff, contract smoke |

##### Account, CLI, TUI, SDK, And Docs UX Requirements

- Account pages must display loading, empty, validation error, auth error, quota error, provider error, stale data, and retryable error states using the same API error codes as docs and CLI.
- Error messages must be near the failed control, not only at page top, and must include the exact next action when known.
- CLI JSON output must include `schema`, `ok`, stable fields, and no color/control characters; text output must preserve machine-actionable error codes.
- TUI views must expose the same status and error vocabulary as CLI JSON, including support bundle and request ID.
- SDK errors must expose typed properties: `code`, `status`, `retryable`, `request_id`, `trace_id`, `doc_url`, `param` or `pointer`, and `support_bundle_id`.
- Docs examples must be generated from fixtures or tested against local routes; hand-written examples need a source marker and stale-example gate.
- Webhook/SSE docs must include copy-paste parser fixtures, not only event names.
- OpenAI-compatible docs must say exactly which parameters are supported, ignored, rejected, or provider-dependent.
- Every account/API/CLI error state must preserve the product privacy rule: `secret_values_included: false` unless a narrowly authorized support flow proves otherwise.

##### Node `37` V2 Relax Redline

Node `37` can relax only when all of the following are true:

- every public and post-auth route, webhook, stream, CLI/TUI JSON output, SDK method, artifact schema, job schema, and readiness schema has a contract registry row;
- every stable public/account/API/SDK contract has request, response, error, examples, auth, rate-limit, idempotency or pagination semantics, and tests;
- OpenAPI either remains on 3.0.3 with an ADR and companion event catalog or migrates to 3.1.x with `jsonSchemaDialect` and webhook support;
- Problem Details-compatible error fields are present for all public/post-auth errors while legacy aliases remain during migration;
- raw `throw new Error` cannot cross a route boundary without typed mapping and redaction;
- `ok:false` legacy responses are either migrated, wrapped, or explicitly classified with compatibility sunset;
- account loading, empty, validation, auth, quota, provider, stale, retryable, and support states come from canonical error codes;
- CLI/TUI JSON, SDK errors, docs examples, OpenAPI, account fetchers, webhooks, SSE streams, and OpenAI-compatible routes parse the same schemas;
- webhook signatures, replay, retries, DLQ state, and parser fixtures are documented and tested;
- SSE streams define event names, chunk schemas, done/error chunks, retry/resume behavior, and parser fixtures;
- OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible endpoints list supported, ignored, rejected, and provider-dependent parameters with fixture evidence;
- production smoke proves the deployed contract, not just local route inventory.

### Authorization, Entitlement, Policy Decisioning, Tenant Boundaries, And Delegated Access Ledger V1

This node turns authorization from scattered route checks into a product control plane. Kolm cannot be complete if capture, compile, distill, datasets, artifacts, registry, marketplace, billing, admin, support, devices, cloud deployments, webhooks, jobs, and docs each decide access differently. The finished product needs one policy vocabulary and one evidence trail for who can do what, to which object, under which tenant, with which entitlement, from which actor, in which environment, and why.

This is a research/spec-only pass. It does not edit `src/auth.js`, route middleware, team logic, billing code, admin pages, tests, or generated docs.

#### Why Node 38 Exists

Authorization is the product boundary between "powerful AI platform" and "enterprise-risk demo." The user-facing promise is not just login. Buyers need to prove that:

- tenants cannot read or mutate each other's captures, datasets, artifacts, jobs, traces, bills, registry assets, or device installs;
- teams and roles behave predictably across API, account, CLI/TUI, SDKs, support, and admin operations;
- plan entitlements and quotas are enforced by backend policy, not just public pricing copy;
- service accounts, API keys, OAuth/OIDC identities, SSO users, SCIM groups, agents, webhooks, workers, and support actors have explicit permissions;
- every denial is explainable, every privileged allow is auditable, and every break-glass path expires and is reviewed.

#### Current Repo Evidence

| Area | Current evidence | 100 percent completion interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Authentication and account keys | `src/auth.js`, `src/oauth.js`, account key routes, `tests/auth.test.js`, `tests/auth-hash.test.js`, `tests/wave507-oauth-route-contract.test.js` | Core identity exists; final state needs a principal model that distinguishes tenant API keys, users, service accounts, OAuth clients, workers, and support/admin actors. |
| Tenant fences | Many modules and route docs force `tenant_id` from `req.tenant_record`, including `tests/wave411-tenant-isolation.test.js`, `wave420-pipeline-tenant-force.test.js`, `wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope.test.js`, and related tenant tests | Strong evidence exists, but completion requires generated authorization matrix coverage for every tenant-owned object and route, not only hand-written route notes. |
| Teams and roles | `src/team.js`, `src/teams.js`, `src/team-events.js`, `tests/wave379-team.test.js`, `tests/wave293-team-events-strict-schema.test.js`, `tests/wave513-teams-route-docs.test.js` | Team primitives exist; final state needs role hierarchy, permissions, team membership proofs, team-owned object access, SCIM group mapping, and account/admin UX parity. |
| Capture RBAC | `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, `/v1/capture/rbac/evaluate`, `/v1/capture/rbac/policy`, capture route docs | Capture governance exists as a dedicated surface; it must join the same policy engine used for datasets, artifacts, jobs, traces, and admin support. |
| Runtime/site policy | `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/site-license.js`, `tests/wc04-runtime-policy.test.js`, `tests/wave279-site-license.test.js` | Policy-like modules exist; node 38 requires clear separation between license policy, runtime safety policy, tenant access policy, and commercial entitlement policy. |
| Billing and entitlements | `src/billing-upgrade.js`, `src/billing-breakdown.js`, `/v1/plans`, `/v1/billing/tiers`, `/v1/billing/usage`, tests around billing and metering | Plan and usage surfaces exist; final state needs plan entitlements, feature gates, quotas, overage behavior, support tier, and downgrade/cancel effects to be enforced by policy. |
| Approvals and privileged actions | `src/distill-approval-queue.js`, `src/federated-approvals.js`, `public/account/approvals.html`, `tests/wave461-federated-approvals.test.js`, `tests/wave782-approval-queue.test.js` | Approval queues exist; policy needs to decide when approval is required, who can approve, whether separation of duties applies, and how denials are explained. |
| Route inventory | `public/docs/api-routes.json` contains many tenant-fence/auth notes, SSO/SCIM/status, billing, teams, approvals, and admin routes | Route comments are useful evidence, but a finished product needs machine-readable route policy metadata and generated allow/deny tests. |

#### External Baseline

| Reference | Baseline to absorb | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| OWASP API Security Top 10 2023, `https://owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x11-t10/` | Broken object-level, object-property-level, and function-level authorization are top API risks. | Kolm must generate BOLA/BOPLA/BFLA tests from the route/object/action policy matrix. |
| NIST RBAC project, `https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Role-Based-Access-Control` | RBAC models users, roles, permissions, operations, objects, role hierarchies, and constraints to reduce access-management complexity. | Kolm roles must be explicit objects, not prose labels: owner, admin, developer, auditor, billing admin, support, service account, publisher, reviewer, and worker roles need permissions. |
| NIST SP 800-162 ABAC, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/162/upd2/final` | ABAC evaluates subject, object, operation, and environment attributes against rules or relationships. | Kolm authorization must include tenant, team, role, plan, data class, region, environment, device, model/provider, risk, time, approval, and purpose attributes. |
| Google Zanzibar paper, `https://research.google/pubs/zanzibar-googles-consistent-global-authorization-system/` | Zanzibar uses a uniform data model/config language for ACLs and makes externally consistent authorization decisions at massive scale. | Kolm does not need Google's scale immediately, but team/artifact/registry/dataset/device relationships should be modeled as relations with consistency and freshness semantics. |
| OpenFGA concepts, `https://openfga.dev/docs/concepts` | Authorization model plus relationship tuples determine direct and implied relationships between users and objects. | Kolm can express owner/member/viewer/publisher/reviewer/installer relations for artifacts, datasets, namespaces, devices, teams, and registry assets. |
| Open Policy Agent, `https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs` | OPA decouples policy decision-making from enforcement and returns structured policy decisions over JSON input. | Kolm should separate policy decision from route code and emit structured decisions with reason, obligations, redactions, and audit metadata. |
| Cedar authorization language, `https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2023/05/cedar-open-source-language-access-control/` | Cedar supports fine-grained permissions, RBAC/ABAC models, local validation, and formally guided correctness claims. | Kolm policy syntax should be analyzable, testable, explainable, and locally validated before production deployment. |
| OAuth 2.0 RFC 6749, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749` | OAuth introduces an authorization layer, separates client from resource owner, and issues access tokens with scope, lifetime, and other attributes. | Kolm delegated access, API keys, service tokens, and OAuth clients need scoped, time-bound, revocable grants tied into the policy engine. |

#### Canonical Policy Decision Input

Every protected operation should be reducible to one policy decision input:

```json
{
  "decision_id": "pdp_...",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "action": "artifact.pull",
  "environment": {
    "deployment": "production",
    "region": "us",
    "ip_class": "customer | support | worker | internal",
    "time": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "risk": "normal | elevated | regulated"
  },
  "actor": {
    "principal_id": "usr_...",
    "principal_type": "user | tenant_api_key | service_account | oauth_client | worker | support | admin | webhook",
    "tenant_id": "tn_...",
    "team_ids": ["team_..."],
    "roles": ["owner"],
    "scopes": ["artifact:read"],
    "auth_method": "api_key | session | oauth | sso | service_token | break_glass",
    "impersonation": null
  },
  "resource": {
    "type": "artifact",
    "id": "art_...",
    "tenant_id": "tn_...",
    "team_id": "team_...",
    "namespace": "default",
    "visibility": "private | team | public | marketplace",
    "data_class": "public | internal | restricted | regulated",
    "owner_id": "usr_..."
  },
  "entitlements": {
    "plan": "pro | team | enterprise",
    "features": ["private_registry", "cloud_distill"],
    "quotas": {
      "monthly_calls_remaining": 1000,
      "concurrent_jobs_remaining": 2
    }
  },
  "context": {
    "approval_id": null,
    "job_id": null,
    "artifact_id": "art_...",
    "provider": null,
    "cost_estimate_usd": 0.0
  }
}
```

Canonical decision output:

```json
{
  "allow": false,
  "decision_id": "pdp_...",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "code": "entitlement_required",
  "reason": "Team private registry requires Team or Enterprise.",
  "policy_version": "policy.2026-05-25",
  "matched_rules": ["registry.private.pull.requires_entitlement"],
  "obligations": [
    "show_upgrade_path",
    "audit_denial"
  ],
  "redactions": [
    "hide_resource_owner_email"
  ],
  "next_actions": [
    {
      "kind": "upgrade",
      "href": "/pricing"
    }
  ],
  "audit": {
    "write": true,
    "privacy_class": "internal",
    "secret_values_included": false
  }
}
```

#### Policy Domains

| Domain | Required decisions |
|---|---|
| Account and tenant | create/delete account, rotate keys, update settings, export/delete data, access billing, configure SSO/SCIM, invite users, manage teams. |
| Capture and data lake | capture traffic, view raw/redacted payloads, create datasets, contribute to lake, opt out, purge, label, export, promote to distill. |
| Compile/distill/train/jobs | submit job, view job, cancel/retry/resume job, approve distill, spend budget, use teacher/provider, use GPU/cloud worker, publish outputs. |
| Artifacts and registry | create, read, publish, pull, install, verify, revoke, transfer, marketplace list, review, moderate, sell, refund, takedown. |
| Models/providers/runtime/devices | configure provider keys, route to provider, access model family, run on device, install runtime, use local/cloud/BYOC/edge. |
| Billing and commercial | select plan, change plan, use entitlement, exceed quota, invoice access, support tier, procurement, enterprise-only feature. |
| Admin and support | cross-tenant view, support bundle, impersonation, break-glass, moderation, tenant provision, incident action, audit export. |
| Public/docs/community | publish public profile/listing, docs examples with keys, community submissions, standards governance, feedback/research grants. |

#### Redlines

- No route may trust `tenant_id`, `team_id`, `user_id`, plan, role, scope, or entitlement from the request body when the authenticated context can derive it.
- No object read/write may depend only on route-level auth; object-level authorization must run at the point of object access.
- No response may expose properties the actor cannot see; property-level authorization and redaction are required for mixed-sensitivity objects.
- No admin/support route may bypass the same policy decision engine used by customer-facing routes.
- No plan feature may be shown as available unless the backend entitlement decision agrees.
- No marketplace purchase, artifact install, registry pull, cloud job, model provider call, or device deploy may skip entitlement and quota checks.
- No delegated token, service account, or OAuth client may have unlimited duration, unlimited scope, or unclear revocation.
- No impersonation or break-glass access may occur without reason, approval or emergency class, expiration, audit event, and review.
- No authorization denial may be vague; it needs a stable code, safe reason, and user/operator next action.
- No policy change may ship without policy diff, generated allow/deny tests, migration notes, and production denial smoke.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `38.1` Principal, actor, tenant, team, service account, token, role, relation, scope, and session model | A canonical identity graph distinguishes users, tenants, teams, API keys, sessions, SSO users, SCIM groups, service accounts, OAuth clients, workers, webhooks, support, and admins; every request gets one normalized actor context. |
| `38.2` Policy decision point, enforcement points, RBAC, ABAC, ReBAC, entitlements, quotas, and feature gates | A policy decision function receives actor/resource/action/context/entitlement attributes and returns allow/deny plus reasons, obligations, redactions, audit metadata, and next actions; all route groups call it or document safe public exceptions. |
| `38.3` Object-level, property-level, function-level, job-level, artifact-level, marketplace, device, and admin authorization | Every protected object and route has generated allow/deny tests for owner/member/admin/auditor/service/support/cross-tenant actors, including property-level redaction and function-level admin separation. |
| `38.4` Delegated access, OAuth/OIDC, SSO, SCIM, service tokens, API keys, impersonation, break-glass, and approvals | Delegated access has scoped, expiring, revocable grants; SSO/SCIM maps groups to roles; service tokens are least-privilege; impersonation and break-glass require reason, expiration, audit, and review. |
| `38.5` Policy UX across account, admin, CLI/TUI, docs, errors, audit logs, support bundles, and customer evidence | Users and operators can see why a feature/action is allowed or blocked, what upgrade/approval/config is needed, and what audit evidence exists without exposing sensitive data. |
| `38.6` Evidence: authorization matrix, generated allow/deny tests, cross-tenant probes, entitlement smoke, policy diff, and production denial proof | Release gates generate tests from the policy matrix, run cross-tenant probes, prove entitlement gates, verify admin/support denials, diff policy changes, and run production smoke for allowed and denied actions. |

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |

| `W-POLICY-001` | `38.1` | Define principal and actor schema. | Every auth path maps to one actor context with principal type, tenant, teams, roles, scopes, session, and auth method. |
