| `W-POLICY-010` | `38.3` | Generate property-level authorization tests. | Mixed-sensitivity responses redact fields according to role, data class, and support/admin context. |
| `W-POLICY-011` | `38.3` | Generate function-level authorization tests. | Admin, billing, support, registry moderation, approval, key rotation, and destructive actions deny insufficient roles. |
| `W-POLICY-012` | `38.4` | Normalize API keys and service tokens. | Keys have owner, scopes, expiry, rotation, revocation, last-used, and least-privilege guidance. |
| `W-POLICY-013` | `38.4` | Complete SSO/SCIM role mapping contract. | SSO groups and SCIM memberships map deterministically to team roles and policy attributes. |
| `W-POLICY-014` | `38.4` | Add OAuth delegated access contract. | OAuth clients have scopes, redirect policy, token lifetime, revocation, consent, and audit. |
| `W-POLICY-015` | `38.4` | Add impersonation and break-glass workflow. | Privileged access requires reason, approval/emergency class, expiry, audit event, review, and customer impact record. |
| `W-POLICY-016` | `38.5` | Build account entitlement and access explainer. | Users see allowed, blocked, needs-upgrade, needs-approval, needs-config, and contact-sales states for product matrix features. |
| `W-POLICY-017` | `38.5` | Add CLI/TUI policy explain output. | Machine-readable commands expose entitlement/policy reasons without leaking sensitive data. |
| `W-POLICY-018` | `38.6` | Add policy diff gate. | Policy changes show affected actions/resources/roles/entitlements and require generated tests. |
| `W-POLICY-019` | `38.6` | Add production denial smoke. | Production smoke proves representative allowed and denied actions for account, artifact, billing, job, admin, and marketplace routes. |
| `W-POLICY-020` | `38.6` | Add customer evidence export. | Enterprise admins can export a dated access-control evidence packet with roles, policies, entitlements, audit samples, and test proof. |

#### Node 38 Relax Redline

Node `38` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Who is the actor for every request, and what tenant/team/session/service/delegation context do they carry?
- What action is being attempted, on which resource, with which object owner, team, data class, and environment attributes?
- Which policy rule allowed or denied the action, and what obligations or redactions were applied?
- Which entitlement, quota, support tier, approval, region, provider, device, or plan condition affected the decision?
- Can every protected route prove owner/member/admin/auditor/service/support/cross-tenant allow/deny behavior?
- Can account UI, CLI/TUI, API errors, docs, support bundles, and audit logs explain the same denial?
- Are SSO/SCIM/OAuth/API-key/service-token identities mapped to the same policy model?
- Are impersonation and break-glass paths expiring, audited, reviewed, and visible to the right operators?
- Does production smoke prove both allowed and denied paths after deploy?

The relax condition for node `38` is not "auth middleware exists." It is: every protected Kolm action is governed by one explainable, test-generated, entitlement-aware policy decision system that proves tenant isolation, least privilege, delegated access, admin safety, and customer-visible access evidence across API, account, CLI/TUI, SDKs, workers, webhooks, jobs, artifacts, marketplace, devices, billing, and support.

#### Node 38 Authorization Policy Spine Addendum V2

This addendum keeps authorization completion attached to master spec tree node `38`. It updates the V1 policy ledger with current code evidence and current authorization baselines. It is a documentation/spec-only pass; no auth middleware, route code, team logic, account UI, tests, or generated artifacts are modified in this lane.

Current repo evidence checked in this pass:

| evidence | current state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/auth.js` | tenant API keys are hashed at rest, legacy raw keys migrate, query-param API keys are removed, admin key fallback is disabled by default, and `requirePlan` gates plans with 401/402 JSON responses | strong core auth exists, but `requirePlan` is a string/list entitlement gate rather than a canonical policy decision with actor, resource, action, reason, obligations, and audit metadata |
| `src/auth.js` key model | file header still says one key per tenant; account key UX elsewhere implies richer key management | final policy must distinguish primary tenant key, scoped service token, OAuth session, SSO user, SCIM user, webhook, worker, support actor, and admin actor |
| `src/teams.js` | teams have roles `viewer`, `member`, `admin`, `owner`, invites, seat limits, ownership transfer, removal, team-scoped readable/writable helpers, and membership checks | role primitives exist, but team roles are local helpers rather than a shared policy schema used by every product surface |
| `src/team-capture-rbac.js` | capture has a clear local RBAC policy with role rank, action rules, accepted scopes, namespace checks, tenant fencing, and safe envelope output | this is the best seed for a general policy decision spine, but it currently covers capture/lake actions rather than all actions |
| `src/router.js` | routes mix `requirePlan`, `teams.requireRole`, manual tenant comparisons, admin-only checks, SSO/SCIM entitlement helpers, enterprise-only gates, local-daemon sentinel tenants, and public exceptions | protected routes need machine-readable policy metadata and generated allow/deny tests instead of repeated route-local logic |
| tenant isolation tests | tests include `wave411-tenant-isolation`, `wave420-pipeline-tenant-force`, `wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope`, `wave426-import-seeds-tenant`, `wave537-eval-tenant-holdout-route-docs`, and more | isolation coverage is meaningful, but must become generated policy-matrix coverage for every route/object family |
| auth tests | `auth.test.js`, `auth-hash.test.js`, OAuth route tests, and production admin fallback tests prove important authentication invariants | authentication proof is stronger than authorization proof; object/function/property-level authorization needs comparable generated evidence |
| policy modules | `src/runtime-policy.js`, `src/distill-onpolicy.js`, `src/team-capture-rbac.js`, and artifact signature policy tests each own domain-specific policy behavior | policy concepts are scattered by domain; final state needs a registry that preserves domain rules while making decisions explainable in one format |
| account/admin surfaces | account pages expose keys, security, SSO, SCIM, teams, audit, approvals, billing, and product readiness | account UX needs a policy explainer that shows allowed, denied, needs upgrade, needs approval, needs config, local-only, and support/admin states in the same language |

Current external baselines bound to this addendum:

| source | current baseline | Kolm implementation requirement |
|---|---|---|
| OWASP API Security Top 10 2023, `https://owasp.org/API-Security/editions/2023/en/0x11-t10/` | broken object-level, object-property-level, and function-level authorization are top API risks | generate BOLA, BOPLA, and BFLA tests from route/action/resource policy metadata |
| NIST SP 800-162, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/162/upd2/final` | ABAC evaluates subject, object, requested operation, and environment attributes against policy/rules/relationships | policy inputs must include actor, resource, action, environment, entitlement, approval, risk, region, and data class attributes |
| NIST SP 800-207A, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/207/a/final` | zero trust removes implicit trust from network location and shifts to application/service identity policies | local daemon, worker, webhook, service, device, BYOC, cloud, and admin paths must be identity-bound, not trusted by topology alone |
| OpenFGA concepts, `https://openfga.dev/docs/concepts` | authorization models plus relationship tuples determine direct and implied relationships between users and objects | Kolm should model tenant, team, namespace, artifact, dataset, device, registry, job, and publisher relations explicitly |
| OPA docs, `https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs` | policy decisions can be decoupled from service code and evaluated over JSON input | route code should call a policy decision contract instead of embedding every authorization rule locally |
| Cedar announcement/spec resources, `https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2023/05/cedar-open-source-language-access-control/` | Cedar supports fine-grained RBAC/ABAC and local policy validation | Kolm policy should be analyzable, testable locally, diffable, and safe to validate before deploy whether or not Cedar is adopted |
| OAuth 2.0 Security BCP RFC 9700, `https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9700` | modern OAuth security emphasizes threat mitigations, reduced scope, and sender-constrained tokens where appropriate | OAuth clients, service tokens, delegated agents, and future app integrations need scoped, short-lived, revocable, sender-bound options for high-risk actions |

Policy spine V2 target:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm-policy-spine-2",
  "decision": {
    "id": "pdp_...",
    "version": "policy.2026-05-25",
    "action": "artifact.pull",
    "allow": false,
    "code": "team_membership_required",
    "safe_reason": "This artifact belongs to a team you are not a member of.",
    "matched_rules": ["artifact.private.requires_owner_or_team_member"],
    "obligations": ["audit_denial", "redact_owner_email"],
    "next_actions": [{"kind": "request_access", "href": "/account/teams"}],
    "secret_values_included": false
  },
  "actor": {
    "principal_type": "tenant_api_key",
    "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
    "team_ids": ["team_..."],
    "roles": ["member"],
    "scopes": ["artifact:read"],
    "auth_method": "api_key",
    "delegated_by": null
  },
  "resource": {
    "type": "artifact",
    "id": "art_...",
    "tenant_id": "tenant_...",
    "team_id": "team_...",
    "namespace": "default",
    "visibility": "private",
    "data_class": "restricted"
  },
  "entitlement": {
    "plan": "team",
    "feature_keys": ["private_registry"],
    "quota_state": "within_limit",
    "billing_status": "active"
  },
  "environment": {
    "runtime": "production",
    "region": "us",
    "origin": "api",
    "risk": "normal",
    "local_only": false
  }
}
```

Policy migration sequence:

1. Inventory every current enforcement mechanism: `authMiddleware`, `requirePlan`, `teams.requireRole`, `teamScopedReadable`, `teamScopedWritable`, `authorizeCaptureAction`, SSO/SCIM gates, admin checks, local daemon sentinel, runtime policy, artifact signature policy, approval queues, billing gates, and marketplace publisher policy.
2. Define the shared actor/resource/action/environment/entitlement schema without deleting domain-specific policy logic.
3. Wrap existing gates so they emit a policy decision envelope and audit event even before rules are centralized.
4. Add route metadata for action, resource type, public exception state, required relationship, required role/scope, entitlement, quota, and denial smoke.
5. Generate allow/deny tests from route metadata for owner, same-team member, wrong-team member, cross-tenant actor, insufficient role, insufficient scope, expired token, pending billing, admin, and support.
6. Move route-local gates into a policy registry only after generated tests prove behavior has not loosened.
7. Add account/CLI/TUI policy explainers so product users see what is blocked, why, and how to resolve it.
8. Add production denial smoke for high-risk actions before any "state of the art" or enterprise-ready claim.

Policy UX contract:

1. Every blocked account action uses a stable denial code and one plain-language reason.
2. Denials distinguish `sign_in_required`, `role_required`, `team_membership_required`, `scope_required`, `plan_required`, `payment_pending`, `quota_exceeded`, `approval_required`, `configuration_required`, `region_blocked`, `local_only`, `support_only`, and `admin_only`.
3. A user can see the exact unlock path: request access, ask team admin, change plan, complete checkout, configure SSO/SCIM, add provider credentials, request approval, export evidence, or contact sales/support.
4. Account screens never rely on hover-only explanations; every blocked button has inline text, accessible labels, and keyboard-visible focus.
5. CLI/TUI errors preserve the same denial code and next action as account/API errors.
6. Admin/support views show policy decision IDs, actor, resource, matched rules, redactions, and audit status without secrets or raw customer payloads.
7. Enterprise evidence exports include the policy version, role matrix, SSO/SCIM mapping, entitlement map, sample denials, and production smoke date.

Node `38` V2 work tickets:

- W-POLICY-V2-001: build a policy inventory that lists every current auth, role, entitlement, tenant-fence, admin, SSO/SCIM, approval, billing, marketplace, runtime, worker, and local-daemon enforcement point.
- W-POLICY-V2-002: define a canonical actor schema covering tenant API key, primary account user, OAuth session, SSO user, SCIM user, service token, webhook, worker, local daemon, support actor, admin, and break-glass actor.
- W-POLICY-V2-003: define a canonical resource schema for captures, datasets, labels, artifacts, jobs, traces, model/provider configs, devices, registry listings, invoices, teams, namespaces, approvals, and admin objects.
- W-POLICY-V2-004: define a canonical action namespace that maps API routes, account controls, CLI/TUI commands, workers, webhooks, and support/admin actions to the same verbs.
- W-POLICY-V2-005: wrap `requirePlan` so plan gates emit canonical policy decisions with entitlement keys, billing state, pending plan, next action, and audit metadata.
- W-POLICY-V2-006: wrap `teams.requireRole`, `teamScopedReadable`, and `teamScopedWritable` so team decisions emit canonical relation/role decisions.
- W-POLICY-V2-007: generalize the capture RBAC pattern into a reusable policy decision helper while preserving the capture-specific scope and namespace logic.
- W-POLICY-V2-008: add route policy metadata for every protected route group in `public/docs/api-routes.json` and generated OpenAPI extensions.
- W-POLICY-V2-009: generate BOLA tests for every route that accepts object IDs, artifact IDs, dataset IDs, job IDs, team IDs, namespace IDs, device IDs, lead IDs, invoice IDs, or registry IDs.
- W-POLICY-V2-010: generate object-property authorization tests for mixed-sensitivity responses such as billing, audit logs, support bundles, traces, provider configs, enterprise identity, and admin tenant lists.
- W-POLICY-V2-011: generate function-level authorization tests for admin routes, support routes, key rotation, SSO/SCIM configuration, team ownership transfer, billing changes, marketplace moderation, approval decisions, and destructive actions.
- W-POLICY-V2-012: add scoped service-token support or explicitly keep "one primary key per tenant" in account UX until real scoped keys are implemented.
- W-POLICY-V2-013: define OAuth/OIDC delegated access rules with scopes, consent, token lifetime, refresh policy, redirect policy, sender-constrained option, and revocation.
- W-POLICY-V2-014: define SSO/SCIM group-to-team-role mapping, deprovision behavior, conflict behavior, and evidence export.
- W-POLICY-V2-015: define support impersonation and break-glass with reason, approval/emergency class, expiration, customer notification policy, audit trail, review, and production smoke.
- W-POLICY-V2-016: add account policy explainers for every product matrix feature so post-auth users see allowed, blocked, needs-upgrade, needs-approval, needs-config, and local-only states.
- W-POLICY-V2-017: add CLI/TUI `policy explain` parity for the same decisions and next actions.
- W-POLICY-V2-018: add policy diff reporting for changed roles, actions, entitlements, public exceptions, admin permissions, and denial behavior.
- W-POLICY-V2-019: add production denial smoke for representative account, API, CLI, billing, artifact, job, team, SSO/SCIM, marketplace, and admin/support actions.
- W-POLICY-V2-020: create an enterprise access-control evidence packet with policy version, actor model, role matrix, entitlement map, SSO/SCIM mapping, generated tests, audit samples, and production smoke proof.

Node `38` V2 relax redline:

Authorization completion can relax only when all of the following are true:

1. every protected action reduces to one actor/resource/action/environment/entitlement policy decision;
2. route code no longer embeds untracked one-off authorization logic without policy metadata and tests;
3. tenant, team, namespace, artifact, dataset, device, job, billing, marketplace, and admin objects have generated object-level allow/deny tests;
4. mixed-sensitivity responses prove property-level redaction by role, support/admin state, and data class;
5. API keys, service tokens, OAuth sessions, SSO users, SCIM users, workers, webhooks, local daemon, support, admin, and break-glass actors are distinct in policy and audit;
6. plan entitlements and quotas are enforced by the same policy spine that explains account feature gates;
7. every denial has a stable code, safe reason, next action, audit decision ID, and account/CLI/API parity;
8. policy diffs and production denial smokes run before release.

### Observability, Diagnostics, Telemetry, Incident Intelligence, And Support Evidence Ledger V1

Node `39` makes operational truth a product substrate. Node `19` already covers observability as a product area. Node `39` is stricter: every route, job, provider call, model run, artifact transition, policy decision, billing event, device action, worker step, account screen, CLI/TUI command, status update, incident, and support bundle must be explainable from the same evidence graph.

State of the art for Kolm is not "we have logs" or "Prometheus scrapes `/metrics`." It is that a customer, support engineer, operator, auditor, and implementation agent can answer what happened, why it happened, which tenant/artifact/model/provider/job/policy was involved, what user-visible impact existed, what evidence is safe to share, and what exact next action follows.

#### Repo Evidence Already Present

| Evidence family | Current repo evidence | What node 39 demands next |
|---|---|---|
| Runtime telemetry modules | `src/otel.js`, `src/otel-attrs.js`, `src/prometheus-exporter.js`, `src/log.js`, `src/trace-capture.js`, `src/trace-compile.js`, `src/trace-translator.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/continuous-monitoring.js`, `src/sla-rollup.js`, `src/diagnostic.js`, `src/drift-alert.js`, `src/drift-alert-store.js`, `src/drift-alert-w813.js`, `src/ab-metrics.js` | Consolidate schemas into one signal envelope, one privacy classifier, one correlation model, one export compatibility matrix, and one release gate. |
| Public/account surfaces | `public/status.html`, `public/status.rss`, `public/sla.html`, `public/account/agent-telemetry.html`, `public/account/audit-log.html`, `public/account/continuous-monitoring.html`, `public/account/diagnose.html`, `public/account/drift-alert.html`, `public/account/sla.html` | Every screen must show source freshness, missing telemetry, customer impact, linked runbook/action, and safe evidence export instead of generic health language. |
| Docs and operator surfaces | `public/docs/observability.html`, `public/docs/observability/opentelemetry.html`, `public/docs/observability/prometheus.html`, `public/docs/cli/doctor.html`, `public/docs/cli/logs.html`, `public/docs/cli/status.html`, `public/docs/cli/trace.html`, `tools/alerts/datadog-kolm.yaml`, `tools/alerts/grafana-kolm.yaml`, `tools/alerts/honeycomb-kolm.yaml` | Docs, CLI, alerts, and dashboards must be generated from the same telemetry catalog, not separately maintained examples. |
| API route evidence | `/metrics`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/chain`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/export`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/stats`, `/v1/trace/append`, `/v1/trace/compile`, `/v1/trace/translate`, `/v1/trace/verify`, `/v1/sla/dashboard`, `/v1/sla/rollup`, `/v1/sla/series`, `/v1/drift/*`, `/v1/admin/diagnostics`, product doctor routes, status routes | Every route group must declare emitted signals, correlation IDs, privacy class, SLO ownership, alert mapping, and support-bundle inclusion/exclusion rules. |
| Verification evidence | `tests/wave-wc06-log.test.js`, `tests/wave215-threshold-alerts.test.js`, `tests/wave298-doctor-loop.test.js`, `tests/wave304-kolm-status.test.js`, `tests/wave306-kolm-metrics.test.js`, `tests/wave307-kolm-support-bundle.test.js`, `tests/wave383-agent-telemetry.test.js`, `tests/wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope.test.js`, `tests/wave425-trace-ownership.test.js`, `tests/wave455-distill-runs-telemetry.test.js`, `tests/wave457-telemetry-reconciliation.test.js`, `tests/wave463-trace-compile.test.js`, `tests/wave517-trace-route-docs.test.js`, `tests/wave730-prometheus.test.js`, `tests/wave733-otel.test.js`, `tests/wave741-diagnose.test.js`, `tests/wave747-drift-alert.test.js`, `tests/wave788-sla.test.js`, `tests/wave823-otel-upgrade.test.js`, `tests/wc04-trace-translator.test.js` | Tests must move from isolated feature checks to generated product-wide coverage: every route/job/provider/artifact/policy/billing surface gets signal, privacy, correlation, SLO, and support evidence assertions. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Standard or practice | Source | Implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| OpenTelemetry signals | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/signals/` | Treat traces, metrics, logs, baggage/context, and profiles as related but distinct signals, with explicit correlation and export behavior. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | Provider/model/token/operation telemetry must map to GenAI conventions before adding Kolm-specific attributes. |
| W3C Trace Context | `https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/` | Propagate `traceparent` and `tracestate` across account, API, SDK, gateway, worker, provider, artifact, and webhook boundaries. |
| Prometheus/OpenMetrics | `https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/` and `https://prometheus.io/docs/instrumenting/exposition_formats/` | `/metrics` needs stable names, HELP/TYPE metadata, label-cardinality budgets, auth behavior, and compatibility tests. |
| Google SRE SLO practice | `https://sre.google/sre-book/service-level-objectives/` and `https://sre.google/sre-book/monitoring-distributed-systems/` | SLIs must start from user-visible outcomes, use percentile/distribution thinking, define SLOs and error budgets, and alert only on actionable symptoms. |
| DORA delivery metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Deployment and incident telemetry must connect product reliability to change failure rate, recovery time, lead time, and deployment frequency. |

#### Canonical Signal Envelope

Every emitted Kolm signal must be representable as this logical envelope even when exported to OTLP, Prometheus, JSON logs, support bundles, status pages, or customer evidence packets:

```json
{
  "signal_id": "sig_...",
  "signal_type": "trace|span|metric|log|event|profile|support_bundle|incident",
  "schema": "kolm.signal.v1",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-25T00:00:00.000Z",
  "service": "api|gateway|worker|account|cli|tui|sdk|runtime|registry|billing|support",
  "environment": "local|preview|staging|production|byoc|airgap",
  "request_id": "req_...",
  "trace_id": "w3c-trace-id",
  "span_id": "span-id",
  "parent_span_id": "span-id-or-null",
  "tenant_id": "ten_... or null",
  "tenant_hash": "stable-safe-hash",
  "actor_class": "anonymous|user|service|worker|support|admin|system",
  "surface": "public|account|api|cli|tui|sdk|worker|webhook|admin|support|status",
  "product_journey": "gateway-capture|compile-distill|runtime-device|registry-marketplace|governance|billing|support",
  "route_group": "trace|sla|drift|artifact|billing|provider|job|policy",
  "operation": "stable.operation.name",
  "outcome": "ok|error|denied|degraded|queued|cancelled|timeout|unknown",
  "error_code": "stable_code_or_null",
  "status_code": 200,
  "provider": "openai|anthropic|gemini|local|byoc|null",
  "model": "model-id-or-null",
  "artifact_id": "art_... or null",
  "artifact_hash": "sha256-or-null",
  "job_id": "job_... or null",
  "policy_decision_id": "pol_... or null",
  "billing_meter_id": "meter_... or null",
  "device_id": "dev_... or null",
  "runtime": "node|browser|wasm|gguf|onnx|ios|android|edge|null",
  "cloud_provider": "aws|gcp|azure|cloudflare|fly|railway|vercel|local|null",
  "privacy_class": "public|internal|tenant_metadata|pseudonymous|sensitive|regulated",
  "raw_prompt_included": false,
  "raw_output_included": false,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "sampling": {
    "decision": "record|sample|drop|aggregate",
    "rate": 1,
    "reason": "slo|audit|debug|budget|privacy"
  },
  "retention_days": 30,
  "links": {
    "trace": "/v1/trace/...",
    "job": "/account/jobs/...",
    "artifact": "/account/artifacts/...",
    "incident": "/status/...",
    "support_bundle": "/account/support/..."
  },
  "evidence_paths": [
    "src/otel.js",
    "src/prometheus-exporter.js",
    "tests/wave733-otel.test.js"
  ]
}
```

The envelope is not a storage mandate. It is the minimum information contract that every output format must preserve or explicitly mark unavailable.

#### Signal Taxonomy

| Signal family | Required correlation | Required measures | Privacy default | Customer-visible proof |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| API request and response | request, trace, actor, tenant, route group, policy decision, deployment version | latency distribution, status, stable error code, retry/idempotency result, payload size class | tenant metadata only | API error body, trace export, support bundle, docs route owner |
| Provider/model call | trace, provider, model, route decision, spend meter, fallback path, artifact/job | latency, token counts, cost estimate, retry count, timeout, model availability, output contract outcome | no prompt/output by default | account provider timeline, cost attribution, routing explanation |
| Capture and redaction | trace, capture namespace, redaction policy, privacy class, dataset/artifact lineage | capture count, redaction class count, drop/quarantine reason, poisoning score, dedupe result | sensitive/regulated until redacted | privacy event, redaction proof, dataset provenance |
| Compile/distill/train job | job, worker, provider/teacher, artifact, dataset, policy, spend budget | queue time, run time, progress, stage outcome, eval score, K-Score, cost, failure mode | tenant metadata plus safe eval aggregates | account job timeline, CLI status, artifact manifest, diagnostic report |
| Artifact registry operation | artifact, version, publisher, signature, install, marketplace listing, policy, billing | publish/install/pull latency, verification result, trust state, revocation/takedown event | public or tenant metadata by registry state | registry proof, signature verification, marketplace audit |
| Policy and entitlement decision | actor, tenant, resource, action, plan, quota, feature gate, approval | allow/deny count, denial reason, quota remaining, upgrade/contact-sales state | tenant metadata | account entitlement explainer, API Problem Details, audit log |
| Billing and metering | account, plan, usage meter, provider/model/job/artifact, invoice | usage units, spend, savings estimate, overage state, reconciliation drift | sensitive financial metadata | billing dashboard, invoice evidence, cost export |
| Device/runtime/BYOC | device, runtime, artifact, deployment, region, worker, tenant | deploy health, inference latency, offline sync, resource use, battery/memory class, rollback result | tenant metadata, device pseudonym | fleet view, deployment trace, device doctor |
| Webhook, notification, and email | event, delivery endpoint, retry, tenant, recipient class, template version | delivery latency, attempts, status, bounce/failure reason, replay state | sensitive recipient metadata | delivery log, replay action, webhook signature proof |
| Frontend/account RUM | trace/session, route, account surface, UI state, API dependencies | load time, interaction latency, error boundary, stale data, empty/error state frequency | pseudonymous; no form payloads by default | account diagnostics, support evidence, UX regression report |
| Status, SLA, and incident | service, dependency, SLI, SLO, error budget, incident, region | availability, latency percentiles, throughput, correctness, degradation, MTTA/MTTR | public aggregate unless tenant-specific | status page, RSS, SLA dashboard, postmortem |
| Support bundle and doctor | tenant, environment, product surface, command, evidence paths, redaction policy | completeness, secret scan result, stale config, missing env, failing probes, next action | safe metadata only | downloadable support bundle, CLI doctor JSON, admin diagnostics |

#### Observability UX And Operability Requirements

| Surface | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Account overview | Shows product health by journey with freshness, current incidents, degraded dependencies, open support actions, and the exact data source behind every status. |
| Account diagnostics | Accepts a trace/job/artifact/provider/device/error code and returns timeline, probable cause, safe evidence, related alerts, and next action. |
| Status page | Separates public platform health from tenant-specific degradation, shows affected surfaces, regions, dependencies, started/updated/resolved times, and RSS/API parity. |
| SLA page | Shows SLI definitions, measurement windows, exclusions, error budget, current burn, historical trend, and whether the displayed value is contractual or informational. |
| CLI/TUI | `kolm doctor`, `kolm trace`, `kolm status`, and support-bundle commands output machine-readable evidence, no secrets, no raw prompts, and stable next-action codes. |
| API errors | Every operational error has RFC 9457-style shape, stable code, request/trace ID, retryability, safe detail, next action, and support-bundle pointer. |
| Admin/support | Support can diagnose without cross-tenant leaks, raw payload access, hidden impersonation, or unreviewed break-glass. |
| Customer evidence | Enterprise users can export dated observability evidence for routes/jobs/artifacts/policies/SLOs/incidents without revealing other tenants or secrets. |
| Implementation agents | Every feature ticket must state emitted signals, SLO impact, dashboard/alert/runbook owner, support-bundle behavior, and tests. |

#### Redlines

- No raw prompts, raw outputs, API keys, provider secrets, customer secrets, PHI/PII, or unredacted regulated data may enter telemetry by default.
- No route, worker, webhook, SDK, CLI/TUI command, account page, provider adapter, or background job may ship without request/job/artifact/provider/policy correlation.
- No alert may exist without an emitted metric, SLI or symptom rationale, owner, severity, runbook, threshold, test fixture, noise budget, and customer-impact mapping.
- No dashboard may display green health when the underlying data is stale, missing, sampled out, unauthenticated, or from the wrong environment.
- No incident may close without timeline, affected surfaces, customer impact, root/proximate cause, remediation, follow-up owner, and customer communication state.
- No support bundle may include secret values, raw prompts, raw outputs, cross-tenant data, or fields outside its declared privacy class.
- No production deploy may be considered complete without trace, log, metric, status, SLA, and support-bundle proof for the changed surfaces.
- No custom `kolm.*` attribute may duplicate a standard OpenTelemetry attribute unless it documents the mapping, schema version, and migration path.
- No `/metrics` label may introduce unbounded cardinality such as tenant ID, user ID, raw route params, artifact IDs, request IDs, prompts, provider keys, or free-form errors.
- No sampling policy may make enterprise audit trails, paid SLA evidence, incident reconstruction, security investigations, or regulatory exports impossible.
- No status page may be manually green while SLOs, dependency checks, or incident evidence say the service is degraded.
- No customer-visible "resolved" claim may ship until synthetic and production probes prove recovery in the affected region/surface.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `39.1` Signal taxonomy, trace/log/metric/event/profile schema, privacy classes, and source ownership | A generated telemetry catalog lists every signal family, schema, owner, source module, privacy class, retention, export mapping, tests, and support-bundle behavior. |
| `39.2` Request, job, provider, model, artifact, policy, billing, device, worker, and customer journey correlation | W3C trace context plus Kolm IDs join API, gateway, SDK, worker, provider, artifact, job, policy, billing, device, account, CLI/TUI, webhook, and support evidence without leaking secrets. |
| `39.3` SLO/SLI/error-budget, alert, runbook, incident, status, escalation, and postmortem evidence loops | Every product journey has SLIs, SLOs, burn alerts, dashboards, runbooks, incident lifecycle, status-page mapping, escalation owner, postmortem template, and drill evidence. |
| `39.4` Diagnostics, doctor commands, support bundles, safe logs, replay links, traces, and customer-visible explanations | Customers and operators can diagnose by trace/job/artifact/error/provider/device ID and receive a safe timeline, root-cause candidates, missing config, next actions, and evidence export. |
| `39.5` Telemetry export, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus/OpenMetrics, vendor dashboards, retention, sampling, and cost controls | OTLP, Prometheus/OpenMetrics, JSON logs, status RSS/API, and vendor dashboard packs are generated from one catalog with cardinality, retention, sampling, privacy, and cost budgets. |
| `39.6` Evidence: telemetry schema diff, redaction tests, alert drills, incident replay, support-bundle smoke, and production observability proof | Release gates fail unless telemetry schemas are current, privacy/redaction tests pass, alert/runbook drills exist, incident replay works, support bundles are safe, and production smoke proves changed surfaces. |

#### Node 39 Relax Redline

Node `39` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- What happened for a request/job/artifact/provider call/device deployment, and which trace, span, metric, log, event, incident, and support bundle prove it?
- Which customer, tenant, product journey, route group, policy decision, artifact, model, provider, worker, device, cloud, and billing meter were involved without leaking sensitive data?
- What was the user-visible impact, which SLI/SLO/error budget did it affect, and which status/SLA surface reflects it?
- Was the data fresh, complete, sampled, redacted, retained, and exported according to policy?
- What alert fired or should have fired, who owned it, what runbook applied, and what noise/error-budget policy justified it?
- Can support diagnose the issue without raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, cross-tenant data, or hidden impersonation?
- Can the same evidence be read through account, CLI/TUI, API, status page, docs, support/admin, and exported customer evidence?
- Can production smoke prove the changed surface emits correct telemetry after deploy?

The relax condition for node `39` is not "metrics exist." It is: every Kolm product surface emits safe, correlated, standard-compatible, customer-explainable operational evidence with SLOs, alerts, incidents, support bundles, status/SLA proof, telemetry export, privacy controls, and production smoke tied to the same master product graph.

#### Node 39 Observability Evidence Spine Addendum V2

This addendum keeps the master spec tree stable. It does not create node `52`; the work belongs to node `39`. The current codebase has real observability primitives, but the completion target is stricter than "we have telemetry." A finished Kolm build needs one executable evidence spine that binds every route, job, provider call, artifact transition, policy decision, support action, account screen, CLI/TUI command, SLO, alert, incident, status update, and customer export to the same traceable contract.

Current repo evidence checked in this pass:

| evidence | current state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/otel.js` | Ships a lightweight OTLP/HTTP JSON exporter gated by `KOLM_OTEL`, with trace and metric queues, 1 MB queue cap, resource attributes, shutdown flush, `KOLM_OTEL_ATTRS`, inference span helpers, tenant hashing, and no hard dependency on `@opentelemetry/sdk-node`. | Keep the small dependency footprint, but add explicit W3C propagation, collector round-trip tests, standard semantic-convention mapping, versioned schema exports, and generated call-site coverage. |
| `src/otel-attrs.js` | Canonicalizes Kolm-shaped fields into the `kolm.*` attribute envelope, drops unknown keys, finite-checks numeric fields, and hashes raw tenant IDs before export. | Promote this into the generated attribute registry; every custom attribute must have owner, type, privacy class, standard mapping, stability status, retention, and deprecation policy. |
| `src/prometheus-exporter.js` | Renders Prometheus text format with registered HELP/TYPE blocks, finite value checks, label escaping, default histograms, and canonical metrics such as `kolm_capture_total`, `kolm_runtime_gpu_memory_bytes`, `kolm_http_request_duration_seconds`, and speculative acceptance histograms. | Metric names are strong; label governance is not finished. The current `tenant` label on `kolm_capture_total` must be hashed, bucketed, dropped, or explicitly private-network-only before production shared metrics. |
| `src/router.js` `/metrics` | Mounts `/metrics` before auth for scraper access, optionally gates it with `KOLM_METRICS_BEARER`, and returns `text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8`. | Development-open is acceptable only as a local default. Production release must prove bearer auth or private network isolation, no high-cardinality labels, and no secret/sensitive fields. |
| `src/log.js` | Provides a structured logging wrapper, redacts API keys/JWT/Bearer/email shapes, key-name redacts secret-like fields, lazy-loads event-store append, and `node --check src/log.js` passed in this pass. | Logging is useful but not yet a product-wide log schema. Add log event IDs, trace correlation, privacy class, retention, sampling, sink status, and encoding/mojibake hygiene checks to release evidence. |
| `src/sla-rollup.js` | Persists tenant-fenced latency and uptime samples to JSONL, returns honest empty states, fixed surface list, p50/p95/p99, uptime percentage, and caps query windows. | Extend from six local surfaces to every journey SLI and generate account/status/SLA definitions from one SLO catalog. |
| `src/diagnostic.js` | Provides deterministic artifact diagnostic envelopes, per-category K-Score rollups, Wilson confidence intervals, worst-sample pointers, and actionable recommendations. | Keep it as model-quality diagnostics, not a universal support diagnostic. The universal diagnostic spine must join model diagnostics with trace, job, provider, billing, policy, deployment, incident, and support-bundle data. |
| `src/router.js` diagnostic and SLA routes | Exposes `/v1/diagnose`, `/v1/diagnose/:cid`, `/v1/sla/rollup`, `/v1/sla/dashboard`, and `/v1/sla/series` with honest no-data paths. | These route groups need generated signal ownership, auth/privacy policy, route-to-SLO mapping, account UI state proof, and production smoke. |
| observability tests | Present tests include `tests/wave307-kolm-support-bundle.test.js`, `tests/wave730-prometheus.test.js`, `tests/wave733-otel.test.js`, `tests/wave788-sla.test.js`, `tests/wave823-otel-upgrade.test.js`, and `tests/wc04-trace-translator.test.js`. | Tests prove components, but final completion needs generated all-route/all-job coverage that fails when a new surface lacks telemetry, SLO, alert, support, and privacy declarations. |

Current external baseline updates:

| baseline | current standard signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenTelemetry signals | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/signals/` describes traces, metrics, logs, baggage, plus events/profiles under development. | Kolm must treat traces, metrics, logs, events, profiles, baggage/context, support bundles, and incidents as connected but distinct evidence objects with explicit correlation. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` lists GenAI spans, metrics, events, exceptions, agent spans, MCP, and provider-specific conventions. The page is still marked development and includes stability opt-in guidance. | Provider/model/token/tool telemetry must emit standard GenAI fields first, record the semconv version/opt-in state, then add `kolm.*` fields only where Kolm has unique artifact, K-Score, distillation, routing, or device meaning. |
| W3C Trace Context | `https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/` defines `traceparent` and `tracestate`, outgoing propagation behavior, and privacy concerns. | API, account, SDK, gateway, worker, webhook, provider, device, BYOC, CLI remote calls, and support replay must propagate or intentionally restart trace context with documented privacy rules. |
| Prometheus metric naming and exporters | `https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/naming/` and `https://prometheus.io/docs/instrumenting/writing_exporters/` emphasize unit/type suffixes, stable names, subsystem ownership, base units, and avoiding high-cardinality labels. | Every Kolm metric must have owner module, base unit, type suffix, stable labels, cardinality budget, no raw tenant/user/request/artifact/prompt values in shared metrics, and a generated scrape test. |
| OpenMetrics 1.0 | `https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/` is the long-term exposition compatibility target. | `/metrics` can remain Prometheus text format v0.0.4 today, but the catalog must document compatibility gaps and future OpenMetrics behavior. |
| Google SRE SLO alerting | `https://sre.google/workbook/alerting-on-slos/` frames alerts around SLOs, error budgets, precision, recall, detection time, and reset time. | Alert rules must be symptom/SLO based, not raw-noise based; every alert needs budget math, owner, severity, runbook, test fixture, and customer-impact link. |
| NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/61/r3/final` ties incident response into cybersecurity risk management, preparation, impact reduction, and recovery effectiveness. | Security and reliability incidents need one lifecycle record: prepare, detect, analyze, contain, eradicate/mitigate, recover, communicate, preserve evidence, and feed follow-up work orders. |
| DORA metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` defines delivery speed and instability measures such as change lead time, deployment frequency, failed-deployment recovery time, and change failure rate. | Release observability must connect deploy evidence to operational outcomes: changed surfaces, smoke result, regression signals, incident link, rollback, recovery time, and change-failure classification. |

Node `39` V2 target architecture:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.observability-spine.v2",
  "sources": [
    "src/otel.js",
    "src/otel-attrs.js",
    "src/prometheus-exporter.js",
    "src/log.js",
    "src/sla-rollup.js",
    "src/diagnostic.js",
    "src/router.js"
  ],
  "catalogs": {
    "signals": "docs/telemetry/signals.json",
    "attributes": "docs/telemetry/attributes.json",
    "metrics": "docs/telemetry/metrics.json",
    "slos": "docs/telemetry/slos.json",
    "alerts": "docs/telemetry/alerts.json",
    "incidents": "docs/telemetry/incidents.json",
    "support_bundles": "docs/telemetry/support-bundles.json"
  },
  "required_record_per_surface": {
    "owner_node": "39",
    "route_or_job": "stable route group, job kind, command, or account page",
    "operation_name": "stable dotted operation name",
    "trace_context": "w3c-propagate|w3c-restart|local-only|not-applicable",
    "otel_semconv": "standard attributes plus kolm.* extensions",
    "prometheus_metrics": "metric names, types, units, labels, cardinality budget",
    "logs": "log event type, fields, redaction, retention, sampling",
    "slo": "SLI, objective, window, error budget, exclusion, owner",
    "alert": "burn rule, severity, runbook, noise budget, customer impact",
    "support_bundle": "include/exclude fields, secret scan, checksum, customer-safe summary",
    "production_smoke": "command, endpoint, expected signal, report path"
  }
}
```

The V2 evidence spine adds these hard rules:

1. Every public or authenticated route group must declare its operation name, emitted signals, safe attributes, metric labels, SLO mapping, alert behavior, support-bundle behavior, and production-smoke proof.
2. Every background job, compile/distill/train step, provider call, model run, artifact transition, registry operation, billing meter, webhook, device action, and admin/support action must emit a joinable timeline record.
3. Every account status, SLA, diagnose, audit, agent-telemetry, drift, and support page must show freshness, data source, missing evidence state, degraded state, and safe export action.
4. Every CLI/TUI diagnostic command must output stable JSON with trace IDs, operation IDs, next-action codes, missing configuration, secret-scan result, and support-bundle link.
5. Every `kolm.*` attribute must state whether a standard OTel/GenAI/HTTP/resource attribute exists, why Kolm needs an extension, and how migration will work if a standard appears.
6. Every Prometheus metric must use base units, type suffixes where appropriate, stable labels, finite values, low-cardinality dimensions, HELP/TYPE metadata, and shared/tenant visibility policy.
7. Every log event must be safe by default: no raw prompts, raw outputs, API keys, provider keys, JWTs, emails, cookies, customer secrets, PHI, PII, or cross-tenant data unless an explicit regulated evidence path exists.
8. Every support bundle must be reproducible, redacted, checksummed, scoped to one tenant/surface/problem, and explainable to customer security reviewers.
9. Every incident must link traces, metrics, logs, status updates, affected surfaces, SLO impact, customer communications, mitigation, recovery proof, and follow-up tickets.
10. Every deploy must prove the changed surfaces emitted expected telemetry in production or explain why no runtime signal should exist.

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-OBS-V2-001` | `39.1` | Generate `docs/telemetry/signals.json` from route, job, CLI/TUI, account, provider, worker, webhook, and support surfaces. | New surfaces fail verification unless they declare signal family, operation name, owner node, privacy class, retention, sampling, and proof command. |
| `W-OBS-V2-002` | `39.1` | Generate `docs/telemetry/attributes.json` from `src/otel.js`, `src/otel-attrs.js`, and route/job declarations. | Every `kolm.*` attribute has type, owner, standard mapping, stability status, privacy class, examples, tests, and deprecation plan. |
| `W-OBS-V2-003` | `39.1` | Add a telemetry schema diff gate. | Changes to signal names, attributes, metric names, label sets, privacy classes, retention, or sampling require a reviewable compatibility diff. |
| `W-OBS-V2-004` | `39.2` | Implement W3C trace context parse/inject helpers. | Incoming and outgoing API, SDK, gateway, worker, webhook, provider, BYOC, and CLI remote calls preserve or intentionally restart `traceparent` and safe `tracestate`. |
| `W-OBS-V2-005` | `39.2` | Map provider/model/tool spans to OpenTelemetry GenAI conventions. | Model calls emit standard GenAI span/metric/event fields first, record semconv version/opt-in state, and add Kolm artifact/K-Score/routing extensions only where needed. |
| `W-OBS-V2-006` | `39.2` | Build the trace-to-product timeline joiner. | A trace can reconstruct request, policy, provider, cost, artifact, job, billing, device, support, and incident context without raw payload access. |
| `W-OBS-V2-007` | `39.2` | Add route and job operation-name registry. | Every route group and job kind has a stable operation name and collisions fail verification. |
| `W-OBS-V2-008` | `39.3` | Generate journey-level SLI and SLO catalog. | Gateway, compile/distill, runtime/device, registry, governance, billing, support, docs/account, and admin journeys have latency, availability, correctness, freshness, durability, and data-loss indicators. |
| `W-OBS-V2-009` | `39.3` | Add burn-rate alert definitions. | Alerts are tied to SLOs, error budgets, precision/recall/detection/reset tradeoffs, owner, runbook, severity, test fixture, and customer-impact mapping. |
| `W-OBS-V2-010` | `39.3` | Generate vendor dashboard and alert packs from the catalog. | Grafana, Datadog, Honeycomb, Prometheus, and account dashboard definitions share the same metric/attribute/SLO source. |
| `W-OBS-V2-011` | `39.4` | Upgrade support bundles into signed evidence packets. | Bundles include manifest, source list, privacy class, redaction result, secret scan, checksum, trace/job/artifact links, next action, and customer-safe summary. |
| `W-OBS-V2-012` | `39.4` | Add universal diagnostic lookup. | A user can paste request ID, trace ID, job ID, artifact ID, provider call ID, error code, device ID, policy decision ID, invoice ID, or incident ID and receive one safe timeline. |
| `W-OBS-V2-013` | `39.4` | Connect model diagnostics to operational diagnostics. | `src/diagnostic.js` K-Score and failure-mode output links to traces, jobs, captures, providers, costs, policy decisions, support bundle, and next capture/distill action. |
| `W-OBS-V2-014` | `39.4` | Add incident lifecycle records. | Incidents track detection, analysis, mitigation, recovery, SLO impact, status communication, evidence, postmortem, follow-up owners, and customer notification state. |
| `W-OBS-V2-015` | `39.5` | Harden `/metrics` production policy. | Production fails release unless `/metrics` is bearer-gated or private-network-only and cardinality checks prove no raw tenant/user/request/artifact/prompt/free-form labels. |
| `W-OBS-V2-016` | `39.5` | Fix Prometheus tenant-cardinality policy. | `kolm_capture_total` and any future tenant-scoped metric use hashed/bucketed/omitted tenant dimensions according to visibility policy and pass high-cardinality tests. |
| `W-OBS-V2-017` | `39.5` | Add OTLP collector compatibility smoke. | Traces and metrics round-trip through an OTLP-compatible collector fixture with resource attributes, GenAI fields, Kolm extensions, and privacy assertions. |
| `W-OBS-V2-018` | `39.5` | Add telemetry cost and retention budgets. | Each signal family has retention days, sampling rules, aggregation rules, audit exceptions, export destinations, and budget estimates. |
| `W-OBS-V2-019` | `39.6` | Add production observability smoke to release control. | Deploy smoke proves changed routes/jobs/account pages emit expected trace, metric, log, status/SLA, support-bundle, and incident evidence or declare no-runtime-signal. |
| `W-OBS-V2-020` | `39.6` | Add customer observability evidence export. | Enterprise admins can export dated SLO, incident, trace, job, artifact, policy, billing, and support evidence with privacy proof and checksum. |

Node `39` V2 relax redline:

- Do not call observability complete until a generated catalog owns every signal, attribute, metric, SLO, alert, incident, support bundle, and production smoke.
- Do not let Prometheus labels include raw tenant IDs, user IDs, emails, request IDs, artifact IDs, prompts, provider keys, free-form errors, or other unbounded values in shared production metrics.
- Do not let OpenTelemetry export rely only on custom `kolm.*` attributes when standard OTel, GenAI, HTTP, resource, service, or error attributes apply.
- Do not let a green `/metrics` scrape imply trace, log, incident, support, status, SLA, and customer evidence are complete.
- Do not let account diagnostics or support tools require raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, cross-tenant data, or founder-only knowledge.
- Do not let any production release close without observability proof for changed surfaces.

#### Node 39 Observability Evidence Spine Addendum V3

Node `52` remains unused. This pass maintains the master spec tree by strengthening node `39`, because observability is not a page, a metrics endpoint, or an OTel exporter. For Kolm, observability is the proof system that lets a buyer, engineer, operator, auditor, and implementation agent answer the same question: what happened, why did it happen, what customer or artifact was affected, what evidence proves it, what data was intentionally withheld, and what must be fixed next.

This V3 addendum is a spec-only edit. It does not change product code while frontend, backend, and research agents are active. It turns the current observability primitives into an implementation target for a generated, privacy-safe, route-aware, job-aware, customer-visible evidence spine.

Current repo delta checked in this pass:

| evidence area | current repo evidence | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Telemetry modules | `src/otel.js`, `src/otel-attrs.js`, `src/prometheus-exporter.js`, `src/log.js`, `src/sla-rollup.js`, `src/diagnostic.js`, `src/agent-telemetry.js`, `src/trace-capture.js`, `src/trace-compile.js`, and `src/trace-translator.js` exist as separate primitives. | Keep the modules separate, but generate one cross-product catalog that declares operation names, signal ownership, privacy class, SLO link, support-bundle behavior, and production proof per route, job, account page, CLI/TUI command, and provider/runtime adapter. |
| Prometheus exporter | `src/prometheus-exporter.js` pre-registers six canonical `kolm_` metrics, emits HELP and TYPE, validates names and labels, escapes label values, emits honest empty states, and renders text exposition format `0.0.4`. | The exporter is a strong primitive, but node `39` is not complete until metric labels have a cardinality budget, tenant and namespace handling policy, public/private visibility, OpenMetrics compatibility decision, and generated scrape tests. |
| Metric label risk | The current scan still finds `tenant` label references in `src/prometheus-exporter.js`, while OTel paths hash raw tenant IDs in `src/otel.js` and `src/otel-attrs.js`. | Shared production metrics must not expose raw tenant, user, request, artifact, prompt, provider key, or free-form error values. A metric-label linter must enforce hash, bucket, omit, or private-network-only policy before release. |
| Route evidence | `src/router.js` exposes diagnostic, trace, SLA, and telemetry-adjacent routes including `/v1/diagnose`, `/v1/diagnose/:cid`, `/v1/sla/rollup`, `/v1/sla/dashboard`, `/v1/sla/series`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/stats`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/chain`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/export`, `/v1/trace/append`, `/v1/trace/compile`, `/v1/trace/verify`, `/v1/trace/translate/providers`, `/v1/trace/translate/detect`, and `/v1/trace/translate`. | These are valuable but still route-local. Final completion needs route-group ownership in the observability catalog and a universal lookup path that accepts request ID, trace ID, job ID, artifact CID, provider call ID, device ID, policy decision ID, billing meter ID, error code, or incident ID. |
| Account UX evidence | `public/account/overview.html`, `public/account/sla.html`, `public/account/agent-telemetry.html`, and `public/account/audit-log.html` expose readiness, SLA, telemetry, and audit concepts. `public/account/sla.html` already states when data comes from `/v1/sla/dashboard` and renders honest connection states. | Account UX must become operational, not explanatory. Every diagnostic panel needs freshness, data source, missing/degraded/no-auth states, support ID copy, privacy disclosure, next action, and a safe export affordance without long paragraphs. |
| Public docs evidence | `public/docs/observability/opentelemetry.html`, `public/docs/observability/prometheus.html`, `public/docs/cli/logs.md`, `public/docs/cli/status.md`, and `public/docs/cli/trace.html` document OTel, Prometheus, logs, status, and traces. | Docs must be generated or audited against the same observability catalog. They must not drift from actual signal names, metric labels, bearer requirements, route auth, privacy guarantees, CLI output, or support-bundle contents. |
| Test evidence | Relevant tests include `tests/wave306-kolm-metrics.test.js`, `tests/wave307-kolm-support-bundle.test.js`, `tests/wave383-agent-telemetry.test.js`, `tests/wave424-agent-telemetry-tenant-scope.test.js`, `tests/wave425-trace-ownership.test.js`, `tests/wave455-distill-runs-telemetry.test.js`, `tests/wave457-telemetry-reconciliation.test.js`, `tests/wave463-trace-compile.test.js`, `tests/wave517-trace-route-docs.test.js`, `tests/wave730-prometheus.test.js`, `tests/wave733-otel.test.js`, `tests/wave788-sla.test.js`, `tests/wave823-otel-upgrade.test.js`, and `tests/wc04-trace-translator.test.js`. | Component tests exist, but final completion needs generated coverage that fails when a new route, job, account page, CLI/TUI command, provider adapter, worker, webhook, or artifact lifecycle step lacks telemetry and support evidence ownership. |
| Prior research evidence | `docs/research/audit-observability-evidence-audit-2026-05-12.md`, `docs/research/audit-observability-evidence-matrix-2026-05-12.csv`, `docs/research/trace-eval-schema-spec-2026-05-13.md`, and trace privacy/import matrices exist. | Fold prior research into generated catalogs instead of leaving it as standalone audit material. Old CSV rows should become machine-verifiable requirements or be retired with a reason. |

External baseline delta checked in this pass:

| source | current baseline | Kolm V3 rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenTelemetry signals, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/signals/` | OTel currently covers traces, metrics, logs, baggage, and profiles, with events treated as a log type and profiles still developing. | Kolm must model traces, metrics, logs, baggage/context, events, profiles, support bundles, incidents, and customer evidence as related but not interchangeable evidence classes. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | GenAI conventions are marked development and define spans, metrics, events, exceptions, agent spans, MCP, and provider-specific conventions; existing instrumentations are expected to handle stability opt-in. | Kolm provider and agent telemetry must record the GenAI semconv version and opt-in mode, emit standard fields first, and only add `kolm.*` extensions for artifact, K-Score, distillation, capture, routing, device, or governance concepts that standards do not own. |
| W3C Trace Context, `https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/` | The processing model defines what to do when `traceparent` is missing or present, how to update parent ID and sampled flag, and how to handle invalid `tracestate`. | Kolm must prove parse, validate, propagate, restart, and sanitize behavior across API, SDK, gateway, worker, provider, webhook, BYOC, device, CLI remote calls, and support replay. |
| Prometheus metric naming, `https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/naming/` | Prometheus recommends application prefixes, base units, suffixes, and metrics that represent one logical quantity across labels. | Every `kolm_` metric must have owner, unit, type, stable label set, aggregation meaning, cardinality budget, examples, and docs generated from one manifest. |
| Prometheus exporter guidance, `https://prometheus.io/docs/instrumenting/writing_exporters/` | Exporters should work with minimal configuration, avoid fragile generated metrics, use YAML examples, and follow naming guidance. | Kolm's Prometheus docs, dashboard, CLI metrics commands, and scrape config must be generated from the exporter manifest and must fail loud when bearer/private-network policy is missing in production. |
| OpenMetrics 1.0, `https://prometheus.io/docs/specs/om/open_metrics_spec/` | OpenMetrics text exposition uses `application/openmetrics-text; version=1.0.0; charset=utf-8`, UTF-8, LF endings, and EOF conventions. | Kolm can keep Prometheus text `0.0.4` today only if the catalog records why, what compatibility gaps remain, and what a future OpenMetrics mode must output. |
| Google SRE SLO alerting, `https://sre.google/workbook/alerting-on-slos/` | Alerts should be judged by precision, recall, detection time, reset time, and error-budget consumption. | Kolm alerts must be symptom and SLO based. Every alert needs SLI, objective, burn math, severity, owner, runbook, test fixture, noise budget, customer impact, and status-page rule. |
| NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/61/r3/final` | The 2025 revision frames incident response inside cybersecurity risk management, preparation, impact reduction, detection, response, and recovery effectiveness. | Reliability, security, privacy, and model-safety incidents must use one lifecycle: prepare, detect, analyze, contain, mitigate, recover, communicate, preserve evidence, and generate follow-up work orders. |
| DORA metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | DORA now frames delivery performance through throughput and instability, including change lead time, deployment frequency, failed deployment recovery time, change fail rate, and deployment rework rate. | Release observability must connect each deploy to changed surfaces, production smoke, failed-deployment recovery time, rollback, hotfix, incident, rework, and customer-facing status evidence. |

Node `39` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.observability-evidence-packet.v3",
  "owner_node": "39",
  "surface_id": "product surface or journey id",
  "route_or_job": "route group, worker kind, CLI/TUI command, account page, webhook, provider adapter, or device action",
  "operation_name": "stable dotted operation name",
  "trace_context": {
    "mode": "w3c_propagate | w3c_restart | local_only | not_applicable",
    "traceparent_policy": "parse_validate_forward | create_new | discard_invalid",
    "tracestate_policy": "sanitize | preserve_safe | drop",
    "baggage_policy": "none | safe_keys_only | tenant_private"
  },
  "metrics": [
    {
      "name": "kolm_example_total",
      "type": "counter | gauge | histogram | info",
      "unit": "base unit or none",
      "labels": ["bounded_label"],
      "cardinality_budget": "integer or bounded enum",
      "visibility": "public | tenant_private | internal | private_network_only"
    }
  ],
  "logs": {
    "event_ids": ["stable_log_event_id"],
    "redaction": "default_safe | regulated_manifest_required",
    "retention": "days",
    "sampling": "rule"
  },
  "events": {
    "domain_events": ["incident_declared", "support_bundle_created", "artifact_verified"],
    "customer_visible": true
  },
  "slo": {
    "sli": "good events over valid events",
    "objective": "target and window",
    "error_budget": "math and exclusions",
    "alert_policy": "burn-rate rule and owner"
  },
  "support_bundle": {
    "manifest": "included and excluded fields",
    "secret_scan": "required",
    "checksum": "required",
    "customer_preview": "required before export"
  },
  "incident_link": {
    "lifecycle": "prepare_detect_analyze_contain_mitigate_recover_communicate_follow_up",
    "status_page": "public | tenant_specific | none"
  },
  "production_smoke": {
    "command": "command or URL",
    "expected_evidence": ["trace", "metric", "log", "slo", "support_bundle"],
    "report_path": "reports/observability/..."
  },
  "privacy": {
    "raw_prompt_output": "forbidden_by_default",
    "secrets": "forbidden",
    "tenant_ids": "hashed_or_private_only",
    "regulated_data": "manifest_required"
  },
  "evidence_files": ["source paths, tests, generated docs, reports"]
}
```

Signal privacy classification:

| class | examples | allowed default location | hard rule |
|---|---|---|---|
| Public aggregate metric | uptime aggregate, status aggregate, anonymous benchmark aggregate | public status/docs if no tenant data | Must not contain tenant, user, artifact, request, prompt, output, provider secret, or regulated fields. |
| Tenant-private metric | per-tenant SLA, usage, billing, capture counts, device health | authenticated account, tenant export, internal tenant-scoped support | Must be tenant-fenced and cannot be emitted to shared `/metrics` with raw tenant labels. |
| Internal operational trace | route spans, provider spans, worker spans, queue spans | internal OTel collector and support timeline | Raw prompt and raw output forbidden by default; use hashes, sizes, token counts, classifications, and stable IDs. |
| Support-only diagnostic | support bundle, doctor output, failed route context, missing env, cloud readiness | customer-controlled export and operator tool | Must include manifest, redaction proof, secret scan, checksum, generated timestamp, environment, and scope. |
| Regulated evidence packet | PHI/PII redaction proof, HIPAA audit export, enterprise incident export | regulated tenant export or reviewed admin/support path | Must state legal basis, retention, access log, redaction, reviewer, and customer visibility. |
| Incident packet | incident timeline, SLO impact, status updates, mitigation, postmortem | public or tenant-specific depending on impact | Must link status state, SLO impact, traces/metrics/logs, customer communications, and follow-up tickets. |
| Release evidence | deploy smoke, changed surfaces, DORA metrics, rollback, rework, hotfix | release reports and customer-safe summary | Must identify commit, environment, commands, duration, failures, recovery, and changed product surfaces. |

Observability UX requirements:

| surface | V3 interaction requirement |
|---|---|
| Account overview | Replace broad explanatory paragraphs with dense status rows: journey, health, freshness, source, changed-since-last-deploy, incident state, and next action. Empty, loading, missing-auth, missing-config, stale-data, degraded, and healthy states must be visually distinct and keyboard reachable. |
| Account diagnostics | Provide one lookup box for request ID, trace ID, job ID, artifact CID, provider call ID, device ID, policy decision ID, billing meter ID, error code, or incident ID. Return a timeline, root-cause candidates, evidence classes present/missing, support bundle action, and safe next action. |
| SLA account page | Show SLI definition, time window, inclusion/exclusion rules, sample freshness, error-budget burn, incidents in window, and whether the number is contractual or informational. No placeholder chart may look live without a data-source and timestamp. |
| Agent telemetry | Separate model behavior quality from operational health. Token confidence, acceptance rate, drift, fallback, cost, tool errors, and latency need separate axes and no raw prompt/output exposure. |
| Audit log | Make audit evidence explorable by actor, action, resource, policy decision, trace/support ID, and export status. Any privileged support access must be visible as its own audit event. |
| Public Prometheus docs | State dev-default versus production policy clearly: local no-auth can exist, production must use bearer or private network, and shared metrics must pass cardinality/privacy checks. |
| Public OpenTelemetry docs | State semconv version, stability opt-in, no-prompt/no-output rule, raw tenant hash behavior, collector examples, and how to verify no-op behavior when OTel is disabled. |
| CLI/TUI doctor | Output stable JSON by default option, with `ok`, `surface`, `operation`, `trace_id`, `missing_config`, `secret_values_included:false`, `support_bundle`, `next_actions`, and `evidence_paths`. |
| Support bundle export | Show a preview of included fields, excluded fields, privacy class, secret-scan result, checksum, retention, and customer-visible summary before download or upload. |
| Status page | Separate public platform incidents from tenant-specific degradation and expose RSS/API parity, incident timestamps, affected surfaces, mitigation, recovery proof, and postmortem link state. |
