| `W-OBS-V3-002` | `39.1` | Add a no-owner telemetry gate. | CI fails if any route group, job kind, account page, CLI/TUI command, provider adapter, webhook, device action, or artifact lifecycle transition lacks a node `39` evidence packet or explicit no-runtime-signal rationale. |
| `W-OBS-V3-003` | `39.1` | Add metric label and cardinality linter. | The linter rejects raw tenant, user, request, artifact, prompt, output, provider secret, email, free-form error, unbounded namespace, and route-param labels in shared metrics. |
| `W-OBS-V3-004` | `39.2` | Add W3C trace context helper tests. | Parse, validate, forward, restart, and discard-invalid behavior is tested for API ingress, provider egress, webhook delivery, worker jobs, CLI remote calls, and support replay. |
| `W-OBS-V3-005` | `39.2` | Add GenAI semantic convention mapper. | Provider/model/tool spans emit standard GenAI fields where available, record semconv version and opt-in state, and document every `kolm.*` extension. |
| `W-OBS-V3-006` | `39.2` | Add universal operation-name registry. | Every route group, job, provider call, account panel, CLI/TUI command, webhook, and device action has a stable operation name; collisions and undocumented changes fail verification. |
| `W-OBS-V3-007` | `39.2` | Build universal evidence lookup. | A user can paste request, trace, job, artifact, provider call, device, policy, billing, error, or incident ID and get one tenant-safe timeline. |
| `W-OBS-V3-008` | `39.3` | Generate journey-level SLO catalog. | Every product journey has SLIs, objectives, windows, exclusions, sample freshness, error-budget math, owner, alert policy, status mapping, and account display copy. |
| `W-OBS-V3-009` | `39.3` | Add burn-rate alert registry. | Alerts are tied to SLOs, precision, recall, detection time, reset time, severity, owner, runbook, test fixture, noise budget, and customer-impact mapping. |
| `W-OBS-V3-010` | `39.3` | Build incident lifecycle registry. | Incidents track prepare, detect, analyze, contain, mitigate, recover, communicate, postmortem, follow-up, affected surfaces, SLO impact, evidence links, and customer state. |
| `W-OBS-V3-011` | `39.4` | Upgrade support bundles into evidence packets. | Each bundle has manifest, include/exclude fields, privacy class, secret scan, redaction proof, checksum, timestamp, source command, tenant scope, and customer-safe summary. |
| `W-OBS-V3-012` | `39.4` | Add account diagnostics UX contract. | Account diagnostics renders lookup, timeline, freshness, source, evidence-present/missing states, no-auth/missing-config/degraded states, support ID copy, and safe export without paragraph bloat. |
| `W-OBS-V3-013` | `39.4` | Add CLI/TUI diagnostic schema parity. | `kolm doctor`, `kolm status`, `kolm trace`, `kolm metrics`, and TUI views expose the same evidence packet fields as account and API. |
| `W-OBS-V3-014` | `39.5` | Harden `/metrics` production policy. | Production release fails unless `/metrics` is bearer-gated or private-network-only, content type is correct for the selected format, and privacy/cardinality checks pass. |
| `W-OBS-V3-015` | `39.5` | Add OpenMetrics compatibility decision. | The catalog records current Prometheus text `0.0.4` behavior, OpenMetrics `1.0.0` gaps, EOF/content-type requirements, and migration acceptance tests. |
| `W-OBS-V3-016` | `39.5` | Add OTLP collector fixture. | Trace and metric payloads round-trip through a local OTLP-compatible collector fixture with resource attributes, GenAI fields, Kolm extensions, tenant hashing, no-prompt/no-output assertions, and backpressure behavior. |
| `W-OBS-V3-017` | `39.6` | Add deploy-to-observability evidence packet. | Each deploy report links changed routes/jobs/pages, expected signals, production smoke output, SLO impact, DORA metrics, rollback plan, incident state, and customer-safe summary. |
| `W-OBS-V3-018` | `39.6` | Add customer observability export. | Enterprise admins can export dated SLO, incident, trace, job, artifact, policy, billing, release, and support evidence with manifest, privacy proof, checksum, and retention policy. |

Node `39` V3 Relax Redline:

- No raw tenant, user, request, artifact, prompt, output, provider key, email, route param, free-form error, or secret value may appear in shared production metrics.
- No `/metrics` endpoint may be production-public unless bearer auth, private-network isolation, content-type policy, cardinality linter, and scrape tests are green.
- No GenAI telemetry may ship without semconv version, opt-in state, standard-field mapping, and explicit rationale for every `kolm.*` extension.
- No support bundle may exist without manifest, included/excluded fields, privacy class, secret scan, redaction proof, checksum, timestamp, source command, tenant scope, and customer-safe summary.
- No account diagnostic, SLA, agent telemetry, status, audit, or support surface may show green, live, complete, or resolved when source data is stale, missing, sampled out, unauthenticated, degraded, or from the wrong environment.
- No changed route, worker, provider adapter, webhook, CLI/TUI command, account page, or artifact lifecycle step may ship without a telemetry evidence packet or explicit no-runtime-signal rationale.
- No incident may close without SLO impact, affected surfaces, customer impact, mitigation, recovery proof, status/customer communication state, evidence links, and follow-up owners.
- No release may count as complete until production smoke proves the changed surfaces emit expected telemetry or documents why runtime telemetry is impossible.
- No documentation page may advertise observability behavior that is not generated from or verified against the current telemetry catalog.
- No master spec node may add observability requirements outside node `39` unless it links back here and names its evidence packet.

### Test Architecture, QA Coverage, Deterministic Regression, And Release Evidence Ledger V1

Node `40` turns Kolm's sprawling verification work into a state-of-the-art quality system. Nodes `28`, `29`, `30`, `31`, `37`, and `39` already mention tests, release gates, UI screenshots, contracts, and observability. Node `40` is the control layer above them: every test and QA artifact must have a product owner, scope size, risk target, fixture policy, determinism contract, evidence output, and release decision rule.

The current repo has substantial testing momentum, but "many tests exist" is not enough for 100 percent completion. The finished state is a living verification architecture where a change-impact engine can tell an implementation agent exactly which unit, contract, integration, browser, visual, security, performance, AI-eval, smoke, and manual checks prove a code change safe.

#### Repo Evidence Already Present

| Evidence family | Current repo evidence | What node 40 demands next |
|---|---|---|
| Node test suite | `tests/*.test.js` currently contains 559 test files, including 528 `wave*` files plus core tests such as `auth.test.js`, `server.test.js`, `site.test.js`, `stripe.test.js`, `artifact-end-to-end.test.js`, and `product-kernel-envelope.test.js` | Generate a test manifest that maps every test to master node, product journey, route group, risk class, scope size, fixture family, data isolation mode, and owning surface. |
| Package scripts | `npm test`, `test:parallel`, `release:verify`, `verify:depth`, `verify:surfaces`, `local:surfaces`, `prod:surfaces`, `ui:audit:*`, `qa:shots`, `verify:brand`, `verify:claims-scope`, `verify:inventions`, `verify:redaction-benchmark`, `verify:quality-calibration`, `verify:package-release`, and many frontier/readiness verifiers | Convert scripts into a formal gate taxonomy: fast preflight, changed-surface gate, full local gate, deep local gate, visual gate, security gate, benchmark gate, production smoke, and release-blocking gate. |
| Release driver | `scripts/release-verify.cjs` runs static refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, full tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, and billing tiers with timeouts and semantic validation | Add gate ownership, expected duration, required environment, artifact outputs, retry policy, flake policy, and change-impact selection so release verify is explainable and scalable. |
| Surface and production smoke | `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs`, `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs`, `docs/product-surfaces.json`, `public/docs/api-routes.json`, and generated product graphs | Every smoke probe must map to a product surface, route group, auth state, entitlement state, expected schema, negative case, and production evidence packet. |
| UI and screenshot QA | `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/qa-screenshots.mjs`, `scripts/screenshot-*.mjs`, `reports/ui-surface-audit/*`, and QA PNG artifacts | Visual QA must have route/theme/viewport/component coverage, screenshot retention rules, diff thresholds, false-positive review, accessibility linkage, and manual-inspection notes. |
| Static and content audits | `scripts/audit-static-refs.cjs`, `scripts/audit-href.cjs`, `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/audit-page-structure.cjs`, `scripts/seo-audit.cjs`, `scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs` | Static audits need one machine-readable policy catalog and generated failure explanations tied to nodes, pages, routes, claims, and product owners. |
| Benchmark and AI-eval gates | `scripts/bench-compare.mjs`, `scripts/bench-proof.mjs`, `scripts/bench-redaction-fixtures.mjs`, `scripts/bench-quality-calibration.mjs`, `scripts/benchmark-evidence.mjs`, `verify:kscore-calibration`, `verify:quality-calibration`, and related tests | AI-eval gates need frozen fixtures, statistical acceptance criteria, seed control, raw report retention, contamination checks, model/provider version pinning, and claim-scope mapping. |
| CI workflows | `.github/workflows/kolm-ci-pipeline.yml`, `lint.yml`, `smoke.yml`, `test-suite.yml`, `sbom.yml`, `sdk-c-rust.yml`, `kolmbench-submission.yml`, and Kolm compile/distill workflows | CI must be generated or audited against the same gate taxonomy, with permissions, secrets, artifacts, matrix strategy, required checks, and branch/deploy environments documented. |
| Evidence artifacts | `reports/full-test-latest.tap`, `reports/full-test-2026-05-22-backend-closeout.log`, live smoke artifacts, UI audit reports, screenshot directories | Evidence needs index files, freshness, commit hash, environment, command, duration, pass/fail/skipped counts, owning node, retention policy, and customer-safe export rules. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Standard or practice | Source | Implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Node.js test runner execution and coverage | `https://nodejs.org/api/test.html` | The primary JS test lane should use process isolation, explicit concurrency, coverage output where useful, deterministic seeds, reporters, and clear timeout ceilings. |
| Playwright best practices | `https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices` | Browser QA should rely on resilient locators, cross-browser/device coverage, CI traces on failure/retry, HTML reports, parallelism, and up-to-date browser engines. |
| OWASP Web Security Testing Guide | `https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/` | Security tests need stable scenario IDs, versioned references, web/API coverage, and repeatable evidence rather than informal penetration-test notes. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://scorecard.dev/` | Repository security and supply-chain posture should be automatically checked in CI and linked to release evidence. |
| Google test sizes | `https://testing.googleblog.com/2010/12/test-sizes.html` | Test classes should be based on measurable constraints such as network, database, filesystem, external systems, concurrency, sleeps, and runtime, not vague names. |
| Mutation testing | `https://stryker-mutator.io/docs/` | Coverage is not proof by itself; high-risk modules need mutation testing or equivalent assertion-strength checks to prove tests catch real behavioral faults. |

#### Canonical Test Manifest Record

Every test file, smoke probe, QA audit, benchmark, manual checklist, and release gate must be representable as this logical record:

```json
{
  "test_id": "test.wave604.product_frontier_contracts_api",
  "path": "tests/wave604-product-frontier-contracts-api.test.js",
  "kind": "unit|contract|integration|e2e|browser|visual|accessibility|security|performance|benchmark|ai_eval|smoke|manual|release_gate",
  "scope_size": "small|medium|large|xlarge",
  "master_nodes": ["37", "40"],
  "product_surfaces": ["public-docs-sdk"],
  "product_journeys": ["public-docs-sdk"],
  "route_groups": ["product"],
  "risk_classes": ["api_contract", "docs_parity", "regression"],
  "owner": "backend|frontend|docs|security|ml|ops|support|release",
  "fixtures": ["public/product-graph.json"],
  "data_isolation": "none|temp_home|temp_data_dir|mock_server|local_server|production_probe",
  "network_policy": "none|localhost|mocked|external_optional|production_required",
  "time_policy": "no_sleep|bounded_poll|fake_clock|wall_clock_required",
  "determinism": {
    "seeded": true,
    "random_seed": "fixed-or-captured",
    "timezone": "UTC-or-explicit",
    "env_overrides": ["NODE_ENV=test"]
  },
  "expected_duration_ms": 5000,
  "timeout_ms": 15000,
  "artifacts": ["tap", "json", "screenshot", "trace", "coverage", "benchmark_report"],
  "release_blocking": true,
  "flake_policy": "zero_tolerance|quarantine_with_ticket|non_blocking_with_reason",
  "last_known_evidence": "reports/full-test-latest.tap",
  "negative_cases": ["invalid_auth", "cross_tenant_denied", "schema_mismatch"],
  "manual_followup": "none"
}
```

The record is not a new testing framework requirement. It is the minimum metadata needed to make current and future tests governable.

#### Test Lane Taxonomy

| Lane | Purpose | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| Small/unit | Prove pure logic, parsers, transforms, validators, policy decisions, schema helpers, and deterministic algorithms without network, database, filesystem, sleeps, or shared global state. | Fast isolated tests, fixed fixtures, seed proof, no external IO, mutation/negative cases for high-risk modules. |
| Medium/contract | Prove module boundaries, API envelopes, OpenAPI/JSON Schema parity, SDK compatibility, CLI/TUI output, product graph, docs parity, and generated artifacts. | Contract fixtures, schema diffs, generated-client smoke, stable error cases, backward compatibility assertions. |
| Large/integration | Prove local server, stores, routes, workers, billing, providers with mocks, job orchestration, artifacts, policy, object storage, and support bundles. | Isolated temp HOME/data dirs, local server boot, deterministic cleanup, timeout ceilings, semantic output validation. |
| Xlarge/e2e | Prove user journeys across account, API, CLI/TUI, SDK, docs, registry, job lifecycle, notifications, and production smoke. | Auth setup, route probes, browser traces, screenshots, API/CLI parity, rollback notes, production evidence packet. |
| Visual/browser | Prove public/account/docs UI renders and behaves across route/theme/viewport/browser states. | Playwright traces on failure, screenshots, interaction review, accessibility checks, route coverage, diff thresholds. |
| Accessibility | Prove keyboard, focus, screen reader semantics, contrast, zoom, reduced motion, forms, errors, media, and charts. | Automated checks plus manual assistive-tech checklist for critical flows and screenshot-linked evidence. |
| Security | Prove auth, tenant isolation, secrets, injections, SSRF/path traversal, file upload, webhooks, rate limits, WSTG cases, and abuse controls. | Versioned scenario IDs, negative tests, exploit fixtures, cross-tenant probes, safe logs, disclosure-ready evidence. |
| Performance/reliability | Prove latency, throughput, memory, queue backpressure, retry, degradation, load, chaos, and cost behavior. | Bench reports, load profiles, SLO impact, resource budgets, regression thresholds, flame/profile artifacts where needed. |
| AI-eval/benchmark | Prove K-Score, redaction, distillation, routing, quantization, calibration, contamination, and model/provider claims. | Frozen datasets, model/provider versions, seeds, statistical thresholds, raw JSON reports, claim-scope links. |
| Manual QA | Prove things automation cannot yet prove: brand feel, confusing copy, sales narrative, enterprise readiness, demo quality, buyer workflow, and high-context UI coherence. | Structured checklist, owner, route screenshots, findings, decision, follow-up ticket, and date/commit/environment. |

#### Required Quality Maps

| Map | Required fields | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| Source-to-test map | source path, primary node, test files, smoke commands, manual QA, uncovered risk | Implementation agents know exactly how to prove every file they touch. |
| Route-to-test map | route, method, auth state, product surface, schema, positive tests, negative tests, smoke probes, production probe | No route can hide behind broad suite success. |
| Page-to-QA map | URL, template, design-system components, theme/viewport/browser coverage, accessibility, screenshots, manual notes | Frontend quality becomes provable instead of subjective. |
| Claim-to-evidence map | claim string, page/API/docs source, readiness requirement, benchmark/cert/package evidence, closeout gate, test proof | Marketing and docs cannot drift from product truth. |
| Feature-to-risk map | feature, user journey, failure modes, data classes, security risks, reliability risks, commercial risks, required tests | Test investment follows user and business risk. |
| Gate-to-artifact map | command, inputs, environment, outputs, reports, retention, owner, release-blocking state | Release decisions are auditable and reproducible. |

#### Redlines

- No test may be release-blocking unless it has owner, scope size, timeout, fixture policy, failure explanation, and artifact behavior.
- No test may be non-blocking if it covers a P0 security, tenant isolation, billing, artifact integrity, provider spend, legal claim, or production deploy invariant.
- No route may be considered covered by `npm test` unless the route-to-test map proves positive, negative, schema, auth, and smoke coverage.
- No UI page may be considered complete without browser/screenshot evidence for desktop/mobile, dark/light, loading/empty/error states, and at least one manual coherence pass for critical pages.
- No benchmark or AI-eval may support public claims unless fixtures, seeds, model versions, scoring method, raw report, and statistical thresholds are recorded.
- No flaky test may be rerun until green without a flake ticket, owner, classification, quarantine decision, and failure artifact.
- No sleeps or wall-clock assumptions may appear in small/medium tests unless explicitly justified and bounded.
- No production smoke may mutate customer data, leak secrets, depend on fragile global state, or leave artifacts without cleanup.
- No coverage percentage may be used as a quality claim without mutation, negative, or assertion-strength evidence on high-risk modules.
- No generated docs/API/product graph/screenshot evidence may be accepted unless source command, input hash, output freshness, and diff check are recorded.
- No CI workflow may use broad permissions, implicit secrets, unpinned external actions, or hidden environment dependencies without security review.
- No "done" claim may rely on a previous report unless the report commit, environment, command, date, and changed files still match the current tree.

| `40.2` Unit, contract, integration, e2e, browser, visual, accessibility, security, performance, AI-eval, and manual QA lanes | Every lane has entry criteria, command, owner, expected duration, data policy, artifact policy, release-blocking rules, and examples from current repo files. |
| `40.3` Deterministic harnesses, seeds, time, network, environment controls, sharding, flake triage, and quarantine policy | Test runs are reproducible with isolated HOME/data dirs, fixed seeds, explicit env, bounded timeouts, network classes, shard safety, flake classification, and no silent rerun-to-green behavior. |
| `40.4` Coverage, mutation testing, negative tests, fuzz/property tests, accessibility proof, API proof, and security proof | High-risk code paths require branch/condition or behavior coverage plus negative/fuzz/property/mutation-equivalent proof, not just line coverage. |
| `40.5` Release gates, CI workflows, artifacts, reports, dashboards, change-impact selection, and test-debt burn-down | Release verify, CI workflows, local/deep/prod smoke, UI audits, benchmarks, and package gates are tied to change-impact selection and produce indexed artifacts with retention and ownership. |
| `40.6` Evidence: test matrix, coverage/mutation reports, flake ledger, Playwright traces/screenshots, release verify, production smoke, and manual QA notes | Completion claims link to current test matrix, reports, traces/screenshots, full release verify, production smoke, and manual QA notes that match the current commit and changed surfaces. |

#### Node 40 Relax Redline

Node `40` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Which tests prove this file, route, page, CLI command, SDK method, worker, product journey, claim, and deployment path?
- Which tests are small, medium, large, xlarge, browser, security, performance, AI-eval, smoke, or manual, and what measurable constraints make them that size?
- Which fixtures, temp dirs, mocks, local servers, production probes, seeds, time controls, network rules, and env vars make the run deterministic?
- Which failures are flaky, what evidence proves that, who owns the flake, and why is it allowed or not allowed to block release?
- Which coverage, mutation, negative, fuzz/property, security, accessibility, visual, and benchmark proofs cover the high-risk code paths?
- Which CI workflows and release gates ran, on which commit, with which command, output, duration, artifacts, and environment?
- Which changed files triggered which required test lanes, and which lanes were intentionally skipped with a reason?
- Can a customer or auditor inspect the release evidence packet without seeing secrets, raw prompts, or cross-tenant data?

The relax condition for node `40` is not "the tests pass." It is: every Kolm change can be mapped to deterministic, risk-weighted, product-owned verification evidence across source, route, page, API, CLI/TUI, SDK, worker, artifact, benchmark, security, accessibility, UI, release, production smoke, and manual QA lanes, with no silent flakes or stale reports hiding unfinished work.

#### Node 40 Verification Operating System Addendum V2

This addendum keeps QA and release proof attached to node `40`; it does not create node `52`. The repo already has a large verification surface, but the 100-percent completion bar is higher than a green console run. The finished state is a verification operating system: every source file, route, page, account workflow, CLI/TUI command, SDK method, worker, AI benchmark, public claim, generated artifact, and production deploy has an explicit proof chain, current evidence, and release decision rule.

Current repo evidence checked in this pass:

| evidence | current state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `tests/` | The current test directory contains 559 `.test.js` files, spanning core tests and wave tests across API, artifacts, auth, billing, capture, CLI/TUI, distillation, docs, evaluation, frontend contracts, providers, security, storage, and product graph surfaces. | Count is strong, but count is not coverage. Generate a test manifest that maps every test file to master node, source paths, route groups, product journeys, risk class, data isolation, lane, timeout, fixtures, and evidence output. |
| `package.json` scripts | Scripts include `test`, `test:parallel`, `release:verify`, `lint:refs`, `verify:depth`, `verify:surfaces`, `local:surfaces`, `prod:surfaces`, `ui:audit:*`, `verify:brand`, `verify:claims-scope`, AI/benchmark gates, package release gates, governance/compliance gates, and frontier/invention gates. | Convert scripts into a generated gate taxonomy with owner, lane, expected duration, required env, artifact outputs, release-blocking state, and changed-file trigger rules. |
| `scripts/release-verify.cjs` | Release verify runs lint refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, full tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, and billing tiers; it supports JSON mode, skip flags, deep surfaces, test sharding, timeouts, isolated test roots, and semantic checks. | This is the release-control seed. It still needs an indexed release evidence packet, gate IDs, flake policy, CI parity, production-smoke link, screenshot link, benchmark link, and changed-surface selection. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` | Boots an isolated local backend, provisions a disposable enterprise tenant, waits for `/health`, and delegates to production-style surface smoke with `--require-auth`. | Strong local smoke shape. Completion requires every declared probe to map to a route group, product surface, auth/plan state, schema, negative case, master node, and prod equivalent. |
| `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` and `docs/product-surfaces.json` | Production smoke and product-surface catalogs exist and are already connected to local/deep smoke scripts. | Final proof requires production smoke artifacts with base URL, commit, environment, auth key class, probe IDs, route groups, response schema, and cleanup notes. |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` | Enumerates critical/static routes, supports all-route mode, desktop/mobile viewports, dark/light themes, health-gated local server boot, screenshots, warning/failure accounting, and reports. | Visual QA needs a route-to-component and route-to-requirement map, trace capture on failures, accessibility checks, manual coherence review, and diff retention, not only screenshots. |
| static/content audit scripts | Static refs, hrefs, claims, product journeys, product kernel, product surfaces, page structure, SEO, render, and link audits exist. | These should be emitted from one policy catalog so failures name owner node, route/page, claim, generator, source path, and blocking state. |
| AI and benchmark gates | Redaction benchmark, K-Score calibration, quality calibration, benchmark evidence, frontier contracts, quant oracle, cloud broker, distill strategy, and invention simulations are wired into `verify:depth`. | These need benchmark manifests with fixture provenance, seed, model/provider version, statistical threshold, raw output, claim mapping, and contamination checks. |
| UI evidence artifacts | Screenshot scripts and QA PNGs exist in `scripts/`, and previous report directories exist under `reports/ui-surface-audit/`. | Artifacts need index records with route/theme/viewport/browser, commit, command, environment, retention, reviewer notes, and linked requirements. |

Current external baseline updates:

| baseline | current standard signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| Node.js test runner | `https://nodejs.org/api/test.html` documents `node --test`, default file patterns, child-process behavior, reporters, and experimental coverage via `--experimental-test-coverage`. | Kolm's JS test lanes should explicitly choose test patterns, reporters, coverage flags, concurrency, isolation env, timeout ceilings, and TAP/JSON artifacts instead of relying on implicit runner behavior. |
| Playwright best practices | `https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices` emphasizes resilient locators and trace viewer evidence for CI failures. | Browser QA should prefer role/text/test-id locators, retain traces for failures, capture DOM/network/action context, and avoid screenshot-only debugging. |
| OWASP WSTG | `https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/` provides versioned WSTG scenario IDs and warns that unversioned/latest references can drift. | Security tests must use versioned WSTG IDs and map every auth, tenant, injection, file, webhook, admin, account, and API abuse case to a stable scenario. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` auto-generates security posture scoring for open-source projects. | Release proof should include Scorecard-style supply-chain checks for branch protection, pinned dependencies, token permissions, vulnerability disclosure, and maintained status. |
| Google test sizes | `https://testing.googleblog.com/2010/12/test-sizes.html` argues test size should be policed by constraints such as network, DB, filesystem, order, and isolation, not just names. | Kolm must classify tests by enforced constraints: small means no IO/network/sleeps/shared state; medium/large/xlarge must declare exactly which boundary they cross. |
| StrykerJS mutation testing | `https://stryker-mutator.io/docs/stryker-js/introduction/` supports mutation testing for JavaScript, TypeScript, Node, and frontend stacks. | High-risk validators, policy decisions, billing, redaction, artifact verification, schema logic, and claim gates need mutation or mutation-equivalent assertion-strength proof. |
| GitHub Actions secure use | `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use` recommends least-privilege `GITHUB_TOKEN`, safe handling of untrusted input, CODEOWNERS, OIDC for cloud access, and dependency monitoring. | CI workflows must be audited as part of QA: permissions, secrets, OIDC, action pinning, untrusted input, CODEOWNERS, artifacts, environments, and required checks. |
| MDN performance budgets | `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Performance/Guides/Performance_budgets` frames budgets as limits to prevent regressions and ties metric choice to user experience and product goals. | UI/performance QA must include route-level budgets, changed-asset checks, Core Web Vitals or equivalent field/lab separation, and product-metric impact. |

Node `40` V2 target architecture:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.verification-os.v2",
  "catalogs": {
    "tests": "docs/verification/tests.json",
    "gates": "docs/verification/gates.json",
    "coverage": "docs/verification/coverage.json",
    "smoke": "docs/verification/smoke.json",
    "visual": "docs/verification/visual.json",
    "security": "docs/verification/security.json",
    "benchmarks": "docs/verification/benchmarks.json",
    "evidence": "reports/release-evidence/latest.json"
  },
  "required_record_per_change": {
    "changed_path": "source, public, docs, script, test, workflow, generated, or asset path",
    "owner_node": "master spec node",
    "risk_classes": ["auth", "tenant", "billing", "artifact", "ai_eval", "ui", "release"],
    "required_gates": ["unit", "contract", "integration", "smoke", "visual", "security"],
    "proof_artifacts": ["tap", "json", "screenshot", "trace", "coverage", "benchmark", "prod_smoke"],
    "manual_review": "required|optional|not_applicable",
    "release_blocking": true
  }
}
```

The V2 verification operating system adds these hard rules:

1. Every test, smoke probe, screenshot audit, benchmark, static audit, and manual checklist must have a manifest record with owner, scope, node, product surface, risk class, timeout, fixture policy, data isolation, and artifact output.
2. Every source file must have a source-to-test row. If a file is generated, the generator source, regeneration command, output freshness check, and diff check are the proof.
3. Every route must have positive, negative, auth, plan/entitlement, schema, docs/OpenAPI, local smoke, and production smoke coverage or an explicit closeout ticket.
4. Every account/public/docs page must have route render, dark/light, desktop/mobile, loading/empty/error, accessibility, content claim, and manual coherence proof if it is conversion-critical or post-auth critical.
5. Every public product claim must link to a test, benchmark, certification/package/partner proof, closeout gate, or removal decision.
6. Every AI-eval and benchmark must pin fixture, seed, scoring version, model/provider version, threshold, raw report, statistical method, and claim scope.
7. Every CI workflow must declare permissions, secrets, environments, matrix, artifacts, required checks, dependency update behavior, and whether it is release-blocking.
8. Every flake must become a record, not a chat note: first seen, lane, suspected cause, reproduction, owner, quarantine decision, retry policy, and release decision.
9. Every release must produce one evidence packet that can be inspected without hidden terminal history.
10. Every "done" claim must cite current evidence matching the current tree, not a stale report from a previous deploy or parallel bot run.

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-QA-V2-001` | `40.1` | Generate `docs/verification/tests.json`. | All 559 current test files plus future tests map to owner node, lane, source paths, route groups, products, risk classes, fixture policy, data isolation, timeout, and artifacts. |
| `W-QA-V2-002` | `40.1` | Generate source-to-test coverage map. | Every tracked source/public/docs/script/workflow/generated path has covered, partial, uncovered, generated, fixture-only, or archived status with proof. |
| `W-QA-V2-003` | `40.1` | Generate route-to-test map. | Every API route group has positive, negative, auth, entitlement, schema, docs, local smoke, and production smoke status. |
| `W-QA-V2-004` | `40.1` | Generate page-to-QA map. | Every public/account/docs route has theme, viewport, accessibility, interaction, loading/empty/error, screenshot, and manual review status. |
| `W-QA-V2-005` | `40.2` | Define gate taxonomy in `docs/verification/gates.json`. | Fast, changed-surface, full local, deep local, visual, accessibility, security, benchmark, package, production, and release gates have owner, command, env, timeout, artifacts, and release-blocking state. |
| `W-QA-V2-006` | `40.2` | Add manual QA evidence protocol. | Manual reviews have route list, task, reviewer, date, commit, screenshots, decision, findings, owner, and closeout ticket. |
| `W-QA-V2-007` | `40.2` | Add versioned WSTG security scenario map. | Auth, tenant, injection, file, webhook, admin/support, account, and API abuse tests cite versioned WSTG scenario IDs where applicable. |
| `W-QA-V2-008` | `40.2` | Add performance budget QA lane. | Critical routes have lab and field-oriented budgets for JS/CSS/image/media bytes, LCP/INP/CLS equivalents, API latency, and product-impact thresholds. |
| `W-QA-V2-009` | `40.3` | Enforce test size constraints. | Small/medium/large/xlarge lanes are classified by IO, network, DB, server, production, sleeps, external credentials, and runtime limits. |
| `W-QA-V2-010` | `40.3` | Add deterministic environment contract. | Test lanes pin timezone, HOME/data dirs, ports, random seeds, fake clocks where needed, local credentials, cleanup, and no accidental operator state. |
| `W-QA-V2-011` | `40.3` | Add flake ledger and no-rerun-to-green policy. | Reruns require a flake record with owner, cause, evidence, quarantine decision, release impact, and expiration. |
| `W-QA-V2-012` | `40.3` | Add parallelism and shard safety manifest. | Tests declare serial or parallel safety; shared-state and port-sensitive tests are isolated from parallel lanes. |
| `W-QA-V2-013` | `40.4` | Add coverage report policy. | Coverage is collected for selected risk areas with reviewed exclusions, source-path mapping, and no broad completion claim from percentage alone. |
| `W-QA-V2-014` | `40.4` | Add mutation or assertion-strength lane. | High-risk modules catch seeded mutants or synthetic logic faults for auth, billing, redaction, artifacts, schemas, policies, and claim gates. |
| `W-QA-V2-015` | `40.4` | Add fuzz/property/adversarial cases. | Parsers, schemas, imports, file paths, route params, provider envelopes, redaction, and artifact manifests have adversarial fixtures. |
| `W-QA-V2-016` | `40.5` | Build changed-file gate selector. | Given `git diff --name-only`, the tool emits required tests, smokes, screenshots, benchmarks, docs rebuilds, manual checks, and production probes. |
| `W-QA-V2-017` | `40.5` | Index evidence artifacts. | TAP, JSON, screenshots, traces, coverage, benchmark reports, prod smoke, local smoke, and manual notes have commit, date, command, env, owner node, and retention metadata. |
| `W-QA-V2-018` | `40.5` | Audit GitHub workflows against QA/security policy. | Workflows declare permissions, OIDC/secrets, action pins, untrusted-input handling, CODEOWNERS, artifacts, required checks, and gate IDs. |
| `W-QA-V2-019` | `40.6` | Generate release evidence packet. | One packet links release verify, local/deep/prod smoke, UI audit, screenshots, benchmarks, claims, generated docs, package gates, and manual QA for the exact commit. |
| `W-QA-V2-020` | `40.6` | Add QA debt dashboard. | Uncovered files, weak assertions, missing negative cases, flaky tests, slow tests, stale screenshots, missing prod probes, and unowned gates have owners and dates. |

Node `40` V2 relax redline:

- Do not call the codebase complete because `npm test` passes; completion requires source-to-test, route-to-test, page-to-QA, claim-to-evidence, gate-to-artifact, and release-packet proof.
- Do not let test count substitute for coverage of product promises, buyer journeys, account workflows, security boundaries, billing, artifacts, AI quality, or production deployability.
- Do not let a generated screenshot report stand in for manual UX coherence on conversion-critical or post-auth-critical flows.
- Do not let AI benchmarks support product claims without fixture provenance, seed, model/provider version, scoring version, statistical threshold, raw report, and claim-scope mapping.
- Do not let CI workflows be considered QA until permissions, secrets, OIDC, action pins, untrusted input, artifacts, environments, and required checks are audited.
- Do not let stale evidence prove a current change; every proof packet must match the current commit, changed files, environment, command, and generated artifacts.

#### Node 40 Verification Evidence Graph Addendum V3

Node `52` remains unused. This pass strengthens node `40`, because the active master spec tree already assigns test architecture, QA coverage, deterministic regression, visual proof, security scenarios, release gates, and production evidence to this node. The completion target is not more tests for their own sake. It is a current, queryable evidence graph that tells any implementation agent exactly what proves a change safe and tells the release owner exactly what cannot yet be claimed.

This V3 addendum is a spec-only edit. It does not change code, tests, workflows, generated assets, or product pages while other agents are active. It converts current evidence into a stricter implementation target for a build-finishing control plane.

Current repo delta checked in this pass:

| evidence area | current repo evidence | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Test inventory | The live `tests/` directory contains `557` `.test.js` files, including `535` `wave*` tests. | The V2 row that referenced `559` files is now stale. Node `40` needs a generated manifest so counts, owners, routes, products, risk classes, and evidence age update from the current tree instead of chat notes. |
| Script inventory | `package.json` currently exposes `57` scripts, including `32` `verify*` scripts, `2` test scripts, and `7` QA/UI scripts. | A gate taxonomy must be generated from package scripts and script metadata. Human memory cannot decide which gate proves a changed file safe. |
| Release verify | `scripts/release-verify.cjs` already runs static refs, OpenAPI sync, SDK manifest, full tests, SDK smoke, local surfaces, doctor, whoami, artifact verify, and billing tiers, with timeout, sharding, JSON, skip, and deep-surface support. | Keep it as the release driver, but require a machine-readable release evidence packet with gate IDs, command lines, env profile, commit, changed files, durations, failure reasons, artifacts, and production follow-up. |
| Surface smoke | `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs`, `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs`, and `docs/product-surfaces.json` provide local and production surface probes. | Every smoke probe must map to product surface, route group, auth state, plan state, schema, negative case, master node, required env, cleanup, and production parity. |
| Visual QA | `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/qa-screenshots.mjs`, screenshot scripts, and `reports/ui-surface-audit/` exist with many report and PNG artifacts. | The visual lane must become requirement-driven: route/theme/viewport/browser, interaction state, loading/empty/error state, accessibility, Core Web Vitals budget, manual coherence, and review disposition. |
| Static and claim gates | `scripts/audit-static-refs.cjs`, `scripts/audit-href.cjs`, `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs`, product journey/kernel audits, SEO/page-structure/render audits, and brand checks exist. | These gates need one policy catalog that names owner node, source file, generated output, route/page, claim, proof, failure severity, and whether a failure blocks release. |
| AI quality gates | Redaction, K-Score, quality calibration, benchmark evidence, frontier contracts, invention simulators, quantization, cloud broker, distill strategy, governance, compliance, federated, and package-release gates are wired into `verify:depth`. | These gates must expose raw report paths, fixture provenance, seeds, statistical thresholds, model/provider versions, artifact hashes, claim scope, and freshness windows. |
| CI workflows | The live `.github/workflows` directory includes `11` workflow files: `kolm.yml`, `kolmbench-submission.yml`, `kolm-ci-pipeline.yml`, `kolm-compile-on-push.yml`, `kolm-distill.yml`, `kolm-template.yml`, `lint.yml`, `sbom.yml`, `sdk-c-rust.yml`, `smoke.yml`, and `test-suite.yml`. | Workflows must be audited into the same evidence graph: permissions, secrets, OIDC, branch/environment rules, untrusted input handling, required checks, artifacts, action pinning, and owner node. |
| Evidence reports | `reports/full-test-latest.tap`, `reports/full-test-2026-05-22-backend-closeout.log`, live smoke artifacts, and many UI surface audit report directories exist. | Report files are useful only when indexed by commit, command, environment, owner node, changed files, route/page/surface coverage, age, and whether they prove local, preview, or production state. |

Current external baseline delta:

| source | current baseline | Kolm V3 rule |
|---|---|---|
| Node.js test runner, `https://nodejs.org/api/test.html` | Current Node docs cover test reporters, coverage events, mocks, timers, rerun features, tags, execution model, and the warning that reporter output is not guaranteed as a stable programmatic interface. | Kolm should parse stable test-runner events or controlled JSON/TAP artifacts, pin reporter choices, record coverage settings, and avoid brittle parsing of human text where a structured event stream is possible. |
| Playwright best practices, `https://playwright.dev/docs/best-practices` | Playwright recommends testing user-visible behavior and using trace viewer evidence for CI failures with timeline, DOM snapshots, and network request context. | Kolm visual/browser QA must not rely on screenshots alone. Critical page failures need trace/video/screenshot/console/network artifacts, user-visible assertions, and locator strategy. |
| OWASP WSTG, `https://owasp.org/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/` | WSTG has stable and versioned releases, with version `4.2` listed as a stable scenario reference. | Security QA must cite versioned scenario IDs for auth, tenant, injection, file, webhook, admin/support, account, API, and session tests. "Latest WSTG" is not stable enough for release evidence. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard, `https://github.com/ossf/scorecard` | Scorecard is an automated tool that scores software-security heuristics from `0` to `10`. | Kolm release QA should include Scorecard-style supply-chain evidence for branch protection, token permissions, dependency update posture, pinned actions, maintained status, and vulnerability handling. |
| GitHub Actions secure use, `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/security/secure-use` | GitHub recommends least-privilege credentials and OIDC for short-lived cloud credentials instead of long-lived secrets when cloud providers support it. | Workflow QA must fail or warn when permissions are broad, secrets are long-lived, cloud deploys do not use OIDC where available, or untrusted input can reach scripts. |
| Web Vitals, `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Core Web Vitals should be assessed at the 75th percentile segmented across mobile and desktop, and lab measurement does not replace field measurement. | Kolm UI QA must distinguish lab screenshot/browser checks from field or production RUM. LCP, INP, CLS, bundle/media budgets, and route-level buyer impact must be recorded separately. |
| WCAG 2.2 quick reference, `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/` | WCAG 2.2 includes Level AA criteria for non-text contrast, text resize/spacing, focus visible, consistent navigation/identification, target size, and more. | Visual QA must include accessibility proof for nav, buttons, focus rings, popouts, forms, charts, demos, videos, account tables, and docs pages; screenshot pass alone is insufficient. |

Node `40` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.verification-evidence-graph.v3",
  "owner_node": "40",
  "generated_at": "ISO-8601",
  "commit": "git-sha",
  "dirty_state": "clean | dirty_with_parallel_agent_edits",
  "inputs": {
    "tests": "tests/**/*.test.js",
    "scripts": "package.json scripts and scripts/*",
    "routes": "public/docs/api-routes.json",
    "surfaces": "docs/product-surfaces.json",
    "pages": "public/**/*.html",
    "workflows": ".github/workflows/*.yml",
    "reports": "reports/**/*"
  },
  "nodes": [
    {
      "node": "40",
      "requirement": "route-to-test coverage",
      "status": "covered | partial | uncovered | stale | external | not_applicable",
      "evidence": ["tests/example.test.js", "scripts/example.cjs", "reports/example.json"],
      "age_hours": 0,
      "release_blocking": true,
      "owner_lane": "backend | frontend | ml | security | docs | release | ops"
    }
  ],
  "changed_file_selection": {
    "changed_paths": ["src/router.js"],
    "required_gates": ["node --check", "focused tests", "local surface smoke", "OpenAPI sync"],
    "required_visual_routes": [],
    "manual_review": ["API contract review"],
    "production_smoke": ["prod surface smoke after deploy"]
  },
  "quality_axes": {
    "functional": "unit, contract, integration, smoke",
    "experience": "render, visual, accessibility, performance, manual use",
    "security": "WSTG, auth, tenant, secrets, supply chain",
    "ai_quality": "benchmarks, K-Score, redaction, calibration, claim scope",
    "release": "CI, artifacts, package, deploy, rollback, production smoke"
  }
}
```

Required generated artifacts:

| artifact | owner branch | contents | release rule |
|---|---|---|---|
| `docs/verification/tests.json` | `40.1` | Test file inventory, test IDs, tags, master nodes, source paths, route groups, products, fixtures, isolation, timeout, owner lane, negative cases, and artifacts. | Must update whenever tests are added, renamed, deleted, or moved. |
| `docs/verification/source-coverage.json` | `40.1` | Every tracked source, public, docs, script, workflow, generated, package, asset, and fixture file mapped to tests, gates, generated status, or explicit uncovered ticket. | No release-critical file may be unknown or uncovered without a closeout ticket. |
| `docs/verification/routes.json` | `40.1` | Every API route group mapped to positive, negative, auth, entitlement, schema, docs/OpenAPI, local smoke, prod smoke, and owner tests. | New routes fail release until route QA state is declared. |
| `docs/verification/pages.json` | `40.1` | Every public/account/docs page mapped to render, screenshot, theme, viewport, accessibility, performance, copy/claim, interaction, loading, empty, and error-state proof. | Conversion-critical and post-auth-critical pages require manual-use evidence. |
| `docs/verification/gates.json` | `40.2` | Package scripts and CI gates with command, lane, owner, env, timeout, expected artifacts, release-blocking state, and changed-file triggers. | A gate cannot block release unless it has a defined owner and artifact output. |
| `docs/verification/security-scenarios.json` | `40.2` | Versioned WSTG, supply-chain, auth, tenant, webhook, admin/support, upload, file/path, and API abuse scenarios. | Security signoff must cite scenario IDs and evidence paths. |
| `docs/verification/visual.json` | `40.4` | Route/theme/viewport/browser/component state, screenshots, traces, accessibility results, Core Web Vitals lab/field status, and manual review. | Screenshot counts alone cannot mark visual QA complete. |
| `reports/release-evidence/latest.json` | `40.6` | Exact commit, dirty state, commands, gate results, durations, artifacts, test counts, smoke counts, screenshots, benchmarks, claims, production follow-up, and reviewer decision. | "Deploy" and "done" claims must cite this packet plus production smoke after deploy. |

Change-impact selector requirements:

| changed file family | required proof |
|---|---|
| `src/router.js`, route modules, auth, billing, policy, provider adapters | `node --check`, focused route tests, API/OpenAPI regeneration check, product-surface verifier, local surface smoke, negative auth/tenant/schema tests, prod smoke after deploy. |
| `src/artifact*`, compile, distill, runtime, quantization, storage | Focused unit/contract tests, artifact verify, benchmark or fixture smoke, storage readiness if relevant, manifest/signature proof, release evidence update. |
| `public/*.html`, `public/account/*.html`, `public/docs/**/*.html`, CSS, nav, media | Static refs, href audit, claim scope, page structure/SEO when relevant, route render, dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots, accessibility checks, manual UX review for critical flows. |
| `docs/product-surfaces.json`, `src/product-experience.js`, generated API docs | Product-surface verifier, local/deep surface smoke, product graph build/check, OpenAPI/API ref build/check, account/CLI/TUI parity tests. |
| `package.json`, scripts, workflows, install/package files | Script syntax checks, gate taxonomy refresh, workflow security audit, package-release readiness, dependency/security posture check, release verify subset. |
| benchmark/eval/K-Score/redaction/quality fixtures | Fixture provenance check, deterministic seed, raw report hash, threshold test, claim-scope audit, calibration freshness check. |
| research/spec/blueprint docs | ASCII/trailing-whitespace/diff-check, heading/ledger search, source links, no product-code side effects, master-node ownership update. |

Visual, accessibility, and UI/UX QA matrix:

| axis | automated evidence | manual evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Render stability | Page loads, no console fatal, no broken refs, screenshots by route/theme/viewport. | Human checks first-screen clarity, hierarchy, spacing, visual cohesion, nav state, button affordance, and copy density. |
| Interaction | Keyboard tab order, click/tap primary controls, form validation, loading/empty/error states, popouts, modals, demos, video controls. | Human tries the workflow as a buyer/operator and records friction, confusing jargon, stale labels, or dead ends. |
| Accessibility | WCAG 2.2 AA checks for focus visible, labels, contrast, target size, consistent navigation, text resize/spacing, reduced motion, alt text. | Screen-reader and keyboard pass on critical routes, plus manual review for charts, code comparisons, demo/video content, and account tables. |
| Performance | Route asset budgets, image/video sizes, LCP/INP/CLS lab data, server/API latency for page dependencies. | Product-impact judgment: does the first screen feel fast and purposeful for an AI infrastructure buyer? |
| Content and claims | Claim-scope audit, SEO metadata check, stale-price/stale-brand scan, docs route parity. | Reviewer confirms hero, docs, pricing, enterprise, account, and demos explain all three product surfaces without jargon bloat. |
| Evidence retention | Screenshot/report index includes command, commit, environment, route, viewport, theme, timestamp, and reviewer. | Manual findings get closeout tickets, owner, screenshot links, and final disposition. |

Node `40` V3 Relax Redline:

- No "done", "deploy", "ship", "100 percent", or "best in world" claim may rely on a test count. It must cite current evidence matched to changed files, product surfaces, routes, pages, claims, environments, and production state.
- No stale generated count may stay in this blueprint without a generated current-state source or a dated note that it is historical evidence.
- No new route, page, job, CLI/TUI command, provider adapter, workflow, generated artifact, or product claim may ship without a verification graph row.
- No screenshot audit may substitute for manual UX coherence on conversion-critical, post-auth-critical, pricing, enterprise, docs, demo, or account workflows.
- No UI page may pass visual QA while failing accessibility basics: focus, keyboard, labels, target size, contrast, consistent nav, resize/spacing, reduced motion, and non-hover access.
- No browser failure may be triaged from screenshots alone when trace, DOM, network, console, or action evidence is available.
- No AI benchmark may support copy or sales claims without fixture provenance, seed, evaluator version, model/provider version, threshold, raw report hash, statistical method, and freshness.
- No CI workflow may be release-trusted without permissions, secrets, OIDC/cloud auth, action pinning, untrusted-input handling, artifacts, environment, and required-check evidence.
- No rerun-to-green may erase a failure; every flake or transient failure needs a record and release decision.
- No local green gate may be treated as production proof; production smoke and post-deploy UI/API/account checks remain separate evidence.

### Portability, Migration, Import/Export, Interoperability, And Vendor-Exit Proof Ledger V1

Node `41` turns "model-agnostic" and "owned AI" into a provable customer outcome. Kolm cannot credibly claim vendor independence if a customer cannot bring existing traffic/models/data in, move artifacts and evidence out, understand what conversion lost, and exit without secret lock-in. Existing nodes mention SDK migration, data export, artifact import/export, lineage, and account offboarding. Node `41` joins them into one portability contract.

The finished state is not merely that `kolm import` or `/v1/account/export` exists. The finished state is that every buyer can run a supported migration path from current stack to Kolm, can run a supported exit path from Kolm to standard formats, can see the exact loss/compatibility report, and can prove privacy, provenance, checksums, signatures, and deletion obligations throughout the handoff.

| Raw model import | `src/import.js`, `apps/import/gguf.py`, `apps/import/onnx.py`, `apps/import/safetensors.py`, `tests/wave740-import.test.js`, `/v1/import/inspect`, `/v1/import/wrap`, `public/docs/import.html` | Promote import into a compatibility matrix with file-size limits, parser versions, checksum policy, metadata coverage, unsupported fields, privacy class, and "not Kolm compiled" honesty labels. |

| Data and governance export | `/v1/account/export`, `/v1/lake/export`, `/v1/audit/export`, `/v1/audit/export/formats`, `/v1/audit/export/preview`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/export`, `/v1/registry/export`, `/v1/compliance/ai-act/export`, `src/audit-export.js`, `src/ai-act-export.js` | Add one account export center with data classes, included/excluded fields, checksums, retention/deletion scope, regulatory caveats, SIEM/CSV/JSONL formats, and customer-safe proof. |

| Research artifacts | `docs/research/trace-import-privacy-policy-2026-05-13.md`, `trace-import-manifest-purge-contract`, import checksum matrices, Langfuse importer fixture spec, competitor trace import wedge docs | Convert research into generated tests and customer-facing limits: privacy levels, purge propagation, importer manifest, checksum canonicalization, redaction-before-persist, and trace import eligibility. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Standard or ecosystem | Source | Implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| GDPR data portability | `https://www.dataprotection.ie/en/individuals/know-your-rights/right-data-portability-article-20-gdpr` | User/account export must be structured, commonly used, machine-readable, technically transferable where feasible, and scoped to rights/freedoms and lawful basis. |
| OCI Distribution Spec | `https://specs.opencontainers.org/distribution-spec/?v=v1.1.1` | Registry and artifact distribution should use digest-addressed manifests, content negotiation, push/pull semantics, and conformance vectors where Kolm artifacts are distributed through OCI-style registries. |
| ONNX | `https://github.com/onnx/onnx` | ONNX is the interoperability target for inference graphs; Kolm exports/imports need opset, shape/type, unsupported-operator, runtime, and hardware compatibility evidence. |
| GGUF on Hugging Face Hub | `https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/gguf` | GGUF is a local inference packaging format with tensors plus standardized metadata and common use in llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio, and GPT4All; Kolm must preserve and inspect metadata honestly. |
| safetensors | `https://huggingface.co/docs/safetensors/index` | safetensors is a safe, fast tensor storage format; imports/exports must distinguish tensor-only storage from full executable/runtime metadata. |
| OpenLineage | `https://github.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/blob/main/spec/OpenLineage.md` | Import/export, distill, compile, eval, and artifact publication should emit run/job/dataset lineage facets so migrated assets remain auditable. |
| W3C PROV | `https://www.w3.org/ns/prov/` | Provenance exports should map entities, activities, agents, derivations, usage, generation, and invalidation to interoperable semantics. |

#### Canonical Portability Envelope

Every import, export, migration, conversion, or vendor-exit operation must be representable as this logical envelope:

```json
{
  "operation_id": "port_...",
  "schema": "kolm.portability.v1",
  "direction": "import|export|round_trip|vendor_migration|vendor_exit",
  "source": {
    "kind": "openai|anthropic|openrouter|ollama|lmstudio|gguf|onnx|safetensors|kolm|account|lake|audit|registry|trace",
    "version": "source-version-or-null",
    "path_or_endpoint": "safe-path-or-endpoint",
    "checksum": "sha256-or-null"
  },
  "target": {
    "kind": "kolm|gguf|onnx|safetensors|oci|jsonl|csv|siem|openlineage|prov|runtime",
    "version": "target-version-or-null",
    "checksum": "sha256-or-null"
  },
  "tenant_id": "ten_... or null",
  "tenant_hash": "stable-safe-hash",
  "privacy_class": "public|tenant_metadata|pseudonymous|sensitive|regulated",
  "raw_payload_included": false,
  "secret_values_included": false,
  "not_kolm_compiled": true,
  "claims_allowed": ["inspect", "catalog", "sign", "run_with_limitations"],
  "claims_blocked": ["kolm_compiled", "kscore_validated", "production_ready"],
  "compatibility": {
    "status": "compatible|partial|unsupported|unknown",
    "supported_features": ["metadata", "weights", "tokenizer", "chat_template"],
    "unsupported_features": ["operator_x", "tool_calling", "structured_output"],
    "loss_report": ["field dropped", "operator not representable"],
    "runtime_targets": ["llama.cpp", "onnxruntime", "browser", "ios"]
  },
  "provenance": {
    "source_evidence": ["file", "manifest", "trace", "dataset", "job"],
    "lineage_ids": ["lineage_..."],
    "prov_mapping": "available|partial|none",
    "openlineage_mapping": "available|partial|none"
  },
  "verification": {
    "checksums_verified": true,
    "schema_validated": true,
    "round_trip_tested": false,
    "runtime_smoke": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "privacy_scan": "passed|failed|not_run"
  },
  "next_actions": [
    "run kolm distill to earn K-Score",
    "run export verify on target runtime"
  ]
}
```

The envelope's key rule is honesty: wrapping or migrating an outside model is useful, but it must never silently become a Kolm-compiled, K-Score-validated, production-ready artifact.

#### Portability Surface Matrix

| Surface | Inbound requirement | Outbound requirement | Required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| Account data | Import team/account setup where applicable; preserve tenant, role, billing, support, and data classes. | Export machine-readable account bundle with secrets stripped, portability scope, retention/deletion state, and checksum manifest. | `/v1/account/export` smoke, privacy scan, deletion/export UI screenshot, bundle schema test. |
| Capture lake and traces | Import traces/logs with privacy mode, consent basis, redaction-before-persist, manifest, and purge scope. | Export JSONL/CSV/JSON trace/lake bundles with tenant scope, redaction status, lineage, and SIEM compatibility. | Trace import policy tests, lake export tests, purge propagation, lineage export. |
| Model files | Inspect/wrap GGUF, safetensors, ONNX, and future formats without fake capability claims. | Export target runtime assets with loss report, checksums, manifest, and verifier command. | Parser fixtures, checksum matrix, import/wrap route tests, runtime smoke. |
| .kolm artifacts | Import previous .kolm versions and external manifests safely. | Export .kolm artifact, signed receipt, provenance, model card, SBOM, runtime payloads, and evidence packet. | Version migration tests, signature verify, provenance binder, registry export, artifact diff. |
| Provider APIs | Accept OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter/LiteLLM-compatible request shapes where promised. | Produce migration guides and compatibility shims that preserve errors, streaming, tools, structured output, and rate-limit behavior. | SDK smoke, route contract tests, error mapping tests, docs examples. |
| Runtime tools | Discover local Ollama/LM Studio libraries, Hugging Face GGUF assets, vLLM/ONNX Runtime targets, and device runtimes. | Push or export compatible runtime packages with unsupported-feature notes. | Discover dry run, wrap smoke, runtime load smoke, device fit report. |
| Enterprise governance | Import existing audit/log/SIEM/procurement evidence where safe. | Export audit logs, AI Act packet, model cards, security evidence, data residency, and SLO/SLA evidence. | Audit export tests, compliance packet, evidence bundle, admin screenshot. |
| Registry and marketplace | Import verified external artifacts/listings only after policy scans. | Export registry listings, manifests, public concepts, ratings, takedown state, publisher proof, and install evidence. | Registry export smoke, policy scan, publisher verification, provenance verification. |

#### Redlines

- No migrated or wrapped model may imply Kolm compilation, K-Score validation, benchmark performance, production readiness, or safety certification unless the corresponding Kolm pipeline and evidence ran.
- No import may persist raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, PHI/PII, trace payloads, or proprietary files without declared privacy class, consent/legal basis, retention, purge scope, and customer-visible warning.
- No export may include API keys, billing tokens, provider secrets, raw tenant data outside requested scope, cross-tenant rows, or hidden support/admin metadata.
- No conversion may hide lost fields, unsupported operators, missing tokenizer/chat template, quantization loss, runtime incompatibility, or target device limits.
- No "OpenAI-compatible", "Anthropic-compatible", or "OpenRouter-compatible" claim may ship without request, response, streaming, tool, error, retry, and SDK smoke coverage for the exact advertised path.
- No runtime export may be considered done until the target runtime loads or verifies it, or the page clearly says "manifest-only" or "not executable yet."
- No account offboarding may be complete until export, deletion request, retention caveats, billing closeout, support handoff, and audit trail are visible.
- No artifact registry export may lose signatures, digests, media types, version, publisher trust state, license, safety state, or dependency graph.
- No portability docs may present a vendor-exit path as one click if it needs manual data review, provider credentials, model license approval, or external tooling.
- No migration wizard may mutate source directories such as Ollama or LM Studio caches; discovery and wrapping must be read-only unless the user explicitly confirms an output path.
- No compatibility matrix may be static marketing copy; it must name last-tested versions, test commands, failure modes, and next retest date.
- No customer-facing claim may confuse "portable artifact", "portable model weights", "portable eval evidence", and "portable runtime executable."

#### Node 41 Relax Redline

Node `41` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- How does a customer migrate from OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter/LiteLLM or a local Ollama/LM Studio library into Kolm without rewriting everything?
- Which data, traces, artifacts, models, audit logs, registry listings, model cards, evals, and evidence can be exported from Kolm, in which formats, with which checksums?
- Which claims are allowed and blocked for a wrapped external model, migrated runtime asset, exported runtime file, or .kolm artifact?
- What feature loss, unsupported operator, missing metadata, runtime limit, privacy caveat, or legal/license restriction exists for each path?
- Can the import/export/migration be repeated with the same input and produce the same checksum, manifest, and compatibility report?
- Can account UI, docs, API, CLI/TUI, SDK examples, support bundles, and production smoke prove the same portability story?
- Can a customer leave Kolm with machine-readable data and evidence without receiving secrets, cross-tenant data, or misleading production-readiness claims?

The relax condition for node `41` is not "migration docs exist." It is: every advertised Kolm import, export, provider migration, runtime migration, artifact portability, and customer exit path is executable, honest, checksum-backed, privacy-safe, loss-reporting, supportable, and verified across API, account, CLI/TUI, docs, SDKs, runtimes, registry, and production smoke.

#### Node 41 Portability Contract Addendum V2

This addendum keeps portability attached to node `41`; it does not create node `52`. The current codebase already has meaningful migration and export primitives. The missing state-of-the-art layer is a single portability contract that prevents three different outcomes from being mixed together:

1. API traffic migration: a customer points existing OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, or SDK traffic at Kolm and keeps working.
2. Runtime/model migration: a customer inspects or wraps GGUF, safetensors, ONNX, Ollama, LM Studio, or future local assets without pretending those assets were compiled by Kolm.
3. Vendor exit: a customer exports account, lake, audit, registry, trace, artifact, model-card, provenance, and compliance evidence in a machine-readable, checksum-backed, privacy-safe bundle.

Current repo evidence checked in this pass:

| evidence | current state | completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/import.js` | Detects GGUF, safetensors, and ONNX by bytes/suffix clues; spawns stdlib Python parsers under `apps/import/`; returns honest `python3_missing`, `unknown_format`, and parser errors; wraps non-Kolm assets with `not_kolm_compiled: true`. | Keep the honesty invariant. Add a versioned compatibility matrix, parser-version field, file-size limits, metadata completeness score, license/source evidence, and runtime verification state. |
| `src/migrate.js` | Discovers Ollama and LM Studio model libraries, uses default path candidates plus env/path overrides, reads local metadata, reuses `src/import.js`, and preserves the `not_kolm_compiled:true` contract. | Add account and CLI migration UX with dry run, selected outputs, read-only guarantee, source path proof, model-size/digest display, loss report, rollback/cleanup, and next distill/compile action. |
| `src/export-provenance.js` | Loads exporter manifests or scans known export files, recomputes SHA-256, validates declared hashes, records backend/targets/options, and builds an export block bound into artifact hashes. | Add target-runtime compatibility status, unsupported operator/capability list, model-card/SBOM/provenance links, destination manifest, and runtime smoke command per target. |
| `src/audit-export.js` | Exports tenant-fenced audit events as CSV, CEF, LEEF, or JSONL with fixed formats, RFC 4180 CSV escaping, SIEM format escaping, row caps, preview caps, and defense-in-depth tenant filtering. | Treat audit export as one part of vendor exit, not the whole exit. It needs a unified export manifest, checksums, privacy class, retention/deletion state, and account export-center linkage. |
| `src/router.js` import/migrate routes | Exposes `/v1/import/inspect`, `/v1/import/wrap`, `/v1/migrate/discover`, and `/v1/migrate/wrap` with auth gates and honest failure envelopes. | Route docs and account UX must show "inspect/wrap" as limited portability, not compiled artifact proof. Production smoke should include one fixture import and one migration dry run. |
| `src/router.js` provider-compatible routes | Exposes OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, OpenRouter-compatible, capture, models, and connector surfaces with fixture mode and upstream-key separation. | Add provider migration scorecards for request/response, streaming, tools, JSON/structured output, error mapping, rate limits, auth headers, model listing, and SDK examples. |
| `src/router.js` export routes | Exposes `/v1/account/export`, `/v1/lake/export`, `/v1/audit/export`, `/v1/audit/export/formats`, `/v1/audit/export/preview`, `/v1/trace/:trace_id/export`, and `/v1/registry/export`. | Add one export center and one export-envelope schema so these routes share data classes, checksums, included/excluded fields, secret scan, tenant scope, and customer-safe proof. |
| portability tests | Present tests include `wave146-export-provenance`, `wave163-export-binder`, `wave188-migrate`, `wave409k-openai-compat-surface`, `wave426-import-seeds-tenant`, `wave739-lineage`, `wave740-import`, `wave743-migrate`, and `wave770-audit-export`. | Tests prove slices. Final node 41 needs a generated matrix proving every advertised import/export/migration/exit path across API, CLI/TUI, docs, account UI, route smoke, and production smoke. |

Current external baseline updates:

| baseline | current standard signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| GDPR Article 20 data portability | `https://www.dataprotection.ie/en/individuals/know-your-rights/right-data-portability-article-20-gdpr` states that applicable personal data should be provided in structured, commonly used, machine-readable form and transmitted where technically feasible. | Account and tenant exports must document scope, lawful basis, data classes, included/excluded fields, machine-readable formats, rights/freedoms limits, and technical transfer feasibility. |
| OCI Distribution Specification | `https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec` defines an API protocol for distributing content and is generic enough for content beyond images when manifests reference blobs. | If Kolm distributes artifacts through OCI-style registries, it needs media types, manifests, blobs, digests, annotations, conformance checks, and push/pull/delete behavior. |
| ONNX | `https://github.com/onnx/onnx` defines an extensible computation graph model, built-in operators, standard data types, versioning, shape/type inference, and opset conversion utilities for interoperability. | ONNX import/export must state opset, IR version, shape/type support, unsupported operators, runtime target, hardware target, and validation command. |
| Hugging Face GGUF | `https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/en/gguf` supports remote/local GGUF metadata and tensor info inspection. | GGUF migration must prove metadata/tensor inspection, tokenizer/chat-template availability where needed, quantization identity, and target runtime compatibility. |
| Hugging Face safetensors | `https://huggingface.co/docs/safetensors/en/index` describes safetensors as a safe, fast, zero-copy tensor storage format with tensor loading and metadata parsing. | Safetensors support must be described as weights/tensor storage unless Kolm also has architecture/config/tokenizer/runtime metadata and a verified loader path. |
| OpenLineage | `https://github.com/OpenLineage/OpenLineage/blob/main/spec/OpenLineage.md` defines run events, jobs, datasets, runs, and facets with JSON Schema/OpenAPI support. | Import/export/distill/compile/eval/registry events should emit run/job/dataset lineage, with Kolm custom facets for artifact, K-Score, privacy class, and portability claims. |
| W3C PROV-O | `https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-o/` models provenance chains using entities, activities, agents, derivation, attribution, and association. | Kolm provenance export should map source files, captures, datasets, teachers, jobs, artifacts, signatures, exports, and operators into interoperable entity/activity/agent semantics. |
| Model Cards | `https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993` frames released models as needing intended use, performance characteristics, evaluation procedures, caveats, and transparent reporting. | Exported or migrated artifacts need model-card status: generated, partial, missing, or not applicable, with intended-use and limitation fields separate from portability claims. |

Node `41` V2 target architecture:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.portability-contract.v2",
  "catalogs": {
    "formats": "docs/portability/formats.json",
    "providers": "docs/portability/providers.json",
    "runtime_targets": "docs/portability/runtimes.json",
    "exports": "docs/portability/exports.json",
    "compatibility": "docs/portability/compatibility.json",
    "loss_reports": "docs/portability/loss-reports.json",
    "evidence": "reports/portability/latest.json"
  },
  "required_record_per_path": {
    "path_id": "openai-drop-in|anthropic-drop-in|openrouter-drop-in|ollama-wrap|lmstudio-wrap|gguf-inspect|onnx-export|account-exit",
    "direction": "inbound|outbound|round_trip|vendor_exit",
    "claim_scope": "drop_in_api|wrapped_external|compiled_artifact|runtime_export|data_export|evidence_export",
    "claims_allowed": ["inspect", "wrap", "sign", "route", "export"],
    "claims_blocked": ["kolm_compiled_without_pipeline", "kscore_validated_without_eval", "production_ready_without_smoke"],
    "proof": ["checksum", "schema", "runtime_smoke", "privacy_scan", "docs", "account_ui", "cli", "prod_smoke"]
  }
}
```

The V2 portability contract adds these hard rules:

1. Every portability path must have an ID, owner node, source kind, target kind, supported versions, last-tested date, parser/exporter version, and proof command.
2. Every imported or migrated external model must carry a visible `not_kolm_compiled` or equivalent honesty state until the Kolm compile/distill/eval pipeline runs.
3. Every export must include a manifest, checksums, data classes, included fields, excluded fields, tenant scope, privacy class, retention/deletion caveats, and secret-scan result.
4. Every provider-compatible claim must list request, response, streaming, tools, JSON/structured output, error mapping, model list, rate limits, auth headers, and SDK smoke status.
5. Every runtime export must list target runtime, target version, operator/capability coverage, quantization/runtime caveats, unsupported features, and verification command.
6. Every "vendor exit" flow must show export, delete/request retention, billing closeout, support handoff, audit trail, and customer-safe evidence packet state.
7. Every portability docs page must distinguish portable API traffic, portable metadata, portable weights, portable runtime executable, portable eval evidence, and portable compliance evidence.
8. Every partial/unsupported path must surface next actions: install parser, provide source path, run distill, run eval, run export verify, contact support, or remove unsupported claim.
9. Every portability proof must be available through at least two surfaces: API plus docs, CLI plus account, or production smoke plus release evidence.
10. Every portability claim in marketing must link to the exact compatibility record and freshness date.
