Node `41` V2 relax redline:

- Do not call portability complete until every advertised path has a current compatibility record, proof command, loss report, privacy class, and claims-allowed/blocked state.
- Do not let wrapped external assets appear as compiled, distilled, K-Score validated, production-ready, or audited artifacts without the corresponding Kolm pipeline evidence.
- Do not let "OpenAI-compatible", "Anthropic-compatible", or "OpenRouter-compatible" appear without exact endpoint, request, response, streaming, tool, error, model-list, rate-limit, auth, and SDK evidence.
- Do not let export routes remain separate islands; account, lake, audit, trace, registry, artifact, billing, support, and compliance exports must converge into one vendor-exit manifest.
- Do not let model-file portability hide missing tokenizer, chat template, config, license, operator support, runtime target, quantization caveat, or hardware limit.
- Do not let docs imply one-click exit when export, deletion, retention, billing, support, or external tooling still requires explicit customer action.

#### Node 41 Portability Proof And Vendor-Exit Addendum V3

This V3 branch keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` remains unused because node `41` already owns portability, migration, import/export, interoperability, and vendor-exit proof. The new work is not another scanner. It is a proof architecture for using the product as a buyer would use it: bring traffic in, wrap or inspect existing model assets honestly, export runtime targets with loss reports, leave with data and evidence, and prove that no public copy overstates the result.

The current codebase already contains more portability machinery than the V2 ledger captured. The completion gap is that these pieces are not yet governed by one catalog, one account UX, one generated evidence graph, and one release/prod smoke story.

Current repo delta checked for V3:

| area | current implementation signal | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Import toolchain | `src/import.js` detects GGUF, safetensors, and ONNX; wraps external assets as partial manifests; stdlib parser scripts live under `apps/import/`; tests pin detection, error envelopes, and route behavior. | Generate a compatibility catalog with parser version, max file size, source hash, license/source fields, metadata completeness, tokenizer/config availability, runtime proof state, and exact claims allowed or blocked. |
| Migration toolchain | `src/migrate.js` discovers Ollama and LM Studio local libraries, preserves read-only behavior, reuses the import path, and keeps `not_kolm_compiled:true`. | Account and CLI migration must show dry-run discovery, source fingerprint, selected output, digest, size, media type, cleanup/rollback, loss report, and next compile/distill/eval action. |
| Export toolchain | `apps/export/` covers Core ML, ExecuTorch, GGUF, MLX, ONNX, safetensors, TensorRT, model cards, SBOM, registry, and AI Act docs; `src/export-provenance.js` validates export manifests and hashes. | Every target must state whether it is runtime-load verified, manifest-only, or unsupported; each export needs a loss report, target runtime version, unsupported feature list, model card/SBOM/provenance links, and verification command. |
| Export routes | Router evidence includes account, lake, audit, trace, registry, and AI Act export surfaces, plus audit format and preview routes. | These routes must converge into one vendor-exit packet with data classes, included/excluded fields, checksums, privacy class, secret scan, tenant scope, retention/deletion state, and customer-safe manifest. |
| Provider compatibility | Router and CLI surfaces cover OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, OpenRouter-compatible, model listing, capture, and upstream-key separation. | Drop-in claims require scorecards for request body, response shape, streaming, tools, structured output, error mapping, model listing, auth, rate limits, SDK examples, and fixture/prod smoke state. |
| Runtime loaders | Runtime evidence includes GGUF and ONNX runners plus export target scripts for mobile, edge, and accelerator targets. | "Runs anywhere" cannot be a slogan; each target must have a runtime load smoke, version pin, hardware target, unsupported operator list, quantization caveat, and fallback path. |
| Docs and research evidence | Public docs and research notes exist for import, migrate, LM Studio import, runtimes, canonical checksums, trace import, sandboxing, and provider route honesty. | A generated portability docs matrix must tie pages to exact route, CLI, account, test, screenshot, and production smoke evidence. |

External baseline delta for V3:

| source | baseline signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| GDPR Article 20 | The portability right centers on structured, commonly used, machine-readable data, with direct transmission where technically feasible. | Account export and vendor exit must state data scope, rights basis, excluded classes, transfer feasibility, and machine-readable package formats. |
| OCI Distribution Specification | OCI distribution defines content distribution through manifests, blobs, digests, and API behavior. | If Kolm uses OCI-style artifact distribution, .kolm media types, blobs, signatures, annotations, digests, referrers, push/pull/delete behavior, and conformance tests must be explicit. |
| ONNX versioning | ONNX interoperability depends on IR version, operator set version, breaking-change rules, and model graph signature/version semantics. | ONNX import/export must report IR version, opset imports, unsupported operators, shape/type support, graph input/output compatibility, and runtime checker output. |
| Hugging Face safetensors | Safetensors is safe and fast tensor storage, not by itself a complete executable model package. | Safetensors claims must distinguish tensor portability from full runtime portability unless architecture, config, tokenizer, chat template, and loader proof are present. |
| OpenLineage | OpenLineage models runs, jobs, datasets, and facets for interoperable lineage. | Kolm import, export, distill, compile, eval, registry, and lake actions should emit compatible lineage facets for source, artifact, K-Score, privacy class, and portability claim scope. |
| W3C PROV-O | PROV-O separates entities, activities, and agents for provenance chains. | Kolm provenance export should map source files, captures, teachers, jobs, artifacts, signatures, exports, and operators into entity/activity/agent semantics. |
| SPDX and CycloneDX | SPDX and CycloneDX are mainstream SBOM/specification ecosystems for supply-chain metadata and attestations. | Export packets need SBOM status, package/license/security metadata, model/dataset dependencies, and a clear mapping between artifact evidence and standard SBOM output. |

Node `41` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.portability-proof.v3",
  "path_id": "provider-drop-in|import-inspect|import-wrap|migration-dry-run|runtime-export|vendor-exit",
  "direction": "inbound|outbound|round_trip|exit",
  "surface_owners": ["api", "account", "cli", "tui", "docs", "sdk", "smoke"],
  "source": {
    "kind": "provider_api|gguf|safetensors|onnx|ollama|lmstudio|kolm_artifact|tenant_data",
    "version": "string",
    "hash": "sha256",
    "license": "string|null",
    "metadata_complete": "complete|partial|missing"
  },
  "target": {
    "kind": "kolm_manifest|kolm_artifact|runtime_binary|audit_export|account_exit_packet",
    "runtime": "none|gguf|onnx|coreml|mlx|executorch|tensorrt|browser|mobile|edge",
    "runtime_version": "string|null",
    "load_verified": true
  },
  "honesty": {
    "not_kolm_compiled": true,
    "kscore_validated": false,
    "production_ready": false,
    "claims_allowed": ["inspect", "wrap", "export", "route"],
    "claims_blocked": ["compiled", "distilled", "audited", "certified"]
  },
  "loss_report": {
    "dropped_fields": [],
    "unsupported_operators": [],
    "missing_runtime_assets": [],
    "quantization_caveats": [],
    "manual_steps": []
  },
  "vendor_exit": {
    "data_classes": [],
    "included_fields": [],
    "excluded_fields": [],
    "tenant_scope": "tenant_id",
    "secret_scan": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "retention_state": "documented",
    "deletion_state": "requested|not_requested|not_applicable",
    "billing_state": "active|closed|manual",
    "support_handoff": "none|bundle_created|ticket_created"
  },
  "proof": {
    "api_route": "string",
    "cli_command": "string",
    "account_path": "string",
    "docs_path": "string",
    "test_path": "string",
    "screenshot_report": "string",
    "production_smoke": "string",
    "freshness": "date"
  }
}
```

Account and operator UX requirements for V3:

| UX surface | required experience | failure mode to prevent |
|---|---|---|
| Account vendor-exit center | One dense operational flow for account, lake, audit, trace, registry, artifact, compliance, billing, deletion/retention, and support exports. Each row shows status, scope, size, format, checksum, secret scan, and next action. | Customers hunting through unrelated pages, or believing one audit CSV equals full vendor exit. |
| Import inspector | A pre-wrap view that shows detected format, parser version, source hash, metadata completeness, license/source status, missing tokenizer/config/template, and blocked claims. | Users mistaking "wrapped" for "compiled" or "production-ready." |
| Migration wizard | Dry-run first; no writes by default; every discovered Ollama/LM Studio asset has path, size, digest, media type, output choice, and rollback/cleanup instructions. | Silent local filesystem mutation or unclear source provenance. |
| Runtime export panel | Each target uses a stable row layout with status, runtime version, load proof, unsupported features, hardware caveats, loss report, and verify command. | A pretty export button hiding that the output is manifest-only or untested. |
| Provider drop-in panel | OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, and future provider paths each show request/response/streaming/tools/errors/auth/rate-limit/model-list coverage. | "Compatible" copy covering only the happy-path request body. |
| Support handoff | Partial imports/exports create bundles with manifest, checksums, parser logs, environment summary, privacy class, and no raw secrets. | Support tickets requiring customers to send private model files or secrets. |

| `W-PORT-V3-020` | `41.6` | Add round-trip and screenshot evidence. | Release evidence includes import/export round-trip checksums, account screenshots, CLI/TUI output, docs links, route smoke, and customer-safe support packet proof. |

Node `41` V3 relax redline:

- Do not call a wrapped external model compiled, distilled, K-Score validated, production-ready, audited, or certified until the Kolm pipeline evidence exists.
- Do not ship an export path without manifest, checksum, included/excluded fields, tenant scope, privacy class, secret scan, loss report, and customer-readable next actions.
- Do not claim provider compatibility without exact endpoint, SDK example, request/response coverage, streaming/tool/JSON/error/rate-limit/auth/model-list evidence, and fixture or production smoke.
- Do not claim runtime portability without either runtime load verification or a visible `manifest_only`/`not_verified` label.
- Do not claim vendor exit without account export, lake export, audit export, trace export, registry/artifact export, compliance export, deletion/retention state, billing state, support handoff, and audit trail.
- Do not let portability UI depend on hover-only details, hidden caveats, unlabeled icon controls, or long prose; the account experience must be dense, scannable, keyboard-accessible, and explicit about proof state.
- Do not let marketing, docs, account UI, CLI, API, or support copy imply one-click exit when manual customer, legal, external runtime, or provider work remains.

### Evaluation, Benchmark Governance, K-Score Calibration, And Model-Quality Evidence Ledger V1

Node `42` turns "better", "cheaper", "safer", "distilled", "production ready", and "K-Score validated" into measurable product contracts. Kolm has many evaluation pieces already: K-Score, frozen evals, redaction benchmark fixtures, quality calibration, holdout handling, benchmark evidence, MMLU/HumanEval/MT-Bench style harnesses, per-language scoring, multimodal distill evals, and public leaderboard routes. The gap is not that evaluation does not exist. The gap is that the evidence must be governed as one product-wide proof layer.

The finished state is not merely that a benchmark script passes. The finished state is that every public and account-facing quality claim can be traced to an evaluation envelope with dataset hash, artifact hash, evaluator version, model/provider/runtime versions, calibration pack, confidence interval, raw report hash, claim scope, freshness, and limitations. If that proof is absent, the claim must be blocked or visibly scoped.

#### Repo Evidence Already Present

| Evidence family | Current repo evidence | What node 42 demands next |
|---|---|---|
| K-Score core and explanations | `src/kscore.js`, `src/kscore-calibration.js`, `src/kscore-bench.js`, `src/kscore-per-language.js`, `public/k-score.html`, `public/k-score-explained.html`, `public/k-score-calibration.html`, `public/docs/k-score-methodology.html`, `public/research/k-score-whitepaper.html`, `tests/wave145-kscore-t-axis.test.js`, `tests/wave185-k-score-explained.test.js`, `tests/wave275-kscore-deep.test.js`, `tests/wave587-kscore-calibration-contract.test.js`, `tests/wave760-per-language-kscore.test.js`, `tests/wave810-kscore-calibration.test.js` | Make K-Score a versioned measurement product with axis definitions, weights, calibration-pack IDs, human-preference fit, confidence intervals, freshness, task family scope, and blocked-claim states when calibration is missing. |
| Benchmark evidence | `src/benchmark-evidence.js`, `src/benchmark-compare.js`, `src/benchmarks.js`, `src/kolmbench.js`, `scripts/bench-compare.mjs`, `scripts/bench-proof.mjs`, `scripts/benchmark-evidence.mjs`, `public/benchmarks.html`, `public/benchmark-results-v0.1.0.md`, `public/kolm-bench.json`, `public/bench/leaderboard.json`, `public/leaderboard.html`, `tests/wave144-bench-compare.test.js`, `tests/wave144-bench-proof.test.js`, `tests/wave589-benchmark-evidence-contract.test.js`, `tests/wave756-kolmbench.test.js`, `tests/wave758-bench-harnesses.test.js` | Promote benchmark evidence into a governed run registry with immutable raw reports, command lines, model versions, hardware profiles, pricing snapshots, evaluator versions, freshness, and replay proof. |
| Quality calibration | `src/quality-calibration.js`, `scripts/quality-calibration.mjs`, `scripts/bench-quality-calibration.mjs`, `docs/quality-calibration.md`, `public/benchmarks/quality-judge-calibration.json`, `tests/wave588-quality-calibration-contract.test.js` | Separate judge score, human label, rubric score, policy pass, and production acceptance so a judge-only score cannot be marketed as human-validated quality. |
| Redaction and privacy eval | `src/redaction-benchmark.js`, `src/phi-redactor.js`, `scripts/bench-redaction-fixtures.mjs`, `public/benchmarks/redaction-public-benchmark.json`, `test/fixtures/redaction-public-benchmark.json`, `tests/wave581-redaction-benchmark.test.js`, `tests/wave586-redaction-public-benchmark-contract.test.js` | Version redaction classes, fixtures, false-positive/false-negative examples, per-class precision/recall/F1, safety limits, and production data restrictions. |
| Holdouts and contamination protection | `src/external-holdout.js`, `src/tenant-holdout.js`, `src/dataset-workbench.js`, `/v1/eval/tenant_holdout`, `/v1/eval/tenant_holdout/:corpus_id`, `public/research/holdout.html`, `tests/wave411-dedupe-and-holdout.test.js`, `tests/wave411-p0-train-holdout-and-metadata.test.js`, `tests/wave537-eval-tenant-holdout-route-docs.test.js` | Build one contamination ledger that proves which rows were train, validation, test, private holdout, public fixture, synthetic, production shadow, or external calibration data. |
| Distillation and student quality | `src/distill-pipeline.js`, `src/distill-strategy.js`, `workers/distill/distill.mjs`, `scripts/distill-strategy.mjs`, `public/distill.html`, `public/account/distill-runs.html`, wave 707 through wave 722 distillation research and tests, plus multimodal wave 771 through wave 773 evidence | Tie every student promotion to teacher version, task taxonomy, capture snapshot, held-out eval, K-Score delta, failure-mode report, cost/latency delta, rollback threshold, and artifact signature. |
| External benchmark harnesses | `src/eval-mmlu.js`, `src/eval-humaneval.js`, `src/eval-mtbench.js`, `src/eval-numeric.js`, `/v1/bench/humaneval`, `/v1/numeric/eval`, `public/docs/evals.html`, `public/docs/cli/eval.html`, `public/docs/cli/bench.html`, `tests/wave345-eval-bench-parity.test.js`, `tests/wave407e-verify-eval-parity.test.js` | Add exact dataset/revision IDs, prompt templates, scoring scripts, model versions, judge versions, run seeds, and raw reports so parity is reproducible and not only a route contract. |
| Public routes and API contracts | `/v1/eval/benchmark-evidence`, `/v1/eval/benchmark-evidence/template`, `/v1/eval/benchmark-evidence/validate`, `/v1/eval/k-score-calibration`, `/v1/eval/quality-calibration`, `/v1/kolmbench/spec`, `/v1/kolmbench/validate`, `/v1/kolmbench/submit`, `/v1/kolmbench/leaderboard`, `/v1/privacy/redaction-benchmark`, `/v1/lang/kscore-by-lang/:namespace` | Return one consistent evaluation envelope and problem details shape across API, docs, CLI, account UI, and public benchmarks. |
| Claim-scope gates | `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/audit-sota-readiness.cjs`, `docs/product-sota-readiness.json`, `docs/product-readiness-closeout.md`, `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`, `tests/wave501-quickstart-capture-bench-route-honesty.test.js`, `tests/wave506-kscore-leaderboard-submission-honesty.test.js` | Make evaluation readiness the reason a claim is allowed, limited, or blocked, and surface the same result in marketing pages, docs, account, API, CLI, and release evidence. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Standard, paper, or framework | Source | Implication for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| HELM | `https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.09110` | A serious model-eval program measures multiple scenarios and metrics, publishes prompts/completions where allowed, and treats transparency as a first-class output. Kolm must not reduce K-Score to one opaque number. |
| MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena | `https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05685` | LLM-as-judge can approximate preference, but position, verbosity, self-enhancement, and reasoning biases must be measured and mitigated. Kolm judge scores need bias checks and human calibration. |
| OpenAI Evals | `https://github.com/openai/evals` | Customers need both public eval templates and private evals that represent their own workflow. Kolm should let teams bring private evals without exposing sensitive data publicly. |
| Inspect AI | `https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/` | Modern eval infrastructure includes datasets, solvers, scorers, logs, tool use, sandboxing, agent evals, and reusable task components. Kolm evals should be runnable, inspectable, and exportable. |
| MLCommons benchmarks | `https://mlcommons.org/benchmarks/` | Benchmark governance must support fair comparison, reproducibility, commercial and research use, affordability, working-group rules, model/data/version control, and hardware performance context. |
| NIST AI RMF and GenAI Profile | `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | Quality, safety, privacy, security, transparency, and governance evidence should map into recognized AI risk-management language, especially for enterprise and regulated buyers. |
| OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/` | Evaluation and production shadow scoring should use consistent GenAI trace, metric, exception, event, model, agent, and provider attributes, while honoring the current stability/opt-in state. |
| Model Cards | `https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993` | Every promoted artifact needs intended use, factors, metrics, evaluation data, training data summary, caveats, ethical considerations, and versioned evidence. |

#### Canonical Evaluation Envelope

Every benchmark, K-Score run, redaction test, quality-calibration run, distillation promotion, routing comparison, quantization result, runtime measurement, multimodal eval, language eval, or public leaderboard submission must be representable as this logical envelope:

```json
{
  "evaluation_id": "eval_...",
  "schema": "kolm.evaluation.v1",
  "evidence_class": "public_fixture|public_benchmark|private_holdout|synthetic_fixture|tenant_shadow|external_calibration|human_preference_pack|red_team_eval|runtime_perf|production_shadow",
  "task": {
    "family": "classification|extraction|generation|redaction|coding|reasoning|tool_use|rag|routing|distillation|quantization|runtime|multimodal|language|domain",
    "domain": "general|healthcare|finance|legal|support|engineering|custom",
    "modality": ["text"],
    "language": "en",
    "difficulty": "smoke|standard|hard|adversarial|production_like"
  },
  "dataset": {
    "id": "dataset_...",
    "version": "2026-05",
    "hash": "sha256:...",
    "split": "train|validation|test|holdout|shadow|calibration",
    "row_count": 0,
    "rights": "public|tenant_owned|licensed|synthetic|restricted",
    "privacy_class": "public|tenant_metadata|pseudonymous|sensitive|regulated",
    "contamination_check": "passed|failed|not_run"
  },
  "candidate": {
    "artifact_id": "art_...",
    "artifact_hash": "sha256:...",
    "model_provider": "openai|anthropic|openrouter|local|kolm|other",
    "model_id": "provider-model-version",
    "runtime": "gateway|worker|browser|ios|android|onnxruntime|llama.cpp|vllm|edge",
    "hardware_profile": "cpu|gpu|npu|mobile|browser|cloud"
  },
  "evaluator": {
    "kind": "exact_match|rubric|judge|human_preference|redaction|latency|cost|energy|safety|policy|hybrid",
    "version": "evaluator-version",
    "judge_model": "model-or-null",
    "rubric_hash": "sha256-or-null",
    "human_label_count": 0,
    "bias_checks": ["position", "verbosity", "self_enhancement", "domain_shift"]
  },
  "k_score": {
    "version": "v2",
    "axes": {
      "A": 0,
      "S": 0,
      "L": 0,
      "C": 0,
      "V": 0,
      "R": 0,
      "T": 0,
      "F": 0,
      "E": 0,
      "Z": 0
    },
    "weights": {
      "A": 0.40,
      "S": 0.15,
      "L": 0.15,
      "C": 0.15,
      "V": 0.15
    },
    "composite": 0,
    "gate": 0.85,
    "ships": false,
    "calibration_pack_id": "cal_...",
    "confidence_interval": [0, 0],
    "calibration_age_days": 0
  },
  "benchmark": {
    "run_id": "bench_...",
    "command": "command-used",
    "seed": 0,
    "code_version": "git-sha",
    "model_versions": ["exact-version"],
    "provider_pricing_snapshot": "price-snapshot-id",
    "hardware_profile": "profile-id",
    "raw_report_hash": "sha256:...",
    "generated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "expires_at": "2026-08-25T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "allowed": ["fixture_passed"],
    "blocked": ["human_preference_claim", "public_leaderboard_claim"],
    "limitations": ["synthetic_only", "not_certified", "small_fixture"]
  },
  "privacy": {
    "raw_prompts_included": false,
    "raw_outputs_included": false,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "cross_tenant_data_included": false
  },
  "next_actions": [
    "run public calibration pack",
    "collect human preference labels",
    "rerun production shadow eval"
  ]
}
```

The envelope's key rule is provenance before score. A score without dataset hash, artifact hash, evaluator version, calibration state, confidence, and claim scope is not a Kolm quality claim. It is only an internal observation.

#### Evidence Class Policy

| Evidence class | Can support | Cannot support alone | Required safeguards |
|---|---|---|---|
| Public fixture | Smoke, regression, examples, docs, reproducibility, onboarding. | Broad quality, domain superiority, production readiness, human preference, safety certification. | Frozen version, row hash, expected outputs, license/rights, raw report, freshness. |
| Public benchmark | Public comparisons, reproducible leaderboard, marketing snippets with context. | Tenant-specific accuracy, regulated compliance, private workflow quality. | Exact benchmark version, model/provider versions, pricing/hardware snapshot, anti-gaming, raw report. |
| Private tenant holdout | Customer-specific production acceptance, promotion, rollback, drift detection. | Public leaderboard claims or cross-customer claims. | Tenant isolation, no leakage to training, access policy, row hash, retention, export limits. |
| Synthetic fixture | Edge cases, rare cases, adversarial coverage, detector development. | Real-world prevalence or human preference claims. | Generator version, seed, review status, class balance, synthetic label. |
| External calibration pack | K-Score human-preference mapping, judge calibration, public credibility. | Production acceptance for a specific tenant unless task matches. | Quarterly freshness, enough human pairs, category coverage, held-out split, fit report. |
| Human preference pack | Preference-aligned ranking, judge calibration, score trust. | Safety certification, legal compliance, exact correctness. | Labeler qualifications, disagreement handling, blind/randomized order, bias checks, sample size. |
| Production shadow sample | Real workload drift, routing risk, latency/cost deltas, rollback thresholds. | Public claims unless anonymized and approved. | Privacy controls, tenant consent/scope, hashed payloads, sampling policy, raw data exclusion. |
| Red-team eval | Abuse, jailbreak, extraction, poisoning, safety regression. | General quality or domain success. | Threat model, attack class, severity, reproduction, mitigation status, retest. |
| Runtime performance eval | Latency, throughput, memory, cost, energy, device fit. | Answer quality or safety. | Hardware profile, concurrency, input/output sizes, warmup, seed, runtime version. |
| Multimodal/domain eval | Modality or domain-specific readiness. | Generalized text-only readiness or unrelated domain claims. | Modality metadata, domain expert review where needed, rights, privacy class, failure examples. |

#### K-Score Governance

K-Score should become a public measurement system, not a private slogan. The governance model must include:

- A versioned formula where each axis has a stable definition, valid range, direction, weight, default gate, and reason for inclusion.
- A calibration pack release train with monthly or quarterly packs, row counts, domain coverage, language coverage, human preference pairs, known exclusions, and deprecation dates.
- A fit report that maps K-Score to human preference or task success with confidence intervals, category residuals, outlier analysis, and drift against prior packs.
- A judge-bias report that checks position bias, verbosity bias, self-enhancement bias, model-family bias, language bias, domain bias, and rubric sensitivity.
- A claim-scope compiler that converts a K-Score result into allowed and blocked copy: example "fixture pass only", "public benchmarked", "tenant holdout passed", "human calibrated", or "production shadow promoted".
- A promotion gate that requires a minimum composite score, no P0 axis failures, confidence lower bound above threshold, fresh calibration, holdout pass, latency/cost guardrails, and rollback plan.
- A per-domain and per-language matrix so "K-Score 0.91" can never imply excellence outside the evaluated domain, language, modality, data class, and runtime.
- A reproducibility bundle with prompt template, scoring script, rubric hash, evaluator version, seed, artifact hash, data hash, model version, hardware profile, and raw report hash.

#### Benchmark And Leaderboard Governance

KolmBench and public K-Score leaderboards should be treated like a product surface with security, anti-gaming, and reproducibility rules:

- Submissions must include model/artifact identity, version, license, run command, raw report hash, evaluator version, dataset version, hardware profile, cost/latency counters, and whether the submitter is first party, verified publisher, customer, or anonymous.
- Leaderboard rows must show evidence class, benchmark date, freshness status, score confidence, task family, language/domain, runtime, hardware, and claim scope.
- A run cannot update a public leaderboard until validation checks dataset hash, no training split, no private row leakage, no missing report sections, no unsupported evaluator version, and no impossible cost/latency values.
- Re-runs must be linked to prior runs with diff explanation, model/provider version changes, calibration changes, and statistically meaningful movement.
- Public pages must explain how scores are computed, what they do not prove, and when a row is stale or superseded.
- Enterprise account pages must show the customer's private scores separately from public scores, with no cross-tenant aggregation unless anonymization and policy allow it.

#### Redlines

- No public quality claim may ship without an evaluation envelope and claim-scope decision.
- No K-Score may be shown without score version, axis weights, dataset hash, artifact hash, calibration pack, confidence interval, and freshness state.
- No K-Score may imply human preference unless the calibration pack includes enough human labels for that task family, language, and domain.
- No leaderboard row may publish without raw report hash, benchmark version, model/provider/runtime versions, hardware profile, pricing/cost snapshot where relevant, and anti-gaming validation.
- No benchmark comparison may mix provider versions, model snapshots, hardware classes, prompt templates, output budgets, or pricing snapshots without labeling the comparison invalid or non-comparable.
- No tenant holdout row may be used for training, synthetic generation, public leaderboard, support debugging, or cross-customer claims without explicit scope and privacy proof.
- No redaction benchmark may claim coverage beyond the exact classes, languages, fixture types, document types, and false-positive/false-negative behavior tested.
- No judge-only score may be treated as ground truth without human calibration, bias checks, disagreement analysis, and documented failure modes.
- No distillation promotion may pass on synthetic fixtures alone; it needs held-out task data, teacher comparison, cost/latency delta, failure-mode review, and rollback threshold.
- No runtime performance claim may omit input/output token sizes, concurrency, warm/cold state, memory ceiling, hardware profile, runtime version, and variance.
- No eval page may hide "not certified", "not public benchmarked", "needs package release", "needs external calibration", or "private holdout only" states.
- No raw prompts, raw outputs, PHI/PII, secrets, or cross-tenant examples may appear in public reports unless deliberately sanitized and approved.
- No stale public benchmark may remain promoted after its freshness window expires without an explicit stale badge and retest ticket.
- No "best", "state of the art", "production ready", "auditable", "safe", or "certified" phrase may appear in public copy unless the claim-scope gate allows that exact phrase.

| `42.1` Eval taxonomy, task suites, public/private evidence classes, data lineage, and claim scope | Every eval is classified by task family, modality, domain, language, evidence class, data rights, privacy class, contamination status, and allowed/blocked claim scope. |
| `42.2` K-Score methodology, axis weights, calibration packs, confidence intervals, and human-preference mapping | K-Score has versioned formula docs, machine-readable axis metadata, calibration-pack releases, human-preference fit reports, confidence intervals, and freshness gates. |
| `42.3` Redaction, quality-judge, distillation, routing, quantization, runtime, multimodal, language, and domain evals | Each product capability has a named eval suite, fixtures, thresholds, failure-mode output, positive/negative tests, API/CLI/account surface, and claim mapping. |
| `42.4` Benchmark execution, provider/model versions, hardware profiles, seeds, raw reports, anti-gaming, and leaderboard governance | Benchmark runs are reproducible from raw report bundles, include exact versions and hardware/pricing snapshots, validate submissions, prevent gaming, and show freshness/confidence. |
| `42.5` Account/docs/API/CLI UX for eval readiness, score explanation, failure modes, promotions, rollback, and limitations | Users can see score explanation, axis breakdowns, evidence class, limitations, failure examples, promotion/rollback state, and next actions consistently across account, docs, API, CLI, and public pages. |
| `42.6` Evidence: frozen fixtures, public reports, calibration deltas, holdout protection, leaderboard audits, production eval smoke, and claim-gate proof | Release evidence links frozen fixtures, public reports, calibration deltas, holdout checks, leaderboard audits, production shadow smokes, and claim-scope gate output for every advertised quality claim. |

| `W-EVAL-019` | `42.6` | Add production eval smoke. | Production smoke runs safe fixtures through K-Score, redaction benchmark, benchmark-evidence validation, leaderboard validation, and private holdout checks without leaking data. |

#### Node 42 Relax Redline

Node `42` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Which exact eval proves this quality, distillation, routing, redaction, quantization, runtime, multimodal, language, domain, or production-readiness claim?
- Which dataset, split, row count, hash, rights class, privacy class, contamination check, prompt template, evaluator, seed, model version, runtime, and hardware profile were used?
- What K-Score axis values, weights, composite, confidence interval, calibration pack, calibration age, and gate decision apply?
- Which claims are allowed and blocked by this score, and does public/account/docs/API/CLI copy match that decision?
- Is the evidence public, private tenant holdout, synthetic, external calibration, human preference, red-team, runtime performance, or production shadow, and what can it support?
- Can the benchmark be replayed from raw report, command, seed, code version, provider/model version, pricing snapshot, and hardware profile?
- What did the model fail, which examples show the failure, what changed since the last run, and what rollback threshold protects production?
- Can a customer or auditor inspect the evidence packet without seeing secrets, raw tenant prompts, PHI/PII, or cross-tenant data?

The relax condition for node `42` is not "K-Score exists" or "benchmarks pass." It is: every Kolm quality, cost, safety, redaction, distillation, routing, quantization, runtime, multimodal, language, benchmark, leaderboard, and production-readiness claim is backed by a reproducible evaluation envelope, calibrated score, raw evidence, confidence, privacy-safe report, limitation label, and claim-scope gate across API, account, docs, CLI/TUI, public pages, release evidence, and production smoke.

#### Node 42 Evaluation Evidence And Claim Calibration Addendum V2

Node `52` stays unused here. The work belongs under node `42` because this is not a new product branch; it is the proof spine for every claim that says Kolm is better, cheaper, safer, smaller, faster, more portable, more accurate, more auditable, or more production ready.

The V2 closeout tightens node `42` from "we have eval files and benchmark pages" into "every public, account, API, CLI, docs, sales, and release claim compiles through a current evaluation envelope." That envelope must carry the dataset hash, artifact hash, evaluator version, calibration pack, raw report hash, model/provider/runtime version, hardware profile, cost snapshot, confidence interval, limitations, privacy class, and allowed/blocked claim text.

Marketing and account UI cannot bypass it. |

##### External Baseline Added For V2

| Baseline | Source | What V2 imports into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| HELM | `https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.09110` | Evaluation must be multi-scenario and multi-metric. Accuracy alone is not enough; calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency, and missing coverage must be visible. |
| OpenAI Evals | `https://github.com/openai/evals` | Kolm must support public eval templates and private customer evals that represent real workflows without exposing private data publicly. |
| Inspect AI | `https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/` | Eval infrastructure should be task-based and composable, with datasets, solvers, scorers, logs, tool use, agents, sandboxing, and reusable task components. |
| MLCommons benchmarks | `https://mlcommons.org/benchmarks/` | Benchmark programs need governance, reproducibility, comparable submissions, hardware context, and rules for fair commercial and research use. |
| NIST AI RMF and NIST AI 600-1 | `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` and `https://www.nist.gov/publications/artificial-intelligence-risk-management-framework-generative-artificial-intelligence` | Quality evidence must map into recognized risk-management language: valid, reliable, safe, secure, resilient, accountable, transparent, explainable, privacy enhanced, and fair. |
| MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena | `https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05685` | LLM-as-judge is useful but biased. Kolm judge scores need position-bias, verbosity-bias, self-enhancement, model-family, and domain-shift checks. |
| Model Cards | `https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993` | Promoted artifacts must expose intended use, factors, metrics, evaluation data, training data summary, ethical considerations, caveats, and versioned evidence. |
| Hugging Face Evaluate and LightEval | `https://huggingface.co/docs/evaluate/index` and `https://huggingface.co/docs/lighteval/index` | Evaluation UX should support reusable metrics/tasks, custom metrics, multiple backends, sample-level results, reproducible bundles, and hub or object-store publication. |

##### V2 Master Evaluation Contract

Every quality-bearing output must be reducible to this product-level contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.evaluation-spine.v2",
  "claim_id": "claim_...",
  "claim_text": "plain-language claim being allowed or blocked",
  "claim_surface": "homepage|pricing|docs|account|api|cli|tui|sales|release",
  "evidence_class": "public_fixture|public_benchmark|private_holdout|synthetic_fixture|external_calibration|human_preference|red_team|runtime_perf|production_shadow",
  "task": {
    "family": "classification|extraction|generation|redaction|coding|reasoning|tool_use|rag|routing|distillation|quantization|runtime|multimodal|language|domain",
    "domain": "general|healthcare|finance|legal|support|engineering|custom",
    "modality": ["text"],
    "language": "en",
    "difficulty": "smoke|standard|hard|adversarial|production_like"
  },
  "data": {
    "dataset_id": "dataset_...",
    "dataset_version": "2026-05",
    "dataset_hash": "sha256:...",
    "split": "train|validation|test|holdout|shadow|calibration",
    "row_count": 0,
    "rights": "public|tenant_owned|licensed|synthetic|restricted",
    "privacy_class": "public|tenant_metadata|pseudonymous|sensitive|regulated",
    "contamination_check": "passed|failed|not_run"
  },
  "candidate": {
    "artifact_id": "art_...",
    "artifact_hash": "sha256:...",
    "teacher_model": "provider/model/version-or-null",
    "student_model": "provider/model/version-or-null",
    "runtime": "gateway|worker|browser|ios|android|onnxruntime|llama.cpp|vllm|edge",
    "hardware_profile": "profile-id"
  },
  "evaluator": {
    "kind": "exact_match|rubric|judge|human_preference|redaction|latency|cost|energy|safety|policy|hybrid",
    "version": "evaluator-version",
    "judge_model": "model-or-null",
    "rubric_hash": "sha256-or-null",
    "human_label_count": 0,
    "bias_checks": ["position", "verbosity", "self_enhancement", "model_family", "language", "domain_shift"]
  },
  "score": {
    "k_score_version": "v2",
    "axes": {
      "A": 0,
      "S": 0,
      "L": 0,
      "C": 0,
      "V": 0,
      "R": 0,
      "T": 0,
      "F": 0,
      "E": 0,
      "Z": 0
    },
    "weights": {
      "A": 0.40,
      "S": 0.15,
      "L": 0.15,
      "C": 0.15,
      "V": 0.15
    },
    "composite": 0,
    "confidence_interval": [0, 0],
    "calibration_pack_id": "cal_...",
    "calibration_age_days": 0,
    "promotion_gate": "pass|fail|manual_review"
  },
  "run": {
    "run_id": "bench_...",
    "command": "exact command",
    "seed": 0,
    "code_version": "git-sha",
    "model_versions": ["exact-version"],
    "runtime_versions": ["exact-version"],
    "pricing_snapshot_id": "price_...",
    "raw_report_hash": "sha256:...",
    "generated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
    "expires_at": "2026-08-25T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "decision": "allowed|scoped|stale|blocked",
    "allowed_phrases": [],
    "blocked_phrases": [],
    "limitations": [],
    "next_actions": []
  },
  "privacy": {
    "raw_prompts_included": false,
    "raw_outputs_included": false,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "cross_tenant_data_included": false
  }
}
```

The contract's controlling rule is: provenance before score. A score without dataset identity, artifact identity, evaluator identity, calibration state, freshness, privacy state, and claim scope is not evidence. It is telemetry.

##### V2 Claim Calibration Rules

- Every quality, cost, safety, speed, accuracy, redaction, distillation, routing, quantization, portability, device-fit, or "production ready" claim must have a `claim_id` and an evaluation envelope.
- K-Score cannot be shown as a naked number. It must show axis version, weights, calibration pack, confidence interval, task family, data class, freshness, and limitation labels.
- Judge-backed scores cannot imply human preference unless a current human calibration pack covers that domain, language, task, and output style.
- Benchmark comparisons are invalid unless model versions, provider settings, runtime versions, prompt templates, output budgets, pricing snapshots, hardware profiles, and dates are comparable or explicitly labeled non-comparable.
- Public fixtures can support smoke and regression claims, but cannot support broad public superiority, human-preference, production-readiness, or regulated-compliance claims alone.
- Tenant holdouts can support tenant-specific promotion and rollback, but cannot support public leaderboard or cross-customer claims.
- Synthetic fixtures can cover rare and adversarial cases, but cannot prove prevalence or production quality alone.
- Production shadow evals must exclude raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, PHI, PII, and cross-tenant examples from public artifacts by default.
- Redaction benchmarks must report per-class precision, recall, F1, fixture count, language, document type, false positives, false negatives, and known unsupported classes.
- Student promotion must compare student against teacher and baseline on quality, latency, cost, memory, failure modes, refusal behavior, security, and rollback threshold.
- Public leaderboards need freshness, submission policy, anti-gaming validation, raw report hash, reproducibility bundle, and stale-row treatment.
- Release evidence must include the claim-scope gate output so copy and UI can be audited against the exact phrases allowed by evidence.

##### V2 Implementation Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-EVAL-V2-001` | `42.1` | Build the evaluation envelope schema and validator. | Every eval-producing module can emit or validate `kolm.evaluation-spine.v2`, and missing dataset/artifact/evaluator/calibration/claim fields fail fast. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-002` | `42.1` | Generate the claim inventory. | Public pages, account pages, docs, API descriptions, CLI help, sales pages, and release notes produce a machine-readable list of evidence-bearing claims. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-003` | `42.1` | Link claims to envelopes. | Each claim either links to a valid envelope, is explicitly scoped as aspirational/internal, or is blocked from shipping. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-004` | `42.1` | Create the split and contamination ledger. | Train, validation, test, holdout, synthetic, public fixture, calibration, and shadow rows have immutable IDs, hashes, rights, privacy class, and leakage status. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-005` | `42.2` | Version the K-Score axis registry. | Axis formulas, weights, ranges, examples, gates, deprecations, and migration notes are public, machine-readable, and covered by tests. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-006` | `42.2` | Add calibration-pack lifecycle. | Calibration packs have release date, expiry, row counts, domains, languages, human labels, fit report, bias report, checksum, and publication policy. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-007` | `42.2` | Add confidence and lower-bound gates. | K-Score promotion uses uncertainty and lower confidence bound, not only mean score. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-008` | `42.2` | Add judge-bias and disagreement report. | Position, verbosity, self-enhancement, model-family, language, domain, rubric, and labeler disagreement checks run for judge-backed scores. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-009` | `42.3` | Expand redaction benchmark governance. | Redaction reports include per-class metrics, false examples, fixture rights, unsupported classes, and public/private claim scope. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-010` | `42.3` | Add distillation promotion envelope. | Distill runs cannot promote without teacher version, capture snapshot, holdout result, K-Score delta, cost/latency delta, failure-mode diff, and rollback threshold. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-011` | `42.3` | Add routing and fallback eval envelopes. | Adaptive routing changes prove quality, confidence, fallback, refusal, provider outage, budget, latency, and escalation behavior. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-012` | `42.3` | Add quantization/runtime evaluation envelopes. | Quantized and edge artifacts report quality delta, memory, throughput, latency, energy, device compatibility, unsupported ops, and runtime version. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-013` | `42.3` | Add multimodal/language/domain eval envelopes. | VLM, audio, video, cross-lingual, per-language, and vertical packs carry modality, language, rights, privacy, thresholds, and failure examples. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-014` | `42.4` | Create benchmark run registry. | Runs store replay command, seed, model/provider/runtime versions, hardware profile, cost snapshot, raw report hash, date, expiry, and comparable-run group. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-015` | `42.4` | Harden leaderboard submission policy. | Public submissions validate dataset hash, row schema, model identity, artifact signature, anti-gaming checks, freshness, and claim scope before publication. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-016` | `42.4` | Add score-diff and significance tooling. | Re-runs explain score movement, confidence, version changes, calibration changes, and whether the movement is meaningful. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-017` | `42.5` | Build score explanation UX contract. | Public/account/API/CLI surfaces show axis breakdown, evidence class, calibration age, confidence, limitations, failure examples, and next actions in the same terms. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-018` | `42.5` | Add private-eval safe mode. | Customers can run private evals and holdouts without raw data entering public reports, cross-tenant stores, or training flows. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-019` | `42.6` | Wire evaluation into release evidence. | Release packets include eval envelope summaries, claim-scope output, redaction benchmark, K-Score calibration, benchmark registry, and production smoke status. |
| `W-EVAL-V2-020` | `42.6` | Add production claim smoke. | Production smoke verifies safe fixtures across K-Score, redaction, benchmark validation, leaderboard validation, private holdout metadata, and claim gate without leaking secrets. |

##### Node 42 V2 Relax Redline

Node `42` V2 is relaxed only when a reviewer can pick any visible product claim and answer all of these from current artifacts:

- What envelope allows this claim?
- What exact dataset, split, hash, rights class, privacy class, row count, and contamination result were used?
- What artifact, teacher, student, provider, runtime, hardware, code version, and pricing snapshot were used?
- What evaluator, rubric, judge model, human label count, bias checks, and calibration pack were used?
- What K-Score axes, weights, confidence interval, lower-bound gate, and freshness status apply?
- What is the raw report hash and replay command?
- What are the allowed phrases, blocked phrases, limitations, and stale-date?
- What false positives, false negatives, drift, regressions, or failure examples matter?
- Can account, docs, API, CLI/TUI, marketing, and release evidence show the same scope without exposing raw tenant data or secrets?

If any answer is missing, the claim cannot be treated as fully shipped even if the underlying feature works.

#### Node 42 Evaluation Standard, K-Score Public Calibration, And Claim Compiler Addendum V3

This V3 branch keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` stays unused because node `42` already owns evaluation, benchmark governance, K-Score calibration, model-quality evidence, and the proof layer behind every quality-bearing product claim. V2 defined the envelope. V3 raises the bar from "evaluation exists" to "Kolm defines a serious measurement standard that implementation, product, sales, support, and customers can trust."

The current codebase now shows evaluation work as a real backend product surface: package scripts include `verify:kscore-calibration`, `verify:redaction-benchmark`, `verify:quality-calibration`, `verify:benchmark-evidence`, and those gates are wired into `verify:depth`; router routes expose K-Score calibration, benchmark evidence template/validation, quality calibration, redaction benchmark, tenant holdout, KolmBench, and leaderboard surfaces; source modules cover K-Score, benchmark comparison/evidence, quality calibration, redaction benchmarks, MMLU/HumanEval/MT-Bench/numeric evals, cross-lingual evals, and holdout controls. The gap is no longer "add evals." The gap is standardization, calibration, statistical defensibility, UX clarity, and copy enforcement.

Current repo delta checked for V3:

| area | current implementation signal | V3 completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| K-Score product surface | `src/kscore.js`, `src/kscore-calibration.js`, `src/kscore-bench.js`, `src/kscore-per-language.js`, `scripts/recalibrate-kscore.cjs`, K-Score public pages, methodology pages, calibration routes, and multiple wave tests exist. | Turn K-Score into a public standard with a stable formula spec, open calculator, versioned axis registry, calibration packs, human-preference fit reports, confidence intervals, and copy-safe limitation labels. |
| Benchmark evidence control | `src/benchmark-evidence.js`, `src/benchmark-compare.js`, `src/benchmarks.js`, `src/kolmbench.js`, `scripts/benchmark-evidence.mjs`, `scripts/bench-proof.mjs`, and public benchmark/leaderboard artifacts exist. | Add an immutable benchmark run registry with replay command, raw report hash, model/provider/runtime versions, hardware profile, cost/pricing snapshot, comparable-run group, confidence method, and anti-gaming status. |
| Quality judge calibration | `src/quality-calibration.js`, `scripts/bench-quality-calibration.mjs`, `public/benchmarks/quality-judge-calibration.json`, and contract tests exist. | Separate judge score, rubric score, human preference, exact correctness, policy safety, and promotion acceptance; require bias/disagreement reports before judge-backed scores can drive public claims. |
| Redaction evaluation | `src/redaction-benchmark.js`, `scripts/bench-redaction-fixtures.mjs`, public redaction fixtures, and tests exist. | Require per-class precision/recall/F1, false-positive/false-negative examples, language/document-type scope, unsupported-class list, and production-data restrictions before safety copy can broaden. |
| Holdout and split controls | `src/external-holdout.js`, `src/tenant-holdout.js`, `src/dataset-workbench.js`, tenant-holdout routes, and dedupe/holdout tests exist. | Build a contamination and split ledger that proves train, validation, test, private holdout, public fixture, synthetic, production shadow, and external calibration rows are never mixed silently. |
| External harnesses | `src/eval-mmlu.js`, `src/eval-humaneval.js`, `src/eval-mtbench.js`, `src/eval-numeric.js`, public eval docs, and parity tests exist. | Add dataset revision IDs, prompt template hashes, scorer versions, seed, raw report, evaluator limits, and honest `runtime_not_wired` or `fixture_only` states where harnesses are not fully live. |
| Distillation quality | Distillation modules, W707-W722 style research/tests, and account distill surfaces exist. | Every student promotion must require teacher identity, capture snapshot, holdout score, K-Score delta, failure-mode diff, cost/latency/memory delta, confidence bound, rollback threshold, and artifact signature. |
| Claim gates | `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, readiness closeout ledgers, route honesty tests, and benchmark/K-Score honesty tests exist. | Make a score-to-copy compiler that returns allowed phrases, scoped phrases, stale phrases, and blocked phrases for each claim surface. |

External baseline delta for V3:

| source | current signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI Evals | The Evals framework supports public eval templates and private evals for workflow-specific patterns without exposing private data publicly. | Kolm must support customer-private evals as first-class evidence without leaking prompts, outputs, secrets, PHI, PII, or cross-tenant data into public reports. |
| Inspect AI | Inspect supports datasets, solvers, scorers, tools, agents, sandboxing, eval sets, logs, cost/token limits, tracing, caching, and parallelism. | Kolm evals must support agent/tool-use and code-completion workflows with logs, sandbox policy, limits, replay, and failure recovery, not only scalar benchmark scripts. |
| HELM | HELM is a holistic transparency program across scenarios and metrics, including multimodal expansions. | K-Score cannot collapse all product proof into one opaque number; it needs scenario, metric, limitation, raw-output, and missing-coverage transparency where privacy permits. |
| MLCommons benchmarks | MLCommons emphasizes fair comparison, reproducibility, commercial/research usefulness, affordability, working-group governance, model/data rules, and hardware performance context. | KolmBench must publish rules, comparable-run groups, hardware context, submission classes, anti-gaming checks, and repeatable run costs. |
| NIST AI RMF and Generative AI Profile | NIST frames AI risk management around trustworthy design, development, use, evaluation, and generative-AI-specific risk actions. | Evaluation reports should map outcomes to valid/reliable, safe, secure/resilient, accountable/transparent, explainable/interpretable, privacy-enhanced, and fair risk categories. |
| Hugging Face Evaluate | Evaluate treats metrics as reusable, documented objects with limitations and supports consistent/reproducible evaluation across local/distributed setups. | Kolm metric definitions must include limitation cards, task fit, input schema, output schema, and reproducibility bundle. |
| Hugging Face LightEval | LightEval supports sample-level results and multiple inference backends including Inspect, transformers, vLLM, SGLang, LiteLLM, TGI, endpoints, and custom backends. | Kolm should store sample-level eval results where privacy allows and normalize provider/local/runtime backend identity in each envelope. |
| MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena | LLM-as-judge can approximate human preference but has position, verbosity, reasoning, and other biases that must be examined. | Kolm judge-backed K-Score axes must carry bias checks, human agreement estimates, pairwise-randomization policy, and domain/language coverage. |

Node `42` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.evaluation-standard.v3",

    }

    "evidence_class": "public_fixture|public_benchmark|private_holdout|synthetic_fixture|external_calibration|human_preference|red_team|runtime_perf|production_shadow|code_completion",
    "dataset_hash": "sha256:...",

    "student_model": "provider/model/version|null",

  },
  "statistics": {

    "paired": true,
    "effect_size": 0,

  },

    "position_bias": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "verbosity_bias": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "self_enhancement_bias": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "model_family_bias": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "domain_shift": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "language_shift": "passed|failed|not_run",
    "red_team_status": "passed|failed|not_run"
  },
  "claim_compiler": {
    "decision": "allow|scope|stale|block",
    "allowed_phrases": [],
    "required_qualifiers": [],
    "blocked_phrases": [],
    "stale_after": "date-time",
    "public_safe_summary": "string",
    "private_customer_summary": "string"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "raw_prompts_included": false,
    "raw_outputs_included": false,
    "secret_values_included": false,
    "cross_tenant_data_included": false,
    "regulated_data_included": false
  }
}
```

K-Score as public standard requirements:

| requirement | product rule | implementation proof |
|---|---|---|
| Formula transparency | Axis definitions, valid ranges, direction, weights, gates, deprecations, and migration notes must be public and versioned. | `k-score-methodology`, public JSON axis registry, open calculator, tests for examples and edge cases. |
| Calibration packs | Each pack needs row count, domains, languages, modalities, human pair counts, labeler policy, fit method, residuals, bias checks, exclusions, release date, and expiry. | Generated calibration pack page and JSON artifact with hash, source list, and reproducible command. |
| Human preference fit | K-Score cannot claim preference alignment without a current fit report for the task, domain, language, and output type. | Pairwise randomized labels, agreement statistics, confidence intervals, and known mismatch examples. |
| Score uncertainty | Promotion uses lower confidence bound and effect size, not only mean composite score. | Bootstrap or equivalent confidence output, minimum detectable effect, and promotion-threshold report. |
| Domain/language scope | K-Score must be scoped to evaluated domain, language, modality, data class, and runtime. | Matrix that blocks general copy when evidence is domain-specific or language-specific. |
| Public calculator | Buyers and competitors can compute compatible K-Score on their own reports. | Open-source CLI/library or documented deterministic calculator with fixtures and version pins. |
| Claim compiler | Every K-Score result emits allowed, required-qualified, stale, and blocked copy. | Marketing/docs/account/API/CLI copy linter consumes the same output. |

Statistical defensibility requirements:

- Every comparative benchmark must state whether it is paired, what rows overlap, what effect size was measured, and what confidence method was used.
- Multiple model/provider/runtime comparisons need a correction policy such as Holm or Benjamini-Hochberg, or must be labeled exploratory.
- Small fixture runs can pass regression gates but cannot claim broad superiority, preference alignment, or production readiness.
- A score movement is not a win unless the confidence interval and minimum detectable effect support the claim.
- Cost/latency comparisons must pin input/output token budgets, concurrency, warmup, retry policy, provider region, pricing snapshot, cache policy, and hardware profile.
- Leaderboard rows must distinguish first-party, verified customer, verified publisher, anonymous, synthetic, and fixture-only submissions.
- Redaction, coding, tool-use, and agent evals must publish failure examples where privacy allows, because aggregate scores hide severe failure modes.

Evaluation UX requirements:

| UX surface | required experience | failure mode to prevent |
|---|---|---|
| Public K-Score pages | Show formula version, axis cards, calibration pack, confidence, evidence class, limitations, and downloadable calculator/artifacts without long prose walls. | A buyer seeing "K-Score 0.91" as unexplained magic. |
| Benchmark pages | Provide filters for task, domain, language, runtime, hardware, evidence class, freshness, and comparable-run group; show stale and non-comparable rows visibly. | Mixing provider snapshots, hardware, or tasks into a misleading leaderboard. |
| Account eval center | Separate private holdout, production shadow, public benchmark, red-team, runtime, and student-promotion evidence; never mix tenant-private with public scores. | Customers believing public benchmark results prove their private workflow. |
| Distill-run detail | Show teacher, student, capture snapshot, eval split, K-Score delta, confidence, latency/cost/memory delta, failure-mode diff, and rollback threshold. | Promoting a smaller model because a single score looks good. |
| Code-completion eval panel | For coding assistant and build-finishing workflows, show repository fixture, task type, patch applicability, tests run, reviewer findings, latency/cost, and unsafe-tool denials. | Treating generic chat quality as code-completion quality. |
| CLI/TUI score output | Print compact axis breakdown, claim decision, calibration age, replay command, and next action; `--json` returns the full envelope. | Operators copying naked numbers into docs, sales, or release notes. |
