Node `42` V3 relax redline:

- Do not call K-Score a standard until the formula, axis registry, calculator, calibration packs, fit reports, and limitation labels are public and versioned.
- Do not claim human preference alignment without current human preference data, fit statistics, confidence intervals, and bias/disagreement checks for the relevant task, domain, language, and modality.
- Do not claim a benchmark win without paired/comparable-run proof, effect size, confidence interval, model/provider/runtime versions, hardware profile, pricing snapshot, and raw report hash.
- Do not promote a distilled or quantized artifact from a single aggregate score; require holdout, failure-mode, latency/cost/memory, runtime, safety, and rollback evidence.
- Do not let public leaderboards mix fixture-only, synthetic, private, stale, non-comparable, first-party, anonymous, and verified-customer rows without visible labels.
- Do not let tenant-private evals leak raw prompts, raw outputs, regulated data, secrets, or cross-tenant data into public reports, support bundles, or training flows.
- Do not let account, docs, API, CLI, TUI, sales, or release copy bypass the claim compiler when the copy contains quality, safety, speed, cost, distillation, coding, routing, or production-readiness claims.

### Codebase Completion, Build Redline, File Closure, And Implementation Finish Evidence Ledger V1

Node `43` is the user's build redline: the point where Kolm can stop saying "we are working toward completion" and can prove the codebase is finished enough to ship, maintain, and improve without hidden rot. Earlier nodes define product surfaces, architecture, tests, release, security, UI, evaluation, and portability. Node `43` is the closure system across all of them. It makes every file, route, page, package, worker, script, generated artifact, and proof output answer: keep, split, fix, generate, archive, delete, or external gate.

The finished state is not "the scanners are green." The finished state is that an implementation agent can pick any path in the repository and see its owner node, current state, product impact, build command, test command, generated/manual status, claim risk, release inclusion, acceptance evidence, and remaining work. If the answer is missing, the codebase is not 100 percent complete.

#### Current Repo Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 43 redline |
|---|---:|---|
| Codegraph | `node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --json` reports `ok: true`, 2,212 files, 7,806 imports, 9,221 symbols, 788 routes, 57 scripts, 57 readiness requirements, and 0 missing readiness evidence. | Use codegraph as the base index, then enrich it with owner node, completion state, generated/manual class, release inclusion, and proof packet. |
| Sampled source inventory | Excluding common dependency/report roots, the sampled tree contained 9,059 text/code files, 268,792,495 bytes, 1,254 HTML files, 2,006 JS-like files, 278 Python files, and 437 Markdown files. | Completion cannot be a manual memory exercise; it needs a generated file-ledger and burn-down dashboard. |
| Root distribution | `src` 392 files, `public` 1,132 files, `tests` 567 files, `scripts` 317 files, `docs` 158 files, `apps` 170 files, `packages` 139 files, `sdk` 4,949 files, `workers` 43 files, `cli` 2 files, `.github` 16 files in the sampled scan. | Every root needs a release policy. Large SDK/artifact-heavy roots need vendored/generated/dependency classification before final clean git. |
| Giant control files | `cli/kolm.js` is 44,833 lines and 2,021,134 bytes; `src/router.js` is 20,677 lines and 953,495 bytes. | These are not automatically wrong, but they require split plans, route/command ownership, security review, test coverage, docs parity, and no hidden generated fragments. |
| High-impact scripts | `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/release-verify.cjs`, `scripts/build-api-ref.cjs`, `scripts/build-openapi.cjs`, `scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs`, and `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs` are release-critical. | Release-critical scripts need input/output manifests, idempotency, dirty-tree safety, serial execution rules, and failure semantics. |
| Existing redline material | This blueprint already has "Atomic Codebase Review And Build Redline V1", node `14`, node `28`, node `31`, node `40`, and node `42`. | Node `43` must consolidate those into a final closure packet instead of scattering the last mile across many sections. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Standard or practice | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| ISO/IEC 25010:2023 | `https://www.iso.org/standard/78176.html` | Completion must evaluate product quality across functional suitability, reliability, performance, security, maintainability, compatibility, portability, interaction capability, and flexibility, not only test pass/fail. |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final` | Secure development evidence must cover root-cause prevention, vulnerability reduction, and impact mitigation; "finished" requires secure-development proof for sensitive code. |
| SLSA v1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/about` | Source, build, provenance, and verification evidence must be tamper-resistant enough that scanned/reviewed code is the code that ships. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` | Repository security posture should be measured by branch protection, dependency update discipline, token permissions, code review, pinned actions, and related automated checks. |
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | Sensitive paths and product surfaces need explicit owners and required reviews, either through CODEOWNERS or an equivalent generated ownership ledger. |
| GitHub code scanning | `https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/concepts/code-scanning/about-code-scanning` | Static analysis and code scanning findings must feed the file completion state instead of being disconnected advisory output. |
| Google Cloud DORA | `https://cloud.google.com/developers/dora` | The final build redline should track delivery performance and operational outcomes such as deployment frequency, lead time, change failure, recovery, and reliability, but never as a substitute for product correctness. |

#### File Completion State Machine

Every file, generated output, and release artifact must have exactly one current state:

| State | Meaning | Allowed to ship? | Required proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `complete` | Owned, current, tested, documented where needed, release-included or explicitly internal, and no open redlines. | Yes. | Owner node, build/test/smoke, review note, last proof command, release inclusion. |
| `implemented_needs_evidence` | Code likely exists, but proof is incomplete, indirect, stale, or missing. | Only behind explicit scope label. | Missing proof list, owner, next action, blocked claim list. |
| `stub_or_placeholder` | Exists to reserve API/page/feature shape but does not provide complete behavior. | No public finished claim. | Stub reason, visible scope, route/page copy restriction, ticket. |
| `external_gate` | Local code is done but proof depends on external package release, certification, partner adoption, live cloud config, or public benchmark. | Only with explicit limitation. | External blocker, closeout owner, done-when proof, copy restriction. |
| `generated_current` | Generated artifact matches source and command. | Yes if source is complete. | Source file, command, input hash, output hash, freshness check. |
| `generated_stale` | Generated artifact exists but source or command has changed. | No. | Regeneration command and stale diff. |
| `fixture_or_testdata` | Data exists only to prove behavior. | Yes if safe and rights-cleared. | Fixture owner, privacy/secret scan, expected use, retention. |
| `archive_or_historical` | Kept for history, research, or migration reference. | Not as current product truth. | Archive label, excluded claim scope, retention reason. |
| `delete_candidate` | Unowned, duplicate, stale, misleading, corrupted, temporary, or unsafe. | No. | Owner decision, delete/ignore/archive ticket, risk if retained. |
| `split_required` | Useful but too large or mixed to remain maintainable. | Temporarily, with owner. | Split plan, target modules, tests, sequence, reviewer. |

No final release may contain `generated_stale`, unclassified `stub_or_placeholder`, unowned `delete_candidate`, or unknown state files outside ignored scratch paths.

#### Canonical Source Completion Record

Every tracked file and generated output should be reducible to this record:

```json
{
  "path": "src/router.js",
  "schema": "kolm.source-completion.v1",
  "primary_node": "2.1",
  "secondary_nodes": ["37", "38", "40", "43"],
  "owner_lane": "backend",
  "file_class": "source|generated|fixture|config|docs|public_page|account_page|script|package|worker|app|archive|media",
  "completion_state": "complete|implemented_needs_evidence|stub_or_placeholder|external_gate|generated_current|generated_stale|fixture_or_testdata|archive_or_historical|delete_candidate|split_required",
  "release_inclusion": "included|excluded|internal_only|package_only|docs_only|test_only|external_only",
  "product_impact": {
    "routes": ["/v1/example"],
    "pages": ["/account/overview"],
    "cli_commands": ["kolm surfaces"],
    "sdk_methods": [],
    "claims": [],
    "data_classes": ["tenant_metadata"]
  },
  "build": {
    "command": "node --check src/router.js",
    "generated_by": null,
    "refreshes": [],
    "serial_after": [],
    "serial_before": []
  },
  "verification": {
    "unit_tests": [],
    "contract_tests": [],
    "surface_smoke": [],
    "screenshot_evidence": [],
    "production_smoke": [],
    "manual_review": "review note id"
  },
  "quality": {
    "large_file_decision": "keep|split|generated|not_applicable",
    "security_review": "required|done|not_applicable",
    "privacy_review": "required|done|not_applicable",
    "accessibility_review": "required|done|not_applicable",
    "claim_scope_review": "required|done|not_applicable"
  },
  "open_work": [],
  "done_when": [
    "route owner and smoke proof exist",
    "OpenAPI and API docs are fresh"
  ]
}
```

The record is intentionally stricter than codegraph. Codegraph says what exists. The completion record says whether it deserves to ship.

#### Build Redline Packet

Every final release candidate must emit one top-level build redline packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.build-redline.v1",
  "commit": "git-sha",
  "generated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "worktree": {
    "clean": true,
    "untracked_allowed": [],
    "dirty_allowed": [],
    "parallel_agents_active": false
  },
  "file_ledger": {
    "total": 0,
    "complete": 0,
    "implemented_needs_evidence": 0,
    "stub_or_placeholder": 0,
    "external_gate": 0,
    "generated_current": 0,
    "generated_stale": 0,
    "delete_candidate": 0,
    "unknown": 0
  },
  "route_ledger": {
    "total_routes": 0,
    "unowned_routes": 0,
    "public_stub_routes": 0,
    "stale_openapi": false,
    "stale_api_docs": false
  },
  "page_ledger": {
    "public_pages": 0,
    "account_pages": 0,
    "docs_pages": 0,
    "missing_type": 0,
    "missing_screenshot": 0,
    "stale_copy_or_claim": 0
  },
  "build_graph": {
    "ok": true,
    "files": 2212,
    "imports": 7806,
    "symbols": 9221,
    "routes": 788,
    "scripts": 57
  },
  "gates": {
    "node_check": "passed",
    "verify_depth": "passed",
    "lint_refs": "passed",
    "release_verify": "passed",
    "local_surfaces_deep": "passed",
    "ui_audit_all_themes": "passed",
    "production_smoke": "passed"
  },
  "exceptions": [],
  "ship_decision": "ship|do_not_ship",
  "ship_reason": "all redline gates passed"
}
```

#### Completion Redlines

- No file may be unknown, ownerless, or outside the master spec tree at final release.
- No generated file may be edited by hand without updating the generator source or declaring the file hand-authored.
- No generator may run in parallel with another process that reads or writes its outputs.
- No route may be public, undocumented, unowned, stale in OpenAPI, stale in API docs, and still considered complete.
- No account page may be marked complete without live/loading/empty/error states, auth behavior, product graph alignment, and screenshot evidence.
- No public page may be marked complete without page type, purpose, metadata, nav state, claim-scope check, responsive screenshots, and stale-copy audit.
- No docs page may be marked complete without runnable command or API/SDK/account link where relevant.
- No SDK/package/worker/app may be marked complete without build, smoke, env, package/release state, and local-only or published status.
- No giant file may be ignored because tests pass; it must have a keep/split/generated decision and reviewer owner.
- No test fixture, benchmark file, screenshot, report, archive, or media file may remain in the repo without retention and release-inclusion policy.
- No old `Kolmogorov` wording, mojibake, key-shaped literal, TODO/mock/stub marker, or stale pricing/certification phrase may survive without an exception record.
- No release may be called final while parallel agents are editing the same generated/public/API files.
- No build redline may rely on chat history. It must be reconstructable from files, commands, reports, screenshots, and production smoke.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `43.1` File-ledger state machine, ownership, generated/manual class, delete/archive/split/keep decisions, and release inclusion | A generated ledger covers every tracked and release-candidate file with owner node, class, completion state, release inclusion, product impact, proof commands, and open work. |
| `43.2` Implementation completeness for source modules, routes, pages, docs, SDKs, packages, workers, apps, tests, scripts, and configs | Every implementation surface has a completeness rule: source modules have tests/docs/errors, routes have OpenAPI/API/smoke, pages have UX proof, packages have build/publish state, scripts have idempotency, configs have env/prod parity. |
| `43.3` Build graph closure, serial generators, stale-output checks, dependency direction, workspace parity, and artifact provenance | Build graph and generator graph are closed: no stale generated assets, no hidden write/read races, no dependency-direction violations, no missing package/app/worker build lane, and artifact provenance is recorded. |
| `43.4` Review packets, code-read notes, large-file split plans, risk decisions, manual QA, and acceptance evidence | High-risk and large files have human review packets with keep/split decisions, security/privacy/claim/accessibility review where applicable, manual QA notes, and acceptance evidence tied to commands. |
| `43.5` Final redline gates: clean git, no unclassified files, no hidden stubs, no stale generated assets, no secret/brand/encoding drift | Final redline fails on dirty/unclassified/unowned files, public stubs, stale generated outputs, secret-like leaks, brand drift, encoding corruption, missing screenshots, or missing production smoke. |
| `43.6` Evidence: source completion ledger, route/page/file proof matrix, build reports, release packet, production smoke, and post-deploy verification | A release evidence archive contains the source ledger, route/page/file proof matrix, build logs, test logs, screenshots, generated docs, OpenAPI/product graph, release verify, production smoke, and post-deploy verification. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-BUILD-001` | `43.1` | Generate `docs/source-completion-ledger.json`. | Every tracked file gets path, class, primary node, owner lane, generated/manual status, release inclusion, state, and proof requirements. |
| `W-BUILD-002` | `43.1` | Add file-state verifier. | Verification fails on unknown, ownerless, generated-stale, unapproved delete-candidate, or public stub files. |
| `W-BUILD-003` | `43.1` | Add release inclusion policy. | Every file is classified as included, excluded, internal-only, test-only, docs-only, package-only, or external-only. |
| `W-BUILD-004` | `43.1` | Add archive, fixture, report, and media retention policy. | Archive, fixture, screenshot, benchmark, report, and media files have retention reason, owner, privacy scan, and release role. |
| `W-BUILD-005` | `43.2` | Add source module proof matrix. | Each `src`, `apps`, `workers`, `sdk`, `packages`, and `cli` source file maps to build, tests, docs, route/page/command impact, and open work. |
| `W-BUILD-006` | `43.2` | Add route implementation closure matrix. | Every route maps to owner, implementation file, auth policy, envelope/schema, OpenAPI row, docs row, tests, smoke, and public/internal state. |
| `W-BUILD-007` | `43.2` | Add page implementation closure matrix. | Every public/account/docs page maps to page type, source/generator, API dependencies, metadata, claim scope, screenshots, accessibility, and next action. |
| `W-BUILD-008` | `43.2` | Add package/app/worker completion matrix. | Every package, SDK, app, and worker has build, test, smoke, environment, lockfile, publish/local-only, and release evidence state. |
| `W-BUILD-009` | `43.2` | Add script safety matrix. | Every script declares read/write paths, idempotency, dirty-tree behavior, generated outputs, environment requirements, and release criticality. |
| `W-BUILD-010` | `43.3` | Build generator dependency graph. | API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout, account pages, CLI docs, sitemap, OG assets, SDK version, and screenshots have serial generation order. |
| `W-BUILD-011` | `43.3` | Add stale generated output gate. | Verification fails if generated outputs are stale, missing source command, or modified without source change. |
| `W-BUILD-012` | `43.3` | Add dependency direction rules. | Imports and generated dependencies obey allowed directions; cycles and cross-surface imports require explicit exceptions. |
| `W-BUILD-013` | `43.3` | Add workspace parity manifest. | npm, Python, Rust, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, C, VS Code, worker, and installer subtrees are discoverable by one manifest. |
| `W-BUILD-014` | `43.4` | Create large-file review packets. | `cli/kolm.js`, `src/router.js`, large generators, and large ML/runtime files have owner, split/keep decision, risk review, and test proof. |
| `W-BUILD-015` | `43.4` | Add manual code-read notes for P0 surfaces. | Auth, billing, routing, capture, privacy, artifact signing, distill, eval, registry, account overview, pricing, and release scripts have human review notes. |
| `W-BUILD-016` | `43.4` | Add risk acceptance ledger. | Any accepted debt has owner, reason, user impact, mitigation, expiry, and release-blocking decision. |
| `W-BUILD-017` | `43.5` | Add final git cleanliness gate. | Release gate rejects untracked/unclassified files, unexpected dirty files, ignored critical outputs, and stale temp artifacts. |
| `W-BUILD-018` | `43.5` | Add brand, encoding, secret, and stale-copy final sweep. | Final redline fails on unapproved Kolmogorov strings, mojibake markers, key-shaped literals, stale pricing/certification claims, and unsupported "best" claims. |
| `W-BUILD-019` | `43.6` | Emit `reports/build-redline/<date>/packet.json`. | Packet links source ledger, route/page matrix, build graph, gates, screenshots, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout, tests, production smoke, and exceptions. |
| `W-BUILD-020` | `43.6` | Add post-deploy completion verification. | Production deploy is rechecked for routes, account auth, screenshots, API docs, product graph, storage/cloud readiness, no secret leaks, and claim-scope parity. |

#### Node 43 Relax Redline

Node `43` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- What is the completion state of every tracked file, generated output, release artifact, page, route, SDK, package, worker, app, script, fixture, report, screenshot, config, and archive file?
- Which node owns it, who reviews it, which commands build or verify it, and is it included in release?
- If it is generated, which command created it, which source inputs changed, and is it fresh?
- If it is a route or page, where are its API docs, OpenAPI, account/docs/public UX, screenshots, auth/error states, and smoke evidence?
- If it is a package, SDK, worker, app, or installer, what proves build, smoke, env parity, release or local-only status, and package provenance?
- If it is large or mixed-purpose, what is the keep/split/refactor decision and who owns the risk?
- If it is incomplete, external-gated, archived, fixture-only, or delete-candidate, what prevents it from being marketed or shipped as finished?
- Can the build redline packet prove clean git, fresh generated artifacts, no hidden stubs, no stale claims, no secret/brand/encoding drift, full local gates, production smoke, and post-deploy verification without relying on chat history?

The relax condition for node `43` is not "one final suite passed." It is: every current and generated file in Kolm has an owner, state, release role, proof path, and closure decision; every route/page/package/worker/script has implementation and evidence parity; generated assets are fresh; git is clean; production is smoked; and the release packet makes the finished codebase independently auditable.

#### Node 43 Source Completion Control System Addendum V2

Node `52` stays unused here. The user's current instruction to "maintain a master spec tree" means the build-completion work must extend node `43`, because node `43` already owns the redline between "many green checks" and "the whole codebase is actually finished, classified, shippable, and independently auditable."

The V2 closeout makes node `43` stricter than a code scan. It defines the source-completion control system that every implementation lane must satisfy before anyone can say the codebase is `100 percent`: every path must have a class, owner node, release role, completion state, generated/manual status, proof commands, evidence links, and explicit keep/split/archive/delete/external-gate decision.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V2 interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| Codegraph | `node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --json` returned `ok: true`, 2,212 files, 7,806 imports, 9,221 symbols, 788 routes, 57 scripts, 57 readiness requirements, and 0 missing readiness evidence. | Codegraph proves structural discoverability, not completion. V2 uses it as a base index and layers ownership, state, release inclusion, and acceptance evidence on top. |
| Root inventory | Current workspace scan found `src` 392 files, `public` 1,132, `tests` 567, `scripts` 343, `docs` 158, `apps` 170, `packages` 139, `sdk` 4,956, `workers` 1,030, `cli` 2, `.github` 16. | Large roots need per-root policy. `sdk` and `workers` require generated/build/dependency classification before they can be treated as release source. |
| Byte distribution | Current scan found `sdk` at 1,593,186,765 bytes, `workers` at 66,533,213 bytes, `public` at 64,617,317 bytes, `scripts` at 25,958,930 bytes, and `src` at 6,540,534 bytes. | Size is not a defect by itself, but it forces retention policy, generated-artifact exclusion, package provenance, and release payload decisions. |
| Largest files | The largest observed files are Rust SDK build outputs under `sdk/rust/target/...`, including `.rlib`, `.rmeta`, debug binaries, and examples. | Build outputs cannot be silently treated as source. V2 requires `target`, dist, generated, cache, screenshot, report, and package artifacts to be classified or excluded. |
| Worktree state | `git status --short` currently groups to 13 modified files and 6 untracked paths, with permission warnings for the user-level git ignore file. | A dirty tree is acceptable during parallel work, but final `100 percent` completion requires an allowlist or clean tree. Chat history cannot be the exception record. |
| Script surface | `package.json` currently exposes `verify:depth`, `verify:inventions`, `verify:brand`, `verify:claims-scope`, `verify:package-release`, `local:surfaces`, `local:surfaces:deep`, `prod:surfaces:deep`, `ui:audit:all:themes`, `test`, `release:verify`, and lint scripts. | Verification breadth exists. V2 requires each script to declare read/write paths, generated outputs, serial ordering, env requirements, and whether it is release-critical. |
| Generator and audit scripts | Release-critical scripts include `scripts/release-verify.cjs`, `scripts/build-codegraph.mjs`, `scripts/build-api-ref.cjs`, `scripts/build-openapi.cjs`, `scripts/build-cli-docs.cjs`, `scripts/build-docs-manifest.cjs`, `scripts/build-sitemap.cjs`, `scripts/verify-product-surfaces.cjs`, `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, and `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs`. | V2 requires a generator dependency graph so API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, CLI docs, sitemap, screenshots, and readiness closeouts cannot race or drift. |
| Existing closure material | Node `14`, node `28`, node `31`, node `40`, node `42`, node `46`, and node `43` V1 already define parts of release, verification, traceability, evaluation, and file closure. | V2 is the aggregator: it must assemble those into one build redline packet rather than forcing agents to infer completion from scattered checks. |

##### External Baseline Added For V2

| Baseline | Source | What V2 imports into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| ISO/IEC 25010:2023 | `https://www.iso.org/standard/78176.html` | Completion must cover product quality characteristics, not only "tests passed": functional suitability, performance, compatibility, usability/interaction, reliability, security, maintainability, portability, and flexibility must map to evidence. |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final` | Secure development evidence must reduce vulnerability count, reduce exploit impact, and address root causes. The file ledger must mark security-review state for sensitive code. |
| SLSA v1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/about` | Source, build, provenance, package, and release artifacts must be linked so the reviewed code is the code that shipped. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | `https://openssf.org/scorecard/` | Repository health must include branch protection, dependency update behavior, token permissions, pinned actions, code review, dangerous workflow patterns, and security hygiene. |
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | Sensitive paths need owner mapping and review requirements. Kolm can implement this through CODEOWNERS or a generated source-completion ledger with equivalent ownership. |
| GitHub code scanning | `https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/concepts/code-scanning/about-code-scanning` | Static analysis findings must feed file completion state and risk acceptance, not sit as disconnected advisory output. |
| DORA 2025 AI-assisted software development report | `https://cloud.google.com/devops/state-of-devops` | Delivery performance and AI-assisted work must still be tied to user value, quality, review, and reliability. Velocity metrics cannot substitute for product correctness or evidence. |

##### V2 Source Completion Contract

Every tracked path, generated output, report, screenshot, build artifact, package artifact, fixture, page, route, command, worker, SDK, and app must reduce to this record:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.source-completion.v2",
  "path": "src/router.js",

  "state": "complete|implemented_needs_evidence|stub_or_placeholder|external_gate|generated_current|generated_stale|fixture_or_testdata|archive_or_historical|delete_candidate|split_required|build_output_excluded",

      "performance_efficiency": "covered|gap|not_applicable",
      "compatibility": "covered|gap|not_applicable",
      "interaction_capability": "covered|gap|not_applicable",

      "security": "covered|gap|not_applicable",
      "maintainability": "covered|gap|not_applicable",

  "open_work": [],
  "done_when": []
}
```

This contract intentionally distinguishes `build_output_excluded` from `delete_candidate`. Generated build products, screenshots, reports, and packaged artifacts may be valid evidence, but they cannot masquerade as hand-authored product source.

##### V2 Build Redline Packet

The final release candidate must produce one self-contained packet:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.build-redline.v2",
  "commit": "git-sha",
  "generated_at": "2026-05-25T00:00:00Z",
  "parallel_agents_active": false,
  "worktree": {
    "clean": true,
    "dirty_allowed": [],
    "untracked_allowed": [],
    "ignored_critical_outputs": []
  },
  "source_ledger": {
    "total_paths": 0,
    "unknown": 0,
    "ownerless": 0,
    "generated_stale": 0,
    "public_stub": 0,
    "delete_candidate": 0,
    "split_required": 0,
    "external_gate": 0,
    "build_output_excluded": 0
  },
  "surface_ledger": {
    "routes": {
      "total": 788,
      "unowned": 0,
      "stale_openapi": 0,
      "missing_smoke": 0
    },
    "pages": {
      "public": 0,
      "account": 0,
      "docs": 0,
      "missing_metadata": 0,
      "missing_screenshot": 0,
      "claim_scope_gap": 0
    },
    "commands": {
      "cli": 0,
      "tui": 0,
      "missing_docs": 0,
      "missing_smoke": 0
    }
  },
  "generator_graph": {
    "serial_order": [
      "build-api-ref",
      "build-openapi",
      "build-product-graph",
      "build-readiness-closeout",
      "build-cli-docs",
      "build-sitemap",
      "ui-surface-audit"
    ],
    "stale_outputs": []
  },
  "gates": {
    "verify_codegraph": "passed",
    "verify_depth": "passed",
    "lint_refs": "passed",
    "verify_brand": "passed",
    "release_verify": "passed",
    "local_surfaces_deep": "passed",
    "ui_audit_all_themes": "passed",
    "prod_surfaces_deep": "passed"
  },
  "exceptions": [],
  "ship_decision": "ship|do_not_ship",
  "ship_reason": "evidence-based reason"
}
```

The redline packet must be generated from the current filesystem, not from chat logs. If another agent is still editing, the packet must say `parallel_agents_active: true` and `ship_decision: do_not_ship` unless the release manager explicitly freezes the tree.

##### V2 File-State Policy

| State | Ship rule | Required evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `complete` | May ship. | Owner node, release role, proof commands, risk reviews where relevant, and no open work. |
| `implemented_needs_evidence` | May ship only behind scoped copy and non-final status. | Missing evidence list, owner, next action, and blocked claims. |
| `stub_or_placeholder` | Cannot be marketed as complete. | Stub reason, visible limitation, route/page/command owner, and replacement ticket. |
| `external_gate` | May ship only with explicit limitation. | External blocker, done-when proof, owner, and public/account copy restriction. |
| `generated_current` | May ship if its source is complete. | Generator, inputs, outputs, serial order, freshness command, and output hash. |
| `generated_stale` | Cannot ship. | Regeneration command and stale diff. |
| `fixture_or_testdata` | May remain if safe and rights-cleared. | Purpose, owner, privacy/secret scan, retention, and release inclusion. |
| `archive_or_historical` | May remain only as non-current reference. | Archive label, retention reason, excluded claim scope, and owner. |
| `delete_candidate` | Cannot ship. | Delete/archive decision, owner, risk if retained. |
| `split_required` | Temporary only. | Split plan, reviewer, target modules, order, tests, and risk window. |
| `build_output_excluded` | Excluded from product source and release claims. | Ignore/exclusion policy, provenance if retained, and no source ownership claim. |

##### V2 Implementation Workstream

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-BUILD-V2-001` | `43.1` | Generate `docs/source-completion-ledger.json`. | Every path under tracked and release-candidate roots has kind, state, owner lane, node, release role, generated/manual class, and done-when proof. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-002` | `43.1` | Add root policy manifest. | `src`, `public`, `tests`, `scripts`, `docs`, `apps`, `packages`, `sdk`, `workers`, `cli`, `.github`, reports, screenshots, and build-output roots each have inclusion/exclusion policy. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-003` | `43.1` | Classify SDK and worker build outputs. | `sdk/rust/target`, dist folders, caches, screenshots, and report artifacts are excluded, retained, or packaged by explicit rule. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-004` | `43.1` | Add final dirty-tree allowlist. | Release gate fails on untracked or dirty paths unless the redline packet names the path, owner, reason, and expiry. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-005` | `43.2` | Build route closure matrix. | Every route maps to implementation, auth, envelope, OpenAPI, API docs, product surface owner, test, smoke, and claim scope. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-006` | `43.2` | Build page closure matrix. | Every public, docs, and account page maps to metadata, nav state, APIs, screenshots, responsive state, accessibility, copy owner, and claim gate. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-007` | `43.2` | Build command closure matrix. | Every CLI/TUI command maps to handler, docs, examples, auth/env needs, test, smoke, and product surface. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-008` | `43.2` | Build package/app/worker closure matrix. | Every package, app, SDK, and worker has build command, smoke, env, package/release state, dependency policy, and provenance. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-009` | `43.2` | Build script safety matrix. | Every script declares read/write paths, idempotency, generated outputs, env variables, dirty-tree behavior, and release criticality. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-010` | `43.3` | Build generator serial graph. | API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, sitemap, docs manifest, OG assets, SDK version, and screenshots have deterministic order. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-011` | `43.3` | Add stale-output verifier. | Generated outputs fail verification when inputs, generator, command, or output hashes drift. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-012` | `43.3` | Add import direction and ownership verifier. | Cross-surface imports, route mounts, generated imports, and package boundaries fail without owner exceptions. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-013` | `43.4` | Create large-file review packets. | `cli/kolm.js`, `src/router.js`, large docs generators, UI audit scripts, and ML/runtime-heavy files have keep/split/generated decision and owner. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-014` | `43.4` | Add P0 manual code-read packets. | Auth, billing, gateway, capture, privacy, redaction, signing, eval, distill, registry, storage, account overview, and release scripts have review notes. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-015` | `43.4` | Add accepted-risk ledger. | Any debt has owner, reason, user impact, mitigation, expiry, release decision, and claim restrictions. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-016` | `43.5` | Add brand and encoding final gate. | Final redline fails on mojibake, stale `Kolmogorov` product naming, unsupported old pricing, fake certification copy, and malformed metadata. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-017` | `43.5` | Add secret and key-shape final gate. | Final redline fails on unapproved key-shaped literals, secrets, raw credentials, token examples outside fixtures, and secret values in public outputs. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-018` | `43.5` | Add UI and account proof join. | Source ledger references all-route screenshot evidence, auth/account manual-use proof, nav state, theme state, and product graph parity. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-019` | `43.6` | Emit `reports/build-redline/<date>/packet.json`. | Packet links source ledger, route/page/command matrices, generator graph, codegraph, tests, screenshots, release verify, local/prod smoke, and exceptions. |
| `W-BUILD-V2-020` | `43.6` | Add post-deploy finality gate. | Production is rechecked for route health, account auth, product graph, readiness closeout, storage/cloud readiness, screenshots, no stale claims, and no secret leaks. |

##### Node 43 V2 Relax Redline

Node `43` V2 is relaxed only when all of the following are true in current artifacts:

- Every release-candidate path has exactly one state in `kolm.source-completion.v2`.
- Every dirty or untracked path is either gone or explicitly allowlisted with owner, reason, expiry, and release impact.
- Every generated output has a generator, input set, output set, serial order, freshness command, and current hash.
- Every route has owner, auth policy, envelope/schema, implementation path, OpenAPI/API docs state, tests, smoke, and claim-scope state.
- Every public/docs/account page has owner, page type, metadata, nav state, data dependencies, theme/mobile screenshots, accessibility state, and claim-scope state.
- Every CLI/TUI command has handler, docs, examples, tests, env/auth behavior, and product-surface owner.
- Every package, SDK, app, worker, installer, and build output is source, generated, retained evidence, packaged artifact, or excluded artifact by explicit rule.
- Every large or mixed-purpose file has a keep/split/generated/exclude decision and review owner.
- Every accepted risk has mitigation, expiry, owner, and public-copy restriction.
- The final build redline packet can be regenerated from the filesystem and commands after deployment, with no dependency on conversation memory.

If any of these fail, the codebase may still be improving, but it is not `100 percent` complete.

#### Node 43 Build-Finality, Release-Freeze, And Source Closure Addendum V3

This V3 branch keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` stays unused because node `43` already owns codebase completion, build redline, file closure, generated-artifact freshness, implementation finish evidence, and the user's "finished enough to relax" state. V2 defined the source-completion control system. V3 adds the release-freeze discipline and build-finality evidence needed when multiple agents, generated files, screenshots, CLI docs, product graphs, account pages, and backend routes are changing at once.

Current repo delta checked for V3:

| evidence | current observed state | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Codegraph | `node scripts/build-codegraph.mjs --json` returned `ok:true` with 2,212 files, 7,806 imports, 9,221 symbols, 788 routes, 57 scripts, 57 readiness requirements, and 0 missing readiness evidence. | Codegraph remains the discovery base, but V3 must bind every discovered object to owner, release role, generator state, risk, evidence, and freeze status. |
| Package scripts | `package.json` currently exposes 57 scripts, including `verify:depth`, product/research verification, local/prod surface smokes, screenshot audits, tests, lint, and `release:verify`. | Script breadth is strong; finality requires a script contract catalog with read/write sets, env needs, order, release-critical flag, and concurrency safety. |
| Checked root counts | Current checked roots contain `src` 392 files, `scripts` 343, `tests` 567, `public` 1,132, `docs` 158, and `.github/workflows` 11. | V3 must not claim full closure from a subset count. It must generate a release-candidate path set across all roots and explicitly classify excluded roots, build outputs, caches, reports, and screenshots. |
| Workflow surface | Current workflows include compile, distill, CI, lint, SBOM, smoke, SDK C/Rust, KolmBench submission, and template workflows. | CI is product infrastructure. Every workflow needs owner, trigger, secrets policy, permissions, pinned-actions state, artifact/provenance outputs, and release-blocking role. |
| Worktree state | Current `git status --short` summary shows 13 modified paths and 6 untracked paths. | During active parallel work that is acceptable; final build redline cannot be relaxed until every dirty/untracked path is committed, removed, or allowlisted in a packet with owner, reason, expiry, and release impact. |
| Generator concentration | Current release-critical generators include API ref, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, account pages, docs manifests, CLI docs, sitemap, OG assets, screenshots, surface smokes, and UI audit scripts. | V3 requires a no-race generator graph and a freeze protocol: serial order, output locks, stale-output checks, and post-generation diff classification. |
| UI proof surface | Screenshot/audit scripts and many QA screenshots exist under `scripts/` and reports, while frontend work may be concurrent. | Build finality requires one source of screenshot truth per release, with routes, themes, viewports, auth state, changed-page focus, and manual-use notes joined to the file ledger. |

External baseline delta for V3:

| source | current signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| SLSA v1.2 | SLSA separates source, build, provenance, verification, and attestation tracks so artifacts can be traced back to source and builder. | Kolm build-finality packets must link source commit, generated artifacts, build command, runner, provenance, verification summary, and artifact hashes. |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218 | SSDF provides a secure software development vocabulary and aims to reduce vulnerabilities, mitigate exploit impact, and address root causes. | Sensitive Kolm files cannot be complete without security review state, vulnerability finding state, root-cause follow-up, and accepted-risk evidence. |
| OpenSSF Scorecard | Scorecard auto-generates security posture signals for repository trust and risk. | Kolm should track branch protection, dangerous workflows, dependency update policy, token permissions, pinned dependencies/actions, and review practices in the build packet. |
| CISA Secure by Design | CISA urges manufacturers to take ownership of product security outcomes and ship only secure-by-design products. | Finality must include owner accountability, transparent exceptions, secure defaults, and customer-visible limits for external gates. |
| ISO/IEC 25010:2023 | The product quality model covers nine product quality characteristics and subcharacteristics for ICT/software products. | File and feature closure must map to functional suitability, performance efficiency, compatibility, interaction capability, reliability, security, maintainability, portability, and flexibility. |
| GitHub CODEOWNERS | CODEOWNERS can request owner review and must be valid, under size limits, and placed in an accepted location. | Kolm needs either a valid CODEOWNERS file or equivalent generated ownership ledger for sensitive paths, generated outputs, workflows, and product surfaces. |
| GitHub code scanning | Code scanning alerts include severity, affected branches, categories such as generated/test/library/docs, and pull-request integration. | Code scanning results must feed source-completion state and risk acceptance instead of living outside the release decision. |
| DORA delivery research | Delivery performance uses deployment frequency, lead time, recovery time, change failure, and reliability, but speed does not replace quality. | Kolm should record delivery metrics in the release packet, while blocking any attempt to use velocity as proof of product correctness. |

Node `43` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.build-finality.v3",
  "release_id": "rel_...",
  "commit": "git-sha",
  "tree_freeze": {
    "frozen_at": "date-time",
    "parallel_agents_active": false,
    "dirty_paths": [],
    "untracked_paths": [],
    "allowlisted_paths": [],
    "allowlist_expiry": "date-time|null"
  },
  "source_completion": {
    "ledger_path": "docs/source-completion-ledger.json",
    "total_paths": 0,
    "unknown": 0,
    "ownerless": 0,
    "generated_stale": 0,
    "public_stub": 0,
    "delete_candidate": 0,
    "split_required": 0,
    "external_gate": 0,
    "accepted_risk": 0
  },
  "generator_graph": {
    "lockfile": "reports/build-redline/latest/generator-lock.json",
    "serial_order": [],
    "input_hashes": {},
    "output_hashes": {},
    "stale_outputs": [],
    "race_detected": false
  },
  "workflow_security": {
    "workflow_count": 0,
    "pinned_actions": "pass|fail|not_run",
    "token_permissions": "pass|fail|not_run",
    "secret_scope": "pass|fail|not_run",
    "sbom": "pass|fail|not_run",
    "provenance": "pass|fail|not_run",
    "code_scanning": "pass|fail|not_run"
  },
  "surface_closure": {
    "routes": {"total": 0, "unowned": 0, "missing_openapi": 0, "missing_smoke": 0},
    "pages": {"public": 0, "account": 0, "docs": 0, "missing_screenshot": 0, "claim_gap": 0},
    "commands": {"cli": 0, "tui": 0, "missing_docs": 0, "missing_smoke": 0},
    "packages": {"total": 0, "published": 0, "local_only": 0, "external_gate": 0}
  },
  "quality_vector": {
    "functional_suitability": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "performance_efficiency": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "compatibility": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "interaction_capability": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "reliability": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "security": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "maintainability": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "portability": "pass|gap|not_applicable",
    "flexibility": "pass|gap|not_applicable"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "codegraph": "reports/build-redline/latest/codegraph.json",
    "tests": "reports/build-redline/latest/tests.json",
    "release_verify": "reports/build-redline/latest/release-verify.json",
    "screenshots": "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/report.md",
    "production_smoke": "reports/build-redline/latest/production-smoke.json",
    "post_deploy": "reports/build-redline/latest/post-deploy.json"
  },
  "decision": {
    "ship": false,
    "reason": "not frozen",
    "exceptions": []
  }
}
```

Release-freeze protocol:

| phase | rule | evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `pre_freeze` | Agents may edit, but every lane must declare ownership and generated outputs it may touch. | Active-agent list, dirty path list, planned generator list. |
| `freeze` | No broad edits; generators run in declared serial order; dirty/untracked paths are classified. | Freeze timestamp, generator lock, diff snapshot, path allowlist. |
| `verify` | Run syntax, build graph, product graph, docs/refs, claim scope, tests, local/deep surfaces, UI screenshots, package readiness, and release verify. | Build redline packet and raw logs. |
| `review` | Large files, sensitive files, public copy, account UX, generated diffs, workflows, and accepted risks get human-review packets. | Review notes and risk ledger. |
| `deploy` | Deploy exact frozen commit or artifact only; no regenerated untracked assets after freeze. | Commit, artifact hash, provenance, deployment log. |
| `post_deploy` | Re-smoke production routes, account auth, product graph, readiness closeout, storage/cloud readiness, screenshots, SEO/metadata, and secret leakage. | Production smoke report and screenshots. |
| `unfreeze` | Only after ship/no-ship decision is recorded. | Decision record and next-wave backlog. |

Product-completion UX requirements for the redline:

| surface | required experience | failure mode to prevent |
|---|---|---|
| Build redline dashboard | Dense table with every root, state counts, blockers, owner lanes, generator freshness, screenshots, production smoke, and risk exceptions. | A release manager reading a 5,000-line log to discover if the product is shippable. |
| File detail view | For each path, show owner node, release role, generated/manual status, product impact, tests, screenshots, security/privacy/a11y/claim reviews, and open work. | "Tests passed" hiding an unowned or stale public surface. |
| Generated-artifact panel | Show generator, inputs, outputs, serial order, last run, hashes, stale status, and race warnings. | API docs, OpenAPI, CLI docs, product graph, sitemap, or screenshots drifting after another agent writes files. |
| Risk exception panel | Show accepted debt, owner, reason, user impact, mitigation, expiry, public-copy restriction, and release decision. | Permanent "temporary" debt or hidden external gates. |
| Post-deploy proof panel | Show prod route smoke, auth/account checks, readiness, screenshots, metadata, and no-secret results for the exact deployed commit. | Declaring local completion while production is stale or misconfigured. |

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-BUILD-V3-001` | `43.1` | Build the release-candidate path resolver. | It enumerates tracked, generated, public, package, report, screenshot, and deploy-relevant paths while excluding dependency/build caches by explicit rule. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-002` | `43.1` | Upgrade `docs/source-completion-ledger.json` to V3. | Every path records owner node, release role, state, quality vector, generated state, proof links, risk ID, and freeze status. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-003` | `43.1` | Add dirty/untracked classification gate. | Final verification fails unless dirty/untracked paths are removed or allowlisted with owner, reason, expiry, and release impact. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-004` | `43.1` | Add root policy and build-output policy. | `sdk`, `workers`, reports, screenshots, Rust targets, dist folders, caches, docs, public, packages, apps, CLI, and workflows have explicit release inclusion rules. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-005` | `43.2` | Generate script contract catalog. | Every package/script file declares read paths, write paths, generated outputs, env vars, idempotency, release-critical flag, and serial constraints. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-006` | `43.2` | Generate workflow security catalog. | Every GitHub workflow records trigger, permissions, secrets, artifact outputs, pinned-action state, branch protection assumptions, and release-blocking role. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-007` | `43.2` | Add CODEOWNERS or equivalent owner verifier. | Sensitive paths, generated docs, workflows, auth, billing, privacy, release scripts, public pages, and account pages have owner coverage. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-008` | `43.2` | Join code scanning and dependency findings to file state. | Security alerts, dependency alerts, secret findings, and SCA/SBOM findings affect completion state or accepted-risk records. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-009` | `43.3` | Build no-race generator graph. | Generators have serial order, output lock, input/output hashes, stale checks, and explicit parallel-safe or parallel-forbidden status. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-010` | `43.3` | Add generated diff classifier. | Regenerated API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, readiness closeout, CLI docs, sitemap, account pages, OG assets, and screenshots are classified as expected or suspicious. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-011` | `43.3` | Build surface closure joins. | Route, page, command, package, worker, SDK, and docs matrices join back to the source-completion ledger and product surface owners. |

| `W-BUILD-V3-013` | `43.4` | Create large-file review packet generator. | Giant files and mixed-purpose files get keep/split/generated/exclude decisions, owner, risk, and targeted test/smoke proof. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-014` | `43.4` | Add P0 code-read closure packets. | Auth, billing, gateway, capture, privacy, redaction, signing, eval, distill, registry, storage, cloud, account, pricing, and release scripts have reviewer notes. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-015` | `43.4` | Add accepted-risk expiry gate. | Any accepted risk without owner, mitigation, expiry, release decision, and copy restriction fails finality. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-016` | `43.5` | Add quality-vector coverage. | File and surface records map ISO/IEC 25010-style quality categories to proof or explicit non-applicability. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-017` | `43.5` | Add final brand/encoding/secret/claim sweep to packet. | Kolmogorov drift, mojibake, stale pricing, fake certification, unsupported superlatives, and key-shaped literals block release unless exceptioned. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-018` | `43.6` | Emit `reports/build-redline/<date>/packet.json` and `index.html`. | The packet and dashboard summarize file state, surface closure, generator graph, workflows, risk, screenshots, local gates, production smoke, and ship decision. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-019` | `43.6` | Add exact-artifact deploy provenance. | Deploy uses the frozen commit/artifact hash, records build provenance, SBOM, release notes, environment, and rollback target. |
| `W-BUILD-V3-020` | `43.6` | Add post-deploy finality verifier. | Production verifies product graph, readiness closeout, account auth, storage/cloud readiness, routes, screenshots, metadata, no-secret outputs, and claim parity for the deployed commit. |

Node `43` V3 relax redline:

- Do not call the codebase complete while any parallel agent is still editing release-candidate files, generated outputs, public pages, API docs, CLI docs, account pages, or screenshots.
- Do not call the build complete while git has dirty or untracked paths unless every path is allowlisted in the build-finality packet with owner, reason, expiry, and release impact.
- Do not accept a generated output without generator, input hash, output hash, serial order, freshness command, and race-safety classification.
- Do not accept a release-critical script without declared read/write paths, env needs, idempotency, generated outputs, and failure semantics.
- Do not accept a workflow without trigger, permissions, secrets policy, pinned-action state, artifact/provenance outputs, and owner.
- Do not mark source complete when code scanning, dependency scanning, secret scanning, brand/encoding scans, or claim-scope scans have unresolved release-blocking findings.
- Do not mark a UI/product surface complete without joining source files, route/API dependencies, screenshots, auth/loading/error states, accessibility, claim scope, and production smoke.
- Do not let DORA-style velocity metrics or large test counts replace file-level closure, product correctness, security, UX proof, and production evidence.

#### Node 43 V4 Atomic File Review, Clean-Tree Closure, And Build Redline Ledger

Node `52` remains unused. The user's latest instruction is still node `43`: finish the codebase atomically, not by scanner theater. V3 defined build finality. V4 defines the per-file review protocol that makes "every single file" reviewable without asking one human or one agent to hold the entire repository in working memory.

The standard is not "a scanner found no errors." The standard is:

- every release-candidate path has one owner node;
- every path has a state: `keep`, `split`, `delete`, `archive`, `generated`, `fixture`, `external_gate`, or `accepted_risk`;
- every path has a proof class matched to its role;
- every generated output has a generator and freshness check;
- every dirty or untracked path is resolved before the final build redline;
- every release-critical path has a human-readable reason to exist.

##### Node 43 V4 Current Evidence Delta

Current root inventory, excluding dependency/build/cache/report-heavy directories, shows the build-redline target is broad and cannot be closed by a single test command:

| Root | Files | Bytes | V4 implication |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `src` | 392 | 6,540,534 | Product runtime/control-plane code needs owner, proof class, tests, route/API links, and security/privacy review where relevant. |
| `scripts` | 342 | 25,946,461 | Scripts are release infrastructure. Each needs read/write sets, generated outputs, idempotency, env requirements, and concurrency safety. |
| `tests` | 567 | 7,319,376 | Tests must map to product requirements and cannot be the only evidence for UI, production, external packages, or certifications. |
| `public` | 1,132 | 64,617,317 | Public/account/docs surfaces are product code; each page needs owner node, claim scope, screenshot state, metadata state, and deletion/keep decision. |
| `docs` | 158 | 9,174,500 | Docs are product surfaces and release evidence; they need freshness, source links, claim scope, generated/manual class, and owner. |
| `cli` | 2 | 2,042,926 | The CLI is a large release-critical surface and needs command-level decomposition, docs parity, smoke state, and generated help proof. |
| `packages` | 131 | 352,610 | Package surfaces need publishability, local-only scope, install smoke, provenance, and package-manager truth. |
| `sdk` | 42 | 348,948 | SDKs need language-specific build, docs, install, compatibility, and package-release evidence. |
| `apps` | 122 | 1,054,647 | Apps need product ownership, build state, deploy state, UI proof, and release inclusion or archive decisions. |
| `workers` | 43 | 346,161 | Workers need runtime envelope, resource limits, queue semantics, smoke, deploy, logs, and rollback evidence. |
| `.github` | 16 | 42,340 | Workflows are privileged code and need trigger, permissions, secret scope, pinned action, provenance, and release-blocking classification. |
| `infra` | 3 | 24,662 | Infrastructure files need deployment target ownership, DNS/runtime proof, rollback impact, and security review. |

Current worktree evidence:

| State | Paths | V4 implication |
|---|---|---|
| Modified public/frontend paths | `public/brand-refresh.css`, `public/home-refresh.css`, `public/kolm-chat.js`, `public/nav.js`, `public/studio.html`, `public/styles.css`, `public/supplement.css`, `public/surface-polish.css`, `public/sw.js`, `public/w604.css`, `public/warm-paper.css` | Final build redline must wait for frontend lane settlement, screenshot proof, CSS cascade classification, and release inclusion decisions. |
| Modified runtime/deploy paths | `src/intent.js`, `vercel.json` | These can affect behavior and routing; they require owner, diff review, smoke, and deployment proof before release-close. |
| Untracked generated/local paths | `.w850-shots/`, `C...site-failures-sorted.txt`, `C...site-failures.txt`, `data/observations.json.*.tmp`, this blueprint doc | Final redline cannot ignore them. Each must become committed evidence, ignored local scratch, archived report, or removed. |

The V4 finding is precise: a codebase can be improving fast and still be unclosable if dirty/untracked artifacts have no lifecycle. V4 therefore treats untracked files as first-class release blockers until classified.

##### External File-Closure Baselines For V4

| Baseline | V4 import into Kolm |
|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS, `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | Owner review must be enforceable or generated for sensitive paths; invalid CODEOWNERS lines cannot be silently trusted. |
| GitHub ignoring files, `https://docs.github.com/ignore-files?platform=windows` | Local-only outputs need ignore or archive rules; a clean release tree cannot depend on developer-local clutter being harmless. |
| Git gitignore docs, `https://git-scm.com/docs/gitignore.html` | Ignore precedence and per-repository exclude rules matter; V4 must distinguish shared ignore policy from local-only excludes. |
| npm package.json docs, `https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json` | Package `files`, `bin`, `engines`, `repository`, and package metadata affect what ships; release closure must verify the package content boundary, not just source files. |
| GitHub Actions token permissions, `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/github_token-reference` | Workflow permissions are release-critical code. Any workflow without least-privilege review stays open in the file ledger. |

##### Atomic File Review State Machine

Every release-candidate path must enter exactly one state:

| State | Meaning | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| `keep` | File is product/source/config/docs/test code intended to remain. | Owner node, purpose, proof class, test/smoke/docs links, release role. |
| `split` | File is too large, mixed-purpose, or unreviewable in current shape. | Split plan, risk, interim owner, deadline, tests before/after split. |
| `delete` | File is obsolete, duplicate, misleading, unsafe, or dead. | Deletion reason, references removed, route/docs/search impact checked. |
| `archive` | File is useful history/evidence but not product runtime. | Archive path, retention, search/discovery exclusion, no stale public claim. |
| `generated` | File is output from a canonical generator. | Generator path, input hashes, output hash, freshness command, race-safety class. |
| `fixture` | File is test/demo/benchmark sample. | Fixture owner, privacy/license review, deterministic expected result, scope limit. |
| `external_gate` | File represents capability blocked by package/cert/partner/public benchmark. | Closeout ticket, blocker, proof required, public-copy restriction. |
| `accepted_risk` | File ships with known debt. | Owner, mitigation, expiry, customer impact, rollback/remediation, release signoff. |

No state may be inferred from folder name alone. For example, a public HTML file can be product code, generated output, archive, or delete candidate depending on its route, claim scope, screenshot evidence, and discovery status.

##### File Review Record Schema V4

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.file-review.v4",
  "path": "public/example.html",
  "owner_node": "44",
  "secondary_nodes": ["45", "32"],
  "state": "keep",
  "release_role": "public_product_surface",
  "source_class": "manual|generated|fixture|package|workflow|config|runtime|doc|report",
  "product_surface": "public-docs-sdk",
  "routes_or_commands": [],
  "dependencies": {
    "reads": [],
    "writes": [],
    "generates": [],
    "generated_by": null
  },
  "quality_proof": {
    "syntax": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "unit": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "integration": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "smoke": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "screenshot": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "accessibility": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "security": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "privacy": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "claim_scope": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "performance": "pass|fail|not_applicable",
    "production": "pass|fail|not_applicable"
  },
  "review": {
    "reviewer": null,
    "reviewed_at": null,
    "notes_path": null,
    "risk_id": null,
    "expiry": null
  },
  "release_decision": "include|exclude|block|archive|delete_after_split",
  "evidence_paths": []
}
```

##### Root-Specific Closure Rules

| Root | Primary closure rule |
|---|---|
| `src` | Every module must map to product kernel, route/API, worker/runtime, auth/billing/privacy/security, storage/cloud, eval/distill/compile, or utility ownership. Dead or duplicate modules get delete/split tickets. |
| `scripts` | Every script must declare whether it is read-only, generator, destructive, release-critical, CI-only, local-only, or deprecated. Generated-output scripts need serial order and output locks. |
| `tests` | Every test file must map to wave ID, node ID, product requirement, or regression class. Obsolete tests must be deleted, not kept as false confidence. |
| `public` | Every page/script/style/media asset must map to a route, component system, generated asset, archive, or delete candidate. Public files need screenshot/SEO/claim proof where user-visible. |
| `docs` | Every doc must be quickstart, reference, research, evidence, policy, generated ledger, or archive. Docs that support public claims need source freshness and claim-scope proof. |
| `cli` | The CLI must be decomposed into command domains even if code remains in one file temporarily; every command needs help, docs, JSON output where relevant, smoke, and API/account parity. |
| `packages` | Every package must declare publish state: published, local-only, dry-run-ready, external-gated, deprecated, or internal. Package metadata must match brand/pricing/docs truth. |
| `sdk` | Every SDK file must map to language build, examples, docs, smoke, package release, or archive. Source-only SDKs cannot be marketed as installable packages. |
| `apps` | Every app must map to product surface, demo, internal tool, archive, or delete. Demo apps must not become stale public proof. |
| `workers` | Every worker must have job envelope, idempotency, resource budget, readiness, logs, retry/dead-letter behavior, smoke, and deploy target. |
| `.github` | Every workflow/action must have owner, trigger, token permissions, secret access, pinned dependency state, artifact retention, and release-blocking role. |
| `infra` | Every infra file must map to deployment target, DNS/runtime/env, rollback, security, and production proof. |

##### Build Redline Sequence V4

The final build redline must run in this order after all active implementation agents stop:

1. Capture dirty/untracked path list and classify every path.
2. Generate or refresh the file-review ledger.
3. Run generated artifacts serially: product graph, readiness closeout, API ref, OpenAPI, codegraph, SDK version, CLI docs, sitemap/discovery assets, screenshots.
4. Re-run diff classification and prove generated outputs are expected.
5. Run syntax checks for JS/CJS/MJS/TS/Python/Rust/Swift/Kotlin/config files by available toolchain.
6. Run product truth gates: kernel, journeys, surfaces, SOTA/readiness, claim scope, brand, package release, invention/frontier gates.
7. Run full tests and focused high-risk suites.
8. Run local surface smoke and deep local surface smoke.
9. Run UI audit after frontend files settle.
10. Run release verify from the frozen tree.
11. Build the build-redline packet and dashboard.
12. Deploy only the frozen commit/artifact.
13. Run production finality Node `29` gates.
14. Archive the packet, screenshots, smoke, logs, generated hashes, and decision.

If any step changes a release-candidate file after freeze, return to step 1.

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-BUILD-V4-001` | `43.1` | Create `docs/source-completion-ledger.json` V4 schema. | Schema validates file state, owner node, release role, proof class, generated metadata, risk, and evidence paths. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-002` | `43.1` | Generate the initial all-path ledger. | Every tracked release-candidate path and every untracked release-relevant path appears exactly once. |

| `W-BUILD-V4-004` | `43.1` | Add root policy verifier. | `src`, `scripts`, `tests`, `public`, `docs`, `cli`, `packages`, `sdk`, `apps`, `workers`, `.github`, and `infra` have root-specific closure rules enforced. |
