| `W-BUILD-V4-015` | `43.5` | Add exception expiry enforcement. | Accepted risks and external gates must have owner, expiry, mitigation, public-copy restriction, and next verification command. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-016` | `43.5` | Add final clean-tree verifier. | No dirty/untracked release-relevant path remains before `production_closed`; ignored local-only paths are documented. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-017` | `43.6` | Add build-redline dashboard. | Dashboard shows root counts, state counts, open blockers, changed paths, generated freshness, screenshots, production smoke, and release decision. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-018` | `43.6` | Add per-file proof drilldown. | A reviewer can open any file row and see why it exists, who owns it, what proves it, and what would block release. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-019` | `43.6` | Add final all-node closure join. | Nodes `0` through `51` have no orphan files, orphan routes, orphan pages, hidden external gates, or stale generated artifacts. |
| `W-BUILD-V4-020` | `43.6` | Add codebase completion certificate. | The certificate is emitted only when all file states, generated outputs, tests, screenshots, production smoke, claim scope, and external gates are resolved or explicitly blocked. |

##### Node 43 V4 Relax Redline

Node `43` V4 is relaxed only when:

- every tracked and release-relevant untracked path appears exactly once in the file-review ledger;
- every file has one state, one primary owner node, one release decision, and proof appropriate to its role;
- every dirty path is committed, reverted by its owner, removed, archived, or allowlisted with expiry before finality;
- every untracked generated/local artifact is classified as evidence, ignored scratch, archive, or deletion candidate;
- every generated file has generator/freshness/hash proof;
- every public page, account page, docs page, CLI command, API route, package, SDK, worker, workflow, and infra file joins to the master tree;
- every accepted risk has owner, mitigation, expiry, release signoff, and public-copy restriction;
- the build-redline dashboard can explain the repository state without requiring chat memory.

If any file cannot answer why it exists, who owns it, and what proves it is ready, the codebase is not 100 percent complete.

#### Node 43 V5 Release-Candidate Path Resolver, Artifact Quarantine, And Clean-Tree Operating Ledger

Node `52` remains unused. This is still node `43` work because the unresolved issue is codebase finality: which files are source, which files are generated, which files are retained evidence, which files are dependency outputs, which files are local scratch, and which files are delete or repair candidates. Node `14` owns source quality policy, node `22` owns supply-chain artifacts, node `40` owns QA architecture, node `46` owns the master tree, and node `43` owns the final build redline that decides whether the current repository can ship.

V4 made every file reviewable. V5 makes the path set itself reviewable. A final build cannot start from "all files under the repo" because this tree contains reports, screenshots, local temp output, build products, SDK targets, data snapshots, and malformed path artifacts. It also cannot start from "git tracked only" because release-relevant untracked evidence and dirty files can change the deployment, the screenshots, the docs, and the proof packet. The resolver must define the path universe before any "100 percent" claim.

##### Node 43 V5 Current Evidence Recheck

The current scan excluding `.git` and `node_modules` found a very large non-dependency working tree:

| Root | Files | Bytes | V5 decision |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `reports` | 95,988 | 11,302,406,649 | Retained evidence or external artifact storage, not normal source. Needs retention policy and release-packet index. |
| `tmp` | 10,652 | 2,549,909,578 | Local scratch unless promoted to evidence. Cannot be release-candidate by default. |
| `sdk` | 4,949 | 1,593,155,744 | Mixed source and build output. Needs package/source split and generated/dependency-output exclusions. |
| `data` | 3,576 | 752,883,110 | Mixed product data, observations, temp backups, and fixtures. Needs data-class and retention decisions. |
| `tmp-screenshots` | 3,197 | 636,588,184 | Local visual scratch unless promoted into a named screenshot report. |
| `public` | 1,132 | 64,617,317 | Release-candidate product surface. Needs page, asset, route, SEO, screenshot, and claim ownership. |
| `.tmp` | 967 | 4,340,444 | Local scratch unless promoted. |
| `tests` | 567 | 7,319,376 | Release-candidate QA source. Needs requirement/node mapping. |
| `archive` | 455 | 10,436,894 | Historical evidence. Must not leak into current claims without explicit route. |
| `src` | 392 | 6,540,534 | Release-candidate source. Needs owner/proof/security state. |
| `scripts` | 343 | 25,958,930 | Release infrastructure. Needs write-set and generator classification. |
| `_audit` | 186 | 28,533,923 | Evidence or scratch, not normal source. Needs retention decision. |
| `apps` | 170 | 1,596,253 | Mixed product apps and tools. Needs build/smoke/release inclusion. |
| `docs` | 158 | 9,284,397 | Product docs, research, evidence, and policy. Needs page/claim/source classification. |
| `packages` | 139 | 380,618 | Package source and manifests. Needs install/publish state. |
| `qa` | 92 | 89,027,228 | Visual/eval evidence. Needs release-bundle policy. |
| `audit-shots` | 54 | 30,400,361 | Screenshot evidence. Needs report index or archive policy. |
| `workers` | 43 | 346,161 | Release-candidate worker source. Needs deploy/smoke/runtime proof. |
| `.w850-shots` | 29 | 11,070,641 | Untracked local screenshot evidence. Must be committed as evidence, archived, ignored, or deleted before finality. |

Largest extension classes in the same scan:

| Extension class | Count | V5 decision |
|---|---:|---|
| `.png` | 100,178 | Mostly screenshots/evidence/media. Cannot be normal source; needs manifest, retention, compression, and artifact-storage policy. |
| no extension | 6,159 | Must be classified because extensionless files often include executables, lockfiles, generated outputs, scripts, or malformed artifacts. |
| `.json` | 5,222 | Mixed source config, generated state, observations, fixtures, reports, and package metadata. Needs generated/manual/data-class labels. |
| `.html` | 1,706 | Public/docs/account product surface or generated output. Needs route/page owner and screenshot/SEO state. |
| `.js` | 1,592 | Mixed runtime, public UI, workers, scripts, generated/vendor. Needs lint/source-class boundaries. |
| `.timestamp` | 1,377 | Build/runtime output. Should be excluded or retained only by evidence policy. |
| `.d`, `.rmeta`, `.rlib`, `.o`, `.exe` | 3,221 combined | Compiled dependency/build output. Must not be mistaken for source closure. |
| `.log`, `.bak`, `.txt` | 1,279 combined | Mixed evidence, temporary logs, backups, and text artifacts. Need retention and secret/data review. |
| `.md` | 720 | Product docs, research, evidence, and plans. Need current/archive/claim classification. |
| `.mjs`, `.cjs`, `.py` | 694 combined | Release automation, workers, ML tooling, and tests. Need script contract and toolchain proof. |

Current control gaps are concrete:

| Control | Current observed state | V5 implication |
|---|---|---|
| CODEOWNERS | No `CODEOWNERS`, `.github/CODEOWNERS`, or `docs/CODEOWNERS` exists. | Owner review is not platform-enforced. A generated owner verifier is required before final release if CODEOWNERS is not added. |
| Source ledger | `docs/source-completion-ledger.json` is absent. | Per-file completion remains a spec, not an enforceable artifact. |
| File ledger | `docs/file-ledger.json` is absent. | Node `14` and node `43` cannot relax. |
| Master tree JSON | `docs/master-spec-tree.json` is absent. | Node ownership is still document-defined, not machine-checkable. |
| Dirty public/deploy files | Current dirty paths include public CSS/JS/HTML, `src/intent.js`, and `vercel.json`. | No release should claim clean finality until frontend/deploy lanes settle and are reverified. |
| Untracked local artifacts | Current untracked paths include `.w850-shots`, site-failure text files with malformed Windows-path names, temp observation JSON files, and this blueprint. | Every untracked artifact needs promote, ignore, archive, repair, or delete decision before final clean-tree proof. |

##### V5 Imported Baselines

| Baseline | Current fact imported into node `43` | Kolm rule |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub CODEOWNERS, `https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners` | CODEOWNERS files are accepted in `.github/`, root, or `docs/`; GitHub uses the first in that order; files over 3 MB are not loaded; invalid lines are skipped; the CODEOWNERS file or `.github` directory should own itself for strong protection. | Kolm needs a compact CODEOWNERS file or a generated equivalent that is self-owned, validates syntax, covers release-critical paths, and fails on skipped/invalid owner rules. |
| Git gitignore docs, `https://git-scm.com/docs/gitignore.html` | Git ignore files specify intentionally untracked files; tracked files are not affected; shared ignore patterns belong in repository `.gitignore`, repo-local-only patterns in `$GIT_DIR/info/exclude`, and user-wide patterns in `core.excludesFile`. | Kolm must not rely on ignore rules to clean already tracked outputs. The release resolver must distinguish shared ignore policy, local scratch exclusion, and tracked artifact cleanup. |
| GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, `https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/authenticate-with-github_token` | GitHub recommends granting `GITHUB_TOKEN` only the minimum access required through workflow or job permissions. | Workflow files are not passive config. They stay open until permissions, secret exposure, artifact outputs, and trigger risks are reviewed in the file ledger. |

##### Release-Candidate Resolver Contract V5

Every file considered by the build redline must be assigned by `kolm.release_path_resolver.v5` before the file-review ledger is generated.

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.release_path_resolver.v5",
  "generated_at": "ISO-8601",
  "commit": "git-sha-or-dirty",
  "parallel_agents_active": true,
  "inputs": {
    "tracked_paths": "git ls-files",
    "dirty_paths": "git status --short",
    "untracked_paths": "git status --short",
    "filesystem_scan": "repo scan excluding declared dependency roots"
  },
  "path_classes": [
    "release_candidate_source",
    "generated_product_output",
    "retained_release_evidence",
    "local_scratch",
    "dependency_or_build_output",
    "archive_or_historical",
    "fixture_or_benchmark_data",
    "malformed_path_repair",
    "delete_candidate"
  ],
  "root_policy": {
    "src": "release_candidate_source",
    "public": "release_candidate_source_or_generated_product_output",
    "reports": "retained_release_evidence_or_local_scratch",
    "tmp": "local_scratch",
    "sdk": "mixed_source_and_dependency_output"
  },
  "outputs": {
    "file_review_ledger": "docs/source-completion-ledger.json",
    "artifact_quarantine": "docs/artifact-quarantine.json",
    "clean_tree_allowlist": "reports/build-redline/latest/dirty-path-allowlist.json"
  },
  "blockers": []
}
```

##### Artifact Quarantine Rings V5

The resolver must place every path into exactly one ring before release.

| Ring | Name | Examples | Release rule |
|---:|---|---|---|
| `0` | canonical source | `src`, `cli`, `workers`, release-critical `scripts`, package sources, workflows, infra | Must have owner node, proof class, tests/smoke, review state, and clean diff. |
| `1` | generated product output | OpenAPI, API docs, product graph, readiness closeout, generated docs/account pages, sitemap, SDK version | Must have generator, input/output hashes, serial order, and no manual drift. |
| `2` | retained release evidence | final screenshot reports, benchmark reports, release logs, SBOM/provenance, production smoke packets | Must be indexed by release ID, node, route/claim, source command, and retention period. |
| `3` | local scratch | `tmp`, `.tmp`, `tmp-screenshots`, transient logs, temporary observations, ad hoc debug output | Must be ignored, deleted, or explicitly promoted to ring `2`; cannot silently ship. |
| `4` | dependency/build output | compiled Rust artifacts, `.d`, `.rmeta`, `.rlib`, `.o`, `.exe`, dependency caches, package build folders | Excluded from source ledger unless the package artifact itself is intentionally shipped with provenance. |
| `5` | repair/delete candidates | malformed Windows-path artifacts, stale site-failure files, abandoned backups, obsolete screenshots, dead generated files | Must be repaired, archived with reason, or deleted before final clean-tree proof. |

No path can move from ring `3`, `4`, or `5` into a shipped release without a promotion record that names the owner, reason, proof command, and retention policy.

##### Path Resolver Rules V5

1. Start with `git ls-files`, then add dirty and untracked paths from `git status --short`, then add filesystem-only evidence roots that may affect release proof.
2. Exclude dependency roots only by declared policy, not by assumption. `node_modules` can be excluded; `sdk` cannot be excluded wholesale because it contains source and build output.
3. Treat generated public files as release candidates until a generator proves they are current.
4. Treat reports and screenshots as evidence only if they are linked from a release packet; otherwise classify them as local scratch or archive.
5. Treat malformed path names as release blockers until repaired, archived outside the release candidate set, or deleted by owner decision.
6. Treat `.bak`, `.log`, `.tmp`, `.timestamp`, and observation files as data/secret review candidates before retention.
7. Treat no-extension files as high ambiguity until classified by content, executable bit, route use, or generator.
8. Treat every workflow as source code with security review, not as configuration noise.
9. Treat every root-level file as release-impacting unless explicitly scoped as local scratch, evidence, archive, or delete candidate.
10. Fail finality if the resolver changes the path universe after freeze.

| `W-BUILD-V5-015` | `43.4` | Join resolver output to master spec nodes. | Every path class maps to a primary node and release role; node `52` stays unused unless a genuinely new branch appears. |
| `W-BUILD-V5-016` | `43.5` | Add human-reviewable release diff grouping. | Final diff groups source, generated outputs, docs, evidence, cleanup, package artifacts, and local scratch decisions separately. |
| `W-BUILD-V5-017` | `43.5` | Add data/secret review for retained logs and observations. | `.log`, `.bak`, `.tmp`, observation JSON, and text reports either pass secret/data scan or are removed from release evidence. |
| `W-BUILD-V5-018` | `43.6` | Add artifact storage handoff. | Heavy evidence roots can move to object storage or release bundle with manifest, hash, and public/private access scope. |
| `W-BUILD-V5-019` | `43.6` | Add final path closure dashboard. | Dashboard shows ring counts, root counts, extension counts, open blockers, malformed paths, dirty/untracked decisions, and retention volume. |
| `W-BUILD-V5-020` | `43.6` | Add release-candidate certificate input. | The codebase completion certificate consumes the resolver output and refuses to run on an unresolved path universe. |

##### Node `43` V5 Relax Redline

Node `43` V5 cannot relax until:

- the path resolver defines the exact release-candidate universe from tracked, dirty, untracked, generated, and evidence roots;
- every path is in exactly one quarantine ring;
- `reports`, `tmp`, `tmp-screenshots`, `.tmp`, `.w850-shots`, `_audit`, `qa`, `audit-shots`, `.shots`, `data`, and `sdk` mixed-output paths have explicit source/evidence/scratch/dependency/archive policy;
- no malformed Windows-path artifact remains release-relevant;
- no `.log`, `.bak`, `.tmp`, observation JSON, or no-extension artifact is retained without data/secret review;
- no screenshot/report evidence is retained without route/node/claim/commit/origin metadata;
- no workflow remains without least-privilege permission review and owner coverage;
- CODEOWNERS or an equivalent verifier covers release-critical paths;
- the final path-universe hash is stable through freeze, verification, deploy, and post-deploy proof.

The V5 standard is: before Kolm claims a finished build, it must know exactly which files are the product, which files prove the product, which files are local exhaust, and which files must leave the release.

### Product Narrative, Conversion Architecture, UX Writing, And Buyer-Decision Proof Ledger V1

Node `44` prevents the site from going in circles on copy. The current product is not "one AI API" only, not "audits" only, and not a vague "owned AI" slogan. Kolm has three product promises that must be visible everywhere:

1. Route and capture model traffic through one governed gateway.
2. Compile, distill, quantize, evaluate, and sign top-model behavior into `.kolm` artifacts.
3. Run governed enterprise models across cloud, VPC, local, browser, mobile, and edge devices.

The finished state is not "better words." The finished state is that every public page, docs page, account screen, CTA, metadata tag, demo, pricing block, enterprise handoff, and hidden SEO/test anchor uses the same product narrative, in the right depth for that surface, without jargon walls, unsupported claims, stale pricing, fake media, hidden contradiction, or multiple competing category definitions.

#### Current Copy And UX Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 44 redline |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage metadata | `public/index.html` currently has a long title with repeated brand/category phrasing and a description that tries to explain workbench, infrastructure, capture, distill, artifact, hardware, license, signing, and cost all at once. | The homepage needs one concise title, one concise description, one visible H1, and one first-screen promise that covers all three product surfaces. |
| Homepage H1 and hidden anchors | The homepage includes a hidden H1/SEO block with phrases such as "owned AI" and a visible H1 framed around compiling workload into an owned model. | Hidden anchors cannot carry stale or rejected positioning. SEO/test anchors must follow the same copy contract as visible UI. |
| Competing product frames | Current visible copy mixes "AI compiler", "open-source AI workbench", "frontier AI on your own infrastructure", "evidence-to-artifact compiler", "gateway", "studio", "wrapper", "compile and own", and "run anywhere". | Pick one category sentence, one short product definition, and one three-promise framework. All variants must be generated from that map. |
| Homepage density | The homepage contains many long proof sections, hidden recovery payloads, repeated technical claims, and deep implementation detail near conversion copy. | First screen and top sections must prioritize comprehension, then let proof expand progressively through demos, code comparison, receipts, docs, and benchmarks. |
| Pricing narrative | `public/pricing.html` includes the right Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise shape, but still carries hidden legacy plan aliases and copy that mixes gateway fees, compile credits, enterprise custom, and old business/starter anchors. | Pricing must explain who each plan is for, what product surface unlocks, what is metered, what is sales-scoped, and what legacy aliases mean without leaking old naming into buyer-facing copy. |
| Enterprise narrative | `public/enterprise.html` focuses on BYOC, SSO, BAA, receipts, deployment, and procurement, but mixes trial, SLA, certification, architecture review, and external review anchors. | Enterprise copy must distinguish implemented controls, available documents, certifications in flight, architecture review scope, live proof, and contact-sales path. |
| Product-page IA | Existing sections already identify weak pages, duplicate H1 risk, old-positioning references, no static video/iframe proof, hash links, and paragraph walls. | Node 44 makes those findings actionable through a copy map, page-type registry, and page narrative grammar. |
| User feedback | The user explicitly rejected "Turn model traffic into owned AI" and said the site is too wordy, jargon-heavy, incohesive, visually inconsistent, and not state of the art. | The copy system must ban vague slogan-only claims and require plain-language outcome, audience, proof, CTA, and limitation for every page. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Baseline | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google title link guidance | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link` | Page titles should be descriptive, concise, distinct, not keyword-stuffed, not boilerplate, and should align with the main visible title. Multiple equally prominent headings create ambiguity. |
| Google snippets and metadata | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/snippet` | Search snippets depend on page content and meta descriptions; descriptions should be page-specific, helpful, and aligned with visible content. |
| Baymard product-page UX research | `https://baymard.com/blog/product-page-usability-report-and-benchmark` | Product pages fail when layouts and content do not support different decision stages. Kolm pages must serve skeptics, evaluators, implementers, and buyers without forcing all of them through one wall of copy. |
| UI/UX Pro Max guidance | local skill `ui-ux-pro-max` | SaaS/operational tools should be quiet, dense, predictable, accessible, professional, and purpose-built; avoid ornamental dashboards, hover-only controls, inconsistent nav states, jargon-first panels, and raw color/style drift. |
| Plain-language UX principle | `https://www.plainlanguage.gov/guidelines/` | Copy should use familiar words, short sections, user-centered headings, and direct action language. Technical detail is valuable only after the user understands the job and outcome. |

#### Canonical Product Narrative Map

Every Kolm surface must derive visible copy from this map:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.product-narrative.v1",
  "category": "AI compiler and runtime control plane",
  "short_definition": "Kolm turns production AI traffic into signed .kolm artifacts you can evaluate, run, and govern anywhere.",
  "promises": [
    {
      "id": "route-capture",
      "plain_name": "Route and capture model traffic",
      "buyer_outcome": "Switch providers, control spend, redact data, and keep receipts without rewriting every app.",
      "proof": ["gateway routes", "capture lake", "privacy membrane", "provider catalog", "drop-in SDK examples"],
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/gateway", "/capture", "/integrations", "/account/captures"]
    },
    {
      "id": "compile-distill",
      "plain_name": "Compile and distill specialist artifacts",
      "buyer_outcome": "Turn the work your app already does into smaller, cheaper, signed models with eval evidence.",
      "proof": ["distill jobs", "K-Score", "quantization", "artifact signing", "benchmark reports"],
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/compile", "/distill", "/forge", "/benchmarks", "/account/distill-runs"]
    },
    {
      "id": "run-govern",
      "plain_name": "Run and govern models anywhere",
      "buyer_outcome": "Deploy governed AI across cloud, VPC, local devices, browser, mobile, and edge without provider lock-in.",
      "proof": ["runtime catalog", "device fit", "BYOC", "storage readiness", "audit export", "enterprise controls"],
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/runtimes", "/devices", "/enterprise", "/security", "/account/storage"]
    }
  ],
  "banned_hero_patterns": [
    "owned AI without a concrete mechanism",
    "one AI API as the whole product",
    "AI OS unless defined and proved",
    "best in the world without benchmark scope",
    "certified without live certification evidence"
  ],
  "required_first_screen_answers": [
    "what Kolm is",
    "who should use it",
    "what changes after install",
    "why now",
    "what proof the visitor can inspect immediately"
  ]
}
```

This map should be machine-readable and imported by homepage, product pages, docs landing, pricing, enterprise, account overview, CLI help summaries, API docs, OpenGraph, structured data, and sales packets.

#### Page Narrative Grammar

Every page type gets a required narrative order:

| Page type | First screen must answer | Required proof below fold | Anti-patterns |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | What Kolm is, who it is for, the three promises, and the first proof action. | Interactive `.kolm` runner or recorded product flow, side-by-side code swap, ROI calculator, product-surface map, proof receipts, social/customer proof or quote cards. | Abstract slogan, multiple H1s, paragraph wall, hidden stale SEO anchors, one-surface story. |
| Product overview | The product matrix and how the surfaces work together. | Surface-to-route map, account screenshots, code examples, artifact lifecycle, readiness scope. | Feature inventory without outcomes, generic cards, repeating homepage. |
| Gateway/capture | Drop-in route and capture outcome. | Before/after SDK examples, privacy labels, provider matrix, receipts, error modes. | Only "one API" copy, no redaction or cost proof. |
| Compile/distill | How captured work becomes a signed artifact. | Pipeline steps, eval/K-Score report, quantization/runtime target, failure modes, rollback. | Model-research jargon before user outcome, unsupported quality claims. |
| Runtime/device | Where artifacts run and what limits apply. | Device fit table, memory/latency profiles, supported runtimes, import/export caveats. | "Run anywhere" without hardware/version limits. |
| Pricing | Which plan fits which buyer and product surface. | Meters, quotas, compile credits, enterprise review, ROI calculator, FAQ, billing API parity. | Legacy plan names, fake self-serve enterprise, unexplained credit units. |
| Enterprise | Why procurement can trust and deploy it. | SSO/SCIM, BAA/DPA, BYOC, residency, audit export, readiness closeout, architecture review. | Certification overclaim, vague trust badges, no contact-sales path. |
| Docs landing | Which job the developer came to do. | Quickstarts by job, API/CLI/SDK tabs, examples, last-verified status. | Docs organized only by internal feature names. |
| Account page | What is happening in this tenant and what to do next. | Live data, loading/empty/error states, readiness and closeout tokens, action history, support/export. | Decorative metrics, hidden local fallbacks, dead CTAs. |
| Research/benchmark | What is invented, measured, shipped, or external. | Methodology, raw reports, sources, scope labels, reproduction command. | Research claims presented as shipped product. |

#### UX Writing Rules

- Lead with the user outcome, then name the mechanism, then show proof.
- Use one concrete noun for each product surface: gateway, compiler, runtime.
- Use `.kolm artifact` only after defining what it is: a signed portable model/workflow bundle with receipts and eval evidence.
- Prefer "route", "capture", "compile", "distill", "evaluate", "sign", "run", "govern", and "export" over vague verbs like "own", "transform", "unlock", or "supercharge".
- Avoid paragraphs over 70 words in first-screen or conversion sections.
- Do not repeat the same category sentence in title, H1, meta description, OG title, visible eyebrow, and hidden anchors.
- Do not use hidden SEO/test anchors to preserve stale claims that visible copy removed.
- CTAs must state the next action: "Run the demo", "Compile a sample artifact", "Calculate savings", "Read the eval report", "Book architecture review".
- Every claim with a number must link to a benchmark, receipt, product graph, pricing source, or readiness gate.
- Every compliance, certification, benchmark, package, ecosystem, and "state of the art" claim must pass the claim-scope gate before appearing in public copy.
- Error, empty, loading, and unavailable states should be calm and operational: say what failed, what data is missing, and what action restores progress.

#### Conversion Component Contract

| Component | Product role | Required states and proof |
|---|---|---|
| Hero | Explain category and three promises in under five seconds. | One visible H1, concise lede, one primary CTA, one secondary proof CTA, real product visual, dark/light screenshots, mobile crop proof. |
| Interactive `.kolm` runner | Create the first "aha" moment. | Sample input, paste-your-call path, compile output, artifact summary, loading/error/privacy labels, no secret logging, screenshot/video proof. |
| Side-by-side code comparison | Show low migration cost. | Before OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter call, after Kolm base URL or SDK swap, output artifact, caveats, copy buttons, language tabs. |
| ROI calculator | Turn cost savings into buyer evidence. | Current spend input, provider mix, capture volume, compile credits, runtime target, assumptions, export/share link, plan recommendation. |
| Product surface matrix | Prevent one-surface misunderstanding. | Gateway, compiler, runtime columns with outcomes, owner pages, account links, API/CLI proof, readiness labels. |
| Proof receipts | Make claims inspectable. | Artifact hash, K-Score, benchmark date, model/runtime version, command, environment, raw report link. |
| Video explainer | Help technical buyers understand sequence quickly. | 90-second max, captions, transcript, poster image, light/dark visibility, VideoObject structured data, no autoplay audio. |
| Social proof or quote cards | Reduce enterprise risk. | Customer/design-partner logo or scoped quote, role, use case, date, permission, fallback to transparent beta quote if no logo. |
| Enterprise sales handoff | Convert procurement without fake checkout. | Contact-sales CTA, architecture review intake, plan scope, docs packet, expected response time, CRM/support handoff. |

#### Narrative Redlines

- No page may carry more than one primary product category statement.
- No first screen may require reading more than one paragraph to know what Kolm does.
- No homepage hero may optimize for only one of the three promises.
- No public page may keep stale hidden copy, hidden H1s, hidden anchors, or recovery payloads that contradict visible copy.
- No marketing page may use "owned AI", "AI OS", "best", "state of the art", "certified", "run anywhere", or "one API" without exact mechanism and claim-scope proof.
- No page may use the same CTA label for different actions or different CTA labels for the same action.
- No proof component may be decorative. If it looks like a demo, calculator, receipt, chart, video, or code example, it must work or display a visible scoped fallback.
- No account page may use marketing copy where the user expects operational state.
- No docs page may use sales copy where the user expects commands, inputs, outputs, errors, and versions.
- No pricing page may hide what is metered, what is custom, what is legacy alias, or what requires sales review.
- No enterprise page may imply SOC 2 Type II, ISO, HIPAA, FedRAMP, or AI Act certification unless the readiness ledger links live evidence.
- No SEO title or description may be a pile of keywords, duplicated brand, repeated boilerplate, or a phrase that does not match the visible H1.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `44.1` Category definition, three-promise positioning, audience segmentation, anti-jargon rules, and proof hierarchy | A machine-readable narrative map defines category, short definition, three product promises, primary audiences, banned slogans, proof hierarchy, and allowed claim language. |
| `44.2` Page narrative architecture for homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs, account, verticals, marketplace, and research | Every page has a page type, audience, first-screen answer, primary CTA, secondary proof action, required proof component, and known anti-patterns. |
| `44.3` UX writing system for titles, descriptions, H1s, CTAs, microcopy, code examples, empty states, limits, and claim scope | Titles, descriptions, H1s, CTAs, microcopy, code examples, empty/loading/error states, and hidden anchors are generated or linted against the same copy rules. |
| `44.4` Conversion components: hero, demo runner, code comparison, ROI calculator, proof bars, video, use-case pages, and sales handoff | The conversion system has working components with source ownership, states, accessibility, analytics events, screenshots, transcripts where needed, and claim-scope proof. |
| `44.5` Measurement: five-second tests, comprehension tests, search snippets, funnels, event taxonomy, experiments, and qualitative review | Copy and UX decisions are verified by five-second comprehension, task success, search snippets, funnel events, experiment guardrails, session review, and manual expert QA. |
| `44.6` Evidence: copy map, page-type registry, metadata report, screenshot/video/demo proof, customer research, and production telemetry | Release evidence includes copy map, page registry, metadata report, screenshots, demo/video proof, customer research notes, telemetry, experiment decisions, and claim-gate output. |

#### Node 44 Relax Redline

Node `44` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Can a new visitor explain in five seconds what Kolm is, who it is for, why now, and the three product promises?
- Does every page use the same product narrative while adapting depth, CTA, and proof to its audience?
- Are titles, meta descriptions, H1s, OG/Twitter tags, JSON-LD, hidden anchors, footers, nav labels, and CTAs aligned with visible copy?
- Do homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs, account, verticals, marketplace, and research pages each have a page type, audience, primary action, proof module, and limitation labels?
- Does every conversion component work in light/dark, desktop/mobile, loading/error/empty states, keyboard/touch, and reduced-motion contexts?
- Do demos, videos, calculators, proof receipts, code comparisons, and quote cards show real product states or honest scoped fallbacks?
- Do analytics and qualitative review prove comprehension, activation, demo engagement, pricing self-qualification, docs success, and enterprise handoff?
- Can claim-scope gates block unsupported slogans before they appear in visible copy, metadata, hidden anchors, screenshots, or sales packets?

The relax condition for node `44` is not "the copy sounds better." It is: Kolm has one product narrative map, one page-type registry, one UX writing system, one conversion component contract, and one evidence packet proving that every public, docs, account, pricing, enterprise, media, demo, and hidden metadata surface communicates the right product clearly, truthfully, accessibly, and measurably.

#### Node 44 Narrative Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` stays unused here. The user's complaint that the site still feels wordy, jargon-heavy, incohesive, visually inconsistent, and overly focused on only one product surface is exactly node `44` territory. The codebase needs one narrative operating system that governs visible copy, hidden metadata, conversion components, CTAs, product-page structure, account state copy, and proof labels.

The V2 goal is not another homepage rewrite. It is a source-of-truth system that prevents the homepage, pricing, enterprise, docs, account, OpenGraph, structured data, hidden SEO anchors, demos, videos, calculators, and sales handoffs from drifting into different definitions of Kolm.

##### Current Public-Copy Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V2 interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage title and description | `public/index.html` still uses a long title with repeated brand/category language and a 216-character description that tries to cover workbench, frontier AI, capture, distill, artifact, hardware, license, signing, and cost. | The homepage needs one crisp category, one buyer outcome, and one proof action. Search metadata must not carry every product claim. |
| Homepage hidden anchors | `public/index.html` still contains hidden copy with phrases like "owned AI", "AI compiler", "OpenAI-compatible gateway", and multiple category variants. | Hidden copy is product copy. It must pass the same narrative and claim gates as visible copy. Hidden stale anchors are a release blocker. |
| Homepage first-screen story | The visible H1 still emphasizes "compile your AI workload into a model you own", while the site also tries to sell routing/capture and runtime/device deployment. | The hero must represent all three surfaces: gateway, compiler, and runtime. A one-surface H1 creates false positioning. |
| Dense copy blocks | The homepage contains very long proof sections and hidden product paragraphs that read like internal architecture notes rather than buyer-facing hierarchy. | V2 requires progressive disclosure: first screen says what/for whom/why now; proof components carry depth; docs carry exhaustive detail. |
| Metadata drift | Public page metadata scan found long titles/descriptions and non-ASCII separator artifacts in many titles. | Metadata needs a generator/linter with ASCII-safe separators, length budgets, unique titles, and visible-H1 alignment. |
| Placeholder and trust risk | `public/about.html` still includes founder-photo and founder-bio placeholders. | Placeholder identity/trust copy cannot remain on a production-grade enterprise site without a scoped fallback. |
| Legacy pricing and plan aliases | Scan found legacy `starter` and `business` aliases in public/admin/API-adjacent visible or semi-visible copy. | Buyer-facing pricing must use Free, Pro, Team, Enterprise. Legacy aliases belong only in compatibility docs/API fields with explicit mapping. |
| Old repo/brand links | Many public pages still link to `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack`; some FAQ copy uses "Kolmogorov tradition". | Open-source repo links may be intentional, but brand-facing copy must decide whether this is public heritage, source path, or stale brand drift. |
| Demo/video risk | Prior passes and current scan show video/demo references, but narrative proof depends on actual media state, transcript, poster, and light/dark visibility. | V2 treats demos, videos, calculators, and code examples as claims. Broken or decorative proof components are blockers. |
| Account copy risk | Account pages mix operational states, marketing language, key placeholders, and product matrix copy. | Post-auth copy must be operational: live state, empty/error/loading, next action, readiness, and no public-marketing slogans. |

##### External Baseline Added For V2

| Baseline | Source | What V2 imports into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Google title-link guidance | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/title-link` | Titles must be concise, descriptive, distinct, not keyword-stuffed, not repeated boilerplate, and aligned with the main visible title. |
| Google snippet and meta-description guidance | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/snippet` | Descriptions should be page-specific, helpful, and aligned with visible content; they should summarize the page rather than stuff keywords. |
| Google VideoObject structured data | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/video` | Product demo videos need stable metadata, thumbnail/poster, duration/upload data where relevant, and accessible playback/transcript proof. |
| Digital.gov plain-language guidance | `https://www.plainlanguage.gov/guidelines/` | Copy must use familiar words, direct headings, short sections, user-centered organization, and enough context for action. |
| Nielsen Norman usability heuristics | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/` | Pages must match the real world, be consistent, prevent errors, support recognition over recall, and expose system status. |
| Baymard product-page research | `https://baymard.com/blog/product-page-usability-report-and-benchmark` | Product pages fail when content does not support buyer decision stages. Kolm pages need summary, proof, comparison, details, and purchase path without forcing all users through one text wall. |
| W3C WCAG link purpose | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/link-purpose-in-context.html` | CTA and link labels must make destination and action clear in context. Reusing "Get started" for unrelated actions is not acceptable. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | local skill `ui-ux-pro-max` | Enterprise SaaS UX should be predictable, dense but scannable, accessible, stateful, and free of hover-only controls, ornamental dashboards, inconsistent nav states, and jargon-first copy. |

##### V2 Product Narrative Source Of Truth

All public, docs, account, CLI/API summaries, metadata, and sales handoff copy must derive from this single narrative contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.product-narrative.v2",
  "category": "AI compiler and runtime control plane",
  "plain_definition": "Kolm captures production AI calls, compiles the useful behavior into signed .kolm artifacts, and runs those artifacts across approved infrastructure.",
  "first_screen_answer": {
    "what": "a gateway, compiler, and runtime for production AI",
    "who": "teams shipping AI features that need lower cost, portability, eval proof, and governance",
    "why_now": "frontier model traffic is becoming too expensive, hard to audit, and too locked to one provider",
    "proof_action": "run a demo, inspect a receipt, compare code, or calculate savings"
  },
  "product_surfaces": [
    {
      "id": "gateway",
      "name": "Route and capture",
      "outcome": "Use one governed entry point for model calls, provider switching, redaction, receipts, and spend control.",
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/capture", "/gateway", "/integrations", "/account/captures"],
      "proof": ["drop-in SDK swap", "provider matrix", "capture receipt", "privacy label", "latency/cost counters"]
    },
    {
      "id": "compiler",
      "name": "Compile and distill",
      "outcome": "Turn approved AI behavior into smaller signed artifacts with K-Score, benchmark, and rollback evidence.",
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/compile", "/distill", "/forge", "/benchmarks", "/account/distill-runs"],
      "proof": ["pipeline receipt", "K-Score envelope", "teacher/student comparison", "quantization report", "artifact signature"]
    },
    {
      "id": "runtime",
      "name": "Run and govern",
      "outcome": "Deploy governed artifacts across cloud, VPC, local, browser, mobile, and edge targets with visible limits.",
      "primary_pages": ["/", "/runtimes", "/devices", "/enterprise", "/security", "/account/storage"],
      "proof": ["runtime matrix", "device fit table", "BYOC plan", "readiness tokens", "audit export"]
    }
  ],
  "hero_h1_contract": {
    "must_cover": ["gateway", "compiler", "runtime"],
    "must_not_use": ["owned AI as standalone", "one AI API as whole product", "run anywhere without limits", "state of the art without benchmark scope"],
    "acceptable_shapes": [
      "Compile production AI into signed artifacts you can run on approved infrastructure.",
      "Route, compile, and run production AI with evidence you can keep.",
      "Turn live model traffic into signed, portable AI artifacts."
    ]
  },
  "copy_depth": {
    "hero": "one H1, one short lede, one primary CTA, one proof CTA",
    "top_section": "three surfaces with one outcome and one proof each",
    "proof_section": "receipts, code, demo, benchmark, or calculator",
    "docs": "commands, inputs, outputs, errors, versions",
    "account": "live state, next action, limits, readiness, errors"
  }
}
```

The important correction is that "own" is not the category. If ownership is mentioned, the copy must define the mechanism: signed artifact, receipt chain, eval evidence, runtime portability, export, and no hidden provider dependency.

##### V2 Metadata And SEO Contract

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `title` | 35 to 65 characters for most pages; unique; ASCII separator; one brand mention; no repeated category fragments; visible H1 alignment. |
| `meta description` | 90 to 155 characters for most pages; page-specific; outcome first; no keyword pile; no unsupported claim; no repeated homepage description. |
| `H1` | Exactly one visible primary H1 per page unless a documented app shell exception exists; hidden H1s cannot carry stale phrases. |
| `og:title` and `twitter:title` | Same narrative as `title`, allowed to be shorter; no extra claims not visible on page. |
| `og:description` and `twitter:description` | Same claim scope as visible lede; no hidden benchmark, certification, or pricing claim. |
| `JSON-LD` | Must match visible copy and supported schema; VideoObject only when video has poster, transcript/caption plan, and load proof. |
| Canonical and sitemap | Must point to the page's intended public URL and page type; no duplicate keyword landing pages without canonical plan. |
| Hidden/test anchors | May support tests, not SEO stuffing. They must contain current narrative only and must not preserve rejected phrases. |
| Non-ASCII or mojibake markers | Block final release unless the page intentionally uses that language/script and the title/description language matches page content. |

##### V2 Page-Type Contract

| Page type | First-screen copy | Proof below fold | Redline |
|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | One H1 covering gateway, compiler, and runtime; one short lede; one demo/proof CTA. | Interactive runner, code comparison, ROI calculator, proof receipts, three-surface matrix, video with fallback. | No one-surface hero, no hidden stale slogans, no architecture essay in first screen. |
| Product overview | Show how the three surfaces work together and what each replaces. | Lifecycle diagram, account screenshots, API/CLI proof, readiness scope. | No feature inventory without buyer outcome. |
| Capture/gateway | Show drop-in migration and governed routing. | Before/after SDK snippets, provider matrix, privacy labels, error modes. | No "one API" as the whole product. |
| Compile/distill | Show capture-to-artifact transformation. | Distill strategy, K-Score, benchmark envelope, quantization, rollback. | No ML jargon before user outcome. |
| Runtime/device | Show where artifacts can run and what limits apply. | Device table, memory/latency, runtime support, unsupported ops. | No "run anywhere" without hardware/version limits. |
| Pricing | Show plan fit and metering by product surface. | Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise matrix, ROI calculator, legacy alias mapping in docs only, enterprise sales handoff. | No fake self-serve Enterprise, no stale Starter/Business buyer copy. |
| Enterprise | Show deployability and procurement path. | SSO/SCIM, BYOC, BAA/DPA, readiness ledger, architecture review, security packet. | No certification overclaim or vague trust badge. |
| Docs | Route by job to commands and APIs. | Quickstarts, SDK/CLI/API tabs, expected outputs, last verified date. | No sales copy where users need commands. |
| Account | Show tenant state and next action. | Live metrics, readiness, empty/error/loading, support/export, billing/account controls. | No marketing hero or decorative metrics. |
| Research/benchmark | Show what is measured, invented, shipped, or external. | Method, raw report, reproduction command, source list, scope labels. | No research claim presented as shipped product. |
| About/trust | Establish credible people, company, repository, policy, and evidence. | Founder/team information, roadmap, repo/source policy, trust docs, contact path. | No placeholder identity copy in production. |

##### V2 CTA Taxonomy

| CTA family | Allowed labels | Destination rule |
|---|---|---|
| Demo | `Run the demo`, `Compile a sample artifact`, `Try the live runner` | Goes to a working demo or scoped fallback with loading/error/privacy states. |
| Developer start | `Start the quickstart`, `Install the CLI`, `Read the API docs` | Goes to runnable docs with commands and expected output. |
| Proof | `Inspect a receipt`, `Read the eval report`, `View benchmark evidence` | Goes to evidence with raw report hash, date, and scope label. |
| Cost | `Calculate savings`, `Model your spend`, `Export ROI` | Goes to ROI calculator with assumptions and plan mapping. |
| Enterprise | `Book architecture review`, `Contact sales`, `Request security packet` | Goes to sales/review form, not a fake checkout. |
| Account | `Fix storage`, `Add provider key`, `Run readiness check`, `Export audit log` | Performs or opens the exact operational action named. |

Every CTA must map to a single action, auth state, analytics event, loading state, error state, and fallback state. "Get started" can remain only when the next action is truly generic onboarding.

##### V2 Content Density Rules

- Hero lede max: 35 words.
- First-screen total visible prose max: 120 words excluding navigation and code.
- Top-section paragraphs max: 55 words each.
- Proof-section paragraphs max: 70 words each before a table, code block, receipt, or diagram must carry detail.
- Hidden SEO/test anchors max: 25 words and no unsupported claim.
- Account page explanatory copy max: one sentence before state/action controls.
- Docs intro max: one short paragraph before commands.
- Enterprise trust copy must separate "implemented control", "document available", "external review in progress", and "certification awarded".
- Long research or architecture explanations must move to docs/research or expandable details with visible summaries.

##### V2 Proof Component Rules

- A demo must have sample input, user input, loading state, error state, privacy label, output artifact summary, and screenshot/video proof.
- A video must have poster, duration, transcript or captions, light/dark visibility, reduced-motion fallback, and no autoplay audio.
- A code comparison must show before, after, copied state, language/provider tabs, and exact caveats.
- A calculator must show assumptions, inputs, computed outputs, plan recommendation, and export/share state.
- A proof receipt must show artifact hash, eval/K-Score scope, model/runtime version, command, date, raw report reference, and limitation label.
- A quote card must show permission state, role, company or design-partner scope, use case, and date. If no logo is approved, use transparent beta quotes.
- A trust badge must map to an implemented control or external credential; badges for pending certifications must be visually and textually scoped.

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-NARR-V2-001` | `44.1` | Generate `docs/product-narrative-map.v2.json`. | Category, plain definition, three surfaces, hero contract, banned patterns, proof hierarchy, and audience map are machine-readable. |
| `W-NARR-V2-002` | `44.1` | Add narrative linter. | Public/docs/account/metadata/hidden anchors fail on rejected slogans, one-surface hero copy, unsupported best/certified claims, or stale product definitions. |
| `W-NARR-V2-003` | `44.1` | Add audience-decision matrix. | Platform, ML, founder/CTO, security, procurement, support, and operator audiences each have outcome, objection, proof, CTA, and page path. |

| `W-NARR-V2-010` | `44.3` | Remove placeholder identity copy. | About/trust pages contain approved founder/company copy or a scoped transparent fallback, not "photo coming soon" production placeholders. |

| `W-NARR-V2-013` | `44.4` | Build interactive runner evidence contract. | Runner has sample input, paste path, output `.kolm` summary, privacy, loading/error, light/dark/mobile screenshot, and no secret logging. |
| `W-NARR-V2-014` | `44.4` | Build demo video evidence contract. | Video has poster, transcript/captions, structured data, theme proof, fallback, reduced motion, and load verification. |
| `W-NARR-V2-015` | `44.4` | Build code/ROI/proof component contracts. | Code comparison, ROI calculator, receipts, quote cards, and trust badges have data models, states, and claim-scope links. |
| `W-NARR-V2-016` | `44.5` | Add five-second comprehension lab. | Reviewers can identify what Kolm is, who it is for, three surfaces, and next proof action from first screen. |
| `W-NARR-V2-017` | `44.5` | Add funnel and event taxonomy. | Demo run, code copy, ROI use, docs start, signup, account activation, enterprise inquiry, and proof-inspection events are defined privacy-safely. |
| `W-NARR-V2-018` | `44.5` | Add search-result and social-card review. | Search snippet, OpenGraph, Twitter, VideoObject, canonical, and sitemap previews are reviewed for top pages. |
| `W-NARR-V2-019` | `44.6` | Emit `reports/narrative-redline/<date>/packet.json`. | Packet links narrative map, page registry, metadata report, CTA matrix, proof component evidence, screenshots, and claim-gate output. |
| `W-NARR-V2-020` | `44.6` | Add production narrative smoke. | Production top pages pass title/H1/meta/CTA/proof/media/claim/account/docs checks in light/dark and mobile/desktop. |

##### Node 44 V2 Relax Redline

Node `44` V2 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Homepage first screen covers gateway, compiler, and runtime without a one-surface hero.
- No visible or hidden copy preserves rejected standalone "owned AI" positioning.
- No public metadata contains mojibake, duplicate brand boilerplate, excessive descriptions, hidden stale claims, or unsupported category stuffing.
- Every top public/docs/account page has page type, audience, H1, title, description, CTA, proof module, product-surface owner, and claim-scope state.
- Every CTA says exactly what happens and does not reuse a generic label for incompatible actions.
- Every proof component works or shows an honest scoped fallback in light/dark, desktop/mobile, loading/error, and reduced-motion states.
- Every compliance, benchmark, pricing, package, runtime, and "best" claim maps to the claim-scope gate.
- Account copy is operational, docs copy is runnable, marketing copy is concise, and research copy is clearly separated from shipped product.
- Search snippets, social cards, structured data, screenshots, and production smoke all match the same narrative map.

If any of those fail, the words may be improved, but the product narrative is not yet complete.

#### Node 44 Narrative Convergence, Buyer Proof, And Copy Governance Addendum V3

This V3 branch keeps the master spec tree intact: node `52` stays unused because node `44` already owns product narrative, conversion architecture, UX writing, buyer-decision proof, CTA clarity, and metadata alignment. V2 defined the narrative operating system. V3 adds a stricter convergence layer so Kolm stops oscillating between "AI compiler", "open-source AI workbench", "frontier on your infrastructure", "one AI API", "owned AI", and "evidence-to-artifact compiler" without a page-specific reason and proof trail.

Current repo delta checked for V3:

| evidence | current observed state | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Copy density and repetition | A public HTML scan found major repeated terminology across `public/docs/api.html`, `public/index.html`, `public/compile.html`, `public/pricing.html`, `public/distill.html`, `public/product.html`, and many docs/articles pages. | Copy cleanup cannot be manual page-by-page polish. V3 requires a generated narrative graph that owns every repeated phrase, title, H1, CTA, footer tag, hidden anchor, and proof label. |
| Competing category lines | Current public/version evidence includes "AI compiler", "open-source AI workbench", "frontier AI on your own infrastructure", "model gateway, compiler, runtime", and older "owned AI" style positioning. | The site needs a primary category contract plus allowed page-local variants. Variants must say why they exist and what proof component supports them. |
| Footer and hidden anchors | Repeated footer tags and `data-test` anchors still preserve "open-source AI compiler" or similar claims across broad page sets. | Hidden/test/footer copy must be governed like visible copy because search, accessibility, QA, screenshots, and users can still encounter it. |
| Metadata surface | Top pages and article pages carry many keyword-rich titles, descriptions, keywords tags, and schema fields around "AI compiler", "compile", "run anywhere", and comparisons. | Metadata must be generated or linted against visible H1, page type, surface promise, claim scope, and Google title/snippet rules. |
| Demo and proof copy | `frontend-version.json` records many hero/demo rewrites, including user rejections and later staged copy decisions; current public copy still references demos, videos, receipts, ROI, benchmark proof, and compiler flows. | Narrative finality requires a proof-module registry: no demo/video/calculator/receipt/code comparison can appear in copy unless it has working states and evidence. |
| Account copy | Account footer and overview copy use the product triad, while account pages also contain readiness, storage, billing, and operational state copy. | Post-auth narrative must be operational first: live state, limits, readiness, error/empty/loading, next action, and support/export. Marketing slogans are exceptions. |
| Documentation copy | Docs pages use strong "the AI compiler" footer language and runnable CLI/API references. | Docs narrative should be job-first: command, input, output, errors, version, and last verified. Footer/category copy must not overwhelm task completion. |

External baseline delta for V3:

| source | current signal | Kolm completion rule |
|---|---|---|
| Google title-link guidance | Google uses title elements, visual titles, H1s, OG titles, prominent text, anchors, and links to form title links; titles should be concise, descriptive, distinct, and not repetitive or keyword-stuffed. | Kolm must align `<title>`, visible H1, OG/Twitter title, large visible text, hidden anchors, and nav labels so search does not synthesize stale or confusing category text. |
| Google snippet guidance | Snippets are generated from page content and sometimes meta descriptions when they are accurate and useful. | Meta descriptions must match visible page purpose and cannot hide claims that the page does not substantiate. |
| Google VideoObject guidance | Video results depend on structured video metadata such as thumbnail, description, upload date, duration, and eligible structured data. | Demo-video copy must be backed by poster/thumbnail, transcript or captions, load proof, duration, structured data, and fallback state. |
| WCAG 2.2 link purpose | Link purpose should be clear from link text or programmatically related context; same destinations should use consistent text, different destinations different text. | CTA labels and nav links must identify exact action and destination. Generic repeated "Get started" is allowed only for true onboarding. |
| Baymard product-page research | Product pages serve buyers at different decision stages, from domain novices to detail-ready evaluators. | Kolm pages must support first-time category comprehension, technical proof inspection, procurement trust, and direct implementation paths without one wall of copy. |
| Digital.gov heading guidance | Headings should be audience-structured, concise, descriptive, and limited in hierarchy depth. | Kolm section headings must be written as user questions/outcomes, not one-word feature labels or internal architecture nouns. |
| Plain-language guidance | Plain language is clear, concise, organized, and appropriate for the audience; use familiar words, headings, lists, and short sections. | Technical depth remains, but first-screen and conversion copy must use familiar verbs before product-specific nouns. |

Node `44` V3 target contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.narrative-convergence.v3",
  "page_path": "/",
  "page_type": "homepage|product|pricing|enterprise|docs|account|article|research|compare|vertical|demo|trust",
  "audience": ["platform_engineer", "ml_engineer", "cto", "security", "procurement", "operator"],
  "category_contract": {
    "primary": "AI compiler and runtime control plane",
    "allowed_variant": "gateway, compiler, and runtime for production AI",
    "variant_reason": "homepage must cover all three product surfaces",
    "banned_variants": ["one AI API as whole product", "owned AI as standalone", "run anywhere without limits"]
  },
  "first_screen": {
    "what": "Kolm routes AI calls, compiles useful behavior into signed artifacts, and runs them on approved infrastructure.",
    "who": "teams shipping production AI that need lower cost, portability, evaluation proof, and governance",
    "why_now": "AI traffic is getting expensive, provider-locked, and hard to audit",
    "proof_action": "run demo|inspect receipt|compare code|calculate savings",
    "word_budget": 120
  },
  "surface_promises": {
    "gateway": {"present": true, "proof_module": "code_swap"},
    "compiler": {"present": true, "proof_module": "artifact_receipt"},
    "runtime": {"present": true, "proof_module": "runtime_matrix"}
  },
  "metadata": {
    "title": "string",
    "description": "string",
    "h1": "string",
    "og_title": "string",
    "og_description": "string",
    "schema_types": []
  },
  "cta_graph": [
    {
      "label": "Run the demo",
      "action": "open_interactive_runner",
      "destination": "/demo",
      "auth_state": "anonymous",
      "analytics_event": "demo_opened",
      "loading_state": "defined",
      "error_state": "defined"
    }
  ],
  "proof_modules": [
    {
      "id": "artifact_receipt",
      "kind": "receipt|code|video|calculator|quote|benchmark|readiness",
      "source_path": "string",
      "claim_ids": [],
      "states": ["loaded", "empty", "error", "loading", "light", "dark", "mobile", "desktop"]
    }
  ],
  "copy_risk": {
    "jargon_score": 0,
    "paragraph_wall": false,
    "hidden_claim_mismatch": false,
    "unsupported_claims": [],
    "stale_metadata": false
  },
  "done_when": [
    "five-second comprehension passes",
    "metadata matches visible page",
    "CTA graph has one action per label",
    "proof modules render and scope claims"
  ]
}
```

Narrative convergence rules:

| rule | requirement |
|---|---|
| One global category | Default category is `AI compiler and runtime control plane`. Page-local variants can exist only with a recorded reason and proof module. |
| Three promises everywhere relevant | Homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs landing, account overview, and sales packets must cover gateway, compiler, and runtime. |
| One-surface pages must declare scope | Gateway, compile, distill, runtime, security, pricing, and vertical pages may emphasize one surface, but must show how it connects to the other two. |
| Hidden copy is copy | Hidden H1s, test anchors, recovery payloads, JSON-LD, OpenGraph, Twitter cards, footer tags, and version notes are linted against the same narrative map. |
| Outcome before mechanism | First use of a product noun must be preceded or followed by what changes for the buyer. |
| Proof before superlative | Words like "best", "state of the art", "frontier", "certified", "run anywhere", "zero per-token bill", and "40 percent savings" require evidence and scope. |
| Action-specific CTAs | A CTA label can map to exactly one action family; different destinations need different labels or visible context. |
| Docs are task-first | Docs pages start with job, command, input, output, version, and error modes. Product positioning belongs in sidebars or footers. |
| Account is operational | Account pages show state and next action. Marketing copy appears only where onboarding requires orientation. |

Buyer-decision graph:

| buyer stage | user question | page/system answer | proof required |
|---|---|---|---|
| First impression | What is Kolm? | Gateway, compiler, and runtime for production AI. | H1, lede, product visual, three-surface strip. |
| Relevance | Is this for my team? | Teams with production AI traffic, cost, portability, eval, or governance pressure. | Audience-specific routes, vertical pages, use-case cards. |
| Mechanism | How does it work? | Route/capture calls, compile/distill behavior, sign/evaluate artifacts, run on approved infrastructure. | Lifecycle diagram, code comparison, artifact receipt. |
| Trust | Is this real and safe? | Evidence packets, K-Score, readiness, security controls, limitations, and external gates. | Receipts, benchmark reports, readiness closeout, trust docs. |
| Cost | Why buy now? | Lower repeat API spend, fewer rewrites, portable artifacts, enterprise controls. | ROI calculator, pricing matrix, plan gates. |
| Implementation | Can I start? | Quickstart, SDK/CLI/API examples, local fixture mode, account next actions. | Runnable docs, demo runner, CLI smoke. |
| Procurement | Can we approve it? | Enterprise plan, sales handoff, BAA/DPA/security packet, BYOC, audit export. | Contact-sales flow, trust packet, architecture review. |

Metadata and page registry requirements:

- Every page gets `page_type`, `audience`, `surface_promise`, `primary_claim`, `proof_module`, `primary_cta`, `secondary_cta`, `title`, `description`, `h1`, `canonical`, `schema_types`, and `claim_scope`.
- Titles should be concise, distinct, and aligned to the visible H1; boilerplate and repeated phrases are blocked.
- Descriptions should be page-specific summaries, not keyword piles or hidden claims.
- If a page has multiple visually prominent headings, one must be marked as the main title and the rest demoted in visual hierarchy.
- Footer tags must use a global short definition or page-local scoped variant; they cannot preserve old category decisions.
- Compare and article pages may use SEO phrases such as "AI compiler" only when their page type and proof modules justify it.
- Metadata reports must include changed title/description previews for mobile and desktop search widths.

Proof-module registry requirements:

| module | required fields | release blocker |
|---|---|---|
| Interactive runner | input mode, sample call, output artifact summary, privacy label, loading, error, mobile, light/dark, no-secret proof. | Copy says "live demo" but runner is static or broken. |
| Code comparison | provider/language tabs, before code, after code, output artifact, caveats, copy buttons, auth/base URL notes. | Copy says "2-line swap" without runnable fixture. |
| ROI calculator | spend input, provider mix, assumptions, compile/runtime cost, plan mapping, export/share, caveats. | Savings number appears without assumptions. |
| Product video | poster, duration, transcript/captions, structured data, reduced motion, light/dark visibility, fallback. | Video CTA exists but media fails or is invisible. |
| Receipt card | artifact hash, eval scope, K-Score version, benchmark date, model/runtime version, command, limitation label. | Claim appears without receipt fields. |
| Trust badge | implemented control, document available, external review in progress, or certification awarded. | Pending certification looks awarded. |
| Quote/social proof | permission, role, company/design-partner scope, use case, date, anonymization state. | Fake logo or unsourced quote. |
