| `W-NARR-V3-020` | `44.6` | Add narrative-to-design handoff. | Node `44` exports exact component copy, proof data, and state labels for Node `45` visual system implementation. |

Node `44` V3 relax redline:

- Do not call the narrative complete while top pages use competing category frames without a page-specific reason and proof module.
- Do not let hidden anchors, footer tags, metadata, JSON-LD, OpenGraph, Twitter cards, comments, or version files preserve rejected or stale positioning.
- Do not let "one AI API", "owned AI", "run anywhere", "frontier", "state of the art", "best", "certified", or savings claims appear without mechanism and claim-scope proof.
- Do not let a proof component appear in copy unless it works or shows an honest fallback in light/dark, mobile/desktop, loading/error, and reduced-motion states where applicable.
- Do not let docs start with sales copy where commands are needed, or account pages start with marketing copy where operational state is needed.
- Do not let CTA labels hide destination, action, auth requirement, sales requirement, or disabled/loading/error behavior.
- Do not call a page finished until title, H1, meta description, OG/Twitter, structured data, nav label, CTA, proof module, and visible copy all tell the same scoped story.

### Visual Design System, Component Library, CSS Governance, And Rendered UI Proof Ledger V1

Node `45` turns the user's visual and styling complaints into an enforceable design-system branch. Node `44` decides what Kolm says. Node `45` decides how Kolm renders, behaves, and proves that every public, docs, and account surface feels like one enterprise product instead of a stack of page-specific patches.

The redline is not "make it prettier." The redline is that nav underlines, popouts, buttons, colors, typography, spacing, theme behavior, demo/video visibility, account layouts, proof cards, tables, forms, and responsive states all come from one governed visual system with screenshots and component-state evidence.

#### Current Visual-System Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 45 redline |
|---|---|---|
| CSS footprint | 20 public CSS files, 786,393+ bytes, about 25,547 lines, about 1,577 raw hex literals, 3,124 CSS variable references, 3,884 `!important` rules, 301 media queries, and 40 reduced-motion references. | Consolidate into token, foundation, layout, component, utility, and page-exception layers with a debt ledger and deletion plan. |
| Largest CSS files | `public/brand-refresh.css` and `public/surface-polish.css` together exceed 295 KB and more than 3,000 `!important` overrides; `public/home-refresh.css` adds hundreds more. | These become migration targets, not permanent system layers. |
| Theme handling | Multiple `[data-theme="light"]` blocks and light-mode rescue overrides exist across public CSS. | Design light and dark together from tokens; no one-off light-mode patches for demos, videos, cards, nav, or account panels. |
| Nav and button evidence | CSS contains active nav, mega menu, buttons, account sidebar, theme toggle, focus-visible, hover, and light-mode overrides. | One nav/button/popout state machine across public, docs, and account with keyboard, pointer, touch, loading, active, and disabled parity. |
| Page scale | Public HTML has 729 pages, including 48 account pages and 221 docs pages. | Every route needs page type, component inventory, theme/viewport screenshot proof, and no orphan one-off style. |
| Account UI risk | Account pages need operational density, real state, live readiness, and low-friction next actions. | Account components must be dashboard-grade, data-backed, accessible, and visually consistent with public/docs shell. |
| Media and demo risk | Prior feedback flagged demo/video/light-mode failures and trash media. | Media components must have posters, transcripts or fallbacks where needed, theme proof, loading/error states, and no invisible light-mode content. |

#### External Baseline Folded Into This Node

| Baseline | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | Visual states must meet accessible contrast, focus, target, keyboard, motion, error-identification, and label expectations. |
| W3C Design Tokens Community Group format | `https://www.w3.org/community/reports/design-tokens/CG-FINAL-format-20251028/` | Tokens should be machine-readable, typed, traceable, and portable across CSS, docs, account, screenshots, and future apps. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Visual quality must preserve LCP, INP, and CLS. Component polish cannot introduce slow hero media, layout shift, heavy JS, or delayed interactivity. |
| Material Design 3 interaction states | `https://m3.material.io/` | Components need consistent state layers for hover, focus, pressed, dragged, disabled, selected, and loading states across pointer and touch. |
| Apple Human Interface Guidelines | `https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/` | Interfaces should be direct, legible, consistent, and respectful of platform expectations, motion preferences, and touch ergonomics. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | local skill `ui-ux-pro-max` | Enterprise SaaS surfaces should be quiet, dense, predictable, accessible, and purpose-built; avoid hover-only controls, ornamental dashboards, inconsistent nav states, and raw style drift. |

#### Canonical Visual-System Manifest

Every visual implementation should compile to a manifest shaped like this:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.visual-system.v1",
  "source": {
    "tokens": "public/design-tokens.css",
    "registry": "docs/component-registry.json",
    "css_debt": "docs/css-debt-ledger.json",
    "screenshots": "reports/ui-surface-audit/<date>/report.md"
  },
  "tokens": {
    "color": {
      "surface": [],
      "text": [],
      "border": [],
      "accent": [],
      "semantic": ["success", "warning", "danger", "info", "neutral", "disabled"]
    },
    "type": {
      "families": [],
      "scale": [],
      "line_height": [],
      "weights": [],
      "letter_spacing_policy": "0 unless typography review approves an exception"
    },
    "space": { "scale": [] },
    "radius": { "scale": [] },
    "elevation": { "scale": [] },
    "motion": {
      "duration": [],
      "easing": [],
      "reduced_motion_policy": "no critical information depends on animation"
    },
    "z_index": [],
    "breakpoints": ["375", "768", "1024", "1440"]
  },
  "themes": ["light", "dark", "high_contrast_optional"],
  "layers": ["reset", "tokens", "foundation", "layout", "components", "utilities", "page_exceptions"],
  "states": ["default", "hover", "focus_visible", "active", "pressed", "disabled", "loading", "selected", "error", "empty", "unavailable", "skeleton"],
  "components": ["nav", "mega_menu", "button", "link", "form", "table", "card", "modal", "toast", "status_token", "chart", "media", "code_block", "account_sidebar", "docs_shell", "pricing_tier"],
  "proof": {
    "screenshots": ["dark_desktop", "dark_mobile", "light_desktop", "light_mobile"],
    "contrast": [],
    "touch_targets": [],
    "keyboard": [],
    "media_visibility": [],
    "performance": ["lcp", "inp", "cls"]
  }
}
```

The manifest should be generated or linted from real CSS and component usage. A component cannot be called complete if it exists only as a screenshot or isolated style without token, state, accessibility, page usage, and proof records.

#### Component State Matrix

| Component | Required states | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| Global nav and header | default, hover, focus-visible, current, expanded, collapsed, mobile open, mobile closed, auth-aware, docs/account variant | Screenshots by theme/viewport; keyboard traversal; no contradictory underline/pill/current styling; no hover-only destination. |
| Mega menu and popover | closed, opening, open, focused item, pointer hover, touch select, outside click, escape, overflow, loading, empty | Pointer, keyboard, and touch proof; z-index and clipping proof; light/dark surface contrast; reduced-motion behavior. |
| Button and link | primary, secondary, quiet, danger, icon, text, disabled, loading, success, destructive confirm, external | Minimum target size, focus ring, icon alignment, label wrapping, disabled semantics, analytics destination, no duplicate CTA labels for different actions. |
| Form and input | idle, focused, filled, validation pending, valid, error, disabled, readonly, loading, server error | Labels, descriptions, errors, autofill, keyboard, mobile input modes, no color-only error state, account/API parity. |
| Table and data grid | loading, empty, populated, filtered, sorted, selected, bulk action, error, overflow, export | Responsive behavior, keyboard, density, sticky headers where needed, no horizontal trap, export proof, row action clarity. |
| Card and panel | normal, selected, focused, unavailable, loading, warning, error, compact, wide | No nested card stacks for page sections; repeated items only; clear hierarchy; status and action visible without hover. |
| Modal, sheet, and command panel | open, closed, focus trap, escape, submit loading, validation error, destructive confirm, mobile sheet | Focus restoration, scroll lock, z-index, backdrop contrast, mobile fit, no clipped primary action. |
| Toast and notification | info, success, warning, danger, persistent, dismissible, action, retry | Screen-reader announcement, timeout policy, audit trail for important events, no hidden failure. |
| Status token and badge | neutral, success, warning, danger, external-gated, beta, unsupported, local-only, certified | Text plus icon or shape; claim-scope mapping; contrast proof; not decorative. |
| Chart and metric | loading, empty, partial, stale, error, updated, anomaly | Axis labels, units, source, timestamp, confidence, no misleading fake data, accessible table alternative. |
| Media, demo, and video | poster, loading, playing, paused, error, transcript, captioned, reduced motion, light/dark | Visible in light/dark, poster not blank, transcript/caption where relevant, no autoplay audio, fallback if asset fails. |
| Code block and copy control | language tabs, line wrap, copy ready, copied, copy failed, long line, error output | Copy button accessible, command output expected, dark/light contrast, no stale commands, mobile line behavior. |
| Account sidebar and shell | collapsed, expanded, current page, attention count, billing restricted, mobile drawer, auth loading | Product matrix mapping, keyboard/touch, no marketing-only labels, live status indicators where relevant. |
| Docs shell | search ready, search empty, version selected, left-nav current, mobile nav, copy buttons, API link | Version and last-verified labels, command proof, no dead nav leaf, route/API/CLI parity. |
| Pricing tier | default, recommended, current plan, upgrade, downgrade, sales-required, legacy alias, disabled | Billing API parity, plan source, feature matrix, enterprise handoff, no fake self-serve enterprise. |

#### CSS Cascade Governance

The final CSS architecture must be boring and enforceable:

1. `reset` may normalize base elements only.
2. `tokens` may define raw values and design-token aliases.
3. `foundation` may map tokens to body, typography, links, focus, media, and layout primitives.
4. `layout` may define shells, grids, bands, split panes, docs/account scaffolds, and responsive containers.
5. `components` may define named components and their states.
6. `utilities` may define low-level helpers with strict naming and no product semantics.
7. `page_exceptions` may exist only with owner, expiry, route list, and migration ticket.

Rules:

- No raw hex, RGB, HSL, shadow, radius, font, spacing, or duration values outside token files unless the value is part of an approved external asset or illustration.
- No new `!important` without a ledger row containing selector, reason, owner, expiry, and deletion ticket.
- No page-specific CSS over a small cap without componentization review.
- No generated CSS without a source command and stale-output check.
- No light-mode-only or dark-mode-only patch unless the component manifest proves both themes.
- No CSS file can be added without assigning it to a layer and owner node.
- No selector can style a component by unrelated page ancestry if a component class or data attribute can express the state.
- No permanent style coupling to hidden SEO anchors, test anchors, or marketing recovery payloads.

Migration targets:

| File | Role to verify | Required decision |
|---|---|---|
| `public/brand-refresh.css` | Large cross-site patch layer with many overrides. | Split into tokens/foundation/components or retire chunks into component CSS. |
| `public/surface-polish.css` | Large polish override layer. | Convert to component states and page-exception ledger; delete redundant overrides. |
| `public/home-refresh.css` | Homepage-specific design layer with heavy overrides. | Reduce to home component composition plus approved hero/media exceptions. |
| `public/warm-paper.css` | Theme/style overlay. | Decide if it is a real theme, a legacy experiment, or a delete candidate. |
| `public/styles.css` | Base/global style layer. | Keep only as foundation/layout if tokenized and documented. |
| `public/frontier.css`, `public/ks.css` | Product-specific layers. | Normalize into components or prove scoped ownership and screenshots. |

#### Visual Redlines

- No active nav state may combine underline, pill, color, and background in contradictory ways.
- No popover, mega menu, tooltip, modal, or mobile drawer may be hover-only.
- No button may ship without min size, focus-visible, disabled, loading, pressed, and error-adjacent behavior.
- No CTA may change style hierarchy between pages without a component variant reason.
- No page section may be styled as a floating card if it is a structural page band.
- No card may contain another card unless it is a deliberate repeated item inside a framed tool.
- No text may overflow buttons, cards, tokens, sidebars, nav items, tables, or mobile panels.
- No font size may scale directly with viewport width.
- No letter spacing may be negative or arbitrary.
- No one-note palette may dominate the site; avoid purple-only, beige-only, dark-slate-only, or brown/orange-only visual systems unless the token review proves contrast and brand rationale.
- No image, demo, video, chart, or product visual may be dark, blurred, cropped, stock-like, blank, or invisible when it is supposed to prove the product.
- No demo or video may fail silently in light mode.
- No account dashboard may use fake decorative metrics where a user expects operational state.
- No docs page may use ornamental panels that make command scanning harder.
- No screenshot audit can be considered complete without manual representative review of hero, nav, account overview, pricing, enterprise, docs, demos, and media.
- No design-system claim can be considered complete while `!important` rules and raw values remain unowned.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `45.1` Token architecture, theme model, typography, color, spacing, radius, elevation, motion, and responsive scales | A machine-readable token manifest defines typed tokens, theme mappings, typography scale, spacing/radius/elevation/motion scales, breakpoints, z-index, and reduced-motion policy. |
| `45.2` Component inventory, variants, states, accessibility contracts, account/public/docs parity, and icon/media rules | Every visual component has owner, variants, states, ARIA/accessibility contract, icon/media rules, public/docs/account usage, and screenshot proof. |
| `45.3` CSS cascade governance, generated versus hand-authored styles, override policy, page-specific CSS limits, and deletion plan | CSS files are assigned to layers, generated/manual state, owner node, raw-value policy, override ledger, page-exception cap, and deletion/migration tickets. |
| `45.4` Navigation, headers, mega menus, sidebars, popovers, modals, buttons, forms, tables, cards, charts, and proof states | Core components behave consistently across route groups, themes, viewports, pointer, keyboard, touch, loading, empty, error, selected, disabled, and reduced-motion states. |
| `45.5` Visual QA: screenshots, contrast, focus, reduced motion, touch targets, Core Web Vitals, media visibility, and manual review | Visual QA includes screenshot matrix, contrast report, focus/touch/motion checks, media visibility checks, Core Web Vitals lab and production evidence, and manual review notes. |
| `45.6` Evidence: token manifest, component registry, CSS debt ledger, route-theme-viewport matrix, visual diff, and production screenshots | Release evidence links token manifest, component registry, CSS debt ledger, page/component matrix, visual diff, screenshot reports, production screenshots, and accepted exceptions. |

#### Node 45 Relax Redline

Node `45` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- What token defines every visible color, type size, spacing value, radius, shadow, z-index, focus ring, motion duration, and breakpoint?
- Which component owns every nav item, button, form, card, table, modal, popover, chart, media block, code block, docs shell, account panel, pricing tier, and status token?
- Which states are screenshot-proved for each component: default, hover, focus-visible, active, pressed, selected, disabled, loading, empty, error, unavailable, skeleton, mobile, desktop, light, dark, and reduced motion?
- Which CSS files are permanent, which are generated, which are migration targets, which are page exceptions, and which selectors are allowed to use `!important`?
- Can a reviewer trace every visual change from token to component to route to screenshot to production smoke?
- Can nav, popovers, CTAs, account panels, demo/video/media, docs, pricing, and enterprise pages be operated by keyboard and touch without hidden states?
- Do screenshots prove there is no text overflow, inconsistent underline/highlight behavior, broken light-mode media, invisible demo state, random color drift, decorative account data, or component mismatch?
- Do Core Web Vitals, contrast, focus, target-size, reduced-motion, and media-visibility checks pass without relying on chat history?

The relax condition for node `45` is not "the site looks better in a screenshot." It is: Kolm has one visual token system, one component registry, one cascade policy, one CSS debt ledger, one route/component screenshot matrix, and one production visual smoke packet proving that every rendered public, docs, and account surface is cohesive, accessible, fast, theme-safe, responsive, and operationally credible.

#### Node 45 Visual Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` stays unused here. The visual quality problem is already owned by node `45`: nav underlines, popout styles, buttons, color drift, typography, spacing, account dashboards, media, video, theme failures, and component inconsistency are not separate tickets. They are symptoms of the same missing visual operating system.

The V2 closeout makes node `45` stricter than "run a screenshot audit." Screenshot audits prove rendered stability at a moment in time. The visual operating system proves that every rendered state has a token, component owner, cascade layer, accessibility contract, theme contract, viewport contract, and production proof.

##### Current CSS And UI Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V2 interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| Public CSS footprint | Current scan found 19 top-level public CSS files, 779,198 bytes, 25,453 lines, 3,057 `var(` references, 1,555 raw hex values, 3,884 `!important` declarations, 296 media queries, 40 reduced-motion markers, and 920 light-theme or light-mode markers. | The design system exists as accumulated patches, not a governed cascade. V2 requires tokens, layers, component registry, and debt ledger. |
| Largest CSS layers | `brand-refresh.css` 169,200 bytes and 1,553 `!important`; `surface-polish.css` 126,141 bytes and 1,532 `!important`; `warm-paper.css` 83,097 bytes; `styles.css` 76,894 bytes; `frontier.css` 55,666 bytes; `ks.css` 49,262 bytes. | Broad patch files must become migration targets with keep/split/delete decisions, not permanent style authority. |
| Token drift | `design-tokens.css` exists but still has 118 raw hex values and only 18 `var(` references; many other files define raw colors, spacing, radius, shadows, font sizes, and theme fixes directly. | Tokens need to become the source of visual truth, not a partial file alongside stronger overrides. |
| Override debt | The largest override files contain thousands of `!important` rules, including nav, buttons, account sidebar, hero, code, logo, light theme, and menu states. | `!important` must require an exception ledger with owner, selector, reason, expiry, and deletion ticket. |
| Theme rescue debt | Current CSS scan found 920 light-theme or light-mode markers, with many one-off light-mode corrections in broad patch files. | Light and dark must be paired at token/component level. V2 blocks one-off theme rescues unless tied to component proof. |
| Nav and menu states | Current scan shows nav active/current state rules, mega-menu styling, underline suppression, account sidebar active states, hover/focus-visible, and mobile behavior spread through patch layers. | Nav, mega menu, sidebar, popover, and account shell need one state machine across public, docs, and account. |
| Button and CTA states | Button selectors are broad (`.btn`, `.cta`, `.button`, submit buttons, historical wave classes), with min-height, radius, letter-spacing, and theme overrides mixed into patch files. | Buttons need explicit variants and states: primary, secondary, quiet, danger, icon, text, loading, disabled, pressed, success, external. |
| Media and proof components | Existing CSS and HTML refer to hero media, video, iframe, demo, receipt, ROI, code, account panels, and proof bands. | V2 treats media/proof components as governed components with theme visibility, poster/fallback, loading/error states, and performance budgets. |
| Screenshot evidence | Latest local report folders include `2026-05-23Tfocused-polish-pass`, `2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account`, and chunked dark/light mobile route audits from 2026-05-22. | Existing screenshot artifacts are useful evidence but not final proof. V2 requires route/component/state matrix and production re-screenshots after deploy. |

##### External Baseline Added For V2

| Baseline | Source | What V2 imports into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | Visual proof must cover perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust UI, including contrast, focus, target size, hover/focus content, keyboard, labels, errors, status messages, and media alternatives. |
| WCAG focus appearance | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/focus-appearance.html` | Focus indicators need visible area, contrast, and not being obscured. A pretty UI with weak focus rings is incomplete. |
| WCAG target size minimum | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/target-size-minimum.html` | Touch and pointer targets need minimum target sizing or documented exceptions; dense account UI still needs operable targets. |
| W3C Design Tokens Format 2025.10 | `https://www.w3.org/community/reports/design-tokens/CG-FINAL-format-20251028/` | Tokens should be typed, named, grouped, serializable, and portable across CSS, docs, account, future apps, and screenshots. |
| CSS Cascade Level 5 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/css-cascade-5/` | Cascade layers are the right native mechanism for separating reset, tokens, foundation, layout, components, utilities, and exceptions. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Visual upgrades must protect LCP, INP, and CLS; media, fonts, animations, and layout polish cannot damage performance or stability. |
| Material Design state guidance | `https://m3.material.io/foundations/interaction/states` | Components need consistent states for hover, focus, pressed, dragged, selected, disabled, loading, and error. |
| Apple Human Interface Guidelines | `https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines` | Interfaces should be direct, legible, consistent, responsive to input, respectful of platform conventions, and safe for motion/touch contexts. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | local skill `ui-ux-pro-max` | Enterprise SaaS needs quiet density, clear hierarchy, predictable navigation, no hover-only controls, stable touch targets, semantic tokens, and visual proof across states. |

##### V2 Visual System Contract

Every visual decision must reduce to this contract:

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.visual-system.v2",
  "version": "2026-05",
  "layers": ["reset", "tokens", "foundation", "layout", "components", "utilities", "page_exceptions"],
  "tokens": {
    "source": "public/design-tokens.css",
    "format": "design-tokens-compatible",
    "families": ["color", "type", "space", "radius", "elevation", "motion", "z_index", "breakpoint", "media", "focus"],
    "themes": ["light", "dark"],
    "contrast_pairs": [],
    "raw_value_exceptions": []
  },
  "components": [
    {
      "id": "global-nav",
      "owner_node": "45.4",
      "source_files": [],
      "variants": ["public", "docs", "account", "mobile"],
      "states": ["default", "hover", "focus_visible", "current", "expanded", "collapsed", "loading", "disabled"],
      "accessibility": ["keyboard", "focus_visible", "label_in_name", "target_size", "escape_close"],
      "themes": ["light", "dark"],
      "viewports": ["mobile", "tablet", "desktop", "wide"],
      "proof": {
        "screenshots": [],
        "manual_review": [],
        "production_smoke": []
      }
    }
  ],
  "css_debt": {
    "important_total": 3884,
    "raw_hex_total": 1555,
    "light_theme_markers": 920,
    "migration_targets": ["brand-refresh.css", "surface-polish.css", "warm-paper.css", "styles.css", "frontier.css", "ks.css", "home-refresh.css"]
  },
  "redlines": {
    "raw_visual_values_outside_tokens": "fail",
    "new_unowned_important": "fail",
    "hover_only_interaction": "fail",
    "theme_unpaired_component": "fail",
    "missing_component_state_proof": "fail",
    "missing_production_screenshot": "fail"
  }
}
```

The system is intentionally evidence-driven. A component is not complete because it has CSS. It is complete when the token source, selector ownership, state model, accessibility behavior, route usage, screenshots, and production smoke all agree.

##### V2 Cascade Layer Policy

| Layer | Allowed contents | Forbidden contents |
|---|---|---|
| `reset` | Browser normalization, base box sizing, default media behavior. | Brand color, page layout, component states, or route-specific fixes. |
| `tokens` | Typed design tokens and theme mappings only. | Component selectors, route selectors, hardcoded exceptions. |
| `foundation` | Body, typography, links, focus, media primitives, selection, scrollbars, global code styles. | Product-specific cards, nav variants, page-only hero styles. |
| `layout` | Shells, grids, bands, docs/account scaffolds, responsive containers, z-index architecture. | Button variants, status tokens, proof cards, route-specific overrides. |
| `components` | Named reusable components and their explicit states. | Page ancestry hacks, hidden SEO/test anchor styling, unowned broad selectors. |
| `utilities` | Low-level single-purpose helpers with strict naming and no product semantics. | Product-specific behavior, layout ownership, or state machines. |
| `page_exceptions` | Temporary route-scoped exceptions with owner, expiry, and migration ticket. | Permanent design authority or broad cross-site patches. |

`brand-refresh.css`, `surface-polish.css`, `home-refresh.css`, and similar patch layers can remain only as migration envelopes until their selectors are moved into these layers or retired.

##### V2 Component Registry Contract

| Component family | Must define | Must prove |
|---|---|---|
| Navigation | Header, nav link, current state, hover/focus, mega menu, mobile drawer, account/docs variants, auth-aware state. | Keyboard path, touch path, escape/outside close, active-state screenshot, no underline/pill conflict. |
| Buttons and CTAs | Variants, size scale, icon placement, disabled, loading, pressed, destructive, external, copy/action mapping. | Target size, focus appearance, text wrap, theme contrast, route-level CTA consistency. |
| Forms | Label, helper, placeholder, validation, error, success, disabled, readonly, loading, server error. | Visible labels, inline errors, mobile input type, no placeholder-only controls, error announcement. |
| Tables and data grids | Density, columns, overflow, sort/filter, selection, bulk actions, export, empty/error/loading. | Mobile behavior, keyboard use, units/timestamps, no horizontal trap, accessible fallback where needed. |
| Cards and panels | Repeated-item card, tool card, account panel, proof card, selected/unavailable/loading/error states. | No nested page-section cards, clear hierarchy, visible status and action without hover. |
| Modals and popovers | Focus trap, escape, outside click, z-index, scroll lock, submit loading, validation, mobile sheet. | Focus restoration, no clipped primary action, light/dark contrast, touch and keyboard parity. |
| Status tokens | Success, warning, danger, neutral, beta, external-gated, local-only, unsupported, certified. | Text plus non-color cue, claim-scope mapping, contrast, not decorative. |
| Media and demo | Image, video, iframe, canvas, interactive runner, receipt, code comparison, ROI calculator. | Poster/fallback, loading/error, transcript/captions where relevant, theme visibility, reduced motion, performance budget. |
| Docs shell | Search, left nav, mobile nav, version, command blocks, copy buttons, API links, troubleshooting. | Current nav state, copy feedback, no stale command, mobile scanning, last-verified label. |
| Account shell | Sidebar, header, metrics, readiness tokens, tables, charts, settings, storage, billing, empty/error states. | Operational state, loading/empty/error, product graph parity, export/support actions, no marketing-only filler. |

##### V2 CSS Debt Ledger Schema

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.css-debt.v2",
  "file": "public/brand-refresh.css",
  "layer": "page_exceptions|migration_envelope|tokens|foundation|layout|components|utilities",
  "owner_node": "45",
  "owner_lane": "frontend",
  "bytes": 169200,
  "lines": 4740,
  "raw_hex": 582,
  "var_references": 145,
  "important": 1553,
  "media_queries": 84,
  "reduced_motion": 2,
  "theme_markers": 638,
  "selectors": {
    "nav": 0,
    "button": 0,
    "account": 0,
    "media": 0,
    "page_specific": 0
  },
  "decision": "split|keep|delete|generate|archive|migration_envelope",
  "reason": "why this file remains",
  "migration_ticket": "W-VIS-V2-...",
  "expires": "2026-06-30",
  "allowed_new_debt": false
}
```

No final design-system claim can pass while CSS files lack this ledger.

##### V2 Visual QA Matrix

Every route or route group must map to:

- Page type and primary component families.
- Theme coverage: dark and light.
- Viewport coverage: mobile, tablet or desktop, and wide where layout changes.
- Interaction proof: keyboard, pointer, touch, focus, hover, pressed, disabled, loading, empty, error, selected.
- Media proof: images, video, iframe, canvas, runner, chart, and code block visibility.
- Accessibility proof: contrast, focus appearance, target size, labels, errors, status messages, reduced motion.
- Performance proof: LCP, INP, CLS, image/font dimensions, lazy loading, no layout jumping.
- Manual review tier: P0 for homepage, pricing, enterprise, docs, account overview, demo/runner, and any sales-critical vertical.
- Production proof: post-deploy screenshots and browser checks for P0 routes.

| `W-VIS-V2-020` | `45.6` | Add post-deploy visual finality gate. | Production proves P0 routes and component states across themes/viewports, with no nav drift, broken media, text overflow, raw claim badge, or account-state mismatch. |

##### Node 45 V2 Relax Redline

Node `45` V2 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every visible color, text style, spacing, radius, elevation, focus ring, breakpoint, z-index, and motion value comes from a governed token or approved exception.
- Every CSS file has a layer, owner, state, migration decision, and debt ledger row.
- New raw values and unowned `!important` rules fail verification.
- Public, docs, and account nav use one state machine for current, hover, focus, open, mobile, and auth-aware states.
- Buttons, CTAs, forms, tables, cards, modals, popovers, status tokens, code blocks, media, and account panels have required states and proof.
- Light and dark themes are paired at token/component level, not repaired by route-specific rescue patches.
- Demo/video/media/proof components remain visible and usable in light/dark, desktop/mobile, loading/error, and reduced-motion states.
- P0 routes have screenshots, keyboard/touch spot checks, contrast/focus/target evidence, and production smoke.
- Account pages look and behave like operational software, not marketing panels.
- The visual redline packet can be regenerated from the current tree after deployment without relying on chat history.

If any of those fail, the UI may look improved, but the visual design system is not complete.

#### Node 45 State-Complete Visual System And Rendered Product QA Addendum V3

Node `52` stays unused. The user's complaints about nav underlines, popout styles, buttons, color drift, branding, font choices, spacing, broken light-mode media, weak demo/video assets, and account post-auth inconsistency are all visual-system defects under node `45`. V3 raises the bar above V2: Kolm does not merely need fewer bad screens. Kolm needs a state-complete design system where every visual atom, component state, route, viewport, theme, media element, account state, docs shell, and production screenshot is owned, measured, and impossible to regress silently.

The V3 operating principle is strict: visual quality is not a taste review. It is a product contract. A surface is done only when the tokens, CSS cascade, components, copy labels, interaction states, accessibility proof, performance budgets, screenshots, and production smoke all agree.

##### Current CSS And UI Evidence Rechecked For V3

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 interpretation |
|---|---:|---|
| Public CSS footprint | Current scan found 20 public CSS files, 786,393 bytes, 25,547 CSS lines, 3,124 `var(` references, 1,577 raw hex values, 3,884 `!important` declarations, 301 media queries, 40 reduced-motion markers, and 4 font-face/import markers. | The visual layer is large enough to require a governed build artifact. V3 adds hard budgets for raw values, overrides, media rules, motion rules, and font loading. |
| Largest CSS files | `brand-refresh.css` 169,200 bytes; `surface-polish.css` 126,141; `warm-paper.css` 83,097; `styles.css` 76,894; `frontier.css` 55,666; `ks.css` 49,262; `home-refresh.css` 48,970; `w605.css` 41,070; `w604.css` 20,752; `w706.css` 20,723; `wf01-components.css` 19,639; `design-tokens.css` 17,186. | The biggest styling authority is still patch-layer CSS, not the token file. V3 requires migration envelopes with owner, expiry, selector inventory, and graduation into cascade layers. |
| Brand and nav debt | `brand-refresh.css` explicitly suppresses active underlines, creates stable nav pills, styles mega menus, patches account sidebar active states, and carries many light-theme rescue rules. | The nav problem is structural. Header, docs nav, account sidebar, mega menu, mobile drawer, and auth-aware state need one component state machine. |
| Button and CTA debt | Broad selectors such as `.btn`, `.cta`, `.button`, submit buttons, and historical wave classes share radius, min-height, letter-spacing, and theme overrides through patch layers. | Buttons are not complete until every variant has a stable size, copy action, icon rule, focus rule, loading rule, disabled rule, pressed state, text wrap behavior, and destination contract. |
| Theme rescue debt | Light-mode and dark-mode behavior is patched across broad CSS files rather than fully paired at token/component level. | V3 blocks finality when a component depends on route-specific theme rescue instead of paired semantic tokens. |
| Media and proof debt | CSS selectors cover video, iframe, canvas, picture, demos, receipts, ROI, code, and proof bands, but proof states are not guaranteed by a component-state lab. | The homepage video/demo and every proof media block need load, poster, fallback, transcript/caption where relevant, reduced-motion, theme visibility, and performance proof. |
| Account and docs shell debt | Account/sidebar/docs styles exist, but the CSS evidence shows they are partly normalized through global patch layers. | Account is operational software. Docs are developer software. Both need dense, stable, keyboard-first components rather than marketing-card styling. |
| Wave-specific CSS | `w604.css`, `w605.css`, `w706.css`, `w687.css`, `w598.css`, and similar files remain visible in the public CSS set. | Wave CSS must graduate into named component layers, be ledgered as temporary, or be deleted. No wave file can become permanent design authority by accident. |

##### External Baseline Refreshed For V3

| Baseline | Current source | V3 import into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| WCAG 2.2 Recommendation | `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | Use WCAG as the minimum accessibility floor: perceivable, operable, understandable, robust; contrast, text spacing, reflow, keyboard, hover/focus content, labels, errors, status messages, and accessible authentication must be route-proved. |
| Focus appearance | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/focus-appearance.html` | Focus indicators need visible area and contrast. Nav pills, popovers, dense account controls, code copy buttons, and icon buttons cannot rely on subtle color-only focus. |
| Target size minimum | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/target-size-minimum.html` | Dense enterprise UI still needs usable targets or documented exceptions. Header, sidebar, menu, table actions, icon buttons, and mobile drawer controls need target-size proof. |
| Design Tokens Format 2025.10 draft | `https://www.designtokens.org/tr/drafts/format/` | Tokens must be typed, named, grouped, aliasable, portable, and serializable so product, docs, account, screenshots, and future apps share one visual vocabulary. |
| CSS Cascade Level 5 | `https://www.w3.org/TR/css-cascade-5/` | Native cascade layers should separate reset, tokens, foundation, layout, components, utilities, and page exceptions. This is the technical answer to patch-layer drift. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Visual polish must protect LCP, INP, and CLS at the 75th percentile across mobile and desktop; media, fonts, animation, nav, and account dashboards cannot damage field performance. |
| Material interaction state model | `https://m3.material.io/foundations/interaction/states` | Every interactive component needs explicit default, hover, focus, pressed, dragged where applicable, selected, disabled, loading, error, and unavailable states. |
| Apple Human Interface Guidelines | `https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/` | Kolm should feel direct, legible, predictable, responsive to input, respectful of platform patterns, and safe for touch/motion contexts. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | `C:/Users/user/.agents/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md` | Enterprise SaaS design should be quiet, dense, component-driven, keyboard/touch safe, contrast-safe, and free of hover-only critical controls or decorative-only cards. |

##### V3 Component State Lab

| Component family | Required states | Proof surfaces | Immediate blockers to close |
|---|---|---|---|
| Global header and nav | default, current, hover, focus-visible, expanded, collapsed, mobile, auth-aware, disabled/unavailable. | Homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs, account, mobile drawer. | Underline/pill conflicts, popout drift, route-specific current-state patches, hover-only menu assumptions. |
| Mega menu and popover | closed, opening, open, hover, focus-visible, keyboard roving, escape, outside click, mobile sheet, error/unavailable. | Product mega menu, docs menus, account menus, profile/auth menus. | No menu is done without focus restoration, z-index proof, clipping proof, and touch proof. |
| Buttons and CTAs | primary, secondary, quiet, danger, icon, text, external, loading, disabled, pressed, success, wrap, long-label. | Hero, pricing, enterprise, account actions, docs copy controls, runner controls. | Broad `.btn`/`.cta` styling must become variant tokens with destination and action semantics. |
| Forms and inputs | empty, filled, focus, helper, validation, error, success, disabled, readonly, loading, server error. | Signup, enterprise inquiry, account settings, storage setup, API key forms, docs examples. | Placeholder-only or top-only error patterns fail. Labels, errors, and status announcements are mandatory. |
| Tables and grids | loading, empty, populated, filtered, sorted, selected, bulk action, overflow, export, permission denied, error. | Account captures, billing, storage, privacy events, docs API tables. | Horizontal traps, clipped actions, hidden units, and hover-only row controls fail. |
| Account panels | onboarding, ready, no data, partial config, external gate, local-only, error, stale, support needed. | Account overview, storage, billing, captures, distill jobs, readiness, settings. | Account pages must read as operational control rooms, not decorative marketing summaries. |
| Docs shell and code blocks | current nav, search empty, search result, code copy idle, copy success, copy error, version stale, command warning. | CLI docs, API reference, SDK pages, tutorials, quickstarts. | Docs need command freshness labels, copy feedback, mobile nav proof, and no stale route promises. |
| Proof cards and receipts | verified, pending, failed, external-gated, benchmark-scoped, certification-scoped, package-scoped. | Trust, benchmarks, product graph, pricing, enterprise, account readiness. | Claim badges must map to backend readiness states and cannot look like completed proof when gated. |
| Media, demo, and video | loading, loaded, poster, error, fallback, transcript, paused, playing, reduced-motion, light/dark. | Homepage hero, product runner, video explainer, ROI calculator, code comparison, media library. | No video/demo component is complete if light mode can hide it, if fallback is absent, or if it shifts layout. |
| Pricing and enterprise trust | free, pro, team, enterprise contact-sales, unavailable, gated, current plan, upgrade, downgrade, sales review. | Pricing, upgrade, enterprise, account billing, FAQ, vertical CTAs. | Enterprise CTAs must be contact-sales/review paths, not fake self-serve checkout, unless backend contracts change. |

##### V3 CSS Burn-Down Policy

| Policy | Rule | Evidence required |
|---|---|---|
| No unowned CSS file | A new CSS file must declare layer, owner node, owner lane, purpose, expiry if temporary, and generation source if generated. | `docs/css-debt-ledger.v3.json` row and source comment. |
| Raw value ratchet | Raw hex, RGB, HSL, px, shadow, font, duration, z-index, and breakpoint values outside tokens must decrease or stay constant per release. | Token audit with previous/current counts and approved exceptions. |
| `!important` ratchet | `!important` is allowed only with selector, reason, owner, expiry, and deletion ticket; total count must trend down. | Exception ledger plus diff gate. |
| Wave CSS graduation | Wave-specific CSS must graduate into components/tokens, be marked temporary, or be deleted. | Decision row for each `w*.css` file and migration ticket. |
| Patch-layer envelope | `brand-refresh.css`, `surface-polish.css`, `home-refresh.css`, `warm-paper.css`, `styles.css`, `frontier.css`, and `ks.css` are migration envelopes until split. | Selector inventory and split/delete/keep decision. |
| Theme pairing | Light and dark variants must be paired at token/component level. | Contrast matrix and component-state screenshots in both themes. |
| Page exception lifetime | Route-specific visual fixes expire within 45 days unless promoted or deleted. | Owner, expiry, and redline review. |
| Media dimension discipline | All image, video, iframe, canvas, and runner components reserve dimensions and define load/error fallback. | CLS proof, screenshot proof, and component registry row. |
