| `W-VIS-V3-020` | `45.6` | Handoff to nodes `46` and `43`. | Traceability consumes the V3 graph and build-finality blocks release when visual, CSS, account, media, or production-smoke evidence is missing. |

##### Node 45 V3 Relax Redline

Node `45` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- CSS debt is governed: 20 CSS files, 786,393 bytes, 1,577 raw hex values, and 3,884 `!important` declarations are baselined, owned, and ratcheting down.
- No route claims visual completion from screenshots alone; every route maps to components, states, tokens, selectors, accessibility checks, performance budgets, and production smoke.
- No nav, mega menu, popover, sidebar, button, CTA, form, table, card, modal, status badge, code block, demo, video, or account panel relies on hover-only behavior.
- No component ships without applicable loading, empty, error, disabled, focus-visible, selected/current, mobile, desktop, light, dark, and reduced-motion states.
- No media proof ships without load, fallback, poster or placeholder, dimensions, theme visibility, and reduced-motion behavior; video gets captions/transcript where relevant.
- No account page is complete unless it shows operational truth: readiness, missing gates, permissions, data states, exports, support paths, and backend parity.
- No docs shell is complete unless current nav, search, command freshness, copy feedback, code overflow, API route links, and mobile state are proved.
- No pricing or enterprise surface is complete unless self-serve and contact-sales behavior match backend billing contracts.
- No visual polish can regress Core Web Vitals, keyboard access, focus appearance, target size, text reflow, contrast, or theme pairing.
- No production release can be called 100 percent until the post-deploy visual smoke packet is attached to the master spec tree.

If any redline fails, the visual layer may be improved, but Kolm is not visually state-complete.

### Master Spec Traceability, Evidence Graph, Requirements Control, And Implementation Handoff Ledger V1

Node `46` makes the master spec tree executable. A 100-percent completion blueprint is useful only if every requirement can be traced to source rationale, implementation files, generated artifacts, tests, screenshots, metrics, production smoke, and a final done/not-done decision. Without that traceability, this document can become another large research artifact that sounds complete while implementation agents still ask what to build next.

This node is intentionally meta-operational. It does not replace node `31` wave governance, node `43` file completion, node `45` visual governance, or the product graph. It binds them together through a requirements control plane: every promise becomes a requirement record; every requirement has evidence; every evidence item has freshness and confidence; every contradiction creates a work order; every implementation agent receives a precise handoff packet.

#### Current Traceability Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 46 redline |
|---|---|---|
| Blueprint scale | This document now exceeds 8,500 lines and 111,000 words, with master-tree nodes `0` through `45` and many work-ticket families. | Large prose must be converted into requirement IDs, owners, status, evidence, and work orders, or it will be impossible to prove completion. |
| Product graph | `public/product-graph.json` tracks journeys, surfaces, route groups, routes, CLI commands, TUI views, account links, and readiness counts. | Product graph requirements need bidirectional traceability into code, docs, screenshots, API route evidence, and production smoke. |
| Readiness closeout | `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` records open proof gates such as package release, certification, benchmark, and external partner work. | Closeout gates must be first-class requirements with blocked status, public-copy restrictions, owner, done-when, and production proof path. |
| Route and API inventory | `public/docs/api-routes.json`, `public/openapi.json`, `src/router.js`, and API docs expose hundreds of routes and route records. | Each route must map to requirement IDs, auth policy, schemas, docs, tests, smoke, account/CLI/TUI parity, and release inclusion. |
| Verification mesh | `package.json` contains dense gates for depth, product graph, SOTA readiness, brand, claim scope, package release, benchmark evidence, frontier contracts, UI audits, local/prod surfaces, and release verify. | A requirement is not done because a broad gate passed; it is done when the requirement's assigned evidence set passed on the current tree. |
| Parallel lanes | The user workflow has frontend, backend, research, CLI, and spec lanes active. | Work orders need write-set boundaries, generated-artifact locks, merge sequencing, and combined-tree verification before "done" or deploy claims. |
| Current codebase scale | The current scan records thousands of files, hundreds of public pages, hundreds of source modules, many scripts, many tests, and many generated artifacts. | Every release-relevant artifact needs a primary master-node owner and requirement trace, not just directory membership. |

#### External Requirements And Traceability Baseline

| Baseline | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 requirements engineering | `https://standards.ieee.org/ieee/29148/6937/` and `https://www.iso.org/standard/72089.html` | Requirements should have clear constructs, attributes, quality characteristics, lifecycle management, and verification relationships. Kolm requirements cannot remain ambiguous roadmap prose. |
| NASA Systems Engineering Handbook V&V guidance | `https://www.nasa.gov/reference/system-engineering-handbook-appendix/` and `https://www.nasa.gov/reference/5-0-product-realization/` | Verification needs objective evidence, requirement identifiers, source traceability, and planned V&V methods. Kolm must prove every "shall" with test, analysis, inspection, demonstration, or production evidence. |
| NIST SP 800-160 systems security engineering | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/160/v1/r1/final` | Trustworthy systems require lifecycle engineering, security requirements, validation, verification, risk treatment, and traceable assurance. Kolm security/privacy/AI-safety requirements need explicit trace and evidence. |
| ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010 architecture description | `https://www.iso.org/standard/74393.html` | Architecture descriptions should support stakeholder concerns, views, viewpoints, model kinds, and rationale. Kolm needs architecture views that link requirements to routes, stores, runtimes, UI, deployment, and evidence. |
| DORA metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Delivery performance should track throughput and instability, not only green tests. Requirement work orders should include lead time, rework, rollback, failed deployment recovery, and product-metric impact. |
| Google SRE postmortem practice | `https://sre.google/workbook/postmortem-culture/` | Incidents and failed changes should produce ownership, actions, and durable learning. Requirement contradictions and failed proof should create work orders and postmortem notes, not disappear in chat history. |
| JSON Schema | `https://json-schema.org/specification` | Machine-readable spec artifacts should have versioned schemas. Kolm requirements, evidence, work orders, decision logs, and trace matrices should validate before release. |

#### Requirement Record Model

Every binding requirement should have a record shaped like this:

```json
{
  "id": "REQ-45-002",
  "title": "Global navigation states are consistent across public, docs, and account",
  "level": "MUST",
  "source": {
    "master_node": "45.4",
    "origin": "user-feedback|repo-scan|external-standard|product-graph|readiness-closeout|security-risk|customer-need",
    "rationale": "Users reported nav underline/highlight/popout drift; enterprise UX requires predictable state.",
    "references": ["docs/research/kolm-ai-100-percent-codebase-completion-blueprint-2026-05-25.md"]
  },
  "scope": {
    "product_surfaces": ["public-website", "docs", "account"],
    "paths": ["public/index.html", "public/docs/index.html", "public/account/overview.html"],
    "route_groups": [],
    "components": ["nav", "mega_menu", "account_sidebar"]
  },
  "owner": {
    "lane": "frontend",
    "primary": "implementation-agent",
    "reviewer": "release-owner"
  },
  "status": "accepted",
  "priority": "P0",
  "dependencies": ["REQ-45-001"],
  "acceptance": {
    "verification_methods": ["inspection", "test", "demonstration", "production-smoke"],
    "commands": ["npm run ui:audit:all:themes"],
    "screenshots": ["homepage", "docs", "account-overview"],
    "manual_review": true,
    "production_required": true
  },
  "evidence": [
    {
      "id": "EV-REQ-45-002-001",
      "type": "screenshot-report",
      "path": "reports/ui-surface-audit/<date>/report.md",
      "freshness": "current-commit",
      "confidence": "strong"
    }
  ],
  "claim_scope": {
    "public_copy_allowed": true,
    "limitations": []
  },
  "completion": {
    "done": false,
    "reason": "No current production screenshot proof attached."
  }
}
```

The requirement record is the smallest unit of "done." A wave, page, route, feature, or release can close only when every included requirement is done, explicitly external-blocked, or deliberately killed with rationale.

#### Traceability Matrix

Kolm needs a bidirectional traceability matrix, not only a list of tickets. Each row must support both questions:

1. Starting from a requirement, what implementation and proof satisfy it?
2. Starting from a file, route, page, test, screenshot, or claim, why does it exist and what requirement does it satisfy?

| Trace target | Required links |
|---|---|
| Requirement | master node, source rationale, priority, owner, status, dependencies, acceptance, proof, claim scope. |
| Source file | primary requirement, secondary requirements, owner lane, generated/manual state, tests, docs, risk, release inclusion. |
| Route | requirement, auth, schema, OpenAPI, docs, smoke, account/CLI/TUI parity, error model, telemetry. |
| Public page | requirement, page type, audience, copy map, visual system components, metadata, claim scope, screenshots. |
| Account page | requirement, live data source, loading/empty/error states, auth, next actions, API routes, screenshots, CLI/TUI parity. |
| Docs page | requirement, command/API/SDK source, examples, expected output, last verified, claim scope, broken-link proof. |
| Test | requirement IDs covered, risk class, fixture/data source, determinism controls, false-positive limits, owner. |
| Screenshot | route, viewport, theme, auth state, component states, commit, audit report, manual reviewer, known exceptions. |
| Generated artifact | source inputs, generator command, freshness hash, downstream consumers, release inclusion, stale-output gate. |
| Metric | requirement, event/trace source, aggregation, guardrail, decision threshold, privacy class, dashboard. |
| Release packet | included requirements, evidence summary, exceptions, external gates, production smoke, rollback, decision record. |

No source file or public surface should remain "orphaned." No requirement should be "implemented" without at least one downstream file or artifact and one proof method.

#### Evidence Strength Model

Evidence must be graded so weak proof cannot support broad claims:

| Grade | Name | Meaning | Can close requirement? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E0` | Missing | No evidence exists or evidence cannot be found. | No. |
| `E1` | Intent | Chat, plan, TODO, or roadmap says it should exist. | No. |
| `E2` | Static presence | File, page, route, or string exists. | No, except for documentation-only requirements. |
| `E3` | Local check | Unit/static check passes for the relevant requirement. | Sometimes, for local-only requirements. |
| `E4` | Integrated local proof | API/account/CLI/docs/generated artifacts work together locally. | Yes for local integrated requirements. |
| `E5` | User-facing demonstration | Screenshot, browser run, CLI run, or demo proves the product path. | Yes for UX/product requirements when production is not required. |
| `E6` | Production smoke | Production route/page/account/API path passes on deployed commit with safe credentials. | Yes for launch requirements. |
| `E7` | Production telemetry | Live usage confirms reliability, latency, quality, cost, and error guardrails. | Yes for operational claims. |
| `E8` | External proof | Public benchmark, package registry, certification, partner adoption, or auditor evidence exists. | Yes for external-proof claims. |

Rules:

- A broad feature claim requires the highest evidence grade demanded by its surface. Local tests cannot prove production readiness. Screenshots cannot prove backend correctness. API smoke cannot prove accessibility. A benchmark fixture cannot prove market leadership.
- Evidence expires. Production screenshots, provider catalogs, benchmark prices, model lists, package status, certification status, and search snippets need freshness windows.
- Contradictory evidence blocks completion. If one page says `Business` while pricing truth says `Team`, or one route inventory says 582 routes while docs say 417, the contradiction must create a work order.
- Evidence must be reproducible. Every report needs command, commit, environment, inputs, redactions, and known limitations.

#### Workorder Packet

Implementation agents should receive work orders generated from requirement rows, not vague paragraphs. A work order should contain:

```json
{
  "workorder_id": "WO-REQ-45-002",
  "title": "Normalize global navigation state and popout behavior",
  "requirements": ["REQ-45-002", "REQ-45-003"],
  "owner_lane": "frontend",
  "write_set": {
    "owned_paths": ["public/nav.js", "public/brand-refresh.css"],
    "read_only_paths": ["docs/component-registry.json", "reports/ui-surface-audit"],
    "generated_paths": [],
    "conflict_paths": ["public/index.html", "public/docs/**/*.html", "public/account/**/*.html"]
  },
  "instructions": {
    "scope": "Unify active nav, mega menu, popout, mobile drawer, focus, and touch states.",
    "non_goals": ["Do not rewrite product copy unless required for nav labels."],
    "acceptance": ["keyboard nav works", "mobile nav works", "dark/light screenshots pass", "no underline/pill conflict"]
  },
  "verification": {
    "local": ["npm run ui:audit:critical"],
    "manual": ["open homepage, docs, and account overview with keyboard and mobile viewport"],
    "production": ["prod visual smoke after deploy"]
  },
  "rollback": {
    "plan": "restore previous nav assets from commit and rerun route screenshots",
    "risk": "public navigation and account discovery"
  }
}
```

A work order is ready only when owned paths and conflict paths are explicit. This is the control that prevents two parallel agents from breaking each other on shared files such as `public/index.html`, `public/nav.js`, `public/brand-refresh.css`, `src/router.js`, `cli/kolm.js`, `package.json`, OpenAPI, product graph, CLI docs, or screenshot reports.

#### Baselines, Decisions, And Drift

The master spec needs a baseline system:

| Artifact | Baseline rule |
|---|---|
| Master spec tree | Every node has stable ID, title, subnodes, owner lane, detailed ledger link, and last reviewed date. |
| Requirements matrix | Generated from the blueprint and product graph; diffed on every spec update. |
| Decision log | Architecture, product, pricing, design-system, readiness, and release decisions get ADR-style records with status and consequences. |
| Evidence graph | Evidence records include path, command, commit, environment, freshness, confidence, and requirement IDs. |
| Workorder queue | Each open requirement maps to one active, pending, blocked, or closed work order. |
| Claim map | Public claims link to requirements and highest evidence grade; unsupported claims fail the gate. |
| Exception ledger | Any blocked, external-gated, local-only, screenshot-only, or accepted-risk item has owner, expiry, and public-copy restriction. |

Drift checks:

- Requirement without owner.
- Requirement without evidence method.
- Requirement marked done with weak evidence.
- File, route, page, test, screenshot, or generated artifact without requirement trace.
- Evidence older than its freshness window.
- Conflicting counts across product graph, API docs, sitemap, docs manifest, release packet, or this blueprint.
- Public copy that cites a requirement whose evidence grade is too low.
- Work order closed while generated artifacts or production smoke are stale.
- Active agent write set overlapping another active lane without explicit coordination.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `46.1` Normative requirement taxonomy, unique IDs, source rationale, priority, owner, status, and master-node mapping | Every binding requirement has stable ID, level, source, rationale, node, owner, priority, status, dependencies, and public-claim scope. |
| `46.2` Bidirectional traceability from requirements to files, routes, pages, tests, docs, screenshots, releases, and metrics | A generated RTM links requirements to implementation and proof artifacts in both directions, and fails on orphan requirements or orphan release-relevant files. |
| `46.3` Evidence graph, freshness, confidence, contradiction handling, verification method, and production-proof grading | Evidence records have type, grade, command, commit, environment, freshness, confidence, limitations, and contradiction rules. |
| `46.4` Implementation handoff packets, work orders, acceptance criteria, dependencies, write sets, and parallel-agent boundaries | Open requirements generate work orders with scope, non-goals, owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance, verification, rollback, and agent lane ownership. |
| `46.5` Change control, baselines, decisions, supersession, drift detection, dashboards, and completion audit workflow | Spec/tree/requirement/evidence changes are baselined, diffed, reviewed, and surfaced in dashboards; contradictions become work orders. |
| `46.6` Evidence: RTM, spec graph, ADRs, decision log, workorder packets, review packet, release audit, and production proof | Final release packet contains RTM, spec graph, decision log, work orders, evidence graph, exception ledger, completion audit, and production proof for every non-external requirement. |

#### Work Tickets

| Ticket | Branch | Work | Done when |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-SPEC-001` | `46.1` | Generate `docs/requirements-matrix.json`. | Every `REQ-*` row has ID, level, source, node, owner, status, priority, dependencies, claim scope, and verification method. |
| `W-SPEC-002` | `46.1` | Add requirement-language linting. | Uppercase `MUST`, `MUST NOT`, `SHOULD`, and `MAY` outside requirement records are either linked to IDs or downgraded to prose. |
| `W-SPEC-003` | `46.1` | Add source-rationale taxonomy. | Requirements classify origin as user feedback, repo scan, product graph, readiness closeout, external standard, customer need, security risk, or business metric. |
| `W-SPEC-004` | `46.2` | Build bidirectional trace generator. | Requirements link to files/routes/pages/tests/docs/screenshots/releases/metrics, and each release-relevant artifact links back to at least one requirement. |
| `W-SPEC-005` | `46.2` | Add orphan-artifact gate. | Release verification fails if a public page, route, source module, test, generated artifact, screenshot, or claim has no requirement trace. |
| `W-SPEC-006` | `46.2` | Add route/page/test trace coverage. | Route inventory, public page registry, account registry, docs manifest, and test inventory all expose requirement IDs. |
| `W-SPEC-007` | `46.3` | Create `docs/evidence-graph.json`. | Evidence rows include ID, requirement IDs, type, grade, path, command, commit, environment, freshness, confidence, limitations, and owner. |
| `W-SPEC-008` | `46.3` | Add evidence strength policy. | Completion checks reject requirements proven only by intent, static presence, stale screenshots, broad gates, or unrelated tests. |
| `W-SPEC-009` | `46.3` | Add contradiction detector. | Conflicting route counts, pricing terms, plan names, readiness counts, product claims, screenshots, metadata, and docs facts become work orders. |
| `W-SPEC-010` | `46.3` | Add freshness windows. | Provider catalogs, pricing, benchmark data, model lists, package status, certification status, screenshots, and production smokes expire on defined schedules. |
| `W-SPEC-011` | `46.4` | Generate implementation workorder packets. | Every open P0/P1 requirement emits a work order with scope, non-goals, owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance, verification, rollback, and claim scope. |
| `W-SPEC-012` | `46.4` | Add active-agent write-set registry. | Parallel agents declare lane, owned paths, generated paths, read-only paths, and conflict paths before editing shared surfaces. |
| `W-SPEC-013` | `46.4` | Add handoff packet templates by lane. | Frontend, backend, CLI, research, docs, release, design, security, and data lanes each receive tailored acceptance and proof templates. |
| `W-SPEC-014` | `46.4` | Add rollback and non-goal requirements. | Every non-doc work order states rollback plan, data migration reversibility, feature flag or kill switch where applicable, and explicit non-goals. |
| `W-SPEC-015` | `46.5` | Create decision log and ADR index. | Architecture, product, pricing, design, readiness, release, and legal decisions have status, context, decision, consequences, supersession, and owner. |
| `W-SPEC-016` | `46.5` | Add spec baseline and drift report. | Every run can compare current master tree, requirements matrix, product graph, readiness closeout, API docs, sitemap, and release packet against baseline. |
| `W-SPEC-017` | `46.5` | Add completion dashboard contract. | Dashboard shows requirements by node, status, owner, evidence grade, freshness, work order, external gate, and release readiness. |
| `W-SPEC-018` | `46.6` | Emit final completion audit packet. | Packet contains RTM, evidence graph, workorder status, exceptions, claim map, generated artifacts, screenshots, release verify, production smoke, and decision signoff. |
| `W-SPEC-019` | `46.6` | Add production-proof promotion gate. | Requirements that need production evidence cannot move to done until deployed commit, prod smoke, telemetry or screenshot proof, and rollback state are attached. |
| `W-SPEC-020` | `46.6` | Add independent reviewer checklist. | A reviewer can choose any claim, file, route, page, or test and trace it to requirement, implementation, evidence, release packet, and production status. |

#### Node 46 Relax Redline

Node `46` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- What exact requirement ID justifies every release-relevant file, route, page, docs page, test, generated artifact, screenshot, metric, and public claim?
- Which source produced each requirement: user feedback, product graph, repo scan, readiness closeout, external standard, security risk, customer need, or business metric?
- Which evidence grade proves each requirement, and is that evidence fresh enough for the claim being made?
- If two artifacts disagree, which one is authoritative, which requirement is blocked, and which work order fixes the contradiction?
- Can each implementation agent receive a work order with owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance tests, screenshots, rollback, and public-claim scope?
- Can a reviewer trace from a homepage hero claim to requirement, source rationale, copy map, component state, page file, screenshot, claim gate, and production smoke?
- Can a reviewer trace from a backend route to requirement, auth policy, schema, OpenAPI, docs, tests, smoke, telemetry, account/CLI/TUI parity, and release packet?
- Can the final "100 percent complete" claim survive requirement-by-requirement audit without relying on chat memory?

The relax condition for node `46` is not "the blueprint is large." It is: Kolm has a machine-readable requirements matrix, bidirectional traceability, evidence graph, workorder generator, decision log, drift detector, completion dashboard, and final audit packet that prove every current requirement, implementation artifact, public claim, and production proof path is owned, fresh, consistent, and reviewable.

#### Node 46 Requirements And Evidence Control Plane Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The newest user request is not a new branch. It is a stricter maintenance demand for node `46`: maintain a master spec tree that can prove the state of every requirement, every evidence artifact, every generated surface, every implementation handoff, and every active external gate without depending on chat memory or a single agent's local assumptions.

Node `46` V2 turns the existing V1 traceability model into a control plane. The goal is not another scanner. The goal is a working specification spine that lets any implementation agent answer, before editing code, exactly what the product must do, which files are owned, which evidence is required, what would constitute a contradiction, what acceptance gates must pass, and what public claims are allowed.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Artifact | Current observed state | Control-plane implication |
|---|---|---|
| `public/product-graph.json` | Generated graph currently reports `12` journeys, `8` customization dimensions, `69` API routes in product experience, `64` CLI commands, `32` TUI views, `33` account links, `7` route surfaces, `163` route groups, `582` routes, `57` readiness requirements, and `8` readiness groups. | Requirements cannot be maintained only from prose. The RTM must ingest the product graph and fail if graph counts drift from `docs/product-surfaces.json`, docs, CLI/TUI/account parity, or route inventories. |
| `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` | Public closeout ledger currently reports `8` open requirements: `2` P0, `6` P1; by status: `2` `needs_external_partner`, `1` `needs_live_certification`, `4` `needs_package_release`, `1` `needs_public_benchmark_data`. | The master spec must not allow local implementation completion to become marketing completion. These 8 items stay visible until proof promotes them. |
| Open closeout waves | Current open rows include `W571-public-leaderboard`, `W573-compliance-certification`, `W575-installer-release`, `W566-runtime-adapters`, `W565-format-governance`, `W572-sdk-release-matrix`, `W570-mobile-sdk-release`, and `W569-runtime-wasm-package`. | Every final-ship checklist must include these waves as external proof gates, not as hidden "later" work. |
| `docs/readiness-gate-workorders.json` | Readiness workorders exist as a generated/planned handoff layer. | V2 must normalize workorders into a durable packet format with owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance evidence, rollback, and claim scope. |
| `scripts/simulate-readiness-gate-workorders.cjs` | Readiness workorders are already simulated. | V2 must promote simulation from "document exists" to an acceptance gate: generated workorders must be executable enough for an implementation agent to begin without reinterpreting strategy prose. |
| `scripts/build-product-graph.cjs` and `scripts/build-readiness-closeout.cjs` | Product graph and closeout ledgers are generated artifacts. | Generated artifact sequencing must be explicit. Agents must not regenerate graph, API docs, OpenAPI, sitemap, and closeout ledgers concurrently without lock order. |
| `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs`, `scripts/audit-sota-readiness.cjs`, `scripts/audit-product-kernel.cjs`, `scripts/audit-product-journeys.mjs` | Claim scope, SOTA readiness, kernel, and journeys are already audited. | V2 must connect these gates to requirement evidence grades, not leave them as independent pass/fail scripts. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` and `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | Local and production smoke lanes exist. | Requirement states must distinguish local proof from production proof. A production-only requirement cannot be promoted by local smoke. |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` and `reports/ui-surface-audit` | UI screenshot evidence exists and is route/theme/viewport based. | Visual and narrative requirements must point to screenshot evidence with route, theme, viewport, timestamp, report path, and changed-surface scope. |
| `scripts/build-codegraph.mjs` | Source/codegraph evidence exists. | Source completion must use the codegraph to map files, imports, symbols, and routes to requirement owners, and to find orphaned implementation artifacts. |
| Package scripts | Current verification family includes `build:product-graph`, `build:readiness-closeout`, `verify:surfaces`, `local:surfaces`, `local:surfaces:deep`, `prod:surfaces`, `prod:surfaces:deep`, `verify:sota`, `verify:claims-scope`, `verify:journeys`, `verify:readiness-workorders`, `verify:benchmark-evidence`, `verify:depth`, and UI audit scripts. | The completion packet must record exactly which verification command proved each requirement, not merely that a broad suite passed once. |

##### External Baseline V2

| Baseline | Current source | Requirement for Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Requirements engineering | ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 defines requirements engineering activities for systems and software life-cycle processes: `https://www.iso.org/standard/72089.html`. | Kolm requirements must have stable IDs, source rationale, statement, acceptance criteria, verification method, owner, status, and change history. |
| Architecture description | ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022 specifies requirements for architecture descriptions across software, systems, enterprises, product lines, and service lines: `https://www.iso.org/standard/74393.html`. | Kolm architecture records must connect stakeholders, concerns, viewpoints, views, decisions, implementation files, and verification evidence. |
| Secure systems engineering | NIST SP 800-160 Vol. 1 Rev. 1 covers engineering trustworthy secure systems and includes security requirements, architecture, verification, validation, and life-cycle risk practices: `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/160/v1/r1/final`. | Security, privacy, tenant, compliance, artifact, and runtime requirements must carry threat, risk, and assurance evidence, not just feature proof. |
| Verification and validation matrix | NASA Systems Engineering Handbook Appendix C describes a Requirement Verification Matrix and V&V planning where verification establishes compliance and validation checks user expectations: `https://www.nasa.gov/reference/system-engineering-handbook-appendix/`. | Kolm must separate verification evidence from validation evidence and record both for every product journey. |
| Trace propagation | W3C Trace Context is a Recommendation that defines a shared format for propagating trace identity across systems: `https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/`. | Evidence records should carry trace IDs or equivalent correlation IDs from requirement to run, route, log, screenshot, report, and deployment proof. |
| Telemetry naming | OpenTelemetry semantic conventions define common names for operations and data: `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/`. | Kolm evidence telemetry should use stable `kolm.*` attributes and avoid ad hoc names that cannot be joined across product, CLI, API, account, and runtime events. |
| Structured validation | JSON Schema is the structured specification family for describing and validating JSON data: `https://json-schema.org/specification`. | Requirement, evidence, workorder, decision, claim, and closeout packets should have schemas and check-only generators. |
| Supply-chain provenance | SLSA defines provenance and supply-chain integrity levels for software artifacts: `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/`. | Build, package, SDK, installer, artifact, and generated-document evidence must record provenance, inputs, builder, digest, and release channel state. |

##### Requirement Record Contract V2

Every node-level requirement must be serializable as `kolm.requirement.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "REQ-046-EXAMPLE",
  "node": 46,
  "title": "Trace every product claim to evidence",
  "source": {
    "kind": "user_request|product_graph|repo_scan|external_standard|security_risk|customer_need|business_metric",
    "reference": "stable pointer or URL",
    "captured_at": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "statement": "The product shall ...",
  "scope": {
    "surface": "public|account|api|cli|tui|runtime|docs|infra|research|legal",
    "journeys": [],
    "customization_dimensions": [],
    "non_goals": []
  },
  "acceptance": {
    "verification_methods": ["inspection", "analysis", "test", "demo", "production_smoke", "external_attestation"],
    "evidence_grade_required": "E6",
    "freshness_days": 30,
    "commands": [],
    "manual_use_steps": []
  },
  "implementation": {
    "owned_paths": [],
    "generated_paths": [],
    "conflict_paths": [],
    "rollback": "description"
  },
  "status": "draft|accepted|in_progress|implemented_local|validated_local|blocked_external|production_proven|retired",
  "claim_scope": "internal_only|docs_scoped|public_scoped|production_scoped",
  "supersedes": [],
  "superseded_by": []
}
```

Redline: no requirement may be accepted if it lacks source, acceptance method, owned path class, evidence grade, freshness, and claim scope. No requirement may be promoted to public-scoped if the required evidence grade is below the public-claim threshold.

##### Evidence Record Contract V2

Every proof artifact must be serializable as `kolm.evidence.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "EVID-046-EXAMPLE",
  "requirement_ids": ["REQ-046-EXAMPLE"],
  "type": "test|smoke|screenshot|manual_walk|benchmark|route_inventory|openapi|codegraph|telemetry|external_attestation|decision",
  "grade": "E0|E1|E2|E3|E4|E5|E6|E7|E8",
  "freshness": {
    "captured_at": "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ",
    "expires_at": "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ",
    "freshness_days": 30
  },
  "artifact": {
    "path": "relative/path",
    "url": "optional",
    "sha256": "optional",
    "command": "optional command",
    "commit": "optional commit",
    "environment": "local|preview|production|external"
  },
  "result": {
    "status": "pass|fail|warning|blocked|superseded",
    "summary": "short result",
    "metrics": {}
  },
  "trace": {
    "trace_id": "optional W3C-compatible trace id",
    "span_id": "optional span id",
    "agent": "human|backend|frontend|research|cli|release",
    "source_run": "optional"
  }
}
```

Evidence grades:

| Grade | Meaning | Allowed claim scope |
|---|---|---|
| `E0` | Mentioned in chat or prose only. | None. |
| `E1` | Requirement recorded but no implementation proof. | Internal planning only. |
| `E2` | Static source presence or generated inventory. | Internal and engineering docs only. |
| `E3` | Unit or contract test passes locally. | Docs-scoped if caveated. |
| `E4` | Local integration or smoke proof. | Docs-scoped and account-scoped if explicitly local. |
| `E5` | Manual journey proof with screenshots/logs. | Public-scoped if claim is limited to the tested route and environment. |
| `E6` | Full release gate, generated docs, local smoke, and UI proof on changed surfaces. | Public-scoped for shipped local behavior. |
| `E7` | Production smoke, live telemetry, deployed commit, and rollback state. | Production-scoped. |
| `E8` | External attestation, package registry publication, benchmark publication, certification, or partner acceptance. | External trust, compliance, package, benchmark, or ecosystem claims. |

Freshness rule: launch-page copy, pricing, hero claims, account status, screenshot reports, production smoke, and package/certification claims expire fastest. Research rationale can live longer, but any volatile external source must be rechecked before being used as public proof.

##### Workorder Contract V2

Implementation workorders must be serializable as `kolm.workorder.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "WO-046-EXAMPLE",
  "requirement_ids": ["REQ-046-EXAMPLE"],
  "priority": "P0|P1|P2|P3",
  "lane": "frontend|backend|cli|docs|research|release|security|data|design",
  "objective": "single concrete outcome",
  "owned_paths": [],
  "read_only_paths": [],
  "conflict_paths": [],
  "generated_paths": [],
  "serial_generation_order": [],
  "acceptance": {
    "commands": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_steps": [],
    "evidence_required": []
  },
  "claim_scope": {
    "allowed": [],
    "forbidden_until": []
  },
  "rollback": {
    "strategy": "description",
    "data_migration": "none|required|irreversible",
    "kill_switch": "optional"
  },
  "handoff": {
    "summary_required": true,
    "file_paths_required": true,
    "remaining_gaps_required": true
  }
}
```

No agent should receive a vague "make it perfect" workorder. The perfect-state demand must be decomposed into workorders with owned paths, acceptance proof, and claim boundaries. For concurrent agents, `conflict_paths` must include generated files, global CSS, shared nav, router, product graph, API docs, OpenAPI, sitemap, package scripts, and any file with active parallel edits.

##### Decision Record Contract V2

Every durable product or architecture decision must be serializable as `kolm.decision.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "ADR-046-EXAMPLE",
  "status": "proposed|accepted|superseded|rejected",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "decision": "short statement",
  "context": "why this decision exists",
  "options_considered": [],
  "consequences": [],
  "requirements": [],
  "evidence": [],
  "supersedes": [],
  "superseded_by": []
}
```

Decision records are required for product category, pricing plan taxonomy, readiness status vocabulary, enterprise claim scope, external benchmark methodology, compliance language, canonical route ownership, generated artifact sequencing, package publication claims, and "Kolm" naming policy.

##### Drift And Contradiction Rules V2

The control plane must flag all of the following as contradictions:

| Drift class | Example | Required response |
|---|---|---|
| Product graph mismatch | `public/product-graph.json` route or readiness counts differ from product surfaces, API docs, or package verification. | Regenerate in the correct serial order or block completion. |
| Readiness closeout mismatch | Open closeout count differs between public JSON, docs markdown, SOTA readiness, and workorders. | Rebuild closeout, rebuild workorders, rerun `verify:sota` and `verify:readiness-workorders`. |
| Route/docs mismatch | Router exposes a route not owned by any product surface or OpenAPI/API docs lag behind. | Update route ownership, rebuild API ref/OpenAPI, rerun surface verifier. |
| Claim/evidence mismatch | Public copy says "certified", "published", "benchmark-leading", "one-line install", or "ecosystem supported" without E8 proof. | Demote copy or attach external proof. |
| Pricing/copy mismatch | Plan names, prices, CTA behavior, or legacy aliases conflict between frontend, API, CLI, billing, emails, and docs. | Use pricing truth source, patch all surfaces, rerun claim and screenshot gates. |
| Screenshot freshness mismatch | Public route changes without updated dark/light desktop/mobile proof. | Rerun focused screenshot audit for changed routes before marking complete. |
| Generated artifact race | Two agents regenerate API docs, OpenAPI, product graph, closeout, sitemap, or SDK docs in parallel. | Stop, choose one owner, run generation serially, then rerun diff and verification. |
| Active write conflict | Two lanes edit shared nav, global CSS, router, product graph, package scripts, or generated docs. | Split ownership or pause one lane; never overwrite parallel work. |
| External gate hidden | Package release, certification, partner adoption, or public benchmark remains open but is omitted from account/CLI/docs/product graph. | Restore closeout visibility and block public finality. |
| Brand/naming drift | `kolmogorov` appears where public product copy, package metadata, docs, or generated surfaces should say `kolm` or `Kolm`. | Classify intentional historical/internal names separately from public naming defects, then patch with owner approval. |

##### Traceability Coverage Targets V2

| Coverage target | Required output | Why it matters |
|---|---|---|
| Requirements matrix | `docs/requirements-matrix.v2.json` and markdown summary. | Every requirement has source, owner, status, acceptance, evidence grade, and claim scope. |
| Evidence graph | `docs/evidence-graph.v2.json`. | Every test, route smoke, screenshot, benchmark, codegraph, generated doc, and production proof is joined to requirements. |
| Workorder queue | `docs/workorders.v2.json`. | Implementation agents get owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance commands, rollback, and handoff proof. |
| Decision log | `docs/decision-log.v2.json` plus ADR markdown index. | Durable choices stop being rediscovered or contradicted in later waves. |
| Source completion ledger | Integrates node `43` codegraph and build redline. | Every file has a completion owner or a documented archival/non-product reason. |
| Evaluation envelope | Integrates node `42` K-Score, benchmark, calibration, claim, and redaction proof. | Quality claims are evidence-gated instead of narrative-gated. |
| Narrative map | Integrates node `44` product narrative, CTA taxonomy, SEO, and public copy. | Public copy cannot drift from product truth or evidence grade. |
| Visual registry | Integrates node `45` component, state, CSS, theme, and screenshot proof. | Styling and UI states are maintained as product contracts, not aesthetic afterthoughts. |
| Journey ledger | Integrates node `47` manual use labs. | "Actually use it" proof becomes part of the RTM, not a separate anecdote. |
| Final audit packet | Generated release packet with RTM, evidence graph, workorders, decisions, exceptions, screenshots, release verify, prod smoke, and external gates. | The phrase "100 percent complete" becomes auditable and falsifiable. |

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `W-SPEC-V2-001` | `46.1` | Generate `docs/requirements-matrix.v2.json` from this blueprint, product graph, readiness closeout, journey map, codegraph, and package scripts. | Check-only generator fails if any accepted requirement lacks source, owner, evidence grade, freshness, claim scope, and acceptance method. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-002` | `46.1` | Generate `docs/evidence-graph.v2.json`. | Every evidence artifact has requirement IDs, type, grade, freshness, artifact path or URL, result, and trace correlation. |

| `W-SPEC-V2-004` | `46.1` | Generate `docs/decision-log.v2.json` and ADR markdown index. | Product, architecture, pricing, readiness, compliance, generated-artifact, and naming decisions have status and supersession. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-005` | `46.2` | Build RTM generator from blueprint nodes and current product graph. | Requirements map bidirectionally to journeys, routes, CLI commands, TUI views, account links, readiness requirements, and docs pages. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-006` | `46.2` | Add artifact-to-requirement orphan gate. | No release-relevant file, route, page, public claim, screenshot report, or generated artifact ships without a requirement owner or explicit archival exemption. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-007` | `46.2` | Add requirement-to-evidence gate. | No requirement can move past `implemented_local` without evidence records that meet grade and freshness policy. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-008` | `46.2` | Add evidence freshness gate. | Expired screenshots, production smoke, pricing proof, package evidence, certification evidence, and external benchmark evidence demote the requirement state automatically. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-009` | `46.3` | Add contradiction detector. | Product graph, readiness closeout, workorders, API docs, sitemap, pricing, claim scope, screenshots, and generated docs cannot disagree silently. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-010` | `46.3` | Add active-agent write-set registry. | Concurrent agents must declare owned paths and generated paths; shared files require a single owner before edit or regeneration. |

| `W-SPEC-V2-012` | `46.3` | Add claim evidence-grade gate. | Public claims require grade thresholds; external benchmark, certification, package, and partner claims require `E8`. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-013` | `46.4` | Add route/page/account/CLI/TUI trace mapping. | A reviewer can start from any URL, route, CLI command, TUI view, account widget, or docs page and find requirements plus evidence. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-014` | `46.4` | Integrate source-completion, visual, narrative, and eval nodes. | Nodes `42`, `43`, `44`, and `45` feed the same RTM instead of separate docs. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-015` | `46.4` | Add production-proof promotion gate. | Production-scoped requirements require deployed commit, prod smoke, telemetry or screenshot proof, rollback state, and environment caveats. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-016` | `46.5` | Add decision supersession workflow. | Replaced choices retain history and downstream requirements know which decision is authoritative. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-017` | `46.5` | Add completion dashboard contract. | Dashboard reports requirements by node, status, owner, evidence grade, freshness, workorder, external gate, and release readiness. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-018` | `46.5` | Generate final completion audit packet. | Packet includes RTM, evidence graph, workorders, decisions, exceptions, claim map, generated artifacts, screenshots, release verify, prod smoke, and external gates. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-019` | `46.6` | Add independent reviewer checklist. | A reviewer can sample any claim, file, route, page, test, screenshot, or package and trace it end to end without asking an agent. |
| `W-SPEC-V2-020` | `46.6` | Add post-deploy traceability proof. | After deploy, production smoke and telemetry attach to the exact requirements promoted to production-scoped. |

##### Node `46` V2 Relax Redline

Node `46` V2 is relaxed only when the repository can generate and verify the following without manual interpretation:

- A requirement matrix that maps every accepted requirement to source rationale, product surface, owned paths, claim scope, acceptance method, evidence grade, freshness, and current status.
- An evidence graph that maps every relevant test, smoke, screenshot, benchmark, generated artifact, codegraph record, route inventory, production proof, and external attestation back to requirement IDs.
- A workorder queue that implementation agents can execute without guessing scope, overwriting parallel work, or hiding external gates.
- A decision log that explains durable architecture, product, pricing, readiness, naming, compliance, benchmark, and generation choices with supersession.
- A drift detector that catches mismatches between product graph, readiness closeout, workorders, API docs, OpenAPI, sitemap, claim scope, screenshots, pricing, account, CLI, TUI, and production proof.
- A completion dashboard and final audit packet that can support or reject the statement "Kolm is 100 percent complete" by requirement, evidence, and production status.

The strict rule is: if a file, claim, route, page, command, screenshot, benchmark, package, external standard, or completion statement cannot be traced through the V2 control plane, it is not part of the 100-percent-complete product yet.

#### Node 46 Audit-Grade Proof Graph And Generated Artifact Lock Addendum V3

Node `52` stays unused. The active requirement is still "maintain a master spec tree," and node `46` is the owner. V3 makes the tree audit-grade: every node, product claim, route, account page, public page, CLI command, TUI view, generated artifact, screenshot report, benchmark, package gate, external certification, and implementation work order must become a connected proof graph with provenance and lock ordering.

The important upgrade from V2 is that traceability is no longer only a requirements matrix. A 100-percent completion claim needs a graph that can answer four questions without chat memory: what changed, why it changed, who or which process produced it, and what current evidence proves it on the right environment.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V3

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Product graph | `public/product-graph.json` currently reports `12` journeys, `7` route surfaces, `163` route groups, `582` routes, `64` CLI commands, `32` TUI views, `33` account links, `57` readiness requirements, and `8` readiness groups. | The product graph must be a first-class input to the proof graph, not a side artifact. Counts become drift-sensitive baselines. |
| Readiness closeout | `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` currently reports `8` open requirements: `2` P0 and `6` P1; statuses are `2` external partner, `1` live certification, `4` package release, and `1` public benchmark data. | Finality cannot hide these gates. The proof graph must keep them visible until E8 evidence promotes them. |
| Script and verifier surface | Current scan found `281` script files, `81` audit/build/smoke/graph/claim/readiness/surface/release-style scripts, and `15,567,813` script bytes. | Scripts need owner, input, output, generated-path, and check-only contracts. Otherwise verification itself becomes another ungoverned surface. |
| Tests and pages | Current scan found `559` test files and `729` public HTML files. | A broad green test count or screenshot count is not enough. Each test and page needs requirement coverage or an archival/non-release exemption. |
| Research and docs | Current scan found `97` research files, plus product, SOTA, readiness, package, benchmark, journey, surface, frontier, invention, and workorder documents. | Research must feed requirement rationale and workorders; it cannot remain detached from implementation acceptance. |
| Existing workorder assets | `docs/readiness-gate-workorders.json` and `scripts/simulate-readiness-gate-workorders.cjs` exist. | V3 should generalize readiness workorders into all-node implementation packets with lane, paths, generated artifacts, acceptance, rollback, and claim scope. |
| Existing proof assets | `reports/ui-surface-audit` and `reports/kolm-live-smoke-data` exist. | Reports must be referenced by evidence records with route/theme/viewport/environment/freshness, not manually cited after the fact. |
| Current verification family | Package scripts include depth, surfaces, local/prod smoke, claim scope, SOTA, product graph, readiness closeout, benchmark evidence, package release, UI audit, and release verify gates. | The final packet must store which command proves which requirement. A monolithic `verify:depth` result is supporting evidence, not a substitute for requirement-specific proof. |

##### External Baseline Refreshed For V3

| Baseline | Current source | V3 import into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| W3C PROV | `https://www.w3.org/TR/prov-overview/` | Evidence records should model entities, activities, agents, derivations, versions, and provenance of provenance. Kolm needs proof about proof. |
| W3C Trace Context | `https://www.w3.org/TR/trace-context/` | Requirement runs, route smokes, screenshots, CLI runs, API calls, release verifies, and production telemetry should carry correlation IDs that can be joined across tools. |
| SLSA v1.2 | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` | Generated docs, SDK packages, installers, artifacts, benchmark packets, and release bundles need source/build provenance and verification summaries. |
| SLSA provenance model | `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.1/provenance` | Provenance should record build definition, run details, builder identity, external parameters, internal parameters, resolved dependencies, byproducts, and artifact digests. |
| SPDX 3.0.1 | `https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/scope/` | Supply-chain and AI artifact records should include BOM relationships, AI models, datasets, build information, provenance, integrity, and lifecycle links where applicable. |
| CycloneDX 1.7 | `https://cyclonedx.org/specification/overview/` | BOM evidence should support software, services, hardware, cryptographic assets, operational configurations, dependencies, lifecycle metadata, and provenance. |
| JSON Schema 2020-12 | `https://json-schema.org/specification` | Requirements, evidence, workorders, decision records, claims, closeout gates, screenshots, and release packets need schemas and check-only validation. |
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218 | `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final` | Secure development evidence should reduce vulnerabilities, mitigate impact, address root causes, and provide a common vocabulary for supplier/customer assurance. |
| OpenTelemetry semantic conventions 1.41.0 | `https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/` | Evidence telemetry should use stable names across HTTP, GenAI, CLI, object stores, CICD, exceptions, logs, metrics, traces, and resources. |

##### V3 Proof Graph Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.master-spec-proof-graph.v3",
  "release_id": "2026-05-node-46-v3",
  "inputs": {
    "blueprint": "docs/research/kolm-ai-100-percent-codebase-completion-blueprint-2026-05-25.md",
    "product_graph": "public/product-graph.json",
    "readiness_closeout": "public/product-readiness-closeout.json",
    "codegraph": "public/codegraph.json",
    "route_inventory": "public/docs/api-routes.json",
    "openapi": "public/openapi.json",
    "visual_graph": "docs/visual-system-graph.v3.json",
    "workorders": "docs/workorders.v3.json"
  },
  "node_types": ["requirement", "source", "decision", "file", "route", "page", "command", "test", "script", "generated_artifact", "screenshot", "benchmark", "telemetry", "external_proof", "workorder", "claim", "release"],
  "edge_types": ["derived_from", "implements", "verifies", "invalidates", "supersedes", "blocks", "generates", "owns", "mentions", "ships_in", "requires_production", "requires_external_proof"],
  "provenance": {
    "model": "W3C_PROV_plus_SLSA",
    "required_fields": ["entity", "activity", "agent", "started_at", "finished_at", "inputs", "outputs", "builder", "environment", "commit", "digest", "limitations"]
  },
  "trace": {
    "format": "W3C trace-context compatible",
    "required_for": ["release_verify", "local_surface_smoke", "prod_surface_smoke", "ui_audit", "benchmark", "package_release", "generated_artifact"]
  },
  "freshness": {
    "volatile_public_copy_days": 14,
    "screenshots_days": 14,
    "production_smoke_days": 7,
    "provider_catalog_days": 14,
    "package_registry_days": 7,
    "certification_days": 30,
    "research_rationale_days": 180
  },
  "promotion_rules": {
    "planning_to_local": ["requirement", "workorder", "implementation_trace", "local_evidence"],
    "local_to_public": ["claim_map", "docs", "screenshots", "release_gate"],
    "public_to_production": ["deployed_commit", "prod_smoke", "rollback_state", "telemetry_or_manual_prod_evidence"],
    "production_to_external_trust": ["package_registry_or_certification_or_partner_or_public_benchmark_evidence"]
  }
}
```

##### Generated Artifact Lock Order V3

Parallel agents are allowed, but generated artifacts are serialized. The proof graph must record lock ownership and order:

1. Source/spec edit lands in an owned write set.
2. Requirement matrix and workorders regenerate from the blueprint, product graph, and readiness closeout.
3. Product graph regenerates only after source/product-surface changes settle.
4. API route inventory regenerates before OpenAPI.
5. OpenAPI regenerates before API reference pages that consume it.
6. Readiness closeout regenerates before readiness workorders.
7. Claim map regenerates after pricing, readiness, benchmark, package, certification, and public-copy changes settle.
8. Visual graph and route/component matrix regenerate before screenshots.
9. Screenshot reports generate after all visual/content changes settle.
10. Release packet collects immutable pointers to the exact graph, docs, screenshots, smokes, tests, and external proof used for the release decision.

Redline: no agent may run two writers for the same generated artifact concurrently. If a race occurs, the artifact is stale until regenerated serially and traced in the proof graph.

##### Completion Promotion State Machine V3

| State | Meaning | Required evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `draft` | Requirement exists but is not accepted. | Source rationale only. |
| `accepted` | Requirement is part of the product target. | Owner, priority, scope, acceptance, non-goals, evidence grade. |
| `workordered` | Implementation packet exists. | Owned paths, conflict paths, generated paths, acceptance commands, rollback. |
| `implemented_local` | Code or content exists locally. | File trace, generated artifacts, focused tests or inspection. |
| `verified_local` | Local behavior passes. | Requirement-specific tests, local smoke, screenshots or manual steps as applicable. |
| `release_ready` | Release packet proves local ship state. | Full required gates, generated docs, claim map, exception ledger, no stale generated artifacts. |
| `production_proven` | Deployed behavior is proved. | Deployed commit, production smoke, production screenshots or telemetry, rollback state. |
| `external_proven` | External claims are proved. | Package registry, public benchmark, auditor/certification, partner adoption, or standards venue evidence. |
| `blocked_external` | Local work is done but outside proof is missing. | Closeout record, public-copy restriction, owner, next wave, done-when. |
| `retired` | Requirement is intentionally removed. | Decision record, supersession or deletion rationale, affected claims closed. |

##### V3 Trace Walks

The proof graph must support these reviewer walks:

- Start from a homepage claim and trace to narrative node, requirement, claim-scope gate, component/copy file, screenshot, source commit, release packet, and production smoke.
- Start from a backend route and trace to product surface, auth policy, schema, OpenAPI, docs, tests, account/CLI/TUI parity, smoke, telemetry, and release inclusion.
- Start from a CSS selector and trace to visual token, component state, route/component matrix, screenshot, accessibility proof, and exception ledger if needed.
- Start from a benchmark number and trace to methodology, fixture, script, raw report, model/provider versions, cost/latency data, claim map, and freshness.
- Start from a package install claim and trace to package build, SLSA provenance, SBOM, registry publication, installer smoke, docs command, and production/docs copy.
- Start from an account readiness badge and trace to backend readiness route, closeout requirement, account component state, CLI/TUI parity, screenshot, and production smoke.
- Start from a generated artifact and trace to generator script, inputs, lock order, output digest, consuming docs/routes, and verification command.

| `W-SPEC-V3-002` | `46.1` | Add JSON Schemas for requirement, evidence, workorder, decision, claim, closeout, release, and proof-graph records. | `--check` validation fails on missing IDs, owners, evidence grades, freshness, provenance, or claim scope. |

| `W-SPEC-V3-017` | `46.5` | Add independent sampling tool. | Reviewer can sample random files/routes/claims/tests and require end-to-end trace proof. |
| `W-SPEC-V3-018` | `46.5` | Add external-proof demotion gate. | If registry, certification, partner, benchmark, or standards evidence expires or disappears, public copy demotes automatically. |
| `W-SPEC-V3-019` | `46.6` | Emit `reports/final-proof-packet/<date>/manifest.json`. | Manifest links proof graph, requirements, evidence, workorders, decisions, generated artifacts, screenshots, smokes, release verify, prod proof, and open external gates. |
| `W-SPEC-V3-020` | `46.6` | Add final audit rehearsal. | Before any "100 percent complete" claim, run a requirement-by-requirement rehearsal and record pass/fail/blocked evidence for every node. |

##### Node `46` V3 Relax Redline

Node `46` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- The proof graph can be generated from the current tree and validates against JSON Schemas.
- Every release-relevant file, route, public page, account page, CLI command, TUI view, test, script, generated artifact, screenshot report, benchmark, package claim, and public claim has a primary requirement owner or an explicit exemption.
- Every generated artifact has source inputs, generator command, output digest, serial lock order, owner, freshness, and consuming artifacts.
- Every requirement promotion is evidence-grade correct: local work cannot become production proof, and production proof cannot become package, benchmark, certification, or partner proof.
- Every external gate remains visible in closeout, workorders, product graph, docs/account/CLI surfaces, and public-copy restrictions until E8 proof exists.
- Every claim can be traced forward to code/content and backward to source rationale.
- Every implementation workorder has owned paths, conflict paths, generated paths, acceptance, rollback, non-goals, and handoff requirements.
- Every release packet can be sampled by an independent reviewer without asking an agent what happened.
- Every "100 percent complete" statement is blocked unless the final proof packet contains fresh local, production, and external evidence for every requirement that needs it.

If any of those fail, the master spec tree may be larger, but it is not yet an audit-grade completion system.

#### Node 46 V4 Master Spec Tree Operating Ledger And Change-Control Addendum

Node `52` stays unused. The active instruction is to maintain the master spec tree, so the work belongs to node `46`. V4 makes that maintenance operational: every future edit, implementation wave, code review, screenshot pass, backend route, frontend page, research invention, package release, claim change, and deploy proof must enter the tree as a change packet before it can be called part of the completion program.

The strict rule is: the tree is not a table of contents. It is the control system for deciding what exists, who owns it, what evidence proves it, what state it is in, which branch it belongs to, which external gates remain blocked, and what must happen before the product can be called finished.

##### V4 Current Tree State

| Signal checked | Current observed state | Maintenance implication |
|---|---|---|
| Canonical node range | Nodes `0` through `51` are allocated; node `52` remains unused. | New work must attach to an existing node unless a genuinely new product/control plane cannot fit the range. |
| Product graph source | `public/product-graph.json`, schema `kolm-product-graph-1`, kernel version `2026-05-22`. | Generated product graph is the current count source; older prose counts become historical when they disagree. |
| Product graph counts | 12 journeys, 7 route surfaces, 163 route groups, 582 routes, 69 API routes, 64 CLI commands, 32 TUI views, 33 account links, 57 readiness requirements. | Tree ownership must cover all product journeys, route surfaces, APIs, CLI/TUI commands, account links, and readiness rows. |
| Readiness counts | 14 shipped, 35 implemented, 2 needs external partner, 1 needs live certification, 4 needs package release, 1 needs public benchmark data. | Public copy, docs, account UI, CLI, sales packets, and release notes must preserve these scopes until evidence promotes them. |
| Closeout ledger | `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` reports 8 open requirements: 2 P0 and 6 P1. | The tree is maintained, not complete; external/package/certification/benchmark gates remain visible. |
| Active doc state | This blueprint is currently an untracked spec artifact in the local worktree. | It can guide implementation now, but release finality requires explicit commit or archive policy. |
| Parallel-agent context | Frontend, backend, research, and CLI workstreams have been active in the same repository. | The tree must record branch ownership and conflict paths instead of assuming one agent controls all files. |

##### V4 Source-Of-Truth Precedence

When two sources disagree, resolve the conflict with this precedence order and create a contradiction workorder under node `46`:

1. Production evidence for the deployed commit, when the claim is production-scoped.
2. Release packet evidence for the exact commit, when the claim is release-scoped.
3. Generated product graph and readiness closeout ledgers, when the claim is about current product counts or open gates.
4. Source code, package manifests, tests, scripts, and generated docs, when the claim is about local implementation.
5. Screenshots, manual journey reports, browser evidence, and support packets, when the claim is about user-visible behavior.
6. External source records, when the claim is about competitors, standards, package channels, certification, partner adoption, pricing, or market proof.
7. This blueprint, when the claim is a desired requirement, design rule, work packet, or redline.
8. Chat logs and agent summaries, only as source rationale until converted into an evidence record.

If a higher-precedence source contradicts the blueprint, do not silently edit product claims to fit the blueprint. Update the tree, demote the claim if needed, and attach the conflict to a workorder.

##### V4 Master Node Record

Every canonical node must eventually generate a record with this shape:

```json
{
  "node_id": "46",
  "title": "Master spec traceability and evidence graph",
  "primary_owner": "spec-maintainer",
  "branch_type": "canonical-maintenance",
  "scope": ["requirements", "evidence", "workorders", "decisions", "proof_graph", "tree_drift"],
  "out_of_scope": ["feature implementation without a downstream owner", "marketing copy without proof owner"],
  "source_paths": [
    "docs/research/kolm-ai-100-percent-codebase-completion-blueprint-2026-05-25.md",
    "public/product-graph.json",
    "public/product-readiness-closeout.json"
  ],
  "generated_paths": [
    "docs/master-spec-tree.json",
    "docs/requirements-matrix.json",
    "docs/evidence-graph.json",
    "docs/workorders.json"
  ],
  "proof_commands": ["npm run verify:depth", "git diff --check"],
  "required_evidence": ["local", "generated", "manual", "production", "external_when_applicable"],
  "open_gates": ["package_release", "public_benchmark_data", "external_partner", "live_certification"],
  "last_verified_at": "manual-update-required",
  "status": "maintained_not_complete"
}
```

##### V4 Change Packet Contract

Any implementation, research, design, docs, or release change that wants to count toward 100 percent completion must create or imply a packet with these fields:

```json
{
  "change_id": "SPEC-CHANGE-YYYYMMDD-NNN",
  "primary_node": "46",
  "secondary_nodes": ["31", "43"],
  "source_event": "user_request|repo_scan|bug|research|deploy|test_failure|manual_use",
  "claim_affected": "what public or internal claim changes",
  "owned_paths": [],
  "read_only_paths": [],
  "conflict_paths": [],
  "generated_paths": [],
  "proof_required": ["tests", "screenshots", "manual_use", "prod_smoke", "external_source"],
  "acceptance": [],
  "rollback_or_revert": "how to undo safely",
  "open_questions": [],
  "status": "proposed|in_progress|implemented_local|verified_local|prod_deployed_unverified|verified_prod|blocked_external|rejected"
}
```

No change packet may use `done` as a terminal state. Terminal states are evidence-scoped: `verified_local`, `verified_prod`, `blocked_external`, or `rejected`.

##### V4 Branch Lock Register

This is the current safe routing rule while multiple agents work:

| Work lane | Primary node | Locked/shared paths | Required handoff |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Spec-tree maintenance | `46` | this blueprint and future tree/RTM/evidence artifacts | Document hygiene, current graph counts, and no-node-52 check. |
| Backend/API/product truth | `2` | router, product kernel, OpenAPI/API refs, API tests | Route ownership, product graph update, local/deep surface smoke. |
| Frontend/site/account/design | `30` | public HTML/CSS/JS, nav, account pages, screenshots | Route screenshots, accessibility, copy/claim scope, production replay. |
| Research/inventions/roadmap | `31` | research docs, invention portfolio, wave registries | Additive work packets with maturity, proof state, and implementation owner. |
| Codebase/build redline | `43` | file ledger, build graph, clean tree, release inclusion | File owner/class/closure record and final build proof. |
| Coding assistant/build-finishing product | `50` | code completion surfaces, MCP, IDE integrations, codegraph | End-to-end build finishing tasks and privacy-safe evals. |
| Market/buyer proof | `48` | compare, ROI, case studies, social proof, sales packets | Source registry, claim ledger, permissions, freshness, and production smoke. |
| Demo/media/interactive runner | `51` | homepage runner, video, media assets, screenshots | Media manifest, video transcript/captions, runner smoke, visual proof. |

If a path is shared across lanes, one agent owns the write and the other agents consume the result. Generated artifacts must run serially from source to output; parallel regeneration is a tree violation.

##### V4 Tree Invariants

The master spec tree must maintain these invariants on every pass:

1. Exactly one primary node owns each requirement, file, route, page, account surface, command, test, script, generated artifact, screenshot report, claim, and release proof.
2. Secondary nodes are allowed only for dependency, UX exposure, evidence, or governance relationships; they do not replace the primary owner.
3. Node `52` remains unused until a written fit check proves nodes `0` through `51` cannot own the work.
4. Open external gates remain open until outside proof exists; local code, screenshots, or agent statements cannot promote them.
5. Generated counts in this blueprint must be explicitly marked current or historical.
6. Every implementation wave must cite the node it advances and the node whose proof gate will verify it.
7. Every public claim must trace to a requirement, evidence object, freshness policy, and claim scope.
8. Every claim of "state-of-the-art" must be decomposed into measurable product, UX, reliability, security, market, and evidence requirements.
9. Every final answer that says deploy, ship, done, complete, or clean must cite the evidence packet and tree nodes covered.
10. The tree must prefer deletion, archival, or explicit exemption over keeping unowned files, stale pages, or speculative copy.

##### V4 Daily Maintenance Loop

The tree maintainer should run this loop whenever the worktree changes materially:

1. Read `public/product-graph.json` and `public/product-readiness-closeout.json`.
2. Check whether route counts, CLI/TUI counts, account links, readiness requirements, or open gates changed.
3. Check `git status --short` for touched product surfaces and assign each to a primary node.
4. Check whether any touched file belongs to a lane another agent currently owns.
5. Update the affected node addendum, not a disconnected note.
6. Add or update work tickets with owned paths, conflict paths, generated paths, acceptance, and proof.
7. Preserve external gates and stale-proof warnings.
8. Run document hygiene: ASCII, trailing whitespace, and `git diff --check` for the blueprint.
9. Report the exact section and paths changed.
10. Do not call the whole codebase complete until node `43`, node `46`, and node `29` all have fresh release/prod proof.
