##### Node `46` V4 Relax Redline

Node `46` V4 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- `docs/master-spec-tree.v4.json`, requirements matrix, evidence graph, workorders, decision log, and proof graph generate from current sources.
- Every node `0` through `51` has owner, scope, out-of-scope, source paths, generated paths, proof commands, evidence grades, and open-gate status.
- Every tracked file, route, page, account link, CLI command, TUI view, test, script, generated artifact, screenshot report, claim, release packet, and external proof item maps to a primary node or explicit exemption.
- Every active agent lane has write-set ownership and conflict paths recorded before shared files or generated artifacts are edited.
- Every stale count, contradiction, hidden open gate, unsupported claim, unowned file, unowned route, or expired proof creates a workorder.
- Every release packet can prove which nodes were changed, which requirements were promoted, which gates remain external, and which production evidence exists.
- Node `52` remains unused unless a formal fit check proves the current tree cannot represent the new work.

Until those are true, the master spec tree is maintained and useful, but not fully operational.

### End-To-End Product Journeys, Dogfooding, Manual-Use Labs, And First-Value Proof Ledger V1

Node `47` is the answer to "do not scan or test, actually use it." A state-of-the-art Kolm build cannot be certified by static scans, route inventories, screenshot counts, or unit tests alone. Those are necessary, but they do not prove that a real evaluator can understand the product, migrate a call, create a `.kolm` artifact, inspect evidence, deploy it, operate it, recover from failure, and trust the account UI without founder handholding.

This node turns product usage into a formal evidence lane. Every critical journey must be walked as a user would walk it: in a browser, through docs, through API calls, through CLI/TUI, through account pages, through failures, through support paths, and through production. A journey is complete only when the user reaches the intended business outcome and the proof packet records what happened, what failed, what was confusing, what was slow, and which requirement or work order owns the fix.

#### Current Journey Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 47 redline |
|---|---|---|
| E2E product walk script | `scripts/e2e-walk.mjs` describes a real-user path: health, admin probe, signup, account state, compile, poll, download `.kolm`, run, capture, evolve, team, tunnel, BYOC, and airgap status. | Convert this from a one-off script into a maintained journey family with persona, prerequisites, expected outputs, screenshots, logs, and product-metric assertions. |
| Buyer journey script | `scripts/audit-buyer-journey.sh` walks 30 public/proof probes across landing, pricing, docs, tutorials, trust, and signup. It contains stale plan/copy expectations such as old pricing labels. | Keep the buyer walk, but regenerate expectations from product narrative, pricing, sitemap, and claim-scope truth instead of hardcoded stale copy. |
| Manual-use report | `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account/report.md` covered 4 of 548 routes and failed with 36 failures and 44 warnings, including missing product media, invisible media, negative H1 tracking, missing shared CSS/nav, and small target warnings. | Manual-use audits must be promoted to release-critical journey evidence and rerun after fixes on current local and production commits. |
| Screenshot and QA assets | The repo has many screenshot scripts and QA images under `scripts/`, `scripts/video/`, and `reports/ui-surface-audit/`. | Screenshots must be tied to journeys and user tasks, not only route rendering. A pretty screenshot without task success is weak evidence. |
| Product journey catalog | `docs/product-journeys.json` and `src/product-experience.js` define product journeys and entry points. | The catalog must include human task scenarios, first-value thresholds, failure states, and required proof packets for each journey. |
| Dogfood hooks | `scripts/dogfood-proxy.ps1`, support automation examples, showcase specs, and support artifacts exist. | Kolm should use its own gateway, capture, artifact, support, eval, telemetry, and docs workflows safely, with privacy and escalation controls. |
| Test suite | E2E and golden-path tests exist, including `tests/e2e.test.js`, `tests/wave255-e2e-compile-distill.test.js`, `tests/wave411-golden-e2e-smoke.test.js`, and `tests/wave458-dod-12-step-e2e.test.js`. | Automated E2E tests should cover journey mechanics, but human/manual labs must still capture comprehension, confidence, friction, and trust. |

#### External Product-Validation Baseline

| Baseline | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Nielsen Norman Group usability testing guidance | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/usability-testing-101/` | Usability testing asks realistic participants to perform realistic tasks while researchers observe behavior and feedback. Kolm needs realistic persona tasks, not only internal route checks. |
| Nielsen Norman Group task scenarios and heuristic frame | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/` | Real journeys should inspect visibility of system status, real-world language, user control, consistency, error prevention, recovery, and helpful documentation. |
| Google HEART framework | `https://research.google.com/pubs/pub36299.html` | Journey validation should map goals to happiness, engagement, adoption, retention, and task success signals, not only clicks or conversions. |
| W3C WCAG conformance evaluation | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/test-evaluate/conformance/` and `https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/conformance.html` | Accessibility evidence should include human evaluation and, where possible, users with disabilities; automated checks alone are not enough. |
| DORA metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Journey failures caused by releases should feed rework, change failure, recovery, and post-ship quality metrics. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | local skill `ui-ux-pro-max` | Journeys must verify accessibility, touch targets, keyboard navigation, responsive behavior, loading feedback, error recovery, performance, and consistent visual states. |

#### Journey Scenario Model

Every journey should have a record shaped like this:

```json
{
  "journey_id": "JNY-DEVELOPER-FIRST-ARTIFACT",
  "title": "Developer routes one OpenAI call and compiles the first .kolm artifact",
  "persona": "developer",
  "environment": ["local", "preview", "production"],
  "channels": ["browser", "docs", "api", "cli", "account"],
  "starting_state": {
    "auth": "new-user",
    "credentials": "safe test key",
    "provider_config": "openai-or-mock-provider",
    "tenant_data": "empty"
  },
  "tasks": [
    {
      "id": "T1",
      "instruction": "Find the migration path from a raw OpenAI call to Kolm.",
      "success": "User can identify the base URL or SDK swap and privacy caveat without searching elsewhere.",
      "max_time_seconds": 120
    },
    {
      "id": "T2",
      "instruction": "Run the first routed call and inspect the receipt.",
      "success": "Account and API show provider, model, policy, cost, redaction, and request ID."
    },
    {
      "id": "T3",
      "instruction": "Compile repeated examples into a signed artifact.",
      "success": "Artifact exists, K-Score/eval scope is visible, verification command works, and rollback/failure states are understandable."
    }
  ],
  "evidence": {
    "screenshots": [],
    "api_logs": [],
    "cli_logs": [],
    "recording": null,
    "session_notes": [],
    "support_bundle": null
  },
  "metrics": {
    "task_success": null,
    "time_to_first_value_seconds": null,
    "friction_events": [],
    "confidence_rating": null,
    "error_recovery_success": null
  },
  "blocking_findings": []
}
```

The journey record is not a vanity UX artifact. It is the bridge between product ambition and implementation quality. If a feature cannot be completed in a journey, it is not finished no matter how many routes or tests exist.

#### Critical Journey Library

| Journey | Persona | First value | Channels required | Redline failures |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Anonymous five-second comprehension | AI company founder, CTO, platform lead | Understand that Kolm routes traffic, compiles/distills artifacts, and runs governed models anywhere. | Browser, mobile, light/dark, homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs landing. | Cannot name what Kolm is; thinks it is only one API; thinks it is only audit software; misses compile/distill/runtime. |
| First routed call | Developer | A real or mocked OpenAI-compatible request routes through Kolm and returns a receipt. | Docs, API, SDK, CLI, account capture log. | Missing key setup, stale code sample, no receipt, no redaction status, no clear fallback. |
| First artifact | Developer or ML engineer | Repeated examples compile into a signed `.kolm` artifact with eval scope and verify command. | Browser demo, API, CLI, docs, account artifact page. | Artifact output unclear, K-Score unsupported, no verification command, hidden failure state. |
| Distill readiness | ML engineer | Account says whether the namespace is ready to distill and exactly what is missing. | Account, API, CLI, docs, K-Score/eval pages. | Jargon wall, fake readiness, no holdout/privacy/label requirement, no next action. |
| Runtime deployment | Platform engineer | Artifact deploys or dry-runs to local, browser, cloud/BYOC, device, or edge target with readiness proof. | Account, CLI, API, storage/cloud readiness, docs, logs. | "Run anywhere" without target limits, missing credentials unclear, no rollback or smoke. |
| Account command center | Operator | User can see product graph, open gates, billing, storage/cloud, capture, artifacts, incidents, and next actions. | Authenticated account, API, docs, CLI/TUI parity. | Decorative metrics, stale data, no loading/error states, no direct action. |
| Enterprise procurement | Security/compliance/procurement | Buyer can export or request a trust packet and understand certification/package/benchmark limits. | Enterprise page, trust pages, pricing, inquiry, account, email/support handoff. | Unsupported certification, fake self-serve enterprise, missing DPA/BAA/security route, no owner/SLA. |
| Support recovery | Customer admin or support engineer | User can report a failed compile/deploy/billing/security issue with safe support bundle and trace ID. | Account, API envelope, docs, status, support form, admin/support export. | Requires raw secrets or prompts, no trace ID, no status, no escalation path. |
| Pricing and ROI | Buyer or finance reviewer | User can map use case to Free/Pro/Team/Enterprise, estimate savings, and know what is custom. | Pricing, ROI, billing API/account, enterprise inquiry. | Legacy plan copy, hidden meters, fake enterprise checkout, ROI assumptions unclear. |
| Marketplace and registry | Developer/operator | User can inspect, verify, compare, install, and revoke an artifact safely. | Public registry, account, CLI, docs, artifact passport. | Trust proof buried, no license/runtime limits, no malware/rights status, no verify path. |
| Accessibility-assisted path | Keyboard/screen-reader/zoom user | Critical journey can be completed without mouse, hover-only UI, visual-only state, or hidden labels. | Browser, keyboard, screen reader spot check, zoom, mobile. | Focus trap, small target, missing label, status by color only, inaccessible demo. |
| Production post-deploy replay | Release owner | Same critical journeys pass on deployed `kolm.ai` with current commit and safe credentials. | Production browser, API, account, CLI, screenshots, logs, telemetry. | Local-only pass, stale deployed assets, missing env vars, no rollback evidence. |

#### Friction Taxonomy

Every manual-use finding should use a stable taxonomy so implementation agents can fix root causes:

| Friction class | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `comprehension` | User cannot tell what the product does or why it matters. | Hero serves one surface but not compile/distill/runtime. |
| `navigation` | User cannot find the next page or active state is misleading. | Underline and pill states conflict; mobile nav hides product surface. |
| `setup` | User cannot configure key, provider, tenant, storage, or environment. | Provider credential missing but page does not say which env var. |
| `trust` | User does not know whether claim is proven, beta, external-gated, or certified. | SOC 2 wording lacks certification scope. |
| `execution` | Task action fails, hangs, or produces unclear output. | Compile job finishes but artifact page lacks verify command. |
| `state` | Loading, empty, error, partial, or stale state is absent or dishonest. | Account table shows decorative data instead of "no captures yet." |
| `accessibility` | Keyboard, screen reader, focus, contrast, target size, zoom, or reduced motion fails. | Copy button is 25px tall or focus is invisible. |
| `performance` | User waits, layout shifts, media blocks first value, or input lags. | Hero media delays LCP or disappears in light mode. |
| `copy` | Wording is jargon-heavy, stale, contradictory, or not action-oriented. | "Owned AI" without mechanism. |
| `claim` | Product says more than evidence supports. | "Run anywhere" with no device readiness. |
| `handoff` | User cannot move from public to account, docs to CLI, account to support, or sales to engineering. | Enterprise inquiry has no owner/SLA/procurement packet. |
| `recovery` | User cannot undo, retry, rollback, export, contact support, or understand failure. | Failed deployment lacks rollback target. |

#### Dogfooding Contract

Kolm should be a customer of its own product before broad claims ship:

| Dogfood workflow | Required proof |
|---|---|
| Kolm support triage | Support tickets route through Kolm with redaction, artifact output, confidence, human review, escalation, and audit receipt. |
| Kolm docs QA | Docs examples route through Kolm eval/check artifacts that flag stale commands, unsupported claims, and missing expected output. |
| Kolm product analytics | Capture and journey events feed Kolm evals that identify repeated friction, drift, and activation blockers without storing unsafe raw payloads. |
| Kolm release review | Release notes, claim-scope changes, and generated docs are checked by Kolm artifacts and then human-reviewed. |
| Kolm incident replay | Incident timelines, support bundles, traces, and customer notifications are summarized by a scoped artifact with no secrets. |
| Kolm benchmark review | Benchmark reports and K-Score explanations are checked for scope, data freshness, model versions, and claim safety. |
| Kolm sales/procurement packet | Enterprise inquiry creates a draft packet from product graph/readiness/evidence, then routes to human review. |

Dogfooding cannot bypass safety. It must use redaction, tenant isolation, no raw secrets, confidence thresholds, human review for external output, and audit logs.

#### Manual Lab Protocol

Each release candidate should run a small, repeatable lab:

1. Pick at least one participant or reviewer for each major persona: developer, ML engineer, platform operator, security/compliance buyer, executive buyer, support/admin.
2. Give scenario tasks, not feature tours.
3. Do not explain the intended UI unless the protocol requires a hint.
4. Record time to first value, completion status, errors, confusion, detours, copy questions, trust concerns, and recovery behavior.
5. Run with keyboard, mobile viewport, light theme, dark theme, slow network, and at least one accessibility-assisted pass.
6. Attach screenshots, browser recording or notes, API/CLI logs, generated artifacts, and support bundle where relevant.
7. Convert every serious friction into a requirement/work order.
8. Rerun the same task after fixes and compare task success, time, confidence, and errors.

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |
|---|---|
| `47.1` Persona scenario library, task scripts, success criteria, friction taxonomy, and first-value definitions | Every persona has realistic scenarios, tasks, success criteria, max-time guidance, first-value definition, friction taxonomy, and evidence requirements. |
| `47.2` Anonymous buyer, developer, ML engineer, operator, admin, compliance, support, and executive journey walks | Critical journeys cover public, docs, pricing, enterprise, signup, first routed call, first artifact, distill readiness, deployment, account command center, support, and procurement. |
| `47.3` Local, preview, production, authenticated, unauthenticated, CLI, API, SDK, TUI, account, docs, and browser proof | Journey evidence spans all relevant channels and environments, with safe credentials and current commit/version recorded. |
| `47.4` Dogfooding of routing, capture, distill, artifact, support, docs, telemetry, incident, and procurement workflows | Kolm uses its own product safely for support, docs QA, release review, incident replay, benchmark review, and procurement packet drafting. |
| `47.5` Human usability review, accessibility-assisted review, session notes, issue triage, metric impact, and regression loops | Manual labs include session notes, accessibility-assisted checks, friction classification, owner, severity, product-metric impact, work order, and rerun proof. |
| `47.6` Evidence: journey lab packet, task recordings, screenshots, transcripts, CLI/API logs, support bundles, and production replay | Release evidence includes local and production journey packets with recordings or notes, screenshots, logs, traces, CLI/API output, artifacts, support bundles, and production replay. |

| `W-USE-020` | `47.6` | Add final production journey gate. | Release cannot be called complete until local and production journey packets pass for buyer, developer, ML, operator, enterprise, support, and accessibility paths. |

#### Node 47 Relax Redline

Node `47` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Can a realistic anonymous buyer understand Kolm's three product surfaces in five seconds and choose the right next action?
- Can a developer route the first call, inspect a receipt, and compile or verify the first `.kolm` artifact without founder help?
- Can an ML engineer understand distill readiness, K-Score scope, holdout/privacy blockers, and quality risk from account/docs/API/CLI?
- Can a platform operator deploy or dry-run an artifact to a target, understand readiness, observe smoke, and roll back?
- Can an enterprise buyer request or export trust/procurement evidence without seeing unsupported certifications or fake checkout paths?
- Can support diagnose failures with safe trace IDs, support bundles, no raw secrets, and a clear escalation path?
- Can critical journeys be completed by keyboard, mobile, zoom, reduced motion, and accessibility-assisted review?
- Do journey packets record failures and reruns strongly enough that implementation agents can fix the product without relying on chat history?
- Do production journey replays pass on the deployed domain and same commit as release evidence?

The relax condition for node `47` is not "screenshots passed" or "the route exists." It is: realistic users can complete the critical Kolm journeys across public site, docs, account, API, CLI, SDK, TUI, production, failure states, support, and enterprise handoff, and every observed friction is traced to a requirement, work order, fix, rerun, and product-metric decision.

#### Node 47 Journey Proof Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The active demand is still owned by node `47`: stop treating scans, screenshots, and route counts as sufficient proof, and maintain a product-use operating system that proves real people can reach first value across every Kolm surface.

Node `47` V2 defines how Kolm should be used, observed, measured, fixed, and replayed. A journey is not finished because an endpoint returns JSON or a screenshot renders. A journey is finished when the intended persona can complete the task, understand the product state, recover from predictable failures, trust the claims, and produce a proof packet that another agent can audit.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Artifact | Current observed state | V2 implication |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/product-journeys.json` | Current journey catalog has `12` journeys: `gateway-capture`, `privacy-lake`, `datasets-labeling`, `train-distill`, `models-backbones`, `multimodal-tokenization`, `compile-verify`, `runtime-inference`, `compute-cloud`, `devices-fleet`, `enterprise-governance`, and `agents-registry`. | The manual lab library must cover all 12 journeys, not only public homepage, pricing, and account overview. |
| `public/product-graph.json` | Product graph currently matches the same 12 journey IDs and reports `33` account links, `69` product API routes, `64` CLI commands, `32` TUI views, `8` customization dimensions, `582` routes, and `57` readiness requirements. | Each journey packet must reference product graph IDs so API, account, CLI, TUI, docs, and public pages stay tied to the same user task. |
| `scripts/e2e-walk.mjs` | One-shot walk covers registry, admin/account, signup, compile, polling, artifact download, recipe run, capture, team, tunnel, BYOC deploy script, improve loop, verify, resynthesize, and airgap status. | Promote this from a script to a journey family: add persona, prerequisites, safe key policy, expected values, task success, time budgets, artifacts, screenshots, support bundle, and rerun proof. |
| `scripts/e2e-flow.sh` and `scripts/e2e-hub.sh` | Curl paths cover signup, compile, upgrade/downgrade/capture/audit surfaces and hub publish/list/pull tenant isolation. | These are mechanical proofs. They should become evidence attachments to developer/operator journeys, not substitutes for user comprehension. |
| `scripts/audit-buyer-journey.sh` | Buyer path still exists as a 30-probe route/content audit. | It should be generated from current narrative, pricing, claim, and product graph truth, not hand-maintained stale copy expectations. |
| `scripts/qa-*.png` and `reports/ui-surface-audit` | Many screenshots and route audits exist; latest listed directories include focused polish, account readiness, and full frontend V597 chunks. | Screenshot proof must be linked to journey tasks and not counted as journey completion by itself. |
| `reports/ui-surface-audit/2026-05-23Tmanual-use-home-product-pricing-account/report.md` | Manual-use report covered 4 of 548 routes and failed with 36 failures and 44 warnings, including missing or invisible product media, negative H1 tracking, missing shared CSS/nav, and small touch targets. | V2 must preserve this as evidence of why route rendering is insufficient and why manual-use labs must be release-critical. |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` | UI audit infrastructure exists and can cover themes, viewports, screenshots, media, nav, target size, and route rendering. | Journey packets must cite the exact route/theme/viewport screenshots that correspond to the user task being proven. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` and `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | Local and production surface smoke lanes exist. | Manual lab status must distinguish local task success from deployed task success. Production replay is mandatory before production-scoped completion. |
| `public/account/overview.html` and generated account pages | Account pages expose product matrix, readiness, storage/cloud, billing, capture, artifacts, devices, and governance surfaces. | Post-auth journey labs must verify the account command center as an operator product, not just as a rendered dashboard. |
| `docs/product-readiness-closeout.md` and `public/product-readiness-closeout.json` | Open external/package/certification/benchmark gates remain visible. | Enterprise and trust journeys must prove that users see these limits clearly instead of inferring that everything is certified or published. |

##### Current Journey-Validation Baseline V2

| Baseline | Current source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Qualitative usability testing | Nielsen Norman Group describes usability testing as asking representative users to perform realistic tasks while the researcher observes behavior and feedback: `https://media.nngroup.com/media/articles/attachments/UsabilityTesting101_Letter_Size.pdf`. | Kolm journey labs must use realistic personas and task scripts, not feature tours or internal checklists. |
| Usability heuristics | NN/g heuristic guidance emphasizes system status, real-world language, user control, consistency, error prevention, recovery, minimalist design, and help: `https://media.nngroup.com/media/articles/attachments/Heuristic_Summary_compressed.pdf`. | Journey findings must classify whether friction comes from status, language, control, consistency, prevention, recovery, clutter, or help gaps. |
| UX metrics | Google's HEART paper defines large-scale user-centered metrics around happiness, engagement, adoption, retention, and task success: `https://research.google.com/pubs/pub36299.html?frame=0`. | Kolm journey proof must connect task success to product metrics, not only to screenshot pass/fail. |
| Accessibility conformance evaluation | W3C WCAG-EM provides a methodology for evaluating website conformance with WCAG: `https://www.w3.org/WAI/test-evaluate/conformance/wcag-em/`. | Critical journeys need keyboard, focus, zoom, reduced-motion, screen-reader spot checks, and documented scope. |
| WCAG 2.2 | W3C WCAG 2.2 is designed to be testable with automated testing and human evaluation: `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/`. | Automated accessibility checks cannot be the only accessibility journey evidence. |
| Core Web Vitals | Google states Core Web Vitals measure real-world loading performance, interactivity, and visual stability; good targets include LCP within 2.5s and INP under 200ms: `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/core-web-vitals`. | Public and account journeys need first-screen performance budgets tied to first value, not only route render completion. |
| DORA metrics | DORA software delivery metrics connect delivery performance, recovery, and failure outcomes to organizational performance: `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/`. | Journey failures caused by release changes must feed change-failure and recovery evidence, not disappear into UX notes. |
| Local UI/UX Pro Max guidance | Local `ui-ux-pro-max` rules emphasize accessibility, touch targets, performance, responsive behavior, visible states, forms, feedback, navigation, and consistent visual systems. | Manual labs must include touch target, loading, empty, error, hover/tap, focus, responsive, and visual consistency proof. |

##### Journey Proof Packet V2

Every completed manual or semi-automated lab must emit `kolm.journey-proof.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "JPROOF-2026-05-25-DEVELOPER-FIRST-ARTIFACT",
  "journey_id": "compile-verify",
  "persona": "developer",
  "environment": "local|preview|production",
  "commit": "optional commit sha",
  "base_url": "https://kolm.ai or local URL",
  "starting_state": {
    "auth": "anonymous|new_user|existing_user|enterprise_admin|support_admin",
    "plan": "free|pro|team|enterprise|test",
    "provider_mode": "mock|live|missing_credentials",
    "data_state": "empty|seeded|dirty|production-safe-test"
  },
  "tasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "T1",
      "instruction": "Find how to compile repeated examples into a signed artifact.",
      "success_criteria": "User reaches artifact command or demo with eval scope and verify path visible.",
      "max_time_seconds": 180,
      "result": "pass|fail|partial|blocked",
      "time_seconds": 0,
      "friction": []
    }
  ],
  "evidence": {
    "screenshots": [],
    "recording": null,
    "session_notes": [],
    "api_logs": [],
    "cli_logs": [],
    "artifact_paths": [],
    "support_bundle": null,
    "trace_ids": []
  },
  "metrics": {
    "task_success_rate": 0,
    "time_to_first_value_seconds": null,
    "errors_per_task": 0,
    "unassisted_completion": false,
    "confidence_rating_1_to_5": null,
    "sus_score": null,
    "heart": {
      "happiness": null,
      "engagement": null,
      "adoption": null,
      "retention": null,
      "task_success": null
    }
  },
  "verdict": "release_blocker|needs_fix|acceptable_with_caveat|passed",
  "workorders": []
}
```

Rules:

- A route screenshot may attach to a journey proof, but it cannot replace task success.
- A script pass may attach to a journey proof, but it cannot prove comprehension, trust, or recovery.
- A production journey must record deployed commit or release identifier, base URL, safe credentials, and rollback note.
- A failed journey is useful evidence only if it produces workorders and gets rerun after fixes.
- Any journey touching secrets, prompts, customer data, support tickets, or enterprise evidence must prove redaction and safe bundle behavior.

##### First-Value Metrics V2

| Journey | First-value event | Time budget | Required proof |
|---|---|---:|---|
| Anonymous comprehension | Visitor can state Kolm's three surfaces: model API traffic, compile/distill artifacts, and deploy/run anywhere. | 5 seconds first-screen, 60 seconds full first impression. | Session note, first-screen screenshot, copy comprehension answer, CTA selection. |
| Gateway capture | User routes an OpenAI-compatible request and sees receipt, provider, policy, privacy, cost, and trace ID. | 10 minutes from quickstart. | API log, account capture row, docs step, CLI or curl output. |
| Compile/verify | User creates or loads a `.kolm` artifact and runs a verification path. | 15 minutes with sample data; 45 minutes with custom data. | Artifact path, manifest/passport, verify output, K-Score or eval scope explanation. |
| Train/distill | User sees distill readiness and can identify exact blockers or start a scoped distill job. | 20 minutes to readiness verdict. | Account readiness, CLI/API readiness, holdout/privacy/label state, next action. |
| Runtime inference | User runs or dry-runs an artifact on a selected target and sees runtime limits. | 15 minutes for local or browser; 45 minutes for cloud/device dry-run. | Runtime command, target readiness, smoke output, fallback/rollback state. |
| Compute/cloud | User configures or understands missing storage/GPU/cloud/provider requirements. | 10 minutes to readiness map. | Object storage readiness, provider list, missing env groups, no secret values. |
| Devices/fleet | User registers a device or understands install prerequisites and health. | 20 minutes to device health or blocked reason. | Device row, tunnel/install output, health status, rollback/uninstall note. |
| Enterprise governance | User finds team, SSO/SCIM/audit/log/export/trust scope and understands certification limits. | 10 minutes to trust/procurement next action. | Account audit/trust view, export/request path, closeout gates visible. |
| Agents/registry | User inspects, verifies, installs, or revokes an artifact. | 15 minutes to verified registry action. | Registry page, CLI/API proof, signature/hash, license/runtime limits. |
| Support recovery | User creates safe support bundle with trace ID and no raw secrets. | 10 minutes after failure. | Support bundle, trace ID, redaction proof, escalation owner. |

##### Journey Evidence Grades V2

| Grade | Meaning | Completion use |
|---|---|---|
| `J0` | Journey described in prose only. | Planning only. |
| `J1` | Static route/script exists. | Cannot claim journey works. |
| `J2` | API or CLI path works locally for a narrow happy path. | Engineering evidence only. |
| `J3` | Browser route renders with screenshot proof. | UI rendering evidence only. |
| `J4` | Local task completed by implementation agent with logs/screenshots. | Local journey evidence if caveated. |
| `J5` | Independent reviewer completes task without hints locally. | Release candidate evidence. |
| `J6` | Accessibility-assisted and mobile/light/dark variants complete. | Public UX readiness evidence. |
| `J7` | Production replay completes with deployed commit, safe credentials, logs, screenshots, and rollback state. | Production-scoped evidence. |
| `J8` | External participant/customer/design partner completes task and substantiated feedback is recorded. | Market/customer validation evidence. |

The minimum for local release readiness is `J5` on critical journeys and `J6` for public/account critical paths. The minimum for deployed "done" is `J7`. Customer claims, social proof, or design-partner claims require `J8`.

##### Persona Lab Matrix V2

| Persona | Critical questions | Required surfaces |
|---|---|---|
| AI-company CTO | What is Kolm, why now, and which of the three product surfaces applies? | Homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs landing, compare pages. |
| Developer | How do I swap one model API call, capture traffic, inspect receipts, and get an artifact? | Quickstart, API docs, SDK docs, CLI, account capture, demo runner. |
| ML engineer | How do I distill/compile, evaluate K-Score, manage holdouts, and understand quality risk? | Distill docs, account builds/datasets/evals, API, CLI, benchmarks. |
| Platform engineer | How do I deploy to cloud, local, edge, browser, device, and observe runtime state? | Runtimes, account storage/devices/artifacts, CLI/TUI, cloud readiness. |
| Security/compliance buyer | What controls are implemented, what is certified, what is external-gated, and how do I export evidence? | Trust, security, BAA/DPA, enterprise inquiry, audit log, closeout ledger. |
| Finance/procurement buyer | What plan, ROI, invoice, PO, enterprise process, and contract path applies? | Pricing, ROI, billing account, enterprise inquiry, legal pages. |
| Support/admin | How do I diagnose failure without leaking secrets, recover, escalate, and prove status? | Account overview, support bundle, API envelope, docs, status, logs. |
| Accessibility-assisted reviewer | Can the same critical paths be completed by keyboard, zoom, screen-reader spot check, reduced motion, and mobile? | Public, account, docs, forms, demo runner, pricing, enterprise. |

##### Manual Lab Protocol V2

1. Pick a journey ID from `docs/product-journeys.json`, not an arbitrary page.
2. Pick a persona and starting state that matches real use.
3. Give task instructions in product-user language, not implementation language.
4. Record whether the user reached first value without hints.
5. Record every friction event using the stable taxonomy from V1.
6. Attach route screenshots for each task checkpoint in dark/light and desktop/mobile where the journey uses UI.
7. Attach CLI/API logs where the journey uses backend or operator tools.
8. Attach artifact, manifest, receipt, trace, support bundle, or export output where applicable.
9. Score task success, time to first value, confidence, errors per task, recovery success, and accessibility outcome.
10. Convert release-blocking friction to workorders in node `46` format.
11. Rerun only after fixes and attach the before/after evidence.
12. Promote to production-scoped only after the same task passes on deployed `kolm.ai`.

##### Dogfood Use Cases V2

| Dogfood case | Implementation target | Evidence required |
|---|---|---|
| Docs example checker | Kolm artifact checks all public quickstarts and SDK examples for stale commands, missing expected output, unsupported claims, and wrong plan names. | Report of changed examples, false positives, human approvals, and skipped cases. |
| Release claim reviewer | Kolm artifact reviews release notes, homepage claims, pricing claims, compare rows, and trust language against readiness closeout and claim gate. | Claim diff, evidence grade, blocked claims, reviewer signoff. |
| Support bundle summarizer | Kolm artifact summarizes errors from safe support bundle, trace IDs, route logs, account state, and environment readiness. | No raw secrets, redaction report, confidence, escalation owner, human approval. |
| Journey friction classifier | Kolm artifact maps manual lab notes to friction taxonomy, severity, product metric, and workorder candidate. | Classified findings, reviewer edits, workorder links. |
| Benchmark narrative checker | Kolm artifact reads benchmark report and blocks unsupported quality/savings/speed statements. | Benchmark source links, caveat map, public-copy diff. |
| Procurement packet drafter | Kolm artifact drafts enterprise packet from product graph, closeout ledger, trust docs, SSO/SCIM/audit state, and legal templates. | Draft packet, external-gate labels, legal review state. |
| Incident replay assistant | Kolm artifact reconstructs incident timeline from traces, support events, deploy info, and status notes. | Timeline, corrective actions, safe redaction, owner approval. |

Dogfood claims cannot be public proof until they use production-safe data, preserve tenant boundaries, redact sensitive content, include human approval, and emit evidence packets.

##### Release Journey Gate V2

The release gate must include:

- `anonymous-comprehension`: public first-screen and first-minute understanding across homepage, product, pricing, enterprise, docs.
- `developer-first-call`: API/docs/SDK/CLI route from key setup to receipt.
- `developer-first-artifact`: sample and custom path to `.kolm` artifact and verify output.
- `ml-distill-readiness`: datasets, holdouts, labels, privacy, K-Score, blockers, next action.
- `platform-runtime`: local/browser/cloud/device readiness, dry-run/smoke, rollback.
- `operator-account`: account overview, product matrix, open gates, billing, storage/cloud, captures, artifacts, incidents, next actions.
- `enterprise-procurement`: trust packet, inquiry, certification scope, DPA/BAA/security, sales owner.
- `support-recovery`: failure reproduction, trace ID, safe support bundle, escalation and retry.
- `accessibility-assisted`: keyboard, focus, zoom, reduced motion, screen-reader spot check, touch targets, mobile.
- `production-replay`: critical paths on deployed domain with current commit and safe credentials.

Each gate must name allowed evidence grade, minimum task success, time-to-first-value budget, required screenshots/logs, and release-blocking failures.

##### Node `47` V2 Relax Redline

Node `47` V2 is relaxed only when current evidence proves the following:

- All 12 product journeys have task scripts, personas, first-value events, time budgets, evidence requirements, friction taxonomy, and proof-grade thresholds.
- Critical public, developer, ML, platform, enterprise, support, account, and accessibility-assisted journeys reach at least `J5` locally and `J6` where UI accessibility is in scope.
- Production-scoped completion claims have `J7` deployed replay proof with current release identifier, safe credentials, screenshots/logs, telemetry or trace IDs, and rollback state.
- Customer/design-partner/social-proof journey claims have `J8` evidence or remain clearly labeled as internal, demo, synthetic, or beta.
- Screenshots, route audits, smoke scripts, and tests are attached as evidence but do not substitute for realistic task completion.
- Every serious friction event becomes a node `46` workorder, and every fixed journey has rerun evidence.
- Kolm dogfoods docs QA, release claim review, support recovery, benchmark narrative review, procurement packet drafting, and incident replay with redaction and human approval.
- The final completion packet can show, journey by journey, that users can understand, operate, trust, recover, and get value from Kolm without founder handholding.

The strict rule is: if a person cannot complete the journey, the product surface is incomplete even when the code, route, screenshot, and test all exist.

#### Node 47 Hands-On Journey Lab And Product-Use Redline Addendum V3

Node `52` stays unused. The active demand is still node `47`: prove the product by using it. V3 adds a hands-on journey lab that treats every Kolm surface as a user task, not as a route inventory. It is designed to catch exactly the class of failures the user called out: unclear hero meaning, too much jargon, broken media, stale pricing, weak account states, hidden readiness gaps, confusing docs, and product surfaces that exist in code but are not usable end to end.

The V3 rule is simple: if the intended persona cannot complete the task in a realistic environment with safe credentials, current docs, current UI, current API/CLI behavior, and a proof packet, the surface is not done. A scanner can support this proof. It cannot replace it.

##### Current Journey Evidence Rechecked For V3

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Product journeys | `docs/product-journeys.json` currently defines `12` journeys: gateway capture, privacy lake, datasets/labeling, train/distill, models/backbones, multimodal tokenization, compile/verify, runtime inference, compute/cloud, devices/fleet, enterprise governance, and agents/registry. | V3 requires a lab packet for every journey, not only homepage, pricing, and account. |
| Product graph | `public/product-graph.json` currently reports `12` journeys, `33` account links, `69` product API routes, `64` CLI commands, `32` TUI views, `8` customization dimensions, `582` routes, and `57` readiness requirements. | Journey tasks must reference product graph IDs so public, account, API, CLI, TUI, docs, and readiness surfaces prove the same user outcome. |
| Journey scripts | Current repo includes `e2e-walk.mjs`, `e2e-flow.sh`, `e2e-hub.sh`, `audit-buyer-journey.sh`, local/prod surface smokes, UI audits, screenshot scripts, and dogfood proxy hooks. | These scripts are raw materials for journey evidence. V3 requires them to emit persona, task, first-value, friction, artifact, trace, and rerun records. |
| Screenshot assets | `reports/ui-surface-audit` includes manual-use, focused-polish, and many frontend route screenshot report directories; `scripts/qa-*.png` assets also exist. | Visual proof must be tied to task checkpoints. A screenshot of a page is weaker than proof that the user completed a task on that page. |
| Manual-use evidence | Existing manual-use report on 2026-05-23 covered homepage/product/pricing/account and found failures/warnings. | V3 keeps failed manual evidence as valuable: it must create workorders and rerun packets after fixes. |
| Code/test inventory | Current scan found `559` test files, `281` script files, and `729` public HTML files. | The codebase is too large for ad hoc judgment. Journey labs must sample and exercise high-value paths while node `46` maps coverage. |
| Open external gates | Readiness closeout still has `8` external/package/certification/benchmark gates. | Enterprise, procurement, docs, and account journeys must make these limits visible instead of letting users infer unsupported proof. |

##### External Baseline Refreshed For V3

| Baseline | Current source | V3 import into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| NN/g usability testing | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/usability-testing-101/` | A lab needs realistic participants or reviewers, realistic tasks, observed behavior, feedback, task success, and time-on-task evidence. |
| NN/g task scenarios | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/task-scenarios-usability-testing/` | Tasks should be framed as user goals and business context, not "click this feature." Kolm labs must ask users to accomplish outcomes. |
| NN/g usability heuristics | `https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/` | Findings should classify visibility of status, real-world language, user control, consistency, error prevention, recovery, recognition, flexibility, minimalism, and help. |
| Google HEART | `https://research.google/pubs/measuring-the-user-experience-on-a-large-scale-user-centered-metrics-for-web-applications/` | Journey packets should connect task success to happiness, engagement, adoption, retention, and task success signals. |
| W3C WCAG-EM | `https://www.w3.org/WAI/test-evaluate/conformance/wcag-em/` | Accessibility journey evidence needs defined scope, representative samples, evaluation records, and where possible real users with disabilities or assistive-tech expertise. |
| ISO 9241-210:2019 | `https://www.iso.org/standard/77520.html` | Human-centred design needs lifecycle activities, requirements, recommendations, and conformance support for interactive systems. |
| Core Web Vitals | `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | First-value journeys must protect loading, interactivity, and visual stability; a page that shifts or lags during the task is not complete. |
| DORA metrics | `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | Journey regressions after release should feed change failure and recovery metrics, not remain isolated UX notes. |

##### V3 Journey Lab Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.journey-lab.v3",
  "lab_id": "JLAB-2026-05-node-47-v3",
  "journey_id": "compile-verify",
  "product_graph_refs": {
    "surface": "compile-verify",
    "account_links": [],
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_commands": [],
    "tui_views": [],
    "docs_pages": []
  },
  "persona": {
    "id": "developer",
    "experience": "uses OpenAI-compatible APIs and owns production app integration",
    "constraints": ["time-limited", "security-aware", "no founder help"]
  },
  "environment": {
    "kind": "local|preview|production",
    "base_url": "",
    "commit_or_release": "",
    "theme": "light|dark",
    "viewport": "mobile|desktop",
    "auth_state": "anonymous|new_user|existing_user|admin",
    "credential_policy": "safe_test_key|mock_provider|live_provider_no_secrets_logged"
  },
  "tasks": [
    {
      "id": "T1",
      "user_goal": "Turn repeated model behavior into a verifiable artifact.",
      "instruction": "Use Kolm to find the artifact path and verify a sample artifact.",
      "success": "The user reaches a .kolm artifact, sees eval scope, and runs or identifies a verify command.",
      "max_time_seconds": 900,
      "hint_policy": "no_hint_until_blocked",
      "result": "pass|partial|fail|blocked"
    }
  ],
  "evidence": {
    "screenshots": [],
    "recording_or_notes": "",
    "api_logs": [],
    "cli_logs": [],
    "tui_logs": [],
    "artifacts": [],
    "support_bundle": "",
    "trace_ids": [],
    "redactions": []
  },
  "metrics": {
    "task_success": 0,
    "time_to_first_value_seconds": null,
    "errors": 0,
    "friction_events": [],
    "confidence_1_to_5": null,
    "recovery_success": null,
    "heart": {
      "happiness": null,
      "engagement": null,
      "adoption": null,
      "retention": null,
      "task_success": null
    }
  },
  "verdict": "pass|pass_with_caveat|needs_fix|release_blocker|external_blocked",
  "workorders": []
}
```

##### V3 Full Product Journey Lab Matrix

| Journey | Primary persona | Must actually do | Required evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gateway-capture` | Developer | Replace or simulate model API traffic, send a request, inspect receipt, privacy policy, provider/model, cost, and trace ID. | Browser/docs/API/CLI output, account capture row, receipt screenshot, missing-credential recovery. |
| `privacy-lake` | Regulated operator | Inspect captured data, run privacy/redaction report, export safe evidence, configure retention or zero-store mode. | Account lake screenshots, CLI/API privacy report, export file, redaction proof, no raw secret leakage. |
| `datasets-labeling` | Model builder | Turn captured opportunities into reviewed labels, dataset, holdout split, and bakeoff/eval input. | Dataset page/account proof, CLI/API dataset output, holdout state, label review notes, failure recovery. |
| `train-distill` | ML engineer | Choose teacher/student/compute, see readiness, launch or dry-run distill, inspect quality gate and blockers. | Readiness map, build plan, K-Score/eval scope, privacy/holdout/label state, job/log screenshot. |
| `models-backbones` | ML engineer | Compare model candidates by task, license, modality, memory, target device, and deployment limits. | Model catalog screenshot, CLI/API recommendation, license/modality proof, device fit explanation. |
| `multimodal-tokenization` | Multimodal builder | Tokenize or simulate image/audio/video/PDF/code input, inspect sidecar, capture event, and review generated rows. | Tokenizer output, sidecar artifact, account row, local/privacy mode proof, error/fallback state. |
| `compile-verify` | Operator/developer | Compile or load artifact, inspect passport, verify signature/hash, diff or export runtime-specific asset. | Artifact file/path, verify output, passport screenshot, eval scope, export preview. |
| `runtime-inference` | App developer | Run or serve artifact locally/browser/edge/server path, stream result, inspect runtime receipt and fallback. | Runtime command/log, account/API receipt, fallback chain state, latency/cost/error proof. |
| `compute-cloud` | Platform engineer | Run storage/cloud/GPU/provider readiness, identify missing env groups, run local smoke or cloud dry-run. | Object storage readiness, cloud broker output, no secret values, missing provider explanation, smoke output. |
| `devices-fleet` | Edge operator | Detect or register device, get target recommendation, run install/tunnel/airgap health or blocked reason. | Device row, CLI output, health state, uninstall/rollback note, compatibility limits. |
| `enterprise-governance` | Enterprise admin | Scope key, inspect audit/billing/privacy/compliance/trust state, export/request procurement evidence. | Account admin screenshots, CLI/API audit/billing output, closeout gates visible, inquiry/export path. |
| `agents-registry` | Agent-platform builder | Compile artifact as tool, serve/install it in an agent path, inspect registry/log/verification state. | MCP/tool output, registry screenshot, hash/signature, install log, revoke/failure path. |

##### V3 Hands-On Lab Modes

| Lab mode | Purpose | Minimum proof |
|---|---|---|
| `founder-critical` | Fast internal check before deeper work; catches obvious comprehension and value failures. | One reviewer, no hints, notes, screenshots, and workorders for blockers. |
| `implementation-agent` | Agent uses the product after building it; catches mismatches between code and real tasks. | Logs, screenshots, task results, trace IDs, artifacts, and rerun state. |
| `independent-reviewer` | Someone not involved in the change attempts the journey. | No-hint completion, confidence score, friction notes, time-to-first-value. |
| `accessibility-assisted` | Keyboard, zoom, reduced motion, mobile, and screen-reader spot checks are used during real tasks. | Focus/target/label/status proof, WCAG-EM scope, screenshots, barriers. |
| `production-replay` | Same task runs on deployed `kolm.ai` after release. | Deployed commit/release ID, safe key policy, screenshots/logs, rollback state, telemetry or trace IDs. |
| `external-design-partner` | Customer/design partner attempts a task and feedback can support roadmap or social proof. | Consent/scope, anonymization, task result, quote permission if used, no unsupported claim. |

##### V3 Friction-To-Workorder Policy

| Friction severity | Meaning | Required action |
|---|---|---|
| `P0-release-blocker` | User cannot reach first value, data/security risk appears, claim is unsupported, or production replay fails. | Create node `46` workorder immediately; release finality blocked until rerun passes or claim is removed. |
| `P1-material-friction` | User completes task but with major confusion, unnecessary detour, hidden state, slow recovery, or trust gap. | Workorder required before broad launch; caveat allowed only with owner and expiry. |
| `P2-polish-friction` | Task succeeds but visual/copy/state inconsistency reduces confidence. | Add backlog workorder and attach to node `45` or node `44` if visual/copy owned. |
| `P3-observation` | Minor issue, preference, or non-blocking improvement. | Record in journey notes; promote only if repeated. |

Every serious friction event must include route/path, screenshot or log, user goal, expected behavior, observed behavior, severity, owning node, owning lane, product metric affected, and rerun requirement.

##### V3 Release Journey Scorecard

| Score | Requirement |
|---|---|
| `0` | Journey not attempted on current tree. |
| `1` | Journey attempted but blocked before first value. |
| `2` | First value reached only with hints or internal knowledge. |
| `3` | First value reached unassisted locally, but recovery/accessibility/production evidence is missing. |
| `4` | First value and recovery pass locally with screenshots/logs and no P0/P1 friction. |
| `5` | Production replay passes with deployed release ID, safe credentials, screenshots/logs, trace IDs, rollback state, and no stale external claims. |

Launch-critical journeys need `4` before release candidate and `5` before production finality. External customer proof needs design-partner or customer evidence in addition to score `5`.

##### Node `47` V3 Relax Redline

Node `47` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every journey in `docs/product-journeys.json` has a hands-on lab packet template, task wording, first-value metric, evidence grade, friction taxonomy, and required channels.
- The 12 journeys are attempted on the current tree with safe credentials or explicit missing-prerequisite records.
- Public, docs, account, API, CLI, TUI, and production surfaces are exercised as user tasks, not as independent scans.
- Critical tasks reach score `4` locally and score `5` on production before any production-complete claim.
- Accessibility-assisted journey evidence covers keyboard, focus, zoom, mobile, reduced motion, visible status, target size, and screen-reader spot checks for critical flows.
- P0/P1 friction creates node `46` workorders and cannot be ignored by screenshots or broad tests.
- Dogfood artifacts review docs, claims, support bundles, release notes, benchmark narratives, and procurement packets with redaction and human approval.
- External participant/customer/design-partner evidence is separated from internal evidence and cannot be converted into social proof without permission and substantiation.

If any of those fail, Kolm may have passing tests and polished pages, but it has not been proved usable end to end.

#### Node 47 V4 Hands-On Acceptance Lab, No-Hint Task Proof, And Rerun Governance

Node `52` remains unused. V4 tightens node `47` around the user's explicit standard: do not merely scan or test; actually use the product. A route audit, lint pass, screenshot pass, or API smoke can show that a surface exists. It cannot prove that a buyer understands Kolm, a developer reaches first value, an ML engineer trusts a distill path, an operator can recover from failure, or an enterprise admin can export the right evidence without founder handholding.

The V4 acceptance rule is strict:

- every launch-critical surface must be exercised as a task with a persona, starting state, no-hint script, time budget, credential policy, success criteria, and recovery path;
- every task must produce evidence: screenshots, logs, trace IDs, artifacts, receipts, session notes, and friction events where applicable;
- every failed or partial task must create a workorder and be rerun after the fix;
- every production-complete claim must have production replay evidence on the deployed release, not just local proof.

##### Node 47 V4 Current Evidence Delta

Current repository evidence shows strong automation but incomplete hands-on acceptance governance:

| Evidence path or family | Current evidence | V4 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| `scripts/e2e-walk.mjs` | 11,876 bytes. | A walking script exists and should become the canonical journey runner, but it needs no-hint task wording, persona state, friction taxonomy, artifacts, rerun state, and production replay fields. |
| `scripts/e2e-flow.sh` and `scripts/e2e-hub.sh` | 5,559 and 6,494 bytes. | E2E shell flows exist; V4 must classify which product journey each flow proves and whether it is API-only, CLI-only, browser-assisted, or production-safe. |
| `scripts/audit-buyer-journey.sh` | 3,900 bytes. | Buyer journey audit exists; V4 must tie it to the current three-surface product narrative and require observed comprehension, not only string checks. |
| `scripts/dogfood-proxy.ps1` | 4,970 bytes. | Dogfood proxy exists; dogfood runs need redaction, tenant safety, real task objective, and proof that Kolm uses Kolm without leaking private data. |
| `scripts/local-surface-smoke.cjs` and `scripts/prod-surface-smoke.cjs` | 7,508 and 13,860 bytes. | Smoke scripts are evidence inputs, not journey proof by themselves. They must attach to task packets with user goal and recovery result. |
| `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs` and screenshot scripts | 33,546 bytes plus many screenshot helpers and PNGs. | Visual proof exists. V4 requires screenshots to be linked to task checkpoints, not only routes. |
| `tests/e2e.test.js` | 9,150 bytes. | Automated E2E exists; V4 needs test-to-journey mapping and human/manual task overlays for comprehension, trust, and recovery. |
| Journey-related tests | Includes quickstart, e2e compile/distill, value-loop, golden E2E, DoD 12-step, TUI, prod-surface, redactor-template, demo loop, and SDK smoke tests. | Test coverage is broad but scattered. V4 needs a journey acceptance manifest that states which tests support which persona task. |
| Current dirty frontend/deploy files | `public/nav.js`, CSS/theme files, `public/studio.html`, `src/intent.js`, `vercel.json` are modified by active lanes. | Any old journey proof is stale for nav, layout, account, and production deploy claims until rerun on the settled tree. |
| Untracked journey evidence candidates | `.w850-shots/`, site-failure text files, temp observation files. | These must be either attached to journey packets, archived, ignored as local scratch, or removed before build finality. |

V4 therefore classifies current evidence as useful but not final. The missing product artifact is the journey acceptance ledger that says exactly which real tasks passed, failed, were rerun, and are safe to cite.

##### External Acceptance Baselines For V4

| Baseline | V4 import into Kolm |
|---|---|
| ISO 9241-11 usability definition, `https://www.iso.org/standard/63500.html` | Usability must be evaluated in a specified context of use with goals, users, effectiveness, efficiency, and satisfaction. Kolm journey packets must name the user, goal, context, success, time, and satisfaction/confidence. |
| W3C involving users in accessibility evaluation, `https://www.w3.org/WAI/test-evaluate/involving-users/` | Accessibility proof should combine conformance checks with users and assistive-technology contexts. Kolm cannot rely only on automated a11y checks for critical journeys. |
| W3C WCAG-EM, `https://w3c.github.io/wai-wcag-em/` | Accessibility evaluation must document scope and sampling. Kolm journey labs need a declared route/task sample, environment, assistive mode, and limitations. |
| Google HEART paper, `https://research.google.com/pubs/pub36299.html?frame=0` | User experience metrics should map product goals to signals. Kolm must tie journey results to activation, adoption, task success, retention, trust, and revenue metrics. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | Accessibility, touch targets, loading feedback, predictable navigation, responsive behavior, state clarity, and performance are critical. Kolm journey tasks must record these as observed task friction, not abstract design preferences. |

##### Hands-On Acceptance Packet V4

Every journey acceptance run must emit or be representable as `kolm.hands_on_acceptance.v4`.

##### No-Hint Task Scripts V4

The release-critical lab set must include these no-hint tasks. The wording must be outcome-based, not feature-based.

| Task ID | Persona | No-hint task | First-value proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `USE-V4-T01` | Anonymous CTO/buyer | In under one minute, decide what Kolm is, which of the three surfaces applies to your team, and what proof to inspect next. | Verbal/written answer, first-screen screenshot, CTA chosen, no jargon confusion. |
| `USE-V4-T02` | Developer | Replace or simulate an OpenAI-compatible call and find the receipt that shows provider, cost, privacy, policy, and trace ID. | API/CLI output, account capture row, receipt screenshot, missing-key recovery. |
| `USE-V4-T03` | Developer | Turn a sample or repeated behavior into a `.kolm` artifact and verify it. | Artifact path, passport, verify output, eval/K-Score scope, docs/CLI handoff. |
| `USE-V4-T04` | ML engineer | Determine whether a namespace is ready to distill and identify the exact blocker if it is not. | Readiness output, holdout/labels/privacy/compute state, next action. |
| `USE-V4-T05` | Platform engineer | Choose where an artifact can run and prove local/browser/cloud/device readiness or blocked state. | Runtime target, smoke/dry-run, object storage/cloud readiness, rollback/fallback note. |
| `USE-V4-T06` | Enterprise admin | Find what is implemented versus externally gated for SSO/SCIM/audit/compliance and request/export procurement evidence. | Trust/export path, closeout gates visible, contact sales or architecture review state. |
| `USE-V4-T07` | Account operator | From the post-auth overview, understand product matrix status, storage readiness, billing, captures, artifacts, incidents, and next actions. | Account screenshots, API/CLI parity, open gates, support path. |
| `USE-V4-T08` | Support operator | Reproduce a safe failure, create a support bundle, prove no secrets leaked, and identify escalation owner. | Support bundle, redaction proof, trace ID, escalation path, status/customer notice decision. |
| `USE-V4-T09` | Accessibility reviewer | Complete the developer first-artifact and account overview tasks with keyboard, zoom, mobile, and reduced-motion constraints. | Focus order, visible labels/status, target sizes, no hover-only blocker, screenshots. |
| `USE-V4-T10` | Release owner | Use Kolm itself to review docs claims, release claims, and build-redline evidence before deploy. | Dogfood artifact output, blocked claims, workorders, human approval, rerun proof. |

##### Journey Acceptance Score V4

| Score | Meaning | Allowed release use |
|---|---|---|
| `A0` | Not attempted on current tree. | No claim. |
| `A1` | Attempted but blocked before first value. | Release blocker for launch-critical journeys. |
| `A2` | First value reached only with hints, internal knowledge, or direct file/path knowledge. | Internal evidence only; public UX claim blocked. |
| `A3` | First value reached unassisted locally, but recovery, accessibility, or production proof is missing. | Local candidate only. |
| `A4` | First value and recovery pass locally with screenshots/logs and no P0/P1 friction. | Release candidate evidence. |
| `A5` | Same journey passes in accessibility-assisted mode on local or preview. | Public UX readiness evidence. |
| `A6` | Same journey passes on production with release ID, safe credentials, screenshots/logs, trace IDs, rollback state, and no stale claims. | Production-complete evidence. |
| `A7` | External design partner/customer completes the task and permission/limitations are recorded. | Customer-proof evidence if permission allows. |

V4 raises the minimum: launch-critical journeys need `A4` before release candidate, `A5` for public/account UX finality, and `A6` before production-close. Social proof requires `A7`.

##### Friction Taxonomy V4

Every friction event must use this taxonomy so fixes are comparable across journeys:

| Class | Examples | Primary owner |
|---|---|---|
| `meaning` | User cannot explain what Kolm does, which surface applies, or why now. | Node `44`, cross-link `30` and `51`. |
| `navigation` | User cannot find next step, active state is misleading, menu/popout is confusing, back path breaks. | Node `45`, cross-link `30`. |
| `state` | Loading, empty, error, partial, blocked, auth-expired, or permission state is unclear. | Node `12`, `25`, `45`. |
| `trust` | Certification/package/benchmark/external gate is hidden or claim feels unsupported. | Node `1`, `23`, `42`, `48`. |
| `credential` | API key, setup token, provider env, storage, SSO, or safe test tenant blocks progress without recovery. | Node `15`, `24`, `29`. |
| `proof` | Artifact, receipt, trace, eval, K-Score, signature, or runtime target is missing or hard to interpret. | Node `3`, `7`, `10`, `42`. |
| `visual` | Typography, spacing, color, media, contrast, target size, or responsive layout reduces confidence. | Node `45`, cross-link `30` and `51`. |
| `accessibility` | Keyboard, focus, labels, zoom, reduced motion, screen reader, or touch target blocks task. | Node `45`, cross-link `47`. |
| `performance` | Slow first value, video/demo jank, page shift, heavy media, or delayed response blocks flow. | Node `19`, `39`, `45`, `51`. |
| `recovery` | User cannot undo, retry, fallback, contact support, export bundle, or roll back. | Node `25`, `29`, `34`, `36`. |

P0/P1 friction must create a workorder with owner node, path, screenshot/log, expected behavior, metric impact, and rerun requirement.

##### V4 Evidence Freshness Rules

| Evidence type | Freshness rule |
|---|---|
| Local hands-on task | Must be rerun after any touched route/page/API/CLI/docs/account/provider path in the task dependency set. |
| Production replay | Must be rerun after every production deploy, relevant env change, pricing/claim change, nav/layout change, or generated-artifact change. |
| Screenshot checkpoint | Must match current route, theme, viewport, auth state, and release ID or be marked historical. |
| Dogfood artifact | Must state input data class, redaction status, human approval, model/artifact version, and limitations. |
| Accessibility-assisted evidence | Must state scope, browser, viewport, assistive mode, keyboard path, zoom level where relevant, and known limitations. |
| External/design-partner evidence | Must state consent, anonymization, permission to quote, limitations, date, and affected product surface. |

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `W-USE-V4-001` | `47.1` | Generate `docs/hands-on-acceptance-schema.v4.json`. | Schema validates no-hint tasks, starting state, evidence, quality checks, decision, rerun, and claim scope. |
| `W-USE-V4-002` | `47.1` | Generate `docs/hands-on-task-library.v4.json`. | Contains `USE-V4-T01` through `USE-V4-T10`, mapped to product journeys, personas, surfaces, time budgets, and required proof. |
