| `W-USE-V4-020` | `47.6` | Add journey acceptance certificate. | Final completion packet includes all launch-critical `A4/A5/A6` evidence, unresolved external blocks, workorders, and claim restrictions. |

##### Node `47` V4 Relax Redline

Node `47` V4 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- every launch-critical journey has no-hint task scripts and a current `kolm.hands_on_acceptance.v4` packet;
- each packet names persona, starting state, auth/plan/provider/data state, time budget, success criteria, and allowed docs;
- the user reaches first value unassisted locally for critical tasks or the task is marked failed/blocked with a workorder;
- accessibility-assisted and mobile/light/dark variants pass where public/account UI is involved;
- production replay exists for production-complete claims and includes release ID, safe credentials, screenshots/logs, trace IDs, and rollback state;
- route screenshots, smoke tests, and automated E2E tests are attached as supporting evidence, not substituted for observed task completion;
- every P0/P1 friction event has a node owner, path, metric impact, fix expectation, and rerun evidence;
- dogfood artifacts are used for docs QA, claim review, support recovery, and release proof with redaction plus human approval;
- journey evidence freshness is invalidated automatically when dependent files, generated artifacts, env state, or deployed release changes.

If any of those fail, the product may be technically broad, but it has not been proved usable, trustworthy, or complete.

### Competitive Intelligence, Market Proof, Social Proof, Case Studies, And Sales Enablement Ledger V1

Node `48` makes the market-facing proof surfaces rigorous. Kolm already has compare pages, ROI pages, benchmark pages, case-study pages, competitor research files, and enterprise sales copy. Those surfaces can either become a serious sales advantage or a liability. A state-of-the-art AI infrastructure site must show why Kolm wins, when competitors are better, what evidence supports each row, what is dated, what is anonymized, what is fictionalized or illustrative, and what a buyer should do next.

This node is not generic marketing. It is a proof governance layer for competitive intelligence and sales enablement. It exists because compare pages can go stale faster than product pages, case studies can overclaim faster than benchmark pages, and social proof can become legally risky if permission and substantiation are missing.

#### Current Market-Proof Evidence

| Evidence | Current observed state | Node 48 redline |
|---|---|---|
| Compare pages | `public/compare` contains 13 HTML pages, including OpenAI, OpenAI fine-tune, OpenPipe, OpenPipe 2026, Predibase, Predibase 2026, Together, Together 2026, Bedrock distill, Proxis, RAG, Ollama, and legacy index pages. | Every comparison row needs source date, competitor source URL, Kolm proof URL, win/tie/loss label, scope, and freshness state. |
| Case studies | `public/case-studies` contains 4 HTML pages: finance SR 11-7, healthcare PHI redactor, legal contract extraction, and index. | Every case study must say whether it is customer-approved, anonymized real, composite, synthetic, demo, or hypothetical, and must link substantiation evidence for numbers. |
| Competitor research | `docs/research` contains competitor landscape, competitor evidence matrices, trace-import wedge audits, and pricing competitor matrices. | Research files must feed public compare pages through a current competitor-source registry; stale matrices cannot silently support public claims. |
| High-risk comparison claims | Current compare pages include claims such as `$0 per call`, `11.6x cheaper`, `0.6 ms`, `near 0`, `42 frontier methods`, compliance packs, FedRAMP/HIPAA/SOC 2 comparisons, and owned-artifact statements. | Numerical, compliance, ownership, safety, and superiority claims require dated evidence, environment, benchmark, legal scope, and public-copy expiry. |
| Competitor freshness risk | Current browsing found that `predibase.com/fine-tuning` redirects to a Rubrik Agent Cloud page, while OpenPipe public sources now describe joining/acquisition by CoreWeave. | Compare pages must refresh competitor status from official sources before release and show last reviewed date. |
| Proof link pattern | Many compare matrices have a `Proof` column with internal links. | Proof links must resolve, match the exact row claim, and expose the evidence class instead of pointing to generic pages. |
| Buyer journey script | `scripts/audit-buyer-journey.sh` probes compare, pricing, docs, proof, and trust paths, but includes stale copy expectations. | Buyer and sales proof scripts must be generated from current narrative, pricing, competitor, and claim-substantiation sources. |

#### External Market And Claim Baseline

| Baseline | Source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| FTC Consumer Reviews and Testimonials Rule | `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/08/federal-trade-commission-announces-final-rule-banning-fake-reviews-testimonials` and `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/consumer-reviews-testimonials-rule-questions-answers` | Case studies, testimonials, quotes, logos, review snippets, design-partner labels, and social proof must not be fake, misleading, undisclosed, or unpermissioned. |
| FTC Endorsement Guides | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/ftcs-endorsement-guides` | Material connections, incentives, employee/insider endorsements, customer permissions, and typicality of results need clear disclosure and records. |
| FTC AI claim enforcement posture | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/blog/2023/02/keep-your-ai-claims-check` and `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes` | AI superiority, accuracy, automation, savings, safety, and "state of the art" claims need substantiation and cannot outrun evidence. |
| G2 buyer behavior research | `https://research.g2.com/2024-buyer-behavior-report` and `https://learn.g2.com/2025-g2-buyer-behavior-report` | B2B software buyers expect ROI, trust, peer proof, review signals, and clear risk evidence. Kolm should produce buyer-proof packets, not only marketing pages. |
| OpenAI model optimization docs | `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/model-optimization` | OpenAI positions model optimization across fine-tuning and related optimization methods; Kolm comparisons should track current official OpenAI docs, not old API assumptions. |
| Amazon Bedrock model distillation docs | `https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-distillation.html` | Bedrock comparisons should use current AWS distillation/custom-model docs and clearly distinguish AWS procurement strengths from Kolm portable-artifact strengths. |
| Together AI fine-tuning docs | `https://docs.together.ai/docs/fine-tuning-overview` | Together comparisons should reflect current hosted fine-tuning and inference workflows from official docs. |
| OpenPipe docs and CoreWeave source | `https://docs.openpipe.ai/features/fine-tuning/quick-start` and `https://www.coreweave.com/news/coreweave-to-acquire-openpipe-leader-in-reinforcement-learning` | OpenPipe comparison pages need current ownership/platform context and should not use stale "2026" narratives without official-source refresh. |

#### Competitor Source Registry

Every competitor and adjacent category needs a machine-readable source record:

```json
{
  "competitor_id": "openpipe",
  "display_name": "OpenPipe",
  "category": "fine-tuning-and-eval-platform",
  "official_sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://docs.openpipe.ai/features/fine-tuning/quick-start",
      "type": "docs",
      "last_checked": "2026-05-25",
      "status": "reachable"
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.coreweave.com/news/coreweave-to-acquire-openpipe-leader-in-reinforcement-learning",
      "type": "ownership-news",
      "last_checked": "2026-05-25",
      "status": "reachable"
    }
  ],
  "comparison_pages": ["/compare/kolm-vs-openpipe", "/compare/kolm-vs-openpipe-2026"],
  "axes": ["data_capture", "fine_tuning", "artifact_portability", "serving", "pricing", "procurement", "governance"],
  "refresh_cadence_days": 30,
  "stale_after_days": 45,
  "claim_scope": "compare-with-dated-sources",
  "known_kolm_losses": ["hosted fine-tuning smoothness", "managed GPU operations"],
  "known_kolm_wins": ["portable signed artifact", "offline verification", "artifact receipt chain"],
  "evidence_owner": "marketing-proof"
}
```

This registry must include direct competitors and substitute categories:

- OpenAI API and OpenAI model optimization.
- Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, and model gateway options.
- OpenPipe/CoreWeave, Predibase/Rubrik, Together, Bedrock distillation, SageMaker, Vertex, Azure AI Foundry.
- LangSmith, Langfuse, Helicone, Humanloop, Braintrust, Vellum, OpenLLMetry-style observability/eval tools.
- Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, ONNX Runtime, Core ML, LiteRT, MLX, WebGPU/WebNN, ExecuTorch.
- Palantir AIP, Databricks Mosaic AI, Snowflake Cortex, Dataiku LLM Mesh, DataRobot, TrueFoundry, NVIDIA AI Enterprise/NIM/NeMo.
- RAG stacks, vector databases, agent frameworks, and marketplace/registry alternatives.

#### Comparison Row Contract

Every row in a compare page should be generated or validated from a row contract:

```json
{
  "row_id": "CMP-OPENAI-001",
  "axis": "Artifact portability",
  "kolm_claim": "Kolm produces a signed .kolm artifact with manifest, receipts, and verification command.",
  "competitor_claim": "OpenAI optimized models are used through OpenAI-hosted APIs.",
  "result": "kolm_win|competitor_win|tie|depends|not_comparable",
  "buyer_relevance": "Vendor exit, offline deployment, and audit replay.",
  "kolm_evidence": ["docs/kolm-format-v1.md", "public/spec.html", "kolm verify sample.kolm"],
  "competitor_evidence": ["https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/model-optimization"],
  "last_verified": "2026-05-25",
  "expires": "2026-06-25",
  "limitations": ["Does not imply Kolm is a frontier general-purpose model."],
  "legal_review": "required_for_public_numeric_or_compliance_claims"
}
```

Rules:

- Mark competitor wins clearly. Hiding competitor strengths makes the page less credible.
- Use `depends` when the answer changes by buyer context, deployment mode, volume, regulatory setting, or runtime target.
- Never use generic proof links for precise claims. A row claiming `0.6 ms` needs benchmark environment, command, hardware, dataset, and date.
- Every pricing row needs plan, meter, date, currency, provider price source, assumptions, and sensitivity range.
- Every compliance row needs exact status: implemented control, BAA template, certification in progress, third-party certification, external partner, or not offered.
- Every "owned", "portable", "run anywhere", "flat compile", "free local run", "near zero", "best", "state of the art", or "leader" claim must link to node `46` requirement/evidence records.

#### Case Study And Social Proof Contract

Every customer proof asset must declare its evidence class:

| Evidence class | Meaning | Public wording allowed |
|---|---|---|
| `approved_named_customer` | Customer has signed permission for name/logo/quote/result. | Named case study with approved logo/quote/result scope. |
| `approved_anonymized_customer` | Customer has approved anonymized description and metrics. | "An anonymized regional health plan..." with approved metrics. |
| `composite_customer_pattern` | Multiple real learnings combined; no single customer result. | "Composite scenario based on observed buyer patterns." No precise ROI unless modeled. |
| `internal_dogfood` | Kolm used its own product internally. | "Kolm internal dogfood result" with environment and limits. |
| `synthetic_demo` | Demo data only. | "Demo scenario" or "synthetic benchmark." No customer implication. |
| `hypothetical` | Illustrative future story. | Clearly labeled hypothetical; no logo, quote, or implied adoption. |
| `unapproved` | No permission or evidence. | Not public. |

Required case-study packet:

- customer or scenario class;
- approval source, date, scope, expiry, and revocation path;
- industry, company size band, workload, region, and privacy scope;
- before/after architecture;
- baseline model/provider/runtime;
- data volume and data class;
- implementation steps;
- cost, latency, quality, security, governance, and operational metrics;
- evidence path for every number;
- quotes and logo permission;
- legal review status;
- limitations and non-applicability;
- support/sales owner;
- screenshot or artifact evidence;
- update date and freshness window.

#### Sales Engineering Proof System

Sales assets should be generated from product truth, not written as separate pitch decks:

| Sales asset | Source of truth | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| Executive one-pager | Narrative map, ROI model, case-study packets, product graph. | One category, three product surfaces, proof stats, current limitations, CTA. |
| Platform-engineering packet | API routes, SDK docs, runtime catalog, deployment readiness, portability matrix. | Commands, architecture diagrams, smoke evidence, security notes. |
| ML-engineering packet | Distill/K-Score/eval/benchmark contracts, model/runtime catalog, artifact passport. | Eval methodology, failure modes, holdout/label/privacy scope, reproducibility. |
| Security/procurement packet | Trust, legal, readiness closeout, compliance packet, audit logs, BAA/DPA. | Implemented versus certified controls, evidence dates, owner, legal scope. |
| Competitive battlecard | Competitor registry, compare rows, win/loss, objections, pricing assumptions. | Official competitor source date, Kolm proof, fair competitor wins. |
| Vertical packet | Healthcare, finance, legal, support, enterprise search, edge/device. | Use-case journey, data rights, compliance scope, case evidence, demo artifact. |
| Demo script | Journey scenarios, current UI, account state, demo data, artifacts. | Works in local and production; no fake data without label. |

#### Objection Handling Matrix

| Objection | Required answer shape |
|---|---|
| "Why not just use OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini?" | Acknowledge frontier generality; explain repeated-task compile, portability, audit, cost, and privacy tradeoff with current evidence. |
| "Why not OpenPipe/Together/Predibase/Bedrock?" | Acknowledge hosted training/serving strengths; show artifact portability, signed evidence, BYOC/device/offline, and procurement fit by use case. |
| "Is this real or just research?" | Show product graph status, readiness closeout, route/API/CLI/account proof, screenshots, and external gates. |
| "Do you have customers?" | Show approved customer proof, anonymized proof, dogfood proof, or clearly state design partner/beta status. |
| "Can you prove savings?" | Use ROI calculator with assumptions, benchmark evidence, provider pricing, workload volume, fallback/retry/review/storage/egress costs, and sensitivity range. |
| "Can you prove quality?" | Show K-Score method, calibration, holdout, public/private benchmark class, confidence interval, failure modes, and raw reports where allowed. |
| "Can my auditor trust it?" | Show artifact receipts, signatures, manifests, audit export, compliance packet, implemented/certified status, and production evidence. |
| "What if Kolm fails?" | Show rollback, vendor-exit, local fallback, export, support bundle, incident response, and deletion/retention proof. |

#### Completion Specification

| Subnode | Definition of done |

| `48.1` Competitor/source registry, freshness cadence, axis taxonomy, win/loss labels, and proof requirements | Every competitor/category has official sources, last-checked date, axis map, known wins/losses, comparison pages, owner, and stale-after policy. |
| `48.2` Comparison pages, alternatives pages, ROI claims, benchmark claims, pricing claims, and objection handling | Compare and ROI pages are generated or linted from current source records, row contracts, proof links, dated assumptions, and fair competitor win/loss labels. |
| `48.3` Case studies, testimonials, logos, design partners, anonymized stories, permissions, substantiation, and revocation | Every social-proof asset has evidence class, permission record, substantiation packet, legal review status, expiry, and revocation path. |
| `48.4` Sales engineering packets, procurement narratives, vertical proof, battlecards, demos, and qualification workflows | Sales assets are generated from product truth and include proof, limitations, buyer objections, qualification, next action, and owner. |
| `48.5` Market feedback loops, win/loss analysis, buyer research, review sites, analyst surfaces, and content performance | Product and GTM decisions use structured win/loss, buyer feedback, review signals, content performance, competitor changes, and objection telemetry. |
| `48.6` Evidence: competitor matrix, claim substantiation vault, case-study packet, sales packet, proof screenshots, and freshness audit | Release evidence includes competitor registry, compare row evidence, case-study packets, social-proof permissions, sales packets, screenshots, and freshness audit. |

#### Node 48 Relax Redline

Node `48` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

- Which official source and date supports every competitor statement on every compare page?
- Which rows does Kolm win, lose, tie, or only win conditionally, and are competitor strengths stated clearly?
- Which proof link supports each Kolm row, and does it prove the exact claim rather than a generic adjacent concept?
- Are every price, latency, quality, savings, compliance, ownership, portability, and "best/state-of-the-art" claim substantiated with evidence grade, date, assumptions, and expiry?
- Are case studies real, anonymized, composite, synthetic, dogfood, hypothetical, or unapproved, and is that visible enough for buyers and legal review?
- Are every logo, quote, testimonial, design-partner label, review, and named customer backed by permission, scope, expiry, and revocation path?
- Can sales and enterprise teams generate a buyer-specific proof packet without inventing facts outside product graph, readiness, competitor, case-study, and evidence ledgers?
- Can production smoke prove that compare, ROI, case-study, enterprise, and social-proof pages still match current market facts after deploy?

The relax condition for node `48` is not "we have comparison pages." It is: Kolm has a current competitor-source registry, fair compare row contracts, substantiated ROI and benchmark claims, permissioned social proof, evidence-backed case studies, generated sales packets, win/loss feedback, and production smoke proving that market-facing proof is accurate, persuasive, current, and legally safe.

#### Node 48 Market-Proof Control Plane Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The next correct maintenance action is to deepen node `48`, because a best-in-world product cannot ship market proof as hand-written persuasion. Compare pages, ROI calculators, case studies, testimonials, logos, enterprise one-pagers, and sales scripts are product surfaces. They need the same requirement, evidence, freshness, and production-proof discipline as APIs and account pages.

Node `48` V2 converts market-facing claims into auditable records. The rule is simple: no market claim may be public unless the company can show what source supports it, when that source was checked, what exact evidence supports the Kolm side, what the competitor wins, what assumptions drive the number, what legal or permission scope applies, when it expires, and which owner must refresh or remove it.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Artifact | Current observed state | V2 implication |
|---|---|---|
| `public/compare` | Current compare directory contains 13 HTML pages: `kolm-vs-bedrock-distill.html`, `kolm-vs-ollama.html`, `kolm-vs-openai.html`, `kolm-vs-openai-fine-tune.html`, `kolm-vs-openpipe.html`, `kolm-vs-openpipe-2026.html`, `kolm-vs-predibase.html`, `kolm-vs-predibase-2026.html`, `kolm-vs-proxis.html`, `kolm-vs-rag.html`, `kolm-vs-together.html`, `kolm-vs-together-2026.html`, and `legacy.html`. | Every page needs source-registry ownership, row contracts, last-reviewed date, expiry, and production smoke. |
| `public/case-studies` | Current case-study directory contains `finance-sr11-7.html`, `healthcare-phi-redactor.html`, `legal-contract-extraction.html`, and `index.html`. | Every case study needs evidence class: approved named, approved anonymized, composite, internal dogfood, synthetic demo, hypothetical, or unapproved. |
| High-risk compare copy | Current public compare copy includes claims around `0.6 ms`, `near 0`, `11.6x`, `5x throughput`, "flat compile", "free local run", "runs anywhere", SOC 2, FedRAMP, HIPAA, receipt chains, auditor attestation, OpenPipe/CoreWeave, Predibase/Rubrik, Together, Bedrock, and OpenAI. | Numeric, legal, compliance, portability, benchmark, superiority, and ownership claims must require higher evidence grades and shorter freshness windows. |
| `scripts/audit-claim-scope.cjs` | Claim-scope gate exists. | V2 should extend it with market-proof classes: compare row, ROI number, case-study metric, testimonial, logo, review, design-partner label, and competitor source. |
| `scripts/bench-proof.mjs`, `scripts/bench-compare.mjs`, `scripts/benchmark-evidence.mjs` | Benchmark proof infrastructure exists. | V2 market claims should consume benchmark evidence instead of restating performance numbers by hand. |
| `scripts/audit-buyer-journey.sh` | Buyer path audit exists. | V2 should regenerate buyer expectations from competitor registry, pricing truth, claim scope, case-proof ledger, and journey proof. |
| `package.json` verification | Relevant gates include `verify:claims-scope`, `verify:benchmark-evidence`, `verify:brand`, `lint:refs`, and `verify:depth`. | Market proof needs its own gate and must be included in the final depth/release packet once implemented. |
| `public/pricing.html`, `public/roi.html`, `public/benchmarks.html`, `public/enterprise.html`, `public/security.html`, `public/trust.html` | Pricing, ROI, benchmark, enterprise, security, and trust pages are market-proof surfaces. | They must be treated as source-bound proof pages, not ordinary marketing copy. |

##### Current External Baseline V2

| Baseline | Current source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| FTC fake review and testimonial rule | FTC announced a final rule banning fake reviews and testimonials, with the Q&A saying the rule went into effect on October 21, 2024: `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/consumer-reviews-testimonials-rule-questions-answers`. | Kolm must not publish fake, AI-generated, unpermissioned, misleading, undisclosed, insider, or unsupported testimonials, reviews, quotes, or logos. |
| FTC endorsement guidance | FTC Endorsement Guides require material connections that affect credibility to be disclosed: `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/ftcs-endorsement-guides`. | Design partners, advisors, investors, employees, paid pilots, incentives, and customer references need permission and disclosure scope. |
| FTC advertising substantiation | FTC policy says advertisers must substantiate express and implied objective claims and meet advertised proof levels such as "tests prove" or "studies show": `https://www.ftc.gov/legal-library/browse/ftc-policy-statement-regarding-advertising-substantiation`. | Kolm must keep evidence at least as strong as the claim language. "Benchmark proves" needs benchmark evidence; "customer saved" needs customer substantiation. |
| FTC AI claims posture | FTC guidance and Operation AI Comply actions target deceptive or unsupported AI claims: `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes`. | AI superiority, automation, accuracy, savings, safety, compliance, and "state of the art" claims need proof and caveats. |
| OpenAI optimization and distillation | OpenAI's current docs and product materials describe fine-tuning/distillation workflows on OpenAI's platform: `https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/distillation` and `https://openai.com/index/api-model-distillation/`. | OpenAI comparisons must be checked against current official OpenAI docs, not old assumptions about only raw API calls. |
| AWS Bedrock distillation | AWS describes Bedrock Model Distillation as a way to use smaller models for faster response and lower operational cost, with Bedrock user-guide support: `https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/model-distillation`. | Bedrock comparisons must acknowledge AWS procurement, IAM, KMS, CloudTrail, S3, and Bedrock runtime strengths while scoping Kolm's portable-artifact advantage. |
| Together fine-tuning | Together docs describe supervised fine-tuning modes and the product page advertises hosted fine-tuning/inference capabilities: `https://docs.together.ai/docs/fine-tuning-overview` and `https://www.together.ai/fine-tuning`. | Together comparisons must credit hosted training/inference and VLM fine-tuning capabilities instead of overstating Kolm as universally better. |
| OpenPipe/CoreWeave status | CoreWeave announced an agreement to acquire OpenPipe: `https://coreweave.com/news/coreweave-to-acquire-openpipe-leader-in-reinforcement-learning`. | OpenPipe compare pages need current ownership/platform context and dated review. |
| Predibase/Rubrik status | Rubrik announced agreement to acquire Predibase: `https://ir.rubrik.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2025/Rubrik-to-Acquire-Predibase-to-Accelerate-Agentic-AI-Adoption/default.aspx`. | Predibase compare pages need current Rubrik context and fair enterprise-governance framing. |
| B2B buyer behavior | G2's 2025 buyer behavior report describes AI reshaping B2B software buying and uses survey and behavioral data: `https://learn.g2.com/2025-g2-buyer-behavior-report`. | Market proof should answer buyer risk, ROI, peer proof, implementation confidence, and procurement evidence, not only product features. |

##### Market Claim Contract V2

Every public market claim should be serializable as `kolm.market-claim.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "MCLAIM-048-EXAMPLE",
  "surface": "compare|case_study|roi|pricing|benchmark|enterprise|trust|sales_packet|social_proof",
  "claim_text": "Kolm cuts repeated support triage cost by 40% in this modeled workload.",
  "claim_type": "numeric|comparative|compliance|customer|testimonial|logo|benchmark|pricing|portability|quality|safety|roi|category",
  "public_locations": [],
  "status": "draft|approved|expired|blocked|removed",
  "kolm_evidence": {
    "paths": [],
    "grade_required": "E6",
    "grade_actual": "E0",
    "fresh_until": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "external_evidence": {
    "sources": [],
    "last_checked": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "fresh_until": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "assumptions": [],
  "limitations": [],
  "permissions": {
    "required": false,
    "record": null,
    "expires": null
  },
  "review": {
    "owner": "marketing-proof",
    "legal_required": false,
    "security_required": false,
    "approved_by": null
  }
}
```

Evidence thresholds:

| Claim class | Minimum evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Category and positioning | `E4` plus narrative map. | Must not imply capabilities outside product graph. |
| Product feature | `E5` local journey proof or `E6` release proof. | Must name scope if not production-proven. |
| Pricing and ROI | `E6` plus assumptions and current provider prices. | Sensitivity range required. |
| Benchmark or performance | `E6` for local claim, `E8` for public leaderboard or external ranking. | Include hardware, dataset, commands, model versions, date. |
| Compliance/security | `E6` for implemented controls, `E8` for certifications or third-party attestations. | Implemented controls must not be worded as certified. |
| Customer result | Permissioned case-study packet plus evidence grade matching the number. | No exact result without substantiation. |
| Testimonial/logo/review | Permission record and FTC disclosure review. | No fake, synthetic, employee, or paid proof without labeling. |
| Competitor comparison | Competitor official source plus Kolm evidence and expiry. | Include where competitor wins. |

##### Competitor Registry V2

Every competitor record should be maintained as `kolm.competitor-source.v2`.

```json
{
  "competitor_id": "together-ai",
  "display_name": "Together AI",
  "category": "hosted-training-and-inference-cloud",
  "official_sources": [
    {
      "url": "https://docs.together.ai/docs/fine-tuning-overview",
      "type": "docs",
      "last_checked": "2026-05-25",
      "status": "reachable"
    }
  ],
  "comparison_pages": ["/compare/kolm-vs-together", "/compare/kolm-vs-together-2026"],
  "axes": ["hosted_training", "inference_cloud", "fine_tuning", "evals", "artifact_portability", "pricing", "governance"],
  "known_competitor_wins": ["managed GPU operations", "hosted inference cloud", "mature developer cloud"],
  "known_kolm_wins": ["portable signed artifact", "artifact receipt chain", "offline verification"],
  "unknowns": [],
  "refresh_cadence_days": 30,
  "stale_after_days": 45,
  "owner": "marketing-proof",
  "public_copy_scope": "dated_comparison"
}
```

The registry must separate direct competitors from substitutes:

- Frontier API/model platforms: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, AWS Bedrock, Azure AI Foundry.
- Fine-tuning and training clouds: Together, Predibase/Rubrik, OpenPipe/CoreWeave, OpenPipe alternatives, SageMaker, Vertex, Modal, Baseten.
- Observability/eval stacks: Braintrust, Humanloop, LangSmith, Langfuse, Helicone, OpenTelemetry/LLM observability.
- Local/runtime stacks: Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, vLLM, ONNX Runtime GenAI, Core ML, LiteRT, MLX, WebGPU/WebNN, ExecuTorch.
- Enterprise AI platforms: Palantir AIP, Databricks Mosaic AI, Snowflake Cortex, Dataiku LLM Mesh, DataRobot, TrueFoundry, NVIDIA AI Enterprise/NIM/NeMo.
- Build/proof substitutes: RAG stacks, vector databases, agent frameworks, artifact registries, model registries, MLOps platforms.

##### Compare Row Contract V2

Every public compare table row should be generated or validated from `kolm.compare-row.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "CMP-048-OPENAI-ARTIFACT-PORTABILITY",
  "page": "/compare/kolm-vs-openai-fine-tune",
  "axis": "artifact_portability",
  "buyer_question": "Can I leave the provider and still run the optimized behavior?",
  "kolm_side": {
    "claim": "Kolm emits a signed .kolm artifact with manifest and verify command.",
    "evidence": ["docs/kolm-format-v1.md", "public/spec.html", "sample verify output"],
    "limitations": ["Does not make Kolm a frontier general model provider."]
  },
  "competitor_side": {
    "claim": "OpenAI optimization runs through OpenAI platform APIs.",
    "evidence": ["https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/distillation"],
    "limitations": []
  },
  "result": "kolm_win|competitor_win|tie|depends|not_comparable",
  "freshness": {
    "last_checked": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "expires": "YYYY-MM-DD"
  },
  "legal_review": "not_required|required|approved",
  "copy_allowed": true
}
```

Row rules:

- No row may say "best", "leader", "near zero", "run anywhere", "free", "certified", "auditor attestation", or "customer saved" unless the evidence grade satisfies the market-claim contract.
- Every competitor comparison must include at least one competitor win or "right answer" section when the competitor has a real advantage.
- A proof link must prove the row, not merely point to a nearby page.
- Dated pages with `2026` in the title must show exact last-reviewed date and source freshness.
- Rows that reference acquisitions, ownership changes, certifications, package releases, or provider product names expire quickly.

##### ROI And Pricing Proof Contract V2

Every ROI or savings claim should be modeled from `kolm.roi-model.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "ROI-048-SUPPORT-TRIAGE",
  "workload": "support triage",
  "inputs": {
    "monthly_calls": 1200000,
    "baseline_provider": "openai",
    "baseline_model": "example",
    "kolm_artifact_runtime": "local|cloud|edge|hybrid",
    "review_labor_rate_hour": null,
    "fallback_rate": null,
    "retry_rate": null,
    "storage_egress_cost": null
  },
  "assumptions": [],
  "provider_price_sources": [],
  "calculation": {
    "baseline_cost": null,
    "kolm_cost": null,
    "savings_percent": null,
    "sensitivity_low": null,
    "sensitivity_high": null
  },
  "evidence": [],
  "public_copy": {
    "allowed": false,
    "required_caveat": "Modeled estimate until customer proof or public benchmark exists."
  }
}
```

ROI requirements:

- Use current provider pricing sources and record date.
- Include retry, fallback, human review, storage, egress, monitoring, support, and GPU/runtime cost where relevant.
- Separate modeled ROI, benchmarked ROI, customer-approved ROI, and internal dogfood ROI.
- Do not publish exact customer savings without customer permission and substantiation.
- Show sensitivity ranges rather than a single overconfident number.

##### Customer Proof And Social Proof Ledger V2

Every customer/social-proof artifact should be maintained in `docs/customer-proof-ledger.v2.json`.

| Field | Required meaning |
|---|---|
| `proof_id` | Stable ID for logo, quote, testimonial, review, case study, design partner, or customer metric. |
| `evidence_class` | `approved_named_customer`, `approved_anonymized_customer`, `composite_customer_pattern`, `internal_dogfood`, `synthetic_demo`, `hypothetical`, or `unapproved`. |
| `permission_record` | Contract, email, form, or approval reference; scope; approver; date; expiry; revocation path. |
| `material_connection` | Investor, advisor, employee, paid pilot, discounted pilot, partner, or none. |
| `typicality` | Whether result is typical, best case, modeled, demo-only, or not representative. |
| `metrics` | Every number has baseline, method, data source, environment, date, owner, and evidence path. |
| `public_locations` | Exact pages, cards, logos, OpenGraph, schema, docs, sales decks, and screenshots where it appears. |
| `legal_status` | `not_reviewed`, `needs_review`, `approved`, `expired`, or `blocked`. |

Public social proof is blocked if it lacks permission scope, material-connection review, evidence class, expiry, revocation path, or public-location inventory.

##### Sales Packet System V2

Sales and enterprise packets should be generated from product truth:

| Packet | Inputs | Must include |
|---|---|---|
| Executive proof packet | Product graph, narrative, ROI model, customer ledger, closeout ledger. | Three product surfaces, buyer outcomes, current proof, open gates, ROI caveats, CTA. |
| Platform packet | Runtime catalog, API routes, CLI/TUI, cloud/storage readiness, deployment docs. | Architecture, smoke commands, target limits, rollback, security posture. |
| ML packet | Distill/build strategy, K-Score calibration, benchmark evidence, quality reports. | Eval method, holdout/privacy, failure modes, model/provider versions, reproducibility. |
| Security/procurement packet | Trust/security pages, compliance packet, audit log, BAA/DPA, closeout. | Implemented controls versus certified controls, evidence dates, owner, legal scope. |
| Competitive battlecard | Competitor registry, compare rows, win/loss, objections. | Fair competitor wins, Kolm wins, disqualifiers, proof links, dated sources. |
| Case-study packet | Customer proof ledger, substantiation, screenshots, journey proof. | Evidence class, permission, before/after, metrics, caveats, revocation path. |
| Demo packet | Journey scenarios, demo data, account state, artifacts, screenshots. | Script, safe data labels, fallback plan, failure recovery, no fake-customer implication. |

##### Market Feedback Loop V2

Market proof should feed product development:

| Signal | Capture | Product action |
|---|---|---|
| Compare page engagement | Page, axis, CTA, scroll depth, proof-link clicks. | Improve unclear positioning, route buyers to correct journey. |
| Objection telemetry | Sales/support notes classified by competitor, concern, missing proof, pricing, trust, deployment, UX. | Create workorders for proof gaps and product blockers. |
| Win/loss analysis | Deal stage, competitor, buyer persona, vertical, use case, decisive factor. | Update roadmap and competitor registry. |
| ROI calculator usage | Inputs, assumptions changed, export/share, enterprise inquiry handoff. | Tune ROI ranges and proof requirements. |
| Case-study demand | Which vertical/use case buyers ask for. | Prioritize proof packets and customer approvals. |
| Trust packet downloads | Role, company size band, requested evidence, missing evidence. | Prioritize compliance, package, and certification closeout. |
| Review/analyst/community signals | G2, GitHub, packages, AI answer engines, forums, analysts. | Update public proof, objections, and docs gaps. |

##### Node `48` V2 Relax Redline

Node `48` V2 is relaxed only when the following are true:

- Every public market claim is recorded in `kolm.market-claim.v2` with type, location, evidence grade, source freshness, assumptions, permissions, review state, and owner.
- Every compare page row is generated or validated from a row contract that includes official competitor source, Kolm evidence, win/loss/tie/depends label, limitations, expiry, and proof-link exactness.
- Every ROI and savings claim uses a current model with provider price sources, workload assumptions, sensitivity ranges, and clear evidence class.
- Every logo, quote, testimonial, review, design-partner label, named customer, and case-study metric has a customer proof ledger record with permission, material-connection review, expiry, and revocation path.
- Sales packets are generated from product graph, readiness closeout, market claims, competitor registry, case-proof ledger, and journey evidence.
- Market feedback loops convert objections, win/loss reasons, proof-link engagement, ROI usage, trust packet downloads, and competitor changes into product workorders.
- Production smoke verifies that compare pages, case studies, ROI, pricing, enterprise, trust, benchmark, structured data, proof links, and source dates match the current market-proof ledgers.

The strict rule is: if a claim helps sell Kolm, it must be traceable, current, permissioned where necessary, honest about competitor strengths, and removable when evidence expires.

#### Node 48 Buyer-Proof Evidence Ledger And Market Claims Addendum V3

Node `52` stays unused. The active market-proof work belongs to node `48`: make every buyer-facing claim credible, current, permissioned, and useful. V3 focuses on the dangerous gap between "we have compare pages" and "a serious buyer can trust every comparison, case study, ROI number, proof badge, and sales packet."

The V3 rule is stricter than V2: every market surface must be able to answer the buyer's next skeptical question. If a page says "run anywhere," the buyer must see target scope and proof. If a page says "auditor attestation," the buyer must see whether that is implemented, external-certified, simulated, or blocked. If a page references a competitor acquisition, the date and source must be current. If a case study implies a customer result, permission and substantiation must exist.

##### Current Market Evidence Rechecked For V3

| Evidence group | Current observed state | V3 implication |
|---|---:|---|
| Compare pages | Current `public/compare` scan found `13` HTML pages totaling `212,677` bytes. | The compare system is large enough to need a generated buyer-proof ledger, not hand-maintained copy. |
| Case-study pages | Current `public/case-studies` scan found `4` HTML pages totaling `53,099` bytes. | Each case study needs evidence class, permission state, metric substantiation, and explicit synthetic/anonymized/customer status. |
| Core market pages | Current pricing, ROI, benchmarks, enterprise, security, and trust pages total `285,648` bytes. | Market proof spans more than `/compare`; ROI, trust, pricing, benchmark, enterprise, and security pages must share the same claim ledger. |
| High-risk claim scan | Current public market pages contain claims around `0.6 ms`, `11.6x`, `5x throughput`, "near 0", "runs anywhere", "flat compile", "free local run", SOC 2, FedRAMP, HIPAA, auditor attestation, CoreWeave, Rubrik, Bedrock, OpenAI, Together, and Predibase. | Numeric, legal, compliance, acquisition, portability, and superiority claims need exact evidence grade, source date, expiry, and public-copy caveat. |
| Compare copy freshness | Current compare pages hard-code time-sensitive 2026 acquisition narratives for OpenPipe/CoreWeave and Predibase/Rubrik. Current official-source search results confirm Rubrik announced a Predibase acquisition agreement on June 25, 2025, and CoreWeave has an official OpenPipe acquisition announcement; the precise status/date wording must be source-verified before release. | V3 must block market finality when public copy hard-codes acquisition timing or status without current official source, last-checked date, and expiry. |
| Proof-link quality | Current compare rows often link to generic proof pages such as `/docs`, `/airgap`, `/baa`, `/compliance-packs`, or `/research#constrained`. | V3 requires proof-link exactness: the evidence must prove the exact row claim, not a related category. |
| Buyer UX risk | Compare pages include many dense rows and technical details. | Buyer-facing comparison needs scan hierarchy: buyer question, conclusion, where Kolm wins, where competitor wins, proof freshness, caveats, and next action. |

##### External Baseline Refreshed For V3

| Baseline | Current source | V3 import into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| FTC Consumer Reviews and Testimonials Rule | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/resources/consumer-reviews-testimonials-rule-questions-answers` | FTC says the rule went into effect on October 21, 2024 and addresses deceptive or unfair reviews/testimonials. Kolm must not publish fake, AI-generated, unpermissioned, undisclosed, or misleading social proof. |
| FTC advertising substantiation | `https://www.ftc.gov/legal-library/browse/ftc-policy-statement-regarding-advertising-substantiation` | Objective claims need evidence that matches the express or implied proof level. "Tests prove", "customer saved", "certified", and "benchmark-leading" need stronger substantiation than ordinary positioning. |
| FTC AI claims guidance | `https://www.ftc.gov/business-guidance/blog/2023/02/keep-your-ai-claims-check` and `https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/09/ftc-announces-crackdown-deceptive-ai-claims-schemes` | AI claims about accuracy, automation, safety, superiority, and earnings/savings must not outrun evidence. |
| AWS Bedrock model distillation | `https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/model-distillation` and `https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-distillation.html` | Bedrock comparisons must acknowledge current AWS distillation claims, teacher/student workflow, speed/cost positioning, supported models/regions, and AWS governance strengths. |
| OpenAI model distillation | `https://openai.com/index/api-model-distillation/` and `https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/distillation` | OpenAI comparisons must reflect current OpenAI optimization/distillation/fine-tuning positioning, not frame OpenAI only as raw API traffic. |
| Together fine-tuning | `https://docs.together.ai/docs/fine-tuning-overview` and `https://www.together.ai/fine-tuning` | Together comparisons must credit hosted fine-tuning, model support, deployment, and managed infrastructure strengths. |
| Rubrik/Predibase | `https://ir.rubrik.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2025/Rubrik-to-Acquire-Predibase-to-Accelerate-Agentic-AI-Adoption/default.aspx` | Predibase comparison pages need current Rubrik context, acquisition status, and a dated source record before stating product direction. |
| CoreWeave/OpenPipe | `https://www.coreweave.com/news/coreweave-to-acquire-openpipe-leader-in-reinforcement-learning` | OpenPipe comparison pages need current CoreWeave context, status/date wording, and official-source freshness before using acquisition claims. |
| B2B buyer behavior | `https://learn.g2.com/2025-g2-buyer-behavior-report` | Market proof should answer ROI, trust, risk, implementation confidence, peer proof, and procurement requirements, not just product capabilities. |

##### V3 Buyer-Proof Ledger Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.buyer-proof-ledger.v3",
  "release_id": "2026-05-node-48-v3",
  "surfaces": ["compare", "roi", "pricing", "benchmarks", "case_studies", "enterprise", "trust", "security", "sales_packets", "social_proof"],
  "claim_sources": {
    "internal": ["product_graph", "readiness_closeout", "proof_graph", "journey_labs", "benchmark_reports", "package_readiness", "compliance_packets"],
    "external": ["competitor_official_sources", "regulator_sources", "provider_pricing", "customer_permissions", "review_sites"]
  },
  "claim_grades": {
    "positioning": "E4",
    "feature": "E5",
    "public_feature": "E6",
    "production_claim": "E7",
    "external_trust": "E8"
  },
  "freshness": {
    "competitor_sources_days": 30,
    "pricing_sources_days": 14,
    "provider_catalog_days": 14,
    "benchmark_claim_days": 30,
    "case_study_permission_days": 365,
    "certification_claim_days": 30,
    "social_proof_permission_days": 365
  },
  "redlines": {
    "unpermissioned_social_proof": "fail",
    "unsupported_numeric_claim": "fail",
    "stale_competitor_source": "fail",
    "generic_proof_link_for_specific_claim": "fail",
    "hidden_competitor_win": "fail",
    "external_claim_without_e8": "fail"
  }
}
```

##### V3 Claim Risk Tiers

| Tier | Claim examples | Required proof |
|---|---|---|
| `R0-positioning` | Category framing, buyer audience, high-level product surface. | Node `44` narrative map plus product graph. |
| `R1-feature` | Route model traffic, compile artifacts, account readiness, CLI command exists. | Local implementation and journey proof. |
| `R2-production` | Runs on deployed domain, account works in production, deployed route is live. | Production smoke, deployed release ID, rollback note. |
| `R3-numeric` | `40% savings`, `11.6x cheaper`, `0.6 ms`, `5x throughput`, `near zero`. | Benchmark or ROI model with raw data, assumptions, environment, date, and sensitivity range. |
| `R4-compliance` | SOC 2, HIPAA, FedRAMP, BAA, auditor attestation, third-party verifier. | Implemented-control evidence for local claims; external attestation/certification for certified claims. |
| `R5-customer` | Customer saved, customer quote, logo, testimonial, design partner, case study. | Permission record, evidence class, material-connection review, substantiation, expiry, revocation path. |
| `R6-competitor` | "Unlike OpenAI", "Bedrock only", acquisition status, competitor product limits. | Current official competitor source, dated row contract, competitor-win section, expiry. |
| `R7-superiority` | Best, leader, state of the art, most advanced, gold standard. | Public benchmark or third-party evidence with legal review; otherwise avoid. |

##### V3 Market Surface UX Contract

| Surface | Buyer must see first | Mandatory states |
|---|---|---|
| Compare page | Who this comparison is for, where Kolm wins, where competitor wins, last reviewed date, proof scope. | `current`, `stale`, `historical`, `depends`, `competitor_wins`, `proof_missing`, `needs_refresh`. |
| ROI page | Inputs, assumptions, sensitivity range, evidence class, export/contact path. | `modeled`, `benchmarked`, `customer_approved`, `internal_dogfood`, `missing_price_source`. |
| Case study | Evidence class, industry, before/after, approved metrics, limitations, permission scope. | `approved_named`, `approved_anonymized`, `composite`, `synthetic_demo`, `hypothetical`, `expired_permission`. |
| Enterprise page | Procurement path, trust packet, implemented vs certified controls, owner/SLA, next step. | `contact_sales`, `security_review`, `external_gate`, `packet_ready`, `packet_requested`. |
| Trust/security page | Control status, certification status, evidence date, export path. | `implemented`, `in_progress`, `external_certified`, `not_offered`, `expired_evidence`. |
| Sales packet | Persona, use case, proof, caveats, competitor fit, next action. | `draft`, `reviewed`, `buyer_ready`, `expired_sources`, `legal_blocked`. |

##### V3 Compare Page Generation Policy

Compare pages should be generated or blocked from `kolm.compare-page.v3` records:

```json
{
  "id": "CPAGE-OPENPIPE-2026",
  "url": "/compare/kolm-vs-openpipe-2026",
  "competitor_ids": ["openpipe", "coreweave"],
  "last_reviewed": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "expires": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "buyer_question": "Should I use a hosted fine-tuning endpoint or a portable signed artifact?",
  "kolm_best_for": [],
  "competitor_best_for": [],
  "neutral_summary": "",
  "rows": ["CMP-..."],
  "source_requirements": ["official_competitor_source", "kolm_proof_source", "competitor_win", "limitations", "next_action"],
  "production_smoke": {
    "required": true,
    "checks": ["route", "canonical", "structured_data", "proof_links", "last_reviewed_visible", "stale_banner_absent_or_present_correctly"]
  }
}
```

Rules:

- Pages with year labels such as `2026` expire faster and must show last-reviewed date.
- Acquisition or ownership claims require official source and exact date/status wording.
- Competitor strengths must be above the fold or immediately after the summary, not buried as a footnote.
- Proof links must map to row IDs and evidence classes.
- Stale pages must either redirect, show a stale banner, or be removed from primary navigation.

##### V3 Social Proof Permission Contract

```json
{
  "schema": "kolm.social-proof-permission.v3",
  "proof_id": "SP-EXAMPLE",
  "type": "logo|quote|testimonial|review|case_study|design_partner|customer_metric",
  "evidence_class": "approved_named_customer|approved_anonymized_customer|composite|internal_dogfood|synthetic_demo|hypothetical|unapproved",
  "permission": {
    "status": "approved|pending|expired|revoked|not_required_for_synthetic",
    "scope": ["logo", "quote", "metric", "industry", "page", "sales_packet"],
    "approver": "",
    "date": "",
    "expires": "",
    "revocation_path": ""
  },
  "material_connection": "none|advisor|investor|employee|paid_pilot|discounted_pilot|partner",
  "typicality": "typical|best_case|modeled|demo_only|not_representative",
  "locations": [],
  "substantiation": [],
  "legal_review": "not_required|required|approved|blocked"
}
```

No customer-facing build can call a logo, quote, testimonial, design partner, or customer result finished without this record.

##### Node `48` V3 Relax Redline

Node `48` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every public market claim is in the buyer-proof ledger with risk tier, evidence grade, freshness, assumptions, owner, and public-copy caveat.
- Every compare page has current official competitor sources, dated review, expiry, competitor-win section, row-level proof, and production smoke.
- Acquisition/status claims for OpenPipe/CoreWeave, Predibase/Rubrik, or any other competitor are current-source verified and expire quickly.
- Every numerical, benchmark, speed, savings, cost, latency, throughput, and "near zero" claim has raw evidence, calculation, sensitivity, and expiry.
- Every compliance, certification, BAA, auditor, FedRAMP, SOC 2, HIPAA, and trust claim distinguishes implemented controls from external attestation.
- Every logo, quote, testimonial, review, design partner, named customer, and case-study metric has permission, evidence class, substantiation, and revocation path.
- ROI pages show inputs, assumptions, sensitivity range, evidence class, and next action rather than a single overconfident savings number.
- Sales packets are generated from product graph, readiness closeout, proof graph, competitor registry, case ledger, and journey evidence.
- Production market-proof replay verifies routes, structured data, proof links, source dates, stale banners, and claim status on the deployed domain.

If any of those fail, Kolm may have persuasive market copy, but it does not have buyer-proof market evidence.

### Vertical Solution Kits, Industry Recipes, Domain Proof, And Regulated Workflow Packs Ledger V1

Node `49` turns Kolm's vertical pages, showcase specs, recipes, and case studies into a governed product system. The current repo already has a strong vertical footprint: medical, finance, legal, code, and support landing pages; healthcare showcase specs for FHIR, X12 EDI, HEDIS, prior authorization, claims, risk adjustment, NPI lookup, and provider credentialing; case studies; cookbook pages; marketplace references; CLI/API promises; and tests. The gap is that these pieces are not yet one vertical-solution contract with maturity, domain standards, benchmark proof, regulated-workflow boundaries, customer onboarding, account UX, and production smoke.

This branch is not a request to inflate the site with industry copy. It is a request to make each vertical credible enough that a buyer in that domain can inspect the recipe, understand what is shipped, see which standards are supported, run a demo or artifact, verify the eval, understand privacy and human-review boundaries, and know exactly what remains blocked.

#### Current Vertical Evidence

| Evidence class | Current repo evidence | Completion gap |
|---|---|---|
| Vertical landing pages | `public/verticals/code.html`, `public/verticals/finance.html`, `public/verticals/legal.html`, `public/verticals/medical.html`, `public/verticals/support.html` | Each page references foundation students, target K-Scores, pending W757/W715 work, API/CLI commands, marketplace listings, and case-study skeletons. Completion requires a single vertical registry that drives these pages and prevents target-only or pending claims from looking shipped. |
| Case studies | `public/case-studies/finance-sr11-7.html`, `public/case-studies/healthcare-phi-redactor.html`, `public/case-studies/legal-contract-extraction.html` | Each case needs proof packet, permission/anonymization status, source metrics, customer or synthetic label, date, methodology, and revocation owner. |
| Healthcare showcase specs | `public/docs/showcase/*.spec.json` includes FHIR/USCDI, EDI 270/271, 278, 834, 835, 837, HEDIS BCS/CBP/CDC-HbA1c, ICD-10/CPT crosswalk, prior-auth review, prior-auth letter, denial appeal, claims adjudication, provider credentialing, NPI directory, MLR rebate, CMS Star, risk adjustment, fraud detection, and member support triage. | These are valuable product assets, but they need a manifest-level domain crosswalk, fixture provenance, validation commands, benchmark scope, UI owner, docs owner, and regulated-use disclaimer per recipe. |
| Cookbook and examples | `public/cookbook/phi-redactor.html`, `public/cookbook/soap-redactor.html`, `public/cookbook/legal-clause-extract.html`, `public/cookbook/finance-disclosure-redact.html`, `examples/claims-redactor`, `examples/predibase-style-customer-support` | Recipes should compile into signed artifacts or explicitly state why they are illustrative. Every recipe needs inputs, outputs, schema, redaction requirements, eval fixture, run command, account import path, and "do not use for" boundary. |
| Tests | `tests/wave751-verticals.test.js`, `tests/wave272-vertical-microsites.test.js`, `tests/wave176-edi-837-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave177-edi-835-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave178-edi-834-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave179-edi-270-271-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave180-edi-278-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave181-fhir-uscdi-recipe.test.js`, `tests/wave182-hedis-measures.test.js`, `tests/wave708-vertical-disclaimer.test.js` | Tests prove parts exist, but completion requires tests to prove end-to-end vertical journeys, standards crosswalks, route/API/CLI/account parity, benchmark evidence, and production screenshots. |
| Product copy risk | Vertical pages currently use "Frontier AI for..." and mention "foundation student" with target K-Score and pending compilation. | Copy must make the three-product promise clear by vertical: route/capture now, compile/distill when evidence is ready, deploy to device/infrastructure when artifact is verified. Pending students must stay visibly pending. |

#### External Domain Baseline

| Source | Current standard or guidance | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| HL7 FHIR overview, `https://hl7.org/fhir/overview.html` | FHIR is the HL7 standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically and has versioned releases such as R4 and R5. | Healthcare recipes must state FHIR version, resources used, profile/IG assumptions, validation command, and unsupported resource classes. |
| CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule CMS-0057-F, `https://www.cms.gov/priorities/key-initiatives/burden-reduction/policies-and-regulations/cms-interoperability-and-prior-authorization-final-rule-cms-0057-f` | Impacted payers generally have until January 1, 2027 for API requirements, and CMS notes flexibility for FHIR-based Prior Authorization APIs. | Prior-auth recipes should not be generic demos. They need CMS-0057-F scope, supported payer types, FHIR/API dependencies, timing claims, excluded-drug boundaries, and human review. |
| HL7 Da Vinci PAS IG, `https://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-pas/STU2/` | Da Vinci PAS is a FHIR implementation guide for prior authorization support and relates to CRD/DTR for determining requirements and documentation. | Prior-auth review and letter specs should crosswalk to PAS, CRD, and DTR concepts or explicitly say they are not complete implementation guides. |
| X12 health care transaction flow, `https://x12.org/flow/health-care` | X12 describes transaction families including 270/271 eligibility, 278 services review, 834 enrollment, 835 payment/advice, and 837 claims. | EDI recipes must specify transaction set, version/implementation-guide assumptions, loop/segment coverage, companion-guide limits, validation fixture, and rejection behavior. |
| NCQA HEDIS measures and MY 2026 resources, `https://www.ncqa.org/hedis/measures/` | NCQA maintains HEDIS measures and current measurement-year resources, including MY 2026 updates. | HEDIS recipes must pin measurement year, measure IDs, denominator/numerator logic, exclusions, data-source assumptions, and whether the output is quality-improvement support or official reporting. |
| Federal Reserve SR 11-7, `https://www.federalreserve.gov/bankinforeg/srletters/sr1107.htm` | SR 11-7 is supervisory guidance for model risk management. | Finance vertical claims should include model inventory, validation, limitations, monitoring, governance, and independent review evidence before implying banking model-risk readiness. |
| ABA Formal Opinion 512, `https://www.americanbar.org/content/dam/aba/administrative/professional_responsibility/ethics-opinions/aba-formal-opinion-512.pdf` | ABA guidance states lawyers using generative AI remain bound by duties such as competence, confidentiality, communication, and reasonable fees. | Legal vertical recipes must require lawyer review, confidentiality controls, citation/source verification, no unauthorized-practice claims, and fee/cost disclosure guidance where relevant. |
| FINRA Regulatory Notice 24-09, `https://www.finra.org/rules-guidance/notices/24-09` | FINRA reminds member firms that AI use does not relieve existing securities-law and regulatory obligations. | Finance/support workflows for regulated firms must keep supervision, recordkeeping, customer communication, and model-risk boundaries visible. |

#### Vertical Solution Contract

Each vertical needs one machine-readable contract. Public pages, docs, account onboarding, marketplace entries, tests, and sales packets should render from it.

```json
{
  "vertical_id": "healthcare",
  "title": "Healthcare workflow artifacts",
  "status": "beta",
  "maturity": "domain_specs_plus_synthetic_fixtures",
  "master_node": "49",
  "product_promises": {
    "route_capture": "shipped_or_implemented",
    "compile_distill": "gated_by_eval_and_customer_holdout",
    "deploy_anywhere": "gated_by_artifact_and_runtime_proof"
  },
  "standards": [
    {
      "id": "FHIR-R4",
      "source": "https://hl7.org/fhir/overview.html",
      "coverage": "USCDI showcase resources only",
      "validator": "FHIR validation command or explicit not-yet-available reason"
    },
    {
      "id": "CMS-0057-F",
      "source": "https://www.cms.gov/priorities/key-initiatives/burden-reduction/policies-and-regulations/cms-interoperability-and-prior-authorization-final-rule-cms-0057-f",
      "coverage": "prior authorization workflow support, not final payer compliance"
    }
  ],
  "recipes": [
    {
      "id": "prior-auth-review",
      "source_path": "public/docs/showcase/prior-auth-review.spec.json",
      "artifact_path": null,
      "fixture_path": "test/fixtures/verticals/prior-auth-review.json",
      "eval_path": "reports/verticals/healthcare/prior-auth-review.json",
      "human_review_required": true,
      "claim_scope": "synthetic fixture demo until customer holdout and domain expert eval exist"
    }
  ],
  "proof": {
    "tests": ["node --test --test-concurrency=1 tests/wave181-fhir-uscdi-recipe.test.js"],
    "screenshots": ["reports/ui-surface-audit/.../verticals-healthcare.png"],
    "production_smoke": ["GET /verticals/medical", "GET /docs/showcase/manifest.json"],
    "account_path": "/account/solutions/healthcare"
  }
}
```

#### Domain Crosswalk Requirements

| Vertical | Current assets | Required standards/proof crosswalk |
|---|---|---|
| Healthcare and health insurance | FHIR/USCDI, X12 EDI, HEDIS, prior auth, claims, provider credentialing, NPI, MLR, CMS Star, PHI redaction, SOAP redactor | Map each recipe to FHIR resources or X12 transactions, HEDIS measurement year where applicable, CMS-0057-F/PAS/CRD/DTR scope for prior auth, HIPAA/BAA scope, PHI redaction class coverage, human clinical or operations review, and safety disclaimer. |
| Finance | SR 11-7 case page, finance vertical page, finance disclosure redaction cookbook, risk/compliance extraction copy | Map to model-risk inventory, validation, monitoring, challenger comparison, audit trail, supervisory review, retention, recordkeeping, and no-investment-advice disclaimers. |
| Legal | Legal vertical page, legal contract extraction case, legal-clause cookbook | Map to lawyer review, client confidentiality, source/citation verification, privilege boundaries, unauthorized-practice boundary, matter data retention, and no-legal-advice disclaimers. |
| Code | Code vertical page, code-review/generation/refactor positioning | Map to repository permissioning, license/security scans, benchmark tasks, generated-code provenance, dependency risk, patch review, and no-autonomous-merge boundary. |
| Support | Support vertical page, customer-support example, churn-predict cookbook, ticket routing copy | Map to customer-data redaction, sentiment/escalation safety, SLA impact, hallucination handling, escalation path, policy-compliant response generation, and A/B/rollback evidence. |

#### Completion Specification

| Branch | Done means |
|---|---|
| `49.1` Vertical catalog, supported industries, solution-package taxonomy, owner, maturity, and claim scope | A canonical vertical registry lists every vertical, recipe, artifact, case study, demo, standard, owner, maturity state, supported/not-supported claims, account path, docs path, marketplace path, and proof command. |
| `49.2` Healthcare, finance, legal, code, and support workflow specs, domain standards, fixtures, and eval requirements | Every domain workflow has standards crosswalk, version pins, fixture provenance, schema validator, benchmark/eval plan, expert-review state, and public limitations. |
| `49.3` Vertical artifacts, foundation students, recipes, marketplace listings, demos, docs, and account onboarding | Each vertical has a coherent first-value path from public page to docs to runnable recipe to account import to artifact status, with pending students and target scores clearly separated from verified artifacts. |
| `49.4` Regulated-domain safety, privacy, rights, compliance, human review, disclaimers, and customer-data boundaries | Regulated workflows require privacy class, rights status, human-review status, compliance scope, disclaimer, retention, audit export, and escalation path before they can be promoted. |
| `49.5` Per-vertical benchmark, K-Score, ROI, latency, cost, adoption, support, and production telemetry loops | Vertical metrics are real measured signals, not target copy: K-Score, task accuracy, precision/recall, latency, cost, error class, human override, adoption funnel, support burden, and ROI are tracked by vertical. |
| `49.6` Evidence: vertical registry, domain-standard crosswalk, recipe manifest, eval report, case proof, screenshots, and prod smoke | Release packet proves every vertical page, recipe, case, marketplace listing, API route, CLI command, account onboarding path, screenshot, and prod smoke exists and has current evidence. |
