| `W-VERT-011` | `49.3` | Add marketplace vertical proof. | Each foundation-student listing shows pending/verified state, artifact hash if available, K-Score evidence, eval date, runtime targets, and limitation labels. |
| `W-VERT-012` | `49.3` | Add CLI/API parity for verticals. | `kolm vertical list/show/fingerprint` and `/v1/verticals` routes return the same registry state and closeout gates as public/account surfaces. |
| `W-VERT-013` | `49.4` | Add regulated-use disclaimer registry. | Disclaimers are generated from domain, risk, human-review requirement, compliance scope, and external evidence, not hand-written per page. |
| `W-VERT-014` | `49.4` | Add customer-data boundary labels. | Every vertical recipe states whether data is synthetic, public, customer-owned, PHI/PII/PAN, attorney-client, or regulated financial data. |
| `W-VERT-015` | `49.4` | Add human-review and escalation workflows. | Healthcare, legal, finance, code, and support outputs include reviewer role, approval state, escalation trigger, and audit trail. |
| `W-VERT-016` | `49.5` | Add vertical benchmark reports. | Reports under `reports/verticals/<vertical>/` include task metrics, raw fixture IDs, model/provider versions, cost, latency, K-Score, and limitations. |
| `W-VERT-017` | `49.5` | Add vertical ROI model. | ROI pages separate synthetic examples, customer-proven cases, public benchmarks, and user-entered estimates by vertical. |
| `W-VERT-018` | `49.5` | Add vertical telemetry events. | Product analytics track vertical selection, demo start, recipe import, data connection, eval run, artifact compile, deploy, and support escalation. |
| `W-VERT-019` | `49.6` | Add screenshot and manual-use proof for vertical journeys. | Dark/light desktop/mobile screenshots and manual task notes cover every vertical page, docs recipe, account onboarding step, and marketplace listing. |
| `W-VERT-020` | `49.6` | Add production vertical smoke. | Deployed domain smoke verifies vertical pages, showcase manifest, recipe files, case studies, account entrypoints, API routes, structured data, and proof links. |

#### Node 49 Relax Redline

Node `49` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

1. Which verticals are supported, which are demo-only, which are research-only, and which are production-ready?
2. Which standards, implementation guides, measurement years, transaction sets, model-risk rules, ethics duties, or supervision requirements apply to each vertical workflow?
3. Which recipes have runnable artifacts, fixtures, validators, eval reports, human-review requirements, and account import paths?
4. Which K-Score, ROI, latency, cost, accuracy, safety, and adoption numbers are measured versus target-only?
5. Which regulated-domain claims are blocked by certification, customer data, human review, external standard adoption, or package release?
6. Which production smoke proves vertical pages, demos, docs, marketplace listings, account paths, API, CLI, and proof links are current on the deployed domain?

#### Node 49 Vertical Solution Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The active work belongs to node `49`, because the repository already has many vertical assets and the missing part is governance: one vertical contract that tells a buyer, operator, auditor, and implementation agent what is shipped, what is synthetic, what is pending, what standard applies, what proof exists, and what can be safely claimed.

Node `49` V2 turns verticals into product packages. A vertical page is not finished because it has a hero and a few recipes. It is finished when a domain buyer can inspect maturity, standards, fixtures, artifact status, human-review boundaries, privacy class, evals, screenshots, account onboarding, marketplace listing, API/CLI parity, and production smoke.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Artifact | Current observed state | V2 implication |
|---|---|---|
| `public/verticals` | Current vertical directory has 5 HTML pages: `code.html`, `finance.html`, `legal.html`, `medical.html`, and `support.html`. Titles currently frame these as "Frontier AI for..." pages and reference foundation students. | Public copy must distinguish shipped route/capture behavior, gated compile/distill behavior, verified artifacts, and target-only foundation students. |
| `public/docs/showcase` | Current showcase directory has 25 files, including 21 `.spec.json` recipes and supporting `manifest.json`, `receipt.json`, `credential.json`, and `phi-redactor.kolm`. | Showcase specs need a domain manifest with standards, fixture provenance, output schemas, eval commands, artifact state, and claim scope. |
| Healthcare and insurance specs | Current specs include claims adjudication, CMS Star, denial appeal, EDI 270/271, 278, 834, 835, 837, FHIR/USCDI, fraud detector, HEDIS BCS/CBP/CDC-HbA1c, ICD-10/CPT crosswalk, member support triage, MLR rebate, NPI directory, prior-auth letter/review, provider credentialing, and risk adjustment coder. | Healthcare/insurance is the deepest vertical and needs the most rigorous standards crosswalk, because it touches PHI, payer operations, prior authorization, transactions, and quality reporting. |
| `public/insurance/templates` | Current templates mirror the 21 showcase specs as HTML insurance templates. | Templates must render from the same registry as specs and cannot drift in naming, standards, maturity, or disclaimers. |
| Marketplace vertical assets | Current related marketplace pages include `claims-redactor.html`, `code-issue-classifier.html`, `kolm-code-7b.html`, `kolm-finance-7b.html`, `kolm-legal-7b.html`, `kolm-medical-7b.html`, `kolm-support-7b.html`, and `legal-clause-extractor.html`. | Marketplace listings need proof labels: verified artifact, sample artifact, pending foundation student, synthetic demo, or target-only. |
| Case studies and cookbooks | Current public assets include finance, healthcare PHI, legal contract case studies plus churn, finance disclosure, legal clause, and support reply cookbooks. | Case studies link to node `48`; recipes link to node `49`; both must share evidence class, maturity, and domain boundaries. |
| Tests | Current tests include vertical microsites, verticals, vertical disclaimer, EDI 837/835/834/270-271/278, FHIR/USCDI, HEDIS, health readiness, support bundle, and recipe bundle tests. | Tests prove parts exist; V2 must add registry-level coverage and journey/account/API/CLI parity. |
| Screenshot evidence | Current UI reports include vertical, case-study, cookbook, finance, healthcare, legal, and marketplace screenshots. | Screenshot evidence must be route/theme/viewport scoped and tied to vertical first-value journeys, not just page rendering. |

##### Current Domain Baseline V2

| Domain | Current source | Requirement imported into Kolm |
|---|---|---|
| Healthcare data exchange | HL7 FHIR overview describes FHIR as a health-care data exchange standard with versioned releases such as R5 and R4 ecosystem usage: `https://hl7.org/fhir/overview.html`. | Healthcare recipes must state FHIR version, resources, profile/IG assumptions, validation approach, unsupported resources, and whether output is exchange-ready or illustrative. |
| US prior authorization | CMS-0057-F says impacted payers generally have API development/enhancement compliance dates beginning January 1, 2027: `https://www.cms.gov/newsroom/fact-sheets/cms-interoperability-and-prior-authorization-final-rule-cms-0057-f`. | Prior-auth pages must distinguish operational support from formal payer API compliance and must name impacted-payer scope, timing, and human review. |
| Prior authorization implementation guide | Da Vinci PAS STU2 is based on FHIR R4 and has official version 2.0.1: `https://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-pas/STU2/`. | Prior-auth review and letter specs need PAS/CRD/DTR mapping or a visible "not full IG implementation" label. |
| Healthcare transactions | X12 health-care transaction flow covers transaction families including 270/271 eligibility, 278 services review, 834 enrollment, 835 payment/advice, and 837 claims: `https://x12.org/flow/health-care`. | EDI recipes must state transaction set, implementation-guide assumptions, loop/segment coverage, companion-guide limits, and rejection behavior. |
| HEDIS quality measures | NCQA HEDIS resources include MY 2026 measure resources and technical updates: `https://www.ncqa.org/hedis/measures/`. | HEDIS specs must pin measurement year, measure ID, value sets or source assumptions, denominator/numerator/exclusion logic, and official-reporting caveat. |
| HIPAA de-identification | HHS OCR guidance names Expert Determination and Safe Harbor methods for HIPAA de-identification: `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/special-topics/de-identification/index.html`. | PHI redaction and healthcare demo data must name whether they are synthetic, Safe Harbor-style, expert-determined, customer-controlled, or PHI-bearing. |
| Financial model risk | Federal Reserve SR 11-7 provides supervisory guidance for model risk management: `https://www.federalreserve.gov/bankinforeg/srletters/sr1107.htm`. | Finance verticals must show model inventory, validation, challenger comparison, monitoring, limitations, governance, independent review, and no-advice boundaries. |
| Broker-dealer AI obligations | FINRA AI guidance and Regulatory Notice 24-09 say AI use does not relieve member firms of existing regulatory obligations: `https://www.finra.org/sites/default/files/2024-06/regulatory-notice-24-09.pdf`. | Finance/support workflows for securities contexts need supervision, recordkeeping, customer communication, and escalation boundaries. |
| Legal ethics | ABA Formal Opinion 512 addresses lawyer use of generative AI and existing duties including competence, confidentiality, communication, supervision, candor, and fees: `https://www.americanbar.org/content/dam/aba/administrative/professional_responsibility/ethics-opinions/aba-formal-opinion-512.pdf`. | Legal vertical recipes need lawyer review, confidentiality controls, source checking, client communication cues, no-legal-advice labels, and privilege boundaries. |
| AI risk governance | NIST AI RMF 1.0 is a voluntary framework for organizations designing, developing, deploying, or using AI systems: `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework`. | Every regulated vertical should map risk, measurement, governance, monitoring, human review, and incident response to a reusable AI-risk control vocabulary. |
| LLM application security | OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications identifies critical LLM application vulnerabilities: `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/`. | Code, support, legal, finance, and healthcare workflows need prompt-injection, insecure output, data leakage, supply-chain, and excessive-agency controls. |

##### Vertical Registry Contract V2

Every vertical must be serializable as `kolm.vertical-solution.v2`.

```json
{
  "vertical_id": "healthcare-insurance",
  "display_name": "Healthcare and health insurance",
  "maturity": "concept|synthetic_demo|validated_fixture|customer_holdout|production|external_certified",
  "claim_scope": "internal|public_demo|public_beta|customer_validated|production_scoped",
  "owner": "vertical-solutions",
  "surfaces": {
    "public_pages": [],
    "docs_pages": [],
    "account_paths": [],
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_commands": [],
    "tui_views": [],
    "marketplace_pages": [],
    "case_studies": [],
    "cookbooks": []
  },
  "product_promises": {
    "route_capture": "shipped|implemented|partial|blocked",
    "compile_distill": "shipped|implemented|gated|blocked",
    "deploy_anywhere": "shipped|implemented|gated|blocked"
  },
  "standards": [],
  "recipes": [],
  "artifacts": [],
  "safety": {
    "data_classes": [],
    "human_review_required": true,
    "regulated_use_disclaimer": "required",
    "external_certification_required": false
  },
  "proof": {
    "tests": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "journey_packets": [],
    "eval_reports": [],
    "production_smoke": []
  },
  "open_gates": []
}
```

No vertical may be public without a registry record. No vertical may claim production readiness unless its registry state is `production` or stronger and every standard, recipe, artifact, account path, and production smoke proof is attached.

##### Recipe Manifest Contract V2

Every vertical recipe must be serializable as `kolm.vertical-recipe.v2`.

```json
{
  "recipe_id": "prior-auth-review",
  "vertical_id": "healthcare-insurance",
  "source_path": "public/docs/showcase/prior-auth-review.spec.json",
  "template_path": "public/insurance/templates/prior-auth-review.html",
  "artifact_path": null,
  "maturity": "synthetic_demo",
  "input_schema": "json-schema path or embedded schema",
  "output_schema": "json-schema path or embedded schema",
  "data_class": "synthetic|public|customer_owned|PHI|PII|financial|legal_confidential|source_code",
  "standards_crosswalk": [],
  "fixture": {
    "path": "test/fixtures/verticals/prior-auth-review.json",
    "provenance": "synthetic",
    "license": "internal demo"
  },
  "eval": {
    "path": "reports/verticals/healthcare/prior-auth-review.json",
    "required_metrics": ["precision", "recall", "schema_valid", "human_review"],
    "k_score_scope": "domain fixture only"
  },
  "human_review": {
    "required": true,
    "reviewer_role": "clinician|claims_ops|lawyer|model_risk|support_lead|security_reviewer",
    "escalation_triggers": []
  },
  "claim_scope": "synthetic fixture demo until customer holdout and domain expert eval exist"
}
```

Recipe rules:

- A recipe with no artifact is not a deployable vertical product.
- A recipe with synthetic fixtures cannot imply customer-proven quality.
- A regulated recipe requires a human-review role and explicit "do not use for" boundary.
- Every recipe must state whether it is demo-only, fixture-validated, customer-holdout validated, production-proven, or externally certified.
- Every recipe must map to account onboarding and support recovery if it is promoted beyond demo.

##### Vertical Maturity Ladder V2

| State | Meaning | Public copy allowed |
|---|---|---|
| `concept` | Idea, research note, or product plan only. | Roadmap only. |
| `synthetic_demo` | Demo page or recipe with synthetic fixtures. | "Demo" or "sample workflow"; no customer or compliance implication. |
| `validated_fixture` | Tests/evals pass on pinned fixtures. | "Fixture-validated"; include fixture scope and limits. |
| `customer_holdout` | Customer-owned or domain-expert holdout validated under permission. | "Validated on approved holdout"; include permission and scope. |
| `production` | Deployed or deployable with production smoke, account path, support, and rollback. | Production-scoped claims for the tested environment. |
| `external_certified` | Third-party certification, official conformance, or standards-body validation. | Certification claims only for exact certified scope. |

The public site must not collapse these states. A pending foundation student, a synthetic demo, and a production artifact are different products.

##### Domain Crosswalk V2

| Vertical | Required crosswalk |
|---|---|
| Healthcare and health insurance | FHIR version and resources, USCDI data classes, CMS-0057-F payer/API/timing scope, Da Vinci PAS/CRD/DTR relationship, X12 transaction sets and versions, HEDIS measurement year and measure logic, NPI/ICD-10/CPT dependencies, HIPAA/BAA/de-identification state, PHI redaction class coverage, clinical or operations review, and no-medical-advice boundary. |
| Finance | SR 11-7 model inventory, conceptual soundness, validation, monitoring, outcomes analysis, change management, challenger model, model-risk owner, FINRA/recordkeeping/supervision if securities-related, no-investment-advice boundary, retention, and audit export. |
| Legal | ABA Formal Opinion 512 duties, lawyer review, confidentiality, privilege boundary, citation/source checking, client communication, supervision, fee/cost disclosure, matter retention, unauthorized-practice boundary, and court/filing prohibition unless lawyer approved. |
| Code | Repository permission, license/security scan, dependency provenance, codegen test suite, benchmark task class, generated-code provenance, human code review, no-autonomous-merge boundary, secret redaction, and supply-chain/SBOM relationship. |
| Support | Customer-data class, escalation policy, sentiment/risk boundary, hallucination handling, SLA impact, policy-compliant response generation, human review for refunds/legal/safety/security, A/B test and rollback evidence, and support bundle safety. |

##### Vertical Account UX Contract V2

Each vertical must have a first-value account path:

1. Choose vertical and see maturity, data class, supported surfaces, and open gates.
2. Import a sample recipe or artifact.
3. Connect or mock the data source with privacy labels.
4. Run schema validation, redaction, and eval.
5. See K-Score/eval scope and human-review requirement.
6. Compile or mark blocked with exact missing proof.
7. Deploy or dry-run target where artifact exists.
8. Export proof packet for buyer, auditor, operator, or support.

UI requirements from the local UI/UX guidance:

- no hover-only critical controls;
- visible loading, empty, partial, error, blocked, and production states;
- 44px or larger primary touch targets;
- keyboard/focus-visible access for import, eval, compile, export, and support actions;
- no color-only maturity labels;
- mobile-friendly vertical onboarding flow;
- clear "demo", "fixture", "customer holdout", "production", and "external certified" badges.

##### Vertical Metrics V2

| Metric | Required interpretation |
|---|---|
| `vertical_activation_rate` | User reaches first vertical value from page to account import or artifact proof. |
| `recipe_eval_pass_rate` | Fixture or holdout pass rate by recipe and version. |
| `human_override_rate` | How often reviewers reject or modify model output. |
| `schema_error_rate` | Invalid input/output rate by recipe schema. |
| `redaction_recall` | PHI/PII/secret redaction recall by class for regulated workflows. |
| `k_score_by_vertical` | K-Score by domain pack with scope and date. |
| `time_to_first_artifact` | Time from vertical selection to artifact or blocked readiness reason. |
| `production_smoke_pass_rate` | Deployed vertical pages, recipes, account paths, APIs, and proof links pass rate. |
| `support_escalation_rate` | Support requests triggered by vertical workflows. |
| `claim_demotion_count` | Number of vertical claims demoted due to expired or insufficient evidence. |

##### Node `49` V2 Relax Redline

Node `49` V2 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every vertical has a registry record with maturity, claim scope, owner, surfaces, standards, recipes, artifacts, safety, proof, and open gates.
- Every showcase recipe has a manifest record with input/output schema, data class, fixture provenance, standard crosswalk, eval requirements, human-review role, artifact state, and claim scope.
- Healthcare, finance, legal, code, and support verticals each have a domain-specific standards/safety crosswalk tied to current official sources.
- Public pages, docs, account onboarding, marketplace listings, API/CLI/TUI, case studies, cookbooks, and screenshots render from or validate against the same vertical registry.
- Pending foundation students, synthetic demos, fixture-validated recipes, customer-holdout validation, production artifacts, and external certifications are visually and semantically distinct.
- Regulated workflows expose privacy class, customer-data boundary, human-review requirements, unsupported-use disclaimers, escalation, audit export, and support recovery.
- Production smoke proves the deployed vertical pages, recipes, templates, marketplace listings, account paths, API/CLI routes, proof links, and structured data are current.

The strict rule is: no vertical claim is finished unless the domain standard, data boundary, recipe proof, human-review requirement, account path, and production evidence are all visible and traceable.

#### Node 49 Vertical Proof Productization And Domain Workflow Addendum V3

Node `52` remains unused. This work belongs to node `49`, because the missing system is not "more vertical pages." The missing system is a vertical proof product: a buyer or operator must be able to choose a domain, inspect the supported standards, import a recipe, run or validate the artifact, see maturity and limits, route the workflow through account/API/CLI, and export a proof packet without guessing what is demo, fixture-validated, production-scoped, or externally certified.

Node `49` V3 upgrades V2 from a registry idea into a productization contract. Every vertical solution kit must be a first-class product surface with a standards crosswalk, recipe manifest, artifact passport, data-rights label, human-review policy, eval packet, account first-value flow, marketplace proof label, case-proof linkage, ROI evidence class, screenshot/manual-use packet, and production smoke. A landing page alone never completes a vertical. A synthetic recipe alone never completes a vertical. A target foundation-student page alone never completes a vertical.

##### Node 49 V3 Current Evidence Snapshot

| Evidence class | Current local state | V3 redline |
|---|---|---|
| Vertical landing pages | `public/verticals` currently has 5 pages and 75,991 bytes: code, finance, legal, medical, and support. | Each page needs a visible maturity badge, supported standards, recipe inventory, proof links, limitation state, and CTA that routes to the matching account or demo path. |
| Showcase specs | `public/docs/showcase` currently has 25 files, 21 `.spec.json` recipes, `manifest.json`, `receipt.json`, `credential.json`, and `phi-redactor.kolm`, totaling 110,915 bytes. | The manifest must become authoritative: each spec needs domain, standard, fixture, output schema, data class, artifact state, eval, reviewer role, and claim scope. |
| Insurance templates | `public/insurance/templates` currently has 21 HTML templates totaling 397,050 bytes. | Templates must be generated from or validated against the same recipe manifest as specs, with no name, standard, maturity, or disclaimer drift. |
| Cookbook pages | `public/cookbook` currently has 36 files totaling 476,644 bytes. | Cookbook recipes must distinguish runnable artifact, API example, synthetic fixture, pending student, and blocked production readiness. |
| Marketplace pages | `public/marketplace` currently has 18 pages totaling 262,200 bytes, including vertical foundation-student pages and regulated recipes. | Marketplace listings need artifact passports: hash/signature, license, model family, runtime, maturity, eval scope, data class, and limitations. |
| Case studies | `public/case-studies` currently has 4 pages totaling 53,099 bytes. | Case studies stay owned by node `48`, but node `49` must consume their evidence class so vertical pages do not cite unsupported outcomes. |
| Healthcare concentration | Healthcare/insurance has the deepest artifact footprint: X12, FHIR/USCDI, HEDIS, prior auth, claims, risk adjustment, NPI, credentialing, PHI redaction, MLR, member support, and denial workflows. | This is the largest near-term opportunity and the largest claim-risk area; it needs the strictest standards crosswalk and human-review policy. |
| UX proof | Existing screenshot reports prove route rendering, but not that a domain buyer can complete a vertical first-value task. | V3 requires task screenshots and manual-use packets: choose vertical, import recipe, validate data, run eval, compile or explain block, deploy/dry-run, export proof. |

##### Node 49 V3 Refreshed External Baseline

| Source | Current fact imported into the spec tree | Kolm product implication |
|---|---|---|
| HL7 FHIR overview, `https://hl7.org/fhir/overview.html` | FHIR is a healthcare information exchange standard; the current published overview is R5, while R4 remains a major implementation baseline. | Healthcare recipes must pin FHIR version, resources, profiles, implementation-guide assumptions, unsupported resources, and whether the output is exchange-ready or illustrative. |
| CMS-0057-F fact sheet, `https://www.cms.gov/newsroom/fact-sheets/cms-interoperability-prior-authorization-final-rule-cms-0057-f` | Impacted payers must implement operational provisions generally starting January 1, 2026, and API development/enhancement requirements generally starting January 1, 2027; required standards include FHIR R4.0.1, US Core STU 3.1.1, SMART 1.0.0, Bulk Data STU 1, OpenID Connect, and USCDI. | Prior-authorization copy must not imply full payer compliance unless those exact APIs, standards, timelines, and payer scopes are validated. |
| Da Vinci PAS STU2, `https://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-pas/STU2/` | PAS STU2 version 2.0.1 is a FHIR R4 implementation guide for prior authorization support. | Prior-auth review and letter workflows need PAS/CRD/DTR mapping or a visible "not full IG implementation" label. |
| X12 healthcare transaction flow, `https://x12.org/flow/health-care` | X12 healthcare flows include 270/271 eligibility, 278 service review, 834 enrollment, 835 payment/advice, 837 claims, and related transaction families. | Every EDI recipe must state transaction set, version or implementation-guide assumption, loop/segment coverage, companion-guide gaps, and rejection behavior. |
| NCQA HEDIS measures, `https://www.ncqa.org/hedis/measures/` | NCQA lists HEDIS MY 2026 resources and technical specifications; HEDIS is a widely used performance-improvement and health-plan reporting system. | HEDIS specs must pin measurement year, measure ID, value sets or source assumptions, denominator/numerator/exclusion logic, audit caveat, and official-reporting boundary. |
| HHS HIPAA de-identification guidance, `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/special-topics/de-identification/index.html` | HHS names Expert Determination and Safe Harbor as the two methods for HIPAA de-identification. | PHI redaction and healthcare demos must state synthetic, Safe Harbor-style, Expert Determination, customer-controlled PHI, or not de-identified. |
| Federal Reserve SR 11-7, `https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/srletters/sr1107.htm` | Model risk management requires disciplined development, implementation, use, validation, governance, controls, inventory, and effective challenge. | Finance verticals must ship model inventory, validation packet, limitations, monitoring, challenger comparison, owner, change management, and no-advice boundaries. |
| FINRA Regulatory Notice 24-09, `https://www.finra.org/rules-guidance/notices/24-09` | FINRA says member firms using GenAI remain subject to existing securities-law and FINRA obligations, including supervision and communications standards. | Securities-adjacent finance/support workflows need supervision, recordkeeping, content review, customer-communication limits, and escalation. |
| ABA Formal Opinion 512, `https://www.americanbar.org/content/dam/aba/administrative/professional_responsibility/ethics-opinions/aba-formal-opinion-512.pdf` | Lawyers using generative AI must consider duties including competence, confidentiality, communication, supervision, candor, meritorious claims, and reasonable fees. | Legal recipes need lawyer review, client-confidentiality controls, privilege boundary, source checking, court-filing prohibition unless approved, and no-legal-advice labels. |
| NIST AI RMF, `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | NIST AI RMF provides an AI risk-management vocabulary for organizations designing, developing, deploying, or using AI systems. | Every regulated vertical should map risk, governance, measurement, monitoring, incident handling, human oversight, and evidence export to a shared risk vocabulary. |
| OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications, `https://owasp.org/www-project-top-10-for-large-language-model-applications/` | OWASP highlights prompt injection, insecure output handling, poisoning, denial of service, supply chain, sensitive information disclosure, insecure plugin design, excessive agency, overreliance, and model theft. | Code, support, legal, finance, and healthcare workflows need prompt-injection, output-validation, poisoning, leakage, supply-chain, tool-permission, and overreliance controls. |

##### Vertical Proof Pack Contract V3

Every vertical must be serializable as `kolm.vertical-proof-pack.v3`.

```json
{
  "vertical_id": "healthcare-insurance",
  "domain": "healthcare",
  "maturity": "concept|synthetic_demo|validated_fixture|artifact_verified|domain_expert_reviewed|customer_holdout|production_scoped|external_certified",
  "claim_scope": "internal|public_demo|public_beta|customer_validated|production_scoped|externally_validated",
  "evidence_grade": "none|synthetic|fixture|artifact|holdout|production|external",
  "product_surfaces": {
    "route_capture": "shipped|implemented|partial|blocked",
    "compile_distill": "shipped|implemented|gated|blocked",
    "deploy_anywhere": "shipped|implemented|gated|blocked"
  },
  "assets": {
    "public_pages": [],
    "docs_specs": [],
    "insurance_templates": [],
    "cookbook_pages": [],
    "marketplace_pages": [],
    "case_studies": [],
    "account_paths": [],
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_commands": [],
    "tui_views": []
  },
  "standards": [
    {
      "id": "FHIR_R4_0_1",
      "official_url": "https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/",
      "coverage": "resources and profiles supported by this pack",
      "unsupported": "resources, profiles, operations, or IG requirements not implemented",
      "validator": "command or service",
      "evidence": "report path"
    }
  ],
  "recipes": [],
  "artifacts": [],
  "safety": {
    "data_rights": "synthetic|public|customer_owned|licensed|regulated",
    "data_classes": ["PHI", "PII"],
    "human_review": {
      "required": true,
      "role": "clinician|claims_ops|lawyer|model_risk|support_lead|security_reviewer",
      "approval_state": "draft|approved|blocked",
      "escalation_triggers": []
    },
    "regulated_use_disclaimer": "required",
    "unsupported_use_cases": [],
    "audit_export": "path or route"
  },
  "proof": {
    "tests": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_journey_packets": [],
    "eval_reports": [],
    "production_smoke": [],
    "case_proof": [],
    "roi_evidence": []
  },
  "open_gates": []
}
```

Every recipe inside a proof pack must include:

- `spec_path`, `template_path`, `artifact_path`, and `fixture_path`, with `null` allowed only when the maturity state explains why.
- `input_schema`, `output_schema`, `schema_validator`, `data_class`, `data_rights`, `standard_crosswalk`, `human_review`, `eval`, `k_score_scope`, `run_command`, `account_import_path`, and `claim_scope`.
- `blocked_reason` and `next_action` whenever an artifact, fixture, eval, standard validator, account path, or production smoke is missing.

##### Vertical Maturity Ladder V3

| State | Required proof | Public copy allowed |
|---|---|---|
| `concept` | Product plan only; no runnable recipe. | Roadmap, research, or preview only. |
| `synthetic_demo` | Synthetic fixture and page/demo render. | "Sample workflow" or "synthetic demo"; no customer or compliance implication. |
| `validated_fixture` | Fixture test, schema validation, eval scope, and limitations. | "Fixture-validated" with fixture date and scope. |
| `artifact_verified` | Signed or hash-stable artifact, run command, runtime target, and eval. | "Runnable artifact" for exact target and fixture scope. |
| `domain_expert_reviewed` | Named reviewer role, review date, acceptance criteria, and issue log. | "Domain-reviewed" within the reviewed workflow scope. |
| `customer_holdout` | Permissioned customer or expert holdout, anonymized proof, and current consent. | "Validated on approved holdout" with scope and limitations. |
| `production_scoped` | Deployed or deployable account path, production smoke, rollback, support, and proof export. | Production-scoped claims only for tested environment and route set. |
| `external_certified` | Third-party certification, conformance, or official validation artifact. | Certification claims only for the exact certified scope and date. |

No page, recipe, marketplace listing, account card, or sales packet may collapse these states. A pending foundation student, a synthetic insurance spec, a validated fixture, and a production artifact are four different product states.

##### Vertical UX And Operator Requirements V3

| Surface | Required product behavior |
|---|---|
| Vertical landing page | Show the category outcome in the first viewport, then display maturity, supported standards, recipe count, artifact count, proof links, open gates, limits, and one primary CTA that starts the matching account or demo flow. |
| Recipe page or template | Show inputs, outputs, sample fixture, data class, standard mapping, reviewer role, run/import action, eval score, limitations, and recovery path. |
| Account onboarding | Let the user choose a vertical, import a sample, connect or mock data, validate schema and redaction, run eval, compile or show the exact blocker, deploy or dry-run, and export proof. |
| Marketplace listing | Show artifact passport, hash/signature, model lineage, runtime support, license, data-rights class, maturity, K-Score scope, known limitations, and install/deploy action. |
| Case study linkage | Pull evidence class from node `48`; show whether the proof is modeled, internal dogfood, customer-approved, customer-anonymized, or externally audited. |
| Docs/API/CLI/TUI | `/v1/verticals`, `kolm vertical ...`, TUI, account, docs, and public pages must return or render the same registry state and open gates. |
| Visual and accessibility states | Use visible labels, not color alone; preserve focus-visible access; avoid hover-only critical controls; keep 44px primary targets; reserve space for async proof cards; show empty, loading, partial, blocked, error, and production states. |

##### Domain Productization Matrix V3

| Vertical | V3 productization requirements |
|---|---|
| Healthcare and health insurance | Map FHIR R4/R5, USCDI, CMS-0057-F, Da Vinci PAS/CRD/DTR, X12 270/271/278/834/835/837, HEDIS MY 2026, NPI, ICD-10, CPT, HIPAA de-identification, BAA scope, PHI redaction class coverage, clinical or operations review, and no-medical-advice boundaries. |
| Finance | Map SR 11-7 model inventory, conceptual soundness, implementation controls, independent validation, challenger comparisons, monitoring, materiality, owner, change management, FINRA supervision, recordkeeping, communication review, retention, and no-investment-advice boundaries. |
| Legal | Map ABA Formal Opinion 512 duties: competence, confidentiality, communication, supervision, candor, reasonable fees, source verification, lawyer approval, privilege boundaries, no legal advice, and no court filing without lawyer approval. |
| Code | Map repository permissions, secret redaction, dependency/license/security scans, generated-code provenance, accepted-edit telemetry, CI/test pass, code review, no autonomous merge, SBOM, and supply-chain controls. |
| Support | Map customer-data class, policy source, hallucination guard, sentiment/risk detection, refund/legal/security escalation, SLA impact, human handoff, A/B rollback, and support bundle safety. |

##### Vertical Metrics V3

| Metric | Required instrumentation |
|---|---|
| `vertical_first_value_rate` | From public vertical page or docs page to account import, runnable artifact, or proof export. |
| `recipe_manifest_coverage` | Percent of recipes with spec, template, fixture, schema, artifact state, data class, standard crosswalk, eval, human review, and claim scope. |
| `standard_crosswalk_coverage` | Percent of vertical standards with version, official URL, coverage, unsupported items, validator, and evidence. |
| `artifact_verified_rate` | Percent of marketplace and recipe artifacts with hash/signature, runtime target, eval, and run command. |
| `human_review_override_rate` | Reviewer reject or edit rate by recipe, reviewer role, and version. |
| `regulated_blocker_resolution_time` | Time from blocked regulated-use state to proof, demotion, or explicit external gate. |
| `vertical_k_score_freshness` | Age and scope of K-Score/eval evidence per vertical and recipe. |
| `claim_demotion_count` | Number of public/account/docs claims demoted because evidence expired, failed, or lost permission. |
| `production_vertical_smoke_pass_rate` | Deployed public, docs, account, API, CLI, marketplace, and proof routes passing per vertical. |
| `domain_support_recovery_rate` | Support tickets resolved by proof packet, recipe diagnostics, escalation, or rollback. |

##### Node `49` V3 Relax Redline

Node `49` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- The master tree still routes all vertical, recipe, standards, regulated workflow, account onboarding, marketplace proof, and production smoke work to node `49`.
- Every vertical has a `kolm.vertical-proof-pack.v3` record with maturity, claim scope, evidence grade, product surfaces, assets, standards, recipes, artifacts, safety, proof, metrics, and open gates.
- Every recipe has spec, template, fixture, schema, artifact state, data class, data rights, standard crosswalk, eval, K-Score scope, human-review role, run/import command, account path, claim scope, and blocker/next action where incomplete.
- Healthcare/insurance standards are mapped with version/date, official URL, coverage, unsupported requirements, validator, and evidence for FHIR, USCDI, CMS-0057-F, PAS, X12, HEDIS, NPI, ICD-10, CPT, HIPAA, BAA, and PHI redaction.
- Finance, legal, code, and support each have a domain-specific proof packet aligned to their governing obligations and operational risks.
- Public pages, docs, account onboarding, marketplace listings, API, CLI, TUI, cookbook pages, insurance templates, case studies, screenshots, and production smoke validate against the same registry.
- Pending foundation students, synthetic demos, fixture-validated recipes, artifact-verified workflows, customer holdouts, production-scoped flows, and external certifications are visually and semantically distinct.
- Case studies and ROI claims consume node `48` evidence classes and never overstate modeled or internal proof as customer-approved or audited.
- Production evidence includes deployed route smoke, account journey proof, API/CLI parity, visual QA, accessibility states, structured data, support recovery, and proof export.
- No regulated vertical ships without visible data class, human-review role, unsupported-use boundary, escalation path, audit/export path, and external-gate state.

The V3 rule is stricter than V2: Kolm can say "vertical solution" only when the product behaves like a vertical solution, not when the marketing site has a vertical landing page.

#### Node 49 V4 Regulated Vertical Proof Rooms, Domain Validator Packs, And First-Value Acceptance

Node `52` remains unused. This work still belongs to node `49`. V4 closes the next gap: V3 defines proof packs, but regulated verticals also need proof rooms. A proof room is the product space where a domain buyer can see the exact standard version, fixture lineage, validator result, human-review role, artifact status, unsupported-use boundary, claim scope, and production path without reading internal docs or trusting a sales narrative.

The rule is strict: no healthcare, finance, legal, code, or support vertical is "complete" because its page exists, its template renders, or its demo fixture passes. It is complete only when a skeptical domain operator can complete a first-value task and export a proof room that shows what was run, what standard was used, what is synthetic, what is blocked, what requires human review, and what can be safely claimed.

##### Node 49 V4 Current Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observed state | V4 implication |
|---|---|---|
| Showcase specs | `public/docs/showcase` currently exposes 21 `.spec.json` recipe files plus `manifest.json`, `receipt.json`, `credential.json`, and `phi-redactor.kolm`. | The proof room must treat the showcase manifest as a typed registry, not as optional docs collateral. |
| Mirrored insurance templates | `public/insurance/templates` currently exposes 21 HTML templates matching the 21 showcase recipe names. | Spec-to-template parity is release-critical: title, ID, standard, data class, maturity, caveat, fixture, and CTA cannot drift. |
| Healthcare depth | The current vertical set includes claims adjudication, CMS Stars, denials, EDI 270/271, 278, 834, 835, 837, FHIR/USCDI, fraud, HEDIS, ICD-10/CPT, MLR, NPI, prior auth, credentialing, and risk adjustment. | Healthcare/insurance needs a domain validator pack first, because it carries the deepest standards surface and the highest PHI/compliance claim risk. |
| Case-study dependency | Finance, healthcare, and legal case studies exist, but node `48` owns buyer proof and permission state. | Node `49` must consume case evidence class; it must not infer customer validation from a vertical page or template alone. |
| Manual-use gap | Existing screenshot reports can prove route rendering, but not domain first value. | Each vertical needs a no-hint task: choose workflow, inspect standard, run fixture, validate output, identify human-review boundary, and export proof. |
| Production gap | Local assets can be inspected, but production proof is not implied. | Vertical public pages, templates, API/account paths, proof exports, and smoke results must be verified on the deployed commit before production-scoped claims. |

##### Node 49 V4 Current Domain Baseline

| Domain source | Current fact imported into the tree | Kolm product requirement |
|---|---|---|
| HL7 FHIR R4, `https://hl7.org/fhir/R4/` | FHIR R4 v4.0.1 is a permanent published specification for healthcare data exchange, and HL7 identifies a newer R5 version. | Every FHIR recipe must pin R4, R4B, R5, or implementation-guide scope; "FHIR-ready" is forbidden without version and profile coverage. |
| CMS interoperability FAQ, `https://www.cms.gov/priorities/burden-reduction/overview/interoperability/frequently-asked-questions/general` | CMS states CMS-0057-F requires impacted payer categories to implement three new FHIR APIs and expands Patient Access API requirements, with payer-scope and data-maintained limits. | Prior-authorization and payer API copy must state payer scope, API class, excluded drug scope where relevant, data availability limits, and whether Kolm is demoing, validating, or implementing the API. |
| NCQA HEDIS MY 2026 update, `https://www.ncqa.org/blog/hedis-my-2026-whats-new-whats-changed-whats-retired/` | NCQA says MY 2026 updates include a new measure format, seven added measures, two retired measures, four transitions to ECDS, and alignment with FHIR data standards. | HEDIS recipes must pin measurement year, measure version, ECDS status, denominator/numerator/exclusion logic, value-set source, and official-reporting caveat. |
| HHS HIPAA de-identification guidance, `https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/special-topics/de-identification/index.html` | HHS identifies Expert Determination and Safe Harbor as the two HIPAA de-identification methods. | PHI redaction, healthcare demos, and case studies must label synthetic, Safe Harbor-style, expert-determined, customer-controlled, or not de-identified. |
| Federal Reserve SR 11-7, `https://www.federalreserve.gov/supervisionreg/srletters/sr1107.htm` | SR 11-7 requires attention to incorrect or misused models through model development, implementation, validation, governance, policies, controls, and effective model-risk management. | Finance vertical proof rooms must include model inventory, owner, intended use, validation method, challenger comparison, monitoring, limitation, change control, and no-investment-advice boundary. |
| FINRA Regulatory Notice 24-09, `https://www.finra.org/rules-guidance/notices/24-09` | FINRA says the notice does not create new legal requirements and does not relieve firms of existing federal securities law and FINRA obligations. | Finance/support workflows must not claim AI-specific regulatory approval; they must show supervision, records, communications review, escalation, and existing-obligation mapping. |
| ABA Formal Opinion 512, `https://www.americanbar.org/news/abanews/aba-news-archives/2024/07/aba-issues-first-ethics-guidance-ai-tools/` | ABA says generative AI use by lawyers implicates duties including competence, confidentiality, communication, and reasonable fees. | Legal vertical proof rooms must require lawyer review, client-confidentiality boundaries, source verification, fee/time caveats, no-legal-advice copy, and no filing without lawyer approval. |
| NIST AI RMF, `https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework` | NIST AI RMF is voluntary guidance for trustworthiness considerations in AI design, development, use, and evaluation; NIST also released a Generative AI Profile and a 2026 critical-infrastructure concept note. | Every regulated vertical must expose risk controls, measurement, monitoring, human oversight, incident response, and evidence export in a common vocabulary. |

##### V4 Vertical Proof Room Contract

Every vertical proof room must be serializable as `kolm.vertical-proof-room.v4`.

```json
{
  "proof_room_id": "healthcare-insurance-prior-auth",
  "vertical_id": "healthcare-insurance",
  "primary_node": "49",
  "secondary_nodes": ["18", "23", "24", "42", "48"],
  "maturity": "synthetic_demo|validated_fixture|artifact_verified|domain_expert_reviewed|customer_holdout|production_scoped|external_certified",
  "claim_scope": "internal|public_demo|public_beta|customer_validated|production_scoped|externally_validated",
  "standards": [
    {
      "id": "CMS_0057_F",
      "version_or_date": "2024-01-17",
      "official_url": "https://www.cms.gov/priorities/burden-reduction/overview/interoperability/frequently-asked-questions/general",
      "coverage_state": "mapped|validated|partial|not_supported",
      "unsupported_items": [],
      "validator": "command-or-manual-review",
      "evidence_path": "reports/vertical-proof/<date>/cms-0057-f.json"
    }
  ],
  "assets": {
    "public_page": "/health-insurance",
    "template_page": "/insurance/templates/prior-auth-review",
    "spec_path": "public/docs/showcase/prior-auth-review.spec.json",
    "artifact_path": null,
    "fixture_paths": [],
    "account_path": null,
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_commands": []
  },
  "data": {
    "class": ["PHI", "PII"],
    "source": "synthetic|public|customer_owned|licensed|regulated",
    "deidentification": "synthetic|safe_harbor_style|expert_determination|customer_controlled|not_deidentified",
    "retention": "policy-id",
    "rights": "policy-id"
  },
  "human_review": {
    "required": true,
    "role": "clinician|claims_ops|model_risk|lawyer|support_lead|security_reviewer",
    "approval_required_before": ["customer_use", "production_claim", "external_export"],
    "escalation_triggers": []
  },
  "first_value_task": {
    "instruction": "Validate one prior-authorization fixture against the proof room.",
    "success": "User can identify standard version, fixture lineage, validator result, human-review boundary, and next action.",
    "max_time_seconds": 300,
    "evidence_required": ["screenshot", "validator_log", "proof_export"]
  },
  "proof": {
    "validator_reports": [],
    "eval_reports": [],
    "k_score_reports": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_use_packets": [],
    "production_smoke": [],
    "case_evidence": []
  },
  "demotion_rules": [
    "expired_standard",
    "missing_validator",
    "missing_human_review",
    "unsupported_production_claim",
    "case_permission_missing"
  ]
}
```

##### V4 Domain Validator Pack Requirements

| Vertical | Validator pack required before production-scoped copy |
|---|---|
| Healthcare and insurance | FHIR resource/profile validation, CMS-0057-F scope matrix, USCDI mapping, X12 transaction/loop/segment coverage, HEDIS MY/version logic, HIPAA de-identification label, NPI/ICD/CPT source version, PHI redaction class report, BAA/data-flow state, and operations-review approval. |
| Finance | SR 11-7 model-risk record, inventory entry, intended-use statement, independent validation plan, challenger/eval report, monitoring thresholds, record-retention policy, supervision and communications-review state, conflict/no-advice boundary, and rollback/escalation path. |
| Legal | ABA Opinion 512 ethics checklist, lawyer-review state, confidentiality/privilege boundary, source citation verification, court-filing prohibition unless approved, client-communication rule, reasonable-fee caveat, and no-legal-advice label. |
| Code | Repository permission boundary, secret/PII redaction, dependency/license/security scan, generated-code provenance, accepted-edit telemetry, CI/test pass, reviewer approval, no-autonomous-merge rule, SBOM, and rollback patch. |
| Support | Customer-data class, policy source, hallucination guard, escalation classifier, refund/legal/security handoff, SLA impact, conversation-retention rule, human override, A/B rollback, and customer-safe proof bundle. |

##### V4 First-Value Acceptance Scripts

Every vertical must pass a no-hint acceptance task before its page can claim more than `public_demo`.

| Script | Persona | Acceptance |
|---|---|---|
| `VERT-V4-T01` | Healthcare operations buyer | From a healthcare page, find one prior-auth or claim workflow, identify the standard version, run or inspect a fixture, see PHI/data class, and export proof. |
| `VERT-V4-T02` | Health plan compliance reviewer | Determine whether a CMS-0057-F, HEDIS, FHIR, X12, or HIPAA claim is implemented, partial, synthetic, externally gated, or unsupported. |
| `VERT-V4-T03` | Finance model-risk reviewer | Open a finance proof room and find intended use, validation, challenger, monitoring, owner, limitations, record retention, and no-advice boundary. |
| `VERT-V4-T04` | Legal reviewer | Open a legal workflow and find lawyer-review requirement, confidentiality boundary, source-checking step, no-filing boundary, and client-communication caveat. |
| `VERT-V4-T05` | Engineering lead | Open a code vertical workflow and see repo permission boundary, generated-code provenance, secret scan, tests, reviewer approval, and rollback. |
| `VERT-V4-T06` | Support operations lead | Open support workflow and see policy source, escalation, hallucination guard, SLA impact, human handoff, and recovery path. |
| `VERT-V4-T07` | Account user | Choose a vertical, import the matching sample, validate schema, run eval, compile or see blocker, dry-run deploy, and export proof. |
| `VERT-V4-T08` | Sales engineer | Generate a vertical proof packet that includes maturity, standards, evidence, open gates, buyer proof, ROI caveat, and competitor/alternative fit. |
| `VERT-V4-T09` | Auditor | Sample a vertical claim and trace it to standard source, recipe, fixture, validator, artifact, human review, screenshot, production state, and open gate. |
| `VERT-V4-T10` | Production operator | Confirm deployed vertical pages, templates, account paths, API/CLI surfaces, proof exports, links, structured data, and stale/demotion states. |

##### V4 Claim Demotion Rules

Vertical claims must demote automatically when proof weakens:

- `production_scoped` demotes to `artifact_verified` if deployed smoke, account path, support path, or rollback proof expires.
- `artifact_verified` demotes to `validated_fixture` if artifact hash/signature, runtime target, eval, or run command is missing.
- `validated_fixture` demotes to `synthetic_demo` if schema validator, fixture provenance, or standard crosswalk is missing.
- `domain_expert_reviewed` demotes to the underlying technical state if review date, role, criteria, or issue log is missing.
- `customer_holdout` demotes if permission, anonymization, typicality, or current consent is missing.
- `external_certified` demotes immediately if certification scope, date, auditor, or official validation URL is absent or expired.
- Any claim using regulated language such as compliance, audit-ready, payer-ready, filing-ready, supervision-ready, or production-ready must show the exact proof room and state.

##### Node `49` V4 Relax Redline

Node `49` V4 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every active vertical has at least one proof room record.
- Every proof room pins standard version/date, official URL, coverage, unsupported items, validator, data class, human-review role, maturity, claim scope, and proof.
- The 21 showcase specs and 21 mirrored insurance templates pass parity checks.
- Healthcare, finance, legal, code, and support each have a domain validator pack.
- No-hint first-value scripts pass for public, account, docs, API/CLI, proof export, and production operator flows.
- Claims demote when proof, permission, standard freshness, validator output, human review, or production smoke is missing.
- Production vertical smoke proves deployed public pages, templates, docs, account paths, API/CLI surfaces, marketplace links, structured data, stale states, and proof exports.
- A domain reviewer can sample any vertical claim and trace it without asking an agent.

Until those are true, Kolm has valuable vertical assets, but the regulated vertical product is not finished.

### Developer Coding Assistant, Code Completion, IDE Integration, MCP Tooling, And Build-Finishing Workflows Ledger V1

Node `50` owns Kolm as a developer productivity product. This is separate from the general SDK/package ecosystem. The user explicitly called out code completion and finishing the build, and the repo already has the beginnings of that surface: an OpenAI-compatible completions bridge, local MCP server, VS Code extension package, passive coding monitor, codegraph builder, natural-language account assistant, builder pages, agent docs, agentic-coding pages, MCP installer, and W851 CLI/TUI 100x plan. The completion gap is that these pieces are scattered and do not yet form a world-class coding-assistant product with measured completion quality, safe tool use, IDE install proof, build-finishing workflows, enterprise controls, and a clear buyer/user story.

The product opportunity is concrete: Kolm should let an engineering team route ordinary coding-agent and IDE traffic through Kolm, capture repeated accepted patterns, compile those patterns into owned `.kolm` artifacts, route low-risk repetitions locally, and help finish builds through evidence-backed codegraph, tests, screenshots, and release redlines. That is not "just autocomplete." It is owned intelligence for software teams.

#### Current Developer-Assistant Evidence

| Evidence class | Current repo evidence | Completion gap |
|---|---|---|
| OpenAI-compatible local bridge | `scripts/completions-server.mjs` exposes `GET /healthz`, `GET /v1/models`, and `POST /v1/chat/completions`; `src/completions-api.js` supports `kolm:`, `kolm-path:`, `anthropic:`, `openai:`, `openrouter:`, and `gemini:` model selectors with provenance fields. | This is strong adapter infrastructure, but it is framed as chat completions, not full IDE/code-completion quality. It needs a code-completion contract, latency budget, prefix/suffix handling, streaming behavior, fallback policy, and safe local-only defaults. |
| VS Code extension | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json` and `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/src/extension.ts` implement a W819 passive monitor, capture queue, cluster detection, status bar, distill dialog, and post-distill routing toggle. | The extension is beta, not clearly published, uses W819 labels in user-facing descriptions, and needs Marketplace package proof, extension-host tests, privacy consent UX, official API event validation, screenshots, and end-to-end IDE smoke. |
| MCP server | `services/mcp/server.js`, `sdk/mcp/README.md`, and `scripts/install-mcp.cjs` expose `.kolm` artifacts as tools and install MCP configs for Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, and Windsurf. | The service currently advertises an older protocol version string and needs current MCP version strategy, tool permission annotations, authorization/sandboxing posture, audit logging, consent UX, and negative security tests. |
| Natural-language command assistant | `src/assistant.js` maps free text to status, usage, list, compile, job_status, run, tune, install, upgrade, help, and doctor with deterministic rules. | This is useful, but limited. It needs the W851 catalog/routing plan: every command reachable by natural language, confidence scoring, disambiguation, copy framing, command preview, dry-run, and recovery suggestions. |
| Codegraph | `src/repo-codegraph.js` and `scripts/build-codegraph.mjs` index files, imports, symbols, routes, package scripts, and readiness evidence. | The codegraph should become the build-finishing substrate: issue-to-spec, owned paths, dependency blast radius, test selection, stale generated artifacts, route/page/file ownership, and agent handoff. |
| Agent/developer docs | `public/docs/dev-agents.html`, `public/docs/agent-guide.html`, `public/docs/cli/agent.html`, `public/docs/vscode.html`, `public/install/vscode.html`, `public/integrations/vscode.html`, `public/use-cases/agentic-coding.html`, and `KOLM_W851_CLI_TUI_100X_PLAN.md`. | Docs exist, but they need one information architecture: install, connect IDE/agent, route first completion, see captures, compile repeated pattern, route local artifact, verify savings, finish build. |
| Build-finishing workflows | `public/builder.html`, `public/cookbook/feature-spec-from-issue.html`, `public/marketplace/code-issue-classifier.html`, `scripts/e2e-walk.mjs`, release/verify scripts, and audit screenshots. | The build-finishing product path needs task scripts, artifact generation, code review packet, test generation, screenshot proof, rollback plan, and "finished build redline" automation. |
| Known polish debt | Several developer-assistant files contain mojibake markers in comments or docs, and W851 explicitly says execution paused. | A state-of-the-art developer product cannot ship corrupted comments, stale wave labels, or paused plan copy as the final user-facing contract. |

#### External Developer-Tool Baseline

| Source | Current standard or guidance | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| Language Server Protocol official page, `https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/` | LSP standardizes IDE/editor language features such as autocomplete, go to definition, find references, and hover through JSON-RPC, with latest spec version 3.17. | If Kolm claims IDE-native code completion, it should either implement LSP-compatible completion/hover/reference features or clearly scope itself as an extension/completions bridge, not a language server. |
| VS Code Extension API language features, `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/language-extensions/overview` | VS Code exposes APIs for rich language features including `registerCompletionItemProvider`. | The VS Code package should use supported extension APIs, define activation/commands/configuration cleanly, and verify completion-provider behavior in extension-host tests or honest beta scope. |
| Model Context Protocol specification, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/index` and current version page `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification` | MCP defines a JSON-RPC protocol for resources, prompts, tools, sampling, progress, cancellation, logging, and explicit user consent/control. The version page lists date-based protocol revisions. | Kolm MCP tools must declare protocol version strategy, tool schemas, output schemas, consent, authorization, progress/error semantics, and version compatibility instead of hardcoding a stale protocol promise. |
| MCP tools and security best practices, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/server/tools` and `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/security_best_practices` | MCP tools are model-controlled; guidance calls for human-in-the-loop, clear tool exposure UI, confirmation prompts, sandboxing, restricted local transports, and least-privilege scopes. | Kolm's MCP and build-finishing tools must not silently mutate repositories or exfiltrate code. Tool calls need consent, scope, audit, sandbox, dry-run, and revocation. |
| OpenAI Codex docs and product page, `https://platform.openai.com/docs/codex`, `https://openai.com/codex/`, and `https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/code-generation` | Modern coding agents read, modify, and run code; they complete tasks end to end across CLI/IDE/cloud workflows and benefit from clear repository instructions and tests. | Kolm must differentiate as the capture/compile/route/prove layer for coding agents: not another agent shell, but a way to turn repeated engineering behavior into owned artifacts with evidence and savings. |
| GitHub Copilot coding agent docs, `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/coding-agent/coding-agent` | Copilot coding agent works through issues/prompts and pull requests in a GitHub Actions-powered environment. | Kolm build-finishing workflows need PR/issue integration, CI command discovery, artifact evidence, and change-review packets if they compete in agentic engineering. |
| Continue customization docs, `https://docs.continue.dev/customize/overview` and config reference `https://docs.continue.dev/reference` | Continue supports configurable providers, context, prompts, and OpenAI-compatible model configuration. | Kolm's OpenAI-compatible endpoint should ship tested Continue/Cursor/VS Code configuration recipes with local/privacy labels and failure-mode docs. |
| Cursor MCP docs, `https://docs.cursor.com/context/model-context-protocol` | Cursor can connect to external tools and data sources through MCP. | Kolm MCP install should include Cursor-specific verification, tool names, consent warnings, screenshots, and rollback/uninstall. |

#### Coding-Assistant Product Contract

Every developer-assistant integration should share one product contract. The same contract should drive docs, CLI/TUI, account UI, extension config, MCP manifests, and release gates.

```json
{
  "assistant_surface": "ide_completion",
  "master_node": "50",
  "hosts": ["vscode", "cursor", "continue", "claude-code", "codex-cli", "mcp-client"],
  "modes": {
    "observe": "capture accepted suggestions only after consent",
    "route": "send request to teacher, local artifact, or fallback chain",
    "compile": "distill repeated accepted patterns into signed artifact",
    "finish_build": "generate implementation packet and verification proof"
  },
  "quality_metrics": {
    "acceptance_rate": "measured per language and task class",
    "latency_p95_ms": "budgeted separately for inline completion and chat",
    "edit_survival_rate": "accepted code still present after review",
    "test_pass_delta": "tests passing before and after suggested change",
    "cost_saved": "teacher calls avoided by local artifact routing"
  },
  "privacy": {
    "default": "local_only",
    "captures": "accepted snippets only unless user expands scope",
    "secrets": "redacted before capture and never logged",
    "upload": "explicit opt-in with preview"
  },
  "proof": {
    "tests": ["extension-host smoke", "MCP protocol tests", "completions endpoint tests"],
    "screenshots": ["IDE status bar", "distill dialog", "route proof", "account capture"],
    "release": ["package tarball", "Marketplace proof or local-source-only label"],
    "security": ["sandbox", "consent", "scope", "audit log", "no secret leakage"]
  }
}
```

#### Completion Quality Requirements

| Capability | Completion definition |
|---|---|
| Inline code completion | Supports prefix/suffix context, language ID, project path scope, max latency, cancellation, streaming or partial result, deterministic fallback, telemetry-off mode, and exact proof of what code was captured. |
| Chat completion for coding agents | OpenAI-compatible endpoint works with common clients, preserves provenance fields, supports model fallback receipts, handles auth safely, and has redacted request/response logging. |
| Accepted-suggestion capture | Captures only user-accepted suggestions after visible consent, stores local first, redacts secrets, tracks source extension/agent, language, task class, timestamp, and user revocation. |
| Repetition clustering | Shows which accepted patterns are repeated enough to distill, with examples, privacy class, expected cost saved, false-positive review, and manual exclude. |
| Distill-to-local routing | Compiles repeated patterns into signed `.kolm` artifacts, runs holdout eval, shows K-Score scope, and routes only matching low-risk requests with fallback to teacher. |
| Build-finishing agent | Converts issue/spec into owned file plan, edits, tests, screenshots, release packet, rollback notes, and traceability, without silent broad rewrites. |
| Account visibility | Account UI shows IDE captures, clusters, artifacts, savings, privacy state, extension installs, MCP tools, build redlines, and next actions. |

#### Security And Enterprise Controls

| Risk | Required control |
|---|---|
| Code leakage | Default local-only capture, preview before upload, secret redaction, repository allow/deny lists, and per-workspace retention. |
| Prompt injection through code/docs | Treat repository content, MCP tool annotations, and extension metadata as untrusted; add source labels, sandboxed tool calls, and policy checks. |
| Silent repository mutation | Dry-run first, patch preview, explicit approval, write-set declaration, branch/worktree isolation, and rollback record. |
| Overbroad tool permissions | Least-privilege MCP scopes, progressive elevation, tool-specific consent, and audit log per tool call. |
| Supply-chain risk | Published package provenance, extension signing/verification, dependency review, SBOM, package-channel status, and install/uninstall instructions. |
| Enterprise governance | Admin policy for capture scope, allowed providers, local artifacts, upload opt-in, audit export, retention, SSO/SCIM linkage, and compliance packet. |

#### Completion Specification

| Branch | Done means |
|---|---|
| `50.1` Coding-assistant product model, supported IDEs/agents, OpenAI-compatible bridge, MCP tools, and capability taxonomy | A canonical developer-assistant registry lists every supported host, mode, endpoint, MCP tool, extension feature, package status, claim scope, owner, and proof command. |
| `50.2` Code completion quality, latency, acceptance, context, privacy, routing, artifact fallback, and local/student proof | Inline/chat completion quality is measured with acceptance, latency, edit survival, test pass, cost saved, fallback, and privacy metrics by language/task class. |
| `50.3` IDE, VS Code, Cursor, Continue, Claude Code, Codex, MCP, LSP, CLI/TUI, and builder integration contracts | Each integration has install docs, config examples, smoke tests, screenshots, uninstall/rollback, version compatibility, and scope labels. |
| `50.4` Repo codegraph, issue-to-spec, build-finishing agents, review loops, test generation, rollback, and evidence packets | Build-finishing workflows turn issues into specs, write-set plans, patches, tests, screenshots, release evidence, and rollback notes tied to the master spec tree. |
| `50.5` Security, consent, sandboxing, tool permissions, prompt/code leakage, supply-chain risk, and enterprise controls | Security model covers consent, local-only defaults, secret redaction, sandboxing, MCP scopes, audit logs, extension/package provenance, and admin policy. |
| `50.6` Evidence: completion evals, IDE screenshots, MCP protocol tests, extension package proof, build-redline runs, and prod/dev smoke | Release evidence includes eval reports, extension-host smoke, MCP protocol compatibility, package proof, route/account/docs parity, screenshots, and production/dev smoke. |

#### Node 50 Relax Redline

Node `50` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

1. Which coding-agent and IDE hosts are supported, beta-only, local-source-only, or not supported?
2. Does Kolm provide chat completions, inline code completions, MCP tools, LSP features, build-finishing agents, or some subset, and is the distinction visible?
3. Which eval proves code completion quality, latency, acceptance, edit survival, cost saved, and test impact?
4. Which consent, privacy, redaction, local-only, upload, sandbox, and tool-permission controls prevent code leakage and silent mutation?
5. Which artifacts prove VS Code package, MCP server, OpenAI-compatible bridge, Continue/Cursor configs, CLI/TUI, account UI, and docs all work together?
6. Which build-finishing workflow proves Kolm can take an issue/spec to a verified build redline without corrupting unrelated files or hiding external gates?

#### Node 50 Developer-Assistant Operating System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. The current user emphasis on code completion, finishing the build, MCP, IDE integrations, and "actually use it" belongs to node `50`, with cross-links to node `43` for codebase completion, node `17` for ecosystem adoption, node `37` for API compatibility, node `39` for diagnostics, and node `40` for deterministic QA. V2 tightens node `50` from a promising developer-tool surface into a buildable operating system.

The central distinction is important: Kolm should not market this branch as "an autocomplete extension" until it proves inline completion. The current code proves a strong OpenAI-compatible chat-completions bridge, a local MCP artifact runner, a VS Code passive-monitor/distill flow, MCP installers, codegraph infrastructure, and coding-agent docs. The state-of-the-art target is broader: capture accepted coding behavior, distill repeated project patterns into owned `.kolm` artifacts, route safe repetition locally, and help teams finish builds through codegraph-backed plans, tests, screenshots, release evidence, and rollback.

##### Current Repo Evidence Rechecked For V2

| Artifact | Current observed state | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| `src/completions-api.js` | `27,218` bytes, `672` lines, no mojibake, supports `kolm:`, `kolm-path:`, `anthropic:`, `openai:`, `openrouter:`, and `gemini:` selectors through an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions shape. | This is a credible coding-agent bridge, but it is chat-completions shaped. Inline completion still needs prefix, suffix, language ID, file path, cancellation, debounce, latency budget, and edit-survival evidence. |
| `scripts/completions-server.mjs` | `7,257` bytes, `205` lines, serves `GET /healthz`, `GET /v1/models`, and `POST /v1/chat/completions`, with optional bearer auth and a `4 MiB` body cap. | Local developer use is plausible. Final product needs install recipes for Continue/Cursor/Codex/OpenAI SDK clients, safe auth defaults, CORS policy review, local-only labeling, and smoke evidence per client. |
| `services/mcp/server.js` | `12,616` bytes, `339` lines, exposes `.kolm` artifacts as MCP tools over stdio or HTTP and records local run logs. It currently declares `PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2024-11-05'`. | MCP is real, but the current MCP spec page redirects to `2025-11-25`; protocol compatibility must be checked, versioned, and surfaced. Tool schemas, annotations, consent, roots, progress, cancellation, and security posture must be explicit. |
| `scripts/install-mcp.cjs` | `11,299` bytes, `299` lines, supports Cursor, Continue.dev, Claude Desktop, VS Code, and Windsurf configs with dry-run, conflict refusal, atomic write, and force. | Installer behavior is product-grade locally. Final completion requires one registry of supported harnesses, current config schemas, client-specific verification, screenshots, uninstall/rollback, and no drift from CLI installer behavior. |

V2 requires buyer-safe naming, no wave labels in user-facing metadata, current repo/brand URL decision, package-state label, and extension package proof. |
| `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/src/extension.ts` | `4,922` bytes, `163` lines, no mojibake, exposes passive monitor, clustering, status bar, distill dialog, routing toggle, and `EXTENSION_VERSION = 'w819-v1'`. | The extension is a strong seed for capture/distill routing, but it needs consent UX, official API validation, extension-host smoke, uninstall/privacy flows, current versioning, and proof that it does not over-capture code. |
