It needs issue/spec mapping, owned write sets, dependency blast radius, selected tests, generated artifact staleness, and master-node ownership output. |
| Relevant tests | Current matching tests include `wave144-completions-api`, `wave144-completions-server`, `wave262-mcp-installers`, `wave308-kolm-completion-install`, `wave418-chat-completions-auth`, `wave553-codegraph-cloud-platform`, `wave555-user-completion-cloud-optimizer`, `wave731-vscode-watcher`, and `wave819-vscode-extension`. | Coverage exists, but final node `50` needs a named `verify:developer-assistant` gate that runs only the coding-assistant contract, extension, MCP, config, codegraph, leakage, and build-finishing proof suite. |
| Public docs/integrations | `public/docs/vscode.html`, `public/install/vscode.html`, `public/integrations/vscode.html`, `public/integrations/cursor-claude-code.html`, `public/integrations/claude-code.html`, `public/docs/quickstart/mcp.html`, and `public/docs/sdk/mcp.html` all exist. Some visible docs still mention `W819`, old repo links, or aggressive claims such as zero API calls/offline without a capability matrix. | Docs need one product path: install, consent, capture accepted suggestions, cluster, distill, route local artifact, prove savings, finish build. Claims must be scoped by supported host and proof state. |

##### External Baseline V2

| Baseline | Current source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| MCP current spec | The MCP specification page currently redirects to version `2025-11-25`; it defines MCP as an open protocol for connecting LLM apps to external tools/data, uses JSON-RPC 2.0, and includes tools, resources, prompts, sampling, roots, elicitation, progress, cancellation, error reporting, and logging: `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification`. | Kolm MCP cannot hardcode an old protocol story. It needs version negotiation policy, schema tests, client compatibility, tool/result schemas, roots behavior, progress/cancel handling, and downgrade labels. |
| MCP trust and safety | MCP says users must explicitly consent to data access and tool actions, tool behavior descriptions are untrusted unless from a trusted server, and implementors should provide robust consent/authorization flows and clear security docs: `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification`. | Kolm MCP, build agents, and IDE captures need consent screens, tool permission display, sandbox/default-deny policy, audit logs, and untrusted-tool-description handling. |
| VS Code inline completion API | VS Code documents `registerInlineCompletionItemProvider`; providers can be registered per document selector and are asked while typing or by user gesture, with cancellation tokens and inline completion items: `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api`. | If Kolm claims inline completion, the VS Code extension must implement or integrate the inline-completion API and test cancellation, language selectors, range replacement, and parallel-provider behavior. |
| LSP completion | LSP 3.17 defines `textDocument/completion`, completion client capabilities, completion provider options, trigger characters, resolve behavior, and completion context: `https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/specifications/lsp/3.17/specification/`. | Kolm must either implement LSP-compatible completion or clearly state that it is an OpenAI-compatible/MCP/VS Code extension product rather than a language server. |
| Continue config | Continue `config.yaml` defines model roles including `chat`, `autocomplete`, `edit`, `apply`, and `summarize`; autocomplete options include debounce, prompt tokens, timeout, suffix percentage, prefix percentage, template, only-my-code, cache, imports, recent files, and OpenAI-compatible `apiBase`: `https://docs.continue.dev/reference`. | Kolm needs tested Continue config for both chat and autocomplete roles, including local `apiBase`, auth, model selectors, only-my-code mode, timeout, and no-code telemetry settings. |
| OpenAI code-generation docs | OpenAI describes code generation as writing, reviewing, editing, debugging, and using Codex through IDE, CLI, web/mobile, CI/CD, and SDK workflows: `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/code-generation`. | Kolm should position this branch as the ownership/evidence/routing layer around coding agents, not another general coding model. |
| GitHub Copilot cloud agent | GitHub says its cloud agent works in a GitHub Actions-powered environment, can research, plan, change code on a branch, and create pull requests, with logs/commits improving transparency: `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/agents/cloud-agent/about-cloud-agent`. | Kolm build-finishing must compete on evidence: issue-to-spec, branch/write-set planning, test execution, screenshots, PR packet, provenance, and rollback, not just local chat. |
| Claude Code MCP docs | Claude Code connects to tools through MCP and documents local stdio, remote HTTP/SSE, config scopes, OAuth, output limits, prompts, resources, and tool search: `https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp`. | Kolm client recipes must be per-host, not generic. Claude Code, Cursor, Continue, VS Code, Windsurf, Codex, and plain SDK clients each need scoped config and verification. |
| UI/UX Pro Max | Local UI guidance prioritizes accessibility, touch/interaction, performance, layout, typography, feedback, navigation, and state clarity. | Developer-tool UX must be operational: no hover-only controls, visible consent, progress, cancellation, error recovery, status bar clarity, settings labels, keyboard access, and accessible logs/output. |

##### Developer-Assistant Registry Contract V2

Every supported coding surface should be represented as `kolm.developer_assistant.surface.v2`.

```json
{
  "id": "vscode-passive-distill",
  "node": 50,
  "host": "vscode",
  "mode": "observe|inline_completion|chat_bridge|mcp_tools|build_finish",
  "status": "prototype|local_proven|beta|package_ready|published|production_proven|unsupported",
  "user_story": "Capture accepted suggestions, cluster repetition, distill local artifact, route safe matches.",
  "entrypoints": {
    "package": "packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json",
    "commands": ["kolm.rag.openDistillDialog", "kolm.rag.viewClusters", "kolm.rag.toggleRouting"],
    "api": [],
    "cli": ["kolm serve --mcp", "kolm install vscode --dry-run"],
    "docs": ["public/docs/vscode.html", "public/install/vscode.html"]
  },
  "data_policy": {
    "capture_scope": "accepted_suggestions_only",
    "default_storage": "local",
    "upload": "explicit_opt_in",
    "secret_redaction": "required",
    "retention": "workspace_policy"
  },
  "quality_budget": {
    "inline_p95_ms": 250,
    "chat_first_token_p95_ms": 1500,
    "acceptance_rate_min": 0.18,
    "edit_survival_min": 0.7,
    "test_pass_delta_min": 0
  },
  "proof": {
    "tests": [],
    "manual_use": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "package_artifact": null,
    "claim_scope": "local_proven"
  }
}
```

Redline: no host can be listed as supported unless the registry names its mode, package state, install path, data policy, auth/sandbox policy, smoke command, rollback/uninstall path, screenshots, and public claim scope.

##### Inline Completion Protocol Contract V2

Kolm should define one local request contract for editor autocomplete even if it remains internally backed by chat-completions or artifacts.

```json
{
  "endpoint": "/v1/code/completions",
  "transport": "local_http|hosted_api|extension_internal",
  "request": {
    "language_id": "typescript",
    "file_path": "src/example.ts",
    "workspace_id": "hash-or-local-label",
    "prefix": "text before cursor",
    "suffix": "text after cursor",
    "cursor": { "line": 42, "character": 17 },
    "max_tokens": 96,
    "stop": ["\n\n"],
    "latency_budget_ms": 250,
    "privacy_mode": "local_only|redacted_remote|team_allowed",
    "context": {
      "imports": [],
      "recent_files": [],
      "symbols": [],
      "diff": null
    }
  },
  "response": {
    "id": "ccmp_...",
    "insert_text": "completion",
    "range": null,
    "provenance": {
      "route": "local_artifact|teacher|fallback",
      "artifact_hash": null,
      "provider": null,
      "capture_eligible": false
    },
    "usage": {
      "prompt_tokens": 0,
      "completion_tokens": 0,
      "cost_usd": 0
    }
  }
}
```

Redline: a generic `/v1/chat/completions` call is not enough to prove inline completion. Inline completion is done only when prefix/suffix, cancellation, timeout, debounce, privacy, accepted-suggestion telemetry, and IDE rendering proof exist.

##### Build-Finishing Workflow Contract V2

The build-finishing product should convert a vague issue into a controlled release packet.

```json
{
  "workflow": "kolm.build_finish.v2",
  "input": {
    "source": "issue|prompt|goal|failing_test|screenshot|support_ticket",
    "text": "Implement feature X without touching billing.",
    "constraints": ["owned_paths only", "no generated docs until tests pass"]
  },
  "plan": {
    "master_nodes": [43, 50],
    "owned_paths": [],
    "conflict_paths": [],
    "generated_artifacts": [],
    "risk": "low|medium|high",
    "acceptance": []
  },
  "execution": {
    "edits": [],
    "commands": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_use": [],
    "rollback": []
  },
  "release_packet": {
    "summary": "",
    "tests": [],
    "known_limits": [],
    "claim_scope": "local|preview|production",
    "evidence_paths": []
  }
}
```

Redline: the assistant cannot call a build finished if it lacks owned paths, conflict paths, acceptance tests, current generated-artifact state, rollback, and user-facing claim scope.

##### Developer-Tool UX Requirements V2

| Surface | UX requirement |
|---|---|
| VS Code status bar | Shows capture state, privacy state, cluster count, routing state, error state, and one obvious action; no cryptic wave IDs. |
| Distill dialog | Explains what data will be used, which teacher/local artifact will run, expected cost/savings, where artifact is stored, and how to cancel. |
| MCP install docs | Per-client path, dry-run output, conflict behavior, scopes, rollback/uninstall, version compatibility, and trust warning. |
| Completion settings | Labels are user intent, not internal implementation: capture accepted suggestions, store local only, route local artifacts, upload approved captures, redact secrets. |
| Account developer page | Shows connected editors/agents, recent captures, repeated patterns, generated artifacts, local-routing savings, privacy policy, and next actions. |
| Build-finishing dashboard | Shows plan, write set, tests, screenshots, generated artifacts, unresolved risks, release packet, and rollback. |
| Error states | Auth missing, provider missing, artifact missing, MCP protocol mismatch, extension not built, stale package, and unsafe write all have recovery steps. |

##### Metrics V2

| Metric | Why it matters | Minimum proof |
|---|---|---|
| Time to first routed completion | Measures install and activation friction. | Manual-use lab from clean workspace to first Kolm-routed suggestion. |
| Inline p95 latency | Autocomplete fails if slow. | Per-host latency report with local artifact, teacher, and fallback paths. |
| Suggestion acceptance rate | Measures usefulness. | Captured accept/reject events by language and task class with privacy controls. |
| Edit survival rate | Accepted code that remains after review is higher-quality than accepted-then-deleted code. | Diff-based survival check after save, test, and commit windows. |
| Test pass delta | Build-finishing must improve code health. | Before/after test result and failure attribution for each build-finishing task. |
| Local routing savings | Proves Kolm's core promise for coding teams. | Teacher calls avoided, token/cost saved, and quality guardrail status. |
| Consent clarity | Prevents enterprise rejection. | Usability checklist: users can explain what is captured, stored, uploaded, and deleted. |
| Tool safety violations | MCP/build tools need strict safety. | Negative tests for unapproved file reads, writes, env leaks, network calls, and prompt-injection tool descriptions. |

| Ticket | Branch | Action | Acceptance evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-001` | `50.1` | Generate `docs/developer-assistant-registry.json`. | Registry lists every host, mode, package state, entrypoint, data policy, proof command, screenshot, and claim scope. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-002` | `50.1` | Remove user-facing wave labels from extension/package/docs. | Marketplace metadata, settings descriptions, docs, status bar text, and screenshots use product language rather than `W819`, `W262`, or internal wave IDs. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-003` | `50.1` | Resolve legacy repository and brand URLs for developer-tool docs. | `sneaky-hippo/kolmogorov-stack` and legacy Kolmogorov links are either removed from public install pages or listed in a brand exception ledger. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-004` | `50.2` | Add `/v1/code/completions` or explicit no-inline decision record. | API/docs/tests either prove inline completion contract or public copy demotes claims to chat bridge and MCP tools. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-005` | `50.2` | Implement code-completion eval harness. | Fixtures report acceptance proxy, latency, edit survival, test delta, local routing, and secret-redaction status by language/task class. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-006` | `50.2` | Add Continue autocomplete config proof. | Tested `config.yaml` uses Kolm local `apiBase`, autocomplete role, timeout, prefix/suffix settings, no-code telemetry policy, and screenshots/logs. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-007` | `50.2` | Add accepted-suggestion capture consent contract. | Extension and docs prove default local storage, opt-in upload, redaction, retention, revoke/delete, and admin policy. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-008` | `50.3` | Upgrade MCP protocol compatibility plan. | `services/mcp/server.js` has a compatibility matrix for `2024-11-05`, `2025-03-26`, `2025-06-18`, and `2025-11-25`, plus tests for current clients. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-009` | `50.3` | Add MCP tool safety metadata and negative tests. | Tools expose schemas/annotations as untrusted, require explicit invocation consent, log calls, limit output, and reject unsafe paths/params. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-010` | `50.3` | Unify `kolm install` and `scripts/install-mcp.cjs` harness registry. | CLI installer, standalone installer, docs, and tests share harness names, config paths, dry-run output, conflict handling, and rollback. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-011` | `50.3` | Package VS Code extension as a release artifact. | Build produces VSIX/tarball, extension-host smoke passes, package provenance is recorded, and docs state package/Marketplace status honestly. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-012` | `50.3` | Add IDE manual-use lab. | Human-readable runbook plus screenshots cover install, consent, accepted suggestion, cluster, distill CTA, local routing, account visibility, and uninstall. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-013` | `50.4` | Extend codegraph to master-node/file-owner output. | Codegraph output can tell a build agent owned paths, conflict paths, likely tests, generated artifacts, and route/page/API blast radius. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-014` | `50.4` | Build issue-to-spec packet generator. | Given an issue, output includes problem statement, requirements, write-set plan, acceptance tests, screenshots, rollback, and claim scope. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-015` | `50.4` | Build release-redline assistant workflow. | Workflow runs inspect, plan, patch, focused verify, generated-artifact check, screenshot/manual-use proof, release packet, and rollback record. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-016` | `50.4` | Add generated-artifact sequencing guard. | Build agent cannot regenerate API refs, OpenAPI, product graph, sitemap, screenshots, or docs concurrently without lock-order and freshness check. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-017` | `50.5` | Add code-leakage red-team suite. | Negative tests prove secrets, hidden files, env vars, git remotes, private snippets, and unapproved context are not captured/uploaded. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-018` | `50.5` | Add enterprise admin controls. | Admin/account UI and API can control providers, capture scope, upload, local artifacts, retention, audit export, and allowed MCP tools. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-019` | `50.6` | Add `verify:developer-assistant`. | Command runs completions server tests, auth tests, MCP installer/protocol tests, VS Code extension build/smoke, codegraph checks, leakage tests, docs refs, and screenshots where applicable. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V2-020` | `50.6` | Add production/dev smoke for developer assistant. | Smoke proves hosted chat bridge, local completions server, MCP stdio/http, VS Code package docs, Continue/Cursor/Claude/Codex recipes, account developer view, and build-finishing packet. |

##### Node `50` V2 Relax Redline

Node `50` can relax only when all of the following are true:

- every coding host is listed in the developer-assistant registry with mode, package state, proof command, screenshot state, privacy policy, and claim scope;
- chat bridge, inline completion, MCP tools, VS Code passive capture, codegraph, and build-finishing are separated in product copy and tests;
- MCP protocol compatibility is current or explicitly scoped, with client-specific smoke for Cursor, Continue, Claude, VS Code, Windsurf, Codex, and generic SDK clients where claimed;
- inline completion is either implemented and verified with prefix/suffix/cancellation/latency, or all inline-completion claims are demoted until it is;
- extension package metadata is clean of internal wave labels, stale repo links, and unsupported marketplace claims;
- the code-leakage suite proves local-only defaults, redaction, opt-in upload, retention, delete/revoke, and least-privilege tool permissions;
- build-finishing produces an evidence packet that a reviewer can trace to master spec nodes, owned files, commands, screenshots, generated artifacts, rollback, and claim scope;
- `verify:developer-assistant`, document hygiene, and relevant release/local/prod smokes pass on the current tree.

The strict rule is: Kolm can call itself the owned-intelligence layer for coding teams only after it proves both developer productivity and developer trust. Useful completions without privacy, consent, package proof, and build-finish evidence are not enough.

#### Node 50 Completion Lab, Coding-Agent Trust, And Build-Redline Addendum V3

Node `52` remains unused. The developer-product work belongs to node `50`, because the repo already has an OpenAI-compatible chat bridge, local completions server, MCP server, MCP installer, VS Code passive monitor, codegraph builder, agent telemetry pages, build pages, coding docs, integration pages, and coding-agent tests. The gap is not "invent a separate developer product tree." The gap is to convert those scattered assets into a state-complete coding-assistant product with measurable completion quality, host-specific installation proof, privacy and tool-permission controls, codebase-finishing workflows, and reviewer-grade release evidence.

Node `50` V3 draws a hard product line:

- `chat_bridge` means OpenAI-compatible chat completions for existing SDKs and coding agents.
- `inline_completion` means IDE-native prefix/suffix autocomplete with cancellation, debounce, language/file context, latency budget, accepted-suggestion telemetry, and edit-survival evidence.
- `mcp_tools` means a versioned MCP server with tool schemas, consent, roots, progress, cancellation, output limits, safety metadata, and client-specific smoke.
- `passive_capture` means accepted coding suggestions are locally observed, clustered, consented, redacted, retained, and optionally distilled.
- `build_finish` means an issue or goal becomes a controlled implementation packet: write set, conflicts, generated artifacts, tests, screenshots, rollback, release notes, and claim scope.

Kolm can be world-class for AI companies here only if it becomes the ownership and evidence layer around all coding agents, not another generic autocomplete brand. The product promise is: route coding-agent traffic through Kolm, capture repeated accepted work, compile project-specific behavior into owned artifacts, replay safe repetition locally, and finish builds with proof that a reviewer can audit.

##### Node 50 V3 Current Evidence Snapshot

| Evidence class | Current local state | V3 redline |
|---|---|---|
| Chat-completions bridge | `scripts/completions-server.mjs` serves `GET /healthz`, `GET /v1/models`, and `POST /v1/chat/completions`; `src/completions-api.js` backs OpenAI-compatible responses and provider selectors. | This proves chat bridge potential, not inline autocomplete. Public copy and docs must not imply IDE-native completion until the inline contract is implemented and measured. |
| Local auth and CORS | The completions server supports optional bearer auth, a 4 MiB body cap, and permissive CORS. | Final local-dev defaults need explicit threat model: local-only bind, auth-on guidance, browser exposure warning, CORS rationale, and per-client smoke. |
| MCP server | `services/mcp/server.js` exposes `.kolm` artifacts as MCP tools and currently declares protocol version `2024-11-05`. | Current MCP spec evidence requires compatibility policy, negotiation, progress, cancellation, tasks, roots, sampling/elicitation capabilities, safety docs, and downgrade labels. |
| MCP package | `sdk/mcp/package.json` publishes `@kolm/recipe-mcp` locally but still points repository metadata at `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack`. | Package metadata, repository URL, namespace, install proof, and package-release state must be deliberate, current, and brand-safe. |
| VS Code extension | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json` describes a passive monitor and distill workflow; metadata still exposes `W819` labels and a stale repository URL. | This must become buyer-safe extension metadata with no internal wave IDs, explicit package state, consent defaults, privacy controls, and extension-host smoke. |
| IDE source files | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag/src` contains capture queue, extension entrypoint, local runtime, passive monitor, pattern detection, routing, and status bar modules. | V3 needs a host matrix proving what each file implements: passive capture, local routing, distill dialog, status, settings, consent, uninstall, and no over-capture. |
| Public developer docs | Relevant pages include `/docs/vscode`, `/install/vscode`, `/integrations/vscode`, `/integrations/cursor-claude-code`, `/integrations/claude-code`, `/docs/quickstart/mcp`, `/docs/sdk/mcp`, `/docs/dev-agents`, `/use-cases/agentic-coding`, and `/account/agent-telemetry`. | Docs need one coherent journey from install to consent to first routed suggestion to distilled artifact to build-finish proof, with host-specific config and no unsupported claims. |
| Account surfaces | `public/account/agent-telemetry.html` and `public/account/builds.html` exist. | Account must become the operator view for connected editors/agents, captured patterns, artifacts, local-routing savings, privacy state, build packets, and unresolved risks. |
| Build graph and scripts | `scripts/build-codegraph.mjs`, `src/repo-codegraph.js`, many build scripts, and build-related tests exist. | Build-finishing must consume the codegraph as product data: master node, owner files, conflict paths, generated-artifact lock order, likely tests, screenshots, rollback, and release packet. |
| Test footprint | Matching tests cover completions API/server, auth, MCP installers, completion install, builder, agent telemetry, codegraph, package build, VS Code watcher, and VS Code extension. | V3 needs one named `verify:developer-assistant` gate that proves this slice, not scattered incidental tests. |

##### Node 50 V3 Refreshed External Baseline

| Source | Current fact imported into the spec tree | Kolm product implication |
|---|---|---|
| MCP 2025-11-25 schema, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/schema` | The current schema includes protocol version negotiation, client capabilities such as roots, sampling, elicitation, and tasks, plus progress-token metadata. | Kolm MCP must advertise supported protocol versions and capabilities explicitly instead of assuming old `2024-11-05` behavior. |
| MCP lifecycle, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/lifecycle` | MCP implementations should set timeouts, issue cancellation notifications when requests exceed timeouts, and can use progress notifications without allowing infinite runtime. | Kolm MCP tools and build agents need timeout, cancellation, progress, max-duration, and hung-tool recovery behavior. |
| Continue config reference, `https://docs.continue.dev/reference` | Continue model roles include `chat`, `autocomplete`, `edit`, `apply`, and more; autocomplete options include debounce delay, model timeout, prefix/suffix percentages, prompt tokens, and `apiBase`. | Kolm Continue recipes must test both chat and autocomplete roles and show exactly which role is supported by the local server. |
| VS Code API, `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/vscode-api` | VS Code exposes inline-completion providers through `provideInlineCompletionItems`, with `TextDocument`, `Position`, `InlineCompletionContext`, and `CancellationToken`. | Kolm cannot claim VS Code inline completion without implementing or integrating this path and testing cancellation and range behavior. |
| LSP 3.17, `https://github.com/Microsoft/language-server-protocol/blob/gh-pages/_specifications/lsp/3.17/specification.md` | LSP standardizes completion capabilities, completion provider options, trigger kinds, and completion resolution. | Kolm must either ship LSP-compatible completion or clearly scope support to OpenAI-compatible chat/MCP/extension workflows. |
| OpenAI Codex use cases, `https://developers.openai.com/codex/use-cases` | Current Codex workflows include PR review, responsive UI work with visual checks, coding tasks from Slack, deployment, API upgrades, creating CLIs for agents, and verified operations. | Kolm should not compete as a raw model; it should provide owned artifacts, local routing, evidence packets, and completion/build proof around coding agents. |
| GitHub Copilot cloud agent docs, `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/concepts/agents/cloud-agent/about-cloud-agent` | Copilot cloud agent works in a GitHub Actions-powered environment, can research, plan, change code on a branch, and open pull requests. | Kolm build-finishing must meet or exceed this with codegraph planning, branch/write-set evidence, tests, screenshots, release packet, and rollback. |
| GitHub Copilot responsible-use docs, `https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/copilot-cloud-agent` | GitHub emphasizes ephemeral environments, write-access gating, approval for workflows, scoped repo access, signed commits, session logs, and firewall controls. | Kolm must ship comparable trust primitives: scoped permissions, no direct default-branch writes, signed/provenanced changes, logs, secret isolation, and exfiltration controls. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | Local UI guidance prioritizes accessibility, visible focus, no hover-only controls, clear loading/error states, keyboard access, responsive layout, and operational state clarity. | Developer-tool UI must show consent, capture state, routing state, build progress, cancellation, failure recovery, privacy policy, and proof export without cryptic internal labels. |

##### Developer Product Host Matrix Contract V3

Every claimed developer host must be serializable as `kolm.developer_host.v3`.

```json
{
  "host_id": "vscode",
  "host_type": "ide|cli|cloud_agent|local_agent|sdk|ci",
  "supported_modes": ["chat_bridge", "passive_capture", "mcp_tools"],
  "unsupported_modes": ["inline_completion"],
  "package_state": "source_only|local_build|artifact_ready|marketplace_published|production_proven",
  "entrypoints": {
    "extension": "packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json",
    "mcp": "services/mcp/server.js",
    "local_server": "scripts/completions-server.mjs",
    "docs": [],
    "account": [],
    "cli": []
  },
  "privacy": {
    "default_capture": "off|accepted_suggestions_only|all_edits",
    "default_storage": "local",
    "upload": "disabled|explicit_opt_in|admin_allowed",
    "redaction": "required",
    "retention": "workspace_policy",
    "delete_revoke": "required"
  },
  "proof": {
    "install_smoke": [],
    "manual_use_packet": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "tests": [],
    "known_limits": [],
    "claim_scope": "source_only"
  }
}
```

Host rules:

- A host cannot be listed as supported unless it has install, auth, privacy, uninstall/rollback, and smoke proof.
- Chat, autocomplete, edit/apply, MCP, passive capture, and build-finish are separate modes.
- Host docs must state whether traffic stays local, is redacted, or is uploaded.
- Any stale internal wave ID, old repository URL, or unsupported marketplace state is a release blocker for that host.

##### Inline Completion Contract V3

Inline completion is its own product. It is not proven by chat completions.

```json
{
  "endpoint": "/v1/code/completions",
  "request": {
    "host": "vscode|continue|cursor|generic_lsp",
    "language_id": "typescript",
    "file_path": "src/example.ts",
    "workspace_id": "local hash",
    "prefix": "text before cursor",
    "suffix": "text after cursor",
    "cursor": { "line": 12, "character": 8 },
    "selection": null,
    "recent_files": [],
    "symbols": [],
    "imports": [],
    "diff_context": null,
    "max_tokens": 96,
    "latency_budget_ms": 250,
    "privacy_mode": "local_only|redacted_remote|team_allowed",
    "cancellation_token": "host-provided opaque id"
  },
  "response": {
    "insert_text": "completion text",
    "range": null,
    "route": "local_artifact|team_artifact|teacher|fallback",
    "artifact_hash": null,
    "quality_gate": "pass|fallback|blocked",
    "capture_eligible": false,
    "cost_usd": 0,
    "latency_ms": 0
  }
}
```

Completion proof must include:

- prefix/suffix tests by language and file size;
- debounce and cancellation tests;
- p50/p95/p99 latency by host, route, and model;
- accepted-suggestion capture and explicit opt-out;
- edit survival after save, test, review, and commit windows;
- local-routing quality fallback when artifact confidence is low;
- secret, license, generated-code, and prompt-injection leakage tests.

##### MCP Trust Contract V3

Kolm MCP tools must be serialized as `kolm.mcp_tool_trust.v3`.

```json
{
  "tool_name": "mcp__project__artifact",
  "protocol_versions": ["2024-11-05", "2025-03-26", "2025-06-18", "2025-11-25"],
  "capabilities": {
    "tools": true,
    "resources": false,
    "prompts": false,
    "roots": "client_supported",
    "sampling": false,
    "elicitation": false,
    "tasks": false,
    "progress": true,
    "cancellation": true
  },
  "security": {
    "tool_description_trusted": false,
    "input_schema_required": true,
    "path_policy": "workspace_or_artifact_roots_only",
    "network_policy": "deny_by_default",
    "env_policy": "deny_by_default",
    "output_limit_bytes": 262144,
    "audit_log": "required"
  },
  "proof": {
    "client_smoke": [],
    "negative_tests": [],
    "compatibility_report": null
  }
}
```

MCP redline:

- no unbounded tools;
- no hidden network or environment access;
- no trusting tool descriptions from untrusted servers;
- no broad filesystem reads;
- no tool call without user or policy consent;
- no stale protocol version without a visible downgrade label.

##### Build-Finishing Packet Contract V3

Every build-finishing task must emit `kolm.build_finish_packet.v3`.

```json
{
  "task_id": "bf_...",
  "source": "issue|goal|failing_test|screenshot|support_ticket|manual",
  "master_nodes": [43, 50],
  "problem_statement": "",
  "requirements": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "owned_paths": [],
  "conflict_paths": [],
  "generated_artifacts": [],
  "lock_order": [],
  "risk": "low|medium|high",
  "plan": [],
  "edits": [],
  "commands": [],
  "tests": [],
  "screenshots": [],
  "manual_use": [],
  "privacy_review": [],
  "rollback": [],
  "release_summary": "",
  "known_limits": [],
  "claim_scope": "local|preview|production",
  "reviewer_checklist": []
}
```

The packet is done only when a reviewer can answer: what changed, why it changed, who or what owns each path, what was not touched, what was regenerated, what was tested, what failed, what screenshots prove the result, what risks remain, how to roll back, and what the public claim scope is.

##### Developer-Tool UX State Model V3

| Surface | Required states |
|---|---|
| VS Code status bar | `capture_off`, `local_capture_on`, `upload_opt_in`, `patterns_detected`, `distill_ready`, `routing_on`, `blocked_secret`, `error`, `update_available`, and `uninstalled_cleanly`. |
| Distill dialog | `explain_data`, `select_teacher`, `cost_estimate`, `privacy_review`, `run`, `progress`, `cancel`, `success_artifact`, `quality_blocked`, `fallback`, and `delete_captures`. |
| MCP install flow | `dry_run`, `conflict`, `write_pending`, `written`, `client_detected`, `client_missing`, `protocol_mismatch`, `rollback`, `verify`, and `trust_warning`. |
| Continue/Cursor/Claude/Codex recipes | `chat_supported`, `autocomplete_supported`, `mcp_supported`, `agent_supported`, `local_only`, `redacted_remote`, `requires_key`, and `unsupported`. |
| Account developer page | `connected_hosts`, `recent_captures`, `clustered_patterns`, `artifact_candidates`, `routing_savings`, `privacy_policy`, `admin_lock`, `open_risks`, and `proof_export`. |
| Build-finishing dashboard | `issue_ingested`, `plan_ready`, `write_set_locked`, `tests_running`, `screenshot_needed`, `generated_artifacts_stale`, `review_ready`, `blocked`, `rollback_ready`, and `production_proven`. |

UX redline: no developer surface may hide a dangerous state behind color alone, hover-only controls, logs that require horizontal scrolling, cryptic wave IDs, or buttons without visible disabled/loading/error explanations.

##### Coding-Assistant Evaluation Metrics V3

| Metric | Required proof |
|---|---|
| `time_to_first_value` | Clean install to first chat response, first MCP tool call, first captured pattern, first local routed result, and first build packet. |
| `inline_p95_ms` | p95 latency by host, language, context size, and route. |
| `acceptance_rate` | Accepted suggestions divided by surfaced suggestions, with host and language breakdown. |
| `edit_survival_rate` | Accepted code retained after save, test, review, and commit windows. |
| `test_pass_delta` | Before/after test outcome for build-finishing tasks. |
| `local_route_savings` | Teacher calls avoided, tokens saved, cost saved, quality gate pass/fallback rate. |
| `leakage_block_rate` | Secrets, env vars, hidden files, unapproved snippets, and private context blocked from capture/upload. |
| `tool_safety_violation_rate` | Unsafe MCP or build-agent tool calls rejected by policy. |
| `reviewer_trust_score` | Reviewers can trace requirements, files, tests, screenshots, and rollback without asking the agent. |
| `claim_demotion_count` | Number of coding-assistant claims demoted because a host, package, protocol, or proof state expired. |

##### Node `50` V3 Relax Redline

Node `50` V3 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- The master tree routes code completion, MCP, IDE host support, coding-agent configs, codegraph, issue-to-spec, build-finishing, privacy/leakage, and release-redline proof to node `50`.
- Every claimed developer host has a `kolm.developer_host.v3` record with mode support, package state, entrypoints, privacy policy, install proof, smoke, screenshots, and claim scope.
- Chat bridge, inline completion, MCP tools, passive capture, and build-finishing are separated in product copy, docs, account UI, API, CLI, and tests.
- Inline completion is either implemented with prefix/suffix/cancellation/debounce/latency/accepted-suggestion proof, or every inline-completion claim is demoted.
- MCP protocol compatibility is current, versioned, and tested against claimed clients with progress, cancellation, output limits, safety metadata, and downgrade labels.
- VS Code and other host metadata contain no internal wave labels, stale repository links, unsupported marketplace claims, or ambiguous privacy text.
- Accepted-suggestion capture has explicit consent, local-first defaults, redaction, upload opt-in, retention, delete/revoke, admin controls, and leakage negative tests.
- Build-finishing emits a packet with master nodes, owned paths, conflict paths, generated artifacts, lock order, tests, screenshots, manual-use proof, rollback, known limits, and claim scope.
- `verify:developer-assistant`, relevant product-depth gates, docs/link checks, package checks, local smoke, and production smoke all pass for the current tree.

The V3 rule is simple: Kolm can sell developer-owned intelligence only when developers can verify completion quality and trust boundaries from the product itself.

#### Node 50 V4 Coding Product Acceptance, MCP/IDE Protocol Proof, And Build-Finishing Redline

Node `52` remains unused. This work belongs to node `50` because the product surface is not generic SDK adoption or generic code quality. It is Kolm as a coding product: code completion, agent routing, IDE host integration, MCP tooling, repo-codegraph context, issue-to-spec conversion, and build-finishing proof. V4 turns the V3 contracts into acceptance gates a developer, security reviewer, or engineering leader can actually use.

The strict rule is: "Kolm helps finish builds" is not proven by a chat-completions endpoint, a VS Code package, or an MCP server alone. It is proven only when a clean developer can install a host integration, consent to capture, route one coding task, generate or consume a `.kolm` artifact, finish a build task with tests, inspect privacy/tool boundaries, export a proof packet, and uninstall or roll back without hidden state.

##### Node 50 V4 Current Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observed state | V4 implication |
|---|---|---|
| VS Code RAG package | `packages/vscode-kolm-rag` has 9 files and 40,259 bytes. `package.json` still exposes W819 descriptions and a `sneaky-hippo/kolm-stack` repository URL. | The developer product is not release-polished until marketplace/package metadata has no internal wave labels, stale repo links, or ambiguous privacy language. |
| Legacy VS Code SDK package | `sdk/vscode` has 10 files and 52,444 bytes. | V4 needs one host matrix that distinguishes current extension, legacy/source-only extension, installable package, marketplace status, and deprecation/alias policy. |
| MCP package | `sdk/mcp` has 10 files and 44,142 bytes, with `@kolm/recipe-mcp` source and dependencies on MCP SDK plus `@kolm/recipe`. | Package state is source/local unless package-channel evidence exists; docs and install CTAs must not imply registry availability without proof. |
| MCP service | `services/mcp/server.js` is 12,616 bytes and still sets `PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2024-11-05'`. | MCP compatibility claims need current protocol negotiation, Streamable HTTP/SSE downgrade labels, header behavior, and negative tests. |
| Completion bridge | `scripts/completions-server.mjs` is 7,257 bytes and `src/completions-api.js` is 27,218 bytes. | Chat-completion compatibility exists as a surface, but inline completion needs separate prefix/suffix, debounce, cancellation, latency, acceptance, and leakage proof. |
| Codegraph and telemetry | `src/repo-codegraph.js` is 8,143 bytes and `src/agent-telemetry.js` is 24,836 bytes. | These should feed build-finishing packets: owned paths, dependency blast radius, generated artifacts, tests, screenshots, privacy events, and acceptance status. |
| Public developer pages | `public/docs/dev-agents.html`, `public/docs/vscode.html`, `public/builder.html`, and `public/use-cases/agentic-coding.html` total 94,337 bytes. | Public pages need one product path: install, connect, consent, capture, cluster, distill/compile, route locally, finish build, verify, and export proof. |
| Tests | Current tests cover completions, agent install, builder, MCP installers, agent telemetry, VS Code watcher, and VS Code extension. | V4 still needs a single `verify:developer-product` gate that proves the integrated experience, not isolated pieces. |

##### Node 50 V4 Current External Baseline

| Source | Current fact imported into the tree | Kolm product requirement |
|---|---|---|
| MCP 2025-06-18 transports, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/transports` | MCP defines stdio and Streamable HTTP transports, requires an `MCP-Protocol-Version` header on later HTTP requests, and treats old HTTP+SSE as backward-compatibility behavior. | Kolm MCP must declare current protocol support, negotiate version, reject unsupported versions clearly, and label legacy SSE or older protocol paths. |
| MCP 2025-06-18 overview, `https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/index` | The TypeScript schema is the source of truth for protocol messages, and MCP reserves `_meta` and namespace prefixes for protocol metadata. | Kolm MCP tool schemas, metadata, tool names, resources, and extension fields must validate against current schema and avoid reserved metadata misuse. |
| VS Code Language Model Tool API, `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/tools` | VS Code supports extension tools, built-in tools, and MCP tools; language-model tools need package manifest configuration and tool registration. | Kolm must choose per host whether it is an extension-native tool, MCP server, chat participant, or local OpenAI-compatible bridge and must not blur those modes. |
| VS Code Language Model API, `https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/language-model` | VS Code requires user consent for Copilot language models, streams responses, exposes cancellation/error behavior, and warns that nondeterministic model calls are hard to integration-test. | Kolm extension flows need explicit consent, deterministic unit tests for prompt/context assembly, simulated model tests, streaming/cancel/error states, and quota failure UX. |
| Language Server Protocol, `https://microsoft.github.io/language-server-protocol/` | LSP standardizes editor/language-server features such as autocomplete, go-to-definition, references, and hover; the latest version is 3.17. | Inline completion should not be conflated with LSP completion; if Kolm claims editor-native completion, it needs a host-specific or LSP-compatible contract. |
| OpenAI Codex product page, `https://openai.com/codex/` | Codex positions coding agents around end-to-end engineering work, multi-agent workflows, Skills, always-on background work, review, testing, and the same agent across app/editor/terminal. | Kolm should position against owned intelligence and artifact routing: capture accepted patterns, compile them into `.kolm`, route local repeats, and prove build completion across app/editor/terminal. |
| OpenAI Codex system-message appendix, `https://openai.com/index/introducing-codex/` | Codex emphasizes respecting repo instructions, running relevant checks, waiting for commands, leaving clean work, and citing files/terminal outputs. | Kolm build-finishing packets need repo-instruction ingestion, command lifecycle, clean-tree state, file citations, terminal evidence, and final proof discipline. |
| SWE-bench organization, `https://github.com/swe-bench` | SWE-bench evaluates AI systems on real-world GitHub issues and maintains related SWE-agent infrastructure. | Kolm coding evals need issue-to-patch tasks, repo snapshots, hidden tests or held-out checks, cost, runtime, and patch survival, not only toy snippets. |
| Terminal-Bench, `https://terminalbench.lol/` and `https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.11868` | Terminal-Bench evaluates agents in sandboxed terminal environments with instructions, files, tools, verification scripts, and reference/oracle solutions; the paper reports hard real-workflow tasks with comprehensive tests. | Kolm build-finishing evals must include shell/file/service workflows, command errors, partial progress, recovery, verification scripts, and reproducible task versions. |

##### V4 Coding Product Acceptance Contract

Every developer-product surface must be serializable as `kolm.coding_product_acceptance.v4`.

```json
{
  "surface_id": "vscode-passive-capture",
  "primary_node": "50",
  "host": "vscode|cursor|continue|claude-code|codex|windsurf|terminal|account|ci",
  "mode": "chat_bridge|inline_completion|mcp_tool|passive_capture|build_finish|code_review|issue_to_spec",
  "package_state": "source_only|local_build|artifact_ready|marketplace_published|production_proven",
  "entrypoints": {
    "package": "packages/vscode-kolm-rag/package.json",
    "server": "scripts/completions-server.mjs",
    "mcp": "services/mcp/server.js",
    "account": null,
    "docs": []
  },
  "privacy": {
    "default": "local_only|redacted_remote|remote_allowed",
    "consent_required": true,
    "captured_events": [],
    "blocked_context": ["secrets", "env", "hidden_files", "private_keys", "unapproved_snippets"],
    "retention": "policy-id",
    "delete_revoke_path": "route-or-command"
  },
  "protocols": {
    "openai_chat": "supported|partial|unsupported",
    "inline_prefix_suffix": "supported|partial|unsupported",
    "mcp_stdio": "supported|partial|unsupported",
    "mcp_streamable_http": "supported|partial|unsupported",
    "lsp": "supported|partial|unsupported"
  },
  "acceptance_tasks": [],
  "proof": {
    "install_smoke": [],
    "host_screenshots": [],
    "protocol_tests": [],
    "privacy_redteam": [],
    "build_finish_packets": [],
    "benchmarks": [],
    "production_smoke": []
  },
  "claim_scope": "internal|docs_preview|public_beta|production_scoped|marketplace_published",
  "demotion_rules": []
}
```

##### V4 Build-Finishing Packet Contract

Every build-finishing workflow must emit `kolm.build_finish_packet.v4`.

```json
{
  "task_id": "BF-...",
  "source": "issue|failing_test|user_prompt|release_redline|manual_use_failure|security_finding",
  "primary_node": "50",
  "secondary_nodes": ["43", "46"],
  "repo_state": {
    "commit": "git-sha-or-local-marker",
    "dirty_before": [],
    "dirty_after": [],
    "untracked_after": [],
    "instructions_files": [],
    "generated_artifact_lock": "acquired|not_needed|blocked"
  },
  "plan": {
    "requirements": [],
    "owned_paths": [],
    "read_only_paths": [],
    "conflict_paths": [],
    "rollback": "description",
    "non_goals": []
  },
  "execution": {
    "edits": [],
    "commands": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "manual_use": [],
    "failures": [],
    "fix_iterations": []
  },
  "verification": {
    "focused_tests": [],
    "release_gates": [],
    "lint": [],
    "privacy_checks": [],
    "build_outputs": [],
    "evidence_grade": "weak|local|release|production|external"
  },
  "result": "verified_local|verified_release|prod_deployed_unverified|verified_prod|blocked|rejected",
  "claim_changes_allowed": []
}
```

##### V4 Acceptance Task Ladder

| Task | Persona | Acceptance |
|---|---|---|
| `CODE-V4-T01` | New developer | Install the local completion bridge, call `/v1/chat/completions`, inspect model/provider/provenance, and understand local versus remote routing. |
| `CODE-V4-T02` | VS Code user | Install the extension from source or package, see consent, capture one accepted suggestion, inspect cluster state, and uninstall cleanly. |
| `CODE-V4-T03` | MCP user | Configure stdio MCP, list tools, call one `.kolm` artifact, see receipt, and reject unsafe path/env/network access. |
| `CODE-V4-T04` | MCP HTTP user | Exercise Streamable HTTP or explicitly see unsupported/downgrade state with protocol version handling. |
| `CODE-V4-T05` | Inline completion evaluator | Test prefix/suffix, debounce, cancellation, latency budget, privacy blocking, and accepted-suggestion event shape. |
| `CODE-V4-T06` | Build finisher | Start from a failing test or issue, generate a build-finish packet, edit owned paths, run tests, update generated artifacts in order, and export proof. |
| `CODE-V4-T07` | Reviewer | Sample a build-finish packet and trace requirement, file, command, screenshot, test, rollback, and final claim scope. |
| `CODE-V4-T08` | Security admin | Enforce local-only mode, provider allowlist, blocked paths, retention, delete/revoke, audit export, and tool permissions. |
| `CODE-V4-T09` | Benchmark owner | Run a SWE-bench-style issue task and a Terminal-Bench-style terminal task in sandboxed mode with cost, time, pass/fail, and logs. |
| `CODE-V4-T10` | Production operator | Verify docs, account developer page, package links, MCP install, completion bridge, proof export, and stale/demotion states on deployed domain. |

##### V4 Host And Protocol State Machine

Developer-product claims must use these states:

| State | Meaning | Public copy allowed |
|---|---|---|
| `source_only` | Code exists but no package artifact or install smoke. | "Available from source" only. |
| `local_build` | Package builds locally and install smoke passes on supported host. | "Local install" with host and version. |
| `artifact_ready` | Signed or checksummed VSIX/package/binary/tarball exists with install/rollback evidence. | "Package artifact available" for exact artifact. |
| `marketplace_published` | Marketplace/registry listing is live and smoke-tested. | Marketplace claim with link, version, and date. |
| `production_proven` | Docs/account/prod smoke, support, rollback, and telemetry proof exist. | Production-scoped claim for exact host/mode. |
| `unsupported` | Host or mode is intentionally not supported. | Honest "not supported yet" label and alternative path. |

No developer page may imply "code completion" when only chat bridge exists. No MCP page may imply current protocol support when only `2024-11-05` behavior exists. No extension page may imply marketplace availability from source-only package metadata.

##### V4 Evaluation And Benchmark Contract

Kolm needs a private and public coding-product eval suite:

- `completion_microbench`: prefix/suffix latency, cancellation, acceptance proxy, edit survival, and language coverage.
- `mcp_tool_safety`: unsafe path, env, network, oversized output, prompt-injected tool description, protocol mismatch, missing consent, and audit logging.
- `ide_install_lab`: clean host install, consent, capture, routing, uninstall, rollback, and screenshots.
- `issue_to_patch_lab`: SWE-bench-style issue tasks with repository snapshot, hidden or held-out checks, cost, time, and patch survival.
- `terminal_finish_lab`: Terminal-Bench-style tasks with shell commands, local services, command failures, recovery, verification scripts, and oracle references.
- `kolm_artifact_reuse_lab`: repeated coding pattern captured, distilled/compiled into `.kolm`, routed locally, and compared against teacher quality/cost.
- `enterprise_privacy_lab`: secrets, env vars, hidden files, private snippets, customer code, retention, delete/revoke, and audit export.
- `review_trust_lab`: independent reviewer can trace any generated edit to requirement, evidence, commands, screenshots, tests, and rollback.

| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-008` | `50.3` | Add MCP/IDE client smoke matrix. | VS Code, Cursor, Continue, Claude Code, Codex, Windsurf, terminal, and account docs state supported/unsupported mode with smoke evidence. |

| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-010` | `50.4` | Connect repo-codegraph to build-finish packets. | Owned paths, conflict paths, dependency blast radius, generated artifacts, likely tests, route/page impact, and master nodes are emitted. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-011` | `50.4` | Add issue-to-spec and failure-to-fix workflows. | Given an issue or failing test, Kolm produces requirements, assumptions, patch plan, verification, rollback, and claim impact. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-012` | `50.4` | Add generated-artifact lock integration. | Build-finish workflows respect node `46` lock order for product graph, OpenAPI, docs, sitemap, screenshots, and release packets. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-013` | `50.5` | Add developer-product privacy admin controls. | Provider allowlist, capture scope, upload policy, blocked paths, local-only mode, retention, delete/revoke, and audit export are enforceable. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-014` | `50.5` | Add leakage red-team suite. | Secrets, env vars, hidden files, private keys, unapproved snippets, git remotes, credentials, and customer code are blocked from capture/upload. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-015` | `50.5` | Add tool safety policy for build agents. | File writes, shell commands, network calls, destructive ops, package installs, and external tools require policy, approval, logs, and rollback. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-016` | `50.6` | Add coding-product benchmark suite. | Completion, MCP safety, IDE install, issue-to-patch, terminal-finish, artifact reuse, privacy, and review-trust labs produce reports. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-017` | `50.6` | Add public/developer docs proof map. | Dev-agent, VS Code, builder, MCP, integration, and agentic-coding pages link to host state, proof, caveats, and install smoke. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-018` | `50.6` | Add account developer command center contract. | Account shows connected hosts, captures, clusters, candidate artifacts, routing state, savings, privacy, open risks, and proof export. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-019` | `50.6` | Add `verify:developer-product`. | Runs package checks, chat bridge, inline bench, MCP protocol/safety, host matrix, codegraph, privacy, docs refs, screenshots, and build-finish packet checks. |
| `W-CODEASSIST-V4-020` | `50.6` | Add production developer-product smoke. | Deployed docs/account/package links, MCP install, completion bridge, proof export, stale/demotion states, and support paths are verified. |

##### Node `50` V4 Relax Redline

Node `50` V4 is relaxed only when current evidence proves:

- Every claimed host/mode is represented in `kolm.coding_product_acceptance.v4`.
- VS Code, MCP, chat bridge, inline completion, passive capture, build finish, and code review are separated by package state, protocol, privacy, and claim scope.
- No source-only extension or MCP package is marketed as marketplace/registry/prod-proven.
- MCP protocol behavior is current, versioned, tested, and explicit about legacy transport support.
- Inline completion has prefix/suffix, cancellation, debounce, latency, acceptance, edit survival, fallback, and leakage proof, or every inline claim is demoted.
- Build-finishing packets capture repo state, instructions, owned/conflict paths, generated artifacts, commands, tests, screenshots, rollback, and evidence grade.
- Coding-product evals include issue-to-patch, terminal-finish, MCP safety, IDE install, artifact reuse, privacy, and reviewer-trust labs.
- Account, docs, CLI/TUI, extension, MCP, local server, and production surfaces expose the same host matrix and open gates.
- A security reviewer can prove no hidden capture, upload, tool execution, path access, or retention behavior exists.
- A developer can install, use, verify, export proof, and uninstall without asking a maintainer.

Until those are true, Kolm has strong developer-product primitives, but the coding-assistant/build-finishing product is not complete.

### Product Demo System, Media Assets, Video Explainer, Interactive Runner, And Visual Proof Library Ledger V1

Node `51` owns the demo and media system. Earlier sections already say that screenshots are required and that the site needs an interactive `.kolm` demo or a product-guided compiler flow. The current repo scan still shows a structural gap: public pages contain many demo claims, screenshots, generated images, OG cards, press-kit references, device demo controls, and screenshot reports, while static public HTML still does not expose a first-class `<video>` or `<iframe>` media surface. User feedback also called out bad demo/video elements, light-mode failures, weak visuals, and unclear hero proof. This node turns that loose media debt into a product contract.

The final state should not be a decorative video slapped into the hero. Kolm needs a demo system that teaches the three product promises through actual product actions:

1. route or paste an existing AI call;
2. capture or compile it into a signed `.kolm` artifact;
3. verify and run that artifact across runtime/device/cloud targets;
4. show cost, latency, quality, privacy, and audit evidence in the same moment.

#### Current Demo And Media Evidence

| Evidence class | Current repo evidence | Completion gap |
|---|---|---|
| Public media gap | The blueprint's static scan records `Video elements: 0` and `Iframe elements: 0`, and the public website/media ledger flags zero `<video>` and zero `<iframe>` elements outside account pages. | Either ship a real accessible product video/explainer or explicitly replace that strategy with an interactive proof demo. Do not keep talking about video if no media surface exists. |
| Homepage demo concepts | `public/index.html` contains a "This box is the CLI. Not a demo." section, live chat comments, huge hidden/prose proof blocks, media-caption CSS, and old W706/W844 references. | Above-the-fold proof must be one coherent aha path with sample input, live or safe simulated output, artifact evidence, and clean copy. Hidden walls of text and wave labels are not a conversion demo. |
| Interactive examples | `public/device.html` has runnable demo buttons and local recipe behavior; `public/captures.html` promotes rows to a playground; `public/k-score.html` has an interactive calculator; `public/roi.html` has sales-calculator concepts; `public/builder.html` and `/run` exist. | These demos need one registry, consistent design, accessible controls, sample data policy, privacy labels, error states, route/account handoff, and screenshot proof. |
| Asset footprint | `public/brand-hero.png`, `public/brand-hero-prism.png`, `public/brand-aurora-field.png`, `public/brand-logo-exploration.png`, `public/img/_generations/*`, `public/og/*`, and press-kit screenshot references exist. | Assets need source/provenance, compression, dimensions, alt policy, light/dark variants, license status, retention, deletion/rebuild rules, and performance budgets. |
| Screenshot evidence | `reports/ui-surface-audit/*/screenshots/*`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/qa-screenshots.mjs`, `scripts/screenshot-prod.mjs`, and final UI audit docs exist. | Screenshot audits are valuable but must be tied to the demo/media registry: which proof screenshot belongs to which claim, which route/theme/viewport, which version, and whether a human inspected media details. |
| Press and proof surfaces | `public/press.html` references screenshots, logo SVG, benchmark numbers, and editorial assets. | Press assets must not expose stale screenshots, unsubstantiated benchmark numbers, old branding, or images without usage rights and update cadence. |
| Multimodal/video backend | API inventory includes `/v1/video` stubs and the blueprint references `workers/video-tokenize/tokenize.mjs`; account has `public/account/multimodal-bakeoff.html`. | Marketing video/demo assets must not be confused with product video-processing capability. Multimodal product routes need fixtures, evals, cost limits, and account proof before broad claims. |

#### External Media Baseline

| Source | Current standard or guidance | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| Google video best practices, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/video` | Google recommends making videos discoverable with accessible video pages, stable URLs, thumbnails, and technical requirements for video indexing. | Public product videos need indexable pages, stable canonical URLs, accessible thumbnails, crawlable video files or embeds, and no hidden/blocked media. |
| Google `VideoObject` structured data, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/video` | Google can use `VideoObject` fields such as description, thumbnail URL, upload date, duration, and video URL to understand video results. | Any Kolm explainer video must ship structured data, thumbnail, upload date, duration, content/embed URL, transcript link, and validation evidence. |
| WCAG 2.2, `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | WCAG includes time-based media success criteria such as captions for prerecorded synchronized media and audio description or media alternative. | Product videos and animated demos need captions, transcripts, audio description or text alternative where visual information matters, keyboard controls, and no autoplay-with-audio traps. |
| W3C Media Accessibility User Requirements, `https://www.w3.org/TR/media-accessibility-reqs/` | W3C describes user needs for captions, transcripts, media controls, playback, contrast, customization, and access to visual/audio information. | Kolm's video player, demo widgets, charts, and animations should expose native controls or accessible custom controls, text alternatives, and adjustable/readable captions. |
| web.dev video performance guidance, `https://web.dev/learn/performance/video-performance` | Video performance guidance covers poster images, preload behavior, lazy loading, and preventing large media from hurting page performance. | Demo/video media needs poster, reserved aspect ratio, preload/lazy strategy, responsive encodes, no LCP regressions, and mobile network budgets. |
| UI/UX Pro Max media and performance rules | The local UI/UX skill requires image dimensions, lazy loading, Core Web Vitals discipline, no hover-only critical interactions, reduced motion, consistent media style, and accessible alternatives. | Every demo/media surface needs deterministic dimensions, non-hover controls, reduced-motion path, light/dark visual proof, and performance budgets. |

#### Demo Registry Contract

Every demo, video, screenshot, generated image, and proof media asset should be registered. This prevents hero assets, press screenshots, social cards, and account proof from drifting apart.

```json
{
  "demo_id": "home-kolm-runner",
  "master_node": "51",
  "surface": "public-home",
  "route": "/",
  "product_promises": ["route_capture", "compile_distill", "deploy_verify"],
  "type": "interactive-runner",
  "status": "required",
  "source_paths": ["public/index.html", "public/kolm-chat.js", "src/router.js"],
  "sample_inputs": [
    {
      "label": "OpenAI call",
      "privacy": "synthetic",
      "input_path": "examples/home-runner/openai-call.json",
      "expected_artifact": "reports/demos/home-runner/sample.kolm"
    }
  ],
  "states": ["idle", "loading", "success", "error", "rate_limited", "offline"],
  "accessibility": {
    "keyboard": true,
    "screen_reader_summary": true,
    "reduced_motion": true,
    "captions": "not_applicable",
    "transcript": "not_applicable"
  },
  "evidence": {
    "screenshots": ["reports/ui-surface-audit/.../home-runner-light-desktop.png"],
    "smoke": ["node scripts/demo-smoke.cjs --demo home-kolm-runner"],
    "structured_data": "not_applicable",
    "performance_budget": "LCP<=2.5s, INP<=200ms, CLS<=0.1"
  }
}
```

#### Demo And Media Taxonomy

| Demo/media type | Purpose | Completion bar |
|---|---|---|
| Homepage interactive runner | Five-second aha: paste model call, see owned artifact/proof. | Works above the fold, has sample data, loading/error states, privacy label, artifact output, copy/download/share, account/docs handoff, and dark/light/mobile screenshots. |
| Product explainer video | Explain category and three promises in 60-90 seconds. | Has poster, captions, transcript, chapters, structured data, reserved dimensions, no autoplay audio, reduced-motion/static alternative, and performance budget. |
| Side-by-side code comparison | Show before/after: raw provider call versus `.kolm` artifact and two-line route swap. | Code is copyable, accessible, syntax-highlighted, not horizontally broken on mobile, and backed by actual docs/API examples. |
| ROI/calculator demo | Make savings tangible and shareable. | Inputs are labeled, assumptions visible, sensitivity ranges shown, no unsupported guarantee, and share/export produces scoped evidence. |
| Device/runtime demo | Prove artifact runs outside hosted API. | Shows runtime target, artifact hash, latency/cost, offline/local state, fallback, and unsupported target labels. |
| Account demo/proof screenshot | Let buyers see post-auth operational product. | Uses synthetic/demo tenant or redacted real data, shows loading/empty/error, account permissions, and current readiness gates. |
| Press/OG/social assets | Make external coverage and search/social previews accurate. | Current branding, correct dimensions, alt text, license/source, last updated date, and no stale claims. |
| Screenshot audit evidence | Prove route rendering after edits. | Each screenshot is linked to route/theme/viewport/version, has retention owner, and humans inspect critical pages beyond automated pass/fail. |

#### Completion Specification

| Branch | Done means |
|---|---|
| `51.1` Demo taxonomy, primary aha moments, homepage runner, product walkthroughs, screenshots, videos, and proof hierarchy | A demo registry defines every interactive demo, video, screenshot, proof asset, product promise, owner, route, state, evidence, and claim scope. |
| `51.2` Interactive `.kolm` runner, sample inputs, artifact outputs, privacy labels, loading/error states, and share/export actions | Homepage/product runner lets users paste or load a sample provider call, produces a scoped `.kolm` artifact/proof packet, and handles privacy, errors, rate limits, copy/download/share, and account handoff. |
| `51.3` Video explainer, captions, transcript, poster, chapters, structured data, performance budget, and fallback content | Any product video is accessible, crawlable, performant, captioned, transcript-backed, structured-data validated, and has a static alternative. |
| `51.4` Asset pipeline, generated images, OG cards, screenshots, press kit, retention policy, accessibility, and provenance | Every image/video/screenshot/OG card has source, owner, dimensions, format, alt policy, license, generation command or design source, compression budget, and stale-after date. |
| `51.5` Screenshot/video QA, light/dark media visibility, mobile/desktop framing, reduced motion, Core Web Vitals, and manual review | Media QA covers dark/light, desktop/mobile, zoom, high contrast, reduced motion, keyboard, screen reader, LCP/INP/CLS, and human review for critical demos. |
| `51.6` Evidence: demo registry, media manifest, video packet, runner smoke, route screenshot matrix, structured-data validation, and production proof | Release packet links demo registry, media manifest, structured data validation, screenshots, smoke logs, performance reports, production URLs, and known blockers. |
