#### Node 51 Relax Redline

Node `51` is done only when Kolm can answer these questions from current product evidence:

1. What is the single primary demo path a first-time visitor should complete in under two minutes?
2. Does the demo prove all three product promises or only the gateway/API surface?
3. If a product video exists, where are the poster, captions, transcript, structured data, performance proof, and static fallback?
4. If a product video does not exist, where did public copy, SEO, and sales materials stop promising one?
5. Which media assets are source-controlled, generated, third-party, temporary, or stale, and who owns each one?
6. Which screenshot/video/manual checks prove the demo and media render correctly in light/dark, desktop/mobile, zoom, reduced motion, and production?

#### Node 51 Demo Media And Proof System Addendum V2

Node `52` remains unused. Demo video, product media, screenshots, runner proof, and visual product storytelling belong to node `51`; code completion belongs to node `50`, SEO belongs to node `21`, public-page conversion belongs to node `6`, account proof belongs to node `12`, pricing and sales handoff belong to node `16`, release evidence belongs to node `30`, and product graph truth belongs to node `44`. This addendum updates the V1 diagnosis: the problem is no longer only that the site lacks a video file. The current tree has hero video assets, but the public site does not yet expose them as a registered, accessible, search-visible, performance-budgeted product proof.

##### Node `51.0` Current Repo Evidence Delta

| Evidence | Current observation | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Hero video files | `public/video/kolm-hero.mp4`, `public/video/kolm-hero.webm`, and `public/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg` exist, totaling about `3.08 MB`. Reference checks across public HTML, JS, and CSS found `Refs=0` for those files. | Either wire these assets into a real product-video packet or delete/demote them. Unreferenced media is not proof and should not be treated as shipped. |
| Public HTML media usage | A scan of `765` public HTML/JS/CSS files found `VideoTags=0`, `IframeTags=0`, `VideoObjectRefs=0`, `VttRefs=0`, `Mp4Refs=1`, `WebmRefs=0`, `PosterRefs=2`, and `TranscriptRefs=117`. | The site has transcript/copy scaffolding but no first-class video surface, captions, or structured video proof. |
| Media footprint | Public media inventory found `238` image assets, about `43.8 MB`; generated images account for `42` assets and about `28.6 MB`. `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png` is about `7.03 MB`. | Node `51` needs asset ownership, compression budgets, dimensions, provenance, source prompts, rebuild/delete policy, and route ownership for every visual asset. |
| Demo tooling | `scripts/video/demo-page.html`, `scripts/video/gen-demo-video.mjs`, `scripts/video/gen-hero-video.mjs`, `scripts/video/render-demo.mjs`, `scripts/screenshot-demo.cjs`, `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/qa-screenshots.mjs`, and `public/cdn/kolm-assets/tui-demo.cast` exist. | The codebase has ingredients for visual proof, but they must be connected to a canonical demo/media registry and release packet. |
| Homepage proof | `public/index.html` still has a visible concept that says "This box is the CLI. Not a demo." while the product needs a five-second first-screen proof of the three product promises. | Hero demo content must show one coherent path: route/capture, compile/distill, and deploy/verify. A non-demo proof block is not enough for the public homepage. |
| Press asset mismatch | `public/press.html` links `/brand-hero.png` while the visible link text says "download SVG". | Press media must have exact format labels, correct file types, usage rights, dimensions, and dated freshness. |
| Product-video ambiguity | `public/docs/multimodal/video.html`, video-tokenization tests, and multimodal account pages exist, but these are product capability surfaces, not marketing explainer assets. | Product demo video and multimodal/video-processing capability must be separated in copy, docs, schema, tests, and screenshots. |

##### Node `51.1` Current External Baseline

| Baseline | Source | Kolm requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Video discoverability | Google video best practices require videos to be crawlable and discoverable with stable pages, thumbnails, and technical video access. | Any public Kolm product video needs a stable canonical page, reachable video or embed URL, thumbnail, transcript/caption links, sitemap or structured data linkage, and no blocked media path. |
| Video structured data | Google's video structured-data guidance and Schema.org `VideoObject` define fields such as name, description, thumbnail, upload date, duration, content URL, embed URL, and related metadata. | A Kolm explainer video must ship a validated `VideoObject` packet with `name`, `description`, `thumbnailUrl`, `uploadDate`, `duration`, `contentUrl` or `embedUrl`, transcript URL, and proof that the fields match the rendered page. |
| Accessible media | WCAG 2.2 and W3C Media Accessibility User Requirements require captions or text alternatives for time-based media and describe controls, transcripts, customization, and access to visual/audio information. | Kolm media must provide captions, transcripts, keyboard operation, visible controls, reduced-motion fallback, no autoplay audio trap, and text alternatives for critical visual information. |
| Video performance | web.dev video performance guidance emphasizes poster images, preload discipline, lazy loading, responsive encodes, and preventing media from hurting page performance. | Hero media needs reserved aspect ratio, explicit poster, `preload` discipline, responsive MP4/WebM/possibly AV1 ladder, mobile network budget, and Core Web Vitals checks. |
| Enterprise SaaS UX | The UI/UX Pro Max local checklist prioritizes accessibility, touch targets, performance, layout, typography/color consistency, reduced motion, and predictable navigation. | The runner/video area cannot be decorative. It must be keyboardable, stable, visually coherent in light/dark, non-hover-dependent, low-shift, and tied to action. |

##### Node `51.2` Media Asset Registry Contract V2

Every product-facing media asset must be registered before it can be considered shipped. This includes videos, posters, OG images, generated bitmaps, screenshots, diagrams, demo casts, press-kit files, account proof screenshots, and hero backgrounds.

```json
{
  "asset_id": "home-hero-video-v1",
  "master_node": "51",
  "type": "video",
  "route_owners": ["/", "/product"],
  "source_paths": [
    "public/video/kolm-hero.mp4",
    "public/video/kolm-hero.webm",
    "public/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg"
  ],
  "generated_by": "scripts/video/gen-hero-video.mjs",
  "provenance": {
    "human_owner": "marketing-product",
    "prompt_or_script": "scripts/video/hero-storyboard.json",
    "source_data": "synthetic",
    "license": "owned"
  },
  "dimensions": {
    "width": 1920,
    "height": 1080,
    "aspect_ratio": "16:9"
  },
  "bytes": {
    "mp4": 1640110,
    "webm": 1438521,
    "poster": 227251
  },
  "accessibility": {
    "captions": "public/video/kolm-hero.en.vtt",
    "transcript": "public/video/kolm-hero-transcript.html",
    "audio_description": "text-alternative",
    "reduced_motion_fallback": "public/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg"
  },
  "seo": {
    "video_object": true,
    "canonical_page": "https://kolm.ai/",
    "thumbnail_url": "https://kolm.ai/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg",
    "content_url": "https://kolm.ai/video/kolm-hero.mp4"
  },
  "performance_budget": {
    "hero_lcp": "<=2.5s on median mobile profile",
    "cls": "<=0.1",
    "initial_media_bytes": "<=350KB before interaction",
    "autoplay": "muted-only-or-disabled"
  },
  "proof": {
    "local_smoke": "node scripts/demo-media-smoke.cjs --asset home-hero-video-v1",
    "structured_data_check": "node scripts/audit-video-object.cjs --route /",
    "screenshots": [
      "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/home-light-mobile.png",
      "reports/ui-surface-audit/latest/home-dark-desktop.png"
    ],
    "manual_review": "docs/release/media-proof-YYYY-MM-DD.md"
  },
  "status": "required"
}
```

The registry must reject:

- public media with no owner;
- generated images with no source prompt/script or rebuild path;
- video files with no caption/transcript or explicit non-speaking exemption;
- press/social assets with stale file-type labels;
- hero assets above budget without a measured exception;
- screenshots that cannot be traced to route, theme, viewport, commit/build, and reviewer;
- assets that imply product claims not present in the product graph or readiness ledger.

##### Node `51.3` Demo Surface Contract V2

The demo system must prove all three product surfaces instead of optimizing only for "one AI API" or one gateway story.

```json
{
  "demo_id": "home-three-promise-runner",
  "master_node": "51",
  "primary_route": "/",
  "secondary_routes": ["/product", "/compile", "/distill", "/devices", "/account/overview"],
  "product_promises": [
    {
      "id": "route_capture",
      "user_question": "Can Kolm own my existing model traffic without a rewrite?",
      "proof": "paste OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini call, produce captured call record and provider-neutral route"
    },
    {
      "id": "compile_distill",
      "user_question": "Can Kolm turn traffic into a smaller signed artifact?",
      "proof": "show signed .kolm artifact, teacher/student summary, K-Score scope, cost and latency estimate"
    },
    {
      "id": "deploy_verify",
      "user_question": "Can Kolm run that artifact on cloud, local, edge, and device targets?",
      "proof": "show runtime targets, artifact hash, readiness gates, verification command, and account handoff"
    }
  ],
  "states": ["idle", "sample_loaded", "running", "success", "error", "rate_limited", "offline"],
  "privacy": {
    "default_input": "synthetic",
    "paste_warning": "do not paste secrets",
    "retention": "session-only unless signed in and saved"
  },
  "accessibility": {
    "keyboard_flow": true,
    "screen_reader_summary": true,
    "no_hover_dependency": true,
    "reduced_motion": true
  },
  "evidence": {
    "api_routes": ["/v1/product/graph", "/v1/artifacts", "/v1/compile"],
    "cli_equivalent": "kolm compile examples/home-runner/openai-call.json --json",
    "screenshots_required": ["light-desktop", "light-mobile", "dark-desktop", "dark-mobile"],
    "production_smoke": "node scripts/demo-smoke.cjs --demo home-three-promise-runner --prod"
  }
}
```

Completion rule: the hero can use video, runner, code comparison, or a hybrid, but the first visible proof must answer "what is this, who is it for, why now" through the three product promises in under five seconds. It must not use a phrase that sounds owned but unclear, such as a vague "turn model traffic into owned AI," unless the adjacent proof spells out routing, compile/distill, and deploy/verify in plain operational language.

##### Node `51.4` Product Video Packet V2

If Kolm ships a video, it must be a product artifact, not a decorative background.

Required files and fields:

| Field | Required shape |
|---|---|
| Poster | `public/video/<id>-poster.jpg` or `.webp`, exact dimensions, under route-specific byte budget, visible fallback. |
| Encodes | At least MP4/H.264 and WebM/VP9 or AV1 where browser support permits; mobile-friendly bitrate ladder for non-hero full video. |
| Captions | `.vtt` captions for spoken words and critical audio cues. |
| Transcript | HTML transcript linked near the player and discoverable from structured data. |
| Chapters | 60 to 90 second product demo chapters: problem, capture, compile/distill, deploy/verify, account proof, next action. |
| Structured data | `VideoObject` JSON-LD matching rendered content, with name, description, thumbnail, upload date, duration, content/embed URL, and transcript reference where supported. |
| Accessibility | Keyboard controls, visible play/pause, no autoplay audio, focus states, reduced-motion/static fallback, and text alternative for visual-only proof. |
| Performance | Reserved aspect ratio, lazy/nonblocking load unless it is the LCP asset, no layout shift, no hero route regression, measured desktop and mobile budgets. |
| Claim scope | Video claims must map to product graph nodes and readiness status; benchmark, package, partner, or certification-gated claims need explicit scope. |

##### Node `51.5` Visual Proof Library V2

The screenshot system is not complete just because an all-route audit passes. Visual proof must be queryable by product claim.

The library needs these indexes:

1. `claim_id -> route -> screenshot set -> reviewer -> pass/fail -> linked product graph node`.
2. `asset_id -> route -> theme -> viewport -> light/dark proof -> accessibility notes`.
3. `demo_id -> state -> screenshot -> API fixture -> CLI equivalent -> account handoff`.
4. `media_id -> poster -> video -> captions -> transcript -> structured data -> performance proof`.
5. `press_asset_id -> file -> format label -> dimensions -> usage rights -> freshness date`.

Manual review remains required for:

- hero first impression and message clarity;
- button/nav underline state;
- light-mode media rendering;
- mobile truncation and horizontal scroll;
- code-block readability;
- video poster and first frame quality;
- account post-auth empty/loading/error states;
- product proof accuracy against readiness gates.

| Ticket | Node | Work | Acceptance proof |
|---|---|---|---|
| `W-MEDIA-V2-001` | `51.2` | Create `docs/demo-media-registry.json`. | Registry covers every video, poster, OG image, generated image, press asset, demo screenshot, cast, and hero background with owner, route, provenance, bytes, dimensions, accessibility, and proof. |
| `W-MEDIA-V2-002` | `51.2` | Decide fate of `public/video/kolm-hero.*`. | Assets are either wired into a complete video packet or removed/demoted with copy updated; no unreferenced hero video remains. |

| `W-MEDIA-V2-020` | `51.6` | Create release media proof packet. | Release packet includes registry diff, assets added/removed, screenshots, video proof, a11y proof, performance proof, structured-data proof, and remaining scoped exceptions. |

##### Node `51` V2 Relax Redline

Node `51` cannot relax until these statements are true on the current local tree and the deployed site:

- every public media asset is registered or removed;
- `public/video/kolm-hero.*` is either wired into a complete accessible video packet or intentionally unshipped;
- every claimed video has captions, transcript, poster, structured data, performance proof, and reduced-motion fallback;
- the first-screen demo proves routing/capture, compile/distill, and deploy/verify without jargon or hidden paragraphs;
- the interactive runner has synthetic sample data, secrets warning, error/rate-limit/offline states, and account handoff;
- screenshot proof is indexed by product claim, route, theme, viewport, asset, and reviewer;
- press/social/OG assets have exact file labels, current brand/copy, and source ownership;
- marketing product video and multimodal video-processing capability are separated in docs, tests, and UI labels;
- local and production demo/media smokes pass without warnings;
- all video/demo claims match the product graph and readiness closeout ledger.

#### Node 51 Demo Conversion Proof, Media QA, And Interactive Runner Addendum V3

Node `52` remains unused. Demo, video, runner, press media, OG assets, screenshots, and product visual proof belong to node `51`. Node `51` V3 exists because the repo now has the ingredients for an excellent demo system, but the product proof is still not enforceable enough. Hero media files exist, screenshot reports exist, demos exist, runner pages exist, generated images exist, and public proof copy exists. That is not the same as a coherent demo product that proves Kolm's three promises in a visitor's first two minutes.

The V3 rule is stricter than V2: every demo surface must be tested as a buyer action, not as a page asset. A first-time AI-company visitor should be able to see the category, understand the three outcomes, run or watch a real proof path, inspect the artifact or readiness state, and choose the next action without reading paragraphs or decoding internal product language.

##### Node 51 V3 Current Evidence Recheck

| Evidence class | Current observed state | V3 consequence |
|---|---|---|
| Hero video files | `public/video/kolm-hero.mp4` is 1,640,110 bytes, `public/video/kolm-hero.webm` is 1,438,521 bytes, and `public/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg` is 227,251 bytes. | These assets total 3,305,882 bytes and must be either wired into a complete video proof packet or demoted/removed from release evidence. |
| Public media references | A current public HTML scan found no real `<video>`, `<iframe>`, `VideoObject`, `.mp4`, `.webm`, or `poster=` reference after excluding unrelated `manifest.webmanifest` links. | The site cannot claim a shipped explainer video from file presence alone. Rendered video proof is missing until the route references, captions, transcript, and structured data exist. |
| Demo proof pages | Current demo-adjacent surfaces include `public/index.html`, `public/builder.html`, `public/device.html`, `public/docs/cli/demo.html`, `public/docs/multimodal/video.html`, `public/k-score.html`, `public/roi.html`, and account proof pages. | These need one canonical demo registry and a first-value sequence rather than disconnected demos with different states and copy. |
| Visual assets | Current assets include brand hero images, generated images, OG cards, press-kit files, and many screenshot reports. | Visual assets need ownership, route use, dimensions, byte budgets, provenance, license, alt/caption policy, and regeneration/delete rules. |
| Screenshot machinery | `scripts/ui-surface-audit.cjs`, `scripts/qa-screenshots.mjs`, `scripts/screenshot-prod.mjs`, and many route screenshots exist. | Screenshot count is not enough; screenshots must be indexed by product claim, route, state, viewport, theme, media asset, and human reviewer. |
| Product confusion risk | Product-video docs and multimodal video-processing docs coexist. | Marketing explainer video and product video-tokenization/multimodal capability must remain separate in route labels, copy, tests, schema, and proof. |

##### Node 51 V3 Refreshed External Baseline

| Source | Current fact imported into the spec tree | Kolm product implication |
|---|---|---|
| Google video best practices, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/video` | Google can find videos referenced by common HTML video elements, recommends stable thumbnail and video URLs, and says videos should not rely on user actions to load. | Kolm video proof needs rendered HTML, stable media URLs, stable thumbnail, crawlable watch route, and metadata that matches the actual visible video. |
| Google VideoObject structured data, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/video` | Required `VideoObject` fields include `name`, `thumbnailUrl`, and `uploadDate`; recommended fields include `contentUrl`, `description`, `duration`, and `embedUrl`. | Any Kolm explainer video needs a validated JSON-LD packet with matching visible content and reachable media bytes. |
| WCAG 2.2, `https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/` | WCAG 2.2 includes prerecorded media requirements for video-only alternatives, captions for prerecorded synchronized media, and audio description or media alternatives. | Kolm videos and animated product demos need captions, transcript, text alternative or audio description where visual information is essential, and no autoplay audio trap. |
| web.dev video performance, `https://web.dev/learn/performance/video-performance` | Video performance guidance calls out posters, preload discipline, lazy loading, codec/container compression, LCP impact, and third-party embed cost. | Kolm hero media must reserve dimensions, use poster/fallback correctly, avoid unnecessary initial bytes, and pass route performance budgets before release. |
| MDN video element, `https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/video` | HTML video supports timed text tracks through nested `<track>` elements and WebVTT files. | Captions and chapters should be real track assets, not just hidden copy elsewhere on the page. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | Local UX guidance prioritizes keyboard access, visible focus, touch target size, loading/error feedback, reduced motion, layout stability, color contrast, and consistent navigation. | The runner and media player must be keyboardable, non-hover-dependent, stable in light/dark, clear in loading/error/blocked states, and visually coherent with the product system. |

##### Demo Conversion Contract V3

Every product demo must be serializable as `kolm.demo_conversion.v3`.

```json
{
  "demo_id": "home-three-promise-proof",
  "master_node": "51",
  "route": "/",
  "audience": "ai-company-founder|platform-engineer|ml-lead|enterprise-buyer",
  "five_second_answer": {
    "what": "Kolm turns existing model traffic into signed, portable AI artifacts.",
    "who": "AI companies and enterprise teams moving from API calls to owned runtime behavior.",
    "why_now": "Costs, lock-in, audit pressure, and edge/device deployment are all converging."
  },
  "proof_steps": [
    {
      "id": "route_capture",
      "input": "OpenAI-compatible call",
      "output": "captured provider-neutral request with cost/latency/privacy metadata"
    },
    {
      "id": "compile_distill",
      "input": "captured repeated behavior",
      "output": "signed .kolm artifact with K-Score scope and teacher/student evidence"
    },
    {
      "id": "deploy_verify",
      "input": "artifact hash",
      "output": "runtime/device/cloud target readiness and verification command"
    }
  ],
  "interaction_states": [
    "idle",
    "sample_loaded",
    "editing",
    "running",
    "success",
    "quality_blocked",
    "rate_limited",
    "offline",
    "error",
    "handoff_to_account"
  ],
  "privacy": {
    "sample_data": "synthetic",
    "paste_warning": "never paste secrets",
    "retention": "session_only_until_saved",
    "screenshots_redacted": true
  },
  "evidence": {
    "api_routes": [],
    "cli_equivalent": "",
    "account_handoff": "",
    "screenshots": [],
    "video_packet": null,
    "production_smoke": ""
  }
}
```

No demo may be marked shipped unless it has:

- a route;
- a user outcome;
- a sample input and output;
- a privacy policy;
- a proof of at least one product promise;
- visible idle/loading/success/error/blocked states;
- keyboard and screen-reader path;
- screenshot evidence in light/dark desktop/mobile;
- a CLI or API equivalent where applicable;
- a production smoke or explicit local-only label.

##### Product Video Packet Contract V3

Every product video must be serializable as `kolm.product_video.v3`.

```json
{
  "video_id": "kolm-hero",
  "master_node": "51",
  "route": "/",
  "purpose": "explainer|walkthrough|proof|support|sales",
  "duration_seconds": 90,
  "storyboard": [
    "existing model call",
    "capture and evidence",
    "compile or distill into .kolm",
    "run anywhere target",
    "account proof and next action"
  ],
  "files": {
    "mp4": "public/video/kolm-hero.mp4",
    "webm": "public/video/kolm-hero.webm",
    "poster": "public/video/kolm-hero-poster.jpg",
    "captions": "public/video/kolm-hero.en.vtt",
    "transcript": "public/video/kolm-hero-transcript.html"
  },
  "structured_data": {
    "type": "VideoObject",
    "name": "",
    "description": "",
    "thumbnailUrl": "",
    "uploadDate": "",
    "duration": "",
    "contentUrl": ""
  },
  "accessibility": {
    "controls": true,
    "captions": true,
    "transcript": true,
    "keyboard": true,
    "reduced_motion_fallback": true,
    "audio_description_or_text_alternative": true
  },
  "performance": {
    "reserved_aspect_ratio": true,
    "preload": "none|metadata",
    "poster_strategy": "preload_if_lcp",
    "initial_bytes_budget": 350000,
    "mobile_route_budget": "pass"
  },
  "proof": {
    "local_render": "",
    "production_render": "",
    "structured_data_check": "",
    "a11y_check": "",
    "performance_check": ""
  }
}
```

Video redline:

- video files without rendered route references are not shipped;
- rendered video without captions/transcript is not accessible proof;
- structured data without visible video is invalid product proof;
- autoplay background video without a static equivalent cannot carry critical product meaning;
- a video that explains only one product promise cannot be the primary hero proof for all three promises.

##### Interactive Runner State Model V3

The runner should behave like a product, not a code-themed illustration.

| State | Required user-visible behavior |
|---|---|
| `idle` | Shows one concise sample input, one primary run action, one privacy note, and one expected output preview. |
| `sample_loaded` | Lets users switch among gateway capture, distill/compile, and deploy/verify samples without leaving the route. |
| `editing` | Accepts pasted provider call safely, highlights secret-looking values, and provides clear reset/sample controls. |
| `running` | Shows progress with deterministic steps: parse, redact, capture, compile/distill estimate, artifact proof, target readiness. |
| `success` | Shows `.kolm` artifact summary, hash, route/provider savings, K-Score/eval scope, runtime targets, and account/docs handoff. |
| `quality_blocked` | Explains why the artifact cannot be claimed yet and names missing examples, evals, provider keys, package proof, or external gate. |
| `rate_limited` | Explains current limit, preserves user input locally, and offers sample replay. |
| `offline` | Falls back to local synthetic demo and states what is simulated. |
| `error` | Provides exact recovery, not a generic failure message. |
| `handoff_to_account` | Carries demo state into account only after explicit user action and privacy confirmation. |

Runner UX redline:

- no hidden paragraphs required to understand the result;
- no hover-only controls;
- no text truncation in buttons/cards;
- no color-only status;
- no layout shift when output appears;
- no raw secrets in screenshot proof;
- no unsupported product claims in sample outputs.

##### Media Proof Library Contract V3

The screenshot and media library must become queryable product evidence.

```json
{
  "proof_id": "home-demo-success-light-mobile",
  "master_node": "51",
  "claim_id": "route_capture_compile_deploy_demo",
  "route": "/",
  "state": "success",
  "theme": "light",
  "viewport": "mobile",
  "asset_ids": ["home-three-promise-proof", "kolm-hero-poster"],
  "captured_at": "ISO-8601",
  "source": "local|production",
  "reviewer": "human|automated",
  "checks": {
    "visible": true,
    "no_overlap": true,
    "copy_clear": true,
    "media_loaded": true,
    "a11y_state": true,
    "claim_scope_visible": true
  },
  "file": "reports/ui-surface-audit/.../home-demo-success-light-mobile.png"
}
```

Library rules:

- every screenshot must know which claim it proves;
- every media asset must know which routes and screenshots use it;
- every demo state must have at least one screenshot in final release proof;
- every production screenshot must record origin, commit/build, theme, viewport, auth state, and route;
- automated screenshot pass is necessary but not enough for hero/demo/media quality; the final packet needs human review notes.

##### Demo Metrics V3

| Metric | Required interpretation |
|---|---|
| `five_second_comprehension_rate` | Testers can state what Kolm does, who it is for, and why it matters after five seconds on the hero. |
| `demo_start_rate` | Visitors who start the runner or video from first viewport. |
| `demo_success_rate` | Demo sessions that reach artifact proof, readiness proof, or account handoff. |
| `time_to_aha_seconds` | Time from first paint to visible proof of `.kolm` artifact, savings, or run-anywhere target. |
| `runner_error_recovery_rate` | Users who recover from invalid input, blocked quality, rate-limit, or offline states. |
| `video_completion_rate` | Users who watch through the capture, compile/distill, and deploy/verify chapters. |
| `media_lcp_ms` | Largest contentful paint impact of hero media by route/device/theme. |
| `caption_transcript_coverage` | Percent of product videos with captions, transcript, and text alternative. |
| `proof_screenshot_coverage` | Percent of claims with route/state/theme/viewport screenshot evidence. |
| `demo_to_account_activation` | Demo sessions that become account imports, compiles, artifact saves, or enterprise inquiries. |

##### Node `51` V3 Relax Redline

Node `51` V3 can relax only when current local and production evidence proves:

- every public demo is registered as `kolm.demo_conversion.v3`;
- every product video is registered as `kolm.product_video.v3` or explicitly unshipped;
- the hero or first product proof demonstrates route/capture, compile/distill, and deploy/verify without relying on hidden paragraphs;
- `public/video/kolm-hero.*` is rendered with captions/transcript/schema/performance proof or removed from shipped claims;
- the runner exposes all required states and has secret-safe sample handling;
- every demo has CLI/API/account equivalent proof where applicable;
- media assets have owner, provenance, route use, dimensions, byte budget, license, accessibility, and rebuild/delete policy;
- screenshots are indexed by claim, route, state, theme, viewport, asset, source, and reviewer;
- video SEO, structured data, WCAG media requirements, performance budgets, and reduced-motion behavior are verified;
- production smoke proves the deployed demo/media system, not only local static files.

The V3 demo standard is: a skeptical buyer can understand Kolm, see it work, inspect proof, and know the next action before trust decays.

#### Node 51 V4 Demo Proof Rooms, Media Production QA, And First-Screen Aha Ledger

Node `52` remains unused. This is still node `51` work because the unresolved branch is not "more frontend" in general; it is the exact proof system for demo, video, screenshots, product media, and the first-screen proof of Kolm's three product surfaces. Public-page conversion stays in node `6`, visual-system execution stays in node `30`, narrative stays in node `44`, accessibility and content integrity stay in node `21`, and rendered-proof mechanics stay in node `45`. Node `51` owns the buyer-facing demonstration packets that prove those branches work together.

The V4 upgrade turns node `51` from a media QA checklist into a production room. A production room is a release unit with script, assets, routes, telemetry, accessibility, performance budgets, screenshots, production smoke, and owner handoff. No hero, demo, video, image, screenshot, or interactive runner may be called complete if it is not attached to one of these rooms.

##### Node 51 V4 Current Evidence Delta

| Evidence class | Current observed state | V4 consequence |
|---|---|---|
| Media file inventory | A current public media scan found 181 SVG files, 44 PNG files, 1 JPG file, 1 WebP file, 1 MP4 file, and 1 WebM file across `public/video`, `public/videos`, `public/media`, `public/assets`, `public/img`, and `public/og`. | Media is large enough to need lifecycle ownership, not ad hoc replacement. Every file must be kept, compressed, regenerated, archived, or deleted with a reason. |
| Heavy first-screen risk | `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png` is 7,031,506 bytes. Many generated PNGs are 700 KB to 1.45 MB. | A 7 MB hero background cannot be a default critical-path asset without explicit performance proof. Generated PNGs over budget need responsive variants, WebP/AVIF alternatives, or demotion below the fold. |
| Existing hero video bytes | `public/video/kolm-hero.mp4` is 1,640,110 bytes, `public/video/kolm-hero.webm` is 1,438,521 bytes, and the poster is 227,251 bytes. | The video packet is plausible, but still not shipped proof unless a route renders it with captions, transcript, schema, stable poster, no light-mode failure, and performance proof. |
| Route proof gap | Earlier V3 evidence found no real rendered `<video>`, `<iframe>`, `VideoObject`, `.mp4`, `.webm`, or `poster=` reference in public HTML after excluding unrelated manifest links. | File presence remains insufficient. The implementation ticket is not "make a video"; it is "make a route-level proof packet that survives crawler, accessibility, screenshot, and production checks." |
| Demo architecture risk | Demo, runner, ROI, K-Score, device, builder, docs, and account pages exist as separate proof fragments. | V4 requires a shared demo-room registry so the homepage, docs, account, and sales handoff all prove the same three product promises instead of fragmenting the story. |
| Copy and jargon risk | Prior hero copy such as "turn model traffic into owned AI" can be interpreted as serving only API routing/capture. | V4 requires first-screen copy to cover all three surfaces: gateway/capture, compile/distill, and enterprise-to-device deployment. Short copy wins only if it is complete. |

##### Node 51 V4 Imported Baselines

| Source | Current fact imported into node `51` | Kolm implication |
|---|---|---|
| web.dev Core Web Vitals, `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | The current Core Web Vitals set is LCP, INP, and CLS, with good thresholds at LCP within 2.5 seconds, INP at 200 milliseconds or less, and CLS at 0.1 or less, measured at the 75th percentile across mobile and desktop. | Demo/video/media release proof must record LCP, INP or lab proxy, and CLS by route and viewport. Hero media cannot be considered good because it looks good in one screenshot. |
| Google Search Central video best practices, `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/video` | Google recommends videos be discoverable through common HTML video/embed/player elements, stable thumbnails, stable video URLs, metadata, and no reliance on user action to load the video. | If Kolm uses an explainer video as conversion proof, the watch route must expose crawlable video structure, stable thumbnail, and matching structured data. |
| UI/UX Pro Max local guidance | Priority guidance emphasizes accessibility, touch/keyboard interaction, performance, layout stability, visual consistency, one primary action, and non-hover-dependent controls. | The demo runner and hero proof must be usable, touchable, keyboardable, and stable in light/dark without relying on popout hover styling or buried paragraphs. |

##### Master Spec Tree Ownership Rule V4

Node `51` is the primary owner for all demo proof rooms. Cross-links are allowed, but ownership cannot be split across vague "frontend" or "marketing" buckets.

| Artifact or concern | Primary node | Required secondary links |
|---|---:|---|
| Homepage first-screen demo | `51` | `6`, `30`, `44`, `45` |
| Interactive `.kolm` runner | `51` | `2`, `7`, `11`, `21`, `37`, `47` |
| Explainer video | `51` | `6`, `21`, `30`, `32`, `45` |
| Product media and generated images | `51` | `14`, `21`, `30`, `45` |
| Screenshot proof library | `51` | `9`, `13`, `40`, `45` |
| Demo telemetry and conversion metrics | `51` | `15`, `16`, `25`, `44` |
| Demo production smoke | `51` | `9`, `29`, `39`, `40` |

If a future change touches a hero video, screenshot, runner widget, demo page, generated product image, media manifest, or "watch how it works" route, it must update node `51` and leave node `52` unused unless the artifact is not a demo/media/proof artifact.

##### Demo Proof Room Contract V4

Every route-level proof room must serialize as `kolm.demo_proof_room.v4`.

```json
{
  "room_id": "home-first-screen-three-surface-proof",
  "master_node": "51",
  "route": "/",
  "status": "draft|implemented|local_proven|prod_proven|retired",
  "owner_nodes": {
    "primary": "51",
    "secondary": ["6", "21", "30", "44", "45"]
  },
  "first_screen": {
    "headline": "",
    "subhead": "",
    "primary_action": "",
    "secondary_action": "",
    "paragraph_count": 0,
    "jargon_terms_allowed": [],
    "five_second_test": "what/who/why-now plus all three product surfaces"
  },
  "product_surfaces": [
    {
      "id": "gateway_capture",
      "plain_outcome": "Replace raw model calls with captured, audited, provider-flexible behavior.",
      "proof": "paste OpenAI-compatible call, inspect captured artifact, see route/cost/privacy metadata"
    },
    {
      "id": "compile_distill",
      "plain_outcome": "Turn repeated frontier-model behavior into a signed task artifact or student.",
      "proof": "show compile/distill plan, K-Score scope, teacher/student evidence, and blocked-claim labels"
    },
    {
      "id": "run_anywhere",
      "plain_outcome": "Move enterprise models from cloud APIs to governed runtimes and devices.",
      "proof": "show target readiness, device/cloud fit, object storage state, and verification command"
    }
  ],
  "assets": {
    "video_packet": null,
    "image_assets": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "captions": [],
    "transcript": null
  },
  "runner": {
    "available": true,
    "sample_inputs": [],
    "states": ["idle", "editing", "running", "success", "blocked", "error", "offline"],
    "secret_handling": "detect, warn, redact screenshots, do not persist until explicit save"
  },
  "performance": {
    "lcp_ms_p75_mobile_target": 2500,
    "inp_ms_p75_target": 200,
    "cls_p75_target": 0.1,
    "critical_media_bytes_mobile_target": 450000,
    "critical_media_bytes_desktop_target": 800000
  },
  "proof": {
    "local_screenshots": [],
    "production_screenshots": [],
    "a11y_check": "",
    "media_check": "",
    "structured_data_check": "",
    "runner_smoke": "",
    "production_smoke": ""
  }
}
```

No room may be marked `prod_proven` until the production route has screenshots in light and dark, desktop and mobile, and until the runner/media states have been manually opened and used on the deployed origin.

##### First-Screen Aha Standard V4

The first viewport must answer the buyer's actual question before it asks them to read.

Allowed structure:

1. One headline that names the category and the outcome.
2. One subhead under 24 words that covers the three surfaces.
3. One primary action that starts proof.
4. One secondary action for docs, sales, or account handoff.
5. Three proof lanes with one verb, one object, and one evidence result each.
6. One trust/status strip that names proof scope without hiding open gates.

Forbidden in first-screen proof:

- internal wave names;
- abstract category phrases without concrete outcome;
- paragraphs that explain what a button should prove;
- hero copy that only describes API routing while compile/distill and run-anywhere are elsewhere;
- decorative media that does not show product state, artifact state, runtime state, or proof state;
- light-mode-only or dark-mode-only demo elements;
- nav highlights, popouts, underlines, or dropdowns that obscure the hero;
- CTA text that promises enterprise self-serve when sales review or external gates apply.

The recommended narrative is:

```text
Compile model behavior into owned AI artifacts.

Capture live API traffic, distill task models, and run signed .kolm artifacts across clouds, teams, and devices.
```

This is not final copy by itself. It is the V4 copy shape: concrete verb, concrete object, three-surface coverage, and proof language tied to `.kolm`.

##### Media Production Room V4

Every asset in `public/video`, `public/videos`, `public/media`, `public/assets`, `public/img`, and `public/og` must have a release decision.

| Decision | Meaning | Required evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `ship_critical` | Asset is needed above the fold or in first-value proof. | Route, dimensions, byte budget, preload strategy, alt/caption, screenshot, LCP evidence, owner. |
| `ship_lazy` | Asset is useful below the fold or on docs/support pages. | Route, lazy-loading behavior, dimensions, alt/caption, owner. |
| `compress` | Asset is useful but too heavy for current placement. | Original path, target format, target bytes, visual acceptance screenshot. |
| `regenerate` | Asset is off-brand, unclear, or inconsistent. | New prompt/brief, target route, acceptance criteria, old asset archive/delete plan. |
| `archive` | Asset is not shipped but may be source material. | Archive path, reason, owner, no route references. |
| `delete` | Asset is orphaned or harmful. | No references, no current proof value, diff reviewed. |

Budget rules:

- any first-screen raster asset above 450 KB mobile or 800 KB desktop needs explicit LCP proof;
- any generated PNG above 350 KB needs a WebP/AVIF variant or a written reason to keep PNG;
- any 1 MB plus image cannot be preloaded without product-proof justification;
- `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png` must be treated as a critical redline until compressed, replaced, moved below the fold, or proven harmless in production LCP;
- video files can exceed image budgets only when `preload` is disciplined and the poster carries first paint;
- every asset used in a screenshot report must map back to a media decision.

##### Interactive Runner Acceptance Scripts V4

The homepage or primary demo runner must pass these scripts before node `51` can relax.

| Script | User action | Required result |
|---|---|---|
| `DEMO-V4-T01` | Open homepage on mobile light mode. | Hero explains all three surfaces without horizontal scroll, text overlap, hidden nav, or broken media. |
| `DEMO-V4-T02` | Use keyboard only from address bar through runner success. | Focus order is visible, actions are operable, and status updates are announced or readable. |
| `DEMO-V4-T03` | Paste a synthetic OpenAI-compatible request. | Runner detects provider shape, warns about secrets, and shows captured request metadata. |
| `DEMO-V4-T04` | Switch to compile/distill sample. | Output names teacher/student scope, K-Score/eval scope, and blocked claims without promising unproven benchmarks. |
| `DEMO-V4-T05` | Switch to run-anywhere sample. | Output shows runtime/device/cloud targets, readiness status, and verification command or explicit simulation label. |
| `DEMO-V4-T06` | Trigger invalid input. | Error is specific, recoverable, and does not erase user input. |
| `DEMO-V4-T07` | Simulate offline or API failure. | Demo falls back to a synthetic local sample and labels what is simulated. |
| `DEMO-V4-T08` | Play explainer video if present. | Controls, captions, transcript, poster, structured data, and reduced-motion fallback are present. |
| `DEMO-V4-T09` | Navigate from runner success to account/docs/sales. | Handoff preserves proof context, does not leak secrets, and routes enterprise actions through the correct sales-review path. |
| `DEMO-V4-T10` | Re-run in dark and light desktop/mobile. | Screenshots show no overlap, no stale copy, no missing media, no broken nav underline/popout state, and no hidden closeout gates. |

##### Screenshot And Review Index V4

Automated all-route screenshots are required but not sufficient. The V4 proof index must include:

- route;
- node ID;
- claim ID;
- product surface;
- demo room ID;
- theme;
- viewport;
- auth state;
- input state;
- output state;
- media assets visible;
- external gates visible;
- reviewer type;
- human notes for hero, demo, media, nav, buttons, spacing, typography, copy clarity, and trust scope.

The final screenshot report must not only say "510/510 passed." It must prove the specific demo room states that matter for conversion and product truth.

| Ticket | Node | Build instruction | Acceptance proof |
|---|---:|---|---|
| `W-MEDIA-V4-001` | `51.1` | Create `docs/media-asset-decisions.json` or equivalent manifest for every public video/image/OG/press asset. | Every asset has owner, route use, decision, dimensions, byte size, alt/caption policy, and delete/compress/archive status. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-002` | `51.1` | Add a media budget verifier for critical-route assets. | Verifier fails on unapproved first-screen assets over budget and prints exact route/asset/node. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-003` | `51.2` | Register `kolm.demo_proof_room.v4` rooms for homepage, runner, ROI, K-Score, device, builder, and account handoff. | Each room has first-screen answer, three-surface proof coverage, assets, runner states, and proof commands. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-004` | `51.2` | Rewrite homepage proof shape around all three surfaces. | Five-second test records what, who, why-now, gateway/capture, compile/distill, and run-anywhere. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-005` | `51.2` | Replace or demote abstract hero media that does not show product proof. | First viewport media shows product artifact, runner state, runtime target, or verifiable proof. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-006` | `51.3` | Wire the explainer video only if the complete packet is ready. | Rendered route includes video element/player, captions, transcript, poster, stable URLs, schema, and production screenshot. |

| `W-MEDIA-V4-010` | `51.4` | Add secret-safe runner handling. | Secret-like values are warned, redacted from screenshots, and not persisted without explicit save. |

| `W-MEDIA-V4-014` | `51.5` | Add light-mode media and UI failure fixtures. | Demo/video/runner controls render in light and dark without invisible controls or broken popouts. |

| `W-MEDIA-V4-016` | `51.6` | Add production smoke for each demo proof room. | Smoke opens deployed route, exercises primary demo action, captures screenshot, and verifies no hidden external gate. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-017` | `51.6` | Add a copy/jargon linter for first-screen proof routes. | Linter flags internal wave names, unsupported enterprise CTAs, abstract one-surface copy, and paragraphs over budget. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-018` | `51.6` | Add runner-to-account/docs/sales handoff proof. | Handoff routes are correct, scoped, and secret-safe for all three product surfaces. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-019` | `51.6` | Add media provenance records for generated images. | Each generated asset has source prompt/brief, generator/tool, date, license/usage status, and replacement criteria. |
| `W-MEDIA-V4-020` | `51.6` | Add final demo production-room packet. | Packet contains media manifest diff, proof-room registry, runner scripts, video packet state, screenshots, accessibility, performance, production smoke, and remaining exceptions. |

##### Node `51` V4 Relax Redline

Node `51` V4 cannot relax until current local and deployed evidence proves:

- every demo/media artifact maps to node `51` or a named secondary node;
- no first-screen product proof serves only one of the three product surfaces;
- every critical media asset has byte budget, dimensions, route use, alt/caption policy, and keep/compress/archive/delete decision;
- `public/assets/hero-warm-paper-bg.png` and other 1 MB plus critical-path candidates are compressed, replaced, demoted, or proven safe by production LCP evidence;
- every explainer video claim has rendered route, captions, transcript, poster, schema, stable URLs, reduced-motion fallback, and production screenshot;
- if no complete video packet exists, the site does not claim one;
- the runner passes all ten V4 acceptance scripts in light/dark desktop/mobile;
- screenshots are indexed by demo room, claim, state, theme, viewport, auth state, asset, and reviewer;
- first-screen copy is concise, non-jargony, and complete across gateway/capture, compile/distill, and run-anywhere;
- nav, buttons, popouts, underlines, media controls, spacing, and typography are reviewed as part of the demo proof packet, not as unrelated polish;
- production smoke proves the deployed demo rooms on the same commit that release claims reference.

The V4 demo standard is: the first screen gives the buyer the product model, the demo proves all three surfaces, and every media byte either earns trust or leaves the release.

### Definition Of Finished Enough To Relax

The build is not finished when a single suite is green. It is finished enough to relax only when:

- every source file is classified in the file ledger;
- every route is implemented, removed, internal-only, or explicitly scoped as an external proof gate;
- every public page has one clear purpose, one primary message, complete metadata, clean nav state, and screenshot proof;
- every account page has real data, loading/empty/error states, and next actions;
- every product claim is backed by product graph/readiness evidence or by a closeout gate;
- every SDK/package either builds and has install proof or is visibly local-source-only;
- every worker/app path has a build/smoke command;
- generated docs/OpenAPI/product graph/closeout ledgers are current;
- full local gates and production smoke pass on the same commit;
- the final git diff contains only intentional Kolm changes with no unrelated corruption, mojibake, stale "Kolmogorov" branding, or formatting churn.

That is the practical meaning of "100 percent completion" for this repository.

#### Global 100 Percent Completion Certificate V2

Node `52` remains unused. The final completion redline is not another product branch; it is the certificate that binds nodes `0` through `51` into one releasable product. A branch can be excellent and the product can still be unfinished if files are unclassified, generated artifacts drift, production differs from local, public copy overclaims readiness, account pages lack real states, packages are source-only while pages imply installability, or screenshots pass while users cannot complete first-value journeys.

The certificate is deliberately stricter than a test suite. It asks whether Kolm is complete as a codebase, product, website, account surface, API, CLI/TUI, SDK ecosystem, model/runtime platform, commercial motion, and production service.

##### Current Repo Snapshot For Final Closure V2

| Snapshot item | Current observed state | Completion implication |
|---|---|---|
| Scanned file volume | A local scan excluding `.git`, `node_modules`, common build outputs, coverage, and nested audit screenshots still found `19,131` files and about `3.399 GB`. | The final file ledger cannot be optional. Unowned temporary, screenshot, fixture, archive, and generated files can hide release risk. |
| Largest extension classes | Current top classes include `.png` `5,171` files, `.json` `3,867`, no-extension `3,058`, `.html` `1,706`, `.js` `1,588`, `.md` `720`, `.log` `434`, `.bak` `389`, `.mjs` `295`, `.py` `278`, and `.kolm` `90`. | The final review must cover public media, generated JSON, executable scripts, docs, logs, backups, Python/Rust/workers, and artifacts, not just JavaScript source. |
| Largest directories in scan | Current largest file counts include `tmp`, `data`, `tmp-screenshots`, `public`, `reports`, `.tmp`, `tests`, `archive`, `src`, `scripts`, `_audit`, `apps`, `docs`, `packages`, `qa`, `audit-shots`, `sdk`, `workers`, and `examples`. | Completion requires an explicit keep/archive/delete/generated policy for non-product directories and large evidence directories. |
| Package script surface | `package.json` currently exposes `57` scripts, including release, depth, surfaces, screenshots, platform, package, benchmark, governance, compliance, brand, codegraph, and production smoke lanes. | The final gate must say which scripts are mandatory, advisory, local-only, external-credential-gated, or production-only. |
| Working tree cleanliness | Current scoped status shows the blueprint doc is untracked, and total `git status --short` count is non-zero. | The final completion certificate cannot be signed while unrelated dirty work, generated churn, untracked evidence, or concurrent-agent edits are unresolved. |
| Product graph and readiness evidence | Earlier nodes now define graph, readiness, closeout, claims, verticals, developer tools, demo media, and final proof packets. | The final certificate must consume those artifacts; it cannot duplicate or bypass node-level truth. |

##### External Completion Baseline V2

| Source | Current fact imported into the certificate | Kolm completion requirement |
|---|---|---|
| NIST SSDF SP 800-218, `https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/218/final` | NIST SSDF defines high-level secure software development practices that software producers can integrate into SDLCs. | Kolm final release needs secure-development evidence: threat modeling, secure defaults, vulnerability handling, dependency review, code review, and release controls. |
| SLSA v1.2, `https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.2/` | SLSA v1.2 is the current approved specification and includes build and source tracks plus provenance and verification-summary attestation formats. | Kolm artifacts, packages, generated files, and release bundles need provenance, source/build traceability, and verification summaries. |
| OWASP ASVS, `https://owasp.org/www-project-application-security-verification-standard/` | OWASP ASVS provides an open standard for normalizing application-security verification rigor and coverage. | Public/account/API/admin surfaces need a mapped application-security verification level, not just ad hoc auth tests. |
| WCAG 2.2 overview, `https://www.w3.org/WAI/standards-guidelines/wcag/` | WCAG is a stable, referenceable W3C standard for accessibility when published as a W3C Recommendation. | Final UI proof must include accessibility coverage for public, docs, account, media, forms, nav, tables, runners, and error states. |
| web.dev Web Vitals, `https://web.dev/articles/vitals` | Current Core Web Vitals are LCP, INP, and CLS; good thresholds are LCP within 2.5 seconds, INP 200 ms or less, CLS 0.1 or less, measured at the 75th percentile across mobile and desktop. | Kolm cannot call the site state-of-the-art without route-level lab and production RUM/performance evidence for critical public/account pages. |
| DORA metrics, `https://dora.dev/guides/dora-metrics/` | DORA uses software-delivery metrics such as change lead time, deployment frequency, change failure rate, failed deployment recovery time, and rework rate. | Kolm's final operational proof should report delivery speed and safety, not only "tests passed." |

##### Final Certificate Contract V2

The final release must emit `kolm.global_completion_certificate.v2`.

```json
{
  "certificate_id": "kolm-100-percent-YYYY-MM-DD",
  "commit": "git sha",
  "environment": {
    "local": "machine and node/runtime versions",
    "production": "https://kolm.ai origin and backend origin",
    "env_parity": "pass|partial|blocked"
  },
  "scope": {
    "master_nodes": "0-51",
    "files_scanned": 19131,
    "routes": "from generated API/docs/product graph",
    "public_pages": "from route manifest",
    "account_pages": "from account manifest",
    "scripts": 57,
    "packages": "from package registry",
    "artifacts": "from artifact registry"
  },
  "node_status": [],
  "file_ledger": {
    "keep": [],
    "fix": [],
    "generate": [],
    "archive": [],
    "delete": [],
    "external_gate": []
  },
  "verification": {
    "local": [],
    "production": [],
    "manual_use": [],
    "screenshots": [],
    "accessibility": [],
    "performance": [],
    "security": [],
    "supply_chain": [],
    "claim_scope": []
  },
  "known_limits": [],
  "external_gates": [],
  "release_decision": "ship|do_not_ship",
  "approvals": []
}
```

No certificate may be valid unless it names:

- the exact commit and deploy target;
- all master nodes and their done/not-done evidence;
- the file ledger decision for every repository path class;
- mandatory versus optional gates;
- local and production smoke results;
- manual-use journey proof;
- screenshot/media/account proof;
- external gates that cannot be solved locally;
- dirty worktree state and generated-artifact freshness;
- final public claim scope.

##### Completion Evidence Classes V2

| Evidence class | Required proof |
|---|---|
| Product truth | Product graph, readiness ledger, closeout ledger, product surfaces, journeys, public claims, pricing, docs, account, API, CLI, TUI, and marketplace all agree. |
| File closure | Every path is `product_source`, `test`, `fixture`, `generated`, `artifact`, `evidence`, `archive`, `temp`, `external_package`, or `delete_candidate`, with owner node and release policy. |
| Generated artifact closure | OpenAPI, API refs, product graph, readiness closeout, docs manifests, sitemap, screenshots, registry packs, SDK metadata, package manifests, and media registries are fresh and generated in a locked order. |
| Public website proof | Every route has purpose, first-screen clarity, metadata, structured data where relevant, nav state, responsive render, light/dark proof, no stale copy, and claim-scope evidence. |
| Account proof | Every account page has authenticated data, loading, empty, partial, blocked, error, success, next-action, support, and audit/export states. |
| API proof | Every public/private route has auth, schema, error shape, rate limit, tenant boundary, OpenAPI coverage, SDK compatibility, docs, and smoke proof. |
| CLI/TUI proof | Every advertised command/view has help, JSON output where relevant, errors, auth state, local/prod behavior, docs, and tests. |
| SDK/package proof | Every SDK/package either builds and installs from a release channel or is visibly source-only with closeout ticket and no public overclaim. |
| AI quality proof | K-Score, eval, benchmark, redaction, distillation, quantization, routing, runtime, and model claims have dataset/artifact/model/provider/runtime hashes and freshness. |
| Security proof | SSDF, ASVS, OWASP LLM risks, RBAC/tenant boundaries, secrets, provider keys, privacy lake, redaction, audit logs, and incident paths are verified or externally gated. |
| Supply-chain proof | SLSA-style provenance, signatures, SBOM, dependency state, CI permissions, release artifacts, package hashes, and verification summaries exist. |
| UI/UX proof | WCAG coverage, keyboard/focus, contrast, mobile, reduced motion, media captions/transcripts, nav/buttons/forms/tables/cards, and manual-use journey screenshots pass. |
| Performance proof | Critical routes meet LCP/INP/CLS targets or have documented exceptions, and heavy media/scripts/fonts are budgeted. |
| Production proof | Deployed `kolm.ai` and backend origins pass route, account, API, auth, cloud/readiness, media, structured data, screenshot, and rollback smoke. |
| Commercial proof | Pricing, plans, entitlements, billing, enterprise inquiry, quotas, support, legal docs, and sales claims align. |
| Operations proof | DORA-style release metrics, incident response, rollback, status, notifications, support bundle, observability, backups, and DR are current. |

##### Mandatory Gate Order V2

| Order | Gate | Why it must run here |
|---:|---|---|
| 1 | Freeze concurrent edits and identify owners. | Prevents parallel-agent churn from invalidating generated artifacts and screenshots. |
| 2 | Build the file ledger. | No gate can be complete if temp/generated/archive paths are unknown. |
| 3 | Build/regenerate canonical product artifacts. | Product graph, readiness, API refs, docs, sitemap, package manifests, and media registries must be current before claims are checked. |
| 4 | Run static hygiene. | Encoding, trailing whitespace, broken refs, stale branding, malformed HTML, schema drift, and generated-file drift must be clean before runtime tests. |
| 5 | Run focused code tests by owner node. | Failures should map back to nodes and file owners instead of one anonymous red suite. |
| 6 | Run product-depth gates. | Ensures routes, journeys, surfaces, kernel, claims, readiness, and closeout are structurally consistent. |
| 7 | Run security and supply-chain gates. | Prevents green product tests from hiding auth, tenant, dependency, package, or provenance gaps. |
| 8 | Run UI, accessibility, media, and performance proof. | Confirms rendered product quality, not only backend behavior. |
| 9 | Run manual-use journey labs. | Proves real users can complete first-value workflows. |
| 10 | Run production smoke on deployed origin. | Local success is not production completion. |
| 11 | Review external gates and claim scope. | Prevents package, certification, benchmark, partner, and live-cloud gaps from being marketed as shipped. |
| 12 | Sign release packet and clean git. | Final state must be reproducible, intentional, and auditable. |

##### Global Done/Not-Done Decision Rules V2

| Decision | Rule |
|---|---|
| `done` | Current local and production evidence proves the requirement at its full scope, and the evidence is linked in the certificate. |
| `done_local_only` | Local implementation and tests pass, but production or external proof is not available; public copy must scope it. |
| `blocked_external` | Requirement depends on certification, package registry, benchmark publication, partner adoption, live customer proof, or credentials outside the local repo. |
| `blocked_env` | Requirement depends on production/provider/cloud credentials missing from the local shell but expected in deployment. |
| `partial` | Some routes/files/states pass, but coverage is narrower than the claim. |
| `contradicted` | Evidence shows the current product does something different from the claim. |
| `unknown` | No authoritative evidence exists. Unknown is not done. |
| `delete_or_demote` | The feature, asset, route, or claim should be removed or visibly demoted if proof is not worth building. |

##### Final Work Tickets V2

| Ticket | Work | Acceptance |
|---|---|---|
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-001` | Generate `docs/global-completion-certificate.v2.json`. | Certificate lists commit, nodes, files, routes, scripts, packages, artifacts, verification, limits, external gates, and release decision. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-002` | Generate full file ledger. | Every path class has owner node, release policy, generated/manual state, keep/fix/archive/delete decision, and proof link. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-003` | Add mandatory gate manifest. | Package scripts and external commands are marked mandatory, advisory, credential-gated, production-only, or obsolete. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-004` | Add generated-artifact lock order. | Product graph, API refs, OpenAPI, readiness, docs, sitemap, packages, media, screenshots, and reports regenerate without races. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-005` | Add node status rollup. | Nodes `0` through `51` each have done/local-only/partial/external/blocked status and evidence path. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-006` | Add public/account route release matrix. | Every public and account route has purpose, owner node, auth state, screenshot state, metadata, claim scope, and production smoke. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-007` | Add codebase language/runtime matrix. | Node/JS, Python, Rust, shell, PowerShell, workers, SDKs, packages, and artifacts each have build/test/smoke commands. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-008` | Add security verification packet. | SSDF, ASVS, OWASP LLM, tenant boundaries, secrets, auth, privacy, redaction, provider keys, and incident flows have evidence or gates. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-009` | Add supply-chain packet. | SLSA-style provenance, SBOM, signatures, package hashes, dependency review, CI permissions, and artifact verification are attached. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-010` | Add UX/accessibility/performance packet. | WCAG, keyboard, focus, contrast, reduced motion, Core Web Vitals, media, screenshot, and manual review evidence is attached. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-011` | Add AI quality packet. | K-Score, evals, benchmarks, distillation, quantization, routing, redaction, runtime, and model claims link to raw reports and limits. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-012` | Add commercial/legal packet. | Pricing, billing, enterprise, terms, privacy, DPA/BAA, support, procurement, and sales claims align with product truth. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-013` | Add operational readiness packet. | Observability, status, alerts, incident response, backups, DR, rollback, DORA metrics, and support bundles are current. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-014` | Add production replay packet. | Deployed origin proves API, account, docs, public pages, media, auth, cloud/readiness, screenshots, and rollback on exact commit. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-015` | Add external-gate ledger review. | Package, certification, public benchmark, partner, credential, and live-customer gates are visible and not marketed as shipped. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-016` | Add stale asset cleanup plan. | `.bak`, temp, screenshot, archive, generated image, log, and large data paths are kept, archived, ignored, or deleted intentionally. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-017` | Add final clean-git proof. | `git status`, `git diff --check`, generated drift checks, and file ownership prove the final diff is intentional. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-018` | Add release notes and rollback package. | Release notes, changed surfaces, known limits, migrations, support risks, and rollback steps are linked. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-019` | Add post-release monitoring plan. | First 24 hours of metrics, errors, conversion, cloud readiness, billing, support, and incident thresholds are defined. |
| `W-GLOBAL-CLOSE-020` | Add final human signoff checklist. | Product, frontend, backend, security, docs, research, support, and founder signoffs cite evidence, not opinion. |

##### Global Relax Redline V2

The repository can relax only when:

- node `52` remains unused unless a genuinely new branch cannot attach to nodes `0` through `51`;
- every node `0` through `51` has a current status and evidence;
- every file path has a ledger decision;
- every generated artifact is fresh and regenerated in lock order;
- every route, page, command, package, artifact, worker, and script is implemented, removed, internal-only, or explicitly scoped;
- every public/account/docs claim maps to product truth or an external closeout gate;
- all mandatory local gates pass on a clean tree;
- all mandatory production smokes pass on the deployed commit;
- manual-use labs prove the critical buyer, developer, operator, account, and admin journeys;
- UI, accessibility, performance, media, and screenshot proof covers light/dark desktop/mobile and important states;
- security, privacy, tenant, supply-chain, legal, and commercial evidence is current;
- external gates are visible and never marketed as shipped;
- final git status is clean except intentionally tracked release evidence;
- the final certificate says `ship`.

Anything less is progress, not completion.
